IncidentDetails: 1 number forward spring line got stuck in mooring bopat propeller area
IncidentEventDetails: - Vessel was coming port side alongside to the berth at Davao under Pilotgae,  - 2 tugs were assisting in the berthing operations alongwith 2 mooring boats,  - 1 number forward spring was passed to the mooring boat,  - Mooring boat approached the dolphin and passed the eye end to the dolphin,  - At the same time the boat manouvered over the rope,  - This resulted in the rope getting stuck in the boat propeller area,
ImmediateAction: - Pilot informed,  - Mooring boat men then dived in the water and after prolonged efforts cleared the rope.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The spring line got minor abrasion marks on the surface at various places totalling to about 2m length.
DirectCause: Rope getting stuck in the mooring boat propeller.
IndirectCause: Incorrect manouevering of the mooring boat.
RootCause: Lack of awareness, unknown (shore team involved)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Close monitoring of the mooring PIC on board and advice / alert immediately if anything is amiss.

IncidentDetails: Foam leak.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was rolling heavily in seaway due to bad weather, foam tank filling line leading to A deck filling cap had a flange which was not tightened completely. Due to roll foam entered this line and started coming out in drops.
ImmediateAction: Flange tightened completely, area cleaned of all foam remnants.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was a loss of about 20ml of foam compound as estimated by looking at the foam sprayed on the Bridge from t bulkhead.
DirectCause: Loose flange connection on the foam filling line.
IndirectCause: Vessel rolling and pitching excessively due to SW monsoon season in the Indian OCean area.
RootCause: Incomplete work by yard staff and imporoper checks by ship-staff.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To check as feasible all flanges on the foam line for tightness.

IncidentDetails: 4engr Found fire on the  incinerator body  below burner unit,due to dripping of wasteoil accompanied by back fire.But was extinguished ontime by 4engr & mm before the fire spreading to save-all tray,where dripped oil was accumulated in small qty.
IncidentEventDetails: 4Engr finished his ER rounds & was monitoring machinery parameters @2050 hrs IN ECR,during then he found some flashing lights on the incinerator camera monitor inside ECR.He immediately activated engineer's call & ran outside ecr to confirm presence of fire.Same time fire alarm too activated picking up signal from flame detector.He found fire on the  incinerator body  below burner unit,due to dripping of wasteoil accompanied by back fire.But was extinguished ontime by 4engr & mm with nearby portable fire extinguisher , before the fire spreading to save-all tray,where dripped oil was accumulated in small qty.
ImmediateAction: Raised Engr's call immediately & extinguished fire by portable fire extinguisher,thereby ceasing the fire spreading to oil accumulated in saveall tray.(fire alarm was triggered at the same time by flame detector.Hypermist was not activated,as smoke alrarm was not triggered ,as there was no  smoke)    CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:  Proper corrective actions is to clear all air holes in side the furnace & fresh build up of refractory,which is possible in drydock only for which DD specs are in place.  Due to damged refractory, solid sludge or oily rags are not burned inthe incinerator since takeover(app.8 months).  Present corrective action in place to monitor hourly & cleaaning of furnace after  4 hour of firing,which was good until now & refractory patch up every fortnight.  INDIRECT CAUSE:  Due to damaged incinerator refractory & pile  up of refractory patching,there is a lack of airholes,resulting in cone formation inside around burner.So there is a regular inspection every one hour by engrs for cone formation ,when incinerator is firing & stopping of incinerator after every 4 hours of firing to clear debris.In Present case, debris formation was not visible from sightglass.  Also FYI-incinerator was fired @1800hrs after refractory patching up on 23rd july 2014.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A small part of lagging on incinerators body got burnt.
DirectCause: Back fire accompanied by dripping of oil.
IndirectCause: Due to damaged incinerator refractory & pile  up of refractory patching,there is a lack of airholes,resulting in cone formation inside around burner.So there is a regular inspection every one hour by engrs for cone formation ,when incinerator is firing & stopping of incinerator after every 4 hours of firing to clear debris.In Present case, debris formation was not visible from sightglass.  Also FYI-incinerator was fired @1800hrs after refractory patching up on 23rd july 2014.
RootCause: Cone formation around burner due to clogged air holes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper corrective actions is to clear all air holes in side the furnace & fresh build up of refractory,which is possible in drydock only for which DD specs are in place.  Due to damged refractory, solid sludge or oily rags are not burned inthe incinerator since takeover(app.8 months).  Present corrective action in place to monitor hourly & cleaaning of furnace after  4 hour of firing,which was good until now & refractory patch up every fortnight.

IncidentDetails: blackout for 20 sec
IncidentEventDetails: while trying to shift load from AE#1 to AE#3,frequency dropped due to sluggish or irresponsive of governor.
ImmediateAction: AE#2 was started from ECr and taken onload.System recovered and back to normal in 5-10 minutes
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NONE other than blackout.
DirectCause: Frequency dip due to irresponsive or sluggishness of governor.
RootCause: May be governor need an overhaul.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: AE governors to be overhauled in DD
Corrective Action: AE governors to be overhauled in DD

IncidentDetails: No Damages / Injuries
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was anchored at Eastern OPL singapore in posn 01 deg 27.09'N, 104 deg 41.88E and on 27th Aug 2014, around 0400hrs LT,  six robbers boarded the vessel from stern. Duty AB on watch noticed this and informed bridge, subsequently raised the alarm. Robbers fled the scene on hearing the alarm in a small dengue boat. As the vessel is in piracy area, all accommodation doors and stores were locked which prevented robbers from taking any ship stores or access to the accommodation.
ImmediateAction: Raised alarm, all crew mustered on bridge. Anti piracy watch were doubled up.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: NIL
ClosingNote: Alert ship staff has averted a possible security Incident

IncidentEventDetails: REEFER CONTAINER LOADED WITH BROCOLLI SET TEMP. -14C WAS LOADED AT SAN PEDRO FOR DISCHARGE AT YOKOHAMA. TEMP. IN THE MANIFEST OF SAID CONTAINER WAS 0*C. AFTER LOADING THIS CONTAINER ON BOARD EL. OFF. WHILE CARRYING OUT THE CONDITION CHECK OF REEFER,DID NOT COMPARE THE SET TEMP WITH THE MANIFEST TEMP. AND REPORTED ALL REEFERS LOADED IN GOOD CONDITION. ON THE SECOND DAY AFTER DEPARTURE FROM SAN PEDRO DURING ROUTINE REEFER ROUNDS, DECK CADET NOTICED THE TEMP. DIFFERENCE AND INFORMED TO EL. OFF. EL. OFF. INFORMED CADET THAT HE WILL INFORM THE SAME TO THE CHIEF OFFICER BUT THE SAME WAS NOT CONVEYED TO CH. OFF. ON SUSEQUENT DAYSDURING ROUTINE REEFER ROUNDS , THOUGH THE CADET NOTICED THE TEMP DIFFERENCE AND SINCE THE SAME WAS INFORMED TO THE EL. OFF., DUE TO HIS LACK OF KNOWLEDGE THOUGHT THE SET TEMP OF -14*C NEEDS TO BE MAINTAINED AND RECORDED UNTIL THE REEFER WAS DISCHARGED AT YOKOHAMA.
ImmediateAction: MEETING WAS CALLED UP ON THE BRIDGE BY MASTERS FOR ALL DECK OFFICERS, EL. OFF. AND CH. ENG. PROCEDURES WERE DISCUSSED IN THE MEETING AGAIN AND ALL THE HANDS WERE GIVEN INSTRUCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES FOR REEFER CARGO MONITORING FROM THE TIME IT IS PLUGGED TO THE TIME IT IS DISCHARGED.CH. OFFICER'S STANDING PORT INSTRUCTIONS / ORDERS AMMENDED AND A NEW SECTION FOR REEFER CARE, HANDLING AND MONITORING INCLUDED FOR THE GUIDANCE OF OOW.
DirectCause: SET TEMPRATURE OF THE REEFER CONTAINER AT THE TIME OF LOADING WAS NOT SAME AS MANIFEST AND SHIP STAFF DID NOT COMPARE MANIFEST AND SET TEMP.
IndirectCause: LACK OF PROPER COMMUNICATION AMONG SHIP STAFF
RootCause: LACK OF COMPLIANCE WITH COMPANY POLICIES FOR REEFER CARGO AS MENTIONED IN CARGO OPERATIONS MANUAL CH. 10.5
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MEETING WAS CALLED UP ON THE BRIDGE BY MASTER FOR ALL THE DECK OFFICERS, EL. OFF AND CH. ENG. PROCEDURES WERE DISCUSSED IN THE MEETING AGAIN AND ALL THE HANDS WERE GIVEN INSTRUCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES FOR REEFER CARGO MONITORING FROM THE TIME IT IS PLUGGED TO THE TIME IT IS DISCHARGED. CH. OFFICER'S PORT STANDING ORDERS AMENDED AND A NEW SECTION FOR REEFER CARE, HANDLING AND MONITORING INCLUDED FOR THE GUIDANCE OF OOW
Corrective Action: - Alert sent to all Vessels regarding this incident to ensure strict compliance with Cargo operations Non Tanker Manual (30th Sept 2014 / SMS Team) - DCR raised to include verification of Reefer monitoring log by Master and Chief Engineer, in addition to Electrical Officer and Chief Officer(15th March 2015/CMS Team) - DCR raised for Electrical Officers to be approved for employing and briefed prior employment by Technical and SMS team. (30th Mar 2015/CMS Team/SMS Team) - Master, Chief Engineer,
LessionLearnt: As per attached Investigation Report
ReviewComments: As per attached Investigation Report
ClosingNote: As per attached Investigation Report

IncidentDetails: While securing the port side accomodation ladder the fall parted and the ladder fell into the water, hanging from the upper platform. At the time of the accident the vessel was manouvering in between pilot station and berth. No injuries. Various parts of the accommodation ladder damaged and deformed.
IncidentEventDetails: Please see attached Statement of Facts
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gangway Wire Parted and Gangway suffered some structural damage
DirectCause: Direct Causes: While securing the Gangway the fall wire parted resulting in Gangway falling in water.  Indirect Causes:  Material failure due to excessive use of Accommodation Ladder, even though material damage not evident on the external surface of the wire during routine inspection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Frequency of renewal of Gangway falls to be changed to annual(12 Months) 2. Fleet alert sent to all Vessels on 17th Oct 2014
Corrective Action: 1. Frequency of renewal of Gangway falls to be changed to annual(12 Months) 2. Fleet alert sent to all Vessels on 17th Oct 2014
LessionLearnt: Material failure due to excessive use of Accommodation Ladder, even though material damage not evident on the external surface of the wire during routine inspection.
ClosingNote: Accommodation Ladder Falls renewal period changed from 30Months intreval to 12Months intreval

IncidentDetails: Stbd Side Accomodation Ladder damage.Turn Table on upper end of the gangway was bent and also the davit arm and the supporting pipe of the gangway to which Davit arm is welded also found bent.
IncidentEventDetails: Cargo operation was in progress with 4 gantry cranes and found all 4 cranes discharging the cargo from stbd side, which caused the vessel to heel to port.OOW started heel correction when heel was 1 * to port,but vessel kept on listing to port.Cargo foreman was informed of this and advised foreman to evenly spread out the gantry operation on port and stbd side.heel was corrected and vessel was upright.AB on gangway watch tended to the gangway and height adjusted to level of wharf.Gangway watch AB handed over the gangway watch to OS who was on duty and proceeded inside the accom to meet Bosun and discuss the work Plan.Vessel had riding crew signed on at Kaohsiung and the management had decided to tempororily change  the roster for watchkeepers and bosun called up the AB on gangway to discuss regarding same.Gangway AB after handing over the watch to OS , failed to inform this to the OOW.OOW after correcting the heel proceeded for his breakfast.Around this time vessel started to heel to stbd side and OS who was on gangway watch informed OOW to rush to gangway immediatly.By the time OOW arrived the gangway ,gangway touched the fender and he found the turn table on the upper part got dented and the davit arm also found to be dented
ImmediateAction: Tech SI and Marine SI  and local agent informed.Additional watchman was put on the wharf to prevent the stevedores and other personal using gangway and access to vessel through gangway restricted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAmaged GAngway turn table and davit arm repaired by shore workshop. Gangway loadtest carried out in the presence of Class NKK surveyor
DirectCause: Gangway watch handed over to OS who has less experience in monitoring such jobs,without informing OOW.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gangway watch AB should not leave his watch from gangway without informing OOW.Whenever gangway watch AB should be relieved,he has to be relieved either by another AB or Bosun.OS & trainee should not be put on gangway watch.Gangway watchman advised to stand in a position where the bottom end of gangway is in line of visual sight at all times.
Corrective Action: Gangway watch AB should not leave his watch from gangway without informing OOW.Whenever gangway watch AB should be relieved,he has to be relieved either by another AB or Bosun.OS & trainee should not be put on gangway watch.Gangway watchman advised to stand in a position where the bottom end of gangway is in line of visual sight at all times.
ClosingNote: Duplicated from 2014

IncidentDetails: AE #1, UNIT #4 CYLINDER HEAD CAMSHAFT SIDE EXHAUST VALVE TO BE DEFECTIVE. AE L.O. IS CONTAMINATED WITH WATER LEAKED FROM THE DEFECTIVE EXHAUST VALVE SEAT
IncidentEventDetails: DURING ROUTINE WATCKEEPING ROUNDS IT WAS OBSERVED THAT THE EXHAUST TEMPERATURE FOR AE #1 UNIT #4 WAS FOUND TO BE HIGHER THAN THE OTHER UNITS. THE PEAK PRESSURE TAKEN SHOWED LOWER VALUE FOR THE AFOREMENTIONED UNIT COMPARED TO OTHER UNITS. AFTER STOPPING AE THE CRANKCASE WAS INSPECTED AFTER 30 MIN AND FOUND WATER LEAKING FROM UNIT #4 INTO THE CRANKCASE.
ImmediateAction: AE #1 CYLINDER HEAD FOR UNIT #4 WAS OVERHAULED AND FOUND THAT THE EXHAUST VALVE AND VALVE SEAT ON THE CAMSHAFT SIDE WAS FOUND TO BE DEFECTIVE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAMAGE TO A/E CYLINDER HEAD COMPONENTS. CONTAMINATION OF A/E LUB OIL SUMP DUE TO COOLING WATER LEAKAGE DROM DAMAGED V/V SEAT SEAL RING.
DirectCause: EXCESSIVE WEAR & TEAR OF COMPONENTS.
IndirectCause: INSUFFICIENT MONITORING OF PARAMETERS & FAILURE TO IDENTIFY & WARN OF IMPENDING FAULT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ENHANCED MONITOING OF A/E PARAMETERS. AVAILIBILITY OF ADEQUATE SPARES FOR REPAIR/ RENEWAL.
ClosingNote: Suitable action taken by Crew, by renewing damaged parts and made engine functional.

IncidentEventDetails: AFTER COMPLETING THE FINISHED WITH ENGINE ROUTINES IN PORT, ALL AE WERE STOPPED AND ONLY AE #1 WAS RUNNING AND WAS ONLOAD. THE OMD HIGH ALARM WAS ACTIVATED AND AE#1 TRIPPED CREATING A BLACKOUT. THE EMG GEN IMMEDIATELY CAME ONLOAD AND THE STANDBY AE STARTED AND CAME ONLOAD, THE POWER WAS RESTORED AND ALL THE ASSOCIATED EQUIPMENTS WERE STARTED BACK ON SEQUENTIAL START.
ImmediateAction: AFTER CONFIRMING A STANDBY AE IS AVAILABLE. AE #1 WAS ISOLATED AND THE SINTERED BRONZE FILTERS FOR THE AE OMD SENSOR WAS RENEWED.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Preferential trip of Running A/E.
DirectCause: Insufficient monitoring of A/E OMD parameters.
IndirectCause: Lack of periodic check & Cleaning of A/E OMD sensor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular inspection & Cleaning of all A/E OMD sensors.
LessionLearnt: insufficient monitoring
ClosingNote: insufficient monitoring

IncidentDetails: Life boat engine space fire broke out while trying out engine. Found starting motor and its control circuit wiring damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1530 hrs, during routine inspection and trying out of life boat engine, engine failed to start and smoke was noticed coming out of the engine space. When cover was removed to check, there was a flame present inside the space. Immediately fire was extinguish by means of DCP portable extinguisher.
ImmediateAction: Life boat  starting motor, alternator and its control circuit wiring continuity checked. Insulation of starting motor and alternator found to be zero. Stator and rotor winding and bearing of starting motor found damaged.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Starting motor, alternator and its control circuit wiring damaged.
DirectCause: Damage to the motor might be a result of overloading of the motor and possible result of damaged bearings of the motor. The battery terminal lugs were noted to be melted/ damaged on an earlier occasion.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renew starting motor and check other electric circuit periodically.
ClosingNote: Damage to the motor might be a result of overloading of the motor and possible result of damaged bearings of the motor. The battery terminal lugs were noted to be melted/ damaged on an earlier occasion.

IncidentDetails: NO.1 MAIN LUB OIL PUMP MOTOR DAMAGED DUE TO INSULATION RESISTANCE OF 2 OF THE MOTOR WINDINGS INDICATING 0 M ohm WHEN CHECKED WITH THE MEGGER METER
IncidentEventDetails: WHILE  STARTING NO.1 MAIN LUB OIL PUMP  AT 1 HOUR NOTICE PRIOR SHIFTING BERTH AT DURBAN OBSERVED THAT THE MOTOR WAS TRIPPING AFTER A FEW SECONDS. UPON INVESTIGATION FOUND 2 OF THE MOTOR WINDINGS SHOWING 0 M ohm READING & 1 WINDING SHOWING 100 Mohm READING WHEN CHECKED WITH THE MEGGER METER
ImmediateAction: VESSEL IN THE PROCESS OF RENEWING THE DAMAGED MOTOR WITH A SPARE MOTOR
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: M/E NO.1 MAIN LUB OILP/P MOTOR DAMAGED WHICH CAN'T BE REPAIRED AS MOTOR WINDING BURNED.
DirectCause: EXCESSIVE WEAR & TEAR RESULTING IN P/P  MOTOR WINDINGS GETTING  BURNED
IndirectCause: INSUFFICIENT MONITORING & CHECKING FOR ABNORMALITY
RootCause: FAILURE TO IDENTIFY THE FAULT BEFORE DAMAGE COULD OCCUR
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CHECK ALL E/R MOTORS FOR ANY ABNORMAL VIBRATION ,SOUND.  FREQUENT MOTOR WINDING INSULATION CHECKS.
LessionLearnt: excessive wear
ClosingNote: excessive wear

IncidentDetails: Port side accomodation ladder MOT platform extending plate which can be connected to gangway lower platform got buckled from sides.
IncidentEventDetails: Duty AB (Amal Raj ) On 23rd Dec 2014  performed four hours of gangway watch in morning from 0400 to 0800 hrs after his duty he was rested to comply with ISF    watchkeeper and was planned to be utilised in evening due to refer loading scheduled to be late in the evening. In the evening after performing his evening watch  from    1600 to 2000HRS again he was releived as there was an hour remaining for the refers to come on board. As soon as the vessel got information about the reefer loading the    duty AB who was releived at 2000hrs ( Amalraj) was called again at 2050 and he came at 2100hrs and releived present duty AB( suresh) at 2100hrs Duty AB ( Amal raj)    took over the watch at 2100hrs. As per the plan present Duty AB ( Suresh) watch timings (2000hrs to 2400hrs) was deployed for refer plugging duty. It was taken into    account that the gangway watchkeepers are not assigned other duties while on gangway and solely were deployed for ISPS and gangway watch. After taking over the watch    around 2120 to 2135 hrs the gangway MOT platform was reported to be buckled due to negligence in attending the gangway. As three gantry cranes were only performing    loading operation gangway was required to be attended regulary to avoid the damages. In failure of attending the gangway regularly the MOT platform extension  got buckled.
ImmediateAction: Immediately the buckled MOT platform was taken out and gangway was placed on jetty to provide access to the stevedores and other people boarding the vessel. Intend to repair the MOT extension at sea . Crew again briefed on the importance of keeping a good gangway watch .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gangway MOT platform extension plate got buckled from ends.
DirectCause: Proper procedures not followed of gangway watchkeeping standing orders not complied with.
IndirectCause: Not complying with COSWP.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting conducted with all crew and again explained procedures of gangway watchkeeping. Emphasis laid on COSWP and allowing for tides and cargo loading / unloading.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: MOT gangway repaired at sea by Crew.

IncidentDetails: 1 number mooring rope protective covering peeled off for a length of 1m about 50m from the eye.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was coming alongside and 2 numbers aft springs were passed from Poop deck witha long lead to shore bitt forward of break of accommodation. When this line was being heaved, it snagged upon the end of support beading of Panama fairleads and about 1m pritective layer of the rope was peeled off.
ImmediateAction: Rope was heaved and tightened, damaged part was protected with sleeve.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: About 1m of one number HMPE mooring rope used as aft spring line damaged.
DirectCause: Protruding beading on Panama choke area.
IndirectCause: Wrong handling of the rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Damaged area of the rope to be close ceased with twine for 2 layers extending 100mm beyond the damaged part on both sides.
Corrective Action: Pls discuss the Manager's instructions 033 of 2014 on HMPE ropes with the crew.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Crew training to be carried out to enhance the familiarity on use HMPE ropes.
ClosingNote: Crew Training carried out wrt Manager's instruction. A training session on HMPE recommended for crew during pre-joining.

IncidentDetails: Microwave oven in officer's pantry found with damaged door sealing rubber.
IncidentEventDetails: Microwave oven rubber packing found burned for about 50mm length probably due to leaving metallic object inside thge oven.
ImmediateAction: Microwave oven removed from service.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Rubber packing of the microwave opven door burnt out for a length of 50mm.
DirectCause: Most probably metal was placed inside the microwave and switched on.
IndirectCause: Negligence, lack of awareness
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training
Corrective Action: Pls carry out the training of crew as proposed. Pls discuss the manufacturer's instruction manual for recommended operating procedures and safety precautions for the oven model. Kindly advise crew not to operate an oven if the door does not close firmly or is bent, warped, or otherwise damaged.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Do not operate a microwave oven unattended. Follow the manufacturer's instruction manual for recommended operating procedures and safety precautions for your oven model. Be aware of whether your microwave oven has a longer or shorter cooking time than average. Don't operate an oven if the door does not close firmly or is bent, warped, or otherwise damaged. Modifications to microwave ovens are not permitted. If a microwave oven has a damaged door, hinge, latch or sealing surface, the oven must be removed from service until it has been repaired and determined to be safe.
ClosingNote: Crew training carried out. The crew to be cautious when moving about work area to look out for unsafe conditions that can create hazardous situations. Training to increase awareness is the key to enhance safety culture on board.

IncidentDetails: EPIRM unit found cracked at the bottom.
IncidentEventDetails: As part of departure checks, checked the EPIRB, found the same with a crack of about 200mm on the bottom casing.
ImmediateAction: Equipment self check carried out, found in working order, Informed office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: EPIRB unit found with cracked bottom half.
DirectCause: The EPIRB unit was not secured properly in the housing after the last monthly inspection/test carried out by the responsible person. The Unit slipped out of the housing due to vibration from vessel's movements.
IndirectCause: Unknown
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To get advice from the Maker and replace the unit.
Corrective Action: The ship staff to be briefed on proper securing of the equipment after carrying out inspection.
ClosingNote: The EPIRB unit was replaced on 08th May at Bordeaux (France).

IncidentDetails: Found out one lifejacket is missing on the stowed box at embarkation deck
IncidentEventDetails: At 1000H UTC 16th Jan 2015 MRCC contact the vessel thru INMARSAT phone and advice that they found one lifejacket with the name of our vessel (Churchill Bulker) immediately conduct investigation and inspection of all lifejackets on board. 20 lifejackets was being stowed in a container located on the embarkation deck (as per DMA requirement) and during tha passage along English Channel and Bay of Biscay (around 9th, 10th and 11th) vessel encounter very rough seas with gale winds. The container which was thelifejacket being stowed the lashing was loosen and the cover of the container sligghtly open and opun resesuring the container deck crew assume that all lifejackets is still intact inside due to no abnormal stowage found not knowing that one lifejacket was probably blown away by the strong gale winds. After checking and verifying the missing one lifejacket. MRCC Dover was been informed and advice them that we confirm that we have one missing lifejacket and advice them that all 20 crew are accounted for and no one is missing
ImmediateAction: Temporarily replace the missing lifejacket with one available on spare cabin and raise requisition
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: one lifejacket is missing insde the palstic box on the embarkation deck
DirectCause: the container box lacking of lashing material
IndirectCause: the lashing loosen up and the cover slightly open due to strong winds
RootCause: the box was not properly secured by lashing materials
Proposedcorrectiveactions: additonal lashing material must be apply to the container box
Corrective Action: Prior onset of Heavy weather responsible officer to thoroughly check all equipment (inside and outside accommodation) are secured properly. Appropriate log entry to be made after thorough checks are made.
LessionLearnt: All storage containers outside the accommodation to be secured properly at all times.
ReviewComments: Heavy weather precautions to be followed prior onset of Heavy weather.
ClosingNote: Supply of Life jacket will be arranged at next port. Please discuss this incident in the next safety committee meeting and reinforce the importance of proper lashing of all equipment especially LSA/FFA outside the accommodation.

IncidentDetails: ONE MOORING ROPE - HEAD LINE - PARTED (100 MTR LENGTH)
IncidentEventDetails: AT PORT LOUIS  DUE TO HEAVY WEATHER FORECAST VESSEL INSTRUCTED BY PORT TO LEAVE THE BERTH AND  PROCEED TO SEA.  1240 HRS - SINGLED UP  1306 HRS - TUGS MADE FAST FWD AND AFT. FWD WITH 2 GOOD SHIPS LINE AND AFT TUG WITH TUGS LINE  1324 HRS - FWD ONE TUG SHIP'S LINE PARTED.    FWD TUG MADE FAST WITH 2 SHIPS LINE OF EQUAL LENGTH. WHILE PULLING OUT VESSEL ONE OF THE TUG SHIP'S LINE PARTED.
ImmediateAction: EXTRA MOORING ROPES WERE KEPT READY.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: one head line rope parted and one slightly damaged which was discovered later
DirectCause: heavy wx
IndirectCause: tugs are not equippped with tug lines.
RootCause: Tug not equiped with tug line
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Suitable Ropes to be used. Use of tug to be well planned, to avoid excessive load on Mooring ropes
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Suitable Ropes to be used. Use of tug to be well planned, to avoid excessive load on Mooring ropes
ReviewComments: To ensure Weather conditions are monitored and situation accessed prior mooring operations.
ClosingNote: Appreciate ship staffs immediate action

IncidentDetails: DUE TO HEAVY ROLLING AND PITCHING ( IN HEAVY WEATHER) 12 CONTAINERS SHEARED OFF FROM BOTTOM CORNER CASTING. IN ADDITION,VESSELS LASHING GEAR(Mentioned Below) AND GRATING OF PORT SIDE OF BAY 52 , AND AT TWO PLACES ON HATCH COVER INDENTATION ALSO OCCURED DUE TO SHEARING OFF OF CONTAINER.    TOTAL  LASHING GEAR DAMAGES                             FLX5-TB-50K (TURNBUCKLE FOR LONG BARS) 5  EUQILASH TURNBUCKLES (ELT-65L)   24  LONG BAR     5  SHORT BAR     4  TWIST LOCKS CV-20   40
IncidentEventDetails: On 14th Jan 2015   @ 0830 hrs SMT ( 14th Jan 0730 UTC)  vessel experienced very rough weather and has been continuously monitoring weather forecast by all available means and also through WNI . Vessel complying with WNI suggested route and RPM /Speed weather permitting.   ( Please refer attached weather chart and route details as received from WNI  )    On 15th Jan  2015  at 0500 hrs SMT ( 0400 hrs UTC ) vessel started rolling violently to strong westerly winds and long swell . During the hours of darkness, it was difficult to estimate the height of the swell, due to  occasional slamming  vessel  started rolling violently and at times on which  course  and speed adjusted to ease her according to good seamanship under the circumstances. Weather was very rough all throughout the day . On checking and tightening lashing the containers noted containers at bay 52 on deck had damages as attached .    Noted container lashing gear such as turn buckles and lashing rods were also damaged ( please refer attached photo’s )      On 16th Jan 2015 between 0830 hrs  ~ 1200 hrs SMT  (0730 – 1100 UTC)  conducted a thorough inspection of the vessel and observed the attached  damages.      Vessel has been following the route and speed advised by WNI since departure Bremerhaven 12th Jan 2015  1800 hrs LT.
ImmediateAction: Vessel adjusted course and speed to reduce the effect of heavy weather. However due to  confused swell and boistorous weather vessel was unable to steady.Broken lashing renewed. Lashings tighten up. wooden chocks placed to prevent further damage.Note of protest for heavy weather filed.P & I club also informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DUE TO HEAVY ROLLING AND PITCHING ( IN HEAVY WEATHER) 12 CONTAINERS SHEARED OFF FROM BOTTOM CORNER CASTING. IN ADDITION,VESSELS LASHING GEAR( ATTCAHED FIND THE LIST) AND GRATING OF PORT SIDE OF BAY 52 , AND AT TWO PLACES ON HATCH COVER INDENTATION ALSO OCCURED DUE TO SHEARING OFF OF CONTAINER.    TOTAL  LASHING GEAR DAMAGES                             FLX5-TB-50K (TURNBUCKLE FOR LONG BARS) 5  EUQILASH TURNBUCKLES (ELT-65L)   24  LONG BAR     5  SHORT BAR     4  TWIST LOCKS CV-20   40
Proposedcorrectiveactions: LASHING TO BE CHECKED DAILY AS PER COMPANY POLICY. CONDITION OF THE CONTAINER LOADED ON DECK TO BE CHECKED.HEAVY WEATHER TO BE CLOSELY MONITORED.
Corrective Action: LASHING TO BE CHECKED DAILY AS PER COMPANY POLICY. CONDITION OF THE CONTAINER LOADED ON DECK TO BE CHECKED.HEAVY WEATHER TO BE CLOSELY MONITORED.
Accident Details: Cargo damage due to movement of cargo
LessionLearnt: Effective Monitoring, and supervision of Lashing check procedure
ReviewComments: Appreciate shipstaff effort to minimise Heavy weather effect
ClosingNote: P&I Report and Photos relevant are attached for reference

IncidentDetails: Pilot ladder port side 7 chocks damaged and two rubber steps cracked.
IncidentEventDetails: at 0830 vessel arrived at R.V position for disembarkation of gaurds and crew change off galle. Swell SSE 2.0 mtr. shore boat approached vessel from port side. pilot ladder port side was prepared 3 mtr above water level as advised by service boat. when boat came alongside vessel had altered course 80° to port for providing leeway for boat. crew change  operation started by pilot ladder. service boat was heaving heavily during the entire operation. All embarkation/disembarkation was carried  out safely by using pilot ladder. after completing operation pilot ladder heaved up it was observed that bottom seven chocks were found broken and two of the bottom most rubber steps were observed to be cracked slightly.
ImmediateAction: port side pilot ladder is out of use untill compltete repairs of chocks. and temprary repairs on rubber steps. alertnatively vessel will use stbd ladder if reapirs not satisfactory. awaiting supply of new piloy ladder requested wide requisition no. SPR/NYGST/D/15/016.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 7 CHOCKS FOR STEPS ON LADDER BROKEN. , TWO RUBBER STEPS FOUND CRACKED.
DirectCause: SWELL IN THE AREA WHICH WAS CAUSING SHORE SERVICE BOAT TO HEAVE UNEXPECTEDLY, IN SPITE OF VESSEL GIVING IT FULL LEE TO APPROACH.
IndirectCause: SHORE SERVICE BOAT HITTING THE PILOT LADDER WHEN IN INFLUENCE OF SWELL.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: HAVE SIMILIAR OPERATION IN CALM WEATHER.
LessionLearnt: Embarkation/disembarkation to happen only at calm sea conditions.Vessel to provide sufficient lee for the service boat to approach & carry out the operation safely.Only after ascertained by the Master safe to carry out the operations,the operations to take place.If required,vessel to steam more closer to the land to get sufficient lee from the high seas.
ReviewComments: Pilot ladder replaced on the 09th Feb'2015.
ClosingNote: NMR closed on the 20th Feb'15.

IncidentDetails: While vessel was anchored at Saigon, during morning rounds observed paint store and steering gear room padlocks were broken and paints and some grease drums missing from Paint store and steering gear room
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel anchored at Saigon on 3rd February 2015, since this port is prone for theft and pilferages antipilferage watches were maintained, since vessel expected pilot at 0830 LT , around 0630 LT duty AB and cadet came inside for cleaning , around 0730 LT when bosun went to open paint store noticed 4-5 persons on board near the poop deck , immediately raised alarm , ship's whistle sounded, all crew mustered and observed all persons running and went down to the boat by rope from poop deck , Thorough checks were carried out and observed paint store and steering gear room locks were broken and paints around 700-800 ltrs missing also some chemicals from steering gear room also found missing.
ImmediateAction: Checked all stores on deck for pilferage , Thoruogh checks carried out , port control and agents were informed,
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel anchored at Saigon on 3rd February 2015 1224 LT awaiting pilot, since this port is prone for theft and pilferages antipilferage watches were maintained by 2 persons round the clock, On 04 Feb 2015 since vessel expected pilot around 0830 LT , around 0630 LT at daybreak duty AB and cadet came inside for cleaning , around 0730 LT when bosun went to open paint store noticed 4-5 persons on board near the poop deck / steering gear room and paint store door opened, immediately bosun rushed inside the accommodation, informed bridge and raised alarm, ship's whistle sounded, all crew mustered and observed all persons running and went down to the boat by rope from poop deck, noticed some chemical drums and paint drums lying near ship side railings, same were secured back in paint store and steering gear room, All stores were checked again and paint store and steering gear room were locked. Vungtau Port control and local agent were notified.  Thorough checks were carried out and after taking inventory following stores were found missing from paint store and steering gear room:  1. Paints : around 800 ltrs  2. Descaling liquid : 200 ltrs  3. Welding cable : 20mtr  4. FAIRCOOL SA 100 : 80 ltrs  5. Spheerol SX 2 : 40 ltrs
DirectCause: Robbers boarding vessel , gaining access to paint store and ateering gear room
IndirectCause: Crew members getting relaxed on the onset of daybreak , not carrying out proper security duties.
RootCause: Improper watchkeeping , security measures not implemented properly, Improper security measures
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Security patrols to be carried out diligently , Procedures as laid down in SSP to be implemented strictly, No relaxing in daytimje also especially in hig pilferage prone areas, Additional locks to be fabricated as additional security measure in addition to the padlock making it difficult for robbers/pirates to gain access.
LessionLearnt: As per Attached Investigation Report
ReviewComments: As per Attached Investigation Report
ClosingNote: As per Attached Investigation Report

IncidentDetails: During unmooring hydraulic line of fwd spring mooring winch ruptured and caused winch failure.
IncidentEventDetails: During unmooring hydraulic line of fwd spring mooring winch ruptured and caused winch failure. Same was informed to bridge.
ImmediateAction: Bridge immediately gave astern movment on engine which slacked the spring lines. The mooring crew on berth released the lines from hook and ship crew picked the lines using hand.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During unmooring hydraulic line of fwd spring mooring winch ruptured and caused winch failure.
DirectCause: Corrosion
IndirectCause: Lack of Maintenance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New Line fabricated and fitted in place.
LessionLearnt: All hydraulic lines on other mooring winches to be checked thoroughly and renewed as required.
ReviewComments: Noted, the ruptured pipe renewed. Appreciate ship staff efforts in timely action.
ClosingNote: Incident report Closed.

IncidentDetails: 4  containers suffered wet damage.  APZU 3928511 (Posn 130102)  FCIU 2127226  (Posn 130202)  CLHU 3920386 (Posn 110202)  TGHU 2251276 (Posn 110102)  Water level in the cargo hold was 1.5m when the first wet container was removed from cargo hold.    The sensor of No.2 cargo hold bilge is damged.
IncidentEventDetails: On 08th Jan 2015 at port of San pedro 840 T of ballast water was transferred by gravity to No.2 WBT(P) & (S) for stability purposes.  Upon departure San pedro the ballast water was pumped out of the 2WBT(P) & (S).  No soundings were taken of cargo hold bilges in the vessel's passage from San pedro to Singapore.  On 28th Jan 15 at 0700 Hrs LT duty officer informed of water spilling out of container TGHU 2251276 removed from bay 110102 and is kept on jetty to dry.  Upon investigation of all the lines(ballast, Fresh water & ballast tank air pipes) it was found that all the lines are in good condition and no signs of leakage were observed.  After further investigation it was found that the water is leaking from the tank top of No.2 WBT(P) but the point of leakage is not visible due to presence of containers.
ImmediateAction: The source of leakage is the tank top of No.2WBT(P) but the spot of leak is yet to be confirmed.  The leaking spot is not yet confirmed due to the presence of containers meant for discharge at San Pedro on 18th Feb15.  No.2 WBT(P) will not be ballasted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Four containers effected by flooding in no.2 cargo hold suffered wet damage-  APZU 3928511  FCIU 2127226  CLHU 3920386  TGHU 2251276
DirectCause: Negligence by ship staff in monitoring the cargo hold bilges and compartments.
IndirectCause: Blindly relying on bilge alarm system.
RootCause: Negligence ,Lack of knowledge and unawareness of the possibilities of water ingress in cargo hold.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1)No.2 WBT(P) & (S) will not be ballasted till the necessary repairs are carried out in next DD.  2)All cargo hold bilges are to be sounded daily and to be inspected visually by responsible staff.  3)All cargo hold bilges to be checked during and after vessel encounters rough weather.  4)Cargo hold bilge to be sounded at regular intervals when the ballasting of the ballast tank is carried out situated in that particular cargo hold.  5)After completion of the ballasting the cargo hold tanktop to be inspected for any sign of water.  6)Ship staff (deck and Engine) to be made familiar with the procedure of transferring the water accumalated in cargo hold to the ballast tank(i.e, in case if vessel encounters heavy rain while she is alongside).Same will be attached in Master,C/Off,C/eng and 2/Eng's handing over reports.
Corrective Action: Incident to be circulated to fleet vessels to ensure strict compliance with procedures
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Cause analysis including failure of barriers a. Direct Cause ? Water ingress from No.2 water ballast tank (port side) when ballast was transferred from Aft Pk to No.2 water ballast tank(port side) due to crack on tank top. b. Indirect cause ? Failure to inspect cargo hold after ballast operations ? Failure to take ballast tank soundings on a daily basis and during / after every ballast operation ? Failure to sound cargo hold bilges on a daily basis c. Root Cause ? Lack of implementation of company procedures with respect to monitoring /recording status of cargo hold bilges and ballast operations.
ReviewComments: Cause analysis including failure of barriers a. Direct Cause ? Water ingress from No.2 water ballast tank (port side) when ballast was transferred from Aft Pk to No.2 water ballast tank(port side) due to crack on tank top. b. Indirect cause ? Failure to inspect cargo hold after ballast operations ? Failure to take ballast tank soundings on a daily basis and during / after every ballast operation ? Failure to sound cargo hold bilges on a daily basis c. Root Cause ? Lack of implementation of company procedures with respect to monitoring /recording status of cargo hold bilges and ballast operations.
ClosingNote: Cause analysis including failure of barriers a. Direct Cause ? Water ingress from No.2 water ballast tank (port side) when ballast was transferred from Aft Pk to No.2 water ballast tank(port side) due to crack on tank top. b. Indirect cause ? Failure to sound cargo hold bilges on a daily basis c. Root Cause ? IncidentDetails: No damages and injuries
IncidentEventDetails: At 0030 Hrs on 7th Feb 2015 while vessel enroute to Hongkong  Bus bar frequency low alarm sounded , No.2 A/E  tripped and ME  slowdown , Immediately No.3 A/E which was 1st standby took load .    The main cause for the trip was the F.O Inlet pressure of No.2 A/E was low due to the automatic start of some reefer containers which resulted in high load which reached to 800 KW
ImmediateAction: Immediately we checked the fuel filters , found normal and same cleaned for keeping as ready standby.Done rigid checking and cleaning maintenace work to the unit 2 A/E. Everything was back to normal operation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nil
DirectCause: Sudden Increase of High Load coming from reefer containers, causes fuel hunting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Run another A/E for parallel service during high loads. Rigid maintenance and checking of A/E
LessionLearnt: When more reefers are loaded, depending upon the AE load second generator to put on load for sharing
ReviewComments: This need to included in CE's standing instructions.
ClosingNote: All crew are appraised of this, CO to inform ER well in advance before loading reefers.

IncidentDetails: Air motor vane broke off causing the motor to seize
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had lowered the gangway to disembark the port authorities who had boarded for port formalities.The vessel was awaiting pilot who would board immediately as and when the port authorities disembarked but the pilot would board using a tug boat which required a higher height requirement,while picking up it was detected that the the gangway would'nt lift as the air motor had seized.The other gangway motor was used immediately and the pilot boarded safetly.
ImmediateAction: Second air motor was used.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Air motor vane broke off causing the motor to seize
DirectCause: Material failure
IndirectCause: na
RootCause: Quality of vane in air motor of poor quality
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The air motor vanes are of good quality and supplied from the original makers. The quality of the air supplied to the motor must be clean and moisture free. The line filters in the air line must be checked frequently and drained off water.
Corrective Action: The line filters in the air line must be checked frequently and drained off water. Kindly carry out a briefing for the crew and duty officers to do the necessary checks.
ClosingNote: GCR raised. Crew briefing carried out.

IncidentDetails: Gaskets/Joints between the flanges of valves and pipeline gave away as the water present in the line freezed due to sub zero temperatures in incheon.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. water mist system valves are supposed to be in open state always.  2. so water was present in the line before the solenoid valves (for operation) and even after the solenoid valves in the lines which were tested in shipyard.  3. Line water turned to ice as the system line does not have any lagging. and neither steering gear room is protected by lagging.  4. because of which the joints between the flanges broke and water started to leak in steering gear room.
ImmediateAction: Fresh water tank valve was shut immidiately, watermist sytem taken out of auto operation. and started replacing broken joints with new one. Regular fire watches kept speciallly, under the areas covered by water mist system. The job is finished and the system is already in use.

IncidentDetails: Shaft generator rotor winding damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: At about 1500 hrs on 14 Feb 2015, during sailing the AE 3 was on load running parallel with shaft generator. Suddenly the shaft generaor tripped and the stand by generator started.  The alarm "shaft genarator control abnormal" flashing on the alarm panel.   The alarm did not reset even after swtching off the power to the panel.  The main control PCB "SGFVC-2" was renewed and found the alarm was reset.  But on restarting the shaft generator, the generator was not starting.  The shaft generator was inspected through the inspection cover on 16 Feb 2015 at Singapore and found that the rotor winding interlinking conductor has burnt off.  On inspection it was found that no voltage was being developed by the shaft generator
ImmediateAction: Shaft Generator was stopped.
Corrective Action: To arrange technician
ClosingNote: shaft generator  damaged  rotor windings renewed by makers. Periodic inspection of the condition of the windings to be monitored by ship staff.

IncidentDetails: 3rd Engineer Mr. Thomas Samuel joined vessel APL Spain on 31 Aug 2014 in Singapore. During departure preparation, AE1 and AE3 ad AE4 were started. AE1 overspeeded and tripped. After inspecting all possible problem, AE1 was started again. It again tripped on overspeed. It was concluded to check this governor later as it was not responding. But since BT was needed AE2 started instead and vsl sailed out.  After departure, governors of AE1 and AE2 were swapped. This time AE1 found normal but AE2 tripped on overspeed. AE2 governor top cover opened for inspection and oil change. It was found tissue papers were stuffed inside. Governor was cleaned as much as was possible, oil renewed and put back on its original engine AE1. Engine ran this time but response of governor was not reliable and later governor stuck and did not response at all. Office was informed, woodward engineer changed AE1 governor in Singapore on 8 Oct 2014. Upon inspection by service engineer he also saw one big tissue paper stuck at the bottom. Same he showed to Chief Engineer as well and all engine room staff.  Ship staff then decided to check all governors themselves. AE2 and AE3 were also found stuffed with tissue papers and silicon respectively. AE4 governor was clean and nothing found in it. Both these governor of AE2 and AE3 were cleaned as much as possible by ship staff.  It was witnessed by Chief Engineer Mr. Dannis Ravel, 2nd Engineer Mr. Kennedy, 3rd Engineer Mr. Thomas, 4th Eng Mr. Anish John, Motorman Mr. Ganesh, Motorman Mr. Suresh, Motorman Mr. Shiva, Fitter Mr.Somusundram.  Governor of AE 3 was found behaving erratically many times but on 1 Jan 2015 while departing Djibouti found governor of AE3 is not responding to speed change and it is stuck. This time it was revealed to me by all engineers that governors were sabotaged.
ImmediateAction: Same was then intimated to office by me ( Chief Engineer Ravinder Jaswal ). Photos of governors stuffed with tissue papers and silicon were also sent to office. Governor of AE3 replaced with good governor of AE1 ( T/c of AE 1 is damaged, it does not take more than 50% load ). Defective governor sent ashore for overhauling in Singapore on 27 Jan 2015.
Corrective Action: O'haul  or renew the Governor
ClosingNote: Suspect the   Governor damage is due to  Human error.Please ensure  engineers are well familiar with  machinaries .Office should be appraised if any officer is having negative attitude and not showing  signs of  improvement.

IncidentDetails: Accomodation Ladder (stbd side) heavily damaged
IncidentEventDetails: MASTER’S FULL REPORT ON DAMAGED ACCOMODATION LADDER      Please find below Master’s full report of incident happened on damaged accommodation ladder (stbd side) which being hit by barge “HARITA PRIMA 201”,.    On the 19th Feb.2015 around 0800hrs lt -  barge “CSC Shanghai” which lies port side alongside encountered large swell and parted mooring ropes astern due to rising up and down and sideways bumping of ship’s shell plating but managed to moved away from ship by the quick assistance of tug boat which was arranged by attending agent.    At 2100hrs lt - Master on bridge and observed weather situation was fine with only slight rain and made his first “ Master’s Night Order”.    At 2200hrs lt – Master left the bridge for taking rest due to hectic familiarization since onboard the vessel with the new company system and communications systems that need to be well familiarized. Advised  duty officer to call Master anytime in doubt as per written in Master’s Night Order Book.    At 2300hrs Duty Officer Samuel  Amido observed sudden strong wind  with rising up and down of barge in the starboard side due to sudden swell . Duty Officer informed immediately attending  agent  onboard  Mr. Haryono and went on bridge and called for tug assistance which  were positioned  around 50m astern of the vessel and also duty officer  informed immediately barge master representative to check barge fenders and barge situation.    Around 2315hrs lt – barge representative arrived on deck together with agent, duty A/B and deck cadet to check the situation.    Around 2320hrs lt – still no tug assistance arrived, duty A/B on deck informed duty officer that dolphin bouy line fwd is broken and Dolphin bouy started moving sideways.    Around 2325hrs lt – receiver representative together with other agent went on bridge to check the situation of barge. Barge master representative ordered the crew to cut the mooring ropes of barge (fwd and aft) due to observed situation where barge sometimes violently bumping ship’s side and fearing of damage to vessel shell plating.    Around 2332hrs lt – all mooring ropes for barge were already cut-off and barge moving astern with sometimes bumping vessel side due to swell and currents pushing astern.    Around 2335hrs lt – barge aft corner (port side) hit the accommodation ladder (stbd side) which safely lies on deck level in preparation for early morning use by off-signing Master Capt.Felipe Panugan.  Around 2338hrs lt – duty officer informed Master & Chief Officer immediately that the stbd gangway was hit by barge and damaged.    Around 2340hrs lt – Master and Chief Officer already on deck and check the situation while the barge who damaged the gangway were already at astern of the vessel around 40-50m.    Around 2345hrs lt – Master called attending agent Mr. Haryono and cargo receiver representative to ship’s office together with Chief Officer and carried out discussion regarding damaged accommodation ladder, contact details of barge company. Master adjourned the meeting and ordered Chief Officer to carry out another inspection during daylight hours.     At 0600hrs lt – Master, Chief Officer and Bosun carried out inspection on said damage as well as ship’s hull from fwd to aft of the vessel (stbd side) and found same damage on accommodation ladder (stbd) seen last night. Observed scratch on shell plating  besided TST no.2 (stbd) were inspected and found okay.    At 0835hrs – 0845hrs lt - Chief Officer together with Bosun carried out checking and inspection of damaged inside TST No.2 (stbd) and found all okay only visible scratches from ship’s shell plating.    At 1030-1100hrs lt – Master carried out safety meeting with crew at the crew mess room highlighting the incident happened for damaged accommodation ladder, reviewing measures and corrective actions.    Furthermore, as per agent advise last 18th February morning  that off-igning Master will be pick up by launch boat on the vessel on the 19th morning however on the 19th Feb. morning  said pick up were cancelled due to rough sea condition and decided to change schedule for pick up on 20th February morning. On the 19th night  starboard accommodation ladder was being prepared for early morning pick up for Master by launch boat.    Therefore, barge master responsibility is to ensure that barge must be manned with personnel as well as monitoring their mooring ropes to the ship and they must have quick assistance by tug which are positioned near to the vessel. Vessel crew attended the gangway all the time but due to unavoidable circumstances happened caused by nature.    Hope will give clear understanding on the incident happened. I sincerely apologized for this incident.
ImmediateAction: Carried out inspection of gangway after the incident,carried out meeting with officers and crew and carried out assesment determine measures to prevent recurrence.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAMAGE OF STBD ACCOMODATION LADDER  HIT BY BARGE.
DirectCause: BARGE MOORING ROPES PARTED, NO BARGE MASTER ON DECK MONITORING THEIR ROPES OR VENE BARGE IS UNATTENDED, ASSISTANCE UG TUG WAS NOT ARRIVED EVEN IT WAS STATIONED AFT FOR ONLY ABT 50 METERS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: SECURE THE GANGWAY WHEN NOT IN USE, ASSIST TUGS SHOULD BE AVAILABLE ANYTIME WHEN NEEDS BY BARGE, BARGE SHOULD HAVE PERSONNEL MONITORING THEIR ROPES TIED ON THE VESSEL FOR VESSEL SAFETY.
ReviewComments: please see attached report.
ClosingNote: Please see attached report.

IncidentDetails: Black out / Loss of Power - While vessel was at Dubai Anchorage.
IncidentEventDetails: CAP Survey was in progress, various machinery had been opened up for the Inspection. U/W Survey of propeller had just been completed and the Surveyor had come to the E/R to inspect M/E Piston & Liner condition through Scav. Ports. IG operation was also in progress as tanks were being inerted after the inspection. #2 & #3 A/E were in operation, when at about 15:20 LT, both generators stopped and A/E #1 (St'by A/E) came on load automatically. A/E #1 also stopped within 2 minutes, causing Emergency Generator to start and come on load automatically.
ImmediateAction: Emergency Generator started (Auto-start). Procedures to restore Ship's Power initiated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss of Man-hours, to transfer DO & restore Power.
DirectCause: Low DO Service Tank level, causing loss of DO Suction. (A/E was on DO as Aux. Boiler was opened up for the Survey).
IndirectCause: Heavy work-load on Ship staff.  DO Serv. Tk. Low level alarm on repose (by previous management crew)
RootCause: Lack of complying with watch-keeping procedure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Alarm was removed from repose and tried out. On-job training was carried out and Duty Engineers and Oilers were instructed proper watch-keeping routines irrespective of various other jobs in progress. Labelled minimum levels to be maintained in the DO Serv. Tk. next to the float gauge.
Corrective Action: Alarm was removed from repose and tried out. On-job training was carried out and Duty Engineers and Oilers were instructed proper watch-keeping routines irrespective of various other jobs in progress. Labelled minimum levels to be maintained in the DO Serv. Tk. next to the float gauge.
LessionLearnt: Watch keeping routines to be maintained at all times.
ReviewComments: Watch keeping routines to be maintained at all times.
ClosingNote: Watch keeping routines to be maintained at all times.

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: IG control panel Touch screen failure leading to Cargo stoppage for 2.9 hours
IncidentEventDetails: At 0130hrs during cargo discharge at SEOT- Jebel ali, IGG panel touch screen went blank . Checked in CCR, the repeater panel working and IGG running normal to deck. Around 0230 hours,  Oxygen content high alarm came and valve to atmosphere fully open, to deck full close. Even after Oxygen reduced to 3 %, to deck not opening since the High O2 alarm not being reset. Reset can only be done from ECR by touch screen. CCR panel only has alarm acknowledge.  Mannual reset inside panel tried, still no reset.  At 0240hours cargo stopped--Shore stop,by terminal. Emergency mode tried but keep on tripping after opening to deck. Cargo resumed at 0950 hrs, till 200mm IG deck pressure then cargo stopped at 1042hrs-ship stop. At 1305hrs technician arrived with new touch screen, same renewed and Cargo resumed at 1336hrs.
ImmediateAction: Tried to fire in Emergency mode, but keep on tripping when IG put to deck.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Though there was no damage to machinery, there was delay in discharging cargo for 2.9 hrs.
DirectCause: 1. Failure of the touch screen panel in ECR. 2. Failure to operate the system in emergency mode.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: One no. spare touch screen has to be onboard. It should be pre programmed.
Corrective Action: One no. spare touch screen has to be onboard. It should be pre programmed.
LessionLearnt: The emergency firing is usually tested only till the successful establishment of the flame and this was being regularly done. However, the system was not run for more than 5 minutes during the testing. In the present case, when the IGG was tried in emergency mode, the flame was established, however, after 5 minutes, the system tripped. Instructions have been provided to run the system for at least 15 minutes and this has been included in the PMS as well. Further, this has been included in the senior engineer’s handing over report as well and will be mentioned during the briefing in the office.
ClosingNote: Closed on 26/11/2015

IncidentDetails: While departing Suape berth, M/E failed to start on fuel. Investigated & found  puncture valves of fuel pumps not de-activating .Safety air and control air were drained off and eased up puncture valves and started Main engine. For the above there was a delay in departure for 53 minutes.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. While departure Suape berth, ME was first blown through and tried out  on fuel from ECR  2. Confirmed that No alarms in ME safety and Governor controls system  3. ME controls handed over to bridge   4. ME tried out from bridge and found not firing on fuel  5. Physically checked & confirmed that all the interlock items viz. Turning gear, Master start air valve, Starting air distributor valve are in correct postion.  6. Checked & confirmed all the parameters of FO Sys vis. temp, viscosity, pressure is in correct levels  7.  Checked & confirmed all the exhaust valves are operating (by checking the test lever movement in the air cylinder)  8. Contorls was taken back to ECR and tried out from ECR control and from engine side local control .ME found not firing on fuel from both side.  Suspected Fuel pump puncture valves. E/R informed W/H and seeked permission for checking the puncture valves by opening the air connections on the puncture valves and check its freeness  10.All puncture valves found free    11.Tried out from ECR. Successul & Then tried out from W/H. Successful.
ImmediateAction: Puncture valves were then eased up after shutting down safety air & control air and then ME tried out  successfully from ECR.Controls was then given to bridge and ME again tried out satisfactorily AHD and ASTRN direction.
DirectCause: Sticky puncture valves.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Puncture valves to be overhauled within 4000 RHRS.
Corrective Action: Inspection of all puncture valves by draining safety and control air to be made a pre-departure routine Bridge  to ensure that before Pilot, E/R is provided sufficient notice to blow through and try out the engine on fuel
ClosingNote: CAUSE ANALYSIS  1. Vessel was at berth for an extended duration and engines were not tested whilst she was alongside.  2. Shore personnel disembarked late and the time available to try out the engines prior handing over controls to bridge was very less. Engines were only tried out on air and not on fuel due to limited time.  CORRECTIVE ACTIONS  Inspection of all puncture valves by draining safety and control air to be made a pre-departure routine Bridge  to ensure that before Pilot, E/R is provided sufficient notice to blow through and try out the engine on fuel

IncidentDetails: 1940: Fire alarm sounded for Crew pantry     1941:Small Fire reported by Bosun in crew mess room on bread toaster,and extinguished     1942: After investigation found bread toaster got over heated and cause the fire     1950: Electrical officer inspected insulation of switch board,and found in good order.     1955: Suspected that the toaster was kept on by some crew without bread and the automatic stop     button malfunctioning,causing over heating.
IncidentEventDetails: 1940: Fire alarm sounded for Crew pantry     1941:Small Fire reported by Bosun in crew mess room on bread toaster,and extinguished     1942: After investigation found bread toaster got over heated and cause the fire     1950: Electrical officer inspected insulation of switch board,and found in good order.     1955: Suspected that the toaster was kept on by some crew without bread and the automatic stop     button malfunctioning,causing over heating.
ImmediateAction: Explained all crew the cause of the fire and instructed to unplug the equipment after every use.  Instructed Crew member to check these items during each fire rounds.  Same will be discussed in this month safety meeting as well.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No injury to crew. Toaster got burned.
DirectCause: Over heating due to malfunctioning of auto stop button.
IndirectCause: Crew not following safe working practice
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Explained all crew the cause of the fire and instructed to unplug the equipment after every use.  Instructed Crew member to check these items during each fire rounds.  Same will be discussed in this month safety meeting as well.
Corrective Action: Pls post a notice "Unplug toaster from outlet when not in use and before cleaning"
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Pls post a notice "Unplug toaster from outlet when not in use and before cleaning". Pls brief the crew that in order to avoid possibility of fire, do not leave toaster unattended during use. The housekeeping to clean out accumulated crumbs frequently to avoid risk of fire or toaster malfunction. Do not use foods that will drip coatings or fillings when heated.
ClosingNote: Crew briefed. Notice posted near Toasters.  This report is closed.

IncidentDetails: Stbd anchor lost at sea.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel departed Primorsk on 18-02-2015 with a cargo of ULSD bound for New York. Since heavy weather was expected during the voyage, all necessary precautions as per Heavy Weather checklist were complied with. Lashings were checked on both the anchors. On 22-02-2015, sea started to get rough and wind freshened up. All checks were carried out as per Heavy weather checklist and lashings checked. Routine checks were carried out by deck crew on a daily basis to ensure vessel is secured for heavy weather. On 26-Feb-2015, at around 1200 Hrs LT, C/O accompanied by Bosun, and OS proceeded forwarded to check lashings. Same were checked and tightened as required. Both Anchors were found to be intact. Since weather was expected to deteriorate further and heavy pitching was expected, a check of lashings was carried out at 1600 Hrs LT and lashings were tighetened as required. At around 1800 Hrs LT before sunset, Deck crew accompanied by C/O went forward for a final check on both anchors. At that point, it was reported that the Stbd Anchor was missing. The pin of the Anchor Shackle had shifted out of place due to the locking arrangement for pin giving way and the anchor seemed to have slid off the pin. The remaining cable was heaved up and the anchor shackle secured. Checks for visual damage were carried out in the FPK store, Paint store and B/T room. FPK Tank and 1S WBT soundings were checked and no ingress found. Port anchor lashings checked and extra lashings were taken. The incident was then reported to the vessel's Marine Supdt and the Owners.
ImmediateAction: Extra lashings taken on port anchor. Anchor position being checked frequently.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Today 26th Feb 2015 at 1800 Hrs, Ch Off on a routine check of the anchors reported that the Stbd Anchor was missing. The chain was heaved up and it was found that the locking pin for the Anchor Shackle securing pin had given way.    Both Anchors were checked in the afternoon around 1200Hrs LT and lashings tightened. Due to vessel experiencing heavy weather, the anchors were again checked around 1600 Hrs LT and were found to be intact.     Before sunset around 1800 Hrs LT, Ch Off, Bosun, OS and Pumpman proceeded forward to check the anchor lashings and reported the incident.    Then chain has been heaved up and anchor shackle along with securing pin have been taken onboard. Forepeak store, Paint Store, and B/T room have been checked for visible signs of damage. Fpk and 1S WBT soundings have been checked for water ingress. None found.
DirectCause: Taper Pin for locking of anchor shackle gave way
IndirectCause: Heavy weather
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All anchor shackles to be checked frequently for any noticeable signs of shifting.
Corrective Action: 1. Fleet Alert to be sent to all the vessels regarding the following: • Checks on the D shackle. • Three point contact. • Bow stopper usage.
LessionLearnt: Attached Incident and investigation report.
ClosingNote: Investigation Completed and all corrective action has been completed.

IncidentDetails: While working in the Engine Room, 2/E Jaydeep got injured on his right upper jaw under the nose. While trying to twist open a flow meter cover for flow D/P unit in the IG system, the opening tool slipped and his fist hit against his face causing a slit of approx. one inch and 2-3 mm deep.
IncidentEventDetails: 1300: 2nd Eng started work on the D/P unit using an angle bar.  1330 : The angle bar slipped due to excessive force ; due to this his right fist hurt his upper jaw causing a slit of approx.one inch and 2 to 3 mm deep
ImmediateAction: The job was stopped and 2nd Eng was given 1st aid treatment onboard.
DirectCause: While trying to twist open a flow meter cover for flow D/P unit in the IG system, the opening tool slipped out due to improper operation and posture.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew briefing to be carried out on the correct postures and operation of hand tools.
Corrective Action: Pls brief the crew on the following: 1) Hand tools should be operated in correct posture and strength.  2) When hand tools with sharp corners or edges are used, their direction of movement should be away from the body. Suitable personal protective equipment such as helmets, aprons or gloves should be used when necessary.  3) One should concentrate on the job when using a hand tool.   4)  Precautions should be taken to prevent tools slipping out from hands while working at height.
ClosingNote: Appropriate crew briefing carried out.

IncidentDetails: Main Engine Lub Oil Sump tank Low Level Alarm sounded at 0242hrs. The Alarm initiates at a level of around 32cms.
IncidentEventDetails: Main Engine Lub Oil Sump tank Low Level Alarm sounded at 0242hrs. The Alarm initiates at a level of around 32cms. The Third Engineer on duty, did not think it prudent to inform Second Engineer or Chief Engineer then. Finally, around 0345hrs, Second Engineer was informed and at 0350hrs, Chief Engineer was informed by Second Engineer. The Lub.Oil Purifier was stopped and soundings of Sump Tank showed a level of 22cms - a drop of over 10cms (about 2500ltrs), since the Alarm sounded. Main Engine Revs reduced and brought to Dead Slow Ahead speed by 0412hrs. Air vent header drain lines from Main Engine Lub.Oil sump tank and Turbocharger Oil Receivers checked. Sludge tank and Waste Oil tanks soundings checked. Found that level in the Waste Oil tank had increased. Air vent cock, Drain cock and Relief valve of Purifier checked. All found normal. Blind flange on the common drain line from Lub.Oil Purifiers checked and found oil inside - confirming the leakage from any one of the above. About 2500ltrs of fresh Oil was taken in the system and the sump tank level brought upto 34cms from the earlier 22cms. Slow Ahead was given at 0441hrs, Half Ahead at 0540hrs and Full Ahead at 0548hrs. Main Engine revs increased to 90 by 0630hrs.
ImmediateAction: The Lub.Oil Purifier was stopped and Sump tank sounding checked, found level went down. M/E  Revs reduced to Dead Slow Ahead speed by 0412hrs. Air vent header drain lines from Main Engine Lub.Oil sump tank and Turbocharger Oil Receivers checked. Sludge tank and Waste Oil tanks soundings checked. Found that level in the Waste Oil tank had increased. Air vent cock, Drain cock and Relief valve of Purifier checked. All found normal. Blind flange on the common drain line from Lub.Oil Purifiers checked and found oil inside - confirming the leakage from any one of the above. About 2500ltrs of fresh Oil was taken in the system and the sump tank level brought upto 34cms from the earlier 22cms. Slow Ahead was given at 0441hrs, Half Ahead at 0540hrs and Full Ahead at 0548hrs. Main Engine revs increased to 90 by 0630hrs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Main engine system Lube Oil loss.
DirectCause: Failure to Close the drain valve of Lub oil Purifier heater, Failure to realize the same when the lub oil purifier was started after approx 3 weeks of operation and failure to monitor the ME lub oil sump sounding and analyse that there was a constant reduction of ME sump sounding since departure and all three wathchkeepers including CE failed to note the same and take corrective action. Even when the alarm was sounded sufficient steps were not taken to avoid further loss of lub oil. There was a long delay in investigation and stopping the drainage of lub oil from Heater. There seemed to be a lapse from all the three watchkeepers and same was not noted by CE as well.
RootCause: Lack of awareness. Timely action on the part of Third Engineer to inform either Second Engineer or Chief Engineer - fully knowing that this was an issue of concern.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All watchkeepers need to be more vigilant and CE must ensure that all the parametetrs and various levele soundings and gauges are in order. The lapse was initiated since the time vessel departed from Dock , however same could be established only after more than 24 hous when the alrm was sounded and there was serios level drop from ME lub oil sump.
Corrective Action: The watch keepers must be trained to escalate a problem when it is critical where a machinery may get damaged.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: 1. Any alarm on a machinery Lube oil system is critical and must be dealt with immediately.  2. This kind of problem must be immediately informed to the Chief Engineer. 3. Watchkeeping standards to be improved.
ClosingNote: All Watchkeeprs need to improve the standards of watchkeeping

IncidentDetails: On 4th March 2015 at around 1800 Hrs vessel had a Violent rolling due to which the old mooring wires which was kept on top of midship store  for landing ashore, broke  their lashing and slid towards the pipelines and damaged the midshipstore railings, 3S COW line bottom machine Valve,3S Cow line supports and sample line for 3S WBT.
IncidentEventDetails: 03 Nov 2014 : Vessel recieved new wires of 34mm Dia, 250m Length total 16 Nos at drydock.  04 Nov 2014 : Vessel departed drydock /  All wires recieved were lashed to the pipelines support on deck  12 Nov 2014 : All lashed wired started moving on deck due to rolling, Vessel repositioned all wires and lashed my using mooring wire tails and by tightening using the  mooring winches.  01 Dec 2014 : Vessel renewed all 16 wires on drum and recoiled the old wires in the same reel and all the 16 reels were collected and placed on top of midship store and it was lashed using 12mm wires and bottle screw (Photographs attached)  04 Mar 2015 : Vessel had an unexpected change in weather since morning, weather started becoming worse by evening, and vessel started experiencing some  violent rolling at uneven intrevals at about 1800 Hrs 1800 Hrs vessel had a Violent rolling due to which the old mooring wires which was kept on top of midship store  for landing ashore, broke  their lashing and slid towards the pipelines and damaged the midshipstore railings, 3S COW line bottom machine Valve,3S Cow line supports and sample line for 3S WBT.  5th Mar 2015 0200 LT all wire reels were secured to pipeline support and various strong points on deck using wire ropes and bottle screw.
ImmediateAction: Vessel reduced speed and course altered  to avoid rolling. All wire reels collected and positioned near by Pipeline supports and the nearest strong point available and lashed with wires and bottle screw.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 03 Nov 2014 : Vessel recieved new wires of 34mm Dia, 250m Length total 16 Nos at drydock.  04 Nov 2014 : Vessel departed drydock /  All wires recieved were lashed to the pipelines support on deck  12 Nov 2014 : All lashed wired started moving on deck due to rolling, Vessel repositioned all wires and lashed my using mooring wire tails and by tightening using the  mooring winches.  01 Dec 2014 : Vessel renewed all 16 wires on drum and recoiled the old wires in the same reel and all the 16 reels were collected and placed on top of midship store and it was lashed using 12mm wires and bottle screw (Photographs attached)  04 Mar 2015 : Vessel had an unexpected change in weather since morning, weather started becoming worse by evening, and vessel started experiencing some  violent rolling at uneven intrevals at about 1800 Hrs 1800 Hrs vessel had a  rolling about 30 Deg due to which the old mooring wires which was kept on top of midship store  for landing ashore, broke  their lashing and slid towards the pipelines and damaged the midshipstore railings, 3S COW line bottom machine Valve,3S Cow line supports and sample line for 3S WBT.  5th Mar 2015 0200 LT all wire reels were secured to pipeline support and various strong points on deck using wire ropes and bottle screw.
DirectCause: 1) Vessel does not have any fixed arrangements to hold these wires at heavy weather and during violent rolling  2) Each wire reels are about 1.3 Tonnes weight and there were total 16 old wires on board  3) All wire reels were lashed with 12mm wire and bottle screw but however, these lashings did not hold, when the ship was rolling about 30 deg.  4) All wires should have been lashed individually  5) Lashing were for whole lot of wires which gave space for rolls to move and lashings to fail.  6) Delay in decision re disposal of wires.
IndirectCause: 1) Heavy weather  2) Violent rolling at uneven intrevals
RootCause: Old wires were not landed ashore timely.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Office informed and wire reels were planned to land ashore at the earliest  2) Wires in reels should be lashed individually
Corrective Action: To be circulated in the next Incident bulletin
LessionLearnt: Securing to be given importance / Items not required should be landed in liaison with Vessel Superintendent
ClosingNote: NMR closed

IncidentDetails: Bosun's Right Hand Fingers Fractured
IncidentEventDetails: Bosun,PumpMan,2/E was involved in the process of rigging the steam hose from the steam drain     line near 1W's for warming up the staffa motor,as the staffa motor was not taking load on heave up   mode. 2/E,Bosun,PP man was involved in the process of connecting hose to the drain line on the    steam main line,as the steam hose was not getting fitted in the drain line easily,it was suggested     that the application of grease to the end of hose would ease up the insertion.      The motors were kept running on idle condition. Bosun voluntarily went forward to collect the     grease. Bosun had gone alone & he decided to take off excess grease applied over the SLIDE IN     gear,as the motor shaft was running,before he cud remove the grease,the dog coupling came to the   engaging position & the little finger on the right hand got caught inbetween the standing part & the    moving part,resulting in the crushing of the finger.
ImmediateAction: Immediately First Aid was given in Ship's Hospital, Agent was called and Bosun was sent to Hospital for Medical Treatment.    All Ship's crew briefed:  To follow proper safety procedures and not to take shortcuts.  To follow good Seamanship techniques  To remain situationally aware
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fracture of Bosun's right hand fingers
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Ship's crew briefed:  To follow proper Safety Precautions and not to take shortcuts  To follow good Seamanship Techniques  To be situationally aware
Corrective Action: Safety Bulletin to be sent to all the vessels in the company to make crew aware ill effects of boisterous behavior and haste
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Attached investigation report for the Injury to Bosun
ClosingNote: Closed 29th Sep
