IncidentDetails: Impact Damage on IC WBT side shell pating  between frame no 78 and 79  1. indentation of appx 3mtr by 2 mtr  on shell plating no H15 and I15, which includes a horizontal Gash of 1 mtr long  on the lower part of shell palte I 15
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl was made fast to the Jetty with Port Anchor down 5 shackles in water, Stbd side alongside (4+2+2). During cast off First Tug was made fast on port shoulder Fwd with 1 Ships Line and second Tug made fast on Port quarter with 1 Ships line. Vsl cast off at 1420 LT. Port Anchor was being heaved up Ship was moving parallely away from the jetty.  Vessel  was pulled  by aft tug to turn the ship to  stbd with engine  running astern. While heaving the anchor , the anchor cable slipped off  due to weight on the chain and current caught Vsl. on the Port quarter. To assist the Vsl.  turning ,Fwd tug was cast off to give a push to the vsl on the Port bow just before the anchor chain started slipping. However the tug was far off to reach and assist vsl. in time. Vessel bodily drifted  towards jetty, because of effect of current and wind .Vsl stern came close to the jetty .In order to save stern hitting the jetty astern movement was given however vsl did not turn to stbd. Finally the current caught on the athwartship and bow started  swinging abruptly to stbd .Vsl made contact with aft spring fender of the jetty resulting in about a meter rupturing of 1'C' W.B.T between fr 78 to 79  at appx height  10 to 11mtr from keel. After contact Bow moved away from the Jetty .Vessel maneuvered itself with its engine power and averted any further incident. Tugs were not powerful enough to pull out the vsl. Once again fwd tug was made fast and anchor heaved up vessel moved away and slowly took stbd turn to clear away from the jetty and entered the outward channel.  No damage to Jetty installation or No damage to fender was noticed.
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately checked that there was no personal injury or environmental pollution. There was no apparent damage to shore installation. The incident was immediately reported to the concerned authorities. The apparent portion of ship's hull was visually checked and initially no other damage was noticed. Stability of the vessel was checked on loadicator and results found satisfactory. After dropping Pilot, vessel came out of the navigation channel and started drifting to further assess any damage. Ship's overside were checked at the point of contact on stbd bow and at suspected area of contact on stbd qtr. Also, 1C WBT was deballasted and visual inspection of the damage was made from inside the tank.
Corrective Action: Corrective Action ? All vessels in the fleet will be alerted of this event and the lessons learnt will be shared. PIC: CMS Team Date: 31st March 2015(Sent) ? The incident will be discussed with all senior and junior deck officers during pre-joining briefing. PIC: CMS Team & respective briefing team Date: 15th May 2015 (Implemented) ? Navigation excellence campaign will be initiated to address the following points: ? Passage planning. ? Pre-arrival information. ? Master pilot exchange. ? Communic
LessionLearnt: Root Cause  ? Lack  of  planning : Bridge  team  did  not  properly  evaluate  the  current  and  the  maneuvers during the passage planning  stage. Master Pilot  exchange, though carried out, was inadequate. They did not discuss  the tug placement and the effects of the current including the control measures . ? Lack  of  clear  communication  and  understanding  between  the  master  and  the  forward  statio n.  Communication  was  not  in  closed  loop.  This  prevented  the  master  from  making  a  clear  assessment  of  the  situation  with  regards  to  anchor  lead  and  weight. Communication  barrier  created  within  the  bridge  team  due  to  relative positioning and frequent radio c ommunication with the forward station. ? Lack of Compliance: Bridge team did not comply with the procedure, with regards  to: ? Proper passage planning and bridge team meeting. ? Monitoring the vessels progress. ? Loss of situational awareness of the Bridge team .  The bridge team  was unable to provide active feedback on vessel speed to the master and Pilot .  ? R isk  assessment  did  not  include  the  special  nature  of  berthing/  unberthing with  current . No limits and boundaries of the turn were planned and clearing  distances  established
ReviewComments: Vessel docked at Port Khalid repair berth and permanent repairs were executed under class supervision.
ClosingNote: Correction action taken as listed in the Investigation report.

IncidentDetails: Forcastle ladder to forward mast bent, hose pipe cover for anchor chain bent, stonchions of walkway bent, forcastle lights broken, water about 200 ltr entered in forcfastle store through for store exhaust.
IncidentEventDetails: on 4th feb 0630 lt weather started  deteriorating vesel was navigating SSW heading in sea of japan, with loaded draft of 14.6 mtr. wind force reached up to 9 bf scale and swell of 8 mtr. swell and wind started hitting vessel from stbd bow. heaving and pitching started violantly with bow going inside swell fwd.accordingly speed/rpm of vessel was adjusted to reduce load on m/e.  at 0815 lt vessel altered course in order to make checks forward for all proper securings. all found properly lashed and secured. and vessel turned back to original charted course. at 1036 forcastle port side bilge high level alarm was recieved on bridge. to investigate again vessel was altered to make access forward. it was found during investigation that water was seeping through exhaust fan inlet. accumulated water was pumped out using wilden pump and exhaust cover tightened further to avoid any more leakages. alarm rested.
ImmediateAction: all access to deck stores accomodation, steering gear room, lockers on deck checked and closed water tight. lashing for any loose items checked stores checked for all heavy weather precautions. acees to deck was restricted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Anchor hawse pipe cover deformed due to heavy swell water hitting on forcastle.   2. Four stonchions on forecastle bent due to sea water force.  3. Forecastle mast flood light for deck broken.  4. Access Ladder to fore mast deformed.
DirectCause: ADEQUATE HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTIONS WERE ALREADY TAKEN INCLUDING COURSE ALTERATIONS WHERE PRACTICABLE AND FEASIBLE. BUT SEA FORCE BEING SO POWERFUL DAMAGING THE FIXED LADDER, STANCHIONS & LIGHT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TO TAKE ALL POSSIBLE PRECAUTIONARY MEASURES TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT OF BAD WEATHER. ALTERATION OF COURSE TO MINIMIZE THE EFFECT OF BAD WEATHER.   STRAITENING THE BEND LADDER, & STANCHIONS, REPAIR OF THE DAMAGED HAWSE PIPE COVER. NEW LIGHT REQUESTED.
LessionLearnt: Agree to the proposed corrective action by the vessel.On trans oceanic voyages,vessel to liaise closely with the CMS department to get the daily weather updates & to monitor closely.Deck fittings damaged during the heavy weather had been attended to,appreciate the ship staff effort.

IncidentDetails: Monorail crane wire ,Nil injuries
IncidentEventDetails: One of the deck crew (BOSUN) operated monorail crane and  lowered  luggages for off signers before departure , After a while  bosun was  preparing to secure the monorail crane for departure.  At the time of securing the  monorail crane, wire parted and broken wire came out from the monorail crane  sheves and floating block fell down on accomodation exterior deck .
ImmediateAction: Monorail crane boom secured and block tied with strong manila rope at the time of departure .After sailed out from Port kaohsiung ,monorail crane wire renewed with new spare wire onboard by ships crew.All safety precautions carried out through out the job .Bosun and senior deck crew attended the wire renewal task after RA.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Monorail crane wire parted and damaged  ,Nil injuries
DirectCause: Condition of the wire known to the crew who operated the Monorail crane  and Previous chief officer.Renewal of wire postponed and delayed action of the same lead total wire damage .
IndirectCause: SMMS not followed strictly ,Condition of the wire was not in good condition before the operation ,same was known by crew who operated and chief officer ,Seamanship not followed.Consequences of getting hurt and permanent disability not understood by crew and officers .RA not followed before operating the deck machinery.Operational limitations and responsibility of the person who is operating the machinery not understood by the crew.
RootCause: Lack in seamanship knowledge,SMMS not followed effectively.Condition of wire status not updated and no information about the Kink on wire rope was  not reflected in handing over notes and same not notified to office through Defect list.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: RA to be done and same to be followed ,SMMS to be followed strictly , routine machinery inspection to be done thoroughly without fail to avoid future accident.Record of inspection of wire ropes ,Mooring ropes,deck machinery to be maintained and same to be followed .Defects and observations to be reflected in Handing over notes for easy reference and follow up.
LessionLearnt: Keep a close watch & regular monitoring of renewal date is easier once Stenciled on bulkhead.
ReviewComments: Keep a close watch & regular monitoring of renewal date is easier once Stenciled on bulkhead.
ClosingNote: Keep a close watch & regular monitoring of renewal date is easier once Stenciled on bulkhead.

IncidentDetails: While gangway was being secured for sailing at Cape Town, the wharf ladder connected the lower platform was removed from the hooks. C/Off who was standing there after taking draft did not notice the hooks being removed and stepped on the wharf ladder which tilted and fell between the shipside and the wharf along with the C/Off. Lost the 2m wharf ladder and one walkie-talkie.
IncidentEventDetails: 26/1720 Vessel completed cargo work and last stevedore away. One tech on board. C/Off was asked to get the gangway net removed wait for pilot. Also instruction given to rig the pilot ladder on the stbd side.  26/1748 - Pilot on board for sailing. Informed C/Off to get the gangway secured and then proceed to stations. Carrying out Master/Pilot Exchange when heard some shouting. Went on the bridge wing and saw a person in the water under the gangway between the shipside and wharf. The lifebouy had been passed and 2/Off reported that C/Off had fallen into the water.
ImmediateAction: Given gantline from wharf to hold on to. Gangway lifebouy was thrown.   Requested leave of the pilot and went down. Pulled C/Off to after side of gangway platform and lowered gangway. Asked Bosun to slack after springs by 3m for vessel to move aft so that gangway could be lowered into the water. 2 crew on footboard halled C/Off onto the bottom platform and then helped him up halfway after which C/Off came up the rest ofthe way alone.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Luckly C/Off fell after the wharf ladder. No injury as he did not hit anything and fall was about 3m.  2. Wharf ladder and walkie talkie lost.
DirectCause: Additionally as per Investigation report
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additionally as per Investigation report
Corrective Action: 1. A specific Risk assessment campaign on transfer of personal will be initiated to address the following in view of this incident: I. Why this incident cannot happen on other vessels. II. Ship specific RA for transfer of personal. III. Specific Ports were we have heavy surging of vessels.
LessionLearnt: As per Investigation report
ReviewComments: As per Investigation report
ClosingNote: Incident closed

IncidentDetails: While going alongside under Pilotage at Haiye Terminal Berth No. 94 (Qingdao,China), Port side Life Boat permanent deck skates came in contact with the Jetty Fender and got deformed.
IncidentEventDetails: on 26 may 2015    vsl was under Pilotage  - vsl manoeuvre was assisted by 3 tugs (1 forward, 1 just forward of manifold      & 1 aft). All made fast with tug's line.  - wind: SE x 8 Kts (aspect stbd quarter)  - Tide: Ebbing with Low water at 1700 hrs LT (Tide pushing aft)  - Ship Heading: 358° (T)  - Jetty: 175° - 355°   - 1st Line (Aft Spring) already ashore at 1600 hrs LT.  - vsl deck was below the Jetty (Ship's main deck railings & Jetty almost same      level)   - vsl almost alongside to Jetty, dropping astern with a speed of 0.2 kts in      order to align the manifolds. Vsl Engines were used to gain astern speed      while tugs pushing the vsl alongside. Vessel was to drop astern about 15      mtrs. Jetty aft fender touch the ship side in the same location where the      Life Boat Skates are welded on the deck. Life Boat Skates bottom part is      protruding out of the ship side by 80 mm. It is this part which came in      contact with the rubber lining of Jetty fender and torsional force acting on      the skates deformed them.  - the skates are below the Life Boat and hence not visible from Bridge wing.
ImmediateAction: Current deformation will not affect launching and recovery of the Life  Boat.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: lifeboat skates of port side pushed inward around 60 MM and bend side ways .
DirectCause: Vessel berthed at a VLCC jetty during low tide under fully loaded condition. Ship should not be allowed by port bss minimum & max freeboard in relation to jetty. Poor fedback from agents on this regard. Ship's deck level was below jetty level and fenders were at side rails height. While berthing Ship came alongside to Jetty fenders near support guide resulting in frame getting pushed inward and side ways as the ship was dropping astern at the same time.The skates are below the Life Boat and hence not visible from Bridge wing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Basis deformity and location, mosvt feasible option is to crop the skates from foundation and shift to E/R WS for heating and straightening.  Below the skates is Voidspace.Or vessel will try to do cold work by hammering and chain blocks .
LessionLearnt: Whenever the vessel calls port wherein the jetty is specially designed for a particular displacement type of vessel(higher or lower than the own vessel's displacement)Master to exercise due diligence & ensure that the forward & aft movement of the vessel becomes restricted once alongside wherein the movement of vessel is restricted due to the presence of unsuitable size fenders or hand rails getting stuck in jetty protrusions.In this case,Nanyang Star has berthed at a VLCC berth wherein the height/distance between fenders were inappropriate to the size of the vessel.
ReviewComments: Agree with the vessel staff comments
ClosingNote: Clsed on 10-Jun-15

IncidentDetails: AFTER VESSEL HAD BEEN MADE FAST ALONGSIDE, A FISHING NET WAS OBSERVED TO BE TRAILING FROM UNDER THE STERN OF THE VESSEL.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel departed Inchon on 1st June/AM and arrived at Qingdao Anchorage 2nd June/AM. During the passage from Inchon to Qingdao, no abnormalities were reported by Engine room or observed on the Bridge during navigation, regarding excessive vibration, increased load, maintaining heading etc.    Vessel remained at Anchorage for a day. During the entire stay at anchor, frequent rounds were made on deck, including checking overside, as part of anti-pilferage watches. There were no sightings of any fishing net attached to the Vessel (FYI, the fishing net which eventually got stuck could be seen from the Bridge, when Vessel was berthed. It could not have gone un-noticed at anchorage).    Vessel picked up anchor 3rd/AM and proceeded to Pilot Station. Again, no abnormalities were reported. During this time, numerous engine movements were given to adjust ETA and/or keep clear of other traffic and navigational aids/hazards.    After picking up Pilot, crew were asked to standby for arrival stations and keep fore and aft ready for berthing. The aft stations crew did not see any fishing net during this time, as well. Vessel entered the breakwater and made fast a tug on the port quarter. There were no sightings of any net by the aft stations crew or the tug crew (during this time, Vessel was proceeding at dead slow, and there was hardly any propeller wash/wake. Also, two crew members were looking overside to keep reporting as the tug rope was being picked up. A green net of this length would have surely been visible).    As the Vessel reached off berth and while coming alongside, few kicks were given with the engine (ahead and astern) to control approach to the berth and steady the Vessel alongside. Once all ropes were fast, the fore and aft crew proceeded to put rat guards in place. It was during this time, that the aft stations crew reported that a fishing net seemed to be coming out from below the aft section of the hull.  The net had in all probability been entangled in one of the fenders on the jetty or on the seabed, and came loose with the engine movements given while coming alongside.  Vessel'd draft on arrival was F: 5.9m, A: 6.1m
ImmediateAction: Office informed of the same and all measures were taken to organise for a diving team. In teh meantime, the excessive net was picked up by the Vessel's crew and placed on the poop deck.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damages to any equipment. Only potential to render propeller incapacitated due to fouling/damage to blades/damage to shaft/ingress of water due to damaged seal.
DirectCause: Due to hidden obstructions/hazards in the water alongside, which came loose with engine movements.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Seriousness of the possible consequences discussed with all crew. A good lookout for any hazards and obstructions to always be maintained and Vessel to be steered clear from them. In the case of hidden obstructions and hazards, all crew briefed about emergency preparedness in case of incidents that might occur due to hidden hazards and obstructions.
LessionLearnt: As a Preventive Measure, Ship Staff to verify any concerns regarding the berth and Fenders, while approaching the berth and record same along with evidence and report to concern authorities along with Note of Protest.
ReviewComments: Vessel Sailed from Incheon to Qingdao Anchorage, possibility of Fishing net fouling propeller during this passage ruled out, as Engine parameters were not a concern during the passage and When Vessel was at Anchorage no obstruction observed by Ship Staff during regular safety rounds. Fishing net fouling the propeller at Anchorage also ruled out as Ship Staff did not observe any fishing net near the propeller when sailing from Anchorage area. While Vessel coming along side the propeller was observed to be clear by Ship Staff. Only after making all fast, Ship Staff observed fishing net floating too close to the propeller.  During the next voyage to Qingdao, Ship Staff observed fishing nets stuck within Fenders, for which Photo's are attached and Masters mail is attached. Possible cause of Fouled propeller is due to drifting Fishing nets in vicinity of the berth.
ClosingNote: This incident with preventive measures circulated to Container Fleet to avoid recurrence.

IncidentDetails: Fire incident in boiler uptake pipeline from Aux boiler # 2 uptake valve to scrubber :-  On 4th June at at 03:24 hrs MSBD 220V low  alarm was triggered and immediately after it was followed by the fire alarm at 03:26 hrs. Duty engineer on watch could smell something unusual and rushed to the boiler platform  and noticed the exhaust pipe from the boiler uptake valve to the scrubber has turned red hot. Emergency alarm was raised . Aux boiler # 2 and IG plant was shut  down.
IncidentEventDetails: 03:24  MSBD 220V low insulation alarm  03:26  Fire alarm  03:27  Second Officer called for confirmation  03:28 Bridge was informed that there is fire on boiler uptake pipe . Boiler and IG shut down  03:28 General alarm raised followed by PA  03:30 Crew mustered , head count taken, preparing for fire fighting  03:32 All openings to engine casings shut  03:34 Fire fighters ready with SCBA  03:40 Bridge was informed that there was no open fire and only the exhaust piping for the boiler            had turned red hot.  03:44 Office informed about the incident.  04:15 Master called off emergency stations.
ImmediateAction: Fire alarm was raised and all crew mustered at the mustered station. Duty engineer had already stopped the Aux boiler and IG plant. All engineer's went on the boiler platform.   All engineer's  kept the close watch on the exhaust pipe till the pipe had cooled down sufficiently so that work could be started.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. A small length of cable for the boiler uptake valve near the exhaust pipe  was damaged.  2. Insulation laggings close to the boiler uptake valve damaged  3. One no. tube light fitting damaged.
DirectCause: 1. The fire in the boiler uptake was caused due to soot deposits in the boiler uptake over a considerable period of time. 2. Probable reason for the accumulation of soot deposit can be due to wrong air/fuel ratio to maintain lower oxygen content in the Inert gas. 3. As the vessel was taken over two months prior, it can not be ascertained as to when the boiler furnace was last inspected/cleaned.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Every month the Boiler furnace to be inspected for accumulation of soot deposits.
Corrective Action: Vessel to inspect the boiler furnace every month to ascertain that  the soot deposit is normal for the next six months. Thereafter the vessel to comply with the PMS.
LessionLearnt: 1. After take over crew to ensure that the boiler furnace is clean and accumulation of soot is within nominal limits. 2. Air fuel ratio to be maintained so as to ensure that adequate fuel combustion takes place and regulations pertaining to I.G quality is maintained.
ReviewComments: Review will be completed upon verification of corrective actions.
ClosingNote: Please treat this as a lesson learnt and in future please ensure the boiler furnaces are inspected thoroughly after every cargo operation. Further close and vigilant monitoring of the Oxygen content of IG being delivered as keeping the O2 content low, could lead to accumulation of unburnt carbon deposits in the piping.

IncidentDetails: The Second Engineer told the Third Engineer to transfer the LO to Generator No.1.Third engineer opened the suction valve from LO storage tank to D/G sump tank. He didn't check other valves on pump discharge line. Before transferring the storage tank had 3580 ltrsof LO in it.After 5 minutes of transfer,he observed that the sump tank level not increasing.He stopped the transfer, checked the LO storage tank level and it was 1300 ltrs. Then he stopped the transfer and checked all other D/G.The level remained the same.
IncidentEventDetails: On further inspection the 3/E observed M/E sump tank was open and all 2280 ltrs was transferred into the M/E sump tank. It was marked on the M/E sump tank level gauge.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the LO transfer. Checked all the valves transferred LO to the DG sump tank after crossing the M/E sump tank filling.
DirectCause: Not checking of all valves prior commencing transfer of LO.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Before transferring ,check all valves are closed properly.Also check the marking of the valve.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: 1 number portable Radio fell overboard.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Vessel was coming alongside to Vopak#6, Fujairah,  2) Aft breast lines were being passed to jetty via mooring boat,  3) PIC was bending near the ship side railings to monitor the progress,  4) PIC had the Radio in his overall chest pocket with the lanyard attached to the Radio and passed through the overall chect pocket flap cover,  5) Radio came out of the pocket, lanyard snapped off causing the Radio to fall in water.
ImmediateAction: Reported to Master and thence to office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 Portable Radio lost to sea, details are as under:  Make - Entel,  Model - HT983
DirectCause: 1) Snapping of the lanyard,  2) Undafe practice,  3) Not using the correct pouch
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Discuss with the ship-staff in the next meeting,  2) Start using the dedicated pouch for the Radio, and  3) Promulgate to the fleet.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Training carried out.

IncidentDetails: Damaged 110V Contactor for Stbd Lighting in the Engine Room.
IncidentEventDetails: Fire hydrant valve in Engine Room 2rd Deck was opened for draining any water in the line while overhauling Isolation valves in the fire station. After completion of work, the fire hydrant was not shut properly. Later at around 10:30 hrs, fire pump was started for deck washing. Water started leaking from the hydrant valve on the 2nd deck and fell on Electrical panel on 1st deck, due to which one contactor got damaged.
ImmediateAction: Fire pump was stopped and all the spilt water was mopped up.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One 110V Conctactor in the Electrical Panel for Stbd side Lighting got damaged due to water ingress.
DirectCause: Improper lock-out/tag-out and improper work procedure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew briefed on the importance of informing concerned HOD when opening any valve for maintenance and for marking Lock-out/Tag-out for opened valves so that they can be cross-checked before resuming normal operation.
Corrective Action: Circulate within the fleet.
ReviewComments: Please carry out lock out/tag out system while working on valves and the equipment put back to normal condition upon completion of the job. Close interdepartmental co-ordination should be exercised for such jobs.
ClosingNote: Incident Report Closed on 30th Apr'15.

IncidentDetails: Failure of No.2 DG unit 1 FO pump.
IncidentEventDetails: While change over No.2 DG from HFO to MDO, change over was carried out quickly without giving sufficient time for temperature to reduce, This sudden change in temperature lead to thermal contraction of HFO pump seizure of unit 1 pump.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the DG. Replaced the pump. Inspected the pump cam surface and found the   fault.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Failure of No.2 DG unit 1 FO pump.
DirectCause: Unsafe act.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Explained to engineers for changing over procedure from HFO to MDO.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: O-ring filter broke off due to high pressure and oil was spilled in E/R.
IncidentEventDetails: Approximately 20 liters of HFO sprayed on bulkhead & staircase. 4th Engineer without informing any one closed the boiler return line valve to HFO service tank, considering it as HFO purifier return line. Due to this there was very high pressure on the line leading to break off of filter O-ring and then oil spill in E/R.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the  boiler. Replaced O-rings and the HFO was cleaned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Own vessel machinery damage.
DirectCause: Unsafe act. Not informing the other engineers while carrying out the job.Lack of knowledge of E/R piping system.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Identifying the purpose of each valves from tanks & stencil in E/R with "TO" & "FROM" on bulkhead near to each valve.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: At Sea it was observed that on Main Switchboard (Fwd) Acc A/C Blower breaker tripped.    Initially low insulation alarm sounded, during investigation only, observed that  on MSB Acc A/C Blower breaker tripped.  It was found that connecting strip bars were damaged and insulation covering were damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Suspect connecting bolts/nuts are not fully tightened, causes heat generation leading to insulation failure. Which cause short circuit.
ImmediateAction: ME stopped and blackout was done to repair on MSB bus bars.    After removing strip bars for A/C blower and A/C compr breakers it was found that Aux blower (Aft) strip bars too were damaged, in fact they were in more bad shape than A/C blower strip bars. Strip bars for Aux Blower, A/C blower and A/C compr removed and repaired. All strip bars were covered with heat shrink insulation tubes.    Bolts securing these bars with MSB busbars were melted and siezed. It was very difficult to remove them as the space inside bus bar is very congested. To remove these bars, complete breakers and their bases had to be removed.    Due to time constraint all the strips bars on Aux Blower breaker could not be repaired as the strip securing bolt to Breaker base was totally melted and siezed. As there was no spare breaker base available onboard same breaker with base fitted on MSB.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MSB busbar damaged. Breaker connecting strip bars melted. Leading to ME stoppage and blackout to repair.
DirectCause: Loose conntion on MSB bus bars
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Complete MSB cleaning and retightening to be done as early as possible.
Corrective Action: Complete MSB cleaning and retightening to be done as early as possible.
ClosingNote: Aux Blower Connecting strip bars were renewed on 27.04.15

IncidentDetails: PARTING OF TUG ROPE MADE FAST ON THE STERN FOR PULL BACK TUG.
IncidentEventDetails: * VESSEL ARRIVED AT BANYU URIP MARINE TERMINAL KEEPING 2 SOFT ROPES AS STANDBY ON POOP DECK AS REQUIRED BY THE TERMINAL INFORMATION BOOKELT.  * AS PER INITIAL INFORMATION A TUG WIRE WILL BE MADE FAST ON THE STERN TO BE USED FOR HOLD BACK TUG WHILE THE VESSEL IS BERTHED AT FPSO GAGAK RIMANG(TANDEM MOORING).  * ON ARRIVAL VESSEL WAS INFORMED THAT SHIPS SOFT ROPE WILL BE USED BY THE TUG INSTEAD OF TUG WIRE. SOFT ROPE SUPPLIED BY VESSEL(80MM X 220 MTR JET FLEX TUG LINE, B/L=115 T).  * TUG MADE FAST ON STERN AND SHIPS CREW WENT FOR FWD STATIONS FOR MOORING.  * WHILE THE MOORING WAS IN PROGRESS THE AFT TUG REPORTED THAT THE TUG ROPE PARTED WHEN HE WAS PULLING BACK THE VESSEL.  * SHIPS SOFT ROPE WAS HEAVED BACK AND THEN THE TUG SUPPLIED A TUG WIRE AND SAME WAS MADE FAST ON THE STERN.
ImmediateAction: PROPELLER STOPPED AND SOFT ROPE HEAVED BACK ON BOARD.PULL BACK TUG MADE FAST AGAIN USING TUG WIRE
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: * VESSEL ARRIVED AT BANYU URIP MARINE TERMINAL KEEPING 2 SOFT ROPES AS STANDBY ON POOP DECK AS REQUIRED BY THE TERMINAL INFORMATION BOOKELT.  * AS PER INITIAL INFORMATION A TUG WIRE WILL BE MADE FAST ON THE STERN TO BE USED FOR HOLD BACK TUG WHILE THE VESSEL IS BERTHED AT FPSO GAGAK RIMANG(TANDEM MOORING).  * ON ARRIVAL VESSEL WAS INFORMED THAT SHIPS SOFT ROPE WILL BE USED BY THE TUG INSTEAD OF TUG WIRE. SOFT ROPE SUPPLIED BY VESSEL  * TUG MADE FAST ON STERN AND SHIPS CREW WENT FOR FWD STATIONS FOR MOORING.  * WHILE THE MOORING WAS IN PROGRESS THE AFT TUG REPORTED THAT THE TUG ROPE PARTED WHEN HE WAS PULLING BACK THE VESSEL.  * SHIPS SOFT ROPE WAS HEAVED BACK AND THEN THE TUG SUPPLIED A TUG WIRE AND SAME WAS MADE FAST ON THE STERN.
DirectCause: Ningbo Dawn was the first foreign tanker to berth at Banyu Urip.Lack of Communication between bridge,pilot and tug master.Tug pulled more than the SWL of rope leading to damage/parting of the rope.Should have emphasized more to make sure that tug master does not increases pull more than the SWL of the Tug rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper communication to be established between Pilot,Bridge and Tug Master.Pilot to be advised of the SWL of ship's rope/Bollard/Lead and concerned parties advised not to increase the pull more than SWL of the equipment.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Report considered closed.

IncidentDetails: Transfer of  HFO from LSFO Service tank to HSFO Setting tank, the gasket flange of FO transfer pipe was damaged and oil spill occurred.
IncidentEventDetails: While transferring HFO from LSFO service tank to HSFO setting tank,after line up the third engineer started the pump. After 10 minutes there was a leakage in FO transfer pipe and Oil spilt into the Engine room. He stopped the pump and checked the system,the filling valve of HFO setting tank(Butterfly valve) which was replaced newly was self slack and not properly closed.Due to high pressure in pump,the gasket flange of Fo transfer pipe was damaged.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the FO transfer,the engine room was cleaned,the gasket was replaced.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While transferring the HFO from LSFO service tank to HSFO setting tank,after line up the pump was started. There was a leakage and oil spilt in the ER.Due to high pressure in the pipe line the gasket flange of FO transfer pipe was damaged.
DirectCause: Lack of knowledge of Fo transfer procedure. Lack of skill/motivation.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training all Engineers on FO transfer procedure and pipe lines.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: Damage of fwd  liferaft cover.
IncidentEventDetails: 28 April 2015/2230 hrs - After all line on deck(fwd & aft), vessel will be shift astern to berth no.16 for bunkering. Bosun found damage on the top of liferaft cover.Informed Chief Officer and Chief Officer informed Master immediately on portable radio. Master advised the Chief Officer to investigate and take photo after made fast on berth No.16.Master called Agent (Mr. Andrew) from Nautilus that we found hidden damage on liferaft and receive advise he will arrange on 29th morning before vessel departure.
ImmediateAction: Informed All concern parties.  Debriefed crew regarding securing of all LSA Equipments well before, specially when loading Log cargo.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LIFERAFT COVER DAMAGED, ON THE UPPER AND LOWER PART  FROM INSIDE MATERIALS AND FITTINGS INTACT(NOT DAMAGED)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PROPER COORDINATION WITH FOREMAN WITH REGARDS TO PROPER LAYING OF LOGS AND HANDLING OF DECK CARGOS BY DIGGER OPERATORS THAT MIGHT FALL TO  ANY SHIPS FITTINGS ALSO THE NEED TO SECURE DECK FITTINGS THE MIGHT BE HIT BY FALLING LOGS.
Corrective Action: 1. Supervision and regular monitoring of cargo work.  2. Proper stowing and securing of cargo. 3. Better to make some kind of protection and put on top of fwd liferaft while loading / discharging logs.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting. protection cover or similar arrangement to be done for preventing damage in future.

IncidentDetails: While un-berthing from FSO, Fwd messenger line parted due to excessive weight. No injuries to crew members.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Pilot requested Slow Astern, too sooner after Dead Slow Astern.    2.Vessel was paying out messenger at maximum rate.    3.Resulting speed from Engine movement was greater than rate of Paying out.    4.Vessel could not arrest the astern movement even though Captain came upto Half Ahead on Engines.    5. Too much weight on messenger rope resulted in parting. No injury to an crew member.
ImmediateAction: 1. Bridge was informed Immediately by C/O when he felt the weight is coming on messenger, despite paying out at maximum rate.    2. To arrest astern movement, Engines were brought to Half Ahead as soon as possible.     3. Upon realizing the fact, an accident is likey to occur, C/O positioned every one in safer area, away from danger zone just before parting of rope.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Fwd messenger rope parted from eye (leading end). No other damage to any other machinery or equipment.
DirectCause: 1. During berthing after making fast chain, vessell has to clear the terminal's messenger line from the drum and disconnect the ship's line.This was overlooked.  2.Astern speed seemed to be on the higher side ( about 1.4 kts), upon Pilot's advice.  Aft tailing tug was also pulling during this time.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Vessel to disconnect own messenger line from shore's messenger prior to unberthing to prevent such incident. This shall ensure quick release of shore pick up & messenger line free from any sort of kinks or entangles. Loading / berthing masters at offshore terminals mostly advise vsl to do this prior to unberthing operations. If no such instruction is given, senior deck officers to remind bosun to perform this task after berthing operations.  2. Astern speed during unberthing to be kept under control (abt 1.0 Kt), subject to pay out speed of fwd winches.
LessionLearnt: Usually after berthing operations,the shore messenger is disconnected from the ship's messenger,enabling quick disconnection during cast off operations.Crew members failed to perform this action in port & the vessel's stern movement was not checked closely by the Bridge team & also by the forward mooring team members in the initial stage,though it was appreciated towards the later stage.RA for SBM / TANDEM mooring operations to be revisited hi liting this issue.Tool box meeting onsite also should discuss about the cast off procedures & letting go the lines in an emergency.Kindly carry out a training session with the deck crew members regarding the mooring procedures during SBM/TANDEM/STS operations.

IncidentDetails: As per Voyage orders due to High pour point cargo precautions, V/l has to Drain the manifolds & line after completion of each oeprations.  In Dumai, Indonesia after completion of Hose disconnection at 14:48 Hrs on 25Apr'15.  V/l was blowing through the manifolds & line using air hose at a pressure of 5Bars, while doing the same coupling from air hose parted. Due to pressure in line oil spray splashed near the manifold.
IncidentEventDetails: Port : Dumai , Indonesia       Date : 25Apr15         1230 LT :  Commenced Line draining.  1448 LT : Completed Hose Disconnection  1450 LT : Commenced building the air pressure in No.2 manifold line to drain the manifolds & lines                   in No4 COT.  1500 LT : Line Pressure reached 5 Bars, Drain v/v to No.4 COT opened, Line drained to No4 COT.  1505 LT : Commenced building the air pressure in No.3 manifold line to drain the manifolds & lines                   in No4(P) COT.  1515 LT : Line Pressure reached 5 Bars, Before opening the Drain v/v, Coupling from air hose parted & Oil splashed near the manifold.
ImmediateAction: Cock at pressure gauge connection closed immediately. Pressure of the line released by opening the Drain v/v to No4(P) COT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oil spray splashed on deck.
DirectCause: a)Improper securing of air hose to the pressure gauge cock. Same was tightened with one jubile clip.  b)Failure to keep a crew member stand by at air hose connection to monitor any possible leaks at the adaptor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ensure the air hose is fixed to the coupling with two new jubilee clips, as a pollution hazard exists for line blow through. One cre member to be standby at the connection adaptor who could close the cock in emergency, if at all a leak develops.
Corrective Action: Couplings of Pneumatic air hoses on board to be secured with two jubilee clips
LessionLearnt: Hoses used on the High pressure system should be provided with two independent securing system to avoid any unpleasant situations.In addition,while working on two independent high pressure systems,as in this case of using deck air at high pressure to blow down the cargo line at high pressure to clear the cargo remains,two men watch system to be involved as the chances of rectifying a mistake is immediate and one person to help each other.RA to be revisited on the incident & shared for future use.
ReviewComments: CA implemented on board,NMR considered closed

IncidentDetails: TWO STERN STBD MOORING LINES PARTED, RAILNG DAMAGED,DEVELOPED HOLE ON DECH DUE TO UPROOTING OF VERTICAL STAND PIPE OF RAILING RESULTING WATER SEEPING IN STEERING FLAT STBD SIDE DURING STS OPERATION AT OFFSHORE LOME   INCIDENT CAUSED DUE TO ERROR IN JUDGEMENT AND ASSESSMENT
IncidentEventDetails: 1) STS OPERATION PLANNED WITH PRETTY JEWELRY WITH MOORING PLAN 4 + 2 FROM BOTH VESSELS.  2) OWN VESSEL EQUIPED WITH WIRE ROPES + SOFT TAILS AND OTHER VESSEL ALL SOFT ROPES  3) FIRST LINE PASSED AT 1800 LT ON 30.04.2015 SUBSEQUENTLY FINISHING MOORING OPERATIONS AT 2130 LT ON 30.04.2015  4) THROUGHT THIS MOORING OPERATION WEATHER WAS SMOOTH WITH OTHER VESSEL ROLLING  HEAVILY AT TIMES.  5) ALL LINES WERE MADE FAST EXCEPT FOR THE LAST TWO STERN LINES AND DISCUSSION IN PROGRESS BETWEEN BRIDGE AND AFT STATION AS TO WHERE THESE STERN LINES TO PASSED FROM.  6) AFT STATION HAD RAISED CONCERN OVER PASSING STERN LINES FROM RIGHT ASTERN DUE TO INCORRECT LEAD. IF THE LINES WERE PASSED FROM RIGHT ASTERN THE LEAD WILL BE AS GOOD AS FWD SPRING AND WAS CONFORMED ON CLOASER ASSESSEMENT FROM FUNNEL DECK  7) DURING THE PLANING FOR PASSING STERN LINE CHIEF OFFICER PROCEEDED TO AFT STATION FOR ASSISTING AFT CREW IN PASSING STERN LINES.  8) CHIEF OFFICER ASSESSED THE SITUATION AND DECIDED TO PASS THE WIRE MOORING LINE FROM RIGHT ASTERN WHICH RESULTED IN PARTING OF TWO STERN LINES AFTER 3 HRS OF VESSEL ALL FAST  9) DURING PARTING OF LINES THE OTHER VESSEL WAS ROLLING HEAVLY AT TIMES
ImmediateAction: CALLED FOR STATIONS AND  CREW MUSTERED. MOORING MASTER ON OTHER VESSEL NOTIFIED.  ALL OTHER LINES TENDED.  BRIEFING CARRIED OUT FOR BETTER ASSESMENT / PRECAUTION BEFORE PASSING MOORING LINES. EXPLAINED ALL CREW TO KEEP MOORING WIRES VERY SLIGHTLY SLACK THEN THE OTHER VESSELS MOORING ROPES AND ALL ROPES MUST WORK IN TANDEM.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: TWO STERN STBD MOORING LINES PARTED, RAILNG DAMAGED,DEVELOPED HOLE ON DECK DUE TO UPROOTING OF VERTICAL STAND PIPE OF RAILING RESULTING WATER SEEPING IN STEERING FLAT FROM DECK ON STBD SIDE DURING STS OPERATION AT OFFSHORE LOME
DirectCause: INCIDENT CAUSED DUE TO ERROR IN JUDGEMENT AND ASSESSMENT
Corrective Action: Safety training was carried out on board immediately after the incident explaining correct mooring procedure while passing ropes, not to come under pressure during critical operations and close monitoring of mooring.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: All the corrective actions are closed

IncidentDetails: Dammage to ship shell plating due to contact with fender at dock 14 , Three rivers

Corrective Action: Fleet Alert to be sent to all the vessels in the company to make them aware of the following: o Constraints and restrictions of Dock 14, Three Rivers Canada. o Additional Precautions that is required when docking this berth. o Any concerns during Master Pilot exchange if required Office to be contacted for further guidance.

IncidentDetails: Aft mast one no of  stay wire Parted.
IncidentEventDetails: While swinging Crane no.2 (Ship Crane) , for loading containers by shore crane as advised by foreman,crane block hit the stay wire of aft mast accidentally and parted.
ImmediateAction: immediately moved out the crane and block from superstructure area, checked radar mast for any damages, found none, checked other stay wires and confirm all properly tightened
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While vessel alogside at New Port Pusan terminal for cargo operations,as instructed by foreman for loading containers(Shore crane) ,ship crane no 2 swinged and crane block  accidentally hit the stay wire (1 no) of aft mast  and stay wire parted.
DirectCause: When crane boom stopped as instructed by foreman,crane block swinged and hit the stay wire of aft mast. Carelessness on part of crew member , COSWP not complied with,
RootCause: As the crane block is at a distance (3m) from the boom ,when the crane boom stopped ,block swinged and hit the stay wire of aft mast,
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Planned for Renewal of stay wire.(Aft mast was addittionally supported by another 3 stay wires) , While swinging crane stay rope to be used to control swinging of block, Emphasis laid od COSWP,
LessionLearnt: Effective communication between Crane operator and Signal person. Area to accessed by the crane operator
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting. Noted Stay wire immediately renewed

IncidentDetails: During Arrival port Ho Chi  Minh City( Vietnam ) after Pilot onboard Bow thruster could not be started.Hydraulic oil  gravity tank low level alarm and Hydraulic oil low pressure alarm displayed on Bow thruster panel on bridge.
IncidentEventDetails: 1)After opening  forepeak doors found forepeak store water logged to about 1 foot high and a part of the bow thruster panel was submerged in water.Opened the BT panel and found transformer and relays submerged in water.  2) Bow thruster hydraulic pump overload relay OCR 2 , TM 4  Timer was not getting energized/ malfunctioned due to seawater ingress.  3) Tranformers too submerged in water and unsafe to start in this condition.  4) After inspection found temporary repair to Starboard Anchor wash line had given way and flooded the compartment.
ImmediateAction: 1) Starboard Anchor wash line isolated and water pumped overboard via eductor.  2)Checked the tank level, oil level was sufficient,still topped up little more - observed no change in alarm.  3) Dried transformer and relays with air and hot air blower.   3) Changed  relay X 17- Hydraulic oil low pressure, X7 hydraulic pump start, X 5-Hydraulic pump overload,X18 - hydraulic oil pump run, X 14 - gravity tank low level.  4) Checked the insulation of Transformer winding and motor insulation - found normal. All relays get power supply from transformer.  5) Checked all fuses found all normal.  6) Almost all relays have moisture content. Removed all cleaned with Contact cleaner and kept in engine room for drying.  7) Contactor and overload relay changed with new spare due to internal short circuit .  8) Bus bars cleaned with electrocleaner and  cargo lights placed for warming up.  9)After about 12 hrs removed cargo lights and checked all insulation and continuity found normal .Bow thruster tried out  working satisfactory.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL

IncidentDetails: While E/R in UMS mode, incinerator alarm sounded and found that there was smoke and flame coming out of the incinerator. It' s metal plates got red hot and bulged to a certain extent with paint burnt. Electric cables & pipes plastic clamps melted. No other damage in both mechanical & electrical equipments were observed. No person was injured in this incident.
IncidentEventDetails: 1504 : Critical alarm sounded on E/R UMS panel  1505 : E/R Manned / 2nd Eng. informed bridge Incinerator fire.  1506 : 2nd off raised general alarm / Announced fire in E/R  1508 : Crew mustered  1510 : Crew entered E/R for fighting fire.  1512 : Reported thick smoke coming from E/R  1522 : Fire under control.    -Only fresh water and foam extinguishers used.  -6 nos. Portable Foam Extinguishers of 9lts used.  -Hypermist system Pre-warning got activated.
ImmediateAction: After extinguishing the fire and cooldown of incinerator, the metal plates were removed to straighten the buckling of the plates. Burner & combustion equipments were O/H. Furnce refractory inspected and found satisfactory. Incinerator tried out satisfactory.    Following instructions were given to prevent recurrence:    1. All E/R staff instructed not to run incinerator during UMS periods. Whenever the Incinerator is running regular monitoring to be carried out like checking the flame thru peep hole, shell temperature, Negative pressure in the chamber, Furnace and Flue gas temperature.     2. Incinerator furnace and Burner front must be inspected on daily basis before starting the Incinerator.    3. The flame must be watched through the peep hole as flame visible from the floor of the Incinerator furnace means the combustion is not proper and oil droplets getting accumulated at the bottom of the furnace.    4. The Pilot burner should not be continuously burning along with the sludge burner which indicates the sludge quality is not good. This could be due to high water content / poor quality of sludge. If it is the case then the operation must be monitored very closely.    5. Only properly trained and authorized E/R personnel to operate & monitor the operation of incinerator.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Incinerator Shell plates got bulged/ buckled.   Electric cables & pipes plastic securing clamps got melted.  Combustion chamber thermocouple found damaged.  Electric junction box plastic cover found deformed.
DirectCause: Due to bad quality of sludge, the incinerator blast tube got obstructed with unburnt carbon deposit leading to dripping and thereof accumulation of hot & burning oil at the bottom surface of furnace. This hot oil found it's way through one of the air cooling furnace holes at the bottom of the furnace leading to annular space between shell plates thereby causing metal plates to become red hot & bulge. Eventually, burning sludge found its way into the save all tray of incinerator.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. All E/R staff instructed not to run incinerator during UMS periods. Whenever the Incinerator is running regular monitoring to be carried out like checking the flame thru peep hole, shell temperature, Negative pressure in the chamber, Furnace and Flue gas temperature.     2. Incinerator furnace and Burner front must be inspected on daily basis before starting the Incinerator.    3. The flame must be watched through the peep hole as flame visible from the floor of the Incinerator furnace means the combustion is not proper and oil droplets getting accumulated at the bottom of the furnace.    4. The Pilot burner should not be continuously burning along with the sludge burner which indicates the sludge quality is not good. This could be due to high water content / poor quality of sludge. If it is the case then the operation must be monitored very closely.    5. Only properly trained and authorized E/R personnel to operate & monitor the operation of incinerator.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Not to operate Incinerator during UMS periods and process / mix the sludge in proper proportion.
ClosingNote: This incident was shared with all the sister vessels with same type of incinerator and a safety meeting was conducted and explained about the safe operation of incinerator.

IncidentDetails: During STS operation at Of shore Lome,Togo.Three of the HMPE rope tails parted(Head lines) and HMPE rope jacket damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Due to heavy rolling of Own ship and daughter vessel during STS operation three head lines tails parted and HMPE rope jacket damaged.
ImmediateAction: Immidetally make Temporary arrangement by making bowline knot on made fast with other vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring line tail parted due to heavy rolling by own ship and daughter vessel during STS operation
DirectCause: Heavy rolling by own ship and daughter vesel during STS operation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The Parting of mooring tails is caused by the vessel movements view the prevalent Swell.  The weather conditions to be monitored while during STS operations.   For safe STS ops, much depends on the effect of the sea and swell on the fenders and the rolling movements induced in the participating ships, taking into account their relative freeboards.   The following are the Company’s guidelines on weather condition limits on STS operations applicable.  § Three (3) meters swell height or more; or § 30-knot winds or more, or § Less than one (1) mile visibility.   The master shall review local contacts and take final decision of continuation of STS.
Corrective Action: A discussion on Ch.09 [Ship to Ship Operations] to be carried out with the ship's staff highlighting the importance of safe mooring practices during STS transfers.
ClosingNote: Training carried out for Ship's staff on 03-Jul-15. Training Record attached.

IncidentDetails: After dropping outbound Pilot at Cape Town, while securing port side accommodation ladder the stowing wire parted and the ladder fell into the water. Vessel dropped anchor. Gangway retrived and stowed. No serious apparent damage to the gangway, however a through check will be carried out. Wire falls, stowing wire and telescopic wire needs to be changed. No injuries.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had a very hectic schedule at Cape Town with loading reefers and a shortage of manpower. Officers and crew were worked to the limit. Cargo work completed 12th/1524. Sailing was delayed due Reefer Tech attending Malfunctions. 12/1200- 1600 Checking Reefers and lashing. 12/1615 - 1700 Stowaway surch carried out. 12/1736 Pilot on board. 12/1745 All shore personnel away and gangway picked up to horizontal position. 12/1754 SBE. 12/1800 3 Tugs made fast. 12/1806 Last line cast off. 12/1812 Pilot Ladder rigged. 12/1836 Last tug cast off. 12/1842 Pilot away. 12/1848 Bow crossed outer breakwater. Crew Instructed to secure pilot ladder and Gangway.  12/1852 Passed outerbouys of channel. 12/1900 Reported that gangway fallen in water.
ImmediateAction: 12/1900 - Stop engine,v/l speed 8.5 kts ahead. Engine given full asten. Inform VTS dropped anchor due to emergency . Engine not responding astern- start air pressure low alarm. Ch eng reported bridge both air bottle empty.Wheel put hard to stbd to avoid collision with anchored vessel. BT full to stbd.Stbd anchor walk back to one shackle on deck.12/1910- Speed of v/l dropped to 6.2 kts.12/1912 - Engine responded D.S.Aster.V/l stopped and swung to stbd with Bow thruster.Ch eng informed one kick possible to give every 5 mins.12/1924 V/l dropded stbd anchor. Inspected gangway found stowing wire parted resulting gangway falling in water. Sliding & Hoisting wire seen apparently intact. However suspected dislodge from sheave.Gangway position incline about 70 degree with telescopic run out 70% and 6 mtrs in water. VTS informed about situation and preparation made for lifting off gangway.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1) One liftine wire parted.  2) One step of Telescope damage while picking up telescope.  3) One hoisting wire damage.  4) Slipping of Sheaves while falling.  5) Will revert with other damages after opening up and inspection.
DirectCause: As per record wire changed as follow:  Port - Hoisting wire 05-Mar-13, Sliding wire -  05-Mar-13, Support & Stowing wire - 21-Nov-14  Stbd - Hoisting wire & Stowing wire- 05-Nov-12, Support &  Sliding wire -  21-Nov-14  Last inspection as entered in SMMS carried out on 30-Mar-15 and maintence carried out on 03-Apr & 07-Apr,Shackle for port side Sliding wire was not appropriate and same cjhanged on 31-Mar-15(Photo Attached). Found Sliding wire with bottle screw which not to be used. Same planned for renewal & new wires prepared. However could not completed due to shortage of time and manpower.  At Capetown - due to loading of reefers all crew were overworked and rest hour violated - Due to plugging of U/Deck & O/Deck reefers,Checking for Lashing and container sitting, attending shore technician for malfunctining of reefers,Picking of spares and stores,Stowaway search. Feel to fatigue, Crew tried to secure gangway without taking proper procedure,resulting in overload of gangway and wire parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Due operation of vessel, company needs to seriously look into compliment of the vessel.  2) Suggest that all Accommodation ladder wires are changed on yearly basis. Rigging & parts used should be as per manufacturer instructions.  3) Certificate for all wire,shackle etc. should be maintained in order on board.  4) Immediate change of all wire & shackles required. Requistion raised.
ClosingNote: Wires to be chabged every two years. Proper lashing gear to be used. All requested materials supplied on 01.05.2015. Suitably renwed materials and checked. All OK.

IncidentDetails: Vessel's ropes parted due to surging of vessel in heavy swell conditions.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was moored to Berth No: 1, Petro Peru Terminal for loading Virgin Naphta. Mooring configuration was (3+3+3), all soft ropes fwd and aft and Port anchor sea side. The port had no break water and was a sea port with swell hitting the vessel on her Stbd quarter. On berthing on 11th April 2015, vessel noticed slight surging of vessel and informed the concern to pilot. Pilot confirmed that it was normal for all vessels visiting the port. As a precaution, vessel fixed canvas covers around the ropes section in closed fairleads to avoid chaffing damage to ropes. Grease was also applied regularly to reduce friction. 3 crew members were kept on deck watch all the time.  On 12th April 2015/around 1000 hrs vessel noticed an increase in swell and surging of vessel increased and continuously. Same was conveyed to Loading master and he informed that the condition was normal. Loading completed at 1236 hrs. At 1340 hrs, Aft spring rope parted due to heavy surging of the vessel. Informed the Loading master and port and asked for tug’s assistance.  At 1400 hrs tug made fast on port quarter with tugs line. With the tugs assistance also, vessel was surging heavily. At 1630 hrs two ropes (Fwd Spring and breast rope) gave way. Vessel asked for another tug for assistance and asked agent to call for pilot for shifting the vessel to anchorage. At 1654 hrs 2nd tug was made fast on port bow fwd to hold the vessel alongside. There was a discrepancy in cargo figures and vessel was waiting for charterer’s permission to sign B/L. At 1850 hrs, vessel received instructions to sign the papers. At 1920 hrs, a head line and aft breast line parted. Vessel requested all port authorities to clear the vessel and disembark as soon as possible. Finally vessel was casted off with the help of two tugs and proceeded to sea.
ImmediateAction: As a precaution, vessel fixed canvas covers around the ropes section in closed fairleads to avoid chaffing damage to ropes. Grease was also applied regularly to reduce friction. Tugs called for assistance.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Five mooring ropes have parted. Three ropes were damaged by chaffing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fixed canvas covers around the ropes section in closed fairleads to avoid chaffing damage to ropes. Grease applied regularly to reduce friction.
Corrective Action: Fixed canvas covers around the ropes section in closed fairleads to avoid chaffing damage to ropes. Grease applied regularly to reduce friction.
LessionLearnt: Master to get weather reports from agents and office for the period of stay of the vessel at port.Same to be included in the risk assessment for the vessel for mooring at this port.Same to be verified during the IA
ReviewComments: Documentation to be verified during IA
ClosingNote: Closed on 26/111/15

IncidentDetails: Stolen cash inside crew cabin by visitors at Suez Canal transit
IncidentEventDetails: At around 0300H LT during vessel transit in Suez Canal, deck crew were very busy attending port formalities, bonded store delivery, mooring boat, suez crew and suez electrician boarding,etc.  Some egyptian nationals (mooring boat crew, mechants, ship chandler, agent, electrician) have entered the ship's accomodation. At around 0330H LT chief officer locked all stores inside and outside the accomodation including the messrooms, recreation rooms, ship's office, gymnasium, hospital, galley, etc. and also ordered the deck crew to lock their respective cabins. Upon checking his cabin before locking it, ordinary seaman Garie Caballero noticed that he has already lost his money amounting to US$400 inside his wallet on his table. He immediately notified Chief Officer and then Chief Officer notified Master regarding the incident.
ImmediateAction: Master then conducted investigation and informed the pilot about the incident. One of the pilot also conducted an investigation regarding the incident and asked his fellow egyptians if they have taken the money but they denied the accusations.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss of cash of one of the crew onboard
DirectCause: Cabin door was not locked during transit at Suez canal. Some unidentified visitor took his cash inside his cabin while he is very busy attending port operations
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All doors should be locked whenever vessel is in port or transit
LessionLearnt: Crew cabins to be locked in areas where there is known chances of Theft. Visitors must not be allowed to wander around inside the accommodation. Only pilots , Electrician and SCA personnel to be allowed inside the accommodation.Mooring crew to be allowed access to their cabin/resting rooms only.
ReviewComments: Petty theft due to non-compliance with onboard security procedures. Cabins should be kept locked at all times.
ClosingNote: Incident to be discussed during the next HSSE meeting.

IncidentDetails: 1245 : Called service boat San Nicholas on VHF Ch. 16 but no reply was received.  1315 : Received a call from San Nicholas on VHF that they are on own vessel's Stbd quarter at 0.5 miles off & requested own vessel to used engines to provide lee on Stbd side. Notice was given to prepare engines.  1320 : San Nicholas conveyed that the boat is coming alongside & own vessel is to lower the rope for picking-up the baggages of technicians first.  1325 : San Nicholas alongside. Weather condition: NE'ly winds x 20 kt, Swell height about 2.0 mtrs.  1326 : Rope was lowered as requested and the bags were being picked.  1330 : Bosun reported that one technician had started to climb on the ladder and had reached the gangway portion of combination ladder.  1331 : Meanwhile second technician who stepped on the pilot ladder, slipped & fell overboard.    The technicians were wearing lifevests. Immediately lifebuoy was thrown for the person to pick-up, Service boat tried to rescue the person by approaching closer. The Man Overboard (MOB) was able to get hold of the boat's fender and tried to climb the boat but couldn't manage to climb on the boat. The MOB then decided to get hold ship's lifebouy while the boat assisted him by maneouvring. While doing so the MOB was pushed closer to the ship's side. The MOB eventually got hold of ship's lifebouy and one of the ship staff went down to hook him and was finally recovered from water.

Notice was given to prepare engines.  1320 : San Nicholas conveyed that the boat is coming alongside & own vessel is to lower the rope for picking-up the baggages of technicians first.  1325 : San Nicholas alongside.
