The technicians were wearing lifevests. Immediately lifebuoy was thrown for the person to pick-up, Service boat tried to rescue the person by approaching closer. The Man Overboard (MOB) was able to get hold of the boat's fender and tried to climb the boat but couldn't manage to climb on the boat. The MOB then decided to get hold ship's lifebouy while the boat assisted him by maneouvring. While doing so the MOB was pushed closer to the ship's side. The MOB eventually got hold of ship's lifebouy and one of the ship staff went down to hook him and was finally recovered from water.
ImmediateAction: PERSON RECOVERED FROM WATER AND FIRST AID GIVEN.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NON-FATAL INJURY,SHORE TECHNICIAN DISEMABRKED THE VESSEL AFTER THAT NO FEEDBACK FROM HIM REGARDING THE SAME.
DirectCause: PERSON BOARDED THE VESSEL WITHOUT KNOWLEDGE OF BRIDGE TEAM AND IN PROCESS SLIPPED AND FELL IN WATER.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: BOAT SKIPPER SHOULD INFORM AND OBTAIN PERMISSION FROM MASTER BEFORE BOARDING.
LessionLearnt: As per attached investigation report.
ClosingNote: Pls refer attached Investigation Report.

IncidentDetails: 1 number fwd breast rope protective covering peeled off for a length of abt 30cm at the split drum.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was alongside at Puerto Moin, Costa Rica.  This Berth is open to swell and thus the continous surging movement on the vessel.  While Casting off from the berth when the fwd breast line was slackened, the protective covering peeled off for a length of abt 30cm at the split drum only.
ImmediateAction: As a preventive measure, duct tape was applied on the affected area
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 number fwd breast rope protective covering peeled off for a length of abt 30cm at the split drum.
DirectCause: Berth was open to swell and therefore resulting in continous surging of the vessel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Damaged area of the rope to be close ceased with twine for 2 layers extending 100mm beyond the damaged part on both sides.
Corrective Action: Carry out briefing for the Ship's staff on the care and maintenance of HMPE ropes. Specific training to be imparted to the staff to identify chafing hazards and provide/monitor adequate protection for same.
ClosingNote: Training carried out.  Periodic inspection of Rope/Chafe protection sleeve to be carried out.

IncidentDetails: When the vessels M/v Prosper and M/v Hansa Calypso passed-by ,about 10 knots with a high tide of 2.4 meters, the Gangway landed on the jetty, hit the mooring bollard and got damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel Port-side alongside at Culf jetty at Chittagong,Bangladesh,undergoing repairs to the AEs.The Third officer was the Duty Officer and the Deck cadet as gangway watch.The Port-side gangway was lowered down by the Third officer while the Superintendent disembarked the vessel. The Gangway was still at the lowered position(on the jetty). Deck cadet went to have breakfast and Third officer as gangway watch.After a while third officer called the Deck cadet for the gangway watch because, he wanted to go to the Forward store to take empty drum as requested by the Cleaning gang.The Deck cadet came and he heard some sound like mooring rope broken,he informed the Chief officer. Deck cadet heard the sound of Gangway hit with the Jetty.The Master and the Chief officer came down and saw the Port-side gangway was bent,damaged and deformed due to the gangway hit the jetty because of high swell when vessels passed-by.
ImmediateAction: 1.Gangway watch maintained.  2.Heave up gangway every time after use to prevent hit with jetty at Port-Side.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gangway damage
DirectCause: Gangway lowered without assessing the proximity of Obstructions. Vessel surged due to passing vessel in the channel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gangway should not be landed on the jetty when there is an obstruction in the proximity of the landing area. Mooring to be attended to when vessel movement is expected in the channel.
LessionLearnt: Gangway to be attended by a trained and qualified person. Cadets / Trainees should not be allowed to handle the gangway and other deck equipment without supervision. Proximity of hazards to be identified before landing the gangway on the jetty. All concerned to be briefed re the dangers.
ReviewComments: Due to a vessel passing at high speed, own vessel surged and mooring ropes slipped/ brakes rendered. As the gangway was lowered close to the mooring bit on the jetty, the gangway made contact with the bit and subsequently got damaged.
ClosingNote: Cost of Repairs not available at the time of review of this report. Lessons learnt to be discussed during the next HSSE meeting.

IncidentDetails: 1.Grab power cable broke and parted off.  2.Safety rails on starboard side weather deck fore part of CH/2 bented the top pipe.Flat bar post detached from its welded base and other safety bar slightly bended.  3.No injuries to crew.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Vsl at Puttalam achorage awaiting to discharge her coal cgo of 55,930MT onboard.  2.Advance routine preparation for disch were carried out prior arrival of stevedores.At 0800LT all deck crew was task to prepare and make ready all ship's grab to position on weather deck on top of the wooden dunnage to avoid from moving or slipping.  3.First, they started to prepare from crane no.4 and lifted up grab no.4 from its rest position and to transfer position on portside maindeck.After grab no.4 placed in position shifted to no.3 and no.2 in sequence on weather deck port and starboard side done at 1000hrs.  4.At 1040hrs.crane no.1 jib hoisted up in vertical position to aligned grab no.1 resting cradle so as to connect the stabilzer wire and power cable wire for the grab.At this instant,grab ready to llift up and the ship crane operator has given signal to start and lift up the grab.stabilizer wire heaved up to tighten while power cable wirewas a little slacked.While lifting up the grab no one sees that power cable catched the grab securing turn buckle and causes the cable to break or parted off.  5.During operation,the swell started to build up abt 0.5-1.5m height, one factor that make the ship to rolls and the grab swinging in and out the ship and makes the grab to turn around 360 degrees and the power cable was stucked and wrapped around the cargo hook and the shackle.  6.Ship's cargo crane operator swung the crane jib away from the crane body to avoid further damage by hitting part of the ship until grab swinging stabilize.He then swung the grab closer to the ship to position the grab on weather deck but again the ship suddenly rolls and the grab hitted the safety rails causing damaged by way of bending the top pipe rails and by way of detaching flat bar post from its welded base.  7.At 1150LT grab positioned on weather deck on top of the wooden dunnage to firmly rest.
ImmediateAction: 1.Power cable fixed and restored for immediate use.  2.All ship's cargo gear ready to use.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Grab power cable parted. 2. Main deck safety rails dented and one flat bar detached from deck weld.
DirectCause: 1. Crew not monitoring the grab power cable before lifting the grab from stored position. 2. Crane operator unable to control the grab movement due to vessel rolling.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Signal man to be posted on deck to communicate with the crane operator.  When vessel is rolling use of crane and grab to be avoided.
LessionLearnt: Importance of securing cables before using grab. Having an officer supervise the operation and testing of Cranes and Grabs, especially when vessel is at Sea or open anchorages.
ReviewComments: Near miss of similar nature have been reported by the vessel in the past. This incident has resulted due to neglect of Ship staff to follow basic safe operation practices WRT cranes and Grabs. Chief officer to supervise operation/testing of cranes and grabs when vessel is in un-sheltered anchorages.
ClosingNote: This incident/damage resulted in the crane down-time, which fortunately did not result in vessel off-hire. This incident to be discussed during the next HSSE meeting and too-box meetings.

IncidentDetails: Compression Joint for Fire Line Starboard side, Size 1-5-8-125M (DAICOUP), located on Dock Tunnel Starboard side above Cargo Hold #4 Discovered LEAKING which resulted to the water ingress.
IncidentEventDetails: March 6, 2015 afternoon, The maintenance routine check was to Inspect Port & Starboard Dock Tunnel structural condition. During the preliminary steps by first sounding the tunnel space, it was discovered with water ingress on the compartment above cargo hold #4.     Open the manhole cover and ventilated for atleast 1 hour then proceed to the next manhole cover to  open but by visually outside the next compartment,  showing the moisture indicating the level of water presence, inside the dock tunnel compartment.      Loosen the bolts nuts of the manhole cover and confirm the ingress of water inside dock compartment with around 1 meter water level.
ImmediateAction: Start draining the water to #4 Cargo Hold Bilge and strip by eductor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Compression Joint for Fire Line Starboard side, Size 1-5-8-125M (DAICOUP), located on Dock Tunnel Starboard side above Cargo Hold #4 Discovered LEAKING which resulted to the water ingress.
DirectCause: Due to vibration and stress,compression joint misaligned.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Isolated, Inspected and jacked up the line, and aligned in order and observed no leaks.
LessionLearnt: To ensure Fire line dresser couplings or compressor joints are inspected as per SMMS and after experiencing rough Weather conditions. To Ensure daily Sounding taken to ensure Space is free of Water.
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting. To Ensure effective Monitoring.

IncidentDetails: DRIVING CHAIN OF STARBOARD PILOT LADDER REEL MOTOR WAS DISCOVERED DAMAGED  DURING REPAIR OF PILOT LADDER STEP
IncidentEventDetails: DURING REPAIR OF PILOT LADDER STEPS, DECK CREW USED THE PILOT LADDER REEL TO SLACK THE PILOT LADDER, SUDDENLY THE PILOT REEL STOPPED TURNING EVEN IF THE MOTOR IS STILL RUNNING. ENGINEERS ATTEND THE INCIDENT AND OPENED THE CASING OF THE DRIVING CHAIN AND FOUND OUT THAT THE DRIVING CHAIN WAS DAMAGED.
ImmediateAction: MADE REQUISITION FOR THE DAMAGED DRIVING CHAIN FOR PENDING REPAIR
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAMAGED DRIVING CHAIN OF PILOT LADDER REEL MOTOR
DirectCause: THE CASING HAS NO GREASING POINT LEADING DIRECTLY TO THE DRIVING CHAIN.  IT HAS TO BE OPENED MANUALLY, AND CHECK VISUALLY THE CONDITION OF THE DRIVING CHAIN AND APPLY GREASE REGULARLY.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: SUGGEST TO INCLUDE IN THE SMMS THE MAINTENANCE FOR THE PARTICULAR DRIVING CHAIN
LessionLearnt: Proper Inspection and maintenance of all moving parts.
ReviewComments: Noted and approve proposed corrective action.
ClosingNote: Incident caused due to lack of maintenance coupled with bad design. Incident to be discussed during the next hsse meeting and all deck crew and officers to be made aware of inspection and maintenance requirements of the pilot ladder drive chain.

IncidentDetails: While taking a regular deck round, it was observed that the Gangway davit hoisting wire was  badly damaged/wasted around a sheave.
IncidentEventDetails: routine inspection, brought to light the condition of the wire.
ImmediateAction: the wire was immediately renewed with a spare wire. requisition for new spare shall be raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gangway davit arm wire which was within one of the sheaves was found corroded and broken.
DirectCause: ineffective monitoring  of the equipment.  the broken stand was not noticed during routine inspection and  that section of wire was not greased well over a period of time.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: entire wire to be inspected, end to end, during next routine inspection and when greasing of wire is being carried out.
Corrective Action: entire wire to be inspected, end to end, during next routine inspection and when greasing of wire is being carried out.
LessionLearnt: It is of utmost importance of carrying out a thorough inspection of entire gangway wires on regular basis as per PMS and also while greasing of wires for any possible wear and tear of wire.
ClosingNote: Kindly ensure entire wire is inspected and greasing is carried out

IncidentDetails: While tending to aft mooring, Breast winch (Drum#11) section of clutch stopper sheared off.
IncidentEventDetails: 12-May/1930 LT : Vessel was berthed to Geelong Refinery#4. 4 +2+2 mooring was deployed fore and aft. Crew tending mooring as and when required.  14-May/2200 LT : Crew member engaged the clutch to Drum#11, but instead of taking slight weight by the clucth (Flushing the clutch with stopper), the brake of Drum#11 was opened which caused a sudden jerk. It was noticed that one side of the clutch stopper sheared off.
ImmediateAction: 1] Drum#11 mooring tightened using the other side of the clutch stopper. Vessel casted of berth on 15-May/1036 LT safely using the other side stopper of Drum#11.  2] Emergency meeting held with all deck officers and crew and explained again the correct method of engaging the clutch and importance of proper tending of mooring wires.  3] Informed managers about the incident and repair plan on the clutch stopper.  4] 20-May/1745 LT : Clutch stopper repaired using hotwork.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Aft breast winch (Drum#11) one side clutch stopper sheared off.
DirectCause: 1] Improper Handling by crew members.  2] Inadequate supervision  3] Rushing into job
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1] Clutch stopper repaired by hotwork, angle bar welded to give additional strength.  2] Training of crew member's for correct procedures while tending mooring in port.  3] Proper supervision required so that crew is not rushing into any job.
Corrective Action: All ship staff involved in the mooring operations to be provided training with the correct mooring procedures,including but not limited to the correct gear engaging procedures & what not to do during mooring operations.
LessionLearnt: Crew members to be effectively trained in handling the mooring equipment.Trainees who have not spend sufficient time at sea are not to handle the deck machineries. Proper planning prior executing the job will eliminate the man-machinery interface error.Proper supervision & guidance from the senior officers & senior crew members will help in understanding the situation in a better way Proper mooring equipment operating procedures will prevent any such future happenings.
ClosingNote: Incident report closed - 27-May-15

IncidentDetails: 4 nos mooring ropes chafed(more than 25%) and 1 no. parted due to surging of the vessel at Pipavav on 19th May 15.  2 head lines , 2 stern lines chafed and 1 aft spring line parted.
IncidentEventDetails: During taking rounds on deck and checking moorings fwd and aft found that ropes had started getting chaffed due to increrase in vessel's surging while alonbgside at pipavav container terminal.One of the aft spring line parted, no one got injured, crew using proper ppe and using safe zones while standing on deck.High water was at 1630hrs and it was spring tide, and due to monsoon effect there was strong wind also prevailing.
ImmediateAction: The parted mooring rope was removed and another rope was immediately passed to the jetty bollard and made fast.Remaining all ropes which were chaffed were slacked one by one and took turns on the mooring roller so as to avoid contact of the chaffed area to the ship structure.Also passed extra rope fwd and aft and made fast.Grease was applied at the chaffing area.Very frequent  monitoring of the ropes carried out.
DirectCause: Heavy surging of vessel caused due to high spring tide and strong winds due to SW Monsoon at berth while vessel was alongside at Pipavav.Also vessel was very light , drafts of approximately 9.5 to 10 mtrs which caused the steep angle of the mooring ropes and caused chaffing.  Also the port is open without any breakwater and hence strong tidal currents effect the vessel and also swell effects the vessel while she is alongside at the berth.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: vessel had only 4 mooring fenders made using old mooring ropes which were not sufficient.Same are being prepared in more numbers so as to tackle such problems in future.  Also more extra ropes will be made fast if vessel expects any surging while alongside.Also turns will be taken on mooring rollers so as to avoid mooring rope contact with the ship side structure.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Efficient Monitoring of moorings, particularly in ports exposed to swell
ReviewComments: Effective mooring watches to be maintained , based on the requirement. Vessel must weigh the option of extra mooring lines. Vessel must not hesitate to call for additional Tugs ( after informing office/owners)
ClosingNote: In event of Mooring rope parting/chafing. vessel must make note of protest. Immediately inform Managers/Owners/Charterer's office regarding the danger to vessel.

IncidentDetails: Port side pilot ladder damaged due to impact of service boat in  rough weather.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel during her port stay in Esmeraldas, pilot ladder was rigged for transfer of personnels from service boat to ship. At 0112 hrs on the 14th while service boat was alongside for ship chandler to board the vessel, the boat happened to bang on the pilot ladder(port side) due to rough weather. The impact of the boat on the pilot ladder caused the 3rd rubber step to break. few chokes found missing as a result of the impact. (Attached photos)
ImmediateAction: Pilot ladder taken out of service.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Port side pilot ladder damaged due to impact of service boat in  rough weather. 3rd rubber step from below found broken and few chokes found missing.
DirectCause: Insufficient fendering  at the service boat and poor manoeuvering of the boat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition rasied for new pilot ladder.Meantime defective ladder to be repaired / new ladder to be made onboard and put in use till vessel receives new pilot ladder.
Corrective Action: Requisition rasied for new pilot ladder.Meantime defective ladder to be repaired / new ladder to be made onboard and put in use till vessel receives new pilot ladder.
LessionLearnt: Attendance is necessary at the site during such operations with continuous communication between the Bridge & the Craft.
ClosingNote: Care should be taken when other crafts approach vessel. Leeward side to be identified & adequate lee should be provided. The ladder is to inspected each time before & after use.

IncidentDetails: V/L was alongside at Cape Town berth, experiencing heavy surging. All mooring ropes were tight and tried to stop surging. Ship movement was still there and cargo was stopped due to heavy surging. V/L aft spring parted first and later fwd spring parted.
IncidentEventDetails: V/L was alongside at Cape Town at 1448. After finishing with station found v/l is surging.Crew were sent to tighten the ropes again. Cargo operation commence  . At 2030lt Port official boarded and informed chief officer that cargo operation will stop due to bad weather which leading to surging of vessel. Chief officer confirms that there is no stoppage from vessel side as lines are all tight. At 1030 3rd officer reported aft 1 spring parted followed by fwd spring 1 line parted too.
ImmediateAction: Crew were called and instucted to pass the line again so as to keep V/L steady.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Ropes are damage due to chafing. needs to change the rope with new rope
DirectCause: Weather was rough which causes v/l to surge
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Winter time( May - Aug), South African ports experienced bad weather. V/L has to pass as many lines possible to stop the surging and keep a regular and thorough checkes on the mooring lines.
LessionLearnt: Effective Mooring system, with frequently tending to Mooring lines depending on Prevailing Weather conditions
ReviewComments: Port Weather conditions monitored
ClosingNote: Noted extra Mooring Lines have been supplied

IncidentDetails: V/L was alongside at Port Elizabeth berth, experiencing heavy surging. All mooring ropes were tight and tried to stop surging. Ship movement was still there and cargo was stopped due to heavy surging. V/L aft spring parted first and later fwd spring and head line parted.
IncidentEventDetails: V/L was alongside at Port Elizabeth.V/L was heavily surging due to heavy weather.Cargo operation were stopped.Agent boarded the vessel and assess the situation.Crew were sent to tighten the ropes. Due to heavy surging ropes get parted.
ImmediateAction: Crew were called and instucted to pass the line again so as to keep V/L steady.
DirectCause: Weather was rough which caused vessel to surge
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Winter time( May - Aug), South African ports experienced bad weather. V/L has to pass as many lines possible to stop the surging and keep a regular and thorough checkes on the mooring lines.
LessionLearnt: Effective Monitoring, and frequently tending to Mooring Lines depending on prevailing Weather conditions
ReviewComments: Port Weather being monitored
ClosingNote: Noted Mooring ropes received

IncidentDetails: Hydraulic oil spray on poop deck from hydraulic inlet line to mooring winch due to small pin hole.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was receiving bunkers at Western Anchorage Bravo Singapore. On poop deck, crew was securing ropes on winches in preparation for sea, the duty AB  observed the small leakage in form of spray coming from the hydraulic inlet line of mooring winch on stbd side just above FO vents. Duty officer was informed.  Framo power pack system stopped immediately, and Ch mate was instructed to inspect the line and area for oil spill, It was observed that the leaking oil from hydraulic line was dropping directly in to the fuel oil vent save all drip tray. Save all drip tray was plugged and oil accumulated in the tray. Leaked oil was collected into the bucket and transferred to engine room.  Office was informed same time.
ImmediateAction: Framo power pack system stopped, Inlet line to aft mooring winch was blanked as vessel was expecting pilot in one hour time.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was receiving bunkers at Western Anchorage Bravo Singapore. On poop  deck, crew was securing ropes on winches in preparation for sea, the duty AB  observed the small leakage in form of spray coming from the hydraulic inlet line of  mooring winch on stbd side just above FO vents. Duty officer was informed. Framo  power pack system stopped immediately, and Ch mate was instructed to inspect the  line and area for oil spill, It was observed that the leaking oil from hydraulic line was  dropping directly in to the fuel oil vent save all drip tray. Save all drip tray was plugged  and oil accumulated in the tray. Leaked oil was collected into the bucket and  transferred to engine room.
DirectCause: Wear & Tear due to age of the pipeline & difficult in accessing for maintenance & inspection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thorough inspection during PMS & replace any pipeline with thick rust scale instead of trying to derust & coat.
Corrective Action: Pipeline permanently repaired on 05-Nov-2015.
ClosingNote: Tested & found satisfactory after permanent repairs.

IncidentDetails: At 1300 it was noticed that A/E 1 L.O. sump was full. On investigation found that No.2 G/E L.O. Storage tank was empty and the oil had gone into A/E #1 and overflowed to G/E L.O. Overflow tank contaminating the fresh oil with old oil in G/E L.O. overflow tank
ImmediateAction: It was noticed that the outlet valve of No.2 G/E L.O. Storgae tank did not close completely as a piece of seizing wire was stuck in between the spindle and wheel. The wire removed and oil from G/E L.O. Overflow tank tested for water and drop test carried out. All the oil transferred back to No.2 G/E L.O. Storage tank and purifictaion started.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 6900 litres of fresh AECC oil contaminated with 2700 litres Old AECC oil
DirectCause: Seizing wires fixed on valve  Stand by A/E sump level not monitored.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Seizing wire fixed on all valves in engine room to be removed. As an alternate notices can be put on important valves (To Be kept in open Position). If valve malfunction is suspected same to be overhauled and repaired instead of lashing with seizing wires.   Stand by A/E sump level and all Storage tank levels to be montiored and logged in Engine room tank sounding book and on sounding board in ECR every watch.
Corrective Action: Seizing wire fixed on all valves in engine room to be removed. As an alternate notices can be put on important valves (To Be kept in open Position). If valve malfunction is suspected same to be overhauled and repaired instead of lashing with seizing wires.  Stand by A/E sump level and all Storage tank levels to be montiored and logged in Engine room tank sounding book and on sounding board in ECR every watch.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper monitoring and proper watch keeping.
ReviewComments: The above incident highlights the importance of proper procedures in Engine Room and the peril of not complying with the same. It also highlights the need for a proper engine room round and identification by senior staff on potential dangerous work practices.
ClosingNote: The incident is reviewed and information disseminated to all fleet vessel's.

IncidentDetails: during discharging at vizag fwd mooring ropes parted due to bad weather.(total 4 nos.)
IncidentEventDetails: As vizag discharge terminal is sheltered for Sw monsoon but during this discharge there was immense low pressure in the area turned in to NW swell which was directly coming from sea and hiiting the vessel bow directly  causing the bow to pound.lot of weight was coming on the rope as the wind from nw hitting the mountain behind and pushing the vessel fwd.  to keep the vessel in posn rope could not be slacked in fwd . pitching motion and lot of weight caused ropes to chaff and part eventhough sleeves were in place..
ImmediateAction: once observed it can not be avoided vessel has put more ropes in fwd to keep her in position.final confrigation fwd was 4-5-3 and aft 3-3-2
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 4 ropes parted on board the vessel.
DirectCause: Apart from the weather factor,the rollers on deck were heavily jammed and badly corroded which could have added to the excessive chafing of the ropes.  Addtitionally the mooring ropes,which parted, were in poor condtion as handed over by the previous management.  The rope sheathing was not properly positioned thereby aggravating the situation.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Rollers to be freed and greased adequately.  Also crew advised to continouslylubricate the point of contact of the rope with the rollers body.
LessionLearnt: Weather to be monitored very closely and mooring ropes to be tended to accordingly in port.  Mooring ropes to be renewed after inspection of ropes. Spares to be ordered and minimum number of spares to be maintained.
ReviewComments: Please monitor weather condition regularly in port and take action including increasing of number of mooring ropes for safe mooring alongside the terminal.
ClosingNote: Report closed.

IncidentDetails: Aft spring mooring winch line leakage
IncidentEventDetails: During berthing at vizag aft mooring winch oil found leaky from pressure pipe due to material failure
ImmediateAction: Leaky pipes along with connectors renewed and line pressure tested with hydraulic pump running found no leakage after renewal of pipe lines
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to rupture of hydraulic line in the aft mooring system,Oil spill occured which was well contained on board and immdietly cleaned.
DirectCause: Line leakage took place because of ageing and material failure of pipe line.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Condition analysis of the pipes to be carried along with ultra sonic gauging to ascertain the condition of the pipelines.All defective pipelines in the hydraulic system to be checked for condition and any defective one to be renewed with the spare pipes on board.
ReviewComments: Pipeline section is renewed and winch operation found satisfactory. Appreciate timely action by ship staff.
ClosingNote: Report closed.

IncidentDetails: While approaching to EPA anchorage Pilot instructed to let go port anchor as the anchorage area was too congested, self requested for walk back the anchor but pilot insisted to let go anchor.As the charted depth was 52 meter we requested pilot to walk back to 2 shackle and then let go anchor, pilot agreed upon it.  Brake was opened and shackle were paid out,at 4th shackle brake was holding, then pilot instructed to pay out till 7 shackle in water, again the brake was released but after that brake did not hold at all until 12 shackle in water.  After that gear was engaged to bring 7 shackle in water.  Lot of smoke was observed from the brake band,further investigation revealed that brake screw is bend and suspect the brake nut threads are damaged due to which the brake was not tightened properly.
IncidentEventDetails: While approaching to EPA anchorage Pilot instructed to let go port anchor as the anchorage area was too congested, self requested for walk back the anchor but pilot insisted to let go anchor.As the charted depth was 52 meter we requested pilot to walk back to 2 shackle and then let go anchor, pilot agreed upon it.  Brake was opened and shackle were paid out,at 4th shackle brake was holding, then pilot instructed to pay out till 7 shackle in water, again the brake was released but after that brake did not hold at all until 12 shackle in water.  After that gear was engaged to bring 7 shackle in water.  Lot of smoke was observed from the brake band,further investigation revealed that brake screw is bend and suspect the brake nut threads are damaged due to which the brake was not tightened properly.
ImmediateAction: Tried to apply the brake, then engaged the gear and heaved up anchor to 7 shackle in water.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Brake lining of port windlass totally worn out,same will be replaced by the spare brake liner.
DirectCause: Brake liner was worn out due to which the brake was not holding.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper monitoring of brake liner and the record should be logged in the maintenance log book
LessionLearnt: Brake liners to be inspected at regular frequent intervals.Maintenance of brake bands & the renewal of brake pads to be carried out in Dry dock. Speed of the vessel during the time of anchoring was checked & found to be less than 1.0 kts,At Singapore its a usual practice to let go the anchor due to heavy traffic congestion & limited turning radius.Due to heavy reclamation in areas around Singapore,the tide/current flow is totally unpredictable.On arrival to anchorage,it was noticed that all vessels were heading in different directions,making it very difficult to predict the tidal / current direction.
ReviewComments: Brake bands were renewed on departure from Singapore & tried out found holding Satisfcatory
ClosingNote: Incident report closed - 27th July-2015

IncidentDetails: Vessel dragged her port anchor in designated anchorage"C" due to heavy weather. She crossed over the electrical submarine cable whilst dragging.
IncidentEventDetails: 02/2342 : Arrival Barranquilla  03/0012 : vessel dropped port anchore (11 05.4 N /074 57.40 w)  03/0024 : V/L BUT port anchore  03/0055 : Observed Anchor dragging /Informed Master and E/R  03/0106 : Commenced Heaving up port anchor   03/0108 : M/E and BT started   03/0130 :Anchor away
ImmediateAction: Anchor was, with little difficulty due to strong wind and heavy swell, picked up safely using engine and bow thruster, with no sign of any kind of debris over anhor flukes. It was clean and clear of obstruction.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage occurred.
DirectCause: Heavy weather caused anchor dragged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Anchoring in BAQ anchorage to be completely avoided in heavy weather as nature of bottom is sand that provide no holding support to vessel.
Corrective Action: Anchoring in BAQ anchorage to be completely avoided in heavy weather as nature of bottom is sand that provide no holding support to vessel.
LessionLearnt: Importance of understanding limitation of equipment and effect of weather conditions on equipment
ReviewComments: the above incident highlights the need of proper passage planning and decision making
ClosingNote: The above incident is in actual an near miss. the vessel started dragging anchor. anchor was picked up and vessel proceeded to drift.

IncidentDetails: Water ingress into AE2 cylinder liners of units 4 and 5
IncidentEventDetails: -  AE2 maintenance in progress, tappet clearance was checked and adjusted as required.   -  Then the engine was blown through with air and noticed water splashing out from unit 4 indicator cock.   - All the crank case doors were opened and found water droplets from unit 4 and 5 cylinder liners.   - Jacket Cooling Water line was isolated and drained from the Engine; water droplets were seen dripping continuously inside the crank case even after an hour.  - Suspecting LT cooler water leakage through air cooler pipes, LT cooling water return line valve was isolated; simultaneously air cooler drain pipe was opened up and about 30L of water was collected through the drain line.  - Air Cooler drain line ‘Pot’ as opened up, found line was choked, ball cock taken out and noticed small orifice was not clear.  - Scavenge Manifold forward and aft drain pipe opened up; found pipe union completely clogged.  - All pipelines and drain valves were opened, blown through with air and cleared.  - Turbocharger drain pipe was opened up and checked, found clear.  - Thence all drain pipes were left open including the Turbocharger drain and engine was blown through with air for 4 times and confirmed that there was no water in the unit.  - AE2 was then tried out on idle for 5 minutes, checked and noted all parameters and found within limits.  - Sump oil for AE2 is presently under purification.  - Checked the drain line pipes for AE 1 and 3; found clear.
ImmediateAction: Office informed and investigation carried out
ClosingNote: The orifice was not cleaned since delivery of the vessel and same has been included in the PMS.

IncidentDetails: While coming along side OTB No4 ( stbd alongside) pressed the fender No 143 which resulted in cracking of the fender.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Pilot boarded vess to berth at OTB 4, Fujairah at 1524LT on 03.07.2015  2.Vessel entered basin at 1615LT on 03.07.2015  with  Tug Wadi Al Fai made fast on port shoulder and Tug  Wadi Saham made fast on accommodation  ford on port side.  3. At 1630LT on 03.07.2015 ,the first line was passed to shored and vessel came alongside parallel to jetty.  4.At 1636Lt on 03.07.2015  the parallel body of the vessel was pushed in hard by ford tug causing excessive compression on the fender No 143 .  5.  A crack was observed on the fender with noise .
ImmediateAction: Asked tug to stopped pushing . Informed pilot about the incident.  Made fast vessel to the jetty.
Corrective Action: All vessels in the fleet will be alerted of this event and the lessons learnt will be  shared.
LessionLearnt: Visual check to be done by vessel when approaching the shore fenders when in sight.Any deterioration and damage to be brought to the attention of the master and pilot prior the vessel makes contact with the fenders and log entries made.
ClosingNote: PI club was on board and has collected all information,documents and samples from ship and shore.Incident is closed on 16/09/15

IncidentDetails: At 2000h LT while hot work was in progress in no.4 FO tank, the repair team reported to bridge that the no.4 FOT compt. was full of smoke and that they had evacuated to the main deck.  The fire alarm was sounded and ships crew rushed to the scene to find heavy smoke billowing through the open booby hatch of pump room leading in to no.3 & 4 FOT.  They found the repair team members on the no.3 hatch cover with one man lying prone.   Immediately a 2 man team wearing fireman outfit and scba entered the pump room and a check of no.4 tank revealed heavy smoke but no trace of fire.    On questioning the repair team it emerged that whilst gouging one of the air vent stub piece, after removing and placing aside the vent pipe, molten metal made contact with the vent pipe (GRP material ) which immediately started burning and in a matter of seconds the entire compt. was filled with smoke. Even the pump room was full of dense smoke and zero visibility . All the personnels managed to evacuate with great difficulty.    If not for the presence and quick thinking of the supervisor the three repair team members would not have been able to make a safe exit from the compt.    After about an hour to check if any fire was present, a 2 man team wearing fireman outfit and scba again entered the pump room . The dense smoke was still there but no fire. The smoke had a strong smell of burning plastic. The team exited the location and reentered again after about half an hour elapsed when smoke density is reduced , with charged fire hose and sprayed the tank top with water. The two man team returned safely and the extraction of smoke is continuing using portable blowers and the pump room mechanical blower.     The area is now clear of smoke as of 2200LT. continuous monitoring in progress.     During the evacuation process, one member of the repair team sustained minor injury after slipping and falling in the pump room. First aid given immediately and at present the personnel is fine without showing any signs of uneasiness. There were no other injuries to any other repair team member, though they were all suffering from smoke inhalation. First aid was  administered and all personnel are fine    All shipstaff are safe and accounted for.
IncidentEventDetails: At 2000h LT while hot work was in progress in no.4 FO tank, the repair team reported to bridge that the no.4 FOT compt. was full of smoke and that they had evacuated to the main deck.  The fire alarm was sounded and ships crew rushed to the scene to find heavy smoke billowing through the open booby hatch of pump room leading in to no.3 & 4 FOT.  They found the repair team members on the no.3 hatch cover with one man lying prone.   Immediately a 2 man team wearing fireman outfit and scba entered the pump room and a check of no.4 tank revealed heavy smoke but no trace of fire.    On questioning the repair team it emerged that whilst gouging one of the air vent stub piece, after removing and placing aside the vent pipe, molten metal made contact with the vent pipe (GRP material ) which immediately started burning and in a matter of seconds the entire compt. was filled with smoke. Even the pump room was full of dense smoke and zero visibility . All the personnels managed to evacuate with great difficulty.    If not for the presence and quick thinking of the supervisor the three repair team members would not have been able to make a safe exit from the compt.    After about an hour to check if any fire was present, a 2 man team wearing fireman outfit and scba again entered the pump room . The dense smoke was still there but no fire. The smoke had a strong smell of burning plastic. The team exited the location and reentered again after about half an hour elapsed when smoke density is reduced , with charged fire hose and sprayed the tank top with water. The two man team returned safely and the extraction of smoke is continuing using portable blowers and the pump room mechanical blower.     The area is now clear of smoke as of 2200LT. continuous monitoring in progress.     During the evacuation process, one member of the repair team sustained minor injury after slipping and falling in the pump room. First aid given immediately and at present the personnel is fine without showing any signs of uneasiness. There were no other injuries to any other repair team member, though they were all suffering from smoke inhalation. First aid was  administered and all personnel are fine    All shipstaff are safe and accounted for.
ImmediateAction: First aid given to injured repair team member , all repair team members who got exposed to smoke inhalation were administered firat aid, parameters were checked and all found to be normal. Boundary cooling done of GRP pipe which caught fire,
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO MAJOR INJURY OCCURED DURING THIS INCIDENT. HOWEVER, THE WORKING ENVOIRNMENT  WAS SUCH THAT A SERIOUS INJURY WAS POSSIBLE IF THE REACTION OF THE AFFECTED PERSONNEL WAS SLOW.   THE MATERIAL DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO FIRE DAMAGE OF THE "GRP" VENT PIPE WHICH WAS THE CAUSE OF THE SMOKE DUE TO ITS CONTACT WITH HOT METAL DURING THE GOUGING OPERATION
DirectCause: GOUGING OF THE AIR VENT FLANGES IN NO.4 FOT WAS UNDERTAKEN WITHOUT REMOVING THE AIR VENT PIPES ITSELF FROM THE VICINITY OF THE GOUGING OPERATIONS. THIS WAS NOT DONE AS THE   VENT PIPES WOULD HAVE TO BE CUT IN ORDER TO REMOVE AND SINCE THESE SAME PIPES WERE MEANT TO BE REUSED THIS WAS NOT DONE.   THE PERSONNEL CARRYING OUT THE GOUGING WERE UNAWARE OF THE CONSEQUENCES OF CONTACT OF THE PIPING MATERIAL WITH A HOT SOURCE AND THE RESULTANT SMOKE GENERATION UPON SUCH CONTACT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: SEEING THAT THE PRESENT ACCESS AND EVACUATION ARRANGEMENT IS INSUFFICIENT IN CASE OF EMERGENCY, IT IS PROPOSED THAT AN ACCESS BE PROVIDED AT THE WORKING LEVEL BY CUTTING AN   OPENING IN THE FO TANK BULKHEAD FORE AND AFT LEADING INTO NO.2 AND NO. 3 CARGO HOLDS. THIS WILL ENSURE SAFE AND QUICK EVACUATION AND ALSO PROVIDE FOR SUFFICIENT LIGHT AND   VENTILATION TO THE PERSONS EMPLOYED IN THE FO TANKS.   AWAITING APPROVAL FOR SAME FROM OFFICE.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper hazard identification.  Importance of alternate methods of communication / escape / lighting
ReviewComments: Agree with Masters cause Analysis and corrective action.
ClosingNote: Work has been stopped and will commence after Tech. Suptd from office visit's vessel and re assesses the risk

IncidentDetails: Vessel was Stbd side alongside at Port San Antonio, Chile. Vessel was experiencing heavy weather of Nw'ly wind, speed of 15 kts and swell of 2.0-2.5m. Vessel's final mooring arrangements were 4+3(F&A). At 1800LT, Vessel was experiencing heavy swell from the Stbd quarter.The Port was closed for any movement due to inclement weather.Despite regular checks on the moorings(F&A), at1800Lt, the top layer of  one  spring line aft gave away.At 2210LT, vessel completed cargo operations,at the same time due to a huge swell heavy surging took place and the other two aft spring lines  gave way .
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately informed agent and Port authorities . As vessel was ready for sailing the tugs and Pilots arrived and vessel was hauled out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two mooring ropes parted and One mooring rope damaged.
DirectCause: Due to heavy surging.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The berth was open to sea.
LessionLearnt: Close monitoring and tending to mooring lines at frequent intervals. Weather conditions closely monitored.
ReviewComments: Weather updates were received and monitored through information from Local agents.
ClosingNote: Weather updates to be received and monitored prior arrival port. Adequate mooring lines to be used at ports based on Weather conditions.

IncidentDetails: Motor for Clear View Screen (STBD) was out of order.
IncidentEventDetails: The 3rd had a routine check for clear view screen and found that it was out of order. He informed the Master,CE and EE to inspect the motor again and found defected.The last inspection for the clear view screen was done on 25-May-15 which was found working satisfactory.As per our PMS overhaul of motor will be done every 60 months which was carried out last on 25-Aug-12.
ImmediateAction: Use of clear view was stopped. Inspection was done for the clear view system and the equipments.The motor was replaced with a new one.
DirectCause: The motor of the clear view screen was defected.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Check and Inspection as per PMS.
ClosingNote: Trust clear view motor is rectified and working satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Small water leaking from liner cracked of No.2 Main air compressor
IncidentEventDetails: While 4th Engineer was cleaning sump tank to renew LO and inspected the crankcase found liner cracking at L.P side have small water leaking.
ImmediateAction: The operation of No.2 Main air compressor was stopped,checked and inspected. Overhaul of No.2 main air compressor.
DirectCause: Due to Liner was cracked water was leaking into sump tank.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To check and to inspect the No.2 main air compressor as per PMS.

IncidentDetails: At about 1100LT on 28th Jul. while vessel was underway,  burning smell came inside accomodation and notified E/R and checked accommodation, found all normal, on further round taken outside accomodation and noticed one rope tail was smouldering, then and there alerted the duty officer by radio and simultaniously water was used to put off the smouldering.
ImmediateAction: Alerted the duty officer by radio and simultaniously water was used to put off the smouldering. All adjacent area checked and found no damage. Tail rope renewed and kept ready for mooring.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One rope  tail damaged.
DirectCause: Funnel spark.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All engine duty personnel re-emphasized to inform bridge prior carrying out soot blow. After soot blow, duty personnel take rounds on poop deck.
ReviewComments: To inform Bridge prior carrying out soot blow. Carryout soot blow only at full load,to get maximum scavenge pressure. Duty personal should ensure to take round during and after soot blow. These to be discussed in safety meeting frequently to avoid this kind of incident.
ClosingNote: Pls register these points in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Leakage of Hydraulic oil from winch
IncidentEventDetails: During berthing at Dahej Hydraulic oil leaked from stbd side aft winch
ImmediateAction: Winch stopped and leaked oil contained in container
DirectCause: Lack of Maintenance.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper maintenance and Repaires.
Corrective Action: Proper maintenance and Repaires.
ReviewComments: It is very essential that mooring equipment's are maintained well ,so that they work in critical operations.

Corrective Action: To pass any line to tug or ashore with maximum possible correct lead.

IncidentDetails: Leak of Hydraulic oil from Port and centre aft winch during berthing.

IncidentDetails: Mooring Ropes Parted while vessel was at berth

IncidentDetails: One of the deck crew operated monorail crane to lower luggages for off    signers ,after that operation while securing the monorail crane onboard    before departure monorail crane wire parted and broken wire came out    from the sheves and floating block fell down on accomodation exterior    deck.
IncidentEventDetails: Condition of the wire not checked before operation . Monorail crane wire parted and broken wire came out from the sheves and floating block fell down on accomodation exterior    deck.
ImmediateAction: After sailing ,monorail crane wire renewed with new    spare wire onboard by ships crew.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No apparent damage or injury observed.
DirectCause: Not complying with SMMS
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper maintenance and checking of the condition of mono rail and its parts at regular intervals to avoide reoccurence.  strictly comply SMMS for maintenance of Mono rail
LessionLearnt: Effective implementation of Monitoring Procedures
ClosingNote: To be discussed with Ship Staff on importance of inspecting Wire prior use of Lifting equipments

IncidentDetails: LEAK AT MANIFOLD PRESSURE GAUGE CONNECTION & MANUAL VALVE FLANGE - CARGO STOPPAGE/TIME DELAY & OFF HIRE
IncidentEventDetails: 1444 hrs LT : Manifold & shore watch reported minor vapour leak at Liquid Manifold (Stbd Fwd) Manual valve flange & Pressure gauge union between the Liquid manifold manual & hydraulic valve  1446 hrs LT : Before leak could be arrested by tightening union, pressure gauge gave way and shot up into the air with cargo liquid leak on deck.  1446 hrs LT : ESD activated.  1450 - 1510 hrs LT : Line pressure drained.  1520 hrs LT : Pressure gauge taopping plugged as vessel could manage/continue discharge with   pressure transmitters at the manifold crossover.  1520 - 1530 hrs LT : Manifold manual valve bolts tightened and  teflon plug inserted to arrest the leak and resume discharge.  1536 - 1548 hrs LT : Manifold valve, pressure gauge tapping plug and other flanges leak test done   using ship's compressors upto 10 Barg.  1554 hrs LT : Resumed discharge.
ImmediateAction: ESD Activated, leaks arrested at both locations followed by thorough pressure test before cargo resumed.
DirectCause: Direct cause: Equipment and material failure        Failure to follow PMS/maintenance instructions.        Indirect cause: Improper Motivation and Lack of skill Inadequate maintenance Root cause: Material failure and lack of maintenance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pressure guage assembly and seat ring changed.   Leak test to be done at the manifold prior arrival every discharge port. Inspection of cargo line mountings included in pre arrival checks.
LessionLearnt: Importance of inspection of cargo line along with mountings.
ReviewComments: Procedural failure to carry out inspection of cargo line mountings.
ClosingNote: Corrective actions taken to prevent similar future incident.

IncidentDetails: Caro Sampler tape & assembly got cut & fell in No.1 Stbd COT whilst sampling cargo tanks. Cargo Was Fuel Oil.
IncidentEventDetails: Prior Commencement Of Cargo(Fuel Oil) Discharge Operations At San pedro de Macoris, Dominican Republic , Sampling of  Cargo Tanks was in progress. Cargo Samples were being drawn from No.2 Stbd Cot using onboard hermetic sampler by pumpman. While Winding up the tape after obtaining the bottom sample suddenly the Caro sampler tape & assembly got cut & fell in No.1 Stbd COT.Due To this Cargo Smapling Process was Not possible & Vessel Had to adjust the same somehow with cargo lines manifold samples. V/L Had Only One Working  hermetic Sampler. Hence V/L had raised requisition for the same in ship palm (SPR/EQUIN/D/15/047).
ImmediateAction: Immediately Vessel had to take other measures inorder to draw samples from other cargo tanks to avoid any further delays from vessel's end.Requsition raised for new sampler (Complete Set) in ship palm & technical supndnt (office) informed about the same.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Prior Commencement Of Cargo (HSFO) Discharging operations, whilst Sampling Cargo Tanks Caro sampler tape & assembly got cut & fell in No.1 Stbd COT.Due To this Cargo Smapling Process was Not possible & Vessel Had to adjust the same somehow with cargo lines manifold samples.
DirectCause: Equipment material Failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition Raised for a new sampler in ship palm.To operate the sampler with additional care & to avoid mishandling of the same in the future.
Corrective Action: Requisition Raised for a new sampler in ship palm.To operate the sampler with additional care & to avoid mishandling of the same in the future.
ReviewComments: Spare sampler complete and 2 nos containers ordered.
ClosingNote: Sampler along with the container connected.

IncidentDetails: on 8th aug 2015 , bosun sustained slight injury causing swelling and pain  due to jerk from  pressure hose of hp  washing machine while cleaning the deck prior arrival singapore.
ImmediateAction: Given bed rest along with antiflammatory gel and pain killers.  He was sent to a doctor in singapore on a precautionary basis keeping long sailing in mind.Pls find attached doctors report and rest hours record.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: swelling and mild pain
DirectCause: neglegance and lack of concentration while handling pressure hose.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: atleast two persons should be on rotation to handle HP hose for washing.
LessionLearnt: Awareness of environment/surroundings. Effective Tool Box meetings
ReviewComments: Incident recreation attached for reference.
ClosingNote: Incident Recreation attached

IncidentDetails: Prior departure (with Pilot on board), while clearing the gangway net from the jetty and gangway, a crew member stepped on an uneven surface of the jetty and twisted his ankle.
IncidentEventDetails: Prior departure (with Pilot on board), while clearing the gangway net from the jetty and gangway, a crew member stepped on an uneven surface of the jetty and twisted his ankle. He suffered moderate pain and was able to walk back up the gangway and carry out his duties at teh aft station without much hinderance or pain.
ImmediateAction: Checked that the concerned crew member was ok. Balm applied to the ankle area and crew member verified that the pain was only moderate.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Prior departure (with Pilot on board), while clearing the gangway net from the jetty and gangway, a crew member stepped on an uneven surface of the jetty and twisted his ankle. The pain reported was moderate and he was able to continue with his normal duties like departure stations etc with an application of pain relief balm.
DirectCause: Situational unawareness, resulting in stepping on an uneven surface on the jetty.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Injury discussed with all crew and emphasis made to situational awareness.
LessionLearnt: 1) Awareness of the surrounding, and identifying hazards 2) Pre-planning task 3) Task not carried out in a hurry
ClosingNote: Ship Staff involved in any task should identify Hazards and rectify/isolate as applicable.

IncidentDetails: Damage to gangway
IncidentEventDetails: While disembarking the Loading master, gangway watchman has to adjust the gangway and same time due to uncontrolled surging of the vessel, gangway came in contact with the bollard on the jetty, which caused damage to the ship's gangway.
ImmediateAction: Gangway heaved up to clear the jetty.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel at Porbandar jetty was made fast and same time one tug was pushing continuously to keep the ship alongside, as vessel was surging and swaying heavily. Vessel used ship's gangway which was not kept resting on jetty due to uncontrolled surging of the vessel. So gangway watchman has to adjust the gangway everytime to embark or disembark personnels.    While disembarking the Loading master, gangway watchman has to adjust the gangway and same time due to uncontrolled surging of the vessel, gangway came in contact with the bollard on the jetty, which caused damage to the ship's gangway. Gangway's upper platform turntable got bent.
DirectCause: Unfavourable condition at port causing uncontrolled surging.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Embarkation and disembarkation to be monitored by Officer.
ReviewComments: In such a unfavorable condition embarkation and dis embarkation to be monitored by officer.
ClosingNote: Near miss closed.

IncidentDetails: No. 1 Crane Failure
IncidentEventDetails: Due to some problem with the No.1 Crane it was stopped for a while and started after 10 seconds while discharging cargo in Colombo. And after sometime the crane technician boarded the vessel for checking the crane along with the ship staff. After checking he found that the timer relay No.K71 was not functioning properly. He bypassed the timer relay K71 for temporary use.The cargo operations was resumed after a while and the discharging was performed using the crane.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the operation of the crane. Checked and inspected all sensors,limit switches and PCB. The timer relay K71 was replaced with a new one and found the crane working satisfactory.
DirectCause: Timer relay was damaged not functioning properly due to high temperature.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To install cooling fan to prevent heating of electronic parts  due to temperature rise. Always keep one set of spare on board for replacement in case of any emergency.(i.e. Magnetic contactor, Relays and Timers)

IncidentDetails: DG No.3 Turbo was damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: DG No.3 turbo was surging due to increase of the load faster.DG No.3 was stopped and the fuel rack pump and turning gear was checked. MAN technician boarded the vessel the following day and inspected the DG No.3 and Overhaul DG No.3 unit 4.The overhaul was complete.After checking he found that turbo charger got damaged at nozzle ring and turbine side.
ImmediateAction: Overhaul turbo. Renew nozzle ring.
DirectCause: Nut of piston crown was broken, piston crown crash with exh. The intake of valve puch rod was damaged and have little fragmented from exh.valve broken and into the turbo charger.Bolt of piston crown was damaged.

IncidentDetails: The subject Reefer MSCU 7474749 was loaded in Durban for Nhava Sheva   discharge(As per the manifest),Slot:100582,Commodity :Oranges,Set temp:-0.5 C.  The above mentioned Container was discharged in Nhava Sheva on 01st August 2015.But it was restowed  again in slot 060784,Neither the Chief officer was informed nor reflecting in plan.  In mundra only one reefer was loaded which is not the same one and Salalah no live reefers were loaded.(Which means the subject reefer was restowed in Nhava Sheva with Same POL as Durban and POD changed to Port louis.  On arrival Port louis vessel disconnected two reefers,but actually supposed to be three.  Furthermore checked plans of Mundra and Salalah,the subject reefer was showing POL:Durban and POL:Port louis(changed from Nhava Sheva to port Louis).
IncidentEventDetails: Container No.  MSCU 7474749  Stow Posn : 10 05 82  POL : Durban  POD : Nhava Sheva  Commodity : Oranges  Set Temp : -0.5C (PPECB)    On 12th night at Port Louis when vessel received cargo plan it was noted that there was a descrepancy of one container to be discharged. On further checking it was found that the above mentioned container was in question.    The container was discharged at Nhava Sheva as per the cargo plan there. The container did not appear on the final departure plan. At Mudra the container appeared in the cargo plan showing POL-Durban and POD-Port Louis. The container was not showing in the reefer loading manifests or restow list at either ports.  Apparently the unit was reloaded at Nhava Sheva without knowledge of the vessel.    Also being a PPECB unit the last temperature was sent to Medlog on 31st July 2015. There was no request from medlog that the unit was on board and to report temperatures.    Kindly note at Nhava Sheva there was a lot of confusion with cargo operations and numerous frequent shifting of units and finally a lot of units were shut out so theat the vessel could sail to accomodate another MSC vessel.    At Mundra only one reefer was loaded and at Salalah no live reefers were loaded.    On arrival Port louis vessel disconnected two reefers, but according to the cargo plan there were supposed to be three, which was when we discovered this descrepancy.    the unit was discharged at Port louis and when plugged into shore power was showing a supply temp. of +17.0C and return temperature of +13.1C.
ImmediateAction: Checked plans of Mundra and Salalah,Office informed by phone and than later with all details
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Container No.  MSCU 7474749  Stow Posn : 10 05 82  POL : Durban  POD : Nhava Sheva  Commodity : Oranges  Set Temp : -0.5C (PPECB)    On 12th night at Port Louis when vessel received cargo plan it was noted that there was a descrepancy of one container to be discharged. On further checking it was found that the above mentioned container was in question.    The container was discharged at Nhava Sheva as per the cargo plan there. The container did not appear on the final departure plan. At Mudra the container appeared in the cargo plan showing POL-Durban and POD-Port Louis. The container was not showing in the reefer loading manifests or restow list at either ports.  Apparently the unit was reloaded at Nhava Sheva without knowledge of the vessel.    Also being a PPECB unit the last temperature was sent to Medlog on 31st July 2015. There was no request from medlog that the unit was on board and to report temperatures.    Kindly note at Nhava Sheva there was a lot of confusion with cargo operations and numerous frequent shifting of units and finally a lot of units were shut out so theat the vessel could sail to accomodate another MSC vessel.    At Mundra only one reefer was loaded and at Salalah no live reefers were loaded.    On arrival Port louis vessel disconnected two reefers, but according to the cargo plan there were supposed to be three, which was when we discovered this descrepancy.    the unit was discharged at Port louis and when plugged into shore power was showing a supply temp. of +17.0C and return temperature of +13.1C.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Total numbers of Reefers on board to be compared and verified with the final plan so that if any discrepancy is there,Same to be checked.
Corrective Action: Total numbers of Reefers on board to be compared and verified with the final plan so that if any discrepancy is there,Same to be checked.
LessionLearnt: Effective Verification process. Reefer Manifest was provided to Vessel Till Navasheva, after which no information received from Terminal/Planner regarding loading of this container, or No Reefer Manifest provided to Ship.
ReviewComments: Appreciate shipstaff reporting regarding this reefer
ClosingNote: No Claims received from Chaterers on this Reefer container. Probably due to the non-availability of the Reefer Manifest from Navasheva to Port Lois.

IncidentDetails: Incident: At 1530H 03 August 2015 while vessel navigating at Java Sea going to   New Zealand, an explosion was heard at bridge portside and a simultaneously fire alarm number 1003 Battery Room. The Third Officer was doing a testing of immersion suit near  battery room.
IncidentEventDetails: As per details of incident on the left.
ImmediateAction: Action Taken: Upon hearing the explosion, the Third Officer immediately closed the ventilation covers and took a portable dry chemical extinguisher when he saw smoke coming out from the battery room. At the same time, the duty officer triggered an emergency alarm and alert all crew via public address for possible fire onboard and assemble to combat electrical fire in the battery room.    Inspection and causes: When it was ascertain that no fire was developed in the room, the watertight door and ventilation covers were open to clear the smoke inside the room. Upon inspection, two batteries for GMDSS are damaged by the explosion. The other four batteries for general use are still intact.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: INJURY NONE  Damage GMDSS battery only(2pcs) exipired already
DirectCause: Due to expired battery
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Supply the requested battery.
Corrective Action: Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels to avoid recurrence in future.
LessionLearnt: To replace batteries  well ahead of its expiry date. In this case, logistical issues were the reason behind non-supply of the batteries. In such a scenario, Company should ensure that the batteries to be repalced well ahead of time instead of waiting till the fag end  of the window period.
ReviewComments: Immediate action, cause analysis and corrective action reported is found to be in order.
ClosingNote: Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels highlighting the incident and preventive measures to be taken to avoid recurrence in the future.
