IncidentDetails: AS THERE WAS LOW TIDE IN PORT, VSL GANGWAY COULD NOT BE USED AS THE LEVEL OF JETTY WAS HIGHER THAN THE LEVEL OF GANGWAY. MOT LADDER WAS SHIFTED FROM STBD SIDE TO PORT SIDE AND RIGGED. WHILE CARGO OPERATION, DUE TO SURGING THE MOT LADDER HIT THE GANTRY TRACK AND SUFFERED A CRACK OF ABOUT 2MM AT THE POINT WHERE LADDER WAS SECURED TO SHIP.
ImmediateAction: THE MOT LADDER WAS PULLED IN AND SECURED EVERY TIME AFTER THE EMBARKATION / DISEMBARKATION OF STEVEDORES COMPLETE. THE SHIPS GANGWAY WAS PLACED ON JETTY AND IN USE AFTER ABOUT 45 MINS DURING HIGH WATER.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CRACK AT THE SECURING POINT OF MOT LADDER. 2MM WIDTH AND LENGTH ABOUT 5 CM.
DirectCause: DUE TO THE HEAVY SURGING OF VSL SURING THE CARGO OPS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MOT LADDER TO BE USED WITH PRECISE CARE. LADDER TO BE PULLED IN IF THERE IS NO PERSON EMBARKING/DISEMBARKING.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Continuous Monitoring and planning for expected conditions
ReviewComments: Vessel to carry out training for watch keepers in gangway watch standing and precautions.
ClosingNote: Agree with the corrective action.  Information promulgated to all vessels. Incident report closed out.

IncidentDetails: 04-Sep 2015  1648 LT : Vessel fwd brest line tail rope parted due to rough weather while the vessel is moored along side berth (VOPAK Terminal Berth # 5)
IncidentEventDetails: 04-Sep-2015  0700 LT : Vessel informed terminal that vessel experiencing continous rolling due to high swell of about 1.5m at berth  0742 LT : Loading master on board  0745 LT - 0755 LT : Safety discussion held between ship & shore regarding wind and swell criteria for  stopping cargo and cast off. But as per the advise recieved from loading master, the ship is holding its position alongside with not too much of impact to the fenders and the manifold chicksons are operating within working envelope and hence it is decided to continue with the operation without any stoppage  1310 LT : Vessel called terminal and informed swell is increasing, requested loading master to assess the situation and if requires to stop cargo / Laoding master informed we can still continue with the loading.  1330 LT : Vessel again called terminal and requested loading master to have a visit onboard to asccess the situation.Terminal informed that they are monitoring from shore side and things are still ok to continue with the operation.  1345 LT : Vessel again called terminal and informed that situation is no more safe to continue with loading operation untill weather improves / Loading master informed that he will come on board to asess the situation.  1400 LT : Loading master came on board, assess the situation and advised that its still safe to continue with the operation.and they are continously monitoring from their end and cargo shall be stopped if they feel unsafe from their side.  1448 LT : A/B - reported that fwd brest line parted  1448 LT : Same informed to loading master who was onboard.  1450 LT : Cargo suspended from shore due to damged mooring  1452 LT : reported to agent  1500 LT : reported to all parties concerned  1510 LT : Contacted fujairah port control to arrnge mooring gang  1545 LT : Shore mooring gang arrived  1548 LT : Fwd brest line cast off  1615 LT : Fwd brest line tail rope renewed  1620 LT : Fwd brest line made fast  1635 LT : All moorings as per our original arrnagement (3+2+2 - Fwd and aft)   1640 LT : Same informed to terminal  / Resumed cargo.
ImmediateAction: 1) Informed terminal / Stopped cargo  2) Stations Called  3) Vessel ensured there is not too much weight on the single brest line which is intact fwd.  4) Port control informed / Shore mooring gang came to cast of the parted brest rope.  5) Tail rope renewed  6) informed terminal and resumed cargo
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One tail rope parted. Same to be replaced.
DirectCause: Loading had to be stopped by terminal in any case of mooring damage.  As the mooring tail parted, same was infomed & shore stopped loading. Loading was resumed as soon the mooring was placed back in place.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Need to have spare tails on board for replacement.
Corrective Action: Need to have spare tails on board for replacement.
ClosingNote: Ref. captioned Damage report, we have carried out investigation about this incident (mooring rope parted during loading). We appreciate the crew members for their swift response in passing the lines immediately.  After review, it was confirmed that the ropes were properly tended to and it was not slack. The last brake rendering test was carried out and marking on the brake was correct.The rope which parted was on use for since   2011 was last visually checked in the month of August 2014 - found in good condition.  Agent was contacted to obtain more information about the port to understand.  Based on the analysis, please find office comments:  Cause ===== 1. Due to sudden jerk caused by the waves , ropes parted prior winch brake could release the ropes.  3. Location of berth - With available information, this possibility could not be ruled out.    Corrective action ============== 1. An alert shall be sent by office to all vessels calling this terminal explaining this incident and asking them to be more cautious when vessels are berthing in this vessel PIC:  Satheesh Target Date: 30/09/2015

IncidentDetails: 1] VESSEL WAS CARRYING OUT TANK CLEANING OPERATION FOR CHANGE OF GRADE FROM CRUDE TO FUEL OIL   2] CARGO TANKS WERE WASHED WITH HOT SEA WATER AND FOLLOWED UPWITH FW RINSING.   3] FW WAS TAKEN IN APK TANK AND IT WAS PLANNED TO CONNECT TO THE COW LINE USING   FIRE HOSE AFTER COMPLETION OF SW WASHING.   4] DURING THE TANK CLEANING OPERATION, TO SAVE TIME IT WAS DECIDED THAT FIRE HOSE    TO BE  CONNECTED TO COW LINE.   5] THERE WAS ONLY BLANK AND NO VALVE WAS AVAILABLE. STILL VESSEL WENT AHEAD AND   OPENED BLANK, CONNECTED THE FIRE HOSE .   6] THE COW BRANCH LINE IS COMMON FOR SLOP(P) & SLOP(S) WITH ONE COMMON ROOT V/V   7] AS SOON SLOP(S) WASHING STARTED(ABOUT 4-5 KG) PRESSURE, THE FIRE HOSE CAME   OUT OF THE COUPLING WHICH WAS CONNECTED TO THE COW LINE.   8] AS A RESULT THE OILY WATER 30-40 LTRS IN THE COW LINE OF SLOP(P) SPILLED ON DECK    FROM THE COUPLING
IncidentEventDetails: 1] VESSEL WAS CARRYING OUT TANK CLEANING OPERATION FOR CHANGE OF GRADE FROM CRUDE TO FUEL OIL   2] CARGO TANKS WERE WASHED WITH HOT SEA WATER AND FOLLOWED UPWITH FW RINSING.   3] FW WAS TAKEN IN APK TANK AND IT WAS PLANNED TO CONNECT TO THE COW LINE USING   FIRE HOSE AFTER COMPLETION OF SW WASHING.   4] DURING THE TANK CLEANING OPERATION, TO SAVE TIME IT WAS DECIDED THAT FIRE HOSE    TO BE  CONNECTED TO COW LINE.   5] THERE WAS ONLY BLANK AND NO VALVE WAS AVAILABLE. STILL VESSEL WENT AHEAD AND   OPENED BLANK, CONNECTED THE FIRE HOSE .   6] THE COW BRANCH LINE IS COMMON FOR SLOP(P) & SLOP(S) WITH ONE COMMON ROOT V/V   7] AS SOON SLOP(S) WASHING STARTED(ABOUT 4-5 KG) PRESSURE, THE FIRE HOSE CAME   OUT OF THE COUPLING WHICH WAS CONNECTED TO THE COW LINE.   8] AS A RESULT THE OILY WATER 30-40 LTRS IN THE COW LINE OF SLOP(P) SPILLED ON DECK    FROM THE COUPLING
ImmediateAction: 1] TANK CLEANING OPERATION SUSPENDED AND EMERGENCY ALARM SOUNDED.  2] ALL CREW IMMEDIATELY STARTED CONTAINMENT AND CLEAN-UP  3] CONFIRMED NO OIL ESCAPED OVERBOARD
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1] ABOUT 30-40 LTRS OF OILY WATER SPILLED ON DECK  2] ABOUT 3-4 HRS MAN HOURS LOST DUE TO CLEAN-UP
DirectCause: 1] ERROR IN JUDGEMENT AND GROSS NEGLIGENCE ON VESSEL PART.  2] COMPLACENCY WITH SHIPSTAFF AS THE LINE-UP WAS NOT DOUBLE CHECKED.  3] INADEQUATE SUPERVISION BY SENIOR OFFICERS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1]  SAFE PROCEDURES TO BE ALWAYS FOLLOWED.   2] SENIOR STAFF SHOULD NOT GET INVOLVED DIRECTLY, BUT SHOULD OVERSEE THE WHOLE PROGRESS.  3] COMPANY PROCEDURES TO BE STRICTLY FOLLOWED AND NO UN-ANALYZED DEVIATION TO BE ADOPTED  4] INCASE ANY CHANGES REQUIRED, SAME TO BE ASSESSED FOR ANY RISK/HAZARD AND DISCUSS WITH  OFFICE PRIOR CARRYING OUT THE TASK.
Corrective Action: 1]  SAFE PROCEDURES TO BE ALWAYS FOLLOWED.   2] SENIOR STAFF SHOULD NOT GET INVOLVED DIRECTLY, BUT SHOULD OVERSEE THE WHOLE PROGRESS.  3] COMPANY PROCEDURES TO BE STRICTLY FOLLOWED AND NO UN-ANALYZED DEVIATION TO BE ADOPTED  4] INCASE ANY CHANGES REQUIRED, SAME TO BE ASSESSED FOR ANY RISK/HAZARD AND DISCUSS WITH  OFFICE PRIOR CARRYING OUT THE TASK.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Direct Cause:-  Master and crew decided to connect the fire hose to the tank cleaning line. There was no isolation valve between the valves. The tank cleaning line was not having any isolation valve between the root valve and the blanked end of the tank cleaning line to which the fire hose was connected. Hence when the crew opened the root valve to wash the Slop (p) tank with hot sea water using the cargo pump, the fire hose coupling gave way which resulted in the spill of oily water on deck.  Indirect Cause  There were numerous calls made by the Charterers to the vessel to prepare the vessel in the soonest possible time as the shore tanks were getting filled up. This perceived pressure lead the Master to interfere in the tank cleaning operation and deviate from the original plan without considering the risks associated in the new plan. Master felt that he can save time by making the connection before completing the washing using the cargo oil pump.  Root Cause  Deviation from the plan without RA  Vessel made plan to complete HW wash first followed by the fresh water rinsing .During the course of HW wash, Master decided to deviate from the plan & made arrangements for FW rinsing by removing the blank connected to the tank cleaning line. A proper deviation plan was not made & associated risk of performing this operation while washing was not considered. Root Cause  Deviation from the plan without RA  Vessel made plan to complete HW wash first followed by the fresh water rinsing .During the course of HW wash, Master decided to deviate from the plan & made arrangements for FW rinsing by removing the blank connected to the tank cleaning line. A proper deviation plan was not made & associated risk of performing this operation while washing was not considered.
ClosingNote: Closed

IncidentDetails: M/E LO Purifier common fail.
IncidentEventDetails: The Oiler went to check No.1 LO  purifier as the alarm was shown common fail and found it appeared to overflow. He did discharging for re-running but did not work,he stopped the and informed the Engineer. The 4/E checked by dismantling and found that the ball bearing was damaged.
ImmediateAction: Overhaul and change ball bearing for M/E LO purifier.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cannot purify LO sump tank M/E.
DirectCause: The ball  bearing was broke and no other was available on board.

IncidentDetails: The alarm on the boiler control unit showed the SHD alarm.
IncidentEventDetails: Oiler went to check and found it appeared to be the boiler stopped. He did discharging for re-running but it did not work,so he stopped and informed the Engineer.The 3/E checked by dismantling everything and found that the electrod broke and could not work.The nozzle was also abnormal.
ImmediateAction: Renew electrol and nozzle.
DirectCause: It been very hard to ignition and has MDO consumption to much.Because it got ground (electrical leaking) and it has been ignited on somewhere else should not be.

IncidentDetails: VESSEL ALONGSIDE LBT NO.51 LIEPAJA, LATVIA LOADING RYE IN BULK. ON 05.09.15 AT 1800 HRS ONE HEAD LINE PARTED DUE TO HEAVY SURGING OF THE VESSEL AT BERTH AND REGULAR CHAFFING WITH THE SHIPS MOORING ARRANGEMENT AS THERE IS IMPROPER LEAD FOR HEADLINE AT BERTH. ON 06.09.2015 WHILE VESSEL SHIFTING FWD ONE SPRING PARTED AND AFTER SHIFTING AT 1730 HRS ONE HEAD PARTED.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL ALONGSIDE LBT NO.51 LIEPAJA, LATVIA LOADING RYE IN BULK. ON 05.09.15 AT 1800 HRS ONE HEAD LINE PARTED DUE TO HEAVY SURGING OF THE VESSEL AT BERTH AND REGULAR CHAFFING WITH THE SHIPS MOORING ARRANGEMENT AS THERE IS IMPROPER LEAD FOR HEADLINE AT BERTH. ON 06.09.2015 WHILE VESSEL SHIFTING FWD ONE SPRING PARTED AND AFTER SHIFTING AT 1730 HRS ONE HEAD PARTED.
ImmediateAction: THE CREW WAS CALLED FOR STATIONS AND THE STAND BY ROPES WHERE MADE FAST IN PLACE OF PARTED ROPES.
DirectCause: Due to heavy surging and inadequate lead to mooring lines.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to ask for local weather condition from pilot and agent. extra mooring lines to be given as required. Fwd and aft ropes to be equally tight and if required engines to be kept ready if there is chances of heavy surging.
ClosingNote: Pls discuss the act in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Fire & GS pump motor coupling broken
IncidentEventDetails: During routine O/haul of Fire & GS pp motor,while inserting coupling back  on the motor shaft, coupling broken with 60% intact,
ImmediateAction: Tried to repair & make temp arr for the coupling since 60% coupling was intact, informed office, raised requised for same, tried out stby fire  pumps
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fire and G.S pump motor coupling broken while assembling .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: BROKEN PIECE WELDED IN PLACE,SAME DIAMETER COPLING FLANG FABRICATED WITH 4MM PLATE AND WELDED ON BROKEN COUPLING TO GIVE MORE STRENGTH AND EQUAL DISTRIPUTION OF LOAD.  PUMP TRIEDOUT WORKING WITH NORMAL  AND SAME AMPS. WE CAN USE IN CASE OF EMERGENCY. RQUISITION RAISED FOR BROKEN COUPLING.NEED TO SUPPLY EARLIEST.
Corrective Action: New spare to supply
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: New spare supplied and fitted in place

IncidentDetails: During fire/safety round, after 2000hrs -2400hrs bridge watch AB-2 (Ashok kumar) has observed one of the   port hole glass of Gymnasium  in damaged condition.  Also the "Dart board" was hanging infront of the port hole and  "DARTS"  was  lying below on deck.
ImmediateAction: Dart board and Darts removed  immediately, called meeting to get the  information from the crew members, but  no information provided by anyone.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Glass of Gymnasium port hole damaged.
DirectCause: Carelessness of crew member/members.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting called and discussed the matter and advised crew members to be more vigilant  about ship's property and do not hide the things.  Also removed "Dart board and Dart" from the site.
LessionLearnt: Equipment to be placed in appropriate locations considering the environment.
ClosingNote: Noted requisition has been raised for replacing the Port Hole Glass.

IncidentDetails: Wire Renewal for Provision Crane Port Side: while renewing wire for port side provision crane , limit switch was bypassed for bringing the crane closer to accommodation block for ease of access to wire shackle. While slewing the crane with limit switch in bypassed condition crane metal stoppers made contact with each other and sheared off.
ImmediateAction: Immediately limit switch was brought back to normal operating condition and crane boom secured using the other metal stopper and pin.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Provision Crane Port Side: Metal stoppers damaged sheared off. See attached photos
DirectCause: Crew negligance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out for all crew on correct operating procedure of cranes.
LessionLearnt: Safety devices should not be bypassed without proper RA & necessary approvals.
ClosingNote: CA adequate & closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Moooring ropes parted during STS operation
IncidentEventDetails: V/ L was doing STS operation Off Lome with M.T Sam pride. Moderate seas , wind force : 5 , Swell ht : 2.0 Mtr.Both the vessel's started rolling moderately towards the last 3-4 hrs of the operation .Due to continous rolling of both the vessels ,4 mooring hawsers( 2 Head line, 2 Stern lines) and 4 mooring tails  ( 2 FWD Springs and 2 Aft Breast ) parted during the operation and while casting off from the daughter vessel .
ImmediateAction: Mooring tails replaced by spare mooring tails and mooring ropes made fast immediately by making a bow line eye  arrangement, vessels were casted off immediately after completion of trasnfer and paper work carried out at anchorage
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: V/ L was doing STS operation Off Lome with M.T Sam pride. Moderate seas , wind force : 5 , Swell ht : 2.0 Mtr.Both the vessel's started rolling moderately towards the last 3-4 hrs of the operation .Due to continous rolling of both the vessels ,4 mooring hawsers( 2 Head line, 2 Stern lines) and 4 mooring tails  ( 2 FWD Springs and 2 Aft Breast ) parted during the operation and while casting off from the daughter vessel .
DirectCause: Rough weather , Moderate seas and swell. Wind direction was perpendicular to the direction of the swell, because of this vessels were started to rolling moderately .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Avoid STS operation while vessel's are rolling .
LessionLearnt: Weather forecasts to be obtained well in advance regarding swell height and wind.Master to make early assessment of the situation and abort the operation if vessel starts rolling adversely.
ReviewComments: Kindly discuss the same in your abort condition during STS planning and risk assessment in order to avoid damages and injury.
ClosingNote: Closed on 23/09/15

IncidentDetails: Pilot Ladder Damaged while disembarking offsigners
IncidentEventDetails: While the vessel was at Lome anchorage, crew change was being carried out.  The launch boat was rolling and pitching heavily due to moderate wind and swell.  While disembarking offsigners, engine was used for creating a lee for the launch boat but it did not help much and the launch boat rolled heavily and banged against the pilot ladder resulting in one wooden step getting damaged.
ImmediateAction: Pilot Ladder was taken up and relaced by the other pilot ladder from stbd side.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While the vessel was at Lome anchorage, crew change was being carried out.  The launch boat was rolling and pitching heavily due to moderate wind and swell.  While disembarking offsigners, engine was used for creating a lee for the launch boat but it did not help much and the launch boat rolled heavily and banged against the pilot ladder resulting in one wooden step getting damaged.
DirectCause: Due to moderate sea and swell condition, wind direction was perpendicular to the direction of swell.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Defer crew change till the time weather is favourable for such operation
LessionLearnt: Weather must be ascertained to be favorable for crew change.Sufficient lee to be made to give the boat alongside some shelter from the swell.If such action is not possible then the crew change to be avoided all together.
ClosingNote: Closed out on 26/10/15

IncidentDetails: Vessel loosing a Sampling running bottle of cargo oil sampler while drawing sample from one of the cargo oil tanks.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was made fast at the port of singapore on 16-Sept-2015/1106 LT to discharge her cargo. While carrying out sampling of cargo tanks prior discharge in presence of cargo surveyor from INTERTEK the sampler bottle detached from the sampler unit and was lost into cargo tank when the cargo sample was was being drawn from SLOP P COT
ImmediateAction: Spare Sampling bottle was connected to the sampling unit and sampling carried out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo Sampler Running bottle Lost in one of vessels cargo oil tank
DirectCause: Sampler bottle parted from the place where it connects to the tape while drawing sample from the cargo tank. The probable cause being the connecting hook getting loose while drawing up the sample.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition for same has been raised and all crew briefed about the proper operation of the sampler and checks to be carried out prior operation.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Earlier Nanyang Star has faced the similar problem wrt Cargo sampler getting detached & falling to the COT.Persons directly involved in the cargo sampling to ensure that the connecting link is well tightened prior every sampling operations. Chief Officer to ensure that the cargo gauging / sampling units are kept ready prior every operation.
ClosingNote: Cargo sampler is yet to be retrieved from Slop-Port

IncidentDetails: During cargo operation whilst opening the hatch cover (Bay 10 ) hydraulic oil hose pipe coupling detached / damaged.Estimated 0.1 m3 hydraulic oil spilled on deck.
IncidentEventDetails: 1543 :- Duty AB started hyd. pump.   1544 :- Commenced opening of Bay 10 hatch cover.  1545 :- Hatch cover started closing automatically,Duty AB saw flow of oil on deck and pressed emergency stop button for hatch covers hydraulic pump.Tr.os informed duty officer.  1546 :- PA announcement made.Listed ship slightly to stbd. side.  1548 :- All crew members rushed  on deck and started cleaning & containment operation.  1555 :-Spilled Hyd.oil contained on board,nothing went overboard.Saw dust,cotton rags and absorbent sheets used to contain oil on board.Visual rounds taken all around the ship for any sign of oil sheen.  1602 :-Damgaed hose renewed.Hydraulic pump tried out,Bay 10 hatch cover opened .Surrounding areas inspected ,found no leakage.  1605 :- Bay 10 cargo operation resumed.Cross deck and main deck cleaning operation in progress.  1700 :-Cleaning operation completed.
ImmediateAction: Vessel all scuppers were plugged prior arrival.Spilled hydraulic oil contained on board.Saw dust,cotton rags  and absorbent sheets used to contain oil on board.Cleaning operation carried out.Visual rounds taken all around the ship for any sign of oil sheen.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1543 :- Duty AB started hyd. pump.   1544 :- Commenced opening of Bay 10 hatch cover.  1545 :- Hatch cover started closing automatically,Duty AB saw flow of oil on deck and pressed emergency stop button for hatch covers hydraulic pump.Tr.os informed duty officer.  1546 :- PA announcement made.Listed ship slightly to stbd. side.  1548 :- All crew members rushed  on deck and started cleaning & containment operation.  1555 :-Spilled Hyd.oil contained on board,nothing went overboard.Saw dust,cotton rags and absorbent sheets used to contain oil on board.Visual rounds taken all around the ship for any sign of oil sheen.  1602 :-Damgaed hose renewed.Hydraulic pump tried out,Bay 10 hatch cover opened .Surrounding areas inspected ,found no leakage.  1605 :- Bay 10 cargo operation resumed.Cross deck and main deck cleaning operation in progress.  1700 :-Cleaning operation completed.
DirectCause: Most of the hatch cover hose couplings are heavily corroded.Need to replace.Presently vessel buffed same with hand brush and covered with denso tape.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition has been raised for same in Jun 2015.Awaiting for supply.
Corrective Action: Company to arrange supply of Hydraulic hose coupling as requisitioned by Vessel.
LessionLearnt: vessel should maintain sufficient number hatch cover hose couplings.
ClosingNote: the supply of hydraulic hoses to be followed up.

IncidentDetails: On 08th June whilst the vessel was enroute from Shanghai to Laemchbang, in the Taiwan Strait, Chinese Dongshan Radio called up the vessel and alleged that the vessel has had a collision with a fishing vessel. After due verifications of the call and seeking office advice, vessel followed the instructions of the Chinese authorities and anchored off Xiamen anchorage for assisting in investigation process by the Chinese MSA.  Three MSA officials boarded the vessel for the investigation along with the agent on the 09th June 0930 LT.   Company representative Mr. Ashish Sanyal and the P&I Surveyor boarded the vessel on the 09th June 1400LT to assist with the investigation.   On the 09th June 2042 LT vessel resumed her voyage after completion of onboard investigation by the MSA and the P&I.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel on a liner trade, departed from Shanghai after completion of cargo operation on 06th June 2015 for Laemchabang. The passage was planned on the regular route that the vessel had been following on the previous voyages. On the 08th of June at 0406 SMT, vessel received a call for Dong Shan radio on VHF channel 16. Thereafter they switched channel to VHF 09 for further communication. Vessel was enquired on her next port of call and was asked to switch on the AIS. Vessel reported her next port and confirmed that the AIS unit is continuously kept on. Dong Shan radio then asked the vessel to call on +86 5966856830. On enquiring the reason for the same by Chief Officer, the Radio reported that their vessel had collided with the fishing boat in position 23 40.5N/118 02.5E at 07th June / 2018UTC-  (08th June 0318 SMT).  Master was informed and the call made to the number provided. Same information as above was passed to the vessel along with the orders to proceed to anchorage off Dong Shan Island in position 30 41 N 113 35E.
ImmediateAction: Master informed the Marine Superintendent immediately, thereafter CCC was manned and activated 2330 UTC on the 07th June 2015. Contact was established and after verification of the calls and the telephone numbers, at 0135 UTC on the 08th June, vessel was advised to turn around and proceed to position as instructed by the Dong Shan radio.  Mean time the company appointed local agents to assist and coordinate the investigation.   On advise by the agents at 0835 LT / 0135 UTC, 08th June 2015 vessel proceeded to Xiamen anchorage for investigation after taking due permission from the MSA.  Master carried out the D&A tests and saved the VDR Data as per the office advice.  Vessel anchored at Xiamen anchorage at 1948 LT / 1148UTC on the 08th June 2015. Master carried out internal investigation by interviewing the Second Officer and the Duty AB as well as analyzing other Navigation records.  On 09th June 2015 at 0930LT, three Chinese MSA officials boarded the vessel along with the local agent to conduct the investigation. At 1400 LT on the same day Company representative Mr. Ashish Sanyal and the P&I Surveyor boarded the vessel to assist in the investigation process.  On 09th June 2015 at 1530 LT / 0730UTC, MSA completed the investigation and disembarked the vessel. P&I surveyor and the company representative disembarked from the vessel at 2015LT on the 09th June 2015.  Vessel was allowed to sail by the MSA on condition of accepting few legal requirements, which the company did. She resumed her passage to Laemchabang at 2124LT on the 09th June 2015.  P&I Surveyors (Represented by Eversafe Insurance Surveyors & Loss Adjusters Co. Ltd) also investigated and inspected the fishing vessel on the 08th/09th and 10th June 2015.  The office received the P&I surveyor’s investigation report for the fishing vessel on the 10th June 2015 and for TS Japan on the 11th June 2015.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Whilst the vessel was investigated at Xiamen by the Chinese MSA, the hull was also inspected by the vessel P&I surveyors and company representative. There were 2 areas of light scratch marks showing white paint on the shell plate of the port side bow region from frame No.191~Frame No.230.  Name of the fishing vessel: Dong Tong MA 0058 Length: 16.5M Breadth: 3.3M Type: Wooden Hull auxiliary vessel On analyzing the report it appears that the fishing boat was present at the location and the position and time of alleged collision are very close. Alleged Time and position of the F/V: 2217UTC // 23 40.5N 118 02.6 E TS Japan time and position of the suspected collision: 2212UTC // 23 40.8N 118 02.9E. Fishing vessel reports that they had seen a black hull vessel but due to poor visibility were unable to read the name of the vessel. Some black color paints were found on the fishing vessel and were alleged to be that of TS Japan.
DirectCause: Detail Investigation report attached
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Detail Investigation report attached
Corrective Action: Container vessels officer matrix to be analyzed regularly and suitable actions or plans to be developed to put suitable control measures in place.
LessionLearnt: Detail report as per attachment (TS Japan - Alleged Collision with the Fishing vessel)
ReviewComments: Detail report as per attachment (TS Japan - Alleged Collision with the Fishing vessel)
ClosingNote: Detailed report attached for reference

IncidentDetails: While operating hose handling crane for lifting tool box from tug boat, hydraulic hose ruptured.
IncidentEventDetails: 0700LT: Vessel arrived rendevous position off Tema Off shore mooring for lifting toolbox from attending tug and crane swung out to stbd side.  0712LT: Tug approached alongside stbd side.  0715LT: While lowering the crane on of the hydraulic hose ruptured and sprayed oil on deck  0716LT: Tug advised and operation aborted. Clean up operation started.  0745LT: Hydraulic hose renewed and crane tried out satisfactorily  0805LT: Tool box lifted on deck from tug.
ImmediateAction: Immediately stopped the operation. Isolated hydraulic supply to crane and clean up of hydraulic oil done. Hydraulic hose renewed with spare one and crane tried out satisfactorily.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Flexible hydraulic hose for haose handling crane damaged. same renwed with spare one on board.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To inspect condition of other hydraulic hoses and renew if required.
Corrective Action: To inspect condition of other hydraulic hoses and renew if required.
ClosingNote: Close inspection of the crane hydraulic hoses to be inspected by removing any protection tapes and have one set of hydraulic hose standby at all times. Spare hose (to compensate for the consumed hose) was supplied on 4th Jan 2016.

IncidentEventDetails: While vessel was maneuvering on 10th/October’2015 under pilotage from Shanghai North pilot station to WaiGaoQiao berth,a close quarter situation was observed to be developing with container shipHyundai Faith (under pilotage) and same was brought to the notice of the pilotby Master.  Hyundai Faith was ahead of own vessel andwas turning in TSS making her approach to her berth. Own vessel observed  Hyundai Faith stopped and drifting rapidly in a NW/N’ly direction due to strong currents and obstructingthe general flow of traffic in both lanes of TSS. Own vessel took avoiding action with Hyundai faith by altering course to Starboard and cleared her.     Own vessel on clearing Hyundai faith noticed chinese coaster on the port bow which was navigating in the wrong lane of TSSintending to pass astern of Hyundai Faith and between own vessel. TheCoastal vessel was not transmitting  AIS dataand could not be detected by radar due to blind sector  created by Hyundai Faith. Coastal vessel was also visually obstructed to own vessel due to size of  Hyundai Faith.     Coastal vessel made contact with own vessel on port bow abreast Bay no.08 (P) and continued on her passage without disclosing her identity.Vessel let go her Starboard anchor in emergency and engines were ordered on Emergency Full astern to avoid collision with anchored vessels in No.7 anchorage outside TSS limits. Pilot called for tugs to assist  andvesselheaved up anchor.Vessel carried out ship side inspection using available tugs and observed Paint scratches/dent abreast of bay No.8. Own vessel reported to VTS  andcontinuedon her passage to her berth.
ImmediateAction: Temporary repairs carried out at Ningbo on 12th/October as per class recommendation.    Two additional brackets were fitted between 2nd deck and side longitudinal No.6 in way of  dented side shell area. Cracked brackets fillet welding was rewelded in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: (1). Side shell plate in way of No.2 C/H(P) between frame No.89-90, which located  between the 2nd deck(passageway deck) and side shell longitudinal No.6 was dented  (without any hole/crack at the shell plate). Depth around:70mm, length:1,600mm; Internal  brackets also found big deformation, the fillet seam of bracket found two locations of  cracks(100mm total in length).    (2). Side shell plate in way of No.2 C/H(P) between frame No.89-90, which beneath the 2nd  deck(passageway deck) between side shell longitudinal No.6 & 6A was dented (without  any hole/crack). Depth around:30mm, length: about 900mm. There is no obvious  deformation or crack was found at associated internal structures.
DirectCause: a. Direct Cause ? Coastal vessel was navigating in the wrong lane of TSS, intending to pass between Hyundai Faith and own vessel against the traffic flow came and made contact with own vessel  b. Indirect cause ? Coastal Vessel not detected by radar at adequate distance due to blind sector created by Hyundai Faith and probably due to own vessels radar settings. ? Coastal vessel was visually obstructed to own vessel by Hyundai Faith. ? All communication during the complete pilotage was carried out in Chinese both by pilots and VTS.  c. Root Cause ? Lack of compliance by Collision regulations while navigating in TSS were not observed by coastal vessel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Recommended corrective actions : 1. Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels appraising and warning them of navigating in restricted waters.  PIC: CMS      Target Date: Completed (Oct 16,2015) 2. Incident details to be discussed during the pre-joining briefing for senior officers. PIC: CMS      Target Date: In progress 3. All navigating incidents in the fleet for the last two years to be reviewed and incorporated in the in house          BTM course  PIC: CMS/Training Division    Target Date: Training Started (Nov 09,2015) 4.  Navigation Safety campaign covering various aspects of Navigation including lessons learnt from       various Navigational incidents should be conducted every quarter. PIC:CMS Division      Target Date: Dec 15, 2015 5.  The following to be emphasized during the in house BTM course Using Radar with SOG inputs, Proper tuning of radar and effective utilization of rain and sea clutters PIC :Training Division      Target Date: Dec 15, 2015
Corrective Action: 1)Bridge team to be more alert during manouvering speacially in confined and high density traffic under plotage.
LessionLearnt: a. Direct Cause ? Coastal vessel was navigating in the wrong lane of TSS, intending to pass between Hyundai Faith and own vessel against the traffic flow came and made contact with own vessel b. Indirect cause ? Coastal Vessel not detected by radar at adequate distance due to blind sector created by Hyundai Faith and probably due to own vessels radar settings. ? Coastal vessel was visually obstructed to own vessel by Hyundai Faith. ? All communication during the complete pilotage was carried out in Chinese both by pilots and VTS.  c. Root Cause ? Lack of compliance by Collision regulations while navigating in TSS were not observed by coastal vessel.
ReviewComments: Comments as per attached investigation report
ClosingNote: Investigation completed and Investigation report attached

IncidentDetails: STBD GANGWAY PUSHED INWARD BY SHIPPING SEAS DURING HEAVY WEATHER ON 24~25 OCT 2015 , RESULTING IN DAMAGING SECURING BOLTS AND DENT IN GANGWAY SIDE PLATE
IncidentEventDetails: 24 OCT 2015 : 0001 HRS VESSEL ENCOUNTERING HEAVY WEATHER  24~25 OCT 2015 : VESSEL EXPERIENCING HEAVY LONG BEAM SWELL , SHIPPING SEAS FROM STBD SIDE MAIN DECK  26 OCT 2015 : 0900 HRS DURING ROUTINE INSPECTION FOR HEAVY WEATHER INSPECTION FOUND DAMAGE IN STBD GANGWAY
ImmediateAction: RENEWED EXTRA LASHING OF PORT AND STBD GANGWAY TAKEN
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 4 numbers of lashing bolts got damaged , Lashing plate got dent at 3 location , slight dent on mid section of stbd gangway apprx 5 mm Dent.
DirectCause: Heavy swell from stbd side pushed the gangway inward causing lashing rod to bent and causing slight dent to gangway
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repaired bend lashing bolts and straightening of gangway plate
Corrective Action: Repaired bend lashing bolts and straightening of gangway plate
LessionLearnt: Master to brief the crew regarding the importance of conducting a good securing of vessel prior vessel's proceeding to sea,It has to be emphasized that it is very important to adjust the vessel's course and speed in heavy weather condition to ensure the safety of life,environment and property while vessel is experiencing heavy weather.
ClosingNote: Closed on 17/11/15

IncidentEventDetails: Passes issued to the visitors not returned to the vessel while disembarking due to the inefficient montioring by gangaway watchkeepers.
ImmediateAction: Search carried out on board to locate the missing passes,Visitors logs checked for missing passed.We are not able to locate the exact time and place when it is lost.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During the ISPS audit it was found that few of visitors ID passes are missing , Pink (17,20) is missing; Yellow(1,4-7,9,10,14-20) are missing.We are not able to locate the exact time and place when it is lost.
DirectCause: Inefficient monitoring of visitors movement during dearture.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Effective training to be carried out with all Concerned persons. Ensure effective supervision.
Corrective Action: All visitors passes to be checked/tallied by SSO prior departure port,Proper training to be given to officers and crew involved.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Effective Monitoring of Visitor Passes and adequate supervision with records being maintained
ReviewComments: Verification of Visitor passes carried out and recorded on departure every port.
ClosingNote: Records of training attached and effective implementation Verified through internal audit process

IncidentDetails: On 11 September 2015 14:30 HRS. After complete change gasket for exhaust manifold DG no.1 We have running test for check leak exhaust manifold found DG 1 running abnormal. We had stop DG 1 for check again found rack of fuel pump unit1 to get stuck.
IncidentEventDetails: On 11 September 2015 14:30 HRS. After complete change gasket for exhaust manifold DG no.1 We have running test for check leak exhaust manifold found DG 1 running abnormal. We had stop DG 1 for check again found rack of fuel pump unit1 to get stuck.
ImmediateAction: Change fuel pump (move fuel pump DG3 unit 5 to installed DG1 unit 1)
DirectCause: Can not run DG no.1.Because rack of fuel pump unit 1 not working
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Change fuel pump (move fuel pump DG3 unit 5 to installed DG1 unit 1)
Corrective Action: Change fuel pump (move fuel pump DG3 unit 5 to installed DG1 unit 1)

IncidentDetails: The Alarm Monitoring System displayer was beeping and gone by shown nothing. We have been check and fond non of the current into computer and checked at Uninterruptible power supply it was non of current to that was mean the battery got worse quality.
IncidentEventDetails: The Alarm Monitoring System displayer was beeping and gone by shown nothing. We have been check and fond non of the current into computer and checked at Uninterruptible power supply it was non of current to that was mean the battery got worse quality.
ImmediateAction: Temporary connected direct power to computer No.1 & 2 for Alarm monitoring system in ECR.

Corrective Action: To replace battery as per makers instructions

IncidentDetails: On 31 Aug 15 at 0515 Hrs the alarm has shown on No. 1 LO purifier common fail. Oiler went to check and found it appeared overflow. He did discharging for re-running but did not work so he was stopped and inform engineer. At 0830 Hrs, 4/E was checked by dismantling and found the ball bearing was damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: On 31 Aug 15 at 0515 Hrs the alarm has shown on No. 1 LO purifier common fail. Oiler went to check and found it appeared overflow. He did discharging for re-running but did not work so he was stopped and inform engineer. At 0830 Hrs, 4/E was checked by dismantling and found the ball bearing was damaged.
DirectCause: Can not purify LO sump tank M/E. The ball bearing was broke and have no spare part on board.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Overhaul and change ball bearing for M/E LO purifier.
Corrective Action: Overhaul and change ball bearing for M/E LO purifier.
LessionLearnt: To check the vibrations of Lo purifier very frequently in order to get early signs of bearing damage. Purifiers should be manually discharged once a day.and checked for vibration each time. Vessel to have adequate bearings at all times.
ClosingNote: Purifier overhauled and brought back to satisfactory condition.

IncidentDetails: LOSS OF ONE MOB BUOY INCLUDING LIGHT AND SMOKE SIGNAL
IncidentEventDetails: 0800-0810H : tool box meeting carried out w/ deck crew prior to working on deck  0810-0900H: all Crew Commence Touch up Painting on Accomodation Area  0900H: Crew Reported that the Port Side MOB Buoy incl. its Light and Smoke Signal Has Accidentary Fallen to the Sea.Crew Reported that He lost His Balance as He was Removing the Buoy from its Bracket that caused the Buoy to fall into the Sea.
ImmediateAction: INFORM CAPT. BAKSHI,LOG THE INCIDENT IN DECK OFFICIAL LOGBOOK AND INFORM NEAREST MRCC -HONOLULU  REQUESTED NEW MOB BUOY INCL. ITS LIGHT AND SMOKE SIGNAL THRU SHIPPALM SPR/D/15/096.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NEEDS ONE REPLACEMENT MOB BUOY INCL. ITS SMOKE AND LIGHT SIGNAL
DirectCause: LACK OF AWARENESS. LACK OF DUE REGARDS TO GOOD SEAMANSHIP.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ONLY A DECK OFFICER IS ALLOWED TO REMOVE THE MOB BUOY IN CASE OF ANY MAINTENANCE WORK.
Corrective Action: ONLY A DECK OFFICER IS ALLOWED TO REMOVE THE MOB BUOY IN CASE OF ANY MAINTENANCE WORK.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Own vessel was on a voayge from Ust Luga, Russia bounded to Rotterdam for discharging cargo, Naphtha.   On 29 Oct2015, while vessel was enroute on TSS MASS approach on a southerly course,Vessel received call from Netherland coast guard on VHF.  Coast guard requested the vessel to forward BOL or cargo manifest to fulfill some  documentation on their part.Same was sent immediately by email.  2100 LT : Vessel received second call from Netherland Coast Guard , informing the vessel that vessel had breached the applicable routing for vessel of her size and cargo.
IncidentEventDetails: On 29 Oct2015, while vessel was enroute on TSS MASS approach on a southerly course,Vessel received call from Netherland coast guard on VHF.  They requested the vessel to forward BOL or cargo manifest to fulfill some  documentation on their part.Same was sent immediately by email.  2100 LT : Vessel received second call from Netherland Coast Guard , informing the vessel that she  had breached the applicable routing for vessel of her size and cargo.
ImmediateAction: Upon primary investigation it was noticed laden tankers carrying cargo of Marpol Annex 1 having GT >10,000 GT shall follow mandatory DW route as per Regulation issued by IMO. No refrence regarding manadatory routing for tankers was given on charts.    Vessel informed the situation to company through operation superintendent.    VDR backup taken and other records preserved.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Not damage to person or property inccured. No fines or penalty inccured so far.
DirectCause: Human error
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Briefing carried out with bridge team.OOWs briefed regarding tanker prohibited area and to consult nautical publications to get local regulations. navigation chart may not give full profile of loacl regulations and routing requirements.
Corrective Action: Briefing carried out with bridge team.OOWs briefed regarding tanker prohibited area and to consult nautical publications to get local regulations. navigation chart may not give full profile of loacl regulations and routing requirements.
LessionLearnt: Passage Planning Appraisal to be done using all available information & thoroughly reviewed by Master before approval.
ClosingNote: Matter discussed with Master during his briefing for next vessel.

IncidentDetails: Tug LAMNALCO FREGAT damaged the vessel's port side Pilot ladder while the vesel at CPC-R terminal, Novorossiysk, Russia.
IncidentEventDetails: On 12th Nov at 1230 Hrs Lt while the vessel was in topping off stage of loading, Mooring master advised vessel to rig Pilot ladder on port side of the vessel 3 mtrs above water level with man ropes for disembarkation of Mooring master and assisting crew. Vessel rigged the pilot ladder as required by the Mooring master. At 1335 LT, Tug LAMNALCO FREGAT appoached the vessel and due to heavy swell, tug's bow fender made contact with the pilot ladder and damaging the Pilot ladder's rubber steps and the rope.
ImmediateAction: Informed Loading Master immediately. Loading Master instructed tug to move away from the vessel. Damaged Pilot Ladder retrived and another Pilot ladder rigged for disembarkation of Loading master and assisting crew. Ship side inspected. No damage to the ship's hull. Water Ballast tank will be inspected during the voyage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damaged Pilot Ladder
DirectCause: Caused due to the heavy swell and improper manouvering of the Tug Boat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Whenever any access ladder are rigged, weather to be closely monitor. Any approaching crafts to be cautioned of the swell alonside.
Corrective Action: Whenever any access ladder are rigged, weather to be closely monitor. Any approaching crafts to be cautioned of the swell alonside.
LessionLearnt: Ship's crew be extra cautious during rough weather work on deck.Ship's crew to warn the small crafts approaching alongside & any overhanging protrusions to be retracted in prior approach of crafts both related to safety & security concerns.
ReviewComments: New Pilot ladder has been supplied to the vessel during her transit at Istanbul
ClosingNote: Closed on 26-Nov-15

IncidentDetails: PARTING CRANE NO 1 WIRE AND GRAB DAMAGE WHILE DISCHARGING COLA CARGO
IncidentEventDetails: AT 1725 HRS ON 09.10.2015 CRANE NO. 1 RUNNER WIRE PARTED WHILE DISCHARGING COAL FROM CARGO HOLD NO.1. AT THE TIME OF OCCURENCE GRAB WAS ABOUT 5-8 METER ABOVE THE CARGO HOLD. AFTER PARTING WIRE CRANE LIFTING HOOK ALSO FELL DOWN ON GRAB CAUSING DAMAGE TO GRAB SPILL PLATE APART FROM SELF DAMAGE TO GRAB WHILE FELL DOWN IN HLD WITH FULL CARGO.
ImmediateAction: CRANE STOPPED IMMIDIATELY AND  CHECKED FORM ANY OTHER AREA OF DAMAGE APART FROM GRAB AND CRANE RUNNER
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CRANE NO.1 RUNNER WIRE PARTED AND GRAB SPILL PLATE DEFORMED, BUCKLING AND DEEP DENT  ON SIDE AND BOTTOM PLATES.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) TO CHANGE CRANE WIRES AT PARTICULAR RUNNING HOURS   2) SMOOTH OPERATION OF CRANE,BRIEF OPERATOR BEFORE OPERATING CRANE.
Corrective Action: 1) TO CHANGE CRANE WIRES AT PARTICULAR RUNNING HOURS   2) SMOOTH OPERATION OF CRANE,BRIEF OPERATOR BEFORE OPERATING CRANE.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Proper cargo watches to be  maintained. Specially while deck cranes are in use. Any rough handing by operator should be immediately brought to the attention of the cargo foreman.

IncidentDetails: Inadverdent closure of L2 port side liquid manifold pneumatic actuator valve during loading.
IncidentEventDetails: Propane manifold L2 port side pneumatic actuator valve closed inadverdently approx 4 hours after commencement of loading. Immediately loading was ceased at 0627 hrs. On investigation, it was found that control solenoid in motor room was leaking. Same solenoid was interchanged with other not in use manifold solenoid valve. The problem was rectified and loading was resumed at 0650 hrs.  The butane loading continued during this time. Propane loading was stopped for 23 min.
ImmediateAction: On investigation, it was found that control solenoid in motor room was leaking. Same solenoid was interchanged with other not in use manifold solenoid valve.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage occured to ship/shore property.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renew the faulty solenoid valve
Corrective Action: Renew the faulty solenoid valve
LessionLearnt: Pre arrival checks and pms should include all the accessories of the equipment in subject
ReviewComments: Please include the inspection of solenoid valve - either in the pre-arrival checks along with the cargo valve operation or add to PMS.
ClosingNote: Same included in pre arrival checks. Solenoid supplied and kept as a spare.

IncidentDetails: At port of Xiamen vessel along side to Stbd with 4 + 1 + 2 ( 3 long head line from port side winch on one bollard, 1 head line from stbd winch on a near bollard, 1 brest line & 2 spring line from center winch on one bollard ).while unberthing master order to single up fwd lowered 2 head line from port winch,1 head line from & 1 brest line from stbd winch and 1 spring from center winch. the mooring gang casted of 1 head line and dropped into water and was trying to take out the other head line and fwd was waiting for the other head to cast off , by the time fwd secured  all other lines.Later the lines man show action that they cannot cast off the line because the rope is under other head line which cannot be seen from fwd station because it was dark near the area of that bollard.immediately fwd heaved up the slack and lowered the other head line.by the time they cast off the head line the rope which was in water drifted towards bulbous bow due to strong head current and wind.while heaving up head lines found one rope crossing bulbous bow leading towards bow thruster and getting tight.Reported bridge that suspect mooring line fouled in BT.
IncidentEventDetails: The BT was in RUN mode to be used to clear bow from contact with jetty on departure and BT being CONTROLLED PITCH PROPELLER it was rotating at a certain RPM even while running idle.The slack in the line in water lead to its entanglement in BT assisted by local currents.
ImmediateAction: BT stopped completely to avoid further damage and Tugs used for Departure.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MOORING ROPE ENTANGLED IN BT.5-6 MTRS LENGTH OF ROPE CUT AND SPLIICED.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TO KEEP BT ON IDLE MODE WHEN LINES IN WATER
Corrective Action: TO KEEP BT ON IDLE MODE WHEN LINES IN WATER
LessionLearnt: RA Prepared and displayed Near BT Control panel.
ReviewComments: RA to be displayed near BT Control Panel.
ClosingNote: To ensure effective implementation of RA as attached.

IncidentDetails: While berthing at Rotterdam, for'd breast winch operating lever connection to valve main block through rocking lever broke and winch could not be used during berthing.
IncidentEventDetails: While berthing alongside at Rotterdam, For'd breast winch operating lever connection to valve main block through rocking lever broke and winch could not be used during berthing.    Mooring confirguration reqd was 4,2,2 fore & aft. After requesting pilot and terminal (Through the pilot), it was accepted/agreed to have for'd mooring (2,2,2). Terminal was informed that within 2 hrs the winch will be made operational and additional lines will be sent . After vessel was berthed, for'd breast winch operating lever was repaired and mooring boat was arranged thru agent for sending additional lines.      Pls note similar incident in another port could have caused significant delays and / or refusal to berth the vessel.    In view of above, pls arrange to supply reqn SPR/CHAMP/D/15/062 on 24.10.15 at the earliest opportunity.Pls increase quantity of item no. 6 (Rocking lever 421766 / Component no. 226) to 6 no.s instead of 2 no.s.
ImmediateAction: 0500 lt : After vessel all fast, for'd breast winch operating lever was repaired using spare rocking lever.Agent was contacted to arrange for mooring boat to send additional mooring lines to have mooring configuration 4,2,2 fore & aft.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to operating lever connection to valve main block through rocking lever.
DirectCause: Material failure. Wear & tear of rocking lever.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: - Crew training to be carried out for winch operation during cold weather.  - 1 hour before planned mooring ops, power packs to be started. Half an hour before planned mooring ops, winches to be put on idling.  - Reqn for addional supply of rocking lever raised.
Corrective Action: - Crew training to be carried out for winch operation during cold weather. - 1 hour before planned mooring ops, power packs to be started. Half an hour before planned mooring ops, winches to be put on idling. - Reqn for addional supply of rocking lever raised.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: NMR closed

IncidentDetails: Chief Cook Rangasamy Kumar was found Positive in External Drug and Alcohol testing carried out on Yeosu.While vessel was discharging her cargo.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was along side Yeosu and discharging Naphtha cargo . While discharging cargo Bond store had arrived . All ship staff were given call for picking up Bond store . While Picking up Bond store some of Beer cases were fallen down while carrying by crew member . During which some cans were totally damage and instead of discarding the cans chief cook had wilfully consumed beer .After some time consuming beer chief cook had gone for under urine sample for External Alcohol testing arranged by Managers .
ImmediateAction: Since thier was no evidence brought forward to master for consuming of damaged cans by chief cook and report  of external alcohol test received via mail from office  while vessel was at open sea. No immediate action was taken and chief cook was signed off next port after receiving test report from external party
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.Responsible officer incharge for bond store was passed wrong information from chief cook for disposal of discarded beer can and same information was forward to Master.  2.Chief cook had wilfully consumed beer from discarded can without the knowledge of any officers or crew member. Chief cook had gone for disposing beer  in sink which he drank where no crew member was present.  3.Master had amended his Alcohol inventory taking in  account of damaged cans from the information he collected from responsible officer.  4.Responsible officer incharge for Bond store was not present on location  where beer case were damage by crew member while transferring of bond from main deck to bond store.  5.Please find attached external drug and alcohol test report for chief cook.
DirectCause: 1.All crew members are briefed by office staff about company  drug and alcohol policy prior joining every vessel and every single time irrespective of how long he is with company. Crew member are well aware any violation regarding Drug and Alcohol policy will not be tolerated by company and will lead to termination of contract.    2.Consumption of beer from Damage can , which should  been discarded as by the order given by  Master.    3.Clear evidence Lack of supervision from Responsible officer incharge of bond store .He was not present at location while picking up of bond .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Briefing and Training by Master to all crew member on drug and alcohol policy . Master was emphasising on company stance on drug and alcohol policy. Company will not tolerate any breach on drug and alcohol policy.    2.Damage cans to be discarded under the presence of Master or any senior officer . To Ensure none of crew member consume alcohol inadvertently.    3.All Crew member were warned any crew under the influence of alcohol during their rest hours should not report to work. Even if any crew member was called inadvertently for any work shall report to his Head of Department or Master he is under the influence of Alcohol.    4.Chief cook was immediately signed off from next Port Singapore for further investigation . It was found chief cook had carried out willful act  violation of drug and alcohol policy and he has been terminated from company    5.Master had carry out  counselling of  responsible officer incharge of bond store and emphasis on supervision and controlling of alcohol.    6.Following incident was circulated among fleet.
Corrective Action: Master should ensure duty officer shall be present while picking up bond stores and he should be responsible and ensure all beer cases reaches safely to bond store and if any cases are damage  he shall collect following cans and report to Master . He shall ensure Drug and Alcohol policy of company is maintain all the time even though some body has gone on deck to assist other crew member for securing bond stores . Master is solely Responsible for Ensuring Drugs and Alcohol policy is maintained on board as per company requirement irerespective whethetr officer working hours or went for sleep.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: To avoid further incident in future Master to ensure responsible officer to be witness for bond store and their is no miss use of alcohol and Drug & Alcohol policy is compliance all time on ship.
ClosingNote: Following incident was circulated among fleet

IncidentDetails: Port Bridge wing MOB Marker & buoy found missing.
IncidentEventDetails: 09 Dec 15 / 1900LT: As per eye witness the MOB marker was positively in place.(26 08'N 148 07.2'W)  09/10 Dec 15 / 2300LT - 0230 LT: Vessel was experiecing heavy rolling to 15 degrees.  10 Dec 15 / 0630: When the day broke it was discovered that the Port Bridge wing MOB marker & buoy was not in place, however the relasing pin was intact and in place.( 27 00'N 145 31.0'W)
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed master. Stbd side MOB marker checked and found in place.  Thorough screening all around the horizon for any smoke / light or buoy.  Nothing found.  Urgency message transmitted and company informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MOB marker on the Port bridge wing was Missing.
DirectCause: Weekly / Monthly checks of the MOB arrangement was carried out on 06 Dec 2015 / 26 Nov 2015.  Vessel experienced heavy rolling and vibration due to long swells in the pacific and vessel was in ballast condition.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Release pin to be shifted towards centre for effective securing of the buoy (Dry dock Specs to be raised )  Meanwhile, Release pin will be passed through the Grab line for avoiding accidental release.  Untill vessel recieves new MOB marker and Buoy, Stbd bridge wing MOB marker/ Buoy to be kept in the wheel house for use in emergency on either side.
Corrective Action: Release pin to be shifted towards centre for effective securing of the buoy (Dry dock Specs to be raised ) Meanwhile, Release pin will be passed through the Grab line for avoiding accidental release. Untill vessel recieves new MOB marker and Buoy, Stbd bridge wing MOB marker/ Buoy to be kept in the wheel house for use in emergency on either side.
LessionLearnt: Securing & safety latches for equipment on deck to be checked prior entering the areas wherein heavy weather can be expected.
ClosingNote: MOB marker buoy supplied to the vessel at the port of arrival after the incident / NMR Closed

IncidentDetails: As ship was to take bunkers the next day, ship staff had decided to pressure test the line as a standard practice. Line was under continuous monitoring from 2.00 Bar. in steps of 0.5 Bar. at 2.5, 3.00, 3.5 and 4.00 bar there was no any sign of leakage and /or pressure drop.    At 4.5 Bar, leakage observed in following section.  " Forward transfer pump discharge line ", which is connected to main bunker line for internal transfer (From fwd tanks to aft tanks). As there is no isolation valve, this line was under pressure during pressure testing. About 3.00 Liters oil got sprayed from the leakage point/crack developed. Immediately designated valve for pressure release opened, alarm initiated and spill contained.
IncidentEventDetails: As ship was to take bunkers the next day, ship staff had decided to pressure test the line as a standard practice. Line was under continuous monitoring from 2.00 Bar. in steps of 0.5 Bar. at 2.5, 3.00, 3.5 and 4.00 bar there was no any sign of leakage and /or pressure drop.    At 4.5 Bar, leakage observed in following section.  " Forward transfer pump discharge line ", which is connected to main bunker line for internal transfer (From fwd tanks to aft tanks). As there is no isolation valve, this line was under pressure during pressure testing. About 3.00 Liters oil got sprayed from the leakage point/crack developed. Immediately designated valve for pressure release opened, alarm initiated and spill contained.
ImmediateAction: De pressurized the line immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A section of pipe line damaged/not ready to re-use. Same has to be renewed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Bunker lines to be pressure tested at regular intervals as described in company SMS manuals. Regular inspection during bunkering
Corrective Action: Bunker lines to be pressure tested at regular intervals as described in company SMS manuals. Regular inspection during bunkering
LessionLearnt: Deteriorated sections to be identified beforehand and replaced as necessary.
ReviewComments: Concur with vessels corrective action
ClosingNote: Pipeline has been renewed and all other pipes checked for integrity. Line ws pressure tested once again and found to be OK.

IncidentDetails: Damage hull due to strong impact in fender
IncidentEventDetails: During maneuvering as the vessel trying to berth port side alongside with two tugs, the C/off and 2/off reporting in bridge the distance of the bow and astern respectively.As the vessel parallel to berth the tug start pushing the bow but the C/off reported that it is not passing clear, so the pilot advise to dead slow astern up to full astern. When the bow is clear, 2/off reported that we are getting closed to the barge behind us rapidly. The Pilot order to stop engine dead slow ahead up to full ahead. But the vessel continuing to move backward. When the vessel is about in position, the tug in starboard quarter start pushing us to berth then an impact coming from port side was observed. When the vessel is already made fast, a damage was found in frame no.35 to 36.
ImmediateAction: Checking of all ballast tanks. Informed Office and Agent regarding the damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage inside WBT no.5 Port side on  frame no.35 to 36
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper Pilot and Master exchange
Corrective Action: Proper Pilot and Master exchange
ClosingNote: During approach to the berth, the bridge team must have good situational awareness. The master must be ready to take over from the pilot, at any time, when he suspects the situation going out of hand. The master must check by all avaliable means, on the berth conditions and if there are any hazards, they must be tackled accordingly.

IncidentDetails: Pls note that during shifting in Dublin(1400UTC) our heaving line (Abt 20)mtrs has got stuck to the propeler. we r trying our best to reverse the engines. we completed reversing the engines but no success. The heaving line with the monkey fist has wounded around the propeler very tightly. Present draft is 6.6 mtrs n we can reduce the draft to 4.5 mtrs. The 100% propeller imersion is at 5.5mtrs.     The length of the propeller blade is 1.86mtrs plus additional .765mm of bush. ie total length from bush to tip of the propeller blade 2.625mtrs.    Even after reducing to minimum draft(4.5mtrs) we are unable to acces the proppler shaft where the line has stuck.    Out of total lenght of 30 mtrs of heaving line we are remaing with only 8 mtrs . Rest is wound around the propeller and shaft along with monkey fist.
IncidentEventDetails: Pls note this is entirely due to the error on the part of mooring gang which threw the whole of heaving line with the monkey fist into the water without the knowledge of the shipstaff.The shipstaff was engaged in tying up the ship when the mooring men threw the heaving line in the water . The line got caught in the shaft(500rpm) and immediatedly parted. No injuries on board.
ImmediateAction: we r trying our best to reverse the engines. we completed reversing the engines but no success.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The divers went and removed the rope . Inspection of propeller carried out . no damage.
DirectCause: due to irresponsible & un professional  behaviour of the linesman
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To advise the pilot in advance so as to inform linesman not to throw the heaving line in the water.
Corrective Action: To advise the pilot in advance so as to inform linesman not to throw the heaving line in the water.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Master Pilot Exchange detailing the limitation of vessels equipment involved.
ReviewComments: The incident was caused due to the reckless behaviour of the longshoremen. Effective Master pilot exchange would have made the linesman more of aware and sympathetic towards the peculiar condition on board vessels
ClosingNote: The incident was caused due to the reckless behavior of the longshoremen. Effective Master pilot exchange would have made the linesman more of aware and sympathetic towards the peculiar condition on board vessels. Incident distributed to all vessels with CPP

IncidentDetails: LSMGO spilled on deck from the filling line flanges of FOT-1P during LSMGO bunkering at Novorossiysk on 24 Nov 2015.
IncidentEventDetails: Upon commencement of LSMGO Bunkering, noticed a minor leak from the flange above the Bunker Tank filling valve of FOT-1P and as well as the newly fabricated Bunker line spectacle blank flange connecting the newly modified HFO bunker Line.
ImmediateAction: 1-Immediately informed CCR to call the loading master who was on board to stop LSMGO bunkering and same stopped.  2-Observed a minor spray on deck with spill of aprrox.2 Litres and was contained on the deck below the bunker line itself without further spreading.  3-Ship’s Emergency alarm was raised immediately and all crew mustered without delay and SOPEP equipments were put in use and the spill was cleaned up within 5 minutes.  4-Both leaking flange above the FOT-1P filling valve and the leaking spectacle blank flange were tightened.   5-Also blanked the line leading to FOT-1P from the LSMGO bunkering line.   6-Entire LSMGO bunker line was inspected and Pressure tested before resuming bunkering.  7-Resumed LSMGO bunkering at a slower rate, after confirming no leakage sighted through out the LSMGO bunker line, bunkering rate increased further.  8-The entire bunker line was monitored continuously throughout the bunkering operation and no further leakages were observed at any event of time there after and completed safely LSMGO bunker reception.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage to environment(the spill was contained on the deck just below the lines and valves) and no injury to person involved in the bunkering operation.  No delay to the Vessel.
DirectCause: In drydock the newly fabricated HFO line pressure testing carried only till the Spectacle flange shown in attached picture.HFO line fabrication job was completed just before the departure of vessel.   Yard done pressure testing only at the last moment of departure. However, Yard have not done Pressure testing of Existing LGMGO line which was modified by yard, although they have issued a   pressure testing certificate.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Bunker lines ( both HSFO & LSMGO) will be pr.tested on regular basis and 2 or 3 days before any bunkering operation, if confirmed regarding bunker supply.
Corrective Action: Bunker lines ( both HSFO & LSMGO) will be pr.tested on regular basis and 2 or 3 days before any bunkering operation, if confirmed regarding bunker supply.
ClosingNote: This is a near miss

IncidentDetails: STBD gangway fwd base plates cracked at base welding seam.
IncidentEventDetails: during departure when vessel ready to heavy up off shore side gangway from deck level to securing position it was observed that fwd base plates at bottom are cracked and the gangway is not resting on angle bars properly. observed that gangway is missalligned and is tilted aft horizontally.
ImmediateAction: STBD gangway taken up and secured in with lashings. all other strethening mebers of gangway fittings checked for any damge, all found in order. fwd plate found half way cracked as was visible through top paint coating.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: STBD gangway at present not resting completly on Setting bars. continous plate fabricated to achieve same. Crack weld temprarily repaired. Gangway tried out for all movements after repair , presently safe for use. Crack on Forward plates bottom Weld running through approx 15 CM in length on fwd plate and 10 cm on second fwd plate. aft plate bent vertically aft from top. causing missallignment while lowering gangway, slight dent observed on the first platform on deck.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During DRY dock gangway to be rewelded in places after bent plates realligned and straightened.
Corrective Action: During DRY dock gangway to be rewelded in places after bent plates realligned and straightened.
ClosingNote: Required repairs for the G'way carried out in DD and same load tested and found satisfactory.Detailed briefing of crew carried out regarding safe operation and upkeep of gangway.Job closed.

IncidentDetails: Poop deck stbd side provision crane wire was found to be damaged during regular inspection, the location of damage is observed to be under the limit activation circular disc,
IncidentEventDetails: It is observed that, the damage is exactly under the disc of limit switch. Due to ships movement the disc has removed the grease apllied on the wire. Further abrasion damage in time has caused upper layer of wire fiber to break causing water ingress to core of which started corrosion.
ImmediateAction: Provision crane wire was changed. Grease tape is applied on the new wire in way of limit switch disc so as to avoid contact damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Provision crane wire found deteriorated and damaged under the contact limit switch.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Length of Wire under Limit switch disc is covered with denso tape so as to avoid direct contacts and rusting contact damge.
Corrective Action: Length of Wire under Limit switch disc is covered with denso tape so as to avoid direct contacts and rusting contact damge.
LessionLearnt: Regular close up inspection of wires need to be carried out.
ClosingNote: New wire renewed and made protection against abrasion.

IncidentDetails: Damage to Anchor chain adjusting Bar(stripper bar).
IncidentEventDetails: At 2300 / 11-Dec-15 Vessel dropped Port Anchor at Hongkong for bunkering operation .   At 1912 / 12-Dec-15 Vessel commenced heaving up Anchor at Slow Speed ,While Picking up Anchor the cable get stuck on Gypsy groves and fell over chain adjusting bar resulted the bar got bent. Vessel  Stopped heaving / adjusted bar position by use of hammer & crow bar, resumed heaving the anchor.    On 13-Dec-15 , vessel dismantled stripper bar and brought to Engine room workshop, Heated at maximum extent to make bar straight, but could not succeeded.
ImmediateAction: Ceased Heaving up anchor, adjusted position of the bar by using hammer & crow bar such that anchor could be heave up with out obstruction.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to Anchor Stripper Bar.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Trainings Carried out of all crew, Briefly Explained the Reason.  Vessel tried straightening the bar in E/R workshop, but same was not possible. Raised repair request SPR/SYMP/D/004 for landing and straigtening at shore workshop.
Corrective Action: Trainings Carried out of all crew, Briefly Explained the Reason. Vessel tried straightening the bar in E/R workshop, but same was not possible. Raised repair request SPR/SYMP/D/004 for landing and straigtening at shore workshop.
ReviewComments: The bent rod was straightened at DD & fitted in place on the 23rd June'2016.
ClosingNote: NMR Closed

IncidentDetails: Portable Blower got damaged in Main Engine NO.1unit crank case.
IncidentEventDetails: M/E Crank case inspection and scavenge space inspection were planed for that day.  After stopping the engine,M/E isolated,risk assesd and  Enclosed space entry check list made.  M/E Crank case doors opened and ventilated using portable blower.  Mean time M/E scavenge space inspection carried out.  While doing M/E scavenge space inspection M/E was turned using Turning Gear,  At that time the Portable blower lashing got loosened and trapped and damaged in M/E crank case.  M/E Turning operation immediatly stopped,damaged Portable blower removed from place.  M/E NO.1 unit crank case detailed inspection carried out for any broken parts from Portable blower.  M/E NO.1 unit found normal after detailed inspection.
ImmediateAction: M/E turning gear immediatly stopped,,damaged Portable blower removed from place.  M/E NO.1 unit crank case detailed inspection carried out for any broken parts from Portable blower.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No personel injury happend,  Portable blower got damaged.
DirectCause: Lashing was not properly done,  Portable blower was placed so close to the crankcase door.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Lashing should be properly done, Blower should not be placed so close to the machinery.
Corrective Action: Lashing should be properly done, Blower should not be placed so close to the machinery.
LessionLearnt: Importance of proper working procedures and proper monitoring
ClosingNote: The vessel was lucky to escape with only portable blower damage. The above could have been avoided by proper securing measures and proper engine room practices.

IncidentDetails: Due to passing of outbound Vessel that was drifted so closed and run full ahead speed while alongside at Port of Vancouver, the vessel violently moved/surged that leads to coming off the vessel  from the berth and damaged the brow and the railings of the loadicator.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1645 hrs - Bulk carrier drifted closed to the vessel and run full ahead speed. Mooring winches got loossed and smoke comimg out. C/Off, 2/Off, Bsn, 2 a/b and O/S hurriedly pull back the vessel to position.  At 1700 hrs - Vessel put back in position.
ImmediateAction: Brought back the vessel in position.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: None
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: Moorings to be tended to at all times. Sufficient personnel to be on deck during cargo watches for tending to the moorings.

IncidentDetails: On 14th Of October whilst the vessel was enroute from Singapore to Djbouti, in the Malacca Straits, vessel collided with General Cargo vessel Poseidon Triumph in the process of overtaking the other vessel at around 1616 Hrs. After the Incident, vessel stopped in the vicinity of the other vessel, rendered possible assistance and resumed her passage to Djbouti.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel on a liner trade, departed from Singapore after completion of cargo on 14th October 2015 for Djbouti. As per the plan vessel joined the west bound Singapore straits after dropping off Singapore Pilots at 1454 Hrs.   There after Master aligned the vessel in the general direction of flow of traffic, set the M.E on 45 RPM to do an intended speed of 13 knots with engines ready for immediate manouvering and handed over the con to Second Officer at 1512 Hrs.  At the time of handing over there were few vessels ahead which were following the traffic flow in the lane. Master instructed second officer to keep all the vessels on the Stbd. side as there was sufficient sea room available on the port side. Second Officer took over the watch and he had one AB and deck cadet on look out.  As instructed by Master, Second Officer overtook Tug and Tow Marcopolo on the port side at 1525 Hrs while steering a course of 2790(T) and speed of 12.1 Kts.  Thereafter he started overtaking the general cargo vessel Poseidon Triumph on her port side. Deck cadet left the Bridge at 1530 Hrs.   2/O watch was getting over at 1600hrs and was to be relieved by Chief Officer.   At 1555 Hrs the 1600 to 2000 Hrs watch AB came on bridge and took over the watch from the 1200 to 1600 hrs AB. Then he went to the Bridge wing, felt all vessels were clear and came inside the Bridge.   Cadet came back on Bridge at 1600 Hrs.  Chief Officer came on bridge at around 1609 Hrs and took over the watch from second officer. At that time, vessel Poseidon Triumph was still being overtaken and was at a distance of 2.1 cables abeam of the vessel. Own vessel was doing a speed of 13 Kts at a course of 2880(T) and Poseidon Triumph speed was 11.6 Knots and she was doing a course of 2840(T).  Second officer informed Chief Officer that the next course alteration is after 1 Nm and thereafter he started checking the chart for the next reporting information’s. Chief Officer looked at the S Band Radar, assumed that all vessels were clear and started altering course to 3140(T) when vessel was at a distance of 0.5 Nm from the point of alteration. He first altered from 2880(T) to 2900(T) and continued to alter course to 3060(T).   At that time he was alerted by the deck cadet on lookout that the vessel Poseidon Triumph on the stbd. qtr. of the own vessel was getting closer. C/O went out of the Bridge and he was not sure what the other vessel was doing. To avoid impact forward of accommodation, he altered course to port to 3030(T) from 3060(T)  At around 1616 Lt, vessel Poseidon Triumph made a contact with own vessel on the stbd quarter in Position 010 14.653’N, 1030 24.736’E.
ImmediateAction: Master who was in his cabin felt the vibration due to collision rushed to the Bridge and reduced the Speed to Dead Slow Ahead (at 1618 Lt.). He established contact with the vessel Poseidon Triumph and checked with the vessel the extent of damage that they had suffered and also checked with them if they are in need of any assistance.   Poseidon Triumph informed that the vessel’s Master was lying down unconscious and was not breathing and requested for immediate Medical assistance. Since Poseidon Triumph could not communicate directly with the VTIS due to poor VHF signal, Master of APL California relayed all communications for Poseidon Triumph to VTIS as well as to their agent in Singapore  At about 1640 Lt, Master contacted Office and informed about the collision incident and CCC was activated in the shore side.  Vessel was stopped at 1643 LT clear of other traffic and was holding position using her Thruster at a distance of approximately 1.3NM from Poseidon Triumph to render assistance if required.  In the meantime C/O, 2/O, C/E & 2/E who ascertained the extent of damage confirmed that there was no hull breach and threat of oil pollution.   Later after obtaining confirmation from Poseidon Triumph that she was structurally not in a peril and assistance was no longer required from own vessel, Master Informed VTIS and requested for their permission to resume the passage. After obtaining due permission at 1730 Lt Vessel resumed her passage after informing all relevant parties.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: As per attached Investigation Report
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As per attached Investigation Report
Corrective Action: As per attached Investigation Report
LessionLearnt: As per attached Investigation report
ReviewComments: As per attached Investigation report
ClosingNote: Investigation closed

IncidentDetails: V/L was discharging Kutubu blend crude oil port side along side.  Ship's port side railings near sopep Locker was bent/damaged due to sudden impact of shore gangway on it on 06th Jan'16/0545lt.  On investigation it was found that the incident took place due to sudden malfunction of shore gangway's automatic angle adjustment setting/mechanism as confirmed by the Loading master on board. Please note that there was a gap of  approximately 2 mtrs vertical distance between the ship side railings  and the bottom of the shore gangway. Due to the malfunction, the shore gangway behaved erratically and came all the way down and hit the ship side's railings under it.
