The possibility of shore g'way malfunctioning was not declared by the terminal /loading master during ship shore key safety meeting on 5th Jan'16.
IncidentEventDetails: Time-0545 LT:Vsl. Discharging at OVL-2 Terminal Singapore.A sudden malfunction of Shore gangway automatic angle adjustment setting/mechanism resulted in damaged ship side railing.
ImmediateAction: Confirmed no injury to person taking rounds on deck.  Immediately Loading master was informed who was in constant touch with Terminal.  Shore Ganway was removed from the Ship.  Damage accertained
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The ship side railing got bent due to sudden impact of shore gangway on it.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The Ship side railings to be cropped and new railings to be fabricated.
Corrective Action: The Ship side railings to be cropped and new railings to be fabricated.
LessionLearnt: Shore gangway while being placed on board lost its control & had an impact damage on to the ship railings creating a bend on the railings. The downward movement of the shore gangway was not able to be controlled as the safety device malfunctioned & there was no other means available to control the vertical down fall of the gangway to the ship side railings. Had any ship staff been in the path of the movement of the ladder , the impact would have caused serious damage / Ship staff to keep clear of any moving objects
ReviewComments: Bent railings repaired temporarily by straightening the pipes
ClosingNote: Incident report closed

IncidentDetails: -Vessel anchored at Fujairah B anchorage on 16/12/2015 with 8 shackles in the water.  On completion of bunkering, deslopping and de sludging, vessel commenced heaving up anchor on 17/12/2015.  2 power packs were started and set at 200 Bar pressure.  At 1815 Hrs C/O, Bosun, O/S and cadet went forward to prepare hawser to assist in picking up anchor.  At 1830 Hrs vsl commenced heaving up anchor.  After 4 shackles on deck, it was observed that the speed of hoisting slowed down and finally the windlass stopped.  After slacking and heaving on the cable a few times, the cable started coming up.  At 1900 Hrs, anchor was sighted clear and after housing the anchor, brake was fully closed, bow stopper was put down and locking and toggle pin were put in place. The clutch was then disengaged.   Fwd station reported to the bridge that brake is closed, bow stopper is down and clutch is disengaged.  Master ordered crew to be standby forward and wait for instructions to take lashings on both anchors.  Vsl started proceeding and in the mean time crew started securing the mooring hawser.  After the hawser was secured, suddenly, the port anchor cable started to run out.   It was observed that the bow stopper lifted and was repeatedly sitting and lifting as the cable was running out. Vsl at this time was doing about 4 Kts.  Bridge was immediately notified and vessel was stopped using asterm movement.  All crew cleared out of the area.  Once the cable stopped, clutch was engaged and cable slowly started coming up.   It was observed that the cable ran out full.  Hydraullic  pressure was increased to 220 Bar.  After heaving to 4 on deck, the cable slowed down again and finally stopped.  After slacking and heaving a few times, vsl was able to heave up the anchor fully.
IncidentEventDetails: 1815: Crew (C/O, Bosun, OS, Cadet) Standing By fwd for heaving up anchor. Preparing rope for additional support while heaving up anchor  1830: Vessel commenced heaving up port anchor.  1900: Anchor Sighted and Clear  1908:  Anchor Secured with Brake on, gear disengaged and bow stopper.  1918: Port anchor slipped at its own from hawse pipe, cable ran out full to bitter end. M/E was stopped with astern movements.  1924: Commenced heaving up port anchor again.  1948: Port anchor was heaved up fully ancd secured, vessel resumed passage.
ImmediateAction: Anchor was fully housed. LocKing pin of Bow stopper renewed. Additional lashings taken on the Anchor. Cable was made to rest on the bow stopper.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight buckling on the bitter end noticed.  Grooving on Bow stopper due to repeated lifting and sitting on the running cable.

IncidentDetails: There was a panic situation onboard, as fourth eng Ashwin John was not found in his cabin nor at the working places.
IncidentEventDetails: 0030: Ch Eng called 4th eng cabin he was not picking up the phone and kept on trying, but didnt find him in cabin or in mess room.   0045: Ch eng, 3 eng, mm and 2 eng searched for him in all the common places.  0105: Master was informed about the situation and master opened 4 eng cabin to check, with the master key. But found 4th Eng was not there in cabin.  0110: PA announcement was made and after few minutes of no response, Ben alarm was raised.   0113: People mustered and commenced search on different locations.   0118: Vessel started taking turn for search.   0120: Bosun and the forward team reported 4 eng was found laying in the forecastle on the bow platform. Ch officer and 2nd officer also proceeded forward and took 4th eng back to accommodation.   0125: Vessel continued the turn and completed 360' and resumed passage. RPM was reduced from the normal 67 to 57 and then increased back.
ImmediateAction: The fourth eng was just crying not in a situation to explain about the incident that time and hence have send him to cabin to take rest and motor man also was made to sleep in his cabin for safety reasons. It was confirmed that he was not under the influence on alcohol.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
DirectCause: 4E didnt report to anyone prior proceeding to forecastle deck. Alarm was not audible at forecastle.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 4E briefed on the consequences and importance of proper reporting.
Corrective Action: 4E briefed on the consequences and importance of proper reporting.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Importance of noticing stress patterns in each team member
ReviewComments: The 4th Eng showed clear signs of stress and was not able to handle the stress of job and the constant push from CE to do his job effectively.
ClosingNote: The top management should notice such signs of stress and fatigue and counsel the concerned officer as to the ways of handling stress. Senior staff must adopt a more polite demeanor when handling juniors

IncidentDetails: At the completion of berthing at SUNCOR terminal, Montreal, a fender was damaged during Mooring operations, as informed by the Loading Master.
IncidentEventDetails: 0412 Hrs LT: Commenced Mooring / FLA (Suncor Terminal)  0500 Hrs LT: All Fast Fwd & Aft (4+2), Vsl Stbdside Alongside  0500 Hrs LT: Pilot Away  0506 Hrs LT: Loading Master, Agent & Cargo Surveyor Onboard  0512 Hrs LT: Commenced Cargo Safety Meeting ( Vessel was informed about the damaged fender)  0618 Hrs LT: Completed Cargo Safety Meeting  0412: Commenced Mooring at SUNCOR terminal
ImmediateAction: After being informed by the Loading Master, the damaged fender was inspected and photographs taken with ships intrinsically safe camera and office was informed immediately .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Terminal fender damaged. No damage to vessel or its equipment
DirectCause: Non Compliance of Company procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Vessel Masters operating in Canadian region have been advised for following: I. Obtain berth information from the agents prior berthing.  II. Terminal booklet is to be studied if available, if not, same to be requested through the agents.   III. Any approach speed restrictions to be incorporated in the passage plan.  IV. Bridge team to record the vessels speed during the final approach to the berth.  V. To keep a close watch on Berth and fender condition during berthing and any shortcoming must be highlighted immediately to the Pilot and through him to the Loading Master.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out completely and all corrective action closed satisfactorily. INvestigation report attached

IncidentDetails: Sea Water ingress into Forecastle(Bosun Store) due to Bad Weather. Sea water spray into No.6 Group Starter Panel.Due to heavy Rolling the water is moving from Port to Stb side and Vice Versa and it is below the alarm level.
IncidentEventDetails: Sea Water ingress into Forecastle(Bosun Store) due to Bad Weather. Sea water spray into No.6 Group Starter Panel.Due to heavy Rolling the water is moving from Port to Stb side and Vice Versa and it is below the alarm level.
ImmediateAction: No 1 Windlass & Arc Welder Breaker replaced. No 2 Windlass Breaker Dismantled, cleaned, carbon removed, dried up and fitted back. Transformer for Fwd was dried up. Cable for Arc Welder 3 m changed. Cleaned the terminals.Checked Insulation. Tried out. Working Satisfactorily.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sea Water ingress into Forecastle(Bosun Store) due to Bad Weather. Sea water spray into No.6 Group Starter Panel.  Due to heavy Rolling the water is moving from Port to Stb side and Vice Versa and it is below the alarm level.
DirectCause: Sea Water ingress into Forecastle(Bosun Store). Sea water spray into No.6 Group Starter Panel. Rough Weather so no body could enter the Bosun Store and check. Also the panel is on the Floor level.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Switch off the Main Power for No.6 Group Starter Panel in ECR in Rough Weather. Fwd Crack on the hull side to be repaired. In Rough Weather pump out Bosun Store Bilges Frequently. Panel to be shifted 0.5 m above the Floor Level .
Corrective Action: Switch off the Main Power for No.6 Group Starter Panel in ECR in Rough Weather. Fwd Crack on the hull side to be repaired. In Rough Weather pump out Bosun Store Bilges Frequently. Panel to be shifted 0.5 m above the Floor Level .
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Morning at 0305hrs ME Fuel cam position abnormal alarm got activated. Checked and found ME Unit no.7 not firing and rack position struck. Tried to ease up the rack with lubrication and gentle push. Found the rack struck and unable to push. Gradually reduced the rpm and engine stopped. Lub oil pump stopped and unit no.7 fuel cam case opened for inspection. Found roller struck on top and not following the cam. Turning gear engaged and engine turned. Fuel pump inspection case opened and manually lubricated while turning the engine. Found the roller still in struck condition. Suspect plunger sticking (seizure).
IncidentEventDetails: ME running was at full load and maximum rpm. ME had been running at 93 rpm on 5th Jan 2016. For the last 1 year the maximum ME rpm used to be 90. In view of the Charterers speed requirement ME had to be run above 90 rpm for the last 2 loaded passages. Vsl faced rough weather throughout the loaded passage causing heavy rolling and pitching for last 19 days of this loaded passage.
ImmediateAction: Tried to ease up the rack with lubrication and gentle push. Found the rack struck and unable to push. Gradually reduced the rpm and engine stopped. Lub oil pump stopped and unit no.7 fuel cam case opened for inspection. Found roller struck on top and not following the cam. Turning gear engaged and engine turned. Fuel pump inspection case opened and manually lubricated while turning the engine. Found the roller still in struck condition. Suspect plunger sticking (seizure). ME stopped and Plunger & Barrel replaced with spare one.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: ME unit no.7 Fuel Pump Plunger and Barrel seized. Plunger and barrel renewed with spare on board.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fuel pump lubrication to be revisited. Manual lubrication frequency to be increased.
Corrective Action: Fuel pump lubrication to be revisited. Manual lubrication frequency to be increased.
ClosingNote: Plunger and barrel were renewed with spare ones and tried out satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: A section of shipside railings(about 5 meters)on port side forward of cargo manifold got damaged by the bunker barge M.T.CHEONG JIN NO.1 due to inadequate fenders during the bunkering operations  at Onsan anchorage. The fenders got caught between the our shipside railings during sudden rolling & pitching due to swell conditions.
IncidentEventDetails: 1706LT : Vessel anchored at Onsan anchorage  1745LT : Bunker barge fast on port side  1825LT : Hose connected  1830LT : Commenced bunkering HSFO  2040LT : Damage to shipside railings by bunker barge fender  2050LT : Completed bunkering  2215LT : Bunker hose disconnected  2224LT : Bunker barge casted off
ImmediateAction: Suspended bunkering operations. Additional coir fenders rigged near shipside railings to prevent further damage
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: About 5 meters of shipside railing damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fendering system of bunker barges to be closely monitored prior bunkering operations. All staff on board advised of the damage caused. All staff to be advised for report any defeciency noted in fendering system of barges/vessels during STS operation.
Corrective Action: Fendering system of bunker barges to be closely monitored prior bunkering operations. All staff on board advised of the damage caused. All staff to be advised for report any defeciency noted in fendering system of barges/vessels during STS operation.
LessionLearnt: The damage to railing would have occurred due  the fact that barge fenders got struck between ship railings. Fenders were of small size and Due to ship rolling, railings ot Damaged
ReviewComments: Direct cause  Improper size of  fenders of barges Ship staff did not consider the risk of fenders getting struct between railings Addl fender was not prepared by ship staff especially after knowing the situation Root Causes  Lack of compliance
ClosingNote: Investigation carried and all corrective actions as been closed satisfactorily

IncidentDetails: While un-berthing at Weipa, engine failed to respond after the last line was cast off requiring the vessel to re-berth.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was berthed at Lorim Point West, Weipa. Pilot boarded for departure 0612 hrs on 19 September 2015 hrs. Main engines were tested ahead and astern soon after pilot boarding. Vessel commenced casting off. Last line was on board at 0642 hrs on 19 September 2015. Main engine started when ‘dead slow ahead’ was given. At engine order Slow Ahead it was noticed No.2 unit high pressure pipe vibrating excessively. In order to prevent the high pressure pipe damage resulting in oil spray on hot surfaces in Engine Room, main engine was stopped to investigate the problem. Pilot ordered re-berthing and first line was ashore at 0707 hrs. Vessel was all fast and at 0748 hrs and finish with engine (FEW) was given at 0752 hrs. Pilot disembarked around this time.
ImmediateAction: Main engine was immediately stopped once excessive vibration of the high pressure pipe on unit 2 was observed. In discussion with the pilot the vessel was re-berthed and technical superintendent informed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Risk assessment and a copy of the relevant section of the maker’s manual shall be posted near the work place for reference during the task. This shall be addressed in the risk assessment as one of the points. PIC: Technical Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015 ? Fleet alert sent to all vessels in the fleet, sharing the incident details and advising vessels to refer and follow maker’s manuals for critical engine room tasks. Brief all engineers during pre-joining briefing. PIC: CMS Team / Respective Marine Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015 ? Technical department in the office will remind vessels to forward risk assessment of planned critical tasks on main engine requiring immobilization, as far as possible, 1 day prior commencement of the job for review. For unplanned tasks this shall be forwarded as early as is practical. PIC: Technical Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015
Corrective Action: Risk assessment and a copy of the relevant section of the maker's manual shall be posted near the work place for reference during the task. This shall be addressed in the risk assessment as one of the points. PIC: Technical Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015 ? Fleet alert sent to all vessels in the fleet, sharing the incident details and advising vessels to refer and follow maker's manuals for critical engine room tasks. Brief all engineers during pre-joining briefing. PIC: CMS Team / Respective Marine Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015 ? Technical department in the office will remind vessels to forward risk assessment of planned critical tasks on main engine requiring immobilization, as far as possible, 1 day prior commencement of the job for review. For unplanned tasks this shall be forwarded as early as is practical. PIC: Technical Superintendent Date: 20 Oct 2015
ClosingNote: Based on above investigation and suggested corrective action has been done, we are closing the incident report.

IncidentDetails: Damage to STBD windlass Gypsy wheel and Chain stopper
IncidentEventDetails: 05th December 2212 Lt ,when vessel was at Anchor, it was Observed that wind was gusting upto to 25 to 30kts and sometimes 35kts.because of which vessel decided to heave up anchor and start driffting.for that engine was prepared and with the help of engine vessel eased off the weight on the cable (very short stay and no weight on cable).After that gear was engaged and brake was opened,at that point Gypsy got cracked and damaged.
ImmediateAction: 1) Emergency Fwd Stations Called  2) ME continously used to reduce the weight on the cable  3) Brakes Fully tightened  4) Two persons kept fwd to continously monitor and report to bridge  5) Bridge Watch Level increased to 3  6) Extra lashings taken  7) Tecnical Superindentant informed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel STBD windlass gypsy wheel, chain counter and chain stopper got damaged.
DirectCause: 05th December 2212 Lt ,when vessel was at Anchor, it was Observed that wind was gusting upto to 25 to 30kts and sometimes 35kts.because of which vessel decided to heave up anchor and start driffting.for that engine was prepared and with the help of engine vessel eased off the weight on the cable (very short stay and no weight on cable).After that gear was engaged and brake was opened,at that point Gypsy got cracked and damged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Company's anchoring procedure to be complied with.
Corrective Action: Company's anchoring procedure to be complied with.
ReviewComments: Investigation carried out and all corrective actions closed satisfactorily.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out and all corrective actions closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: ME UNIT NOS 3 & 7 FUEL OIL PUMP ROLLER GUIDE FOUND STUCK AT TDC POSITION AND IT WAS NOT TOUCHING ON CAM PROFILE. AS A RESULT ME BOTH UNITS WERE NOT FIRING AND ME LOAD WAS UNEVEN ON THE SHAFT.
ImmediateAction: STOPPED ME.  CHECKED REVERSING MECHANISM MANUALY.  CAM CASE DOOR OPENED AND INSPECTED.  BOTH UNITS FUEL PUMPS CUT OFF MANUAL MECHANISM ACTIVATED AND PUT AGAIN INTO NORMAL POSITIN.  ME STARTED AND FOUND AFTER SOME TIMES, NORMAL AS BOTH UNITS STATED FIRING.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO DAMAGE / INJURY OCCURED
DirectCause: ALL ME UNITS FUEL PUMPS ARE PRESENTLY OVERDUE FOR OVERHAULING.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: AS SOON AS POSSIBLE, ALL ME FUEL PUMPS NEED TO BE OVERHAULED.
Corrective Action: AS SOON AS POSSIBLE, ALL ME FUEL PUMPS NEED TO BE OVERHAULED.
LessionLearnt: Importance of regular upkeep of fuel pump
ReviewComments: Overhauls schedules need to be followed particularly for propulsion and steering machinery, where a failure can have serious impact
ClosingNote: The incident highlights the importance of regular Planned maintenance and the need to follow overhauls schedules. The incident description has been circulated to all fleet vessels

IncidentDetails: At the time of lowering stbd life boat, the self lowering wire got entangled and became tight with weight and due to the weight the angle bars that hold the pulley of the self lowering wire got damaged
IncidentEventDetails: 0825: Permission granted by Port authorities to lower Lifeboats.  0910 : Stbd Lifeboat lowered and the guiding bar of self lowering wire got damaged
ImmediateAction: Guiding bar of self lowering wire removed and sent to E/R workshop for repairing.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Self lowering wire guiding Bar got damaged
DirectCause: Non Compliance of procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: •No proper checks carried out by crew prior lowering the lifebaot.
ReviewComments: Training to be carried out on procedure of checking the self lowering wire
ClosingNote: Guiding bar of self lowering wire to be checked prior every lowering of vessel  and Pre Joining Familiarization for Senior Officers to include briefing on this accident.

IncidentDetails: While rigging the pilot ladder one side of the manila rope parted.
IncidentEventDetails: 1345 LT : Completed Loading   1645 LT : BL figure agreed  1800 LT : Rigging pilot ladder (side rope parted while rigging)  2000 LT : Pilot on-board
ImmediateAction: The second pilot ladder was shifted and used.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While rigging the pilot ladder the manila side rope had parted.
DirectCause: Manila rope had become stiff and brittle due to sub-zero temperatures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pilot ladder to be removed from position and kept in store while in cold Wx. Same to be brought to deck only when required.
Corrective Action: Pilot ladder to be removed from position and kept in store while in cold Wx. Same to be brought to deck only when required.
ClosingNote: Incident report closed

IncidentDetails: While one of a crew member was lifting a small electrical motor from the floor, the motor slipped from the hand as soon as it was lifted. By his instincts, the crew member tried to catch the same during which the fingers got stuck between motor and floor plate resulting in a minor cut on the fingers.
IncidentEventDetails: FO Pfr feed pump motor was removed for overhaul.  Since the motor was small one and was easy to carry, it was decided to be taken to workshop for dismantling and overhauling.  While lifting the motor from floor, the above injury occurred
ImmediateAction: The crew member was given first aid and was dressed with ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The injury was a minor cut and first aid was sufficient.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repeated emphasis to be given in toolbox meetings, drills, safety meetings etc to educate the personnel regarding the dangers involved in doing such actions driven by instincts.
Corrective Action: Repeated emphasis to be given in toolbox meetings, drills, safety meetings etc to educate the personnel regarding the dangers involved in doing such actions driven by instincts.
LessionLearnt: Tool box meeting to be more elaborate where focus is given to the nature of the job and the circumstances present.Hazards to be identified during such meetings and the crew member made aware of them.Cautionary notices to be prominently posted at work sires making thre crew member aware of the hazards.
ClosingNote: Closed on 02/05/16

IncidentDetails: Vessel at Mailiao E7  Berth loading Gasoil. Mooring rope used as aft breast line parted due to excessive chaffing on the panama lead. The vessel was subjected to gusting winds of 25 to 35 kts winds persistently along with a tidal range of 4.0 m.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0830 Hrs, one stern line parted and immediately The parted rope was replaced with a spare rope. During the period, wind was gusting abt 30kts and vessel was surging.
ImmediateAction: The parted rope was replaced with a spare rope. Loading operation was not hampered due to this incident.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One rope damged
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Apply grease regularly at contact points of panama choke, maintain equal weight on all the mooring lines.
Corrective Action: Apply grease regularly at contact points of panama choke, maintain equal weight on all the mooring lines.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out and all corrective actions closed satisfactorily

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: During sailing after departing from Al Jubail to Rastanura, around 0430 hrs, sounded Fire alarm for Zone 9 (Boiler Platform). Noticed that LT expansion tank is overflowing. Due to this hot water was splashing out of tank and from its overflow pipe. Due to reduced cooling of HT water for Generator, it tripped on High HT Temp. leading to black out. Emcy Gen came on load immediately. HT temp had gone to 114 Deg C. Other gen was started to restore power but it also tripped on HT High temp. Air was purged from all vents of LT line.  Fresh water was taken into LT tank and all machineries and coolers purged.  It was noticed that in Both LT coolers water was not coming from vents. In between when generators were started to restore power and also to circulate HT water so its temp comes down, main air compressors were running to fill up air bottle. Suspected air leaking from air compresssors in LT system as air was coming from both LT cooler vents with pressure.
IncidentEventDetails: 0430 hrs LT : fire alarm from zone 9  0445 hrs LT : Blackout  0447 hrs LT : Emergency generator on load(auto)  0447 hrs LT - 1100 hrs LT : purging of  L/T system done water taken in the system  1100 hrs LT : MSB power restored  1100 hrs LT - 1200 hrs LT: vsl manouevred to keep clear of rastanurrah approach channel and other traffic  1200 hrs LT - 1430 hrs LT : Cause analysis and necessary repairs of No 1 MAC cooler tubes carried out  1430 hrs LT : Vsl resumed passage to proceed to rastanurrah anchorage as per port advice.
ImmediateAction: Isolated both compressors, one by one and noticed that No1 Main Air Compressor is creating problem.  Meanwhile #2 Generator was started as its HT temp was lower than other two generators, This helped overall HT temp to come down and normalcy in power from Main Generators obtained.  Removed valve and found O ring broken. Same replaced with spare and compressor tried out but again noticed air leaking into LT system. Air compressor stopped and decided to remove its cooler to check if tubes leaking. On removing noticed two tubes are fractured on bend.  Overall normalcy obtained on cooling temp,(LT & HT) and power and vessel continued its voyage to Rastanura at 1430 hrs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: THERE WAS A BLACK OUT AND THE VESSEL WAS STOPPED. LT EXPANSION TANK HAD OVERFLOWN AND WATER SEEPED INTO THE AUX BOILER #1 CONTROL PANEL DAMAGING THE 24 V TRANSFORMER. THE COMPLETE CONTROL PANEL HAD TO BE BLOWN DRY. THE HOUSEKEEPING OF THE ER WAS ALSO AFFECTED.
DirectCause: MAC#1 INTERCOOLER GAVE AWAY AND THE HIGH PRESSURE AIR GOT ACCESS TO THE LT CW SYSTEM CAUSING THE LT CW PUMPS TO LOOSE SUCTION AND THE OVERFLOW OF THE EXPANSION TANK.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MAC#2 INTERCOOLER IS RENEWED AND AWAITING THE SPARE FOR MAC#1. ER STAFF IS INSTRUCTED TO KEEP MONITORING THE MAC CW OUTLET SIGHT GLASS FOR AIR BUBBLES.
Corrective Action: MAC#2 INTERCOOLER IS RENEWED AND AWAITING THE SPARE FOR MAC#1. ER STAFF IS INSTRUCTED TO KEEP MONITORING THE MAC CW OUTLET SIGHT GLASS FOR AIR BUBBLES.
ClosingNote: CLOSED ON 06/07/2016

IncidentDetails: Bearing cover (P.no.513) of No.2 LO Purifier has been damaged while doing routine overhaul of its vertical shaft due to dropping onto the floor by the person handling it. The person lost his balance and tripped at the Purifier room entrance when his helmet slipped from head due to sweat and blocked his vision. The person narrowly escaped from injury by holding on to the bulkhead aside but dropped the part that was held in his hand.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. LO Purifier vertical shaft overhaul work was in progress.  2. Bowl discs and other parts were being cleaned near the space at the side of Sewage Treatment Plant [due to convenience].  3. The vertical shaft assy was removed and overhauled [bearings renewed] and was fitted back in the purifier.  4. After the above, other parts which were cleaned near the STP was brought to purifier room.  5. While bringing the Bearing cover (P.no.513), the person's helmet slipped from his head due to sweat and his vision got blinded moementarily and he tripped at the purifier room entrance step.  6. He immediately dropped the bearing cover from his hand and held the bulkhead and managed to not get hurt.  7. Though the person escaped from getting hurt, the bearing cover got cracked due to falling down.
ImmediateAction: The person advised to wash his face, dry up the sweat and wear the helmet more tightly and resume the job.    Since there is no new spare available for the bearing cover, informed Tech Supdt and planned to weld the cracked area with Cast iron rods and machine the same to dimensions and fit in the purifier. Expect the purifier to be operational with the above. But, since it is of cast iron, the same shall be confirmed after successful try out the purifier once it is boxed back.    A reqsn for required parts shall be raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The bearing cover that was cracked shall be welded on board using Cast iron rods and machine the same to dimensions and fit in the purifier. We expect the purifier to be operational with the above. But, since it is of cast iron, We shall try out the purifier once it is boxed back and revert with the condition.    Renewal of the part is necessary to be done at earliest opportunity. A PR shall be raised for the same and shall be intimated to Tech Supdt.
DirectCause: Main cause of this is the unsuitable PPE being donned.    Onboard, there are no suitable Earmuff assemblies available that can be fitted in to the helmet. So, the ear muffs are first worn and the helmet is worn above the earmuffs. There is genuine difficulty / hazard in wearing this combination because the helmet doesn't fit snugly on the head.    If the helmet is tightened in excess, it slips off the head more easily because its head attachment is not actually seating on the head in right position. Also, the ear muffs doesn't fit properly on ears and it doesn't seal the noise properly. For few persons, due to their head profile, it causes ear pain too after some time.    The chin strap prevents helmet to fall off from the head but doesn’t prevent its slipping from the head and falling in front of face thereby blinding the vision [as shown in attached pic].
Proposedcorrectiveactions: We have included for 10 nos. of Ear cup kits to be supplied in next stores reqsn that can be fitted in the helmets so that the above cause is addressed.
Corrective Action: We have included for 10 nos. of Ear cup kits to be supplied in next stores reqsn that can be fitted in the helmets so that the above cause is addressed.
LessionLearnt: PPE effectiveness and suitability to be evaluated when on reception of new store and on issuance to the concerned person on board  and is to be done periodically.
ClosingNote: Closed on 07/06/16

IncidentDetails: The main connection for the runner wire of hose handling crane was ruptured while proceeding to pick up the MOT Ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) The crew was proceeding to pick up the MOT ladder  2) The crane hook was removed  3) The crane was moved slightly when the high pressure hydraulic hose parted
ImmediateAction: The runner wire stopped and the winch power was tripped, cleaning up the adjoining areas commenced immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nil Injury/Damages
DirectCause: During operation Hydraulic hose ruptured due to wear and tear.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Stopped Crane operation and replaced hydraulic hose immediately. Requisition raised for the same for spare Hydraulic hose to be kept onboard for future use.
Corrective Action: Stopped Crane operation and replaced hydraulic hose immediately. Requisition raised for the same for spare Hydraulic hose to be kept onboard for future use.
ClosingNote: job closed

IncidentDetails: STARBOARD SIDE PILOT LADDER BOTTOM SPREADER DAMAGED BY PILOT BOAT WHILE COMING ALONGSIDE TO PICK UP PILOT.
IncidentEventDetails: The pilot boat at Bilbao while coming alongside to pick up pilot after departure came alongside and due to swell damaged the bottom spreader of the pilot ladder.
ImmediateAction: Reported to the Pilot about above incident
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bottom spreader of pilot ladder damaged completely by pilot boat during dropping of pilot after departure Bilbao, Spain
DirectCause: High swells outside breakwater, although good lee given to pilot boat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: This ladder to be replaced & tested or is to be replaced with a new certified ladder.
Corrective Action: This ladder to be replaced & tested or is to be replaced with a new certified ladder.
ClosingNote: Corrective action satisfactorily completed. Vessel to take adequate precaution when any craft approaches alongside & set the height of the ladder appropriately from water level. Ladders to be inspected before & after each use for any damages.

IncidentDetails: Pliot ladder damaged due to poor manoueuvring of supply boat.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel while on her laden passage from Al Shaheen to Map ta phut arrived OPL Galle to disembark the security guards. Weather during the time was moderate with wind force 3-4. As advised by the transfer boat, pilot ladder was rigged on the port side, 2 mtrs above water level for disembarkation. All arms and ammunition were landed to boat by heaving line. The boat while trying to manoeuvre towards the vessel happened to contact the vessel where the pilot ladder was rigged.The boat rammed the pilot ladder damaging 3rd rubber step and the first spreader of the pilot ladder . No personnel injuries reported.
ImmediateAction: Pilot ladder removed from use. Same being replaced with spare Pilot ladder
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 3rd rubber step and 1st spreader damaged
DirectCause: Improper manoeuvering of supply boat
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Height of pilot ladder to be adjusted depending on the prevailing weather condition to avoid any damage.
Corrective Action: Height of pilot ladder to be adjusted depending on the prevailing weather condition to avoid any damage.
ClosingNote: Cause identified & the damaged ladder has been replaced & tested for use.

IncidentDetails: While heaving up port anchor it fouled with another abandoned anchor chain.
IncidentEventDetails: 17-MAR-2016 /0630LT : Vessel anchored at Pengerang anchorage in position 01 18.60'N & 104 07.87'E as per instructions from port control using port anchor 6 shots on deck at a depth of 30.5metres  17-MAR-2016/0800LT : Pilot on Board for proceeding to berth and commenced heaving up port anchor.    0826LT: Observed own vessel anchor chain fouled with another abandoned anchor chain. Tried letting go the anchor several times to clear the foul but in vain.With the assistance of tugs which were alongside the vessel for berthing, mooring line was passed through the fouled chain and cleared of the vessel's anchor. At 1125LT vessel's anchor was clear and proceeded to berth.
ImmediateAction: Stopped heaving and let go the anchor  to clear the foul.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 0826LT: Observed own vessel anchor chain fouled with another abandoned anchor chain in position 01 18.60'N & 104 07.87'E. Tried letting go the anchor several times to clear the foul but in vain.With the assistance of tugs which were alongside the vessel for berthing, mooring line was passed through the fouled chain and cleared of the vessel's anchor. At 1125LT vessel's anchor was clear and proceeded to berth.
DirectCause: No navigational warning regarding regarding existence of foul at seabed on navigational chart.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident reported to Johor Port control through the pilot for existence of abandoned anchor chain at the location.(position 01 18.60'N & 104 07.87'E)
Corrective Action: Incident reported to Johor Port control through the pilot for existence of abandoned anchor chain at the location.(position 01 18.60'N & 104 07.87'E)
ClosingNote: Many thanks for bringing the above incident to out notice, We have taken the issue with port authorities to ensure to keep anchorage clear of foul anchors. In addition pls discuss the issue with all staffs during SCMM meetings and train them on the procedures how to clear the foul anchors.

IncidentDetails: 20.03.2016@ am hours:- x band radar stopped working(display freezed) by itself, continous sounding of alarm.
IncidentEventDetails: suspect motherboard and hardisk damage, require spares and tecnician for repair work.
ImmediateAction: main power switched off for 30 seconds and switched on again,still not working.  on the screen showing"DIGITAL RGB SEARCHING"  trouble shooting carried out as per mail and telephonic conversation with MARINE MECHNICS.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: X band RADAR is out of order, Awaiting RADAR tech to attend the same.
DirectCause: RADAR system motherboard and hardisc failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel presently sailing under dispensation from Flag valid untill 31st MArch 2016 as not able to get RADAR Tech in Karachi PAkistan.RADAR Tech to attend the vessel at the earliest opportunity

IncidentDetails: Framo hydraullic  main line flange O ring gave away, resulting in hydraullic oil spill on deck.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0800 hrs Deck crew reported hydraullic oil near accomodation stbd exit on upper deck.  on investigating it was found that it was coming from the framo hydraullic main line flange
ImmediateAction: confirmed all scuppers in place.  Feed pump stopped.  no oil went overboard.  Oil containment and cleaning started.  Started opening leaking flange for investigation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: High pressure O ring gave way near stbd side accomodation, Hydraulic oil leaked on to deck about 180-200 litrs.
DirectCause: Periodic wear out of Orings
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ship made Orings have been used as per FRAMO advise, Awaiting original spares to change the defective Orings.

IncidentDetails: Fishing vessel contacted to our vessel on Stbd side while vessel was safely anchored at Yosu OPL.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel's status :  Position: 34'09.02" N 128 05.86E   Heading : 238'  Wind    : S x SW   Force   : 4    Swell   : S X 1M  Depth : 85mtrs    1620- Fishing boat approach from own vessel's stbd quarter & contacted own vessel's stbd side abreast No 4 & No3 COT. No casualties/injuries to Fishing vessel's crew, or own vessel's crew. Fishing vessel was throughout seen under engine power. No sign of oil Pollution from either vessel.      1620:1745- Vessel & Fishing board assessed respective damages,     1647: Vessel tested her Engine upto Main engine blowing through & all found         satisfactory.  1710: Vessel initiated also Alcohol test to Duty officer & crew who were         present at the time of incident - Found crew sober.  1715:1720- Over side inspection completed for own vessel- found satisfactory.    1745:1800- Korean Coast guard vessel contacted vessel on incident over VHF & the initial communication stated that the coast guard contacted vessel also on Fishing boat's behalf. On asking vessel's Particulars & some details, Vessel cleared by Korean Coast guard & Korean Authority side. No physical boarding was made.  1850:1912- 4S WBT entered for assessing extent of visible damage from inside & found satisfactory.  1854:1912- Korean coast guard vessel approached own vessel's stbd side, took one length round & went away.  2000- Fishing boat & Korean coast guard vessel departed from own vessel's anchored area & set to respective sea passages at full speed.
ImmediateAction: VHF Communication establishment was not possible. Boat of Korean origin Number painted "K07-E021". Verbal communication through Officers on deck cross confirmed with boat - confirmed no crew injuries of any extent and are safe to sail at sea till next port under her own power.  Vessel & Fishing board assessed respective damages.  Communication with Korean Coastguard vessel.  Alcohol test to Duty officer & crew who were present at the time of incident
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Paint scratches on Stbd side hull above water line abreast No 4 & no 3 COT approx   Locations: 1) Between Stbd side Frame No : 62/63 & Strake No : 33/34    2) Between Stbd side Frame No : 60/61 & Strake No : 33/34
DirectCause: Basic navigation practices not adhered properly
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to keep better Anchor watch & pay special attention to passing traffic by acuqiring all approaching targets untill she is finally past & clear.
Corrective Action: Vessel to keep better Anchor watch & pay special attention to passing traffic by acuqiring all approaching targets untill she is finally past & clear.
ClosingNote: Job completed 10/06/16

IncidentDetails: Main Engine Breakdown
IncidentEventDetails: Main Engine Hydraulic Pressure too low
ImmediateAction: Trouble shoot the problem, found out the Main Hydraulic pipe, No. 1 & 2 HCU inlet pipe were cracked and no. 3 Accumulator Bladder has hole. Repaired the cracked on the Hydrauli pipe by welding. Renewed the Bladder.
DirectCause: The pipe were cracked due to vibration
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Nitrogen pressure to be checked at regular interval as per manual instruction. Tightness of hydraulic pipe bolts to be checked. Inspection door to be monitored at regular interval during sailing.
Corrective Action: To modify the design of Hydraulic high pressure pipe & install the warning device.
LessionLearnt: Regular check has to be carried out for nitrogen pressure in accumulator.  Hydraulic pipe tightness to checked at regular interval.
ClosingNote: Vessel advice to check the hyd line during sailing & every 1000 hrs nitrogen pressure test carried out for accumulator

IncidentDetails: VESSEL'S BERTHING CANCELLED AS PILOT BOARDING WAS CANCELLED
IncidentEventDetails: PILOT STATION INFORMED VESSEL AROUND 0800 LT THAT VESSEL WILL RECEIVE PILOT AT 1500 LT, AS VESSEL’S DRAFT WAS 14.85 MTRS AND THE DEEP DRAFT PILOT AREA WAS FOR VESSEL’S 15.0 MTRS DRAFT , IT WAS ASSUMED THAT THIS IS THE DESIGNATED PILOT BOARDING AREA, HOWEVER THE CLEAR INTENTIONS OF THE PILOT WAS NOT CONFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES.    25-JAN/0800 LT: PILOT STATION INFORMED VESSEL TO ARRIVE AT P/STN AT 1500 LT AND GIVEN PILOT BOARDING ARRANGEMENT.    25-JAN/1030 LT: PILOT STATION INFORMED VESSEL TO ARRIVE AT PILOT STATION AT 1500 LT. VESSEL CONFIRMED THE SCHEDULED ARRIVAL TIME TO THE PBG.    25-JAN/1030 LT : VESSEL INFORMED QINGDAO VTS THAT ETA TO DEEP DRAUGHT PILOT STATION IS 1500 LT AND REQUESTED PERMISSION TO HEAVE UP ANCHOR    25-JAN/1142 LT : ANCHOR AWEIGH AND PROCEEDING TO PILOT STATION     25-JAN/1350 LT : PILOT STATION RECONFIRMED WITH VESSEL ABOUT VESSEL'S ETA. VESSEL INFORMED ETA TO DEEP DRAUGHT PILOT STATION WILL BE 1500 LT. HOWEVER, PILOT STATION INFORMED THAT PILOT BOARDING IS AT INNER PILOT STATION (AT NO.2 PRECAUTIONARY AREA).    VESSEL INFORMED ETA TO INNER PILOT STATION IS 1545 LT AND PROCEEDING AT MAX SAFE SPEED.    25-JAN/1400 LT : PILOT STATION INFORMED THAT VESSEL WILL MISS THE PILOT AND TO ABORT APPROACH.    25-JAN/1410 LT : VESSEL INFORMED VTS ABOUT THE CANCELLATION AND TURNED BACK.
ImmediateAction: VESSEL ABORTED APPROACH, INFORMED VTS AND RE-ANCHORED AWAITING   BERTHING INSTRUCTIONS.  INFORMED MANAGERS, AND SUBMITTED STATEMENT OF FACTS TO HARBOUR MASTER.  VESSEL WAS RE-SCHEDULED TO BERTH ON 26-JAN.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: VESSEL'S BERTHING SCHEDULE CANCELLED FOR 24 HRS.  COMMERCIAL LOSS DUE TO OFF-HIRE FOR 24 HRS.
DirectCause: 1] ASSUMPTION THAT VESSEL WILL RECEIVE PILOT AT DEEP WATER PBG (WHICH IS FOR 15M DRAFT VESSEL'S) AS VESSEL'S STATIC DRAFT WAS 14.85M.   2] IMPROPER COMMUNICATION WITH PILOT STATION, THE CLEAR INTENTIONS OF THE PILOT WAS NOT CONFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES.  3]THE AVAILABLE DEPTH IN THE INBOUND PASSAGE WAS CONSIDERED NOT SAFE DUE TO THE PREVAILING WEATHER CONDITIONS (FORCE 5 WIND AND 2 MTRS SWELL). HENCE, IT WAS ASSUMED VESSEL WILL RECEIVE PILOT AT DEEP DRAFT PBG FOR SAFER NAVIGATION THROUGH DEEP WATER CHANNEL.   4]THE CONCERN OF AVAILABLE DEPTH FOR INBOUND PASSAGE ALTHOUGH IDENTIFIED, WAS NOT COMMUNICATED TO VESSEL'S AGENT DURING PRE-ARRIVAL INFO  RMATION EXCHANGE.  5]PRE-ARRIVAL COMMUNICATION NOT EFFECTIVE AS PILOT BOARDING AREA POSITION WAS NOT GIVEN BY AGENT AND NEITHER CONFIRMED BY VESSEL.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ] THOROUGH PLANNING AND PROPER EXECUTION OF PASSAGE PLAN.  2] IN CASE OF ANY CONCERN, SAME TO BE IMMEDIATELY CLARIFIED WITH LOCAL AGENTS, MANAGERS TO BE  INFORMED IF THE CONCERN IS NOT ADDRESSED SATISFACTORILY.  3] PROPER VHF WATCH KEEPING  / TWO WAY COMMUNICATION, REPEATING OTHER PARTIES INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY FOR CLEAR UNDERSTANDING OF BOTH PARTIES.
Corrective Action: ] THOROUGH PLANNING AND PROPER EXECUTION OF PASSAGE PLAN. 2] IN CASE OF ANY CONCERN, SAME TO BE IMMEDIATELY CLARIFIED WITH LOCAL AGENTS, MANAGERS TO BE INFORMED IF THE CONCERN IS NOT ADDRESSED SATISFACTORILY. 3] PROPER VHF WATCH KEEPING  / TWO WAY COMMUNICATION, REPEATING OTHER PARTIES INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY FOR CLEAR UNDERSTANDING OF BOTH PARTIES.
LessionLearnt: Effective two way communication to be established to ensure that the intentions are well understood and confirmed positively by parties at both end.This miscommunication has lead to a probable off hire charges & claims by the charterers , awaiting for further details from the Commercial team, Master and the Navigating Officer to ensure that the Critical positions are well marked on the chart after proper assessment & clarification of the position & same being communicated to all ship team members
ClosingNote: Incident report closed, as vessel completed the Port turn around time within the allowable C/P time , there was no claim from the Charterers

IncidentDetails: Soft rope given to Tug as tugline during casting off at Karachi Parted while vessel was being pulled out of the jetty.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel started unmooring at 1820 tug was made fast aft with one tugs line and one ship's line.   After unmmoring tug started pulling vessel out of berth.  Ships line given to tug parted from mid way.
ImmediateAction: Passed another ship's line immediately and ship came out safely from the berth.

IncidentDetails: The  vessel’s cash (1,518 us$) in master cabin (safe cabinet) and myself cash (300 us$, 3500 Baht) were robbery by thief. I went to sleep on 0030 lt.  After I wake up around 0530 lt. and found the cabin door not lock. I very surprised and after that I saw my wallet on the top of refrigerator and nothing inside. I was checked safe cabinet, fond only 10 us$ inside. The other items (watch, phone & laptop computer) not stolen. Probably thief is our crew due to at that time have no anybody inside (except Agent & cargo surveyor) and he know where’s the location of safe cabinet and not take any items except cash. Also 2/O was informed, his cash (400 us$, 76 Singapore dollars & 400 Bath) have stolen at same time.
IncidentEventDetails: All the time vessel have 3 crew were duty (at Gangway, round on deck & cargo operation) and all accommodation doors were locked from inside. Have one accommodation door was opened only at the gangway. After I was inspection every crew cabin with CE, CO & 2E have no found anything. I have investigating with crew and found 2 crew have behave suspiciously but I have no evident for make sure thief is them.
ImmediateAction: Warning to all crew for locked own cabin all times. Increase watch crew to frequently patrol in accommodation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Property Loss.
DirectCause: Further to the above cause analysis, Ships cash was not kept in the Ship's safe.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Issued maintaining a key log. Kept CTM and other importance documents in the ship’s safety deposit and both Digital and manual locks are engaged at all times.
LessionLearnt: Key log to be maintained. Master keys to be issued only to senior officers when there is a particular need. Ships cash to be placed only in the ship's safe.
ReviewComments: Near miss and review comments to be discussed during the next QHSE meeting. Crew to keep the cabins closed and their valuable stored safely.
ClosingNote: Incident report closed after investigation.

IncidentDetails: POB: 9th 0712hrs,  0727: Passing Buoy no.1&2,  0740: Tug "Guaroa" made fast fwd with Tug's line in port bow,  0750: First line (Aft Spring) ashore; Engine movements as per Telegraph printer, 0740-0825: Ahd & Astern movements given. Vessel moving astern and sideways. Ship's hull stbd side i.w.o. no.4 hold came in contact with damaged, protruding concrete jetty pillar and made indent- ation at 2 places of abt 3 cms deep, in an area of 50x50 cm at a draft of 5.00 mtrs.0842: All fast F&A. FWE. Let go tug, 0850: Pilot away 1050: Commencd ldg, 1230: Found water seepage from one of the dents, close to Fr.86, in no.4 hold.

IncidentDetails: Vessel alongside in port of Bangkok - cargo operations in progress - while moving the empty lashing gear flat rack ashore which was stowed on hatch cover broke, spilling gear bins on hatch cover.
IncidentEventDetails: Flat rack broke due to weakening of material over a period of time.
ImmediateAction: Bins were put back & rope lashings taken - slowly flat was lifted a little above the hatch cover  & transfered to another hatch cover where cargo operation was completed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Weakening of material over a period of time.
LessionLearnt: Close monitoring of Lashing gears and SDR's to be raised as and when applicable
ClosingNote: New gear bin supplied on 30th Dec 2015, and Old gear Bins were replaced. Old Gear Bins were discharged along with Garbage at Laemchabang on 20th Feb 2016

IncidentDetails: Damage incurred to Port Lifeboat remote control wire davit and brake handle lever.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at anchorage off dwarka. During lowering of Port side lifeboat, the remote control wire drum(support drum) did not turn, causing the balance weight block to get stuck in the remote control wire davit and in turn heaved on the brake handle lever.   Crew were unable to control the brake and lifeboat lowered completely and touched water.   Remote wire then completely unwound from support drum and lifeboat stowed back
ImmediateAction: Remote control wire completely unwound from support drum and lifeboat stowed back
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: brake handle lever bent  remote control wire davit bent and deformed
DirectCause: remote control wire on the support drum may not have been fully taut
Proposedcorrectiveactions: more vigilance when lowering lifeboat
Corrective Action: more vigilance when lowering lifeboat
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: During top up at about 1915 LT found that the cargo valves of 1,2,3 wings(Controllable and operated from CCR) sudenly stopped operating from CCR.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. At 0730 Hrs LT on 9Feb'16 the cargo valves of 1,2,3 wings(Controllable and operated from CCR) stopped operating from the CCR. The control solenoids for these valves are located in the FPK store. These valves were then operated from their respective solenoids and were operating normally thus ruling out the valves beings stuck.  2. The trouble shooting was then narrowed down to the electric part of the hydraulic valves. All connections were checked including the solenoid for receipt of signal however found that the solenoids in the FPK store were not receiving signal from the CCR.  3. Upon further checking the diagram found the problem with the fuse which went off. This is located behind the CCR panel. The fuse was renewed and these valves started to operate normally with full control from CCR.  4. During top up at about 1915 LT same day found that the valves suddenly stopped operating again. v/l had to stop loading in an emergency for avoiding spillage as the loading rate here is 75000 bbls, though the other slack tanks were opened up for dropping the level the 2p valve which was still open was getting cargo continuosly. Meantime the Pumpman was sent to the focle for shutting the tanks valve using the solenoid valve.The fuse was changed again and then these valve started to operate again.
ImmediateAction: 1) v/l had stopped loading in an emergency for avoiding spillage.  2) Other slack tanks were opened up for dropping the level.  3) Meantime Pumpman was sent to the f'castle store for shutting the tanks valve using the solenoid valve.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage & No Injury
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Maker to be informed & it should be inquired about frequent fuse damage.
Corrective Action: Maker to be informed & it should be inquired about frequent fuse damage.
ClosingNote: job completed

IncidentDetails: Head line wire (outboard ) parted. No injuries occured and the event was immediately reported to the terminal.No other sign of damage observed on any other line.The damaged wire was reeved in and was observed to have been parted at the eye over the point where it connects to the Mandal shackle.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0510 LT on 09th march 2016, the Head line wire (outboard ) parted. No injuries occured and the event was immediately reported to the terminal. The terminal advised to re-pass the head line wire.All mooring lines were checked. No other sign of damage observed on any other line.The damaged wire was reeved in and was observed to have been parted at the eye over the point where it connects to the Mandal shackle.  Vessel passed the Head line wire again at 0618 LT after turning it end to end.  Weather conditions : The wind started changing from Southerly to Northerly winds with wind force 7 , gusting upto 8. The swell was attributed to be 0.5-0.7 m with gradually increasing.
ImmediateAction: All mooring lines were checked. No other sign of damage observed on any other line.The damaged wire was reeved in & Vessel passed the Head line wire again at 0618 LT after turning it end to end.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Head line wire (outboard ) parted.
DirectCause: Cause : Adverse weather conditions caused undue strain on the already long Head line ( 140-150 meters outside the ship). The adverse weather conditions along with lot of weight of the hanging wire forced the wire to give way.  Weather conditions : The wind started changing from Southerly to Northerly winds with wind force 7 , gusting upto 8. The swell was attributed to be 0.5-0.7 m with gradually increasing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting carried out with all the crew and briefed about the incident. The cause explained.
Corrective Action: Meeting carried out with all the crew and briefed about the incident. The cause explained.
LessionLearnt: Ship staff to follow PMS routines diligently for the proper checks should be made to access the exact condition of mooring wires and their eyes at regular interval and as required should discard a wire which shows deterioration or damage due to rust from inner cores.
ClosingNote: A detailed briefing of deck team was conducted for this matter addressing all mooring safety matters for entire deck team.Job completed-04/04/16

IncidentDetails: While carrying out Stowaway search prior departure Takoradi port, crew found 3 unauthorized persons hiding inside rane mast No 2, above hoisting drum platform. All ship's staff and SSO were alerted and when they were being escorted down by ship's staff, without giving any warning of their intent, they rushed down and before vessel staff could stop them, they jumped into the water.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0430 hrs lt- Ship commenced stoway search as per checklists SF-08, at around 0500 hrs lt, while conducting search in Crane mast no 2, crew found 3 unauthorized persons sitting above Hoisting drum platform . On alerting SSO and other ships crew, when they were being escorted down by ship's staff, without giving any warning of their intent, they rushed down and before vessel staff could stop them, they jumped into the water, which was informed to port authorities. 0600 hrs: Port police boarded the vessel and carried out stowaway search along with ships' staff. 0630 hrs: search completed and two bags which were found returned/handedover to port police.
ImmediateAction: Informed Port authorities on sighting them.  informed CSO-Capt.D.S.Bakshi
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While carrying out Stowaway search prior departure Takoradi port, crew found 3 unauthorized persons hiding inside rane mast No 2, above hoisting drum platform. On alerting SSO and other ships crew, when they were being escorted down by ship's staff, without giving any warning of their intent, they rushed down and before vessel staff could stop them, they jumped into the water. No ships crew  were injured and no equipement was damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: More frequent security rounds to be made on deck and accomodation to avoid this incident to reoccur.
Corrective Action: More frequent security rounds to be made on deck and accomodation to avoid this incident to reoccur.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: to be discussed in safety meeting

IncidentDetails: Lifeboat portside exhaust & cooling water common pipe leakage
IncidentEventDetails: During normal 3 monthly routine of lifeboat lowering and manoeuvring, on starting the lifeboat engine in water the exhaust/cooling water line was leaking profuesely into the boat (fumes and water). On further inspection found the pipe inside the lagging has been wasted.
ImmediateAction: Boat was immediately picked up and stowed back. Detailed inspection done and found the exhaust pipe completely wasted. New pipe fabricated and replaced.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Exhaust pipe due to corrosion broke into two pieces inside the lagging resulting in leaking of gas/water into the lifevoat while trying out when waterbourne.
DirectCause: Corrosion of exhaust pipe inside the lagging.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Old pipe removed and new pipe fabricated, replaced and tried out.
Corrective Action: Old pipe removed and new pipe fabricated, replaced and tried out.
ClosingNote: Pipe renewed,

IncidentDetails: After frying eggs for breakfast, C/ck Zolina transfering the hot used oil in the container when the handle of frying pan rotated and pour the hot oil into his left wrist.
ImmediateAction: Immediately wash with fresh cold water and apply burn ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First degree burn on left wrist of  C/ck Zolina caused by hot cooking oil.
DirectCause: Deffective utensiles used.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All utensils handles to be checked prior to use for their firm grip and tightness
Corrective Action: All utensils handles to be checked prior to use for their firm grip and tightness
ClosingNote: to be discussed in next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: During discharging operations at Chennai No1 COP suction side pipe got over pressurised and leaked from the seperator flange. Same noticed during pump room round that drops falling from seperator flange (one used for maintenance)
IncidentEventDetails: No.1 COP was being used for educting the tanks during final stages of cargo work. Due high bearing temperature we shifted educting via no.2 COP. 0225 : No.1 COP stopped. 0230 : No.2 COP started for educting drive (personnel available in p/room that time). 0305 : During pump room round by pumpman found No.1 COP seperator flange leaking.
ImmediateAction: No.1 line suction side valves opened & pressure neutralised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No equipment or pipeline damage occurrred.
DirectCause: 1. As line was left closed for abt 30 minutes whilst educting other group / no.2 pump; this no.1 line got overpressuried due oil expansion within pipeline.   2. Vessel arrived 14th Apr PM and was very busy thereafter. There were multiple visits by authorities, FSI, Vetting etc which kept Ch.Off engaged. Although rest was taken but fatque cannot be ruled out. Also this occurred during early AM hrs.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Internal expansion in pipelines cannot be visually seen and hence expansion of cargo to always be considered when opening or closing lines. Breathing or release of pressure to be cosidered in all actions.
Corrective Action: Internal expansion in pipelines cannot be visually seen and hence expansion of cargo to always be considered when opening or closing lines. Breathing or release of pressure to be cosidered in all actions.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Importance of the correct line of the cargo system. Expansion in the system can occur in short period of time.
ReviewComments: Training to be carried  out with the ship board team with regard to the cargo systems
ClosingNote: Good Planning ad monitoring of Cargo operations to be carried out,

IncidentDetails: During discharging in Tuban, suspected leakage of Slop (S) COT valve 171.
IncidentEventDetails: Possible damaged seat ring & material failure.
ImmediateAction: At Singapore Anchorage, Slop (S) COT gasfreed & valve inspected.  Seat ring of Slop (S) COT valve 171 found damaged.  Renewed the seat ring of Slop (S) valve.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Seat ring of Slop Stbd main suction valve damaged
DirectCause: Material Failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Seat ring to be replaced
Corrective Action: Seat ring to be replaced
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: 01. Close monitoring of the non-nominated Cargo tank space ullage/ sounding for early identification of innage/outage 02. During replacement of the valve located in the pump room , the Slop-Stbd had to be gas freed as there was continuous emanation of gases being sucked to the Pump room by the P/R blower
ReviewComments: Alert ship staff has identified the defect well in time before a potential cargo contamination has occurred, Vessel carried out the job at Singapore EOPL while awaiting for berthing instructions, integrity status needs to be verified during next loading & discharge ops
ClosingNote: Seat rings for the High suction Slop-Stbd valve located in the Pump room was replaced with spare , Integrity checked during loading & found in order

IncidentDetails: FWD  LIFERAFT (6 PERSON) WASHED OFF FROM SECURED POSITION AND GOT INFLATED ON MAIN DECK NEAR 1P COT.
IncidentEventDetails: 1] VESSEL WAS EXPERIENCING VERY ROUGH SEAS DUE TO PASSING LOW FRONT WITH LONG SWELL OF 6 M AND WIND FORCE 8/9.  2] AT 1530 LT, DUTY OFFICER SPOTTED FWD LIFERAFT CARRIED FROM ITS SECURED POSITION TO 1P COW MACHINE BY SHIPPED SEA ON FWD DECK.  3] SOON AFTER, LIFERAFT  INFLATED AS THE PAINTER WAS STILL ATTACHED TO THE STRONG POINT OF SECURING CRADLE.
ImmediateAction: 1] CREW PROCEEDED FORWARD AFTER CARRYING OUT RISK ASSESSMENT.  2] INFLATED LIFERAFT ALONGWITH ITS SHELLS AND EQUIPMENT RETRIEVED FROM FWD DECK AND SECURED INSIDE STORE.  3] CONFIRMED NO PARTS OF LIFERAFT IS WASHED TO SEA.  4] INFORMED OFFICE REGARDING THE DAMAGE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FWD LIFERAFT INFLATED AND OUT OF SERVICE, NEW LIFERAFT REQUIRED TO BE SUPPLIED AT NEXT PORT.
DirectCause: 1] VERY ROUGH SEA AND HEAVY SWELL, WAVE HEIGHT OF 6M . VESSEL WAS PITCHING HEAVILY AND A RESULT HEAVY SEAS WERE SHIPPED ON FWD DECK.  2] LIFERAFT LASHING PARTED DUE TO LARGE WAVE AND LIFE RAFT WAS CARRIED TO 1P. AS THE PAINTER WAS STILL SECURED, THE LIFERAFT INFLATED BY ITS OWN AFTER PAINTER WAS STRETCHED.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1] REQUISITION RAISED FOR NEW LIFERAFT  2] IN CASE OF SEVERE WEATHER IS EXPECTED,THOROUGH RISK ASSESSMENT TO CARRY OUT AND ADDITIONAL LASHINGS IF REQUIRED TO BE TAKEN.  3] RISK ASSESSMENT CARRIED OUT FOR CREW WORKING ON DECK WITH FWD LIFERAFT BEING INOPERATIVE.
Corrective Action: 1] REQUISITION RAISED FOR NEW LIFERAFT 2] IN CASE OF SEVERE WEATHER IS EXPECTED,THOROUGH RISK ASSESSMENT TO CARRY OUT AND ADDITIONAL LASHINGS IF REQUIRED TO BE TAKEN. 3] RISK ASSESSMENT CARRIED OUT FOR CREW WORKING ON DECK WITH FWD LIFERAFT BEING INOPERATIVE.
LessionLearnt: Lashings of all equipment to be checked & replaced as required. Prior entering an area of expected heavy weather , all lashings to be double checked to ensure all lashings are effectively in place. Additional lashings not to be taken for Life rafts & lifebuoys , but well secured with the original securing arrangements
ClosingNote: NMR Closed

IncidentEventDetails: A pilot ladder found damage during internal audit inspection. Suspected that the rubber damage during loading in Punta Lobitos last 14 April 2016. While the crack on spreader maybe due to weather exposure and builds for long time used.
ImmediateAction: Reported  to office when found out and raised requisition for immediate supply on next port.

Corrective Action: Proper securing of equipment
