IncidentDetails: Vessel was riding to port anchor with 8 shackles in water. Depth of water was 35 mtrs.  Anchor position and cable were checked frequently since late PM of 25/04/16.  At 0750 lt, it was decided to heave up the port anchor and proceed to sea as the swell height was increasing and strain was coming on the cable   Main Engines were on 15 mins notice.  At 0805 lt, ME was tried out and 0806 lt Stand by was given.  0808 lt: Vessel commenced heaving port anchor.  The cable was not coming in easily and Main Engines had to be used to release the strain on the cable.  As soon as the 6th shackle was heaved, Chief Officer reported that the chain was slipping automatically even while the windlass was in gear. At this time vessel encountered at 3 mtr swell.  We then applied the brake on the cable and tried to operate the port windlass. But there was no response either in heave or lower motions.  Chief Engineer was called to inspect the windlass. During the course of the inspections, it was noticed that excess strain was coming on the cable and the brake was not holding and cable was freely slipping away.  Already then 9 1/2 shackles had run out from the spurling pipe.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was riding to port anchor with 8 shackles in water. Depth of water was 35 mtrs.  Anchor position and cable were checked frequently since late PM of 25/04/16.  At 0750 lt, it was decided to heave up the port anchor and proceed to sea as the swell height was increasing and strain was coming on the cable   Main Engines were on 15 mins notice.  At 0805 lt, ME was tried out and 0806 lt Stand by was given.  0808 lt: Vessel commenced heaving port anchor.  The cable was not coming in easily and Main Engines had to be used to release the strain on the cable.  As soon as the 6th shackle was heaved, Chief Officer reported that the chain was slipping automatically even while the windlass was in gear. At this time vessel encountered at 3 mtr swell.  We then applied the brake on the cable and tried to operate the port windlass. But there was no response either in heave or lower motions.  Chief Engineer was called to inspect the windlass. During the course of the inspections, it was noticed that excess strain was coming on the cable and the brake was not holding and cable was freely slipping away.  Already then 9 1/2 shackles had run out from the spurling pipe.   0840 lt: Anchor cable was slipped from bitter end in position 38 37.311 S, 058 31.740 W
ImmediateAction: With the port windlass inoperative and the brake not holding, it was decided to slip the port anchor cable as a safety measure in order to avoid any further damage to the port windlass and the vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The cross coupling between the striping valve assembly and hyd motor crankshaft broken.    Upon further inspections found hairline cracks on both  cranksaft and striping valve coupling area.
DirectCause: Vessel was unable to heave the port side anchor in inclement weather and hence same had to be slipped off from the bitter end at Nechochea anchorage. All efforts were made to procure a new cable and anchor at port of Nechochea however same could not be arranged due to lead time
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Procure a new hydraulic motor, anchor and cable to be received and installed at the next port of call (Port Klang or SIngapore) in 1st Week of June '16.A detailed investigation into this incident will be conducted.Corrective actions arising out the incident investigation will be implemented and leanings conveyed to all vessels in the fleet.Navigation Audit to be done on board to ascertain the quality of compliance with Navigation Practices and procedures.
Corrective Action: Procure a new hydraulic motor, anchor and cable to be received and installed at the next port of call (Port Klang or SIngapore) in 1st Week of June '16.A detailed investigation into this incident will be conducted.Corrective actions arising out the incident investigation will be implemented and leanings conveyed to all vessels in the fleet.Navigation Audit to be done on board to ascertain the quality of compliance with Navigation Practices and procedures.
LessionLearnt: • Weather conditions (near gale force winds/over 3 mtrs swell) caused excessive strain on the anchor cable, when it was decided to heave up the anchor on 26th April AM. Considering the extent of yaw/pitching/shipping sprays on forecastle deck in the prevailing weather conditions, it would have been extremely challenging to coordinate vessel engine movement with feedback from forecastle deck to reduce strain on the cable.
ReviewComments: Port side windlass were used to heave up the anchor with heavy strain on cable the windlass motor is not designed to perform in that condition due to which it gave away.
ClosingNote: 1. Inadequate execution of PSP(Policies/Standards/Procedures). Vessel remained at anchor in rough weather condition. In accordance with the procedures given in the navigation manual the vessel is required to heave the anchor if the wind force exceeds 6.  2. Inadequate actions to the weather warnings:- Bridge team failed to positively act on the information available  The bridge team was monitoring weather through AWT/EGC weather forecast /coastal warning on Navtex. Forecast was wind increasing to Beaufort 6 and above from 25th April PM. Information in sailing direction warns of steep swell developing in persistent S/SW wind conditions. Information from the local agent and port control was available that port was closed was due to swell and wind condition on 25th Afternoon.

IncidentDetails: While cutting salads , the G.S. got a minor cut on his finger.
ImmediateAction: First aid was given to the G.S.
DirectCause: finger caps were not worn
Proposedcorrectiveactions: to ensure the finger caps are worn during cutting of vegetables.
Corrective Action: Adequate Hazard Identification at Toolbox Meeting Stage for all tasks to be done diligently. This injury case to be discussed with all staff during next Safety Meeting and the potential of having greater injuries should be brought to light to all crew.
LessionLearnt: Finger Caps or Cut Resistant Gloves to be worn when handling Knives to prevent recurrence. Presence of mind is required, even when carrying out the seemingly most routine jobs and a slight lack of concentration can result in an Injury.
ReviewComments: On board where professional medical help may not be immediate available, it is important to avoid any minor injuries as same can easily escalate into serious proportions.
ClosingNote: Finger Caps or Cut Resistant Gloves to be worn when handling Knives to prevent recurrence. Presence of mind is required, even when carrying out the seemingly most routine jobs and a slight lack of concentration can result in an Injury. On board where professional medical help may not be immediate available, it is important to avoid any minor injuries as same can easily escalate into serious proportions.

IncidentDetails: Rainwater from scupper fell into Sludge barge ' MAIN VIII'  resulting in overflow of barge spill tray
IncidentEventDetails: At 0700 hrs LT, crew observed very little oil sheen in the water alongside the  vessel. At this time sludge barge (M.T. Main VIII) was made fast alongside  as vessel was discharging sludge. Immediately sludge discharge was stopped  for investigation. Upon investigation, vessel observed no leakages in the  hose and also no traces of oil on vessel's deck. It was reported by the  barge that vessel's crew opened the ship's scrupper to remove rain water  which inadvertently fell on sludge barge's small spill tray. Also it was  reported by the barge crew that some Oil splashed from this spill tray and a  small amount of oil ( about 2 ltrs ) went overboard from the barge's deck  (which we suspect was opened as well by the barge crew for draining rain  water on barge's deck at that point of time). Also please note that it has  been raining frequently with heavy showers at times for the last few days  which is continuing even now and rain water is being discharged periodically  overboard by ship's crew after obtaining permission from the terminal.
ImmediateAction: Scrupper was closed immediately.At 0720 hrs, Port of Amsterdam authorities were informed over VHF Ch-14 and also the local agents regarding sighting of oil sheen near ship's side stbd  side. As Port Authorities patrol boat was nearby, immediately they replied  and came alongside (0725 Hrs LT) and investigated both on the barge and  boarded the vessel. The port authorities inspector took rounds on deck  alongwith Master and Ch/Eng and was convinced that no oil traces were on deck  and the oil sheen which was on the water was due to spillage from the barge.  However, they assumed that it happened accidentally due to water from the  scrupper that fell on the barge's spill tray.  Port authorities issued vessel with a verbal warning and may also send an  email warning to prevent this kind of occurance in future. He asked the vessel crew to  refrain from clearing rain water from the stbd side as long as the barge is  alongside at that side. Till then he advised vessel to discharge rain water  from port side (dock side) using portable pump if required.  Master enquired with the Port Authoritity inspector if any further action is  needed from his side in respect of reporting to any other parties including  my P & I club. However, Port authority inspector spoke to his office and assured that since this  is a very small spill, no further action is needed from vessel's side.  At 0800 hrs LT, port authorities inspector disembarked. 0805 hrs LT, DPA was  informed about this incidence. Traning / instructions by Master in presence of the Port Authority Inspector was carried out  to all deck officers and crew.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minorspill (about 2 litres) from the barge deck
DirectCause: Carelssness from deck crew
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training held for all deck crew and officers
Corrective Action: Training held for all deck crew and officers
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Care to be taken & seamanship to be given importance in all task performed on board.

IncidentDetails: On 19.05.2016 during ballast operation at Rotterdam on completion of use Cool SW and  Ballast pump, when closing the pump Sea Suction valve,  found hand wheel was keep rotating freely. Then valve gear mechanism opened found valve stem in broken condition.Valve used to be hard to operate. Actually Butterfly valve  Stem was broken at the time of opening.
IncidentEventDetails: Due to corrosion at stem and gland area of valve becomes hard to open and close.  Continuous use of vavle with that condition finally stem broken.
ImmediateAction: Checked condition of Stby Ballast pump found in good condition. Informed Deck officesr. Requisition raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
DirectCause: when closing the pump Sea Suction valve,  found hand wheel was keep rotating freely. Then valve gear mechanism opened found valve stem in broken condition.Valve used to be hard to operate. Actually Butterfly valve  Stem was broken at the time of opening.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ease up all SW valves regularly and greasing of gear mechnisam.
Corrective Action: Ease up all SW valves regularly and greasing of gear mechnisam.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Requisition connected and valve renewed. Corrective action to be discussed in safety meeting.
ClosingNote: Requisition connected and valve renewed. Corrective action to be discussed in safety meeting

IncidentDetails: Vesseel was deparing kandla Port. A tug was along side for assistance. While tug was pushing during unberthingit came against port side railing in way of hold no 3. The railings were bent / broken as tug's fenders were higher than vessel's main deck.
IncidentEventDetails: 2212-Pilot On Board (POB)  2212-Stand by Engine (SBE)  2212-Commenced Single Up Fwd & Aft.  2215-Tug was pushing from Port side mid ship area it found railing for the ship side got bent and          broken.  2216-Forward station crew inform the bridge and the damage.  2217-Pilot was informed about the circumstances to change location of the tug push.  2217-Tug change the location of the push to Container out board pedestal.  2220-Fwd Single up.
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed the pilot who instructed the tug to push at ship side container pedastal and avoid coming close to railings.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Top railing 3 meter bend.  Second top railing 2 meter broken.
DirectCause: Oversight by tug's crew.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel's crew to be vigilent when tug with high freeboard is being used.
Corrective Action: Vessel's crew to be vigilent when tug with high freeboard is being used.
ReviewComments: 1. Details of Tugs to be discussed during Master Pilot exchange. 2. OOW to alert Master if there is potential of damage due to the tug. 3.Request for different Tug if possible.
ClosingNote: Closed.

IncidentDetails: A section of railings on stbd side near forward spring winch got damaged while mooring at Antwerp locks. There was no fenders provided inside the locks. As such vessel rigged portable fenders to prevent any contact damage while mooring inside the locks. The portable fender forward got jammed while positioning the vessel inside locks and the rope attached to the fender parted causing damage to the railings. PFA the snaps. Temporary repairs in progress. ADI report initiated on Shippalm.
IncidentEventDetails: 28th May 2016  0800LT : Crew on stations for mooring at Antwerp locks  0804LT : Potable fenders rigged on stbd side at beginning of parallel body  0810LT : Vessel arrives Antwerp locks inbound  0814LT : Tugs fast fwd and aft  0828LT : Bow entered locks to go stbd side alongside inside the locks  0835LT : Forward portbale fender broke off causing damage to stbd shipside railings  0906LT : Vessel all fast at Antwerp locks
ImmediateAction: Warned all crew to keep clear of damaged railings
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A section of ship side railings on stbd side near forward spring winch got damaged while mooring at Antwerp locks.    There was no fenders provided inside the locks. Vessel rigged portable fenders to prevent any contact damage while mooring inside the locks. The portable fender forward got jammed while positioning the vessel inside the locks and the rope attached to the fender parted causing damage to the railings.   The fender was positioned over the handrails. Fairleads were available forward and aft of the parallel body position. The mooring team forward judged the best fender position as shown in picture to avoid any contact damage.
DirectCause: Incorrect mooring procedures - Portable fender was rigged over the shipside railings instead of the fairleads.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be briefed of correct mooring procedures as given in chapter 9 of HSM Manual and Ch-26 of COSWP. Temporary cold work to be done to the damaged part of the railings. Permanent work to be done during next drydock.
Corrective Action: All crew to be briefed of correct mooring procedures as given in chapter 9 of HSM Manual and Ch-26 of COSWP. Temporary cold work to be done to the damaged part of the railings. Permanent work to be done during next drydock.
LessionLearnt: In correct fender rigging procedures
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out. Correct fender procedures explained to all crew.

IncidentDetails: During pasaage from Leamchabang to Singapore on 03rd Nov 2015 0533 UTC G/E No.4 tripped on High CFW temp, causing balckout and Main Engine stoppage.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0533 UTC No.4 G/E CFW high temperature alarm and trip occurred causing blacckout and main engine stoppage. Emergency Generator immediately came on load in AUTO Mode. STBY G/E automatically started and came on load. All engine room machineries reset and vessel resumed course at 0539 UTC.  On Investigation found that the ferrul connecting the air line from CFW temperature controller to the CFW temperature controlling valve gave way casuing a break in the signal from the controller which inturn lead to malfunction of the control valve and high temperature trip
ImmediateAction: Main engine controls taken to engine room.   Informed duty officer the cause of blackout.  All machineries reset and voyage resumed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: None
DirectCause: copper pipe sheered off and shut control air supply to positioner,hence causing cooling water high temperature and sequentially tripping of generator.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All connections for other generators checked,and couplings and connections renewed as nessacary.
Corrective Action: All connections for other generators checked,and couplings and connections renewed as nessacary.
LessionLearnt: Importance of proper checks
ClosingNote: Vessel to carry out proper PMS checks on equipment and associated piping with special attention to connection and joints

IncidentDetails: On  29th May, 2016 ROUND 1630H LT., underway from Glomfjord, Norway to Cartagena, Colombia, vessel encountered adverse weather, with  gale wind force  7/8 ( WNW ), caused vessel excessive heavy rolling , pitching, and strong vibrations forced the MOB bracket clamps were detached and unnoticed MOB smoke cignal trigered.
IncidentEventDetails: On  29th May, 2016 ROUND 1630H LT., underway from Glomfjord, Norway to Cartagena, Colombia, vessel encountered adverse weather, with  gale wind force  7/8 ( WNW ), caused vessel excessive heavy rolling , pitching, and strong vibrations forced the MOB bracket clamps were detached and unnoticed MOB smoke cignal trigered.
ImmediateAction: Tied up seizing tie wire and replaced temporary spare self igniting light and smoke signal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On  29th May, 2016 ROUND 1630H LT., underway from Glomfjord, Norway to Cartagena, Colombia, vessel encountered adverse weather, with  gale wind force  7/8 ( WNW ), caused vessel excessive heavy rolling , pitching, and strong vibrations forced the MOB bracket clamps were detached and unnoticed MOB smoke cignal trigered.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:  Poor securing clamps, easily to detached by vibrations and strong winds. Indirect Cause: Inadequate knowledge of ship procedures, during heavy weather conditions. Root Cause : Poor securing clamps design.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Comply heavy weather checklists, checked or keep inside during adverse weather.
Corrective Action: Comply heavy weather checklists, checked or keep inside during adverse weather.
LessionLearnt: As stated in the cause analysis, the MOB marker clamps to be checked regurarly for fatigue to to vibration.
ClosingNote: The MOB clamps to be padded with rubber to dampen out the vibrations. However in rough wx, the MOB markers to be brought inside the bridge and kept ready for deployment in emergency.

IncidentDetails: During the voyage from Portland, Australia to Port Chalmers, Newzealand on the date of  20th May 2016, vessel encountered heavy weather with gale force winds and heavy seas of up to 6.5 meters which caused her to roll, pitch, labour and strain heavily, and waves breaking on the decks, resulting in damage to her starboard side accommodation ladder even though the ship's course and speed were altered to ease her according to good seamanship under the circumstances.
IncidentEventDetails: During the voyage from Portland, Australia to Port Chalmers, Newzealand on the date of  20th May 2016, vessel encountered heavy weather with gale force winds and heavy seas of up to 6.5 meters which caused her to roll, pitch, labour and strain heavily, and waves breaking on the decks, resulting in damage to her starboard side accommodation ladder even though the ship's course and speed were altered to ease her according to good seamanship under the circumstances.
ImmediateAction: Continue navigating but bosun and deck crew added the securing lashing to the gangway to prevent falling into the sea. Take pictures for evidence and reported to office and all parties concerned
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During the voyage from Portland, Australia to Port Chalmers, Newzealand on the date of  20th May 2016, vessel encountered heavy weather with gale force winds and heavy seas of up to 6.5 meters which caused her to roll, pitch, labour and strain heavily, and waves breaking on the decks, resulting in damage to her starboard side accommodation ladder even though the ship's course and speed were altered to ease her according to good seamanship under the circumstances.
DirectCause: Due to heavy weather and high swell encountered from starboard quarter direction to the gangway reached by very strong waves effect of rolling and pitching.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Inorder to put recurrence of the similar issue , vessel to take all heavy weather precautions as mentioned in the navigational manual. Vessels staff must make an accurate assessment of the weather conditions and confirm by Sat C, and Wx forecast programs. Navigation  in extreme heavy weather must be avoided if possible and vessel have to tolerate till the weather becomes good.
Corrective Action: Inorder to put recurrence of the similar issue , vessel to take all heavy weather precautions as mentioned in the navigational manual. Vessels staff must make an accurate assessment of the weather conditions and confirm by Sat C, and Wx forecast programs. Navigation  in extreme heavy weather must be avoided if possible and vessel have to tolerate till the weather becomes good.
ClosingNote: Maneuvering in heavy wx must be done to protect the vessel and the equipment from damage. Failure to do so can result in the equipment failure resulting in huge costs. In case if required, vessel to stop an heave to if practicable till the weather abates.

IncidentDetails: While overhauling ME exhaust valve, the exhaust valve stand got damaged. The exhaust valve was secured to the stand with the help of securing plate. After completion of the job while shifting the exhaust valve from the stand the securin plate was not detached and the exhaust valve was lifted with the help of engine room crane, thus damagind the stand coupling and pedestals at both ends.
IncidentEventDetails: While overhauling ME exhaust valve, the exhaust valve stand got damaged. The exhaust valve was secured to the stand with the help of securing plate. After completion of the job while shifting the exhaust valve from the stand the securin plate was not detached and the exhaust valve was lifted with the help of engine room crane, thus damagind the stand coupling and pedestals at both ends.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the engine room crane. Repaired the pedestals and coupling by welding

IncidentDetails: Leaking of DBT#4 Manhole ( P) (fwd) part leading to ingress of Water at Cargo Hold #5
IncidentEventDetails: June 09, 2016  0700hrs LT = Take sounding of ballast tank and hold bilges.  0825hrs LT = Press-up/Overflow of all ballast tank for Arrival / Berthing condition.  June 10, 2016  0815hrs LT = Carried-out inspection of all Cargo Hold as preparation to recieve cargo upon berthing.  0820hrs LT = Upon opening of CH#5, notice water inside the CH about 3-4 meters of water.                          ( Water Ingress Alarm System ) WIAS have not alarm on the bridge.  0830hrs LT = Immediately start pumping-out of water from CH#5 using eductor.  1130hrs LT = No changes of water level by taking constant sounding.  1135hrs LT = Ordered to the engine department that to use GS pump to pump-out the                                              water and stopped the eductor.  1338hrs LT = Started Pumping-out of water at WBT#4(P/S) due water sounding at CH#5 unchange.   1500hrs LT = Additionally,Set-up submersible pump in the CH#5 to transfer the water to WBT#4(S).     * Cont.Monitoring/Pumping-out of water until CH#5 is empty and refill again the WBT#4 to check                 where the leak came from *    June 11,2016  0900hrs LT = We confirmed that the water ingress in CH#5 came from WBT4 (Portside/Forwardpart)                      manhole due to some nuts are worn out due age,replaced with new one.  1100hrs LT = Loaded ballast  in WBT 4 P/S checked and confirmed that no more leaking.  2000hrs LT = Press-up all Ballast tanks and checked/confirmed that no signs of leak in the Cargo                        Holds and all are ready to recieve cargo.  June 12,2016  0800hrs LT = Again checked and confirmed of all Cargo Holds have no signs of leak.
ImmediateAction: Pump-out the water and take soundings of all involve tanks, all old ordinary nuts are Changed/RENEWED with Stainless Steal Nuts.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: None
DirectCause: Since vessel was delivered June 2012 and newly drydock (Dec 2015), all manhole nut must be installed and renewed with stainless steel hexagon nuts.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To renew all deteriorated hexagon nuts with stainless steel hexagon nuts.
Corrective Action: To renew all deteriorated hexagon nuts with stainless steel hexagon nuts.
ReviewComments: Good observance by ships staff
ClosingNote: Good observance by ships staff

IncidentDetails: While aligning the vessel in the lock, as the tugboat on port shoulder was pushing vessel from port side, stbd side of vessel came in contact with knuckle causing indentation.
IncidentEventDetails: The conn was taken by pilot E. Correa at 0740 hrs / 14th June. Canal rules stipulate pilot is responsible for Navigation in canal waters.  At the time of Incident, bridge team consisted of Master (Capt. T. Kumar), 3rd Officer (Manish Narwal) and AB seaman on the wheel and the pilot.  0900 hrs Panama canal crew (19 crew members) onboard. Fwd station manned by Ch Officer, Bosun, OS, Cadet to assist Panama crew for mooring stations and operations (the moorings and all operations are carried out by Panama Canal crew and assisted by ship crew)  Aft tug was fast 0918 hrs and fwd tug fast at 0948 at port shoulder.  The RADARs were put at standby at 1000 hrs as per pilot's instructions, to meet with Panama Canal Regs.  The vessel was approaching the Gatun lock jetty to be stbd side to. At around 1003 hrs vessel passed rowing boat the messenger line to pick up the wire for making fast locomotive ashore on her stbd side. Approx distance from jetty was 30 mtrs. The fwd tug was pushing the vessel from port shoulder to bring her closer to jetty. Aft tug was being used to slow down the vessel.  The communication with the tug by the pilot was on portable radio carried by Pilot and was in Spanish/English/codes (eg 2 whistles by tug etc)  Vessel does not have a bow thruster.  At 1004 hrs the bow was in line with knuckle and distance was about 5-10 meters. The knuckle had neoprene fenders.  A swing of bow to stbd was observed a little time later as vessel was coming closer to jetty due to tug pushing. Master requested the pilot to watch the same and use tug to pull however at 1005 hrs the vessel stbd shoulder touched the knuckle of the jetty. speed was observed to be 1.6 knots ahead.  A damage assesment carried out from the deck of vessel showed INDENTATION APPROXIMATELY AT A HEIGHT OF 9 MTRS FROM KEEL, 80 CMS IN LENGTH BY  30-40 CMS IN HEIGHT WITH 8 - 10 CMS IN DEPTH AT FWD OF FRAME 81.   Vessel was brought alongside the jetty and the locksmaster present at the jetty reported the indenation depth as 2 inches.  Weather was calm and visibilty was good.
ImmediateAction: Damage assessment was made by ship staff. Pilot was made awae of the damagw who informed Canal port Captain(Cristobal) and Locksmaster. Once vessel was alongside the lock  damage assessmemt was made by locksmaster and same was informed to Pilot and Master on bridge. Office was duly informed of the above incident immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NK Surveyor attended the vessel at Galveston Anchorage on 19th June 2016 to assess the damage.  Damage Found; -The indentation of damage - size:1500mm x 1000mm x Max Depth 50 mm located at No. 1 W.B.T. Stbd side 2 Mtrs above the water in ballast condition. -Internal examination of No. 1 W.B.T. Stbd side was carried out and found folliwng damage; 1) Long. No.28 was slight bent, 29 was bent at the bracket from Fr. No.81 BHD. 2) Side shell plating between Long. No.28 and 29,29 and 30 were indented. 3) Plate of Fr.81 BHD between Long. 28 and 29 were indented size; Max Depth 25mm x 300mm x 400mm, between Long. 29 and 30: size- Max Depth 30mm x 400mm x 600mm. 4) Stiffener in way of Long. 29 and 30 was bent. 5) Found no cracks in the affected parts and other internal members. 6) Pressure test of NO.1 W.B.T.Stbd side was carried out: found in order.  Following comments made by NK Surveyor after assessment;  Considering above conditions, vessel is seaworthy and no need other restrictions; set out the outstanding recommendations as follow; The damaged side shell plate, Long. No.28, 29 and Fr.81 BHD of No.1 WBT Stbd side are to be repaired permanently at the next docking survey due on 18 July 2018 and subjected to the result of internal examination on Annual survey by attending surveyor.
DirectCause: Incorrect communication betwwen Panama Canal Pilot, Locomotive handler and Tug - communication was carried out in local language and hence Master could not follow the intentions of the Pilot; By the time Master advised the Pilot of the incorrect swing, the momentum could not be reduced
Proposedcorrectiveactions: No significant Corrective Action; Incident reported to owners to take it up with Pilots through agents
Corrective Action: Monitor vessel more closely
LessionLearnt: Close Monitoring of Pilots actions and actions of the Tug boats
ClosingNote: Incident shared with Owners for their agents to advise the Panama Pilot of the incident and of potential Corrective actions to be taken up by them.

IncidentDetails: While shifting position at Chittagong anchorage, to a more favourable one, vessel was turning around to port to clear an anchored vessel, when she came into contact with the anchored vessel (MV Evangeline M) on 09 February 2016 / 1245 LT in position 22 17.99N / 091 44.20E. Both vessels sustained damages. There were no casualties or pollution. No breach of hull on either vessel.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was to anchor at Chittagong anchorage with the intention of lightering her cargo of cement clinkers to reduce drafts before proceeding to berth at Chittagong. She was advised by Charterer’s agents on this intention. She arrived  pilot station on 09 February / 0848 LT drawing 8.60 m fore and aft.  She tendered NOR at the same time. She then proceeded to anchorage Alpha. She let go port anchor on 09 Feb 2016 / 1224 LT in position 22 17.55N / 091 44.45E. She had dropped anchor close to where the shallows commence towards the shore (around 2 cables). At 1230 LT Master decided to commence heaving anchor to move to a safer anchorage position with sufficient depth. On 09 Feb 2016 / 1235LT port anchor was aweigh. Vessel was turning hard to port and moving ahead. On 09 Feb 2016 / 1245LT while executing the turn to port her starboard side abreast No.5 hold made contact with the port bow of an anchored vessel ‘M/V Evangelia M’ in position 22 17.99N / 091 44.20E. She then continued to manoeuvre and let go anchor in position 22 17.88N / 091 42.70E on 09 Feb 2016 / 1312 LT. Agents, customs, cargo and draft surveyors boarded 09 Feb 2016 / 1550 LT and at 1750 LT draft survey and port formalities were completed and vessel cleared to lighten cargo.
ImmediateAction: Master informed office on 09 Feb 2016 / 1320 LT (first contact with Marine Manager Capt. Ajith). Crisis control centre was mobilized immediately. Crisis control team consisted of Capt. Sairaj (DPA / CSO & Head of operations), Mr.Umesh Iyengar (Technical Manager) at Singapore and Capt.Ajith (Marine Manager), Capt.Krishna Mundath (CMS), Mr.Rajesh Inigo (Sr.Technical Superintendent), Capt. Chetan Sharma (Marine Superintendent / Auditor) Capt Vinod Kumar (Marine Superintendent), Mr. Srinivasan Swaminathan (Marine Executive) and Ms Jestle Renita (CMS Executive) at Chennai to provide necessary support to the vessel. No crew injuries were reported from the vessel. Master was advised to revert with name and details / particulars of the vessel contacted with and carry out damage assessment, sound all tanks and revert with statement of facts (SOF).
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Photographs as attached... *Damaged hull on stbd side aft abreast hold No.5 *Damaged pilot access door & Bulwark *Buckled deck plate area *Damaged collapsible log stanchions *Bent fixed log upright damaged *Compressed retaining channel of No.5 hatch cover *Damaged ship side longitudinal & bracket in void *Damaged overhead deck longitudinal *Damaged web frame inside void space *Damaged aft bulkhead iwo TST no.5S *Bulged cross deck forward of #5
DirectCause: Master’s and bridge team’s knowledge of Chittagong and approaches Master had not been to the port of Chittagong in the past but was aware of the conditions in the port and the general precautions that were required to be considered when calling the port in terms of piracy and stealing. Master was aware of the strong currents at the anchorage and approaches to the anchorage and port. Bridge team did not have previous experience on Chittagong. Master had been provided with detailed information on the port and its approaches by the Marine Manager handling the vessel 5 days in advance prior vessel calling Chittagong port. Master had read the instructions sent from office. Master had not consulted the sailing directions on approaches to Chittagong. However agents had provided information on approaches. Conditions at Chittagong anchorage Agents had advised Master to anchor at Chittagong anchorage ‘Alpha’ for lightering operations. Anchorage as per Master and from analysis of the VDR was congested with over 50 vessels anchored in and around anchorage Alpha. The current was reported to be setting NW’ly at 2.2 knots as per the passage plan. According to Master it was setting N’ly at 3 - 4 knots. Weather was slight. Visibility was around 5 NM.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ? Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels in the fleet sharing the incident details and carry out a briefing session of deck officers and crew on board addressing the root causes identified. Share incident with other ships in the fleet and during pre-joining briefing of deck officers. PIC: CMS Team / Training team Date: 31 March 2016 ? If unable to avoid, then Master to consult office on the anchoring location and plan. PIC: Technical Superintendent / Marine Manager/ Date: 31 March 2016 ? PIC: Competency / Training Team Date: 31 May 2016 ? Masters to visit Synergy training centre and put through bridge simulator every 2 years to review navigation practices and understanding. . Recruitment / assignment for Master and deck officers to strictly comply with requirement of a BTM course within last 36 months. PIC: Competency / Training Team Date: 15 April 2016 ? Include Chittagong and approaches in navigational special area in the Navigation manual highlighting concerns and establishing precautions. PIC: CMS Team Date: 15 April 2016 ? Marine superintendents to call vessel when sending port / transit information to vessels and brief Master regarding particular hazards in the area and precautions required including plans for anchoring and drifting. PIC: Marine Superintendent Date: 31 Mar 2016 ? Random review of passage plan of vessel’s prior transiting a navigational special area or when calling a port known for difficult navigation to be carried out. This can be sought when sending port / transit information to vessels. A system of reviewing passage plans online to be explored and implemented. PIC: CMS / Marine Superintendent / Ship palm Team Date: 31 May 2016 ? Random review of VDR data of vessels that have transited a navigational special area and select ports to be carried out to identify navigational risks and alert vessels accordingly. IncidentDetails: While dock master and his team of four people were embarking our vessel for berthing at Zhoushan, The tug boat came in contact with the starboard side pilot ladder,and damaging same.(Four rubber steps and 1 st spreader dettached from Ladder)
IncidentEventDetails: Tug Boat hit the Ladder Forcefully.
ImmediateAction: Inform to bridge team and damage report raised against Zhoushan dockyard. infored the SRM of the yard about the damage and has asked to replace the ladder with certificate.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Four rubber steps and 1st spreader of the pilot ladder got detached and the pilot ladder cannot be put in use again.
DirectCause: Rough manoeuvring and coming alonside by the tug boat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To brief pilot station staff to pick up the ladder clear of the boat, if they feel that the boat is coming alongside too hard or the boat operator is unable to control the boat as required.
Corrective Action: Yard t supply new pilot ladder with certificate to replace the damaged ladder.
LessionLearnt: Importance of observing the pilot boat during pilot stations
ClosingNote: Master to regularly brief station staff to pick up the ladder clear of the boat, if they feel that the boat is coming alongside too hard or the boat operator is unable to control the boat as required.

IncidentDetails: Port Anchor lashings found broken and anchor rendered till 10 shackles in water, whilst safety inspection on deck following heavy weather.
IncidentEventDetails: 27/1615 LT: Wx abated slightly, vessel Course altered to take wx on the stern and crew sent on deck for general safety and reefer checks.    27/1630 LT: C/O informed that Port anchor lashings were found broken and anchor was not found in place and was rendered out till 10 Shackles in the water.    27/1635 LT: Port Anchor breaks tightened up to max. Vessel manoeuvered to drop speed and negate any slamming on the focsle for crew safety.  Alarm raised and all deck crew sent to Focsle.    27/1650 LT: Commenced heaving Port anchor.    27/1750 LT: Port anchor sighted and clear / No apparent damage to anchor.    27/1800 LT: Port anchor housed in Hawse pipe.
ImmediateAction: 1. Vessel speed reduced and maoeuvred to give max lee and safe conditions to deck crew on Focsle.  2. Port anchor put in gear and heaved up and secured in place.  3. Extra + improvised lashing taken on both anchors to prevent re-occurence.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.  a) Port side anchor lashing wire sling parted.  b) Both the Pipe Turnbuckles (Hook and Jaw type) used for tightening the aforesaid sling are damaged. The Hook end has flared open.    2.  Stbd side anchor lashing pipe turnbuckle found missing.
DirectCause: Vessel was encountering very heavy weather on the East coast of Oman whilst enroute from AEKLF to KEMBA andl had altered her course to 225 (T) as per FNSC - CMA CGM Captain on Duty's strong recommendations.  Vessel was encountering heavy long head swells of 6 M + and was pitching moderately to heavily at times. Wind - WSW / BF - 8.  Vessel speed had already been dropped to 6 Kts on the commencement of heavy weather.    The anchor weight is 8.7 MT.  Anchor securing arrangement comprises of a single wire lashing of specs as follows : 22 mm Dia - 6x36WS-IWRC / BL - 40 MT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Suggest -   Anchor lashing arrnegment be modified to provide a total BL of system > 50 MT.  New eyes to be welded on strong points and a double wire lashing system be used in lieu of single wire dependancy.  Anchor securing arrangement to be incorporated in SMMS, with Anchor lashing wires to be renewed every 2 years.
Corrective Action: Suggest -  Anchor lashing arrnegment be modified to provide a total BL of system > 50 MT. New eyes to be welded on strong points and a double wire lashing system be used in lieu of single wire dependancy. Anchor securing arrangement to be incorporated in SMMS, with Anchor lashing wires to be renewed every 2 years.
LessionLearnt: IMPORTANCE OF HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTIONS
ClosingNote: ADDITIONAL HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTIONS SHOULD BE TAKEN . VESSELS SHOULD BE EQUIPPED WITH ENHANCED LASHING WIRES TO PLACE ADDITIONAL LASHINGS FOR HEAVY WEATHER

IncidentDetails: One of the Lifebuoys on main deck went overboard due to heavy rainfall/Vessel rolling and restricted visibility.
IncidentEventDetails: -Vessel was transitting coastal waters with heavy rainfall and vessel rolling moderately.  -At 0800 LT visibility was reduced to less than 1 mile due to fog/heavy rainfall.  -As the vessel was partly loaded,all scuppers on deck were plugged resulting in water accumulation on main deck.  -Moderate traffic was being encountered and all bridge watchkeepers were busy handling same in restricted visibility.  -At 0856 Duty officer saw that Lifebuoy aft of Stbd. gangway has come out of its position and is floating in accumulated water on deck and is about to go overboard.  -Duty AB was rushed down on deck to stop it but by the time he could reach main deck,Lifebuoy had gone overboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lifebuoy lost at sea.
DirectCause: One of the Lifebuoys on main deck went overboard due to heavy rainfall/Vessel rolling and restricted visibility.Due to excessive water accumulation on ship side in heavy rainfall ,Lifebouy came out of its cradle and went overboard.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Aft Scuppers to be kept opened in heavy rainfall.
Corrective Action: Aft Scuppers to be kept opened in heavy rainfall.
LessionLearnt: All deck scuppers to be left opened during periods when vessel exposed to heavy weather or heavy rain conditions. The accumulated water on deck had lifted up the life buoy from the crutches & during the next rolling moment of the vessel, the life buoy had been washed off the vessel. Master informed local Port authorities & Coastal stations thru the Agents & an Urgency message was broadcast on the VHF on the loss of LSA. A spare life buoy was immediately replaced in location of the lost life buoy
ClosingNote: IR Closed

IncidentDetails: Vessel made contact with the SBM and she moved away to  the port side while the SBM was on stbd side and vessel went ahead of the  SBM by about 30 mtrs (As reported by the 3/Off who was in the fwd stations)  before starting to fall back  There is a scrap on the Ship side (STBD of the Bulbous bow).
IncidentEventDetails: 07 July 2016 / 2330 LT: Vessel arived 2nm S-SW of SBM 1  08 July 2016  0001 LT: Pilot onboard along with mooring master and other connection crew  0006 - 0018: Meeting held by mooring master advising papers required and  signatures being done on the usual safety notices etc.  0018: CON with pilot ,pilot informed to make fast the aft tug ,sent 3/off Nandha to aft station.  0024 LT: Aft tug made fast and vessel started proceeding to the SBM.  0024 LT - 0026 LT: Engine speed increased from Stop to Half ahead - Distance  from SBM at this time was about 3 NM  The speed then went up to about 9 kts and when distance was 1.6 NM. This information was passed to the pilot however he was busy making conversation with the mooring master. The conversation was in russian language. All communication with the Tug was also in russian language.  Mooring boat was near SBM waiting to assist in mooring operations.   All crew sent fwd for stations. 3/O was fwd with the crew along with bosun,  2 AB's, Trainee OS and OS.  0047 LT: Slow ahead  0053 LT: DSA  0057 LT: Stop.   0058 LT: DSA  0058 LT: Slow astern  0059 LT: Half astern / Full astern.   The contact was made in the above period between  0055LT to 0100 LT  0130 LT : Visual inspection carried out by standby tug and no apparent damage found on   SBM and as well as on the hose. They also reported that no pollution whatsoever was noted.   0148 LT: Vessel received instructions from onboard mooring master to proceed to SBM no.2 as per terminal instruction(CPC Marine). Vessel commneced approach to SBM 2  0230 LT: Commenced mooring with SBM 2  0242 LT: Vessel made fast with One chain on her port side.
ImmediateAction: Full Astern on telegraph.  There is a scrap on the Ship side (STBD of the Bulbous bow).  Visual inspection carried out by standby tug and no apparent damage found on   SBM and as well as on the hose. They also reported that no pollution whatsoever was noted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No personal injury. Joint inspection of the vessel carried out by LR, P & I and owners representative along with ships staff, no damage found. Report on same line sissued by class.
DirectCause: 1. Navigation procedures under Pilotage as given in the Navigation manual were not complied. 2. Master-Pilot information exchange was not adequately carried out . Ref attached detailed investigation report
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pls see below the list of CA proposed
Corrective Action: All owners should stand for pilots being held equally responsible for their actions. Lessen paper work. Too much in the mind at all times, zero patience levels, habituated to cell phones. Minimum conversation on the wheel house between pilots and mooring masters. No mobile phones should be bought up on the wheel house. They should be deposited in the CCR and then should be used only after completion of mooring. All loading masters paper work should be done by e-mail prior arrival and no paper work should be done on the wheel house. Only when pilots are fully held resposible for their actions, such incidents will reduce.  Investigations can show lapses by Ships staff for the occurance however such things are bound to occur again.
ClosingNote: All corrective actions implemented and closed

IncidentDetails: 1 tail rope of the forward spring winch parted
IncidentEventDetails: 12-Jul-2016  --------------  2230 LT: Pilot Onboard  2342 LT: Tug made fast centre lead aft  2354 LT: Tug made fast aft (break of accomodation stbd side)  2355 LT: Tug made fast Fwd (stbd shoulder )    13-Jul-2016  --------------  0012 LT ~ 0024 LT : vessel approaching berth NOVATEK 6   0024 LT : First line Ashore (2 fwd spring line ) using heaving line   0024 LT ~ 0035 LT : Passing aft spring line ashore .                                   2 Aft spring passed from poop deck for adequate lead .2 aft spring were being                                 manually cleared (by shore crew)from the fenders  over which they were passing                              to reach the   shore mooring hooks/claws.   0035 LT : Aft spring line ashore ,  0035 LT : Pilot informed  6mtrs to go ahead   0035 LT ~0037.5 LT : Vessel heaving tight the aft spring to move ahead.There was no movement                                    due to wind(BF4/5) from right ahead pushing the vessel astern.  0037.5 LT : engine DS AH  0037.5 LT : Engine stopped after 3 secs giving a short kick ahead to stop the vessel falling back .  0038 LT : Vessel very slowly moving ahead.Fwd station informed to monitor fwd spring to adjust                 accoordingly   as   vessel was slowly moving ahead ,shore reporting the pilot to align manifold  0042 LT : C/O reported 1 tail rope of the fwd spring snapped  and the other came out of the hook                     ashore  0046 LT : Aft Tug centre lead cast off  0048 LT : 1 Fwd spring ashore again  0052 LT : Fwd spring heaving tight to fall back 2 mtrs .   0056 LT : Vessel in position   0058 LT : Fwd and Aft spring heaved tight and made fast .  0120 LT : All lines made fast aft   0124 LT :Aft Tug cast off (break of accomodation)  0125 LT : Vessel renewed the tail rope for 1  fwd spring and passed ashore .Same time made fast .  0136 LT : 2 forward breast lines ashore and made fast .  0136 LT : All made fast fwd & Aft (4+2+2)  0136 LT : Fwd Tug Cast Off ,Pilot Away
ImmediateAction: The tail rope for the spring line forward renewed emmidiately and passed ashore for making fast second spring.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 mooring tail damage
Corrective Action: A training session shall be developed and included in officer's training module on mooring operations which shall include thorough watch keeping, maintenance of mooring equipment and additional precautions to be taken while Berthing the vessel
ClosingNote: Ref. captioned Damage report, we have carried out investigation about this incident (mooring tail rope parted during berthing). We appreciate the crew members for their swift response in passing the lines immediately.  After review, it was confirmed that the ropes were properly tended to and same was used to position the vessel The last brake rendering test was carried out as per Company policy and marking on the brake was correct  The rope which parted was last visually checked in the month of June 2016 - found in good condition.  Based on the analysis, please find office comments:  Cause ===== 1.Due to sudden jerk caused by the movement of our vessel during positioning the vessels, Speed of our vessel was on higher side. 2.Location of berth - With available information, this possibility could not be ruled out.

IncidentDetails: Loss of Liferaft
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel while on her laden passage from CayoArcas to Sikka, during her routine inspection found port side, aft liferaft missing. Liferaft lashing found hanging overboard and painter cut.
ImmediateAction: All liferafts inspected and found in place. Nearest port state, flag state, MRCC and owners notified.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Liferaft Lost
DirectCause: Possible accidental Loss
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Dispensation and replacement of the lost liferaft at next port. Increased lashings and more stringent weekly inspection routines
Corrective Action: Dispensation and replacement of the lost liferaft at next port. Increased lashings and more stringent weekly inspection routines
ClosingNote: The Liferaft lashing is suspected to have broken loose as a result of weakening due to prolonged exposure to rain & sunlight.

Vessel: DK ABDUL RAZZAK KHALID ZAID AL-KHALID
IncidentDetails: While Unberthing operation at Terminal # 1, La Pampilla, Peru, and Singling-up to be done with one rope at a time as per the Master - Pilot Information exchange. On slacking the wire rope, the shore mooring crew attempted to release the wire rope from the hook by a messenger rope tied to the mooring tail and manoeuvring the mooring boat in order to release from the Buoy hook. Several attempts were made by the Mooring Boat/Crew, in doing so, the Buoy turned either sides in circular motion which would have led to the wire / tail entangle in the buoy/chain underwater. The Wire Rope parted from Approx. 20 Mtrs from the Wire Tail End due to excessive stress on wire to free the rope from buoy entanglement. As a result, Vessel's Tail Rope, Tonsberg Shackle and Approx. 20 Mtrs of Parted Wire Rope Remained / left entangled to the Buoy / Chain underwater. No Visible Damage to the Buoy was noted or reported by the Shore Mooring Crew / Boat. The damage to the broken end of the wire rope was frayed wire rope/strands and broken strands at 2 locations in a length of 15 mtrs from the broken end.
IncidentEventDetails: STATEMENT OF FACT  Mooring line parting at La Pampilla CBM terminal #1 while casting off / Departure    28/07/2016 (All in Local time (GMT+5))   • 1042-Vessel completed Loading.  • 1806- Hoses disconnected  • Master pilot exchange completed as attached for unmooring sequence  • 1848- Commenced unmooring with after stern soft ropes from centre lead.  • About 1858- Slacked port quarter one wire & Fwd port bow one wire  • As mooring team unable to cast off the lines due to night darkness & probably weight  • About 1903- Slacked both wires form port quarter & Port Bow  • About 1915- All lines on board from port Bow & Quarter.  • During this vessel’s stern swung slight to her stbd with spring reaction & eased off                   wire weight, approx. to extent both wires forming neck less just under water surface,                 though enough weight for mooring team attendance.  • About 1918- Both Stbd quarter lines slacked for casting off  • For casting off the practice followed was the mooring boat will approach buoy after                 releasing of mooring tension; mooring crew will embark on CBM buoy for hand lifting                   of rope eye for casting off & re-join his mooring boat.  • In this case observed they were facing difficulties for long to cast of both the ropes,  • Mooring boat was engaged with messenger ropes fast to buoy & was making several                 attempts of swinging the buoy left & right.  • Additional mooring boat were deployed for attending the issue ( total two boats)  • One of the wire cleared & same picked by ship staff.  • After several failure attempt pilot request consent from master to cut tail rope eye from                 hook area, same was permitted in prevailing situation.  • After eye cut too the line did not clear.  • Approx. 1942- Pilot request to try taking tension & may the line get clear from the kink  • Same followed, with close monitoring from two mooring boats closer to buoy.  • During this one stand by tug was assisting vessel to control her position till her final anchor                 aweigh & clearing of CBM area.  • During this unforeseen case, tug was assisting from port side & some times to stbd side as                 per Pilots instruction  • Approx. 2006- At one time the swing increased & wire position happened long stay &                 across, the wire parted without the positive confirmation to any of the team members  • In doubt from bridge order to heave & try taking tension & kept heaving till full broken wire                 came on board.  • 2012- All lines on board; same reported to pilot & asked to check, Mooring boat checked &                 all reported to be in order except for the loss of one mooring line.    (1952- Commenced heaving anchor- but this was intermittent & was only to the extent of as far as possible heave till no leads/ weight on anchor cables; the weight on both anchor cable during the above entire period remained up and down.)
ImmediateAction: No Personal Injury incurred.  On-board Pilot & Local Agent witnessed; Verbal confirmation & visual indication of no damage to CBM buoy reported by the Mooring boat after inspection & same confirmed by Pilot to vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One No. Mooring Wire Damaged and Mooring Tail + Tonsberg Shackle Lost.
DirectCause: Ternial resourses & Weather condition
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew training to be carried out onboard about mooring procedures.
Corrective Action: Crew training to be carried out onboard about mooring procedures.
ClosingNote: Job Closed 26/19/16

IncidentDetails: Dueing routine rounds,found MAC#2 amperes was low,and on checking at local,found LP pressure is very low as 2.5 bar,whereas,actual to be about 5.5 bar.
IncidentEventDetails: Observation during rounds.
ImmediateAction: MAC#2 stopped and LP valve opened up for inspection.Found valve springs broken and same renewed with a spare overhauled one.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LP valve spring broken.Same renewed.
DirectCause: Found the reason to be the faulty check valve on the air discharge side,which is not sealing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Check valve seat was worn out and the same has been reconditioned onboard and fitted after sealing test.
Corrective Action: Check valve seat was worn out and the same has been reconditioned onboard and fitted after sealing test.
LessionLearnt: PMS routine for the check valve seat to be followed.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Closed

IncidentDetails: While taking deck rounds at 2130hrs by duty officer found one mooring rope parted & other mooring ropes were in normal taught condition.No injuries , damages to vessel & environment.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Vessel made fast (4 + 2) fore & aft & starboard side alongside at shanghai berth.  2) Cargo operations (loading & discharging) was in progress.Tide was flooding.  3) No ballasting / deballasting or any other operations at the time of incident.  4) At 2100 hrs duty ab went for checking the mooring ropes (fore & aft) and tended to the moorings.  5) At 2130 hrs duty officer found one mooring rope parted when taking deck rounds.
ImmediateAction: Immediately replaced the parted mooring rope with another rope .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope got parted & not in useable condition
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be properly trained for attending the moorings while vessel is alongside keeping in mind the Loading/discharging location of cargo on board whether Fwd or Aft and also Tidal condition through out the duration of theircargo watch.Also they have to be alert in case any ballasting/deballasting operation is in progress with the location of the tanks being operated.  Training session for the above already carried out with full deck team on board including officers and crew.
Corrective Action: All crew to be properly trained for attending the moorings while vessel is alongside keeping in mind the Loading/discharging location of cargo on board whether Fwd or Aft and also Tidal condition through out the duration of theircargo watch.Also they have to be alert in case any ballasting/deballasting operation is in progress with the location of the tanks being operated.  Training session for the above already carried out with full deck team on board including officers and crew.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Vessel to check the condition of the mooring ropes more frequently and PMS routines to be modified accordingly.

IncidentDetails: Port side Accommodation Ladder Stowing and Supporting wires parted while preparing the ladder for arrival at Cagliari
ImmediateAction: Port side Accommodation Ladder immediately hoisted and secured in place.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 08-Aug-2016, 1000 hrs , Vsl was under pilotage, Deck crew started preparing the port side  Accommodation ladder as the vessel was going along side port side to, as informed by the pilot. After removing the lashings and verifing that there were no obstrctions. its was intended to lower the  Accommodation ladder  up to deck level. While lowering the ladder the davit started moving the stowing wire and it suddently parted, this lead to the accelerated movement of the davit winch, immediately the manual brakes were applied and the lowering of the ladder ceased. The same was reported to the bridge and  measures were taken to heave the ladder up to  the deck level by using the sliding and hoisting ropes which were in good order. The ladder was then brought up to deck level and secured using  appropriate means without any damage to the ladder and also without any injury to the crew. Meanwhile the berthing plan was altered  and vsl berthed stbd side alongside without any delay.
DirectCause: Improper Inspection regime, thereby failing to detect the poor wire condition. Failure to Comply with PMS
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper Adherence to Inspection regime for Wires,especially accommodation ladder wires
Corrective Action: Stowing and Supporting wire renewal in progress.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper Adherence to Inspection regime for Wires,especially accommodation ladder wires
ReviewComments: The parted wire has been replaced by vessel. The reqn raised is being arranged for supply.
ClosingNote: Improper Inspection regime, thereby failing to detect the poor wire condition. Failure to Comply with PMS All other sister vessels specifically told to inspect the same area and responses received. All other fleet vessels informed of the incident.

IncidentDetails: Minor fire broke out on damaged rubber Gas hose of the Gas cutting unit.
IncidentEventDetails: When the Eng fitter was trying  to Heat up a frozen nut in the engine room work shop using the gas torch, he had the rubber gas hose coiled lying on the floor on his right side. He held the torch on his left hand and lighted  the torch. Un fortunately the rubber hose in use had a leak that came in line of the flame, this lead to minor fire catching at exit point of the leaking gas on the rubber hose.
ImmediateAction: Immediately fitter put off  the torch & closed the regulator Valves  in the work shop. That put off the fire immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No injury , No damage to property
DirectCause: No proper  checking of the equipments prior  use.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gas hose and all other related equipments to be properly inspected prior every usage.  To prevent similar incidents, all Engine room staff explained about the importance of  checking  equipments prior use particularly welding and cutting equipments as this could lead to direct fire.
Corrective Action: Gas hose and all other related equipments to be properly inspected prior every usage. To prevent similar incidents, all Engine room staff explained about the importance of  checking  equipments prior use particularly welding and cutting equipments as this could lead to direct fire.
LessionLearnt: The safety officer must inspect the equipment before any work activity. The same is included in the RA and must be followed proactively
ClosingNote: Agree with the vessels cause analysis. Please be guided by Ch 2 of the H&S manual on hot work & Ch3 of the H&S manual regarding the the duties of the safety officer. Please discuss this incident on the next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Emergency fire pump room flooded with sea water. Motor fully submerged in to the water.
IncidentEventDetails: Main fire pump started for deck washing , Fifteen minutes later duty engineer went for routine rounds and found that water is ingressing in to the emergency fire pump compartment.  Upon water draining noticed that water hose on discharge line connected to pump suction filter for priming got unfastened which lead to water ingress.
ImmediateAction: Emergency fire pump main breaker isolated , Main fire pump stopped and isolation valve closed, started draining water from the compartment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Motor flooded with sea water , chance of getting low insulation , Possible motor damage
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Emergency fire pump drain valve should be kept cloased at all time . Emergency fire pump compartment to be checked when fire pump is in operation .    Planned to install bilge high level alram in the compartment.
Corrective Action: Emergency fire pump drain valve should be kept cloased at all time . Emergency fire pump compartment to be checked when fire pump is in operation .  Planned to install bilge high level alram in the compartment.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Bilge level sensor and alarm unit ready for supply and retrofit. Will be supplied and installed next call Kochi.
ClosingNote: Bilge alarm unit supplied onboard and installed, to provide audio/visual indication

IncidentDetails: Vessel was being made fast at SASREF Terminal, Al Jubail, Berth # 61  with Pilot onboard. As advised by the Pilot, Vessel passed 3 Headlines (HMPE Ropes) from the vessel's bow to the Mooring Boat in order to be taken ashore and was following mooring boat's crew instructions to slack the ropes. During the Boat's manoeuvre and due to improper handling of ropes by the Mooring Boat Crew, one no. headline got entangled in the Mooring Boat's propeller and same could not be cleared by the Mooring Boat Crew despite various attempts.     After discussion between the Pilot, Mooring Boat Crew and Port Control (Jubail Industrial Marine) on VHF, the Mooring Boat was instructed by Pilot and Port Control to cut the entangled Headline.                                                                 The Rope was subsequently cut by the Mooring Boat Crew and the remaining part of the Rope was taken back by the vessel and is beyond use now.    Vessel requested the Mooring Boat to check if the Rope Tail was lying free and not entangled and to pass the same back to the ship for possible use in the future but was informed that the eye is also entangled in their propeller.    Subsequently, the mooring boat was towed to inner habour and entangled rope along with the tail was cleared from the mooring boat propeller and same was retturned to the ship on 27 Aug 2016 (next day).    Vessel issued a LOP for the same and presented to the to the Pilot for his acknowledgment and signatures, but he refused to sign the same. Same with the Loading Master.
IncidentEventDetails: 18:00 - Anchor Aweigh   19:50 - POB  19:55 - Master/Pilot information exchanged  20:12 - Fwd tug made fast with two ship lines   20:15 - Aft tug made fast with two ship lines  20:30 - First line ashore (Spring line Fwd) passed by heaving line  20:32 - Spring line Aft passed ashore by heaving line  20:38 - Vessel in position (Spring lines made fast fwd & aft)  20:40 - Gangway down (Shore Gangway)  20:43 - Breast line (fwd & aft) passed ashore by Mooring Boat  20:48 - Breast lines made fast (fwd & aft) / passing Head lines & Stern lines  20:52 - C/O reported one Head line stuck in propeller of Mooring Boat /    Mooring Boat crew trying to release the entangled rope.  20:55 - 3 Stern lines passed ashore by Mooring Boat. Aft Mooring Boat    proceeding fwd to assist fwd Mooring Boat as per Pilot's instructions  20:56 - Other 2 Head lines released from Mooring Boat & picked up by vessel  20:58 - Both Mooring Boats trying to clear the entangled rope / Pilot informed           Harbour Master by phone about the above incident  21:00 - All made fast aft (3+2+2)  21:02 - 1 Head line passed ashore using Mooring Boat / Aft tug cast off  21:20 - 2nd Head line passed ashore using Mooring Boat  21:28 - Mooring Boat crew cut the entangled Head line as instructed by Pilot &           Port Control / remaining rope picked up by vessel.  21:30 - Fwd tug cast off  21:35 - 3rd head line (PP rope used for fwd tug) passed ashore by Mooring  Boat  21:40 - All fast fwd (3+2+2)   21:45 - Pilot away
ImmediateAction: Vessel informed the incident to Operators, Owners and Charterers.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel's 1no Mooring rope (HMPE) was damaged .
DirectCause: Improper handling of ropes by mooring boat crew and improper manuevering of mooring boat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ropes handling by mooring boat and mooring gang shall be observed by vessel officer, and any miss handling observed shal be notified to pilot immediately.
Corrective Action: Ropes handling by mooring boat and mooring gang shall be observed by vessel officer, and any miss handling observed shal be notified to pilot immediately.
ClosingNote: Ship staff briefed for better co-ordination between ship staff and mooring gangs during mooring and un-mooring operations.
