IncidentDetails: Vessel was coasting off the Australian Great Barrier Reef approaching the Grafton Passage for arrival to port of Cairns, where it would have entered the REEFVTS area.  The vessel crossed limits of the Designated Shipping Area (DSA) and went in restricted area. This was reported by Great Barrier Reef Marine Park Authority (GBRMPA) to Australian Maritime Safety Authority (AMSA), which in turn boarded vessel when vessel got alongside. Vessel was detained till further investigation.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was coasting off the Great Barrier Reef approaching the Grafton Passage where it would have entered the REEFVTS area. A short distance before a scheduled course change REEFVTS called the vessel on VHF Ch 11 and enquired what the vessel's intention was.OOW responded to REEFVTS that the vessel would alter as per the Passage Plan which would keep the vessel in the Designated Shipping Area. Due REEFVTS's query, OOW decised to alter early but mistakenly he altered to 315(T) inestead of 345(T). Due to this incorrect alteration, vessel crossed the lane & entered in restricted area crossing DSA. As soon as the OOW became aware of v/l being off track, he took corrective action to bring the vessel into the DSA. As the corrective action was made, REEFVTS called the vessel and informed they had crossed the DSA, for which OOW informed that vessel has been altered & proceeding out of restricted area. Master became aware of the incident later that day.
ImmediateAction: As soon came to know about the detention imposed by AMSA on vessel, P&I club correspondent was requested to board vessel. Managers were infomred by Phone & e-mail. AMSA was follwed by GBRMPA investigators who collected evidences & interviewd ship's crew for the incident.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage, injury, pollution etc resulted due to incident.
DirectCause: a. Passage plan form does not identify the need for master to visually check the route and post his comments when using ECDIS as the primary means of Navigation. Although procedures were available in the navigation manual, but were not in the passage plan. b. Appraisal section in passage plan did not include mandatory publications to be considered when planning a voyage in the Great Barrier Reef. c. Appraisal section in passage plan needs improvement regarding requirements of the GBR/DSA.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. In Port Navigation Audit to be carried out by an External Auditor to ascertain effectivemness of the SMS.  2. Re-training on ECDIS type specific/Generic to be carried out for all navigating Officers on board.  3. Training to be carried out on Australian Routeing guidelines and regulations. The training to include routeing and reporting requirements of vessels whilst navigating in Australian waters.  4. Training on the environmental regulations of Australian waters for merchant for merchant vessels that include awareness on the importance of coral reefs. (Great Barrier Reef).  5. Additional remarks in Master's Standing Orders, to require OOW to immediately inform Master when contacted by shore authority and of any infraction.   6. Passage plan (wappont sheet) to be amended to give more actual status of vessel's mointoring with datas like, ETA / speed / Actual time of arrival / cross bearing references
Corrective Action: 1. In Port Navigation Audit to be carried out by an External Auditor to ascertain effectivemness of the SMS. 2. Re-training on ECDIS type specific/Generic to be carried out for all navigating Officers on board. 3. Training to be carried out on Australian Routeing guidelines and regulations. The training to include routeing and reporting requirements of vessels whilst navigating in Australian waters. 4. Training on the environmental regulations of Australian waters for merchant for merchant vessels that include awareness on the importance of coral reefs. (Great Barrier Reef). 5. Additional remarks in Master's Standing Orders, to require OOW to immediately inform Master when contacted by shore authority and of any infraction.  6. Passage plan (wappont sheet) to be amended to give more actual status of vessel's mointoring with datas like, ETA / speed / Actual time of arrival / cross bearing references
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Good passage planning ,execution ,monitoring is must at all times for safe navigation. Also it is most prudent to make Officers understand the importance of a Good type specific ECDIS training ,especially for working on vessels which are dual ECDIS vessel.
ClosingNote: Closed on 01/11/2016

IncidentDetails: CARGO HOLD NO.3 HYDRAULIC HOSE LEAKING
IncidentEventDetails: OLD AND WEARY HYDRAULIC HOSE
ImmediateAction: Immediately stop the hydraulic pump and checked and it was from no. 3 hydraulic jack portside. Checked for spare and found that the fittings is not fit,(previously wrong supply of spare) so we need to cut fitting of old and new hose and welded the old one to the new one. At around 1054Hrs completed replacement
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: HYDRAULIC HOSE ON PORT SIDE AFT PART BURST
DirectCause: WEAR AND TEAR OF THE HYDRAULIC HOSE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TO MAINTAIN WEEKLY CHECKS OF ALL HYDRAULIC HOSES
Corrective Action: TO MAINTAIN WEEKLY CHECKS OF ALL HYDRAULIC HOSES
ClosingNote: All hydraulic hoses which are exposed to the weather, must be periodically checked prior arrival port. Sufficient spares (SIZE AND QUANTITY)  to be arranged and same kept as a part of the minimum spares.

IncidentDetails: Reported Uncorrected  Depth while getting alongside at Port-Au-Prince
IncidentEventDetails: 0830 - POB, 0841-Aft Tug Made fast, 0918 - First Line Ashore.  0920 - Noticed vessel Bow not coming alongside the berth with Bow To "Full to Stbd". pilot Confirmed depth 10m near the Berth.Vessel Draft F-8.5m & A-8.9m  0924 - Vsl Noticed Bow Stuck 15m off the berth at Bollard no 15 & 16 with thrusters effective & tug assistance.repeatedly observed "Echo Sounder Alarm" & vessel observed listing to port even with Tug Assitance.
ImmediateAction: 0926 - Master Abort Maneurve.Advised Ships Personnel to take Ships soundings Stbd Bow & midships.   0930 – Reported Soundings at Fwd : approx 7.8m, Stbd Bow 8.0, midship 13.0m, Quarter 13.5m. Vessel Draft F-8.5m & A-8.9m  0945 – Pilot Confirmed with Port Authorities And agreed to Shift The Vessel To Forward Floating Barge  1042 -  All Fast 3+2 Port side at floating barge. F.W.E  2030- Tank Inspection Done WBT-110(FORE-PEAK TANK) for any Hull Damage.Observed "NO" damage to Ships Hull.  21-June-16 - 0500-  Tank Inspection Done WBT-111 & 112(No1 P& S) for any Hull Damage.Observed "NO" damage to Ships Hull.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damages observed
DirectCause: Incorrect declaration of depths by pilot and charts.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Effective master pilot exchange to be done. To collect information of berth depths from agents prior maneuvre
LessionLearnt: Importance of master/pilot exchange
ReviewComments: Importance of master/pilot exchange
ClosingNote: The touch bottom was due to unknown shallow patch in the forward side of the berth. The vessel had called Haiti in the past without any issue. The shallow patch may have been caused by unexpected silting.  Master / Pilot exchange to include the same. Vessel acted quickly to avoid any damage and moved away. Subsequent underwater inspection indicated no damage to vessel.

IncidentDetails: During the arrival manouuvering in the entrance channel of Maputo Port, Own vessel touched bottom and got grounded at south of No.3 N buoy of the channel.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) V/l was in continuous contact with Maputo port control and was being advised various courses to steer to keep the vessel in the middle of the channel.  2) Since V/l's arrv draft was 11.80 mtrs, V/l's arrival was planned in the rising tide.  3) There was shallow water near No.3 N buoy and V/l was advised by the port control to cross that buoy after 1430 lt, However V/l reached that point at 1515 hrs and still got aground.  4) After waiting for abt 30 - 40 mins, V/l again got refloated and anchored safely at the inner anchorage.
ImmediateAction: Due to the rising tide, V/l re-floated on her own power and anchored at inner anchorage. The nature of the bottom was soft sand and thus No damage is expected on the ship side. Office was informed and incident report being sent.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NONE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A CIRCULAR  TO  ALL COMPANY  SHIP REGARDING SAME.
Corrective Action: A CIRCULAR  TO  ALL COMPANY  SHIP REGARDING SAME.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: While opening the Bolts of Turbo charger for generator rust particle enetered the crew members eye.
IncidentEventDetails: Concerned cerw member was working on turbo charger wearing the safety goggles. But due to sweat issue he removed the same & contnued working. Small rust particles entered his eye.
ImmediateAction: Crew member washed his eye with eye solution provided in first aid kit , but the particle was sticking onto his eyeball. He was then advised to take shower & attempt to remove the same . Afetr takiing shower rust particle got removed & he resumed work without any problems.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor uneasiness to left eye.
DirectCause: Inproper PPE. crew memebr not wearing eye protection
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Suitable eye protection to be worn. In case of excessive sewt face sheild to be worn instead of removing the goggles.
Corrective Action: Suitable eye protection to be worn. In case of excessive sewt face sheild to be worn instead of removing the goggles.
LessionLearnt: Company is providing the PPE. Best use of same to be made at all times. Even during a Job it is causing un comfort it should not be removed and job continued.
ReviewComments: Appropriate PPE to be used during the Job. RA/Tool box meeting to include the proper PPE to be used for a particular job
ClosingNote: Concur with the corrective action to avoid such incident in future.

IncidentDetails: On 30.08.16 about 1220 Lt, some of the crew members reported that a very loud sound was heard from outside of accommodation. Immediately outside of accommodation was inspected by crew members, it was found that provision crane’s tension bar got parted and fell down,luckily there was no crew members underneath of the provision crane. Please REF attached pictures.No injuries .
IncidentEventDetails: On 30.08.16 about 1220 Lt, some of the crew members reported that a very loud sound was heard from outside of accommodation. Immediately outside of accommodation was inspected by crew members, it was found that provision crane’s tension bar got parted and fell down,luckily there was no crew members underneath of the provision crane. Please REF attached pictures.
ImmediateAction: Same was properly inspected,secured for sea and reported to office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Provision Crane wire parted. No further damage or injury to Crew members.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PMS to be followed. Periodic inspection of Provision crane and components to be carried out.
ClosingNote: Senior Officers to briefed on the importance of Crane wire inspection.

IncidentDetails: Damaged to deck line shell plate.
IncidentEventDetails: On 02.09.2016 at around 0900 hours, deck crew carried out routine rounds around the deck and noticed that the top of deck line shell plate in way of Fr. 172-173 and 173-174 has shown damaged. The time of the damage was not noticed but definitely it happened at the time of vessel's transit into the Panama Canal lock possibly during vessel's rising inside the locks hitting a protrusion from any part of the canal walls.
ImmediateAction: Nil
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deckline top plate gashed iwo Fr. 172-173 & 173-174 stbdside.
DirectCause: Presence of unsuspected protrusion inside Panama Canal lock wall
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Build up by welding runs into the damaged area and grinding to flaten the surface.
Corrective Action: Build up by welding runs into the damaged area and grinding to flaten the surface.
ClosingNote: Master reported completion of repair.

IncidentDetails: Damage to port side lifeboat.
IncidentEventDetails: While lowering the port side lifeboat for drill purpose the harbour pin safety hook did not detach from the davit smoothly due to which the lifeboat after clearing the hook swung and accidently touched the ship side railings causing a crack at the bottom of the lifeboat.The extent of the crack is 10 cms.
ImmediateAction: Damage noticed and assessed,life boat stowed for sea and repair possibilities accessed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The damage to the port side lifeboat is on the bottom of the lifeboat and the crack extent is 10cms.
DirectCause: The harbour pin safety hook did not detach properly after the harbour pin was removed.This was due to the rust present on the hook which did not allow the free operation of the same.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The harbour pin safety hooks to be chipped painted and then to be checked for smooth operation.
Corrective Action: The harbour pin safety hooks to be chipped painted and then to be checked for smooth operation.
ClosingNote: Job Closed 07/10/16

IncidentDetails: During Discharging, inboard headline mooring wire got parted at the wire eye, at the same time loose rope passed ashore as a temporary means of mooring. Wire rope heaved back and turned end to end.
IncidentEventDetails: During Discharging, inboard headline mooring wire got parted at the wire eye, leaving the mooring tail rope and mandal shackle at the dolphin at jetty and at the same time loose rope passed ashore as a temporary means of mooring. Mooring tail rope,mandal shackle and wire rope heaved back and wire turned end to end. Wire rope passed back to shore, made fast on board and the loose rope heaved back on board.
ImmediateAction: Immediately wire rope heaved back, soft rope passed ashore for mooring and wire rope turned end to end and passed again on the berth. Loose rope heaved back to the vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During Discharging, inboard headline mooring wire got parted at the wire eye, leaving mooring tail rope and mandal shackle at the dophin on jetty at the same time loose rope passed ashore for mooring and wire rope heaved back and turned end to end
DirectCause: Due to deteriorating condition of wire
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Wire ropes/eye needs to be inspected on regular basis and any defects to be adequately addressed. Change end to end or renew as required
Corrective Action: Wire ropes/eye needs to be inspected on regular basis and any defects to be adequately addressed. Change end to end or renew as required
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: NMR Closed

IncidentDetails: waste oil service tank no.1 overflow
IncidentEventDetails: TME started sludge pump to fill Waste oil service tank no.1 at 0830 hrs. After that he got busy with other jobs and forgot to STOP the pump. Waste oil service tank gauge got stuck and therefore high level alarm did not activate which resulted in overflow at 1110 hours.
ImmediateAction: Sludge pump stopped and cleaning commenced
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Waste oil tank lagging soaked with oil.   Garbgae collection area covered with oil.   Stbd bulkhead in Ae platform covered with waste oil.
DirectCause: Waste oil service tank gauge stuck  Human error
Proposedcorrectiveactions: BEfore starting pump to ensure gauge is working satsfactory.  Ensuring pump is stopped before taking up any other job
Corrective Action: BEfore starting pump to ensure gauge is working satsfactory. Ensuring pump is stopped before taking up any other job
LessionLearnt: while transferring oil from one tank to another person must remain standby till the procedure is over. After completion stop the pump and take other job in hand.
ReviewComments: Area cleaned immediately after the incident. Stuck float gauge repaired. And high level alarm tested satisfactorily.
ClosingNote: Area cleaned immediately after the incident. Stuck float gauge repaired. And high level alarm tested satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: ME DGU servo unit fail & Fuel actuator too low alarm activated. Soon ME rpm started increasing and there was no control from WH. ME control was taken back to ECR, but there was no control from ECR too.
IncidentEventDetails: 0950LT: ME DGU servo unit fail & Fuel actuator too low alarm activated. Soon ME rpm started increasing and there was no control from WH. ME control was taken back to ECR, but there was no control from ECR too.  1000LT: Engine stopped & found governor motor struck in 60 % load.   1027LT: Engine started from local EMR station.
ImmediateAction: Engine stopped & found governor motor struck in 60 % load. Engine started from local EMR station. Links from governor motor disconnected.  On investigation, on ME governor servo motor panel found servo motor break relay energised even after the engine was changed over to local station. Servo motor reseted by switching off contol power. When the servo motor was brought to 0 (zero) position, vessel was able to reset the activated alarms.(DGU servo drive unit fail&Fuel Acuator too slow).  1343LT: Manoevring chenged over to bridge controls
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: None
DirectCause: Battery life has expired and battery to be replaced
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition raised for new batterries and to be replaced.
Corrective Action: Requisition raised for new batterries and to be replaced.
ClosingNote: Contacted with maker for troubleshooting. Suspected battery trouble. Old spare battery replaced and problem rectified. New battery to be supplied to vessel.

IncidentDetails: Damage to tail rope .
IncidentEventDetails: 08/09/16 2300lt Vessel is moored at Oiltanking  617-619 terminal Antwerp . The breast lines had a very short lead .  Vessel is berth inside  a narrow channel where frequent large vessel are passing . In view above maximum number of lines from the ship were passed .  However due to the passing of a fast moving large vessel  ,there was a sudden surging causing the tail rope to part. Although the line got slightly rendered but still the line parted due to the large amplitude of surge. Second breast line rendered but tail rope was not damaged .
ImmediateAction: Tail rope renewed and all break adjustments checked again for all the mooring ropes .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 mooring tail damaged
DirectCause: Sudden surging of  own vessel while passing of nearby vessel
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safe mooring adjustments  of vessel with break testing to be done in due time at all times . Crew should be alert with nearby movement of vessels .
Corrective Action: Safe mooring adjustments  of vessel with break testing to be done in due time at all times . Crew should be alert with nearby movement of vessels .
ReviewComments: Ref. captioned Damage report, we have carried out investigation about this incident  . We appreciate the crew members for their swift response in passing the lines immediately.  After review, it was confirmed that the ropes were properly tended to and same was used to position the vessel The last brake rendering test was carried out as per Company policy and marking on the brake was correct (hope we checked it). The rope which parted was last visually checked in the month of June 2016 - found in good condition.  Based on the analysis, please find office comments in closing notes
ClosingNote: Cause ===== 1. Speed of the passing by vessel was excess enough to create a strong wave resulting in parting of mooring hose. 2. Due to sudden jerk caused by the waves of the passing by vessel, ropes parted prior winch brake could release the ropes.  3. Location of berth - With available information, this possibility could not be ruled out.    Corrective action ============== 1.  In such locations / river ports, more intensive monitoring of ropes shall be carried out by watch keepers especially while vessels are passing by.

IncidentDetails: Ship's crew opened hatch covers 2,3 then proceeded to open hatch # 5.All holds were battened down prior to opening at Wellington. While opening hatch # 5 it was found that 2 cleats was not removed and the lever operated to open hatch cover.On realising that cleats were still in position the lever was brought back to neutral position. But by then damage to the cleats had already occured.
IncidentEventDetails: While opening hatch cover # 5,    2 cleats were not removed before opening the hatch cover. This resulted in damage to the 2 cleats. One got bent and the others holding arrangement opened out.
ImmediateAction: Hatch lever was immediately brought back to the close position to reduce  pressure on the hydraulic system and the cleats.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Prior opening the hatch covers,it must be ensured that all the cleats are removed fully. This has to be checked by the duty officer.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was manoeuvring for berthing at Wellington bunker berth AQ3. At the forward stations heaving line was thrown to the jetty. This Heaving line fell on a car which was parked close to the bollard. There were many cars parked in 2 rows close to the bollard. The impact of the heaving line's monkey fist which was made of rubber resulted in cracking of the windshield of a Nissan Bluebird car.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was approching bunker berth AQ3. During berthing statations a heaving line was thrown fron forward, to pass vessel's mooring lines. This heaving line landed on a Nissan Bluebird car parked close to the jetty. The impact of the heaving line resulted in cracks formiing on the windshield of the car.
ImmediateAction: Moorings lines were passed very slowly to ensure no damages are caused to other cars parked close to the bollards.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Prior throwing the heaving line, the seaman must ensure that the areas are clear.

IncidentDetails: O/S Coronel Left finger ring sustained 2 cuts while picking up provisions at Douala port.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1800h 22 sept.2016 ship's provisions arrived and everybody helped in picking up/securing.During that time the O/S did not recognized that the pallets have protruding nails and when he picked the provision his left ring finger accidentally hit the nails causing a 2 cuts on his left finger ring.
ImmediateAction: He was immediately brought to the hospital for immediate treatment and 6 stitches was performed on his left ring finger cuts.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Situational awareness.  PPE to be worn during work place. Hazards of work to be discussed during tool box meeting and complied.
ClosingNote: to be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Injury to Terminal reefer technician onboard the vessel during vessel stay at Manzanillo. Technician Injured his Left Leg while climbing down the Ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1725 hrs LT, two reefer technicians (Jorge Enrique & Gustavo) boarded the vessel to attend Faulty reefer in position 161284, Assisted by Onboard Electrical officer.  At around 1800 hrs LT reefer technicians completed their work and Technician Jorge Enrique was decending through the ladder of Catwalk manhole of port side in Bay 16 while talking with other technician Gustavo, slipped from ladder but not falling on deck completely & managed himself to hang on the ladder with his hands. Due to the Fall Technician Jorge Enrique injured his left leg, with no visible apparant blood and was consious.   Same time Technician Gustavo informed Terminal about the incident. At around 1810, Ambulance and Paramedics arrived. With the help of Stevedors Injured Technician Jorge Enrique was placed on stretcher and took from vessel into the ambulance.
ImmediateAction: Immediately, Informed Agent, Office Terminal and other concerned parties. P&I surveroyor Boarded the vessel and Investigated about the incident. Letter Of Protest was issued by the Vessel to Harbour Master,Agent, Terminal Operator and Other concerned parties.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO FURTHER REPORT ON CONDITION OF INJURED REEFER TECHNICIAN.
DirectCause: UTTER CARELESSNESS OF THE INJURED REEFER TECHNICIAN.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: concur with vessel comments. no claims made

IncidentDetails: FWD 2 SPRING MOORING WIRE  ROPES TAILS PARTED DUE TO SUDDEN GUST OF WIND .
IncidentEventDetails: 1200 lt : Sudden increase in Wind speed 20-23 kts gusting to 30kts.Forward spring rope soft tails parted. Duty AB informed CCR   1202 lt : Master / D/O & C/O informed / Cargo stopped.Informed loading master for stopping cargo same informed to the port.  1203 lt :Alarm raise.Station called forward & aft.  1204 lt :Informed port control and asked for tug assistance & mooring gangs as soon as possible.  1206 lt :Engines ready.  1210 lt :vessel requested port control again for  tugs assistance on ch-13/09  1210 lt :crew preparing spare soft rope forwars for passing ashore. Gangway lifted to deck level.Loading master onboard with shore men for manifold disconnection.  1215 lt : vessel awaiting for tugs and shore mooring gag assistance  1220 lt : 2 tugs reached pushing from port side one fwd and one aft.  1223 lt : shore mooring gangs arrived, vessel passed 2 spring lines.  1225 lt : loading master requested to disconnect manifold.  1250-1306 lt : Hot gasing started to clear the loading arm for disconnection  1312 lt : Loading arm disconnected.   1315 lt : Moorings resumed.  1345 lt : Ship brought alongside with the help of Tugs & moorings.  1400 lt : Tugs away/Vessel confirmed maitianing alongisde.  1430 lt : Resume loading arm connection
ImmediateAction: GA Sounded, Port control reported, Cargo stopped, Tugs called for assistance,Manifold disconnected,Mooring stn called and New spring lines passed & Made fast, Vessel brought in position and in control.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two Nos. Of Mooring soft tail ropes parted
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Continous monitoring of weather conditions changes by all available means.  Engaging additional mooring in weather deteriotion conditions.
Corrective Action: Continous monitoring of weather conditions changes by all available means. Engaging additional mooring in weather deteriotion conditions.
ClosingNote: Effective immediate action was taken. Tugs were immediately asked for assistance and mooring stations called. Appreciate that the vessel was alongside again safe. Noted that the ship staff was trained again on mooring procedures.

IncidentDetails: While ballasting APK Stbd Tank in order to flush the tank before making entry into the tank for carrying out inspn, ballast water back flowed into Crew Laundry room and Upper deck Alleyway   (S/s) through the line meant for draining the content into the APK stbd which is designated as sewage hoiding tank.
IncidentEventDetails: 0510: APK Starboard ballasting started  0700: Oiler Vincent reported to CCR that water is accumulated in Upper deck alleyway.  0701: E/room asked to stop the aft pk ballasting immediately and to check the lineup  0702: After examine it was found that it is SW water and it has found ingress to Crew Laundry room also. It is noted that the water is coming from the drain Line.   0705 - 0800: All crew members were called and asked to rig up the Welden pumps. 2 Welden pumps were rigged up (1 in Laundry room and 1 in Alleyway) and accumulated water pumped out.  C/E reported that Grey water line filling Valve which is going to APK starboard tank was kept opened.   Water stopped comming through drain scuppers   APK Starboard tank deballasting started  0800-0820: Upper deck alleyway and Laundry room fresh water rinsed and mopped
ImmediateAction: 1) All crew member called and APK Tk ballasting stopped  2) Fault finding started ,Welden pump rigged and started pumping out accumulated water from laundry room and Upper deck alleyway.  3) Grey water line valve to APK Starboard Tank Closed.  3) Deballsting of APK Starboard tank
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 No of  Laundry machine stopped working in Crew Laundry room
DirectCause: 1) Grey water Line valve was kept open  2) Continuous manual sounding of tank was not taken during operation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Line up will be checked properly prior commencement of such operation and continuously sounding will be checked duriing such operation.
Corrective Action: Line up will be checked properly prior commencement of such operation and continuously sounding will be checked duriing such operation.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Line up will be checked properly prior commencement of such operation and continuously sounding will be checked duriing such operation
ClosingNote: Line up will be checked properly prior commencement of such operation and continuously sounding will be checked duriing such operation

IncidentDetails: Vessel was berthed alongside at Vishakapatnam, India on 22 June 2016 / 1020 LT. While ballasting APT tank steering gear compartment and emergency fire pump space was flooded, resulting in steering gear electrical panel and emergency fire pump motor getting damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was alongside at Bulk Berth, Vishakapatnam, discharging cargo of Pet Coke. Technical Superintendent (TSI) of the vessel was to visit the vessel at Vishakapatnam for a routine inspection. Prior his visit he was co-ordinating with vessel on various matters needed to be addressed during his visit. Master had received an email from the TSI to prepare FPT, APT, 3 TST (P) & 3 DB (S) for inspection.   19 June 2016 – Accordingly the tanks were opened (except No.3 DB S that was inaccessible due to cargo). 3 manhole covers of APT were opened in the steering gear room and ventilated while at sea prior arrival.       20 June 2016 – Inspection of Aft Peak ballast tank was carried out by TSI and Bosun between 1710 LT to 1730 LT after which bosun put the APT manhole covers back in place however he did not put the bolts and tighten them since he was not instructed to do so. There were no instructions to box up the inspected tanks and ship’s staff assumed that the tanks would be inspected by the Class NK surveyor the following day during annual survey that had been planned at Vishakapatnam.   21 June 2016 – After Class NK surveyor disembarked around 2230 LT, Chief Officer instructed 3rd Officer to send the duty AB (AB 3) to get all the manholes of the open ballast tanks boxed up for ballasting as required by vessel’s cargo discharge / draft condition at that time.     22 June 2016 – During the changeover of watch at around 0001 LT 3rd Officer informed 2nd Officer that all open manholes of inspected tanks have been boxed back. Around 0930 LT Chief Officer instructed 3rd Officer to ballast 3 TST (P&S) followed by pressing up 1 TST and 2 TST (P&S). However Master instructed 3rd Officer to start filling up APT instead as the tank was expected to take more time to ballast. APT tank is usually ballasted using GS pump. According to the ballasting sequence APT was to be ballasted last. Master’s advice was to ballast APT tank first and he did not want multiple tanks to be pressed up which would be difficult to monitor and cause an overflow.   Around 0950 LT APT ballasting was commenced monitored by 3rd Officer and duty OS (OS 2) who was to take soundings. APT was to be filled until full up to a maximum sounding of 6.49m. Around 1105 LT Chief Officer enquired on the status of APT. 3rd Officer reported APT sounding to be 5.50m (476m3) and continued ballasting. Around 1145 LT, 3rd Engineer reported on walkie-talkie steering gear room is flooded
ImmediateAction: Ballasting was immediately stopped.   De-ballasting of APT was commenced.   TSI on board informed who in turn informed the office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: • No.1 steering gear electric panel shorted and damaged rendering No.1 steering gear system out of use. • Dispensation from Flag state to sail without one steering gear unit. • Vessel exposed to risks, sailing with one steering gear unit • Possible off hire claim as vessel delayed departure from berth by six hours.   • Cost of EFP motor rewinding (appx. USD 1949) • Additional berth hire charges for 6 hours (appx. USD 415) • Cost of additional tug for un-berthing (appx. USD 655)  • Repair costs (approx. USD 30000 including shipment cost of new panel)  • Occasional Survey by Class to close the dispensation (appx USD 500)
DirectCause: APT was ballasted to full capacity without boxing up tank manholes resulting in flooding of steering gear room causing damage to No.1 steering gear electrical panel and emergency fire pump motor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Call for safety meeting to avoid re-occurrence of the said incident, 2. Additional warning posters to be posted in ship's office and ECR, cautioning Manhole cover status. 3. Persons employed for opening the manhole cover should be assigned for boxing up of the same.
Corrective Action: 1. Call for safety meeting to avoid re-occurrence of the said incident, 2. Additional warning posters to be posted in ship's office and ECR, cautioning Manhole cover status. 3. Persons employed for opening the manhole cover should be assigned for boxing up of the same.
ClosingNote: Case closed .

IncidentDetails: WHILE VESSEL WAS AT CHITTAGONG 'C' ANCHORAGE, THE MF/HF ANTENNA WAS STRUCK BY LIGHTNING, CAUSING THE UPPER PART OF THE ANTENNA TO BREAK OFF AND FALL ON THE MONKEY ISLAND DECK.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL ANCHORED AT CHITTAGONG 'C' ANCHORAGE. ONSET OF THUNDERSTORM WAS OBSERVED. AS THE THUNDERSTORM PASSED OVER THE VICINITY OF OWN VESSEL, IT WAS ACCOMPANIED BY LIGHTNING, WHICH LEAD TO THE INCIDENT.
ImmediateAction: THE MF/HF EQUIPMENT WAS POWERED 'OFF'. OTHER HIGH VOLTAGE EQUIPMENT ALSO POWERED 'OFF'. ALL CREW INFORMED TO REMAIN CAUTIOUS WHILE VENTURING OUTSIDE ACCOMODATION AND AVOID MOBILE PHONE USAGE.

IncidentDetails: On 06-Oct-2016/1645 LT, vessel departed from Khor Fakkan anchorage to proceed to anchor at OPL and wait for orders.  While preparing for anchor station prior heaving up stbd anchor, T-shaped bar (used for taking anchor lashing) which was kept on the gelutin bar stand, was being removed to clear the bow stopper. Two crew members lifted the bar, but it slipped out of hand of one of them. As the other crew member could not hold on the bar due to sudden disbalance of weight, it slipped from his hand and into water through the hawse pipe.
IncidentEventDetails: 06 Oct 2016 - Khor Fakkan  C Anchorage  1530 hrs - 30 Min Notice to E/R for Main Engines and Anchor Stations.  1540 Hrs - Control Test done.  1550 Hrs - Loss of Stbd Anchor Lashing T-Bar reported to bridge.  1554 Hrs - SBE  1645 Hrs - Anchor Aweigh / Vessel Proceeding to OPL  1900 Hrs - Anchored at OPL
ImmediateAction: Checked for any personal injury to the crew and damage to the anchoring system. No injury and damage found. Vessel procceded to OPL. Office was informed once vessel was out of port limit and clear of any traffic.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: T-shaped bar required for anchor lashing lost.
DirectCause: The weight of the heavy lashing bar was not taken on a rope prior lifting, in order to save it during accidental slip from hands.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As the bar is heavy, and diffcult to pick up due to the access to its resting position, its weight should be taken on a rope prior lifting, in order to save it during accidental slip from hands.
Corrective Action: As the bar is heavy, and diffcult to pick up due to the access to its resting position, its weight should be taken on a rope prior lifting, in order to save it during accidental slip from hands.
ClosingNote: T - shape bar fabricated and supplied

Vessel: DK ABDUL RAZZAK KHALID ZAID AL-KHALID
IncidentDetails: A tail rope parted near the eye under stress whilst  transitting the lock gate in Gent - Terneuzen canal.
IncidentEventDetails: After loading at Gent the vessel  was transitting the Gent  Terneuzen canal under pilotage. In the canal there is a lock gate which the vessel had to transit enroute to the sea.  The vessel entered the 'Westsluis lock in the early morning and was moored inside using her spring lines 2  of them and a  single head and stern line soft rope. On completion of mooring the gate behind the vessel was shut and the lock filled with water. After the level had been reached the gate ahead of the vessel was opened at which time it can be apparently said due to the difference in the level of the water inside the lock and outside , the vessel surged ahead, the aft spring became slack , seeing which  dead slow astern movement was given, but the vessel was still surging ahead, and so slow astern was given,but by then one of the spring rope broke near the eye attached to the bollard. All through the incident 2 tugs were fast forward and Aft.
ImmediateAction: Tug which was made fast aft was asked to pull and all the moorings cast off and the vessel made its way outside the lock.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Tail rope parted
DirectCause: There was congestion at the lock, with many vessel waiting to transit the lock, therefore sufficient time had not been given before th lock gate was opened, leading to the water inside the lock moving out, leading to the movement of the vessel.  The brake on the mooring winch was tightened more than the marking therefore it did not render.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Watch to be kept whilst transitting lock and action taken early, training to be given on brake rendering and renedering markings checked
Corrective Action: Watch to be kept whilst transitting lock and action taken early, training to be given on brake rendering and renedering markings checked
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Crew Briefing conducted ,Job closed!!!

IncidentDetails: Found that Ballast tank FPK,1W'S,2W'S  were showing empty and Forward Draft was shown as 0.36 Mtrs in the CCR monitor.On further investigation found that Copper lines for Air supply forBallast and Forward Draft have broken due to heavy weather leading to wrong reading in the CCR monitor(Please refer attached picture).
IncidentEventDetails: Found that Ballast tank FPK,1W'S,2W'S  were showing empty and Forward Draft was shown as 0.36 Mtrs in the CCR monitor.On further investigation found that Copper lines for Air supply forBallast and Forward Draft have broken due to heavy weather leading to wrong reading in the CCR monitor(Please refer attached picture).
ImmediateAction: Ermato couplings are in place and no leaks noticed thereafter.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to Copper line of Forward draft gauge,FPK,1W'S,2W'S
DirectCause: WEAR AND TEAR OF THE EQUIPMENT
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL LINES HAS BEEN CHECKED AGAIN AND FOUND IN SATISFACTORY CONDITION
Corrective Action: ALL LINES HAS BEEN CHECKED AGAIN AND FOUND IN SATISFACTORY CONDITION
LessionLearnt: Vessel's fittings and loose gears on deck to be well secured prior entering area of expected heavy weather and all items to be inspected once vessel out of the heavy weather area.
ReviewComments: We agree to the vessel's proposed CA.
ClosingNote: NMR Closed

IncidentDetails: While lowering offsigning crew baggage using gantline on jetty, bag handle broke and bag fell into the water between ship side and jetty.  Rope was turned around the bag and tied on bag's handle.
IncidentEventDetails: 1800: Signing off crew baggage being lowered to jetty.  1802: Last bag being lowered to jetty. Bag was secured with gant line rope and one heaving line attached for handling from jetty side.  1803: While bag was about to reach jetty bag handle broke and bag fell into the sea between jetty and ship side.  1803: Informed to terminal loading Master and security. Bag sighted below the jetty structure.  1805: Security personnel arrived on jetty.  1815: Boat arrived and lifted the bag form water.  1818: Bag handed over to respective crew member.
ImmediateAction: Terminal Loading Master and Terminal Security was informed and terminal boat arrived to pickup bag from water.
ClosingNote: Agree with the vessel's CA. IR closed

IncidentDetails: Minor Oil Spill on deck While bunkering
IncidentEventDetails: We were to receive 900 MT bunker at Ust Luga, It was decided to take this bunker in No1 & No2 FOT. ( Both 85% ).  We had Pre Bunkering meeting held. Bunkering Started 05th Oct 16 2040 Lt With agreed pumping rate of 250 m3/hour , It was observed oil temp to be 24 Deg c. The sounding of No1 & No2 FOT tank was monitored frequently  & Air pipe Cont Monitored as these tanks are not connected to Overflow tank, 22:00 Hrs The sounding of No2 FOT = 1.0M & No1 FOT = 0.96 M  2215 We observed oil started to come out of No2 FOT air pipe slowly. Immediately bunkering was ordered to be stopped, there was few minutes delay as the bunker barge watch keeper did not act Quickly. Bunkering stopped at 2218 Lt. 2220 Master arrived at the scene of Spill  & quick assessment done , No spill over board, All oil contained mostly in Save all & about 30 Ltrs on deck same contained & collection started. Soundings of tanks ordered It was assessed that Due to Cold Oil & Air lock the oil came out of air Pipe, sounding revealed the Pumping rate has been more than agreed upon. Around 2230 office informed of the incident by phone,  After investigation & checks Bunkering Resumed at 2300 Hrs  with great Caution in N01 FOT , When we reached 85% Tank capacity  , We resumed bunker in no2 FOT & Completed bunkering 06 Oct 16 00:40 LT
ImmediateAction: Bunkering Stopped, Oil contained, Soundings taken , Investigated . Oil Cleaned up , Save all Cleaned. Office informed by phone.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: More vigilent on the pumping rate as agreed between Vessel & Bunker Barge
Corrective Action: More vigilent on the pumping rate as agreed between Vessel & Bunker Barge
LessionLearnt: Yes
ReviewComments: In investigation report
ClosingNote: Understand, magic pipe found in tank and same is removed. Much appreciated the ship staff for effforts to find the root cause of same.pls discuss same in next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: 1.During Mooring for STS Operation After Vessel Had Anchored . Vessel was Passing Head lines, after picking up the slack on the lines. vessel was trying to eqalize both lines, While Doing so daughter vessel rolled viloently and first her one rope parted and than own vessel one wire rope parted.    2.Fwd Spring Line tail rope parted Due to Excessive Strian On Line    3.Parting of fwd head line tail Rope    4.During STS Ops Aft Mooring Tails 2 Nos Parted    5.After UnMooring from Daughter Vessel found Fwd Spring Rope Tail Partly Damage.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Vessel had planned to carry out STS ops with Pretty World, after making fast all preety worlds lines.V essel was Passing Head lines, after picking up the slack on the lines. vessel was trying to equalize both lines. While Doing so daughter vessel rolled viloently and first her one rope parted and than own vessel one wire rope parted.    2.After Making fast all Vessels Line, While Other vessel was adjusting its FWd Spring Lines, Excessive load came on own vessels fwd sprongs Due to excessive rolling of daughter vessel, Excessiveload came on Fwd spring an dtails parted    3.While Discharging ops, the daugther vessel started violently rolling and causing excessive strain on own vessel and one fwd head line tail rope got damaged and broke    4.While Discharging ops, the daugther vessel started violently rolling and causing excessive strain on own vessel and 2 Af tstern  line tail rope got damaged and parted due to chaffing    5.While Discharging ops, the daugther vessel started violently rolling and causing excessive strain on own vessel and 2 Af tstern  line tail rope got damaged  due to chaffing
ImmediateAction: 1.Wire Rope changed after Completion of STS    2,3,4 & 5. Tail Rope Renwed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 Wire Damaged and 6 Tail Ropes are Damaged
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renewal of Tails and wires , request other vessel for proper lubrication of vessel rope tails during all STS Ops.Mantianing Equal weight on both Mother vessel and daughter lines.
Corrective Action: Renewal of Tails and wires , request other vessel for proper lubrication of vessel rope tails during all STS Ops.Mantianing Equal weight on both Mother vessel and daughter lines.
ReviewComments: Weather at the incident:- Wind :- 3 X SW  Sea  :- moderate Swell :- 0.5 to 1 m   Mooring Station list :- FWD :- chief officer,Bosun,AB,OS,Tr.OS Bridge:- DO,AB (AB sent for Aft station after first Line fwd and aft made fast) Bridge wing :- Master Aft: - Pumpman,AB,Cadet.2nd Officer  Mooring plan as follows :- Receiving Vessel :- 4 + 2 Fwd / 4 + 2 Aft Ruby Express:- 4 + 2 Fwd / 4 + 2 Aft  A ) Fatigue Work rest hour records were inspected and found that there was no contravention  of the rest hour’s requirement which could have contributed to this incident.  Safety Bulletin   b) Role of D&A in this incident Alcohol issue records were checked and found not exceeding the limits as per company requirement.   d) Analysis of evidences including review of statements, photographs and other evidences statement from master were analyzed and noticed to be in tandem.    CAUSE ANALYSIS  Direct  Due Excessive violent rolling and Pitching of Daughter Vessel. Indirect Causes  No proper lubrication done near the closed fairlead to avoid chaffing. Root Causes  Lack of compliance – Good seamanship practices of passing the rope.
ClosingNote: Weather at the incident:- Wind :- 3 X SW  Sea  :- moderate Swell :- 0.5 to 1 m   Mooring Station list :- FWD :- chief officer,Bosun,AB,OS,Tr.OS Bridge:- DO,AB (AB sent for Aft station after first Line fwd and aft made fast) Bridge wing :- Master Aft: - Pumpman,AB,Cadet.2nd Officer  Mooring plan as follows :- Receiving Vessel :- 4 + 2 Fwd / 4 + 2 Aft Ruby Express:- 4 + 2 Fwd / 4 + 2 Aft  A ) Fatigue Work rest hour records were inspected and found that there was no contravention  of the rest hour’s requirement which could have contributed to this incident.  Safety Bulletin   b) Role of D&A in this incident Alcohol issue records were checked and found not exceeding the limits as per company requirement.   d) Analysis of evidences including review of statements, photographs and other evidences statement from master were analyzed and noticed to be in tandem.    CAUSE ANALYSIS  Direct  Due Excessive violent rolling and Pitching of Daughter Vessel. Indirect Causes  No proper lubrication done near the closed fairlead to avoid chaffing. IncidentDetails: Cheif cook had minor cut injury due to glass tumbler breaking on pouring hot water from the water boiler.
IncidentEventDetails: During tea break , cheif ccok poured hot water from Electric water boiler kept in crew mess room into a glass tumbler. As sson as hot boiling water was poured , tumbler shattered into peices ,& minor cut injury was sutained by cheif cook.
ImmediateAction: First aid was given to cheif cook & cut was cleaned & dressed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut in right palm. First aid given.
DirectCause: Defective Boliler where auto cut off did not work & Incorrect procedures of using glass tumbler without handle for hot beverages instead of Proper tea Mugs with handle. Also
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Notice posted on microwaves / boilers to use proper tea mugs/cups instead of plain glass tumbler  when using for hot beverages. New requesition for Electric water boilers will be raised .
Corrective Action: Notice posted on microwaves / boilers to use proper tea mugs/cups instead of plain glass tumbler  when using for hot beverages. New requesition for Electric water boilers will be raised .
LessionLearnt: Use of proper tool or equipment for the proposed use.
ReviewComments: Chief cook should be aware of consequences of pouring hot water into the bowl not suitable for the use.Its a case of sheer negligence leading to this injury.
ClosingNote: Noted notice posted. Injury report closed as suitable action taken.

IncidentDetails: MOORING ROPE  PARTED

As Al Betroleya passed slightly ahead of the anchored Mother vessel,Pilot advised tugs to Pull Max in order to align the vessels.  Due to the above excessive load on the lines both aft ships lines that  were passed to the tug parted.  This happened around 1736hrs LT.  Vessel had prepared another set of lines to be passed to the tugs,but Pilot in consultation with port control at 1742hrs L.T Postponed the operation  to next day due to insufficient daylight.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel required high MBL rope for making fast tugs, especially operation like STS where vessel require to manuever with tugs made fast.
Corrective Action: Vessel required high MBL rope for making fast tugs, especially operation like STS where vessel require to manuever with tugs made fast.

IncidentDetails: Reported by chief cook, After starting Galley A/C , its cooling effect didnot observed.Inspected Galley A/c found comp is running hot,pin hole in suc pressure gauge line ,oil  & refrigerent leaks.A/C compressor stoped vv closed

ImmediateAction: A/C compressor stoped vv closed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: leaks of 3.5 kg of refrigarent.
DirectCause: material failure due to vibration.

Corrective Action: Brazing of leaking refrigerant line & recharge of refrigerant in system.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: Casing of one of the hoisting motors of No.2 crane developed a hole, leading to hydraulic oil leaking.
IncidentEventDetails: During discharging operations, the crane operator stoped cargo work due to oil leaking inside the crane housing. Upon inspection by ship's staff, it was found that there was a hole in the hoisting motor casing, through which the system lub oil was leaking.
ImmediateAction: Crane power stopped. Oil collected. Situation assessed and the only solution was to replace the entire motor. Office contacted and it was decided that shore workshop assistance was required.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hole in casing of No.2 crane hoisting motor casing, requiring the motor to be replaced.
DirectCause: Machinery component breakdown due to fatigue (when the damaged motor casing was removed, a piece of the gear inside was found broken. Safe to presume that this broken piece hit the casing inside, causing the hole). It is also possible that due to excessive misuse or rough handling of the cranes over the years, could have added to the machinery fatigue.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crane operators (ship staff and stevedores) to be advised to operate cranes carefully and avoid un-necessary jerky operations. Also, machinery spares to be supplied from reliabel sources and to be of good quality.

IncidentDetails: Damage to the Lifeboat porthole glass.
IncidentEventDetails: During lowering of port L/Boat at S'pore EOPL anchorage, the small D shackle of self lowering wire connecting to the L/B davit got dismantled suspect due to vibration and the pin might have been loose. The D shackle unfortunately fell on the small porthole round glass on the outboard side of the L/B and the gllass got cracked.  For the time being we have temporarily replaced the cracked glass with an acrylic sheet. Awaiting suply of new glass.
ImmediateAction: Temporarily replaced the cracked glass with an acrylic sheet. Awaiting suply of new glass. D-Shackle has been changed with a new one.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: L/B porthole glass damaged
DirectCause: Procedural error while inspecting L/B
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regularly check any irregularities in safety equipments including L/B for early detection of faults.
Corrective Action: Regularly check any irregularities in safety equipments including L/B for early detection of faults.
LessionLearnt: Responsible officer to carry out the checks on the equipment diligently.Safety officer to ensure that the life boat is ready for lowering and maneuvering prior lowering. Lesson learnt to be shared with the fleet vessels
ClosingNote: Incident report closed

IncidentDetails: Newly built vessel by Dalian COSCO KHI Ships Engineering Co., Ltd. contacted with the channel buoy BY(3) after pilot disembarkation. The incident caused damge to the propeller blades and channel buoy. No injuries and pollution occured.
IncidentEventDetails: 14th of Sep. 2016 at 1548 hours newly bulit MV BW Durum departed Dalian COSCO KHI Ships Engineering Co., Ltd. At 1612 hours about 2.2 nm prior to Pilot Station, Pilot informed of his disembarkation and he was escorted by Third Officer to the pilot ladder. The Pilot advised Master to pay attention to the fishing boat before he left the wheel house, the vessel on dead slow ahead.  Noticed fishing vessel approaching from the port bow crossing towards the starboard bow and then stopped. Noticed that BY(3) buoy was closing towards the port bow.  Helm ordered 5 degrees to starboard and placed engine on full ahead to bring the rudder effect. Due to the presence of fishing boat on the starboard bow, could not place the helm hard over to starboard. When the fishing vessel started to cross back to port, helm ordered hard to starboard. Due to strong abeam SE current above 2 knots, at 1618 hours vessel contacted with BY(3) buoy on the port side hull of no. 4 cargo hold. Upon contact, helm ordered hard to port to clear of propeller. BY(3) buoy was sighted clear of vessel by Third Officer.
ImmediateAction: Informed the Company. At Dalian OPL while anchored, de-ballasted no. 4 WBT P/S and lowered rescue boat into the water. Turned the propeller to check the five blades and inspected no. 4 WBT P/S against dents/damages.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damge to five propeller blade resulting to untimely dry docking. No injuries
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During take over process of a new vessel, sufficient time must be devoted to the appraisal process while passage planning,especially with a vessel operating with ECDIS as a primary means of navigation.ENC and the power to the ECDIS must be available well in time (at least three days) in advance of the takeover date.All items of the appraisal section must be verified. Vessel and superintendent takeover checklist (OF MA 40/OFMA 09) and risk assessment must be discussed with the crew and clear timelines and work delegation must be identified and followed to achieve the desired targets. Passage plan meeting is held prior depature.All hazards and duties, during critical passages like pilotage or transit through constrained waterways must be discussed. Master pilot exchange to be done as per the points covered in the MPX checklist. Any shortcut to this exercise is filled with the probability of missing out on important navigational information.
Corrective Action: During take over process of a new vessel, sufficient time must be devoted to the appraisal process while passage planning,especially with a vessel operating with ECDIS as a primary means of navigation.ENC and the power to the ECDIS must be available well in time (at least three days) in advance of the takeover date.All items of the appraisal section must be verified. Vessel and superintendent takeover checklist (OF MA 40/OFMA 09) and risk assessment must be discussed with the crew and clear timelines and work delegation must be identified and followed to achieve the desired targets. Passage plan meeting is held prior depature.All hazards and duties, during critical passages like pilotage or transit through constrained waterways must be discussed. Master pilot exchange to be done as per the points covered in the MPX checklist. Any shortcut to this exercise is filled with the probability of missing out on important navigational information.
LessionLearnt: Identified master not having lookout placed on the bridge during maneuvering. Master not verifying the passage plan on ECDIS. 2/Officer not studying the whole passage and hastily planning the passage
ReviewComments: As per attached investigation report
ClosingNote: As per attached investigation report

IncidentDetails: During departure Hongkong. Chief officer reported Mooring winch middle hydrulic oil line bursted and oil leaking. On Inspection by CE found oil control valve inlet  line nut dislodged from place and found completely wasted.
IncidentEventDetails: 1648 : Reported Hydraulic oil leak from forward station from port side mooring winch.  1650 : Trying to release the ropes out from gear for picking up the ropes by hand.  1654 : One of the winch gear been released and the line casted off(one spring).  1658 : All crew from aft station shifted to forward for assistance except Second Officer.  1700 : Trying to release the ropes from the gear but unable to do so.  1705 : All crew from engine and galley send forward for assistance.  1708 : All headlines casted off by shifting vessel ahead and pulling with forklift.  1709 : All stern lines casted off.  1710 : Two headlines onboard by pulling with hand.  1711 : All stern lines onboard and aft holding on one spring.  1715 : Reported from fwd. station that headlines are very heavy for picking up by hand, coming very slowly.  1716 : 2 no’s headlines stuck with the stern lines of fwd. vessel and very hard to come up.  1720 : Third officer send to aft station and Second officer transferred fwd.  1725 : Started using the tug capstans by gantline arrangement for pulling the rope to get some assistance.  1730 : 3rd head line on board by pulling with hand.  1732 : Fwd. reported 4th head line coming in slowly as it is jammed in our anchor fluke.  1736 : Rope cleared off from the fluke and hove in using hand.  1740 : 1 spring line on board using hand.  1741 : Vessel shifted aft to get slack on the second spring line. Same casted off by pulling with forklift.  1742 : All lines cast off F & A, all lines onboard BT and Propeller clear.  1800: Vessel cleaned up all oil contained within the save all trays.
ImmediateAction: Same pipe removed from place and brought to workshop .Nut renewed from spare after cutting and welding the same pipe(stainless steel).
DirectCause: Excesive wear and tear of the machinery / aging factor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular checking of hydraulic pipelines and connections / do not cover the pipleline with denso tape and all.
Corrective Action: Regular checking of hydraulic pipelines and connections / do not cover the pipleline with denso tape and all.
LessionLearnt: IMPORTANCE OF ROUTINE INSPECTION
ClosingNote: HYDRAULIC PIPELINES AND COUPLINGS ARE TO BE REGULARLY INSPECTED AND TO BE REPAIRED/REPLACED ON FIRST SIGN OF DAMAGE/WEAR DOWN.

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: A mooring soft rope at aft springs is chafed (outer  skin is cut) due continuos rolling motion of vessel whilst alongside berth at Fujairah Vopak #2 .  Among all the lead for only this line is short (abt 20 mtrs).
IncidentEventDetails: At 2035 LT a mooring soft rope at aft springs is chafed (outer  skin is cut) due continuos rolling motion of vessel whilst alongside berth at Fujairah Vopak #2 .  Among all the lead for only this line is short (abt 20 mtrs).
ImmediateAction: Vessel requested terminal to send in mooring team for replacing the line,  and changed the respective rope to end to end.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One number soft mooring rope chafed / cut 15~20 mtrs from eye
DirectCause: Terminal located without breakwater protection and ships always rolling and surging. Almost near continuos monitoring of ropes is required.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: canvas cover can be applied at fairleads for normal mooring ropes as well.
Corrective Action: canvas cover can be applied at fairleads for normal mooring ropes as well.
LessionLearnt: 01. Vessel made fast to open berth, mooring lines to be continuously tended to. 02. Lead of the aft spring was not adequate - Only 20 mtrs length was the lead leading to increased tension on the ropes,Spring lines should be run, so far as practicable, parallel to the fore and aft line of the vessel.  03. Vessel's port stay was more than 40 hours - leading to greater exposure of these ropes to stress factors. 04. Unequal weight distribution on the lines - To avoid tension on lines due to continuous surging, as per Mooring master's advise, the remaining mooring lines were kept slack a little bit, but as the loading arms were connected, to hold the vessel in position,, the spring lines were kept tight 05. Canvas sheathing was not provided to the mooring ropes at the point of contact at fair lead , Though grease was applied at the fair lead at few occassions, canvas sheathing was not provided. Grease was applied on the previous day of the incident , but was not applied over the fair lead on the day of the incident. 06. Probable over tightening of the brakes, thereby inhibiting the BRC of the winch, leading to parting of the ropes 07.
ReviewComments: Analysis of the weather reports indicates that the vessel was subject to continuous westerly swell and SSW wind conditions, causing the vessel to surge at the berth continuously. Vessel is exposed to high seas as no break water is available.
ClosingNote: All corrective actions closed and the IR submitted to the Terminal

IncidentDetails: Please note that vessel is experiencing severe weather since yesterday. During morning rounds on deck found pilot ladder choks scattered on the main deck. Upon further investigation and inspection of the pilot ladder found one spreader cracked.  Waves were not so high to reach the ladder but strong wind blow caused
ImmediateAction: Ladder tightly secured in place with cover on top.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pilot ladder spreader and choks damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pilot ladders to be kept in stores during heavy weather and ocean passage.
LessionLearnt: All loose items, however big, which is not rigidly attached to the ships structure, during heavy wx must be protected from movement, by lashing, securing in place or if possible moved to a constricted space. The consrticted space may not necessarily be inside a store but can be designated on deck also.
ReviewComments: Nil
ClosingNote: All loose items, however big, which is not rigidly attached to the ships structure, during heavy wx must be protected from movement, by lashing, securing in place or if possible moved to a constricted space. The consrticted space may not necessarily be inside a store but can be designated on deck also.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was at Sea and engine room in manned mode. Fourth engineer was on watch and working in the bottom floor when he found water leaking from #4 unit followed by M.E jacket water pressure low which led to automatic slowdown of main engine. All engineers assembled in engine room and stopped M.E in consultation with bridge. No. 4 unit was isolated and damage assessed. Office informed and as per instruction dismantled and started renewing #4 unit cylinder head cooling jacket.
IncidentEventDetails: 02- Nov 16  22:32 LT - Main Eng jackt cooling water pr loww activated Main Eng slow down.  22:54 LT- Maqin Eng Stopped Control transfered to ECR.  23:00 LT - Drifting at safe location.  23: 15 LT - Informed Tecn Supdt ( Mr.Kunal Chandra)  23: 45 LT - All spares fetched comence repair.  03- Nov- 16   01:36 LT- Telecon with MArine Supdt ( Capt. Moses)  01:42 LT - Informed Navig8  01:52 LT - Informed Cheveron QI Via voice.  02:12 LT - Informed Cheveron voyage manager Via voice.  02:20 LT - send initial emergency notification all concern parties.  03:15 LT - # 4 unit cylinder head removed from place.  05:00LT -  Cracked jacket removed / Cleaning of cylinder head and liner top surface, in progress.  09:30LT - New spare jacket fitted on cylinder head.   10:35LT-  Cylinder head fitted back in position.  15:00LT - Completed repairs.  15:24 Lt - Main Eng blown thru   15:30 LT - Main Eng tested/ Stby/ Resumed voyage  16:00 Lt-  RFA/ Main Eng on 82 rpm and all parameters normal.
ImmediateAction: All engineers assembled in engine room and stopped M.E in consultation with bridge. No. 4 unit was isolated and damage assessed, Office informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MAin Eng # 4 unit cylinder head jacket cracked.
DirectCause: Refer to the Incident Investigation report
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Refer to the corrective actions as identified below
Corrective Action: 1. As per instruction manual jacking tool was used to fit the cylinder head jacket in place, hammering and forcefully fitting in place was avoided. 2. Fitting and alignment carried out and centering - aligning screws matched properly as per instruction manual. 3. New jacket was physically check for any crack or material defects. 4. All M.E units thoroughly purged off for any air pockets after the repairs. Engine parameters maintained so as to avoid any sudden surge in temperature. 5. Cooling water system chemical dosing corrosion inhibiting properties are maintained as per instruction manual. Main engine cooling water temp and cylinder exhaust temperature maintained as per instruction manual.  6. More frequent monitoring and maintaining of Main Engine parameters during engine watch.
LessionLearnt: Attaching the Incident Investigation report
ReviewComments: Incident Investigation completed, waiting for closure of CA
ClosingNote: Investigation and corrective measures completed

IncidentDetails: Vessel at sea, proceeding to Bejaia Pilot station.  Vessel expericiening Heavy sea, swell and Gale.  Fwd Storage box for Lifejackets and Immersion suit lashing gave way and resulted in one life jacket missing  in the period of darkness.
IncidentEventDetails: 09 Nov 16 /1600 LT: EOSP / Vessel Arrived.  09 Nov 16 /1600 LT : Vessel instructed by the port to drift outside the port limits and await berthing instructions.  09 Nov 16 /1700 LT : Vessel started slow steaming to minimise the effect of swell and sea.  09 Nov 16 /1700 LT - 10 Nov 16 /0800 LT: Vessel experiencing Heavy Rolling and wind gusting upto 35 Kts.
ImmediateAction: Same was not noticed till 10 Nov 16 morning when crew members went for normal day work.  Storage box was lashed and ships crew searched the deck area for the missing lifejacket, none found.  Expected went overboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fwd Lifejacket missing.
DirectCause: Additional Lashing was not taken for the storage box.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Storage box lashed with additional lashing.
Corrective Action: Storage box lashed with additional lashing.
ClosingNote: Missing lifejacket was immediately replaced from spare one. Extra lashing was carried out. Crew trained again for heavy weather precaution.

IncidentDetails: Nipple provided to connect main line and pressure reducing line of M/E F.O Flowmeter inlet  line located in the purifier room, which got cracked due to heavy vibration .Fuel oil spilled heaviely with pressure  all over the purifier room pipe lines and pumps  .
IncidentEventDetails: On 11-Nov-2016,at about 13:10 Hrs during routine round 3rd Engnr. found fuel oil leaking heavily with pressure from M/E fuel oil flow meter inlet pipe. Bridge was informed and started slowing down M/E . Upon inspection, found Threaded nipple which connects pressure reducing valve and flow meter inlet pipe broken. M/E stopped at 1348 and M/E flow meter isolated from line. After reducing the pressure from line, broken piece of nipple in pipe thread removed by stud extractor and valve fitted with new nipple. System put in use and found no leak. M/E started and vessel resumed passage at 14:24 Hrs.     Appx. 300 Litrs of fuel oil spilled in purifier, pumps and pipe line laggings.  Ship staff cleaned spilled oil and presently purifier room is free of gases and no fire hazard present.
ImmediateAction: Engine stopped imediately , Pump stopped and locked mannualy.Valves for the flow meter reducer line and valve from service tank outlet to M/E  closed imediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 11-Nov-2016,at about 13:10 Hrs during routine round 3rd Engnr. found fuel oil leaking heavily with pressure from M/E fuel oil flow meter inlet pipe. Bridge was informed and started slowing down M/E . Upon inspection, found Threaded nipple which connects pressure reducing valve and flow meter inlet pipe broken. M/E stopped at 1348 and M/E flow meter isolated from line. After reducing the pressure from line, broken piece of nipple in pipe thread removed by stud extractor and valve fitted with new nipple. System put in use and found no leak. M/E started and vessel resumed passage at 14:24 Hrs.     Appx. 300 Litrs of fuel oil spilled in purifier, pumps and pipe line laggings.  Ship staff cleaned spilled oil and presently purifier room is free of gases and no fire hazard present.
DirectCause: Heavy vibration and material failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Frequent checking of all connections and regular monitoring
Corrective Action: Frequent checking of all connections and regular monitoring
LessionLearnt: FREQUENT MONITORING OF MACHINERY
ClosingNote: FREQUENT MONITORING OF MACHINERY, ESPECIALLY IN SUSPECT AREAS PRONE TO VIBRATION

IncidentDetails: IG BLOWER MOTOR TRIPPED DURING COT PURGING . FOUND THAT TWO OF THE MOTOR WINDINGS WERE SHORT, END COVER WAS CRACKED AND END COVER BOLTS CAME OUT. PURGING OPERATION WAS STOPPED.
IncidentEventDetails: 02NOV16/1200 HRS- COT PURGING STARTED  02NOV16/1415 HRS- IG BLOWER MOTOR TRIPPED  02NOV16/ 2200 HRS- BLOWER IMPELLER AND MOTOR  REMOVED FROM PLACE. MEANWHILE TRIED TO START THE IGG SYSTEM WHICH HAS BEEN INOPERATIONAL FOR LONG AND INVESTIGATION WAS GOING ON IN CORRESPONDENCE WITH THE MAKERS. BUT THERE WAS NO RESPONSE FROM THE PANEL ON 'AUTO' AS WELL AS 'MANUAL' MODE. STARTED COMMUNICATION WITH THE MAKERS REGARDING THE SAME.  03NOV16/0600 HRS- IMPELLER FITTED IN PLACE WITH THE OVERHAULED MOTOR  03NOV16/0800 HRS- ALL PIPE CONNECTIONS RESTORED  03NOV16/0900 HRS- IG STARTED AND COT PURGING RESUMED. BUT VIBRATION WAS FOUND VERY HIGH SAME AS BEFORE  04NOV16/0130 HRS- IG BLOWER MOTOR TRIPPED, END COVER BOLTS CAME OUT DUE TO HIGH VIBRATION AND ONE OF THE BOLTS HEAD SHEARED OFF. RENEWED THE BROKEN BOLT AND TIGHTENED ALL THE BOLTS. STARTED AGAIN BUT FOUND THE END COVER BOLTS COMING OUT AGAIN.   04NOV16/ 0330 HRS- IG STARTED AGAIN AFTER SECURING THE END COVER BOLTS WITH NUTS  04NOV16/ 0600 HRS- IG BLOWER MOTOR TRIPPED AGAIN IN THE SAME WAY AS THE OTHER MOTOR.  FOUND THAT MOTOR INSULATION IS ZERO AND END COVER BOLTS BROKEN.   04NOV16/ 1600 HRS- STARTED FOR GIBRALTOR TO PICK UP SPARE NEW MOTOR AND TECHNICIAN
ImmediateAction: REPLACED THE BLOWER MOTOR WITH AN SPARE OVERHAULED ONE.  AFTER RUNNING FOR AROUND 21 HRS, THIS MOTOR ALSO TRIPPED IN THE SAME WAY. MEANWHILE TRIED STARTING IGG SYSTEM BUT THERE WAS NO RESPONSE FROM THE PANEL ON 'AUTO' AS WELL AS 'MANUAL' MODE. STARTED COMMUNICATION WITH THE MAKERS FOR THE SAME.  AS PER OFFICE INSTRUCTION, STARTED MOVING TO GIBRALTOR TO PICK UP SPARE MOTOR AND TECHNICIAN.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: TWO BLOWER MOTORS DAMAGED BEYOND REPAIR, COT PURGING DELAYED.
DirectCause: IG BLOWER IMPELLER WAS IMBALANCED RESULTING IN VERY HIGH VIBRATION WHICH WAS CAUSING THE REPEATED FAILURE OF THE BLOWER MOTORS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: THE BLOWER MOTOR AND IMPELLER HAS BEEN RENEWED AND FOUND SATISFACTORY. THE IMPELLER AND MOTOR IN USE HAVE BEEN OFFLANDED FOR REPAIR. THE VIBRATION OF THE IG BLOWER TO BE CLOSELY MONITORED. IGG SYATEM TO BE KEPT OPERATIONAL ALL THE TIME.
Corrective Action: THE BLOWER MOTOR AND IMPELLER HAS BEEN RENEWED AND FOUND SATISFACTORY. THE IMPELLER AND MOTOR IN USE HAVE BEEN OFFLANDED FOR REPAIR. THE VIBRATION OF THE IG BLOWER TO BE CLOSELY MONITORED. IGG SYATEM TO BE KEPT OPERATIONAL ALL THE TIME.
ClosingNote: The blower and motor has been renewed. Old impeller was suspected unbalanced hence same is being planned for balancing

IncidentDetails: Incident happened during berthing in port of Vishakhapatnam we did several maneouvering commands and main engine at normal operation, after several astern command condition ME at stop waiting for another command from bridge, then astern telegraph was received again and start the engine but remote to open starting air was not given we did several times but to no avail since we have pilot on board and maneouvering is so critical we decided to transfer immediately to emergency maneouvering at engine side until along side safely. At this point of time we continue trouble shooting the event of problem cause it shows only ME-B system alarm and failure. We did reset the alarm and acknowledge the same. We try to start again main engine but the problem is still the same. We even put selector to FWE and back to stanby engine if there was any reset but still to no avail we check auxiliary contactorTAHx,TASX and FEX yet there is signal on both.
IncidentEventDetails: So far there is no damage in person nor property at all
ImmediateAction: The immediate action taken was to maneouver it on engine side until finish engine and everything was secure at port.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Maker SE attended vessel and rectified the issue.

IncidentEventDetails: Damage had occured during bunkering operation last call at Rotterdam (27th october 2016).    Crew members who made fast the Bunker barge had lead the bunker barge's rope wrongly between the ship's railing on to the bollard and due to load on the railing, same got bent.
ImmediateAction: Nil
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew members who made fast the Bunker barge had lead the bunker barge's rope wrongly between the ship's railing on to the bollard and due to load on the railing, same got bent.No injuries.  Damage had occured during bunkering operation last call at Rotterdam (27th october 2016)  Damage in ship's main deck railing abreast No.3 cargo tank on Starboard side.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting with crew members called and briefed on importance of understanding mooring patterns, especially when making fast small crafts alongside. The damage, consequences and corrective actions. Damage Report will also be discussed during Safety Meeting.
Corrective Action: Meeting with crew members called and briefed on importance of understanding mooring patterns, especially when making fast small crafts alongside. The damage, consequences and corrective actions. Damage Report will also be discussed during Safety Meeting.

ReviewComments: Many thanks for bringing the incident to our knowledge, Understood that permanent repairs has been carried out. Pls educate crew members on maintaining strict vigilant deck watch during STS or STBarge operation. Pls discuss the same in SCMM meeting.
