IncidentDetails: M/E AUX BLOWER MOTOR NO.2 BURNT WITH EXPLOSIN & FIRE in engine room
IncidentEventDetails: On 23 Nov 16 around 05 11 hrs. ,Fire alarm in Engine room  along with low insulation 440v and No.2 Main Engine Auxiliary blower abnormal alarm activated. Found smoke and flame near to No.2 Main Engine Auxiliary blower. After few seconds Main Engine OMD abnormal alarm activated.  Immediately informed bridge and main engine rpm reduced and stopped.   All three Auxiliary blowers stopped and power supply Isolated.  Turning gear engaged and Engine turned .After sufficient cooling inspected the no.2 Aux Blower motor, checked the continuity of the motor windings and found motor showing    zero insulation.  Removed no.2 Aux  blower from the place.  Found following damage to Aux Blower no: 2  1. Found motor shaft bend.  2. Impeller damaged.  3. Labyrinth packing broken into pieces  4. Fixing bolts found damaged    5. Motor fixing steel flange damaged.  6. Main Engine OMD sensor  and connecting cable  near  no.2 Aux Blower found damaged.(Presently we are getting OMD abnormal alarm.)    Removed impeller from the motor shaft .Due to non-availability  of required spares for fixing the spare motor, We have blanked the no.2 Aux Blower.  After complete ventilation, scavenge inspection carried out .Found  few  damaged labyrinth  packing broken parts in scavenge manifold. All parts removed from scavenge manifold and checks carried out in under piston areas of unit no.5, 6,7.   After blanking, Main Engine tried out with no.1 and no.3  Aux Blower running. Found satisfactory.
ImmediateAction: All 3 Aux Blowers to Manual mode and stopped all three blowers.  engines put to stop.Fire brought under  control using Foam extinguisher.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Found following damage to Aux Blower no: 2  1. Found motor shaft bend.  2. Impeller damaged.  3. Labyrinth packing broken into pieces  4. Fixing bolts found damaged    5. Motor fixing steel flange damaged.  6. Main Engine OMD sensor  and connecting cable  near  no.2 Aux Blower found damaged.(Presently we are getting OMD abnormal alarm.)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Motor along with shaft & impleer landed ashore for rewinding & repairs. vsl will make leve2 RA and navigate with caution. Even steam above blower cut out range.
Corrective Action: Motor along with shaft & impleer landed ashore for rewinding & repairs. vsl will make leve2 RA and navigate with caution. Even steam above blower cut out range.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Makers guidelines to be followed.. Presently maker to revert with the recommended running hours

IncidentDetails: On Nov 15th   1300 Lt vessel arrived Chennai pilot station and pilot boarded the vessel ( Capt. Anil Prasad ) to berth at JD-2 at 1436 Lt.  Vessel was provided with three tugs ( One in forward, one in aft and One assisting in positioning the vessel to enter jawahar dock) and to assist vessel in maneuvering safely to pass lock gates to enter Jawahar dock and subsequently to berth at JD-2.  1505 LT Tug Fast Forward (Ocean Fame) / 1500 LT Tug fast Aft (Sea Lion Apex).   At 1535 LT while vessel with pilot on board and aligning itself with the assistance of the tugs to enter the Jawahar dock, master observed there is inadequate fenders near the Jawahar dock entrance Knuckle on the stbd side. Master also observed that in that same location there were few damages to wharf due to some other vessel contact with the Warf which was already visible and same has been brought to the notice of attending pilot. Master immediately alerted pilot and asked to be more cautious and pass slowly through the lock gates. Now under these circumstances while vessel was getting aligned with the dock entrance it came in contact with the jetty creating severe abrasive damages to the hull paint coating and indentations in way of DOTK bulkhead between frame numbers 28 to 34.  In spite of master’s pro-active action, and repeated warning, this incident happened due to lack of adequate maneuvering by pilot and inadequate fenders at the Jawahar dock entrance, which is a matter of severe concern and myself here by put port authorities and pilot on notice for these damages.   Considering all the above facts we consider it as unsafe port; Hence vessel and/or owners will not be responsible/ liable for any claims/cost and/or time lost that may arise, and hold you responsible for all the consequences/ damages/delays to own vessel due to said issue.  And also, Vessel and/or its owners will not be held responsible for any kind of claims/damages/ delays which may be raised against the vessel from any third party involved in the said incident.   Also reserve the rights of owners to refer to this matter at some later date and take any actions, or make any claims as they may deem necessary, as direct result of this occurrence.
ImmediateAction: Master immediately alerted pilot and asked to be more cautious and pass slowly through the lock gates.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor hull indentations and paint rubbing marks in way of Break of accom on the shell frame between fram no-31 and 32.   Class surveyor attended the vessel at chennai on 18th Nov and no recommendations or COC issued as the indentations is of very minor in nature and doesn't afftect the sea worthiness of the vessel.
DirectCause: Direct cause :   o Inadequate maneuvering by the pilot and improper vessel handling / tug maneuvering.     Indirect cause :   o Inadequate Master/ pilot communications, pilot not paid attention to master's repeated warning.   o Lack of situational awareness by pilot and tugs despite repeated warning of existing situation and reporting of clearances from the Aft station.   Root Cause :    o Inadequate fender placement at the knuckle of the dock - unsafe port arrangements.   o From the damages to Knuckle to the Jawaher dock entrance by various vessel’s and the approach of the port towards providing adequate safety measures like fendering etc. illustrates inadequate proactive approach from the port to make its facility to be safer for vessel's with high beam of 32 mtrs. The dock breadth is only 34 mtrs.  Port completely ignores this high breadth risk factor while planning vessel berthing inside Jawaher dock.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediate Corrective action:   o Master informed all the parties, Managers and owners regarding this incident.   o LOP (Letter of protest issued by Master holding pilots and port responsible) under unsafe berth category due to non-availability of sufficient fenders near the Knuckle - Please find attached   o Superintendents both Marine and Technical boarded the vessel immediately at Chennai and assisted Master / vessel to initiate further actions.   o Class surveyor were briefed regarding this incident and requested their attendance on board to assess the damages and to analyze further course of actions.   o The respective cofferdam where damages occurred were opened, ventilated and inspected to analyse the extent of damages.   o Prepared for class surveyor entry inside cofferdam for close up inspection.  o P & I surveyor has been informed regarding this incident and attendance has been arranged to protect owners interest.   o On board training and briefing carried out to Bridge team about this incident and reemphasized roles and responsibilities of bridge team whilst vessel under pilotage.
Corrective Action: Immediate Corrective action:   o Master informed all the parties, Managers and owners regarding this incident.   o LOP (Letter of protest issued by Master holding pilots and port responsible) under unsafe berth category due to non-availability of sufficient fenders near the Knuckle - Please find attached   o Superintendents both Marine and Technical boarded the vessel immediately at Chennai and assisted Master / vessel to initiate further actions.   o Class surveyor were briefed regarding this incident and requested their attendance on board to assess the damages and to analyze further course of actions.   o The respective cofferdam where damages occurred were opened, ventilated and inspected to analyse the extent of damages.   o Prepared for class surveyor entry inside cofferdam for close up inspection.  o P & I surveyor has been informed regarding this incident and attendance has been arranged to protect owners interest.   o On board training and briefing carried out to Bridge team about this incident and reemphasized roles and responsibilities of bridge team whilst vessel under pilotage.
ReviewComments: From the extent of various damages to Jawaher dock Knuckle it is evident that there is insufficient fendering arrangements prevails in the dock which leads to vessel touching knuckle often and subsequently damaging the hull.  Moreover, when vessel with high beam it demands a high precise maneuvering and good seamanship skills from the pilot to manoeuver the vessel without touching the jetty.  In general it is observed bridge team, has been proactive and warning the pilot regarding the unsafe nature of the dock and its repercussion. Despite repeated warnings and subsequent pilot’s reactions the unsafe nature of the dock and inadequate maneuvering from pilot contributed to this incident.  Hence from all the above it is apparent that the port is unsafe which acted as a major contributing factor for this incident.
ClosingNote: o Reemphasized importance of more stubborn bridge team assertiveness towards any issues which relates to lapses in pilots maneuvering. Completed training by MSI Visited the vessel at Chennai.   o Class inspection carried out on the subsequent days and observed damages marginal no internal structure damages, hence no recommendations inserted in the class report and cleared by class.    o Circulate safety alert to all vessels to alert vessel's calling Chennai regarding this issue.    o To Run a Campaign on the Hazard Identification and risk perception

IncidentDetails: While lowering the Port side Life boat when the ship was stopped, The wire rope end holding the forward skate in the place from the bottom of it got loose, while the boat was swung out, the lower end of the skate swung out towards the ship side and got stuck in the fish plate edge, the D - shackle sheared off due to the swing of the skate in the opposite side of the boat's out - board swing and the skate got lost overboard.
IncidentEventDetails: While lowering the portside Life boat when the ship was stopped, The wire rope end holding the forward skate in the place from the bottom of it got loose. The condition of the wire was very bad and the end was held together with the siezing wire. While the boat was swung out, the lower end of the skate swung out towards the ship side in the opposite direction of the boat's outward swing. The lower end of the skate got stuck into the fish plate on the ship side and the D shackle  1.2 m higher on the top side of the skate, on which it was pivoting sheared off. The skates thus got lost overboard.
ImmediateAction: The life boat was heaved in place immediately. Temporary fendering provided and new Skates are being fabricated on board.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LIfe Boat Skate got lost overboard. This loss would render the Lifeboat hull susceptible to damage while lowering the life boat next time. This could alos lead to adverse remarks during the Vessel's imminent USCG inspection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New skates are being fabricated. Wires of both port and starboard life boats have been checked and the wires with deteriorated conditions are being changed.Inspections of the same to be carried out at regular intervals.
Corrective Action: New skates to be placed in place
LessionLearnt: Lifeboat Lowering to be done in a controlled and precise manner. Toolbox meeting to be done prior undertaking the job to ensure that the lowerer is aware of correct procedure in doing same.
ClosingNote: New Skates put in place. Lifeboat underwent USCG inspection.

IncidentDetails: After completion discharging operation of 04th STS with MT.King David,While un-mooring,the D/V came into contact with M/V on stbd side shipside at various places starting from fwd of break of accommodation towards stern due to D/V main engine failure. Vessel sustained Hull breach at one place and indentation at three places on shipside.
IncidentEventDetails: All lines ( F&A : 3+2 ) from M/V were casted off and D/V Fwd spring lines and stern lines were casted off. As soon as D/V head lines were casted off her bow started swinging to stbd,while D/V's only aft two spring lines were still on M/V's bollard near midship manifold area. The main engine of D/V could not start and she started drifting onto M/V and the corner edge of her portside stern part came into contact with our shipside.The same time M/V rudder was given hard to stbd and D/V's aft spring lines casted off. After all cast off and all lines were onboard the main engine put on D'slow ahead.    M/V commenced picking up port anchor. Due to D/V drift direction towards M/V she kept touching M/V shipside at various places and finally passed clear of M/V's stern.
ImmediateAction: Mother vessel rudder was given hard to stbd and D/V's aft spring lines casted off. After all cast off and all lines were onboard the mother vessel main engine put on D'slow ahead.    M/V commenced picking up port anchor. Due to D/V drift direction towards M/V she kept touching M/V shipside at various places and finally passed clear of M/V's stern.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hull breach and indentation :    ON THE SHIP’S HULL, STARBOARD SIDE DAMAGED IN THREE COMPARTMENTS:-    -AFT COFFERDAM IN ACCOMODATION BLOCK: the hull dent about Length 400mm , Breadth 500mm and Depth 100 to 150mm between frame 10-11 and hull holed about Length 300mm , Breadth 100mm between frame 10-11  -ENGINE ROOM: the hull dent about Length 300mm , Breadth 250mm and Depth 50 to 100mm between frame 34-35  -FOREWARD COFFERDAM IN ACCOMODATION BLOCK: the hull dent about Length 800mm , Breadth 900mm and Depth 50 to 100mm between frame 44-45
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Re-confirm the Main Engine availability and readiness of other vessel prior un-mooring.
Corrective Action: Mother vessel also to heave up her anchor and be underway prior unmooring operation, if stand-by tugs are not available prior commencement of unmooring of both the vessels.
LessionLearnt: To be prepared for eventualities due other vessel's machinery failure during STS  operations.
ClosingNote: Masters advised about confirming own and other vessel machinery readiness prior STS mooring and unmooring operation.

IncidentDetails: During routine pressure testing of Bunker lines using LSMGO, air purge valve at bunker manifold on port side aft gave way from its bonnet resulting minor oil spray on bunker spill tray.
IncidentEventDetails: Routine pressure testing of bunker lines using LSMGO was in progress. Aftre filling the HFO line with LSMGO by transfer pump, a flexible hose was connected to purge cock on top side of bunker manifold port side aft to drain the trapped air. After draining the air the purge cock valve was closed. Upon pressurising the line using pressure hand pump oil was seen to leak through the purge cock, so the valve was tighetened, but it gave way from bonnet side. This resulted in minor oil spray on bunker spill tay.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the pressure testing. Released the pressure from the line into HFO tank. Cleaned the area thoroughly.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During routin epressure testing of bunker lines, the air purge cock valve located on port side aft bunker manifold gave away from bonnet while tightening. This resulted in minor oil spray.The spill was contained on board and the area cleaned thoroughly.
DirectCause: Wera & Tear fo the valve / material failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Purge cock on port side aft  bunker manifold to be renewed. The condition of all such valves on FO system to be checked and renewed if necessary.
Corrective Action: Purge cock on port side aft  bunker manifold to be renewed. The condition of all such valves on FO system to be checked and renewed if necessary.
LessionLearnt: Purge cocks on all bunker manifolds to be inspected periodically and same to be included in PMS
ReviewComments: Above incident is very clear example why pressure testing of bunker lines and associated valves are carried out annual to ascertain the condition of pipelined and other fittings during operation. More close up inspection to be carried out on all associated fittings and valve prior every operation , any short comings to be attended at the earliest.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out and all corrective actions closed satisfactorily. Considering the nature of the incident, Fleet wide alert has been carried out.

IncidentDetails: During routine inspection of lifeboat air bottles it was observed that the bottle pressure was lesser than the permissible value. To recharge the same the air hose was connected to recharge the bottles. On commencement of charging it was observed that the hose was damaged and leaking from several places.
IncidentEventDetails: 1330 : Officer checks life boat air bottles and observes less than permissible value  1350 : High pressure air charging hose rigged and connected to air bottle   1355 : Leak detected in the charging hose and recharging operation suspended.
ImmediateAction: Recharging suspended and air bottle valve secured.
DirectCause: CHARGING HOSE MAINTENANCE AND INSPECTION LACKING.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Vessel had the hose on board .

IncidentDetails: Port side bridge wing M.O.B marker got activated , Need to replace the same
IncidentEventDetails: At 1415 LT after completion of cargo operations by shore gantry at MIT port , both of our ship cranes had to be secured. 1420 LT Crane No 1 was secured by Duty AB, followed by that Crane No 2 was being secured in stowed position at 1430 LT . Duty officer (2/0) was on bridge attending with magnetic compass technician , Duty AB went to crane cabin and liased with second officer that he would secure the hook . Hook was picked all the way up right to the top of the boom , Crane boom was swung over the monkey Island from sea side. After this crane hook was lowered to secure. (Lowering distance from monkey island to bridge deck) , wind state at this time was force 3 inside port and out at sea it was force 4 . This caused hook to swing slightly .Duty officer alone at the bridge wing corner, by the time he could control hook swing, hook touched M.O.B marker slightly at 1435LT causing it to get activated.
ImmediateAction: Efforts were made to cover the same but it was hot , port authorities were informed .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bridge wing port side M.O.B marker has to be renewed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Further even if slightest doubt on wind, 2 people shall go for the crane hook securing . Before hook securing duty officer shall inform cheif and reconfirm wind speed.
Corrective Action: Further even if slightest doubt on wind, 2 people shall go for the crane hook securing . Before hook securing duty officer shall inform cheif and reconfirm wind speed.
LessionLearnt: IMPORTANCE OF PROPER CRANE PROCEUDRES
ClosingNote: CRANE SECURING IS AN INHERENT RISKY PROCEDURE. DUE CONTROL MEASURES SHOULD BE PRESENT TO REDUCE THE RISKS TO CREW AND VESSEL EQUIPMENT.

IncidentDetails: SPREADER OF PILOT LADDER BROKEN, PILOT BOAT TOCHING THE SPREADER.
IncidentEventDetails: DURING PILOT DISEMBARKATION, PILOT LADDER SPREADER WAS TOUCHED BY PILOT BOAT, CAUSING THE SPREADER TO BE CRACKED AND LATER FOUND TO BE BROKEN.
ImmediateAction: DAMAGED PILOT LADDER REPLACED WITH PORT SIDE PILOT LADDER.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pilot Ladder Spreader damaged. no personel injury.
DirectCause: Pilot ladder came in contact with pilot launch during bad weather. pilot ladder was prepared as per pilot instructions. due to wave height and rolling, pilot ladder bottom length came  in between boat and ships hull causing pilot ladder to twist and spreader to come in twist force. full force caused bottom spreader to crack and later when ladder picked up spreader broke in peices.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: More caution is required when lowering pilot ladder. during bad weather, pilot ladder to be adjusted as per wave height expected and vertical height  reach of boat in consultation with pilot.
Corrective Action: More caution is required when lowering pilot ladder. during bad weather, pilot ladder to be adjusted as per wave height expected and vertical height  reach of boat in consultation with pilot.
ClosingNote: Job Closed 30/12/16

IncidentDetails: PARTING OF FWD BREAST LINE MOORING ROPE TAIL
IncidentEventDetails: During discharge at Sint Nicolas (Aruba), one of the Fwd breast line rope tail parted due to chafing of rope tail on the closed fairlead.
ImmediateAction: Stations called and Old mooring rope tail used to replace damaged/broken rope tail
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fwd breast line mooring rope parted during discharge operations.  Reasons for parting :    Inadequate mooring arrangement for size of vessel at Sint Nicolaas.  Lead of Breast line almost perpendicular to the jetty.  Chafing of mooring rope tail at Fairlead  No teepol/grease in use to avoid damage due to chafing  Mooring tended but kept excessively tight by deck watches.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to be conducted on safe mooring practises/handling of mooring and mooring precautions whilst alongside.
Corrective Action: Training to be conducted on safe mooring practises/handling of mooring and mooring precautions whilst alongside.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Appreciate the reporting of the incident. We concur with the cause analysis done and the corrective action taken.

ImmediateAction: Informed BSN to lashing properly

IncidentDetails: BA COMPRESOR FAN AND FAN HOLDER  BROKEN
IncidentEventDetails: DURING RE-CHAGRE OF SCBA BOTTLE THIRD OFFICER AND FOURTH ENGINEER CHECKED THE COMPRESOR ALL IS NORMAL. WHEN STARTED THE COMPRESOR ALL IN GOOD ORDER AFTER 2 MINUTES NOTICES THE STARTED VIBRATION THEN STOP AND CHECKED  THE CAUSE OF VIBRATION BUT NOTHING IS FOUND.  START AGAIN THE COMPRESOR AFTER 1 MINUTE 1 BLADE OF FAN BROKEN LUCKILY THE COMPRESOR HAVE A SAFETY GUARD. WHEN THIRD OFFICER AND FOURTH ENGINEER CHECK. FAN AND FAN HOLDER BROKEN.

IncidentDetails: Fwd and aft station mooring ropes were getting parted continously due vsl surging heavily in Limon Port stay , couple with cargo operations . As of now on the average every two hours , two ropes are getting parted. Vsl expected to sail 14th Dec PM only.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed Puerto Limon on 12th Noon . Immedeately FWE of engines and thrusters , when gangway was being rigged , some crew were rigging up rope sleeves fwd and aft , in no time , vsl started surging momentum developed ropes were 2 head lines and 2 stern lines got parted . Further to this on the average every two hours , two ropes were getting parted expecially in the stern .
ImmediateAction: Crew were posted on watch , fwd and aft stations immedeately round the clock , during Limon port stay , so they could adjust the ropes when the vsl starts surging .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: 1. Please immediately raise a letter of protest for the same when one rope parts,  2. keep all ropes fully tight to increase friction with fenders and reduce movement of vessel. 3. If facing trouble staying alongside, evaluate the need for operation of BOW & Stern Thruster.  4. If unable to stay alongside, despite best efforts, Evaluate using tug assistance or moving out of berth.
ClosingNote: DETAILED INSTRUCTION SENT TO VESSEL ON EMAIL EVERY TIME THE VESSEL CALLS PUERTO LIMON

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: IG Alarms start giving low oxygen reading of zero,and  calibration was carried out to check the analysing unit and was satisfactory.But upon opening sample ig flow was not there and probe was getting overheating,due to IG sample line and line filter got choked,due to multiple start stop of plants,resulting in carryover of mild carbon deposits in the main line and blower casing to get deposited in sample line.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1810 IG oxygen readings gave low oxygen reading and eventually during trouble shooting IG tank pressure went to 200 and COP's got tripped.
ImmediateAction: IG sample line choked and same cleared and line filter renewed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Time loss Direct: 48 minutes  Time Loss Offhire >48 hrs
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Since the PMS of the system already covers the calibration routines and cleraning of the sampling lines and filters, vessel will hence forth initiate the same PMS routine in its entirety, including blow through of the sampling line, followed by calibration, prior each commencement of the IG system after stoppages.  2. Ship staff has been advised to have the inert gas system running at all times during short/intermittent stoppages in the cargo operation to avoid any soot carry over during START / STOP operations in the Inert Gas System
Corrective Action: 1. Since the PMS of the system already covers the calibration routines and cleraning of the sampling lines and filters, vessel will hence forth initiate the same PMS routine in its entirety, including blow through of the sampling line, followed by calibration, prior each commencement of the IG system after stoppages. 2. Ship staff has been advised to have the inert gas system running at all times during short/intermittent stoppages in the cargo operation to avoid any soot carry over during START / STOP operations in the Inert Gas System

IncidentDetails: Bilge tank contamination by treated sewage which got overflowed to ER tank top and  then by mistake transferred to Bilge tank.    There was no damage and injury due to this incident.But since system is not  accepting the near miss report without selecting anyone option from Near miss  potential,so we have selected the option potential minor damage,repairable  onboard.
IncidentEventDetails: On 31st Oct.around 2130 hrs 4th Engineer was doing the STP back flushing but  mistakenly he kept the discharge pump in manaul mode and opened the SW valve for  flushing,when the high level alarm of STP came,in hurry he forgot to shut the SW  valve and went to ECR to acknowledge the alarm.By that time,,the treated sewage  from the plant overflowed to E/R tank top(port side below bilge pump) and not to bilge  well directly.When 4th Engineer came down after acknowledging the alarm, he felt  sewage smell and by that he realized that sewage has overflowed.Only then he shut  the SW valve and put the discharge pump on auto to dischagre STP contents to stop  sewage overflow. After that without reporting this incident to senior staff , he washed  away the sewage from the tank top,which got carried away with dust on tank top to  bilge well.  Next day morning Vessel arrived in Anchor at Suez and by that time the sewage  particles has settled on the bottom of aft bilge well and during inspection,2/E could not  make out that bilge well is contaminated and it looked clean water only.So this bilge  well was transferred to bilge tank.  Due to subsequent filling of bilge tank by ME air cooler condensate water OWS was  started on 7th Nov 2016. OWS started giving high PPM alarm after 10-15 minutes  only, even though the OWS sample was found satisfactory.  Finally after struggling for 3 hours,we stopped OWS and drained chambers and  coalescer filter was taken out.Found sewage particles in both chambers.Due to this  high PPM alarm was getting activated.  After boxing up OWS,it was started but only after 40-50 minutes it again started giving  high PPM alarm.OWS was stopped.  On 8th Nov.morning again we opened the OWS coalescer filter and cleaned the  chamber and coalescer filter and boxed back.This time we found very less amount of  particles in the chamber.Vessel was transiting through the Gibraltar strait hence we  could not try out the OWS.  On 9th morning, after cleaning all the filters we started the OWS but after 40 minutes  only it started to give the high PPM alarm and even cleaning the filters again and  again,it was still giving the alarm.Also,we could not run OWS on SW for long time to again,it was still giving the alarm.Also,we could not run OWS on SW for long time to  flush the OWS chambers because it will fill up the bilge tank,which was already  high.Hence we stopped the OWS and same was reported to office for further advise.  e particles were settled down and not visible.In the evening watch 4th Engineer  transferred 0.79m3 sewage contaminated bilge water from the bilge well to bilge  tank(Ret:6.56m3).
ImmediateAction: Training to all Engine Room staff was carried out for STP backflushing.It was stressed  that Sewage discharge pump should be kept in Auto mode only,so that it maintains  the level of water inside the plant to normal level and it does not overflow.Same instructions were posted near STP for furure reference.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sewage entered into bilge system as overflow pipe of the plant is connected to the bilge tank.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Correct method of backflushing to be used by keeping the sewage discharge pump in auto mode. 2) Atleast one management level Engineer to be present at the plant while backflushing to control sea water flow to plant and immediately pump it out also,so that it doesnot overflow. 3) Even after taking all precautions if sewage has overflowed, prevent it from going to bilge wells , and same to be manually cleaned. Sewage contaminated bilge water should not be transferred to bilge tank.
Corrective Action: There are always min of two personnel in engine room at any time. Other resource could have been used to monitor and control the sewage plant flushing cycle.  One more option was to close the flushing valve, then attend to the alarm & restart the flushing cycle. Tool box meeting for the job to include lessons learnt from this incident.
LessionLearnt: Daily work planner / watch routines - to be discussed prior to commencement of watch. Simultaneous operations to be avoided.
ReviewComments: points mentioned in CA to be followed prior back flushing
ClosingNote: Points mentioned in CA to be followed prior back flushing STP

IncidentDetails: Flexible hose came off pipe while transferring sludge from bilge sludge tank to fuel oil overflow tank, via the sludge pump. No property damage, personal injury or environmental release occurred.
IncidentEventDetails: Sludge was to be landed at Laemchabang on 7th June , but was not possible due damage to vessel pump. Sludge tanks were full and vessel was going to idle at Kaoshiung anchor waiting for fresh voyage instructions. At Kaoshiung anchor, sludge could not be offlanded due considerable costs. TSI procured dispensation for vessel to  use fuel oil overflow tank on a temporary basis. Transfer was proposed to be done by sludge pump; using a flexible hose in the discharge of pump. However it was decided that it would be as last resort and as minimum as possible.   On 23rd june vessel got a few hours notice to commence her next voyage. M.E. maintainence was in progress and all out attempts had to be made to avoid delay.  On the next day, since all sludge tanks were full by the time it was decided to transfer about 10 cu.m to fuel oil overflow tank.   It was nearly the end of a full day maintainence work on the main engine; and then decided to commence the sludge transfer operations. RA and Tool box meeting done at site;  flexible connections made and transfer commenced. Three people were standby at the pump region monitoring the flexible hose and transfer.Initially when water and oily sludge were mixed all went well. When content of oil in mixture increased, pump pressure increased and hose came off the pipe; leading to internal oil spill.  Although the volume of oil spill was not much, it splashed around large areas.
ImmediateAction: Pump stopped immediately and valves closed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ReviewComments: When using a combination of equipments along with the main equipment, the working pressure must be to suit the weakest part of the combination (eg. hose band, material of pipe,etc)
ClosingNote: When using a combination of equipments along with the main equipment, the working pressure must be to suit the weakest part of the combination (eg. hose band, material of pipe,etc)

IncidentDetails: While carrying out preparations for 3 monthly life boat lowering and maneuvering of the STBD life boat. After taking necessary precautions, it was planned to check the hook operation in stow position prior to lowering the boat into water. The 'safety pin release handle' should operate by moving from stow (vertical) position to 90 deg (horizontal) position. The 'safety pin release handle' was not moving beyond 30 degrees and thus no safety pin release was happening in order to allow unhooking of l/b hooks.  Thereafter, the fwd and aft "safety pin release cable" was traced and checked for integrity.  The release mechanism cover box was opened for detailed inspection.  It was noticed that "safety pin release cables" were found parted and the supporting bracket for same was found bent. In the given circumstances, stbd lifeboat hook release mechanism cannot be operated in either mode (onload & offload mode).
IncidentEventDetails: 21 Dec 2016 1030 hrs : Decided to carry out lowering and maneuvering of both L/boats  21 Dec 2016 1200 hrs : Obtained port permmision for lowering lifeboats.  21 Dec 2016 1400 hrs : All precautions including FPDs and additional securing arrangements were put in place.  21 Dec 2016 1415 hrs :  Safety pin release handle operated as per manual, during operation the 'safety pin release cable', both fwd and aft, were found parted.
ImmediateAction: 1. Called off the stbd boat lowering operation.  2. Inspected the lever mechanism and boat secured.  3) As a precautionary measure, port l/b release mechanism cover box was opened and visually checked for safety pin release cable integrity.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Both safety pin release cables parted and support bracket for same distorted.
DirectCause: EQUIPMENT MAL-FUNCTION
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Makers technician assistance is required
LessionLearnt: Checks of all lifeboat components prior lowering drills.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: completed

IncidentDetails: Minor Fire Incident on board during the early Hrs. of 06/01/2017 after the vessel departed YOKOHAMA and the pilot disembarked the vessel.  Due to funnel sparks, an OLD Radar Scanner caught fire, which was secured on C-Deck Port side on a wooden pallet & wrapped in tarpaulin  The fire caused burning & tarring of the paint on the Bulkhead & deck around the secured Scanner  The fire is completely in control and there was no injury or major damage caused.  All adjacent spaces checked, no damage noted except minor paint peeling off without discoloration inside a store directly under the affected area.
IncidentEventDetails: - 01:00 lt - V/L departed Yokohama berth.  - 02:48 lt - Pilot Away.  - 04:00 lt - V/L well clear of all coastal area, Con handed over. RPM set to 55.  - 04:15 lt (approx)- Sparks were noted from the funnel & E/R informed by 2/Off.  - 04:16 lt - Engineers decided to carry out Soot Blow to mitigate the sparks.  - 04:20 lt - RPM set to 65 to carry out Soot Blow.  - 04:27 lt - RPM reduced back to 55. Serv & Sett Tk also drained of water.  - 04:42 lt (approx) - Duty AB saw bright glow on C-Deck Port side.  - AB immediately went to check and found the OLD Radar Scanner on Fire.  - 04:45 lt (approx) - Emergency alarm raised by 2/Off from Bridge.  - Duty AB when down and at the same time, 00~04 Duty AB joined him after hearing the alarm and they both used Portable Ext and dampen the fire.  - 04:50 lt (approx) - a PA was made indicating location of fire.  - All crew mustered directly around the area of fire and immediately rigged a Fire Hose from E- Deck & D- Deck and started Fire Fighting.  - 04:55 lt - Accom AC Blower stopped.  - 05:00 lt - the fire completely extinguished. Only presence of thick smoke noted inside C- Deck.  - All crew & officers spread out in different team and inspected all adjacent areas. Adjacent stores emptied and inspected. AB & Motorman wore SCBA & started inspecting adjacent areas where there was heavy smoke.  - Smoke inside the accommodation was later identified to be due to smoke ingress from a vent very close to the seat of fire.  - No damage noted inside the accommodation, funnel decks or outside accommodation decks, except minor paint peeling off without discoloration in Deck Store directly underneath the affected area.  - 05:45 lt - V/L carried out another Soot Blow to completely mitigate any possibility of funnel sparks.  - Boundary cooling continued until 06:00 lt.  - Through inspection carried out & Duty watch keepers on constant fire watch. No smoke or heat noted anywhere inside & outside the Accom & Funnel decks.  - 06:15 lt - all crew & officers dispersed. All systems set back to normal operation.    - 10:30 lt - a meeting was called for and all crew were briefed regarding fire safety measures and commended the quick action of the Duty AB Mr Velu and all the other ship staff, which prevented a bigger accident from happening.    A management meeting conducted: Intend to clean, scrape & paint the entire area before arrival Pusan.
ImmediateAction: Emergency alarm raised. 2/Off called and informed about Fire on C-Deck Port Side  Immediately (0001~0400 ) A.B used the nearest foam extinguisher to dampen the fire.  Subsequently fire hose was rigged and extinguished the fire & commenced boundary cooling.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Paint damage to the exterior bulkheads where the radar scanner was stored.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All combustible material to be made clear of the the area which are prone to falling sparks. Hazardous areas to be identified and warning notices to be posted.
Corrective Action: Report to be shared by to other vessels
ClosingNote: Agree with masters cause analysis

IncidentDetails: The damage to freefall libeboat's front window and its polycarbonate glass took place at the time of connecting the wires those are used with davit for launching the lifeboat .Whole front window damaged and fell down on the poop deck.
IncidentEventDetails: It was planned to connect the hanging wire ropes to the lifeboat davit as an additional safety precaution toward adverse weather forecast.Wires were connected and while picking up the slack, part of the wire rope caught in the outer frame of the front window due to which the complete window pulled out from its inner frame and fell down on the poop deck, breaking  the glass and window frame hinges.
ImmediateAction: Lifeboat front opening sealed tightly with plastic sheet and canvas to avoid any water ingress.  Started reparing the damaged window frame and its hinges on the priority basis.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FF LIFEBOAT FRONT WINDOW DAMAGED.  GLASS AND HINGES OF WINDOW BROKE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MORE CAREFUL WHEN DOING CRITICAL JOBS.  ADEQUATE NUMBER OF PEOPLE TO WATCH BOTH SIDES OF BOAT FOR POTENCIAL DAMAGE.
Corrective Action: MORE CAREFUL WHEN DOING CRITICAL JOBS. ADEQUATE NUMBER OF PEOPLE TO WATCH BOTH SIDES OF BOAT FOR POTENCIAL DAMAGE.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in next safety meeting

IncidentDetails: Vessel was experiencing very bad weather, In the cabin, while C/E bent down to pick up one of the fallen things, due to sudden very jerky roll movement of vessel, lost his balance and could not take support of any structure in cabin and realised that he cannot prevent falling down and he was almost taken near to the bed room entrance door by the ship's movement.    During that spur of the moment, to prevent himself from having severe head injury by banging to the edge of the entrance, he managed to fall on ground but landed by resting his right arm awkwardly and experienced severe pain on his right wrist / elbow and also had a bang on his neck & shoulder area.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at sea sailing towards Uraga P/S Japan. Vessel already facing adverse weather since last 4-5 days.    At around 02:00, 09-Jan-2017, the atm pressure dropped rapidly and vessel started rolling/pitching violently. Many things inside accommodation were found to get loose & move due to violent rolling.    C/E in his cabin, after hearing movement & falling of items in his cabin, woke up and came to Day room. All the loose items like paper, pens, remotes, chair etc had fallen down and was moving all around.    While he was picking up the fallen things in his day room, he lost his balance and fell down. In course of fall, to prevent servere head injury by corner of door sil, he ended up with injury to right arm.
ImmediateAction: Immediately, he was told to apply a pain relief cream as a first aid. Later, he reported that pain started increasing and started to experiencing radiating pain until neck and shoulders. On verifying by movement of arm & hand that there might not be any fracture etc and the pain must be result of impact due to improper landing, he was issued tablet of IbuBrufen as pain killer & anti-inflamatory. Logged down in Medical Log and kept under observation. While doing normal work, he is experiencing pain and hence he has been advised to wear a crepe bandage and do minimum work required and give maximum possible rest for 2-3 days.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Immediately, he was told to apply a pain relief cream as a first aid. Later, he reported that pain started increasing and started to experiencing radiating pain until neck and shoulders. On verifying by movement of arm & hand that there might not be any fracture etc and the pain must be result of impact due to improper landing, he was issued tablet of IbuBrufen as pain kille & anti-inflamatory. Logged down in Medical Log and kept under observation.
DirectCause: During the rolling and pitching of vessel, the chief engineer was not following safe procedures. The C/E had not secured items in his cabin in view of vessel facing adverse weather whereby all loose items should have been secured to avoid falling.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During such situations, though the persons are moving inside the cabin itself, it is advised to everyone that increased care should be exercised and to avoid bending down which will result in loss of balance to the body.
LessionLearnt: The shipstaff should secure the loose items at all times in anticipation of bad weather. Shipstaff movements in the adverse weather conditions should be cautious and whilst holding support as much as possible.
ReviewComments: we agree with corrective action. Pls discuss the issue in the safety meeting and brief all shipstaff to take preventive measures when vessel rolling excessively.
ClosingNote: closed

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: Transformer inside the Reefer starter panel located in the store adjacent to the reefer shorted and heated up and eventually burned down.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1515 call was received on the bridge that smoke was noticed coming  out of the Reefer Starter panel located in the dry store adjacent to the Reefer.   Crew who had come to the messroom for tea smelled something burning, on following the smell, they found smoke coming out of the starter panel in the dry store room. Immediately calll was made to the bridge ,alarm raised on the PA about the scenario.
ImmediateAction: Hearing the PA announcement Electrician went to the Air condition room where the breaker was located for the reefer and switched off the unit.  Once the  density of smoke coming out of the starter panel reduced  the panel door was opened, it was noticed that the transformer had burned emitting smoke.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Control transformer 440/220v got burnt.
DirectCause: INEFFECTIVE ROUTINE ROUNDS BY ENGINEERS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replacing earth leak test switch,as well defective drain heating coil.
ClosingNote: TRNASFOMRER RENEWED

IncidentDetails: While taking ullages of 1S COT the ullage tape slipped from the instrument and fell into the tank.
IncidentEventDetails: 19 Dec 2016 / 0800 LT : The UTI was sent ashore for annual servicing  19 Dec 2016 / 1600 LT : UTI delivered to vessel  19 Dec 2016 / 2000 LT : The UTI was put to use in 1S COT for checking oil level.  19 Dec 2016 / 2010 LT :  The ullage tape slipped from the instrument and fell into the tank.
ImmediateAction: Awaiting suitable opportunity to recover tape from the tank
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The UTI was correctly secured on the UTI deck ullage point (as no gas leak was noted). Upon lowering the UTI tape, the UTI slipping out was heard and reported to CCR.  Based on investigation, the only reason attributed for this is due to incorrect / non existent securing of UTI tape to the UTI body by shore service technican and thus resulted in slippage when using for the first time after receiving from servicing.
DirectCause: IMPROPER USE OF GAUGING/SAMPLING EQUIPMENT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To verify integrity of the tape after repairs and servicing.
Corrective Action: To verify integrity of the tape after repairs and servicing.
LessionLearnt: Check instruments thoroughly after delivered from shore servicing.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: In order.

IncidentDetails: Crane#4 hook block was lowered into CH#5 to pick up coal residue after discharge. The operation was being carried out in BF scale 2 and vessel upright.  When the hook block was lowered midway into the hold, the vessel started to roll gently and this caused "pendulum effect" of the hook block causing it to gain momentum, in an athwartship direction. The crane operator tried to heave on the runner wire in an attempt to shorten the runner wire length, thus causing the "pendulum effect" to reduce. Concurently, the Crane boom was also raised in an attempt to swing the crane over the shipside to prevent any damage to the hatch coamings from the swinging block. As the crane lifted its boom, the swing direction of the block changed from Athwartship to Fore & Aft and touched the Crane operator's cabin causing the windshield glass to crack. There were no injuries to the crane operator or any personnel involved with the operation.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was carrying out cargo hold cleaning opeartions whilst vessel was drifting off Safi, Morocco. As the weather was Fair, with BF Scale 2(wind spd 5.0 Knts), Crane#4 was being used to pick up a jumbo cargo bag of Coal which was leftover after discharge port. A gantline was tied to the crane block to prevent any swinging.
ImmediateAction: After the Crane was secured, the hold cleaning operation was stopped and investigation carried out about the incident. Crew members involved with crane operations were briefed about the nearmiss/damage. The gravity of the situation was explained to all crew members. Had the factors attributing to this incident been slightly different, the consequences would have been catastrophic/fatal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crane#4 Cabin window glass broken
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crane operations must have atleast one gantline tied to the block and the other end to be wound round a strong point which will be slackedned/heaved as per the hooks movments. Crane operator must take prior permission from OOW before any crane operation is carried out.
Corrective Action: All crane operations must have atleast one gantline tied to the block and the other end to be wound round a strong point which will be slackedned/heaved as per the hooks movments. Crane operator must take prior permission from OOW before any crane operation is carried out.
ClosingNote: to be discussed in safety meeting

IncidentDetails: While taking rounds for the checks on deck after Heavy weather, it was observed that the forward Bell Hanging support deformed and the striker bob missing.
IncidentEventDetails: 03 Jan 2017 to 04 Jan 2017 : Vessel Experienced Heavy Weather.  05 Jan 2017 0830 LT : During routine round on deck after heavy weather,  it was observed that the forward Bell Hanging support deformed and the striker bob missing.  05 Jan 2017 0850 LT : Striker bob found near 2P COT
ImmediateAction: DD Specs to raise.
DirectCause: IMPROPER HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTION
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Heavy weather securing to be done prior onset.
LessionLearnt: Heavy weather securing to be done prior onset.
ReviewComments: in order
ClosingNote: Heavy weather precautions to be followed prior onset.

IncidentDetails: 13th Jan -  2155 UTC - Fire alarm emitted fm Fire alarm Panel  2157UTC - Confirmed fire at DG no.3 exhaust manifold / crew mustered  2158UTC -  DG no. 2 started, stopped DG no.3/shut off fuel supply & ER supply fan, stopped Incinerator waste oil blower fan & drained waste oil tank to BSOT  2159UTC - Fire team fighting the fire using portable extinguisher  2202UTC - Fire team reported "fire under control"   2205UTC - Fire team reported "fire extinguished" - but smoke still present from DG 3 manifold -  Called Mr. Raj informed the incident  2215UTC - Confirmed no any sign of re-ignition, smoke disappeared / 2nd call to Mr. Raj informing the smoke totally out.
IncidentEventDetails: Incinerator waste oil tank blower leaked splashing sludge down to running DG no.3 exhaust manifold.    No any damage to machinery nor injury o/b.
ImmediateAction: 2157UTC - Confirmed fire at DG no.3 exhaust manifold / crew mustered  2158UTC -  DG no. 2 started, stopped DG no.3/shut off fuel supply & ER supply fan, stopped Incinerator waste oil blower fan & drained waste oil tank to BSOT  2159UTC - Fire team fighting the fire using portable extinguisher    Incinerator Waste oil tank totally drained to BSOT
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No any damage to machinery nor injury to crew
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tank never to be filled more then 85%. We suggest not to go more then 60%.
Corrective Action: After thorough investigations of the incident, to avoid reoccurence, we suggest to place protection cover of blower fan shaft & to fabricate oil deep tray with capacity 20-50 ltrs in order to prevent waste oil spray which may lead into accident.
ClosingNote: Training to be carried out and same put in the joiners familiarization file for reference

IncidentDetails: During Cargo disch operation in NY it was observed that the COW line drain valves had cracked due to cold weather.
IncidentEventDetails: 10 Jan 2016 AM : Vessel discharging in NY  10 Jan 2016 PM : Dip in ambient temperature leading to sub zero.  11 Jan 2016 AM : Ambient temperature increased to five degrees  11 Jan 2106 PM : Leak observed in the T/C line drain valves. On close investigation found that the valves had developed cracks due to icing.
ImmediateAction: Valves removed and blanked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo Tank Cleaning line drain valves totalling 12 nos have been cracked.
DirectCause: IMPROPER COLD WEATHER PRECAUTIONS FOR WATER DRAINAGE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: T/C lines to be drained fully especially when cold Wx is expected.
LessionLearnt: Cold weather precautions.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: Cold weather precautions to be taken.

IncidentDetails: During routine lowering of Free fall lifeboat by lifeboat davit , hydraullic oil leak was observed from the Stbd ramp.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1600hrs LT Lifeboat was made ready for lowering using lifeboat davit in presence of Marine and Tech Supt..  At 1630hrs LT Lifeboat was lowered out of the cradle and lowered to the water   At 1700hrs LT Lifeboat was placed back in position and at the same time hydraullic oil was notice to be leaking from the top part of the stbd davit ramp.
ImmediateAction: Hydraullic motor for the davit was stopped. A drum was places beneath the leak to contain the leaking oil.  At 1730 Hrs Stage was rigged up for inspection and area inspected by Supt and CO.  It was noticed that the leak was from 2 places. first from the top portion of the ramp from the metal part and second was from the Lip seal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lip seal of ramp leaking and crack in the top metal portion of the stbd ramp
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repair to be carried out with shore assitance and makers advice
Corrective Action: Order for new hydraulic ram in place. Complete ram to be renewed once received
ClosingNote: New hydraulic ram supplied and fitted by ship staff

IncidentDetails: WHILE DISCHARGING CONTAINER SPREADER HIT THE STEVEDORE
IncidentEventDetails: AT 0910 HRS WHILE DISCHARGING AT BAY O6 BY SHIPS CRANE, A STEVEDORE GOT HIT BY THE CONTAINER SPREADER ON HIS RIGHT LEG
ImmediateAction: IMMEDIATELY INFORMED TO CHIEF OFFICER AND MASTER.
Corrective Action: REPORT TO ALL PARTIES AND EVIDENCE COLLECTED TO TACKLE ANY CLAIM THAT MAY ARISE.

IncidentDetails: Two forward mooring lines damaged due to ship line stuck under tugs propeller during mooring operation
IncidentEventDetails: Ageo Terminal, Barnabe Island, Santos on the 15th of September 2016:             15/Sep/2016 1836LT: First Line Ashore (Fwd Springs), Via Heaving line.           15/Sep/2016 1838LT: Aft Springs Ashore, Via Heaving line     15/Sep/2016 1840LT: Vessel Aligned manifold and springs made fast Forward and Aft.         15/Sep/2016 1842LT: Vessel passed 2 head lines to Mooring Boat, forward as per Boatmen/Pilots Advise. Both lines where slackened for the Boat to haul to shore bits.    15/Sep/2016 1844LT: Mooring boat casted off the lines, after hauling the ropes for approx.35meters into the water. Reason Uknown.          15/Sep/2016 1844LT: Vessel immediately started hauling the lines back on board as lines begin to close in to the Tug Made Fast on Port Break of Fxl (TUG-SN JATOBA ).    15/Sep/2016 1845LT: Both lines got stuck under the tugs propellor. Vsl Stopped Hauling stuck fwd lines.    15/Sep/2016 1845LT: Fwd Mooring boat departed from vicinity of vessel and did not return.        15/Sep/2016 1920LT: Aft lines made fast.       15/Sep/2016 1924LT: Spare lines passed to shore via another Mooring boat to make fast forward to which approached.           15/Sep/2016 1925LT: Aft Tug cast off.        15/Sep/2016 1936LT: After accessing the situation, Fwd Tug cut the two ships line stuck under its propeller. Approx Length of damaged section/ section lost - 30mtres each rope.           15/Sep/2016 1942LT: Vsl All fast at AGEO Terminal, STBD Side alongside, Mooring Fwd(3,2) Aft(3,2).        15/Sep/2016 1945LT: Fwd Tug Cast Off.
ImmediateAction: Spare lines passed to shore via another Mooring boat which approached  to make fast forward and then the damaged lines wer hauled up, A letter of protest was issued to the mooring boat, and same has been forwarded to the Charterers and Agents, Requisition for two New mooring ropes has been raised
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two mooring Ropes damaged ,  Length of damaged section/ section lost - 30metres each rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A training session carried out for officers and crew memebers to explain that There should be proper communication and reporting betwen mooring station and Bridge. Lowering of mooring rope to mooring boat should contionously monitored and ropes to be slackened only when the signal is given to lower.
Corrective Action: A training session carried out for officers and crew memebers to explain that There should be proper communication and reporting betwen mooring station and Bridge. Lowering of mooring rope to mooring boat should contionously monitored and ropes to be slackened only when the signal is given to lower.
LessionLearnt: Emphasis on communication during toolbox meeting prior mooring operations.
ReviewComments: Review Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Concur with the cause analysis and corrective action.

IncidentDetails: On the 11th of Jan'17 at 1953LT(1053Z), Fishing boat 12 Chang Myeong, made contact with vessel on the port shipside.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Vessel was drifting about 30 NM Off cheju jo island, S Korea. On 11.01.2107, At 1953 LT ( 1053 UTC ), in position 33 09.2N/127 24.46E, OOW, 3rd Officer Scoatte Sobrevilla Gelvoligaya, called me and informed that a fishing boat( 12 Chang Myeong ) has made contact with vessel on the port side.    2. Rushed up immediately on the bridge and found the above mentioned vessel alongside the vessel on the port side. OOW tried to make contact with vessel but no answer.     3. When on deck found the vessel rubbing along the shipside and moving astern. Informed all heads of department about the incident. Around 2030LT Fishing boat cleared the vessel astern. Again tried to contact the boat but no answer.    4. Chief Officer, Self and Bosun commenced a visual check of the shipside with flash lights and since it was dark, was unable to check for any damage on the shipside. From what little we could see by the flash lights, no damage was evident and no pollution seen over the side in the water.    5. At around 2100 LT, Korean coast guard contacted vessel for some information about the ship and whether any damage inflicted upon the vessel by the Fishing boat, Crew on board, Ships particulars, any injured persons, Last port of call and reason for drifting.    6. At 2110 informed Port captain Abhishek Tiwari of the incident and called DPA capt Shivaram and apprised him of the incident. Meanwhile Soundings of all Port ballast and fuel oil tanks taken, Checked for any possible damage in the cargo hold nos 3 and 4. Engine room bulkheads also checked on the port side. No damage seen. Steering gear tried out both the sides with maximum rudder angle( harder port to harder stbd). Found all paramters to be ok. Engines tried out on turning gear and same found to be ok. Then tried out engines ahead/astern on power and found to be ok. No abnormalities noticed. Next tried out engine at various maneouvering speeds( Dead slow to Full ahead ) and same found to be ok.
ImmediateAction: Alarm raised, Tried to contact the vessel but no answer, Heads of department informed, Port captain Abhishek Tiwari informed, DPA informed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage noticed except some paint on transom of port quarter scratched
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: 1. Fleet alert sent to all vessels to notify them of this incident
ClosingNote: Corrective actions found adequate

IncidentDetails: On 25.12.2016 1500 LT (26.12.2016 0000 UTC ) at posn Lat 53 34.85'N Long 155 59.12'W while taking routine rounds it was observed that the 6 containers  loaded in Bay no 66 found damaged and  moving as the vessel was rolling due to Stbd quarter swells in rough seas
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel sailed out from Busan for Tacoma on 19-Dec-2016. Vessel encountered bad weather after passing Unimak passage. Due to moderate to heavy rolling container in bay 66 got damaged.
ImmediateAction: Speed reduced and course altered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: on 25.12.2016 1500 LT (26.12.2016 0000 UTC ) at posn Lat 53 34.85'N Long 155 59.12'W while taking routine rounds it was observed that the 6 containers  loaded in Bay no 66 found damaged and slightly moving as the vessel is rolling due to Stbd quarter swells in rough seas
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Speed reduced and course altered
Corrective Action: To Alert all Vessel and ensure compliance with CSM requirement if Software does not have alert for Lashing Force
Accident Details: Cargo damage due to movement of cargo
ClosingNote: Proper voyage planning must be done taking present circumstances into account.

IncidentDetails: Aft Stbd side mooring winch exterior shell got cracked with oil leak . No injuries were reported
IncidentEventDetails: Post coming alongside making all fast fwd and aft with (3 x 3) lines, M/E and thrusters were turned off .Tugs forward & aft were also cast off.Meanwhile there was another vessel which was berthing ahead of own vsl with 1 bollard space was coming alongside to berth and also due to swell, own vsl was surging heavily moving out and in from berth. Ships crew were adjusting lines to get ship alongside again fore and aft . Finally after getting alongside picking up all lines as usual it was noted that aft stbd side winch motor holding two stern lines one on winch and other on wrapping drum had an oil leak from the motor. We were almost transferring line from drum to bitt oil leak was identified. On investigation it was found that there had been a crack developed on the external alloy metal casing of the hydraulic driving gear in obviously three  locations around its body. Immediately winch motor was switched off, oil flow stopped, and oil restricted within drip tray .1106LT : Pilot on board (Puerto Limon)  1118LT : Tug fast aft , StbdQrtr, with ships line (Which is being used as loose stern line)  1122LT : Tug fast fwd , Stbd Bow, with ships line (Which is being used as loose head line)  1130LT : First Line Ashore (Fwd Spring)  1140LT :Vsl in position, well Alongside the berth.  1142LT : Tugs cast off   1148LT :Vsl all fast fore and aft with ( 3 x 3)   1154LT : Pilot Away . F.W.E & Thrusters  1220LT : Free Pratique granted  1230LT : Leak was identified
ImmediateAction: Leak was completely cleaned with Rags . Extra stern lines were passed , Temporarily this line was kept in same state just holding with brakes . Further to that winch was completely isolated under chief engineer supervision, by blanking off hydraulic lines .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Stbd side aft mooring winch there had been a crack developed on the external alloy metal casing of the hydraulic driving gear in three  locations around its body. Immediately winch motor was switched off, oil flow stopped, and oil restricted within drip tray .No injury reported
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immedeately training was given to all deck crew and officers on mooring winch operations
Corrective Action: Immedeately training was given to all deck crew and officers on mooring winch operations
LessionLearnt: IMPORTANCE ON SAFE MOORING PROCEDURES
ClosingNote: THE ROPE THAT WAS ON THE DRUM WAS HAVING HIGHER BL COMPARED TO THE MOORING FITTINGS. NEW ROPES HAVE BEEN SUPPLIED SINCE.

IncidentDetails: Rough weather condition,the engine tripped on low lube oil pressure,engine room had some drums tied up with wire rope,due to rolling the wire got sheered off and the drums got loose one of the drum suspect hit the drain valve of the MGO tank and oil started gushing out of the valve which was broken by the hit of the drum.In the mean time Engines started again and the ship was tseady,but still rolling.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Rolling of the vessel,  2.Drums getting loose and hitting the valve.  3.MGO gushing out of the drain.
ImmediateAction: Chief engineer when coming down due to engines stopped,sighted the oil gsuhing out of the oil from MGO tank.Immediatly came to ECR and told Fourth engineer to raise alarm,and immediately went to stop the oil coming out of the drain.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Drain valve damaged and while blanking the same oil splashed on the fitter, 4th Eng and 3rd eng.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All lashings of equipments to be re-checked for any chaffing signs or any wear and tear. Especially during bad weather conditions frequent rounds to be made to check all parameters of machinery are ok.
Corrective Action: All lashings of equipments to be re-checked for any chaffing signs or any wear and tear. Especially during bad weather conditions frequent rounds to be made to check all parameters of machinery are ok.
ClosingNote: Verified by onboard TSI.

IncidentDetails: 3 out of 4 stay wires that supports the radar mast were cut due to wire deterioration by rust.
IncidentEventDetails: While at sea during bad weather the 3 stay wires of the radar mast were cut and departed from the upper fixing point on the radar mast.  The 3 wires were not cut simultaneously but rather just few minutes after the first one departed then the 2 others follow one by one.  after checking, we found out that the vinyl covered wires were already corroded by rust from the inside which is the main cause of the incident.
ImmediateAction: We have temporarily replaced the damaged wires with the spare vinyl covered wires for the safety line on main deck which is smaller in diameter that the original radar mast stay wires. We just doubled the lashing to be secured.  Requested for new stay wires as per specification on shippalm.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 3 radar stay wires were damaged/cut due to rust deterioration.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To renew with brand new vinyl covered wires same specification with the original one.
ClosingNote: Stay wires are included in the PMS and the inspection schedules must be carried out accordingly. If PVC wires covered wires are used, the PVC coating must be checked for cracks and tears.

IncidentDetails: a ) Port and Starboard Side railings at Forecastle Deck found distorted and damaged after heavy weather at sea.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was at Sea on her passage from Kaohsiung to Taichung and experienced rough weather. All the necessary measures were taken anticipating the same. Additionally, vessel's speed and course was adjusted in order to minimise the effect of rough seas. However, on arrival port it was noticed that the railings at Forecastle deck were damaged. A detailed summary of events and follow up inspection is as below in chronological order :    30 JAN 2017 (Dep KAOHSIUNG)  1624: POB/SBE   1630: All cast off  1730: RFA / Vessel on Max RPM (94). Complied with Heavy Weather Bridge     Checklist (OP-09D).Vessel Secured for Sea and Rough Weather. All stores and common accommodation areas secured and loose items lashed. Informed Engine Room & Galley to secure all loose objects.  1800:  Co 312 / Speed 17.4 Kts/ Wind : N’ly  Force :8  Swell: 3.0m (N’ly)                 Pressure:1018mb     RPM:94    M/E Load:70                                           2000:  Co 000 / Speed 15.5 Kts/ Wind : N’ly  Force :8/9  Swell: 3.0m (N’ly)               Pressure:1017mb     RPM:91    M/E Load:72    31  JAN 2017  0000: Co 036 / Speed 8.3 Kts/ Wind : N’ly  Force :10  Swell: 5.0m (N’ly)              Pressure:1018mb     RPM:72    M/E Load:76    0400: SBE & EOSP               Co 055 / Speed 4.0 Kts/ Wind : N’ly  Force :8/9  Swell: 4.5m (N’ly)               Pressure:1018mb     RPM:60    M/E Load:70    0430: Deck party removed anchor lashings for arrival port approaches and Bosun reported damage of railings at forecastle station.     Arr TAICHUNG (31  JAN 2017)  0506: POB     0542: First line ashore    0600: All fast FWD&AFT (3+2) . Vessel Port Side Alongside at TAICHUNG container             Terminal Berth #10 / FWE    After Arrival  Inspection Carried Out .Details as follows:  Port and starboard side railings at forecastle station were noted deformed and damaged due to heavy weather encountered during the voyage from Kaohsiung port to Taichung. The manual ropes (additional mooring) used for making fast the vessel alongside were found entangled to the damaged railing. Manual ropes were secured at the dedicated location next to the railing and lashed  to the lashings eyes fitted on deck. It was deducted from given condition that the lashing ropes gave way during heavy weather, slackening the mooring rope and partly setting them loose which in turn struck against railings repeatedly during heavy pitching of vessel in rough seas and caused damage. The lines were cleared and extent of damage noted.  Forward life raft was also noticed dislodged from stowed position, however no apparent damage was noticed. Same was restored to correct stowing position after inspection and verifying satisfactory condition.  Forward embarkation ladder was noticed moved from stowed position, however no damage noticed after inspection. Same was restored and secured to designated stowed position.  Forepeak store inspected and no damages noticed. No indentation, deformation or any kind of fracture was noticed. Bow thruster room, Cargo Holds and main deck were also inspected for similar apparent damages and verified for satisfactory condition.   Complete inspection of lashing and stowage of containers carried out and no damages noticed.  Hull condition was also verified for good order, a thorough inspection was carried out from wharf and no apparent damages noticed.  All main deck stores and other storage places checked and found in satisfactory condition.
ImmediateAction: Follow up thorough inspection was carried out.   Temporary arrangement to fit the damaged railings were made. Vertical stauntions were welded in place and lines rigged for safety.  Additionally, requisition raised for the required materials to carry out hot work and fabricate the new railings.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Maneuvering in heavy wx must be done and complied with as per the Navigational Manual

IncidentDetails: Split pin of port anchor 'D' Shackle slipped away and main pin slipped out about more than 50% during rough weather.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel loaded cargo at Rastanura S.A. and came to Incheon Korea through South China Sea. During the passage vessel experienced rough weather force 6 to 7 in South China Sea. Due to heavy rough weather, split pin of port anchor 'D' shackle is slipped away and gave way to slip main d shackle pin which came out partially about more than 50%. Vessel port anchor was hanging on one side of D shackle only.
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed to Tech. super .  Extra lashing taken by mooring rope.   Barge and shore assistance arranged by office on immediate basis. Port anchor lowered to barge,   Main D shackle pin aligned and new split pin fixed in place. D shackle main pin welded on both the side by workshop. Anchor heaved up and secured for sea.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Condition of anchor, split pin, kenter shackle, D shackle to be checked during dry dock, If required weld the D shackle pin in place.
ClosingNote: Main D shackle pin aligned and new split pin fixed in place. D shackle main pin welded on both the side by workshop. Anchor heaved up and secured for sea

IncidentDetails: While Ballasting the FPK WBT During Discharging operation at Pembroke UK , FPK WBT tank overflowed from the vents and got collected near the aft scupper in front of break of accomodation .
IncidentEventDetails: While Ballasting the FRK WBT During Discharging operation at Pembroke UK , FPK WBT tank overflowed from the vents and got collected near the aft scupper in front of break of accomodation .
ImmediateAction: Immediately stopped the Ballast pump. The level from FPK WBT was dropped onto other 1W WBT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessels reputation with shore staff affected .   No Damage to equipment .  No. Personnel Injury
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Purging of pneumatic gauge to be carried out prior every arrival port .   Manual comparission of sounding in WBT to be carried out atleast once every watch .
Corrective Action: Purging of pneumatic gauge to be carried out prior every arrival port . Manual comparission of sounding in WBT to be carried out atleast once every watch .
ClosingNote: Concur with the proposed measures to prevent recurrence.

IncidentDetails: CLASS SURVEYOR WAS CARRYING OUT ANNUAL SURVEY AND CARGO DISCHARGING WAS IN PROGRESS, HE INSISTED FOR TRYINGOUT OF  EMERGENCY STOP OF ENGINE ROOM FANS. DURING PRESSING OF EMERGENCY STOP BUTTON, AUX BOILER FD FANS AND IG FAN ALSO CONNECTED WITH SAME SWITCH AND TRIPPED..
IncidentEventDetails: AUX BOILER FD FAN AND IG FAN TRIPPING, STEAM PRESSURE GONE DOWN AND CARGO PUMPS  TRIPPED. CARGO DISCHARGING SUSPENDED.
ImmediateAction: IMMEDIATELY ALL ALARMS AND TRIPS PUT BACK TO NORMAL, AUX BOILERS FIRED, IG PLANT STARTED TO RESUME CARGO DISCHARGING.  THERE IS NO DETAILS IN ELECTRICAL DRAWINGS TO IDENTIFY WHICH ALL  MACHINERIES ARE CONNECTED TO EMERGENCY STOP OF ENGINE ROOM FANS BUTTON.   LIST MADE OF ALL MACHINERIES WHICH ARE CONNECTED TO OTHER EMERGENCY STOPS TOO AND POSTED NEAR TO EMERGENCY STOPS PENEL.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CARGO DISCHARGING HAMPERED, DUE TO BOILER TRIPPED CARGO PUMP DISCHARGE PRESSURE CAME DOWN, CHANCES ARE TO BECK FLOW OF CARGO DUE BACK PRESSURE IN LINE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The cause of this inadvertent stoppage of the Auxiliary Boiler is attributed to misunderstanding that the Emergency stop switch is only for E/Room fans and lack of any list posted near the emergency stop panel highlighting the fans that would be stopped on its activation. In order to prevent recurrence, it has been decided that during any critical operation like discharging, any alarms and trips related to that operation should not be tried out. A circular has been flashed to all vessels in the fleet, highlighting this observations & the precautions to be taken to avoid a similar incident, which attached for reference. A list has been prepared of all machineries connected to the emergency stop of Engine Room fans button and other emergency stops and same posted next to the Emergency Stop switches. Please find below the pictures showing the posted list.
Corrective Action: The cause of this inadvertent stoppage of the Auxiliary Boiler is attributed to misunderstanding that the Emergency stop switch is only for E/Room fans and lack of any list posted near the emergency stop panel highlighting the fans that would be stopped on its activation. In order to prevent recurrence, it has been decided that during any critical operation like discharging, any alarms and trips related to that operation should not be tried out. A circular has been flashed to all vessels in the fleet, highlighting this observations & the precautions to be taken to avoid a similar incident, which attached for reference. A list has been prepared of all machineries connected to the emergency stop of Engine Room fans button and other emergency stops and same posted next to the Emergency Stop switches. Please find below the pictures showing the posted list.
ClosingNote: Matter discussed with all Engineers & highlighted the potential damage of such an unassessed action.

IncidentDetails: While using buffing machine to remove barnacles from flange the rotating cup came in contact with the left hand and caused abrasion.
IncidentEventDetails: 10 Feb 2017 / 0800 LT : Daily work tool box meeting was carried out and instructions given to Pump man to change gasket in FW line on deck, cadet was assigned to assist the P/Man.  10 Feb 2017 / 0830 LT : All tools were taken to the site of work and work commenced.  10 Feb 2017 / 0840 LT : Pumpman goes aft to get additional tools. Cadet starts using buffing machine.  10 Feb 2017 / 0900 LT : The flange was being held in position with left hand and buffing machine was operated with the right hand. Inadvertently the machine slipped and came in contact with the left hand causing abrasion of the skin.
ImmediateAction: Work stopped and first aid applied. Anti-septic cream applied over affected area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On visual examination appears to be superfical abrasion.
DirectCause: UNSAFE WORKING PRACTICE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Enhanced training.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: In order

IncidentDetails: Mooring line parted in lock during Panama canal transit.
IncidentEventDetails: During panama canal transit , while transiting Mira Flores locks,  vessel entered 2nd lock at 0555. Due to changeover of locomotives required in the 2nd lock, ships mooring lines were used to hold the vessel  in position. One rope each on port and stbd bow was used. Mooring lines were made fast and  locomotives  cast off .  The dock ballasting had commenced which caused the vessel to surge. Due to this movement the  port side fwd mooring line parted.   There was no injury to any personnel on forecastle.
ImmediateAction: The next set of locomotives were made fast to secure the vessel in position.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During panama canal transit , while transiting Mira Flores locks,  vessel entered 2nd lock at 0555. Due to changeover of locomotives required in the 2nd lock, ships mooring lines were used to hold the vessel  in position. One rope each on port and stbd bow was used. Mooring lines were made fast and  locomotives  cast off .  The dock ballasting had commenced which caused the vessel to surge. Due to this movement the  port side fwd mooring line parted.   There was no injury to any personnel on forecastle.
DirectCause: Direct causes 1. Improper monitoring of rope during the operation. The rope during ballasting should have been monitored and if excessive weight was coming, same should have been communicated to the pilot.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Damage mooring rope has been replaced. Incident will be discussed in safety committe meeting. Also during each panama canal transit, same to be discussed with pilot.
Corrective Action: During the safety committee meeting, the incident to be discussed with all shipstaff .
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: Propeller fouling with the mooring rope whilst unmooring. The diving team was called to clear the rope. The rope was cleared by turning around. No damage was found on the propeller, rudder and associated parts. Class survey carried out a survey and found the propeller,rudder and associated parts to be satisfactory and no recommendations. Vessel took 4.5 hours to clear the fouling.
IncidentEventDetails: On 30th January 2017 at 1805 hrs. LT, Fujairah Port Marine Department Vehicle came to the Berth(SBB2) and informed all vessels made fast to SBB2 Jetty to cast off immediately in order to clear the berth for the inbound vessel. Own vessel and other vessels stern moored on SBB2 berth, commenced unmooring and prepared to shift berth. After the disembarkation of technicians at 1823 hrs.LT, one crew member went down to the jetty and cast of the 2 stern lines one by one.(These stern lines were one on  stbd side and one on port side. Own vessel was holding the position by having the engines on aster, so as to after casting off the stern lines, the crew member can board the vessel.) At 1825 hrs. LT after casting off the stbd side line the line was dropped in to the sea on the far extreme on the vessel and the other crew member was pulling the rope in. (This is how normally it was being followed on the vessel). However the adjacent vessel(Offshore Supply Vessel) also started moving using its engines. Due to the thrust from her propellers and the suction effect created by our astern motion of propellers the mooring line was sucked in to the propeller and got fouled. Due to the fouling the Starboard Main Engine tripped immediately after the ME alarms got activated. same time Master placed the telegraph on neutral for both the engines. As most of the mooring rope was taken on board and only the end was fouled, the Master decided to shift to the other berth using only the Port Engines. At 1845 hrs. LT, the vessel was safely berthed at the main berth. There after the necessary reporting's were made to office
ImmediateAction: The fouled line was cleared by the diving team. Propeller, Rudder and associated parts checked and no defects found. Starboard ME was run and tested in the presence of class surveyor ad all found in satisfactory condition. The Class surveyor gave a statement of fact to the effect that all equipment are in satisfactory condition, and there is no recommendation from the classification society. The Mooring rope was also turned and cleared. Same was inspected by the vessel team and found to be in satisfactory condition.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage to any part of the vessel. However time was lost due (4.5 Hrs) to propeller fouling and clearing the same. The vessel was not engaged in any operation and there were no lube oil deliveries planned at the time of the incident.
