Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. It is advised that the engines are not used when the mooring line is in water in accordance with the procedures. However when it is necessary to keep the engines turning so as to keep the vessel in position during unmooring, the lines will be kept clear of the water by having the shore end after removal from bollard connected to heaving line. The mooring line will be hauled in by the rating on board and the crew member supporting and keeping the line above the water with the help of heaving line. This was tried out and training on same imparted to the deck ratings and Chief Officer for future unmooring's by Master and Superintendent. Date Training carried out: 06th Feb 2017 2. This incident will be passed across to the other fleet barges for learning and compliance. Near miss discussed with the other company barge in Fujairah. Date done: 06th Feb 2017 3. Local mooring procedure will be made for the barges for the Port of Fujairah and displayed at the aft mooring stations. Date done: 06th Feb 2017
Corrective Action: 1. It is advised that the engines are not used when the mooring line is in water in accordance with the procedures. However when it is necessary to keep the engines turning so as to keep the vessel in position during unmooring, the lines will be kept clear of the water by having the shore end after removal from bollard connected to heaving line. When the crew member returns on board, the mooring line will be hauled in keeping clear of the water with the help of support from heaving line. If required, the propeller movement will be stopped to haul the rope. This was tried out and training on same imparted to the deck ratings and Chief Officer for future unmooring's by Master and Superintendent. Date Training carried out: 02nd Feb 2017
LessionLearnt: Local unmooring operations at Fujairah Main Berth
ReviewComments: Crew trained on the unmooring procedures at Fujairh SBB berth. Verified by superintendent and the found operations satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Closed.

IncidentDetails: Head injury (minor Cut) of Electrical officer
IncidentEventDetails: While checking the Stores in Emergency Generator room.  when standing his head smashed the Telephone's Cabin angle.  Minor Cut on the Head.
ImmediateAction: Taken to Hospital - Dressing done.  Pain killer Given.  Patieint advised Bed rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Head injury (minor Cut) of Electrical officer
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training of crew members during next safety meeting
LessionLearnt: Importance of proper PPE wrt  job being undertaken.
ReviewComments: Pls ensure that engine room staff is using proper PPE at all times.
ClosingNote: Noted PCA has been completed.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was at anchor, awaiting berth in Rastanura. During rounds for security and safety it was noticed that vessels portside liferafts lashings had parted and liferafts missing from location.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel riding to six shackles on port anchor at Rastanura Northern Holding anchorage, awaiting berth at LPG terminal.    During LT 0600 hrs AM rounds for security and safety by duty watch keeper, it was noticed that vessels portside liferafts were missing from place.    It was immediately reported to officer on watch, Master was notified at the same time.     Master with Chief Officer and duty AB went to the location to investigate.    At the location it was noticed that both liferafts on port side boat deck were missing from stowage location. Painter lines for both liferafts were leading into sea with end immersed in water.  Life raft or the casing could not be seen anywhere in the vicinity.    Both HRUs were found intact with the painters securely attached to liferaft support stand.    Lashing straps were found to be broken for both life rafts.
ImmediateAction: Painter line was pulled in carefully to check if life rafts are still attached to it.     Entire painter line was taken out of water and it was noticed that there was no life raft at its end.    Photographs were taken of the site.    Vessels Operators were informed through telephone about the incident.       MRCC Jeddah was notified through Email. Agents were informed and were requested to inform Rastanura port authorities accordingly.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Accidental loss of life rafts due to failure of lashing straps.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New lashing material supplied.
LessionLearnt: Importance of regular inspection to check the condition of the lashing material
ReviewComments: New lashing material supplied. Regular checks to be done to ascertain the condition of lashing material.
ClosingNote: Regular condition monitoring of lashing material

IncidentDetails: During  rounds on deck,it was observed that the foward mooring lines were chaffing due to swell conditions.
IncidentEventDetails: During cargo operations it was noticed that the vessel was swaying due to the wind ad swell. All the mooring ropes were immediately checked,to which it was noticed that te foward springs were chaffing due to the movement of the vessel.
ImmediateAction: The mooring ropes were immediately changed from end to end and a protective canvas was wrapped throughout the length which was in contach with the rollers.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 mooring ropes were slightly damaged and the strands partially sliced due to chaffing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The mooring ropes was immediately changed end to end and a protective canvas was placed throughout the length of the rope which was in contact witht he rollers.
Corrective Action: Training on mooring practices including appreciation of hazards and related mitigating measures when the berth is open to the effects of sea / swell.
LessionLearnt: Importance of vigilance in mooring practices. Extra attn to mooring at berth open to the effects of sea/swell
ReviewComments: satisfactory
ClosingNote: Better appreciation of the hazards that may be faced when the berth is open to the conditions of Sea and Swell. Also requirement for closer monitoring of moorings at such ports to be un=derstood by all staff.

IncidentDetails: FIRE NOZZLE DETACHED FROM FIRE HOSE AND DROOPED INTO SEA (AT SBB2, FUJAIRAH), WHILE   PORTABLE EMERGENCY PUMP TRIED OUT.
IncidentEventDetails: RIG FIRE HOSE, CONNECT WITH EMG PUMP, PUMP STARTED, AB HOLD FIRE HOSE,  FIRE NOZZLE FELL DOWN TO SEA WHEN SEA WATER PASS THROUGH IT
ImmediateAction: INFORM TECH. SUPR. (ALREADY ONBOARD). PURCHASE LOCALLY NEW ONE. INFORM AGENT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: nil
Proposedcorrectiveactions: crew handling hose , should always double check the nozzle is propely engaged to the hose adapter before opening the hydrant valve.
Corrective Action: to ensure and double check the nozzle is seated fully inside the hose female coupling and the self locking mechanism is fully locked after nozzle inerted.
ClosingNote: adequate training provided to crew to ensure possiblity of reoccurance is avoided.

IncidentDetails: Flexible hose came off pipe while transferring sludge from bilge sludge tank to fuel oil  overflow tank, via the sludge pump. No property damage, personal injury or  environmental release occurred.
IncidentEventDetails: Sludge was to be landed at Laemchabang on 7th June , but was not possible due  damage to vessel pump. Sludge tanks were full and vessel was going to idle at     Kaoshiung anchor waiting for fresh voyage instructions. At Kaoshiung anchor, sludge  could not be offlanded due considerable costs. TSI procured dispensation for vessel to  use fuel oil overflow tank on a temporary basis. Transfer was proposed to be done by  sludge pump; using a flexible hose in the discharge of pump. However it was decided  that it would be as last resort and as minimum as possible.  On 23rd june vessel got a few hours notice to commence her next voyage. M.E.  maintainence was in progress and all out attempts had to be made to avoid delay.  On the next day, since all sludge tanks were full by the time it was decided to  transfer about 10 cu.m to fuel oil overflow tank.  It was nearly the end of a full day maintainence work on the main engine; and then  decided to commence the sludge transfer operations. RA and Tool box meeting done  at site; flexible connections made and transfer commenced. Three people were  standby at the pump region monitoring the flexible hose and transfer.Initially when  water and oily sludge were mixed all went well. When content of oil in mixture  increased, pump pressure increased and hose came off the pipe; leading to internal  oil spill.  Although the volume of oil spill was not much, it splashed around large areas
ImmediateAction: Pump stopped immediately and valves closed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL ACCORDING TO ATTACHED REPORT
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL A/P ATTACHED REPORT
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: When using a combination of equipments along with the main equipment, the working pressure must be to suit the weakest part of the combination (eg. hose band, material of pipe,etc)

IncidentDetails: Chief officer tripped on deck at night over split cargo (coke) lying on deck during draft check causing minor sprain to right foot wrist while preventing the fall
IncidentEventDetails: Cargo spilit on sea side deck not noticable by stevedores & crew at night due to colour of cargo
ImmediateAction: Stevedores informed to clear safety walkway of sea side immediately
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor sprain to right foot wrist
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Foreman to be informed if stevedores not taking action.
Corrective Action: Duty Officers to inform Stevedore foreman to clear safety walkways and main deck of  any cargo residues.
LessionLearnt: Importance of keeping main deck and safety walkway clean at all times.
ReviewComments: Ship shore safety meeting to address the point of clearing cargo residues.
ClosingNote: Main deck to kept clear of cargo residues at all times.

IncidentDetails: On completion of discharge on the morning of the 9th February 2017 at Pembroke UK , difficulty was experienced in blowing the cargo hoses with compressed air.  The No 3 manifold when initially charged with compressed air suddenly gave way at 3.5 bar allowing the line to be blown without opening the manifold v/v.
IncidentEventDetails: On completion of discharge on the morning of the 9th February 2017 at Pembroke UK ,it was decided to drain the shore arm through the ships manifold and into the tank . Manifold was Presurized , difficulty was experienced in blowing the cargo hoses with compressed air.  The No 3 manifold v/v  when initially charged with compressed air suddenly gave way at 3.5 bar allowing the line to be blown without opening the manifold v/v.
ImmediateAction: Upon completion of Discharge , the shore hose was pressurized with air ,with the intention of draining the line through the manifold valves into the tank. Initially when the shore line was pressurized, the pressure was not holding in No. 3 Manifold. The ,manifold valve was opened and then closed . Same manifold valve was pressurized again and found in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO Damage or Injury Occured . No Time loss Occured on vessels account.
DirectCause: The valve
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Manifold Valve pressure tested and now in satisfactory condition
ClosingNote: Manifold valve seat ring renewed, pressure tested and found satisfactory.

IncidentDetails: On 17th Feb while correcting the trim and list by de ballasting Forepeak and Filling 6S WBT we got a Flow failure alarm from Void space no.1 in Fixed gas detection alarm panel. Then we initiated checking the sounding of Void space( which is being monitored and recorded on daily basis and logged in sounding log) we found 1.3 mtr of water in void space and sounding kept on increasing. Throughout the voyage vessel has been experiencing bad weather. Vessel was slamming, rolling pitching heavily.
IncidentEventDetails: 17-02-2017  0830: Started educting FPK and filling 6S for correcting list  17-02-2017 1030: Got flow failure alarm in fixed gas detection system  17-02-2017  1040:Sounding noted for Void no.1 as 1.3 mtrs ( Previous day sounding-nil)  17-02-2017 1100: Started Educting No.1 Void space.  19-02-2017  1030: No.1 Void inspected and found to have soft repair done in past with a lot of extra lashings like wire lashings , bottle screw,metal bars, Tripod stands . Few were broken and few out of place.This would have caused most likly due to bad weather which the vessel encountered in Cross Atlantic voyage and also due to over the time weather of these lashing materials as these were done 5 yrs back in 2012 as per the records available.
ImmediateAction: We started the ballast pump to de ballast the Void space no.1. On 19th Feb we made an entry in Void Space no.1 to do a thorough inspection of the space. We found that leak was from the port side ballast line and also noticed that soft patch repair was carried out earlier by Previous mgmt  staff and full line has been secured with extra support( wire lashings, Bottle screw, Metal support). We found few lashings broken and lying on bottom. Then we went through the previous inspection record and found that repair work was carried out  during March-April 2012 and the inspection report made by Previous mgmt staff dated 27 April 2012 states soft repair work was done and tested satisfactory.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Inspection of the Void Space and the ballast pipelines within at an increased frequency of not more than 3 months, instead of the present frequency of 6 months.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: bad weather incident happened during STS operation at offcshore STS area Lome,Togo
IncidentEventDetails: Discharge operations between the NAV Pulsar (MV) and the Kingis (DV) had been completed at 1848 hours 20th March.  The two cargo hoses (1x8”, 1x10”) had been blown through by compressed air immediately after completion. They had not been dis-connected at this time as we were waiting on confirmation of figures and charterers instructions.  Cargo figures had been calculated on both vessels and passed to the respective offices.    At about 2300 hours 20/03/17 there was an un-expected and sudden increase in wind velocity from the ten knots experienced during the day.   The onset was sudden and with no warning and quickly increased to a sustained 45 knots and gusting to 55 knots. At the same time the rain swept across the anchorage increasing in intensity to obscure the view of the main deck, becoming horizontally driven with the wind. It maintained this force until about 2350 hours, subsiding to between 25 – 30 knots and then to a speed of 10 knots by about 0030 hours 21/03/17
ImmediateAction: Own v/l had engines on stand-by at 2320 hours and was riding to the port anchor with nine shackles in the water.   Due to the vessel swinging quickly to starboard and into the wind there was a gap opening between the two vessels and during this time the lead wire from No.1 fender parted, causing the fender to drift aft towards the other fenders in the string.  Under instruction from the mooring master, the aft wire from No.4 fender was quickly transferred from the Kingis to the Nave Pulsar and secured on the aft main deck mooring bitt. This stopped the drift of the fenders and allowed the Nave Pulsar retention of the four primary and one secondary fenders.    The two cargo hoses were disconnected from the daughter vessel between 2330 – 0001 hours and lifted onto the Nave Pulsar. No pollution occurred during this operation which was safely done by the crews of both vessels.  Un-mooring commenced at 0001/21 however there were two incidents of metal / metal contact despite the fender No.4 preventing full contact. Both contacts were in the region of frames 31 – 33 with hull plating being set in between one and three cms at two separate points. The plate was about two meters below the upper deck level with the contact area being marked by paint scraping. No plate fracture or holes were noted on an internal inspection damage being seen to some frames and beam being impact distorted  All mooring lines from both vessels were safely retrieved and the Kingis moved slowly off the Nave Pulsar and anchored about a mile distance away.    In the extreme weather conditions which suddenly occurred across all of Lome anchorage both vessel crews carried out the work required for the safe emergency cast-off without further incident or injury.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two dents observed on the ship side 2 m below deck about 2-3 cm in depth between frame 29-32. One longitudinal in ERm found to be deformed.
DirectCause: Sudden change in weather condition with very little time to react.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Multiple, as listed below.
Corrective Action: Fleet Alert to be sent to all vessels describing about the incident and the learnings from this investigation.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Aft station mooring rope ( used as tug line ) parted during unberthing operations
IncidentEventDetails: Whilst unberthing at Port Said, v/l had issued ships line to tug at Aft Station for pulling out. Soon after, this rope parted as the tug was pulling the v/l. The parting occured at the lead and 100m of mooring rope was compromised.  All ships staff were at a safe distance during time of incident.
ImmediateAction: The parted mooring rope was heaved up and a new ships line was issued to the tug to carry out the unberthing operation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Aft mooring stn , stbd side inner side, mooring rope ( double braid ) mooring rope to have parted at 100m from the eye.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper communication between the Pilot and the tug is recommended.
Corrective Action: Training to be carried out on board for the Master pilot information exchange
LessionLearnt: Proper MPEX must be complied with
ClosingNote: The bridge team was not aware of the the maximum pull of the tug, due to improper master pilot information exchange. Contributory causes will be the language barrier also between the pilot and the bridge team as the pilot speaking to the tugs in local language, which was not understood by the bridge team

IncidentDetails: No. 4 hatch cover touched the shore gangway
IncidentEventDetails: At 03:10 Hrs. Lt. 19 Feb. 2017 during the opening of Hatch No.4 cover  the AB  was handling the   control and O/S is on the port side checking if any obstacle while the 2nd officer is on the starboard side   doing the checking and after satisfied that there is no obstruction the 2nd officer  gave   the order to open the hatch cover.  At  this point on he went on the forward part of no. 4 hatch to   make sure that all is well and just when he is going back he noticed that the clearance of the shore   gangway to the hatch cover is coming very close so immediately hail  to halt but the hatch cover still   touched the shore gangway causing a scratched on the stainless steel handle while the force slightly   bending our railings.
ImmediateAction: Report incident to office take photos and asses damage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To :follow procedure of openning hatches inspect and check around for any lock or pin is still in lock position and to determin if any object close in the area.
Corrective Action: Close attention to be paid during opening and closure of hatch covers.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Surroundings to be always checked when carrying out any critical operation

IncidentDetails: One of breast rope parted due to heavy surging of vessel while alongside loading Urea at Jose terminal.
IncidentEventDetails: Berth was directly exposed to open sea. In 1.5-2 mtr swell vsl rolling and pitching while alongside causes chaffing of ropes.Due to which one of breast rope parted.
ImmediateAction: Other rope pass to shore bollard and grease applying regularly to save the ropes.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: one mooring rope 20 mtr section parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: continue monitoring ropes.
Corrective Action: Extra ropes to be put fwd / aft and ropes to be bar tight.
ClosingNote: To be discussed during safety meeting

IncidentDetails: One of the two aft  breast Line Parted.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel Port Side Alongside @ MLT berth duely to wind vessel was continously surging.  Vessel took all anti chaffing procedures which included regularyl attending of mooring ropes , applying teepol to reduce frictions,using teflon sheets for protection.  But to swell directing to the beam, one of the  breast line parted.
ImmediateAction: Rope immediately heaved and Made fast again making a temporary Eye using bowline.  After departure new eye was spliced .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One of aft breast line parted. Appx length 20 mtrs.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To ensure length of breast ropes not very short & v/l will in dicussion with Pilot pass extra ropes to distribute excess load on breast ropes due to surging .  Even after taking all possible precautions against chafing ( use of teepol , teflon sheets , regular tending of mooring ropes) rope parted towards the end of port stay when v/l was at the ballast condition . V/l will order extra new ropes .
ClosingNote: Noted. All PCA in order.

IncidentDetails: Slude oil spill from FO/LO sludge tank during internal transfer
IncidentEventDetails: On 17.03.2017 around 2245 hrs fourth Engineer as usual was carrying out sludge and bilge transfers.  He  was about to transfer scavenge box drain tank to Bilge separate oil tank.   Before transferring checked the sounding of scavenge box drain tank. With the sounding cap open , he started opening the scavenge drain tank suction valve to sludge pump.oil started gushing out from the sounding pipe.   Not realising the reason he closed the sounding cap.Then transferred scavenge drain tank to BSOT.   After transferring , opened Main Engine valve to scavenge drain tank. He started cleaning the oil spill along with the duty oiler thereby missed to close the suction valve of scavenge drain tank to sludge pump.   Sludge pump suction valves before strainer closed.  As the FO/LO sludge tank suction valve already in open condition ( fourth engineer not aware ) the pressure made sludge to pour out from  purifier drain funnels.   Oil in the purifier tray has spilled through the pipeline openings to the bottom platform bulkheads and to the bilges. Oil found only on the starboard bulkhead side in the bilges with oil in the STBD bilge well.   Cleaning in progress. Purifier tray oil removed. Bulkhead and bilges cleaning in progress.   Oil collected from purifier trays pumped in back to FO/LO sludge tank(cap:4.2 cu.m)
ImmediateAction: FO/LO sludge tank suction valve as well as Scavenge Box drain tank suction valve to sludge pump closed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Engine room staff especially engineers briefed about the importance of cross checking the valves before carrying out transfers, also to close the valves after the operation.
Corrective Action: All Engine room staff especially engineers briefed about the importance of cross checking the valves before carrying out transfers, also to close the valves after the operation.
LessionLearnt: Tool box meeting to be carried out before transfering
ReviewComments: To be discussed in safety meeting
ClosingNote: Senior engineers to monitor/guide  the line setup in order  to avoid recurrence of such incidents till junior engineers are confident enough in transferring operation.

IncidentDetails: AFT ASSISTING TUG "MIRBAT (OWN TUG LINE) PARTED DURING UN-BERTHING OPERATIONS
IncidentEventDetails: At 0545hrs / 19th March 2017 - Vessel on standby and ready for departure Salalah.  At 0600hrs - Fore (RAKHYUT) and aft (MIRBAT) tugs made fast to own vessel's stbd side quarter.  At 0612hrs - Pilot onboard.  At 0615hrs to 0620hrs  - Master and Pilot exchange information as per checklist OP # 02 while  single up lines fore & aft.  At 0624hrs  - Last line onboard.  At 0630hrs  - AFT tug boat "MIRBAT" which is assisting to own vessel's stbd side quarter parted its OWN tug line. Ship's line immediately sent to tug MIRBAT to complete the un-berthing operations.  At 0635hrs  - Fore and Aft tugs casted-off.   At 0648hrs  - Pilot off    REMARSK:  No any injuries to own crews nor tug's crews on the above incident.  Also no any further damage or whatsoever on both sides except the tug's mirbat parted assisting line.  Pilot/tug Mirbat master reported the incident to port control accordingly.  After the inicident, Master sent same above report/notification to local agent clarifying that Master has reserve the rights of ship's owners, disponent owners to take whatever action they might find suitable in this matter. Vessel and her owners is not responsible for any losses, claims or delays that may arise.
ImmediateAction: SHIP'S LINE IMMEDIATELY SENT TO TUG TO COMPLETE THE UN-BERTHING OPERATIONS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ASSISTING TUG TO ENSURE ALL LINES BEING USED DURING BERTHING/UNBERTHING ALWAYS IN GOOD CONDITION. MASTER ALSO TO CONFIRMED WITH PILOT THE SAME DURING "MASTER AND PILOT EXCHANGE INFORMATION" PRIOR ANY BERTHING OR UNBERTHING OPERATIONS.
Corrective Action: sample
ClosingNote: INCIDENT REVIEWED & CLOSED

IncidentDetails: At around 2050H/20th March2017, fire has broken out in the Incinerator.  From the Engine Cadet’s account, while he was putting in more garbage to burn, an explosion occurred and a rapid/rushing fire followed causing him to panic and unable to close to Incinerator door. This led to the spread of the fire outside of the Incinerator body and its vicinity. The C/E, who was down the Engine Room at that time, was able to respond positively and together with help of the Duty Oiler and 3/E, were able to extinguish the fire.      At around 2055H, the fire was declared under control.    Extent of damage: Outside part Incinerator Control Panel burnt (the inside part seem intact except the PCB which is located near the burnt side.
ImmediateAction: C/E, who was down the Engine Room at that time, was able to respond positively and together with help of the Duty Oiler and 3/E, were able to extinguish the fire.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: INCIDENT REVIEWED & CLOSED

IncidentDetails: B.A compressor which was being used for filling SCBA bottles , got ceased with cracking sound , presently unable to run the compressor .
IncidentEventDetails: 1030LT : Master releived 3/0 from bridge . 1100 LT: 3/0 & C/O came to AirCon room with SCBA & EEBD bottles which are to be topped up .Intially to start with EEBD bottle was connected to charging hose .All starting checks carried out as posted in bulkhead. Compressor was started . Both the drain pipes were kept open intially when being started.Its general practise to keep them open prior starting. After about 15 mins operation , when the gauge in EEBD was showing in orange zone crackling sound was heard. (Like fan fin hitting the guard ) the next very moment compressor was switched off . Bottle was disconnected . Engineers were informed , by then it was lunch time . Compressor was kept idle for an hour in this due time.
ImmediateAction: Compressor was switched off , bottle was disconnected . Engineers were informed , by then it was lunch time . Compressor was kept idle for an hour in this due time.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: When opened it was identified .Piston rings were broken and connecting rod of the compressor was also broken and found loose .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Further operation of the compressor shall only be done in presence of engineer with prior notice to them.
Corrective Action: To include job for detailed inspection of SCBA compressor as per maker instruction in PMS
Cause Analysis: Operating equipment or machinery without authority
ClosingNote: The failure of the compressor indicates  lack of lubrication and or correct specification of oil not being used for the machinery. SCBA compressor should be maintained as per makers instruction and the same is to be in PMS.

IncidentDetails: While operating Hose handling crane, the slewing pump hydraulic hose which was rather old and needed renewal, had ruptured , thus spraying hydraulic oil on the crane platform and trunk.
IncidentEventDetails: While operating Hose handling crane, the slewing pump hydraulic hose which was rather old and needed renewal, had ruptured , thus spraying hydraulic oil on the crane platform and trunk.
ImmediateAction: The power pack was shut and oil flow from the damaged hose was stopped. A spare hose was brought and replace. The system was purged and crane tried out. Office advised and BP casuality report connected.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: hydraulic hose ruptured due to fatigue of material.
DirectCause: PMS not followed strictly and spares not renewed inspite of Wastage / Deterioration
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PMS to be strictly followed. If hoses show signs of wastage/deterioration, same to be renewed even before PMS schedule.
Corrective Action: All flexible hoses which are exposed weather should be renewed every 5 years. This applies to all vessels including dry vessels
LessionLearnt: Critical Equipment spares should be on board
ReviewComments: Technical Fleet alert sent to all vessels // Master briefed // Included in PMS
ClosingNote: Technical Fleet alert sent to all vessels // Master briefed // Included in PMS

IncidentDetails: Attached - Master Statement of Facts, MPA Processed based corrective Action, relevant Photos
IncidentEventDetails: Attached - Master Statement of Facts, MPA Processed based corrective Action, relevant Photos
ImmediateAction: Attached - Master Statement of Facts, MPA Processed based corrective Action, relevant Photos
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Stowaway Incident

Security Measures may include dedicated personnel with Walkie Talkie’s for monitoring of Potential access points as suitable, employing external security service for stowaway Search, etc.,”

IncidentDetails: During rounds noted that steam was leaking through Composite Boiler main steam stop v/v and within small time  the steam leakage increased considerably.
ImmediateAction: Master informed and ensured vessel is afe for M/Estoppage. M/E stopped,Comp boiler depressirized, gasket renewed and Comp boiler put in use & passage resumed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to leaking Comp Boiler Mointing M/E had to be stopped for repairs, which caused the offhire of the  vessel.
DirectCause: Failure of Equipment and Non Compliance of company procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renewed the damaged gasget & damaged nut for the Comp boiler main steam v/v.
Corrective Action: Fleet Wide circular to all vessels on the incident to use only correct type of gaskets in steam systems.
LessionLearnt: See attached report
ReviewComments: See attached Investigation rpeort
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out  and Report attached

IncidentDetails: Loss of Portable 2-way radio overboard, during un-mooring at Jing Tang.
IncidentEventDetails: Crew were involved in Un-berthing the vessel at Jing tang. Chief officer had manned forward station with Bosun and 2 more crew members. During the course of the operation, the bosun leaned overside while lowering the tug line. His Walkie Talkie,which was kept in the top pocket in his cover-alls slipped and fell overboard.
ImmediateAction: Crew were advised to Clip their walkie talkies to their lower pockets in their cover-alls.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 Portable UHF walkie talkie lost overboard.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition to be raised for supply of Covers with slings for use with Walkie Talkie onboard.  Crew training in the proper use of Ships equipment
ClosingNote: Agree with masters cause analysis

IncidentDetails: OS-Sprain on Left ankle.
IncidentEventDetails: While working daily routine job on deck,slipped from the last step of the stairs.  left ankle twisted.
ImmediateAction: immobilaize the left ankle.  Pain killer , crepe bandage , volini ontment given.  Completed bed rest advised.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training provided to the person.
LessionLearnt: Importance of proper walking technique and posture
ReviewComments: Regular briefing to crew about proper posture while walking.
ClosingNote: Noted training provided to the respective crew

IncidentDetails: Oiler-Sprain in left wrist.
IncidentEventDetails: while Tighten the bolts he hits hammer on his left hand wrist.
ImmediateAction: Pain killer given.  Immobilize the wrist.  Crepe bandage and volini ointment given.  Advised avoid heavy jobs.
DirectCause: Improper positioning and lack of exp
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training provided to the person concerned and discussed in Safety Meeting.
LessionLearnt: Proper positioning for the job is required.
ReviewComments: All PPE to be used when carrying out such job.
ClosingNote: Person assigned such job has to be properly positioned and experienced to carry out the job.

IncidentDetails: NOTICED WATER LEAKAGE BEING COLLECTED IN SOOT COLLECTION TANK AND   ALSO FROM MAIN ENGINE TURBO CHARGER DRAINS.  ON FURTHER INVESTIGATION COMPLETE ECONOMISER COILS WERE HYDRO-PRESSURE   TESTED AND FOUND COIL  LEAKING.
IncidentEventDetails: Pls find attached time sheet and communacation log.
ImmediateAction: All valves leading to and from EGE were closed and pressure tested, found  leakage in coils, Same were plugged and pressure tested again, found remaining coils and plugs are intact. Vessel resumed passage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 3 coils out of 12 were plugged due to leakage, The effective waste heat recovery from EGE is reduced to 75%.
DirectCause: Material failure due to Oxygen corrosion
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All plugged coils to be renewed at the suitable opportunity.
ClosingNote: Boiler tubes have been ordered and kept on board for renewal in the forthcoming scheduled

IncidentDetails: While cutting Vegetables GS cut his left hand index finger
IncidentEventDetails: 0710 Hrs: While cutting beans GS cut his index finger  0711 Hrs: Chief cook put cotton and applied pressure to stop bleading  0712 Hrs : Call recieved on bridge about incident   0715 Hrs : GS arrived on bridge,wound cleaned and First aid administered.
ImmediateAction: First Aid administered
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Cut on left hand index finger including part of nail
DirectCause: Inadequate skill and precaution.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To be evaluated for chopping skills prior his next joining
Corrective Action: Training to be provided and cutting knifes to be inspected and defective items to be condemned and removed from service
LessionLearnt: To be careful while with sharp tools & not to lose concentration even momentarily.
ClosingNote: All closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Lifebuoy with light was lost during rough weather.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl experienced very rough weather due to which the lifebuoy with light at the forecastle area was lost at sea. During routine rounds in the morning, it was found that the lifebuoy was missing.
ImmediateAction: Vsl does not have spare lifebuoy to replace the lost . Urgent requisition raised and company to provide lifebuoy at Canaport.(Next port).   Nearest MRCC informed.
DirectCause: Heavy Weather procedure not complied with
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: New lifebuoy to be placed in lieu of losted one.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out. Loss of lifebuoy is due to adverse weather condition

IncidentDetails: During Life boat manouevring  life boat engines remote control lever, for ahead/astern direction control  seized in astern direction  after 15 minutes of engine running, making ahead movement of propeller impossible.
IncidentEventDetails: 1608: Life boat lowered until waterborne using lifeboat davit.  1610 : Life boat waterborne  1614:   Floating blocks released, Lifeboat manoeuvred ahead and astern, tried out sprinkler system.  1630:  life boat engines remote control lever, for ahead/astern direction control  seized in astern direction, making ahead movement of propeller impossible.  1640: Vessel passed gant line to the boat so as to bring the boat close to the vessel.  1635:  Lifeboat heaved upto the stowed position.
ImmediateAction: Upon investigation of the remote unit, we observed pieces of plastic bush of the direction change over gear entangled into the gear, this caused restriction of the ahead movement. The remote control unit observed sealed by rivets, the rivets were drilled out and later reinstated, . A new bush for the direction change over gear fabricated and installed in the sealed unit.  The lever is now operating satisfactory to control the ahead/astern direction of the propeller from the Coaxwain's seat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Noted, A new bush for the direction change over gear fabricated and installed in the sealed unit.  The lever is now operating satisfactory to control the ahead/astern direction of the propeller from the Coaxwain's seat. Ensure to carry out testing as recommended on periodical intervals and maintain state of LSA in state of readiness at all times.

IncidentDetails: Shore grab wire parted
IncidentEventDetails: Loading time shore gab wire parted and fall on the top of the cargo in No. 3 hold
ImmediateAction: Checked & inspected of Ship's crane No. 3 and working satisfactory. No damages to Ship's property
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Grab wire damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Foreman to be warned by the duty officer in case of rough handling by the grab operator.
Corrective Action: Proper inspection and greasing of the grab wire.
ClosingNote: To be briefed during Pre-joining breifing

IncidentDetails: Vessel was at Dijibouti for discharging.Boiler was continuously firing at 54 % load,suddenly boiler flame became unstable and smoke started coming from boiler aft casing.Eventually boiler tripped.During this period some soot gone to sea through scrubber overboard.This was noticed by loading master and then he informed harbour master.Found fd fan controller and atomizing steam controller malfunctioning for the bolier which caused to shutdown..After this boiler burner was taken out,cleaned,pilot burner cleaned,flame eye was cleaned.Bypass of atomizing steam was opened.Boiler was started again and slowly load was increased and cargo operation was resumed. Harbour master and port authority imposed a fine of 10000 USD fine to vessel for environmental damage.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was at Dijibouti for discharging.Boiler was continuously firing at 54 % load,suddenly boiler flame became unstable and smoke started coming from boiler aft casing.Eventually boiler tripped.During this period some soot gone to sea through scrubber overboard.This was noticed by loading master and then he informed harbour master.Found fd fan controller and atomizing steam controller malfunctioning.After this boiler burner was taken out,cleaned,pilot burner cleaned,flame eye was cleaned.Bypass of atomizing steam was opened.Boiler was started again and slowly load was increased and cargo operation was resumed.Harbour master and port authority imposed a fine of 10000 USD fine to vessel for environmental damage.
ImmediateAction: Boiler wass stopped,Boiler burner taken out cleaned.Atomizing steam by pass valve opened and atomizing steam given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Boiler was tripped due to flame failure as flame became unstable.found smoke coming out from boiler casing aft.Because of this monentarily soot carried out in sea through scrubber overboard.This was noticed by Loading master and later it was passed on to harbour master. Port authority has imposed a fine of 10000 USD for environmental damage.
DirectCause: The manual operation of the boiler during the discharge without correctly adjusting the air fuel ratio also contributed to the repeated flame failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Atomizing steam valve was oevrahuled on board along with FD fan positioner abd system is working well,
ClosingNote: Spares on order

IncidentDetails: Planned to carry out furnace inspection of Aux. boiler and while opening the furnace door noticed seepage of water. On further opening noticed more seepage of water. Suspect prolonged Atomizing steam leakage and / or some water wall tubes leaking.  After draining water from furnace further just below Manhole level, inspected and saw steam and water leakage from Screen wall tubes.  Suspect either screen wall / steam generation tubes.
IncidentEventDetails: Pls find attached Boiler Repair Timings - SOF
ImmediateAction: Changed over both M/E and A/E’s to LSMGO and M/E stopped at 1215 LT.  Furnace water draining in progress. On draining completion, shall inspect furnace to ascertain the probable leak.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 out of 432 tubes of Rear Bank tubes plugged due to leakage.  Less than 0.5% only for the Rear Bank tubes.   Effective Steam Generation  will not be affected for continuous service.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Plugged Rear Bank tubes to be renewed at the suitable opportunity / in the forth coming Dry-Dock.
ClosingNote: Noted that the tubes were plugged and pressure tested and no leaks observed. Meanwhile, upon our further investigation, maintaining feed water temperature around 85 deg C should prevent recurrence. Pl maintain Hot Well temperature around 85 deg C at all times

IncidentDetails: Gaurd rails on the poop deck bent and damaged. DW tank vent head pipe bent.
IncidentEventDetails: During STS operation with M/T Breeze, daughter vessel's stern lines were made fast on the poop deck passing through the fairlead in the aft due to incorrect assesment of the lead of lines. Later daughter vessel moved ahead causing all the weight of mooring lines coming on ship's rail and causing damage to ship's guard rails and bending of DW tank vent pipe.
ImmediateAction: Stern lines were cleared and made fast froom fairlead from starboard quarter. Officer incharge and crew breifed about correct assesment of lead of mooring lines and to always check with bridge/master whenever in doubt.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Poop deck railing and one air vent got bent while passing rope to other vessel. Caused due passing the rope without assessing the correct lead.
DirectCause: STS MOORING STANDARD PRACTICE NOT FOLLOWED. IMPROPER MONITORING AND ASSESSMENT OF THE SITUATION.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All briefed to carry out planning for deployment of mooring lines to the STS vessel with special attention to the lead of the mooring rope.
Corrective Action: DD spec will be raised
LessionLearnt: Proper planning, monitoring and supervision during mooring.
ReviewComments: In order
ClosingNote: Proposed CA in order.

IncidentDetails: Port side gangway stanchion stuck inside jetty & shore fender and broken.
IncidentEventDetails: The terminal did not have the facility to place shore ladder and did not provide any safe location to place vessel's portable MOT ladder. Subsequently Port side accommodation ladder/gangway was lowered and used as means of access between shore and ship. As the distance between gangway platform and wharf was not safe for boarding, ganway was pulled outboard and placed on jetty. Due to tidal variation, during low tide it was not safe to keep the gangway on wharf. So it was decided to pick up the gangway. During the process of picking up the gangway, a stanchion got stuck in between the wharf and shore fender and got damaged.
ImmediateAction: The ganway was safely picked up and carefully examined for any structural damages. Apart from the broken stanchion, no other damage was obsereved.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis - Lack of Supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Officer to be present during the handling of the Gangway to oversee. WHen carrying out training of Ch-06 of COSWP pls include the learning from this incident.
Corrective Action: Officer to be present during the handling of the Gangway to oversee. WHen carrying out training of Ch-06 of COSWP pls include the learning from this incident.
LessionLearnt: Importance of supervision
ReviewComments: Comments satisfactory
ClosingNote: Ensure that the defect is repaired.

IncidentDetails: On 17.04.2017,0345hrs reefer compressor abnormal trip.  When checked found compressor very hot near valve plate area.  Other compressor was started.  During alarm all parameters were normal and there was sufficient oil  level in c/case.  Condenser was cleaned two days back only and compressor was overhauled   on 26.03.2017 and was changed all inlet/outlet reeds in valve plate.   When dismantled the compressor found crankshaft broken in two piece   near bottom end bearing area.when checked found crankshaft is broken near bottom end bearing area.Seems breakage has occured due to material failure.  As installed shaft was not original from makers,material might not be of good quality.There is no other damages as such ,Piton,rings ,suction and discharge valve plates all are intact so chances are that it is a csae of material failure.Also compressor was overhauled on 29.03.2017 and that time there was no sign of any failure or breakages.During breakage suction pressure was 0.7kg,discharge pr was 19.5kg,sea water temp was 31 deg,outlet temp was 33 deg,oil level was normal.
IncidentEventDetails: when checked found crankshaft is broken near bottom end bearing area.Seems breakage has occured due to material failure.  As installed shaft was not original from makers,material might not be of good quality.There is no other damages as such ,Piton,rings ,suction and discharge valve plates all are intact so chances are that it is a csae of material failure.Also compressor was overhauled on 29.03.2017 and that time there was no sign of any failure or breakages.During breakage suction pressure was 0.7kg,discharge pr was 19.5kg,sea water temp was 31 deg,outlet temp was 33 deg,oil level was normal.
ImmediateAction: compressor was changed over and started.damaged compressor was isolated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 17.04.2017,0345hrs reefer compressor abnormal trip.  When checked found compressor very hot near valve plate area.  Other compressor was started.  During alarm all parameters were normal and there was sufficient oil  level in c/case.  Condenser was cleaned two days back only and compressor was overhauled   on 26.03.2017 and was changed all inlet/outlet reeds in valve plate.   When dismantled the compressor found crankshaft broken in two piece   near bottom end bearing area.  when checked found crankshaft is broken near bottom end bearing area.Seems breakage has occured due to material failure.  As installed shaft was not original from makers,material might not be of good quality.There is no other damages as such ,Piton,rings ,suction and discharge valve plates all are intact so chances are that it is a csae of material failure.Also compressor was overhauled on 29.03.2017 and that time there was no sign of any failure or breakages.
DirectCause: Prolonged operation with incorrect compressor parameters and valve settings was a contributory factor in addition to the age of the components. No supply details OR history of maintenance on the crank shaft to determine its origin.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: new cranshaft from original maker to fit.
ClosingNote: COMPRESSOR REONDITIONED AND FITTED. ONE SPRE COMPRESSOR COMPLETE AVAILABLE ON BOARD

IncidentDetails: AUX. ENGINE NO 2 COOLING WATER LT PUMP SHAFT SHEARED OFF.
IncidentEventDetails: ON 05.05.2017 AROUND 0625 HRS RECIEVED AE2 LUBE OIL HIGH TEMPERATURE ALARM. ON CHECKING FOUND NO SYMPTOM OF ATTACHED CW LT PUMP RUNNING. GENERATOR  STOPPED. PUMP DISMANTLED AND OVERHAULED.
ImmediateAction: GENERATOR STOPPED. PUMP DISMANTLED OVERHAULED AND FITTED BACK. REPLACED SHAFT, IMPELLER, MECHANICAL SEAL, BEARINGS
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: COOLING WATER ATTACHED LT PUMP SHAFT SHERED OFF, IMPELLER RUBBED, MECHANICAL SEAL DAMAGED.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: REGULAR CHECK OF THE ATTACHED PUMPS  INBETWEEN NORMAL MAINTENANCE SCHEDULE
Corrective Action: Please inspect LT PUMP PERIODICALY
ClosingNote: Vessel supplied with new shaft and impeller to maintain as spare.

IncidentDetails: 2nd officer was leaving CCR and while he opned the door, the door hit on teh forehead of OS who was on the alleyway walking towards the mess room. The impact made a wound on his forehead above the left eyebrow about 1 cm and 3 mm deep.
IncidentEventDetails: 2nd officer was leaving CCR and while he opned the door, the door hit on teh forehead of OS who was on the alleyway walking towards the mess room. The impact made a wound on his forehead above the left eyebrow about 1 cm and 3 mm deep.
ImmediateAction: First aid administered. The wound was carefully checked and concluded that no sutures were necessary
DirectCause: Non Compliance of COSWP procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed in Monthly SCMM
LessionLearnt: Below Conditions were assessed .  1.Condition of Weather at the time of incident: Fair and calm weather  2)Condition of the OS: Presently wound healed. Administered First Aid immediately and dressing everyday.  - Work/ Rest hours ( kindly send us the copy of his work / rest hour record) ===NO Viloations -Whether any influence of alcohol ===Negative, no influence of alcohol  -Was he capable of doing the job now ===Yes, he ia capable of performing his duties now  -Any comments from 2nd off WRT to his frame of mind (In your opinion and past experience was he careless etc ) ===No comments. He is careful person  3)Does the door is provided with Hydraulic door closer arrangement ===Yes, door with closer arrangement  4)Obstructions in the area ===No obstructions but the alleyway is quite narrow.
ReviewComments: The door is fitted with door closer arrangement, however if more force applied door can be opened faster. This often happens when people are in hurry,which is what caused the injury.The door closes in controlled manner. Therefore as a safety reminder a note has been displayed on such doors with  such potential.
ClosingNote: Pls instruct all crew and officers to use the doors easily without forcing Door closer arrangement, so that we can avoid such injuries in future and also discuss the same with all crew and officers during monthly SCMM to close the incident form our end.

IncidentDetails: Pilot ladder last step got damaged due to sudden movement of Pilot boat when alongside due to swell.
IncidentEventDetails: During the mooring master and cargo surveyors disembarkation at Malta OPL ,Pilot boat which was along side had sudden movement due to swell causing the boat to hit and damaging the 1st Spreader step of stbd pilot ladder .
ImmediateAction: Stbd pilot ladder was inspected thoroughly after picking up ladder found damage to the 1st Spreader step of ladder.  Stbd ladder was removed from use . PR raised for above incident
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pilot ladder Spreader was damaged by the pilot boat while disembarking Mooring Master. This was caused due to sudden movement of boat while alongside own vessel due to swell.
DirectCause: Non Compliance of Pilot ladder rigging rocedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pilot ladder should be lowered on to the boat only after it is alongside.
Corrective Action: New Pilot ladder to be supplied to vessel
LessionLearnt: 1. PIlot Ladder arrangement for size of the Craft was ascertained. 2. No proper inspection of ladder was carried out on completion of job or while securing up.
ReviewComments: Damage to pilot ladder was noticed after two days while preparing the pilot ladder for next operation. Pilot ladder was not rigged to proper height considering the freeboard the service launch boat. When launch damaged the pilot ladder, no crew noticed the damage. it is always advisable to keep close lookout of service launch while approaching the vessel.. In addition crew should briefed on the incident.
ClosingNote: Investigation Carried out

IncidentDetails: During discharging by ship's crane,Water boy climbing on crane no 2 outside  ladder get hit by crane moving ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: Ship staff noticed one man walking near no.3 cargo hold port side with other stevedores. This person was the water boy who boarded the vessel to supply water to other stevedores and crane operators. As per Stevedores, this water boy went up the no.2 crane which was being operated at that time when he climbed the ladder. He was hit by the rotational movement of the crane when he was climbing the inside ladder.    He came down the ladder and walked on deck and boarded a waiting car on the barge. He was seen by the ship staff sitting and drinking water inside the car.
ImmediateAction: None
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: As per stevedores person Died in hospital.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Keep more lookout on stevedores, crew training and frequent round on deck
Corrective Action: Keep more lookout on stevedores, crew training and frequent round on deck
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Ship staff to be vigilant and safety is paramount.

IncidentDetails: Cadet  suffered a deep cut on his right thumb region.
IncidentEventDetails: While carrying out routine rounds on deck, officer and the cadet went to check the cleanliness and securing arrangements of old wasted pipes stowed near the funnel deck. The officer along with the cadet tried to clear one of the wasted deck spray lines in order to secure the same. While shifting the pipe the crew member's thumb got stuck inbetween the wasted pipes and the pipes which were already in stowed condition leading to a deep cut in the thumb region.
ImmediateAction: Immediately administered following first aid:  Stopped the bleeding immediately by applying pressure on the wound with cotton.  After bleeding stopped, washed the wound thoroughly with dettol and povidone iodine solution  dressed the wound with gauze soaked in povidone iodine solution. Patient given bed rest and to avoid any type of work can cause stress on the right hand.   Administered tetanus toxoid vaccine 0.5 ml ( as injury due to rusted old pipelines) for prevention of infection  paracetamol 500 mg 1 tablet as a pain killer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut on the thumb of right hand
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training and safe working practices briefing given to all crew regarding handling and storage of pipes on deck.
LessionLearnt: Importance of proper PPE and work procedure
ReviewComments: Department head to ensure that crew is following proper work procedure and using proper PPE.
ClosingNote: Noted injury report discussed with all and training on safe working practise given to all

IncidentDetails: Mooring buoy chain detached from shore connection. Part damaged to wharf horizontal beam and wharf metal ladder fell below bottom. Ship portside gangway bent bottom aluminum plate and bent aluminum lower platform.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor damaged to portside accommodation ladder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gangway not to be placed on the Jetty in NAABSA port and Gangway to be porperly manned and duty AB to advised to be vigilant at all times.
ClosingNote: Gangway not to be placed on the Jetty in NAABSA port and Gangway to be porperly manned and duty AB to advised to be vigilant at all times. Mooring ropes to be tended as required to avoid over stress on them.

IncidentDetails: At 1815 LT, 26 April 2017 while manoeuvring approaching PDM anchorage area ME failed, including anchor windlass.
IncidentEventDetails: Chief Engineer must report the actual events, notify chief engineer for this matters.
ImmediateAction: Dropped anchor by hammering the anchor gear clutch to let go anchor since gear could not be release due shut down windlass, to prevent drifting towards nearby anchored vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The G/E no.2 was out of order
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Started G/E no.1 and Re-started G/E no.3 and put into service
Corrective Action: Condition and reliability of A/Es to be upgraded to prevent recurrence.
ClosingNote: Dropped anchor by hammering the anchor gear clutch to let go anchor since gear could not be release due shut down windlass, to prevent drifting towards nearby anchored vessel.

IncidentDetails: 0441-0445: While Unberthing at Port Douala, Cameroon, with the Pilot on board, Vessel's bow abreast of  break of forecastle came in contact with protruding piece of pipe from shore hopper while the vessel was swinging to Port.
IncidentEventDetails: 0412 hrs - Pilot boarded the vessel for Unberthing at Port Douala from Sea side from a Tug.  0420 hrs - Commenced Single up.  0436 hrs - Aft tug "Performance" was made fast.  0440 hrs - Ordered "Let go all Lines" (Fwd & Aft), Fwd Tug standing by for assistance.  0441 hrs - M/E was ordered D.S Astern, Vessel swinging to Port while M/E was D.S.Astern  0441~0445 hrs - The vessel made contact with the protruding piece of pipe from shore hopper port side of the hull abreast of the "break of f'stle". Vessel continued to swing and cleared from hopper.  0445 hrs - Vessel cleared the knuckle of the jetty on the port side, swung around to proceed towards the channel.
ImmediateAction: The extent of damages checked, No significant damages observed except few scratch marks on hull abreast break of f'cstle (Port side )
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Visually Observed no significant damages except for sew scratch marks on the hull abreast of break of f'stle port side.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To ensure that in such a scenario both fwd and aft tugs to be made fast with the vessel to clear sufficiently from the wharf prior to swinging in either direction.
Corrective Action: To ensure that in such a scenario both fwd and aft tugs to be made fast with the vessel to clear sufficiently from the wharf prior to swinging in either direction.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: While Discharging at Canaport, Canada large amount was ROB was left on board upon completion of discharging. ROB at the time found to be sludge in nature which was unpumpable and not reachable by ships cargo pumps. COW was carried out for 1W's (40-0-40) and 4W's (120-0-120) cargo oil tanks. There was no change observed in the ullage even after carrying out the crude oil washing.
IncidentEventDetails: 20.04.17   ---------    1454 : Pilot Onboard    1500 : Mooring Gang and Surveyor Onboard    1506 : Commenced Gauging    1630 : Commenced Mooring    1648 : Completed Mooring    1654 : Completed gauging    1648-1742 : Key Meeting.    1730 : Completed Calculations.    1730 : 30 Min Notice To E/R.    1800-2018: V/L could not start discharging due to COP starting problem.    2018 : After obtaining office permission, Commenced Discharging after bypassing the em'cy trips via No. 2 and No. 3 COP    2130 : V/L was notified by loading Master if V/L continues to discharge with this rate. Terminal would have to cast the vessel off.    2318 : All three COP's were started. V/L started to discharge at full rate.        21.04.17     --------    0001 : Protest was issued to V/L by surveyor and terminal about delay in starting of Cargo. V/L was notified that V/L would have to complete the discharge operation by 2000 Hrs. Else V/L would be removed from Berth.    0445 : Cargo Tank Temperatures were checked manually by MMC. Average Temperature found to be 15 C    0730 : Shore Booster Pump was stopped.    1115 : Large ROB were observed in 1W's and 4W's COT.     1130 : Commenced COW of 1W's COT (40-0-40).      1210 : Completed COW of 1W's COT.      1210-1240 : Educting Carried out of 1W's COT    1240 : Ullage of the tank was found same without any change. Loading Master was notified about the situation and V/L's intention to carry out COW of all the tanks if such ROB is found in other tanks.  V/L was instructed by Loading Master to carry out only minimum MARPOL COW and V/L will be removed from the SBM at 21.04.17/2330 LT with what ever cargo remains onboard. Also terminal does not want to receive such sludge as it can chock there loading arms.    1240 : Commenced COW of 4W's COT (120-0-120)    1340 : Completed COW of 4W's COT.    1340-1400: Educting Carried out of 4W's COT.    1420 : Loading master was notified about the ROB quantity. V/L was instructed by Loading Master to carry out only minimum MARPOL COW and V/L will be removed from the SBM at 21.04.17/2330 LT with what ever cargo remains onboard. Also terminal does not want to receive such sludge as it can chock there loading arms.    1630 : Commenced Tank Inspection for preliminary ROB calculations and to determine the state of Cargo. V/L was unable to draw any cargo sample due to sludge being thick and dense.     1900 : Preliminary ROB calculations carried out.      1920 : Vessel advised Cargo Surveyor to write in OBQ certificate as   "ROB CONTAINS SLUDGE AND SEDIMENTS WHICH ARE UNPUMPABLE AND UNREACHABLE BY SHIPS PUMPS SHIPS PUMPS ARE IN GOOD ORDER"        1930 : Loading Master gave 4 hours notice towards final completion.         1945 : Cargo Surveyor informed if above remark is in our ROB certificate then he will not sign. In his ROB report he will mention "NON LIQUID". No other remark.      2020 : Vessel trying to convince Cargo Surveyor to but same remark. And explain vessel has 3 mtrs trim by stern. In some of the tanks innage is more from mid ullage point and less at aft ullage point.    2040 : Mail send to office.      2050 : Called to office and informed about the high ROB.    2120 : Various calls to and from office to carry out COW of all the tanks and how to reduce the ROB.      2130 : Informed Ms. Dawn Booth (Navig8 Tankers) about the situation.     2140 : Received instruction from office to stop pumping out Slop'S'. By that time almost all the slop's were pumped out. During that time all shore personnel were present in CCR. Monitoring V/L discharge.    2148 : Completed Bulk Discharge. Commenced Line stripping.    2150 : Office instructed to call P&I. Mr. Thomas O'neil and Capt. Kevin F. Quinn from P&I club contacted for same.    2230 : Received instruction from office to stop line stripping and start cargo heating to discharge the ROB onboard. Loading Master was present in CCR at that time. Loading Master told C/O that he would not allow V/L to discharge the Cargo after 2330 LT and he has his own shore crew to disconnect the cargo hoses. V/L continued with line stripping awaiting further instructions.    2245 : Instruction received from office to continue with discharging as V/L could be black listed by terminal.    2318 : Completed discharging.       FYI:  Please note Cargo heating could not have been started during mid discharge as V/L was running low on LSMGO during that time(Approx. 19 MT of LSMGO). Before heating could have been started ship's boilers had to be changed to HSFO. For the process of changeover to be done, discharging had to be stopped for around 2 to 3 Hours for starting the tank heating. Which was not allowed and possible under those circumstances.
ImmediateAction: Office was informed about the large amount of ROB observed. P&I club informed. As per the instructions received from P&I club and Office relevant protests were issued
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While Discharging at Canaport, Canada large amount was ROB was left on board upon completion of discharging. ROB at the time found to be sludge in nature which was unpumpable and not reachable by ships cargo pumps. COW was carried out for 1W's (40-0-40) and 4W's (120-0-120) cargo oil tanks. There was no change observed in the ullage even after carrying out the crude oil washing.  Temperature of the ROB was checked and was found to be 14 C. Which was more than the heating instructions provided to the vessel by charterers    THE CARGO TEMPERATURE MUST NOT DROP BELOW 50 DEGREES FAHREINHEIT/ 10 DEGREES CELCIUS  AT ANY TIME DURING THE LADEN VOYAGE.  HEAT TO BE APPLIED TO THE CARGO AS NECESSARY.
DirectCause: Cause for the Unpumpable ROB is due to no proper heating instructions from Charterers
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: 1. No Proper Heating instructions recvd from charterers.
ClosingNote: Cargo residue sample was taken and same was tested in SGS lab in Rotterdam.It was noted that given the  high pour point and wax content of the ROB in the port slop tank the cause of the problem appears to be inherent to the characteristics of the crude oil. However, it cannot be excluded that inadequate heating played a role in this matter too. This may have been due to two reasons. One reason may be due to inadequate instructions from the shippers. The other reason may be due not having heated the cargo in accordance with the heating instructions, if any, received from shippers upon loading. If not heated adequately during the voyage constituents with a high pour point may have precipitated from the crude oil, forming non liquid ROB on the tank bottoms.

IncidentDetails: During berthing operations at Surabaya Tug - TUBANAN INDAH (for berthing) approached own vessel's forward bow at high speed at 1132 hrs LT / 01-May-2017.    Due to reckless uncontrolled maneuvering by the tug, the tug's superstructure came in hard contact with the ship side causing damage to the paint coating of the ship side, as far as can be visually ascertained.
IncidentEventDetails: at 1127 tug approaching vessel's bow to make fast at Stbd Bow - during berthing operations at Surabaya Tug - TUBANAN INDAH (for berthing) approached own vessel's forward bow at high speed at 1132 hrs LT / 01-May-2017.    Due to reckless uncontrolled maneuvering by the tug, the tug's superstructure came in hard contact with the ship side causing damage to the paint coating of the ship side, as far as can be visually ascertained.
ImmediateAction: Vessel was under pilotage and at abt 150mtr from the berth - vessel engines were stopped and vessel could not have done much to avoid the impact - It was tug whose control was subsequently taken over by the senior tug master and tug was then made clear.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Scratch of paint on the hull for about 5 mtrs IWO frame 216 - 209
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel shall moniter the course & speed of the approching Tug during Berthing/Unberthing operations. If Tug is approching at high speed same shall be notified to Pilot.
ClosingNote: The tug hit the vessel, when the vessel was very near berth. We called for P&I inspector to protect owners interest and LOP was issued

IncidentDetails: 24th April 2017 / 21:00  - 23:00 LT : Vsl shifted to inner anchorage , Muara Berau /Samarinda Indonesia and  finished with anchor  stations and antipiracy watches resumed.    25th April @ 00:30 LT A/B Pandian and Cadet Wilson who were on antipiracy watch found 3 persons on forecastle deck during their round on deck.  They reported immediately to duty officer on bridge and came running towards accommodation.    We saw from bridge one mooring drum on forecastle was rotating and rope is being running out from the drum.all crew divide into two group . one group towards fwd and one group aft.    Fwd group found forepeak store lock was broke open and 2 nos of old mooring ropes were lost from one of the mooring drum. Also observed one gantline was tied up with another mooring rope on other drum . immediately the  gantline was cut and prevented from any further loss of mooring ropes .Aft group reported nothing lost from aft. No damages observed to any personnel on board or to the vsl.
ImmediateAction: Master was reported immediately / General Alarm raised.  Announcement made on PA system  for all personnel to assemble on bridge.  All personnel were immediately advised by master to divide in two groups, one group to go towards forecastle with steel rods in hand and one to go towards poop deck aft.  Thorough inspection of all the stores on board were carried out and found nothing lost except 2 old mooring ropes from one of the mooring drum.  No damages has been done to any personnel on board and to the ship.   In the meantime, vsl tried to contact Port control but no response received.  Immediately called CSO Capt.Dominic Jerry and reported the incident occured .Soon after the incident  all mooring ropes shifted  inside store and sealed  store entrances s by welding steel plates. increased number of watchkeeperto 4 persons in each watch ,2 fwd and 2 aft.CSO Capt.Dominic Jerry called back and advised on further necessary measures need to be observed. CSO was informed about entire  course of action carried out .vsl in strict compliance with antipyracy watches.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring Rope Stolen
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper anti-piracy watches to be maintained. All stores and machinery spaces to be kept locked.
ClosingNote: strict anti piracy watches to be maintained.

IncidentDetails: Accomodation Ceiling Light Fitting Short Curcuit .
IncidentEventDetails: At 09:15 Hrs LT, fire alarm was given by fire detection system for Loop A, Zone 2, Detector No.9 (C/E's bedroom)    >> 1. Immediately checked chief engineers cabin, found smoke coming from C/E's  >>  bedroom ceiling light also noticed pungent smell in cabin and alleyway.  >> 2. Portable fire extinguisher used on smoking light fitting.  >> 3. MCCB for this section of lighting had tripped due to short circuit,  >>  Lights powered from MSB were still on in the cabin.  >> 4. Ceiling light fitting dismounted, found light PF capacitor melted, also  >>  Light fitting cables & shade discolored due to carbon/smoke.  >> 5. Few other lights fittings in accommodation inspected to check the condition,  >>  Found for most lights of this type the PF capacitors have turned yellowish  >> (from original white colour) and brittle, possibly due to ageing.
ImmediateAction: Alerted crew and used nearby portable extinguisher on smoking light fitting.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Light fitting cables insulation damage
DirectCause: Equipment Failure – Capacitor for Tube light failed and caused short circuit.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition No. SPR/NAPU/E/17/064 raised for PF capacitors for renewal.
Corrective Action: Pls renew on receipt
ClosingNote: renewed with spare

IncidentDetails: Today on  20th.May’2017/0830lt in posn . 08-28.0N/094-50E , OS  Vineesh  reported bridge MOB  Marker on port side has been  released from its stored position and hanging  on its attached  line . Photo  attached.( D.O.E of the marker was  March'2018.- Maker - EUROSUL)

DirectCause: Due to vibration
Proposedcorrectiveactions: recd new one and stored properly.

IncidentDetails: Momentarily loss of Astern movement while departing Port Tracy
IncidentEventDetails: At 2254 vessel tried out ME ahead and astern, responded satisfactorily.    Vessel Completed Unmooring at Port Tracy on the 17th May 2312 hrs lt.    Vessel gave Ahead movement of D.slow ahead at 2310 hrs lt, engines responded.    Vessel was then turning to stbd with the help of tugs.    2316 : Telegraph was to put to stop.    At 2317 : Telegraph was put to D Slow Astern but the engines did not respond and RPM went astern to -15 and then back to zero,   Agin astern kick was tried out, but engines did not respond. ME were then again put to astern. starting Air pressure 18-19    2317 : Start air bottle pressure low alarm in E/rm.
ImmediateAction: At 2254 vessel tried out ME ahead and astern, responded satisfactorily.    Vessel Completed Unmooring at Port Tracy on the 17th May 2312 hrs lt.    Vessel gave Ahead movement of D.slow ahead at 2310 hrs lt, engines responded.    Vessel was then turning to stbd with the help of tugs.    2316 : Telegraph was to put to stop.    At 2317 : Telegraph was put to D Slow Astern but the engines did not respond and RPM went astern to -15 and then back to zero,   Agin astern kick was tried out, but engines did not respond. ME were then again put to astern. starting Air pressure 18-19.    2317 : Start air bottle pressure low alarm in E/rm.    2319 : ME responded and RPM went astern.    2320 : Pilot refused to take the vessel further prior trying out the engines again.    2321 : ME were again tried out ahead and astern to the satisfaction of the pilot and vessel proceeded. starting air pressure 21.0
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: At 2254 vessel tried out ME ahead and astern, responded satisfactorily.    Vessel Completed Unmooring at Port Tracy on the 17th May 2312 hrs lt.    Vessel gave Ahead movement of D.slow ahead at 2310 hrs lt, engines responded.    Vessel was then turning to stbd with the help of tugs.    2316 : Telegraph was to put to stop.    At 2317 : Telegraph was put to D Slow Astern but the engines did not respond and RPM went astern to -15 and then back to zero,   Agin astern kick was tried out, but engines did not respond. ME were then again put to astern. starting Air pressure 18-19.    2317 : Start air bottle pressure low alarm in E/rm.    2319 : ME responded and RPM went astern.    2320 : Pilot refused to take the vessel further prior trying out the engines again.    2321 : ME were again tried out ahead and astern to the satisfaction of the pilot and vessel proceeded. starting air pressure 21.0
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To check same in prior arrival/departure ports
Corrective Action: Direct cause of problem to be advised and what corrective action taken
ClosingNote: Crew have been advised to check governor linkages tightness before each manoeuvring.

IncidentDetails: Fuel oil contamination into MGO sett tank during boiler changeover to LSMGO.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was arriving to Port Triesta for cargo discharging, as port regulation Boiler to be changed over to LSMGO. During changing over process  flushing of HFO takes place and HFO goes back to HFO serv tank. In automation wrong selection of cooler bypass, HFO gone to MGO sett tank.
ImmediateAction: Immediately boiler stopped, fault investigated consultation with maker's. Sett tank drained to Overflow tank and MGO sett tank cleaned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Contamination in MGO sett tank of Fuel Oil. sett tank to be cleaned immedaitely as vessel was arriving at discharge port.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: After doing any work on automation, function test to be carried out.
ClosingNote: Burner setting in boiler control panel was not correct as LSMGO cooler was selected as NO. After changing cooler setting to YES, valve change over took place.

IncidentDetails: 1) Hatch Cover trackway of cargo hold #4 stbd /s near corner aft, length of damage: 1.3M, depth of damage: 45mm.    2) Mushroom vent for cofferdam aft of hold #4, stbd/s, damaged.    3) hatch cover compression bar of hod # 4, stbd/s near corner aft, length of damage: 80cm, Depth of damage: 10mm.    4) Dent on crossdeck between cargo holds # 5 & 4, Lenth of damage:65cm, Depth of damage: 20mm.    5) Damaged 4 numbers of ballast control valves & 2 numbers of cargo hold bilge control valves on crossdeck between hold # 4 & 5. (no.4 bilge v/vs P&S, no. 3 DB Tanks v/vs P&S, no. 4 heavy weather ballast v/vs P&S. Conduit pipe for the hydraulic hose of control v/vs of above. All severely damaged.    6) Sounding Pipe for no.3 DB tank stbd/s., damaged

ImmediateAction: Crew personne- senior managementl & stevedore's foreman immediately proceeded & assessed the situation for safety, then checked the damaged sustained. Reports immediately prepared to concern parties for the required immediate repair according to class rules.

(Length of dent damage-1.3M,Depth of damage-45MM)                                                                                                      2. Mushroom vent for cofferdam aft of No.4 cargo hold. (Stbd side)                                                          3. Hatch cover compression bar of No.4 cargo hold starboard side near corner aft. (Length of dent damage-80CM,Depth of damage-10MM)                                                                                                      4. Dent on crossdeck between Cargo hold No.5 & cargo hold No.4 .(Length of dent damage-65CM,Depth of damage-20MM)  5. Damaged 4 number of ballast control v/v and 2 numbber of bilge control v/v on cross deck between cargo hold No.4 and Cargo hold No.5 ( No.4 bilge v/vls P & S, No.3 DB ballast v/vs P & S, No. 4 heavy weather ballst v/vs P & S)                                                                                                                           6. Sounding pipe for No.3 DB starboard side damaged.

IncidentDetails: while tightening the ME fuel valve on test pump, spanner got slipped and Engine fitter get muscle sprain on his back
ImmediateAction: Rest Given immediately and same reported to master by self
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: while doing work on ME fuel valve pressuretesting, while tightenning the fuel valve fixing nut  fitter get catch on the back
Proposedcorrectiveactions: adviced before doing any job check the body position for easy work and using proper tool and give proper levarage while tightening.
ClosingNote: The Risk assessment did not include the control measure of improper positioning of ones self to carry out the task. Understood that additional strain was used causing the fitter to slip and injure himself. The 2ND engineer must continue to review the RA during the work activity.

During this period vessel    was hit by lightining and vessel lost SatC and FBB signal and fwd anchor light was non functional,          upon investigation, it was found that the  antenna of both i.e FBB-500, Sat C and fwd anchor light          were hit by lightining FWD Anchor Light completely Broken. Sat-C Antenna Scarred with Burn mark & Damaged. FBB - 500 Antenna damaged & Non operational.

IncidentDetails: While commencement of discharging at Canaport, Canada. Vessel faced issue with COPs Emergency stop NOT getting reset resulting in delay in starting of discharge operations
IncidentEventDetails: 20th April  ----------    0900 : NOR RETENDERED.      1436 : Anchor aweigh.     1454 : Pilot onboard.     1500 : Mooring crew onboard.   1506 : Commenced Tank Gauging.    1630 : Commenced mooring to SPM.      1648 : Completed mooring at "IRVING SPM"    1648 : N.O.R Accepted.  1654 : Campleted Gauging  1700 : Pilot Away / Half hour notice to E/R  1730 : Completed Calculations.  1730 : Completed Hose Connection (2x16)  1745: Office informed about problem.  1830: Received permission from office to bypass emcy trips button and start the COP for discharging  2018 : Commenced Discharging.
ImmediateAction: Office Informed and receivedpermission to bypass COP emcy trips to start the discharge operations
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While commence of discharge at Canaport SBM Canada, V/L faced problem with reseting of Em'cy trips for COP's. Which resulted in delay for starting discharge operations.
DirectCause: Failure of equipment
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: RA to be prepared for COP operation without EM trip ( remote), noted that manual local EM trip is working in order.
