ClosingNote: Defective relay renewed and COP trips tried out satisfactory

IncidentDetails: v/l ship side got stuck with oil due to floating oil stains in the harbour at Amuay bay. Same informed to loading master. V/l port side had an oil stain of around 30 to 40 cm height from fwd to aft.
IncidentEventDetails: 31.01.17/1015:Manifold watch observed light oil sheen coming from jetty side.Same reported to the duty officer.(as per port log)    31.01.17/1015:D/O reported to the terminal on VHF.     31.01.17/1600:Chief officer and master noticed oil floating around the ships port side from fwd to aft with complete stain on ships side.    31.01.17/1600: V/l informed loading master about staining of ship side.    31.01.17/1700: V/l noticed cleaning up operation of oil by shore staff on the sea surface between the jetty and ship side using small boats.    31.01.17/1710:PSC/coastguard  boarded the vessel for inspection.    31.01.17/1835:PSC inspectors left the vessel.    31.01.17/1900: V/l noticed completion of cleaning operation.V/l was unable to ascertain the damage/condition of the ships port side due to insufficient lighting.    31.01.17/2230: Master contacted charterers,agent and managers.    01.02.17/0006: V/l suspended loading as per the instruction from office to ascertain the situation and prevent further staining/damage to ship side.
ImmediateAction: V/l suspended loading at 01.02.17/0006 LT and protested same to the loading master. As per the loading master ships hull could not be cleaned in the jetty and v/l has to complete loading and proceed to anchorage for ships hull cleaning.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: V/L ship side got stuck with oil due to floating oil stains in the harbour at Amuay bay. Same was informed to loading master. V/l port side is had an oil stain of around 30 to 40 cm height from fwd to aft.Cleaning had to be carried out on ship side before proceeding to next port.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Complete ship side to be cleaned by terminal prior leaving the port
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Root Cause:  Leakage in terminal pipeline  INVESTIGATION  Conversation was held with the Master, CO. V/L ship side got stuck with oil due to floating oil stains in the harbour at Amuay bay. Due to leak in one of the pipelines in the terminal Same was informed to loading master. V/l port side is had an oil stain of around 30 to 40 cm height from fwd to aft. Above incident was reported to charterers / owners / club.  LOSS  VSl had to wait for three days to complete the hull cleaning, Entire off hire was claimed from charterers

IncidentDetails: 1.Cargo pump booster pump coupling along with the gaurd broken and flew off around 15metres periphery.  2.Pump foundation bolts sheared off  3.Pump shaft bent along with the coupling  4.Pressure gauges broken (3nos)  5.Suction valve flnage bolts sheared off and the valve gaskets torn and protruded out  6.Discharge vlave flange bolts found slackened  6.Discharge
IncidentEventDetails: 1.During discharge found gas leaking from NRV flap hinge bolts,valve removed from place, tighteend the hinges and put back. Pump stopped during this period.  2.Booster pump started and it tripped twice due low current.  3.Suspecting NRV put back in a wron position,decided to open again.  4.Within 5 minutes of pump tripping second time, pump started freewheeling at a very high speed with vibration. Huge noise follwed by gas leak notied.
ImmediateAction: ESD activated immediatly. Cargo booster inlet isolated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Following Damages:  1.Pump shaft bent  2.Coupling & gaurd broken  3.Foundation bolts sheared off  4.Suction valve and discharged valves gaskets damaged.  5.3 Nos pressure gauges broken
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Followling Repairs To Be carried Out:  1.Pump bearing housing bearing mounting cicumference for both ends to be checked for trueness  2.Bearing housing foundation to be checked for any cracks  3.Alignment of the bearing housing with the pump casing to be checked.  4.Pump to be assembled with new shaft, double seals, bearings ( 3nos),oil seals ( 2 nos), gaskets,shaft sleeve,etc.  5.Laser alignment of the motor and the pump attached couplings to be done and new coupling joining both to be done.  6. Broken bolts on the pump and motor couplings to be removed.  The assembling of the pump and final mounting on board shall have to be carried out by the maker's techincian.
LessionLearnt: It was noted that during the course of repairing the leak, booster pump line NRV valve was removed from the location. After repairs, the valve was fitted in wrong direction, thereby making the flow towards the booster pump from manifold.  As a result, booster pump tripped on re starting.  Reverse flow of heated liquid at high pressure caused freewheeling of the pump at a very high speed in the wrong direction causing damage to the booster pump, omega coupling and coupling guard.   Poor supervision of the work carried out, evident form the above. Poor cargo  operation practices.
ReviewComments: Direct cause: 1. Wrong fitting of booster pump line non-return valve.           Indirect cause: 1. High back pressure on the discharge line. 2. Work pressure to complete the job fast. 3. Lack of supervision Seniors                                      Root cause: 1. Lack of Knowledge about function of non-return valve.
ClosingNote: Investigation report attached. Please discuss at the next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Auxilliary boiler whiich was firing on low mode with the fuel oil pressure @2.9 bar and with the steam pressure @3.5 bar,suddenly smoke and sparks  started emanating from the boilerfront and also found oil coming out of the fan casing through the duct.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.The boiler was on load after burner overhaul for almost 14 hours.  2.Every two hours the furnace fron inspected for any abnormal deposits  3.The funnel smoke also observed normal.  4.At@ 11210 smoke accompanied by sparks emanated from the furnace door.  5.The fire extinguisged by DCP
ImmediateAction: The boiler burner immediatly tripped and DCP used to extinguish the fire which was just protruding out.  Freshwater generator stopped.Both DGs changed over to diesel oil.  LPG compressors 2 nos stopped and one DG off loaded
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Direct cause:          Oil accumulation inside the furnace due to incomplete combustion.          Indirect cause: • Lack of immediate corrective measures • Lack of effective watch routines. • Lack of effective maintenance. Root cause: Damaged bearing for the rotary cup burner - Improper atomization and thereby improper primary flame due to the rotating cup coming in contact with the outer nozzle because of the damaged bearing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Burner was overhauled with new main shaft and shaft casing. Parts consumed as Per below Table 1.  Draught fan cover was opened up and cleaned.  All parts were boxed back in accordance with the manual complying with various clearances viz, clearance between the cup and the nozzle tip kept 0.2mm (limit is between 0 and 0.5 mm) and the clearances for shaft pulley and the motor pulley for proper alignment. Burner drive belt renewed with new spare. Boiler was tried out on DO and found to be satisfactorily firing which was followed by furnace inspection. Everything found to be in order.  • Routine maintenance for the boiler to be taken up wrt the bearing renewal of the rotary cup burner. • During burner routines, inspect the furnace for any signs of incomplete combustion and also check for the gap between the cup and nozzle tip to be within limits (Limit: between 0 and 0.5 mm). • Brief all engineers for the effective watch routines to be followed. • Regular soot blow to be done while having the burner operate during long anchorages/port stays.
LessionLearnt: • Routine maintenance for the boiler to be taken up wrt the bearing renewal of the rotary cup burner. • During burner routines, inspect the furnace for any signs of incomplete combustion and also check for the gap between the cup and nozzle tip to be           within limits (Limit: between 0 and 0.5 mm). • Brief all engineers for the effective watch routines to be followed. • Regular soot blow to be done while having the burner operate during long anchorages/port stays.   Poor maintenance as evident form the above Poor watch keeping practices/
ReviewComments: Cause analysis: Direct cause:          Oil accumulation inside the furnace due to incomplete combustion. Indirect cause: • Lack of immediate corrective measures • Lack of effective watch routines. • Lack of effective maintenance. Root cause:        Damaged bearing for the rotary cup burner - Improper atomization and thereby improper primary flame due to the rotating cup coming in contact with         the outer nozzle because of the damaged bearing.
ClosingNote: Attached investigation report. Please discuss the same with during the next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: At 06th 1800 LT, one of the crew members reported that he has lost some cash.
IncidentEventDetails: As per the concerned person, he has last seen the cash on 5th PM and kept the cash in his cabin drawer. On 5th evening, vsl berthed Port Louis and departed 6th Noon.   On 6th evening, he reported, when he found his cash is not in place.
ImmediateAction: Had a meeting with all crew. Primary investigation was carried out. Views of different crew members were enquired. During enquiry, it came up that there was also an incident of cash loss in the recent past. Gangway watchkeeper reported there was a person who came onboard at Port Louis reporting he was a port authority but went only on deck and left the vessel after 15mins.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Monetary loss for the crew member. Breach of vessel security measures. Shadow of doubt on the ship personal.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting has been called with all the staff and detailed on the importance of keeping personal stuffs safe and cabins locked. Also briefed on each individuals responsibility towards vessel security.
ClosingNote: Agree with masters corrective action

IncidentDetails: While unberthing 1  ships line was passed to the Tug from port quarter(just behind accomodation) parted causing damage to the Tug Antenna.
IncidentEventDetails: While unberthing 1  ships line was passed to the Tug from port quarter(just behind accomodation) . wind speed was 15 kts , direction of the wind almost abeam. v/l was in medium loaded condition. All lines were cast off & tug commenced to pull. It was observed that the vessel's stern  did not come out.The tug tried to pull hard at that moment the tug line parted & part of the tug line recoiled & hit the Mast of the tug causing damage to one of the antenna. The pilot informed master And the vessel was brought alongside again and made fast.There was 2.1 hrs delays & thereafter 2 ships line were used to make fast tug but this time on the port poop deck. The vsl casted off again the tug had difficulty pulling her out & 2nd tug was used to assist on the std quarter.
ImmediateAction: The vessel was brought alongside again and made fast. 2 ships were passed from port poop deck since there was no option of making fast 2 ships line from the quarter(just behind accomodation).
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: damage to the antenna of the tug.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 2 ships line passed later to the tug.
Corrective Action: 2 ships line passed later to the tug.
ClosingNote: Training on proper mater pilot information exchange, mooring operations and effective communication must be followed up on.

IncidentDetails: AB feel pain on groin after cargo hold cleaning. He has no history of current medical problem.  He is on previous medication.Tool box meeting carried out prior to work and RA made.  He operate cargo hold cleaning nozzle with air hose to wash cargo hold.
ImmediateAction: When vessel at berth AB went ashore for medical check up.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Same Hold cleaning equipment has been and is been used by all our vessel and no one has suffered from said pain. This is most probably due to some other reason. Concerned person has gone to doctor and is fit for duty.

IncidentDetails: 1. At around 2330 Hrs SMT during R/Up of the M/E as started from the Wheel House control the D/G  Jacket cooling water out temp started t to rise together with LO inlet temp also began to rise in D/G  No 1 & D/G No.2. At that time both D/G No.1 & 2 was in running mode and D/G No.3 in stby mode.  This was followed subsequently with the rise in temp of M/E Jacket outlet high temperature.    2. At around 0050 Hrs SMT M/E was being triggered for slowdown automatically.    3. At around 0057 Hrs SMT D/G No.1 Tripped. And this further increased the Full load on D/G No.2.  Ship staff under the supervision of Chief engineer started change over sea chest & Main Cooling  Seawater Pump & Jacket cooling water pump from No.1 to No.2.  4. At around 0125 Hrs SMT D/G No.3 was started to share load from D/G No 2.    5. At around 0126 Hrs SMT D/G No.3 caught fire in the Turbo / Charger. Engine Room staff  immediately started combating the Fire using the nearby 45 Ltrs Foam applicator. As Engine room  was filled with smoke and traces of fire still in the No.3 D/G turbocharger at around 0145 Hrs SMT  the Fire Mist Detector got activated and this assisted in putting out the Fire completely. Fire watch  was being maintained.    6. 0146 Hrs tried to Restart D/G No.1 but failed due to overspeed trip & High Temperature outlet for  jacket cooling & same was being rectified.  7. Throughout a Fire watch was being continuously monitored after the Fire with D/G No.3 and its  adjoining areas.  8. At around 0201 Hrs SMT D/G no. 1 was successfully put in sync with D/G No. 2. As the vessel  slow steamed to anchor about 12 nm off Horsburgh Lighthouse and at around 0412 Hrs Vessel  dropped anchor for M/E and Generator inspection.
IncidentEventDetails: The Cooling System for All the condensers seemed to have issues in the inlet to the condensers.   All the LO in the jacket was found to rise in temp due to insufficient cooling as the coolers were   not getting sufficient water for cooling. Similarly same occurred with the Main/Engine Cooler also  that made the temp rise of M/E Jacket and LO and subsequently triggered the M/E slowdown.  The No.3 D/G Fire seems to have occurred as its T/C had a LO leakage in the shaft.   Spares were ordered & were just received in Singapore and were scheduled to change in this voyage.
ImmediateAction: M/Eng RPM reduced immediately. Subsequently AE3 was already on fire   and all necessary precautions taken to stop fire from spreading. E/Rm Crew fought the fire with the  45 ltrs Portable Foam Extinguisher near the AE Platform located just beside AE3. Aircon was stopped  Fire Mist got activated and that also assisted in controlling the E/R Fire and Fire was completely  extinguished & Fire watches maintained for recurrence found none.

IncidentDetails: On 9th June 2017 at 0815 hrs  vsl had a black out due to high A/Es cooling water temp. Noticed high pressure air entered into the common cooling water system due to Main Air Compressor after cooler gasket damage.
IncidentEventDetails: Before approaching Adelaide pilot satation at 0754 Hrs on 09th June 2017 ME tried out , found satisfactory. 09/0815 hrs: Vsl was about to proceed to pilot station to pick up pilot at 09th/0930 hrs, At 0815 hrs vsl had a black out due to high A/E cooling water temp.09/0816 hrs: Emer generator came on load automatically.09/0817 hrs: Noticed water is splashing out from the cooling water expansion tank due to air entered into the cooling water system from main air compressor damaged gasket.
ImmediateAction: Main Air Compressor isolated, all AEs, LT and HT cooling water system air purged out, AEs started and power restored.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Main air compressor after cooler gasket damaged and black out situation araised.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL WATCHKEEPER ENGINEERS INSTRUCTED TO REGULARLY CHECK FOR AIR LEAKGES IN WATER SYSTEM OF COMPRESSORS DURING ENGINE ROOM ROUNDS.  ALL ENGINEERS WERE BRIEFED ON THE PROCEDURE FOR ABOVE. UNDERSTANDING FOR THE SAME WAS CHECKED AND VERIFIED WITH ALL ENGINEERS FOR THEIR UNDERSTANDING AND COMPLIANCE.  ALL ENGINEERS WERE INFORMED TO STRICTLY FOLLOW THE TORQUENESS OF THE BOLTS AS PER MANUAL AND EXERCISE DUE CAUTION DILIGENTLY.
ClosingNote: ALL WATCHKEEPER ENGINEERS INSTRUCTED TO REGULARLY CHECK FOR AIR LEAKGES IN WATER SYSTEM OF COMPRESSORS DURING ENGINE ROOM ROUNDS. ALL ENGINEERS WERE BRIEFED ON THE PROCEDURE FRO ABOVE. UNDERSTANDING FOR THE SAME WAS CHECKED AND VERIFIED WITH ALL ENGINEERS FOR THEIR UNDERSTANDING AND COMPLIANCE.   ALL ENGINEERS WERE INFORMED TO STRICTLY FOLLOW THE TORQUENESS OF THE BOLTS AS PER MANUAL AND EXERCISE DUE CAUTION DILIGENTLY.

IncidentDetails: At/around 2030LT/24 - 1 Aft Spring from Stbd Outer Drum parted. ( parted Approx 20-25mtrs from the eye ) due to Surging at Berth.  Same line was passed again using ships crew by making bowline.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl all fast at PGL1B, Suape, Brazil. - 2018/23 June 2017.  Mooring - Fwd and Aft ( 2,2,2 ), STBD SIDE ALONGSIDE.  Vsl has onboard Rope guards Fitted to 2 Springs Fwd and Aft.    All springs Fwd and Aft are passed through roller fairlead. ( From WINCH ) All Breast Line Fwd and aft are passed through fairlead.  All Headlines are through Panama Fairlead.   All Sternlines are through Roller fairlead. ( FROM WINCH )    Vsl is surging continuously 0.5-1mtrs to Slight swell.    At/around 1100LT/24 - Observed Fwd Springs from Stbd Winch Chafing due to surging. Chafing was observed on fwd Headlines.  Vsl passed additional Spring and head line fwd using ships crew ( New Mooring Combination Fwd 3+2+3 )    At/Around 1340LT/24 - Observed Rope GUARD on Aftersprings severely chafed and same readjusted on the rope. ( Chafed side turned away from the roller )     At/around 2030LT/24 - 1 Aft Spring from Stbd Outer Drum parted. ( parted Approx 20-25mtrs from the eye ).   Same line was passed again using ships crew by making bowline.   Additionally 2 More Springs aft were passed ( SOFT ROPES, MADE FAST TO BITTS AFT ). ( New Mooring Combination Aft - 2,2,4 )
ImmediateAction: Same line was passed again using ships crew by making bowline.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After Departure Suape All Ropes checked for damage, chafing.  Found total 7 ropes effected due to chafing with varying degree of damage.  Additionally 2 Rope Guards were damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: None
ClosingNote: Master has been briefed to monitor weather and if need arises inform all concern parties vacate berth till weather is conducive.

IncidentDetails: At 0816 Hrs , while vessel was proceeding at Slow ahead, it was noticed that one of the starting air valves was leaking and same was being apparent from the Main starting valve relief hole.    -Upon investigation, it was found that starting air valve No.1 Unit was leaking and connecting pipe to unit had become hot and paint started burning.    - At 0818 HrsRequested Bridge to stop engine  , upon learning that the Man'g is five hours- Engine stopped.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0816 Hrs , while vessel was proceeding at Slow ahead, it was noticed that one of the starting air valves was leaking and same was being apparent from the Main starting valve relief hole.    -Upon investigation, it was found that starting air valve No.1 Unit was leaking and connecting pipe to unit had become hot and paint started burning.    - At 0818 HrsRequested Bridge to stop engine  , upon learning that the Man'g is five hours- Engine stopped.
ImmediateAction: - Initial idea was to tap the starting air valve - to remove any foreign matter between valve and seat - This was planned by removing the Pilot Benjo bolt connection and tap the valve with a Mandrel.    -Upon opening the pilot line , it was found that the nut had loosened and come in the way between the spindle and end cover preventing the valve to close.    -It was then decided to replace with spare , as continuing with the same valve(with cut off fuel pump) having a loosened nut would result in the other nut slackening due to movement of valve by air and can result in the spindle dropping into the combustion space .This can wreak an extensive damage.    - The valve was replaced in Fifteen minutes , since the jacking out was hindered due to expanded valve, on account of exhaust leak.    -At 0842 Hrs Engine was started and proceeded to Lay-by Berth under its own power.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NONE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PMS routines for the starting air valve for the fleet to be reviewed to ensure there are clear  instruction on the torque requirement for tightening the lock nut.
Corrective Action: PMS routines for the starting air valve for the fleet to be reviewed to ensure there are clear  instruction on the torque requirement for tightening the lock nut.
LessionLearnt: In case of inadequate or ambiguous instructions given in Maker Manuals, same to be clarified/cross-checked with them. Vsl to highlight instances when they feel makaer-manual instructions are inadequate.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Investigation Report Attached - Incident uploaded on OCIMF SIRE website

IncidentDetails: Parting of hose handling davit wire during bunkering operations.
IncidentEventDetails: During bunker operations after hose hose disconnection , the barge pulled at the bunker hose while the hose was still being held by ship's hose handling davit, and the davit wire parted. The hook and counter weight fell in the barge and could not be recovered.
ImmediateAction: None
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Parting of the hose handling davit wire, and loss of hook assembly
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Briefing carried out. The steps to be discussed more thoroughly during the initial meeting with the barge. Senior officer to communicate with barge during hose connection and disconnection.   2. Bunker davit wire and hook to be replaced on receipt of spares.
Corrective Action: Review Bunkering operations, and provide feedback on inadequate supervision, considering the person incharge for supervising the operation at Bunker station
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Appreciate ShipStaff reporting

IncidentDetails: 1 nos reefer socket was damaged in bay 64, the crane operator picked up the container before the reefer socket was disconnected, thus damaging it .
IncidentEventDetails: Stevedore requested the ship staff to remove the reefer sockets- while able seaman on the way to the bay 64 saw that the crane operator picking up the container before he could try stopping it the socket was damaged completely.
ImmediateAction: Immedeately informed to the forman and chief officer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 NOS REEFER SOCKET WAS DAMAGED AT BAY 64 ,CRANE OPERATOR PICKED UP THE CONTAINER BEFORE THE REEFER SOCKET WAS DISCONNECTED BY THE ABLE SEAMAN.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: INFORMED FOREMAN REGARDING THE INCIDENT AND INTIMATE ATLEAST 20 MIN BEFORE THE OPERATION.
LessionLearnt: The corrective action suggested by the master, must be implemented in all ports, by briefing the stevedores when they come on duty.
ClosingNote: Agree with the immediate corrective action

IncidentDetails: Leak in the pumproom on the No 2 COP NRV gasket leading to No2 Riser line.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was in loaded condition on voyage from Pointe a Pierre to Panama Transit.  At 1700 hrs on 07 Jul 2016, Pumproom gas alarm was activated on dectectors near No1 and No2 COP.  On investigation, found leak in the pumproom on the No 2 COP NRV gasket leading to No2 Riser line.  Pumproom bilges was observed to have 3 cm of Fuel oil on the aft end and abt 1 cm of fuel oil in the ford.
ImmediateAction: Stripper pump was started and No2 riser line was stripped to Slop P. Stripper pump could not be used for removing the fuel oil from the bilges as the level of the bilge suction was above the oil in the bilges.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAMAGE TO NRV GASKET OF NO 2 COP
DirectCause: Insufficient draining of cargo lines and failure to appreciate the risk of trapment & expansion.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ENSURE THOROUGH AND EFFECIENT STRIPPING IS CARRIED OUT, AS THE DISCHARGE LINE PRESSURE GAUGE TAPPING IS BEFORE THE NRV, THUS UNABLE TO MONITOR THE PRESSURE IN THE RISER LINE AFTER THE NRV.    TO KEEP DISCHARGE VALVE , LINE MASTER AND DROP VALVES OPEN ON COMPLETION OF LOADING / DISCHARGE OPERATIONS TO AVOID THERMAL EXPANSION.
Corrective Action: ENSURE THOROUGH AND EFFECIENT STRIPPING IS CARRIED OUT, AS THE DISCHARGE LINE PRESSURE GAUGE TAPPING IS BEFORE THE NRV, THUS UNABLE TO MONITOR THE PRESSURE IN THE RISER LINE AFTER THE NRV.  TO KEEP DISCHARGE VALVE , LINE MASTER AND DROP VALVES OPEN ON COMPLETION OF LOADING / DISCHARGE OPERATIONS TO AVOID THERMAL EXPANSION.
ClosingNote: The effectiveness of the process was verified for the next voyage and proved effective.

IncidentDetails: Loading in No 5 Hold using Vessel's Crane No 4 was in progress, during lifting of fully loaded skip it was  lifted with a heavy jerk causing a violent swing which banged on ship side railing and fish plate.Same resulted in parting of Crane wire and causing damage to ship side railing & fish plate.Skip container together with crane block fell on the jetty.Also one end of parted wire hit the crane cabin front window & broke the window glass.No injury caused to any personnel, including Crane operator.
IncidentEventDetails: Due to mishandling of crane operator the violent swing caused twisting of crane wire thus resulting in wire getting stuck at the sheave on top of the boom , same resulted in parting of the wire.Earlier a verbal warning was given to crane operator of no 4 crane and same was conveyed to stevedores supervisor.
ImmediateAction: Wire for crane no 4 renewed, also temporary repairs done for FWD sheaves rope guard and crane handed over back to shore operators.Continous attendance by ship staff for monitoring crane operation and any mishandling immedeately reported to Foreman/Master & Ch Off and made sure that cranes are operated with due care.On 12 July 2017 Joint survey carried out for damage inspection & assessment and it was concluded that damage occured due to mishandling of Crane Operator. Same had been notified to all concerned parties.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.No 4 Crane wire broken.  2.No 4 Crane cabin window glass broken.  3.Wire rope guard at boom front top damaged.  4.The ship side hand rails adjacent to no 5 Hold on port side broken,total 3 stanchions.  5.The fish plate adjacent to no 5 Hold on port side damaged at 2 places.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.No.4 Crane wire renewed.  2.No.4 Crane cabin glass temporarily repaired.  3.Wire rope guard at boom front top temporarily repaired.  4.The ship side hand rails adjacent to no 5 Hold on port side repaired.  5.The fish plate adjacent to no 5 Hold on port side damaged at 2 places, same planned to be repaired.
ClosingNote: Condition & Running hours of Crane wires to be closely monitored.

IncidentDetails: ON ARRIVAL SINGAPORE MAIN HYDRAULIC SYSTEM STARTED FOR ANCHOURING ,SUDDENLY NOTICED THE FLEXIBLE HOSE FOR HATCH COVER BUSTED AND SPILLAGE OF HYDRAULIC OIL ON MAIN DECK STBD SIDE
ImmediateAction: STOP THE HYD PUMP, CLOSED THE VALVE TO NO:5 HATCH COVER,ALL SCUPPERS RE CHECKED FOR TIGHTNESS , GENERAL ANNOUNCEMENT MADE, SOPEP INITIATED , AND CLEANED ALL SPILLAGE ON DECK
Corrective Action: Hydraulic lines and flexible hoses to be checked and replaced as required.
ClosingNote: invalid report

IncidentDetails: 1. Pedestal roller uprooted due to daughter vessel bow swinging movement.  2. Parting of Foward headline Mooring tail.
IncidentEventDetails: Mother vessel: MT Grace Victoria - Partially Loaded 11.9 EK  Daughter Vessel: MT Cavendish - Ballast condition / F:-4.2 M, Aft:- 6.2 M  1700 LT / 11.07.2017  Wind:- SSW X 18 kts  Sea :- 0.5 M  Sky :- Clear Sky    1712 :- MT King David cast off.  1754 :- First line (daughter vessel 2 Headlines received onboard and made fast)  2nd :- Daughter vessel Spring lines received on board and made fast  Daughter vessel Outboard headlines received onboard and made fast  (Same time aft 2 stern lines and 2 springs from daughter vessel made fast )  1824 :- Dropped port anchor  1850 :- Sending Mother Vessel2fwdheadlines.  During this period while Daughter vessel were making fast Mother vessel’s two headline, Excessive weight on headlines forward due to heavy rolling of MT Cavendish resulted in parting of 3 headlines of MT Cavendish Daughter vessel.  Mother vessel 2 headlines to daughter vessel made fast.  Mother vessel sends 2 more headlines to daughter vessel and made fast.  Received 4 headlines from daughter vessel and secured on board but observed to be very slack.    Mother vessel sends 2 spring lines to daughter vessel and made fast.    Excessive rolling experienced by MT Cavendish, on mother vessel High tension on Forward Headlines, brake renders. Daughter vessel bowswinging. Tighten back forward headlines.    Daughter vessel sends repaired 1 headline inboard.  Daughter vessel sends repaired 2 more headlines outboard    Excessive rolling experienced by MT Cavendish, high tension on Mother vessel forward headlines, daughter vessels headlines not under tension.    2054 :- Anchor aweigh mother vessel ready for any maneuvering and preparing 5th headline from Forward Port side to pass to MT Cavendish  Passed 5th Headline and made fast.    2054:- ALL FAST.    Wind:- SSW X 18 kts ( APPROX )  Sea :- 1.0 M  Sky :- Clear Sky      Excessive rolling Experienced, brakes render, more than excessive forces experienced on forward headlines, forward crew take cover.  Mother vessel’s Fairlead slips off the block and mother vessel’s one headline tail parts.  2112 :- Abort STS operation Due to Receiving Vessel Cavendish heavy rolling &Lines parting of both vessels( Mutual agreement with Mooring master &MT Cavendish ).
ImmediateAction: Abort STS operation Due to Receiving Vessel Cavendish heavy rolling &Lines parting of both vessels
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One forecastle Most Inboard Stbd side roller and guard rail damaged  One Mooring rope tail
DirectCause: STS operation carried out during long swell condition. Also Daughter vessel MT Cavendish were not keeping their lines taut during the mooring opeartion resulting in al weight coming on Grace Victoria mooring lines.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Avoid STS operations in adverse(rolling) conditions subject to LOA of both vessels.
Corrective Action: STS vessels to get underway during sudden onset of long swell or weather deterioration.   DCR for the same raised.
LessionLearnt: STS operation during adverse rolling condition to be avoided.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: STS operation to be avoided during adverse rolling conditions.

IncidentDetails: After vessel alongside and dropped anchor( UACC ) 3 mooring tails ( head lines )from our good vessel and one from UACC parted as she was rolling moderately.
IncidentEventDetails: 17/july/2017  1706 :- Commence approach/Checklist no 3 completed  1718 :- First line  2000 :- All fast / NOR Re-Tendered  2148 :- Commenced Discharging  2248 :- Ceased discharging due to mooring lines parting.
ImmediateAction: Cargo discharging ceased and vessel made fast head lines again with extra soft ropes.......Resumed discharging with vessel underway.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Own vessel's 3 mooring tails parted.
DirectCause: INCORRECT ASSESSMENT OF THE PREVAILING WEATHER CONDITION FOR STS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Keep extra tails standby, keep all lines on even load, both mother and daughter vessel
ClosingNote: STS vessels to get underway in case of rolling due long swell while at anchor.

IncidentDetails: While disconnecting the loading arm, after completion of the discharge, black coloured residues were found in bottom section of the ship's manifold &  loading arm .
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl received instructions for Deslopping on 30th June 2017 ,Vsl arrived at Kawasaki anchorage on 03 july 2017 and dropped anchor . Then Deslopping barge came alongside and made fast for Deslopping Operations. From 0630 lt to 0724 ltDeslopping carried out . After that marpol line was blown through to slop barge and Hose disconnected during disconnection of hose nothing was observed abnormal ( No black particles / soot remaining observed at manifold .) After completion of gauging and certification bunker barge was cast off at 0754 and manifold blanked , same time crew called for Anchor stations   . After few minutes Pilot came onboard at 0836LT  andvsl proceeded for Berthing . Since there wasn’t any time for stripper pump draining , same was discussed & planned for after sailing from Kawasaki .  Vsl berthed at 1024 LT and commenced loading ALKYLATE in 2ws &4ws  at 1230 LT . Vsl Completed loading at 2224 LT and Departed berth at 0942LT on 4th July 2017 .  Dropped outbound pilot at 1215 LT . After lunch Stripper pump strainer and line drained and found 5 buckets of oily water mixture and put in ROT.     Vsl arrived Onsan and dropped anchor at 1300 LT on 07thjuly 2017 awaiting Berthing instructions .On 8thjuly 2017  pilot embarked at 1630 for berthing . Vsl all Fast at 1806 LT and gauging and calculations completed at 1930 , thereafter vsl awaiting sample analysis results. At 2130 safety inspector informed that result is passed and due to change in shift at terminal loading arm will be connected after abt 1-2 hrs . At 2306LT loading arm connected and 2318 vsl commenced discharging .    09th July 2017 , 1718 bulk discharging completed  and at 1730 Stripper pump started for stripping NO. 4 Top line used for discharging  ,  1800 LT vsl completed discharging . Then tanks were inspected for ROB from 1800-1842 LT found Empty . 1842 Loading arm disconnected  , During disconnection fine strip of black particles about one inch on the bottom side of shore  manifold as well as on ship’s manifold observed by safety inspector and duty officer / Pump Man  and same was informed to CCR .
ImmediateAction: 09th July 2017 , 1848 LT CH. Off inspected the manifold and Same was informed to Master . After that loading master called his superior for further inspections.Loading Master informed vsl that they are checking the shore lines at various sampling point .Ship’s Manifold and loading arm inspected by master found black residue on both the sides on the bottom part.  Samples were drawn by shore personnel  at the bottom of the loading arm on the shore side and about 40 mtrs from the shore manifold on the shore line , same found to be grey in color with black residue. Another sample was drawn about 200 mtrs which was found to be clean. Master briefed  office about the situation by telcon and followed by email to all concerned parties. Manifold samples were drawn from the main top line ( NO 4)  after opening the manifold valve , same was found to be clean. At 2200 Pumproom samples drawn in presence of SGS surveyor , Loading master , safety inspector &ch. Off / pump man from No 4 Cop strainer ( Found clean ) , stripper pump strainer gooseneck ( found clean ) , stripper pump strainer bottom drain valve ( found dirty with black residue).    After assessing it was concluded , that the black residue have passed while using the stripper pum for stripping the No4 top line . At 2320 P&I was contacted by master ,and requested to attend the vessel.  On 10th July 0230 P&I surveyor boarded the vessel and discussed the issue with master.    On 10th July 0800 Loading Master , Safety Inspector boarded the vessel and informed master about the shore situation .Meeting held with P&I surveyor , Loading Master , Safety inspector , SGS Surveyor , Agent and ship staff  , it was decided as requested by loading master to flush the shore line 1000 BBLs to ship slop port tank to clear the black residue from the shore line . Later on permission obtained from the owners for Shore Line Flushing into ship slop port tank. 1048 loading arm connected  & 1100-1136 shore line flushing carried out and samples drawn during flushing and at the end of flushing . Final  Sample was sent for analysis and at 1240 analysis result was passed as informed by the agent . At 1300 loading arm disconnected and 1345 Documentation completed .At 1400 Pilot boarded for unberthing and 1424 vsl Departed berth ( F-1 , S-OIL).  At 1524 vsl dropped anchor at E-3 Anchorage area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After observing the dark colour residues in the loading arm, Loading Master informed the Master that the Terminal staff are checking the shore lines at various sampling point to confirm condition of the cargo in the shore pipeline used for discharging this cargo. Ship’s Manifold and loading arm inspected by master found black residue on both the sides on the bottom part. It was said that the samples drawn at the bottom of the loading arm on the shore side at various distances from the shore manifold, towards the shore tank were as follows:  About 40 mtrs: Grey in color with black residue.  About 200 mtrs: Found to be clean.  Subsequently, Pumproom samples were drawn in the presence of Loading master, Cargo Surveyor, Terminal Safety inspector and Chief Officer as follows:  No 4 COP Strainer - Found clean  Stripping pump strainer gooseneck - Found clean  Stripping pump strainer bottom drain valve - Found dirty with black residue.  At 2320 hrs LT, P&I Club Correspondent was contacted by to attend the vessel. On the 10th of July 0230 hrs LT, the P&I Club Surveyor boarded the vessel and discussed the issue with Master.  On 10th of July 0800 hrs LT Loading Master and Terminal Safety Inspector boarded the vessel and had a meeting held with the Master, P&I surveyor and the Cargo Surveyor. It was planned to push about 1000 Bbls from the shore line back to the Ship’s Slop (Port) tank in order to clear the black residue from the shore line. After obtaining permission from the concerned parties at 1048 hrs LT, the loading arm was connected & from 1100 hrs LT to 1136 hrs LT the shore line content was pushed to the ship’s Slop (Port) cargo tank. Samples were drawn at different times during the flushing operation. As informed by the agent, at 1240 hrs LT, the sample analysis passed. At 1300 hrs LT, the loading arm was disconnected and at 1345 hrs LT, the Documentation was completed. At 1400 hrs LT Pilot boarded the vessel for unberthing and at 1424 hrs LT, the vessel departed from the berth and dropped anchor at E-3 Anchorage area at 1524 hrs LT
DirectCause: Lack of understanding the consequences of insufficient line flushing and blowing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A Detailed briefing session to be conducted by Master onboard for entire team involved in the cargo operations, emphasizing the need to ensure that the deslopping is well planned, after verifying sample of the residues to be deslopped.
Corrective Action: 1 )A Detailed briefing session to be conducted by Master onboard for entire team involved in the cargo operations, emphasizing the need to ensure that the deslopping is well planned, after verifying sample of the residues to be deslopped.  PIC: Master   2) Procedures in our system for deslopping to be elaborated to include drawing of samples from the tank prior deslopping and from the manifold during deslopping.  PIC: CMS Department  3 )  Include procedures in our system for compulsory flushing of the Stripping Pump and pipelines on completion of the deslopping. Effectiveness of the flushing will be evaluated by drawing samples at the manifold.  PIC: CMS Department
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: On 23rd.July’2017/1030LT while the vessel was alongside Coal Terminal at Maputo for Loading, "motor vsl Blue fin" passing very close by, vsl experienced excessive surging .Crew were stand by at the fwd and aft station keeping the ropes tight, but due to excessive surging initially the aft spring rope and later the fwd spring ropes slipping off the drum resulted vsl to come in and out off the berth which  caused  vsl's port gang way railing  and   small MOT  ladder  attached to the gangway bottom platform  got damaged and uprooted one shore railing which was hanging with the MOT ladder . Cargo operation continued without any stopages and within short span of time vsl was brought alongside.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Observed MV Blue Fin which was enroute for berthing at Maputo will be passing very close to our vessel.  2.People were  kept standby at FWD and AFT stations and all ropes were kept tight.  3.While the vessel was passing by, it caused excessive surging initially the aft spring rope and later the fwd spring ropes slipping off the drum resulted vsl to come in and out off the berth.  4. Vsl's port gang way railing and small MOT ladder attached to the gangway bottom platform  got damaged and uprooted one shore railing which was hanging with the MOT ladder.
ImmediateAction: As crew were standby at stations, within a short span of time vessel was brought alongside.  Engines were also made ready in a very short notice and anchor kept in readiness.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Lower part of Port side Gangway bent  2. Inner part of Stanchions arrangement Bent   3. While securing it was noted that the Lower part of the gangway ( damaged area )       was not seated on the securing arrangement
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. More Frequent checks for Mooring ropes and adjusted   2. Any Berthing/Unberthing Vessel to be monitored closely and deck hands to be standby at stations until the vessel is past and clear
ClosingNote: OOW & Gangway watch needs to be Alert at all times.

IncidentDetails: While picking up LUB oil drums from the service boat, which came along side of LUB oil barge on port side, and at the same time  ship was receiving bunkers (FO) on the stbd side and also  receiving provisionsfrom stbd side service luanch and picking up baggages of joining crew with the same crane from Starboard side. Jib of the crane No 4 touched the parking crutch of crane 3 while adjusting the Jib and Hook of No 4 Crane  to reach out  the LUB oil supply Launch and also to prevent the drums hitting the boat structure/boat crew while lifting.  The operation was bye passed. As a result, crane jib dented/bulged.
IncidentEventDetails: 15 Jul 17 :-  2254:anchored, 2254 : boat along side starboard side with joining crew and Provisions,2300  : LUB oil bunker barge along side portside, making fast,2310:  HFO bunker barge approaching and honking horn to hurry up and move the provision/crew service luanch. 2315:Launch with LUB oil drums along side LUB oil barge,  rigging up ladder for the joiners, picking up their baggages &  Provisions. 16 Jul 17 - 0001: HFO bunker barge made fast stbd.0015: crane turns from port to  sideto pick up Lub oil drums and made contact with crutch.
ImmediateAction: Crane operations were called off for a while.  Convened all deck crew and warned the crane operator to be more careful and not to get carried away with growing demands to be hurried up by the suppliers' boats/barges  Need more cautious with the crane operations to avoid damages and injuries..
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No 4 crane jib Bulged and/or dented at about 1.5 m istance from the pivot (Jib end)    Injury - None
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ship's staff to anticipate/be aware of simultaneous operations prior arrival port/anchorage.  Tool box meeting to be conducted  prior commencement of simultaneous operations  Proper communication and signalling.  Crane operator is informed about the nominated signal man.  Clear understanding of the signals given by signalman.  Proper visibility/adequate lighting/Lights which hampers the vision of crane operator to be switched off.
Corrective Action: Need to repair the dented jib with a workshop.
LessionLearnt: Person operating the crane to be alert & cautious.
ReviewComments: Master briefed the crew.
ClosingNote: Crew briefed about operation. Repair plan obtained from maker & included in DD spec.

IncidentDetails: 2 Nos of Personal Multigas monitor got damaged during tank cleaning operation.
ImmediateAction: Requisition raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The gas detector has been rendered unoperational.
DirectCause: Lack of Compliance - Failure to secure the equipment correctly.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional securing arrangements to be made . And crew were informed to ensure that the equipment does'nt fall off similar fashion.
Corrective Action: Additional securing arrangements to be made . And crew were informed to ensure that the equipment does'nt fall off similar fashion.
ClosingNote: Appreciate the reporting and continue with the suggested procedure.

IncidentDetails: TWO NUMBERS OF CARGO SAMPLER FALLEN INSIDE CARGO TANK WHILE SAMPLING.
IncidentEventDetails: CARGO SAMPLING WAS CARRIED OUT AT ANCHORAGE PRIOR DISCHARGE PORT.  CARGO CARRIED ONBOARD WAS FUEL OIL.

DirectCause: Failure to appreciate the restriction with cargo properties and limitation of the sampler.

Corrective Action: REQUISTION FOR NEW SAMPLER HAS BEEN RAISED.TRAINING TO CREW ON PROPER USE OF SAMPLING EQUIPMENT.

Invalid Report

IncidentDetails: While vessel lies in Port of Tamatave, port side alongside with 4+2+2 lines Fwd and Aft, she's  discharging her cargo of Sulphur using ship's grabs. Vessel had been On & Off from the berth due  to long swell seas from open sea to shore side, that cause 4 mooring lines to be damaged & parted.  Two breast lines forward and 2 breast lines aft .On the 2nd day one breast line in fwd parted and hit the top cover of fairlead roller port side that causes the said cover to detach from her position.    Nuts of detached fairlead roller top cover were damaged, and needed to pull out from her position and be brought to engine work shop to properly unscrew and remove said remained half nuts from fairlead roller.
ImmediateAction: Crew made and insert a plastic can protection in between base & mooring lines with grease on it   just to minimize the friction between ropes and base corner of fairlead rollers.(see photo attached)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: During rough weather make sure the lines are tended at regular intervals, also plastic covering is also a good idea to prevent abrasion damage.

IncidentDetails: parting of mooring wire from the eye
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Vessel passed mooring wire rope as a head line at shell europort, from the forward station.  2. As the rope was fast on the dolphin, fwd station was asked to make the rope tight and make fast.  3. Rope had a loop, although it had been tightened enough, since the distance of the dolphin was pretty far.  4. On further instructions on tightening, when the crew heaved the line, the eye of the rope parted, leaving the mooring tail still fast to dolphin.
ImmediateAction: Vessel retrieved the wire rope and the mooring tail, passed another mooring rope for the headline  from outboard side
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Eye of the mooring wire rope parted, rendering the mooring rope unusable
DirectCause: Lack of lubrication
Proposedcorrectiveactions: to check all the mooring wire rope eye condition, and change the wire rope if required.
LessionLearnt: Pls refer to attached IIR
ClosingNote: Pls refer to attached IIR

IncidentDetails: Loading Port - Sikka, Vessel was all fast at SPM 4. To make fast the tug aft(Static tow) prior berthing, 2 ship's line were passed after carrying out the rope assessment by ship and shore staff. Load test Upto 100% was carried out after making fast the tug, Load test result - OK, confirmed with Tug, mooring master and Pilot. Grease was being applied regularly as per the shore request.   During loading operation, arnd 0830 LT on 12-Aug-2017 1 rope out of 2 ropes passed on to tug parted. Immediately Loading operation stopped. No casualty. Wind Force 5/6, Swell height abt 2.0 mtr, Wind gusting upto 30-32 kts. Rope parted due to chaffing / Rough weather
IncidentEventDetails: 12/08/2017 0830LT: 1 line out of 2 ropes passed on to the tug for making fast parted. (Due to chaffing - line parted at fairlead), no casualty.  12/08/2017 0830LT: Loading stopped immediately, SBM Posn 11 o'clock 50m, Short notice given to E/rm for M/E.  12/08/2017 0832LT: M/E Blown through  12/08/2017 0833LT: M/E ready for use  12/08/2017 0835LT: M/E used to keep the vessel in position, SBM Posn 10 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0840LT: Commenced picking up the second line from the tug, SBM Posn 10 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0846LT: 2nd line onboard, SBM Posn 10 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0855LT: Started passing 2 lines to the tug for making fast,SBM Posn 10 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0912LT: Tug made fast aft - 2 ship's line, SBM Posn 11 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0918LT: Tug confirmed all in order, SBM Posn 12 o'clock 50m.  12/08/2017 0924LT: Resumed loading, SBM Posn 12 o'clock 50m.
ImmediateAction: Loading operation stopped immediately. Short notice given to E/Rm for M/E. M/E used to keep the vessel in position. SBM position monitored continously. Intact line was picked up. 2 ships line were passed again to make fast the tug. After making fast the tug, lines were checked and confirm with Pilot and tug. SBM Position 12 O clock 50m. Resumed loading operation after confirming with terminal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One mooring rope got damaged.Requisition raised
DirectCause: NON COMPLIANCE OF COSWP
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Hawser protection sleeves to be used in addition during adverse sea conditions.
Corrective Action: Training to be carried out to all deck crew on importance of greasing near chocks/Fairleads to avoid damage to ropes by chaffing
LessionLearnt: 1. Proper greasing of  ropes to be done at the chaffing area.
ReviewComments: MBL OF ROPES WHICH WAS USED 58.8 T TUG - BOLLARD PULL - 52 T TUG TESTED THE PULL UPTO 100% CAPACITY DURING THIS TIME ON PILOT'S  RESULT WAS GOOD, SAME CONFIRMED BY PILOT, MOORING MASTER AND  TUG TESTS THE ROPE TO ABT 70-80% RATED PULL USUALLY.
ClosingNote: MANY THANKS FOR BRINGING UP THIS INCIDENT TO OUR KNOWLEDGE. AS PER MASTER PILOT EXCHANGE MBL OF ROPES WHICH WAS USED 58.8 T TUG - BOLLARD PULL - 52 T TUG TESTED THE PULL UPTO 100% CAPACITY . SAME WAS AGREES BETWEEN PILOT AND MASTER AND TUG. SAME CONFIRMED BY PILOT, MOORING MASTER AND  TUG TESTS THE ROPE TO ABT 70-80% RATED PULL USUALLY.   DUE TO CHAFFING EFFECT ON CHOCKS ONE OF THE ROPE WAS CUT AND NEW ROPE WAS PASSED IMMD. CHOCKS WAS NOTED WITHOUT ANY GROOVE WHICH CAN POSSIBLE DAMAGE. DAMAGE TO ROPE IS DUE TO CHAFFING EFFECT. AS NO PROPER OR EFFECTIVE GREASING WAS DONE IN THAT AREA. PLS COMPLETE THE CORRECTIVE ACTION TO AVOID SUCH INSTANCE IN FUTURE

IncidentDetails: Sakai , Japan - Prior Loading Jet A1 :  Tank Inspection of  COT's found water traces in 6S COT.  24 June 2016:  All COT including 6S was Washed / Gasfreed / Mop Dry & Pipelines Drained / Re-inerted.   After reinerting and Prior arrival load port all COT's including 6S on 25 June 2016 were checked for water - None Found.  However prior loading Tank Inspection Found Traces only in 6S COT.   Possible reason could be ship sweat inside cargo tank  due to temperature variation in Night/Day.  After Loading Completed 6S COT was again checked and No Traces of water were found.  03/04 July 2016: At discharge port Hong Kong  6S was again inspected during tank inspection prior/after discharge no water traces found.
IncidentEventDetails: 26 June 2016 / 1106 : Completed Mooring   1112: Cargo Surveyor & Berth Master Onboard  1130-1212 : Tank Inspection COT's  1212-1524 - Vessel Waiting Loading Instructions from Charteres   1524: Commenced Loading - 1P/S , 2P/S , 3P/S , 4P/S , 5P/S , 6P , Slp S.

Corrective Action: To monitor IG inlet temperature vs adjoining ballast temperature.

IncidentEventDetails: 0024 Hrs LT : Panama Canal Crew Onboard  0030 Hrs LT : 2nd Pilot Onboard  0032 Hrs LT : Deck crew was ordered to heave up & secure stbd gangway  0040 Hrs LT : Pumpman Reported Gangway heaved upto deck level but was unable to heave the gangway inboard of the railings. IncidentDetails: After completion of loading Exxal 10 from barge 'Newton' at Vopak Botlek jetty no. 5, approximately 5 ltrs of cargo was spilled by the barge into the harbour.
IncidentEventDetails: 23/0218: Barge Newton alongside, stbd side of the ship.  23/0440: Cargo hose connected to 5P stbd manifold of the vessel for loading.  23/0454: Commenced loading Exxal 10 from the barge Newton.  23/1210: Completed loading Exxal 10 from the barge Newton.  23/1215: Commenced air blowing the cargo hose from the barge to the ship(As discussed in Key meeting)  23/1220: Completed air blowing of cargo hose by the barge.  23/1306: As the barge personnel was disconnecting the cargo hose from the barge end, cargo splashed on to his face and he left the dripping hose to wash his face. Approximately 5 ltrs of Exxal 10 spilt on the barge deck which found its way into the harbour due to inadequately plugged barge scuppers.
ImmediateAction: Informed the harbour master officials who were on board the vessel. Collected evidence of the incident. Started cleanup of cargo from water by using an oil boom.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After completion of blowing of the cargo hose from the barge to the ship, as agreed the barge personnel was disconnecting the cargo hose from the barge end. The cargo hose had to be lifted up eventually on the vessel. Vessel's crew were stand by awiting disconnection of the cargo hose from the barge end. Vessel's manifold drain was open to ensure no pressure in the cargo hose. Vessel's manifold valve was shut. The barge crew opened the top bolts of the cargo hose at their manifold without ensuring that the cargo hose is completely empty of any cargo. As he was shaking the cargo hose to loosen it, cargo splashed from the cargo hose onto his face and some of it went into his eyes as he was not wearing proper PPE. He left the dripping hose unattended and went to wash his face. This lead to approximately 5 ltrs of cargo dripping from the cargo hose onto the barge deck which eventually found its way into thewater through ineffectively plugged barge scuppers.
DirectCause: Poor seamanship and safety standards of the barge crew.  1.  The barge personnel had not adequately drained the hose prior attempting to disconnect. Though the line was blown through couple of times by the barge, it is possible that the hose section at the barge may contain some cargo due to the height difference between the ship and barges manifold. This as a normal practice is drained by the barge personnel prior disconnecting the hose.  2.  The correct practice is to slacken alternate bolts; however as witnessed and stated by the ship staff present at the scene; in this case, it was noted that the barge personnel opened the top bolts of the cargo hose first and kept the bottom bolts intact which might have caused the cargo to spray in the upward direction causing cargo to spurt on crew members face. At this time he left the manifold leaving the cargo dripping from the hose on the barge deck which stopped after sometime(Approximately 5 ltr cargo escaped).  3.  Only one personnel at barge end was handling the entire hose disconnection operation. There was no supervision or support as noted from vessel crew members.  4.  The scupper was not plugged effectively due to which cargo seeped into the water.  5.  Delayed SMPEP response by the Barge to contain the cargo on board.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To discuss the line blowing and cargo hose disconnection procedure during safety meeting more effectively. To request the barge personnel to ensure they have carried out their cargo transfer checks properly. To request barge crew to ensure that the cargo hose is drained prior disconnection of the hose. To report any unsafe practice by the barge crew to the terminal loading master/agents/charterers immediately.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Invalid

IncidentDetails: PARTING OF TAIL ROPE OF PORT SIDE INBOARD MOORING WIRE
IncidentEventDetails: 1. VESSEL LOADING BRASS CRUDE FROM NAVE CELESTE IN STS TRANSFER AT LOME STS AREA.  2. BOTH VESSELS BEING IN LIGHT CONDITIONS, WERE LARGELY EXPOSED TO LOCAL WINDS AND SEAS.  3. HEADLINES WERE SUBJECTED TO EXCESS TENSION, WHEN THE VESSELS ROLL, HEAVE AND POUND TO THE SWELL.  4. DURING ONE SUCH ROLL,HEAD LINE OF NAVE CELESTE PARTED RESULTING IN EXCESSIVE WEIGHT COMING ON OUR MOORING LINES ,RESULTING IN ONE MOORING TAIL BEING PARTED.
ImmediateAction: WIRE AND REMAINS OF ROPE TAIL RETRIEVED BACK ONBOARD, SPARE TAIL ROPE (OLD) WAS ATTACHED AND THE WIRE AGAIN PASSED TO MOTHER VESSEL AS HEAD LINE
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: ONE MOORING TAIL ROPE, WHICH WAS IN USE SINCE DECEMBER 2016, PARTED
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TO APPLY GREASE ON THE AREA LIABLE TO CHAFE, BRAKES NOT TO BE TOO TIGHT, TIGHTENED ONLY TO THE RENDERING CAPACITY AS MARKED.EQUAL WEIGHT TO BE ON ALL LINES LEADING FROM BOTH VESSEL'S
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's proposed CA, moorings to be attended to and monitored closely while vessels involved in STS during drifting operations. Weather conditions to be monitored closely and passing additional mooring lines to be considered basis the prevalent wx conditions

IncidentEventDetails: 0001-0600hrs lt on 30th Aug when discharging operation was in progress one nos mooring rope was found missing from aft poop deck by duty crew members at 0230hrs lt. At 0215hrs lt Crew reported from aft poop deck that all the mooring ropes and stores in the area are locked and spares mooring rope on deck is there. At 0220hrs lt Duty officers instructed watch man to lined up for ballasting operation in pump room and other two watch keeper were adjusting the MOT ladder near the manifold area. at 0230hsr LT after adjusting the MOT ladder watch man came for the aft round and found mooring rope missing. Watch man reported incedent to duty officers and duty officers informed Ch off. Ch officers informed Master/SSO. At 0240hrs lt Master Instructed Ch officer to check all the stores and confirm nothing else lost from vessel. At 0300hrs lt Ch off reported all stores on deck are intact and locked. Master reported incident to vessel marine supdt at 0300hrs lt. Also Port control was informed on VHF ch.14 and over the phone. Two security guard boarded vessel at 0400hrs lt for further investigation.
ImmediateAction: Port Control was informed about breach of security on VHF ch 16 and over the Phone.  Vessel Marine Supdt was informed.  All others stores on deck were checked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope stolen
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Extra security measure as per BMP 4 to be taken if vessel calls such type of port.
ClosingNote: Vessel has enough ropes including spare ropes on board, Security measures are increased in ports identified for pilferage, office cautions vessel's to be extra vigilant.

IncidentDetails: Plug of the scupper near break of accommodation port side on main deck lost in heavy weather.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel crew had put scupper plugs in preparation for transferring bunkers from forward FO tank to aft FO tank. During the transfer operation weather deteriorated. After completion of bunker transfer crew not remove scupper plugs and stow it inside store.  Although the scupper plug was secured to ship side railing with the rope spliced to scupper plug, it was lost due to bad weather experienced for next 2 days.
ImmediateAction: All scupper plugs secured after weather improved. Requisition for spare scupper plugs raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lost / Missing Scupper Plug at Break of Accommodation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Briefing of crew to re-emphasize of the importance of adequate securing of the vessel when proceeding to sea. Briefing to cover the importance of Scupper Plugs as a Critical part of vessel's Spill Prevention (containment) equipment and to inform that correctly maintained and placed scupper plugs shall be the vessel's 'Last line of Defence" to an Spill into the sea.
Corrective Action: Briefing of Crew and OOW and Chief Officer regards the importance of Adequate securing of all deck items after departure from Port. Briefing to cover the importance of Scupper Plugs as the final line of defence against an Oil Spill.
LessionLearnt: Importance of adequate securing when vessel proceeds to sea and of sufficient and good oversight of all activities on board.
ReviewComments: satisfactory
ClosingNote: New Scupper Plugs supplied to the crew at Aarhus/13 Aug 2017

IncidentDetails: During berthing at Kieldrechtsluis Lock, Antwerp the vessel's bow suddenly canted to port causing a hard landing of the aft stern part on the lock wall. This caused a dent on the area.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1300 hrs LT on 18-Aug-2017 the Stbd side stern of the vessel made a hard landing on the lock wall at Kieldrechtsluis Lock, Antwerp. The vessel was being moored Stbd side to in the lock and the first two lines (fwd back spring and stern line) had already been passed ashore. As the vessel war nearing the lock wall the bow started swinging to port. Prior to this the bow thruster was running full to Stbd and had been stopped. The main engine was stopped. The master confirmed with aft stations that they are not heaving the stern line. He asked the Pilot if the tug was pulling the stern towards the lock wall as it was behind the funnel made fast at the center lead astern (SWL of bitts 64 MT). The bollard pull of the tug was 30 MT. The Pilot informed that the tug was not pulling the vessel towards the lock wall as it had ordered it to stop. With the sudden swing of the bow to port side, there was a lateral speed and vessel made a hard landing on the lock wall on the stern. The lock does not have any fenders and vessel was using its own manila rope fenders to come alongside the lock wall. The vessel was eventually pulled alongside by heaving on all the four ropes (1 head line and 1 back spring fwd. 1 stern line and 1 back spring aft). On investigation from outside we observed paint scratch marks and no damage to the lock wall except some vessel paint marks on the top edge. The harbor Pilot disembarked at 1310 hrs LT on 18-Aug-17 and the river pilot came on board at 1312 hrs LT on 18-Aug-2017. The master advised the Chief Officer and third officer (who was at aft stations) Mr. Swapnil Shete to go to the Engine Room and observe if there is any damage to the vessel. They reported that there has been a small dent. We took photographs of the impact and sent them to our office from review. Vessel left the lock at 1400 hrs LT on 18-Aug-17. The incident appears to be due to sudden pulling of the vessel sideways by the tug.
ImmediateAction: Checked the hull integrity. No leakage found. Checked all the tanks and found no change in sounding. Took photographs of the damage and sent it to the company for analysis.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There is a dent on the stbd side hull in the engine room section between frame number 16 and 21 on the upper deck. Photographs and drawing attached.
DirectCause: It appears that the escorting tug number Antwerpen 10 made fast at aft centre lead had pulled the vessel's stern towards the lock wall without informing the Pilot as he was also unaware of the cause of the sudden movement of the vessel's stern towards the lock wall. This action of the tug caused a lateral speed resulting in hard landing of the aft stern part of the vessel on the lock wall.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Advised all crew members advised to be more vigilant towards the action of the assisting tugs and shore personnel. Any action of the tug or shore personnel inconsistant with the orders from the bridge or not in compliance with safe berthing/unberthing procedures to be reported to bridge immediately.
ClosingNote: Invalid

IncidentDetails: During mooring station at Juaymah terminal, the port side winch side plate got bent towards the split drum side due to excessive weight on pick up rope.
IncidentEventDetails: During mooring station at SBM, loading master was on bridge alongwith master while fwd station was manned by asstt mooring master,chief officer & deck crew. While approaching the SBM, Port side pick up rope was made fast to the messenger on board. While port side pick up rope was being heaved in, there came the excessive weight on the rope which got further transmitted to side plate causing it to bend towards split drum. Same was informed to loading master / Asstt mooring master.
ImmediateAction: Loading master / Asstt mooring master informed of same. Port side winch was  later checked for operation and any further damage. Found none.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The plate between the split drum and Main drum on port side winch is deformed and requires hot work.
DirectCause: Lack of communication / Lack of leadership quality
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Hot work to be carried out.
Corrective Action: Damaged section  to be repaired at the earliest convenience
ClosingNote: Poor communication or feed back from forward station to and fro the bridge. The damaged section was cropped and renewed and mooring gears are made back to normal working condition

IncidentDetails: During R/B drill and training limit switch of rescue boat found damage.
IncidentEventDetails: Training session, for R/B, conducted for crew memeber in view of upcoming port of call of Chornomorsk Ukraine. Davit operation of Rescue boat davit Explained to crew member and Limit switch tried out. During the testing it was found that limit switch cover wasted due to rust.
ImmediateAction: Close up inspection of Limit switch carried out. Wasted cover replaced with new fabricated cover.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Limit switch for hoisting wire found wasted. Front cover wasted due to rust.
DirectCause: Limit switch painted over the top of rust. Close up inspection was not carried out during the monthly inspection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repaired the wasted part of cover and raised new PR for the same.
Corrective Action: All parts of the Rescue boat Unit to be inspected thoroughly during the weekly and monthly checks by the concerned officers during their rounds.

IncidentDetails: while vessel approaching halmsted,sweden harbour under pilotage,vessel proceeding close to jetty at low speed .ON 08.09.2017 0030 LT vessel made contact with fender and corner of jetty(Berth no:300) at starboard side forward abreast no.1 cargo hold.
IncidentEventDetails: the details in chronological order:  07.09.2017 2330 LT: PILOT ON BOARD (2 PILOTS)  07.09.2017 2334 LT: MASTER PILOT INFO. EXCHANGE CARRIED OUT.   07.09.2017 2358 LT: TUG MADE FAST AFT (TUG NAME :AGIR)  08.09.2017 0006 LT: FWD TUG MADE FAST (TUG NAME:VEN)  08.09.2017 0017 LT: VESSEL PASSING BREAKWATER  08.09.2017 0026 LT:PILOT ADVISED FWD TUG CAST OFF AND FWD TUG CASTED OFF  08.09.2017 0030 LT: VESSEL MADE CONTACT WITH FENDER AND CORNER OF JETTY(BERTH NAME :300)  08.09.2017 0045 LT:FIRST LINE ASHORE  08.09.2017 0048 LT:AFT TUG CAST OFF  08.09.2017 0118 LT: VESSEL ALL MADE FAST  08.09.2017 0130 LT: PILOT AWAY AND VESSEL MASTER AND CH.OFF CHECKED AND INSPECTED  THE DAMAGE.

TOOK PICTURES.INSPECTED CARGO HOLD NO.1 FOR ANY DAMAGE,NO DAMAGE NOTICED IN NO.1 CARGO HOLD.FOUND NO WATER COMING OUT OF THE DAMAGED AREA DENT OUTSIDE SHIP'S HULL.THE DAMAGED AREA IS ON THE OUTER SHELL PLATING OF NO.1 WATER BALLAST TANK DOUBLE BOTTOM AREA ABREAST NO.1 CARGO HOLD.

IncidentDetails: Jetty Damage while berthing

The heavily coroded/broken condition metal block got bent and subsequebtly  caused damaged to concrete portion of the jetty corner.  NO VISIBLE DAMAGE OBSERVED ON SHIP'S HULL.
