IncidentDetails: Damage to the Port accomodation ladder
IncidentEventDetails: After sailing from Wakayama it was noticed that the port accomodation ladder lower upport plate  was bent. During discharging at IDEMITSU While lowering the accomodation ladder it was lowered more than required resulting the ladder resting on the shore fender.  It has now been straightened and in satisfactory condition.
ImmediateAction: Ladder was taken up  and crew were instructed to tend the ladder continuously.  Support plates  straightened using wood piece during sailing.  Crew were instructed to pay attention during lowering of gangway and continiously tend the gangway during loading/discharging operations and especially in tidal ports.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The support plate was bent which was straightened by soft tapping using wood
DirectCause: Improper monitoring of the gangway by the shipstaff.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All deck crew were instructed to be more vigilant and tend the gangway during cargo operations especially when vessel is loading and bound to go down during loading operations and to be more cautious in tidal ports.
Corrective Action: The matter to be discussed during the safety committee meeting. The shipstaff be advised to keep good watch on the gangway alongwith manifold to prevent damage to the ship or shore equipment
ClosingNote: It is the gangway watchkeeper duty to monitor the condition of the gangway that it is always lowered to the correct level and no damage occurs to it.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was loading MDBS cargo at TMPC terminal , Pisco. The mooring configeration was 2 stern lines , 3 breast lines , 3 spring lines fwd and aft. All the ropes were regularly monitored and throughly greased near the fairlead. As the terminal was an open sea berth ,the effect of current and swell caused the aft spring (1 no) to part
IncidentEventDetails: 02 Sept 2017  -------------    0124 Hrs : Commenced mooring    0206 Hrs : All fast     0518 Hrs : Commenced loading    03 Sept 2017   -------------    0748 Hrs : Completed loading    0812 Hrs : Hose disconnected    0945 Hrs : Aft spring parted    0950 Hrs : Fwd and Aft tug made fast    0954 Hrs : Commenced un-mooring    1012 Hrs : All lines on board    1018 Hrs : Pilot away
ImmediateAction: Tug assitance was available , since the vessel had completed operations and ready for unberthing.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Aft Spring Line Was parted approximately 20 mtrs from eye.
DirectCause: Loss of mooring rope strength due to chafing as the berth was exposed to the swell from open sea. The MBL of the rope was reduced to less than the rendering load as a result of the chafing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Monitoring of mooring ropes and change as required
Corrective Action: Monitoring of mooring ropes and change as required
ClosingNote: Effectiveness verified during onboard audit by the Supdt.

IncidentDetails: While connecting the cargo hose at Conchan, the crane hoisting wire has parted. There is no injury to any person .
IncidentEventDetails: 08.SEPT.2017  0545 LT : Loading Master instructed to start Cargo Hose Connection.  0548 LT : Hose lifted on Ships main deck on Port side.  0636 LT : Hose handling crane Hoisting wire parted while hose connection.  0715 LT : Informed Managers over Phone.  0747 LT : Inofrmed Managers/Charterers/Owners by Mail.  0854 LT : Cargo Hose connected by alternate means.  1000 LT : Resumed Discharging Operations.
ImmediateAction: Cargo hose was secured immediately by additional lashings.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Dent on Main deck near Portside manifold - iwo Frame 58-as per the attached photos.-dimension :50cmx50cmx5cm  2. Grating of Port side manifold damaged-dimension-90cmx110cm  3. Gratings support bent : approx area -180x100 cm(2 supports)
DirectCause: Equipment failure – Crane wire Parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crane wire to be renewed at regular interval
Corrective Action: Renew the Crane wire, checks on monthly basis and renew on time.
ClosingNote: Crane wire renewed and monthly checks with permits to follow up.

IncidentDetails: Deck staff removing mooring tail from steering gear room.  While operating the winch hydraulic oil return line flange in the steering flat started leaking heavily.  On investigation found gasket of that particular flange gave way and around 500 ltrs of oil leaked via the broken gasket.
IncidentEventDetails: Deck staff removing mooring tail from steering gear room.  While operating the winch hydraulic oil return line flange in the steering flat started leaking heavily.  On investigation found gasket of that particular flange gave way and around 500 ltrs of oil leaked via the broken gasket.
ImmediateAction: Aft wich motor stopped.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 500 ltrs of oil lost from the system.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Broken Gasket.  Indirect Cause: Material Failure  Root Cause:Damaged Gasket used by the yard.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gasket to renew and same renewed.
ClosingNote: We concur with the corrective action taken. Appreciate the reporting of the incident and effective closure.

IncidentDetails: During regular maintenance job (painting on bridge wing of canopy) on stbd side, the concerned deck personnel accidenly pulled out the lever of MOB marker/buoy, thereby causing the MOB marker+ buoy to get released into water. Vessel position 05 47.4S/115 36.8E at 1105 LT/12Sep (0305 Z, 12Sep), in open sea. Sea state: Slight sea, low swell, Wind: SE’ly X BF4
IncidentEventDetails: A deck crew member was assigned the job of painting final coat on bridge wing canopy structure (newly fitted in DD as per Panama Canal reqt). He also was doing touch up paint on the adjoining areas, which included bracket for MOB buoy. While painting, he accidently moved the releasing lever, thereby causing the buoy, alongwith MOB marker, to get released and fall into water. Deck crew member immidiately informed OOW and Master.
ImmediateAction: Navigational broadcast to vessels in the vicinity sent on VHF 16 and closest RCC (Surabaya) informed over email. Flag (Panama) intimated about the accidental release.  Requisition SPR/NIDN/D/17/066 in place for a spare MOB marker. Vessel's ETA Singapore: 15th Sep/AM hours, and expect delivery of the new MOB marker upon arrival in Singapore.  Risk assessment for the same in place untill new marker is installed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 01 MOB marker and buoy lost to the sea. No damage to ship's propert, and no injury. However, platsic body of buoy/marker was released into the sea. Appropriate entry for same has been made in the GRB.
DirectCause: 01. Ineffective tool box meeting 02. Poor competency 03. RA not effective
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Better monitoring and more detailed delegation of job to the crew members.   Concerned crew member to be more alert and careful when working in the vicinity of such critical LSA/FFA equipment.
Corrective Action: Crew members to be briefed on the importance of compliance and adherence to RA and the tool box meetings
ClosingNote: MOB marker buoy replaced, adequate training provided to crew members on inadvertent release of MOB markers during maintenance

IncidentDetails: Flag state not informed on raising Marsec Level-2
IncidentEventDetails: 12:00hrs lt  EOSP at Kakinada vessel arrived at Pilot Station at 1312. Pilot boarded at 1312 hrs lt on arrival. No information of Security level 2 was conveyed by the Pilot to Master.1454hrs lt all made fast fore and aft. 1515hrs lt Gang way was rigged. 1515: Custom boarded vessel and 1615hrs lt Vessel was cleared by Custom. During the discussion with Custom Officer about port Custom Oficer informed that Port is at MARSEC Level-2. No information was relayed to vessel by the agent.1645hrs lt on 28th Aug. Loading Master/Surveyor and receiver boarded the vessel. 1654-1724hrs Lt on 28th Aug 2017 Key meeting with Loading Master and Cargo Surveyor.1724hrs lt on 28th Aug Hose connected for discharging. At 1730hrs lt on 28th Aug 2017. Vessel started Discharging Sulphuric acid. As Marsev Level was reported by the Custom officer not by the PFSO to vessel and due to busy schedule Master was busy with port authority for vessel clearance forgot to Inform CSO and Flag state about change in the Marsec Level.
ImmediateAction: Marine Supdt was informed instead of CSO.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Port security information to be requested from agent well in advance before arrival to avoid re-occurance of security breach.
ClosingNote: Pre arrival information included with security level at ports.

IncidentDetails: While Disembarking Piot, stbd Gangway came in contact with Pilot boat thereby causing piece of choke of stbd gangway to chip off
IncidentEventDetails: While disembaking Pilot after shifting on warping , the stbd gangway was lowered for Pilot to Disembark. After Pilot Disembarking Pilot boat came in contact with Gangway. A small Piece of the wooden choke of gangway got chipped off.
ImmediateAction: Gangway Immediately picked up & inspected . Duty AB told not to Lower Gangway & all deck crew briefed that only Pilot combination to be hence forth used .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A piece of the wooden choke of the stbd Gangway got chipped off size 30 cm x 4 cmx 10 cm
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Only Combination Ladder to be used for Pilot Diembarkation & under supervision of Duty officer.All crew to be briefed about same & discussed in safety meeting.
Corrective Action: Only Combination Ladder to be used for Pilot Diembarkation & under supervision of Duty officer.All crew to be briefed about same & discussed in safety meeting.
LessionLearnt: prior performing such operations, senior staff should be consulted
ReviewComments: to be discussed amongst ships staff
ClosingNote: Embarkation and disembarkation to be always performed with Master/Ch.Off orders

IncidentDetails: During berthing at Mina Al Ahmadi LP 1 berth port side alongside, aft spring winch drain line got ruptured and hyd oil leaked.
IncidentEventDetails: During berthing at Mina Al Ahmadi LP 1 berth port side alongside, aft spring winch drain line got ruptured and hyd oil leaked.
ImmediateAction: Immediately winch stopped and duty officer informed, dip tray plug checked, scupper checked,  line depressurised, amout of oil leak was about 2 to 4 ltrs only.  Oil cleaned and one person kept standby there only through out the cargo operation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One section of hyd line ruptured. line removed and new line fabricated and fixed back.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Line removed and new line fabricated and fixed in position.
ClosingNote: V/L has taken immediate corrective action & appropriate action to control re occurrence.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was loading at Mina Al Ahmadi LP 1 berth.  During loading operation hydraulc line for cargo valve system got ruptured and around 50 ltr of hyd oil leaked through out the oeration.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was loading at Mina Al Ahmadi LP 1 berth.  During loading operation hydraulc line for cargo valve system got ruptured and around 50 ltr of hyd oil leaked through out the oeration.
ImmediateAction: Crew called, Scupper checked, Oil contained on board, soft patchup carried out. Oil continusly refilled in hyd tank as small amount of oil  was leaking through out the cargo operation.  Two person kept standby at all time to complete the loading operation safely.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During cargo operation one of the hyd line for cargo valve system is ruptured and around 50lt of oil leaked on deck, On departure, line removed and leakage rectified by insterting new pipe in place.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: On departure damaged line removed and new line fabricated and fixed in plcae.  All line to be inspected for any sign of corrosion and leak, suspected line to be renewed with immediate effect.
ClosingNote: Agree with the proposed corrective action

IncidentDetails: Steam mixed with oil vapours from Waste oil Service & Settling tank air line drips on deck aft of funnel area causing oil sheen on deck.  It was observed that one rubber hose was connnected and put inside the scupper to let this oil vapour/steam mixture go overboard. Small traces of mixture also spilled onto shipside.
IncidentEventDetails: Steam mixed with oil vapours from Waste oil Service & Settling tank air line drips on deck aft of funnel area causing oil sheen on deck.  It was observed that one rubber hose was connnected and put inside the scupper to let this oil vapour/steam mixture go overboard.
ImmediateAction: Chief Engineer was called immidiately and asked to remove the hose and asked to cotain any oil vapour/steam mixture inside the bucket or drum.  This is a serious MARPOL voilation and warned E/R staff that of serious conseqences for repeated offense.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor oil traces seeped onto shipside.
DirectCause: Complete negligence, not folllowing procedures / regulations
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training of crew will be given on board, and crew must be trained ashore well in MARPOL regulations and consequnces for not complying.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: INVALID

IncidentDetails: Personnel was told to carryout pre-arrival checks in pumproom. While he was climbing down , BW-XL Personnel gas meter clip unfolded leading to Gas meter slipping to the bottom platform. Screen and clip for the Gas meter found broken.
IncidentEventDetails: Personnel was told to carryout pre-arrival checks in pumproom. While he was climbing down , BW-XL Personnel gas meter clip unfolded leading to Gas meter slipping to the bottom platform. Screen and clip for the Gas meter found broken.
ImmediateAction: Gas meter has been segregated and removed from service.Vessel carrying sufficient personnel gas meters.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Screen and clip for the gas meter broken.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All gas meters to be checked including accessories on regular basis.
ClosingNote: Defective equipment replaced / All equipments to be checked for the intended use prior putting to service

IncidentDetails: During pre berthing preparation today at Batangas  anchorage, ship staff noticed 6 S COT leaking IG very slightly from tank dome .Ship staff planned to tighten the tank dome in order to stop IG leakage .    However the tank dome  gaveaway at the tightening spindle bush,causing sudden release of tank pressure on deck through the dome.
IncidentEventDetails: 13/ 0736lt : vessel Anchored, Awaiting port formality before berthing  13/1100: During pre berthing preparation today at Batangas  anchorage, ship staff noticed 6 S COT leaking IG very slightly from tank dome .Ship staff planned to tighten the tank dome in order to stop IG leakage .However the tank dome  gaveaway at the tightening spindle bush,causing sudden release of tank pressure on deck through the dome.
ImmediateAction: Ensured all Scuppers in place.  Checked tank atomosphere < 5% Oxygen content. Checked for damage assesment. No Damage/ deformation noticed on deck and hull. Prepared IG plant.    Later ship staff  successfully  replaced the damage spindle bush using spare from  ship stock and the O2 contain in the tank was found to be less then 5%.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During pre berthing preparation today at Batangas  anchorage, ship staff noticed 6 S COT leaking IG very slightly from tank dome .Ship staff planned to tighten the tank dome in order to stop IG leakage .    However the tank dome  gaveaway at the tightening spindle bush,causing sudden release of tank pressure on deck through the dome.
DirectCause: Close  observation of the damaged  bush revealed material deterioration which resulted in sudden release  of pressure  even though the pressure was less then 14 Kpa(Primary PV valve setting ).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. ship staff  successfully  replaced the damage spindle bush using spare from  ship stock and the O2 contain in the tank was found to be less then 5%.  2..Vessel  plan to inspect  all the tank dome  bushes in the earliest tank cleaning opportunity .
Corrective Action: Vessel to inspect all the tank dome bushes in the earliest tank cleaning opportunity .
ClosingNote: Job completed 15/11

IncidentDetails: The Panama choke and the nearby bulwark and railings were damaged/ ripped off due to excessive force applied by the tug while undocking at Wellington, New Zealand.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel have just let go the last line from berth and the tug commence backing and stretching her line to pull the ship out from the berth,  she heaved her winch to pick up the slack on her line while backing, the momentum force of a sudden tug resulted the damage.
ImmediateAction: Push the vessel back to berth and moored her again to investigate and assess seaworthiness of the vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No injury but damage is noted in bulward port quarter and panama shoke.   Bulwark was ripped off and recovered from the area.
DirectCause: Sudden apply full tug astern power caused damage to railings, bulwarks and chock
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repaired the damage bulwark and panama shoke invited class for approval and certificate
Corrective Action: Repaired the damage bulwark and panama shoke invited class for approval and certificate
ClosingNote: Ship staff to be well clear

IncidentDetails: While enroute from Suez to Mombasa vessel entered IBF High Risk Area, Same was notified to vessel  by MOL and Synergy at 0530LT . Immediately vessel altered course to 090 Deg True and cleared IBF High Risk Area by 0915 LT.Vessel maintaining more than 400 NM from Somalioan East coast as per MOL guidelines .
IncidentEventDetails: While enroute from Suez to Mombasa vessel entered IBF High Risk Area, Same was notified to vessel  by MOL and Synergy at 0530LT . Immediately vessel altered course to 090 Deg True and cleared IBF High Risk Area by 0915 LT.
ImmediateAction: Immediately vessel altered course to 090 Deg True and cleared IBF High Risk Area by 0915 LT.IBF High Risk Area marked in ECDIS and highlighted. Bridge  team meeting held and discussed the matter. MOL guidelines regarding HRA is to be strictly followed when making passage plan in future. MOL circular No : TSGSOI 15_065(MRN-15/028) discussed in detail during bridge team meeting
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel entered IBF HRA and the situation may lead to piracy attack and loss of insurance claims
DirectCause: IMPROPER UNDERSTANDING OF COMPANY’S GUIDELINES. IMPROPER PASSAGE PLANNING.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediately vessel altered course to come out of IBF HRA .Bridge team meeting held ,guidelines discussed,IBF HRA plotted in ECDIS passage plan reviewed and vessl to maintain more than 400 NM from somalian East coast .The guidelines regarding HRA to be followed strictly in future when making passage plans.
Corrective Action: a. IBF High Risk Area co-ordinates to be marked permanently on ECDIS (user map option) and Indian Ocean paper charts for the area of all MOL vessels. Snapshot Photo of the ECDIS and paper charts once marked to be sent to respective Marine Superintendents.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: IBF HRA area to be avoided on MOL vessels.
ClosingNote: All MOL vessels informed about the same and the required corrective actions implemented.

IncidentDetails: PERSONAL GAS DETECTOR LOST OVERBOARD
IncidentEventDetails: While vessel was discharging at Sendai, Japan, crewmember on deck was adjusting the mooring lines. While leaning over to adjust the rat-guard and chafing gear, his personal gas detector came undone and was lost overboard.
ImmediateAction: Crewmember immediately informed the duty officer. Chief Officer and Master notified of the same. Deck crew instructed to take proper care of issued equipment (radio, torch, gas detector) and PPE (hard hat, gloves) and ensure that these are used carefully to avoid damage or loss.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: PERSONAL GAS DETECTOR LOST OVERBOARD
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident brought to the attention of all crewmembers and importance of proper use of equipment was stressed. Examples shared of radio (walkie-talkie) damage due to use without carry case, loss of hard-hats overboard due to wind etc shared. As an extra precaution, all gas detectors will be provided with a sling to wear around the neck. Same will be done for flashlights. Replacement Gas detector ordered, as per attached PR.
ClosingNote: Appreciate the reporting of this incident. we concur with the cause analysis and corrective action taken.

IncidentDetails: During Ullage and temp comparison while loading, UTI hand broke while picking up the UTI from 5S COT.
IncidentEventDetails: 6 Hourly ullage /Temp comparsion veing carried out, UTI handle broke while picking up after checking the temp.
ImmediateAction: Coiled the damaged UTI from the reel and replaced the UTI for the remaining ops
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: UTI handle to be replaced .
DirectCause: Material failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Order Spare and replace UTI handle
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Crew members should be regularly emphasized on the importance of taking care of gauging portable instruments. Rough handling of these delicate instruments to be avoided. Spare handle suppled to vessel and replaced in good order

IncidentDetails: At around 1120H LT, 05 November 2017, in position Lat: 13-27.1N / long: 042-39.8E, vessel transiting IRTC bound for Jakarta. A small fire broke out in Engine Cadets cabin.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1120H LT, 05 November 2017 OS Felix L. Nunez while on the way out from the Crew shower room noticed smoke coming from Engine Cadet door ventilation. He immediately informed the OOW on the bridge about the situation. After a few seconds, the Fire Alarm was also triggered on “B” Deck.  OOW immediately raised the alarm and announced on public address system that a fire had broke out on “B” deck. Master at that time was on the bridge.   Bosun Elegio C. Pacatang who is in the vicinity, carried out initial fire fighting with the use of Portable Foam fire extinguisher, the fire was extinguished immediately. Chief Officer reported fire extinguished in Engine Cadets cabin.  Investigation carried out and found that the source of the fire was from Engine Cadet’s garbage bin.  As soon as it was ascertained that the fire was out, the area was assessed and found that the fire was isolated only inside the trash can and no other items in the cabin was burned.  The source of the ignition was a cigarette butt, presumably still containing embers at the time it was thrown in the garbage bin. A thorough inspection was carried out in the adjacent areas and no sign of damage observed.
ImmediateAction: Carry out initial firefighting to put out the fire, ensure that the cabin occupant is not present in the room.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage was contained inside the garbage can only. No damage to the ship's structure. No injuries to personnel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To prohibit smoking inside the cabin and only allow smoking to designated smoking areas.
ClosingNote: Smoking to be allowed in Designated areas as per SMS manuals and crew to educated on the dangers of smoking.

IncidentDetails: Shinyo Ocean was berthed at SBM # 24, Mina Al Ahmadi, Kuwait at SBM#24. After completion of  loading operations, Vessel was using her port side midship hose handling crane to disconnect three  Cargo hoses from port manifold 1,2 & 3, While lowering the second cargo hose after disconnection  when the crane was under pressure, one of the Hydraulic hose for lowering got ruptured. Vessel  managed to lower the second cargo hose as it was very close to sea.  Approximately 10 ltrs of hydraulic oil leaked from the ruptured hose onto the main deck. There were  no injuries or other damage due to this incident. Vessel’s unberthing was delayed by an hour.
IncidentEventDetails: Hydraulic hose ruptured during the disconnection of Cargo hoses using port side midship crane.
ImmediateAction: After the hose rupture was noticed by the crane operator, the lowering of the hose was stopped  immediately.  Hydraulic oil that sprayed on deck was contained on board and cleaned up immediately by the deck  team using SOPEP gear.  The damaged hose was renewed immediately with the spare available on board. Thereafter the crane  was tried out and found working satisfactory.  The third cargo hose was disconnected and lowered safely.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: hydraulic oil spill / spray on main deck in the vicinity of manifold
DirectCause: Failure of equipnet (Hose)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: all vessels more han 10 years of age shall have all exposed hydraulic hoses renewed and carry 100 % spare always on board.
Corrective Action: incorporated in PMS all vessels more than 10 years of age shall have their hoses on exposed deck replaced and also carry 100 % spares always. New take overs should have an MOC included to check for vessel's age and the condition of hydraulic hoses to be replaced if appropriate for the age of the vessel.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: issue closed upon taking corrective measures

IncidentDetails: During cargo discharging operation at Kashima (Japan) JX Terminal, a small hole developed in the drain line (nom dia 50 mm) of No 1 COP in P/Rm bottom. This hole was detected during the final line stripping stage, and No 1 COP was immediately stripped/emptied/isolated to stop the leak.
IncidentEventDetails: During final line stripping stages of discharging, P/M went to pumproom for valve operation and reported a leak in No 1 COP drain line in P/Rm bilges. No 1 COP was immediately stripped and leaked oil contained/collected. Temporarily, a rubber tube was tightened around the holed line section.
ImmediateAction: No 1 COP was stripped immediately. Crew taking round in PRm had a personal gas meter, and used rags in P/Rm to contain the spread of leaked oil (approx 25 litres of oil).
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Drain line (Nom dia 50 mm) of No 1 COP casing developed a small hole during cargo discharge operation. Approx 25 ltr of cargo oil leaked out from the hole into P/Rm bilges. Same was immediately contained.
DirectCause: Material failure due to ageing
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Holed pipeline section to be renewed in E/Rm workshop and to be  fitted back.
ClosingNote: Alert ship staff has averted a possible oil spill of greater extent in the pump room.Defective pipe line has been replaced, the other pipelines were checked and found in order

IncidentDetails: While hoisting stbd L/boat in High Speed mode,  at halfway from water level the hoisting motor was unable to hoist the boat. Motor was still found to be running both   in High speed and Low Speed.    After securing the boat, hoisting motor was disconnected and it was found that motor shaft had sheared at drive end just after the key-way.
IncidentEventDetails: During 3 monthly L/Boat lowering & manoeuvring drill, initially Stbd boat was hoisted at Low Speed and thereafter at High speed.  During High Speed operation at halfway from water level, the hoisting motor was unable to hoist the boat. Motor was still found to be running both   in High speed and Low Speed.  So the boat was picked up by hand cranking.     After securing the boat, hoisting motor was disconnected and it was found that motor shaft had sheared at drive end just after the key-way.
ImmediateAction: Stbd L/Boat was picked up manually by hand cranking.  Reqn SPR/CDRM/D/17/101 raised for immediate supply of electric motor at Jose.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hoisting motor shaft shearing off due to material failure
DirectCause: Maintenance procedures not followed
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Reqn raised for new motor. Original motor to be supplied.
ClosingNote: Spare motor supplied and replaced

IncidentDetails: Vessel's main engine fuel pump unit #3 VIT was noted in seized condition while sailing.
IncidentEventDetails: M/E fuel injection pump overhauling sequence to be reduce.
ImmediateAction: Vessel is stopping now to carry out fuel pump renewal. Makers were also contacted to seek further guidance and advise. Master Confirmed that, vessel will be stopped  5-6 hours.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis of the Incident Direct Causes a) Poor Quality Of Fuel Bunkered at US Vessel was using fuel which was bunkered in US. Vessel have had issues of fuel pump seizures earlier due to the poor quality of oil bunkered in US. Presence of contaminants cause sludge formation which eventually seizes the fuel pump components.          b)    Frequent change-over of fuel Vessel reported that there had been at least 9 fuel change overs ( from high temp HFO to low temp LSMGO) in past 3 months  due to vessel trading pattern. Such change overs can cause the fuel pumps to mal-function, also pump seal rings get harder and lose their property.   Root Cause  The pump removed from ME unit #3 dismantled and found sludge accumulation at top of the barrel and also some amount sludge found accumulated between pump body and barrel bottom surface. Barrel seal rings found hardened, broken, thereby losing their sealing property. It may be inferred that fuel oil leakage from the seals of the pump barrel must have caused  fuel accumulation at the bottom of the barrel, timing gear and in between the barrel and the  pump body. This leaked off fuel oil at the bottom of the barrel must have become hard  sludge thus jamming the movement of the barrel eventually seizing pump VIT.     This leakage may be due to the frequent change-over of the fuel oil from HFO to LSGO and vice versa. Due to changes in temperature, the seal rings become hard and lost their property.  Vessel had carried out fuel oil change over 9 times in period of 3 months.  Also, quality of the HFO, which was producing excess amount of sludge.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediate Corrective Action   Vessel checked performance of main engine and makers were contacted. Basis recommendation, fuel pump was renewed at earliest opportunity.
Corrective Action: Fuel pump renewal to be carried out. Defective pump to be opened up and overhauled. Check the internal condition to find root cause of failure.
ClosingNote: All preventive and corrective measures has been taken.

IncidentDetails: During heavy  weather main mast stay broke , fell and hit on the port  side bulkhead of the bridge.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel proceeding to Shekou, China for dry dock. Vessel in light condition, keeping minimum required draft as per stability reqd  due draft restriction at pilot point and during the passage from pilot station to dry dock.Around 0200 Hrs Wind picked up to  force 6 and gusting upto force 8. Vessel experienced few Jerky movements due to which  main mast stays broke .
ImmediateAction: Loose broken main mast stay secured with stairs to restrict its free movement to avoid further damage. Crew informed not to venture near main mast.Bridge equipments re-checked for proper function. All found in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: All Main mast stays on close inspection found parted with strands broken.
DirectCause: The main mast stay wires were wasted. However due to it being covered with a PVC coating same could not be noticed in routine inspections .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mast stay wires to be inspected carefully through the PVC coating and renewed promptly.
Corrective Action: Mast stay wires to be inspected carefully through the PVC coating and renewed promptly.
LessionLearnt: To be checked within the inner core too
ReviewComments: To be discussed with ship staff and made aware.
ClosingNote: To be inspected within the plastic covering

IncidentDetails: 2  Stowaways found hiding on main deck under the stairway leading to Forecastle deck on Stbd side.
IncidentEventDetails: During the routine Pre departure Stowaway checkTr OS Mr Lokesh Malik,found 2 Stowaways hiding onmain deck under the stairway leading to Forecastle deckon stbd side.He immediatly notified Bosun and Bosun reported this on Walkie Talkie.Chief Officer along with Agents representative rushed to the location,followed by Master and chief engineer.Agents representative Informed port security and Port security was on board,followed by immigration officer and local Police.As advised by the immigration officer ,stowaway's were brought to the tally office on main deck port side.LocalPolice interrogated stowaway's at the Tally office.Stowaway's were disembarked under police custody after interrogation .As informed by Port security guards,Stowaway boarded the vesselaround 0300 Hrs on 19th Nov and they were dressed like stevedores wearing high visibility vests and hard hat.
ImmediateAction: Informed Port Security through local agents representative who was on board.Master informed CSO.A thorough Stowaway check was immediatly initiated to check for more stowaway presence on board.
DirectCause: Stowaways boarded Vessel pretending to be Stevedores. Strict access control implemented onboard. Vessels Pre-departure stowaway Search was effective in identifying the stoways. SSO, immediately alerted the PFSO, and CSO, followed by extended Stowaway Search. eMail from P&I Club attached for reference.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: This incident Circulated to all Fleet Vessels through Shippalm
Corrective Action: Circulate to Fleet Vessel regardingthis incident at Malta Port
LessionLearnt: At Malta Port, during a routine pre-departure inspection, two suspected stowaways were observed hiding below the stairway leading to Forecastle Deck. They were both wearing flouresent vests and one had a yellow hard hat. The trainee OS informed the Bosun who attended to on the spot. The two stowaways, allegedly a Libyan and a Moroccan, were apprehended and taken to main deck in the ship's office. They had a bag with provisions which included biscuits, chocolates and bread.  Both men did not have any identity documents.  At about 1100 hrs the agent, who at the time was on board,  informed the Freeport Security and the Freeport’s Duty Manager who boarded the vessel at around 1118 hrs followed by the Immigration Police who arrived shortly thereafter. Police officer boarded the vessel and briefly interviewed the stowaways in the tally office.  After the interviews the Master was informed by the police officer they they were  going to disembark the two stowaways from the vessel and take them back to their accommodation at Hal Far, not far away from the Freeport Terminal. At about 1220 hours the stowaways were disembarked under police custody. The stowaways had presumably boarded the vessel some time between midnight and 0400 hours on the previous day, gaining access from the gangway, pretendingto be stevedores. They had high visibility vests and could have easily been mistaken for stevedores.  According to Immigration Police the two stowaways had entered the Malta Freeport Terminal by jumping  boundary  fence on the road onto some containers.    As per P&I This is quite an atypical instance, and the first whereby stowaways boarded a vessel from Malta. Shipstaff Pre-departure stowaway Search was effective, and Vessel maintained a strict Gangway watch.
ClosingNote: P&I Assistance taken. No claim for delays received from Charterers. No additional cost other than P&I attendance involved

IncidentDetails: DURING DEPARTURE STATION WHILE DEPARTING FROM  PORT OF BRISBANE, ONE OF OUR MOORING HAWSER PARTED IN FORWARD STATIONS (HEADLINE ON PORT SIDE INNER WINCH DRUM) . NO INJURIES OR CASUALTIES.
IncidentEventDetails: I) SPRING LINES AND BREAST LINE WERE CAST OFF AND V/L WAS HOLDING ON TO HEADLINE NOW- FWD STATIONS.  2) AFT STATION SINGLED UP (HOLDING ON TO (1+1).  3) SUDDEN GUST OF STRONG WIND BLEW ACROSS THE BERTH WITH HEAVY RAIN.  4) VESSEL'S BOW SUDDENLY SWUNG OUT WITH FULL WEIGHT ON ALL FOUR HEAD LINES.  5) ONE OF THE HEADLINE ON THE PORT SIDE WINCH SUDDENLY PARTED. BOW THRUSTER WAS OPERATED TO EASE THE WEIGHT ON OTHER HEADLINES AND WERE SUBSEQUENTLY CAST-OFF.   6) VESSEL DEPARTED SAFELY WITH ALL THE MOORING HAWSERS HAULED ON DECK WITH THE EXCEPTION OF 30-35 MTRS OF HEADLINE THAT PARTED.
ImmediateAction: ALL CREW MOVED AWAY FROM THE SITE  UPON HEARING THE JERKS ON THE ROPE AND ALLOWED ROPE TO PART TO AVOID SPLASHING ON  STATIONS,  WHICH COULD HAVE LEAD TO FATAL INJURIES. BOW THRUSTER WAS OPERATED TO PREVENT OTHER HEADLINES FROM PARTING AND TO EASE THE LOAD ON THE WINCH.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 30 -35 meters of Mooring Hawser lost.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Local Weather comndition to be obtained from the local agents prior departure and discussed with the Pilot during Master/Pilot information exchange and accordingly plan the operation to avoid such recurrence
ClosingNote: Proper monitoring of  mooring procedures and proper adequate of mooring station can avoid such accidents

IncidentDetails: Oily water seperator overboard pipeline double seggregation Overboard  valve towards the ship side found the valve spindle freely rotating on either  direction of the Close/Open.
IncidentEventDetails: 1700 HRS: OWS was stopped after pumping out bilge tank.  1705 Hrs : Tried to shut the OWS Obd valve and found spindle rotaing freely. It was not evident that valve was shut fully.  1706 Hrs : Valve before OWS OBD valve No. 11V shut and isolated the system.
ImmediateAction: Oily water seperator operation checked and was  confirmed the overboard valve seat is in open condition. Vessel now using  the inward valve for seggregation of the system. Requisition raised for OWS OBD valve.
DirectCause: Valve need to be replaced with new one
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Valve need to be renewed with Class approved one
ClosingNote: The overboard valve spindle was just rotating and valve seat was stuck in open position. We supplied new valve with ClassNk approval, on 18th Dec, diving co was arranged to plug the chest, valve was renewed in Singapore anchorage with new gaskets and bolts. Chief confirmed everything normal.

IncidentDetails: Forward bosun store and ships hospital water tight doors pad locks found broken / damaged /tampered - Forced entry into Bosun store and ships hospital  by stevedores .    On inspection found bosun store door pad lock broken /damaged /tampered and a lashing gear extension hook lying below the bosun store door with pad lock on the door handle in forced tampered condition (please refer attached photos).
IncidentEventDetails: During deck security rounds at 1545 hrs LT on 18th Nov 2017 at KICT bosun found one stevedore (Mr. Wasif  Gul ) running towards the forward bosun store. On following the stevedore (Mr. Wasif Gul ) bosun found another stevedore (Mr. Fazalqadar) coming out from bosun store (stbd side entrance). On enquiring was given to understand that he entered the bosun store for hand gloves.   On inspection found bosun store door pad lock broken /damaged /tampered and a lashing gear extension hook lying below the bosun store door with pad lock on the door handle in forced tampered condition (please refer attached photos).
ImmediateAction: At 1615 Hrs LT interviewed offending stevedores Mr. Wasif Gul and Mr. Fazalqadar -Both accepted that they entered the Bosun store for hand gloves.  At 1630 Hrs LT master called for KICT terminal supervisor and PFSO for official complaint.  At 1645 Hrs LT started inspection and vessel search as per checklist SF-08.   At 1705 Hrs KICT terminal superintendent Mr. Naeem Ahmad boarded vessel –Incident explained in presence of offending stevedores. Both offending stevedores accepted that they entered the Bosun store for hand gloves in the terminal suptd.  presence.  At 1736 Hrs LT Security supervisor Mr. Abdul Munawar Hussain boarded vessel - Incident explained in presence of offending stevedores.  Both offending stevedores accepted that they entered the Bosun store for hand gloves in the Security supervisor’s   presence.  Thorough vessel's search for missing items , contarband , bomb and imminent security threat  carried out .   Further informed CSO on telephone about the incident on 19th Nov morning around 0530 UTC / 1100 IST on the security incident .   Following reports submitted :      1) Unlawful Act Report     2) Incident report and Note Of Protest issued to KICT terminal with acknowledgement from KICT terminal , stevedoring company and PFSO KICT .  Also attached security incident photo's of damaged/tampered and missing  pad locks .    3) SOF from C/O Bimal Mohan,2/O Yokesh Jayaraj and Bosun Ramesh Pooranapackiam.     Please note vessel has carried out a thorough search on board on 18th Nov from 1645 - 1830 hrs LT and again on departure KICT from  19th Nov 1015 hrs LT -  1130 Hrs LT  as per check list SF-08 and found areas free from any items / stores missing , Stowaways , Contraband , Bomb or imminent security threat .      Additional security precautions taken as per SSP and watches maintained to prevent re-occurence .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On inspection found bosun store door pad lock broken /damaged /tampered and a lashing gear extension hook lying below the bosun store door with pad lock on the door handle in forced tampered condition Hospital Watertight door pad lock damaged .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional security precautions taken as per SSP and watches maintained to prevent re-occurence . Vessel has carried out a thorough search on board.
Corrective Action: To be communicated within fleet for information and to avoid similar situations
ClosingNote: Unlawful act report along with Communication with Charterers attached for reference

IncidentDetails: During discharge of Banyu Urip crude oil, around 20 litres of oil splashed /spilled outside the heating coil drip tray due to leakage from 4P cot aft coil
IncidentEventDetails: 1200: V/L all made fast at ESSO MBM Sriracha.  1500: Warming up  steam for Cargo Tank heating was opened to warm up the deck line.  1542: Commenecd discharging of Banyu Urip Crude oil  1600: Main steam was crack opened to drain steam coils for 1W's and 4W's COT.  1610: Manifold watchkeeper reported oil coming out from 4P cot aft steam coil drain valve.  1612: Discharging operation was suspended as per loading master instructions and deck clean up was initiated.  1642: Resumed discharging after obtaining approval from loading master.
ImmediateAction: Discharging operation was ceased as per loading master instructions. Deck Clean up was initiated instantly & entire spilled oil was contained to drums prior reaching the fish plate.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While discharging at ESSO MBM Sriracha V/L had a slight cargo spillage from 4P COT aft coil. Discharge of Banyu Urip crude parcel commenced on 23rd Nov/1542 Lt. At 1612 Lt, manifold watchkeeper reported oil spill from 4P aft heating coil drain. Return line drain v/v shut instantly & also E/Rm advised to stop heating supply steam.  Loading master who was stationed in CCR advised to stop the cargo operation.  Cargo was stopped instantly. About 20 litres of oil splashed /spilled outside the heating coil drip tray. Clean up was initiated instantly & entire spilled oil was contained to drums prior reaching the fish plate.    With loading master's permission discharge was resumed at 1642 Lt (30 min stoppage).
DirectCause: Improper securing of steam lines previously closed.  The drain valve for 4P was left open. No checks were carried out prior putting the steam system in use. Heating coils were not tested prior use.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Though vessel was continuously on heated crudes, it is always required to pressure test the heating coils prior carrying out cargo heating.
Corrective Action: The incisent to be discussed in the safety committee meeting.
ClosingNote: we appreciate the incident reporting and investigation.

IncidentDetails: V/l was doing STS operation in Kaohsiung OPL with OCEN AQUAMARINE. v/l was moored stbd alongside with daughter vessel. 3 Hoses connected and v/l was preparing for discharging.Due to moderate swell daughter v/l started rolling heavily and mooring ropes started parting.Mooring master decided to cast off vessel and started disconnecting hoses. At around 1915 while the vessel was casting off three mooring ropes and and three mooring tails parted.
IncidentEventDetails: 24/1512:ALL MADE FAST TO OCEAN AQUAMARINE.  24/1724:HOSES CONNECTED 3 X 8"  24/1830:COMMENCED DISCONNECTION OF HOSES DUE TO HEAVY ROLLING OF DAUGHTER VESSEL AND ROPES STARTED BREAKING.  24/1842:HOSES OFF.  24/1842:COMMENCED UNMOORING.  24/1915:ROPES PARTED.  24/1930:COMPLETED UNMOORING.  BOTH VESSEL STEERED AWAY TO SAFETY
ImmediateAction: Both vessels started casting off after disconnecting hoses. Both vessels cast off and moved to safety.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Three mooring ropes and three rope tails broken
Proposedcorrectiveactions: STS operation should be conducted in sheltered area orfair weather.
Corrective Action: Apply grease regularly at contact points of panama choke, maintain equal weight on all the mooring lines.
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out and all corrective actions closed satisfactorily

IncidentDetails: Please to inform you that around 2200 Hrs. November 20, 2017, Sea Water inlet pipe for A/E jacket cooler no.2 burst, and goes direct to Alternator no.1 that cause the 440 volts feeder panel alarmed Low insulation. Immediately started A/E No.2 or standby engine and stopped A/E engine No. 1 and investigate the cause of the alarm.  As of today, we opened up both side and upper side cover of Alternator no. 1 to for further investigation to the unit. We already pulled out rectified the said A/E jacket cooler No.2 leaky sea water inlet pipe.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. At 2145 hrs, when oiler make a routine inspection. He saw sprinkled water came out from the bursting S.W inlet pipe of A/E jacket cooler no. 2 reaching Alternator no. 1 and he temporarily stopped the leakage and called 4th/Engr. and they changed over the cooler from No.2 to No.1, when they already secured the cooler  2. At 2200h when they are about to start A/E no.2 the alarm from 440V Feeder low insulation activated. Immediately put up load on A/E #2 and parallel with # 1 and after changed over A/E # 1 F.O to D.O consumption.    3. Stopped A/E # 1 @ 2230H
ImmediateAction: At 2200h when they are about to start A/E no.2 the alarm from 440V Feeder low insulation activated. Immediately put up load on A/E #2 and parallel with # 1 and after changed over A/E # 1 F.O to D.O consumption. Stopped A/E # 1 @ 2230H
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always monitor sea water cooiling piping line for any possible leakages and installed deflecator plate avoid any damages to the machineries.
Corrective Action: Deflector plate to be fabricated in order to prevent the leakage water(if any) from directly reaching the alternator
ClosingNote: New deflector plate installed to avoid direct spray of water into alternator in case of leakages in future.

IncidentDetails: Upon arrival off Kochi SPM, v/l was approached by tug boat KB7, from which the tool box was to be lifted. After connecting the ships crane hook to the lifting gear of the tool box and prior commencing the lift, it was noticed that the tug boat suddenly moved off from ship side. This caused the crane wire to get  pulled outward with excessive strain. By the time the tug boat was manoeuvered back into position the box was off the boat and being pickedup. The excess load that came on the runner wire, caused damage to it which resulted in parting of 3 strands. Ship has accordingly issued LOP to terminal.
IncidentEventDetails: Upon arrival off Kochi SPM, v/l was approached by tug boat KB7, from which the tool box was to be lifted. After connecting the ships crane hook to the lifting gear of the tool box and prior commencing the lift, it was noticed that the tug boat suddenly moved off from ship side. This caused the crane wire to get  pulled outward with excessive strain. By the time the tug boat was manoeuvered back into position the box was off the boat and being pickedup. The excess load that came on the runner wire, caused damage to it which resulted in parting of 3 strands. Ship has accordingly issued LOP to terminal.
ImmediateAction: Wire inspected and photographic evidence taken. LOP issued to terminal. Crane runner wire to be renewed at the earliest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage sustained to crane runner wire.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: pick up of material from tug, to be done only after tug confirms they are ready. Once crane hook is connected to tool box, time not to be wasted in heaving. Proper signalling between crane operator and signal man on deck to be established.
LessionLearnt: Ensure that the supplying boat is steady and maintaining position prior to taking weight on the crane wires.
ReviewComments: Continuous monitoring and communication with the boat to be maintained by verbal/ hand signals.
ClosingNote: Proper supervision of lifting operations to be ensured while operating ships cranes

IncidentDetails: WHILE ASSEMBLING MAIN ENGINE AUTO START STOP VALVE AFTER OVERHAUL,THE LOWER PART OF THE VALVE WAS RESTING ON A WOODEN PIECE FOR ENGAGING E/R OVERHEAD CRANE.THE WOODEN PIECE SLIPPED AND AT THAT TIME THE VALVE BODY WAS SUPPORTED BY OTHER 2 CREWMEMBERS.THE CREWMEMBERS THEN INSTANTLY TRIED TO HOLD, PREVENT THE FALL OF THE VALVE BODY AND PUT THE VALVE BODY ON THE PLATFORM. DURING THIS PROCESS OF PUTTING VALVE BODY ON THE PLATFORM, ABOUT 20 CM ABOVE THE PLATFORM, THE VALVE BODY SLIPPED FROM THE CREWMEMBERS AND THE FINGERS OF THE INJURED MOTORMAN, WHO WAS SUPPORTING THE VALVE BODY, GOT TRAPPED IN BETWEEN THE VALVE PISTON AND LINER.THERE WAS A GAP IN THE VALVE PISTON AND LINER DUE TO THE SPRING IN THE EQUIPMENT AND AT THE TIME OF FALL DUE TO JERK THE SPRING COMPRESSED, IN TURN CRUSHING THE INJURED MOTORMAN'S FINGERS MOMENTARILY.
IncidentEventDetails: 1300 - ME AUTO START STOP ASSEMBLY IN PROGRESS AFTER OVERHAUL.    1335LT - WHILE ASSEMBLING MAIN ENGINE AUTO START STOP VALVE AFTER OVERHAUL,THE LOWER PART OF THE VALVE WAS RESTING ON A WOODEN PIECE FOR ENGAGING E/R OVERHEAD CRANE.THE WOODEN PIECE SLIPPED AND AT THAT TIME THE VALVE BODY WAS SUPPORTED BY OTHER 2 CREWMEMBERS.THE CREWMEMBERS THEN INSTANTLY TRIED TO HOLD, PREVENT THE FALL OF THE VALVE BODY AND PUT THE VALVE BODY ON THE PLATFORM. DURING THIS PROCESS OF PUTTING VALVE BODY ON THE PLATFORM, ABOUT 20 CM ABOVE THE PLATFORM, THE VALVE BODY SLIPPED FROM THE CREWMEMBERS AND THE FINGERS OF THE INJURED MOTORMAN, WHO WAS SUPPORTING THE VALVE BODY, GOT TRAPPED IN BETWEEN THE VALVE PISTON AND LINER.THERE WAS A GAP IN THE VALVE PISTON AND LINER DUE TO THE SPRING IN THE EQUIPMENT AND AT THE TIME OF FALL DUE TO JERK THE SPRING COMPRESSED, IN TURN CRUSHING THE INJURED MOTORMAN'S FINGERS MOMENTARILY.
ImmediateAction: INJURED CREWMEMBER TAKEN TO ECR AND THEN HOSPITAL FIRST AID ADMINISTERED.VITAL SIGNS AND MOVEMENT OF FINGERS CHECKED.NO DAMAGE TO BONES OR TISSUES APPARENT.FINGERS MOVEMENT NORMAL. CREWMEMBER GIVEN MEDICATION & REST FOR THE DAY.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two Fingers crushed with injury to nails of the middle finger and the ring finger.  Signs of blood clot in the nails of the two injured fingers.  No bone or tissue damage apparent.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper procedures for lifting loads in confined/congested spaces as required to be followed.  Work place being congested,make use of proper tool to lift/lower the weight of equipment for safe overhauling.  Use of makeshift levers,chain blocks,Pallets to support heavy loads as in this scenario.  Extreme caution when lifting heavy loads and handling heavy equipment , with focus on personal safety alongwith equipment handling safety to be kept in mind at all times.
ClosingNote: Extreme caution when lifting heavy loads and handling heavy equipment , with focus on personal safety alongwith equipment handling safety to be kept in mind at all times.

IncidentDetails: After discharging at Negishi, while disconnecting the manifold chickson arm along with the shore team, it was noted a minor IG leak from No 1 manifold after the chickson was seperated from vessel's manifold.
IncidentEventDetails: 29th Nov 17 1400 LT : Completed Discharging  29th Nov 17 1630 LT : Commenced Loading arm disconnection  29th Nov 17 1632 LT : Vessel lined up to drain the shore chickson into the ship's cargo tank (Manifold Valve 301,303,305,307 Shut, Manifold drain Valve for No 1 Manifold 601 opened & drained to 3S COT Valve 609 opened)  29th Nov 17 1635 LT : Shore chickson No 1 was drained into ship's No.1  manifold by blowing with nitrogen.  29th Nov 17 1640 LT : Draining of No 1 manifold Completed  29th Nov 17 1641 LT : No 1 Manifold drain Valve 601 Shut.  29th Nov 17 1643 LT : No 1 Chickson Arm seperated from manifold.  29th Nov 17 1644 LT : Realised there was a slight leak of IG from No 1 Manifold after the chickson arm was seperated  29th Nov 17 1645 LT : Immediately Drain to 3S COT, Valve no 609 was shut.  29th Nov 17 1646 LT : Terminal instructed vessel to Blank No 1 Manifold completely with all nut bolts in place.  29th Nov 17 1650 LT : No 1 Manifold was fully Blanked & bolted  29th Nov 17 1650 LT : Resumed blow through & disconnection of No 2, No 3 & No 4 Chickson Arm  29th Nov 17 1742 LT : All chickson Arm disconnected.
ImmediateAction: Valve No 609(Drain to 3S COT)  immediately shut to prevent outflow of IG from No 3 Stbd Cargo Oil Tank into No.1 manifold.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. After Sailing out from Negishi-Japan , Vessel Opened No 1 Manifold Drain Valve & Confirmed Seat ring of the drain Valve(601) was damaged.  2. Remaining 9 drain valve seat ring checked & found to be intact.  3. Requisition made for Seat ring in Ship Palm, req No SPR/SNKA/D/17/085
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel Raised requisition for manifold drain seat ring via req no SPR/SNKA/D/17/085. Manifold Drain seat ring to be renewed once the new seat ring is received.
ClosingNote: Seat ring for the drain valve replaced / Please refer to the IIR attached

IncidentDetails: Vessel was discharging Gasoline at Mombasa using No : 1,2 COP .During routine P/R inspection rounds  taken at 1000LT it was noticed that minor cargo leak from  welding seam of pipe self-priming pipe line connecting no :2 separator and pump casing .
ImmediateAction: Immediately no : 2 COP stopped and started no : 3 COP for discharging operation to void any delay or protest from terminal. Once pump stopped leakage reduced , same arrested with temporary cold work repairs  and area cleaned .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor welding seam crack ,same can be repaired on board with HOT work after discharging operation with all the precaution and  procedures
DirectCause: MATERIAL FAILURE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pump RPM  and pressure to be monitored very closely during operation especially terminal with high back pressure at manifold  .At no case max permitted RMP of pump to be exceed  .Regular monitoring of pumps and  P/R during operation .
Corrective Action: Permanent repairs of the leak.
LessionLearnt: Monitoring of cargo equipment.
ClosingNote: Monitoring of cargo equipment.

IncidentDetails: During a STS operation at Pacific lightering area on 03 Dec 17/ 1354LT M.T.Everbright were maintaining a heading of 132T with speed of 2.5 kts and swell/ wind  direction from stern, one stern line from the STBL, EVERBRIGHT parted. Crew of Everbright started replacing broken wire. At 1506LT before first parted stern wire was replaced, second stern wire from the STBL also parted. At this time, it was agreed that it is not safe to remain alongside and cargo transfer operations ceased and vessel separated .   Weather conditions: As per mooring master a long period swell at 8.5 feet was recorded at Tanner Bank weather buoy prior to commencement. Prior to the commencement of STS operation Actual observed weather was with Swell around 6  to 8 feet height , Wind NE 15kt. After the operation started the weather did not improve anymore and the long period swell  at around 10 feet height and at about 16 second period. Wind NNW 18 knots, seas 4 feet, swell 10 feet, swell period 16 sec.  No equipment damage or Failure on own vessel. No structural damage to own vessel. All wires /tails/Mooring equipment in good condition. All crew member were well rest through  out the operation
IncidentEventDetails: 03 Dec 2017  0600LT : Vessel Arrived Lightering Position  0824LT : Mooring Master Onboard  1018LT : STS Checklist No. 2 Complied  1030LT : STS Checklist No. 3 Complied  1106LT : Commenced Mooring  1218LT : Completed Mooring Port side to M.T.Everbright  1306LT : STS checklist No. 4 Complied.  1318LT : Commence Loading  1354LT : One stern line from the STBL, EVERBRIGHT parted.Crew started Replacing Wire with New spare wire.  1506LT  :Before One broken wire was replaced with New wire , second stren line of STBL Everbright parted. At this time it was agreed that it is not safe to remain alongside and Cargo operation ceased.  1506LT : Commenced Cargo hose disconnection.  1606LT : Cargo Hoses disconnected  1618LT : STS Checklist No.5 Complied.  1618LT : Commenced Unmooring  1642LT : Completed Unmooring  1654LT : Vessel Separated
ImmediateAction: Cargo transfer suspended, hoses disconnected and vessels separated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two mooring wire parted of STBL - M.T.Everbright  .  No equipment damage or Failure on own vessel. No structural damage to own vessel. All wires /tails/Mooring equipment in good condition
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Condition of mooring wires to be checked as per PMS  and before operation.Winch beake rendering to be tested as per PMS and marking to be done.Weather conditions to be closely monitored during the operation
ClosingNote: Please refer to the attached IIR

IncidentDetails: V/l was experiencing 30-35 kts wind speed and gentle rolling of 10-15*. At approx 2300 the FBB dome antennae foudation bolts had sheered away(already old and rusted) and the dome fell from its stowed position onto the monkey island(approx 2mtr height). The antennae was moved aside and secured, in order to inspect and repair during daylight hours. All communication from ship was terminated, except for the Sat-C telex.
IncidentEventDetails: V/l was experiencing 30-35 kts wind speed and gentle rolling of 10-15*. At approx 2300 the FBB dome antennae foudation bolts had sheered away(already old and rusted) and the dome fell from its stowed position onto the monkey island(approx 2mtr height). The antennae was moved aside and secured, in order to inspect and repair during daylight hours. All communication from ship was terminated, except for the Sat-C telex.
ImmediateAction: Antennae was secured on monkey island. FBB power was turned off. Telex msg was sent to office wrt ships inability to communicate via FFB.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The FBB dome securing nuts and foundation plate were very old and wasted. No damage sustained on the Dome exterior. The wiring was connected to dome and found operating in order.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The base plate and corresponding bolts have been renewed.
LessionLearnt: Proper securing of antennae and loose objects when rough weather and strong winds prevail.
ReviewComments: Care to be exercised during rough weather conditions.
ClosingNote: Base has been fabricated and installed in place to secure the FBB antenna.

IncidentDetails: Fire on Hot plate in Galley while cooking food.
IncidentEventDetails: 0600: Chief cook started prepartion of morning breakfast.  0710: Chief cook wanted to add water in dal which is next to Oil bowl kept on hot plate.  Water jug slip from hand and directly went into hot oil bowl kept on hot plate. Hot oil splashed on plate and created fire on hot plate.  Immediately chief cook removed the hot oil bowl from hot plate, switched off the plate and closed the exhaust, used fire blanket to extinguish the fire.
ImmediateAction: Immediately chief cook removed the hot oil bowl from hot plate, switched off the plate and closed the exhaust, used fire blanket to extinguish the fire.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fire blanket have oil stains and burn stains. Need to replace Fire blanket.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew member should pay extra attention while cooking.
LessionLearnt: Oil and water never to be mixed instantly while cooking
ClosingNote: Immediate replacement of Fire blanket used during fire

IncidentDetails: While discharging cargo at Shanghai, Stbd side gangway came in contact with the shore crane thereby casuing damage to the gangway and crane wire cable.   Due to the impact the turn table was lifeted and came to rest on the stepping platform, the wooden fender on the gangway was completely damage, roller located under lower platform was sheerd off, most the stanchions were damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1200 Hrs: handing over of the duty officers took place.  At 1205 Hrs: Duty officer and crew checkd all morring ropes to confirm that the ropes were taught enough. At the same time the crew member stationed at the gangway checked that clearance betwwen the lower platform and the crane was approh. one meter.  1244 Hrs: M/V Kuo Cang Shan passed very close to our vessel with good speed which was captured in the shore camera.  1245 Hrs: Due to the above reason our vessel surged to about 2 meters resulting in the gangway making contact with the shore crane.
ImmediateAction: On seeing the vessel surge, slack on all mooring ropes was taken to ensure vessel does not come off the berth. Combination ladder was rigged up to ensure safe access from ship to the shore.  Owners representative,vessel's managers, agents and P&I  were immediately informed of the accident. As owners representative was on boarding during the time of the accident, he arranged for workshop to get the gangway repaired.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to the impact the turn table was lifted and came to rest on the stepping platform, most of the stanchions on the gangway was damaged, the wooden fender located on the lower section of the gangway was completey damaged, the gangway roller located under the lower landing platform was sheered off.  As the vessel moved ahead the gangaway damaged the crane wire cable which was lying on the jetty.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All brake liners to be maintained as per the makers specifications and PMS. Spare brake liners to be kept on board for replacement. Frequent round to be taken while the vessel is at port to ensure all mooring ropes are kept taught at any given time. While the gangway is placed on the quay the duty offiver and gangway watchman to ensure the lower platfrom of the gangway is placed well clear of any obstructions and mooving crane.
Corrective Action: All brake liners to be maintained as per the makers specifications and PMS. Spare brake liners to be kept on board for replacement. Frequent round to be taken while the vessel is at port to ensure all mooring ropes are kept taught at any given time. While the gangway is placed on the quay the duty offiver and gangway watchman to ensure the lower platfrom of the gangway is placed well clear of any obstructions and mooving crane.
ClosingNote: CA adequate

IncidentDetails: Before arrival port, the Trainee Wiper was asked to clean the Refer Compressor tray. He found that the space between the Refer compressor#2 and the motor is dirty. So he started cleaning the same with a rag without realizing that the same unit is in use. During then the compressor started and the rag got pulled by the movement of the belt. When the rag got in between the belt and the pulley, his hand also got pulled. As an involuntary reaction, he pulled his hand back but by that time one of his fingers slightly got between the pulley and the belt causing a 2~3 mm deep and around 3 cm long cut.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: To give Clear instructions during the tool box meeting and to ensue crew member has understood the same.  Risks involved to be discussed in detail.
Corrective Action: To give Clear instructions during the tool box meeting and to ensue crew member has understood the same. Risks involved to be discussed in detail.
LessionLearnt: Job instructions varies with the experience and cognitive skill of each member involved in the job.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed after onboard verification.

IncidentDetails: VESEEL EXPERIENCING HEAVY WEATHER SINCE  05TH OF DECEMBER AND TAKEN ALL HEAVY WEATHER  PRECAUTIONS AS PER 0P 09D,BUT ABOUT 1700 LT (08TH DEC2017) ONE LARGE WAVE HIT THE  PORT SIDE OF THE VESSEL AND DAMAGE THE  GANGWAY LASHINGS AND  EXTRA LASHINGS TAKEN AS A HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTION.
IncidentEventDetails: VESEEL EXPERIENCING HEAVY WEATHER SINCE  05TH OF DECEMBER AND TAKEN ALL HEAVY WEATHER  PRECAUTIONS AS PER 0P 09D,BUT ABOUT 1700 LT (08TH DEC2017) ONE LARGE WAVE HIT THE  PORT SIDE OF THE VESSEL AND DAMAGE THE  GANGWAY LASHINGS AND  EXTRA LASHINGS TAKEN AS A HEAVY WEATHER PRECAUTION.
ImmediateAction: VESSEL HAS SECURED THE GANGWAY BACK AND TAKEN EXTRA LASHINGS AGAIN.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gangway became inoperational due to bending of structural parts
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Gangway repairs to be carried out at the earliest and to make operational.
Corrective Action: Gangway repairs to be carried out at the earliest and to make operational.
ClosingNote: Extra precautions to be taken by ship staff

IncidentDetails: one of the Hatch cover pump safety valve securing Elan bolts sheared off.
IncidentEventDetails: In the heavy weather objects made contact with the  the safety valve and the bolts broken and the hydraulic oil from the tank leaked into FPK store,Elan bolts damged.
ImmediateAction: FPK store cleaned and the leaking flage isolated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The hatch cover motor hit by rolling object spare crane hoisting wire during heavy weather inside the bosun store that will cause one mechansm from hatch cover motor  bursted  and  leak the hydraulic oil. It was noticed  almost half drum of oil were leak and spread inside  the bosun store. Immediately collected, no any sign or quantity of oil leak overboard.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Corrective action has immediately been done,  request immediately a hot work permit from the office attached with the risk assesment in order to put and weld strong stopper in the spare crane hoisting wire  and deteriorated lashing has been replaced with new wire together with turnbuckles.
Corrective Action: All the loose gears to be secured properly. Elan bolts to renew with spares.
