IncidentDetails: Cargo hold No.1: While collecting residual cargo from tanktop, Shore Bulldozer hit & broke a stud of WBT DB No. 1P manhole cover. The damage resulted in ingress of Ballast Water from DB No.1P inside Cargo hold No.1.
ImmediateAction: Water level inside DB No.1P dropped to less than tanktop level to prevent further ingress of water. Foreman informed. Shipstaff removing manhole cover of DB No.1P to estimate the extend of damage & carry out necessary repairs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The stud for securing manhole cover was broken through which water ingress took place.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tap the hole for correct size of stud & renew the broken stud.
Corrective Action: Tap the hole for correct size of stud & renew the broken stud.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: To be discussed with deck team.

IncidentDetails: Steering Failure due to tripping of One Steering Motor (bearing failure that subsequently damaged the winding).
IncidentEventDetails: 1000LT : River POB  1002-1053LT : All checks carried out as per 33CFR164.25 (Dep. Axeon Paulsboro)  1036LT : Docking POB  1112LT : Vessel unmoored from Axeon Paulsboro  1148LT : Vessel Anchored at Mantua Creek Anchorage  1310LT : Vessel Re-Anchored at Mantua Creek Anchorage  1842LT : Anchor Aweigh (from Mantua Creek Anchorage after completion of Bunkers) / Vessel commenced her outbound passage under Pilotage in Delaware River   2345LT : Received "No Volt" Alarm on No.1 Steering Gear Motor / loss of steering with rudder stuck at 5 deg Port
ImmediateAction: Immediately called for Anchor Stations, informed E/R to proceed to Steering Gear room for Emergency Steering and used M/E to stop the vessel.  Emergency Steering engaged from Steering Gear Room and vessel brought to stop condition away from Danger.  No. 1 Steering Gear Motor isolated and Steering Controls taken back on Bridge using No. 2 Steering Gear Motor and No. 2 Control System.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Steering Gear Motor No. 1 damaged.
DirectCause: Damage of the bearing caused due improper maintenance and missing out on signals like sound or vibration to anticipate trouble.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Burnt motor landed to shore at Philadelphia for complete overhaul.(Including rewinding).  In future at cold climate condition frequently steering motor insulation to be checked. If found insulation low, 500 watts heating lamp need to be fixed over the motor to improve the low insulation.
Corrective Action: 1) As far as possible try and source the bearings for major motor overhauls from makers instead of supplying through the stores vendor. Same to be raised as a spares requisition. Instruction regarding raising a separate spare requisition for motor bearings, instead of including in the regular stores requisition has been sent to vessel and the purchase team.
LessionLearnt: Engine-room rounds to be done in a more effective manner. Maker spares to be used. Bridge team to react proactively to emergency situation.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: All measures as per corrective actions to be implemented and monitored.

IncidentDetails: One of the forward spring line tail rope parted during mooring operations at Mumbai JD-4. Luckily no one got injured as everyone including shore mooring gang were well clear of the line. We have renewed the tail rope and continued the mooring operations. This happened while one spring line each was made fast fwd & aft and the Dock master advised the fwd tug to pull out the vessel little to clear the shore fender which was not in proper position.
IncidentEventDetails: One of the forward spring line tail rope parted during mooring operations at Mumbai JD-4. Luckily no one got injured as everyone including shore mooring gang were well clear of the line. We have renewed the tail rope and continued the mooring operations. This happened while one spring line each was made fast fwd & aft and the Dock master advised the fwd tug to pull out the vessel little to clear the shore fender which was not in proper position.
ImmediateAction: Tug was advised to stop pulling. Vessel renwed the mooringtail rope and continued the mooring opearation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One taile rope parted due to excessive load.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Brake settings of all mooring winches to be recked to ensure it renders at 60% of MBL of wire rope. Ensure all tail ropes in good condition
Corrective Action: Brake settings of all mooring winches to be recked to ensure it renders at 60% of MBL of wire rope. Ensure all tail ropes in good condition
LessionLearnt: Close monitoring of wire stresses during mooring operations, ensure optimal brake tightening as per BHC setting.
ReviewComments: Officers at stations to closely monitor mooring lines for over-stressing. Pilot to be briefed and Bridge team to be proactive and alert during mooring operation, to identify strained mooring ropes.
ClosingNote: Mooring wires and tails to be inspected regularly and prior to each operation

IncidentDetails: Vessel making contact with moored vessel while unberthing
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was unberthing from Coassa terminal, Panama. While unberthing, once the tugs were cast off the vessel swung to stbd to make Bow to Bow contact with moored container vessel CMA CGM Lisa Marie    0630 Hrs: Ford and aft tug made fast  0636 Hrs: ME Standby  0638 Hrs: Commenced unmooring  0648 Hrs: Completed unmooring  0655 Hrs: Forward tug cast off  0700 Hrs: Allison with CMA CGM Lisa Marie  0742 Hrs: Pilot away  0842 Hrs: Anchored at outer anchorage.
ImmediateAction: Forward tug made fast again and pulled own vessel back to avoid more time in contact.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Deformation in bracket no. 17, 18 and 19  2. Deformation in angle bulb no. 54 and 55  3. Deformation in angle bulb no. 5 - in between frame no. 251 to 254
DirectCause: Uncontrolled swing of the vessel as soon as the tug was casted off.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper usage of tugs with due regard to proximity of navigational hazards.  Prudent to monitor pilot's advice prior executing in the prevailing circumstances.
Corrective Action: Proper usage of tugs with due regard to proximity of navigational hazards. Prudent to monitor pilot's advice prior executing in the prevailing circumstances.
ClosingNote: Verified the effectiveness of the action during onboard visit while departing from Houston.

IncidentDetails: On 25th Oct 2017 0930 LT vessel was at sea, en route to Bata, Equatorial Guinea, in calm weather. Crew AB Zhou Zhongchang was carrying out greasing jobs of crane wires using Bosun Chair. Vessel was in position 6-29.0S 008-47.9E during the incident. During the work activity the AB fell from a height on the hatch cover due to the parting of Bosun chair rope. He suffered few fractures. He was shifted the ship’s hospital and medical guidance from a doctor assigned by the company was obtained. On arrival Bata he was transferred to the shore hospital via ambulance.
IncidentEventDetails: On 25th Oct’17, around 0930lt the AB Zhou Zhongchang, who had finished lubrication of short luffing wire of deck crane No.1, began to be lowered down to hatch cover no.2 using bosun chair by the AB Zhang Zhengmin. The chief officer was also supervising the operation. The AB who was lowering him, kept the safety line (fall preventer line), slack to allow for the lowering of the bosun chair. At a height of 2mtrs above the hatch cover, the rope of bosun chair parted, and AB Zhou Zhongchang fell on the top of hatch cover no.2. The safety line (fall preventer line) was too slack to arrest the fall. The C/O and the other AB immediately rushed to the assistance of the AB who had fallen.
ImmediateAction: on noticing the AB fall to the hatch cover, the OS and the Chief officer rushed to the spot.  The AB Zhang Zhengmin, rushed to the accommodation to gather all the other crew and sound the alarm. On 25th October ’17 at about 0934 lt, the master called the vessels Technical Superintendent Anto and appraised him of the situation. The vessels crisis center was manned immediately.  On 25th October’17 at around 0936lt, the 2/O and other crew arrived on the scene of the accident, with stretcher & first aid. The medical team, on seeing the condition of the causality, carried out immediate first air measures. To check fully for any hidden injuries, they cut away his work suit carefully, and found no bleeding. The AB was observed to be talking and indicating to pain on both his ankles and waist. Pain relief spray was used to relieve the pain for his ankles, and his back.  On 25th October ’17, at around 1030lt the AB Zhou Zhongchang was transferred to the to vessel’s hospital from top of hatch cover no.2 using stretcher. The vessel then proceeded at full speed towards her destination port, as there were no favorable ports in the vicinity for medical emergencies.  At the crisis center, the vessel commenced the crisis management. All concerned parties were informed of the situation, and planning was carried out to see if the vessel could be diverted to the nearest feasible port for medical evacuation of the AB. The Chinese manning office was requested to get in contact with the local Chinese doctor, with whom the master established communication. The agents in Bata were contacted and appraised to provide medical assistance on arrival.  The Chinese doctor advised the master to monitor the vital signs of the causality, administer liquids and sugars orally if able to, and to monitor for any worsening symptoms. The doctor further advised to be careful when shifting the patient and to monitor for any worsening in his condition.  The superintendent sent the vessel the company’s patient monitoring chart and the same was updated by the vessel and sent to all parties on an hourly basis. The was no worsening observed of the seaman’s medical condition.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 25th October’17, at around 0800lt, the deck crew carried out the daily safety meeting for the jobs to be done. One of the jobs was to grease the hoisting and luffing wires of all the cargo cranes (#1 - #4), to get the cranes ready for the discharging operations in Bata, Equatorial Guinea. After the safety meeting was completed, the bosun proceeded to check all the tools and equipment required for the job. In view of the job required to be done at a height where a person can injure himself, a working aloft permit was generated and attested by the master on 25th October’17 around 0825 LT. The risk assessment was also discussed with the crew during the safety meeting. One receipt of the working aloft permits all crew involved in that operation, proceeded to don the PPE, and proceeded to their work station. By 25th October’17 around 0830LT, the crew began their work by transferring the tools and grease to the top of the deck crane No.1. On 25th October’17, at around 0850 lt, the AB Zhou Zhonchang, commenced the lubrication of the short luffing wire of deck crane No.1, using the bosun chair. A polypropylene rope of about 20mm, attached to the safety harness was used as a fall preventer. Another line made of manila rope was used as a lowering line. This was tended to by the AB Zhang Zhengmin, who was standing on top of the crane. Once the AB Zhou Zhonchang was settled and commenced the greasing, the, Bosun, OS Zhang Zhengmin and the deck cadet commenced preparing the equipment for working on deck crane No.2.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels to prevent occurrence of similar incident on board other vessels in the fleet.  2. On board procedures for the different working aloft scenarios with inclusion of proper structured tool box meetings, will be reviewed individually with the vessels and feedback implemented.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ReviewComments: Incident report prepared and circulated in the fleet
ClosingNote: Incident investigation report attached

IncidentDetails: The top plating of Hatch cover No. 1 & No. 3 were observed with indentations caused due to the improper stowage of containers.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel loaded containers on hatch covers in Tianjin on 23/12/17.  While carrying out routine lashing check for the containers on the morning of 24/12/17, it was observed that plate of hatch cover No. 1 has been indented at 2 places  & hatch cover No. 3 has been indented at 4 places.
ImmediateAction: Supdt. informed, additional dunnage placed beneath the containers to avoid further indentation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hatch cover No. 1 - Indentation / plate buckling at 2 places.  Hatch cover No. 3 - Indentation / plate buckling at 4 places.
DirectCause: The load bearing shoe of the container is not resting on the strengthening member of hatch cover (stiffener/frame) & directly on the plate which caused the plate to indent.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Indented portions to be cropped & renewed. Class attendance required.
Corrective Action: Indented portions to be cropped & renewed. Class attendance required.
ClosingNote: Adequate dunnage and of proper size to be used. Any doubt, to double check with office for PLD.

IncidentDetails: Heavy oil leaks in Engine Room
IncidentEventDetails: While tightening of M/E FO return line union, union got slipped and Heavy oil leaked in E/R.
ImmediateAction: FO supply and circulating pump stopped and union renewed. Pump started no leaks found.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Approx 10liters Heavy oil leaked in E/R. Immediately it was cleaned.
DirectCause: FO supply and Circulating Pumps were running.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: While working on High pressure lines always pump to be stopped and line should be depressurised.
Corrective Action: While working on High pressure lines always pump to be stopped and line should be depressurised.
ClosingNote: To be discussed with team and ensure all precautions are taken prior working on any high pressure equipment

IncidentDetails: While picking up Pilot for berthing at Yangjiang, Due to the heavy swell, V/l's Railings got damaged by the Tug/Pilot Boat.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) V/l was at Anchor and awaiting berthing.  2) Pilot was acheduled to board the vessel after picking up the Anchor at 2030 hrs on 03rd Jan 2018  3) After picking up the Anchor, Pilot advised the Master to give Lee to the Tug/Pilot boat and to turn to Port side.  4) Master started altering the course to Port and gave Lee to the Pilot boat.  5) Pilot boat came along side and First Pilot boarded the vessel and while waiting for the 2nd Pilot to board, suddenly due to the heavy swell, both the vessel and Pilot boat started rolling heavily and due to this Ship's railings near the Pilot ladder got damaged with the tug boat hitting them.  6) Meanwhile, The Pilot ladder was already heaved up by the deck crew and thus it did not got damaged.  7) Now the boarding of the 2nd Pilot was aborted and later he boarded in the inner channel.
ImmediateAction: Tug boat moved away from the vessel and a rope was tied along the damaged railings. After the vessel's berthing at Yangjiang, same was informed to the Local agents and the Shore personnel repaired the railings before the vessel's departure from the berth.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Shipside Railings damaged and same were repaired by the Shore People.
DirectCause: Due to sudden roll caused by swell, the side rails got damaged by tugboat
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To be more vigilant with Wx and assessment of swell height and direction
Corrective Action: To be more vigilant with Wx and assessment of swell height and direction
LessionLearnt: Be sure of boarding Pilots when good lee is provided
ReviewComments: to be discussed in safety meeting
ClosingNote: Discussed with ships crew

IncidentDetails: We started the incenerator around 0800 hrs. The incinerator was firing normally. However the furnace temperature was low around 300 deg. c. We were investigating the reasons for such low temperature while burning the sludge. Around 1130 hours the wind box started heating up and within a short time turned red hot. We stopped the incinerator immediately and noticed accummulation of burning sludge on furnace refractory.We used a portable foam fire extinguisher to put off the fire. Apparently there was some accumulation of oil in wind box and this caught fire.
IncidentEventDetails: We started the incenerator around 0800 hrs. The incinerator was firing normally. However the furnace temperature was low around 300 deg. c. We were investigating the reasons for such low temperature while burning the sludge. Around 1130 hours the wind box started heating up and within a short time turned red hot. We stopped the incinerator immediately and noticed accummulation of burning sludge on furnace refractory. We used a portable foam fire extinguisher to put off the fire. Apparently there was some accumulation of oil in wind box and this caught fire.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the incinerator and extingushed the fire using a portable foam fire extingusher. Removed all the flammable materials from the vicinity.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Superficial paint damage to incienrator casing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To open up,clean and inspect all parts of the incinerator. Seek advice form makers.
LessionLearnt: Minimum furnace vacuum to be maintained and alarm operational function to be checked prior to every operation.
ReviewComments: Regular inspection of furnace and skin cooling passages (where applicable) to be done.
ClosingNote: Makers advise received vide email dtd 18 Jan 2018. Ship staff opened up and cleaned incinerator furnace and wall holes for air circulation. Noticeable improvement in furnace pressure achieved (-20 mm Aq)

IncidentDetails: On 9th Nov 17 at 1400 LT stevedore damage occured due to rough handling of shore gears, resulting in damage to the australian ladder, its railing & hatch coaming (stbd side) of No1Cargo hold.
IncidentEventDetails: On 9th Nov 17 at 1405 LT, crew member reported stevedore damage due to rough handling of shore gears, resulting in damage to the australian ladder, its railing & hatch coaming (stbd side) of No.1 Cargo hold.
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed all parties concerned. Cargo discharging was completed on 10th Nov 17 at 0130 LT, but vessel was awaiting repairs to be completed by stevedores repair team (shore team). The stevedore shore team reported that there was no insert of correct specification available, so ship staff provided the insert size of 600 X 400 mm, with thickness of 12 mm. cargo operations were completed, to speed up the process & keeping safety as priority onboard, ship staff assisted & monitored shore workers till completion of the job.      Stevedores shore workers team completed repair of Hold no. 1 Australian Ladder, railing & hatch coaming (stbd) on 10th Nov 17 at 0845 LT. Vessel all casted off from  Cartegena (Spain)port//berth on 10th Nov 17 at 1036 LT , (without any delays) on ships account.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 9th Nov 17 at 1400 LT stevedore damage occured due to rough handling of shore gears, resulting in damage to the australian ladder, its railing & hatch coaming (stbd side) of No.1 Cargo hold.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediately informed all parties concerned.  stevedore shore team   carried out hatch no 1 repairs , after complition of repair vessel departed port of Cartagena without any delays/claims/offhire on vessel accounts
ClosingNote: Damage repaired by Stevedores to master satisfaction.

IncidentDetails: Due to heavy weather port side Accommodation ladder's pillar and accommodation ladder wire rope are damaged. Vessel encountered heavy beam & following swells during the night. Gangway has been moved upwards due to waves hitting from underneath and due to this upward movement the top part of pillar was lifted up too and got cracked and bent (3 pillars cracked & bent). DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Impeller (2)-321-337179001. Bearing Cover(2)-537-463621001 and Ball Bearing(3)-532-I27304J2 found damaged.

IncidentDetails: Hose Handling Crane was inopertional due to failure of Aft Hydraulic Power System
IncidentEventDetails: 1. It was reported that the Hose Handling Crane was sluggish in operation and eventually not operating after the STS operation during hose disconnection.   2. It was noticed that the system pressure of both the Aft hydraulic power packs were gradually dropping from 22MPa to  5MPa, which is  below than the normal operating pressure of 22 MPa.   3. The hydraulic line return Filter clogged alarm was also triggered as the oil temperature was low.   4. Crane opertion was stopped and additional lashing of cargo hose were put in place.
ImmediateAction: 1. Paper filter element was renewed and return line fine-mesh filter cleaned. It was noticed that there were some white particles inside the filter elements.  2. But after renewing the filter, the system pressure did not increase above 9MPa. It was inferred that due to cold climate and the low temperature of return oil was causing the problem. Please note that as per Manual the viscosity of Lub Oil in this region should be VG36 W or below. But the Lub Oil for the system presently in use is Visga46. The ambient temperature was near 5degC.  3. The unloading solenoid valve (610) of Pressure Control Valve (618) was renewed with available new spare. But the system pressure did not increase.   4. By-pass valve connecting the High-Low pressure pipe-line near mid-ship Hose Handling Crane  was opened for oil re-circulation in return line and the temperature of the return oil was raised from 5degC to 24degC.    5. The hydraulic pump relief valve and Unloader relief valve were adjusted, but the working pressure of the system did not increase more than 12MPa even with discharge valve only crack opened. The relief valves of the pumps checked and found white particles blocking the orifice.   6. The hose connecting the hydraulic pump to the unloader of Pressure Control Valve was removed and noticed that oil was flowing out at low pressure.  7. As per office advice, one of the three Hydraulic Pumps (No3) from Fwd Hydraulic Power Pack was  dismantled, in order to interchange it with Aft Hydraulic Pump No1. But when both the pumps were removed it was noticed that the spider coupling pads between the Motor and Pump are in damaged condition.    8. The Pressure Control Valves with solenoid block was removed from Fwd station and fitted to Aft Hyd Power Pack as per Office advice.   9. The Check Valve (set at 150bar) of Aft Power Pack oil return line was dismantled and few white particles were found inside the block.  10. The By-pass valve of the hydraulic line was spanner shut.   11. The fitted pump (removed from Fwd Power Pack No3) for Aft Power Pack No1 tried out and found developing normal working pressure. Aft Power Pack No2 also tried out and found working satisfactory.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was no damage to the equipment
DirectCause: Malfunctioning of the unloader system and valves in the hydraulic system due to dirt accumulation in the Orifices and valve spool. As the unloader was not getting activated the oil discharge from the pump was getting recirculated to the tank thus the discharge pressure was not raising more than the pressure at stand by condition.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Please refer to proposed CA
Corrective Action: Incident to be shared with fleet vessels to re-emphasize on the importance of cleanliness of oil in the hydraulic system and avoiding usage of sealants with the gaskets
LessionLearnt: Pls refer to the attached IIR
ClosingNote: All identified CA closed, pls refer to subject IIR

IncidentDetails: On 27th November 2017, vessel whilst riding to her starboard anchor ( 7 shackles )  at MAAS Approach anchorage ( to Europort / Rotterdam) was observed to drag her anchor.  Vessel was unable to heave up anchor due to damaged claw coupling of the starboard windlass.  Temporary repairs were carried out on the claw coupling and anchor was heaved up.
IncidentEventDetails: On 27th Nov 2017, morning, at about 0615hrs, the vessel was observed to be dragging her anchor.  Duty officer immediately called up Master and further called for anchor stations. Main engine was always   on short notice and brought to stand by condition. Vessel called up VTIS and informed about dragging   of the anchor.  Vessel commenced heaving up anchor. During heaving up, the hydraulic oil started leaking, which resulted in loss of power to windlass motor. The loss of power to motor lead to the anchor chain being paid out very fast. The ship’s crew started tightening the brakes immediately, which had little effect because the momentum of the chain being paid out was very high. However, as the load on the chain eased off, the paying out of chain was stopped. Vessel informed VTIS again about her inability to heave up anchor.   Subsequent Actions:  Vessel was maintaining position by using her engines as and when required.  The hydraulic leak was identified. It was due to a leaking Ferrule of a coupling. The connector ferrule   was replaced and leak arrested.  Vessel tried to heave up the anchor after the repairs, but noticed a damage on the claw coupling.  Due to this damage, it was noticed, that the claw coupling did not have a proper traction to engage   and instead was slipping over the same.
ImmediateAction: This necessitated rectifying the unwanted clearance on the claw coupling by inserting plates by   Welding.     As per company’s procedures and in accordance with industries guidelines, hot work can only be   carried out if all the adjacent spaces within 30 mts radius are gas free.  This required purging of all NO 1 cargo tanks to reduce the hydrocarbon levels.  After successful hot work at location, the anchor was brought home successfully.   Vessel re-anchored using port anchor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1) Damage to the Claw Coupling of the Windlass.  2) Undue Strains Suffered by Windlass Might have Caused Undetected Wear & Tear on the Windlass Motor & its Gears.  3) Damages to the Foundation of the Windlass & Associated Mrg Winches on Stbd Side.  4) Wear & tear on the Brake Band Liner.  5) Delays Caused & Loss of Man Hours.
DirectCause: 01. Failure to warn during on set of heavy weather 02. Adverse sea/weather conditions
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Brake Band Linning of the stbd Windlass Need to be Renewed.  2) Stbd Windlass Motor to be Overhauled & Thoroughly Inspectedly.  3) Foundation of the Windlass & Fwd Stbd Mrg Winches need to be Thoroughly Inspected.  4) Fabrication / Modification of the Bow Stopper to be Done During Dry Docking.
Corrective Action: Incident to be shared with fleet vessels to re-emphasize on the importance of heavy weather precautions at anchor and shifting the vessel to sheltered location or safe drifting area
ClosingNote: All proposed CA closed, please refer to the attached IIR

IncidentDetails: Vessel “Nave Electron” on her Voyage from Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia to Kiire Japan, loaded with four parcel of crude oil. Vessel arrived & berthed at JX Terminal, Berth No. 4, Kiire on 11th Dec 2017 at 1000hrs LT for discharging part cargo.  Vessel first completed discharging parcel (part discharge) of Arab extra light from COT 1C, 3P/S & Slop(S). While discharging parcel of Arab light crude oil from COT 2P/S, 4C and Slop(P), drop in ullage of 3P COT containing Arab extra light was noted. Cargo of Arab extra light leaked out from 3P COT and got discharged along with Arab light crude oil.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel “Nave Electron” on her Voyage from Ras Tanura- Saudi Arabia to Kiire-Japan, loaded with four grade of crude oil. Vessel arrived & berthed at JX Terminal, Berth No. 4, Kiire on 11th Dec 2017 at 1000hrs LT. After completion of safety checks and in consultation with Berth master, Vessel commenced discharging part parcel of 1st grade of Arab extra light crude oil from 1C,3W & SLOP(S) at 1118hrs on 11th Dec 2017.  No. 1 & 2 COP & all three bottom lines were used.  Vessel completed discharging part parcel of Arabian extra light crude oil at 1406hrs on 11th Dec 2017.    Thereafter, vessel commenced discharging second grade of Arabian Light crude oil at 1418hrs on 11th Dec 2017 from COT 4C, 2W & SLOP(P). All three COP & all three bottom lines were being used. During discharging operation, at 2000hrs, noted ullage drop from 3P COT having Arab extra light crude oil. At this time discharging from 4C & Slop(S) was in progress. #2 bottom line was isolated by closing all bottom crossover and noted steady ullage of 3P. Adjacent 3P- WBT sounded and no oil traces noted. Vessel attempted to open and close both main & stripper valve (3P) (from remote & local control box). Issue was discussed with berth master & attending port captain. Berth master after discussing with terminal, allowed vessel to continue discharging 2nd Grade of Arab light crude oil followed by 3grade of Al Shaheen crude oil from COT 2C, 4W & 5W (Part parcel for Kiire). Terminal also requested to prepare ullage report for Arab extra light crude oil each time upon completion of each grade of crude oil at Kiire terminal.  At 0042hrs on 12th Dec 2017, Vessel completed discharging 2nd grade of Arab light crude oil.  12th Dec 2017 @ 0118- Vessel commenced discharging 3rd grade of Al Shaheen crude oil from COT 2C, 4W & 5W. All three COP & All three bottom lines were used. At around 0345Hrs, once 2C COT, dropped to about 8.5mtr Ullage, Noted COT 3P level started to drop again.   12th Dec 2017 @ 0400Hrs- Completed discharging 3rd grade of Al Shaheen crude oil.   12th Dec 2017 @ 0424: Commenced discharging Das crud 4th grade of Das crude oil from COT 1W, 3C & 5C. All three COP & All three bottom lines were used. Initially 3P level was holding but at around 0530Hrs, once 5C dropped to below 5m ullage, Noted gradual drop in 3P COT level.  12 Dec 2017, during last stage of stripping Das Crude from no.2 group, noted malfunction of valve CL132 connecting no.2 bottom line to eductor suction.  Tried operating from solenoid without success.   12th Dec 2017@ 1748 Hrs: Completed discharging Das Crude Oil and complete discharge for the port. As per calculations, 1996.6Bbbls of Arab extra light crude oil got discharged with other three grades of crude oil.    13th Dec 2017- During Voyage from Kiire to Sakai, valve CL132 was operated using hand pump and later on from remote control. Valve was operated several times and found working in satisfactory condition.    14th Dec 2017@ 1230hrs LT, Vessel made fast at A-1 Berth. After discussion with terminal and in order to avoid further contamination, Parcel of Arab extra light crude oil was decided to be discharge first.    14th Dec 2017@ 1500hrs; Commenced discharging Arab extra light crude from 3 P/S & 1C.    15th Dec 2017@ 0030hrs: Completed discharging Arab extra light crude oil and commenced discharging Al Shaheen crude oil from COT 2C, 4P/S, 5P/S.    15th Dec 2017@1648hrs: Completed discharging for the port.    During above operation, Valve CL 132 was used and operated several times without any problem.
ImmediateAction: Issue was discussed with berth master & attending port captain. Berth master after discussing with terminal, allowed vessel to continue discharging 2nd Grade of Arab light crude oil followed by 3grade of Al Shaheen crude oil from COT 2C, 4W & 5W (Part parcel for Kiire). Terminal also requested to prepare ullage report for Arab extra light crude oil each time upon completion of each grade of crude oil at Kiire terminal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo Valve seat ring found damaged. No claim yet made by terminal/receivers for cargo contamination.
DirectCause: Vessel has completed around TEN cargo operations after the last DD. Suspect the rope could have passed through the Cargo manifold at the load port
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Difficult to propose a CA for Terminal
ClosingNote: Seat ring for the 3 P COT valve replaced and line pressure tested, found satisfactory

IncidentDetails: Vsl at Pusan port, cargo completed, pilot boarded the vessel, while trying out the Main engine - Main Engine Starting Air line Drain accumulator Gasket busted.  Gasket renewed - but Main Engine Starting Air line Drain accumulator Gasket busted again.   Pilot informed to Port control on VHF Ch.10 to re-schedule the vessel.  Pilot Dis-embarked/tug casted off.  Repair completed / Main engine tried-out/found in working order.  Vessel delayed for 4hrs.
IncidentEventDetails: •0045LT/1545UTC :-Given one hour notice to E/R  •0115LT/1615UTC :- Half an hour notice given for B.T  •0125LT/1625UTC :- POB  •0130LT/1630UTC :- Both Steering motors tested and All bridge Equipment checked, All                  found satisfactory.   •0200LT/1700UTC :- Cargo operation completed and all stevedores away.  •0200LT/1700UTC :- Master / Pilot information exchanged.  •0206LT/1706UTC :- Aft Tug made fast (Tug No.9)  •0210LT/1710UTC :- E/R informed that Main Engine Starting Air line Drain accumulator                  Gasket busted.  •0215LT/1715UTC :- Pilot informed to Port control on VHF Ch.10 to re-schedule the vessel                  for traffic details.  •0222LT/1722UTC :- Ch.Engr informed that  Main Engine Starting Air line Drain accumulator                 Gasket busted again and it will take another 10 minutes. Same informed to pilot and                  requested pilot to wait for another ten minutes  •0240LT/1740UTC:- Master reported to Synergy [Marine supdt. Capt. Chetan Sharma], Incident  report to owners by Synergy.  •0245LT/1745UTC :- Pilot informed to prepare the pilot ladder on sea side(stbd) and to cast off the   tug.   •0255LT/1755UTC :-  Aft Tug (tug no.9) casted off and Pilot disembarked.  •0309LT/1809UTC:- Master updated the Owners / Charterers / Synergy / Local agent via e-mail.  •0315LT/1815UTC :- Reinforced Gasket of thicker size, cut to size and fitted in place. Starting air  line tried out and there after Engine blown through.  •0319LT/1819UTC :- Main Engine control on bridge.  •0320LT/1820UTC :- Main Engine tried out ahead and astern, all found satisfactory. Same informed  to Local agent via phone and local agent re-booked the pilot at 0600LT/2100UTC.  •0337LT/1837UTC:- Master updated the Owners / Charterers / Synergy / local agent via e-mail,  confirming that the vessel is ready for sailing in all respects.   •.0540LT/2040UTC:- Pilot on-board  •0542LT/2042UTC:-Main engine tried-out/found in working order.  •0548LT/2048UTC:-Stand-by engine, aft tug made fast.  •0554LT/2054UTC:-All casted-off / Vessel underway.  •0636LT/2136UTC:- Pilot away  •0700LT/2200UTC:-RFA, Vessel proceeding to Dalian as per schedule.  •0707LT/2207UTC:- Master updated the Owners / Charterers / Synergy / local agent via e-mail that  vsl departed safely from Pusan heading to Dalian.
ImmediateAction: Reinforced the Gasket of thicker size, cut to size and fitted in place. Starting air line tried out and there after Engine blown through and then engine tried out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Main Engine Starting Air line- Drain accumulator Gasket busted
DirectCause: Root Cause - insufficient  engine room watch keeping procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Spare gaskets to be kept stby near the defective flange.
ClosingNote: Proper engine watch-keeping procedures can avoid re occurrence of such incidents.

IncidentDetails: While conducting quarterly lowering of the lifeboats, the starboard lifeboat Aft block disengaged without activation of relase mechanism when the boat became waterborne. No personnel were inside the boat at the time of the incident. Subsequently, personnel manged to access the boat. Once they were inside the boat and making checlks on the release mechanism, the forward block also disengaged from the hook. The painter parted shortly afterwards.
IncidentEventDetails: Please see attached sheet with timeline.
ImmediateAction: C/O and 1 AB accessed the boat and managed to connect both the blocks and vessel was able to safely retrieve the boat.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The lifeboat suffered damage to the sprinkler rail, causing the rail to be bent out of shape. There are some scuffing marks on the fore and aft end of the boat, where the release blocks have made repeated contact with the boat.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:  Inadvertent release of link from the hook.  Indirect Cause:  1. Boat Made waterborne without personnel. It was not pre-planned to lower the boat upto water level and instantly lowered without RA.   2. No additional securing to fall hook. 3. Weather not suitable for lifeboat lowering.  Root cause: 1. Lack of planning. 2. Procedure not defined for not making the boat waterborne without personnel onboard
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Review Risk assessment and revise as required  Highlight possibility of fall release if boat is waterborne without personnel inside  If in doubt about weather conditions, abort the operation
Corrective Action: Review Risk assessment and revise as required Highlight possibility of fall release if boat is waterborne without personnel inside If in doubt about weather conditions, abort the operation
ClosingNote: We appreciate the reporting and efforts and cooperation of the ship staff in positively identifying the root cause for the incident.  The efforts of the Master in evidence collection and analysis of the evidences was commendable.

IncidentDetails: The vessel was loading Soyabean oil in San Lorenzo,voyage 14 in 6 cargo tanks. During topping off of 7S COT, it was observed that there was a slight leakage (drop by drop) from the aft fixed tank cleaning machine.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel was loading Soyabean oil in San Lorenzo,voyage 14 in 6 cargo tanks. During topping off of 7S COT, it was observed that there was a slight leakage (drop by drop) from the aft fixed tank cleaning machine.    The crew members on deck were extremely vigilent to notice it. On futher investigation by the chief officer it was observed that the tank cleaning machine hydrant was crack open which could not be noticed visually but by physically closing the valve.  All the tank cleaning machines were immediatley checked as the vessel was loading upto 96% in all her designated tanks.    The oil spilled on deck was less than a litre and was immediately cleaned up and secured without any traces to avoid any redundant attention by the surveyors and loading master.
ImmediateAction: All the tank cleaning machines were immediately checked as the vessel was loading upto 96% in all her designated tanks.  The oil spilled on deck was less than a litre and was immediately cleaned up and secured without any traces to avoid any redundant attention by the surveyors and loading master
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The oil spilled on deck was less than a litre and was immediately cleaned up and secured without any traces to avoid any redundant attention by the surveyors and loading master.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) All tank cleaning machines were immdeiately checked and confirm all physically shut. The entire deck area was monitored Sporadically for any such future occurrence.  2) I would highly recommend that " checking of the fixed tank cleaning machines prior loading" is added in the "Cargo transfer checklist OP CHEM 04" as it will assist the chief officers in the future to accentuate on that aspect.
Corrective Action: Training to ship staff to include the checking of the Tank Cleaning machine valve in the pre-cargo transfer checks done by Ship Staff
LessionLearnt: Importance of Pre-CArgo Transfer Checks Importance of Alert & VIgilant Watchkeeping Practices Importance of re-evaluating existing procedures to plug any inadequacies
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: DCR has been raised for changes to the OP-CHEM-04 form

IncidentDetails: At 1420 there was sudden heavy vibration and leakage of Ammonia from the manual manifold valve gasket, the outer hydraulic valve was immediately closed and the leak stopped.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had stopped cargo discharge at 1042 hrs on 21st as per terminal request and had closed the inner manual valve of the manifold as per the terminal request.    Pressures were being monitored at regular intervals, the maximum reported on the outer gauge was 3.8 bars.    At 1420 there was sudden heavy vibration and leakage of Ammonia from the manifold valve, the outer hydraulic valve was immediately closed and the leak stopped, all personnel moved out of the area, the area was washed with water to clear the same
ImmediateAction: The outer hydraulic valve was immediately closed and the leak stopped, all personnel moved out of the area, the area was washed with water to clear the same, inspected and repairs carried out. Full safety precautions were followed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Manifold valve outer side gasket was damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: N.A from vessel side. However, possibility to disconnect shore arm during long waiting to be considered.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Noted the cause analysis and the corrective action taken. Appreciate for reporting.  Root cause - Failure to follow industry best practice &  Noncompliance with procedures.  Direct Cause - Possibly Sudden pressure surge from the shore side.  Indirect cause - Lack of attention.  Lack of training on handling of the valves.  Lack of awareness on pressure surge.  Miscommunication.  Suspected weak gasket installed by the yard

IncidentDetails: Anchor Lashing wire almost parted due to Rough Sea.
IncidentEventDetails: 1600: Master Instructed to carry out Heavy Weather checks.  1610: Chief Officer and P/man Informed Bridge and Proceeded Forward.  1620: Lashing in Stbd Side Anchor Found to be almost Parted.  1630: Lashing Removed and renewed with new lashing wire.
ImmediateAction: Renewed the Lashing with New Wire
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lashing damaged, ( Almost Parted) Due to Heavy Weather
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Raised PR, Renewed damaged wire with spare wire.
Corrective Action: Renew Damaged wire with Spare Wire
LessionLearnt: Keep Spare for important consumables that are prone to wear & tear. Replace such consumables before failure if condition is suspected to be deteriorated
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Report closed after supply of new Wire for Lashing at Singapore

IncidentDetails: 4/E sustained a twitch in his nerve or muscle in the lower back just to hte right of his spine, resulting in inability to take weight on his right leg, due to severe pain, and sudden occasional shhoting pain down his right leg an sometimes up to his right shoulder.
IncidentEventDetails: While taking inventory in the Engine store, 4/E tried to move a box, while he was standing up, which was on a shelf in line with his chest. This resulted in the injury.
ImmediateAction: 4/E was taken to his cabin and advised to lie down. He was administered pain relief ointment on his lower back and a hot water bottle was administered on his lower back.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 4/E sustained a muscle or nerve twitch in his lower back, just to the right side of the spine. This occured as he was shifting some spares while taking monthly inventoies, and while he was in the standing position. He was unable to put any weight on his right leg (due to pain). He also experienced shooting pain down his right leg and sometimes this pain also travels up to his right shoulder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Training to be given for safely lifting the weights and manual handling prior joining.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper handling of weights
ClosingNote: Training to be given for heavy weight handling prior joining

IncidentDetails: Details of incident:    On 06th January 2018 at 1250 LT whilst the vessel was discharging  Anhydrous Ammonia at PEMEX North Pier at Toplolbampo, Mexico, Port  Side Alongside, there was a cargo leak in the midship section of the vessel  near the booster area,forward of the manifold. Emergency  procedures were immediately initiated, cargo discharge stopped, pumps  tripped, PEMEX terminal informed.    On investigation, it was found that the gasket on the manifold side of the  isolation valve on the 8-inch branch line connecting the manifold line of 12  inches, leading to the booster pump suction had been blown out.    Repair work on the isolation valve were carried out in consultation of the terminal  loading master. After the repairs, cargo pipe lines were pressure tested and  found no leaks and resumed discharging works.    Master was called upon for the investigations by Harbor Master. P & I club  was called upon, Master gave an interim guarantee letter supported by the  E-mail from P & I club, and vessel sailed without any delays.
IncidentEventDetails: 05 Jan 2018.    1136Lt: Vessel was made all fast at North Pier (port side alongside).  1230 Lt: One Manifold line connected (8-inch x 300 ANSI) on vessel’s port side  forward liquid line.  1700 Lt: Commence discharging with No. 2 Port cargo pump.  2145 Lt: Vessel at maximum manifold pressure of 6.5 bar as per Terminal  instruction.    06 Jan 2018.    0630 Lt: Vessel reduced manifold pressure to 3.0 bar as per terminal instruction.  0745 Lt: Commence discharging from No 1 Stbd cargo pump and stopped No. 2 port  pump.  0816 Lt: Vessel increased manifold pressure to 3.5 bar as per terminal instruction.  0830 Lt: Vessel increased manifold pressure to 4.5 bar as per terminal instruction.  0845 Lt: Vessel increased manifold pressure to 5.5 bar as per terminal instruction.  0905 Lt: Vessel reduced manifold pressure to 5.0 bar as per terminal instruction.  1248 Lt: Terminal instructed to reduce manifold pressure to 4.8 bar, second officer  closed the cargo pump discharge valve by 3% to reduce the manifold pressure and arrived   at 4.8 bars.  Ordinary seaman was at the Port Manifold monitoring the manifold pressure through  pressure gauge and same was being reported to second officer.  1249 Lt: Ordinary seaman reported that there was a slight hammering noise at the  manifold area, but soon settled down to normal. Soon thereafter the duty OS reported  that pressure has increased to 5.2 Bar, the 2nd officer on watch started to adjust the  cargo pump valve settings to reduce the same to near around 5.0 bars. Almost  immediately thereafter duty OS Mr. Ashwani reported heavy hammering in the  manifold area and deck cargo lines,and ice formed on the lines due to cold cargo  discharge, falling down.  1250 - 1300 Lt: Ordinary seaman reported cargo leak in the midship section of the  ship near the booster area, starboard side forward of the manifold.
ImmediateAction: The following were the sequence of action taken immediately on noticing the leak.    1250 - 1300 Lt: Ordinary seaman reported cargo leak in the midship section of the  ship near the booster area, starboard side forward of the manifold. Leaking ammonia  immediately formed a vapor cloud as moisture from the air condenses on the  extremely cold vapors, this was being blown towards the aft end of the ship due to the  prevailing winds.     Deck spray pump started and Emergency procedures were immediately  initiated.    Cargo discharge stopped, pumps tripped, PEMEX terminal informed of the  emergency and actions.    Vessel started to drain the cargo line system and depressurize the  same.     At the same time water spray was started on board and within a few minutes  by the terminal water spray system to contain the vapor cloud and form a water  curtain around the leaking vapors.    At this time the source of the leak was not clearly  known.     In about 10 minutes the leak had subsided substantially.    1300 Lt: Loading Master on board. On investigating it was found that the gasket on  the manifold side of the isolation valve on the 8-inch branch line connecting the  manifold line of 12 inches, leading to the booster pump suction had been blown out.  Repair plan was discussed with the Loading Master.    1304 Lt: Loading Master Away.    1305 Lt: Leak is contained to minimal level and started repair work on the valve  gasket by ship staff.    1415 Lt: Damaged valve gasket renewed.    1418 – 1442 Lt: Cargo pipe line pressure tested to 7 bar by ship staff, No Leaks  observed.    1448 Lt: Ship staff informed Terminal that vessel is ready to resume discharging  operations.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gasket on Cargo liquid line leading to booster suction valve blown out.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: AA)  Ship’s cargo lines to be pressure tested to confirm integrity of all gaskets  onboard prior arrival at all times.  PIC: Master Closed (14th Jan 2018)    BB) Ship Staff to be trained on phenomenon of pressure surge.  PIC: Master Closed (14th Jan 2018)    CC) All vessels in the fleet will be alerted about this incident and a detailed  instruction to avoid surge pressures shall be shared.  PIC: Marine Superintendent Closed (14th Jan 2018)    DD) Ship staff to identify the vulnerable areas, in consultation with office and  replace the gasket with difference material and better quality.  PIC: Technical Superintendent / Master Target (15th Feb 2018)
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Vessel renewed the gaskets as per attached list from sigra flex  to meta flex.   All gaskets on liquid line and condensate line have been changed to meta flex..   After completion of all gasket renewal, the liquid and condensate lines have been pressure tested to 9.5 bar pressure with a holding  time of more than 3 hours. All the places where gasket are renewed have been checked closely for leakage and none found.  Also see attached investigation report.  This incident was also shared with the fleet vessels

IncidentDetails: Pilferage of fire hosw Couplings - 5 no.,Fire hose Nozzle - 7 pcs, Fire Hydrant Caps - 1 pc
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed at port cape town on 15-Jan-2018 at 0400LT, Port Side Alongside.  · All Accesses to cargo holds and Accommodation were restricted and verified on arrival at berth.  · Cargo operation commenced at 15.01.2018/ 0654LT.      · Fire/ safety and security  rounds were taken on deck  and accommodation at regular intervals  · Gangway was manned at all times   · At 2000 Hrs while the vessel was working cargo on  Bay 52 (loading Reefers Under deck) and 32 (discharging reefers), one of  the stevedore removed the fire hoses coupling, caps  and nozzles from the  of the vessel (V/l was loading 1100 plus reefers in the port). During this time 3 crew were on deck, one on bay 32 disconnecting reefers, another on bay 52 connecting reefers and one at the gangway   · At around 2345hrs Lt /15.01.2018 the port police boarded the vessel and informed that one of the stevedore was caught ashore with the below said items   · Instantly all fire hose box on deck  and accommodation were searched and found  the following  missing          1. From Bay 1 (P) one nozzle and 1 set coupling         2. From Bay 1 (S) one nozzle and 1 set coupling         3. From bay 4 (P) one nozzle and 1 set coupling         4. From bay 4 (S) one nozzle and 1 set coupling         5. From bay 16 (s) one nozzle and 1 set coupling and cap         6.  from bay 24 (S) one nozzle          7. from bay 32 (S) one nozzle    Couplings - 5 sets   Nozzle - 7 pcs  Caps - 1 pcs    · It was Understood from the port police that the stevedore has managed to through the couplings and other items ashore  one by one when  the ship’s crew was busy with loading and discharging reefers. The Gangway Crew vision was obscured by the Position of the gantry.  · The port police opened a case for theft  through the agent and handed over the pilfered items to the ship through the agent.
ImmediateAction: All LSA/FFA Equipments and other store rooms  re-checked, Briefed all officers and crew,  Deck/accom. rounds fortified
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Theft of following items fro Deck fire hose boxes,     Fire Hose Couplings - 5 sets   Fire hose box Nozzle - 7 pcs  Fire hydrant Caps - 1 pcs    ·
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting to be conducted prior arrival African shores, Deck rounds to be fortified, If crew busy with reefers , additional hand for deck round will be considered.Contnuous patrol to maintain on deck/accomodation.
Corrective Action: Meeting to be conducted prior arrival African shores, Deck rounds to be fortified, If crew busy with reefers , additional hand for deck round will be considered.Contnuous patrol to maintain on deck/accomodation.
LessionLearnt: Ports with possibility of pilferage to be recognized and security measures to be enhanced accordingly.
ReviewComments: LSA not to be kept under lock due to security risk.
ClosingNote: Extra hands to be used for security sensitive areas as required.

IncidentDetails: During maneuvering, HFO got mxed with LSMGO, contaminating the DO service tank
IncidentEventDetails: At notice, oiler started and changed over the additional generator. During this process, the DO return valve was not changed over, allowing the fuel to go back to DO service tank instead of HFO service tank. By the time it was noticed, already one mt of HFO mixed with LSMGO in DO service tank
ImmediateAction: Line changed over to normal. DO service tank drained and cleaned. Filed with fresh LSMGO. All generators and bolier run in LSMGO for some time to flush out the residual contaminated fuel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: About 15 m3 of LSMGO contaminated with HFO
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Procedure for changing over the generators from LSMGO to HFO and HFO to LSMGO is made and pasted near all generators.  The valves required to be operated for changing over are identified and labelled to avoid any future confusion.  All crew in Engine room instructed that from now on, Starting, stopping and changing over of the generators will be done by Watch keeping engineers only.
Corrective Action: Fuel oil Change over should be carried out by Duty Engineer and not by the Oiler. Clear procedure to follow at the time of Change over and Precautions to be put in place to be identified, put in place and strictly implemented. Training to be carried out to all Engine Crew.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Crew Training carried out.

IncidentDetails: DUE TO BAD WEATHER LOWER PORT QUARTER SIDE TOUCHED THE UNFENDER BERTHED. 6 MOORING LINES PARTED NOT ON THE SAME TIME DUE TO DURGING AND YAWING.
IncidentEventDetails: a) DETERIORATING WEATHER  b) SURGING  c) YAWING  d) BERTHED NO GOOD FENDERS
ImmediateAction: RIGGED ADDITIONAL 2 MOORING LINES FORWARD AND 2 MOORING LINES IN THE AFT. VESSEL PUT SHIP'S  FENDER ON BERTH HAVING NO FENDERS TO AVOID DAMAGE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Vessel did not check the condition of fenders on arrival or brought to the notice of the Port authorities , poor fendering at berth.

IncidentDetails: VESSEL WAS APPROCHING MUMBAI THROUGH SAFETY FAIRWAY. TWO GENERATORS WERE RUNNNG AND SUDDENLY BOTH OF THEM TRIPPED DUE TO LOW LUB OIL PRESSURE RESULTING IN BLACK OUT.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL WAS APPROCHING MUMBAI THROUGH SAFETY FAIRWAY. TWO GENERATORS WERE RUNNNG AND SUDDENLY BOTH OF THEM TRIPPED DUE TO LOW LUB OIL PRESSURE RESULTING IN BLACK OUT.
ImmediateAction: EMERGENCY GENERATOR CAME ON LOAD AND ONE GENERATOR WAS STARTED WITH OTHER NON-ESSENTIAL SERVICES OFF.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LOSS OF POWER AT SEA DUE TO BOTH GENERATORS TRIPPING ON LOW LUB OIL PRESSURE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CAUSE OF LOW LUB OIL PRESSURE TO BE INVESTIGATED AND RECTIFIED AS EARLY AS POSSIBLE.
