IncidentDetails: During discharging at Qinzhou China, vessel has used COP no.1 and COP no.2 for STS operation as Max. Discharge rate was 5500 m3/ hr and Pressure required 7.0 Bar. In last stage of Discharge,   vessel  opened Valve VCS107 connected to COP`s  drain , Stripping pump and Eductor suction  . Small amount of Oil was sight from the upper Bearing of COP no.3 .
IncidentEventDetails: During discharging at Qinzhou China, vessel has used COP no.1 and COP no.2 for STS operation as Max. Discharge rate was 5500 m3/ hr and Pressure required 7.0 Bar. In last stage of Discharge,   vessel  opened Valve VCS107 connected to COP`s  drain , Stripping pump and Eductor suction  . Small amount of Oil sight from the upper Bearing of COP no.3
ImmediateAction: Same was shown to master and  Head of Eng Department . Vessel cleaned and Done Pressure testing of All  3 COP on 15 - Jan-2018. ( Evidence is attached prior Daesan,Korea ) No leakage was observed . During Discharging at Daesan, Korea , Vessel has Discharged at 10.5 KG/CM2 using All three COP`S , No leakage was Observed.Slight leakage from COP No.2 & 3 has  already been  reported in Chief Officer Audit and Handing over  from last Chief officer Report in November 2017. COP`s are  under observation .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damages noticed. Slight leakage observed was arrested on the spot.
DirectCause: Not following procedures
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Valve line up to be double checked for all operation.Duty officer to verify the line up along with chief officer.Valve have to be physically checked in the location prior starting of any pump or machinery.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA

IncidentDetails: During Mooring operation, while throwing heaving line ashore the personal gas monitor worn by one of the crew fell into sea.
IncidentEventDetails: - During Pre-arrival (Pre cargo transfer) meeting, It was brifed to the crew that the Cargo onboard contains high H2S and all the precautions have to be taken for high H2S cargo as per our company's SMS including keeping a personal gas monitor while keeping a Cargo Watch.    - One of the crew member at aft station assumed that the Gas meter has to be carried even during mooring operation and took the gas meter while going for mooring stations.    - While throwing the heaving line ashore, the gas meter kept in upper pocket of the crew member fell into the sea.
ImmediateAction: The I/C at aft station informed about the occurance. It was confirmed further that, none of the other crew members are carrying any personal Gas meters during the mooring stations.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Equipment loss, H2S meter fell into the water
DirectCause: Hazard of losing the equipment was not identified
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be briefed regarding purpose of H2S meter, during mooring stations only the OOW and the winch operator should keep the personal meters and crew members directly handling mooring ropes will not keep the meters, if any alar is noted the OOW and the winch operator will alert the subordinates in the mooring station.
Corrective Action: On board attending Marine Manager /  DPA conducted a briefing to all the crew members to lock /secure the meter with the locking arrangement provided to the boiler suits, when vessel is carrying high H2S cargoes,  the crew member engaged in throwing the heaving line should keep the meter away during the time mooring.
LessionLearnt: The master and the attending DPA briefed all the crew regarding proper care of personal gas meters, during the SCMM. The following guidelines were established; Mooring area shall be confirmed gas free prior mooring station.  Apart from this the OOW  and the person operating the winch shall carry personal gas meters during the mooring stations. persons handling mooring ropes shall not carry the gas meters
ReviewComments: Closed satisfactory
ClosingNote: The lessons from these incident were discussed in the onboard SCMM conducted on the 31st Jan 2018

IncidentDetails: Hyd oil flown into no.4 hold from access bobby hatch due to rupture of hyd. oil pipe in upper stool space in way of entrance to the hold aft section.
IncidentEventDetails: On 31st Jan/PM with one team, vessel used the petcoke remover followed by SW wash to check effectiveness on the aft bulkhead of no.4 hold and found to be effective in removal of stains. On 1st Feb/AM while opening the holds for completing sweeping, found low level alarm being shown at Hyd p/rm. On investigation and checking all lines and traces, found one of the lines ruptured in upper stool space area in way of aft entrance to the hold, which had also flown into hold no.4 on to the collected water at the bottom aft section. The amount of leaked oil(about 1000 ltrs) and mixture of oil/water estimated by entering the hold by mgt team, found to be about 4000ltrs.
ImmediateAction: Hyd. lines isolated and outflow of oil from ruptured pipe stopped.Vessel arranged all hands required for attending to same and complete renewal of the section of line, transfer of the oil/water mixture to the void spaces(under cranes no.4 and no.3) since not enough drums to collect and keep on board safely.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1- ruptured hyd pipe.  2- Loss of hyd oil.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: renewal of pipe lines
Corrective Action: renewal of pipe lines
ClosingNote: Regular checks to be done for hydraulic pipes.

IncidentDetails: Service boat approached vessel for personnel transfer in choppy sea condition damaging ship's pilot ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: 1820 hrs - Boat attempted to come A/S but failed due to strong swell.  1855 hrs - Several attempts made by the boat, but in vain.  1856 hrs - Boat stern banged against pilot ladder, breaking the spreader.
ImmediateAction: Boat advised to control speed in order to avoid more damage and for personnel safety.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: spreader damaged
Proposedcorrectiveactions: refuse such approach if weather is not calm
Corrective Action: refuse such approach if weather is not calm
ClosingNote: Ladder to be heaved up and only lowered once boat along side.

IncidentDetails: Oil spill pollution
IncidentEventDetails: At around 2045 hours on 26th January 2018 duty oiler found fuel oil is overflowing at starboard side fuel oil airvent. Immediately notified the Bridge and the duty engineer. Bridge duty officer immediately made broadcast thru PA system announcing "OIL POLLUTION" at starboard side accommodation main deck. Crew rushed to the scene and immediately carried out spill containment and collecting spilled oil on deck, scuppers in placed. At 2145 hours completed the cleaning operation.
ImmediateAction: Transferred fuel from fuel oil overflow tank to fuel oil settling tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: N/a
Proposedcorrectiveactions: De-briefing watchkeepers on the proper handing over of watch.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: Steam line for FOT's, inside void space, found leaking.  To carry out further investigation/repairs, while blowing through the steam line, due to back pressure some hot water spilled and fall over Junior Eng, who was one of the team members working next to the line.    Minor burns noted on Junior Eng. neck [back side/right portion] and back of his right hand.
IncidentEventDetails: 11:00LT Steam line for FOT's, inside void space, found leaking.  11:30LT To carry out further investigation/repairs, while blowing through the steam line, due to back pressure some hot water spilled and fall over Junior Eng, who was one of the team members working next to the line.  Minor burns noted on Junior Eng. neck [back side/right portion] and back of his right hand.
ImmediateAction: Immediately cold water shower and first aid given.  Initially skin was red, but later on skin color became normal, but one small blister formed on back of his neck.  Ointment [Burn & scald cream] applied and Ibuprofen 400mg given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor burns noted on Junior Eng. neck [back side/right portion] and back of his right hand.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: In addition to the proposed corrective action,  tool box meeting to include all risks for any tasks. same to be included in the RA
LessionLearnt: Importance of Awareness of all risks prior commencement of tasks
ClosingNote: Effective tool box meeting and RA are paramount to increase situational awareness to each of the team member. The effectiveness of the tool box meeting is to be regularly reviewed by the master to ensure that all risks are covered and the same is effectively communicated

IncidentDetails: Vessel  berthed  at VOPAK -B   which is exposed to the severe surging conditions due to its location being  in the open sea.Vessel mooring ropes used to keep the vessel alongside are subjected to heavy swell and excessive surging condition. From the time of all fast to cast off 10 of the mooring ropes have parted/damaged beyond repairs. Other ropes are also showing visible signs of damages.MOT /ships railing/wharf ladder too suffered damages.
ImmediateAction: Even after taking all measures to avoid rope/property damages, when situation could not be controlled by ship staff,Vessel decided to cease cargo,disconnect hose and shift from berth immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel berthed  at VOPAK -B,Puerto cabello ,Venezuela   which is exposed to the severe surging conditions due to its location being  in the open sea.                                                                                  Vessel mooring ropes used to keep the vessel alongside are subjected to heavy swell and excessive surging condition. From the time of all fast to cast off 10 of the mooring ropes have parted/damaged beyond repairs. Other ropes are also showing visible signs of damages.  MOT/Wharf ladder /Ships railing sustained substantial damages.   No crew injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Avoid berthing in open sea terminal during period of heavy swell.  2.Request tug assistance.  3.Only good condition moorings to be used.  4.Cease cargo ops. and cast off incase of situation go beyond control.
Corrective Action: 1.Avoid berthing in open sea terminal during period of heavy swell. 2.Request tug assistance. 3.Only good condition moorings to be used. 4.Cease cargo ops. and cast off incase of situation go beyond control.
LessionLearnt: Importance of VIgilance. Bold Action by Master. Support and encouragement from Office to Master's which encourage and motivate to always take action in the best interest of vessel safety, irrespective of commercials pressures from charterers etc.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: The vessel informed all Parties. DID NOT START ANY CARGO OPERATIONS - OR - EVEN CONNECT CARGO HOSES. When the ropes got damaged, vessel UNBERTHED and all parties were informed. MAnagers concurred with the master on the severityt of the conditions and liaised with the charterers to find a better solution. At the end, at next port, New Ropes were procured from the charterers for the vessel.

IncidentDetails: Damage to the port side life boat due to dynamic/ sudden jerks caused by momentarily freezing of Aft davit during routine lowering.
IncidentEventDetails: Routine lowering of Port Side Life Boat to Embarkation deck was being carried out for testing purpose as part of external audit at Vietnam 07th / Feb/ 2018.     Crew mustered at boat staion and prepared lifeboat for lowering as per respective duties on command of officer in charge.      On releasing the brake, the aft davit was momentarily frozen in place and did not start  lowering at the same time as forward davit. When the aft davit got free from its stowed position, it caused the aft end of the boat to lower with a very heavy jerk. Additionally, this caused the wire on the aft davit drum to slip further and finally the aft of boat was noted to be lower than the forward side.      Due to the heavy jerk caused by uneven lowering of the davits, the boat sustained following damage:      1.  Crack noted ( from inside and outside) the body of hook securing area of the boat.  2.  Crack noted on the port quarter body  ( outboard side ) of the boat. These is no sign of impact here so it may have been caused due to stress during the jerk.  3.  Very slight indent on the outer circular frame of rudder.
ImmediateAction: Immidiatedly brake applied and boat drill stopped.   Tool box meeting carried out and additional risk identified for change in operating conditions.   The weight of davit and boat was taken off the Life boat wire by use of chain blocks and wire and the L/B wires were adjusted again. The boat was stowed back and secured in place.  Thorough checks carried on the 07th and 08th AM again in daylight hours for damages to the boat and office informed of the damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.  Crack noted ( from inside and outside) the body of hook securing area of the boat.  2.  Crack noted on the port quarter body  ( outboard side ) of the boat. These is no sign of impact here so it may have been caused due to stress during the jerk.  3.  Very slight indent on the outer circular frame of rudder.
DirectCause: 1.  The aft davit was frozen momentarily. Suspect it was due to the last routine maintenance paintwork carried out.  2.  The risk of one arm of the davit not moving was not properly identified and discussed in the tool box meeting.   3.  Initial lowering was very fast causing a large difference in the two davit angles before the operation was stopped.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Risk of one davit arm being stuck added to Risk Assessment and to be discussed in all tool box meetings.    2. Incident discussed and importance of co-ordination between the boat team and safety checks at each stage of operation discussed.   3. Shore technician will attend to survey the boat and propose repair plan.
Corrective Action: Fleet Alert sent to all vessels describing the incident and highlighting following concerns:a)importance of adequate monitoring / supervision, by Master, and Chief Officer when Lifeboat Drill are being carried out.  b)Ensuring Lifeboat Launching Procedures are followed as per Makers recommendation, and Duties in Muster List Should be inline with the Launching Procedures. Same to be verified onboard all the Vessels. To designate person specifically for Manually unwinding the Winch and taking the Load on the Falls, before lowering the Boat from designated positionc)To refer the sample RAs sent by company while doing risk assessment of a particular task and make same specific to Vessel. The control measures should be specific and not be very wide such as refer the manual/ publication. All person involved in the operation to be included in RA.d)To adopt toolbox meeting where work plan and RAs are discussed seriously.  Safety tools must be assimilated as integral part of work routine & procedure. e)After every maintenance on movable parts in safety equipments, it is advised that the function test should be done under the direct supervision of senior shipboard officer such as Chief Officer. In the case of Lifeboat, it may be incorporated along with weekly test of moving of lifeboat from its stowage position.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Investigation report attached for reference
ReviewComments: Investigation report attached for reference
ClosingNote: Shared with CMS team and Training team inorder to prevent recurrence

IncidentDetails: Minor back flash during incinerator in operation from rotary cup burner.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Incinerator started after carrying out burner routines at 2030hrs from no#2 waste oil tank.  2) Furnace temperature was maintained within limits by adjusting feed.  3) Rounds taken at regular interval during sea watches.  4) Minor back flash  from rotary cup burner observed.
ImmediateAction: Igniter and Main burner switch was put off from incinerator panel . Back flash stopped soon after the switch was put off.  For safety precaution following carried out,  DO v/v closed , waste oil feed valve was shut,  waste oil tank outlet v/v and return valve from incinerator was closed.  Used DCP as further safety measure.  Chief engineer and second engineer was informed.    There was light flickker indication at bridge fire panel.Before fire alarm  activated flash was stopped.  After the furnace temperature came down, inspected the furnace and found all satisfactory. burner rouines carried out and started again. all parameters were found with inlimits.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage to the machinery and no injuries.
DirectCause: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery and lack of standards
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To increase the burner cleanig interval.
LessionLearnt: proper maintenance of Incinerator burners to be done,Oil temperature to be maintained more than 110degc for good atomisation,continuous monitoring to be done while Incinerator is in use.
ClosingNote: proper maintenance of Incinerator burners to be done,Oil temperature to be maintained more than 110degc for good atomisation,continuous monitoring to be done while Incinerator is in use..

IncidentDetails: While approaching eastern Holding anchorage "A", Singapore, vessel came in contact with the buoy ACW1 ( Mano East).
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel picked up pilot at PEBG "C" on 02/0800 Hrs. After master pilot info Exchange, vessel was proceeding towards Easter Holding anchorage "A". In order to align vessel`s heading , vessel had to execute 180 Deg stbd turn. While vessel was turning and positioning as per planned anchor position, Vessel came in close contact with the buoy at Break of accomodation. The buoy went underneath the hull and later resurfaced.   The geographical position of the buoy moved. MPA was informed immediately and vessel safely anchored.
ImmediateAction: After buoy came in contact with the ship side, engines were stopped and wheel was turned hard to port. MPA was notified immediately and vessel was manouvered safely & anchored in Eastern Holding anchorage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Diving team was called for underwater inspection of the hull and propeller. Slight damage was observed on the edges of propeller blade.   Next day(03rd Feb 2018), diving team was employed for necessary repairs in presence of class surveyor. Repairs were carried out and completed as to the satisfaction of attending class surveyor.
DirectCause: The uncontrolled maneuver combined with the effect of current and wind allowed the vessel to make contact with the buoy. The effect of south westerly tidal current and Easterly winds was not fully taken into consideration.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Please refer attached
Corrective Action: Fleet alert on the incident to be sent to all vessels in the fleet.
ClosingNote: Identified CA closed aptly

IncidentDetails: Vessel transitting up river Mississippi with Pilot on board. Radar X-Band suddenly lose video on the monitor.
ImmediateAction: Invistigate the fault with ships crew but without progress. Vessel seek assisstance from Technical Dept. through SI unfortunately intsructions given on board were not succesful.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Radar X-Band out of order.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Technical assisstance with office and maker.
ClosingNote: Spares supplied and Radar operational

IncidentDetails: While departure un mooring stations, One of the crew member fell down on wet deck during the operations. he got an ankle sprain on his left foot.
IncidentEventDetails: 0155: Vessel asked to make fast tug by pilot from Forecastle deck, as the tug line is normally make fast on abreast of port side Bay 18. Crew were standby at bay 18 with messenger ropes. Creq and Officer proceeded to forecastle deck to make fast tug. It took some extra minutes to lower the line to tug from the spare winch drum.  0200: There was a pressure from the pilot to do it fast, Tr OS was hearing the communication and he started doing it fast with Bosun. During the restricted time frame Tr OS fell down on deck as he was in a hurry to lower the line. His left foot ankle got twisted and sprained with minor swelling.
ImmediateAction: Taken him to hospital and given first aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: It was a left foot ankle sprain, Plastered with crepe bandage and applied pain relief cream. Given rest for 1 week to get a complete recovery.
DirectCause: Trying to complete task in a hurry. Inadequate supervision and effective tool box meeting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: On site tool box meeting required just before stations by Duty officers whoever is the incharge.
Corrective Action: To discuss with all Ship Staff regarding this incident in compliance with COSWP to increase awareness and various dangers involved in Mooring Station.
LessionLearnt: Effective Supervision and Effective tool box meeting
ReviewComments: Appreciate Ship Staff reporting. First Aid Case.
ClosingNote: Concerned Crew member completely recovered after providing first aid treatment.

IncidentDetails: While carrying out weekly routine inspections and testing of Free Fall Boat. Battery selector was tested from both battery. Battery selector was broken while turning it.
ImmediateAction: Repair by bonding and PO at Shi Palm.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To supply spares on board for an any event
Corrective Action: During routine weekly inspections Lifeboat and rescue boat batteries and chargers should also be tested and checked without fail
ClosingNote: Batteries will be supplied upon berthing

IncidentDetails: During routine maintenance with the lifeboat falls. Wire rope ( hoisting wire ) came out from the storage drum while lowering and caught in between the wire drum and the davit resulting in damage of the hoisting wire rope.
IncidentEventDetails: 1100 LT - Third mate was briefed Master to perform routine maintenance hoisting wire preparation for lowering Free Fall L/B at achorage (Majishan).   1200LT - Risk Assessment discussed and reviewd. (Date: 9th Feb 18)   1330 LT - Third mate together with one AB prepared all neccessary tools for maintenance, precautionary measure as per RA are in place and commence the task.  1340LT - Third mate position on main deck to operate the remote control of life boat falls and one AB position on davit platform upper part guiding to lower the hoisting wire.  1355LT - AB noticed that the hoisting wire came out storage drum and caught in between davit post and damaged the hoisting wire.
ImmediateAction: Removed hoisting wire rope caught in between storage drum and davit post.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hoisting Wire of Free Fall Lifeboat was bended/kind and cut.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Master / COff must verify the task and ensure that RA are in place. Additional resources in perfroming the task. Ensure that proper positioning of the person involved in the task that all moving parts are monitor and observe.
ClosingNote: Senior Officer to monitor similar tasks to avoid such incidents

IncidentDetails: While Using Davit motor for renewal of Wire for Port side life boat, Low insulation alarm sounded and Motor tripped.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Port side lifeboat Wire was being renewed.  2. Davit motor was used for wraping the wire to the drum.  3. while using  motor, insulation alarm sounded in Engine room.   4. Duty enggineer observed low insualtion was fluctuating intermittently.  5. Motor finally got tripped
ImmediateAction: Motor was isolated and taken out of service.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Motor found water Ingress and insulation found to be showing zero. Motor cannot be used further without repair.
DirectCause: Defective motor was dismantled, thoroughly inspected including shaft bearings. The water has entered into the motor junction box thru gland packing and then into the stator winding. The cable gland was defective  as the cable was loose in the gland connection. Immediately the stbd side cable gland was checked for its integrity.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Spare Motor to procur and to renew davit motor
Corrective Action: Spare Motor to procur and to renew davit motor
LessionLearnt: After dismantling the motor, suspect the water has ingressed thru cable gland. The new motor was fitted, cable gland well checked, tightened and put in use. Ship staff have checked the gland tightness of other life boat motor as well.
ReviewComments: Motor was last overhauled in Nov-2015.
ClosingNote: spare motor was immediately procured locally wit same specs, supplied and made operational.

IncidentDetails: Brake failure on Port Side Gangway
IncidentEventDetails: At 0345 hrs, after unmooring stations Dep. Salalah 3/Off from aft station reported that the Port side gangway is lowering uncontrolled automatically. Gangway stopped at about 5 mtrs from waterline due to the tripping line which was made fast on the bitts. Weight was taken on the tripping line and the gangway wire continued to slacken. Immediately after departure station Port side gangway attended to. Additional line was passed on the gangway and the load was taken using Capstan. Gangway wire which got slackened all the way was then cleared and put back on the drum. Wire was then heaved slowly until the weight of gangway was in the wire. Simultaneously the tripping line and additional line also kept taught to prevent lowering of gangway. Gangway was then heaved up all the way and secured with additional lashings. On further investigation found gear assembly and engaging clutch worn out completely.
ImmediateAction: Port side gangway heaved up completely and secured with additional lashings
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Gear assembly and Engaging clutch worn out completely
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Inspect the gear assembly periodically
ClosingNote: Interpinion  was renewed after  recieving spare complete winch from sister vessel.  One no. interpinion shaft also fabricated in shore workshop and same kept as emergency spare on board.

IncidentDetails: At 1235 LT, No4 crane hoisting wire parted while loading the cargo. Th grab fallen down on the jetty and damaged badly. No casualty and injury.
IncidentEventDetails: Property loss  Life risk
ImmediateAction: Cleared the area and stared crane wire renewal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Grab damaged very badly
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renewed
ClosingNote: Running hour log to be maintained for crane wires. Regular inspections to be done prior, during and after cargo ops. Adequate spare wire to be maintained.

IncidentDetails: While carrying out drills, Port side lifeboat was Moved out from Stowed Position, and stowed back in position. While Tightening the gripes, Lifeboat Fall wire parted. Since gripes and Harbor pins  were already in position, Lifeboat was secured and hence nothing futher happened.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Abondship alarm sounded to carry out lifeboat drills  2.Both lifeboat moved out from the stowed position and secured back in position.  3.While tightening the gripes,the port lifeboat fall parted.
ImmediateAction: The gripes were tightened and the davit was taken extra lashings.Harbour safety pins were reconfirmed to be secures in place.Extra lashings were takne on the lifebaot
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While carrying out drills today, Port side lifeboat was Moved from Stowed Position.Life boats were moved only 1 feet from their stowed position. Thereafter they were heaved up using manual handle. Once life boat was in position, both "harbour safety pins" were put in place. After this stage, when gripes were being made fast, aft fall wire of port life boat parted. At no stage falls were over tightened. They were tightend as normally they are, in order to place harbour safety pins in their designated slots.   Since gripes and Harbor pins  were already in position, Lifeboat was secured and hence nothing further happened..
DirectCause: Improper greasing and  irregular maintenance of L/Boat wires, Wire found lacking lubrication at the inner core level, while the top lyres were greased satisfactorily.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: For better lubrication results, wires are to be greased at more frequent interval and while greasing it would be ensured to remove old grease from the surface to help minimize dirt and water from sticking to the grease. As a best practice, to ensure that wires are stored in well lubricated condition and routine inspection schedules for the same should be prepared so that they are well lubricated during storing period as well.
Corrective Action: For better lubrication results, wires are to be greased at more frequent interval and while greasing it would be ensured to remove old grease from the surface to help minimize dirt and water from sticking to the grease. As a best practice, to ensure that wires are stored in well lubricated condition and routine inspection schedules for the same should be prepared so that they are well lubricated during storing period as well.
ClosingNote: Job Closed 22/03/18

IncidentDetails: One of the battery of Emergency generator exploded during routine testing of emergency generator.
IncidentEventDetails: During weekly safety routine checks,Emergency generator engine inspected and starting procedure followed, Once start button pressed in local start panel,Battery got exploded with heavy noise. Immediately crew evacuated the place and informed duty engineer and chief engineer. No personnel got hurt in the incident.
ImmediateAction: Immediately isolated the battery charger panel and immediately made mechanical ventillation with portable blowers to remove acid fumes from the space. Once space ventillated,disconnected battery terminals and checked for alternator insulation,battery charger output.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Battery damaged permenantly and cannot be used futher. No further damage sited
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Battery testing and visual examination to be carried out  on routine basis.History of the battery need to be recored near the battery and on the records for easy reference in Battery maintenance log.
ClosingNote: Battery to be carefully inspected every week during routine inspection. New batteries replaced.

IncidentDetails: PORT ANCHOR SLIPPED OFF FROM THE BITTER END LINK. VESSEL LOST HER PORT ANCHOR IN SUEZ CANAL.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL HAD STOPPED IN SUEZ CANAL DUE TO VESSEL AHEAD IN CONVOY WAS REPORTED TO HAVE RUN AGROUND, ONCE VESSELS AHEAD WERE CLEARED, OWN VESSEL STARTED TO CLEAR OFF. HOWEVER DUE TO TIME CONSTRAIN, PILOT AND THE PORT PRESSURISED THE VESSEL AND WAS NOT ALLOWED TO HEAVE UP HER PORT ANCHOR. THE PORT CONTROL AND PILOT  HELD THE VESSEL RESPONSIBLE FOR OBSTRUCTING THE CONVOY AND EVEN WITH THE DANGERS OF THE VESSEL RUNNING AGROUND. SUEZ PORT AND ONBOARD PILOT,  PRESSURIZED AND THREATENED MASTER WITH DIRE CONSEQUENCES AND CRIMINAL ACTION IF THE PORT ANCHOR IS NOT SLIPPED OFF AS INSTRUCTED.
ImmediateAction: PORT ANCHOR WAS SLIPPED OFF FROM THE BITTER END AFTER SEEKING PERMISSION FROM THE OWNERS IN THE SUEZ CANAL AND VESSEL CONTINUED HER TRANSIT IN SUEZ CANAL UNDER PILOTAGE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LOSS OF PORT ANCHOR AND CABLE.
DirectCause: The anchor could have been successfully heaved up by careful engine / steering movements and tugs/Pilots assistance. However due to pressure imposed by the Port authority and the Pilot, vessel had to slip off the anchor from her bitter end.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: In instances when the vessel have to anchor with a headway due to emergency, Beware that the lifting of anchor will need more precautions and careful movements with assistance of tug and Pilot when in shallow and/or constrained waters. In this case at SUEZ : Ensure that all parties involved are made aware of the chances of slipping anchor, if vessels waits for a later time when the convoy Would begin and the port might start pushing the vessel to hurry in the heaving of the anchor.  Instead the possibility of hiring tugs and Pilot for heaving up anchor to be considered before the convoy begins, so that the anchor is easily heaved in and then vessel re-anchored if needed, thereby not having the chance of slipping anchor at all. If the anchor has to be slipped, An anchor buoy to be prepared and lashed with the cable end, prior the release of the bitter end for an easy detection of anchor chain for recovery purpose.
Corrective Action: Ensure that all parties involved are made aware of the chances of slipping anchor, if vessels waits for a later time when the convoy has begun and the port might start pushing the vessel to hurry in the heaving of the anchor.  Instead the possibility of hiring tugs and heaving up anchor to be considered before the convoy begins, so that the anchor is easily heaved in and then vessel re-anchored if needed, thereby not having the chance of slipping anchor.  If the anchor has to be slipped,Time permitting an anchor buoy to be prepared and lashed with the cable end, prior the release of the bitter end.
LessionLearnt: Take pro active action if needed by help of shore side assistance
ReviewComments: To be discussed with entire ships staff
ClosingNote: To be aware of anchor slipping when maneuvering in constrained waters

IncidentDetails: HYD.OIL LEAKAGE IN ENGINE ROOM DUE TO WRONG HANDLING OF VALVES
IncidentEventDetails: During cargo discharge operation at Lihir Berthing, First parcel discharge  was completed. Cargo pumps were stopped. Only power pack was running for Correcting the mooring lines. The system Hyd.pressure was reduced and set at 100 bar.    1.Received FRAMO Hyd.power pack tank low oil level.2.Confirmed from CCR that there was oil leakage in Spring  Mooring winch STBD. 3. Temporary arrangement was made to minimise the leakage by Deck crew.(Confirmed no oil spill on deck, dripped oil was collected in the Plastic drum). 4. Low Oil level was confirmed from Hyd.power pack tank. 5. Preparation was made to transfer Hyd.oil  from Storage tank to Power pack tank.6. Transfer Hoses were  connected to the Hyd.transfer pump (i.e. Main Hyd.pressure line was wrongly chosen and connected to Transfer pump suction line, then Power Pack tank hose was connected to Transfer pump delivery line). 7. When  HP Line ball valve was crack opened , Noticed that Hyd.oil leakage went through the Transfer pump casing.
ImmediateAction: 8. Immediately the ball vv was shut., Rechecked the hoses connection to the pump, found wrongly connected, same was changed to normal.(Confirmed cleaning of oil spray was done immediately in ER, Additional checks were carried out  to ensure no oil traces on Engine room floor). 9. Attempted  to start Hyd.transfer pump, it was tripped due to overload.10. Informed Chief Engineer and E/O. 11.Confirmed areas were safe to work.Checked Transfer pump motor terminals , found IR  values were normal, Fan  cover was removed, tried to turn the shaft. Noticed the shaft was too tight to turn.12.Pump and Motor were dismantled and checked. Both motor and pump shafts were found bent and misaligned.13.Alternative arrangement was made to transfer the  hyd.oil from Storage tank to Hyd.power pack tank through the portable pneumatic  lub.oil pump. Confirmed the oil level was normal. Reported to Office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. The High pressure hyd.oil  sparay might have caused the fire hazard in case if sprayes  on Power pack starter panel. It could have damaged the equipment and may lead to the cargo pumps inoperative.  2. The high pressure Hyd.oil would have caused the burst out of the Hoses and transfer pump,that leads to injure the person very badly.  3. The sparyed oil would have created  the the slippery of the surface that leads to person injury.
DirectCause: Failure to comply with Framo procedure for filling up the hydraulic oi. There was no physical or written barrier available at the site for not allowing the transfer line to be connected to the HP line.  FRAMO system checks prior port arrival / cargo operations did not identify the transfer pump connected to the HP line.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Hyd.oil transfer procedure is posted near the point of Transfer.2. Safety meeting was conducted, the near miss was discussed.  3. Explained the Officer about the potential extent of damage and injury.
Corrective Action: Training carried out for all Engineers regarding the following of correct procedures in Framo hydraulic system.
LessionLearnt: See attached investigation report.
ReviewComments: Direct Cause:     • Failure of Framo Hydraulic System (Hose burst). Rupture of the Portable hydraulic hose that was left connected to the HP line after oil transfer, led to loss of system oil and subsequent stoppage of cargo pumps and discharging.   Indirect Cause:      • During the last transfer of oil from storage to system tank, portable hose of the transfer pump was connected the HP line instead of the LP line as recommended by FRAMO.  Portable hose was not disconnected after completion of hydraulic oil transfer. • Engine room team’s members did not intervene 2E decision to keep the hoses on after filling the tank nor was not noticed by watchkeepers during routine rounds.
ClosingNote: Complete investigation carried out and see attached investigation report

IncidentDetails: Vsl arrived in Callao on 12 Mar and anchored at assigned anchorage at 1342 hrs   .Bunkering started thereafter.Vsl was maintaining bridge and deck anchor watch and the anchor hawse pipe was secured with grating arrangement as part of security measures. Wx condition and visibility was moderate.     During the incident the deck light which is facing towards the bridge was switched on for bunkering/provision/crew change operation and due glare from the flood lights the clarity of the eye vision from bridge towards FWD was  reduced.      On 13 Mar at 0200 hrs bunkering was completed and crew cast off the bunker barge and at the same time crew changing also carried out by service boat.    At 0300 hrs deck round was taken by deck watch and found in order, same reported to bridge.    After barge was cast off Vsl had to wait till 0400 hrs for agents to bring port clearance for departure. At 0430 hrs after agents port formalities were concluded, and agent departed, anchor party went forward to heave anchor, bosun noticed 4 mooring ropes (owners supply) and 1 messenger rope which were placed on foc'sle deck were missing.    The lashings were cut with a knife  and the ropes passed through the fairleads.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl arrived in Callao on 12 Mar and anchored at assigned anchorage at 1342 hrs   .Bunkering started thereafter.Vsl was maintaining bridge and deck anchor watch and the anchor hawse pipe was secured with grating arrangement as part of security measures. Wx condition and visibility was moderate.     During the incident the deck light which is facing towards the bridge was switched on for bunkering/provision/crew change operation and due glare from the flood lights the clarity of the eye vision from bridge towards FWD was  reduced.      On 13 Mar at 0200 hrs bunkering was completed and crew cast off the bunker barge and at the same time crew changing also carried out by service boat.    At 0300 hrs deck round was taken by deck watch and found in order, same reported to bridge.    After barge was cast off Vsl had to wait till 0400 hrs for agents to bring port clearance for departure. At 0430 hrs after agents port formalities were concluded, and agent departed, anchor party went forward to heave anchor, bosun noticed 4 mooring ropes (owners supply) and 1 messenger rope which were placed on foc'sle deck were missing.    The lashings were cut with a knife  and the ropes passed through the fairleads.
ImmediateAction: Vsl conducted a full stowaway and drug search, found all in order crew brief about the security breach and vessel departed .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 4 Mooring ropes ( Owners supply ) and 1 Messenger rope were stolen at Callao anchorage Peru.
DirectCause: Inadequate rounds taken in darkness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The hawse pipe  gratings used to secure with nuts n bolt arrangement  and it is easy to open with shifter or any other similar equipments, so in future we wil be using  padlocks to avoid such incidents.  In the piracy prone areas the deck watch level to be increases and one watch keeper to be posted in FWD at all times .
Corrective Action: The hawse pipe  gratings used to secure with nuts n bolt arrangement  and it is easy to open with shifter or any other similar equipments, so in future we wil be using  padlocks to avoid such incidents. In the piracy prone areas the deck watch level to be increases and one watch keeper to be posted in FWD at all times .
ClosingNote: Effective security watch is required in areas where there is security threat, especially in the darkness.

IncidentDetails: After Heavy weather checks carried out. Inside Forepeak store, it's found that one Oil Drum was crumpled, Lashing broken and About 50 Ltr of Oil leaked from the drum.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel experienced very heavy weather off the South coast of japan with winds upto BF 9 and beam swells in excess of 5 metres.
ImmediateAction: Collected all the Oil into the small drums, Rearranged & Lashed all the Drums , Cleaned the oilstains in the store.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: All damages contained. Lub Oil Spill of about 100 litres was cleaned up.
DirectCause: Lashing by rope alone was the cause of the damage caused in the very heavy and violent rolling caused by the beam swells in excess of 5m.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior sailing maximum oil to be transferred to the storage tanks if any. Rigid Lashings to be carried out with Flat bars and supports. No ropes to be used for lashing.
Corrective Action: Prior sailing maximum oil to be transferred to the storage tanks if any. Rigid Lashings to be carried out with Flat bars and supports. No ropes to be used for lashing.
LessionLearnt: To never lash using only ropes
ReviewComments: To be discussed with ships staff and ensure lashings are checked by senior management prior departure.
ClosingNote: Rigid lashings to be used always.

IncidentDetails: While the vessel was at STS anchorage area, Offshore Lagos - A fishing vessel in vicinity signaled and informed for presence of stowaways in the rudder trunk area.
IncidentEventDetails: 16-03-18  --------  1130 Lt: A small fishing boat approached very close to vsl, Vsl investigated why its            coming too close, two men on the boat shouted that there are 2-3 persons near            Iris Victoria's propeller.  1135 Lt: Vsl confirmed that there is someone on the other side of Rudder Trunk Manhole,            since there is knocking noise from Rudder Trunk.  1136 Lt: Vsl called NNS Sparrow(Navy Patrol Boat) and informed the situation and called            for help, but NNS Sparrow denied to come as they need to have confirmation from             TOC to proceed.  1139 Lt: Vsl called NNS Sparrow many times as it is an emergency, but NNS Sparrow            replied S/by and need conformation by e-mail from TOC.  1142 Lt: Agent called his office and informed the situation.   1143 Lt: NNS Sparrow advised the vsl to tell agent to contact the TOC and agency office.  1149 LT: Vsl called TOC on phone, explained the situation and requested to send            NNS Sparrow  1159 Lt: Vsl called CSO and informed the situation.  1203 Lt: NNS Sparrow called the vsl and informed that they are proceeding, Vsl            acknowledged.  1234 Lt: NNS Sparrow alongside vessel and called a small fishing boat to check the            rudder area.  1245 Lt: NNS Sparrow informed vsl that stowaways are not willing to come, ask agent to            call port control.  1250 Lt: NNS Sparrow tried to get them out once again.  1253 Lt: Two stowaways came out of rudder trunk and they were taken to NNS Sparrow via            small boat.  1300 Lt: NNS Sparrow enquired the stoways and informed vsl no more stowaways in the            Rudder Trunk area and advised vsl to open trunk and check.  1320 Lt: After Rudder Trunk inspection Vsl Informed NNS Sparrow Rudder trunk is clear.  1325 Lt: NNS Sparrow asked agent to arrange them to be taken ashore.  1330 Lt: NNS Sparrow left the area with 2 Stowaways.            Agent Confirmed, he will take the Stoways in his boat ashore.
ImmediateAction: Alarm raised all crew mustered, head counts taken, all entrances to ship checked for any loose opening, steering flat all manholes checked -confirmed all secured. Look outs posted, Informed all relevant authorities.
DirectCause: In-effective lookout at the vessel's stern while at high risk area anchorage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Dedicated lookout to be posted at the poop deck while in high risk area anchorage. 2) Rudder trunk area sorrounding from inside Engine-room to be checked by the Watch-keepers every hourly and look for any kind of sound from there.
Corrective Action: 1) Dedicated lookout to be posted at the poop deck while in high risk area anchorage. 2) Rudder trunk area sorrounding from inside Engine-room to be checked by the Watch-keepers every hourly and look for any kind of sound from there.
LessionLearnt: More alert security watch to be kept by ship-staffs.
ClosingNote: More alert security watch to be maintained in areas known for stowaya boarding.

IncidentDetails: BARGE MADE CONTACT WITH SHIP DURING MOORING.
IncidentEventDetails: While berthing at Rotterdam Botlek 62 with Pilot Mr. Kerkalaan B.P.T (406148) onboard and two tugs made fast forward and one tug made fast aft, vessel was within the mooring buoys, stopped and had Lowered Starboard anchor and made fast 3 headlines from port while other 3 from starboard were in the mooring boat. Tug Reinod 14 was maneuvering with 4 barges on the Port quarter of the vessel. The tug wire snapped and the tug lost control of the barges. Edge of one of the barge RADAMI made contact with the ship side making an indent.
ImmediateAction: Upon completion of Mooring, inspected and assessed the damage, Owners and Managers informed LOP issued.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor indention of Hull, same checked by Class surveyor report: Damage was made at shipside in way of Fr no 10 at Port side 2nd share strake dimension approx 1100mm x 700mm deep 50 mm
DirectCause: Tow wire snapped causing the barges to drift and the Tug was not able to control the drifting of the barge towards the vessel, leading to the hull contact and dent.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Whenever any maneuvering operations is going on near the vessel when own vessel is berthing/unberthing - Pilot is to be informed and made aware so as to alert the Port control and also keep the tugs on standby for assistance if needed. Crew members on forward and aft stations to be made aware and asked to keep a good lookout for any untoward incident taking place.  If damage does take place, All concerned parties to be informed and evidences collected to safeguard owners interests.  Class to be involved to access damage and suggest further action.
Corrective Action: Damage to be rectified during next Dry Dock before 2020.
LessionLearnt: Master and members on stations to inform PILOT about existing manouevers by small crafts near the vessel when berthing/unberthing.
ReviewComments: To discuss with Bridge Team and entire ship staff
ClosingNote: To be cautious when approaching in restricted waters

IncidentDetails: Aux Engine - 1 tripped due to malfunction of overspeed trip limit swtich causing a blackout.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was at sea , calm weather. Engine room was manned  and normal watch routines on progress. Aux engine -1 was running at 700 kW load (Capacity 1280 KW) . Aux engine tripped causing a black out of the vessel . Emergency generator started within 45 sec but came onload only after 2 min . Standby aux engine 2 & 3 did not start and PMS panel was showing abnormal alarm . Aux engine - 3 was started manually and normal power was restored , vessel started to proceed the voyage . On investigation found A/E # 1 showing overspeed trip indication but the mechanical overspeed device which activates the overspeed trip has not operated(Engine did not overspeed). The cause of engine tripping on overspeed is due to malfunction of overspeed trip limit swtich due to vibration. Vessel has made extra securing arrangements for overspeed trip limit swtich. REQN SPR/NACE/E/18/061 already raised for spare. Vessel has to carryout a Blackout test and confirm Stby A/E and Emergency generator starting and coming ON Load at a safe anchorage.
ImmediateAction: Emergency generator started by 45 sec and came onload only after 2 min . Aux engine - 3 was started manually and main power was restored .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage or Injury
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Extra securing arrangements made, REQN raised for spare.
Corrective Action: Pl raise REQ
ClosingNote: Req raised and will be supplied at next oppurtunity

IncidentDetails: This is to inform you that during the HFO Bunker line pressure testing today on 26 Mar 2018,   the bunker line ruptured from one place on top side of the longitudinal line few meters before the manifold.   The oil splashed out on to the grating plates and spread on the area around.
IncidentEventDetails: 1030 : COMMENCED PREPARATIONS FOR BUNKER LINE PRESSURE TESTING .  1100 : COMMENCED FILLING BUNKER LINE USING FUEL TRANSFER PUMP.  1130 : COMMENCED PRESSURING TO 4.5 KG  1140 : BUNKER AT 4.5 KG  1145 : LEAK ON BUNKER LINE AT MIDSHIP AREA
ImmediateAction: The pressure testing operation was stopped immediately and the line was depressurized.   Emergency alarm was sounded, crew was mustered and the area was cleaned.   Total quantity of oil leaked out was around 50 ltrs which was collected.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During the HFO Bunker line pressure testing today on 26 Mar 2018,   the bunker line ruptured from one place on top side of the longitudinal line few meters before the manifold. The oil splashed out on to the grating plates and spread on the area around.
DirectCause: Inadequate maintenance.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renewal with spare pipe onboard.
Corrective Action: Renewal with spare pipe onboard.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed by replacing the pipe section and pressure testing.

ImmediateAction: Reported to bridge and picked up the line.

Parted mooring line then picked up by mooring winch.    29 Mar 2018, Departure Port Said.  1005: Tugs made fast  1012: All cast off and clear  1026: dead slow ahead  1027: Slow ahead  1028:Aft tug line(ships line) parted during pulling the vessel out, engines stopped immediately.  1031:Picked up the rest of the line, 5 meters of line and eye were parted and left out in the tug.

IncidentDetails: ER OH Crane  upper hoist limit failed to stop the block which moved at high speed and damaged the wires
IncidentEventDetails: With regard to the incident that occurred on 28th March,    •  At around 1730 lt, 4th Engineer was operating the ER crane with no observers to assist or guide the crane, Senior staff were in the ECR during that time.  They heard a loud noise before they proceeded to the scene.   •  The Crane block had broken the stopper plate and limit switch.    Why it Happened:  •  Firstly, 4th Engineer was operating the crane without supervision   • There are two modes, high speed and low speed, the crane was operated at high speed hoist resulting the block hitting the stopper plate at high speed causing damage to stopper plate and limit switch.   • The remote operation was in working condition, it is possible that it was due to human error and wrong operation of switches while multitasking.      Root Cause :  • Operation of the ER crane by inexperienced staff, without supervision.  • Operation of high speed switch, causing the block to strike the stopper plate at greater momentum.      Corrective actions:  •  Operation to be under strict supervision of CE/  2nd Eng or Electrical officer.     Adding some insight to the incident based on observations    Witnessing the operation and manual,  Point  NO 3 in attached from manual looks like a probable cause. The crane has two brakes one mechanical and other electromagnetic , both are related to each other. It says the electromagnetic brake prevents the motor from excessive running due to inertia.  The failure of the electromagnetic brake to act would have resulted in the block striking hard.    What the manual also says under point 4 is that is the load oscillates when the crane is operated the mechanical brake will hold, Its is possible that it was seen as a fault rather than a safety feature , the high speed operation was not working at this time.    As per electrical officer the problem was resolved with adjustment of the brake and operation was normal.    The crane was operated prior arrival to Gangavaram but not problem was noted.    Reverting    What is not known  yet is      1.       Was the electromagnetic brake adjusted at Richards bay or only mechanical brake ?    2.       Was the mechanical brakes adjusted as per manafacturers instructions ?    3.        IS there a faulty electrical switch in the operation control?    4.       Did the failure of the electro magnetic brake not prevent the block from stopping despite the limit activating due excessive running of the motor due to inertia.
ImmediateAction: Operation suspended,  Investigation of roout cause, identification of fault  Incident reported to Technical Manager.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to limit switch,   Damage to wire
DirectCause: Malfunction of equipment
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repair of the circuit and adjustments as per manafacturers recommendation
Corrective Action: Repair of the circuit and adjustments as per manafacturers recommendation
LessionLearnt: Only trained staff to operate electric lifting appliances
ReviewComments: To be discussed among ships staff
ClosingNote: Always to be operated under supervision

IncidentDetails: On 05th Nov 2017, while vessel was at sea in Indian ocean en-route to Port said in Egypt from Singapore, a Bilge alarm for Cargo Hold no.4 observed on ballast panel in ship’s office. The visual check carried out for the hold no.4 and found water ingress above tank top. There were 20 loaded general-purpose containers underwater in the hold as per the load plan.  The bilges pumped out, Master and C/E informed. Adjacent holds checked for hull integrity, WBT no.4 pumped out to drop the level and no hull damage observed. After reducing water level in cargo hold bilges, the WBT no.4 filled again to check tank manhole door integrity and found the WBT no.4 stbd aft manhole leaking profusely inside the cargo hold. Operation stopped, water level reduced in ballast tank by 50 cms and manhole packing renewed and cover tightened. Water was trickling from the containers effected by water ingress, but the extent of damage could not be ascertained due inaccessibility of cargo inside the containers.
IncidentEventDetails: Failure of the rubber gasket used in the manhole door of WBT no.4(S).The deteriorated condition of rubber packing and the rusted condition of the manhole seat lead to the leak indicates poor maintenance of manhole door cover.     The WBT no.4 hydraulic valve found in open position on ballast panel on the day of incident.  The ballast panel not secured against inadvertent use at sea when no ballasting/de-ballasting operations in progress.  Two manual valves in Engine room always kept in open position on the ballast line leading to WBT no.4 being pressed up.   The neglect of C.H. bilge alarm received on bridge and ship’s office by the watchkeeping officers.  PMS routines were not complied for cargo hold inspection including checking of manhole door packings.
ImmediateAction: Office was notified about the water ingress and counter measures taken to avoid further flooding.  After pumping out water from the cargo hold and dropping water level in WBT no.4, visual inspection carried out to ascertain source of flooding. No sign of hull breach or structural damages observed. Water filled up in WBT no.4 to max. level by gravity and observed WBT starboard manhole leaking heavily. Operation was stopped, and WBT#4 level dropped to renew the packing on the manhole cover. On removing the manhole cover it was observed that between the rubber packing and the manhole cover there was a patch of rust on the site of leakage which was scraped off and cleaned prior renewing the packing.  To check the integrity of the manhole the ballast tank was pressed up again by gravity and to check the source of ingress, the main sea chest valve was closed. The level of water was still increasing in the tank constantly.  Upon further investigation it was found that two manual valves (047 VPW & 007VPH) on ballast educator line were in open position and on shutting the same in engine room lower platform, the level of water stopped increasing any further. The water level in WBT no.4 kept at 95% and all holds inspected including all bilge alarms trying out to confirm integrity.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 20 General purpose containers submerged under water.Loss being assessed by P&I and concerned parties.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fleet circular sent to all vessels on the incident to address the potential causes of hold flooding and ways to prevent.  DCR raised to include renewal of ballast tank rubber packing at every annual tank inspection. Train ship’s staff for use of correct valves for ballasting pump out procedures and closing of manual valves after completion of operation. Rectify the malfunctioning system on ballast panel and replace the mouse to start using both panel independently for separate operations. Place controls to limit use by authorized personnel only and post procedures for action required in case of alarms received. Include reporting procedures in C/O’s standing orders and Post notifications on bridge              and CCR panels to call C/O immediately upon receiving any Bilge alarm without fail. Place posters near ballast panel indicating all the valves to be operated during the ballasting/ de-ballasting operations.
Corrective Action: Fleet circular sent to all vessels on the incident to address the potential causes of hold flooding and ways to prevent.  DCR raised to include renewal of ballast tank rubber packing at every annual tank inspection. Train ship's staff for use of correct valves for ballasting pump out procedures and closing of manual valves after completion of operation. Rectify the malfunctioning system on ballast panel and replace the mouse to start using both panel independently for separate operations. Place controls to limit use by authorized personnel only and post procedures for action required in case of alarms received. Include reporting procedures in C/O's standing orders and Post notifications on bridge              and CCR panels to call C/O immediately upon receiving any Bilge alarm without fail. Place posters near ballast panel indicating all the valves to be operated during the ballasting/ de-ballasting operations.
LessionLearnt: All alarms to be attended and followed by visual inspection.No valves on ballast and bilge line to be open when not in use.Manholes to be checked for integrity after every tank inspection.
ClosingNote: ALL CORRECTIVE ACTIONS IN PLACE

IncidentDetails: Emergency fire pump found not taking suction.
IncidentEventDetails: Emergency fire pump tried out. Found not taking suction.
ImmediateAction: Emergency fire pump opened up for inspection. Found shaft bent , neck bush damaged , mouth ring worn out. Same was informed to office / superintendent. As per officei instruction Requisition has been raised. Requisition no :- SPR/ORST/E/18/040. Same was informed to class NK by office.Class attened the vessel at GUAM. Class NK surveyor checked & confirmed alternative arrangements for fire fighting & condition of both the fire pumps. Issued dispensation for Emergency fire pump till 23 rd May 2018. Please find attached the same below. Awaiting for pump shaft. Fire rounds are been taken in the engine room frequently. Fire round log is being maintained and updated on hourly basis.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Emergency fire pump opened up for inspection. Found shaft bent , neck bush damaged , mouth ring worn out. No spares onboard.As per officei instruction Requisition has been raised. Requisition no :- SPR/ORST/E/18/040.
DirectCause: Mechanical failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Awaiting for pump shaft. Once the vessel receives the pump shaft. Will assemble, box back and fit back the same. Then will try out the pump. Will check all the parameters including the discharge pressure. Afterwards will inform office.
Corrective Action: New Spares to be connected to vessel
LessionLearnt: Proper Maintenance on emergency Equipment
ClosingNote: Many thanks for bringing the same to our knowledge . kindly ensure all the fire fighting equipment are maintained in order and  PMS routines are followed diligently. We Completely agree with your root cause and corrective action report.

IncidentDetails: While walking back port anchor at Ningbo anchorage 5th Kentor shackle got stuck on gypsy groove and unable to lower or heave anchor any further.
IncidentEventDetails: While walking back port anchor at Ningbo anchorage it was decided to have 6 shackles in water. Till 4 shackles cable were lowered normally without any issue, 5th Kentor shackle came up from spurling pipe with twist and when it reached top of gypsy drum cable slipped and one link jumped on gypsy hence the kentor shackle which was suppose to be horizontal in gypsy drum stuck in vertical position in gypsy groove and unable to lower or heave anchor any further.
ImmediateAction: Immediately stopped lowering anchor, bridge was informed and vessel position monitored for any dragging, found vessel holding anchor with 5 shackles on deck.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage and injury
DirectCause: Twisting of chain inside the chain locker.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Continuos monitoring of chain links coming out from spruling pipe to avoid chain kinks getting stuck on gpysy. Handling of anchor should be carried out from only main control stand.
Corrective Action: Procedure of rectifyingthe issue to be prepared and attached for future reference
LessionLearnt: Close monitoring of Anchor Cables when lowering and Heaving up Anchor
ClosingNote: To ensure that Cable links are closely monitored while anchoring and heaving up anchor.

IncidentDetails: ME FO Circulating pump mechanical seal leaking badly
IncidentEventDetails: Generator was changed over to LSMGO and boiler stopped as atmospheric condenser cleaning was planned. Due to temperature reducing of HFO, mechanical seal of #1 ME FO Circulating pump started leaking.
ImmediateAction: Isolated the pump after starting the stand by pump. Isolated by putting blanks in the suction and discharge side valves of the pump to minimize the oil leakage. The mechanical seal was renewed on the next immediate opportunity and requisition has been raised for new mechanical seals

IncidentDetails: Recently one of the walkie talkies was lost overboard during mooring stations, where the mooring line which was being lowered and tranported by mooring boat to dolphin got jammed on mooring drum and hit officer of forward station on chest where walkie talkie chest cover case was worn, the impact resulted in the walkie talkie coming out of the case and falling overboard through the roller fairlead.No injuries sustained.
ImmediateAction: stand by walkie talkie used for communication with bridge for mooring operations.Office informed and  Requisition for walkie talkie raised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Recently one of the walkie talkies was lost overboard during mooring stations, where the mooring line which was being lowered and tranported by mooring boat to dolphin got jammed on mooring drum and hit officer of forward station on chest where walkie talkie chest cover case was worn, the impact resulted in the walkie talkie coming out of the case and falling overboard through the roller fairlead.No injury sustained.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discuss the incident in the next safety meeting with all staff to explain the correct working practices when paying out ropes from the drums and the understand the importance of good hazard identification during the pre-work toolbox meetings. Emphasise on using of Carrying Case and hanging strap and avoid carrying in hand or in pocket.
Corrective Action: 1. keeping sufficient length of rope cleared well before.  2. to be provided with mouth piece for radios.
LessionLearnt: Importance of good vigilance and situational awareness during mooring stations.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Staff to be aware of basic care of important and expensive equipment. Senior officer to lead by example and enforce good practices

IncidentEventDetails: 03.04.2018  Please note Pilot boarded the vessel at 1408 LT and vessel came alongside at 1512 LT.    Vessel was made fast at 1548 LT and Pilot disembarked.   Vessel then commenced passing additional ropes forward and aft as recommended by the Pilot due to heavy surging due to beam swell at the berth.    At 1612 LT the extra ropes were made fast.  Anchor dropped to One Shackle on deck.    1615 LT, One outboard stern Line parted.  1618 LT, 2nd outboard stern line parted, due to continuous surging.    1620 LT, Pilot and Tug assistance was requested on VHF Ch-14, who confirmed that Pilot would board the vessel shortly.    1621 LT, 3rd Stern Line parted (Inboard line).    REMARKS : The Berth is Exposed to open sea, not sheltered By Breakwater.
ImmediateAction: 1620 LT, Pilot and Tug assistance was requested on VHF Ch-14, who confirmed that Pilot would board the vessel shortly.    Vessel then slacked all lines to avoid stresses on other lines.    1630 LT, Tugs were made fast and Pilot boarded the vessel.    1648 LT, Anchor was retrieved.    1657 LT, All mooring lines picked up on board. Vessel departed berth.    1703 LT, Both tugs cast off.    1709 LT, Pilot disembarked.    1730 LT, vessel dropped anchor as advised by Pilot
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 3 mooring ropes parted
DirectCause: Berth exposed to the high swell condition. Vessel experienced the swell on its beam.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior berthing, Master to confirm Wx warnings received from Agents, Port Information, Sat C, ship managers and all other available sources. Berthing manouevres to be performed only if master is confident of the existing situation and berthing conditions suitable for the vessel. Any surging or sway noticed during berthing to be immediately brought to the notice of the PILOT and the berthing abandoned.
Corrective Action: Prior berthing, Master to confirm Wx warnings received from Agents, Port Information, Sat C, ship managers and all other available sources. Berthing manouevres to be performed only if master is confident of the existing situation and berthing conditions suitable for the vessel. Any surging or sway noticed during berthing to be immediately brought to the notice of the PILOT and the berthing abandoned.
LessionLearnt: When approaching a berth exposed to sea conditions, masters to be assertive in taking decision to abort berthing if needed
ReviewComments: Discussed with master and ships staff
ClosingNote: Discussed with ships staff and master

IncidentDetails: Vessel was berthed at Puerto Brisa, Colombia. Pilot finished operations with configuration of 4+2 F&A. Soon after some time vessel noticed winds picking up and swell pushing the vessel away from the berth. Permission was taken from the port to put additional 2 breast ropes F&A so thet vessel can stay safely A/S and complete her loading operation. In due course vesel damaged 5 of her mooring ropes.
IncidentEventDetails: Strong offshore winds ranging between 20 to 30 knots and swell of upto 3 mtrs kept pushing the vessel away from the berth. Additional moorings breast 2 fwd and 2 aft were employed, deck watch doubled up with total 4 hands at any given time. Moorings were constantly monitored, grease applied at place where lines were turning on fairleads and rollers. However in the course of loading vessel damaged mooring lines at 6 times, total 5 ropes damaged.
ImmediateAction: Additional moorings breast 2 fwd and 2 aft were employed after taking permission from the port via agent, deck watch doubled up with total 4 hands at any given time. Moorings were constantly monitored, grease applied at place where lines were turning on fairleads and rollers.  Post damage, broken pieces picked up, bowline tied to the end and line passed again.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Upon departure all lines were laid up and checked. Length of the good and damaged part was measured. Damage repaired as reqired. Requisition raised as per advise received.
DirectCause: Winds and Swell in excess. Berth exposed to weather as is a open port. Master to take a call in such situations and call for unberthing if needed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Wx update to be received from Ship managers, Local agents, pilots, NAVTEX and SATC. Same to be utilized when berthing to decide whether will be safe to berth. After berthing if vessels notices that it is unsafe to stay on berth, Master to take a decision to unberth the vessel.
Corrective Action: Mooring rope condition to be inspected and any damage to be reported and rope identified. In difficult port, with surge/swells, additional care to be given to tend the ropes, well in advance. To investigate further
LessionLearnt: Wx warnings to be received. Master to be assertive and decide if vessel needs to leave berth due to existing conditions
ReviewComments: To be discussed amongst ship staff
ClosingNote: To be discussed with ships staff

IncidentDetails: While derusting inside the 4S ballast tank, one of the crew member tripped by the rungs of the drain hole Lower stool platform and fell on his right knee.
IncidentEventDetails: While derusting inside the 4S ballast tank, one of the crew member tripped by the rungs of the drain hole Lower stool platform and fell on his right knee.
ImmediateAction: He himself came out of the ballast tank and reported to 2nd officer about his injury.  Slight swelling was observed which was treated with cold pack and analgesic cream with crepe bandage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While derusting inside the 4S ballast tank, one of the crew member tripped by the rungs of the drain hole Lower stool platform and fell on his right knee.
DirectCause: Lack of safety barriers and  In effective work space knowledge
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Covering the Drain hole with temporary covering arrangement of wooden ply.
Corrective Action: On board training session to be conducted by Safety Officer covering general safety precautions while working inside ballast tanks
ClosingNote: Cause Analysis:- Direct Cause:- While Working inside ballast tank, his leg tripped on rungs and fell down on right knee. No proper awareness about the work area.. Indirect Cause:- 1. Physical and mental condition of the OS  He was not paying attention to the surrounding while moving on deck. Root Cause:- 1. Slippery surface. and restricted Space Corrective Action:- 1. Circulate this incident to all vessels in the next injury bulletin. 2. On board training session to be conducted by Safety Officer covering general safety precautions while working inside ballast tanks

IncidentDetails: Mandal Shackle of No. 2 Mooring drum found damaged during routine inspection and greasing.
IncidentEventDetails: Mooring wire greasing,drum chipping/painting and mandal shackle maintenance(freeing) /inspection was in progress. Noted No.2 Drum Mandal Shackle damaged.Probably expect that same has occured while the vessel was loading at Barsah during her last voyage.
ImmediateAction: All shackles onboard has been inspected. No.2 Drum Mandal shackle replaced with spare shackle.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No. 2 Drum Mandal shackle damaged.
DirectCause: Material failure due to ageing
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Monthly Mandal shackles and mooring equipments checked. Tool box meeting carried out and all hands advised to check mandal shackle,tails , wire and other mooring related equipments after use.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's proposed CA

IncidentDetails: Three aft breast mooring rope parted. One mooring rope parted from 35 from meter from the shore eye  and the other two parted 15-25 meter from the shore eye.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel alongside Coronel coal pier S/S alongside with Moorings 4 +2+2 & Port Anchor 6 shackles in water, Aft 4+3+2+ 1 ( port side preventer line).Vessel heading 278deg and discharging coal with two cranes which can discharge 5 hatch lengths at a time.Weather condition - wind SSW x BF3, Swell Max 1.5mtrs, Currents Offshore . Draft about 12.5 meters & UKC about 3.5 meters.Pier is ideal for handy max vessel & No vessel can berth on the other side or of the pier. There is no warning from port for abnormal currents / swell. Mooring watch One person Fwd, one Aft & duty officer.  Most probable reason for parting of ropes are as below:  1. Variable Currents .  2. Stern hanging out off the berth about 75 meters.  3. Varying tension on ropes due to some ropes being on Floating buoys & some are on Bitts. Breast ropes fwd were on floating buoys & aft on shore bitts.  4. After the change of shift new foreman advised duty officer / Chief officer to tightened the Breast ropes aft and bring the vessel alongside , as crane operator having problems in discharging.the ropes parted soon after the ropes were tightened by one meter.  5. Age of the rope - about 7-8 years.
ImmediateAction: 1. Called Port control and informed about the parted ropes & requested for shore mooring crew/ boat.  2. Called Charterers and informed about the parted mooring ropes and requested for Tug assistance to keep vessel alongside as foreman kept pressuring duty officer for keeing the vessel alongside.  3. Called PIC synergy.    4. Called Owners and informed them about vessel situation at berth.    5. Took out spare mooring rope & two other ends of  parted  ropes eye spliced and sent ashore.  6. Called foreman and told him that with the prevailing condition and berth mooring arrangment even new ropes also cannot keep the vessel alongside and you will need to continue discharge with vessel 2 meters out   7 . Duty officers advised to  maintain the Moderate tension on aft breast ropes.  8. Asked for threes ropes on rental from agents, incase of further emergency.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Three Aft Breast Mooring ropes Parted.    One rope parted 35 meter from shore eye.    Other two parted 15-25 meters from the shore eye.
DirectCause: 1. Berth exposed to swell. 2. Variable Currents .  3. Stern hanging out off the berth about 75 meters.  4. Varying tension on ropes due to some ropes being on Floating buoys & some are on Bitts. Breast ropes fwd were on floating buoys & aft on shore bitts.  5. Foreman advised duty officer / Chief officer to tighten the Breast ropes aft and bring the vessel alongside, as crane operator having problems in discharging.The ropes parted soon after the ropes were tightened by one meter.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to maintain equal tension on all ropes. Avoid having some ropes on buoys and others on shore bitts. If not avoidable, ensure that ships staff are aware that tension on the buoys will not be readily visible as there will always be a catenary on the ropes. In ports exposed to the swell like coronel, precaution to be taken always. Master to be assertive if needed to depart from berth incase of surging/swaying.
Corrective Action: Vessel to maintain equal tension on all ropes. Avoid having some ropes on buoys and others on shore bitts. If not avoidable, ensure that ships staff are aware that tension on the buoys will not be readily visible as there will always be a catenary on the ropes. In ports exposed to the swell like coronel, precaution to be taken always. Master to be assertive if needed to depart from berth incase of surging/swaying.
LessionLearnt: Mooring practices to be revised time and again
ReviewComments: To be discussed in safety meeting
ClosingNote: Ship staff briefed.

IncidentDetails: A/E No.3 was running and at 18.15 hours there was a black out and duty engineer was present in ECR, st-by generator No.2 Auto started and came on load. No alarm in alarm list, only MSB Abnormal alarm.  MAIN CAUSE WAS MALFUNCTION OF 2- WAY PNEUMATIC VALVE.
IncidentEventDetails: Checked the fuel system found ok, checked the fuel pumps for any seizuerr found ok.  Opened the crank case after 2 hrs inspected found ok.  Tried to start the generator not starting fuel cut off lever was not allowing the fuel(LAMBDA CONTROLLER), removed the air connection to lambda controller and noticed air leaking.  Iinspected the overspeed device found ok.  Generator started and noticed the RPM was 900(Normal).
ImmediateAction: Removed the 2-WAY PNEUMATIC VALVE of over-speed device  which activates to cut off the fule in actual over speed of Generator. Pneumatic valve cleaned and checked tis function.  A washer add in on pneumatic valve to increase the distance so that valve will not leak.  Mechainical  over speed tried out fpond working satisfactory.  Opened the crank case door and inspected found all ok.  A/E no.3 started and put on load with other generator for 2 hrs   A/E no.3 was put on full sea load and tried out for 4 hrs.  All three generator started and maxiume load (power pack, IGG and Bow thruster) and tried found satisfactory.  A/E no.3 was kept running through out night and was in observation,.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO DAMAGE, NO INJURY
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Overhaul the valve, and chk fr other A/E's. Raise a req for new to keep as spare.
Corrective Action: REPLACE PNEUMATIC VALVE
LessionLearnt: Pneumatic valve overhaul to be carried out with every Decarb.
ClosingNote: Valve overhauled and other A/E's chkd. A/E running alone and in parallel  satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: HEAVY WEATHER DAMAGES.  This is to bring to your attention that on 18th April 2018 1020LT(1320 UTC), the following incident has happened.  Vessel was at sea (South pacific ocean) at position 51.45 S 075.56 W, proceeding to Esmeraldas,Ecuador. Vessel was experiencing heavy wind(Beaufort scale 9) and heavy and long head swells (swell height 5.0M).   At 1020LT it was observed by the OOW that one of the stay wire of forward mast got parted and the forward liferaft was found on the port side of main deck, out of its stowed position with the container opened. OOW notified Master and Chief Officer. By 1025LT Master came upon Bridge, Notified E/R to reduce RPM and altered the course to 160 degrees. Chief Officer along with P/P Man and two OS ,proceeded on main deck to check the extent of damage.  By the time Chief Officer and party reached forward it was observed that the Liferaft got inflated.   On further investigation it was found that the gratings of catwalk in forward area  and the protective metal covers of forward mooring winch control was also damaged.   The inflated Liferaft and equipments was  transferred to poop deck and secured properly. By 1050LT the securing was completed and vessel resumed the passage.
