The inflated Liferaft and equipments was  transferred to poop deck and secured properly. By 1050LT the securing was completed and vessel resumed the passage.       19TH April:    AA) It was found that the Forward liferaft Embarkation ladder lashing was broken  was lying on deck out of its stowed position, same has been transferred to Forepeak store and secured properly.  BB) The Securing stand of Forward liferaft Embarkation ladder has been deformed, same has to be repaired when the weather improves.  CC) The Forepeak tank sounding pipe cap was found missing and 30 cbm of water was found in Forepeak tank.  A plug is fabricated to block opening  and the water has been pumped out.  Requisition for brass round bar to fabricate cap has been raised no. SPR/JSPR/D/18/053.  DD) Spurling pipe covers have been found in damaged condition, same has to be repaired and put back.  EE) Chain locker was found filled with water which has to be educted out. Intending to do the same when the weather improves(no water ingress inside store).  FF) Metal covers of Fwd mooring winch control was found broken on deck , a new assembly of protection cover has to be fabricated.  GG) Fwd mast mooring winch project lights have been found filled with water & alignment position changed , same has to repositioned when the weather improves.  HH) Mast stay wire port side has parted.  II) Forepeak store found free of water. No water ingress observed. All items are found in secured condition. No deformation of Hull structure observed. Forepeak store bilge alarms tried out and found in satisfactory condition.   JJ) BT room found in satisfactory condition. No ingress of water observed.
ImmediateAction: All items with broken lashings were relashed. inflated liferaft deflated and removed to safe place with all items for landing. sounding cap fitted with plug arrangement.

The inflated Liferaft and equipments was  transferred to poop deck and secured properly. By 1050LT the securing was completed and vessel resumed the passage

Proposedcorrectiveactions: vessel to be deviate route to avoid such weather condition as far as practicable.

ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Better monitoring of weather and closer communication with charterers is preferred to take up alternative routes avoiding areas of worst weather.

IncidentDetails: Damaged accomodation ladder port side
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at Tauranga New Zealand port. Crew member assisting port state control inspection  and one crew standby on gangway. At 1137 H accomodation ladder was damaged due to the movement of the vessel when big contaier vessel passed by, create surge and move the vessel. The gangway watch try to heave the gangway but the flatform caught and stock on the bollard causing heavy damaged on the accomodation ladder
ImmediateAction: Free the accomodation ladder from the shore bollard, carried out briefing and instruction
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The upper platform of the portside gangway was seriously damaged.  Required to be sent ashore for repair due to lack of materials, tools and equipment onboard.
DirectCause: Gangway watch lack of proper monitoring and adjustment for the safety of the gangway.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Conduct immediate safety meeting for the proper monotoring and adjustment of gangway especially when there is movement of the ship while in port due to surge, swell, tides and other factors.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: CA found adequate.

IncidentDetails: After unberthing at Jeddah, Tugs were pulling the vessel out by using ships line. Vessel was swinging to port to head to the channel. Suddenly the ships line which was used for the fwd tug got parted just about 20 degree swing left to enter the channel. Ship safely entered the channel by using Bow thruster,helm and speed and continued safe passage.
IncidentEventDetails: After unberthing at Jeddah  1342: All cast off  1402: Aft tug cast off  1404: Fwd tug line(ships line) parted while pulling  1407: Ships line onboard  1414:Pilot away   Tugs were pulling the vessel out by using ships line. Vessel was swinging to port to head to the channel. Suddenly the ships line which was used for the fwd tug got parted just about 20 degree swing left to enter the channel. Ship safely entered the channel by using Bow thruster,helm and speed and continued safe passage.
ImmediateAction: Ships line picked up by mooring winch and secured onboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After unberthing at Jeddah  1342: All cast off  1402: Aft tug cast off  1404: Fwd tug line(ships line) parted while pulling  1407: Ships line onboard  1414:Pilot away   Tugs were pulling the vessel out by using ships line. Vessel was swinging to port to head to the channel. Suddenly the ships line which was used for the fwd tug got parted just about 20 degree swing left to enter the channel. Ship safely entered the channel by using Bow thruster,helm and speed and continued safe passage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To advise the pilot about the breaking strength of the rope, bollard pull of the tug and bit used onboard.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Good condition ropes to be used for high tension jobs.
ClosingNote: Ship's lines can be used by doubling up for pulling ops with tugs.

IncidentDetails: On 14th March 2018 / 1200 Hrs LT (Z+8) vessel arrived at Yangpu, China to load. By 1700LT the cargo  tank inspection was completed, however, it was noted that Cargo tanks 5W & 6W were not to  surveyor’s satisfaction. Vessel was thereby instructed to shift to anchorage for further cleaning of 5w’s  and 6W’s.  On 15th March 2018 at 0054 LT vessels re-anchored at Yangpu anchorage and subject tanks were  recleaned, thereby vessel berthed again on 16th AM, 5W’s and 6W’s were found to be satisfactory  condition this time and were cleared by attending surveyors for loading
IncidentEventDetails: For changing over of cargo grade from Unleaded Gasoline to Jet A1, vessel was instructed to carryout  complete tank cleaning. Vessel consulted SHELL Tank cleaning guide to prepare their tank cleaning  plan and concurrence for same was taken from commercial operator. Accordingly, vessel conducted  complete tank cleaning, which involved Cold sea water washing, gas-freeing, mopping and re-inerting.  Vessel conducted this tank cleaning and mopping from 09thMar’18, 1830hrs to 12thMar’18, 2150hrs  followed by re-inerting of all tanks completed by 13th Mar’18/ PM hrs. and arrived load port on  14thMar’2018/1200LT, with all her tanks ready for next loading.  Upon berthing cargo surveyors checked all her cargo tanks for load-readiness. Vessel’s cargo tanks  from 1w’s to 4w’s were found suitable for loading, however, cargo tanks 5w’s and 6w’s were found to  have a brownish residue sticking to MMC bob’s bottom area.  Re-checking of the tanks were carried out a further two times, however, cargo surveyors were not  satisfied with the tank preparation for 5w’s and 6w’s COTks. The terminal duly informed Master that  vessel will have to be shifted to inner anchorage and re-cleaning of 5W’s and 6W’s would be  required. Master communicated same to all parties and further guidance from charterer’s was  sought.
ImmediateAction: Vessel was thereby shifted to inner anchorage on 14th Mar / 2245 Lt and commenced re-cleaning of  subject tanks. On 15th Mar’18 / 1900LT, vessel completed the re-cleaning of 5w’s and 6w’s COTks and  NOR was re-tendered.  Vessel safely re-berthed on 16th Mar/0024 LT and all cargo tanks were re-checked, all tanks were  found in satisfactory condition this time.  Thereby, vessel was permitted to load, and she safely loaded her nominated cargo.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Re-checking of the tanks were carried out a further two times, however, cargo surveyors were not  satisfied with the tank preparation for 5w’s and 6w’s COTks. The terminal duly informed Master that  vessel will have to be shifted to inner anchorage and re-cleaning of 5W’s and 6W’s would be  required. Master communicated same to all parties and further guidance from charterer’s was  sought.
DirectCause: Inadequate Illumination, Inadequate Supervision. Lack of Planning,
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A detailed briefing/ training session should be conducted onboard by Master, emphasizing on the usage of all resources while tank mopping up operation, including that of pneumatic lights for better illumination inside the tank to ascertain the correct condition of tank.
Corrective Action: Fleet alert has to be sent to all vessels regarding the incident. Emphasizing on the fact that tank cleaning operations should be planned well with utmost dispatch, in order to avoid any rush towards last stages in case of vessel charterer’s opting for early loading.
ClosingNote: All corrective actions closed.

IncidentDetails: Damage to shore Rupture Disc located on the surge pot.  No injury to any personnel.  There was interruption in cargo operation due to activation of ESD.
IncidentEventDetails: The SP1 berth, Emergency Shut-Down system (ESD) was activated by one of the ship’s crew member on his way back to the vessel from shore leave on the 9th of March 2018, at 0348 hrs LT.  The concerned crew member was under the influence of alcohol which was evidenced by alcohol test carried out on him immediately post the incident.    The cessation of loading was noticed in the CCR at 0350 hrs LT and the same was communicated to the terminal immediately. At that time the reason for disruption was not known to the terminal or vessel, and was being investigated by both.   At 0415 hrs LT it was informed by terminal that the disruption was due to activation of shore ESD by one of our crew member who was returning to the vessel and this was recorded in the Terminal CCTV footage. The same was later viewed by Chief Officer Sanjeev Kumar with Nutrien Operating Technician, Michael De Freitas.    At 0442hrs LT loading was resumed by the terminal.
ImmediateAction: Crew was summoned in the CCR and alcohol test was carried out. GS Rajan and Oiler Gopinath were tested to be beyond company's permissible alcohol content.    Both of them were immediately removed from any further duties onboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to shore Rupture Disc located on the surge pot. No injury to any personnel. There was interruption in cargo operation due to activation of ESD.
DirectCause: Non compliance with company alcohol policy.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Concerned ship staff removed from further duties immediately. To be signed off from next port for further de-briefing and investigation ashore.
Corrective Action: Concerned ship staff removed from further duties immediately. To be signed off from next port for further de-briefing and investigation ashore.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Alcohol abuse was accepted by both GS and Oiler. Both have promised for not repeating the incident. The Just Culture policy shall prevail and further action will be taken.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was drifting off Rio de Janeiro to await pilot boarding at 1530 hrs LT on 19-Apr-18. While trying to start the main engine to proceed to the pilot station at 1418 hrs LT on 19-Apr-2018, the ME did not respond on fuel. Hence the engine had to be stopped for about an hour to rectify the problem resulting in vessel missing the window to pick up the pilot at Rio de Janeiro on 19-Apr-2018.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had arrived at Rio de Janeiro from Houston at 1100 hrs LT on 19-Apr-2018 and was instructed to drift off Rio de Janeiro pilot station to pick up pilot at 1530 hrs LT on 19-Apr-2018. Vessel stopped engines at a safe distance from the pilot station and nearest land and commenced drifting at 1130 hrs LT on 19-Apr-2018. Vessel started preparing to proceed to the pilot station and engines were ordered standby at 1412 hrs LT on 19-Apr-2018. However due to problem with the fuel racks on 5 ME units, vessel could not use the ME.
ImmediateAction: Additional lookout was posted and vessel's status on AIS was changed to 'Not under command'. Relevant day light signals were displayed. Power pack and bow thruster were started and anchor party was ordered to be standby at the forecastle to drop anchor if required. Vessel's set and drift was monitored. Pilots and agents were informed of the situation and agets request to notify the parties as required. Office was informed about the situation. Engine room team started rectification of the problem with additional hands.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to ME fuel racks on unit number 3, 4, 5, 6 and 7 getting stuck, ME could not be started.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: Lubrication of Main Engine fuel pump needs to be closely monitored
ReviewComments: Overhaul interval of main engine fuel pumps were reduced from 8000 hrs to 6000 hrs
ClosingNote: Vessel carried out overhaul of all Main Engine fuel pumps .

IncidentDetails: On the 23rd March’18, the oiler suffered an fall through open floor gratings, resulting in injuring his knee. The oiler rejected any medical treatment and the 2nd engineer did not report the same to the master. On the 31st March’18, the 2nd officer came to know about the incident and on observing the wound, concluded that the wound was healing, and did not report the same to the master.  On the 3rd of April’18 the oiler reported to the 2nd engineer, that the wound was pulsating and swelling up. The master was informed at this juncture, and only rest was given. The following day, the oiler developed a fever, with constant pain in the injured area accompanied with severe swelling. The master dispensed antibiotics and painkillers to the oiler, for 7 days, but there was no improvement.  On the 10th April’18, the company doctor was consulted, where the oiler was recommended to be signed off asap for immediate medical attention as the wound has reached a very serious state
IncidentEventDetails: Please see attached "incident report".
ImmediateAction: Reported same to office for immediate medical advise.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The crew / patient need for repatration for further medical attention.
DirectCause: Please see full report.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediate reporting as required.
ClosingNote: All incidents and near miss to be report to seniors on board and in turn reported to office

IncidentDetails: Mr. Anton OS reported that he fell down on deck between frames 92 and 90 while crossing longitudinals. He had a slight impact on his back with the longitudinal resulting in back pain. He has been given rest after this.
IncidentEventDetails: Mr. Anton OS was crossing the deck longitudinals with his needle gun during routine deck maintenance. He slipped and fell down and had impact on his back. Given rest from work after medical advise.
ImmediateAction: He has been given first aid after reporting by him. Given medicines as per advise from Dr. Balaji. He was later signed off from Mailiao, Taiwan on 13th April 2018.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No visible injuries to the OS. He has been given off duty due to back pain.
DirectCause: Wrong practice of walking over deck longitudinal instead of crossing them over.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed about the incident. Avoid crossing longitudinals during routine maintenance jobs. Use walkways for crossing deck. Always follow safety precautions.
Corrective Action: dgfdg
LessionLearnt: All advised to avoid crossing bays on deck by stepping on longitudinals frames. Senior Officers to monitor the same on continous basis.
ClosingNote: All advised to avoid crossing bays on deck by stepping on longitudinals frames. Senior Officers to monitor the same on continous basis.

IncidentDetails: 2 thieves boarded vessel whie at Lubuk Gaung (Dumai) anchorage, broke the door lock and entered Engine Room. Took Oiler & 3rd Engineer under custody, threatened these two to give ship's spare. When the thieves did not find what they wanted, they disembarked.
IncidentEventDetails: 0234 Lt: 3/E reported from E/R that 2 robbers with long knives entered E/R  0234 Lt: Alarm Raised  0235 Lt: Witnessed robbers leaving the ship by boat  0237 Lt: All crew assembled on bridge, all accounted for & no injury reported  0239 Lt: Situation briefed to all crew members  0245 Lt: All staff were divided into teams of 4 members for search  0247 Lt: Commence searching the vessel for security breach, each teams were allotted areas on board to cover. 4 persons were stationed on AP watch, 2 forward & 2 aft during the search.  0310 Lt: Vessel tried to contact Port Control on VHF & agent on phone, no response  0330 Lt: Vessel tried to contact Port Control on VHF & agent on phone, no response  0247 to 0345 Lt: During the search, all teams were reporting to bridge on completion of searching spaces/decks within their allotted areas.  0345 Lt: All teams reported as search completed, all spaces found secured and doors found locked.  0350 Lt: All crew members assembled back on bridge and all accounted for.  0350 to 0400 Lt: Briefing of the incident and possible cause & additional watch arranegements, precautions discussed.  0400 Lt: Reported CSO about the incident.
ImmediateAction: Alarm Raised, all crew assembled on bridge, all accounted for & no injury reported.  Situation briefed to all crew members, all staff were divided into teams of 4 members for search.  Commence searching the vessel for security breach, each teams were allotted areas on board to cover. 4 persons were stationed on AP watch, 2 forward & 2 aft during the search.  Vessel tried to contact Port Control on VHF & agent on phone, no response  Vessel tried to contact Port Control on VHF & agent on phone, no response  During the search, all teams were reporting to bridge on completion of searching spaces/decks within their allotted areas.  All teams reported as search completed, all spaces found secured and doors found locked.  All crew members assembled back on bridge and all accounted for.  Briefing of the incident and possible cause & additional watch arranegements, precautions discussed.  Reported CSO about the incident.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One door lock leading to engine room is broken. Same has been reparied on board.
DirectCause: Insufficient number of anti-piracy watches for prevailing weather conditions (raining & reduced visibility).  Proper lock down procedure not followed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional anti-piracy watches should be posted basis weather conditions & vessel's requirement in order to cover watches around entire ship side contiuously.  Hourly checks of all doors and entrance to be done to ensure all openings are shut properly.
ClosingNote: Investigation completed, corrective and preventive action implemented.

IncidentDetails: Two hours after the topping off of Slop P COT during loading at Yosu, noticed change of ullage of Slop P COT & ROT. On calculation, it was observed that 16.8 m3 of cargo had shifted from Slop P COT to ROT.
IncidentEventDetails: 1005: Topping off of Slop S COT completed, final ullage 3.20 M.  1005: Topping off of Slop P COT completed, final ullage 3.40 M.  1100: Hourly ullages monitored by duty officer, no discrepancy noted, Slop P ullage 3.40 M, ROT ullage 11.81 M  1200: Hourly ullages monitored by duty officer, no discrepancy noted, Slop P ullage 3.40 M, ROT ullage 11.81 M  1200: Topping off of 3P COT completed, final ullage 1.35 M.  1200: Topping off of 3S COT completed, final ullage 1.34 M.  1210: Noted ullage of Slop P COT to be 3.45 M.  1220: Noted ullage of Slop P COT to be 3.55 M, noted ROT ullage 11.40 M  1220: Commenced checking of all valves to/from Slop P & ROT in CCR, Hydraulic room, Pumproom & Deck.  1240: All valves checked & found shut.  1245: All controllable hydraulic valves lever put in shut position from neutral. Hydraulic oil level checked & found normal.  1250: Slop P ullage 3.84 M, ROT 10.59 M.    1300: Noted Slop P ullage steady at 3.84 M, ROT 10.59 M.  1531: Topping off of 7P COT completed, final ullage 1.30 M.  1531: Topping off of 7S COT completed, final ullage 1.30 M.  1542: Topping off of 5S Completed, final ullage 2.76 M.  1542: Completed loading operations.
ImmediateAction: Checked position of all valves to/from Slop P & ROT in CCR, Hydraulic room, Pumproom & Deck. All controllable hydraulic valves lever put in shut position from neutral. Hydraulic oil level checked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Two hours after the topping off of Slop P COT during loading at Yosu, noticed change of ullage of Slop P COT & ROT. On calculation, it was observed that 16.8 m3 of cargo had shifted from Slop P COT to ROT.
DirectCause: Cause analysis:  Direct Cause    1. The valves 012V and 013V are position controlled hydraulic valves operated from the foam room. These valves had their control lever kept in the neutral position since last operation. Due to long period of not being operated, the valves might have opened slightly as there was no pressure in the hydraulic line causing cargo to drain from Slop-P to ROT tank. When the slop-P tank was loaded at Chiba, the level of cargo was below the stripper pump discharge line with these valves. However, when the reloading of slop-P was done with cargo of reformate and the cargo level went above the pipeline with valves 012V and 013, cargo drained from Slop-P to ROT.   Indirect Cause 1. The ship staff were monitoring the hourly records of COTs however, it seems the ullage of ROT was not being monitored. Root Cause 1. Failure to follow procedures
Proposedcorrectiveactions: refer below
Corrective Action: 1. A notice to be pasted in CCR and the hydraulic room to guide all watchkeepers the correct operation of the handling lever for controllable hydraulic valves.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: damage to store while lowering from  sky light. The wooden pallet topped while lowering and store fell down on engine room platform from the skylight..
IncidentEventDetails: • 0100LT: Vessel All fast at Hong Kong.   • At 0330LT, delivery person for engine room stores came onboard with invoices. Duty A/B informed 3/Eng who was standing by at the gangway about the stores.  • Duty officer ( 2nd Officer) relieved duty AB on gangway for preparing the Monorail who was assisted by 2 O/S on watch.  • Duty Engineer ( 3/Engr) and Oiler came on deck for assisting and checking of E/R stores.  • The pallet was secured with 2 slings as per normal practice and AB started lifting the store from Jetty. 3/Engr, Oiler and OS were stand by.   • Once secured on monorail hook, duty AB started heaving up the pallet.  • Once the stores were on deck level, the AB started to slide the stores towards the skylight where Duty Engineer and oiler were standby.  • Duty AB and Duty Engineer noticed that the air draft from skylight was coming out with great force and noticed the pallet shaking slightly. However, assuming that this is just at mouth of skylight, they continued to lower the pallet further. .  • Just about 3-4meters below the skylight, the wooden pellet on which the stores were kept toppled completely and stores fell on E/R Main Engine platform.  • Duty A/B reported the incident to duty officer who was on the gangway who further informed master and chief officer.   Upon further investigation, it was noted that stores on pallet were not secured to the pallet but kept on top wrapped in plastic as one unit.
ImmediateAction: office informed  ,investigation carried out to find out the lead which cause the damage.   tried to repair on board by using the old spare.
DirectCause: Improper supervision while lifting and lowering the stores.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to be done. Procedure put in place to have senior officer in charge of any lifting operation.
ClosingNote: This incident was discussed during safety meeting .

IncidentDetails: Minor fire occured over Main Engine Unit#7 exhaust pipe due to paint spillage while painting on M/E Jacket cooling water line.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) After toolbox meeting, duty motorman and wiper was instructed to carryout painting over M/E JCW line.   2) The paint container was kept over the exhaust valve # 7 and painting carried out.   3) Small amount of paint spilled over the M/E Unit#7 exhaust pipe and immediately caught fire.   4) Crew immediately removed the paint container and cleared from the fire site to safety.   5) 2/E and Crew immediately used nearby two portable fire extinguishers and extinguished fire. Same time fire alarm occurred. C/E was present at the site.  6) Bridge informed about the incident and fire watch kept to avoid re-ignition.   7) Used fire extinguishers inspected and refilled.
ImmediateAction: Paint container removed from sight and fire put off immediately by using Portable Foam extinguishers.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight Paint peal off in adjacent area.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) NO Paint container to be kept near any hot surface.   2) RA to be made and followed while painting near hot surface.   3) Fresh Water hose and Fire extinguisher to be kept st-by at all times.   4) Duty Engineer to be st-by at the site.
ClosingNote: 1) NO Paint container to be kept near any hot surface.   2) RA to be made and followed while painting near hot surface.   3) Fresh Water hose and Fire extinguisher to be kept st-by at all times.   4) Duty Engineer to be st-by at the site.

IncidentDetails: On 19 April 2018 , at Sri racha ESSO MBM terminal, While disconnecting flexible hose after completion of cargo operation by the shore gang , butterfly valve of the hose was not closed properly by them ,once the hose was disconnected from manifold and lifted by crane .All the oil content of the hose spilled into the drip tray and sprayed on deck.
IncidentEventDetails: After competion of the cargo operation , all the manifold lines was drained into 4S COT . After draining ,manifold lines and drains was closed .Flexible hose was provided  with Butterfly valve and a  blank. Same time butterfly valve of flexible hose was closed by shore crew which was not crosscheck by foreman handling the disconnection operation, Once the hose was disconnected from manifold , foreman signalled crane operator to lift the hose for blanking the hose. Hose was lifted by crane operator and due to gravity all the oil conent of hose spilled into the drip tay and sprayed on deck
ImmediateAction: All the scuppers checked , Butterfy valve tightened by the shore personal and immediately hose blanked.Oil in drip tray transfetred into 4S COT .and deck cleaned  by ship and shore personals
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage or injury.
DirectCause: Lack of skill, lack of procedures and Inadequate leadership
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Same to be discussed in every cargo meeting
Corrective Action: the accident injury to be discussed in the safety committee meeting.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: Upon departure from Pembroke (U.K.) on 1st April 2018, vessel has been experiencing severe rough weather conditions. on 06th April, after alteration of course to provide lee,  inspection of deck was carried out & it was observed that the starboard side manifold drip tray gratings (4 Nos) were displaced and lying on main deck. Lashing of one reducer had broken and it was lying on main deck. Also found one of the bottom railings at the starboard manifold below the hose rest  broken from the joint. The life jacket/ immersion suit box(near forecastle) cover was not there and the box was found empty. After thorough search it was concluded that 3 life jackets and 2 immersion suits were missing from the box.
IncidentEventDetails: Upon departure from Pembroke (U.K.) on 1st April 2018, vessel has been experiencing severe rough weather conditions.   5th Apr 2018 /     1700 LT - It was observed from bridge that one of the life jacket from forward was lying on the main deck near  manifold area.   1745 LT - Chief Officer and team of 2 crew members went on deck to collect the life jacket and inspect the forecastle area.   After collecting the life jacket, the deck team proceeded to forecastle deck for inspection and to check the condition of Life jacket/ Immersion box.  Due to heavy gusts of wind and continuous shipping of seas/ spray on deck, it was not possible to go forward.  It was decided that inspection of forecastle area will be carried out when the weather subsides.  6th Apr 2018 /     0400 LT - It was observed that the starboard side manifold drip tray gratings (4 Nos) were displaced and lying on main deck. Also, lashing of one reducer had broken and it was lying on main deck.  0500 LT -  After alteration of course to provide lee, the deck team proceeded for securing and inspection. Two life jackets were found near the manifold area.  After securing, crew members proceeded forward for inspection. One immersion suit was collected from the main deck near fwd spring winch.  The life jacket/ immersion suit box cover was not there and the box was found empty. After thorough search it was concluded that 3 life jackets and 2 immersion suits were missing.  Also, one of the bottom railings at the starboard manifold below the hose rest was found broken from the joint.
ImmediateAction: Forecastle deck / Fore peak store and paint store were inspected and everything found to be in order. Anchor lashings were tightened. Gangway lashings were also tightened.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss of 03 Life Jackets and 02 Immersion suits.
DirectCause: Vessel experienced very rough weather gale force winds of 50 kts. Formation of new low pressure in the vicinity.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Monitor weather forecasts, extra lashings to be in place taking into account  severe weather condition that could be encountered during the voyage.
Corrective Action: Monitor weather forecasts, extra lashings to be in place taking into account  severe weather condition that could be encountered during the voyage.
ClosingNote: Satisfactory training and effectively closed.

IncidentDetails: Damage to fwd mooring spring  line tail rope.
IncidentEventDetails: 1448 : Commenced Unmooring  1506 : All cast off (springs last)  After casting off the springs the tugs were pulling the ship off the berth and vessel was heaving up its spring lines   vessels fwd spring line tail got stuck with fender securing chain shackle and got damaged  As soon as the tail which was suppose to slide off easily got stuck with one of the shackles of the chain used to secure   the shore fender, vessel immediately stopped heaving but due to the movement of the vessel ,weight was taken by the   rope tail and In the process the strands got damaged. The tail was taken onboard. Please see the attached pictures .  Same was brought to the pilot's notice and no damage to shore fender was reported.
ImmediateAction: Rope tail will be changed with the spare available
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: the rope strands of rope tail have got damaged, beyond re-use.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Closer co-ordination with the Pilot and Tug boat
Corrective Action: Master to brief the crew members involved in mooring stations to identify the obstructions during berthing and highlight this to the Bridge so that same can be considered during Master Pilot exchange and cast off pattern can be discussed to clear the berth without any incident or damage to property.
ClosingNote: All Crew members at FWD and AFT stations were briefed by the Master to  identify the obstructions during berthing and highlight this to the Bridge so that same can be considered during Master Pilot exchange and cast off pattern can be discussed to clear the berth without any incident or damage to property.

IncidentDetails: Cargo leak at manifold on increasing rate.
IncidentEventDetails: 10:45 LT - Commenced loading Propane.  11:25 LT - Requested for max loading rate.  11:30 LT - Observed leak at manifold connection / Informed terminal.  11:32 LT - Propane loading stopped.
ImmediateAction: Terminal operators were informed and cargo loading suspended. They attempted to tighten the connection and re-start, but the leak occurred again. Thereafter manifold was disconnected and addtional sealing was applied by the terminal operators, leak test was carried out again at 3 bar. Cargo re-started and loading resumed normally.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage or injury occurred. Loss of time - commercial (Total 3Hrs 10mins of stoppage time).
DirectCause: Proper Information not shared by the terminal during ship shore information exchange.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Information to be shared with all Fleet vessels.
Corrective Action: Nil. Actions taken by ship staff was in line with response and procedures for such cases. Letter of Protest will be issued to the Terminal for delays.
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting of the incident and providing the evidences for investigation. The information is shared with all fleet vessels.

IncidentDetails: During discharge operation at Mormugao anchorage, #1 crane wire parted and grab fell onto the receiving barge. Grab incurred damage - bucket dented badly.
IncidentEventDetails: - 0925 hrs  Commenced discharging of Gypsum by #1 crane, from #2 C/H.  - 1105 hrs  While operating ,fully loaded grab touched the cargo in the barge and then sudden jerk due to wrong operation caused the runner wire to part. and grab fell onto receiving brage.  - Due to impact of fall grab bucket got dented.  - No damages to barge / third party and no personal injuries.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the cargo operation and damage assessment carried out.    Further made arrangement to land the grab ashore for repairs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crane runner wire rendered useless and need to be replaced.  Grab bucket dented badly and not able to open because of the dent, shore assistance required for the repairs of the same.  No damages to any third party / No personal injuries.  No damages to ship structure or the cranes in use.
ClosingNote: Owners Surveyors, Zebec Marine carried out the investigation. They found the ship's crane/grab maintenance, condition and documents to be in order. The cause of damage is attributed to stevedore rough operation / mishandling of crane which may be to saving cost in not using payloaders in the cargo holds to move the under deck cargo and also to expedite the discharging. operations.

IncidentDetails: Propane liquid was found dripping from the presentation flange of reducer connected to Portside manifold (Outward side) while the Stbd side manifold was used for loading.
IncidentEventDetails: In the pre-arrival questionaire, vessel was told to prepare 2 manifolds on Stbd side for loading propane with 12" 300 reducers connected. Vessel had the reducers connected with the lineup ready for loading from both the Systems I & II. When the vessel was alongside, the attending Terminal Marine advisor informed the vessel that due the horizontal difference between the connecting Chickson to System I manifold, an S bend would be required to be used. S bend was arranged however vesse and terminal did not have sufficient supporting wood supports for this arrangement. It was then agreed that System II Manifold would be used for loading as the corresponding terminal Chickson was able to be connected without any additional arrangements. The loading arm to System II manifold was connected and after completion of all formalities, loading of propane was commenced. However the line master between System I and System II crossover manifold lines was not closed. During loading System I manifold line was also filled with cargo. Following is the SOF leading to the incident.   29.04.2018:  1506: Commenced Loading on System II (L2 M/F) Stbd side.  30.04.2018:  0300: Cargo found leaking from blank flange on System I manifold portside.  0306: Informed terminal to stop loading.
ImmediateAction: 0306-0436: System I manifold line drained, hydralic and manual valves ascertained to be closed using emg hydr. pump and wheel spanned respectively. After line drained, blank flange removed and Metaflex gasket was installed in place instead of Low temp GP gasket which was used. Compressors started on H/G mode and leak test carried out on System I manifold line. No leak was found on the presentation flange of the System I manifold line.   Manifold crossover valve on System I line to System II line was closed and line up check to ensure to entry of liquid into System I manifold line.   0436: Advised terminal to resume loading.   0512: Resumed loading. System I manifold line monitored , no leaks found.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No injury or damage to the vessel.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:   1) Gasket used for blanking of the manifold was old. 2) Hydraulic valve was inadvertently operated. 3) Manual valve seat ring was damaged.  Indirect Cause:   1) Gasket was considered good for reuse by the officer in-charge. 2) Status of manifold valves was required to be updated on CCR notice board.   3) Wear and tear with time of manual valve seat ring material.  Root Cause:   1) Lack of defined procedure for routine pressure testing of cargo lines to working pressure. 2) Lack of Training on inadvertent opening of cargo valves and use of appropriate tools for the jobs.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All officers keeping cargo watch and Gas Engineer to be trained for operation of cargo valves and associated systems on LG cargo panel.
Corrective Action: All officers keeping cargo watch and Gas Engineer to be trained for operation of cargo valves and associated systems on LG cargo panel.
ClosingNote: Appreciate the investigation process and effective corrective actions taken.

IncidentDetails: While at sea, observed 3S Fwd COW machine Control unit condensate hole leaking drip by drip.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had completed loading Kimanis Crude Oil and sailed out from Kimanis Malaysia. While at Sea, Observed small Oil leak from 3S F Cow machine.(approx 2 to 3 Litres of Crude Oil). Confirmed all scuppers were plugged and no oil went overboard. Announcement made and Crew mustered for clean up. Leak was arrested by lowering IG pressureand subsequently opening machine valve . Spill  cleaned up and all equipments secured.
ImmediateAction: IG pressure lowered to 3.3 kpa. Oil spill was cleaned up immediately. IG pressure at the time of incident was 8.9 kpa.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No injury.
DirectCause: material failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Control unit to be removed and O Ring to be checked/renewed.
Corrective Action: Control unit to be removed and O Ring to be checked/renewed.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: During routine Master weekly inspection all equipment and condition of spaces were checked.  We found out that Public Address System(PAS) on compass deck out of order.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0830LT 06 May 2018 Master, Chief Officer & Chief Engineer carried out weekly inspection and found defect on Public Address System(PAS).
ImmediateAction: Cheif Engineer called 2/E and 4/E to invistigate the cause of malfunction and found out that wire coils on the speaker was corroded and teared. To order locally on next port as local purchase per sample. If not available to tendered as PO in Ship Palm.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor
DirectCause: Deterioration due to weather exposure even it is covered by tarpauline still prone to climatic changes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To order locally as per sample depending on availability of speaker.
ClosingNote: Agree with masters ccorrective action.

IncidentDetails: During Ballasting at Porto Acu using Ballaster Water System observed to be in good working condition until system keep on initiation only.
ImmediateAction: Immediately C/E invistigate the situation and replace with spare TRO sensor RC1 unfortunately systmens keep on initiation due to out of RC1 Electrolite.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
DirectCause: Improper inventories
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Raise PO
Corrective Action: Raise PO
ClosingNote: CA in order

IncidentDetails: Fracture of Right Leg Ankle of O.S Raju Mohan due to falling off from Vertical ladder in Hold No 1 while doing Hold cleaning at TopoloBampo Anchorage.
IncidentEventDetails: While doing hold cleaning at Topolombampo Anchorage for next Cargo Loading while O.S Raju Mohan was going down on the Fwd Vertical ladder of Hold no 1 while trying to reach out on his right side from the ladder to wipe the stains on the bulkhead lost his grip from the ladder and fell down directly on tanktop from a height of  four steps below the lower platform of the ladder.  He landed directly on his feets thereby fracturing Ankle bone of his right Leg.
ImmediateAction: Immediately attended my master & choff, Given Ice treatment & Pain killer Tablets there after transferred to ships Hospital for further treatment reported to Office,Agents,CRIM After vessel was alongside next day morning patient attended by Shore Doctor and shifted to Shore hospital.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fracture of Right feet Ankle
DirectCause: Please complete Cause Analysis
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Risk Assessment,2. Proper PPE as per PPE Matrix as per HSM ch 2.16.4,3. Crew Training of Safe Working Practices.
Corrective Action: PTW for working at height to be completed and strictly complied with when working from platform or ladder. Crew to be briefed/trained and this is to be discussed during safety meetings for May and June'18
ClosingNote: CA adequate

IncidentDetails: While picking up anchor, Windlass motor got damaged and anchor chain slipped
IncidentEventDetails: 0900 LT : MAster came on bridge observed strong wind 25 knots and gusting upto 35 knots  0900-0920 LT : Observed weather forecast and decided to pick up anchor, However as per local weather forecast there were no heavy weather forecast  0923LT : Notice to engine room  0933 Lt : Engine SBE , Fwd anchor party standby  0934 LT : Hydraulic Power pack started and confirmed by fwd.  0935 LT : Commenced heaving up anchor  0936 LT : Reported Chain across the bow long stay 9  O clock. Engines were used and helm given chain started slacking and started heaving up anchor  0939 LT : Reported 8 on deck short stay.  0939 LT : Reported Stbd Anchor motor give way and due to wait and chain slipped off  0940 LT : Brake tight and found 11 shackle in water  0942 LT : Confirmed vessel holding her position
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Windlass hydraulic motor got damage , No injury
DirectCause: Delay in analysing weather conditions and heaving anchor
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to be carried out.
Corrective Action: Incident to be shared with fleet vessels to re-emphasize on the importance of heavy weather precautions at anchor and shifting the vessel to sheltered location or safe drifting area
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Refer to attached IIR

IncidentDetails: During mooring operations at Tuzla, found that the aft spring rope passage was obstructed by the shore staging equipment. The passage to the fairleads was not clear. The aft station crew tried to pass the rope without noticing the obstruction.
IncidentEventDetails: Passage to the fairleads found obstructed and aft station crew cleared.
ImmediateAction: The staging equipment removed to provide a clear passage and the yard informed.
ClosingNote: To be raised as Near Miss.

IncidentDetails: fitter  got injured on his left foot thumb , while opening galley door   cleaned with povidone iodine solution and applied betadine , dressed the wounded area     plz find the attached medical inventory and injury pic
IncidentEventDetails: he tried went to to crew messroom through galley, ang he opened the galley door with full force without noticing his feet, door banged on his left foot thumb and his nails injured and bleeding occured from wounded area
ImmediateAction: cleaned with povidone iodine solution and applied betadine , dressed the wounded area
DirectCause: he was wearing sandals without front cover, and didnt give attention to his feet position while opening the door
Proposedcorrectiveactions: asked him to wear sandals with front cover while off duty, and advice him to care has to be taken for such situation in future.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: First Aid Case
ReviewComments: Ship Staff reported recovered on 15th Apr 2018
ClosingNote: Discussed during Safety committee meeting to ensure adequate foot wear worn during Duty and Off duty

IncidentDetails: Small fire on the canvas cover of aft  ETA. No damage or injury caused. Vessel in ballast condition
IncidentEventDetails: 1533 LT : Bosun saw smoke near the starboard quarteraft poop deck when proceeding to work after tea break. He went further to check where the smoke was coming from. He saw canvas cover of aft ETA on fire.   1534 LT : Bosun quickly ran inside the accommodation shouting " Fire ! Fire ! Fire !" and raised the alram in fire control station. Meanwhile the other crew members, in the changing room, took the exinguishers which was kept in the alleyway port and starboard to fight the fire.  1534 LT : Bridge was informed and Fire pump started. Announcement made on the PA system about location of the fire.  1539 LT : Bosun, Pumpan and OS extinguished the fire with portable extinguisher. Meanwhile other crew members mustered at the muster station and carried out their respective duites.  1540 LT : 2 numbers fire hoses rigged and cooling of adjacent areas started.  1542 LT : All area found to be clear and no sign of re-ignition of fire/ smoke observed.  1545 LT : Continued hosing down near aft ETA and adjacent compartments
ImmediateAction: Alarm was raised and portable fire extinguishers, foam type, used to fight the fire. Fire pump started and 2 hoses rigged. Hosing down near aft ETA and adjacent compartments carried out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was no damage / injury caused, only the canvas cover for the aft ETA got burnt.
DirectCause: Inadequate Supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Inform bridge when carrying out AE turbocharger washing so as to monitor aft poop deck area.  All crew members mustered and briefing about the incident carried out.
Corrective Action: Any soot blow or T/C washing in engine room to be carried out after informing bridge and with regular checks on poop deck.
LessionLearnt: It should be a continuous process and area checked thoroughly weeekly adn everyday during rounds.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: Cargo oil pump No1 Shaft Damage
IncidentEventDetails: Cargo oil pump No1  was running during cargo discharge operation at Sriracha . Leak was observed from lower oil seal . Immediately pump was stopped and discharge operations continued with No2 Cargo oil pump to avoid any further delay. Later after vessel departure from port. Pump opened up for further inspection revealed pump shaft was broken in two halves in Impeller key way area. Lower mechincal seal was completely damaged and oil seal damaged. Upper mechincal stationary seal damaged. Scoring marks seen on pump impeller and casing ring.
ImmediateAction: New shaft was ordered and Pump overhauled and fitted back.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pump shaft broken/damaged. Mechincal seal and oil seal damaged. Scoring marks on Pump impeller and Casing ring
DirectCause: Cyclic stress causing fatigue and material failure over a period
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Please refer to proposed CA as below
Corrective Action: PMS to be complied and Cargo oil pump shaft trueness and condition/ strength to be checked  at every pump overhaul. Pump Vibrations monitored and Logged at every hour during pump  operation. Vessel has planned to carry out checks for other two pumps also during vessel DD  @ Aug 2018.
ClosingNote: All proposed CA attended to

IncidentDetails: At about 1520 hrs, 1 fwd mooring line (head line) parted while trying to bring the vessel alongside. No injury and no other damages occured. At the time of parting Forward station had (3+2) made fast.
ImmediateAction: Parted line retreived back on board and another line was made fast.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: About 25 mtrs of mooring line parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tension on mooring rope to be kept at appropriate limit
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Good ropes to be used for tug operations and tug pull strength to be confirmed prior operations.
ClosingNote: Mooring rope status updated and requisition raised for new ropes.

IncidentDetails: After anchoring at "C" Anchorage Flushing, Chief Officer reported bow stopper drum is slightly damaged. Upon inspection it was observed that plates of drum have cracks.
IncidentEventDetails: 1920- Pilot advised to let go stbd Anchor at "C" Anch. Flushing. Depth 20mtr. After due consultation with Master, Pilot advised and Master ordered to pay 5shackles in water.  2000- Stbd Anch. BUT 5shackles in water.  2005- Ch.Off reported some crack observed on stbd windlass bow stopper roller.  17.05.18-0500- v/l picked up anchor and resumed voyageunder pilotage. It was observed crack has increased and plate deformed. Same reported to office.  Taken advise from technical superintendent and new plate fabricated and welded. Satisfactory.
ImmediateAction: Was under observation until v/l depart from anchorage and come out of port limit. After consulting technical superintendent, plate fabricated and welded. Satisfactory now.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to stbd. windlass bow stopper drum.
DirectCause: wear down of material
Proposedcorrectiveactions: new plate fabricated and welded.
Corrective Action: new plate fabricated and welded.
ClosingNote: Routine and visual checks to be done for material wear / tear and corrosion more frequently. Equipment exposed to weather will deteriorate early as expected.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was alongside at Parker Point , Dampier. It had completed discharge operation and awaiting pilot for unberthing due to daylight restrictions. Berth was exposed to open seas. Vessel was continuously surging owing to moderate wind & swell. Vessel's crew took all possible precautions by adjusting mooring lines as per tidal variations and weather conditions and also against chaffing. Mooring  rope parted (Stern line) at 2245 LT/ 29-May-2018.
IncidentEventDetails: Mooring  rope parted (Stern line) at 2245 LT/ 29-May-2018.  Vessel had been attending all mooring lines and they were always kept tight as per ship shore safety checklist. At berth vessel was surging continously due to moderate wind/swell. Due to continous movement of vessel at berth, above line chafed and broke. Vessel had taken all precautions necessary against chaffing of mooring line but unfortunately above  mooring line broke. No personnel injury or third party damage was caused.
ImmediateAction: Upon parting of rope, vessel immediately called up terminal and terminal swiftly called up mooring gang for attending the broken line and passing additional line.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was alongside at Parker Point, Dampier.  It had completed discharge operation and was awaiting pilot for unberthing due to daylight restrictions.  Berth was exposed to open seas. Vessel was continuously surging owing to moderate wind & swell. Vessel's crew took all possible precautions by adjusting mooring lines as per tidal variations/ weather conditions and also against chaffing.  Even after all precautions taken, mooring rope parted (Stern line) at 2245 LT/ 29-May-2018.
DirectCause: Adverse sea conditions, Exposed berth, Lack of proper supervision.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mooring line has been replaced. Incident will be discussed in safety meeting and tool box meeting. Condition of berth to be discussed during master/ Pilot exchange and with Loading master.
Corrective Action: Mooring line has been replaced. Incident will be discussed in safety meeting and tool box meeting. Condition of berth to be discussed during master/ Pilot exchange and with Loading master.
ClosingNote: Job completed 27/07

IncidentDetails: Injury (Severe Lumber Sprain) to Ch. Cook while Picking up provision.     Ch. cook and GS were inside the provision room while deck crew was passing the provision from main deck to the lobby.   While shifting a Bag of rice, Ch. cook suffered  severe lumber sprain and lied motionless on the deck due to acute pain. GS informed Ch. Officer immediately and crew mustered.
IncidentEventDetails: 1130 LT : Supplier arrived with provision in truck.   1210 LT : Ch. Cook suffered Lumber sprain and lied on deck, motionless. GS informed ch. officer of the incident.  1210 - 1300 LT : Master informed Company, consulted Company Doctor, requested agent to board vessel and arrange medical attention.  1318 : Immigration officer boarded vessel.   1350 LT : Medical team boarded vessel.   1405 LT : Ch. Cook evacuated
ImmediateAction: After observing for a while, Master informed Company. Initial care and pain relief was provided to the Cook. Master consulted Company Doctor over telephone and then contacted agent to arrange for Medical team on board.  Agent arranged for Immigration and Medical team.   Medical team arrived and evacuated the Ch. Cook. Ch. Cook Hospitalized for few days and later repatriated home with escort Nurse.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury (Severe Lumber Sprain) to Ch. Cook while Picking up provision.     Ch. cook had to be Hospitalized for 03 days, vessel sailed without Cook after receiving dispensation from Flag state.   Ch. cook diagnosed of Lumber sprain and was Hospitalized for 03 days. Later was repatriated home along with an Escort nurse.
DirectCause: Precondition aggravated by excess load lifted wrongly
Proposedcorrectiveactions: a) Matter discussed in current safety meeting. b)  Code of safe practice fro Merchant Seafarers - Ch. 10 discussed at length.  C) MCA poster displayed A-deck as reminder to crew.
Corrective Action: a) Matter discussed in current safety meeting. b)  Code of safe practice fro Merchant Seafarers - Ch. 10 discussed at length.  C) MCA poster displayed A-deck as reminder to crew.
ClosingNote: Precondition aggravated by excess load lifted wrongly

IncidentDetails: Mr./OS Calo-oy) got an injury (laceration) on his right little finger.  The injury was incurred during the setting-up or connecting of grab no. 4 into crane no. 4 where OS Calo-oy  was almost finishing the tightening of deck crane’s hook pin’s shackle when the shackle itself suddenly moved  that crushed his right little finger (as per attached photos).
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging her cargo using deck gears and grabs when the stevedores requires the grab no. 4 to de-tached from crane no. 4 to picked-up some items from shore and brought inside hold no. 5.   The injury to OS Calo-oy happened when the stevedore need to re-attached the grab to deck crane.
ImmediateAction: Master called the agent in order to send the concerned crew to the nearest shore clinic / hospital for immediate medical attention/administration.     Appreciated agent pro-active response on the same in timely manner.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Laceration on right little finger - as per attached photos.
DirectCause: Un-attentiveness in carrying out the job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety briefing & risk assessment.
Corrective Action: Safety briefing & risk assessment.
ClosingNote: Tool Box Meetings should be carried out prior commencement of job.  Proper supervision is essential in carrying working with heavy objects

IncidentDetails: VESSEL WAS ALONGSIDE AT NEWYORK ON 22 MAY 2018. CARGO OPN WAS IN PROGRESS, SAME TIME BUNKER BARGE ARRIVED AT 1615LT AND MADE FAST STBD SIDE. WHILE PICKING UP BUNKER HOSE WITH VESSELS STBD SIDE BUNKER DAVIT, THE FLOATING (LOWER) BLOCK HAS REACHED THE MAXIMUM UPPER LIMIT OF THE DAVIT AND GOT IN CONTACT WITH THE UPPER BLOCK AND BOTH BLOCKS AND WIRE GOT DAMAGED .
IncidentEventDetails: 22 MAY 2015  1348LT: VESSEL ALL MADE FAST.  1406LT: CARGO OPN COMMENCED.  1615LT: BUNKER BARGE MADE FAST.  1700LT: BUNKER HOSE WAS HEAVED UP.  1706LT: BOTH BLOCKS MADE IN CONTACT BY MISOPERATION.   1710LT BUNKER HOSE CONNECTED.
ImmediateAction: GIVEN TRAINING TO CREW MEMBERS ONBOARD.  EXPLAINED THE LIMITATIONS OF THE BUNKER DAVIT WINCH.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PROPER TRAINING CONDUCTED TO CREW ONBOARD. LIMITATIONS OF BUNKER DAVIT EXPLAINED.
Corrective Action: Limit switch to be checked for the bunker davits.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Limit switch for davits to be checked prior critical operations.
ClosingNote: Training carried out to avoid such damages in davit operation.

IncidentDetails: Engine fitter sustained a burn injury on his left hand palm upper side
IncidentEventDetails: He was carrying out gas cutting of pipe in the engine room workshop, while cutting, the cutting torch turned due to the twist in the cable and and the flame hit his left arm, he was wearing a non welding glove on his left hand in order to hold the pipe which he was cutting.
ImmediateAction: The Engine fitter did not report the incident, he assumed that it was not necessary as there was no pain in his hand and it looked normal. After a couple of days it started to show up on his hand and he reported the incident seeking medical attention. Case referred to company doctor and medication in progress as per doctors advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The engine fitter sustained a burn injury on his left hand palm upper side.
DirectCause: He was carrying out gas cutting without proper welding gloves.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During hotwork use proper HW checklist with all proper PPE.   Fitter hand is cured with no pain and irritation. photo attached.
Corrective Action: During hotwork use proper HW checklist with all proper PPE.  Fitter hand is cured with no pain and irritation. photo attached.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting

IncidentDetails: 1730lt – Crew about to knock off, while lowering the basket the vessel rolls, the safety line connected to the crane block loosens and basket start to swing violently. As the basket swings, AB Tagulo falls from the basket and hanging by the Safety harness. While hanging on the harness his left leg is also caught on the nozzles assist line. He is hanging horizontally as his left leg is also attached to the Nozzles safety line while his body is supported by harness. As he swings, his helmet falls and his head hit the CH-2 forward bulkheads flat surface. He suffered a cut on his head, near the top; about 5 to 7 cm and bleeding mildly. Patient is conscious and no bleeding observed on the nose and ears. Patient recognize his colleagues and able to move his limbs.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at Durban anchorage cleaning the cargo holds with the use of ships crane. Holds are coated with lime wash and Muriatic acid is used to remove the lime coatings.   26th May 2018  0830lt – Vessel arrived at Durban anchorage  1030lt – Start applying chemicals in cargo hold no. 4 with ships crane. A basket is connected to the crane to reach the upper hoppers. AB Tagulo is handling the nozzle on the basket.   1800lt – Completed chemical wash on cargo hold no.4    27th May 2018  0730lt – Completed tool box meeting and resume chemical wash on cargo hold no.2  1730lt – Crew about to knock off, while lowering the basket the vessel rolls, the safety line connected to the crane block loosens and basket start to swing violently. As the basket swings, AB Tagulo falls from the basket and hanging by the Safety harness. While hanging on the harness his left leg is also caught on the nozzles assist line. He is hanging horizontally as his left leg is also attached to the Nozzles safety line while his body is supported by harness. As he swings, his helmet falls and his head hit the CH-2 forward bulkheads flat surface. He suffered a cut on his head, near the top; about 5 to 7 cm and bleeding mildly. Patient is conscious and no bleeding observed on the nose and ears. Patient recognize his colleagues and able to move his limbs.  1750lt – Assist patient to come up from cargo hold no.2 and bring him to the ships Hospital. He can walk and climbed the Australian ladder on his own with the crew assisting him.  1800lt – Clean the wound of the patient, applied povidone ointment and cover with bandage. Check his vitals, BP = 138/90, Pulse = 73, Body temp = 36.2, Respiratory Rate = 19.  1830lt – Evaluate the patient’s condition, asked him if he is ok, he said yes.
ImmediateAction: 1750lt – Assist patient to come up from cargo hold no.2 and bring him to the ships Hospital. He can walk and climbed the Australian ladder on his own with the crew assisting him.  1800lt – Clean the wound of the patient, applied povidone ointment and cover with bandage. Check his vitals, BP = 138/90, Pulse = 73, Body temp = 36.2, Respiratory Rate = 19.  1830lt – Evaluate the patient’s condition, asked him if he is ok, he said yes.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MILD CONCUSION AND SMALL CUT ON THE TOP OF HIS HEAD.
DirectCause: LACK OF COMMUNICATION & COMMUNICATION EQUIPMENTS
Proposedcorrectiveactions: IN CARGO HOLD CLEANING , USE OF CRANE SHOULD BE AVOIDED.
Corrective Action: IN CARGO HOLD CLEANING , USE OF CRANE SHOULD BE AVOIDED.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Careful planning and monitoring is essential in hold cleaning.  Proper supervision and leadership is required when working aloft.

IncidentDetails: 0810LT/03 JUNE 2018 – 2 STOWAWAYS FOUND WALKING ON THE EXTERNAL STAIRCASES OF THE ACCOMMODATION STARBOARD SIDE IN POSITION LAT 28-28S / LONG 032-43.3E, EAST COAST OF SOUTH AFRICA. INTEROGATED THE STOWAWAY, THEY CONFESSED THAT HIDE IN THE ENGINE ROOM FUNNEL SPACE AREA AND THERE ARE 4 OF THEM ONBOARD. THEY PASSED ON THE FUNNEL ACCESS DOOR ON THE WAY OUT TO EXTERNAL ACCOMMODATION AND BRIDGE WINGS.
IncidentEventDetails: 03 JUNE 2018/0810LT – 2 STOWAWAYS FOUND WALKING ON THE EXTERNAL STAIRCASES OF THE ACCOMMODATION STARBOARD SIDE IN POSITION LAT 28-28S / LONG 032-43.3E, EAST COAST OF SOUTH AFRICA. INTEROGATED THE STOWAWAY, THEY CONFESSED THAT HIDE IN THE ENGINE ROOM FUNNEL SPACE AREA AND THERE ARE 4 OF THEM ONBOARD. THEY PASSED ON THE FUNNEL ACCESS DOOR ON THE WAY OUT TO EXTERNAL ACCOMMODATION AND BRIDGE WINGS.     0820LT – ORDERED TO CARRY OUT THOROUGH STOWAWAY SEARCH FOUND 2 MORE STOWAWAYS IN THE ENGINE ROOM BOILER AREA, EXPOSED WAITING TO BE DISCOVERED. INTEROGATED THE STOWAWAYS, THEY SAID THAT THERE ARE 4 OF THEM ON BOARD AND THEY HAVE HIDE ENGINE ROOM FUNNEL SPACE AND GAINED ACCESS TO THE ENGINE ROOM BY TAMPERING THE PADLOCK OF THE BOILER DECK ACCESS DOOR OF THE E/R.    0840LT – CALLED DPA AND ALTERNATE DPA AND INFORMED THEM THAT 4X STOWAWAYS HAD BEEN DISCOVERED ON BOARD 11 HOURS AFTER SAILING FROM DURBAN, S.AFRICA.   STOWAWAYS THEN LOCKED IN THE TALLY OFFICE, PROVIDED WITH SUITABLE ACCOMMODATION AND REGULAR FOOD AND WATER AND PLACED THEM WITH 24 HOUR SECURITY WATCH.    0900LT – ORDERED BY DPA AND ALTERNATE DPA TO PROCEED DIRECTLY TO MAPUTO WHERE THE STOWAWAYS WILL BE OFF LANDED.
ImmediateAction: CALLED DPA AND ALTERNATE DPA AND INFORMED THEM THAT 4X STOWAWAYS HAD BEEN DISCOVERED ON BOARD 11 HOURS AFTER SAILING FROM DURBAN, S.AFRICA.   STOWAWAYS THEN LOCKED IN THE TALLY OFFICE, PROVIDED WITH SUITABLE ACCOMMODATION AND REGULAR FOOD AND WATER AND PLACED THEM WITH 24 HOUR SECURITY WATCH.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: VESSEL SHOULD BE ON SECURITY LEVEL 2 ON STOWAWAY PRONE AREAS/PORTS
Corrective Action: VESSEL SHOULD BE ON SECURITY LEVEL 2 ON STOWAWAY PRONE AREAS/PORTS
ClosingNote: CA noted

IncidentDetails: Ship's touched the bottom due to insufficient depth in  pier.
IncidentEventDetails: After berthing at the Ocean port terminal in Claymont on 11 June'18 at 12300 hrs LT vessel started cargo discharging operation at 1430. (Perlite cargo in bulk).                                                                                                                                                                      On day of 12 June'18 at 0130 hrs LT While Continuing on her cargo discharging operation vessel noticed some discrepancies in cargo operation.  0145-0230 hrs LT vessel checked sounding of all ballast tanks  0230-0300 hrs LT Vessel checked sounding all around vessel by hand lead line  0330 Hrs confirmed vessel touching bottom
ImmediateAction: Started de-ballasting operation from  fore peak tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: To be ascertain after u/w inspection
DirectCause: Insufficient depth in pier
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Agents message on the depth at the pier mentioned as WOG and vessel berthed with her stern overhanging from the pier. Attach message of agents correspondence along with this report. In cases such as this, Master should immediately express his concerns and address this issue.
Corrective Action: repaint
LessionLearnt: Information exchange with the agents  prior berthing to be studied through the lines and understood.CP clauses to be checked for clause NAABSA.
ClosingNote: Verified and closed

IncidentDetails: While entering the accommodation the crew member slipped with right foot on the step inside the upper deck.Due to the the slip the left foot got hit on the Door Sil which lead to skin minor scratches and swelling.

IncidentDetails: During trial run of emergency fire pump during fire drill, the mechanical seal got damage, rendering critical equipment in operational.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the pump and renewed the seal.
DirectCause: Due to normal wear and tear. Mech seals are susceptible to damage in murky SW and where sand is there in SW.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The vacuum pump to be always checked for working and Seal and pump function to be checked atleast weekly. Spares are already as part of critical spares.
Corrective Action: The vacuum pump to be always checked for working and Seal and pump function to be checked atleast weekly. Spares are already as part of critical spares.
LessionLearnt: all critical equipment working to verify weekly, and spares to verify every month.
ReviewComments: all checked.
ClosingNote: Pump to be manually checked every week, min critical equipment spares to always maintain.

IncidentDetails: Minor oil spray spilled on deck due to pin hole leak developed in the COW branch line of # 5P COT
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was carrying out Crude Oil washing and Internal stripping of cargo tanks as bulk discharging was  completed at 11th Jun’1400 hrs LT. Around 1555 hrs LT, Vessel completed crude oil washing of 5Ws  COTs for the second time as per Surveyor’s request, and after checking the residues in the tanks, which  was around 0.5-1.0 cm of sludge, Surveyor insisted again for crude oil washing of 5Ws for the third  time to get NIL ROB in the COT, during which a pin hole developed on the COW line, leading to minor  Oil spill on deck.
ImmediateAction: Pump RPM was reduced immediately, and the main COW line isolating valve was closed cutting off the  supply in the COW line. Pump RPM was further reduced to minimum and discharge valve was closed.  The route and branch valve of the affected line was shut and COW line was drained to slop S.  Straub coupling was used in location to arrest further leaks from the holed pipeline. Clean-up was  engaged immediately using SOPEP equipment and same completed by around 1645 hrs LT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: PIN HOLE IN THE COW BRANCH LINE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To immediately check the condition of other COW branch lines and identify those which needs  replacement. To replace with spare available onboard.DD specs made for rotation of COW lines and renew lines as applicable.
Corrective Action: To immediately check the condition of other COW branch lines and identify those which needs replacement. To replace with spare available onboard.
ClosingNote: Pls refer to attached report. Oil Majors reported thru OCIMF

IncidentDetails: Hydraulic pipe in Hatch No. 1 ruptured.
IncidentEventDetails: While opening of cargo holds to commence discharging operations, hydraulic pipe ruptured. Causing oil leaking on deck and eventually some delays to the cargo operations.
ImmediateAction: Control the oil leakage to prevent oil going overboard. Remove the broken pipe, repaired in the engine room and  installed back again
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The pipe was corroded. Due to its condition, with the hydraulic pressure during opening, the pipe bursted.
DirectCause: the oil leakage is due to the corroded pipes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All hydraulic pipes must inspected and derrusted. Replace the sub standard pipes and/or corroded pipes
Corrective Action: All hydraulic pipes must inspected and derrusted. Replace the sub standard pipes and/or corroded pipes
ClosingNote: Routine checks to be done more frequently.

IncidentDetails: Fwd breast line - Fwd wire tail rope parted , Fwd breast line - Aft wire rope - 1 strand out of Six strand was found broken near the Panama choke  - (Mooring rope wire construction - 6 x 36 ,  and Dia 36 mm ) . Location of broken strand - around 20 mtrs from one eye end . lenghth of wire rope - 250 mtrs .  No personal injury & No enviremental release.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging cargo and was doing stripping of the Cargo Tanks.  1930 LT :- Mooring ropes were last tended by the Duty AB.  1950 LT :- Rounds on deck by taking over Duty officer   High tide timing - 2014 LT -   Local tide Table timing available in CCR & at Manifold. All duty hands aware of tide timing .  Present Port is tidal port having tidal range of up to 5.50 Mtrs and tidal stream up to  5-6 Knots.  Vessel safely stayed at anchorage for 10 days and did discharge of first parcel on same berth safely on 20-22 June 2018 .  Same widely discussed many times .     Vessel Port side alongside . ( Mooring arrangement - 4+2+2 Fwd & Aft All mooring wires )  Vessel was having 0.5 Degree List to Port to facilitate the Stripping of Stbd COT .  2000 LT :- As Stbd COT were stripped and for stripping the Port COT , Stbd list was required ( Away from jetty ) .  List was being adjusted from Port to stbd ,   2015 LT :- Fwd breast line - Fwd wire tail rope parted , Fwd breast line - Aft wire rope - 1 strand out of Six strand was found broken near the Panama   choke  - (Mooring rope wire construction - 6 x 36 ,  and Dia 36 mm ) . Location of broken strand - around 20 mtrs from one eye end . lenghth of wire rope - 250 mtrs .    Last Brake rendering test was done on 12-MAY-2018.
ImmediateAction: Fwd breast line -Aft wire rope was slacken to reduce the weight and broken tail of Fwd breast line - Fwd wire was renewed . Contineous Monitoring & tending of Mooring lines. (There was no delay on vessel's account. Cargo was completed at 2136 LT and POB for Dep at 0136 LT )
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fwd breast line - Fwd wire tail rope parted , Fwd breast line - Aft wire rope - 1 strand out of Six strand was found broken near the Panama  choke  - (Mooring rope wire construction - 6 x 36 ,  and Dia 36 mm ) . Location of broken strand - around 20 mtrs from one eye end . lenghth of wire rope - 250 mtrs . No Personnel injury and Enviromental release
DirectCause: Mooring practice not properly followed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1 :-Crew rebriefed to tighten the winch brake to the marking done for rendering and recheck to ensure that winch brake is not tighten more .  2 :-Duty officer to recheck & ensure that winch brake is not tighten more during the rounds .  3 :-Tide timings are to be borne in mind & should be repeated & reminded to all the duty personal on hourly basis by duty officer  & during the taking over / handing over the watch.  4 :-If List is being adjusted , Same to be communicated to all deck hands with instructions to be extra cautious for the moorings.  5 :- All the Duty personnel ( including duty officer & Duty AB ) to take extensive & effective rounds before taking over the watch as per the well laid company procedure .  6 :- Duty AB & Duty OS to have continuous monitoring of Gangway , Manifold & tending of Moorings lines. If being assigned other duties, There should be proper replacement for continueous monitoring of Gangway , Manifold & tending of Moorings lines . If required extra persons to be called without any hesitation at any time  but continuous monitoring of Gangway , Manifold & tending of Moorings lines not to be left unmonitored .   7 :- Retendering test  to  be again carried out  for the mooring winches for Fwd Breast lines.  8 :- Mooring tails to be inspected before every mooring operation .
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Brakes never to be tightened more than the rendering settings.
ClosingNote: Brakes never to be tightened more than the rendering settings.
