IncidentDetails: Bunker Fuel Oil overflowed on deck from 2S HFO tank vent due to primary means of containment failure.
IncidentEventDetails: 07.07.2018 / 0800 LT : Deck crew found HFO overflown on deck from 2S FOT vent. Alarm was raised immediately from Upper deck manual call point followed by announcement on PA.  07.07.2018 / 0802 LT : All crew mustered on deck for clean up operation. Tank valve for 1S & 2S FOT were opened to reduce ullage of 2S FOT.  07.07.2018 / 0803 LT : Commenced clean up operation. Commenced taking sea water in 4P WBT to make 1degree port list in order to contain all spilled oil on deck.  07.07.2018 / 0802 - 0810 LT : Checks carried out all around the ship and in water. It was confirmed that no oil spill has gone overboard. At the time of incident all the scuppers on Poop deck were in place which have prevented oil going overboard.  07.07.2018 / 0810 LT : DPA & MSI were informed about oil spill incident on phone.  07.07.2018 / 1300 LT : Completed clean up operation and spilled oil treansfeered to sludge tank.
ImmediateAction: Alarm raised and all crew mustered for clean up operation. Tank valves for 1S & 2S FOT were open to reduces the ullage in 2S FOT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Heavy Fuel oil overflown from 2S FOT vent on deck.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis Direct cause 1. The weather conditions on the passage deteriorated with vessel rolling to approx. 5 deg. 2. The vessel had a starboard list of 0.5-0.8 deg.    3. The overfill alarm for the 2S HFO tank was inoperational. Spares had been ordered and were awaiting supply to vessel.  4. 2S HFO tank was filled more than 90%(upto 93.9%) without complying with company procedures.  Indirect cause 1. Chief Engineer had joined the company only 1 month ago and did not understand that internal transfer is also to be considered as bunkering and tank should not exceed 90% without approval from office. 2. The camber of the vessel which effectively reduced the ullage in the tank at the vent as compared  to the sounding pipe. 3. The overfill alarm for the 2S HFO tank was inoperational. Spares had been ordered and were awaiting supply to vessel.    Root Cause 1. Lack of Planning 2. Lack of standards 3. Lack of compliance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All bunker tanks not to be filled up more than 85% of total capacity at any given time. Regular sounding of bunker tanks and constant monitoring of temperature to be carried out religiously.Integrity of tank valves and steam line valves to be checked and fault to be rectified without delay.
Corrective Action: 1. The equalizing valve for 2S HFO tank and 1S HFO tank was opened to avoid any oil coming on deck.
ClosingNote: attached investigation report

IncidentDetails: Nave Dorado experienced engine problems while attempting to depart the Phillips 66 Rodeo terminal on the evening of 23 June 2018. The issue caused nearly a two hour delay before the vessel vacated the berth.
IncidentEventDetails: On 23rd June 2018 at around 1930hrs LT Nave Dorado while preparing Main engines for departure from P66 Terminal, Rodeo, after loading, a leak had developed from the flange between L.O. cooler Sea water inlet pipe and the L.O. cooler, in the bottom section. The leak was rectified and the vessel safely sailed out of the port. There was a delay of about 2 hours due to this trouble.
ImmediateAction: The securing bolts for the flange connection were checked and found fully tight. It was suspected that the leakage could be due to worn out gasket in the flange and this necessitated stopping the sea water supply to the L.O. Cooler and open the pipe section and replace the gasket.  Pilot was informed about the plan and requested to inform the Port Control accordingly. 2 tugs were attending the vessel during this time.  After obtaining clearance from the Pilot, the pipe section was removed. As suspected, the gasket was found cracked and a same was replaced with a new gasket immediately. As soon as the repairs were completed and the Main Engines tested, the vessel safely unberthed at around 2115 hrs and resumed her transit.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage/Injury
DirectCause: After investigating the cause of failure of the gasket, it was contributed to normal wear and tear. When the cracked gasket was inspected, it was observed that due to ageing, the gasket had become hard and brittle.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Since there is an existing policy to maintain onboard sufficient spare gasket material to fabricate the necessary gaskets onboard, there was no need for any additional procedures. It was reinforced that permanent repairs needs to be completed as soon as tell-tale signs are sighted for any pipelines in the form of drop by drop leakage or seepage stains.
Corrective Action: Since there is an existing policy to maintain onboard sufficient spare gasket material to fabricate the necessary gaskets onboard, there was no need for any additional procedures. It was reinforced that permanent repairs needs to be completed as soon as tell-tale signs are sighted for any pipelines in the form of drop by drop leakage or seepage stains.
LessionLearnt: During daily, weekly and montly cecks if any gasket or pipe is found dripping or susceptible with stains, it is to be immediately repaired.
ReviewComments: All SW pipes and gaskets checked.
ClosingNote: All SW pipe gaskets and connections checked.

IncidentDetails: Bended railings and deformed flatform
IncidentEventDetails: While duty AB is carrying out deck rounds (heavy rain), he noticed that Port side Combi ladder was running without someone in the control. AB immediately shut off the electrical supply but it’s too late, it causes to damage the railings of the ladders. Duty officer inform CO & Master to assess the situation. Electrician was called to check the condition of electrical wires & motors. Electrician found out that some part of the wires was sand blasted during dry-dock causes it to short circuit the system.
ImmediateAction: Repair damaged wirings and ladder railings
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage electrical wire, bended railing and deform flatform
DirectCause: Sand blasted electrical wires while on dry-dock
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To install pipe protection electrical wire areas. For the ship's staff, after drydock ensure all wirings must be in good and opearational condition prior departure DD.
Corrective Action: To install pipe protection electrical wire areas. For the ship's staff, after drydock ensure all wirings must be in good and opearational condition prior departure DD.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting. Electrical supply to be shut while the equipment is not in use.

IncidentDetails: While loading operation was in progress at Trinidad STS area, vessel dragged anchor. While relocating to anchorage position, mooring lines parted resulting in separation of the vessels and complete damage to the cargo hose.   2 mooring lines from aft rolled out completely from drum when brakes were tight resulting in damage to brake liners.   Two fwd rope tails parted.
IncidentEventDetails: 14/1730 Hrs LT:- All made fast  14/2118 Hrs Lt:- Commenced loading  14/2248 Hrs LT:- Commenced heaving up P Anchor  14/2312 Hrs LT:- Anchor aweigh  15/0053 Hrs LT:- First line from SV parted / cargo transfer stopped  15/0057 Hrs LT:- Cargo hose parted / SV separated  15/0106 Hrs LT:- Aft mooring lines paid out completely  15/0110 Hrs LT:- Angcy alarm raised. Clean up on deck started  15/0212 Hrs LT:- Vessel proceeded to safe anchorage
ImmediateAction: Vessel was manouvered clear of other vessels and proceeded to safe anchorage. Emgcy stations called for clean up operation on deck. Local authorities informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Present extent of damage ( own vessel):-  1. Fwd rope tails parted, mooring line intact  2. Aft winch brake lines totally worn out. Astern lines unrolled from drums and hanging on service vessel.   3. Considerable weight on the crane & the block resulting in parting of securing straps.   STS Gear:-  1. 2 cargo Hoses parted at the ends.  Around 5 Bbls of oil spill on deck which is being contained and transferred to the cargo tanks. So far no oil has gone overboard. Surrounding water areas was inspected by port authorities using service boat and confirmed that no traces of oil in water.   No injuries on own vessel and the service vessel
DirectCause: Lack of standard / Lack of planning / LAck of compliance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pls refer to addressed CA
Corrective Action: Fleet alert sent to all vessels describing about the incident and emphasized on analysis of situation during STS operation

IncidentDetails: Feed water valve for automatic feeding water to boiler cascade tank malfunction due to broken of valve seat
IncidentEventDetails: At 12:00H ,16.AUG.2018 while carry out handling overwatch, Third engineer found that the water overflow from cascade tank, he consider that the automatic feed water valve incorrectly operation. Overhauled the feed water valve and found that the valve seat was damage, three small hold on the valve seat, pls see pic for more information.
ImmediateAction: Close auto feed water valve136 VPV, open manual feed water valve138 VPV to manual feed water to cascade tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Can not automatic feed water to cascade tank
DirectCause: Due to valve seat damage
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replace with new feed water valve
ClosingNote: This had been raised as a defect report earlier

IncidentDetails: During Drydock yard crew was working on replacing old deck air line.While gas cutting one section of deck air pipeline on main deck the sparks from the gas cutting machine fall on the mooring rope which was kept on deck uncovered.The rope caught fire causing damage to rope.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel on dock at Zhoushan china.Deck air line and ballast guage air line pipes renewal jobs assigned to yard.The job scope was to gas cut the bad pipe section on deck in place and replace with new.While gascutting one of the section of pipes the sparks flew on to the mooring rope that was lying below on deck.Small section of rope got burned due to sparks thereby damaging the rope.
ImmediateAction: Fire was extinguished immediately by the yard crew.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The mooring rope few sections got burned because of which the rope cannot be used again
DirectCause: Before carrying out the job the area around the work not secured properly.The safety aspect of the job was not analysed correctly.Jobs to be planned in a more better way considering all the aspects of work prioritising the saftey aspect.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The jobs to be planned properly and communication between the yard and ship staff should be proper.The ropes were removed and stowed in othe place clear of hotwork areas and covered properly.
Corrective Action: The jobs to be planned properly and communication between the yard and ship staff should be proper.The ropes were removed and stowed in othe place clear of hotwork areas and covered properly.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily investigated and actions completed.

IncidentDetails: 2215 Hrs. LT on 12/08/2018 – Bunker barge LIPUMA port side alongside made fast.  2240 Hrs. LT on 12/08/2018 – C/O informed cargo stopped due to conveyor breakdown.  Around 2300 Hrs. LT on 12/08/2018 – Duty officer informed HO Bunkering commenced, Instructed Duty officer to ensure all safety measures are in place. Duty officer reconfirmed all in order. Same has been confirmed with chief engineer all in order.   Around 0230 Hrs. LT on 13/08/2018 – Received a call from D/O and C/E that Oil ingresses into cargo hold no 5, no oil leak overboard.
ImmediateAction: Same investigate with chief engineer, came to know that initial bunker was taken in 1C then changed over to 4 P but bunkers filled in no 2 P leaked in no 5 cargo Hold.   As per chief engineer initial sounding in 2 P was about 50% full and after bunker observed tank is full.   Checked no 5 cargo hold observed oil accumulated near Australian ladder along the forward bulk head and in-between the cargo piles at center.  C/O informed no 5 Hold Bilge checked no oil traces and Nil and at center where oil accumulated found sounding 1.3mtrs.  Informed C/E to close all overboard values and informed C/O to re confirm all ballast/overboard valves are closed.   Informed DPA/MSI/TSI Regarding the Oil leak in cargo hold no 5 and briefed about the situation.   Informed agent and local P&I as instructed by DPA.  At 0650 Hrs. lt,  Bunker barge cast off as per company instruction.  At 0750 Hrs. lt on 13/08/2018 owners P&I Surveyor Mr. Rob onboard, self-informed about the incident and investigated further along with P&I Surveyor.  Mangers were updated continuously monitoring the Incident and further assessment being carried out.  Vessel shifted from berth no 306 to berth no 302 (from 0920 Hrs. lt to 1012 Hrs. lt)  As per Mangers Instruction vessel resumed loading as per agreed pre stowage plan including no 5 Cargo hold.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo contaminated by mixing of oil, quantity to be ascertained.
DirectCause: Bunker checklist not complied with leading to over pressurization of non bunker tank
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Follow company procedures and checklists diligently.  Rest hours to be managed correctly.
Corrective Action: Follow company procedures and checklists diligently. Rest hours to be managed correctly.
ClosingNote: CA in order

IncidentDetails: Ukrainian custom during inward clearance confisticated 30 drums of Aquatuff chemical at Yuzhny
IncidentEventDetails: Fail to present complete set of documents during inward clearance at Yuzhny.  A sheet with Aquatuff chemical was missing based on which custom declared as undeclared items.  Confisticated the chemicals and fined 2437.50 USD as fine.
ImmediateAction: Checked all other stores are declared correctly
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Confistigated 30 drums of Aquatuff  and a fine of 2437.50 USD
DirectCause: Chemicals were kept in the Fore Peak store and the Fore Peak stores Inventory List was not included in the entire set of inventories  The entire set was not rechecked as the turnaround was very quick. Vessel was maneuvering from 1800 hrs LT. At 2112 Pilot away. After making fast,cargo holds prepared for discharge, draft survey, cargo sampling, subsequently all authorities boarded custom, immigration, quarantine, harbour master, PFSO. Vessels Inspection was at 0300 hrs LT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior arrival more adequate checks made on the port papers by master and may be counter checked by one of the Senior officer to ensure all in order.
Corrective Action: Prior arrival more adequate checks made on the port papers by master and may be counter checked by one of the Senior officer to ensure all in order.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: All stores inventory to be prepared well before arrival and to be collected and rechecked prior arrival
ReviewComments: To be discussed with ships staff.
ClosingNote: To be discussed with Officers

IncidentDetails: Ballast water sample failed
IncidentEventDetails: ECOLOGICAL INSPECTION ONBOARD MV BW ACORN on 30th July 2018:On 30 July 2018 at about 1730 hrs LT Ecological Inspector came onboard.  At the same time Master informed the local P&I and Mr. Matias of BW Ops.The pre-arranged P&I correspondent discussed with Eco. Inspector to wait till he arrived onboard, subsequently the Eco Inspector left the vessel and returned with the P&I correspondent.By 1415 Lt on 30th July, the vessel had already completed de-ballasting operations.Vessel on berthing prior commencing the de-ballasting operations, had collected samples from FPK, No.1 & 4 WBT(S) through the Air escape pipes (sampling points)As advised and agreed by the P&I Correspondent and the Eco Inspector the samples were mixed and 5 bottles of the sample were made, the same was witnessed by P&I correspondent, Eco Inspector and ships staff.The sample distribution of the samples are as follows:  - 3 Bottles to Eco Inspector - 1 Bottle to the P&I -1 Bottle Sealed and signed by P&I retained onboard.Agent informed the result as Negative and the same was informed to Mr. Matias. On 31 July, when vessel was at anchorage, agent boarded the vessel along with the Eco Inspector.Master informed Mr. Matias immediately and as per his advice all the documents brought by Eco Inspector at anchorage were scanned and sent without signature for his advise.The agent also spoke with Mr. Matias when he was onboard. On advise form Mr. Matias, vessel signed the papers and lodged a LOP for not allowing to write in all the papers "as receipt only without prejudice, not accepting any liabilities. Vessel sailed out once the Cargo Documents were cleared for sailing on 01/2230 hrs.
ImmediateAction: P and I correspondent and Owners were informed. All records were demonstrated to the Ecological inspector onboard.
DirectCause: Bunker Samples taken were tested and found negative. The Ballast exchange was done as per mandatory requirements
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior arrival to such ports, keep P and I correspondent informed and called onboard before hand. Samples to be witnessed by P and I and samples numbered and tagged. Keep all concerned parties involved.
Corrective Action: Prior arrival to such ports, keep P and I correspondent informed and called onboard before hand. Samples to be witnessed by P and I and samples numbered and tagged. Keep all concerned parties involved.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: To involve P and I prior arrival such ports
ReviewComments: To be discussed with ships staff.
ClosingNote: To be very careful when going to such ports

IncidentDetails: Ship's crane no. 1 hoisting wire jump-off on the drum.
IncidentEventDetails: 18.08.2018  0942 - vsl dropped anchor at Krishnapatnam "B" Anchorage.  1030 - vsl's crew remove the adjusting of 4 grabs due to heavy cargo.  1300 - vs'ls crew commence testing ship's cranes and grabs  1430 - Chief Officer inform Master that hoisting wire of crane no.1 was jump-off on the drum and stocked in between and removing of the same in progress.  1600 - Master rechecked the situation but without any progress  1630 - Inform Superintendent with the situation through satfon and advise vsl to rectify the problem prior vessels berthing and utilize all hands. Immetiadely prepare to lay out the spare hoisting wire while other crew member removing the stock hoisting wires for about 5-6 turns.  2400 - Vsl's crew manage to remove the hoisting wires that was stocked in between drum and spare ready and layed on deck. Ship's engineer start fixing the possible cause of jumping-off of hoisting wires by welding the protection bar from drums side to prevent reoccurence.  0030 - Vsl's crew start winding up the spare hoisting wires on crane no. 1, same progress report sent to Superintendent.  0300 - Replacement of new hoisting wires of ship's crane no. 1 completed, again recheck limit switch and roller guide in place found to be in order and in working condition.
ImmediateAction: Vessel's crew try to fix the problem immidiately. Inform Superintendent with situation through satfoncon and follow-up progress report through mail.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Ship's crane hoisting wires and possible damage to the protection guide to prevent wires to jump-off over the side.
DirectCause: Possible cause of incident due to ship's crane mishandling.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior utilize of Ship's crane to imphasize to either ship's crew or foreman/stevedores crane operator the importance of proper handling and limitations of ship's crane and regular monitoring once in use.
Corrective Action: Prior utilize of Ship's crane to imphasize to either ship's crew or foreman/stevedores crane operator the importance of proper handling and limitations of ship's crane and regular monitoring once in use.
ClosingNote: CA Noted. Prior operation of crane, inspection of cranes should be made and prior handing over cranes to stevedores the limits and safetys should be tried out.

IncidentDetails: Damage/Crack on De-Mucking winch motor casing found during hold cleaning
IncidentEventDetails: On 17-Aug-2018 while lifting the remaining cargo from No.8 CH, De-mucking winch broke down and on observation found, the inner side bearing failed after which the driven shaft got misaligned and broke the cover.
ImmediateAction: Temporarily stop using De- Mucking Winch and taken to E/R for inspection by Engineers and found cannnot be repaired onboard.
DirectCause: General wear down of the bearing which failed in operation and damaged the casing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Shippalm PMS job to be included for complete overhaul of winch annually.
ClosingNote: Please raise as a Hull and machinery defect

IncidentDetails: While vessel is discharging at Yantai berth no.103, Loading Master  requested to adjust the moorings to align the shore loading arm with the vessel’s manifold by 15 cms.. Vessel’s crew adjusted the forward mooring lines. While they were proceeding aft to adjust the aft lines, excess weight and surge due to change of tide(High tide) resulted in parting of aft breast line (approx. 4 mtrs after the tail rope.) The breast rope had a very short lead leading to excessive load.
ImmediateAction: Immediately terminal was informed and spare mooring line was passed and made fast.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to mooring rope aprox 4 to 5 mtrs after the tail rope.
DirectCause: Excessive  weight on that line and surge due to change of tide(High tide) ,Line was leading very short.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out,Attending mooring lines continous in tidal ports.
ClosingNote: We concur with the cause analysis and the corrective action taken.

DirectCause: Crack in pipe due to vibration.

IncidentDetails: At 2000 hrs LT on 23rd August 2018, when ship was alongside last chamber (pacific side) of Cocoli locks and water level was dropping, the shore fender which was protruding outside caused damage to ships railings and structure on starboard side main deck forward of rescue boat area.  As a result of initial inspection:  1. Top railings of about 2 meters’ length  2. The vertical support of railings and   3. Cable conduit to Starboard navigation light of about 2 meters in length   were found damaged. Please find attached pics for same
IncidentEventDetails: 1940 hrs LT: Vessel entered lowest chamber of Cocoli locks.  1946: Aft gate of the chambber began closing and the morring operations were commenced.  1948: All lines were made fast inside the chamber.  2000: Loud sound was heard and it was reported that a partially damaged fender inside the chamber, which was protruding came in contact with the ship's rails forward of the rescue boat on the starboard side and damaged the railings and the navigational light electrical cable conduit.
ImmediateAction: Pilot was advised and he requested the tugs to pull the vessel away from the sidewall of the locks. Meanwhile the forward and aft mooring teams slacked the mooring lines to enable the vessel to be pulled away from the sidewall, in order to prevent further damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: tAt 2000 hrs L.T. when ship was alongside last chamber (pacific side) of Cocoli locks and water level was dropping, the shore fender which was protruding outside caused damage to ships railings and structure on starboard side main deck forward of rescue boat area.  As a result of initial inspection:  1. Top railings of about 2 meters’ length  2. The vertical support of railings and   3. Cable conduit to Starboard navigation light of about 2 meters in length   were found damaged.
DirectCause: Poor condition of the fenders in Cocoli locks (Panama)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discussion with pilot to be carried out prior entering the locks and pilot to be advised to make use of the tugs to keep the vessel clear of the sidewall, while the water level is dropping in the chamber.
Corrective Action: Discussion with pilot to be carried out prior entering the locks and pilot to be advised to make use of the tugs to keep the vessel clear of the sidewall, while the water level is dropping in the chamber.
ClosingNote: The master pilot exchange should have included the protrusion of the fender. This would have enabled a timely action by the pilot and the ship to prevent the damage.

IncidentDetails: Last rubber step of the pilot ladder got  broken, as hit by pilot boat
IncidentEventDetails: For Transit of Messina Straits ,   1515LT: During Tea Break Notice was given to rig port side pilot ladder , 1 Mtr AWL for boarding of pilot.  1530LT: Commence Rigging of pilot ladder  1533LT ,Main Engine was stopped and astern movement tried out.  1545LT: Port side pilot ladder rigged, 1 mtr AWL , Same has been confirmed and verified by Bosun & then Chief mate who went to attend and bring the pilot to bridge .  1558LT: P.O.B  1600LT: Pilot adviced vsl to pick up the ladder by three steps .Same time it was informed to bosun to adjust the ladder. No complains received from pilot regarding pilot ladder.  1604LT: Pilot in the Bridge , Master and pilot information exchange carried out .  1620LT-1648LT: During Transit of Messina Strait ,Heavy rain with drop in to zero visibility experienced .  1654LT : Vsl almost out of the straits , Visibility also got improved , Pilot was about to get Off   1700LT: Chief Mate escorted pilot to the port side pilot ladder , Before pilot getting off, ladder was checked by chief mate , it was noticed that the last rubber step  was hanging to one side . Pilot got off the vessel in the same ladder . After getting off the boat, from the boat  he asked mate to replace the ladder step before using next time .
ImmediateAction: On inspection , suspect boat has hit the last step due to swell. Hence the last rubber step was found hanging .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Last rubber step got damaged by boat during pilot transfer at messina strait.
DirectCause: Pilot ladder rigged as per requested pilot boarding arrangement, due to inadvertent surge of swell pilot boat banged at  lower part(rubber step) of ladder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replace pilot ladder with new one.
ClosingNote: COSWP to be emphasized and training to be given for safe use of pilot ladder.

IncidentDetails: Deck Crane no.1 jump-off the Hoisting Drum and got jammed between the hoisting drum end and supporting structures causing damage to the wire rope.
IncidentEventDetails: 18.Aug.2018  1000LT - Vessel dropped anchor at Krishnapatnam, India Anchorage Bravo.  1030LT - Toolbox meeting carried out.  1045LT - Commence removing the adjusting plates (spill plates) of Grabs 1,2,3 & 4. After removing the adjusting plates for each grab, ship`s crew carried out routine testing of the grabs and deck cranes.  1240LT - Completed testing of grabs and deck cranes 1,2,3 & 4 and all found to be working satisfactorily. No any abnormalities were observed during the test.  1245LT - Deck crew start securing the deck cranes and upon bringing the deck crane no.1 to jib rest position and its hook to lashing position, while heaving the hoisting wire a sudden slack from the hoisting wire was felt by the operator (vessel crew) and the crane no.1 cannot heave the hoisting wire. The vessel crew stopped the crane and upon checking the in the machinery space of the crane it was found out the the hoisting wire jumped off from its drum and got jammed between the hoisting drum end and its supporting structure. Upon further checking, damages/deformities were observed on the hoisting wire.
ImmediateAction: Replaced the hoisting wire rope of Deck Crane no.1, tried out and adjust limit switches.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The incident causes damage to the hoisting wire of crane no.1 which required immediate replacement.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Thorough checks should be carried out by ship staff prior handing over the cranes to stevedores for cargo operations. Also frequent checks on the cranes and its operations should be done by the OOW to prevent such incidents in future.
ClosingNote: CA in order

IncidentDetails: L I (Port) Liquid Manifold manual valve failed to operate when operated for line draining after completion of discharge at IDEMISTU Berth 2.
IncidentEventDetails: 1530 Hrs LT : On completion of Propane discharge, Shore informed to close the Liquid manifold Manual Valve. Valve was effectively closed as requested  1536 hrs LT : Shore requested to re-open the valve to 5% to start nitrogen purge of shore line cargo to the ship’s tank as agreed in the ship Shore information exchange.  1538 hrs LT : Manifold watch reported that valve is not opening and indicator not moving. Wheel was rotating free indicating closed valve which prevented shore from purging liquid drain into ship’s tank for which an LOP was lodged against ship.  1540 hrs LT : Shore Cargo Line was being drained through ship’s Cross over Drain Line into one of Ship’s tank but terminal wanted to expedite the process and specified  purging is not sufficient enough compared to line drain remaining.  1545 hrs LT : Ship’s Cross over Drain Line was closed and liquid remains purged back to shore via Loading arm .  1600 hrs LT : Line clearing with Nitrogen completed.  1605 hrs LT : L 1 manifold manual valve gear box assembly interchanged with L 1 manifold (Stbd) Manual valve and L1 manifold (Port) manual valve tried out to the satisfaction of the terminal.   1600 - 1636 hrs LT :Manifold disconnected without any delay
ImmediateAction: Line ndraining completed by purging back to shore via Loading arm.  L 1 manifold manual valve gear box assembly interchanged with L 1 manifold (Stbd) Manual valve and L1 manifold (Port) manual valve tried out to the satisfaction of the terminal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1530 Hrs LT On completion of Propane discharge, Shore informed to close the Liquid manifold Manual Valve. Valve was effectively closed as requested  1536 hrs LT Shore requested to re-open the valve to 5% to start nitrogen purge of shore line cargo to the ship’s tank as agreed in the ship Shore information exchange.  1538 hrs LT Manifold watch reported that valve is not opening and indicator not moving. Wheel was rotating free indicating closed valve which prevented shore from purging liquid drain into ship’s tank for which an LOP was lodged against ship.  1540 hrs LT Shore Cargo Line was being drained through ship’s Cross over Drain Line into one of Ship’s tank but terminal wanted to expedite the process and specified  purging is not sufficient enough compared to line drain remaining.  1545 hrs LT Ship’s Cross over Drain Line was closed and liquid remains purged back to shore via Loading arm .  1600 hrs LT Line clearing with Nitrogen completed.  1605 hrs LT L 1 manifold manual valve gear box assembly interchanged with L 1 manifold (Stbd) Manual valve and L1 manifold (Port) manual valve tried out to the satisfaction of the terminal.   1636 hrs LT Manifold Disconnected without any delay  1800 hrs LT Vessel departed terminal without any delay
DirectCause: Failure to follow PMS routine  Incorrect operation of Manifold Valve.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Manifold manual valves completed gear box assembly to be thoroughly inspected annually.  Manifold and similar critical manual valves on cargo system to be operated by Gas Engr or Duty Officer under strict instructions from Chief Officer. Same to be included in Chief Officer Standing Order, cargo plan and notice displayed in CCR
ClosingNote: The near miss did not result in any damage or injury.

IncidentDetails: AA) Declaration of the Incident  1.) On or about 1500H LT July 08, 2018; ships staff raise concern regarding inside his left ear  (FOREIGN PARTICLE LIKE)  2.) On or about 1530H LT July 08, 2018; Second officer check his left ear and cannot extract the  FOREIGN PARTICLE LIKE. Seek advice from company doctor.  3.) Company doctor advise to take below medicines for five days.  a.) C. Amoxycillin and Clavulanate 1-0-1  b.) T. Paracetamol 1-1-1  c.) T.Metronidazole 1-0-1  d.) T. Ibuprofen 1-0-1  e.) T. Cetrizine 0-0-1  4.) Before giving the prescribe medicines; concerned ships staff were asked if he has any allergy for  medicines and confirmed NO allergy.  5.) On or about 1140H LT July 09, 2018; ships staff took the prescribe medication for the first time  after taking his lunch.  6.) On or about 1220H LT July 09, 2018; ship staff went to officer mess room complain of itchiness  to his whole body, increase body temperature and observe redness on his whole skin. Face become  swollen.  7.) Immediately ask him to stop work and accompanied him to his cabin to have some rest with  simultaneously ask Second officer to verify his vital sign and informing office for same. Offered to  drink lots of water. While constant communication to shore is being conducted.
IncidentEventDetails: BB) SHIPS STAFF UNDERGO SEIZURE (EPELEPSY LIKE SCENARIO)  1.) Right after reaching his cabin on or about 1225H LT July 09, 2018 he experiences SEIZURE  a.) His whole body is shaking  b.) Difficulty in breathing  c.) Feeling NUMBNESS to his fingers and arms  d.) Eyes cannot see  e.) Become Unconscious on 1226H LT July 09, 2018  2.) Second officer and ships staffs who is in his cabin immediately took necessary action to keep  him awake.  a.) We continuously monitor his VITAL SIGN.  b.) Continuously slap his FACE to kept him awake and a wet towel is being RUB in his  whole body.  c.) Slight massage was applied in his body.  d.) On or about 1227H LT July 09, 2018; he become conscious.
ImmediateAction: CC) OBSERVATION AFTER REGAINED CONCIOUSNESS  1.) Whole Body is recovering  2.) Itchiness was loss  3.) Increase body temperature decrease  4.) Body redness/swollen was loss.  5.) VITAL SIGN is NORMAL.  DD) Action Taken after REGAINED CONCIOUSNESS  1.) Continuously monitor his vital sign every hour and stop all his medication as per company doctor  advise.  2.) Assigned a watchman in his cabin for 24hours for monitoring and ask him to take as much as water.  3.) Everything went well thereafter and ships staff is now in good condition.  4.) He was asked to have a BED rest for a couple of days more.  EE) Recommendation  1.) With the quick response of the crew; a fatal event was avoided. Highly suggested to circulate  this information for everybody’s understanding.  (No any photo were taken because we priorities to attend and give remedy.)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Injury.  The Patient complained of some foreign particle inside his left ear and while taking some medication, the patient complained of itchiness and rashes in his body. later he recovered and the seaman came back to normal health.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Though no specific reason could be attributed to the illness mentioned, it is a good practice to ensure crew wear appropriate PPE for the job. Crew should be aware of the allergies to the medicines and inform in advance.
ClosingNote: CA in order

Extreme Parallel Body passed over Soft Mud Patch    Present Status, of Actions taken as below:    1) All Water Ballast Tanks and E/R DB Tanks’ soundings checked. Confirmed no Hull Breach.  2) Alcohol Test of all Bridge Team Members, including Pilot carried out. All observed Zero Percent  3) VDR Data Saved.  4) Mobile Video Recording of complete Notore Channel Transit, saved  5) Photographs taken for evidence.  6) While at Berth, Propeller turned on turning gear. No apparent damage or abrasion noted. ( 0.6 m outside water)  7) Rudder checked for any damages, above water line and tried out. All Parameters were found normal.  8) All Ballast Tanks have been checked internally, for damages  NO APPARENT SIGNS OF DAMAGE/DENT NOTED.  E/R Bottom floors sighted. No apparent sign of any damage noted.  9) Stbd. Ship side, checked externally, for any damage by Tugs. No Apparent damage noted.
ImmediateAction: MSI and TSI Contacted over Tlephone. Briefed about Situation.  1) All Water Ballast Tanks and E/R DB Tanks’ soundings checked. Confirmed no Hull Breach.  2) Alcohol Test of all Bridge Team Members, including Pilot carried out. All observed Zero Percent  3) VDR Data Saved.  4) Mobile Video Recording of complete Notore Channel Transit, saved  5) Photographs taken for evidence.  6) Rest Hour Records taken  7) While at Berth, Propeller turned on turning gear. No apparent damage or abrasion noted. ( 0.6 m outside water)  8) Rudder checked for any damages, above water line and tried out. All Parameters were found normal.  9) All Ballast Tanks have been checked internally, for damages  NO APPARENT SIGNS OF DAMAGE/DENT NOTED.  E/R Bottom floors sighted. No apparent sign of any damage noted.  10) Stbd. Ship side, checked externally, for any damage by Tugs. No Apparent damage noted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: -While at Berth, Propeller turned on turning gear. No apparent damage or abrasion noted. ( 0.6 m outside water)  -Rudder checked for any damages, above water line and tried out. All Parameters were found normal.  -All Ballast Tanks have been checked internally, for damages  NO APPARENT SIGNS OF DAMAGE/DENT NOTED.  E/R Bottom floors sighted. No apparent sign of any damage noted.  -Stbd. Ship side, checked externally, for any damage by Tugs. No Apparent damage noted.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Not sufficient UKC. Indirect Cause: Actual Depth as recorded by the vessel was lesser then anticipated. Root Cause: False declaration from Agents and Terminal.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Considering the evidences and communications records between vessel and agents, there was false declaration of depth data for Notore channel. During investigation it was noted from sources  that the channel was not dredged for last 2 years. In consultation with owners, it has been decided to declare Onne as an unsafe port. No other vessel to call Onne, Nigeria until satisfactory dredging and depth records are made available by Onne port authorities.     Information to be circulated to all Fleet Vessels.

IncidentDetails: Mooring rope parted during mooring operation at Carville port in USA
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel arrived at the Total terminal in the port of Carville(USA) on 27 June 2018 at 1624 Lt. Two tugs were made fast for forward and aft stations. Forward tug was made fast at break of forecastle deck  on port side and aft tug was made fast on main deck,break of accomodation on port side.Once the vessel was close to the terminal, spring lines were passed ashore with the help of heaving lines. On aligning the vessel according to the terminal's loading arm, forward and aft stations were asked to make fast their spring lines. As soon as the forward made fast one of their spring lines and was in the process of tightening the second spring , a sudden forward momentum was caused due to inappropriate angle of pushing by the forward tug. This caused excessive stress on one of the spring lines that was already made fast. As a consequence the spring line parted. There were no injuries as the crew members were away from the taut lines as usual practice.
ImmediateAction: Forward station immediately informed bridge of the situation and slackened the other spring line to avoid it's parting. Tug was adviced to stop pushing and other lines were passed in successions to make fast the vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope parted at a length of about 6 mtr from the eye secured.
DirectCause: Miscommunication between the tug and pilot, causing the tug to push at wrong angle and causing the forward momentum.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional check to be carried out on the movement of tug boats .
Corrective Action: Discuss during the Bridge Team MEeting with Pilot on the need to inform the Tug boat regards the status of the ropes on board especially when same being handled by Ship staff. (such as when casting off ropes or when making fast initial ropes). Pilot to be advise to instruct the Tug boat regards excat angle to push the vessel at and whether momentum required in any particular direction.
LessionLearnt: Importance of VIGILANCE and SITUATIONAL AWARENESS in Mooring Stations. Also the importance of monitoring Pilot - To - Tug communications.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Highlights the supreme importance of VIGILANCE and SITUATIONAL AWARENESS during Mooring stations

IncidentDetails: On 24th June 2018, during the course of the cargo discharging, vessel faced issues in the Inert Gas system. First, difficulty in maintaining the pressure in the cargo tanks and subsequently black smoke was observed from the ship’s funnel. As a result of these troubles there was stoppage of discharging for about 2 hours and additionally, reduced pumping performance for about 3 hours.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was carrying out line displacement before starting the bulk cargo discharging. Both the Auxiliary Boilers were running, however it was noticed by terminal that black smoke was being emitted intermittently from ship’s funnel from 0700 – 0715 LT.
ImmediateAction: Further checks revealed that Auxiliary Boiler No. 2 FD fan duct bellow had developed a hole at the folded section and that some amount of air was leaking from this hole. This was resulting in incomplete combustion within the boiler and causing black smoke.   Since both Auxiliary Boilers were running at the time of incident, load on No. 2 Boiler was immediately reduced and the boiler was stopped. Vessel carried out remaining discharging only with one boiler and 2 COPs. Due to this restriction, vessel could not achieve the discharge rate / pressure requested by the Terminal until the time the shore booster pump had kicked-in, once the bulk discharge had commenced.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 24th June 2018, during the course of the cargo discharging, vessel faced issues in the Inert Gas system. First, difficulty in maintaining the pressure in the cargo tanks and subsequently black smoke was observed from the ship’s funnel. As a result of these troubles there was stoppage of discharging for about 2 hours and additionally, reduced pumping performance for about 3 hours.
DirectCause: Inadequate work standard and inadequate supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The checklist for pre-arrival checks and preparation for cargo operation shall include a separate check to verify the correct position of the automatically operated valves, to ensure smooth operation to their full range. Until inclusion in the Checklist, this shall be monitored using a ship-specific list of such valves. One spare bellow to be kept onboard.
ClosingNote: Spare bellow was supplied to vessel / Pre-arrival checklist amended to include valve checks.

IncidentDetails: Main engine failed to accelerate (From DS Ahead to Slow ahead)
IncidentEventDetails: On 27th March 2018, at around 0810 hrs. vessel noticed that on giving command from dead slow to slow, RPM did not increase.  On investigation found that both Auxiliary blower were off which in turn lead to this problem.
ImmediateAction: Auxiliary blower switched on to Local control and started, Found main Engine normal behaviour.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One timer unit (27T) was found loose on the base.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Checking of timer units was added in the planned maintenance schedule.
ClosingNote: Engine alarms and trips to be carried out additionally (Wheel house imperfect condition) for checking cuting-in/off of Auxiliary blowers.

IncidentDetails: MINOR LEAK OBSERVED BY GAS ENGINEER AFT OF CARGO TANK NO.1. LEAK WAS OBSERVED FROM A FLANGE IN THE NO.1 CARGO LIQUID LINE, AFT OF NO.1 CARGO TANK.
IncidentEventDetails: ON 26-JULY-2018, AS VESSEL WAS DISCHARGING HER CARGO OF PROPANE AND BUTANE AT NMPT, MLIF, BERTH-9. FOLLOWING INCIDENT TOOK PLACE.    AT 2145HRS, MINOR LEAK OBSERVED BY GAS ENGINEER AFT OF CARGO TANK NO.1. LEAK WAS OBSERVED FROM A FLANGE IN THE NO.1 CARGO LIQUID LINE, AFT OF NO.1 CARGO TANK. AT TIME OF INCIDENT, CARGO LINE PRESSURE WAS 9.5 BAR.
ImmediateAction: ESD WAS ACTIVATED AND LOADING MASTER WAS INFORMED OF THE SAME. CEASED DISCHARGING CARGO. AS A PRECAUTION, SHORE ACTIVATED THE SHORE WATER CANONS IN DIRECTION OF LEAK.     ALL THE MANIFOLD VALVES WERE CONFIRMED SHUT AND PRESSURE IN CARGO LINE WAS RELEASED INTO THE OTHER TANKS. ONCE IT WAS CONFIRMED THAT THE LINE HAD BEEN DRAINED, THE SHORE WATER CANON WAS DIRECTED AWAY FROM SIGHT TO RECTIFY THE LEAK, HOWEVER WERE KEPT RUNNING FOR IMMEDIATE USE. SHIP’S FIRE HOSES WERE RIGGED AT SIGHT OF LEAK AND KEPT ON WITH RUNNING WATER. VESSEL ALSO STARTED DECK SPRAY SYSTEM TO SUBSIDE ANY GAS ON DECK. THE FLANGE WAS LOOSENED AND GASKET WAS FOUND TO HAVE GOT DAMAGED. SAME WAS REPLACED WITH A NEW GASKET AND FLANGE TIGHTENED BACK.    DEPUTY CONSERVATIVE, NMPT AND CHIEF MANAGERS, SAFETY & OPERATIONS, VISITED THE VESSEL TO TAKE STOCK OF THE DEVELOPMENTS AND CONTROL MEASURES.    INSPECTION AND TESTING OF SAFETY EQUIPMENT AND DECK WATER SPRAY SYSTEM CARRIED OUT IN PRESENCE OF CHIEF MANAGERS, SAFETY & OPERATIONS. THE LINE WAS TESTED FOR ANY LEAK BY RUNNING NO.1(S) CARGO PUMP IN THE PRESENCE OF HPCL CHIEF MANAGERS, SAFETY & OPERATIONS AND CONFIRMED THERE WAS NO LEAK.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MINOR LEAK OBSERVED BY GAS ENGINEER AFT OF CARGO TANK NO.1. LEAK WAS OBSERVED FROM A FLANGE IN THE NO.1 CARGO LIQUID LINE, AFT OF NO.1 CARGO TANK. AT TIME OF INCIDENT, CARGO LINE PRESSURE WAS 9.5 BAR.
DirectCause: A NORMAL GASKET WAS FOUND IN PLACE INSTEAD OF TEFLON GASKET WHICH RUPTURED DUE TO THE INITIAL KNOCK WHEN CARGO PUMP NO.1(S) WAS STARTED`
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Checked all joints on liquid line and ensured only Teflon gaskets in use.
LessionLearnt: Lack of pre arrival testing
ReviewComments: 6.21 EMERGENCY SHUTDOWN SYSTEM - ESDS All liquefied gas carriers are provided with an emergency shutdown system such that, by the operation of a push button or the opening of a control valve, cargo valves will close, pumping equipment will shut down and the control is such as to minimize the risk of any pressure surge. All personnel involved with cargo operations should be aware of the operation of ESD system. The instrumentation, control process and power actuators necessary to fulfil these functions are therefore of paramount importance and any maintenance is only to be undertaken by competent personnel. Automatic shutdown systems require particular attention. They are normally designed to shut the tank loading valves if the liquid level rises above the pre-set and there is a danger of the tank being overfilled. Care should be taken to ensure that the activation point is accurately set and the operation of the device is tested prior arrival every load and discharge port and prior starting cargo operation. The precise timing and rate of closure of the cargo valves must be known, reliable and reproducible in order that shore personnel may be advised of the closing times and in liaison with the ship’s officers, agree an acceptable cargo transfer rate.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: 8th/0610 hrs:Fire alarm activated on  "Fire Control Panel" on Nav. Bridge for zone 5 (B-Deck).  Master informed immidiately.Master on bridge.  Chief Officer reported  thick smoke coming out from B-Deck near port side entrance(Crew laundry) with some burning smell.    All vents shut for B-Deck including crew laundry(Natural vent and sanitary exhaust blowers 1 & 2).     Elec. off found that power supply for washing machines got tripped for Crew laundry.    Accomodation A/c and A/c blower shut     Power supply to the Crew laundry lightings isolated by Elec.Off .    Boundary cooling initiated for B- Deck.    Emergency team ready with fire party with SCBA / Firemans outfit and Co2 extinguisher for fighting the fire.    Requested permission to make man entry for  crew laundry - B Deck with all precautions.    A/b Retheesh and Oiler Herrington made man entry with SCBA / Firemans outfit doned with Co2 extinguisher and torch light , reported "Flame with Thick smoke" can be seen inside the crew laundry and saw one of the washing machine was on fire.    Activated Co2 extinguisher immidiately to fight the fire.    Fire party reported that flame has been put off.     Both the fire fighters came out from the laundry room, monitoring the situation for any further development of fire.    All 6 adjacent sides of laundry checked for any hot spot/sign of fire or peeling of paint,Found none.Inside the laundry due to fire and black smoke,bulkheads and over head structure got discolouration.    Continuous boundary cooling  was in progress.    Once the smoke was clear,Oiler Herrington  made man entry with SCBA / Firemans outfit doned to remove the washing machine,Washing machine power cord removed from the socket by cutting its wire as it was found completely melted.    Continious fire watch maintained,    Area has been isolated till further notice.    Cause of fire:Electrical short circuit,There was no cloth in or on the washing machine.    Fire rounds taken  by  A/b Ratheesh from (0405 - 0425) after his watch and reported all found in satisfactory condition.

DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Laundry room aft bulkhead burnt,paint peeled off.  Bulkheads/over head structure shows discolouration due to fire and black smoke.  Washing machine and socket plug fully burnt .  No injury to any person.
DirectCause: After Investigation, found that the water leaking from nearby pipeline is cause of short circuit.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Washing machine plug to be switched off when not in use.Sockets/power supply to be periodically checked.

LessionLearnt: Low insulation alarm of any kind must not be ignored and attended to ascertain the cause.
ClosingNote: All machines checked for any fire hazards and found one other machine with a similar condition and same repaired and new order placed for new machines.

IncidentDetails: Vessel had rigged barbed wires around ship's railings-outboard, as one of the hardening measures against piracy attacks. However vessel encountered heavy weather from 23rd to 29th July 2018, with seas shipping across main deck, vessel rolling and pitching heavily. Due to the continuous impact from seas, the barbed wires got damaged and detached from ship's railings- subsequently going overboard.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Vessel rigged barbed wires around the vessel, on ship's railing- as a hardening measure against piracy attack in GOA and Arabian Sea.  2. Vessel encountered heavy weather from 23rd to 29th July 2018, with seas shipping across main deck, vessel rolling and pitching heavily.  3. On 29th July at 0800SMT, it was noticed that all barged wires rigged on main deck were washed overboard by the seas shipping across main deck.
ImmediateAction: The details of the damage were reported to the company. Entry made in Deck Log Book and Garbage Record Book under exceptional discharge section.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 32 coils of barbed wires lost.
DirectCause: Heavy weather for prolonged period.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: Weather prognosis to be obtained beforehand and adequate securing arrangements to be in place so as to avoid incident of this nature.
ClosingNote: Weather prognosis to be obtained beforehand and adequate securing arrangements to be in place so as to avoid incident of this nature. Closing the report.

IncidentDetails: While mooring to CBM bouys at Talara, Peru, one of the mooring lines on the Port Quarter Parted due to excessive tension.
IncidentEventDetails: While mooring to CBM bouys at Talara, Peru, one of the mooring lines on the Port Quarter Parted due to excessive tension. At the time of berthing, there wa s strong offshore wind which was felt on vessel's Port quarter. Due to this high wind condition, there was additional tension on the Port Quarter lines.
ImmediateAction: The line was picked up from the water and was replaced with the spre line.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The mooring rope is non usable and has to be discarded.
DirectCause: Lack of supervision, improper maintenance, procedures not followed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All mooring ropes to be checked for wear and tear prior putting into use. All mooring ropes suspected of wear and tear shall be removed from service. Frequent training of the crew.
Corrective Action: All mooring ropes to be checked for wear and tear prior putting into use. All mooring ropes suspected of wear and tear shall be removed from service. Frequent training of the crew.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Though the rope should have rendered when the load had increased, its parting indicates that the rope had deteriorated below the rendering limit. Please check all the ropes thoroughly as suggested.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Difficulty in Recovering the Lifeboat from Water. Not able to Pick up.
IncidentEventDetails: This is to inform that while the vsl safely afloat at Anchorage of Off Gothenburg Anchorage C.    01st Sep'18 @ 0700H LT / 0500H UTC - Vsl Dropped Anchor.    Bunker Supplier informed vsl on VHF that they will come alongside in/abt 4 hrs.    Since Lifeboat & Rescue Boat Lower & Manoeuver was Overdue and wx was good, calm sea and sunny, we planned to carry out the operations.    Vsl took the Permission from Gothenburg VTS for lowering & Manoeuvering the Boats.    Rescue Boat sucessfully lowered and manoeuvered in water and stowed back in position.    Lifeboat - First Lifeboat Davit tried out without connecting the Boat. Stretch the boom completely out and lower the hook to the water level and picked up the hook and brought the Boom IN to the stowed position, found working in good order.    Lifeboat Engines tried out and connected the boat with the Davit hook for lowering the boat in water.    Lifeboat safely lowered to the water level, Boat Crew boarded inside the boat, disconnected the Hooks and Manoeuver the Boat close to the ship side, after trying out, connected the Davit Hooks and tried heaving up the Boat, but found that Runner wire Drum is not operating, we are not able to slack, neither heave the boat. Boom found working in good order but Runner wire drum is not responding.  Bss past experience with the boat, we suspected that runner wire drum Hydraulic Motor shaft/bearing got broken and hence not responding.  Vsl had 2 new spare motor onboard but with different design, hence planned to dismantle the new motor and replace the parts  We immediately reported to vsl suptd and started working on changing the motor.  We have disconnected the Boat from the Davit hook by connecting both the Painter and Gaint Line, keeping the Boat safely alongside close to the vsl stern.  Brought the Davit Boom back to the stowed position, in order to replace the motor.  Immediately prepared the Stage, and replace the Hydraulic Motor.  After sucessful replacement, cleared the stage and connected the Davit hook to the Boat and recover the Boat safely to the stowed Position.

IncidentDetails: Bursting of fire hose due to excessive pressure in fire line during fire hose pressure testing.
IncidentEventDetails: Alll fire hoses has been laid on deck and connected to fire hydrants for fire hose pressure testing. Anchor wash and 2 fire hydrants were opened for avoiding immediate pressure hammering in fire line. Then informed to duty engineer to start fire pump. As soon as the fire pump started it has been observed that the pressure is more than the normal, and one hose connected to the fire hydrant on port side upper deck got burst out. Immediately the fire pump has been stopped from fire station.Total 2 fire hose  burst & 2 coupling leaked out.
ImmediateAction: Fire pump has been stopped immediately from fire station and Informed to engine room.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 FIRE HOSE GOT BURST OUT. NO PERSONNEL INJURIES.
DirectCause: SUDDEN PRESSURE INCREASE IN FIRE LINE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always start the fire pump with minimum pressure in line.Increase pressure only after found all working satisfactory.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: to be discussed in safty committee meeting.

IncidentDetails: The securing Strap of the foam extinguisher inside lifebaot snapped & thus foam extinguisher fell inside the lifebaot leading to its accidental activation.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Vessel having foam Extinguishers inside life boats.  2) The extinguisher is fitted inside on a bracket & strapped with a thin metal strap provided by the manufacturer.  3)Due to Continous localized vibration inside the lifeboat while sailing over time the Metal strap became weak at its rivetted location.  4)While carrying out routine inspection it was found that the metal strap securing the Extinguisher was broken & extinguisher had fallen inside the life boat.  5)The extinguisher upon impact inside got activated & thus foam was released inside the Life boat.
ImmediateAction: The extinguisher was thus taken out , lifeboat was cleaned & the bracket where the Extinguisher rests was taken out .                                                                                                                               The foam extinguisher was recharged immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Foam extinguisher activated & securing bracket got damaged due to vibration.
DirectCause: LOCALIZED VIBRATION & THIN PLATING OF SECURING STRAP  The regular vibration near the securing strap location caused the strap to become weak overtime & thus snapped.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To prevent such scenario in future own vessel is fabriacting a metal plate larger in thickness & providing extra thickness near the rivetted location to sustain more vibration.
ClosingNote: We concur with the cause analysis and the corrective action taken. Appreciate reporting of this incident.

IncidentDetails: While Loading at Acajutla port, the railings near 3S COT were damaged by the fender during topping off tanks and at low tide.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel was fully loaded at low tide, the fender was pressing against the ship's railing instead of the ship's side.
ImmediateAction: Ship's fender's placed to reduce the damage done by the shore fender.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Railings Bent on the stbd side near 3S COT, the vertical section of the railing bent by about 10 Degrees. Only one vertical section bent, the adjoining horizontal sections also bent by 10 Degrees.
DirectCause: Railing bent at low tide when the vessel was fully loaded, the fender positioning was such that it was coming on to the railing instead of the ship side.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out of deck department of precautions to be taken in tidal ports during low tide, same to be discussed in the pre arrival meeting everytime going to a tidal port. Also training carried out on the use of deck fenders and adjusting the moorings to reduce the effect of shore fender on the railing.
Corrective Action: Training carried out of deck department of precautions to be taken in tidal ports during low tide, same to be discussed in the pre arrival meeting everytime going to a tidal port. Also training carried out on the use of deck fenders and adjusting the moorings to reduce the effect of shore fender on the railing.
LessionLearnt: Any peculiarity about berth where vessel will be berthing is to be obtained by Master from agents before arrival and suitable preventive measures is to be applied to safeguard vessel against any damage.
ClosingNote: Closed on implementing CA.

IncidentDetails: On 23rd June 2018 when vessel was on a loaded passage from Al Shaheen to Nipah, M/E No 6-unit exhaust valve got stuck in open position. Vessel stopped for 5.8 hrs, replaced the valve with an overhauled one and continued on her voyage.   Same problem recurred with M/E No 6 unit again on 26th June 2018. Vessel again stopped for 5.8 hrs, replaced the valve with another overhauled one and continued her voyage.The vessel was stopped and off-hired in OPL Galle from 29th June 2018 to 2nd July 2018 for 2.33 days for carrying out the repairs to M/E. All valves were dismantled checked and No 3 unit was overhauled with new valve spindle, seat and o-rings. Rest all valves found in good condition and without any doubt for working satisfactory or developing any defect.Post the repair vessel has not reported any stoppages and all machinery parameters are normal.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel loaded crude oil in Fujairah (UAE), Mina Al Ahmadi(Kuwait), Ras-Tanura(Saudi Arabia) and Al Shaheen(Kuwait). She departed from Al Shaheen on the 21st June 2018 for her discharge port, Nipah, Indonesia.    On the 22nd June 2018 at 23:56 hrs LT, knocking sound was observed from No 6 Unit Exhaust Valve of Main Engine while running at 65rpm and exhaust smoke was seen coming out from the exhaust bellow. Simultaneously, Main Engine auto-slow down alarm was received due to Main Engine exhaust gas temperature deviation.    Investigation revealed that No. 6 unit was not firing, and it was suspected that the exhaust valve is stuck in open position. Main Engine was stopped on 23rd June at LT 0012 Hrs (in position Lat 24 34 N, Long 058 59 E at 51 NM from land in safe waters).
ImmediateAction: All Units Exhaust valves to be pulled out, inspected and overhauled, if required, with new valve Spindles and spares.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Exhaust valve was removed and replaced with spare overhauled valve which was available on board. After replacing, necessary testing of the M/E was carried out and the vessel resumed passage at 0600 hrs LT after a stoppage of 5.8 hrs. The parameters of the M/E were closely monitored.  On the 26th June 2018, same trouble with the same unit exhaust valve occurred. Vessel was again stopped at 1500 hrs LT (in position 13 56N 071 10E at 180 NM from land in safe water) and the exhaust valve for No 6 unit was removed and replaced with another spare overhauled valve which was available on board. Again, the necessary testing of M/E was carried out after the repair and after satisfactory results, the vessel resumed passage to Nipah, Indonesia at 2048 hrs LT. The total stoppage for the repair was 5.8 hrs.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:  The Exhaust valve spindle got stuck in Liner due to scoring.   Indirect Cause:   Wrong supply and use of HVOF only compatible seals with NIM Chromium coated spindles. Failure to  identify the wrong spares being used during the overhaul.   Root Cause:  Vessel earlier had 3 Nos old NIM type spindles which were not segregated and marked, as not to be used, and same used for overhaul with the seals compatible with HVOF type spindles.   Failure to identify the wrong spares being used during the overhaul.  The Maker had come with Service letter stating the change of spindles and compatibility of seals, but during dry dock the valves got mixed up, as were not segregated and marked clearly, and were used for overhaul.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The information pertaining to issue will be sent to all vessels with MAN B&W engine and this will be included in MOC for all new takeovers with the MAN B&W Main engines, and shared with PMS team.The existing NIM exhaust valves will be off landed at first convenient port in order to avoid  future confusion.The C/E and 2/E to be briefed to ensure the correct spares are used in compliance with the maker’s manual. The spares if not compatible with the vessel machinery to be segregated and labelled ”not to be used”.
Corrective Action: As a proactive measure new HVOF spare exhaust spindles, and compatible seals, to be supplied  and if required exhaust valves to be overhauled using same.
ClosingNote: All the exhaust valves checked and the possibility of the recurrence avoided.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was berthed at Berth#8, North Pier at the port of Ras Tanura. Upon commencement of discharge, a small leak was observed from no. 3 manifold. Loading master was informed and discharging was suspended. Loading arm was disconnected and gasket renwed. Loading re-connected & discharging was resumed  immediately after renewing the gasket.
IncidentEventDetails: 9th Aug/0012 LT : Commenced Discharging for line displacement  9th Aug/0017 LT : Leak noted on No.3. Manifold  9th Aug/0018 LT : Ceased discharging and terminal informed.  9th Aug/0018 - 0024 LT : No.3 Manifold reducer retightened  9th Aug/0024 - 0026 LT : Manifold opened to check line / Leak observed.  9th Aug/0026 - 0036 LT : Loading arm for No.3 drained  9th Aug/0036 - 0045 LT : Loading arm Disconnected  9th Aug/0045 - 0118 LT : Gasket Changed & Reducer fit back  9th Aug/0118 - 0124 LT : Loading arm Connected  9th Aug/0130 LT : Resumed Discharging
ImmediateAction: No.3 manifold gasket was renewed and discharging resumed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Discharging was suspended from 9th Aug/0018 LT to 9th Aug/0130 LT leading to total delay of 1 hr 12 mins on vessel account.
DirectCause: Not checking properly the condition of new gasket prior use and confirming effectiveness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As per corrective actions 1 and 2.
Corrective Action: Proper checking of new gasket condition prior use.
LessionLearnt: Good condition gaskets to be used and leak testing of connections to be carried out after fitting of reducers.
ClosingNote: Proper checking of gaskets and soap solution leak test after fitting of reducers to be carried out.

IncidentDetails: A part of Ship side railing broke in process of securing midship crane
IncidentEventDetails: After completion of STS cargo operation, hose was disconnected and landed to the mother vessel.  Ship's crew secured the midship crane to ships side side railings temporarliy and proceeded for departure mooring stations.  After mooring stations while the fwd crew were securing , aft crew came fwd and on seeing the crane not  being  secured proceeded for securing.While clearing the crane hook from the rails instead of slacking the runner wire the crane operator heaved up, resulting in railing breaking from the weld connection.
ImmediateAction: Broken railings was removed and new railing was put in place with nut and bolt arrangement.   Permanent welding to be carried out at next suitable oppurtunity.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A section of ship side railings broke from the weld connection
DirectCause: Mis operation of crane
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Both the crane operator and signal man to have walkie talkie with them during the full course of operation
Corrective Action: Broken railing to be replaced with new one with bolt and nut arrangement, at next opportunity will plan for permanent repair by welding.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Vessel to weld the railings at the earliest

IncidentDetails: 11 Sep 2018   ===========    0740-0800 LT : Cargo Surveyor, sampler / Loading Master, Agents boarded the vessel  0823 LT : PSC Inspector Boarded – 01 Person  0836 LT : Two Harbour Master boarded the vessel for inspection.  0915 LT : Second PSC inspector boarded the Vessel    Vessel PSC and Harbour Marpol inspection was in progress. Sampling, Gauging and other activities were in progress.    1000-1003 LT (Estimated Time ) :  Crew at Manifold  (AB- Anil) observed fwd & aft movement of vessel (Surging of vessel in FWD and Aft Direction) at this time the Fwd Spring brake rendered due to high tension & A Passenger vessel Royal Princess was crossing vessel’s head at excessive speed during this time. The duty AB reported to  CO on walkie talkie and proceeded fwd station to keep a check on the mooring lines. CO Confirmed the same and instrcuted the crew to proceed FWd and aft for mooring, Due to this sudden surging movement, Vessel’s headlines became taught and the brakes started rendering. The terminal was alerted by Chief Officer so as to keep a check on the shore gangway, this was acknowledged by the shore personnel on the walkie takie.  1003 LT : Master made an annoucement on PA immediately and instrcuted all crew on board to proceed for moooring station. HarbourMasters were at the gangway at this time for disembarkation, on seeing the surging the stayed back till the surging was over, The PSC inspectors was informed and Master rushed to the bridge and instrcuted ERM to prepare ME.  1004-1015 LT : All mooring winches were manned and slowly controlled the vessel movement and all line were heaven tight to reduce the surge. The vessel’s position was reconfirmed with the terminal regarding to the hose connection, Terminal confirmed that Vessel is in position and can proceed with Hose connection.  1015 LT : Observed Damages to Shore gangway and vessel Port Side gangway Support and Portable ladder ( Kindly refer attachement), Same was advise to terminal also.  1015-1020 LT – Incident was discussed with Harbour masters and vessel lodged the complaint through Harbour masters to port authority.  1020-1025 LT : Photo’s were taken and damages were shown to Harbour masters.  1025 LT : Harbour masters advises that they have informed the port control about the incident took place on board.  1015-1048 LT : Terminal change the platform of Shore gangway for Safe access.  1054 LT : Harbour Masters -02 Person disembarked.  1310 LT : PSC Inspectors disembaked after inspection.
