IncidentDetails: MAIN COOLING SW PUMP NO 2 MOTOR  BURNT.
IncidentEventDetails: Around 1400 hrs when Main cooling SW pump no 2 started in order to carry out suction filter cleaning of No 1 pump. Within short duration while monitoring the pressure found smoke from the motor of the pump and motor tripped. Immediately pump changed over back to No 1 pump and cooling system restored to normal. On checking the insulation at the motor terminlas found reading "0" in all three terminals. Motor dismantled from place , shifted to workshop and opened. Found motor Non drive end bearing damaged.
ImmediateAction: MAIN COOLING SW PUMP CHANGED OVER TO STANDBY PUMP NO 1
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Motor windings burnt
DirectCause: Bearing failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Motor to beOverhauled or Replaced
Corrective Action: Ship staff to carry out periodic motor overhaul. Motor condition to be monitored basis Vibration monitoring report. Pump to be started with discharge valve in closed condition. Motor overload setting to check and adjust if required.
ClosingNote: Motor rewinded.

IncidentDetails: 13-SEP-18  ==========  1630 Hrs LT : Vessel noticed indication of gas in 2S WBT.  1700 Hrs LT : Increased sounding in 2S WBT found with a sounding approximately 67 CM. Same time noted 2S COT ullage increased from 4.18 m to approximately 4.85 m (fluctuating reading between 4.0 to 5.4 m)  1720 Hrs LT : Vessel Notified all concerned parties including Valero Marine incident report via Phone call followed by email as per voyage orders.  1730 Hrs LT : Vessel received call from Mr. Christopher Occonor from Valero.    14-SEP-18  ==========  1346 Hrs LT : Vessel Received Permission To Carryout Internal Transfer  1448 Hrs LT : Commenced Internal Transfer  1800 Hrs LT : Commenced Inerting 2S WBT  1900 Hrs LT : Completed Internal Transfer  2359 HrS LT : Completed Inerting of 2S WBT
IncidentEventDetails: Structure Failure in 2S WBT
ImmediateAction: Owners, Managers & Charterer's Notified. Cargo in 2S COT transfered to 1W/3W/4W/5W/SL-S COT. 2S WBT inerted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: SUSPECT PITTING DAMAGE.
DirectCause: STRUCTURE FAILURE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL CARGO TANKS TO BE WASHED,GAS FREED & INSPECTED FOR PITTING ON TANK TOP. EXTENT OF REPAIRS TO BE ASCERTAINED
Corrective Action: Additional job included in the existing Job description of Cargo Tank inspection (30 monthly)       for all tankers as below: Suction well of the cargo tank to be inspected closely for any pitting or damage particularly under the Bell mouth. Use of lights and mirrors is recommended to check inaccessible area. In case of any abnormalities found, Bellmouth to be removed for a thorough close up inspection. Photos of suction well bottom to be attached in the report.
LessionLearnt: Underneath bell mouth inspections to be done in a thorough manner for all cargo tanks.
ReviewComments: Incident investigation report attached.
ClosingNote: Defect closed. All other cargo tanks underneath bell mouth inspected and found to be in order.

IncidentDetails: Duty AB found two people stealing Vessel's lashing material [Turn buckles and twist locks] and lowering into a small boat.  Duty AB informed the Duty officer. Seeing the Vessel’s crew alert robbers jumped into the water and ran away in their boat.
IncidentEventDetails: 2230lt - Duty AB found two people stealing Vessel's lashing material [Turn buckles and twist locks] and lowering into a small boat.  2232lt-  Duty AB informed the Duty officer. Seeing the Vessel’s crew alert robbers jumped into the water and ran away in their boat.
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately informed local agent and PFSO.  Protest letter issued by the Vessel to local agent.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lashing material [ Turnbuckles and twist locks] stolen.
DirectCause: Lack of surveillance by ship staff.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: One crew member permanently posted on the sea side of the vessel to avoid similar incident in future.
ClosingNote: Ship security patrol to be increased in pilferage prone ports.

IncidentDetails: Ingress of water In cargo hold No 7.
IncidentEventDetails: ARRIVAL LONGBEACH        22-Mar-18 21:10 Three hrs notice for CARB Entry      22-Mar-18 21:36 Commenced CFR test      22-Mar-18 22:45 Completed CFR test        22-Mar-18 23:30 Change over to LSMGO complete      23-Mar-18 01:07 Vessel entered CARB      23-Mar-18 07:26 Vessel under Green Flag speed       23-Mar-18 11:00 One Hr Notice To engine room       23-Mar-18 12:00 EOSP arr Long Beach        23-Mar-18 12:10 Con with master      23-Mar-18 13:00 Pilots on board(2nos)      23-Mar-18 13:30 Vessel entering breakwater      23-Mar-18 13:36 Tugs made fast FWRD & AFT(3 Tugs)       23-Mar-18 14:18 First line ashore      23-Mar-18 14:48 All made fast FWRD & AFT      23-Mar-18 14:50 Customs & Immigeration on board       23-Mar-18 15:06 Gangway ready with net       23-Mar-18 15:20 Customs & Immigeration away       23-Mar-18 17:24 Commenced cargo operations      24-Mar-18 08:33 Commenced discharge O/Dk Bay 50        24-Mar-18 20:25 Completed discharge O/Dk Bay 50      24-Mar-18 20:37 Commenced discharge U/Dk Bay 50       25-Mar-18 8:40 Commenced discharge from O/Dk bay 54       25-Mar-18 13:05 Completed Discharge from O/Dk bay 54      25-Mar-18 13:10 Commenced discharge U/Dk Bay 54      25-Mar-18 18:10 Completed discharge U/Dk Bay 54      26-Mar-18 2:15 Completed discharge U/Dk Bay 50      26-Mar-18 2:20 Commenced loading U/Dk Bay 50        27-Mar-18 2:05 Commenced loading U/Dk Bay 54      27-Mar-18 15:00 Completed Loading U/Dk Bay 54      27-Mar-18 15:05 Hatch Cover closed for Bay 54      27-Mar-18 15:10 Commenced loading Bay 54 O/Dk      27-Mar-18 20:00 Cargo Hold inspected by the ETO for the overload trip alarm for the heeling pump - (Nothing Abnormal found by the ETO, cargo hold dry)      27-Mar-18 21:18 Completed loading U/Dk Bay 50      27-Mar-18 21:25 Hatch cover for Bay 50 closed      28-Mar-18 2:45 No.7 Cargo Hold Port side bilge high level alarm      28-Mar-18 2:54 "No.7 Cargo Hold Stbd side bilge high level alarm "      28-Mar-18 8:25 Port side alarm reset automatically      28-Mar-18 9:19 Stbd side alarm reset automatically      28-Mar-18 14:25 Cargo Hold bilge soundings reported to C/O ( Reported No.7 Port & Stbd at 2 metres )      28-Mar-18 14:25 Anti-heeling gauge readings checked by C/O and found Port side at 7 % and Stbd at 56%       28-Mar-18 14:30 Deck hands entered Cargo Hold 7 for inspection         28-Mar-18 14:35 Reported water on tank top       28-Mar-18 14:35 Informed Master      28-Mar-18 14:45 Master, C/O, C/E & 2/E went to No.7 CH for visual inspection       28-Mar-18 14:50 Confirmed 2.5 – 3ft water in the No.7 CH      28-Mar-18 15:20 "Master, C/O, 2/E out of hold and initated transfer of ingressed water to No.9 SWBT ( S )   "      28-Mar-18 15:30 Reported to office on water ingress       28-Mar-18 15:50 Water level reducing in the CH 7  ( Reported by C/E )      28-Mar-18 17:30 C/E reported vaccum breaker valve body ruptured      At this time(28th March’18/1730LT )Chief Eng Confirmed that the water ingress into the hold was from valve BA-39 only after BA-19 valve was opened to test the system and it was visually confirmed of the ingress of water. So even without the pump running, with BA-19 valve open, because of the head of 10mtrs plus, the water gushed into the cargo hold no.7 from No. 7 Stbd side heeling tank.                                28-Mar-18 19:40 The water level went down sufficient to enable access the valve BA-39, the pipe line        and the pump for inspection. The ruptured valve was removed from the line, blank put        in place and valve was brought to the ship office for inspection. The transfer of water        from the cargo hold no.7 bilges was still in progress to the no.9 SWBT Stbd side.      28-Mar-18 20:30 The transfer of water from the hold no.7 to the no.9SWBT Stbd side was complete        and there was no water on the tank top of no.7 cargo hold.               28-Mar-18 20:30 No. 9 SWBT Tank final reading : 833 Cubm       28-Mar-18 20:30 No. 7 Cargo hold bilges at Port : 0.92m and Stbd : 0.93m        Anti heeling tank was used on Manual mode with one crew standby in the No.7 cargo hold for observation of the heeling system.                DEPARTURE LONG BEACH        29-Mar-18 4:45 One hour notice to E/R      29-Mar-18 5:18 Cargo operations completed      29-Mar-18 5:35 Pilot onboard      29-Mar-18 5:45 Departure checks completed      29-Mar-18 6:06 Standby Engines      29-Mar-18 6:06 Fwd & Aft tug made fast      29-Mar-18 6:18 All Cast off      29-Mar-18 6:54 Tugs Cast off      Mid sea passage – No.9SWBT ( S ) & CH No.7 Bilges Pumped out. Ventilation initiated for No.7 CH. Carried out bilge alarms for all Cargo hold bilges                        ARRIVAL OAKLAND         30-Mar-18 3:10 One hour notice to E/R       30-Mar-18 4:00 EOSP      30-Mar-18 4:15 M/E Tested ahead & Astern      30-Mar-18 4:54 Pilot onboard      30-Mar-18 6:00 Harbour pilot onboard      30-Mar-18 6:24 4 Tugs made fast to vessel      30-Mar-18 8:18 First Line Ashore      30-Mar-18 8:42 All F
ImmediateAction: Informed Master,Visual inspection carried out , Confirmed 2.5 – 3ft water in the No.7 CH due to vacuum breaker valve body ruptured , The ruptured valve was removed from the line, blank put in place and started to  the transfer of water from the cargo hold no.7 bilges  to the no.9 SWBT Stbd side.Anti heeling tank was used on Manual mode with one crew standby in the No.7 cargo hold for observation of the heeling system.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo hold no. 7 flooded due to breach in the vacuum breaker valve body connecting the two heeling tanks. During transfer of water to upright the vessel during the cargo operations, the water flooded in to the cargo hold instead.  The cargo hold high bilge level alarm occured for both cargo hold bilges which was not attended by the duty officer. This resulted in flooding of the no. 7 cargo hold to 2.5 to 3feet of water which submerged the container cargo in the hold to 3 feet.
DirectCause: 1. Breach in the body of the vacuum breaker valve.  2. Cargo hold bilge alarm unaudiable, unattended, not acknowledged on the Aconis pannel by the duty officer.  3. Valve BA19 kept left open for auto operations of the heeling system.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Broken vacuum breaker valve to be removed.  2. Line to be blanked with flange.  3. Flooded water to be transfered from cargo hold no. 7 to No. 9 SWBT stbd side.
Corrective Action: Vacuum Breaker Valve from the Heeling main line should be removed from use onboard all Six Vessels.
LessionLearnt: Technical Suptd Mr.Daniel boarded Vessel at Busan on 15th April 2018, to carryout investigation on the incident. Following considered as causal factors for this incident 1) Failure of Equipment 2) Inadequate Maintenance procedure for Heeling System 3) Failure to comply with Procedure After taking over of Vessel, Ship Staff were not familiar with the Valves associated with the Heeling system, especially the Valves on the Heeling Line. BA39 was not verified from the time of take over of the Vessel, and ship staff were not familiar with same. Both heeling tanks have High level and low-level alarms. When the Low-level alarm activates, the heeling pump should stop operation. It was observed that the Low-level alarm was not in operation condition. Low level alarms where not tested by ship staff since takeover of Vessel.  PMS system did not capture inspection of Heeling line and associated Valves. PMS System did not include testing of Low-level alarms of heeling tank, when heeling System in automatic mode.  Cargo Hold bilge Alarm was received at 0245Hrs Lt on the ACONIS Monitoring system, but same was failed to be identified by the Watch Keeper, and as per the monitoring system the alarm was reset at 0825Hrs Lt. Based on physical verification of the bilge alarm system, we suspect that the alarms would have been reset automatically due to water ingress into the level switches present in the Cargo Hold, by shorting the open contacts. The Cargo Hold Bilge Alarm is received in Ships office, and Nav Bridge. Once the alarm is acknowledged the sound does not recur, however the light indicates the presence of alarm, and the monitoring system indicates the details of the Alarm, with the Start time of the Alarm and stop time of the Alarm. The Alarm which was received in Ships office and Bridge, was of feeble sound. The history of Alarms was not being verified by Ship Staff during Takeover of Watch and during the Watch. Further on analysis of the Aconis system noted that in CCR (Ships Office), all cargo holds bilge alarms (Audio & Visual) are received. On Bridge, only visual alarm is available. There is no cargo hold bilge alarms in the ECR Aconis system.  Physical verification of Cargo Hold bilges was being carried out onboard, however on that specific day, Ship Staff delayed same due to Vessel’s other operations.
ReviewComments: On 28h March 2018, when vessel was at Long beach port, Ship Staff observed excessive water in No.7 CH Bilge while carrying out CH bilge sounding. Visual check was carried out for the Cargo hold no.7 and found water ingress above tank top. There were 33 Container Units in the bottom tier of the Cargo Hold suspected to have Water ingress due to this incident. Bilge water internally transferred to No.9SWBT(Stbd), after which observed that the Vacuum breaker Valve was damaged. Managers, Owners, P&I Club and Charterers informed regarding this incident and Vessel Suspects water ingress into the 33 Container Units Stowed at the lower tier. On 30th March 2018, P&I Local correspondent attended Vessel at Oakland port for investigating this matter. No injuries, or damage to Vessel, or delay to Vessel Schedule experienced due to this matter.
ClosingNote: Direct Cause:   - Failure of Vacuum Breaker Valve on the heeling line  Indirect Cause:   - Failure to monitor Cargo Hold Bilge Alarm, which was received on the ACONIS monitoring system. - Failure to capture routine inspection of Valves associated with Heeling System in PMS. - Failure to capture routine testing of High Level and Low-level Alarm of Heeling Tanks in PMS.  Root Cause:   - Pulsating Internal pressure on the Valve during Heeling Pump operation - Negligence to monitor the Alarm system by Watch Keeper - Inadequate Training/Familiarization of Ship staff in close monitoring of Cargo Hold Bilge Alarm - Inadequate procedure for routine check of Heeling System

IncidentDetails: Newly received spares (Piston rings for #AE) damaged while lowering from skylight into Engine Room.
IncidentEventDetails: 12:00 LT - Supply barge alongside vessel for supplying stores, spares, etc.  13:50 LT - Vessel picked up Spares box for AE Decarb from barge and place on main deck.  14:00 LT - 3/E was attempting to lower spares box (approx. weight 30-35 Kgs) with the help of ETO, using rope and did not tie/secure the rope properly to the box. As soon as box was brought to the sky-light opening, it crashed down into the Engine room, damaging the Piston rings for AE.
ImmediateAction: Ensured no injury to anyone in the E/R, or damage to any other equipment below.  Checked all the spares in the box for damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 6 Piston rings received as part of AE Decarb spares found broken.
DirectCause: Lack of knowledge / skill by the concerned person in properly securing the box.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training of personnel for proper securing of equipment when lowering from a height.
ClosingNote: The tool box meeting should ensure that the proper securing of the equipment being lowered is carried out. A responsible person should be designated during the lifting operations. A risk assessment shall be in place which must identify satisfactorily, all the risks involved with the lifting operation.

IncidentDetails: Vessels hull made contact with Fenders while transitiing locks in Antwerp
IncidentEventDetails: Pilot Jan Van Leewen boarded vessel at 1948 LT at 281P ATPC Terminal.   At 2042 vessel was entering the locks , vessel heading was 317 (T) and her rudder was at stbd 10 Deg.  It was noticed that the stbd clearence from the fenders was getting too narrow and a port 20 Deg helm order was given by the pilot.  Vessel hull made contact with the fenders near frame number 83 (FWD of PBL).  Indentation of around 5 cm in an area of 4 m2 notiiced.
ImmediateAction: A Letter of protest made and given to the Pilot. P&I Club contacted
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Indentation of hull near Frame no 86     Size : Height 1.2 m X Width 0.6 m X Depth 4 cm
DirectCause: thorough master pilot exchange was not done.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fenders not appropriate for locks. (no rubber fenders available). Pilot should communicate in english languae to tugs so same can be understood by bridge team. very common occurance at anterp locks as understood from Local Agents & pilots. so accordingly sam eshould be transmitted to all vessels in fleet calling antwerp.
Corrective Action: Tank 2S to be inspected internally and externally for any damage.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: The job order given by the chief officer was to continue the pending job which was reconditioning   The Cargo Hold Cleats.  At 0830lt the deck crew were busy taking out the CH cleats on CH no. 04.  At 1045lt the 2 OS started to transport the CH cleats which were taken on CH no.04 port side  By the use of the push cart. The plan was to recondition the cleats inside the forecastle due to rain.  During the said transport of the 14 cleats, a sudden rolling happened and the 2 OS manoeuvring   The cart lost control of it and hit the railings that causes the 7 cleats to go overboard.
ImmediateAction: The OS inform the bosun  of the incident and the bosun inform the chief officer. We check right away how many were missing and check for spares, after the checking right away inform the master of what was happened.  We had replaced the missing cleats with the spare and right now we don’t have any spare cleats for the CH.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lost of Cargo Hold cleats on CH.04 port side
DirectCause: Immediate cause was the sudden roll experienced.  The root cause being that the crew members were not cautious and vigilant in lashing the loose gear being transported and  had not thought of the hazards and consequences of the job being carried out. During the roll the trolley hit an eye on deck and the trolley was going to overturn, During this time the Cleats on the trolley fell over into the sea.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tool Box meetings being carried out in the mornings by the departmental heads to include the Hazards and consequences of the job being carried out. Same to be conveyed to the crew members performing the job on deck. Training of crew members to be carried out and a Meeting to be held among the deck crew to educate them and make them more vigilant in the daily activities being performed.
Corrective Action: Tool Box meetings being carried out in the mornings by the departmental heads to include the Hazards and consequences of the job being carried out. Same to be conveyed to the crew members performing the job on deck. Training of crew members to be carried out and a Meeting to be held among the deck crew to educate them and make them more vigilant in the daily activities being performed.
LessionLearnt: RA to be performed in the smallest of jobs being carried out
ReviewComments: To be discussed in safety meeting
ClosingNote: To be cautious when on deck

IncidentDetails: While departure from Agua clara lock Gate -2 , AB was operating mooring winch under supervision of duty officer as per advice of PC Mooring Master. While casting off stern line, Mooring rope parted due to excess tension owing to rapid increase of water level in the lock gate . Mooring master got panicked once he heard mooring rope/ winch  tension sound , and start shouting to AB  '' HEAVE UP MOORING ROPE”, Due to wrong order given by  Mooring master, mooring rope which was already under tension was heaved up  resulting in mooring rope parting. All men were standing well clear and no person was injured.
ImmediateAction: Rope end to end changed
DirectCause: Communication problem, Panic, unfamiliar Mooring hand signal .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Training of all crew done for mooring operations

Vessel: DK ABDUL RAZZAK KHALID ZAID AL-KHALID
IncidentDetails: While receiving HFO/LSMGO bunker through Bunker Barge MARWAH-9 , made fast to Vessel Stbd Side .Bunker barge due to its very light condition was pitching moderately to Low long head Swell , condition Aggravated after completion of LSMGO bunker , as wind direction changed to Fine on Stbd on Vessel bow Barge started Pitching heavily , leading to parting of its mooring line .  Barge slacken its mooring line leading to lateral movement Also , As both Vessel Accommodation blocks were adjacent & size of rigged fenders were very small given the size of Barge , During violent rolling pitching , Bunker barge made contact with own vessel & damaged Protection Guard below stbd side Lifeboat.
IncidentEventDetails: 0424 Hrs. LT: EOSP  0530 Hrs. LT: Vessel Anchored  Weather condition 0400-0800 Hours LT: Slight sea , Lt Airs & low long head swell ( swell ht-0.5 metres , length-apprx-25-50 metres)      (After Anchor Vessel heading was steady, no Rolling/ Pitching or any ship movement, Anchor Holding its position.  Wind & swell Direction from Right ahead).    0700 Hrs. LT: Bunker barge started making approach, Barge fender were rigged on her Port side & informed Vessel to come alongside on Vessel Stbd Side.  As sea & wind condition were same on both Side of vessel & as weather condition was Slight sea  & light Airs, Vessel Accepted Barge Request.    During making Approach & while coming Alongside Barge was steady with very slight pitching.    0748 Hrs. LT: Bunker barge along side  0755 Hrs. LT: Completed mooring  0900 Hrs. LT: Bunker hose connected (HFO)  0906 Hrs. LT: Bunker hose connected (LSMGO)  0930 Hrs. LT: Bunker Barge started Pitching moderately  1005 Hrs. LT: Commence Bunkering (HFO)  1025 Hrs. LT: Commence Bunkering (LSMGO)  1050 Hrs. LT: Bunker barge pitching Heavily , Own vessel was steady without any change in Vessel's Heading , Wind Direction have changed to Stbd Bow with Swell low & long from Head , Notify barge And asked barge to keep all line attended all times  1100 Hrs. LT: Bunker Barge stern line parted  1112 Hrs. LT: Additional Lines were taken from Barge forward & aft.  1118 Hrs. LT: Vessel advised Barge to rig additional fenders around Accommodation block  1125 Hrs. LT: Additional fenders placed on both Barge & Vessel    Weather condition 0800-1200 Hrs. LT.: Slight sea, Lt Airs & low long head swell (swell ht-0.5 metres, length-apprx-25-50 metres) Ships heading almost steady between 252 -245 Degrees    1210 Hrs. LT: Completed LSMGO Bunkering  1212 Hrs. LT: Barge made contact with Vessel on Stbd side, below Stbd side lifeboat at protection Guard causing dent of apprx 1-2 mm on plate & fish plate below it  1215 Hrs. LT: Ceased bunkering operation As Barge MARWAH-9 is rolling/pitching heavily & barge not able to control its movement,                      Barge drifted off vessel by 15 mtrs, fully stretching Bunker Hose.  1215 Hrs. LT: Vessel notified all concerned Parties  1236 Hrs. LT: Bunker Hose Disconnected  1248 Hrs. LT: Barge cast off  1248-1330 Hrs. LT: Barge Manoeuvred from Vessel Stbd side to port Side, transferring YOKOHAMA fenders to its stbd side, All tyre fenders & Coir fenders were transferred.  1330 Hrs. LT: Bunker barge along side  1342 Hrs. LT: Completed mooring  1506 Hrs. LT: Bunker hose connected (HFO)  1512 Hrs. LT: Resumed HFO Bunkering  1612 Hrs. LT: Completed bunkering  1712 Hrs. LT: Bunker hose disconnected  1842 Hrs. LT: Bunker barge cast off.
ImmediateAction: Ceased bunkering operation As Barge MARWAH-9 is rolling/pitching heavily & barge not able to control its movement
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: damaged Protection Guard below stbd side Lifeboat.
DirectCause: Inadequate planning, Lack of supervision, Inadequate procedures
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.  Weather Forecast through Weather service-“Dosca “  or through Local Agents  2.  Prior Arrival information of Bunker Barge through Updated Q88, Exchange of information between barge & vessel to determine Compatibility of two vessel involved, Determination of size of fender & additional Fendering requirement.  3. Provision of larger diameter fenders to increase separation distance.  4.  Upon  Arrival , communication between vessel to Review & agree operational procedures , Manoeuvring pattern , mooring Arrangement & preparation , Weather consideration  5. Alignment of Bunker Manifold in such a way to maintain sufficient distance between two vessel accommodation Blocks.  6. Proper & effective communication between both vessel & barge & appropriate preparation are undertaken prior Unmooring.  7. Suspend bunkering   operation , notify all concerned parties & cast off barge if condition are unfavourable.

IncidentDetails: ME JCW heater out of operation. Closer inspection is needed. No injuries.
IncidentEventDetails: 06.10.2018 at 17-05  LT, an auto fire alarm was activated for ER bottom platform Stb side area. Bridge informed.  Was observed smoke & a fire from ME JCW preheater. Fire was elimininated using one CO2 extinguisher. Permanent watch with a VHF was arranged on site to monitor the situation till heater will cool down for further investigation
ImmediateAction: Fire was eliminated, monitoring watch arranged. Office informed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Following parts of preheater was damaged:  • Heating coils completely damaged. Heater elements were completely burned.  • Burn out all associated wires and terminals on casing of heater.  • Damaged Temperature probe.  • Damaged Starter panel and Temperature controller  • Burn out heater insulation (glass wool).
DirectCause: • The heating coil had developed a hole probably due to ageing, which caused water to penetrate the hole and cause short circuit and subsequent burning of the terminals. It is assumed that due to arc flashing (because of short circuit) inside of heater, outer shell got red hot and lead to fire of glass wool insulation and other cables in contact with the casing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: • Heater coils physical verification to be included in PMS.  • Regular Maintenance of Starter Panel and temp controller  • Insulation monitoring of heaters to be included in PMS  • Verify condition of temp controllers and ensure temp setting as per makers guidance  • To check with makers regarding the replacement interval of heater element if any.
Corrective Action: Heater coils physical verification to be included in PMS as a 2.5 yearly routine.
ClosingNote: close watch was kept on heater for last 5 months with satisfacyory results. Crew awareness was noted.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was all fast with FPSO Armada Olombendo Terminal and hose was lifted up on deck to be connected to ship's manifold no.2 on port side. The blank flange was being removed as per instruction from the mooring master. About 75 ltrs of oil spilled on drip tray and deck.
IncidentEventDetails: 0824LT : Vessel all fast with FPSO by single chain on port chain stopper  0845LT : Commenced hose connection on port side  0930LT : Blank flange of hose opened and oil spilled on deck  1042LT : Hose connected to No.2 manifold on port side
ImmediateAction: Ship-staff alerted and contained all the spilled oil on half drums and area thoroughly cleaned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oil spill of about 75 ltrs on drip tray and deck occurred while opening the blank flange of shore hose while it was being connected to ship's manifold.The spill was contained onboard and cleaned up immediately. The blank flange was cam lock type and no means of control if there was a leak.
DirectCause: As per mooring master the butterfly valve at the hose end was not closed properly after the last operation and oil leaked into space between the valve and the blank flange. No drain facility provided between the valve and the blank flange.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Shipstaff to be advised to open the blank flange of sbm/ fpso hoses  to ensure no leak during future operations.
Corrective Action: Shipstaff to be advised to open the blank flange of sbm/ fpso hoses  to ensure no leak during future operations.
ClosingNote: Vessel has confirmed that crew briefing and training carried out

IncidentDetails: At Norfolk Terminal 29th July 2018. While discharging containers from Bay 70, gantry spreader came in contact with 20 foot containers which stacked in bay 70 and three 20 foot containers toppled to the adjacent row and damaged two reefer containers on bay 70 row 16 & 18 and eight 40 foot containers went overboard.
IncidentEventDetails: 1443Hrs Lt: Commenced discharging on deck Bay 70  1545Hrs Lt: Duty A.B. informed Duty Officer(3/O) that he saw 8 containers floating overboard (port side) / Discharging of on deck containers stopped for Bay 70  Observed no injuries to ship staff as well as stevedores.  See attached statement of fact and letter of protest.
ImmediateAction: Relayed safety message to pilot station to avoid any damage to vessel navigating the channel. Informed Managers, CMA CGM Marine manager, Charterers, Qualification individual, P&I club, Agents and USCG. Checked for any damage to vessel structure or injury to crew.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to three 20 foot containers and two 40 foot reefer containers and  eight containes went overboard and no damages observed to vessel structure or ship staff.
DirectCause: Negligence and rash operation of gantry crane operator.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Issued LOP to the terminal and charterers.
ClosingNote: Terminal took responsibility for the incident due to crane operation.The Malta flag notified,USCG notified & NOP issued to terminal & charterers.

IncidentDetails: During Discharging of ULSD at POTSA Terminal, Pier-06,Baboa Oil Sippage noticed on No.2 Cargo Pump's  drain line in Pumproom.
IncidentEventDetails: 1400: Entry made in Pumproom for inspection.      1405: Cargo smell was felt on COP platform near NO.2 COP, which was in use.      1410: After thorough inspection it was found that oil is seeping from No.2 COP drain line.      1411: No.2 COP stopped.       1412: Engine room informed and duty engineer was asked to prepare no.1 COP.      1415: Commence temperoary arrangement (Cold Work) to arrest the leakage       1418: No.1 COP lined up and No.1 COP on rolling.      1424: No.1 COP running to discharge instead of No.1.      1425: Leak arrested and COP platform cleaned.
ImmediateAction: 1) Discharging immediately ceased and concerned parties informed. 2) Discharging resumed with no.1 COP. 3) Temperoary arrangement to arrest the leak. 4) COP platform cleaned . 5) Thorough inspection of  drain lines of all seperaors. 6) Cheking of spare pipe line of correct size ( Spares on board). 7) Incident was discussed among the crew and frequency of pumproom round increased.8) Work plan made for permanent repairs ( Drain Pipe will be replaced .)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During Discharging of ULSD at POTSA Terminal, Pier-06,Baboa Oil Sippage noticed on No.2 Cargo Pump's  drain line in Pumproom.After inspection 2 holes noticed on the Line. Spare pipe line of correct size onboard.
DirectCause: Due to age of the vessel pitting formation on the line which is not visible from outside of the pipe.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Drain line will be replaced. As a precautionary measure the condition of drain lines of other COP's will also be checked and same will be renewed if required.
Corrective Action: Drain line will be replaced. As a precautionary measure the condition of drain lines of other COP's will also be checked and same will be renewed if required.
ClosingNote: Drain lines renewed

IncidentDetails: VESSEL WAS CARRYING OUT THE JOB OF MAIN ENGINE EXHAUST VALVE REPLACEMENT WHEN DUE TO SUDDEN STRONG WINDS VSL STARTED TO DRAG ANCHOR AND ENTERED THE FAIRWAY     26 OCT 2018  ==========  2215 LT:  REPORTED VTIS VSL DRAGGING ANCHOR                   VESSEL DRAGGED ANCHOR DUE TO SUDDEN STRONG WINDS AND PREVAILING CURRENTS                   VESSEL UNABLE TO HEAVE ANCHOR DUE TO STRONG WINDS / DRIFTING TO FAIRWAY                   VESSEL IN CONTINOUS CONTACT VTS AND OTHER PASSING VESSELS   2220 LT:   VSL REQUESTED FOR TUGS VIA VHF   2250 LT:    VTIS ADVICED PILOT WILL BOARD THE VSL WITH TUGS  2320 LT:   ANCHOR AWEIGH  2325 LT:   VSL CONTACTED PILOTS AND INFORMED M/E IN WORKING CONDITION AND BEING USED   2326 LT:   PILOTS INFORMED TO MAKE FAST TUGS AT PORT QTR & STBD QTR  2336 LT:   TUGS MADE FAST PORT QTR. TUGS NAME: YAZHOUIZ HAO  2336 LT:   PILOT ONBOARD  2342 LT:   TUG MADE FAST STBD QTR.TUGS NAME: VAZHOU JIU HAO                     STBY TUGS : HUAN LIU HAO   YA ZHOU 6 HAO     27 OCT 2018  ===========  0024 LT:   ANCHORED AT NO 1 ANCHORAGE    0100 LT:   TUGS CAST OFF  0112 LT:   PILOT AWAY.
IncidentEventDetails: 26 OCT 2018  ==========  2215 LT:  REPORTED VTIS VSL DRAGGING ANCHOR                   VESSEL DRAGGED ANCHOR DUE TO SUDDEN STRONG WINDS AND PREVAILING CURRENTS                   VESSEL UNABLE TO HEAVE ANCHOR DUE TO STRONG WINDS / DRIFTING TO FAIRWAY                   VESSEL IN CONTINOUS CONTACT VTS AND OTHER PASSING VESSELS   2220 LT:   VSL REQUESTED FOR TUGS VIA VHF   2250 LT:    VTIS ADVICED PILOT WILL BOARD THE VSL WITH TUGS  2320 LT:   ANCHOR AWEIGH  2325 LT:   VSL CONTACTED PILOTS AND INFORMED M/E IN WORKING CONDITION AND BEING USED   2326 LT:   PILOTS INFORMED TO MAKE FAST TUGS AT PORT QTR & STBD QTR  2336 LT:   TUGS MADE FAST PORT QTR. TUGS NAME: YAZHOUIZ HAO  2336 LT:   PILOT ONBOARD  2342 LT:   TUG MADE FAST STBD QTR.TUGS NAME: VAZHOU JIU HAO                     STBY TUGS : HUAN LIU HAO   YA ZHOU 6 HAO     27 OCT 2018  ===========  0024 LT:   ANCHORED AT NO 1 ANCHORAGE    0100 LT:   TUGS CAST OFF  0112 LT:   PILOT AWAY.
ImmediateAction: Vsl reanchored
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MAIN ENGINE EXHAUST VALVE LEAK
DirectCause: 1. The vessel position was not suitable to carry out repairs to M/E. The vessel did not       consider proceeding out from the congested waters to a more open position       outside for repairs. 2. Insufficient length of anchor cable used. The weather conditions were force 5       gusting to force 7.             As per navigation manual -       We recommend the use of below formula which is as per Japanese Publication        Theory of Ship Operation in deciding the number of shackles that need to be used         in anchoring a vessel.         Fine weather: L=3d+90m (d=Depth of water in metres)         Rough weather: L=4d+145m (d=Depth of water in metres)          The depth of water at anchorage was approx 30 meters. The recommended          shackles were 10 instead of actual 7 shackles lowered by vessel.  3. There was no standby tug for the vessel anticipating the possibility of the vessel       dragging anchor with M/E immobilised.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: RENEWAL/OVERHAULING OF ALL EXHAUST VALVES
Corrective Action: The dynamic navigation audit to be carried out of the bridge team.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: The fire hose box located near the mid ship crane getting damaged due to shifting of wire rope drum reel in  adverse weather.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) The vessel was at sea proceeding to Panama following a GC route & heavy weather was expected.  2)During the heavy weather lashing were checked for the drum reels of the wire ropes which were recived in Incehon , Korea & secured on Main Deck.  3)The drums during heavy weather have started shifting during heavy rolling of about 20 Deg which crushed the fire hose box.  4)The fire hose & nozzle are secured and safe , photos for the damage are attached here with.  5)The wire rope drum was 200 KG approx &  wire lashing has come adrifit due to heavy rolling.
ImmediateAction: Extra lashing for the drum reel was taken & kept clear of the fire hose box.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The fire hose box was crushed in such a way that the box was broken in 2 peices , however the fire hose / nozzle & spanners were not damged.
DirectCause: The damage to the fire hose box was caused in heavy weather due to the drum reeels for wire ropes having a impact on the box as it shifted during heavy weather. The lashing for the same drums were checked as vessel was expecting heavy weather but the lashing taken was not effective overall.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A thorough lashing must be taken for all the loose items on deck & also securing to be made in such a way which does not damage any equipments in vicinity.
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting of this incident. Noted that additional lashings were taken. Noted the preventive action taken.

IncidentDetails: DURING ROUTINE ROUNDS OF AB ON DECK AT AROUND 0100H WHILE VESSEL IS AT JORF LASFAR ANCHORAGE AND EXPERIENCING HEAVY PITCHING AND ROLLING DUE TO BAD WEATHER. HE FOUND OUT THAT THE ANCHOR CHAIN STOPPER HAS OPEN UP BEACAUSE THE SECURING PIN WAS BROKEN DUE TO EXCESSIVE FORCE ON CHAIN CAUSED BY HEAVY PITCHING AND ROLLING OF THE VESSEL. ALSO FOUND OUT THAT THE CHAIN STOPPER HAS BENDED. PLS SEE ATTACHED PHOTO
ImmediateAction: IMMEDIATELY CALLED BSN ON DECK.TEMPORARILY REPLACED THE PIN WITH AN UN-USED MARLIN SPIKE AND AND TIED WITH ROPES. TO FABRICATE NEW PIN FOR THE BOW STOPPER.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: PIN DAMAGE AND BOW STOPPER BENDED.
DirectCause: EXCESSIVE FORCE ON CHAIN CAUSED BY HEAVY ROLLING AND PITCHING OF VESSEL DUE TO BAD WEATHER.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TO FABRICATE NEW BOW STOPPER PIN
Corrective Action: New Bow Stopper and pin to be supplied
ClosingNote: New Bow Stopper and Pin supplied and renewed.

IncidentDetails: While discharging at Wilmington,USA, the vessel recieved an EGC warning and MSI message regarding Hurricane Michael. Prior departure the vessel was secured for heavy seas. Rounds were taken by Master and C/Off and the vessel was found well secured for sea. After departure on 10th October morning re-checks were carried out and the vessel was found to be secure.   The vessel commenced driftiing on 11th Oct 0712 hrs to avoid Hurricane Michael. The vessel was experiencing B/F 8-9 and swell up to 5 mtrs. During the course of drifting the vessel was pounding very heavily between 0200-0600 hrs while in close proximity of Hurricane Michael.  After the weather improved on 12th Oct 0900 hrs C/Off visited the forecastle store with the  Bosun and found hydraulic oil drums toppled over and badly damaged due to making contact with the angle bar of lashing arrangment.   Lashing rope was in place and damage had occured due to heavy impact on the bulkhead, the sharp edge of angle bar and metal plate. Approx 1050 ltrs of oil had leaked from drums and the floor was slippery.  The Master was informed regarding the same. The Master rushed to store immediately and clean up procedure commenced. Oil was collected in empty drums and secured. Company MSI, TSI and Officers were informed.
IncidentEventDetails: Upon receiving EGC and MSI messages,the vessel was well secured for sea and all heavy weather precautions were taken.   On 11th Oct 0712 hrs the vessel departed from Cape Henry Anchorage to avoid Hurricane Michael. During the course of drifting the vessel was experiencing winds of upto B/F 8-9 and swell of upto 5 mtrs. Vsl was steaming at various courses and speeds to avoid heavy swell. The vessel  drifted for around 31.3 hrs.  On Oct 12th 0900 hrs after the weather improved, the C/Off and Bosun went on deck for rounds and found that the oil drums have been badly damaged. The Master was informed regarding this and clean up process was initiated. Around 1050 ltrs of oil had leaked inside store due to the damage of the drums.
ImmediateAction: Oil cleaned, all drums kept in place and secured well, informed TSI/MSI, area painted. Waste oil transferred to empty drums.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Heavy Weather damage

IncidentEventDetails: On 19 Sept. 2018 at 1030H-LT, while embarking to the lifeboat by embarkation ladder the portable radio (W/T) in adventantly fell into the water. The portable radio was lost to the sea but could not be retrieved as it sunk.
ImmediateAction: Reported to the officer in charge.
DirectCause: Potential risks should be assessed prior commencement of any work to prevent loss of ship's property.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The portable radio (W/T) must be having radio casing & straps to avoid felt overboard. If the strapping and casing is not available then alternate methods of lowering the W/T to personnel in the boat should be planned. Conduct a meeting with the team and discuss the above and upload the meeting minutes.

If the strapping and casing is not available then alternate methods of lowering the W/T to personnel in the boat should be planned.
