IncidentDetails: Bunker Overflow in the purifier room while bunkering at Singapore
IncidentEventDetails: While bunkering at Singapore Sudong special anchorage on 31st October 2018 at 1330 LT previous Chief Engineer who was in charge of bunkering operation noticed that the tank in which the bunkering was going on, was reaching 92% of the tank capacity.   In order to make space in the tank he asked one of the engine ratings to transfer the oil from bunkering tank to the settling tank & C/E forgot to stop the transfer.   Oil overflowed to the overflow tank which eventually filled the sludge tank and Spilled out in the purifier room.
ImmediateAction: Bunkering stopped and clean up started, approx. 10 barrel of spilled oil contained & transferred to sludge tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nil
DirectCause: Non compliance with company bunkering procedures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Company guidelines & procedure to be followed at all times.  Person involved in bunkering operation to be further trained prior taking responsibility of any critical operation.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: On 6th Nov 0500lt while coming alongside at FOS SUR MER,France, damage occured to the Tug boat VHF antenna as alledged by the TUG boat and was informed to Pilot. Vessel had made fast forward tug through centre lead. Tug was pushing on the stbd bow under the flare when the incident occured.    The damage was not witnessed by shipstaff as alleged by the tug boat.
IncidentEventDetails: vessel was manouvering adjacent to the berth to come alongside. Tug was fast centre lead. tug came under the flare to push. choppy waters & tug rolling & heaving slightly.     Wx conditions were harsh steady winds 25 kts gusting 30 kts, heavy rainfall. Waves at berth 1.0mtrs. Tug boat was rolling & heaving.Vessel was heavy loaded and it was difficult to turn her in stron wind.Arr draft fwd:13.90mtrs,aft:14.10mtrs.
ImmediateAction: Incident was observed & reported later by Tug master to the Pilot.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: VHF antenna of the tug boat damaged.Extent of damage unaccounted.
DirectCause: Due to Wx conditions tug had to come under the flare & it was negligance on the part of the Tug master to not have checked his antenna height.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Forward stations must follow up & report position of tug.
ClosingNote: No claims received from the tug company.

IncidentDetails: Sea water entered into Motor room Switchboard upto bottom frame.
IncidentEventDetails: Cargo Compressure #3 was ran for Tank cooling for around 3 hrs & then it was stopped, after that CIP was running for cleaning Cargo condenser #1, 3E wanted to take the AE #2 performance after 1600 Hrs, so for that CIP was stooped around 1540 hrs & then after two comp. was started for AE performance to increase the Load.After the performance was taken, GE was informed to stop the comp. same was done by GE around 1650 hrs.Since the comp. was not run for a long time, after around 10 minutes Cargo SW pump was also stopped.There is an interconnection between Cargo condenser outlet and CIP outlet which might have been not shut properly which lead sea water to overflow into the CIP unit and there by into the Motor room. As the Drain plugs of motor room were closed, the sea water filled up to the level of bottom frame of Switchboard.    Checked the CIP Tank & found it filled completely. Tested the water in it & found SW. It is suspected that after stopping the CIP unit, some SW valve must have not been shut properly, due to which the SW leaked to the CIP tank &   water overflowed from the Vent which is a 2- 3-inch length, pipe with a mesh on the tank.
ImmediateAction: Drain plugs opened immediately and water emptied out using portable wilden pump. Insulation was checked for the Cargo related machinery in the motor room.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO damage to machinery or personnel occured.
DirectCause: Due to improper supervision during CIP procedures and improper valve isolation. Sea water has leaked into the CIP tank and into the motor room.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CIP routines to be done with physical attendance during the entire procedure and continuous monitoring through out the job.
Corrective Action: The lines to be kept blanked off while not in use
ClosingNote: CIP cleanings routines are not frequently carried out, lines blanked-off. Blanks would be removed prior cleaning and put back after cleaning completion.

IncidentDetails: The lifeboat Oar for Stbd side Lifeboat was damged & broken.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Lifeboat lowering and manouvering was under progress at Balboa , Panama anchorage where after lowering the Stbd side lifeboat was in water & the ships staff was using the engines as well as the Boat hooks & oars to move clear of the ship side.  2) While the lifeboat was rolling and current was pushing the boat towards the vessel during which one of the boat oars was trapped between the lifeboat and the curvature of the ship which caused it to get damged and it broke.
ImmediateAction: The ship staff immidiately took the remaining oar part inside & avoided using any other equipement to keep clear of the ship side except the engines.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The boat oars onboard for the stbd side lifeboat have been borken into two pieces & the half part for the same was lost at sea.
DirectCause: The ships staff had not taken into consideration of the swell and current acting upon the lifeboat while using boat equipments to keep clear of the ships side.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The vessel ships staff was trained for such conditions in near future where only boat hooks to be used for keeping the lifeboat clear of the ship side and avoiding to use such equipements if vessel cannot avoid touching the ship side due to swell/current. The ship staff was breifed that only engines to be used to keep clear of ship side during such sceanrio.
ClosingNote: We concur with the cause analysis and the corrective action taken. Suggest to include this hazard to all future Risk Assessment for lifeboat lowering.

IncidentDetails: After Departure from Foynes, Ireland V/L encountered heavy weather which lead to heavy rolling and pitching,seas coming on deck  due to which the secured oil drums at the poop deck were damaged/contaminated, Damage also caused to fire hose boxes,hoses and nozzle.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Depature from Foynes, Ireland.  2. Heavy seas and rough weather .  3. Heavy rolling and pitching.  4. Damaged Oil drums secured at the poop deck.  5. Damaged Fire Hose Boxes and Nozzles.
ImmediateAction: After the sea was calm all the drums were transferd inside mast house and secured. Fire hose boxes were renewed and the damaged nozzles were replaced with the new one's and everything was secured on the deck.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After Departure from Foynes, Ireland V/L encountered heavy weather which lead to heavy rolling and pitching,seas coming on deck  due to which the secured oil drums at the poop deck were damaged/contaminated, Damage also caused to fire hose boxes,hoses and nozzle.
DirectCause: Rough Sea and Strong winds.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: After the sea was calm all the drums were transferd inside mast house and secured. Fire hose boxes were renewed and the damaged nozzles were replaced with the new one's and everything was secured on the deck.
Corrective Action: After the sea was calm all the drums were transferd inside mast house and secured. Fire hose boxes were renewed and the damaged nozzles were replaced with the new one's and everything was secured on the deck.
ClosingNote: CAR found satisfactory and closed

IncidentDetails: On 09th November during the voyage from Ras Laffan to Jakarta, Fuel Oil pressure was found  fluctuating.  Main engine was stopped for further inspection. The pressure adjusting valve in Fuel Oil line  was found malfunctioning, same was overhauled. Whilst the pressure adjusting valve was  being overhauled, scavenge space inspection was carried out and it was observed that the  piston rings of Unit No 4 & 5 were broken. Overhauling of Unit No 4 & 5 was carried out. The  fuel valves for the above units were pressure tested and fitted back.  Vessel resumed passage at 1500 hrs. on 11th Nov. There was no damage to property,  pollution to environment or injury to any person due to the incident.  Total Off hire time was: 09th Nov / 2200 hrs. LT to 11th Nov / 1500 hrs. LT – (41 hours).
IncidentEventDetails: During the voyage from Ras Laffan to Jakarta, Fuel Oil (FO) pressure was found fluctuating.  Main engine was stopped at 2200 hrs. LT on 09th Nov for further inspection.  The fuel lines and fuel filters were checked, and all found in order. The pressure adjusting  valve in FO line was found malfunctioning and hence the pressure was fluctuating. The FO  line was isolated and pressure adjusting valve was overhauled. The fuel system was put back  in use and all found normal.  Whilst the pressure adjusting valve was being overhauled, scavenge space inspection was  carried out, keeping in mind the recent incident on 31st Oct arrival Mesaieed. Piston rings of  Unit No 4 & 5 were noted broken. No 4 ring on Unit No 4 and No 3 ring on Unit No 5 were  found broken.  Master immediately appraised Managers, Owners and Charterers of the subject incident.  Overhauling of Unit No 4 & 5 was carried out. Fuel valves were pressure tested and found  normal.  Vessel resumed passage at 1500 hrs. on 11th Nov.
ImmediateAction: Upon noticing the fluctuation of fuel oil pressure main engine was stopped at 2200 hrs LT  South of RAS AL KUH TSS for further investigation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Main Engine Unit 4 & 5 piston rings broken and renewed from spare.
DirectCause: Causes for Fuel oil low pressure:  1. The pressure adjusting valve was found malfunctioning. On inspection internals were  found sticky that restricted the smooth operation leading to pressure fluctuations.  Most Probable Causes for Piston ring breakage:  1. All evidences indicate to the fact that the piston breakages occurred due to the  extended low-load operations of the vessel encountered due the very short voyages  (Average duration of 10.5 Hours) between Ras Laffan – Mesaieed for the complete  month of October, since entering the charter.  2. Due to the short voyages and terminal restriction on immobilization, the increased  frequency of scavenge space inspection and cleaning was not considered. No plans  were considered for stoppage at sea for inspection and cleaning of the scavenge  space.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Corrective and Preventive actions to avoid recurrence:  1. Makers Engineer was arranged to inspect the engine at Jakarta on 25th Nov. Complete  health check of the Engine was carried out including scavenge and under piston space  inspection. Basis his inspection recommendations, No: 2 unit was overhauled, and  piston rings renewed at Jakarta Anchorage.  a. Date Done: 26th Nov 2018  2. When vessels are on short voyages with frequent low load operations, the Scavenge  space inspection interval of one month shall be reduced to 10-15 days in consultation  with office. Technical bulletin on the same to be circulated and attached to the PMS  job description of Scavenge space inspection  a. Due Date: 15th December 2018  3. During continuous low load operations, ship staff should ensure a practice to increase  the ME rpm to NCR for one hour at least once in a week. This will assist in reducing  the carbon deposits within the combustion spaces and piston ring grooves.  Same will be reiterated to the fleet vide a Technical bulletin.  a. Due Date: 15th December 2018  4. Post the completion of the investigation Learnings from the above incident will be  shared with fleet vessels.  a. Due Date: 30th December 2018  5. On completion of the investigation, where required additional corrective / preventive  actions will be added.
ClosingNote: All units decarb carried out. CLO feed rate adjusted as per technician advice. Scavenge inspections to be carried out at regular intervals.

IncidentDetails: On 21st Nov 2018, Vessel was enroute to Jakarta from Ras Laffan. At 0924 hrs LT, Main Engine  high FO viscosity alarm activated as the FO temperature dropped due to steam leak from a  pin hole leak, on Heater no 1 Steam inlet line. Subsequently high exhaust temperature  deviation alarm activated & this was due to sticky fuel pump plungers of Unit no 1 & 2.  Engine was stopped at a stop location with no immediate navigation risks. The Fuel pumps  for No 1 Unit was overhauled and Fuel pump for No 2 unit was replaced from spare. FO heater  was thoroughly drained, vented & purged.  All fuel pumps and fuel lines were purged. Main engine tried out and found in satisfactory.  Vessel resumed passage by 1700 hrs. Vessel offhired from 0924 hrs LT to 1700 hrs LT on 21st  Nov 2018. No damage, environmental pollution or injury was caused due to the incident.
IncidentEventDetails: On 21st Nov 2018 at 0924 hrs LT, vessel was enroute to Jakarta and main engine was running  at 70 rpm, when the FO high viscosity alarm was received at Engine control panel. FO Viscosity  had increased to 17 Cst as against the set point of 13 cst.  A steam leak was observed from a pin hole on the ME FO heater No 1 Steam Inlet pipe.  Duty engineer immediately informed Chief Engineer regarding the steam leakage. It was  decided to change over the FO heater from No 1 to No 2 and complete the repairs on the  steam line.  The Standby heater No 2 was put on line and heater No 1 was isolated.  By 0929 hrs LT, FO low pressure alarm activated. Immediately ME exhaust temperature  deviation alarm also activated leading to Slow down of Main engine.  By 0930 Hrs LT, the fuel oil low pressure alarm and the high viscosity alarm got reset however  the exhaust temperature deviation alarm were still active.  On closer observation, it was noted that the deviation alarm was activated, as Unit No 1 & 2  were not firing. All Main engine FO inlet and return lines were purged thoroughly however  there was no improvement.  During further checks, it was noted that the fuel pump plunger for both the units was stuck.  The fuel pump for Unit No 1 was overhauled and the fuel pump for Unit No 2 was replaced  from spare. Fuel valves pressure tested and fitted back. HCU block accumulator pressure and  ELFI parker valve were checked and found in order. Heater No 2 was drained off all sediments.  All fuel oil lines and heaters for main engine was purged off air.  Main engine tried out and found in satisfactory.  Vessel resumed passage by 1700 hrs.
ImmediateAction: Main engine was stopped at sea - Cocos Basin (Indian ocean area) for further investigation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was no damage or injury but vessel was off hired for  7 hrs and 36 minutes for rectifying the stoppage.
DirectCause: On reviewing the sequence of events it is understood that FO high viscosity alarm was  received at 0924 hrs LT. At 0929 hrs LT, FO low pressure alarm was activated. Immediately  ME exhaust temperature deviation alarm also activated, leading to Slow down of Main  engine.  It is also further understood by interviewing the staff that Main engine FO low pressure alarm  was activated after change over of FO heater to No 2.  Though there is no objective evidence, apart from the timings of the alarms, from the above  sequence it is suspected that the changeover (i.e putting on line of stby heater & isolation of  leaked heater) & operation of the heater valves was too fast, to allow for pre-warming of the  standby heater and reduction/avoidance of FO system pressure fluctuation.  The Main engine FO low pressure alarm which was active, got reset after purging of all air  from the Heater no 2 for a few minutes, at 0930 Hrs.From this sequence it is clear that Air  pockets were present in the Stby heater, which was not fully purged out before putting the  heater on line.  After the FO low pressure alarm got reset, the Exhaust temperature deviation alarm was  found to be still active. Upon closer obervation, it was noted that the units No 1 and 2 were  not firing. Initial actions taken were to purge the system by opening up return line of fuel  pumps and both units fuel valves. As there was no change in firing status observed during  engine try outs, both the fuel valves were renewed. Units 1 and 2 HCU block accumulator  pressure were checked which was found stable at 135 bar. ELFI Parker valve was checked and  found spool pulsating movement normal. Subsequently suction valve for both units were  checked for leakages. No leakage was found.  Ship staff decided to open up fuel pump top cover centre plug and inserted plunger retaining  tool to check free movement of plunger. Found Unit No 1 & 2 plungers were stuck.  The Fuel pump of Unit No 1 was overhauled and Fuel pump of unit No 2 was replaced from  spare.  Before starting the engine, the heater No 2 was drained again. This time it was noted that  thick sludge was draining out initially. From this it was evident that old cold oil in the heater  had not been completely flushed out prior putting on line the heater. It is also suspected that  sediments in the heater got carried away in the line and got stuck in the first FO pump block  supplying FO to Unit No 1 & 2. Alternatively, it could have the momentary dry run of the fuel  pump, with low system pressure, which might have caused the plunger barrels to stick –  however, there were no scoring observed on the plungers.  Probable causes:  1. Pin hole on defective weld seam of STPG pipe  2. Change over sequence to standby heater was not followed and done too fast  3. Standby heater was not drained completely during the last routine changeover sequence.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. The steam Inlet line for heater No 1 was replaced with a new fabricated piece.  a. Done date: 21st Nov 2018  2. Fuel pump from Unit No 2 was overhauled and kept as spare.  a. Done date: 23rd Nov 2018  3. All associated steam line / laggings were visually inspected and confirmed in good  order.  a. Done date: 22nd Nov 2018  4. Local procedures for heater changeover sequence prepared and approved by Chief  Engineer and same posted at location.  a. Done date: 22nd Nov 2018  5. On completion of the investigation a Leraning from Incident will be shared with the  fleet.  a. Target date: 10th December 2018
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Steam inlet line renewed, fuel pump overhauled and kept as standby, local procedures posted and training given to all crew.

IncidentDetails: While discharging steel coils in the port of New haven, No.1 crane wire parted while lifting cargo i.e 2 nos steel coils from no 1 cargo hold . The coils along with the crane block fell on top of the steel coils resulting in damage to 4 nos coils. This happened by rash and improper handling of shore crane operator.LOP and third party damage report issued to them
IncidentEventDetails: 26.02.18  0412LT : VSL BERTHED/ ALL FAST   0600LT: SHIP SHORE SAFETY CHECKLIST DULY FILLED AND DISCUSSED.  0610LT : COMMD DISCH OPS WITH 2 CRANES,CHS 3 AND 5  0736LT ; CHANGED OVER TO NO 1 CH FROM 3 CH   0740LT ; NO 1 CRANE HOIST WIRE PARTED/ CEASE DISCH NO 1CH  2200LT : WIRE RENEWED AND CRANE TRIED OUT & FOUND SATIFACTORY
ImmediateAction: All concerned parties were informed and crew were engaged to renew the wire for operation of the crane.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Crane hoisting wire was parted and same has to be renewed.  2. Two coils that were lifted and the coils below lifted coil was damaged due to the fall.  The weight of two coils lifted was well with in the limits of the SWL of the crane.    P&I surveyor, Cargo surveyor from the stevedores side inspected the damaged coil and the crane.
DirectCause: Rash and improper handling of shore crane operator
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Periodical renewal of the crane wire.
ClosingNote: CAR verified and closed

IncidentDetails: Mooring rope was noticed chaffed
IncidentEventDetails: During inspectuion of mooring rope the it was noticed the rope has chaffed
ImmediateAction: Extra strand of rope fitted between the rope
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope was noticed chaffed
DirectCause: Frequent usage in the tidal port
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replace with new mooring rope
ClosingNote: invalid

IncidentDetails: While  cargo heating was in progress, oily water mixture came out from deck steam main return drain when inspected. about 4 Ltrs oil mixture spilled on main deck near slop S.
IncidentEventDetails: 0020LT: E/R reported that receiving traces of oil in hot well tank.  0025LT: stopped supply steam to deck   0045: steam opened after shuting 2W and 5W (suspected) heating coils.  0115LT: steam stopped after observing oil traces in hot well tank.  0145LT: main deck steam return valve opened to check the content. Oily water mixture sprayed on deck when collecting in a 20 LTR drum.  0230LT: cleaning completed.  0355LT: heating resumed after identifying one leaking coil of 4S COT (same isolated).
ImmediateAction: Drain valve shut immediately and cleaning carried out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oil  traces observed in E/R hotwell tank due to leak of 4S heating coil.
DirectCause: Leaking steaming coils of 4S COT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Steam coil identified and isolated
ClosingNote: Material failure, leaking coil identified and isolated, Same entered to defect list for future follow up

IncidentDetails: FWD whistle motor burn rewind coil
IncidentEventDetails: uty officer inform that FWD whistle (horn) when tested not working, investigate checked opened terminal connector of FWD whistle motor and took out megger test found that the no 1 and 2 rewind coil was totally ground and the no 3 coil was 4 mega ohms.
ImmediateAction: Puulled out the motor assembly and bring into the engine work shop dismantled found that the rewind coil was totally burn out the no 1 and 2 coil.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FWD whistle motor burn rewind coil
DirectCause: Sea spray was getting inside of the rewind coil trough shaft the result of the front cover was rusted and short circuit the rewind coil.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: In order to avoid sea spray / moisture due to the motor was expose in the weather condition, the motor most be wrap with canvas.
ClosingNote: The same has been raised as machinery defect report - MD/HAZL/2018/DEC/2

IncidentDetails: Incinerator 2nd chamber blower fan damage & ignition coil burn
IncidentEventDetails: At 03:30 hrs 11 Dec. 2018 while incinerator burning sludge the duty Engr notice that there is smoke coming out from the secondary burner immediately stop burning the sludge and cooling down the incinerator furnace temperature.
ImmediateAction: Immediately Stop burning the sludge and cooling down the incinerator furnace temperature, carried out investigate pulled out assembly burner of secondary chamber found that the impeller was damage and ignition coil burn.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Incinerator 2nd chamber blower fan damage & ignition coil burn
DirectCause: The impeller fins was detached and break all the impeller fins and no air forged to the chamber the result smoke fire going back to the burner housing
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular routine check and inspection cleaning on the impeller to avoid carbon deposit on the impeller fins.
ClosingNote: Machinery defect already generated and attended

IncidentDetails: The (S) accommodation ladder wire parted and the acc. ladder fell onto the bunker barge causing minor damage (scratches) on barge deck surface.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. While transitting Mississippi river it was observed that both gangway (P&S) were not operating with respective remote control. ER was informed and troubleshooting was in  progress. The problem with both the gangway could not be resolved till the vessel arrived the anchorage. In order to receive the Authorities, upon arrival anchorage, it was  decided to use the (P) gangway using the local control from the panel to operate the (P) gangway.    2. After the Authorities left, later it was observed that (S) gangway wire was completely slackened from the winch (suspect that while trying to identify the local switch for  (P) gangway must have also operated the (S) gangway). ER was informed and again with the help of local controls, the (S) wire was completely taken out from the winch. ER managed  to repair the (S) gangway remote control and remote control for (S) gangway was confirmed working normal. The (S) gangway wire was reeved back into the winch with remote control  operation. During all this, the (S) gangway was in its securing position with all the cleats secured.    3. The troubleshooting for (P) gangway remote control continued till the following day, but no conclusion could be reached. Next day when USCG boarded using (P) gangway the  local controls were again used. While the USCG had just completed their Inspection, bunker barge arrived on stbd side and same was made fast. Deck crew rigged the (S) gangway,  for access to the bunker barge, upto the level where the railings and manropes could be rigged (about a metre from the davit).    4. It was about lunch time, when the USCG disembarked from (P) gangway and deck crew were proceeding for lunch break. While going for lunch break, it was decided to heave the  port gangway clear from the water level. One of the deck crew, with walkie talkie, operated the switch from local panel. While operating the switch he tried to confirm, using walkie talkie, if (P) gangway was being heaved up. The duty AB replied that there was no movement on (P) gangway and a loud noise was heard from (S) gangway.     5. When the (S) gangway got heaved, to the surprise of crew attending bunker station (S),  and it went alarmingly close to the davit level, the crew shouted to stop heaving the  (S) gangway (as they were unaware why the (S) gangway was being heaved, by whom and from where as no one was attending the remote control for (S) gangway). By the time they  could alert somebody, the wire had already parted. The crew attending the bunker station had a walkie talkie but did not pay heed to the walkie talkie communication, wrt the (P)  gangway being heaved up, as they were concentrating on the bunker stations.    6. The crew went to (S) gangway and started to retreive the gangway.    7. Later on after the incident it was found that (P) gangway magnetic proximity switch (for manual handle) was defective, same was by passed and remote control worked  satisfactorily.
ImmediateAction: Ship crew immediately lifted the starboard side accommodation ladder using the crane davit, wire ropes renewed and tested after replacing new wire.  Ship staff cleaned and painted the damaged surface for the satisfaction of the barge Master.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Minor scratches on the bunker barge. 2. Acc. ladder (S) wire parted. 3. Acc. ladder (S) railings damaged. 4. Acc. ladder (S) side roller damaged. 5. Cracks observed, in way of welding,on 2nd and 3rd step from lower platform of acc. ladder (S).
DirectCause: a. Unable to identify/rectify the fault in time for remote control operation of (P) gangway. b. Normalcy with operating from local control. c. Deck crew operating the switch from local control instead of ER. d. Inadequate communication between the operator and the gangway watchman. e. Inadequate monitoring of internal communication, as the crew at bunker station felt the communication wrt (P) gangway was irrelevant. f. Crew at bunker station not realising to activate the emergency stop switch on the remote control or at the winch for (S) gangway to stop the inadvertent heaving. g. Fatigue due to long river transit. h. Stress due to ongoing USCG inspection as crew had to attend the Inspection and bunker barge at the same time. i. Might have been confusion between (P&S) gangway, as both were rigged at the time of incident, and in attempt to heave the (P) gangway, (S) was heaved.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Repair or procure the proximity switch and fit to see proper running. 2) Place clear instructions for operations from the Control panel in AC room 3) Training to be carried out for personnel who will operate from the Control panel. 4) Ensure that Port and Starboard side is clearly stenciled in the panel. 5) Plexi glass cover to be fabricated over the control switches, to prevent accidental usage when gangway is being operated.
Corrective Action: Repair or procure the proximity switch and fit to see proper running.
LessionLearnt: Issues to be discussed, RA to be carried out and Training to be done.
ReviewComments: Discussed in safety meeting.
ClosingNote: Repairs to be done prudently. RA to be carried out in case of change in procedures. Training to be rendered in case of any changes in operations and continuous monitoring by senior heads of the department.

IncidentDetails: MOB Smoke signal fell down from the rack due to ship's vibration that tends the hook and locking bar removed and released the MOB smoke signal.
IncidentEventDetails: On the 08th Dec while underway to Crofton, Canada MOB Smoke signal fell down from the rack due to ship's vibration. The lifebouy was lodge on the railings and we had recover it and we immediately returned on the rack.
ImmediateAction: We had rectify the locking hook which was the cause of the incident.
DirectCause: Ship's vibration
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular checking of locking device.
Corrective Action: Regular checking of locking device.
ClosingNote: Locking arrangement to be checked frequently by third officer and arrangement should not fail due to ship movement or vibration.

IncidentDetails: Permanant metal racks in Electrical room, Store rooms broken and fell down
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl encounterd rough weather after departure from port, In adavnce vsl secured all items for rough sea, But permanant metal rack in store rooms and electrical room broken from base and fell down.
ImmediateAction: items in the rack shifted to safe place, no items damaged. Rack secured temperorily
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Permanent rack fell down, broken from base due to rough sea
DirectCause: Lack of standards , inadequate supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A briefing session was conducted by Master and CE for entire crew emphasizing the importance of not overloading racks in store and thus damaging them. A proper supervision should be done for better store management and avoiding similar damages in future.
Corrective Action: Repairable onboard, Hotwork to be done inside accomodation, Hot work planned and proposal sent to office,  Approved by Office on 26/12/2018and HW completed on 26/12/2018.
ClosingNote: Job closed 27/03

IncidentDetails: On the 19th Dec 2018, slight Oil Sheen was observed on the Over-side in the Water at around 1000LT Hrs, while the VL was Drifting out-side ECA more than 200NM (Lat 24 33N; Long 094 19W), and awaiting proceeding orders.  CE and CO were notified to check for the cause.  It was noticed that the after most Scuppers were not holding and some water was passing through.  The rain accumulated water was immediately transferred into the ROT Tank.  Only the sheen was observed on the water surface close to ship, where quantity of Oil was not measurable and appeared insignificant.
IncidentEventDetails: 19th Dec 2018  0850LT: VL experienced heavy rain shower.  1000LT: Noticed sheen of Oil over side on the water surface. Ch. Engineer and Ch. Officer informed. 1005LT: It was noticed the water seepage from after most scuppers, the traces of Cargo Oil ULSD from Dip-Tray(stbd), probably contaminated the rain water and caused the sheen.  The seepage was arrested and accumulated rain water was then transferred to ROT.  1020LT: The water on Deck cleared and scupper removed to check. It was noticed that the Scupper edges were rough and did not allow the scupper to sit properly.  Since only sheen was observed, it was difficult to stablish the quantity, which we estimated as insignificant with environmental impact nonexistent.
ImmediateAction: The discharge was immediately arrested, and the accumulated water transferred to ROT. Once cleared, scuppers were removed and the scupper edges for both (P&S) scuppers were buffed, and painted. The scuppers were later tried out for tightness.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On the 19th Dec 2018, slight Oil Sheen was observed on the Over-side in the Water at around 1000LT Hrs, while the VL was Drifting out-side ECA more than 200NM (Lat 24 33N; Long 094 19W), and awaiting proceeding orders.  CE and CO were notified to check for the cause.  It was noticed that the after most Scuppers were not holding and some water was passing through.  The rain accumulated water was immediately transferred into the ROT Tank.  Only the sheen was observed on the water surface close to ship, where quantity of Oil was not measurable and appeared insignificant.
DirectCause: Presence of ULSD traces in Dip-Tray (stbd). Contaminated rain water overflowed to Deck and seeped out through leakingf scupper. The cause of leak was the rough edges of the scuppers.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The Edges to be buffed and painted. Upon completion of action same to be tested for tightness.  Training to Officers/Crew carried out on Marpol Regulations and Reporting Requirements.
Corrective Action: The Edges to be buffed and painted. Upon completion of action same to be tested for tightness. Training to Officers/Crew carried out on Marpol Regulations and Reporting Requirements.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: While the vessel was discharging cargo at berth no.2 at Mejillones (Chile), both the aft spring mooring ropes parted one after the another.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was berthed at a complete open berth to sea at Mejillones. The wind speed was about 14 Knots with low swell, but the vessel was surging and facing difficulty in maintaining moorings. The ship was surging in spite of crew tending to moorings continuously and all mooring ropes taut at all times. Additional ropes were passed to shore by ship's crew as an additional safety measure. The ME and BT were kept on short notice. One of the aft spring mooring rope parted and as soon as this rope was made fast again the second spring line parted. Vessel had taken all precautions to avoid damage to mooring ropes due to chaffing (chaffing guards placed in places were chaffing could happen). But due to swell and surging, the aft spring lines parted for the 2nd time. At this point the cargo discharging was stopped and hose disconnected. Terminal and port was requested to cast off the vessel and to drop anchor until weather conditions improved. Vessel departed the berth and achored near the terminal.
ImmediateAction: Cargo stopped, hose disconnected and vessel shifted to nearby anchorage. To berth again when the weather condition improves.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 moorings ropes parted and damaged to the extent that it cannot be used again.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Mooring rope broke due to swell and surging of vessel at berth unprotected from sea and swell  Indirect Cause: Presence of swell at berth. Inadequate protection from chafe guards. Inadequate analysis of hazards  Root Cause: Berth design Berth unprotected from Sea and Swell. Inadequate planning
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to procure pre arrival information through local agents which must include weather/swell condition at unprotected berth. Adequate layers of Chafe protector should be used on mooring ropes.
Corrective Action: Vessel is to be supplied with new mooring ropes. Vessel to assess the situation at open berths and take precautions & action to avoid damage to mooring ropes.
LessionLearnt: Vessel calling ports where berths are unprotected from sea/swell, master to seek pre arrival information about such berths and make adequate arrangements to avoid failure of mooring ropes.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Corrective action implemented. Vessel to monitor effectiveness of CA.

IncidentDetails: While picking up stores at Singapore, stbd side provision crane leaked hydraulic oil. Quantity of leaked oil approx 4ltrs. All oil contained onboard on A, B and C deck itself. No oil spilled overboard.   Clean up operation was immediately started and all the oil cleaned. Crane was safely parked and secured without any delays. Upon investigation observed that source of oil leak is from pin hole crack of motor control valve fitting.
ImmediateAction: SOPEP team immediately deployed and all the oil cleaned. Office notified and entires made in log book.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DUE TO SMALL PIN HOLE CRACK ON FIITING FOR HOSITING MOTOR CONTROL VALVE HYDAULIC OIL SPRAYED/LEAKED. NO DAMAGE TO VESSEL , NO OIL SPILLED OVERBOARD . SPILLED OIL COLLECTED AND DECK CLEANED.
DirectCause: DETERIORATION OF FITTINGS DUE TO AGE AND EXPOSURE TO OUTSIDE WHEATHER.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: RENEW FITTINGS. DENSO TAPE TO PREVENT EXPOSURE TO WEATHER
Corrective Action: RENEW FITTINGS. DENSO TAPE TO PREVENT EXPOSURE TO WEATHER
ReviewComments: Vessel advised not to use Denso tape on the fittings.
ClosingNote: Defective fitting replaced with new one and made operational.

IncidentDetails: Stbd gangway could not be hoisted prior to prior securing on dep Newark port, No damage to property and loss of time.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Vessel departed Newark port at 0115hrs and was under Pilotage.  2. While securing gangway from deck level to its stow position, the Able Seaman noticed gangway was getting lowered on its own.  3. The Seaman immediately pressed hoist button and stopped its lowering, Electrical Officer was informed.  4. Chief Engineer & Electrical Officer came, checked the electrical connections and limit switches.  5. Emergency stop was pressed to stop the further lowering of gangway, but noted gangway going down & finally stopping on the tripping line rigged always.  6. Vessel was in stop condition inorder to turn to align with the channel, the gangway was hoisted with the hoist button pressed continuously until it reached the stow position & securing of all butterfly nuts.  7. Vessel proceeded out of port without any delay.
ImmediateAction: Stopped the lowering by pressing hoist button & secured  following the same procedure and locking with securing nuts.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Upon opening the gear box for investigation found the gear box pinion shaft sheared off at the step. The same needs to be renewed.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Repairs carried out and additional measures discussed in case of such failures.

IncidentDetails: Due to heavy rolling of the vessel during rough weather on 15th Dec 2018 some fuel had spilled from No.1(P) FOT air vent. As per the initial assessment, due to failure of the primary containment, some oil has splashed on to the main deck and we suspect a part of which has escaped overboard.
IncidentEventDetails: No. 1(P) FOT bunkered in Paldiski on 04 Dec 2018 and final quantity after bunker was 343.88m3 which is 87% of the tank capacity.  Abt 3.5 m3 transferred from FO drain tank to No. 1(P) FOT on 10 Dec 2018 and final quantity after transfer was 347.23m3 which is 89% of the tank capacity.  On 13 Dec 2018 No 1(P) FOT sensor fail alarm was coming and vessel was experiencing rough seas and so alarm was inhibited.  On 15 Dec 2018 fuel oil spilled from air vent.
ImmediateAction: FO was transferred from No.1(P) FOT to No. 2(P) FOT throught the transfer pump and a risk assessment has been prepared for proper clean-up operations and the disposal of oily rags. The sounding of all the bunker tanks after the incident was taken and there has been no appreciable change in the sounding. However due to rough weather the sounding was eratic and exact quantity would be assessed after calm sea.
DirectCause: Rolling, pitching due to very rough seas and heavy swell.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To follow SMS procedures, do not exceed max permitted quantity as per SMS.
ClosingNote: Investigation completed and report attached with all details

IncidentDetails: Whilst tendering the mooring lines during loading at Linggi ( STS - MT EL GURDABIA), a major hydraulic leak was observed from the Mooring winch no.3 and winch no. 5. No delay to the vessel while unmooring.
IncidentEventDetails: 12-Dec-2018 2400 LT: Vsl all fast - STS MT EL GURDABIA  13-Dec-2018 0500 LT: Commenced loading  13-Dec-2018 1630 LT: While adjusting the mooring lines, a major hydraulic leak was observed from winch no. 5 - Aft Spring.   13-Dec-2018 1631 LT: Winch no. 5 Inlet and outlet were closed. POAC informed.  14-Dec-2018 0100 LT: While adjusting the mooring lines, a major hydraulic leak was observed from winch no.3 - Fwd Breast.  14-Dec-2018 0101 LT: Winch no. 3 Inlet and outlet were closed. POAC informed.
ImmediateAction: Immediately isolated both the winches and drained the lines in Empty drum. All deck crew informed, not to use the winches till further notice. Mooring master (POAC) was infomed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Both Mooring winches motor were removed for examination,i t has been observed Motor front cover is damaged in both cases and due to whcih oil seal moved from its location, that resulted in major leak. No delay was caused to vessel.
DirectCause: Cause for this incident could be the over-pressure generated in the system leading to the failure of motor front cover.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Sufficient spares shall be available on board to attend it in case of emergency.
LessionLearnt: Proper operating procedure for hydraulic machineries to be followed at all times.
ClosingNote: Proper operating procedure for hydraulic machineries to be followed at all times.

IncidentDetails: Oxy-acetylene hose in workshop caught fire, same was damaged completely.
IncidentEventDetails: While engine crew was cutting plates by using portable grinder in workshop in welding area, a hot metal piece flew and got stuck on the oxy-acetylene hose lying in its stowed position, immediately this hose caught on fire, fire was extinguished immediately by using portable DCP extinguisher.There was no gas in oxy acetylene hose and was empty, both valves in workshop and near cylinder were kept closed always.
ImmediateAction: Fire was extinguished immediately by using portable DCP extinguisher.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oxy-acetylene hose was damaged completly.
DirectCause: No additonal watch for fire was maintained, impoper precautions taken.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional watch to be maintained while carrying out any hot work or any job which can create a fire hazard.
ClosingNote: Lack of hot work permit compliance.

IncidentDetails: Fire - exterior of accomodation C deck - Safety Net sling for picking up Provision / Stores and the nearby Bulkhead paint was partly burnt/ damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: 1548LT: Burning smell was observed by the bridge team through AC blowers.        1548: Informed Engine Room, Galley Department and Commenced Fire Round of Accommodation & Engine Room.        1553: All reported on Bridge NO fire in Accommodation/ Galley & Engine Room.        1555: Bridge team sighted Smoke coming from “C” Deck (Aft) outer side of the Accommodation, subsequently ALARM was raised followed by PA announcement.        1556: Fire Pump started and Hoses kept ready.        1556-1600. Emergency team fought the live fire in the Wooden Pallet/Box with cling wrap and Nylon Sling. Fire was extinguished with charged fire hoses.        Later full area was cleaned up and washed with Fresh Water.        1600-1605: Close watch kept on the subsequent stores adjacent to the site of fire – All found Satisfactory.
ImmediateAction: Immediately ALARM was raised followed by PA announcement, Fire Pump started and Hoses kept ready. Emergency team fought the live fire in the Wooden Pallet/Box with cling wrap and Nylon Sling. Fire was extinguished with charged fire hoses.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Safety Net sling for picking up Provision / Stores and the nearby Bulkhead paint was partly burnt/ damaged.
DirectCause: Upon investigation observed no source of ignition at the site of fire, other than the Spark fell from Funnel exhaust uptake into the wooden Box partly wrapped with cling foil in which nylon Net sling was kept, which caught Fire.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: C deck exterior is being used to pick up the stores and net slings are being kept for easy deploymet inside the wooden box/pallet - Immediate all wooden boxes are reloacated to safe and shelter stores and area is kept clear of any combustible material. All crew are thereafter briefed about the same and at all time C deck landing area to be kept clear of any and all combustible material
ClosingNote: Proper safety officer inspection  to include check on such dangerous occurrences

IncidentDetails: Bunker Overflow in the Engine room while bunkering at Singapore
IncidentEventDetails: While bunkering at Singapore Sudong special anchorage on 31st October 2018 at 1330 LT previous Chief Engineer who was in charge of bunkering operation noticed that the tank in which the bunkering was going on, was reaching 92% of the tank capacity.   In order to make space in the tank he asked one of the engine ratings to transfer the oil from bunkering tank to the settling tank & C/E forgot to stop the transfer.   Concurrently the rating was also involved in draining the settling tank and left the drain valve open. The drained oil eventually filled up the oily bilge tank and spilled in the Engine room.
ImmediateAction: Bunkering stopped and clean up started, approx. 3 barrels of spilled oil contained & transferred to sludge tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nil
DirectCause: Non compliance with company bunkering procedures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Company guidelines & procedure to be followed at all times.  Person involved in bunkering operation to be further trained prior taking responsibility of any critical operation.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Please see attached Investigation report

IncidentDetails: Investigating the fire alarm which sounded for the boiler area, revealed that the water & steam leaking through the boiler gauge glass is the cause of alarm.
IncidentEventDetails: - 0436 Fire alarm sounded for boiler area.  -0437  Engine room manned and investigation started.  -0438 Observed steam leaking through the boiler gauge glass.  -0440 With due precautions Boiler gauge glass valves were closed.   -0450 Gauge glass dismantled.  -0500 Observed packing material failure.  -0530 Gauge glass assembled with new packing after cleaning of gauge glass and mountings.  -0600 Observed satisfactory operation of associated machinery.  -0600 Engine room maintained in manned condition.
ImmediateAction: Vessel staff renewed the last set of packing material for the gauge glass and raised the requisition for the same.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Vessel have taken immediate action.

IncidentDetails: AE-2 tripped while  running on parallel load due to Low Lub oil pressure trip.
IncidentEventDetails: Cargo Discharge operation completed on 05-01-2019 2330 Hrs. LT.  Afterwards vessel was lying idle at terminal as pilotage was scheduled at 1230 Hrs on 06-01-2019.  AEs No:2 & 3 were running on parallel load.  At 0545 Hrs. LT on 06-01-2019,  AE-2 trip operated due to Low LO pressure and AE tripped.  Preferential trip activated on switchboard and non essential loads tripped.  Preferential trip breakers reset back after Standy AE-1 came on load.
ImmediateAction: AE-2 isolated and manual turning done for several times with lub oil priming pump running.  Necessary checks carried out on both running generators.    Checks carried out to analize the cause of Low LO pressure trip activation. Found LO pump not developed pressure and has further lead to Low Lube oil Pressure Trip activation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LO attached pump drive gear shaft damaged. Renewed with new spare drive gear shaft , sleeve, bearings and Seal rings.
DirectCause: Past history shows similar breakdowns occurred with AE-1 during 23 Sep 2016 when V/L with previous management and further in AE-3 on 01 Dec 2017. In both cases, the cause was attributed each time with the attached drive gear shaft shearing off.The shearing-off location is very similar in the previous cases as well as with the present occurence.  Could understand the new type attached drive gear shafts are modified shafts from the maker.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Maker's advice may be seeked for review this similar repeated occurrences and advise regarding maintenance or replacement of the drive gear shaft accordingly.
ClosingNote: cause, Maker has came up with new design shaft for replacement. For Aux engine 1 / 2 & 3 has already been replaced , Where as tightening procedure has not clearly given on the manual, Maker has reverted with the latest circular with tightening torque.   Now for AE :2 lube oil pump shaft replaced with new design shaft with recommended torque, system found to be operating in good condition. Vessel Seto Express is taken under our management on Dec 2016, Since than Lube oil pump gear shaft on the Aux engine is sequentially break down down one after other , We have  checked with makers for the  We have recommended to check the other 2 aux engine lub oil pump shaft also to check for the recommend tightness , in order to prevent another breakdown of lube oil pump.

IncidentDetails: While discharging various grade of vegetable oil at Cargill Botlek Jetty at Rotterdam,about 100 Litres of Crude Coconut Oil came out from 6P Tank Cleaning machine flange of which about approximately 50 Litres CCNOl escaped overboard from Port  Main deck aft scupper.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was putting hot water and steam on the No.6 Cargo Line to clear the cargo solidified inside the cargo line,due to which there was hot water collected on deck in aft area. Due to the restriction of discharging the cargo from selected tanks only and restricted ballast intake underneath adjacent  cargo tanks, vessel was listed to about 3 deg to port with a trim of 3.5 mtr. Ship crew standby at the scupper was draining the deck water after verifying the content visually,   @1520LT:- Duty Seaman noticed white flakes in water overboard and immediately he plugged the scupper and informed CCR. Discharging Suspended.  @1520 LT:- Alarm was raised /Terminal (Loading Master) Informed about the incident.  @1522 LT:- All  crew mustered and commenced clean up operation on main deck.Leaking tank cleaning machine flange was tightened and leak arrested.  @1523 LT:- Commenced reduction of 6P COT tank vapour pressure   @1525 LT:- Vessel called Rotterdam port control.  @1526 LT :- Vessel informed Rotterdam port authorities(Harbour Master)  @1528 LT:- Informed P&I Club for the spill.  @1530 LT:- Loading Master adviced vessel to resume discharging   @1540 LT:- Vessel collected spilled oil/water mixture in Drums (about 8 Drums of Oily mixture of 50 Litres Coconut Oil and rest Water)  @1630 LT:- Harbour Mastere Onboard   @1635 LT:- Harbour Master inspected spilled area and informed Master that Clean up operation in water is not required as the spill is very minor and further action is required  @1642 LT:- Port State Control(Marpol Inspector Onboard)
ImmediateAction: Scupper Plugged  Cargo operation suspended  Commenced ballasting to reduce trim  Leaking tank cleaning machine flange was tightened and leak arrested
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage / Injury
DirectCause: a) Direct Cause (Unsafe act, Unsafe condition) : ? The blank fitted to the tank cleaning machine flange was not adequately tightened causing the escape of cargo from the primary containment system.  ? Scupper plug on the port side was kept in an open state to allow escape of Hot water applied on the frozen 6W cargo line. Due to uncontrolled flow of water personnel standing by did not realize the cargo spill mixed with water flowing through the scupper until it was observed in sea. Coconut oil mixed with hot water did not gave an appearance of ‘oil sheen’ to the person standing by near the scupper to keep a check on the flow of water and arrest the flow if required.  b) Indirect causes (Personal or Job factor) :  ? Implied Haste: The cargo leakage from the flange was caused due an implied haste on the crew members’ part in completing the job, without adequately verifying the effectiveness of the work performed.  ? Inadequate Design: i. It would be ideal to have a spectacle blank on the tank cleaning branch line which would have avoided the need for removal of the distance piece. ii. The Tank Cleaning Pump is provided a drive from the Ballast pump, thereby restricting the full use of both tank cleaning and ballasting system simultaneously and thus vessel could not use ballast pump to its full capability to counter the port list.  ? Inadequate assessment of potential failures: Attempt to clear the frozen cargo line using hot water was not ideal for the conditions considering the design constraints. Vessel should have continued with external application of live steam to clear the lines. Officers in-charge of the operation did not assess the potential risk of cargo seeping out from the flange due to improperly tightened blank.  ? Poor Judgement: The water should have been released through the scupper in a controlled manner after verifying for any miscible mixture or contaminants rather than allowing free flow water through the scupper.  c) Root Cause (Lack of planning/Lack of compliance/Lack of standards) :  ? Inadequate implementation of procedure: There was procedure and risk assessment in place for re circulation of cargo and blowing of lines after re-circulation to eliminate potential seepage of cargo through the valves. The officers failed to blow the lines from manifold after the last re-circulation of 6w tanks inspite of knowing that line master was inadvertently open while re-circulation. There was risk assessment in place for handling solidifying cargoes. Ship staff failed to effectively utilise the full potential of the Risk Assessment process due to pre-occupation with other tasks.  ? Inadequate Guidelines in Procedures : There was no definite guidelines in the procedures that Hot water cannot be used at port for clearing solidified line. Due to this the officers took decisions to use Hot water instead of direct application of steam for clearing lines. ? Inadequate Technical Design: The tank cleaning pump and the ballast pump cannot be used simultaneously, hence when the vessel was listing to port, vessel could not use ballast pump to counter the list resulting into seepage of cargo from the tank cleaning machine flange. ? Diminished Performance: The near sub zero temperature conditions might have affected the performance of the crew member thereby resulting into the inadvertent errors. ? Lack Of Compliance – Simultaneous Operations (SIMOPS) : Hazards associated with simultaneous operations (SIMOPS) were not identified adequately and subsequent Risk Assessment made for SIMPOS was not effective.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Marine Superintendent to sail with the vessel and carry out training for handling Solidifying cargoes
Corrective Action: Marine Superintendent to sail with the vessel and carry out training for handling Solidifying cargoes
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Shared with the fleet for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: Sewage Aeration blower motor one rewind coil burn
IncidentEventDetails: Last 02 of Jan 2019 at 21:00 hrs L.T while stay in port the duty oiler was heard sound of alarm sound in the control room after check found the slarm list the sewage earation blower was overload, after pulled out and dismantled found one coil was burn.
ImmediateAction: Pulled out and dismantled and took out megger test found one coil was zero resistance. tried to apply insulating varnish after dry took out megger test found zero resistance. fabricated air hose connection from working air using oxygen regulator to produce 0.12 kg/cm2 to supply air to all sewage chamber
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During on watch oiler heard alarm sound after checking on alarm list found aeration blower was overload. after checking took out megger test found zero resistance.
DirectCause: due to very cold ambient temperature create moisture the result of low resistance of the rewind coil
Proposedcorrectiveactions: change motor and instruct duty oiler and engr to alert on every respective watch, regular monitoring of all running machineries.
ClosingNote: Already raised as machinery defect

IncidentDetails: Due to bad weather and sudden gust of winds Forward spring Parted while vessel was in berth
IncidentEventDetails: On 27-dec-2018 1330 LT Ship was made fast Stbd side 4+2 Fwd and Aft in Lihir berth. After Key meeting  Cargo hose was connected at 1542 LT. AT 1700 LT Loading master informed cargo discharge will commence tomorrow on 28-dec-2018 0700 LT. At 0400 AM on 28th wind speed and swell picked up wind gusts upto 45 kts, During this time vessel started Surging and rolling heavily. Vessel tried to contact port control but noreply, Shor gangway moved away fro the ship. At 0415 Hrs forward spring rope parted.
ImmediateAction: 0415 LT shore truck driver passing by was  signalled to call port authority 0420: One watch man reached the spot , at 0440 , the parted rope was taken out , Temporary mooring Eye made with Bowline and made fast to shore, All the mooring were  checked found all the mooring ropes rendered due to the strong surging and rolling. All ropes were made tight and Bow thruster started to keep ship alongside.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One mooring rope Parted and many more ropes got chaffed
DirectCause: Adverse Weather condition, Inadequate Maintenance, Lack of Standards.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Condition of ropes and mooring winch brakes should be monitored and recorded on regular basis. Rope should be changed if condition deteriorates. Brake liners should be renewed based on their condition. Regular Brake testing should be performed to ensure rakes render at their set BRC and brakes should never be over-tightened beyond their set BRC point.
Corrective Action: Condition of ropes and mooring winch brakes should be monitored and recorded on regular basis. Rope should be changed if condition deteriorates. Brake liners should be renewed based on their condition. Regular Brake testing should be performed to ensure rakes render at their set BRC and brakes should never be over-tightened beyond their set BRC point.
ClosingNote: Brake testing of all winches were re-performed, BRC marking were re-painted as required. 5 No. new ropes were supplied at Singapore on 15/Jan/19.  Job Closed 18/01/19

IncidentDetails: During carrying out routine Boat and Fire drills , found oil mist with water vapour comming out of funnel mist catch area.  There is no any damage or injury.
IncidentEventDetails: During Routine fire and boat drill ,when all the participents were at the muster station , found oil mist with water vapour comming out of the funnel mist catch area.  Found this oil mist with vapour comming out of waste oil sett tank vent pipe.There was no any continuous sleek of oil.  The spray of mist sticked to the starboard and aft side of funnel and few portions of poop deck bulkhead and accomodation exterior.  Incinerator was stopped well before the incident happened and waste oil settling tank content was 2.0 m3 ( Max.capacity 3.0 m3 ) with tank temperature of 110 DegC . The steam inlet to the heating coil of the same tank was adjusted to maintain a temperature of approx 110 DegC.
ImmediateAction: Immediately stopped the heating steam of waste oil settling tank.The spray of mist stopped within a minute.  Cleaning of Funnel area as well as poop deck bulkhead done.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage
DirectCause: 1.Chances of water ingress into the tank.  2.Flashing of water and converting into steam inside the tank , that mixed with oil to form mist.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Care to be taken to avoid water mixed sludge being transferred to the tank above 100 DegC. Level gauge of waste oil sett tank to be calibrated .
Corrective Action: prepare detailed waste oil transfer procedure
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: CA completed ad advised belwo, oil transfer procedure and deflector plate arrangement.

IncidentDetails: Loss of stbd anchor while heaving up.
IncidentEventDetails: During heaving up of stbd anchor at AMA anchorage ( 29 57.37 N / 090 18.15 W ), Chief office reported that the stbd side anchor is missing, The joining shackle pin is missing along with the anchor.
ImmediateAction: USCG/Local authorities/agents informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss of stbd anchor and connecting shackle pin.
DirectCause: 1) Without direct evidence of the missing “D” Shackle pin, we are unable to ascertain the condition of the spile pin. However, in all probability the spile pin of the D- shackle got dislodged either by breakage or by coming loose.  2) History of failure of spile pins in the industry due to various reasons, at the same time it is very difficult to identify the exact reason for the failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As per the list of actions mentioned beneath.
Corrective Action: On Nave Pulsar, both anchors have been thoroughly checked and confirmed their “D” shackle (including spile pin) and Pear-shaped joining shackle are in good condition. Port anchor that has “D” Shackle has the got the pin welded and on the starboard anchor the “D” Shackle pin has been changed to Pear shaped Joining shackles.
ClosingNote: Investigation completed and all closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Please note that during discharging operations at muroran vessel no.4 manifold valve on stbd side cracked.
IncidentEventDetails: 22-Jan-2018  ===========  1348: Commenced discharging Jet A1.  1406: Vessel increased to max discharge pressure as per terminal request (7.0 Bar at ships Manifold)  1424: Manifold watch reported minor leak from No.4 Maiolfd valve bonnet.  1430-1435:Leak was inspected by Chief officer and Berthing master and found No.4 Manifold is cracked.  1436: Discharging supeneded.  1436-1500: Draining of No.4 Manifold  1500-1518: Disconnection of No.4 Manifold  1518-1536: Connection of loading arm to No.3 Manifold.  1542: Resumed discharging
ImmediateAction: Vessel has shifted the manifold connection to no.3 manifold and resumed discharging.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Please note that during discharging operations at muroran vessel no.4 manifold valve on stbd side cracked.Vessel has shifted the manifold connection to no.3 manifold and resumed discharging.
DirectCause: Discharging cargo temp is 14 C and air temp is -3 C.Vessel suspects that due to sudden change in temp the bonnet of the valve cracked.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Valves to be reqularly checked
Corrective Action: Valves to be reqularly checked
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Briefing session was conducted by Master and CE , it was advised that most probable cause of this incident was due to ageing of vessel , the O-rings sealing the bonnet chamber has given way to moisture inside bonnet assembly and due to extreme cold weather and freezing of this leaked water the bonnet of the valve cracked. Crew were encouraged to continue doing thorough checks on valves as per PMS and early signs of ageing damage of gaskets and O-ring should be ascertained and rectified as requrd

IncidentDetails: 17th Jan 2019:    At 0400 lt Own vessel completed loading at Corpus Christi, Oil dock 1 and was waiting for outward pilot boarding. Duty officer was stationed on bridge with two deck watch-keepers.  At 1554 hrs lt Tug and Tow (Tug OSG Colombia and Tow OSG 242) entering harbour swung rapidly towards own ship's stbd shoulder and Tow OSG 242 stbd bow made contact with own vessel stbd shoulder (Frame No. 168 to 187) at 1556 hrs lt.  By 1600 lt Assist tug CR HARDEN pushed away Tug and Tow (Tug OSG Colombia and Tow OSG 242) from own ship’s side.  By 1608 lt Tug and Tow (Tug OSG Colombia and Tow OSG 242) cleared and proceeded inward to harbour.
ImmediateAction: 17th Jan 2019:    At 1602 Vessel contacted and reported the incident to company (Synergy Maritime).   By 1610lt Vessel investigated the ship’s side and took soundings for stbd side ballast tanks (3S, 2S, 4S) and confirmed no hull breach or pollution.  1612 lt Vessel contacted Owners - BW tankers and reported the incident.  By 1630 lt Port Police boarded onboard and took pictures of ship side.  At 1635 lt vessel contacted BP Emergency Notification canter and reported the incident   1654 lt Vessel contacted USCG Corpus sector and reported the incident.  1656 lt Port Police completed procedure and away.  1708lt Vessel contacted QI O’BRIENs and reported the incident and vessel confirmed no hull breach and no pollution took place.  1718lt vessel informed Charterer ST Shipping and reported the incident.  1725lt First Pilot onboard.  1805lt second Pilot onboard.  1830lt two USCG personal Onboard.  1836lt USCG confirmed to pilots VSL will stay at berth until class inspection is conducted; pilots were advised to cancel departure.   1848lt both pilots disembarked.  1930lt Both USCG away.     On 18th Jan 2019, 0812 Hrs LT, Class Surveyor on board to investigate. He completed his investigation and inspection of effected area in 3S WBT and issued memo, stating vessel can sail and repairs can be effected at next dry dock / opportunity. Class report forwarded to USCG and USCG cleared the vessel to sail.   On 19th Jan 2019, at 0100 Hrs LT, pilot boarded the vessel. Vessel sailed out for next port, Rotterdam at 19/0512 Hrs LT
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Allision caused damage to external hull IWO of 3 S WBT. Additionally, 3S WBT, between frame 168 to 187: side shell, longitudinals, web frames and stiffners buckled. Stringer plate 1 to side longitudinal No.5 and also between frame 174 to frame 186 & stringer plate 2 to side longitudinal  No. 4 crack noted on side shell plating. No rupture of hull observed. Visual inspection and hose test carried out by class. Area inspected by P&I / H&M surveyor also.     Vessel was expected to receive pilot to sail out on 17/1730 hrs LT, however due to above allision, finally pilot boarded on 19/0100 Hrs LT.
DirectCause: To monitor the operation closely and pick up any indications of trouble at the budding stage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Inspection of the damaged area within the 3S WBT shall be done closer and if any further deterioration in the condition shall be brought to the notice of the Office for further action.

IncidentDetails: During mooring operations 2 mooring tails  of aft spring lines parted.No injuries.
IncidentEventDetails: During mooring operations , vessel was experienced strong opposite currents of about 5 kts . First line passed was 2 Spring lines forward and aft. Both were made fast and after that breast lines were being passed.  When the breast lines were being passed , the tugs could not hold the vessel alongside and vessel bow started drifting away from the berth.The aft spring lines became tight and the mooring tails parted without any damage to the main mooring lines.3rd tug was called and vessel was pushed alongside.Thereafter new mooring tails were fitted and 3 spring lines were passed from aft to shore.   No person was standing nearby and no injuries occured.
ImmediateAction: The mooring tails were replaced immediately and extra spring line was passed.Vessel has no spare tails onboard and same will be oredered in next port of call , Rotterdam
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 mooring tails damaged irrepairably.
DirectCause: Heavy opposite current.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training on mooring practices to be given to the crew. Winch brake to be re-tested
Corrective Action: Training on mooring practices to be given to the crew. Winch brake to be re-tested
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: New BRC test done and rendering set to 27.6 MT

IncidentDetails: Vessel sustained heavy weather damage whilst on her voyage(Voy073) to Hong Kong. Refer attached damage report for details.
IncidentEventDetails: 25-Dec-2019-Vessel departed from Singapore and proceeding to Hong kong for Unloading.  28-31 Dec 2018- Vessel experienced Heavy Weather in South China Sea.  01-Jan-2019- Damage Assessment was carried out and a NoP issued by vessel.  02-Jan-2019- Inspection carried out by P&I Surveryor(Refer Attached report)
ImmediateAction: 1. Damage assessment was carried out and Office was notified.  2. Water removed from FPK store  3. Windlass gear cover debries removed and windlass made operational.  4. Deformed Anchor and SPM stopper Bar Pin straightened.  5. Nav Lights and Flood Lights were made operational.  6. 1S Cargo Tank pressure and Temperature Sensors repaired and made operational.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.Ingress of Sea Water in FPK Store  2. FWD Life Raft Inflated and Capsule damaged  3. Fwd winlass gear cover damaged and missing.  4. Fwd SPM and Anchor stopper locking pin deformed  5. Hawse pipe cover deformed.  6. Winch gratings missing  7. Fwd bulwark step ladder damaged and missing.  8. 1s COT pressure and temperature sensor malfuncting due to ingress fo water in Radar Tank domes.  9. Nav Lights and Flood lights on fwd mast damaged.  10. Fwd liferaft embarkation ladder damaged and wooden steps broken.  11. Fwd Emer Lighting switch box broken.
DirectCause: Due to heavy weather experienced in South China Sea
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Sea Water from  FPK Store to be removed  2. New FWD Life Raft installed  3. New Fwd winlass gear covers to be fabricated.  4. Fwd SPM and Anchor stopper locking pin to be straightened and installed.  5. Hawse pipe cover to be straightened and installed  6. Winch gratings to be replaced with new gratings  7. Fwd bulwark step ladder to be fabricated and installed  8. 1s COT pressure and temperature sensor to be rectified/replaced.  9. Nav Lights and Flood lights on fwd mast to br replaced  10. Fwd liferaft embarkation ladder to be replaced  11. Fwd Emer Lighting switch to be replaced

IncidentDetails: mooring line parted due to surging casued by vessel passing at close range at unsafe speed, while vessel made fast at berth.
IncidentEventDetails: Time log after 0920 LT :  About 0920 hrs Lt: Boson noticed vessel’s ropes rendering and raised alarm. He noticed M.T Petalidi  passing our vessel from port side at visibly high speed.   About 0920 hrs Lt : : Master announce in PA all crew mooring stations. All winches attended continuously to control vessel’s movement.  About 0925 hrs Lt : 2 nos Aft spring lines parted due to surge after rendering.  About 0935 hrs Lt: Both aft spring lines temporary eye made and put back on the bits.  About 0936 hrs Lt : one of Aft outer spring line parted again, temporary eye made and put back on the bits.  About 0938 hrs Lt: one Fwd spring line parted, same temporary eye made and put on bit.   About 0940 hrs Lt: The same Fwd spring line parted again, Temporary eye made again and put back on the bit.   About 0945 hrs Lt : One forward head line parted.  About 0954 hrs Lt:  First Tug Eleanor f Moran alongside    About 1045 hrs Lt: Second Tug Benson George Moran Alongside.   1200 hrs Lt: Vessel all made fast (2+4+2)  1230 hrs Lt : Vessel passed one more Fwd and aft line (Loose Rope) (3+4+2)  1245 hrs Lt : Tug Cape Ann Alongside  1245 hrs Lt : Tug Eleandorf Moran &  Tug Benson George Moran Away   Since above rendering & later parting of mooring lines tug boat assistance was requested from Port of port Arthur. Vessel had continued tug assistance at full time stay at berth to avoid any movement surge of damage to vessel or terminal.
ImmediateAction: tugs assistance requested, reberthing done with new eyes made on broken lines, extra head lines & aft stern line passed.  vessel stayed alongside with tugs assistance.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: mooring ropes parted 6 times, also elongation in mooring ropes.
DirectCause: Causal factors     Direct Causes 1. The Suezmax Tanker M/T Petalidi was transitting at the max allowable draft of 40 feet in the Sabine Neches channel. Reduction of speed from initial 8.50 knots to 3.0 knots to pass the Sandpiper pacific was made only when the vessel was approximately 0.60 NM from the Sandpiper Pacific. Owing to the fact that the banks were close to the channel and there was not room for the displaced water to escape sideways, the water displacement caused by the large vessel moving ahead at the original speed of the Petalidi was considerably large and these bow waves possibly arrived at the location where Sandpiper Pacific was berthed even before Petalidi actually arrived abeam of the berth (as she had physically commenced reducing her speed before reaching CTL #3).   Her increasing speed to about 7.5 knots soon after passing Sandpiper Pacific caused a strong throwback of water prolonging causing . This combined with the Bow Waves and the effects of the Wake would have created a predominantly Fore & Aft surge movement on the Sandpiper Pacific.   2. The location and design of the vessel in which the CTL # 3 is situated would have additionally caused the displaced water and the wake water to resonate against the wall of the recess and caused a Piston like effect to be generated the surge waves and caused the resonance to continue for a lengthy time even after the vessel had passed clear. Thus the ropes continued part even 20 minutes after the Petalidi had passed clear of the vessel.   3. The vessel mooring pattern was 2+4+2 forward and aft. Considering the danger posed by vessels passing by in the channel, there was no additional headlines or spring lines considered for the mooring as the vessel would be prone to movement whilst other vessels passed close by in the channel.  Indirect causes     1. The ship staff revealed that the they could make out that the ropes were rendering when the excessive surging was experienced. However it cannot be exactly ascertained which ropes rendered. The brake rendering limit was observed not visible to the person tightening the brakes which could have resulted in overtightening the brakes beyond the rendering point thereby parting.  2. The Master had ordered for the tugs only 25 minutes after the first ropes            Parted even though he had been guided by terminal to request through         port control. The tugs came within 7 minutes of requesting which makes             a time delay of 32 minutes from the time the first ropes parted.  3. The vessel has only 6 ropes on the mooring drums. The vessel had 2+2+2        ropes forward and aft on the drums as the 2 other loose breast ropes       were not equally tight which rendered them not equally effective as the       load was borne on the 6 ropes forward and aft.  Root cause 1. Lack of standards (Reduction of speed and increase of speed by the passing Suezmax causing excessive surge movements, vulnerability of the berth layout, insufficient spring lines laid our from the berthed vessel’s fixed mooring drums to counter the excessive loads caused by the fore and aft surge waves)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: regular / continues tending of mooring lines, regular monitoring of all mooring lines. training of deck crew members for attending of mooring lines to avoid such incidents, with river berths.
Corrective Action: ? The 6 damaged mooring ropes to be replaced.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: CARGO HOLD FLOODING, 20 CONTAINERS ENGULFED IN WATER BT 1 METER, EXTEND OF DAMAGE UNKNOWN.
IncidentEventDetails: DURING BALLAST OPERATION, PRIOR OPENING THE VALVE OF PORT SIDE-SIDE TANK, THE MANHOLE COVER  OF NO.2DB(S) WAS NOT FULLY SECURED. THE CROSS VALVE WHICH INTERSECTS PORT SIDE-SIDE TANK ENSURED TO BE SHUT, BUT IN THIS CASE VRC SYSTEM FAILED AND THE VALVE WAS IN OPEN POSITION. HENCEFORTH, THIS LED TO FLOODING OF CARGO HOLD NO.2
ImmediateAction: CORRECTED THE LIST IMMEDIATELY TO KEEP THE VESSEL UPRIGHT.  CONTINUOUS DE-BALLASTING OF NO.2 DB,2(S)SIDE TANK, 2(P) SIDE TANK WERE CARRIED OUT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CARGO HOLD FLOODING, 20 CONTAINERS ENGULFED IN WATER BY 1 METER, EXTEND OF DAMAGE UNKNOWN.
DirectCause: FAILURE OF VALVE REMOTE CONTROL SYSTEM ( OPENING & CLOSING OF VALVE MANUALLY )
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL CRITICAL EQUIPMENT TO BE TESTED AND MAINTAINED REGULARLY, ENSURE ENOUGH SPARES TO BE AVAILABLE ONBOARD AT ALL TIMES.  SAFETY PROCEDURES TO BE ABOVE ALL OPERATIONS. ( eg: Time, commercial pressure etc... )

IncidentDetails: VSL WAS DISCHARGING LIMESTONE AT BERTHED JD-2 IN CHENNAI. AT 0000HERS ON 20/12/2018 WATCH CHANGED FROM ONE D/O TO OTHER D/O  WITH BALLASTING IN PROGRESS IN WBT #3(P/S) BY ONE BALLAST PUMP.  AND 0015 D/O SEND ONE CREW TO TAKE SOUNDING OF WBT #3(P/S) AND INFORMED THAT SOUNDING IS NOT CHANGING THEN D/O WENT IN V/V CONTROL ROOM AND FOUND CH#3 V/Vs OPENED INSTEAD OF WBT #3(P/S). D/O IMMEDIETLY OPENED V/Vs OF WBT #3(P/S) AND CLOSED CH #3 V/Vs. AT THIS STAGE THERE WERE CARGO IN CH #3 APRX 5000MT AND LUCKLY THERE WAS NO WATER FOUND IN CH #3.
IncidentEventDetails: *VSL WAS DISCHARGING LIMESTONE AT BERTHED JD-2 IN CHENNAI  *0000HERS ON 20/12/2018 WATCH CHANGED FROM ONE D/O TO OTHER D/O  WITH BALLASTING   IN PROGRESS IN WBT #3(P/S) BY ONE BALLAST PUMP.  *0015 D/O SEND ONE CREW TO TAKE SOUNDING OF WBT #3(P/S) AND INFORMED THAT   SOUNDING IS NOT CHANGING.  *SAME TIME D/O WENT IN V/V CONTROL ROOM AND FOUND CH#3 V/Vs OPENED INSTEAD OF WBT   #3(P/S).  *0600HRS D/O INFORMED ME(CHIEF OFFICER) THIS WAS THE CASE HAPPEN.  *CALLED BOTH OFFICERS AND EXPLAIN THEM THE CONQUENCES FOR SMALL MISTAKES AND   ADVISED THEM TO RECHECK THE V/Vs AFTER OPENED BY CREW OR CADET.  *0600HRS CH#3 CHECKED AND NO WATER INGRESS FOUND(LUCKLY NOTHING HAPPENED).
ImmediateAction: D/O IMMEDIETLY OPENED V/Vs OF WBT #3(P/S) AND CLOSED CH #3 V/Vs. AT THIS STAGE THERE WERE CARGO IN CH #3 APRX 5000MT AND LUCKLY THERE WAS NO WATER FOUND IN CH #3.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: VESSEL CAN DAMAGE TO BALLAST LINE AND DAMAGE TO CARGO AND ALSO DAMAGE TO BALLAST PUMP AND HULL.
DirectCause: DUTY HAS BEEN BYPASSED TO CREW BY QUALIFIED OFFICER & BLINDLY BELIEVED ON THEM AND NOT THINK TO RECHECK AND IGNORED HIS RESPONSIBILITIES.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: BRIEFFING TO ALL CREW AND DUTY OFFICER AND DUTY OFFICER ADVISED TO CHECK WHILE OPENING ANY V/V.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: To be raised as a near-miss report. Ship informed.
