IncidentDetails: During bunkering operation at Cedar Grove anchorage, the starboard side accommodation ladder wire parted whilst hoisting right after 4th Engr finished with bunker barge sounding. The starboard side accommodation ladder fall to the bunker barge about 2.5 meters causing minor damage (scratches) on barge deck surface. The hand rails of the starboard side accommodation ladder bended and need to be repaired.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1045 LT, 07.11.2018, Bunker Barge PBL 3027 alongside. Hose connected around 1145 LT. At 1200 LT, 4th engineer went down the bunker barge to take bunker barge sounding then came back onboard after 45 minutes. At 1245LT, the Bosun while standing with line of sight to the ganway started to hoist the gangway ladder. The wire parted when about 2.5 meters height and fall on deck of the bunker barge. Ship staff put sling belt on the gangway ladder end then lifted using the ships crane situated on the starboard side. Wire replaced then starboard side gangway secured.
ImmediateAction: Ship crew immediately lifted the starboard side accommodation ladder using the crane davit, wire ropes renewed and tested after replacing new wire.  Ship staff cleaned and painted the damaged surface for the satisfaction of the barge Master.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The gangway wire had to be replaced due to damage. The Barge had scratch marks on their deck due to gangway fall which was painted.
DirectCause: The wire condition was due to for renewal on Nov 24th on completion of one year in service . Although external condition was looking satisfactory . The wire seems to have deteriorated over the time.  Inspection intrerval to be made shorter or a thorough inspection prior every usage is recommended after this incident as the wire was within one year of usage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thorough Inspection of wire prior usage especially if being used after long interval.  The gangway sheaves & associated rollers to be checked for any gaps or deteroration in condition.
ClosingNote: Redone with NEW REPORT

IncidentDetails: Engine Cadet Rajendran Karunya Moorthi lost Usd 205 missing from his cabin. During enquiry with Eng/Cdt came to know his cabin key was missing for past few days.The matter of missing key was reported only after money lost.
IncidentEventDetails: Engine Cadet Rajendran Karunya Moorthi reported through Chief Engineer that Usd 205 missing from his cabin.His cabin key was also missing which was later found in Engine room.The matter of missing key was reported only after money lost.
ImmediateAction: On discussion with Management team, unannounced cabin & stores inspection was carried out by summoning all hands on the bridge. Each crew member was called & cabin searched for the missing money. Also checked total amount of money available with each crew and compared with the past cash advance records. All ship staff were asked regarding any suspicious activity noticed. On completion of search, could not find anything suspicious in cabin or stores.    There was similar past incident due to which clear instructions were given in the safety meeting & crew joining to keep all cabins locked at all times.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Engine Cadet Moorthi's money lost from cabin. On enquiry found his key was also missing few days back which was later found in Engine room. Cabin search was carried out and money not found.The matter of missing key was reported only after money lost.
DirectCause: Cabin not locked even after instructed to do so.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All personnel instructed to keep cabin locked at all times. All crew informed to report missing things at the earliest to take suitable action.
ClosingNote: Security breach report prepared and all crew advised to be responsible for their personal belongings and report immediately if the cabin/store key is misplaced.

IncidentDetails: When the vessel loading at La coruna Jetty no # 4 suddenly Wx deteriorates and swell started to increase and causing the vessel to roll moderately, same informed to terminal and stopped the loading operation. Due to the improper location and arrangement of bollard the mooring tails were started rub against the bollard surface and causing damage to 2 FWD spring tail ropes and one stern tail rope parted. Immediately called tug and mooring crew to re arrange the mooring to avoid further damaging of mooring tails. Parted mooring tails renewed and passed the mooring line. once vessel safely moored, released the assistance tug and Cargo ceased by terminal until the Wx gets better.
IncidentEventDetails: When vessel loading at La coruna Jetty no # 4 suddenly Wx deteriorates and swell started to increase and casuing the vessel to roll moderately, same informed to terminal and stopped the loading operation. Due to the improper location and arrangement of bollard the mooring tails were started rub against the bollard surface and causing damage to 2 FWD spring tail. One stern tail rope parted due to excess wait in the tail due to roiling. Immediately called tug and mooring crew to re arrange the mooring to avoid further damaging of mooring tails. Parted mooring tails renewed and passed the mooring line. once vessel safely moored released the assistance tug and Cargo ceased by terminal until the Wx gets better.
ImmediateAction: When Wx deteriorates immediately stopped cargo operation and terminal disconnected the loading arm. Vessel called for tug assistance and mooring crew to rearrange the mooring to avoid further damaged to mooring tails. Parted mooring tail renewed and passed to shore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 mooring tails damaged and 1  mooring tail parted.
DirectCause: When  Wx deteriorates and swell started to increase and casing the vessel to roll moderately. Due to the improper location and arrangement of bollard the mooring tails were started rub against the bollard surface and causing damage to 2 FWD spring tail and one stern tail rope parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Notify the terminal about the bollard arrangement and mooring can bee relocated if another option is available. Monitor Wx report and preparation can be done well in advance to avoid major damages .
LessionLearnt: Regular monitoring of mooring required.
ReviewComments: Protection against rubbing by means of Canvas or ropes to be placed on the fairlead when found chafing.
ClosingNote: Protection against rubbing by means of Canvas or ropes to be placed on the fairlead when found chafing.

IncidentDetails: While Picking up the gangway for securing, crew member forgot to remove the chain connecting the gangway railings which caused a slight bend in the railings.

ImmediateAction: Immediately stopped picking up and connection chain removed. Later, using chain block the bend on railing has been straigtend.

IncidentDetails: As per routine, one of the crew member woke up around 0945 and immediately smelt some object burning.He looked around the cabin for the ignition source and noticed the plug of the extension cable for the portable heater heated up and burning (at the electrical point).  See attached pics
IncidentEventDetails: The portable heater was connected to an extension cable that was plugged into the electrical point.  The crew member had switched on the heater around 0430 once he had returned to cabin after watch. Vessel was rolling and pitching at times due to which, the cable plug must have dislodged and created a spark.
ImmediateAction: Immediately pulled out the socket using an alternative item at hand.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The extension cable and the socket damaged.
DirectCause: Faulty plug receptacle.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discussed the incident in the Safety Meeting. Instructed all crew to have un-used electrical pluggings and lights kept off when not in use.   Time permitting, have Elec/Off to have a QC on all plugs.
ClosingNote: Overloading of electrical equipment, lack of inspection regime and compliance.

IncidentDetails: 1. Bilge Alarm Sounded at No 1 CHD  2. Started Pumping out using Bilge Eductor  3. Level started raising instead going down.  4. Direct pumping lined up and started pumping out.  5. Water Level observed to raise to about 90 cms from Tank Top.
IncidentEventDetails: 0913 : Bilge Alarm receieved.  0915 : C/O informed  0918 : Checked hold and found water till Bilges level  0921 : Started Pumping out using Bilge eductor  0927 : Observed Water raising instead of going down  0931 : stooped pump out and started lining up for direct pump out  0935 : Direct pump out using Fire pump started  0945 : Water Level below Coontainer shoes
ImmediateAction: 1. Pumped out Water using Fire Pump.  2. N/R Valve of No 1 CHD bilge overhauled
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Water filled in No 1 CHD to a height of 90 cms for about 30 minutes.  2. possiblr damage to container cargo due water ingress
DirectCause: 1. n/r valve was not overhauled.  2. Hold bilge line to Ballast Eductor line was left open to Sea.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Overhauling of all N/R Valves.  2. Integrity test of all N/R Valves.  3. Close the valves and blan the line connecting Bilge Line to Ballast eductor  4. Training on same to be done
Corrective Action: PMS schedule for inspection of NRV for cargo holds.
LessionLearnt: 1.The correct line set up for bilge pumping out to be known to all officers. 2.Any valve on ballast/bilge system to be operated in E/R must be informed to C/E. 3.Monitoring of CH Bilge must have a team with proper PPE & bridge reported.
ClosingNote: Alert sent to all vessels and following CA in place:  1. All N/R Valves overhauling completed and found only following Valves frozen during opening up:   a. 1P  b. 3P  c. 4S  d. 6S  e. 7S    2. Training and discussion regarding this incident done with all engineers and deck officers. Following course of action agreed, the the following order:   a. N/R Valve will be overhauled  b. Integrity test will be completed - informed all of the procedure to  be followed for same.  c. Valves 007, 039, 002 to be closed after integrity test is finished.  d. Bilge eductor to be tried out. Most probably the reason for low  efficiency of bilge eductor could be due N/R valves not moving freely  and also suction loss due 002/003 opened. Once overhaul is completed   and  002 is closed, trust the bilge eductor should work  satisfactorily with  right amount of drive pressure from Fire Pump.  Any problem faced to be  informed to office.  e. Only Bilge eductor to be used for pump out Cargo hold bilges. Any  point of time, if C/O / OOW see that bilge ductor is unable to cope  up,Master to be informed immediately and Ballast eductor can be used  with following precautions - Start the pump for Ballast eductor drive  first, Open Eductor Drive and Overboard. Open 039. Keep a Person  standby at the Cargo hold with W/T and then open the cargo suction  Valve. Monitor if the Bilge level is going down.  3. Plan for tomorrow Bilge integrity test:   a. Tool box meeting with all concerned for integrity test.  b. 1 person will be sent on each Cargo hold with W/T.  c. Chief Offcier to be standby at Ship office  d. C/E & 4/E will be opening Valves in E/R once infomed by C/O  e. Self will go to No 1 Cargo Hold.  f. Open 007, 039 and 002 to pressurise the line.   g. Confirm with all people if there are any leaks observed.  h. Inform C/O to open Cargo Hold Suction Valve for No 1 CHD. Check for  any leaks from N/R Valve.  i. Same to be done for all holds after self go in the relevant hold.  j. Once completed 007, 039 and 002 will be closed by C/E  k. Line will be set for Bilge eductor in No 7 CHD and start stripping  the Line.  l. After this, will try out Bilge pumping out for No 1 CHD.

IncidentDetails: STOLEN FIRE HOSE NOZZLES FROM MAIN DECK IN CHINESE PORT
IncidentEventDetails: AFTER DEP QINGDAO ON ROUTINE INSPECTION FOUND 5 FIRE HOSE NOZZLES MISSING FROM FIRE HOSE BOX ON DECK.
ImmediateAction: REPLACED BY SPARE NOZZLES OF JET TYPE
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: PROPERTY LOSS
DirectCause: IN ADEQUATE MONITORING OF SHIP'S PROPERTY DURING CARGO WATCHES / PORT STAY
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. MORE REGULAR CHECKS OF STORE, LAS/FFA ITEMS AND MONITOR MOVEMENTS OF  STEVEDORS BY WATCH KEEPER.   2. ALTERNATIVELY -- PROPOSED PAD LOCK SYSTEM WITH MASTER KEY WITH D/OFFICER, GANGWAY AND CHIEF OFFICER. HOWEVER SUBJECT TO ARRANGEMENT NOT BEING A BREECH OF SAFETY.
ClosingNote: Lack of ISPS security watches.

IncidentDetails: On 21.02.2019 while vessel discharging at Beira parted both her fwd spring lines (wire rope). Vessel was discharging Gasoil, Gasoline and Jet A1 with all chicksons connected.  Vessel was experiencing very heavy current to the order of 4-5 kts and range of tide was 6.30 mts.  Though Lines were tight and being tended to however due to slight uneven tendering one of the break rendered on tentision and the other wire did not. The break which didnot render had very heavy weight on it and gave way. As one of the spring parted the complete weight shifted to the other spring and it also parted. As the stern lines were soft ropes and very long line as compared to springs(wires) these stern lines have taken weight/stretched  after vessel moved about 3 mts ahead.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel experiencing very strong following current, to the rate of about 4-5 kts.  Range of tide : 6.30 mts  0450lt / 21.02.2019 : Vessel parted one of her fwd spring line.  0452lt / 21.02.2019 : Notice to E/R for M/E.  0455lt / 21.02.2019 : Vesssel parted her second fwd spring line. All cargo operation stopped.  0457lt / 21.02.2019 : Vessel reported to have shifted about 3.0 mts fwd, which was also due to very strong following current.  0505lt / 21.02.2019 : Port control informed, requested for one tug assistance.  0510lt / 21.02.2019 : M/E ready for maneuvering.  0520lt / 21.02.2019 : First fwd spring ashore.  0530lt / 21.02.2019 : Tug A/S for pushing.  0545lt / 21.02.2019 : Second spring ashore.  0557lt / 21.02.2019 : Vessel in position.  0600lt / 21.02.2019 : Tug away  0624lt / 21.02.2019 : Resumed discharging
ImmediateAction: 0452lt / 21.02.2019 : Notice to E/R for M/E.  0455lt / 21.02.2019 : All cargo operation stopped.  0505lt / 21.02.2019 : Port control informed, requested for one tug assistance.  0510lt / 21.02.2019 : M/E ready for maneuvering.  0520lt / 21.02.2019 : First fwd spring ashore.  0530lt / 21.02.2019 : Tug A/S for pushing.  0545lt / 21.02.2019 : Second spring ashore.  0557lt / 21.02.2019 : Vessel in position.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: two number wire rope parted, changed end to end. Vessel proceeding to DD , ends to be hydraulically recrimped.
DirectCause: Lack of people on deck for continuous mooring operation.  Lack of supervision.  Lack of knowledge.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting held with all officers and crew. Explained effect of tide and strong currents. Explained correct procedures to tend to mooring. Continuous tranning to be given prior every tidal port.
Corrective Action: Rope pieces prepared and kept near winches to placed between ropes/wires to prevent them getting stuck. Also the panama leads to have ropes/canvas tied to prevent chafing.

IncidentDetails: While shifting berth at Antwerp, vessel had a minor allision with moored barge.
IncidentEventDetails: Pacific Endeavor loaded various grades of Chemical from USG , Houston and New Orelans to discharge at the port of Antwerp and Rotterdam . Vessel arrived at Antwerp  roads on 16th October 2018. Prior arrival to Antwerp and prior shifting of berth, Pre Arrival Navigation and cargo related Checks were completed as per company procedures.  Vessel arrived Antwerp roads on 1100 Hrs on 16th October 2018 and tendered Notice of Readiness at the same time. Prior to vessel berthing at LBC 275, she discharged at berth  Vopak 499 ON 16th October , followed by Vopak ERO tank on 17th October to discharge various grade of chemicals . On completion of discharge at Vopak ERO tank, she was  made fast at Layberth at 313 on 18th October , awaiting berth availability . As bow thruster was installed , all movements including berthing and unberthing operations  were performed using one tug which was made fast aft . Relevant checklists as per company procedures were complied with. Passage plan from Pilot  Embarkation Point to berth was discussed with bridge team. Master and Pilot information was exchanged for shifting from Layberth 313, port side alongside to berth No LBC -275. For berthing the vessel at LBC-275, vessel had to swing the vessel to port inside the basin using Bow thruster and one tug which was made fast stbd quarter .Intention was swing the  vessel and proceed on a reciprocal course to make the vessel port side alongside . Dock pilot boarded vessel at 0054 hrs. on 21st October to shift to next berth. As per initial Master  and Pilot exchange, Pilot required the tug to made fast at aft centre lead. Master requested Pilot to secure aft tug on starboard quarter in order to have aft station clear of tug’s line during  unmooring and mooring operation. On Master’s request Pilot visually checked the location of the fairlead and bollard and approved to make fast the tug on stbd quarter. At 0136hrs LT, swing to port was initiated with the use of Bow thruster and Tug made fast at stbd quarter. During the process of port swing , engines command was give towards astern to  keep the forward end clear of the barge alongside at opposite dock. AT 0139 Dead slow astern command was given by the pilot, followed by slow astern ,Half astern  and finally full astern to maintain sufficient distance between bow and barge moored forward during the swing. As the port swing progressed, Tug made fast at stbd quarter was pull to pull the vessel to keep a check on the forward movement and assist vessel in turning in the basin. During the port swing, tug was suppose to haul tight on the ropes to restrict the forward  movement of the vessel . As the swing of the vessel progressed, the tug rope was observed slack causing a delay in hauling the ropes and holding it tight to restrict the forward movement  of the vessel . The short delay in action caused stbd side of vessel Bulbous bow making a contact with the moored barge “SYNTHESE 4” in spite of using the ship engine to full astern movement. Later during the course of investigation , vessel Master was enlightened about momentarily mechanical failure on tug’s winch delaying the response of tug in hauling the tug line.  The Barge was completely empty and in a light condition, Vessel was partially loaded, and the bulbous bow was below the water line there by brushing the midship section of the barge below  the bottom hull plating.
ImmediateAction: Vessel continued the astern movement and with the help of Tug and Bow thruster, vessel safely moored at berth.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor scratch marks on bulbous bow. 1. The Forepeak tank space inspected by ship staff. No serious damage noted. 2. Class surveyor inspected the forepeak tank space and warranted for NO repairs as damage was minor.
DirectCause: Poor coordination of reaction time: Due to slow coordination/reaction between pilot and tug, the tug could not haul the ropes fastened to vessel ‘Pacific Endeavor’ resulting delay in swinging the vessel and to control the forward movement of the vessel. This caused brushing contact with Barge “Synthese 4” moored on the forward side of the vessel.  Inadequate Master-Pilot information exchange: Master-Pilot information exchange did not account for the congestion within the proximity of LBC berth . Additional tug should have been used knowing the condition.  Execution of Passage Plan: OOW failed to apprise Master and the Pilot about vessel’s speed during critical stages of manoeuvre in the basin, especially after the vessel’s entry in the basin until the vessel started swinging. Lack of situational awareness among bridge team.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Refresher training for the Master and 2OFF in ship-handling and bridge team resource management prior next joining. 2. Ship’s bridge team to be briefed on safe navigation in pilotage waters 3. Briefing on Master/Pilot exchange,emphasize on the language of internal and external communication to ensure there is an agreement . Bridge team should bring to pilot’s attention if there is a deviation from the above agreement. 4. Marine superintendent will sail on board carry out dynamic navigation audit , , verify corrective actions and monitor bridge team during the passage. 5. During the safety briefing of Masters/ Chief officers in office following points to be reiterated: a) Highlight master’s responsibility during Pilotage, including speed control and situational awareness. b) Even if Bow thruster is fitted on board, Master should exercise his discretion to supplement the harbour manoeuvres especially when the vessel has to be moored in estuaries in relation to the width of the harbour, the length, draft and constrains of own vessel by ordering two tugs. c) To emphasize on proper communication and information exchange with pilot especially if the vessels is in the area where language might be a concern. 6. Navigational Alert to be issued and lesson’s learnt to be shared with all vessels 7. The root causes analysis and the findings to be included in courses like BTM and command course at the training institute dealing with speed controls, situational awareness, decision to request for additional tug boats for vessel’s having working bow thruster in confined estuaries, ports with communication problem to be discussed giving guidance to the masters/deck officers what measures to be taken to avoid such incidents.
Corrective Action: 1. Refresher training for the Master and 2OFF in ship-handling and bridge team resource management prior next joining. 2. Ship’s bridge team to be briefed on safe navigation in pilotage waters 3. Briefing on Master/Pilot exchange,emphasize on the language of internal and external communication to ensure there is an agreement . Bridge team should bring to pilot’s attention if there is a deviation from the above agreement. 4. Marine superintendent will sail on board carry out dynamic navigation audit , , verify corrective actions and monitor bridge team during the passage. 5. During the safety briefing of Masters/ Chief officers in office following points to be reiterated: a) Highlight master’s responsibility during Pilotage, including speed control and situational awareness. b) Even if Bow thruster is fitted on board, Master should exercise his discretion to supplement the harbour manoeuvres especially when the vessel has to be moored in estuaries in relation to the width of the harbour, the length, draft and constrains of own vessel by ordering two tugs. c) To emphasize on proper communication and information exchange with pilot especially if the vessels is in the area where language might be a concern. 6. Navigational Alert to be issued and lesson’s learnt to be shared with all vessels 7. The root causes analysis and the findings to be included in courses like BTM and command course at the training institute dealing with speed controls, situational awareness, decision to request for additional tug boats for vessel’s having working bow thruster in confined estuaries, ports with communication problem to be discussed giving guidance to the masters/deck officers what measures to be taken to avoid such incidents.
LessionLearnt: 1. Master-Pilot information exchange is key to safe and effective manoeuvering. 2. Master to exercise his authority to question pilot's action to ensure safe navigation. 3. Bridge team should work in cohesion and important information is to be immediately brought to the attention of Master and Pilot. 4. Duty watchkeepers standing by at mooring station is to alert bridge team should there be any concern about vessel's movement especially in confined channels and basin. 5. Master should not hesitate in requesting additional tug if he has any concern reg vessel's safety.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Incident investigation report shared with IMT.

IncidentDetails: 2nd Engineer noticed smoke and slight flame on the incinerator burner top side and immediately used a portable Extinguisher and extinguished the fire. Burner stopped, all Fuel V/Vs shut and both Waste Oil Tanks outlet V/Vs shut immediately. There is no abnormal alarm received in Incineator.        - At the same time Fire Alarm received on the Bridge, Master informed immediately to the Drill Point.        - 2nd Engineer informed immediately on walkie talkie, fire in incinerator and same extinguished and incinerator stopped. C/E and 4th Engineer entered E/R and found fire was completely extinguished and Incinerator Furnace temperature found 810°C.        - Condition under control informed to Bridge immediately.        - Incinerator Exhaust fan kept running to reduce the Furnace Chamber Temperature.        - After Incinerator cooled down, Vessel carried out Inspection on the Burner and Furnace. Found oil accumulation in the Furnace damper passage and Honey Comb pieces inside the Furnace and Burner tip. Due to formation of Honey Comb in front of the Main Burner, the Flow/Spray of Waste Oil got blocked and small amount of oil found accumulated inside the  Furnace Air Damper passage which got fired and outside area paint got burnt.        - Photographs attached for your Reference.        - Main Burner dismantled and found Main Burner oil pipe not aligned properly and which was touching on the edge of the Atomizing Cup. Lot of Sludge deposits noticed in the Atomizing Cup. Main Burner oil pipe removed and cleaned and found slightly bent on the oil supply tip which was found brazed on the supply pipe.  Damaged parts:          - Vessel found Burner Door Limit Switch inside part burnt which needs renewal.        - Main Burner oil pipe with oil tip found bent also needs renewal.        - Main burner oil inlet solenoid valves (02no's) found non-operational - needs renewal        -Refractory patch up required in the Burner area
IncidentEventDetails: incinerator started at 1000 hrs LT  The furnace temperature was maintaining 850 -900 degree centigrade   1630 hrs all crewmember called for drill expect duty engineer in engine room .  Around 1700 duty engineer noticed minor fire on the incinerator main burner assy top area.   Same time fire alarm noticed on bridge fire alarm panel ,and bridge informed  the other crew memebrs  regarding the engine room fire alarm     1704 the duty engineer extigushed  the fire with a DCP portble extingusher .   1706. All the associated valves shut and incinerator stopped .
ImmediateAction: Extingushed the fire with portable  extingusher , incinerator stopped . Alll the associated valves  and tank valves shut.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There is no potential damage on mechinery or its contorl /associated systems
DirectCause: Formation of honey comb deposits infront of the burner, which caused  the improper spray pattern form the oil burner and oil got accumulated in the Furnace air drum caused a minor fire .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The burner assy and associated systems to be inspected after every 6-7 hours of operation. Frequent monitoring required  when incinerator in operation.
Corrective Action: The burner assy and associated systems to be inspected after every 6-7 hours of operation. Frequent monitoring required  when incinerator in operation.
ClosingNote: The inspection  interval for the burner has been reduced and vessel inspects the burner every 6-7 hours of operation

IncidentDetails: During L/B annual survey the Stbd Life Boat aft skate got damaged and accidentally released into water at Singapore Sudong Anchorage ( 01 12.4N 103 40.7E).    The skate got damaged while lowering the stbd L/B in order to test the brake and the sudden stop resulted L/B to swing heavily and caused damage to aft skate which subsequently fell in water and sank at posn-01 12.4N 103 40.7E. The size of fallen skate is apprx 20 X100 cm.
IncidentEventDetails: The skate got damaged while lowering the stbd L/B in order to test the brake and the sudden stop resulted L/B to swing heavily and caused damage to aft skate which subsequently fell in water and sank at posn-01 12.4N 103 40.7E. The size of fallen skate is apprx 20 X100 cm.
ImmediateAction: The boat was hauled in immediately and a damage assessment was carried out. No apparent damage to the L/boat hull or structure was observed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: STBD LIFEBOAT AFT SKATE AND ITS SECURING TURNBUCKLE.
DirectCause: The skate got damaged while lowering the L/B by the shore technician (Techno Lifeboat Pte Ltd) without informing vessel staff, though ship’s staff were present for assisting but he started lowering the boat himself and resulted in causing damage to aft skate which subsequently fell in water and sank.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Lowering of the life boat shall be carried out only by the designated ship personnel. The activiies of shore personnel shall be monitored at all times. Any unsafe act by the Shore perosnnel shall be stopped and brought to the notice of Master at the earliest.
Cause Analysis: Operating equipment or machinery without authority
ClosingNote: we agree with your corrective and preventive action.

IncidentDetails: LO Purifier overflown and approximately 800 ltrs of  M.E .system oil lost to  sludge tank.
IncidentEventDetails: During normal watching routines AT 1030 HRS   (02- march -19) The duty enginer recieved lube oil sludge tank high level alram. He acknowldged the alarm and inspected the L.O. Purifier. Found the L.O.Purifier was overflowing  with out giving any alarm or warning in  control  panel (model GBC-2).  The purifier was running in auto mode and the multi monitor whcich is attached with the L.O.Purifier was showing error signal. The duty engineer stopped the purifier immediately. Later the inspection on multimonitor function revealed  that the DISCHARGE DETECTOR FUNCTION OF  multi monitor was mulfunctioning and it was not giving alarm signal to automatic control panel when it detect an abnormal discharge  or overflowing .
ImmediateAction: The L.O. Purifier stopped , and investigated the fault and found that the multi monitor device is mulfunctioning. The vessel interchanged the multi monitor device with G/E  LO Purifier  and found the system is working satisfcatory with all safties  and alarms.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: M.E .L.O .PURIFIER MULTI MONITOR -MM1  PCB Mulfunctioning ,which caused  fail  to give the alarm in the control panel while purifier in operation .  LO Purifier overflown and approximately 800 ltrs of  M.E .system oil lost to  sludge tank..
DirectCause: Defective  MULTI MONITOR -MM1
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NEW MULTI MONITOR DEVICE SHOULD BE FITTED
Corrective Action: NEW MULTI MONITOR DEVICE SHOULD BE FITTED
ClosingNote: Multi monitor Renewed with new one

IncidentDetails: Vessel detained at Ningbo, China
IncidentEventDetails: Pls see the attached investigation report.
ImmediateAction: Pls see the attached investigation report.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel detained due to 4 detainable deficiencies.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:   1. The Defect already identified on Steering gear was not repaired. 2. The condition of the quick closing valve Air Cylinder was assumed to be good based on previous trials. 3. Pipe for the dosage pump was not confirmed to be tightened properly after maintenance.    Indirect Cause:   1. The defect on the steering gear was not reported on company defect reporting module. Which caused lack in follow up of the defect and early rectification. 2. The steering gear had other external leaks on Steering gear No.2. Which shifted the focus and prioritization of the job from already identified defects on the steering gear. 3. No previous overhaul records for quick closing valve Air Cylinder were available. The maintenance status of Air Cylinder was inadequately identified. 4. The maintenance on dosage pump was not supervised by the senior ship staff.   Root Cause:   1. Company procedures for defect reporting were not complied with.  2. Following PMS schedule were inadequately defined. a. 4 yearly overhaul of Steering gear pump b. No PMS schedule defined for Sewage treatment plant Dosage Pump. 3. Policy for assigning last done date, in case of previous maintenance records are not available on takeover of the vessel, not well defined.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pls refer the corrective and preventive actions assigned to various PIC.
Corrective Action: Fleet alert regarding the incident to be sent to all vessels.
ClosingNote: Detailed investigation carried out into the detention. Preventive actions taken to avoid recurrence.

IncidentDetails: On 17th July 2018 Vessel arrived layberth (Pier 16 SST) for USCG COC Inspection. 3 Nos. of USCG inspectors along with a Marine Chemist boarded the vessel for inspection. After the inspection, USCG noted one deficiency with code 30 on vessel’s deck spray system.
IncidentEventDetails: On testing of deck spray system, no sufficient coverage was noted on Tank #1, Tank # 2, Tank # 3 and on Manifolds. USCG allowed vessel 4 hours of time for rectification. On second testing, sufficient coverage was still not seen. This was noted as a detainable deficiency.
ImmediateAction: Spray line was cleared. Class attendance was made to inspect.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel detained.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:   1. Spray line had accumulated rust within the line which was not cleared. 2. There was no record in evidence if orifice plates were removed for clearing the lines of accumulated rust.     Indirect Cause:   Water spray system was tested 11 days before arrival Houston. During this intervening period loose rust from pipe internals had come free to choke the line again (Vibration due to large course alteration, astern movement, anchoring, or similar operations).  Additionally, spray line was not flushed with fresh water after testing.     Root Cause:   Detailed Maintenance and testing procedure for water spray system was not available onboard. Instructions available on board did not include removal of orifice plate for clearing the line of accumulated rust.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pls see the corrective / Preventive assigned to individual PIC.
Corrective Action: Fleet alert regarding the incident to be sent to all vessels.
ClosingNote: Detailed investigation carried out into the detention. Preventive actions taken to avoid recurrance.

IncidentDetails: Hoisting motor of starboard side Accommodation Ladder broke down while approaching port (TELUK RUBIAH MARITIME TERMINAL, LUMUT MALAYSIA
IncidentEventDetails: Starboard side accommodation ladder was swung out to deck level during arrival port preparations. Suddenly loud sound, the shaft for Gangway hoisting drum sheared off and the Gangway ladder lowered all the way to the water level, at the same time gangway wire partec.
ImmediateAction: Requested to Pilot on board to berth portside alongside and was permitted to do so. Gangway was retrieved and stowed back in position after vessel berthing safely.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hoisting drum shaft sheared, and gangway wire parted.
DirectCause: Shaft for Gangway hoisting drum sheared due to material fatigue.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Sheared shaft for hoisting drum planned to be replaced with new spare shaft which will be supplied at the earliest. Inspection of gear case, renewal of winch gear oil and checks for contamination of gear oil to be enforced strictly during periodic PMS inspection routines.
ClosingNote: Proper inspection and lubrication of accommodation ladders / gangways is to be done every month and supervised by C/O. Wire ropes to be changed every 30 months. The ladder winch is to be overhauled every 60 months and load tested. Drum shaft is to be inspected during this time.

IncidentDetails: During heaving up of stbd anchorage Fujairah B anchorage for departure to Load port, anchor cable  was 5 shackles in the water. Windlass was unable to heave anchor further with 5 shackles in the water.
IncidentEventDetails: vessel was scheduled to depart for load port.  Anchor stations was called and vessel started heaving anchor at 1330 hrs LT on 24.Mar.2019.  when vessel had 5 shackles in water , windlass could not heave anchor further. Depth at anchorage 98 metres.
ImmediateAction: Forward stations informed Bridge immediately , while attempting to heave.  OIl level in the system checked was at normal level. Ensured all pumps were running still couldnot heave. OIl temperature was around 60 deg c.  engine room was informed and ETO and CH eng came forward.With continous attempts to heave further the oil temperature went to 65 deg c. Anchor was put on brakes, bow stopper engaged  and gear out. main hydrauli pumps stopped , cooling wtaer continously running, small pump kept running and idle turning of winch to reduce oil temperature. when oil temperature was at 50 deg c, again attempted to heave stbd anchor without any progress.Then forward stbd inboard wire was re reeved in oppsite direction then coupled with port inboard Head line wire. onve oil temperature was around 50 deg c . stbd anchor was heaved with stbd windlass and also coupled wire on port windlass. Few links came up , to around 4 shackles the could not heave any further. office was appraised of the situation. ch eng checked andconfirems all pumps developing pressure to normal working 200bar. further attempt was made by taking out mooring rope from fpk store and coupling the stbd windlass warping drum to stbd fwd breast winch wire and attempted to heave stbd windlass, prt windlass coupled wire and mooring rope on warping drum with out any success. The attempt was made to connect mooring rope to anchor cable and heave using breast winch warping drum along with stbd windlass without any success. finally attempt was made with coupled wire on stbd inboard headline drun coiled only in half th edrum increasing the height of coil to increase leverage of pulling. vessel was able to succesfully heave in this way few links. Head line wire was transferred and re reeved with increased height for about every 15 links and heaved up. once about 3 shackles on deck, stbd windlass could heave without any assistance. finally Anchor was Aweigh at 2230 hrs LT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The delay caused by the incident could lead to claims / and vessel off hire.
DirectCause: Suspect windlass Motor capacity to heave at deep anchorages such as Fujairah.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: technical evaluation to be carried out and SHore health check / maintenance on the system.

IncidentDetails: At the port of Felixstowe on 31st march while lowering the port side gangway   to checck the position of the gangway on the jetty  one of the gangway steps got damaged. The extra strap lashing was not removed from the step & the gangway was continued to lower down, one side of the step completely tore off.
IncidentEventDetails: While lowering the port side gangway   to checck the position of the gangway on the jetty  one of the gangway steps got damaged. The extra strap lashing was not removed from the step & the gangway was continued to lower down, one side of the step completely tore off.
ImmediateAction: Temperory repars carried out to fix the step by using wooden blocks underneath the step.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: At the port of Felixstowe on 31st march while lowering the port side gangway   to checck the position of the gangway on the jetty  one of the gangway steps got damaged. The extra strap lashing was not removed from the step & the gangway was continued to lower down, one side of the step completely tore off.
DirectCause: Able Seaman overlooked the safety strap used as additional lashing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New step willl be ordered & welded.
Corrective Action: New step willl be ordered & welded.
ClosingNote: training to be carried for all crew and two crew to be present during lowering of gangway.

IncidentDetails: AT 2200 HRS THE OUTWARD STERN ROPE ON NUMBER : #9 MOORING WINCH  ROPE PARTED.
IncidentEventDetails: THE VESSEL WAS ALONGSIDE ESPIGON SOUTH SIDE BERTH, MANZANILLO.  THE BERTH BEING EXPOSED TO THE TRAFFIC LANE LEADING TO ENTRY/ EXIT TO THE PORT CAUSES SLIGHT  TURBULANCE IN WATER WHENEVER THERE IS A LARGE VESSEL PASSING IN THE VICINITY OF THE BERTH RENDERING THE VESSEL TO SWAY.OTHERWISE TOO VESSEL IS ROLLING VERY EASY AT THE BERTH.  ALL THE ROPES WERE DEPLOYED WITH CHAFING GAURDS.  AS THE VESSEL SWAYS/ SURGES CONTINUOUSLY ,    THE OUTERMOST STERN , PARTED DUE TO CHAFING AT THE FAIRLEAD .
ImmediateAction: VESSEL INFORMED TERMINAL .  NEW EYE SPLICED ON THE PARTED ROPE AND MADE FAST ASHORE WITH SHORE ASSISTANCE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: THE VESSEL WAS ALONGSIDE ESPIGON SOUTH SIDE BERTH, MANZANILLO.  THE BERTH BEING EXPOSED TO THE TRAFFIC LANE LEADING TO ENTRY/ EXIT TO THE PORT CAUSES SLIGHT  TURBULANCE IN WATER WHENEVER THERE IS A LARGE VESSEL PASSING IN THE VICINITY OF THE BERTH RENDERING THE VESSEL TO SWAY.OTHERWISE TOO VESSEL IS ROLLING VERY EASY AT THE BERTH.  ALL THE ROPES WERE DEPLOYED WITH CHAFING GAURDS.  AS THE VESSEL SWAYS/ SURGES CONTINUOUSLY ,    THE OUTERMOST STERN , PARTED DUE TO CHAFING AT THE FAIRLEAD .
DirectCause: POOR QUALITY CHAFING GUARDS  WASTED WITH TIME AGAINST THE FAIRLEADS EXPOSING THE ROPES.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MOORINGS TO BE ATTENDED REGULARLY W.R.T.  TIDAL CONDITIONS , VESSEL MOVEMENTS LIKE SURGING/SWAYING .  GOOD QUALITY CHAFING GUARDS TO BE SUPPLIED.REQUISITION FOR THE SAME TO BE RAISED.  VESSEL NOW MAKING MOORING CHECKS EVERY 30 MINUTES.
ClosingNote: Agree with the proposed corrective action.

IncidentDetails: Ball joint connecting Main engine governor linkage and Fuel pump regulating shaft was found broken and due to which governor behaved abnormally.
IncidentEventDetails: During rounds on 31-Mar-19 @ 0500 hrs noticed M/E governor linkage was moving abnormal.  After thorough investigation found that BALL JOINT of governor linkage to fuel pump was broken.  Bridge was informed and controls taken to ECR immediately at 0545 hrs.    Informed TSI regarding this and sought permission for stoppage to renew the ball joint.  M/E stopped at 0552 hrs and governor fuel linkages opened up, BALL JOINT  renewed with the new available spare and fuel linkages fitted back.  Vessel resumed passage at 0640 hrs and all engine parameters found to be normal.
ImmediateAction: Stopped Main engine immediately and broken ball joint was renewed with spare available and fuel linkages fitted back.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: BALL JOINT DAMAGED AND SAME HAS BEEN REPLACED WITH SPARE.
DirectCause: Due to ageing of the Ball joint
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Routine greasing  and proper inspection of ball joints to carry out.
Corrective Action: BALL JOINT TO RENEW , Hunting and loose connection to check.
ClosingNote: Ball joint renewed

IncidentDetails: V/l experienced a sudden pressure surge in all 3 cargo lines & All 3 COP's stopped due to COP discharge over pressure trip(15Bar) . Vessel’s cargo manifold & Cargo line flanges suffered a leak causing oil spill in manifold drip tray & sprays on deck & pumproom. Various damages occured to ship flanges, dresser couplings and valve flanges.    1) Inspection indicated iin pumproom 12Flanges, 8valve flanges found leaking.  2)  On deck 32 Flanges, 8valve flanges & 5 Dresser coupling found leaking.  3) Oil spill of approx. 4.6m3 in manifold drip tray & High pressure oil sprays on deck & in pumproom from flanges & valve flanges( Approx 50Ltr). No spillage of oil overboard
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel arrived Longkou customary anchorage on 11th/ Apr at 0836 LT.NOR tendered and vessel was awaiting berthing prospects & instructions while laying adrift in the customary anchorage. Pilot boarded at No.4 Pilot station on 11th / Apr at  1118 LT. Vessel was made all fast port side along side at Berth no 24 (4+2+2) fwd & aft on 11th / Apr at  1354 LT.   11th / Apr at  1518 LT : Loading master on board.  1530 LT – 1600 LT : Key Meeting carried out and following points discussed   a) Max back pressure at the terminal : 6.0Bar  b) Or Max Pumping rate : 8100m3/Hr  c) Distance of Shore tanks : 800 Mtrs  d) Initial Line displacement of 1000m3 with a rate of 1000m3/hr , Ship stop.  e) Shore Communication channel VHF 05 ( US)  Immigration & customs cleared 11th / Apr at  1540 LT.  1642 LT to 1742 LT Chicksan connected ( 1x16”) & (1x14”).  Reducer (16” x 14”) provided by terminal .  At 1906 LT Vessel commenced discharging (ESPO crude oil) from 4P/S COT using No1 COP & through No1 & No2 Manifold, for line displacement .  At 2006 LT Line displacement completed and vessel ceased discharging. And vessel awaited  shore readiness to resume discharge.  At 2118 LT Discharging resumed using No1 COP & through No1 & No2 Manifold with min rate as per shore request.  At 2142 LT  Loading Master requested to increase rate to 2000m3/hr.  At 2200 LT Shore requested for hourly figures which was provided by duty officer.   At 2220 LT  Loading Master requested to increase rate to 2800m3/hr.  Note : Shore was calling on hourly basis for discharge quantity & ROB quantity .  Regular fire ,safety and security rounds were taken on deck and pumproom . Overside was  checked for any oil sheens during safety rounds . Moorings tended regularly. Gangway manned at all times.   At 2336 LT Loading Master requested to increase rate to 8000m3/hr.  12Th Apr 2019  At 0030 LT Loading Master informed shore was  receiving a rate of 7200m3/hr , and advised to increase rate to 8000m3/hr  At 0500 LT  Loading Master requested for all the cargo figures from Midnight to 0500 LT, same was  provided by duty officer in CCR .  0736 LT : V/l experienced a sudden pressure surge in all 3 cargo lines & All 3 COP's stopped due to COP discharge over pressure trip(15Bar) . Vessel’s cargo manifold & Cargo line flanges suffered a leak causing oil spill in manifold drip tray & sprays on deck & pumproom. Various damages occured to ship flanges, dresser couplings and valve flanges.  Emergency alarm raised, Terminal informed. Ship Staff immediately operated the emergency stop of the cargo pumps from the manifold .( However it was realized later that the pumps already  stopped due to activation of discharge over pressure trip). All 3 drop valves (VCD 207,208,209) opened immediately to release the discharge line pressure. Crew mustered & oil spill cleanup started. No spillage of oil overboard. Found Oil spill of approx. 4.6m3 in manifold drip tray & High pressure oil sprays on deck from flanges & valve flanges. Pumpman & OS entered pumproom taking all the necessary precautions for inspection, High pressure oil sprays found in pumproom from various flanges & valve flanges.    Manifold drip tray drain valves to 4P ( VCD 236 & 232) opened.  Drain valves of NO.1 & No.2 Manifold to 4P ( VCD222,224 & 230) opened.  NO1 & No2 Manifold( VCD 202,204) Shut.  0805: Loading master came on board.  While attending and supervising the  clean up operation Chief officer noticed  that Automatic shut down(ASD) valves at shore chicksan  had activated. He immediately informed Master who was already on deck to meet the Loading Master . When Master discussed with the loading master to verify how had the Automatic shutdown valve  activated Loading Master informed it has happened inadvertently and terminal has informed the technicians to come onboard and do the required inspection .    0830 LT : Oil spillage was contained, Manifold drip tray was drained to 4P COT . Off watch keepers were send for rest. Cleanup was in progress with Deck & Engine watch keepers and day workers .  Thorough inspection of all 3 COP’s & cargo pipe lines on deck & in pumroom carried out. Visual inspection indicated no seal leakages of COP’s & in pumproom 12Flanges, 8valves found leaking. On deck 32 Flanges, 8valve flanges & 5 Dresser coupling found leaking.  1042 LT : No2 Manifold drained & disconnected. Reducer (16” x 14” )  provided by shore was  removed.  At 1330 LT  2 teams were made for tightening of leaked flanges, valves & Dresser couplings.   At 1524 LT No1 Manifold drained & chicksan arm disconnected by shore crew .   At 2000 LT oil spill clean up completed.  At 2042LT Tightening of flange bolts, Valves  & flexible pipe coupling completed.  At 2100 LT pressure testing of all 3 cargo line started using ESPO crude as a medium to check the integrity of lines, flanges & valves.  On 13TH /Apr at 0010 LT : Pressure testing of all 3 cargo lines completed. All 3 cop’s , cargo lines, valves & flanges were thoroughly inspected & f
ImmediateAction: Ship Staff immediately operated the emergency stop of the cargo pumps from the manifold .  (However it was realized later that the pumps already  stopped due to activation of discharge over pressure trip).  All 3 drop valves (VCD 207,208,209) opened immediately to release the discharge line pressure.   Manifold drip tray drain valves to 4P ( VCD 236 & 232) opened.  Drain valves of NO.1 & No.2 Manifold to 4P ( VCD222,224 & 230) opened.  NO1 & No2 Manifold( VCD 202,204) Shut.  Crew mustered & oil spill cleanup started. No spillage of oil overboard.   2 teams were made for tightening of leaked flanges, valves & Dresser couplings.    After tightening of flange bolts, Valves  & flexible pipe couplings Pressure testing of all 3 cargo line started using ESPO crude as a medium to check the integrity of lines, flanges & valves.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel’s cargo manifold & Cargo line flanges suffered a leak causing oil spill in manifold drip tray & sprays on deck & pumproom. Various damages occured to ship flanges, dresser couplings and valve flanges.  Iinspection indicated in pumproom 12Flanges, 8valves found leaking & on deck 32 Flanges, 8valve flanges & 5 Dresser coupling found leaking.  Oil spill of approx. 4.6m3 in manifold drip tray & High pressure oil sprays on deck from flanges & valve flanges
DirectCause: The Incident occurred due to closing of both Auto shutdown device (ASD)  valve which is fitted at shore side chicksans and connected to both ship manifold, Resulted in sudden pressure surge in the ships cargo discharge pipeline which leads to leakage of Manifold flanges, valve flanges, flanges & dresser couplings. Also all 3 COP’s tripped due to “COP discharge over pressure trip” which is set at 15Bars.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1)  A fleet alert to be made of Incident report in order to alert other company vessel’s calling this port.  2) To liaise with technical team and prepare a plan for pressure testing of cargo pipelines  to MAWP after next loading to ensure the integrity of cargo pipelines & repairs to be made if necessary. (Requisition raised for necessary items for repairs)
Corrective Action: Vessels calling Longkou to discuss with the loading masters and confirm the proper functioning of ASD.
ClosingNote: A incident report has been made and sent to charterers. The same is attached. Vessel is cross checking with loading master when calling Longkou about the proper functioning of ASD. An alert was sent to all ships regarding the incident.

IncidentDetails: On voyage daesan to singapore dated 09 april 2019, one  lifebuoy with light  lost due to heavy weather from posn @ 0600 LT- 35 07.83 N/ 125 01.6E to posn1600 LT- 33 15.80 N/124 48.97E, as per plan there was 12 lifebuoys on deck which was counted and kept in midship locker but during placement of lifebuoys there was one extra lifebuoy cradle found with missing lifebuoy near port side gangway used for pilot.
IncidentEventDetails: On 9th april 2019 weather got very rough, vesel experincing heavy rolling, high sea and swell during which wind speed was more than 40 kts,one  lifebuoy with light was lost.
ImmediateAction: Immediatly informed Master and Chief officer, new lifebuoy with light was placed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: one number of lifebuoy with light was lost.
DirectCause: Adverse weather, Inadequate Supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew to be briefed about securing ship properly during sea voyages , specially if heavy weather is expected.
Corrective Action: Crew to be briefed about securing ship properly during sea voyages , specially if heavy weather is expected.
ClosingNote: job closed 18/04

IncidentDetails: On 25.Apr.2019 MT. Aseem collided with own vessel on port bow, whil vessel was departing Fujairah anchorage area. Hull damaged and breache IWO 1p WBT and 1p COT
IncidentEventDetails: vessel heaved up anchor at 2230 hrs on 24.03.2019, from Fujairah 'B'anchorage. vessel was departing from Fujairah anchorage enroute to ALshaheen , at 0005 hrs on 25.apr. 2019 , MT. Aseem collided with own vessel on the port bow. Hull damaged and 1p WBT and 1 PCOT breached.
ImmediateAction: All crew accounted for  and confirmed no injuries. vessel manoeuvred clear in to open area to assess damage. emergency notification to port, SOPEP , company an dcharteres carried. contingency checklist for collision and hull breach complied with. Mt. Aseem contacted and confirmed no injuries or assistance required for her. vessel manoeuvred back to charlie anchorage as per port control instructions
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: hull breached IWO of 1p WBT and 1 p COT , between frames 94 to 97
DirectCause: investigation in progress
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As per office
Corrective Action: A Fleet alert was sent to all vessels to alert them about incident and to advise caution.

IncidentDetails: Fwd spring line tail rope parted under tension and struck ships railing causing railing to bend.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was engaged in sts with M.V Kapellen. Spring ropes from both vessels were comming under tension alternatively due to rolling between vessels and both vessels were only partly loaded. Tail rope of fwd spring line parted under tension and struck ships railing causing bend on the railing.
ImmediateAction: Mooring master informed . Tail rope replaced with spare and passed to other vessel. Receiving ships crew advised to apply grease on fairleads regularly to reduce chaffing caused by vessels movement.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring tail damaged, Bend on ship side railing.
DirectCause: Vessels movement at anchor as both vessels were partly loaded caused chaffing on mooring tail ropes . Tension and chaffing caused tail rope to part .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pre arrival meeting : Crew should be advised to maintain equal tension on both own ship and receiving ships ropes. Chaffing guards to be placed correctly on ships lines. Safety - shore meeting:  Receiving ship should be advised to apply grease on their fairleads regularly to reduce chaffing.
ClosingNote: We concur with the cause analysis and the corrective action taken.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was safely berthed at Pier 9T IMTT Bayonne, New York Stbd side along-side moored with 3+2+2 mooring ropes Fwd and Aft.   At around 2030 hrs LT (23 Feb 2019) one large Container vessel passing close by. Vessel manned fwd and aft mooring stations and tightened the ropes. One of the vessel Stern line (Aft stbd winch aft drum rope) parted due to excessive vessel surge.  Although vessel’s mooring drum brakes are set for 60 % MBL of mooring ropes and mooring rope in good condition but due to sudden excessive surge vessel’s rope parted.
IncidentEventDetails: 0518: Commenced Mooring  0624: All Fast (3+2+2) Fwd and Aft  Various Large vessels passing by own vessel.  1412: Commenced Discharging  2030: One large container vessel passing.  Vessel manned fwd and aft moorings and kept tight all ropes.  One stern rope parted due to sudden excessive surge.
ImmediateAction: Vessel informed terminal loading master that one of the vessel's mooring rope has parted due to surge of passing by vessel. and enquired about stopping cargo discharging. Terminal advised to continue discharging as vessel is safely along side with other ropes made fast. Also advised to call agent to arrange for mooring gang for passing extra mooring ropes.   Agent was informed about same.  Port Control was informed about the incident and requested to pass large vessels at slow speed when passing own vessel.   Mooring gang arrived and vessel passed other rope
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope parted around 15 mtrs from eye
DirectCause: Caused due to pressure surge as a deep draft vessel was passing by at a close distance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Port control to inform Vessel of passing of Deep draft Vessel at a close range.  Additionally deep draft Vessel's to pass other Moored Vessels at minimum speed
Corrective Action: Port control to inform Vessel of passing of Deep draft Vessel at a close range. Additionally deep draft Vessel's to pass other Moored Vessels at minimum speed
ClosingNote: Vessel had identified the concern and other vessels' movements shall be monitored closely.

IncidentDetails: The vessel was in Lock-2 of Agua clara locks moored with 2 head, 2 stern lines and 1 spring line forward and aft. AB was operating mooring winch under supervision of duty officer as per advice of Panama Canal Mooring Master. As the lock was filling up with water, the crew were slacking the ropes as they came under tension. However, the mooring master was discouraging the ship's staff from slacking the ropes as the vessel was starting to shift aft. Since only 1 spring was fast, it came under a lot of tension and snapped. All men were standing well clear and no person was injured.
IncidentEventDetails: V/l transitting Panama Canal Southbound
ImmediateAction: Rope end to end changed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One mooring rope parted approx 10 metres from end.
DirectCause: Too much strain on one mooring rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To send out two springs during the next canal transit.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Crew training carried out on mooring operation at Panama Canal locks

IncidentDetails: Upon dep from Batangas, Philliphines Vsl noticed a possible pilferage occurance. Found the forward forepeak store entrance WTD lock arrangment tampered with and a total of 8- No's Fire hose nozzles and 5- No's Hydrant caps stolen by petty thieves. Upon immediate investigation, understand the last security rounds taken in the area by watchkeepers around ONE HOUR prior to lashing checks/square up close to cargo completion. As all deck hands were called for Dep lashing checks and other preperations, assume the pilferage was carried out at the same time. Also noticed, 1 fire hose box open on the P/S main deck (Seaside) which was indicative of the intention.     Vsl practice of securing all nozzles, hydrant caps, butterfly nuts prior to arrival China, Vietnam and Philliphines was a routine. A note indicating the location (FOREPEAK STORE AND LSA STORE) of the same also posted inside the fire hose boxes making the case more convinient for the petty thieves.    Since it was observed all crew busy with dep preperations, assume a hand-in-glove job of stevedores and small boats on the seaside which may have lead to the incident.
IncidentEventDetails: 20/0840LT - Vessel alongside at Batangas port.  20/0842LT - Commenced cargo operations.  21/2000LT - Final bay 40 loading in progress.  21/2030LT - Crew called for dep checks and stowaway search as per routine practice.  21/2100LT - All deck hands commenced lashing checks and square up on deck.  21/2118LT - Loading completed.  21/2130LT - All shore stevedores away.  21/2230LT - PILOT ONBOARD for departure/Crew st-by for stations.  21/2236LT - SBE  21/2306LT - Pilot Away.  21/2320LT - Bosun reported BT flaps/vents shut. Same time reported to bridge, fwd store lock arrangement tampered.  21/2340LT - Forepeak store and all other areas thorough inspection by Ch/Off and Bosun. Found 8-No's nozzles and 5-No's caps missing from the location. All other equipments found intact.
ImmediateAction: Thorough checks of all LSA/FFA equipment within deck and accomodation areas checked for avaliability. Aftpeak store inspected and found intact. LSA store on the P/S gangway area checked and found intact.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss of critical FFA equipment as below:  8-No's Fire nozzles and 5-No's fire hydrant caps (Brass materials which are a common item for petty thieves to steal).
DirectCause: All crew reportedly busy for pre-dep preperations and lashing checks/deck square up.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional preventive Master lock & Key system to be put in use at all China, Vietnam and Philliphines ports (At anchor and during port stay).  One crew member to be designated for security rounds at regular intervals even during vunlerable times (Such as departure time) and ensure security of Vsl not hampered.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Lack of security patrolling and surveillance.

IncidentDetails: Missing stud for anchor chain and Annemometer blades due to bad weather.
IncidentEventDetails: APRIL 19-20 - Worst bad weather days  APRIL 21 - Upon self check carried out, the stud for anchor chain and Annemometer blades were   missing.
ImmediateAction: Check for possible replacement spares onboard, reported to office and raised requisitions.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Missing one stud for anchor chain and blades for Annemometer.
DirectCause: Severe weather.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular inspection of chain exposed area showing any signs of crack or broken studs.
Corrective Action: Regular inspection of chain exposed area showing any signs of crack or broken studs.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting

IncidentDetails: On 30th march 2019, After Deslopping Operation, vessel anchored OPL fujairah for orders. Later on 31st March 2019,  Received call from Agent regarding vessel anchored in restricted area  at OPL, Fujairah
IncidentEventDetails: On 30th March 2019  0930LT: Departed "B" anchorage, Fujairah  1106LT: Vessel anchored @ OPL Fujairah, as per NTM 170  On 31st March 2019  1600LT: Received call from Agent regarding vessel anchored in restricted area at OPL, Fujairah
ImmediateAction: 1642LT: Vessel departed restricted anchorage area
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 30th March 2019, after Deslopping, vessel anchored at OPL, Fujairah. on 31st March 2019, Vessel received call from Agent regarding anchored at restricted area at OPL, Fujairah
DirectCause: No information received from the agent regarding the OPL retricted area.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Office to be informed about the retricted areas in OPL Fujairah and the same information to be circulated to other fleet vessels from the Office.
Corrective Action: Fleet circular issued.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: To avoid any such incident in the future all vessels to follow below 1. Plot manual user chart indicating the restricted anchorage area as per the attached NTM from Sharjah port authority and Port of Fujairah. This user chart should be displayed anytime the vessel is expected to call port of Fujairah or port of Khor Fakkan. 2. Agents should be consulted for any restrictions, if the vessel has plans to anchor outside port limits.

IncidentDetails: During routine inspection in the Steering Gear Room, ER Staff observed abnormal sound from No. 1 Steering Motor system.
IncidentEventDetails: - 17.02.19 : Based on previous available information, it seems an abnormal sound was heard from the No. 1 Sterring System whilst vessel was at San Nicolas Anchorage awaiting berth.   -  17.02.19 : No. 1 Steering System was isolated and No. 2 Sterring System put into use. Parts for repairs was ordered through the company for supply at anchorage.  -  19.02.19 : Vessel received necessary spares and repairs carried out, No. 1 Steering Gear System tried out and found satisfactory.    (P.S: The author was not onboard at the time of the incident, e&oe )
ImmediateAction: To avoid any further damages, the system has been isolated and after investigation, it was noticed that the bearing was partly damaged. No. 2 Steering Motor in use.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No. 1 Steering Gear System motor bearings partly damaged
DirectCause: 1. The Bearing had completed its stipulated running hours & had failed due to fatigue. 2. The Bearing was more than 5 year old & Hence was due for renewal 3. The PMS schedule was not reflecting the due renewal as same was not updated for running hours & renewal period
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To replace bearings with newly received spares and to be tried out
Corrective Action: Renew both steering gear Motor bearings
ClosingNote: Corrective action found adequate.

IncidentDetails: Stbd side bridge wing Life buoy losed over board
IncidentEventDetails: Crew are instructed to renew stbd side MOB Which is due for renewal  Removed the life buoy and the lifebuoy safely removed  Replaced the new MOB Marker  Trying to place the lifebuoy due to improper handling lifebuoy slipped from hand and went over board
ImmediateAction: Informed to office,nearest MRCC and safety mgs broadcasted in channel 16
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel losed one life buoy
DirectCause: Improper handling/failure to rig the lifebuoy
Proposedcorrectiveactions: In future if requried to work on the MOB,Decided to carry out only in port with superior officer's supervision
ClosingNote: The RA level 2 to be carried out for any work carried out on over side with permit and precautions to secure all loose items before commencing the job.

IncidentDetails: AE#2 was started and run in parallel with AE#3 after receiving 1 hour notice from bridge.Vessel  started manoeuvering with pilot onboard for berthing at Beira for discharge operation.Around 1730hrs LT , Sparks emission from AE#2 unit#4 exhaust joint observed with smoke.AE#1 started on MGO and taken on load . AE#2 to off-loaded. AE#2 changed over to MGO inorder stop.At that time Load on AE#1 shifted to AE#3 on its own. After few seconds AE#3 also stopped on its own. Emergency generator started automatically.Vessel restored power for all machinery by starting AE#1 and AE#3 immediatly and Manoeviring resumed.Due to above reason Vessel dropped stbd anchor at Beira channel to counter the move of the vessel and had called for tug assistance.
IncidentEventDetails: 1)AE#2 was started and run in parallel with AE#3 after receiving 1 hour notice from bridge.  2)Vessel  started manoeuvering with pilot onboard for berthing at Beira for discharge operation.  3)Around 1730hrs LT , Sparks emission from AE#2 unit#4 exhaust joint observed with smoke.  4)AE#1 started on MGO and taken on load .  5) AE#2  off-loaded .  6)  AE#2 changed over to MGO inorder stop.  7)At that time Load on AE#1 shifted to AE#3 on its own.  8) After few seconds AE#3 also stopped on its own.  9) Emergency generator started automatically.vessel dropped stbd anchor at channel and called for tug assistance.  10)Vessel restored power for all machinery by starting AE#1 and AE#3 immediatly and Manoeviring resumed by heaving anchor.
ImmediateAction: Vessel restored power for all Machineries by starting and running  AE#1 and AE#3 in parallel to resume Manoeuvering.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1)A gasket at AE#2 unit#4 exhaust connection failed during operation.  2) One or more valves in Generator FO return line suspected leaking.
DirectCause: Material failure due to ageing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to renew agsket for AE#2 unit#4 exhaust connection.  Suspected Leaking FO return valve to be replaced with new one.
Corrective Action: FO return valve to renew.
LessionLearnt: Gasket condition to be regularly checked.
ClosingNote: Gasket condition to be regularly checked.

IncidentDetails: While heaving up anchors on 13.04.2019 at 12 Mile Anchorage, New Orleans, vessel lost the Starboard Anchor along with some part of anchor chain
IncidentEventDetails: While heaving up anchors on 13.04.2019 at 12 Mile Anchorage, New Orleans, vessel lost the Starboard Anchor along with some part of anchor chain. Vessel had anchored at this location on 06.04.2019 at 1206Hrs LT using both port and starboard anchors.  The approach maneuver and anchoring was performed with pilot onboard and 1 tug in assistance. While at anchorage, the vessel maintained her position without any issues. On the morning of 13.04.2019, the Port Anchor was heaved up first which came up without any problem. However, the starboard anchor could not be heaved up after 2 shackles on deck. Vessel tried a few maneuvers under advise from the pilot and could heave the Starboard Anchor until up to 1 shackle in water when (at 0648Hrs LT) the anchor chain snapped and broke away. Estimate that vessel lost roughly 1 shackle and about 10 meters of chain along with the Starboard Anchor. There are no signs of damage to ship’s hull and the Windlass / associated machinery.  There has been no injury to any of the crewmembers or any other incident.   As per pilot, due to excessive rains in the northern part of the country, the river is flowing at levels much higher than usual with currents as high as 3-4 knots and because of this they have witnessed several such cases of loss / dragging of anchors. Even the agents (Southport Agencies) and Odfjell Operator (Mr. Maroje) confirmed the same.
ImmediateAction: Vessel's agents were informed( by pilot) who in turn informed the USCG. A quick decision was made that it was safer to proceed to next berth(Stolthaven Dock) where vessel could remain safely moored and avoid any commercial losses also. Initiated process for procurement of suitable anchor and chain through local agents. P&I club, Class and Flag state administration were also informed.
DirectCause: a. Direct Cause: - • The anchor chain parted under constant excessive forces resulting from the combined effects of high currents in the river as well as the use of engines to free it up from the river bed. b. Indirect Cause: - Prolonged Exposure to Adverse Environmental Conditions • Due to excessive rains in the northern part of the country, the Mississippi river was flowing at levels much higher than usual with currents as high as about 4~5 knots. • The level gauge at the time of incident was close to 16 feet, only a couple of feet below the flood mark. As a result, there was heavy silting all along the river which caused anchors / cables to get buried deep under silt, mud and debris thereby causing fowling of anchors and chains. • As the berth was not ready on arrival on 06th April 2019, the vessel had to remain at anchor in the 12-mile anchorage for a longer than usual period of almost 7 days. This resulted in additional siltation over the anchor cable and caused the cable and anchor to get further deeply embedded within the silt/mud. Inadequate Warning and Information • A detailed briefing was done with the vessel from office regards the special nature of Mississippi River anchorages. However, there were no local warnings on Navtex or VHF about High Water conditions or even an alert or recommendation from Pilots or Agents for vessels at anchor for longer durations and any preventive measures to consider such as heaving and re-anchoring. • Lack of information available at the company end due to which the concern was not effectively raised with the vessel where no particular instructions were provided to the vessel to pick up and re-anchor c. Root Cause: - Inadequately sheltered anchorage • The Mississippi River Anchorages are always exposed to varying Currents which can increase considerably due to macro factors in northern and central America such as melting of Snow in Mountain ranges and heavier than usual rainfall. • This puts excessive strain on the anchor and chain. Inadequate Assessment of Risk • The Mississippi River at that time of the year, was known to be at High River Conditions and same had been alerted to the vessel to ensure additional vigilance was put in place. • Under conditions of high current and heavy siltation, due to prolonged stay at anchorage, the hazard of anchor and cable settling deeper in the soft mud/silt was not identified adequately or addressed by the vessel and pilot. • Lack of information available at the company end due to which the concern was not effectively raised with the vessel where no particular instructions were provided to the vessel to pick up and re-anchor during prolonged anchorage. No attempt was made by the vessel to re-anchor periodically when berthing got delayed beyond the initial 1-2 days.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: When it is known that the mississippi river is high( meaning level gauge is above normal levels), the charterers to be notified of the potential risk of dragging / fowling of anchors. If anchored for long periods, charterers to be asked to arrange for shifting of anchors( under pilotage) every 2-3 days.
Corrective Action: When it is known that the mississippi river is high( meaning level gauge is above normal levels), the charterers to be notified of the potential risk of dragging / fowling of anchors. If anchored for long periods, charterers to be asked to arrange for shifting of anchors( under pilotage) every 2-3 days.
LessionLearnt: •The Importance of heightened degree of vigilance when anchoring in the Mississippi River. • Importance of pre-warning and alerts to the vessels prior arrival to assess the situation. This includes collecting data from local sources such as agents regards the current condition of the River levels. • Importance of pro-active actions by vessel to prevent deep entrenchment of anchors and cables, which is exacerbated by extended stays at Mississippi River anchorages and heavy current flows in high river water conditions. When needed, charterers to be notified to arrange pilots, and if needed, Tugs, and the vessel to heave and re-anchor. • Incident analysis within the industry to be reviewed for follow up action whenever the vessel call’s in the Mississippi River.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory

IncidentDetails: Stbd side Pilot Ladder Spreader Damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: 0800 : Master advised Fwd and Aft station to rig Stbd side Pilot ladder for Hongkong  pilot disembarkation.  0810 : One crew from Aft and one from fwd station were sent to rig the stbd side pilot ladder.  0820 : Crew member reported Stbd side Pilot ladder rigged and tested 2 Mtrs above water level.  0821 : Master advised the crew the stand by at the ladder as pilot would be disembarking shortly.  0842 : Pilot away.  0843 : Master advised crew members to secure the ladder.  0845 : Commence securing Stbd side pilot ladder.  0848 : Crew member from the stbd side pilot ladder reported that the spreader was broken on heaving.  0850 : On further investigation it was found that the spreader has jumped from the pilot ladder reel and came inbetween the pilot ladder reel and bulk head on which further heaving parted the ladder. There was already a crack on the spreader which resulted in breaking of spreader.
ImmediateAction: The spreader was removed and ladder was secured.  Crew were briefed to look out for the ladder getting jammed in between the ladder reel and the bulkhead area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pilot ladder spreader was completely damaged and not usable.
DirectCause: Pilot ladder was getting stuck in between the frame and reel of the ladder, resulting in damaging/chipping off steps/spreader.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Reel where the pilot ladder is secured have to be monitored while lowering/securing to avoid contact of ladder with the frame. At present the ladder renewed with the spare available onboard.
ClosingNote: On detailed inspection found that pilot ladder touches the frame when heaved up at higher speed as the ladder becomes slack, the weight is not adequate to keep the ladder on the reel.  In view of the above, pilot ladders are being heaved up slowly after the operation and one crew standby to clear the ladder accumulation while securing.  At present the operation is safe and all crew involved have been briefed regarding the same.

IncidentDetails: HYDRAULIC LINE FAILURE DURING MOORING OPERATION AT KOCHI
IncidentEventDetails: 0548 Hrs - SBE  0618 Hrs - Anchor aweigh (Port Anchor)  0648 Hrs - Pilot Onboard  0700 Hrs - Customs and Immigration on board  0712 Hrs - Aft Tug Fast  0720 Hrs - Fwd Tug Fast  0830 Hrs - Port winch Hydraulic line ruptured while proceeding to berth preparing mooring fwd. Mooring aborted.Hydraulic power stopped and oil contained within savealls. Clean up in progress  0900 Hrs - Ruptured pipeline removed from location transferred to E/Room workshop for repairs.  1200 Hrs - Repaired pipeline fitted back and tried out. Resumed Mooring operation.
ImmediateAction: Power pack for Hydraulic mooring winch stopped. Leak stopped and Oil contained within savealls cleaned up. Defective line removed from from location, repaired at E/room workshop and fitted back. Hydraulic motors the tried out and found in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 0548 Hrs - SBE  0618 Hrs - Anchor aweigh (Port Anchor)  0648 Hrs - Pilot Onboard  0700 Hrs - Customs and Immigration on board  0712 Hrs - Aft Tug Fast  0720 Hrs - Fwd Tug Fast  0830 Hrs - Port winch Hydraulic line ruptured while proceeding to berth preparing mooring fwd. Mooring aborted.No injury reported.  Power pack for Hydraulic mooring winch stopped. Leak stopped and Oil contained within savealls cleaned up. Defective line removed from from location, repaired at E/room workshop and fitted back. Hydraulic motors the tried out and found in order.  0900 Hrs - Ruptured pipeline removed from location transferred to E/Room workshop for repairs.  1200 Hrs - Repaired pipeline fitted back and tried out. Resumed Mooring operation.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis Direct Causes ? The Incident occurred due to localized external corrosion and weakening of the main hydraulic pipe section. Indirect causes ? Weak spots /corroded area that required immediate attention were not detected on pipelines during periodical PMS inspection.  Root Causes ? Onboard inspection as per PMS by ship-staff did not adequately cover the areas that are in the vicinity of winches and windlass due to access difficulty.  ? Onboard inspection by office team was inadequate as it was not captured in the routine vessel inspection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To renew adjoining pipe fittings on receipt of spares in both windlass and tested for integrity.
Corrective Action: Leaking pipeline to be removed and replaced with new pipeline.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: 2115LT – DURING CXRANE NO.3 HYDRAULIC SYSTEM WAS UNDER REPAIR BY THE SHORE LOCAL TECHNICIAN ASSISTED BY SHIPS STAFF  , CRANE NO. 3 JIB FELL DOWN HITTING THE STBD SIDE FIXED LOG STANCHION POST AND THE FOREWARD PANEL OF HATCH COVER (OPENED POSITION) CAUSING A DAMAGED TO CRANE NO 3 JIB AND SMALL PORTION OF FOREWARD PANEL OF HATCH COVER NO 4 (OPENED POSITION) ,NOBODY IS HURT.
IncidentEventDetails: 13 APRIL 2019  0448LT – VESSEL ARRIVED AT NELSON PILOT BOARDING ARE/PILOT ONBOARD   0606LT – VESSEL BERTHED AT PORT NELSON TO HER CARGO OF LOGS  0630LT-  0700LT – CARGO GEAR/LASHING MATERIALS INSPECTION CONDUCTED BY THE PORT AUTHORITY PRIOR TO COMMENCED LOADING  0700LT – VESSEL COMPLIED WITH ALL THE REQUIREMENTS/INSPECTIONS TO COMMENCED HER LOADING OPERATIONS/ VESSEL COMMENCED HER LOADING OPERATION  0755LT – RECEIVED A COMPLAIN FROM STEVDORES THAT CRANE NO. 3 WAS HAVING TROUBLE AND UNABLE TO LIFT THE CARGO / SHIPS ENGINEER ATTENDED TO CHECK/REPAIR THE ALLEGED ISSUE.  1230LT – VESSEL CE REPORTED TO MASTER THEN TO SHORE TECHNICAL MANAGEMENT THE STATUS AND THE RESULT OF TROUBLE SHOOTING ON CRANE NO. 3 / CONTINUED TROUBLESHOOTING BY SHIPS ENGINEERS AS INSTRUCTED BY SHORE MANAGEMENT TECHNICAL SUPPORT  1539LT- SHORE TECHNICAL MANAGEMENT CONFIRMED ARRANGEMENT OF A LOCAL ELECTRICIAN TO HELP WITH CRANE NO. 3 TROUBLE SHOOTING  1712LT – LOCAL SHORE
