ImmediateAction: The tape and zone bottle to be recovered from the 5P cargo tank at next possible gas free condition & Requisition made for new sampler.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sampling tape along with the bottom zone bottle assembly fell into 5 P Cargo tank.
DirectCause: Suspected Material failure of tape as newly purchased tape broken from about 10 CM from top connection inside sampler body.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: OEM spares to be only used.  Sampler to be inspected prior every use.
Corrective Action: The broken tape to be retrieved at the first  opportunity and ensure that all precautions are taken during every use. Raise requisition for a new tape and renew
ClosingNote: Noted

IncidentDetails: On 31st July 2019,at 1605 Hrs stevedores informed that there is damage cargo inside hold no-4.  Inspect the cargo and found that at the aft part of the hold adjacent to the bulkhead some cargo are black in colur and apparently in wet condition.Remaining cargo inside the hold is about 100 Mts.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed at berth no-2 at port of Jeddah on 24th july 2019/2248 Lt. Cargo operation commenced at 25th July 2019/0100 Lt.On 31st July 2019,at 1605 Hrs stevedores informed that there is damage cargo inside hold no-4.
ImmediateAction: P&I representative informed about the incident.P&I surveyor and Surveyor from glencore jointly inspected the cargo inside CH#4.As per them out of 100 Mts of cargo approx 10 Mts or less of cargo is in bad condition and  about 90 Mts of cargo seems to be in good condition.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: P&I surveyor and Surveyor from glencore jointly inspected the cargo  inside CH#4. After inspecting the cargo as per them out of 100 Mts of cargo approx 10 Mts or less of cargo is in bad condition and  about 90 Mts of cargo seems to be in good condition.
DirectCause: Suspect CH#4 ballast well valve and  manhole cover leakage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Test for Leakage from ballast well manhole and valve during hatch cleaning operation.  Continuous monitoring  of soundings of hold bilges/ballast tanks/ lower stool spaces.
Corrective Action: Test for Leakage from ballast well manhole and valve during hatch cleaning operation. Continuous monitoring  of soundings of hold bilges/ballast tanks/ lower stool spaces.
LessionLearnt: To be 100% sure that the manholes and the valves are tested prior loading and the valve is not operated after that till next hold ballasting
ReviewComments: Ships staff trained and cautioned.
ClosingNote: Tests were carried out effectively

IncidentDetails: Gangway sustain minor damage by bending inward.
IncidentEventDetails: 15 Aug. 2019  1800 - Mooring ropes tendered prior change of watch  2000 - Mooring ropes tendered due to some ropes slack and water proceeding to lowest tide at 2200  2005 - While crew tendering ropes two tanker vessel passing together causing our vessel to move away from berth of about 1 meter and roll over to stbd side back to port side were vessel alongside. During that moment portside gangway was heave a bit up, dragging out of the berth and while vessel roll over to port on its way back to its original position the portside gangway touches the edge of the jetty and dragging back the portside gangway causing it to bend inward.
ImmediateAction: According to OOW after tendering ropes they immediately heave up the gangway clear from berth. Give notice to Master and Chief Officer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Port side gangway sustain minor damage bend inward.
DirectCause: Umtimely and improper monitoring of mooring ropes that causing vessel moving away from berth and lead  to portside gangway damage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Give warning to crew involve and OOW to take particular attention to its sorrounding especially during the night. Awareness with tidal tables as discussed during safety meeting.
Corrective Action: Gangway deformation to be straightened using hot work and chain blocks
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Deformation straighten by ship staff

IncidentDetails: Detected hydraulic oil leak from no 3 hatch cover pipe on main deck, while opening hatch cover of no-3 cargo hold. Immediately the hydraulic motor was stopped.
IncidentEventDetails: While preparing the vessel for loading at Cargill berth, vessel was at anchor in mississippi river. Deck crew were opening the hatch cover once the drizzle stopped. and a minor leak was detected by the standby crew.
ImmediateAction: Reported to master over the walkie talkie. Hydraulic pump stopped.  Master activated the emergency alarm,Commenced collecting / cleaning all traces of hydraulic oil contained onboard.     1330 hrs lt : Initial notifiation done on +91 - 4443215551 , Mr. Nitin Kandwal.  1453-1500 hrs lt : QI notification done BRINA FORD.  1512 : LOCAL AGENT Nova Int'l Shipping Svcs MR Ms. Dawn Droddy UPDATED.  1515 : Charterer's protective agents Agri Port Services LLC Mr Guy UPDATED.    Transferred collected oil to waist oil tank and all cleaning material were collected and transferred to E/R for incineration.    Hotwork approval obtained from the office, Risk assessment carried out.  Leaked line section cropped and renewed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hydraulic oil leak from no 3 hatch cover pipe on main deck aft of No-3 hatch coaming.
DirectCause: Wear & tear, Oldage of the vessel. Incorrect operation of the hatch cover opening controls.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Slow operation of opening / closing of hatch covers. Controls only to be operated by designated persons who are already trained to do so.  Given job specific training to all deck crew on, how to correctly operate the hatch cover opening/closing controls without applying excessive stresses on the hydraulic pipelines.   Hatch cover opening / closing jobs assigned to specific crew members. Training to be documented and evidence uploaded for the closure, as corrective action.
Corrective Action: Slow operation of opening / closing of hatch covers. Controls only to be operated by designated persons who are already trained to do so.  Given job specific training to all deck crew on, how to correctly operate the hatch cover opening/closing controls without applying excessive stresses on the hydraulic pipelines.   Hatch cover opening / closing jobs assigned to specific crew members. Training to be documented and evidence uploaded for the closure, as corrective action.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Frequent rounds on deck during port watches
ReviewComments: Discussed and ships staff briefed.
ClosingNote: Ship staff briefed and discussion had

IncidentDetails: DURING PICKING UP PROVISIONS USING MID SHIP CRANE, ONE OVERSIZED PALLET FULL OF BOTTLED WATER GOT STUCK IN BETWEEN THE RAILINGS ON CROSS CATWALK AFT OF STBD MANIFOLD WHILE LOWERING THE PALLET ONTO THE CATWALK.  TO CLEAR THE PALLET, THE CRANE OPERATOR WAS SIGNALLED TO HEAVE THE RUNNER WIRE. AS SOON AS THE RUNNER WIRE WAS HEAVED, THE RAILING GOT BENT(DAMAGED).
IncidentEventDetails: 1306 : VESSEL BERTHED AT TB#2 BERTH, CAPE TOWN.  1324 : SHIPS GANGWAY DOWN  1324 - 1418 : SHIP SHORE KEY MEETING WITH LOADING MASTER.  1400 : STARTED PICKING UP PROVISION FROM JETTY, USING MID-SHIP CRANE.  1418 -1454 : TANK INSPECTION BY CARGO SURVEYOR.  1438 : WHILE LOWERING ONE OVERSIZED PALLET FULL OF BOTTLED WATER ONTO THE CATWALK, THE PALLET GOT STUCK IN THE RAILING.  1440 : WHILE TRYING TO CLEAR THE PALLET, THE CRANE RUNNER WIRE WAS HEAVED UP, RESULTING IN THE PALLET GETTING STUCK IN THE RAILING AND DAMAGING SAME.  1445 : THE PALLET WAS CLEARED OF THE RAILING AND LOWERED ONTO CATWALK.
ImmediateAction: THE CRANE OPERATOR TOLD TO STOP HEAVING. THE PALLET WAS LOWERED AGAIN AND CLEARED FROM THE RAILINGS.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DURING PICKING UP PROVISIONS USING MID SHIP CRANE, ONE OVERSIZED PALLET FULL OF BOTTLED WATER GOT STUCK IN BETWEEN THE RAILINGS ON CROSS CATWALK AFT OF STBD MANIFOLD WHILE LOWERING THE PALLET ONTO THE CATWALK.  TO CLEAR THE PALLET, THE CRANE OPERATOR WAS SIGNALLED TO HEAVE THE RUNNER WIRE. AS SOON AS THE RUNNER WIRE WAS HEAVED, THE RAILING GOT BENT(DAMAGED).
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TRAINING TO BE CARRIED OUT FOR CRANE OPERATION. ALL JOB TO BE CARRIED OUT UNDER SUPERVISION. ALL PERSONNEL TO BE BRIEFED REGARDING "TIME PRESSURE" NOT TO BYPASS SAFETY AT ANY TIME.
Corrective Action: TRAINING TO BE CARRIED OUT FOR CRANE OPERATION. ALL JOB TO BE CARRIED OUT UNDER SUPERVISION. ALL PERSONNEL TO BE BRIEFED REGARDING "TIME PRESSURE" NOT TO BYPASS SAFETY AT ANY TIME.
LessionLearnt: Safety takes precedence over any other requirement. Lifting equipment should be operated carefully under the supervision of responsible person.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Shared with the fleet.

IncidentDetails: While vessel was laden passage to Changxingdao,vessel encountered very rough weather due to  TRS Lekima in close vicinity of 350 Nm .Vessel was encountering following seas and heavy swell from the port quarter resulting in shipping seas in the midship area after the passage of the cyclone. When the weather improved and deck rounds were taken, observed that the protective bottom plate of No.3 Hydraulic box was bent and cut from the securing bolts.Upon trying out the VRCS pump vessel observed 2 leaks from the inlet line in way of deck penetration weld seams for ballast tank v/v's 731,733 (3S main & stripper)
IncidentEventDetails: 10-Aug-2019  0001-1800 Hrs :Vessel encountered very heavy weather and quatering seas  11-Aug-2019  0900 : Deck rounds  were taken, observed that the protective plate of No.3 Hydraulic box was bent and cut from the securing bolts.  11-Aug-2019  0900-1100 Hrs: All hydraulic boxes inspected only no 3 box found affected .  11-Aug-2019  1200 Hrs:Tried out the VRCS hydraulic pump andl observed 2 leaks from the inlet line in way of deck penetration weld seams on No.3 Hydraulic Box.
ImmediateAction: Removed the protective plate and inspection carried out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hydraulic Inlet line for WBT v/v's  damaged and cracked from weld seams at 2  places
DirectCause: Due to shipping seas ,the thinned down( due to corrosion ) plate of protective covering gave way and struck the inlet lines causing cracks on the weld seam for ballast tank v/v's 731,733 (3S main & stripper)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New Protective plates to be fabricated of adequate thickness and placed on the underside of No3 Box.All hydraulic boxes,supports and plates to be derusted and inspected for the condition of protective plates and renewed as required. Hydraulic line penetration area to the box to spot chip & paint during next ballast voyage.
Corrective Action: Defective section to be renewed
ClosingNote: Assigned CA duly attended. Pipeline for the VRCS inspected closely and the suspected weak sections replaced accordingly

IncidentDetails: On 08th April 2019, while Casting off from Berth no.5, Tanjong Penjuru, Singapore, Vessel’s main hydraulic line suffered a leak causing oil to spray on deck and jetty in vicinity of damaged pipe.    Vessel immediately initiated emergency SOPEP procedures and cleaned up the onboard spill immediately.    Vessel was safely unmoored and sifted to anchorage were further repairs were carried out to ensure the hydraulic system were back in operation.   There was no injury to personnel, pollution to sea or any other damage to equipment due to this incident.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel arrived at # 5 Berth, Tanjong Penjuru, Singapore on 08th Apr /0218 LT. Vessel conducted her loading operations of HSFO 180 CST from 08/0424 LT to 08th Apr / 0906 LT. At 1130LT documentation completed and agents booked pilot for departure from berth.   At 1312LT pilot boarded the vessel. Chief officer set the “Framo” pump and system pressure required for operation of mooring machinery and bow thruster. Tugs were made fast forward & aft and vessel started casting off his mooring line. Unmooring commenced with casting off headlines/stern line which was supposed to be followed by breast lines and spring lines forward & aft.    At 1320 LT vessel was holding on to springs forward & aft, when crew member witnessed hydraulic leak from the Framo main pressure hydraulic line near midship. High-pressure oil sprayed on deck and a small quantity sprayed beyond ship’s rails on to the jetty.   Mooring machinery on forecastle deck and bow thruster that was powered by the “Framo” system got incapacitated due to failure of hydraulic pipe. Vessel remained alongside the jetty with the help of spring mooring lines and tugs.   All operations were ceased immediately using Framo emergency trip and containment procedures were initiated for the oil spilled on deck. Emergency stations were called and SOPEP procedure followed.   Ship over-side was checked and reported that no visible spillage of oil was noticed at sea surface.  However, a small quantity of oil found sprayed on to the jetty in way of manifold.   Initial notification to Owners, managers, agents and MPA was made by master.   Vessel inquired terminal for requirement of any assistance in cleanup operation of jetty, same was politely declined by terminal representative.    Terminal requested pilot to hold vessel alongside until vessel is cleared by MPA. At 08th Apr/1430LT MPA official visited the ship side in a boat and took rounds around the vessel.     After initial review of the terminal surroundings, vessel was cleared to sail by MPA at 08th/1445 LT. Vessel departed at 1600 hrs LT and shifted to Singapore AWPA anchorage for bunkering.    Vessel sailed out of Singapore on 09th / 1600LT after completing of necessary repairs and bunkering operation.
ImmediateAction: At 1600 LT vessel unberthed from the terminal. On her passage from berth to AWPA anchorage, ship staff carried out necessary repairs on the pipeline in E/R workshop. At 1735 LT, shipstaff reinstalled the repaired pipe and hydraulic power was restored
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel arrived at # 5 Berth, Tanjong Penjuru, Singapore on 08th Apr /0218 LT. Vessel conducted her loading operations of HSFO 180 CST from 08/0424 LT to 08th Apr / 0906 LT. At 1130LT documentation completed and agents booked pilot for departure from berth.   At 1312LT pilot boarded the vessel. Chief officer set the “Framo” pump and system pressure required for operation of mooring machinery and bow thruster. Tugs were made fast forward & aft and vessel started casting off his mooring line. Unmooring commenced with casting off headlines/stern line which was supposed to be followed by breast lines and spring lines forward & aft.    At 1320 LT vessel was holding on to springs forward & aft, when crew member witnessed hydraulic leak from the Framo main pressure hydraulic line near midship. High-pressure oil sprayed on deck and a small quantity sprayed beyond ship’s rails on to the jetty.   Mooring machinery on forecastle deck and bow thruster that was powered by the “Framo” system got incapacitated due to failure of hydraulic pipe. Vessel remained alongside the jetty with the help of spring mooring lines and tugs.   All operations were ceased immediately using Framo emergency trip and containment procedures were initiated for the oil spilled on deck. Emergency stations were called and SOPEP procedure followed.   Ship over-side was checked and reported that no visible spillage of oil was noticed at sea surface.  However, a small quantity of oil found sprayed on to the jetty in way of manifold.   Initial notification to Owners, managers, agents and MPA was made by master.   Vessel inquired terminal for requirement of any assistance in cleanup operation of jetty, same was politely declined by terminal representative.    Terminal requested pilot to hold vessel alongside until vessel is cleared by MPA. At 08th Apr/1430LT MPA official visited the ship side in a boat and took rounds around the vessel.
DirectCause: Direct Causes: ? The Incident occurred due to localized external corrosion and weakening of the main hydraulic pipe section, on the spot that was resting on pipe support. ? Metal to metal contact due to wasted rubber element accelerated the fretting damage of the weak spot. Indirect Causes ? Weak spots /corroded area that required immediate attention were not detected on pipelines during periodical PMS inspection. ? During inspection, ship staff did not detect the missing rubber element in the bottom part of the resilient clamp. Root Causes ? PMS for hydraulic line job is generic and inadequate as it does not guide the user on the identification of weak points. (Lack of standards) ? Onboard inspection did not adequate cover the areas that are in the vicinity of crane due to access difficulty. (Lack of compliance) ? Onboard inspection by office team was inadequate as it was not captured in the routine vessel inspection (Barrier failure)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To inspect all resilient pipe clamps and renew rubber elements as required, if missing or wasted off. Same to be included in PMS job scope.   To make ship-board PMS more detailed for carrying out maintenance of all onboard pipes and their attachments, for the areas which are not easily accessible or densely populated especially area underneath cranes.
Corrective Action: Immediately check the condition of all hydraulic oil pipelines on deck and prepare a plan which should categorize all pipes into three categories as Critical /Concern / Satisfactory. Same to be shared with office and added in vessels defect list module in ship-palm.
ClosingNote: All CA's completed

IncidentDetails: Provision crane Hook was heaved up from poop deck, before releasing from the securing sling in Funnel C-deck.
IncidentEventDetails: 1630 LT : Provision Boat alongside  1631 LT : For picking up provision, crew started to prepare provision crane  1632 LT : Provision crane Hook was heaved up from poop deck, Without having to confirm clearanc of hook. crew member showing signals for Crane operator did not realize the hook being secured.   1633 LT : Securing sling (around 1 m ) got parted.
ImmediateAction: Stop operation and tool box meeting organized, briefed all crew about crane operation should be done with presence of signalling person.  crane signalling methods and importance of one man giving clear and standard signals – briefed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to securing sling attached to the crane hook.  Securing sling (around 1 m ) got parted.
DirectCause: not following correct procedure/tool box meeting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Familiarisation with equipment and usage  2. Use of company safety checklist for lifting equipment.  3.crane signalling methods and importance of one man giving clear and standard signals.  4. Operating crane clear of obstructions.  5. Safety precautions as per maker when using crane  6. Use of correct personal protection equipment.
Corrective Action: discuss in the SCMM. Please brief the crew to follow correct procedure while using cranes. The crane should be only operated when a person is guiding the user.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: While carrying out lowering and sprinkler testing of portside lifeboat , the propeller shaft bearing box was damaged.  The upper cover was thrown out and the teeth of shaft bearing was observed to be slightly damaged
IncidentEventDetails: After lowering Port side life boat and when it became water borne , engine was started for trying out sprinkler system.After successful sprinkler testing , the stopping lever of engine was pulled to stop the engine but could not stop even with several attempts.  2. On account of failure to stop from lever, engineer tried to stop it by pulling the fuel lever locally. The tank fuel valve could not be easily located because of smoke near the aft portion of lifeboat.  3. Officer's asked to retrieve the life boat as black carbon residues from engine and ship's crew started heaving portside life boat.  4. Meanwhile,engineer found the fuel oil tank valve and closed it to stop the engine successfully.
ImmediateAction: Stopped engine by shutting the fuel supply valve from tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Port side L/Boat engine gear box damaged.
DirectCause: Malfunction of fuel supply unit
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Egine to be repaired upon receipt of spare parts. Class & FS to be informed and despensation taken.
Corrective Action: Egine to be repaired upon receipt of spare parts. Class & FS to be informed and despensation taken.
ClosingNote: Vessel took dispensation for the period when lifeboat engines were in-operational. After rectification of the defect, flag was informed and SEQ certificate was reissued.

IncidentDetails: PARITNG OF MOORING LINES AT OJ 5 (IFFCO TERMINAL - Kandla) DURING VERY STRONG EBB TIDE
IncidentEventDetails: 1906 hrs LT : Vessel was ready to commence discharge (Master in CCR & Chief Officer on deck).  1912 hrs LT : Chief Officer reported vessel 2m off berth.  1915 hrs LT: Vessel 3m off berth, master at aft stations and Chief Officer at Fwd. Alarm raised and   Station were called For’d & Aft to control the situation.  1920 hrs LT : Vessel nearly 4~5m off berth and aft breast lines on excessive weight. Aft loose breast   line parted, Master on Bridge with cadet.  1936 hrs LT :  Manifold disconnected and chiksan arm clear off vessel.  1938 hrs LT : One tug assistance requested.  1954 hrs LT : Tug alongside for pushing.  2000 hrs LT : Ship’s MOT un-rigged and cleared from jetty.  2006 hrs LT : Requested 2nd tug and pilot.  2006 ~ 2030 hrs LT : Master taking all necessary steps to maintain position 5m off berth.  2042 hrs LT : 2 tugs assisting.  2100 hrs LT : No change in vessel position even by two tugs pushing from Port Quarter.  2118 hrs LT : Pilot on board.  2120 hrs LT : One tug made fast.  2120 ~ 2132 hrs LT : Pilot gave ahead kick and rudder actions which brought BOW in and resulted in parting of 08 more ropes (04 x Stern lines, 02 x aft breast line, one loose spring fwd & aft each).  2136 ~ 2142 hrs LT : Aft springs and fwd springs cast off.  2145 hrs LT : Fwd breast lines cast off.  2200 hrs LT : Unable to heave anchor.  2206 hrs LT : Unable to cast of head lines due to excessive weight.  2215 hrs LT : Vessel 50~100m off berth and maintain position.  2230 hrs LT : Vessel maintained position, suspect touched bottom as tug reported aft draft as 9.2m  2300 ~ 2330 hrs LT : Chief Officer and cadet taking soundings by lead line all around vessel.  2330 hrs LT : All head lines cast off / ERoom reported sounding verified for all cofferdams, tanks and   bilges – ALL NORMAL.  2335 hrs LT : Vessel inspection in ERoom bilges completed, No dent or indentations.   2348 hrs LT : Alcohol test conducted for entire ship staff. Thereafter harbour master boarded vessel and pilot away and anchor was heaved up.  0042 hrs LT : Vessel re-anchored off berth and awaiting suitable flooding tide for re-berthing.  0200 hrs LT :Parted mooring lines replaced with spare ropes and vessel ready for re-berthing.  0442 hrs LT : Vessel all fast / One tug standby for assistance and harbour master away. Thereafter master decided to observe vessel in next very strong ebb tide before connecting Manifold and rigging gangway.   0718 hrs LT : Excessive weight on mooring lines, crew standby and vessel again moved nearly 1.5m off berth despite of one tug assistance.  Despite of all above actions, vessel stern drifted off berth with excessive weight on moorings. Thereafter, it was decided to unmoor vessel at next available tide and proceed to anchorage.
ImmediateAction: All cast off and vessel anchored off berth.  Preparating of mooring lines for re-berhting vessel at next flooding tide
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 09 Nos mooring ropes parted.   (01 s loose Breast Fwd, 01 each – Loose spring & Breast Aft, 02 Nos breast lins fdrom MW and 04 stern lines)
DirectCause: Unsafe berthing conditions during Spring tides (Very strong ebb tide).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Terminal to have standby tug if berthing vessel’s during spring tide period.  Pilot on board for berthing to guide master on berth specific instructions during spring tides.  As a learning from incident, berthing in tidal ports should be avoided during spring tide.  Terminal to provide / pass higher strength / MBL mooring to avoid vessel drifting off berth.  Terminal chiksan to have QC/DC arrangement along with well documented procedures.
Corrective Action: Fleet Alert regarding the incident to be sent to all vessels.
ClosingNote: Detailed investigation into the root cause done. Lessens learnt shared with all vessels.

IncidentDetails: Around 2041 Hrs LT, HFO purifier No.2 abnormal alarm came. On inspection observed that Purifier amps were low and had some vibrations.  Observed that Worm gear and vertical shaft gear damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Around 2041 Hrs LT, HFO purifier No.2 abnormal alarm came. On inspection observed that Purifier amps were low and had some vibrations.
ImmediateAction: Purifier stopped and dismantled for inspection.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Purifier No.2  Horizontal shaft gear damaged.  Purifier No.2 Vertical shaft damaged.  Purifier No.2 Friction clutch damaged.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Need to identify the cause. Purifier routine maintenance running hrs needs to be amended basis on the cause analysis.
Corrective Action: Carry out the repairs at first opportunity and make keep the No:2 HFO Purifier in operation. Identify the cause for the damage.
ClosingNote: Refer to your incident analysis understood that bowl nut marks were not matching and nut found to be slack 3 cm. Basis above improper box up of the Purifier is cause of the incident.  In future always ensure the purifier overhauling to be carried out with one of the Senior engineer onboard either 2 Engg or Chief Engg.  It shouldnt be left alone to junior engineers onboard. Amended running hours interval for overhauling to be followed to avoid re occurrence.

IncidentDetails: PORT GANGWAY STOWING WIRE PARTED
IncidentEventDetails: 2-Sep-19/2130 lt - Port gangway swung out and prepared by Bosun and 2 x AB's prior arrival mooring stations with all safety precautions.    2-Sep-19/2148 lt - Vessel all fast.  Duty A/B from bridge proceeded to gangway to lower and check bollard clearance on jetty.    2-Sep-19/2149 lt - Duty A/B reported Stowing wire jumped over the sheave and parted.  Manual brake engaged on the runner wire by duty A/B.  Crew from fwd/aft stations arrived at gangway.  Stowing wire joined with bulldog grip.  Weight of the gangway taken on monorail using sligs and gangway lowered safely to jetty.      2-Sep-19/2212 lt - Pilot disembarked and agent boarded.  Vessel rigged the safety net and kept the gangway ready for stevedore use.    2-Sep-19/2330 lt - Completed renewing Stowing wire with new wire rope.    There was no delay claim on vessel due to above incident.  Cargo ops was planned to commence on 3rd Sept at 0100 LT.    3-Sep-19/0130 lt - Stevedores boarded and commenced cargo ops.
ImmediateAction: RENEWED THE DAMAGED WIRE ROPE
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: PORT GANGWAY STOWING WIRE PARTED
DirectCause: STOWING WIRE JUMPED OVER THE SHEAVE WHILE LOWERING AND PARTED
Proposedcorrectiveactions: THERE SHOULD BE ATLEAT 2 CREW MEMBERS DURING OPERATING THE GANGWAY
ClosingNote: Change to Near Miss

IncidentDetails: SOPEP Wilden Pump hose attached with plenty of patches and band due to leaks
IncidentEventDetails: During SOPEP Drill of bunker tanks overflow it was notice hose use in wilden pumps are with patches/band of rubber and rags. While testing by means water it was observed hose are with holes on it that create a leaks.
ImmediateAction: Check for previos requisition in ship palm and follow-up for supply.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: SOPEP wilden pump hose attached with leakages
DirectCause: Wear and tear
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replace hose as it is not acceptable condition if use

IncidentDetails: 2 Nos. Mooring Ropes chaffed and cut due to vessel ranging and surging at berth.
IncidentEventDetails: Berth length was not adequate to the vessel length, hence the mooring pattern was improper. The headlines and stern lines were of very short nature and were passing almost perpendicular to the vessel. Due to strong current from south to north vessel was ranging . The aft 2 nos. stern lines were passed from the drum leading from the roller fairleads towards the jetty. Due to the ranging and surging at berth the lines chaffed and got cut and were replaced. Additional lines were passed as required. LOP was issued to APM terminal for unsafe berth.
ImmediateAction: 3 Nos. loose ropes passed from the centre lead to replace the 2 damaged ropes one by one.  LOP issued to terminal for unsafe berth.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 Nos. mooring ropes damaged (cut about 15 m from one end)
DirectCause: Vessel not equipped with anti-chaffing mats / Unsafe berth as the length of the berth is not adequate for the vessel of LOA 183m
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to be supplied with good quality anti-chaffing mats/hawser sleeves to protect ropes.
Corrective Action: Vessel to be supplied with good quality anti-chaffing mats/hawser sleeves to protect ropes.
ClosingNote: All observations closed

IncidentDetails: Main engine stopped at sea  from 08th Sept  2200hrs LT to 09th Sep 0212 hrs LT to rectify fuel oil leakages in the high pressure lines leading from Fuel Oil Accumulator to Fuel Oil Rails. This leakage  while on LSMGO operation was caused   due to O rings which had become brittle & leaky. One pipe is leading from Fuel Oil Accumulator to the free end (forward) rail and other pipe is leading from the accumulator to the drive end (aft) rail. We removed both pipe assemblies, dismantled and renewed all oil rings. M/E put back in operation after that. Resumed passage and all satisfactory.  Since all O rings have  been renewed after this leakage, now there is no more any risk of any such leak happeniing again with new O rings.
IncidentEventDetails: At about 2130 on 8th Sept 2019, about 3 hours after commencement of sea passage after departure Oakland, duty engineer observed lagging of both high pressure fuel oil pipes, leading from Intermediate fuel oil accumulator to the Fuel Rail, soaked in oil (LSMGO). Immediately bridge was informed and main engine stopped to investigate the cause of this soaking. On removing the lagging of both pipes, leakage was observed at various joints of both pipes.
ImmediateAction: Main Engine stopped, fuel oil line isolated and 2  pipes from accumulator to fuel rail  removed. All sealing  O rings renewed and pipes refitted. Passage resumed at 0212 LT on 9th Sept 2019 - All Satisfactory.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Some O Rings were brittle & Leaky.
DirectCause: Leaky O Rings.Norecord of previous renewal.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All O rings in both high pressure pipes renewed. Requisitions raised to keep adequate stock of all Fuel Oil High Pressure Pipe O rings
Corrective Action: Vessel to keep sufficient orings
LessionLearnt: vessel should have sufficient spare orings
ClosingNote: vessel completed the job by stopping and changing the oring

IncidentDetails: Vessel at Douala, Cameroon port with Marsec Level 1 enforced and tightened security measures implemented. We had reviewed Security Campaign 004-2019 - Gangway Access Control and Guidance to prevent stowaways prior each arrival port. Deck crew were in double watch and no other works permitted other than security measures and port watch.  We had carried out stowaway search properly around the vessel with shore personnel team at berth and also vessel search teams were employed when vessel departed at berth as pilot away, seals, padlocks were intact and in place but still security was breached due to one stowaway found after 3 days.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at Douala, Cameroon port discharging rice in bagged, by the time completion of operation at 2350Hrs Lt on 16th of August 2019. I had carried out draft survey by going to shore with one draft surveyor and heavy rainfall at the time observing these 5 search team personnel on the gangway ready to commence stowaway search. I had advice to wait until cargo holds will be clear of plastic, pipes & paper garbage. While draft survey computation in progress by the surveyor (Vessels draft survey completed already) and some final tally reports attended at ship’s office with 3 surveyor’s and agent. At 0230Lt on 17th August 2019, agent advised to start the stowaway search around the vessel. I left the ship’s office leaving those surveyors to assist the stowaway search team from forecastle to aft. I observed these personnel were restless, sleepy and no personal flashlights so we let them borrow flashlights, they had posted 1 person on aft with the gangway up and confirmed no any stevedores onboard. They had arranged in separated team 2 person on forecastle and 2 started at forward station including mast. I had seen them thoroughly checking those unwanted areas inside forecastle including cargo holds booby hatches spaces. By the time we go crane no.1 with O/S Jacinto C. while to others were assisting in opening cargo holds, one shore team personnel assigned for cranes complaining that he wants to change his torch because the light is insufficient, I took that flashlight leaving O/S Jacinto C. and one personnel climbing up to Crane No.1, by the time I reached ship’s office those surveyors were asking my signature and ship’s stamp, thus I heard the report that crane no.1 and cargo hold no.1 were clear. As I returned on deck A/B John at Crane No.2 reported 1 person found sleeping in the cabin, and identified as Crane Operator. We let this operator disembark and continue the stowaway search, after searching forward one crew was posted as per stowaway search team leader. Normal routine followed as per vessels search checklist, each area was checked thoroughly and confirmed clear. By the time were coming aft part after checking garbage drums and stores I was again called for final signature of tally and draft survey reports while one stowaway search personnel going to E/R accompanied by E/R personnel. At 0340Hrs. Lt. Stowaway search was completed and shore search team disembarked, gangway up with 3 surveyors and 1 agent still onboard, continuous patrols by duty watch keepers with one person posted forward until pilot onboard. In campaign scope Serial No.7  - giving a security badge to each and every stevedores , crew failed to  follow due to large number of stevedore coming on board in and out including businessmen’s as vessel experiencing of losing badge in previous ports because stevedores were not handling and returning properly those badges given.
ImmediateAction: Inform all relevant parties, stowaway was investigated politely and P & I checklist followed as when vessel found stowaway on board including company’s instructions. We had carried out series of searches around the vessel for any other stowaways, drug and contraband search and confirm where the stowaway was hiding. Two crew was posted attending his daily needs. Brief all crew and conducted trainings in reference with ships SSP and gangway access control procedures, reminded to issue passes/badge to all visitors/stevedores coming in and out on board and log the same.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage or injury. There's a breach of security that failed ship's SSP and gangway access control including Vessels Search procedures.
DirectCause: Stevedores coming in out and vessel wasn’t properly monitored, number of badge only given to Visitors and same logged, due to large number of stevedores embarking and disembarking the vessel. Crew search team have trust in shore search personnel not taking extra and thorough searches to all unreachable and unwanted areas of the vessel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew were then trained and briefed properly in taking serious precautionary measures at each port by completely abiding all procedures laid out on ship's SSP and Gangway Access Control Procedures.  Maximum time and strict thorough searches not limited to all places listed on Vessels Search Checklist but also to those high unreachable and unwanted areas of the vessel.
Corrective Action: Crew were then trained and briefed properly in taking serious precautionary measures at each port by completely abiding all procedures laid out on ship's SSP and Gangway Access Control Procedures. Maximum time and strict thorough searches not limited to all places listed on Vessels Search Checklist but also to those high unreachable and unwanted areas of the vessel.
LessionLearnt: To identify locations of hiding and include in stowaway search checklist
ReviewComments: Discussed with staff and training carried out
ClosingNote: spot identified and closed

IncidentDetails: During loading operation  leak at manifold between pipeline and reducer.
IncidentEventDetails: 0400 Lt : Vessel all fast at IPP terminal berth 27  0400 – 0436 Lt : Key meeting  0400 – 0500 Lt : Tank re-inspection by surveyor   Vessel has one reducer of 10” of 28 mm flange thickness and other 10” reducer cannot be used due to lesser thickness . The reducer had pitting and the surface was made even by applying devcon steel putty before arrival and same was pressure tested prior berthing.  Vessel had 10” reducer connected to Manifold no. 4 for loading of UAN. During berthing pilot suggested to use no.6 manifold for loading same changed during pilotage as he said that by connecting into no.6 manifold we can lower our accommodation ladder . On reaching berth we found that the accommodation ladder can be lowered even by connecting to no.4 manifold .As no.4 manifold was last tank be loaded C/O requested we connect back to no.4 manifold .   0600 – 0630 Lt : Loading arm connected to no. 4 manifold , 10”  0630 Lt : Commence loading UAN   0642 Lt : Ceased loading due to leak at manifold between pipeline and reducer and same could not be rectified even after tightening  0642 – 0648 Lt : Loading arm disconnected  Vessel applied extra steel putty after disconnection and time was given for putty to dry up.  0812 – 0842 Lt : Loading arm connected to no.4 manifold , 10”  0842 Lt : Resume loading UAN
ImmediateAction: Loading stopped. Loading arm disconnected. Devcon steel putty applied to reducer surface to make the surface smooth.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage or injury.
DirectCause: Reducer surface not smooth.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Reducers to be landed ashore for machining of surface.
Corrective Action: The reducers face to be chipped and surface prepared.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: On 30th October 2018, whilst approaching Bonny River Terminal for berthing, vessel touched the concrete jetty on her port side at midship (port) in way of her draft marks. The vessel sustained damage due to the contact with the concrete jetty on her port side shell plating between Fr-129 and 130 near 8.5 to 8.8 meter midship draft marks  The size of the tear was of about 400 mm X 70 mm above the water line.   Vessel was shifted to inner anchorage and permanent repairs were carried out by authorized work shop in supervision of vessel’s class DNV/GL.   There was no injury to any personnel and no damage to environment.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0900 Hrs. LT Vessel aborted berthing as per pilot’s advice and awaited drifting around fairway buoy for further instructions from port control. Pilot advised vessel that he shall remain onboard until further notice.  1453 LT: Vessel commenced proceeding to inner anchorage with plan to anchor and berth next day morning. During channel transit, terminal confirmed that Tugs will be available and plan was changed to go ahead with berthing. But, plan to go alongside was changed to Port side.  1742 LT: Tug “Rajuno” made fast on the forward starboard shoulder using 1 ship’s line. At this moment 2nd Tug as about two miles from the vessel and approaching to vessel.  1750 LT: Whilst approaching closer to the berth, the forward tug line parted.  1752 LT:  Vessel allided heavily against the foremost Yokohama fenders on the jetty.  1755 LT: Vessel bow swung inward to the jetty, resulting in hull making contact with the concrete edge of the jetty, in way of the port manifold area.  1815 LT: Aft tug arrived at the Starboard break of accommodation and commenced pushing vessel in such a manner, so as to align the vessel with the berth.  1818 LT:  Vessel was aligned and positioned alongside the berth and mooring operations commenced.  1942 LT:  Vessel All Fast at Bonny River Terminal.  Below was the vessel’s track. Vessel turned around prior approaching the berth to go port side alongside.
ImmediateAction: Vessel departed from the berth without cargo operation and anchored at Bonny inner anchorage. Permanent repairs by an approved workshop were done under supervision of vessel’s class DNV/GL. After completion of the repair, the weld was vacuum tested. Magnetic Particle Inspection (MPI) was also done on the weld area. After satisfactory completion of the repair, vessel departed from Bonny.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Ship side hull breach on her Port side near midship draft marks between frame 129 and 130.
DirectCause: Root Cause:   1) Loss of situational awareness by Pilot. He missed to consider high lateral speed. 2) Lack of training on Bridge Team Management. Master had never been in such situations previously and all his previous pilots were reliable to his expectations, with safe berthing. There were no inputs from the Bridge Team members. 3) Pilot was mis-informed on the availability of the Tugs. 2nd Tug suspected to have a breakdown which caused delay in arrival. 4) Lack Of communication between Master and Pilot. The plan for approach and making fast the tug was not sufficiently discussed and recorded. 5) Lack of planning and arrangements by Terminal. Arrangements for the tugs as required by the Terminal Rules were not sufficiently made. Minimum required number of tugs (2 Nos.) were not kept ready in time leading to frequent change of berthing plan.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: • Fleet Alert regarding the incident to be sent to all vessels. • Training on Pilotage Navigation Procedures to be carried out for the Bridge team. • Master to be relieved for further briefing ashore. • A campaign on safe speed of approach to berth to be initiated for Fleet Vessels. • Lessons learnt from the incident to be given as input to the in house BTM course. Structure of the exercise should give emphasis on the Bridge Teams areas of monitoring and input required to be given to Master at the time of berthing and unberthing.
Corrective Action: Fleet Alert regarding the incident to be sent to all vessels.
ClosingNote: All corrective actions as identified during the investigation taken.

IncidentDetails: ROBBERY AT ANCHOR: that two robbers boarded the vessel while at anchorage at Kakinada (17°03.8’ N , 082°26.2’ E) and took away two nos. mooring ropes from the forward. A small wooden motorised boat was observed with another two  persons tying the loose end of the ropes.
IncidentEventDetails: 24TH SEPT 2019 ( below timings in local time, UTC+5.5)  ============  0037 : Deck watchkeeper took security rounds and reported to bridge -all in order, no breach of security.  0055: Deck watchkeeper saw two persons on board at the forward area removing the mooring ropes and lowering it to the boat and reported to duty officer on bridge  0056 : Duty officer raised emergency alarm   0056 : Deck watchkeeper reported that 2 robbers were throwing the mooring rope ends and then jumped into the water and took away 2 mooring ropes  0100 : Reported the robbery to port control on VHF Ch 14
ImmediateAction: 1.Raised emergency alarm  2.Reported to Port control on VHF  3.Reported to CSO over phone and e-mail  4.Reported to PFSO and agents by e-mail
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: two robbers boarded the vessel while at anchorage at Kakinada (17°03.8’ N , 082°26.2’ E) and took away two nos. mooring ropes from the forward. A small wooden motorised boat was observed with another two  persons tying the loose end of the ropes.    Below is the details of the incident:    24TH SEPT 2019 ( below timings in local time, UTC+5.5)  ============  0037 : Deck watchkeeper took security rounds and reported to bridge -all in order, no breach of security.  0055: Deck watchkeeper saw two persons on board at the forward area removing the mooring ropes and lowering it to the boat and reported to duty officer on bridge  0056 : Duty officer raised emergency alarm   0056 : Deck watchkeeper reported that 2 robbers were throwing the mooring rope ends and then jumped into the water and took away 2 mooring ropes  0100 : Reported the robbery to port control on VHF Ch 14
DirectCause: lack of proper lookout
Proposedcorrectiveactions: increased freuency of deck rounds
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting of this incident and noted the frequency of deck round was increased. Noted reporting to all shore authorities done.

IncidentDetails: 2 Mooring ropes while being passed ashore got sucked in by the bow thruster, ropes entangled in the thruster propeller.
ImmediateAction: BT system power turned off. Additional tug boat called for assistance & vessel berthed safely.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 mooring ropes stuck in Bow Thruster propeller & had to be cut & removed
DirectCause: 1. Unsafe Act – Incorrect use of Equipment or Machinery  Unless in an emergency the Bow thruster is not operated at full power specifically to the side alongside while the mooring line is being passed & when vessel is on ballast, till the mooring line is on the shore bitt & no slack in water. In our situation, eventhough the bow was swinging to starboard with thruster running at 50% to port, given the rate of swing & other conditions, it was not an emergency situation.   Master should have intervened & delayed the order to put thruster on full power to port till the mooring line is put on shore bitt.    2. Unsafe Act – Failure to ascertain the conditions/limitations thoroughly  When it was noticed that there was only one mooring crew man going to be handling the rope, given the fact that the vessel’s mooring ropes are very heavy & huge to handle, vessel could have passed the rope one at a time eventhough the mooring crew man & pilot insisted to pass two lines together.    3. Unsafe Act – Failure to fully appreciate the thruster load on two huge mooring lines.  Possibly the Mooring crew person let go the line due to the thruster pull on the floating rope & being only one person he was unable to handle the pull.     4. Unsafe Condition – Huge & Heavy mooring ropes  Mooring ropes on board are too big in size compared to the size of the vessel & are difficult to handle by ship staff & shore mooring crew.    5. Unsafe Condition – Bow Thruster protective cover/mesh opening is too big.  The protective mesh/cover for bow thruster is too big, vessel’s mooring line (57mm) which in itself is too big could easily pass through the opening in the cover/mesh.   Vessel’s forward draft at the time of incident was F/3.0m & A/5.8m, bow thruster was immersed about 80cm & still the rope was sucked in easily/immediately, almost without leaving no time for ship staff to react after the rope was let go by the mooring crew person.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Bridge team & Mooring crew team training to be conducted, cause analysis & corrective action discussed.   Briefing on Hazards & consequences.  Briefing on Limitations & conditions.    2. Bow thruster usage to be discussed in the on site tool box meeting that is conducted at mooring station before mooring/unmooring operation.    3. Poster “CAUTION - DO NOT OPERATE THRUSTER WHILE PASSING THE ROPES” to be posted at the operating panel & remote control.
Corrective Action: 1. Bridge team & Mooring crew team training to be conducted, cause analysis & corrective action discussed.  Briefing on Hazards & consequences. Briefing on Limitations & conditions.  2. Bow thruster usage to be discussed in the on site tool box meeting that is conducted at mooring station before mooring/unmooring operation.  3. Poster “CAUTION - DO NOT OPERATE THRUSTER WHILE PASSING THE ROPES” to be posted at the operating panel & remote control.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Shared with the fleet for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: slight damage to port gangway prior completing operations at Xiamen at about midnight of 26th Sept. Port gangway was positioned floating with weight on wire fall whilst alongside at Xiamen International container terminal due to the fact that the gantry was very close to the edge of the pier. When tide was falling close to mid-night, while duty officer was manning the gangway as duty ratings were engaged in plugging of reefers and attending to diving operation for bow thruster, the gangway went below the level of the jetty and touched the fender causing it to bend inward toward shipside. Although, the gangway was immediately picked up upon sighting this in a few minutes, it suffered from a slight deformation on inner side rail.
IncidentEventDetails: Refer to SOF by relevant watch staff
ImmediateAction: Gangway was picked up safely and checks carried out.  Immediate safety briefing was carried out after departure with all deck crew to prevent recurrence of such incidents in future. Importance of gangway vigilance explained to all crew especially where there is large tidal variation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Found Gangway Slightly deformed / bent on bottom of the sheave area. Visible cracks found almost on 20 steps on the welding seemp.
DirectCause: Due to negligance, lack of Awareness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Given Brief training to all crew and officers onboard.
Corrective Action: Please update if repairs needed and carried out.
ClosingNote: The gangway to be checked regularly in port with due consideration of tides & loading patterns. The gangway inspected in dock and deflection reports as attached.

IncidentDetails: during night time when taking fire and safety round. Found rice kuker in plug
ImmediateAction: immediately unplug , inform morning time head of galley department
Corrective Action: Please discuss this Near Miss with all staff and ensure that this unsafe condition does not happen again.

IncidentDetails: At 0930 during the routine weekly cargo hold visual inspection observed heavy fuel oil layer on tank top of cargo hold no.4, suspecting oil leaked from the port side manhole of fuel oil tank no.5 , which was recently open & inspected and after that same tank was filled 77%  during last bunkering in Hong Kong on 19th Sep’19.    - On investigation found that after #5P fuel tank inspection either the man hole is not evenly tightened or gasket hardened.  - Spilled oil inside the C.H.4 well below the container ISO socket and approx. 1-2cm layer & approx 0.5 to 1 mt.  - Containers (cargo) inside cargo hold not been affected due to the spillage.  - Cargo inside the cargo hold no.4 loaded in Hong Kong and discharge port is Melbourne.  - Cargo hold bilge soundings taken on daily basis. And as per visual inspection stbd side bilge is fully dry and port side bilge having oil but well below the striker plate.  - Oil sleek also seen on mid center part of the cargo hold and sides found dry.  - Last cargo hold visual inspection was carried out on 21st Sep’19 and daily sounding been taken.  - As per initial investigation no.5 (P) manhole was not inspected during or after bunkering.  - Vessel was started heating tank on 23rd Sep’19 and start consuming no.5 (P) FOT on 24th Sep evening.
IncidentEventDetails: On investigation found that after #5P fuel tank inspection either the man hole is not evenly tightened or gasket hardened.
ImmediateAction: -Transferring of oil from #5P and cleaning of spilled oil.  -Cleaned all the spillage around the manhole & other adjacent area.  -Port side bilge cleaned with diesel and eco cleaning material.  -Increased trim by taking ballast in AFT (all stability parameters under control) to drain the possible oil from below the containers.  -Used absorbent pads, booms, rag and sawdust for cleaning.  -Boom arrangement made around the CH bilges to avoid any further oil going inside the bilge.  -Notice posted “Do not operate” on cargo hold bilge console to avoid any accidental operation of CH bilge system.
LessionLearnt: Tasks carried out on oil transfer /oil storage systems to be supervised by senior staff adequately, keeping possible consequences in mind
ReviewComments: None

IncidentDetails: Black out during load testing of No.4 A/E,which caused gyro power failure and required 4-5 hrs for settling down.Vsl could not join the convoy as per schedule,however joined convoy later without any delays after gyro settled down.
IncidentEventDetails: AE no.4 load performance test was in progress.    A.E No.4 did not have sufficient load to carry out pre scrubber installation test at 75%(2850KW) and 85%(3250KW), as sea load was only 2200KW during the passage from TPP to Suez. No.4 AE performance test at 75% and 85% was carried out at Suez Anchorage after F.W.E 27th/2212.    On 27th/2330,load was increased on No.4 AE to 75%(2850KW) with both thruster motors running idle with additional sea water pumps. Performance test carried out without any problem.To increase the load on AE to 3250KW (85%), following motors were run in addition to normal sea load.  1.Both the thruster’s motors were run at zero pitch (Interlock bypassed).  2.M.E was kept in standby mode and Aux blowers were run.  3.No.1 and 2 Ballast pump (sea to sea),   4.No.2 Fire Bilge ballast pump.  5. No.1 and 2 CSW pumps.  6. Main Air compressor and  7. Cargo hold fans.    No.3, 2 and 4 AE were running in parallel while increasing the load on A.Es to 3250KW. After achieving the desired load No.2 and 3 A.E was offloaded and No.4 A.E was running at 3250KW.    Performance test was carried out at 85%(3250KW) without any issues from 27th/2330-28th/0015    Bridge was informed to switch off the thrusters and sea water pumps.At 00:34 when thrusters were switched off  black out happened instantaneously without any alarm or warning.    All stand by AEs failed to come on load due to ‘Start Inhibit” status on HIMAP BCG. At 00:35 after 45 sec, Emergency Gen came on load, and immediately No.1 AE DO pump was started and all AEs fuel oil inlet side changed over to LSMGO.Tried starting AEs from MSB in manual mode after pressing the acknowledge key at HIMAP, but start inhibit status was still ON and unable to start the A.E.     Tried to start the AEs from Local but could not start.    No.4 AE’s Lub Oil priming pump’s power change over switch was changed to emergency and started the priming pump and A.E No.4 was started, found starting without any issue. No.4 AEs VCB was closed and normal power was established by 00:44.    Emergency generator was offloaded and stopped at 01:00 hours.    After Black out it was observed both Gyro's was taking time to settle down.Gyro is supplied by 220V and DC24V power source during a black out until Emergency Gen was on load.The 220V as well as DC 24V source was not available due to poor condition of General service emergency battery. Also during the Blackout condition the telephone system was found dead, fire control panel power source fail alarm was active. All these loads were supplied by General service battery. It took about four hours for Gyro to settle down.
ImmediateAction: No.4 AE’s Lub Oil priming pump’s power change over switch was changed to emergency and started the priming pump and A.E No.4 was started, found starting without any issue. No.4 AEs VCB was closed and normal power was established by 00:44.Manual of gyro consulted and found it takes around 4-5 hrs for settling down procedure.Same conveyed to office and assisatance requested.  Compared gyro/magnectic heading and found a difference of 5 deg.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL

IncidentDetails: One mooring wire and one rope tail parted forward whilst moored alongside during STS operations.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging whilst alongside the MT Portman at the STS anchorage in Lagos, Nigeria.  Due to the moderate swell (2-3 m) and sudden gust of winds (upto 25-kts), some strain came over the forward headlines. This caused one mooring wire and one tail to part.
ImmediateAction: The rope tail was replaced with a spare. The mooring wire was replaced with a spare loose fibre rope.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One mooring wire and one rope tail parted during STS operations. No injury or other damage to vessel.
DirectCause: 1. Moderate swell causing both vessels to roll putting strain on the head lines.  2. Sudden intermittent gusts of wind upto 25 kts  3. Age of the mooring wires and rope tails.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. New rope tails have been supplied to the vessel and same are being installed.  3. Office is in the process of renewing all wire ropes in phases. same to be completed by year end.
Corrective Action: Noted, kindly take due cautioue while doing STS at Dakar. Pls brief all crew memebers about the same & carry out proper RA in this regard for Mooring in Dakar. Pls paste the pictures of the mooring tail renewed & Wire ropes
ClosingNote: Vessel to be more alert during any change of swell condition during STS operation.

IncidentDetails: At 2210 HRS LT on 03 Oct 2019 Upon completion of cargo, vessel surged due to passing of a large vessel which caused the shore gangway to  damage the vessel railing about 2.0 m in length.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had completed loading and cargo line push was being carried out into 5W COT. Gangway was manned by ship crew and was placed fwd (abreast No 2 COT).Two other crew members were at manifold assisting in the cargo line push. At around 2210 LT a large vessel passed close to own vessel in high speed which caused the vessel to surge. The shore gangway damaged the ships railing of around 2.0 m. No damage to shore gangway or injury to crew members.
ImmediateAction: Terminal was contacted and shore gangway was lifted from main deck to assess the damage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage the vessel railing about 2.0 m in length. No injury to crew members, No damage to shore gangway.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:   The Aframax product tanker MT BW KALLANG was transiting at 9.0Kt in the channel. As noted in ECDIS playback BW Kallang did not reduced her speed while passing close to Albetroleya, Owing to the fact that the banks were close to the channel and there was not room for the displaced water to escape sideways, the water displacement caused by the large vessel moving ahead at high speed was considerably large and these bow waves arrived at the location where Albetroleya was berthed. This caused Considerable surging and movement of vessel at berth.    INDIRECT CAUSES   The vessel mooring pattern was 4+2+2 forward and aft. Considering the danger posed by vessels passing by in the channel, there was no additional headlines or spring lines considered for the mooring as the vessel would be prone to movement whilst other vessels passed close by in the channel.   ROOT CAUSES Lack of standards – No Reduction of speed by the passing aframax tanker causing excessive surge movements, vulnerability of the berth layout, no additional mooring lines laid by vessel to counter the excessive loads caused by the fore and aft surge waves.   Lack of compliance - Placement of shore gangway above ships railings, which are neither collapsible nor strengthened.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew training on monitioring crew moorings and safe gangway watch.
Corrective Action: Damaged section of Ship-side railings to be renewed

IncidentDetails: WHILE CARRYING OUT GREASING OF THE BOX HOLDING THE MOB MARKER THE ROPE GOT ENTANGLED WITH THE BOUYANT SMOKE SIGNAL AND ACCIDENTAL RELEASE OF THE SAME OCCURRED.IMMEDIATELY THE SMOKE SIGNAL WAS CONFINED TO A CORNER AND BY USE OF WATER WAS CONTAINED ON BOARD.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL WAS ALONGSIDE AT NINGBO AND WHILE DOING ROUTINE MAINTENANCE OF LSA/FFA ITMES THIS ACCIDENT HAPPENED.
ImmediateAction: THE SMOKE WAS CONTAINED ON BOARD AND ANY FURTHER DAMAGE DUE TO ANY FIRE HAZARD WAS ALSO ELIMINATED BY USE OF WATER AS EXTINGUISHING MEDIUM.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MOB MARKER SMOKE SIGNAL WAS DAMAGED DUE TO ACCIDNETAL RELEASE
DirectCause: AS THE BOUYANT SMOKE SIGNAL WAS A SMALL CONTAINER NOT ATTACHED TO THE S.I. LIGHT WHICH IS NORMALLY THE CASE AS BOTH ARE ALWAYS USED AS A COMPOSITE UNIT SO AS TO AVOID ANY ACCIDENTAL RELEASE OF THE SMOKE SIGNAL. ON BOARD THE SMOKE SIGNAL UNIT  GOT ENTANGLED WITH THE RELEASING ROPE OF THE FULL SYSTEM AND IN CLEARING THE SAME THE ACCIDENTAL ACTIVATION OF THE SMOKE SIGNAL OCCURED WHICH COULD HAVE BEEN AVOIDED IF IT WAS A COMPOSITE UNIT WHICH IS SEPERATELY STOWED IN A SAFE LOCATION KEEPING IT WELL CLEAR OF THE RELEASING  ROPE AT ALL TIMES AND IS EASY TO HANDLE AS IT IS ONE UNIT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: COMPOSITE UNIT TO BE INSTALLED ON BOARD AT ALL TIMES SO AS TO AVOID SUCH OCCURENCES.
ClosingNote: RA with above hazards to be included when carrying out above job

IncidentDetails: Vessel at Douala, Cameroon port with Marsec Level  enforced and tightened security measures implemented. We had reviewed Security Campaign 004-2019 - Gangway Access Control and Guidance to prevent stowaways prior each arrival port. Deck crew were in double watch and no other works permitted other than security measures and port watch. We had carried out stowaway search properly around the vessel with shore personnel team at berth and also vessel search teams were employed when vessel departed at berth as pilot away, seals, padlocks were intact and in place but still security was breached due to one stowaway found after 3days.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at Douala, Cameroon port discharging rice in bagged, by the time completion of operation at 2350Hrs Lt on 16th of August 2019. I had carried out draft survey by going to shore with one draft surveyor and heavy rainfall at the time observing these 5 search team personnel on the gangway ready to commence stowaway search. I had advice to wait until cargo holds will be clear of plastic, pipes & paper garbage. While draft survey computation in progress by the surveyor (Vessels draft survey completed already) and some final tally reports attended at ship’s office with 3 surveyor’s and agent. At 0230Lt on 17th August 2019, agent advised to start the stowaway search around the vessel. I left the ship’s office leaving those surveyors to assist the stowaway search team from forecastle to aft. I observed these personnel were restless, sleepy and no personal flashlights so we let them borrow flashlights, they had posted 1 person on aft with the gangway up and confirmed no any stevedores onboard. They had arranged in separated team 2 person on forecastle and 2 started at forward station including mast. I had seen them thoroughly checking those unwanted areas inside forecastle including cargo holds booby hatches spaces. By the time we go crane no.1 with O/S Jacinto C. while to others were assisting in opening cargo holds, one shore team personnel complaining that he wants to change his torch because the light is insufficient, I took that flashlight leaving O/S Jacinto C. and one personnel climbing up to Crane No.1, by the time I reached ship’s office those surveyors were asking my signature and ship’s stamp, thus I heard the report that crane no.1 and cargo hold no.1 were clear. As I returned on deck A/B John at Crane No.2 reported 1 person found sleeping in the cabin, and identified as Crane Operator. We let this operator disembark and continue the stowaway search, after searching forward one crew was posted as per stowaway search team leader. Normal routine followed as per vessels search checklist, each area was checked thoroughly and confirmed clear. By the time were coming aft part after checking garbage drums and stores I was again called for final signature of tally and draft survey reports while one stowaway search personnel going to E/R accompanied by E/R personnel. At 0340Hrs. Lt. Stowaway search was completed and shore search team disembarked, gangway up with 3 surveyors and 1 agent still onboard, continuous patrols by duty watch keepers with one person posted forward until pilot onboard.
ImmediateAction: Immediately call relevant parties, stowaway was interrogated search and health checked, actions to be taken when stowaway found as per P & I followed, series of searches carried out around the vessel including unwanted/unreachable areas. Posted two crew outside his cabin door.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIl
DirectCause: Visitor Pass was not issued to Stevedores due to large no of Stevedores boarding vessel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Visstor passes will be issued to all persons boarding vessel.
ClosingNote: Invalid report

IncidentDetails: One of the stern mooring rope almost parted due to chafing while vessel was along side Nelson Mandela Pier, Oryx Iberia Sau terminal, Las Palmas for Cargo Discharging operation.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was all fast at Nelson Mandela Pier, Oryx Iberia Sau Terminal, Las Palmas for Discharging Operation on 18 Oct 219/2224 LT (Mooring Config: Fwd- 4+2 / Aft- 4+2).  At times Vessel was surging excessively due passing traffic as the berth was located at the terminal entrance.  Constant monitoring / tending of Moorings was being carried out. All lines were being greased at the vessel's closed chocks to prevent chafing/damage to ropes.  One of stern mooring Line almost parted at 1500 Lt/19 Oct 2019, owing to above but vigillant crew noted and slackenit, before it could part completely.   At the time of incident, Vessel had completed Cargo Discharging operation at 1330 Lt/19 Oct 2019 and documentation was in progress.   No injury occurred. No other damage to ship structure or jetty installation.  A Letter of Protest was issued to the Terminal.
ImmediateAction: The terminal was informed and permission taken from Loading Master for passing additional mooring rope.  1525 LT: Another Mooring Rope was made fast by the ship's crew in lieu of broken stern line.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was all fast at Nelson Mandela Pier, Oryx Iberia Sau Terminal, Las Palmas for Discharging Operation on 18 Oct 219/2224 LT (Mooring Config: Fwd- 4+2 / Aft- 4+2).  At times Vessel was surging excessively due passing traffic as the berth was located at the terminal entrance.  Constant monitoring / tending of Moorings was being carried out. All lines were being greased at the vessel's closed chocks to prevent chafing/damage to ropes.  One of stern mooring Line almost parted at 1500 Lt/19 Oct 2019, owing to above but vigillant crew noted and slackenit, before it could part completely.   At the time of incident, Vessel had completed Cargo Discharging operation at 1330 Lt/19 Oct 2019 and documentation was in progress.   No injury occurred. No other damage to ship structure or jetty installation.  A Letter of Protest was issued to the Terminal.
DirectCause: Surging of vessel being located at Terminal enytrance, due to regular traffic movement in the channel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Chafe Guards to be used at the chocks to prevent chafing / abrasion on the mooring ropes. Requisition no. SPR/PRJA/D/19/046 raised for Chafing Sleeves.
ClosingNote: The damaged rope removed from service. New rope replaced with the damaged one. vessel has total 11 spare ropes on board.

IncidentDetails: While cooking, a small amount of oil splashed out of the cooking vessel onto the hot plate. The spilled oil immediately gave rise to small fire.
IncidentEventDetails: Chief cook was preparing lunch as usual and during the preparation small amount of oil spilled on to the hot plate which immediately caught fire.
ImmediateAction: flame was instantly extinguished with the Fire blanket and the DCP Extinguisher in the galley.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was no damage to Ship's property and equipment. The equipment used for extinguishing the fire was Fire Blanket and DCP Fire extinguisher.   1. The Fire blanket which was covered on flame on the hot plate got burn marks, same is discared and replaced with new Fire blanket, as can be seen in picture attached.
DirectCause: To deep-fry the onions, Cook had cut the onions and kept in deep freezer in the morning. The Oil was hot, and onions were frozen. As soon as Cook put the frozen onions in Sauce pot containing hot Oil, the water content of the onions reacted with hot oil resulting in splash. This lead to small amount of oil spilling on hot plate and resulting in small flame.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Following procedures to be followed to ensure NIl recurrence of such incident:  1- Onions not to be frozen when using the same for deep frying.  2- Onions to be cut fresh before use  3- To use bigger frying pans for frying / deep-frying  4- To avoid putting open oil during vessel's rolling/ rough weather (Although, vessel was not rolling in this incident, same to be followed to avoid recurrance.  5- To reduce deep-frying items from Menu to minimum
Corrective Action: Following procedures to be followed to ensure NIl recurrence of such incident: 1- Onions not to be frozen when using the same for deep frying. 2- Onions to be cut fresh before use 3- To use bigger frying pans for frying / deep-frying 4- To avoid putting open oil during vessel's rolling/ rough weather (Although, vessel was not rolling in this incident, same to be followed to avoid recurrance. 5- To reduce deep-frying items from Menu to minimum
LessionLearnt: Frozen food should not be deep fried in oil so as to avoid splash of oil due to presence of water in it when using the same for deep frying. Food stuff to be freshly cut for deep frying.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Shared with the fleet for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: While picking up Pilot's baggage , heaving line parted and baggage fell in water between the pilot boat and vessel .
IncidentEventDetails: Pilot boarding arrangement of Combination Ladder , 2.5 m above water level was rigged on port side to receive Haldia Pilot at Waypoint no.09 of Haldia Approaches .  Pilot boat approached port quarter towards the pilot boarding area .  Deck crew comprising of Bosun , Ordinary Seaman and Duty officer was stationed to receive pilot .  After Pilot baorded the vessel , deck crew lowered the heaving line to pick up pilots baggage . Pilot boat crew made fast two bags of pilot in series ,after which the bags were heaved by crew present .  While heaving up the heaving line was getting abrased along the ship's railing . While the bags were in hand reach the deck crew member reached out to get a hold of the bag , when the heaving line parted and it fell in the water .
ImmediateAction: Pilot continued on his way to the bridge and informed Sagar VTS about the incident . Pilot boat crew retrieved the bags from water and same was again picked up by ships crew one bag at a time .  A gantline was used instead of a heaving line this time to heave the baggage . Pilot bag contents were checked and found that some articles were wet due to seepage of water .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1) Parted Heaving Line   2) Pilot's Laptop ( Dell Laptop Model T02H , Dpin : X1V12A01DPC , Sr. No.ESD – 1511632C ) which did not start up by Power Button .   3) Pilot's AIS Wifi ( Pilotstech ) SrNo.AW2016 was found not working in AIS Mode .
DirectCause: Improper technique used to heave the baggage , Excessive weight on the heaving line in use , Abrasion of the ropes in use with the ship's railing , Regular inspection of the ancillary ropes in use .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renewal of Heaving line , Discontinue use of heaving line and use gantline in its place , Heavy bags to be heaved one at a time , Inspection of ropes before use .
ClosingNote: Discontinued the use of heaving line near pilot point/embarkation point. Gantline is used henceforth.

IncidentDetails: Bolts sheared off for No. 10 Unit High pressure Lube oil pipe - resulting in splashing and spillage of the oil.     After Repairing of the HP hose, ME resume at full speed and onserved Scavange fire for Unit No. 10.
IncidentEventDetails: 0050 Duty Engr observed splashing of oil from Unit No.10. And immediately informed bridge to reduce the M/E RPM (while engine was running at Full speed 80RPM).     0052 - Vessel commence reducing RPM     0100 - Main Engine Stopped. @ Posn. 34°08.6"N, 133°14.2"W    0110 - M/E Revs stopped to Zero    0100-0110 - All Engineers in the Engine room.    0115 - All Deck crew in the Engine room to assist cleaning the spillage.    0115 -0252 - Observed that No.10 Unit L.O High Pressure pipe (Bottom) all bolt sheared off and resulted in aggressive L.O spalshing/spillage.Subsequently necessary repairs (by removing the damaged bolts from the body and pipe fitted with new bolts) and cleaning carried out near the immediate area enabling to restart the main engine and resume the sea passage.    0254 - Vessel resume at slow speed. @ Posn. 34°06.4"N, 133°09.4"W    0330 - Vessel on full 80 RPM.    while vessel resumed her passage and was at 80 RPM from 0330-0400Lt, Please find below sequence of events:    @0400lt E/R informed bridge to reduce RPM as suspected Scavange fire as flame noticed (By crew cleaning the spilled oil nearby) in the Scavange drain line filter leading to bilge sludge tank. Same confirmed with the rising temp. of No. 10 Unit under piston space temperature 58°C while other units were maintaining 46°C    @0400Lt - Vessel commenced reducing immediately     @0406 - M/E stopped @ Posn.34°00"N, 132°53.8"W.    @0418 - RPM came to zero.    Subsequently following checks carried out from 0418-0600lt:  -----------------------------------------------------------  a) Vessel opened steam smoothering to the unit No. 10 Underpiston space and waited for 10 mins checked the temperature - Temp. found normal.    b) Under piston space door opened and inspected (after keeping necessary fire precautions in the near vicinity) - found burnt carbon particle.    c) Under piston space was then cleaned and inspected.    d) Piston and liner inspection carried out - found satisfactory.    e) Crankcase inspected for No. 10 Unit - found satisfactory.    All above reported through telcon for necessary guidance from the office for not being able to identify the cause of the same.    Vessel was guided to proceed at slow speed and subsequently to increase gradually while close monitoring the exhaust temperature and under piston space temperature.    @ 0600lt in posn. 33°57.1"N, 132°37.4"W vessel resumed at slow speed. At 50 RPM checked No. 9 and 10 unit performance was taken and found satisfactory and aligning. Vessel continue to gradually increased RPM to 80 by 0718lt - found all parameters normal.
ImmediateAction: 1) For High pressure hose shearing off - Vessel stopped and emergency alarm sounded and all crew engaged in cleaning of the spillage.  2) For Scavange fire - Vessel stopped immediately and steam smothering used and scavange inspected.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bolts sheared off for the High Pressure L.O pipe for No. 10 Unit  Exhaust Valve - Resulted in Oil Splashing/ spillage and delay at Sea for abt 3.8 hrs
DirectCause: Vessel continously running at high load to preserve her ETA under heavy weather condition of abt 4-5 mtr long Swells.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to check the tightness and condition of the Associated high pressure pipes equipments.
Corrective Action: Root cause of the problem needs to be identified. Bolts found to be slacked and sheared off. It might be the long time action. Hence please cross check with the Job for checking the bolt tightness is available on the Ship Palm. If so revert with the current hrs. If not needs to be included on the same.
ClosingNote: Ensure the PMS routines are carried out in order to avoid such incidents.

IncidentDetails: Electrical officer reported that one reefer container is in unplug condition since last two days because he was in the impression that it is an empty reefer container.
IncidentEventDetails: During evening reefer rounds while the cargo operation was in progress at port of Charleston.vessel suspect stevedore might have unplug the particular reefer container during lashings.As Electrical officer was not carrying the proper reefer monitoring sheet, he missed total counts of reefers on board and assumed that the particular reefer container to be an empty reefer.    The next day While taking the morning reefer round OS found the same reefer container in unplug condition and he reported this only to Electrical officer. EO informed the OS that it is an empty reefer with out consulting with the chief officer. so thereafter OS also stop monitoring that particular reefer.After two days when Electrical officer was writing the reefer temperature in the actual reefer monitoring sheet, he had noticed the particular reefer container temperature column empty and he then realize it was a live reefer which somebody had unpluged in that port so he immediately re connect it.
ImmediateAction: Immediately connected the reefer and the return temperature was 21 Deg C at the time of re connection. The set temperature of the reefer container was 25 Deg C.    Immediately chief officer instructed EO and OS to take the total count of reefer containers on board and found no other reefer container was un pluged.    Traning of crew on board carried out as per attach SF-17
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessal has not suspecting any damage to the cargo inside the container at present because when the reefer reconnected the return temperature was 21 Deg C and it reached to 24.8 without taking much delay. which is very close to the set temperature of 25 Deg C of the particular reefer and the maximum ambient temperature noted from the time of loading till the re connection was 28 deg C.
DirectCause: 1) Not followed the proper procedure and instruction of monitoring the reefer containers with proper reefer monitoring log sheet with all details of the container like Number of the container, position, vent setting  and set point of the reefer.     2) Not reported or cross check with chief officer prior departure the total number of reefers on board and the condition of the reefer including existing and any reefer loaded from that paticular port.     3) Disision taken independentantly by EO
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Chief officer to prepare and give reefer monitoring sheet daily for reefer rounds.    2) Morning and evening reefer rounds to be taken with actual monitoring sheet as given by chief officer. No use of rough papers or self made monitoring sheets.    3) Daily status of reefers to be informed to chief officer,chief engineer and Master including small malfunction or any doubts. Not to take decisions alone.    4) After departure every port all onboard reefers to be checked physically by electrical officer and inform same to chief offier.
ClosingNote: Crew/officer training carried out and Apndx 4 from CNTM discussed.

IncidentDetails: During berthing at King Fahd Industrial Port, Yanbu one of the fwd back springs parted.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel commenced mooring at berth no: 41 at KFIP at 1536 hrs LT on 25-Oct-2019. Both of the fwd back springs were passed ashore using heaving line. One back spring aft was passed ashore using heaving line and the other was passed ashore using mooring boat. After passing both fwd and aft back springs, vessel commenced tightening the fwd back springs slowly to move 1 mtr aft using mooring lines while keeping aft back springs about one meter slack.
