All this time the vessel was held alongside the berth using two tugs. As the vessel started moving aft and was just about to get in position, one of the fwd back springs gave way and parted. Pilot advised that vessel will be held in position by the tugs and vessel should pass the breast lines, head lines and stern lines and make them fast and the second fwd back spring will be passed last. Vessel immediately put a spare mooring rope on the fwd back spring mooring drum and mooring of the vessel was completed safely at 1618 hrs LT on 25-Oct-2019. No crew or mooring gang was injured and no other properth was damaged, except one fwd back spring.
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately arranged a spare mooring rope and put it on the fwd back spring drum and completed the mooring of the vesel safely at 1618 hrs LT on 25-Oct-2019.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 Mooring Rope Parted without any injury or additoinal damage anywhere (vsl or berth).
DirectCause: Pilot used the tug at the wrong time to arrest the vessel's movement resulting in excess weight on the mooring line, causing it to part.  Pilot to Tug Communication was in local language resulting in disconnect between master's understanding of the tug's instructions and intended actions
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To advise the Pilots to consider the SWl of the mooring ropes and avoid sudden tug movements which cause sudden unexpected load on vessel's mooring system and mooring lines. To discuss the incident with all crew members so that the lines can be slackened if excessive weight is experienced on the lines to avoid parting of ropes. Safety during mooring operations to be discussed with all crew members.
Corrective Action: To advise the Pilots to consider the SWl of the mooring ropes and avoid sudden tug movements which cause sudden unexpected load on vessel's mooring system and mooring lines. To discuss the incident with all crew members so that the lines can be slackened if excessive weight is experienced on the lines to avoid parting of ropes. Safety during mooring operations to be discussed with all crew members.
LessionLearnt: Importance of staying clear of snap zones and ensure no shore personnel are in way of mooring rope (behind bollard on wharf) when the ropes are under tention
ReviewComments: satisfactory
ClosingNote: Importance of Master Pilot Exchange and onus on Master to emphasise on safe navigation throughout the pilotage. OOW to contribute in constructive way by highlighting any uncomfortable situaiton immediately to Master

IncidentDetails: Fire in Ship Yard Oxygen and Aceteline hose and electrical cable which was lying on the second floor of Engine room beneth the  welding area  funnel casing.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. at 1558 duty oiler noticed slight smoke during rounds.  2. Informed other crew. Found smoke developing on the second floor of engine room outside control room.  3. Minor Fire was observed on the Oxygen Aceteline hoses and electrical cables which was laying on the floor.  4. Immedietly with the help of Portable DCP fire extenguished.
ImmediateAction: Activated Portable DCP to extenguish the fire.  Obsereved the area for Re ignition.  Informed the Yard fire brigade and investigatigated.  Immediately called a safety meeting of all the crew members and briefed on the incident and importance of increasing the safety level.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Shore gas hose burned and fire put off.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Discussed with the yard team to carry out hot work under supervision of vessel staff with proper fire rounds at regular intervals and a permit in place.

IncidentDetails: Parting of rope ( forward spring) while alongside berth no 202 at Petrobras Terminal . No injuries to any personnel.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was being made alongside berth 202 with Fwd (4+2) and Aft (3+2+2) and it was noticed that the adjacent vessel of a smaller size was surging heavily due to the berth being an open type exposed to sea. Wind was north east and vessel heading south,Weather condition was with windforce 5 and high swell (0.5 m-1 m) pushing vessel outwards from berth.    Master advised the pilot for use of breast ropes but it was declined by pilot stating insufficent mooring fittings on the jetty to accomodate additional ropes.  After the vessel had made fast all her lines, still the vessel was surging heavily 1-3 meters in forward-athwarthship direction. All ropes were greased and rope sheaths used to protect against chaffing and damage.     Within an hour of making fast all lines the forward spring (one number) parted and started causing extra tension on other ropes as well . Even though there were personnel on the jetty none of them were ready to assist vessel in passing extra lines to compensate for the parted rope. Master informed port control and two tugs were sent immediately for assistance. With the help from tugs vessel passed extra spring line and the rectified spring line as well. Vessel made fast with Fwd (4+3) and Aft (3+2+2) and tugs released.
ImmediateAction: Spare rope made ready , Damaged rope taken back on board to be able to use back immediately , Tugs called in after informing Port control.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was being made alongside berth 202 with Fwd (4+2) and Aft (3+2+2) and it was noticed that the adjacent vessel of a smaller size was surging heavily due to the berth being an open type exposed to sea. Wind was north east and vessel heading south,Weather condition was with windforce 5 and high swell (0.5 m-1 m) pushing vessel outwards from berth.    Master advised the pilot for use of breast ropes but it was declined by pilot stating insufficent mooring fittings on the jetty to accomodate additional ropes.  After the vessel had made fast all her lines, still the vessel was surging heavily 1-3 meters in forward-athwarthship direction. All ropes were greased and rope sheaths used to protect against chaffing and damage.     Within an hour of making fast all lines the forward spring (one number) parted and started causing extra tension on other ropes as well . Even though there were personnel on the jetty none of them were ready to assist vessel in passing extra lines to compensate for the parted rope. Master informed port control and two tugs were sent immediately for assistance. With the help from tugs vessel passed extra spring line and the rectified spring line as well. Vessel made fast with Fwd (4+3) and Aft (3+2+2) and tugs released.    No damage to shore equipments , No injury to Personne, No pollution incident , No delay to cargo operational
DirectCause: Insufficent mooring arrangements on shore side, Rough weather and unsafe sea conditions , Location of berth ( open sea berth ), Vessel being pushed away from berth due to swell from midship,Even though agents and pilots had information about similar incidents in the past but vessel was not cautioned.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Expected weather conditions at berth to be received prior berthing. Expected mooring arrangement at berth to be reviewed prior berthing, Master shall seek any anomaly & past incidents from agent and pilot to remain prepared to handle adverse conditions , Master shall intervene and not hesitate to challenge pilot if they are not comfortable with pilots proposal.
Corrective Action: Expected weather conditions at berth to be received prior berthing. Expected mooring arrangement at berth to be reviewed prior berthing, Master shall seek any anomaly & past incidents from agent and pilot to remain prepared to handle adverse conditions , Master shall intervene and not hesitate to challenge pilot if they are not comfortable with pilots proposal.
ClosingNote: job closed 03/01

IncidentDetails: 2 Shore lines on Port side, both FWD and AFT parted and Shore gangway shifted off from the vessel leaving damage to the Ship’s Railing on the aft mooring deck.
IncidentEventDetails: At around 1224 LT duty officer noticed that the wind is picking up from the stern and ropes getting tightened.  Vessel called for emergency stations forward / Aft and informed the dock master on Channel 13 about the Strong wind and unsafe condition of the gangway, as it was getting shifted from the vessel and the stand, that was kept on the quey side is about to fall.  At 1228LT 2 Shore lines on Port side, both FWD and AFT parted and Shore gangway shifted off from the vessel leaving damage to the Ship’s Railing. By the time, the shore team also, including the dock master has arrived at the warf, aft of the vessel.  FWD and AFT stations manned and attended the mooring lines, All ships ropes were kept tight and vessel maintained position. Shore team boarded the vessel and removed the Gangway and damaged railing.  Vessel passed extra ropes in place of the parted shore lines.  By 1315LT, Wind subsided and stations called off.
ImmediateAction: FWD and AFT stations manned and attended the mooring lines, All ships ropes were kept tight and vessel maintained position. Shore team boarded the vessel and removed the Gangway and damaged railing.  Vessel passed extra ropes in place of the parted shore lines.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Railing on the aft mooring deck full set for 2 mts damaged and uprooted on one side.
DirectCause: The wind picked up when vessel was inside dock and 2 shore lines parted, transfering the full wait to ship lines. Also the strong wind has caused the shore gangway tower to collapse.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pass extra mooring lines, even in dock, no considration must be given to the shore ropes. Shore gangway tower must be secured properly to avoid collapsing.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Weather forecast to be discussed with the docking team to prepare vessel accordingly. Alert sent to all vessels to avoid similar incidents.

IncidentDetails: While the Cargo loading arm was being manouvered by shore loading arm connection personnel , the loading Arm Hydraulic Hose Bursted and all the hydraulic oil from the hose and its piping spilled/drained on vessel main deck adjacent to the loading Manifold in midship area and adjacent Ship Side
IncidentEventDetails: After berthing at SOJ Berth at paradeep , the loading arm connection was in progress . The loading arm functions mechanically via a remote device hydraulically . While manouvering the loading arm near to the loading Manifold , the hydraulic hose of the loading arm bursted resulting in all the hydraulic oil getting drained / spilled on MainDeck , Manifold Fender & Ship side to a small extent .
ImmediateAction: Spill clean up commenced by collecting the accumulated oil on deck and transferring to portable cans and drums . Saw dust application in the area where the oil has spread . Charterer's/ DPA  informed and " Casualty report " was made and informed all concerned parties .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage to any ship equipment or structure
DirectCause: Lack of Maintenance of Shore Loading arm ,  Lack of knowledge in proper use of Shore Manifold remote control & Careless Attitude of Shore staff due to employees of Government agency due to which there is no accountabilty and responsibility .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ask Shore personnel to manouvere and check the loading arm outside the ship's railing to ensure its working in order prior manouvering it near the ships manifold .
ClosingNote: Ship staffs

IncidentDetails: OSV “CMV ATHOS”  made contact with ship’s hull (P) near 6P WBT ( around Fr. 49 to Fr. 52, about 10 to 12 mtrs from keel). Contact was made at two places. At lower point there is dent and paint scrape. At upper point there is paint scrape.
IncidentEventDetails: 0512 - Vsl commenced approach to RV position as advised by Pilot.  0600 – Lee side presented to OSV ‘CMV ATHOS” for approach for pilot boarding. Vsl stationary.  0610 – While approaching towards pilot ladder, aft starboard quarter of OSV “CMV ATHOS”  made contact with ship’s hull (P) near 6P WBT ( around Fr. 49 to Fr. 52, about 10 to 12 mtrs from keel). Contact was made at two places. At lower point there is dent and paint scrape. At upper point there is paint scrape. 6P WBT in ballast condition.    0615 -  OSV alongside assessing situation for pilot boarding.  0642 -  Pilot boarding aborted due to unfavourable conditions.
ImmediateAction: - Soundings checked of immediate tanks. Found unchanged.  - Integrity of the vessel checked. Found in order.  - Soundings further regularly monitored to confirm no change.  - Inspection of 6P WBT planned immediately after clearing of SBM area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Dent on ship's Port side near 6P WBT ( around Fr. 49 to Fr. 52, about 10 to 12 mtrs from keel) At lower point there is dent and paint scrape. At upper point there is paint scrape. 6P WBT Tank inspection will be done when vessel is clear of SBM area.
DirectCause: OSV "CMV ATHOS" was rolling due to swell due to which the aft starboard quarter of the OSV made contact with the ship's hull.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Monitor movement of small crafts when manouvering in close proximity and communicate with the other vessel if in doubt. If weather seems unfavorable consider aborting the operation until favorable weather.

IncidentDetails: On 3rd Nov’19 at 0912 LT vessel completed securing to SBM at Wandoo Terminal. After passing the cargo hose at the manifold at 1000 LT, OSV “CMV ATHOS” proceeded aft to secure static tow line. While passing messenger line for the same, at around 1030 LT OSV “CMV ATHOS” made contact with vessel’s rudder.
IncidentEventDetails: 0730 - Commenced approach for berthing    0842 - Fwd messenger line passed to OSV "CMV ATHOS".    0912 - Forward SBM chain secured to vessel    1000 - OSV "CMV ATHOS" proceeded to port midship, cargo hose line picked.  Cargo hose picked up.    1010 - OSV "CMV ATHOS" proceeded aft to secure static tow line.    1030 - OSV "CMV ATHOS" approached aft to pick up messenger line, while picking up the line the  OSV made contact with vessel’s rudder.
ImmediateAction: Rudder visibly checked. No visible damage.  Rudder trunk inspected. No visible damage.  Integrity of vessel checked. found in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Rudder visibly checked. No visible damage.  Rudder trunk inspected. No visible damage.
DirectCause: Inappropriate manouvering by OSV "CMV ATHOS"
Proposedcorrectiveactions: After underwater inspection and Class attendance, if required vessel may need to proceed for repair of rudder and machinery associated steering system.
ClosingNote: Underwater inspection carried out at Tanjung bin. All found in order. No damages observed.

IncidentDetails: Vessel received Voyage Instruction from Charterer with NAABSA Clause at Buenaventura and same was sent to Manager/Owner to verify. Precautions/Instruction received from MSI with NAABSA operations at berth through email.   The Vessel proceeding berth at rising tide for alongside. While during discharging vessel touches bottom at lowest tide,  advised all concerned party regarding the incident. It was noted vessel setting at the bottom for several days until vessel reaches lighter condition.
IncidentEventDetails: 12.10.2019  1130: EOSP Buenaventura  1230: POB  1446: Tugs fwd and aft made fast  1548: First line ashore  1606: Cast-off fwd and aft tug  1612: All fast  1620: Pilot-off / FWE  Port formalities / Cargo Inspection / Draft Survey  1930: Vessel Portside touches bottom / vessel strat to move away at berth  2400: No discharging operation due to rain
ImmediateAction: On vessels touches bottom start to tender ropes to avoid parting/damages. Report to all concerned that vessel touches bottom. Owners and Manager schedule with UWI prior departure and Occasional Class survey.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
DirectCause: Vessel's draft exceed with Berth depth during low tide.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Since NAABSA clause as part of Chartered party agreement vessel shall ensure prior departure UWI shall be requested to verify any dammage sustain on ship's hull and compliment.
Corrective Action: Since NAABSA clause as part of Chartered party agreement vessel shall ensure prior departure UWI shall be requested to verify any dammage sustain on ship's hull and compliment.
ClosingNote: CA in order

IncidentDetails: One fire fighting radio was misplaced & found damaged on deck after the fire drill.
IncidentEventDetails: One firefighting walkie talkie was misplaced and not to be found after the drill. Upon searching the vessel, the radio had probably fallen of the fire fighter during the fire drill as it was found near the forecastle deck. The walkie talkie when checked was found damaged and not functioning.
ImmediateAction: It was informed to all crew that all fire fighting equipment is to be secured after the drill. Care to be taken for safety equipment. It is replaced with another UHF walkie talkie from vessel's regular use till replacement is received.Requisition is raised for one walkie talkie.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One fire fighting radio misplaced / damaged during the last fire drill carried out onboard.
DirectCause: Radios to be used with proper care during drills and training.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Specific duties assigned in Muster card for handling the radios.
Corrective Action: Crew to be briefed on proper usage of the walkie talkies. New walkie talkie to be requested for and renewed upon receipt
ClosingNote: Noted that new walkie talkie has been received

IncidentDetails: Vessel is carrying out the STS in open Sea condition in the Designated STS area at Lagos .  Daughter Vessel is moored to Mother Vessel in the running moor and Mother vessel needs to drop the anchor as per the mooring master after coming closure to port area  in designated STS area. Mooring master access the local condition and decides about the anchoring or steaming.  Weather is fair and both the vessel rolls easily due to open sea condions .  There are other minimum four vessel carrying out the STS operation at anchor.  Vessel is provided with wire ropes with mooring tails whereas  Daughter Vessel MT ST Nenne equipped with synthetic mooring ropes . As Elasticity in Wire ropes are less than mooring ropes . To have the equal weight for arresting the surge/movment of both the vessel moored to each-other , It is required to keep the wire rope little slack than synthetic mooring ropes .  Vessel had explained this to mooring master & daughter Vessel  and again  duty officer of daughter vessel in the night and had requested  inform the vessel if they intend to slack  their  mooring ropes.  However the attending crew of the daughter vessel slackened their mooring lines more than the wire ropes without informing and without understanding /accessing the situation.This caused all weight to come on Mother vessel's Wire mooring line.  Vessel lost four mooring tails at following times during the operations:-  09th Oct 2019// 2130 - One stern line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0148 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0430 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0440 - One stern line tail rope.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel is carrying out the STS in open Sea condition in the Designated STS area at Lagos .  Daughter Vessel is moored to Mother Vessel in the running moor and Mother vessel needs to drop the anchor as per the mooring master after coming closure to port area  in designated STS area. Mooring master access the local condition and decides about the anchoring or steaming.  1312 LT/ 09-Oct-2019 - Vessel anchored after running moor with Daughter Vessel.  Weather is fair and both the vessel rolls easily due to open sea condions .  There are other minimum four vessel carrying out the STS operation at anchor.  Vessel is provided with wire ropes with mooring tails whereas  Daughter Vessel MT ST Nenne equipped with synthetic mooring ropes . As Elasticity in Wire ropes are less than mooring ropes . To have the equal weight for arresting the surge/movment of both the vessel moored to each-other , It is required to keep the wire rope little slack than synthetic mooring ropes .  Vessel had explained this to mooring master & daughter Vessel  and again  duty officer of daughter vessel in the night and had requested  inform the vessel if they intend to slack  their  mooring ropes.  However the attending crew of the daughter vessel slackened their mooring lines more than the wire ropes without informing and without understanding /accessing the situation.This caused all weight to come on Mother vessel's Wire mooring line.  Vessel lost four mooring tails at following times during the operations:-  09th Oct 2019// 2130 - One stern line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0148 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0430 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0440 - One stern line tail rope.
ImmediateAction: 1:- Immidiately changed the parted tail rope with the spares tail rope onboard.  2:-Vessel again explained to duty officer of daughter vessel in the night that Vessel is provided with wire ropes with mooring tails whereas  Daughter Vessel equipped with synthetic mooring ropes . As Elasticity in Wire ropes are less than mooring ropes . To have the equal weight for arresting the surge/movment of both the vessel moored to each-other , It is required to keep the wire rope little slack than synthetic mooring ropes and requested  inform the vessel if they intend to slacken their  mooring ropes.  3:- keeping one person in fwd and one person in aft  to contineously monitor the situation and informing the CCR and tending the mooring ropes accordingly .  4:- informed all parties and issued LOP to Service provider for STS Operation & Daughter Vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel lost four mooring tails at following times during the operations:-  09th Oct 2019// 2130 - One stern line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0148 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0430 - One Head line tail rope 10th Oct 2019// 0440 - One stern line tail rope.
DirectCause: Vessel is provided with wire ropes with mooring tails whereas  Daughter Vessel MT ST Nenne equipped with synthetic mooring ropes . As Elasticity in Wire ropes are less than mooring ropes . To have the equal weight for arresting the surge/movment of both the vessel moored to each-other , It is required to keep the wire rope little slack than synthetic mooring ropes .  Vessel had explained this to mooring master & daughter Vessel  and again  duty officer of daughter vessel in the night and had requested  inform the vessel if they intend to slacken their  mooring ropes.  However the attending crew of the daughter vessel slackened their mooring lines more than the wire ropes without informing and without understanding /accessing the situation.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1:- Information about the specail  requirement of tending of mooring ropes to be discussed prior mooring . During initial safety meeting & before the any scheduled watch change over .  2:-Crew to be trained more to understand the cause & requirement of contineous monitoring . It is advised to keep one crew in fwd & one crew in aft to monitor the situation continously  if mooring lines of both the vessel are not the same .
ClosingNote: Vessel to be more alert during STS operation.

IncidentDetails: Parting of 3 nos mooring ropes while vessel berthed at Jaigarh Berth No.1
IncidentEventDetails: On 07/Aug/2019 @1624 LT (IST) vessel berthed at port of Jaigarh India Fwd and Aft 5+4 Lines and riding to her port side (sea side) anchor .  As per pilot's verbal Advice due to south west monsoons and since  berth no. 1 was open without break water swell heights were very high and chances of mooring ropes parting was high. Vessel  at all times kept crew standby on fwd and aft stations to attend to mooring ropes continously ,rope fenders were inserted on all contact points and grease applied to contact points.Vessel was contionously surging and rolling due to high beam swells.  on 08/Aug/2019 @1100 Lt one of the Aft Stern lines (outbound line) parted due to heavy rolling of vessel.   on 08/Aug/2019 @1330Lt one of the Aft Spring lines parted.  on 08/Aug/2019@1745Lt one of the fwd breast lines parted .
ImmediateAction: Additional ropes were passed fwd and aft making it fwd 5+5 and aft 5+6 lines. Rope fenders were put on all contact points and all contact points greased. Contionous watch posted on both fwd and aft stations to tend to mooring lines at all times. Main engines on standby for immediate use and master on bridge.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 3 nos of mooring ropes parted while vessel berthed at Jaigarh Berth No.1.  5 nos of mooring ropes heavily chaffed and not usable.
DirectCause: Incident happened due to unsafe berth and port condition and adverse weather and sea conditions due to SW monsoons. Berth No.1 at Jaigarh port was not protected by a break water and thus was directly exposed to open sea. The high swells and adverse weather due to the SW monsoons also contributed to the incident.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: At Unsafe berths like Jaigarh crew to be kept 24x7 standby at both fwd and aft stations to provide complete attendance to moorings at all times. All contact points of ropes to be well lubricated by excess grease and protected by canvas and additional rope fenders. Main Engines to be standby at all times to be used in emergency and master to be on bridge during heavy surging or rolling
ClosingNote: In Monsoon climate vessel has to take extra precautions wrt keeping the vessel safe at all times.

IncidentDetails: It was observed that the garbage bag used for covering the GRVE pipe opposite to engine control room was melting due to sparks.  With the help of Portable DCP and water extinguisher, fire extinguished. The yard fire watch also joinined in and area was cleared.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. At 2000 Hrs duty oiler and yard working personnel noticed smoke on garbage bag covered on pipe during rounds.  2. On checking it was observed that the garbage bag used for covering the GRVE pipe opposite to engine control room was melting due to sparks.  3. The hot work that was carrying out close to the GRVE pipe was stopped.  4. Immediately with the help of Portable DCP and water extinguisher, fire extinguished. The yard fire watch also joinined in and area was cleared.  5. The garbage bag was melted almost half and the GRVE pipe was noticed red hot.
ImmediateAction: 1. With the help of Portable DCP and water extinguisher, fire extinguished. The yard fire watch also joinined in and area was cleared.  2. Discussion was carried out with Site manager Mr Simon, Fayad supervisor Mr Jon and all other team heads and underlined the extra precautions we have to follow to avoid similar incidents.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The garbage bag was melted almost half and the GRVE pipe was noticed red hot.
DirectCause: The hot work carried out without hot work permission from ship staff and fire hazard garbage bags were used on the pipes for covering the area.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Hotwork must be carried out with permits and with proper fire proof equipments for safe guarding.  Discussion was carried out with Site manager Mr Simon, Fayad supervisor Mr Jon and all other team heads and underlined the extra precautions we have to follow to avoid similar incidents.
ClosingNote: Yard to be debriefed for necessary fire precautions during hot works.

IncidentDetails: During port stay at Bataan, Due to continuos surging & rolling of vessel 2 Nos of mooring ropes parted and other sustained partial damage.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was berthed at Liquigaz terminal  at Bataan , Philippines for discharging. As it was an open berth , the vessel was exposed to weather , sea and  swell . Vessel continuosly experienced long swell throughout the discharging causing the vessel to surge and role moderately . Due to continuos surging & rolling of vessel 2 Nos of mooring ropes parted and other sustained partial damage.
ImmediateAction: To avoid the other mooring ropes from parting due to excessive stress  and to keep the vessel alongside , Vessel immediately passed additional mooring ropes and attended to. Port authorities were informed in case of tugs assistance required.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was berthed at Liquigaz terminal  at Bataan , Philippines for discharging. As it was an open berth , the vessel was exposed to weather , sea and  swell . Vessel continuosly experienced long swell throughout the discharging causing the vessel to surge and role moderately . Due to continuos surging & rolling of vessel 2 Nos of mooring ropes parted and other sustained partial damage.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Vessel was made fast to an open berth exposed to sea and long swell causing the vessel to surge and roll moderately, putting excessive stress on the mooring ropes which resulted in parting of mooring lines.    Indirect Cause: Effects of passing tropical cyclone " "QUEIL" (NAKRI) which exposed the berth/vessel to sea & swell. Vessel had visited the same berth in past without incident and at the  time of berthing swell was not significant.   Root Cause : Vessel exposed to mod sea & swell due to non existence of breakwater or any shelter from external forces.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: - During passage planning vessel to refer latest weather information available. Vessel to liase with the agent for latest local weather condition vessel might encounter while its stay and to delay berthing if any lilkely hood of bad weather during port stay.  - Training carried out for Duty Officer and crew on handling of mooring ropes , with special emphasis on handling of ropes while the vessel is surging due to bad weather .
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting and detailed cause analysis and corrective actions.

IncidentDetails: Accidental activation of starboard MOB Marker smoke signal while removing for bracket maintenance
IncidentEventDetails: In order to close the Internal Audit NC of corroded MOB Bracket, maintenace was planned on the Starboard MOB Bracket. For purpose of the same, it was decided to remove the MOB Marker and Lifebuoy to avoid damage/accidental release of the MOB Marker & Liferbuoy. As per the procedure, MOB Marker bracket has to be unscrewed from base bracket and the upper bracket has to be slided up along with the marker. While sliding out the bracket, which was tight in place, a little force had to be applied when the firing mechanism of the marker got triggered and the smoke signal got activated.
ImmediateAction: MOB Marker and Lifebouy placed at safe place and requisition has been raised for new MOB Marker to be delivered on priority to the vessel in Fujairah.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Activation MOB Marker smoke signal rendering it out of service and requiring replacement.
DirectCause: While sliding out the bracket, which was tight in place, a little force had to be applied when the firing mechanism of the marker got triggered and the smoke signal got activated.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Requisition has been raised for replacement MOB Marker.
Corrective Action: Pls. replace the MOB marker once recvd on board
LessionLearnt: Vessel to be more careful while carrying out maintenance on LSA FFA items in order to prevent accidental release
ClosingNote: New MOB marker supplied and replaced in location

IncidentDetails: STATEMENT OF FACTS  Slow discharge rate/ Stoppage and Moisture in Cargo Holds at Discharge Port    Holds were swept, washed and rinsed with Fresh water, Dried, Bilges cleaned and tested prior to arrival loading port.  Hose test of Hatch covers was carried out on 24th August 2019.  Vessels arrived NOLA anchorage on 26th Aug 2019 for loading.  Cargo holds were inspected by surveyor prior loading & found in order. 1  During berthing clouds were observed. There was lightening threat warning in place at UBT Terminal due which despite V/L coming alongside, Linesmen did not attend to vessel. Upon waiting at berth and holding vessel alongside using engine & tugs, as per various discussions between pilot & terminal vessel departed berth & went back to anchorage. Clouds were observed2 (picture attached) upon arrival anchorage the lightning warning threat was cleared & vessel went back to terminal for berthing LOP3 for above incident issued.  Upon berthing at UBT Davant Terminal on 27th Aug, pre-loading draft survey was carried out by the shore draft surveyor & vessel.  Shipper’s cargo declaration4 was handed over by agent upon berthing on 27th. Moisture content was noted to be 15.36%   & TML 20.83%.  Scanned copy of same was emailed to office upon receipt on 27th Aug4.  During berthing cargo was noted to be stowed in open stockpile & open barges. 5  Upon completion of loading final draft survey was carried out5A.   SOF was remarked “Cargo loaded from open stockpile exposed to elements”.6  Vessel had stern trim at departure about 0.40m.  Bilges became full within first days of departure. Water was more than 75cm (Max Bilge well height) and 75cm was used to fill in the bilge sheet so as to tally with the quantity pumped out, Bilge level more than 75cm was remarked in the mail. View special area Pumping out of the Cargo hold bilges was withheld information was relayed in email. 7  Alarms were observed on Water Ingress Alarm System (WIAS) in cargo holds & same were remarked in daily reporting of cargo hold bilge log. Summary of Reporting attached.7  Upon clearing the Special area vessel on 5th sept commenced pumping out the bilges & same was remarked in daily reporting of Bilge log.  Vessel was weather routed by AWT.  Upon departure NOLA vessel was expected to come across Storm Dorian off Florida, as per AWT recommendation vessel altered voyage plan to pass through Old Bahama channel to avoid storm Dorian. 8   On 7th Sep route plan was amended to pass clear of the Storm Gabrielle.   Vessel was able to stay clear of heavy weather conditions, no severe weather was encountered associated with both the storm.  On 7th Sep the frequency of pump out was increased as the Bilges were becoming full at the end of pump out  cycle.  WIAS alarms continue to be present & were remarked in daily reports.  10th Sept Advise was received from office to pump out more than once as required to minimise the water in bilges. 7a   Bilges were found to be filling up slowly on 11th and 12th and did not become full.   12th Sep trim was increased to 1.40 mtrs and bilges pumped out as required.  15th Sep vessel entered English Channel & Special area. In vicinity of other vessels, TSS & coastal passage, pump out withheld & bilge wells were not full .  After peak pumped out to achieve arrival condition for Lock Gate and berthing. Trim about 0.30 M.  16th Sep vessel passing Dover area & EOSP 16th /2354LT.  Vessel arrived & anchored @ Amsterdam anchorage.  Until arrival vessel schedule was to berth on 18th  Sep & ETD -22nd Sep but on arrival day same was postponed to 19th  Sep berthing & 23rd Sep dep. However berthing kept on getting delayed and vessel finally berthed on 21st  0856 LT.  During this waiting no Cargo Hold bilges pumped out at anchorage and berth.  Draft survey was carried out by Charterer’s, Receiver’s appointed surveyors & vessel. Discharging commenced @1340LT/21st Sep.  No water was observed on top of cargo in holds when holds were opened for discharge.  23rd/0856LT cargo discharging was stopped by stevedore as per charterer’s/Receiver’s decision. No information/ reason was given to vessel at the time of stopping. Later on it was advised by Agents cargo stopped due to alleged wet cargo and water in holds.  On 23rd Sep sampling of moisture/water in cargo holds carried out by Charterer & Receiver’s surveyors after permission received form Owners9&10. Vessel collected samples of water from holds at same time in presence of surveyors. Chloride tests carried out by vessel using meter. Chloride reading showing “0.0” for all samples. 9  Samples of the water from all cargo holds used for sampling by Charterers and Receivers surveyors were also retained onboard.  23rd  - LOP issued by vessel for Slow discharge rate / stoppage and Moisture in Holds13.  23rd /2200 LT all cast off from OBA berth, vessel commenced shifting to Rietlanden Afrikahaven Terminal.  About 63,733.030 mt was discharged at OBA terminal   2312Lt/23rd First Line ashore.   2348 LT
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Free water in Cargo holds, due to inherent nature of the cargo. Free water cogualting in various pockets at differnt height and places in cargo holds with ship's motions/ vibrations and interfering with discharge operations at Disport.    Vessel had to shifted to another berth for completion of discharge.  Freewater from cargo holds transfered to Collections tanks using portable pumps.    Owners and Chrtrs P&I surveyors attended the vessel.    Vessel was put under commercial arrest due to dispute between Owners and Chrtrs and arrest was lifted as per arrangements/ understanding between them.
DirectCause: Due to inherent Nature of cargo, Free water in cargo holds at various levels, interefering with the discharge equipment of the terminal during discharge operations.  Free water in cargo holds at various levels  accumulating at the bottom during discharge operations.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Where possible as per DWT available trim should be increased to max to facilitate drainage to bilges during transit.  LOP should be issued each time loading from Open stockpile and if moisture content is on the higher side as per Shipper's Declaration / Moisture certificate.  During Ballast Passage staff should identify lines, V/vs etc and exercise/ practice the internal transfer of hold bilges to Collection tank to have awareness of the system and it's limitations if any.  Cargo Hold Bilges should be monitored from the time of commence  loading operations until completion of all holds discharge operations and not only departure loadport to arrival  disport anchorage/ berth. Same should be included in Chief Mates pre-joining briefings/ training.
LessionLearnt: for group A & B cargoes always issue a LOP for high moisture content.
ClosingNote: Where possible as per DWT available trim should be increased to max to facilitate drainage to bilges during transit.

IncidentDetails: On 07th Nov 2019 during 2000-0000 watch  #1 HFO transfer pump  was started to transfer fuel oil to  LSFO settling tank, unnoticingly Duty engineer left that place and went for Engine room rounds overflow occurs & resulted in Oil spill.
IncidentEventDetails: On 07/11/21019 at 21:03 HRS Duty Engineer started #1 HFO Transfer pump for transferring oil from 3(P) bunker tank to settling tank from ECR Aconics screen. After checking the machinery parameters unnoticed that the pump was running he left control room for engine room rounds.    At  21:23 HRS settling tank alarm came  and followed by ME fuel pump drive end leak high’ level alarm received.  Then Duty Engineer realized and stop the HFO transfer pump immediately, meanwhile oil spilled near ME Fuel pump scupper area and the oil spread to forward of Engine room bilge wells.    After receiving call from Duty Engineer, Chief Engineer & second Engineer came to engine room, Further control measures taken to minimise the oil spreading to other spaces in the Engine room.
ImmediateAction: Control measures taken to minimise the oil spreading to other spaces in the Engine room.  After assessing the situation Informed bridge and extended help for the cleaning up operation and started removing & cleaning the space.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: Vessel port anchor entangled and fouled by strayed chain, as sighted by deck crew when picking-up her anchor during departure maneuever from Duncan 1 Terminal at Port Alfred.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl arrived and berthed at Duncan 1 Terminal, Port Alfred on 07 Oct.2019, The Pilot used her Port Anchor (1 shackle in hawse-pipe) as a drag during her berthing maneuver. Had difficulty & unabled to heave-up the anchor upon berthing. So, the Pilot decided to have it pick-up during departure maneuver.
ImmediateAction: Vessel have to re-berth and informend agents, owners and charterer for planning to free vsl anchor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vsl idle at berth awaits shore presonnel to clear the engtabgled chain on vsl port anchor.
DirectCause: No information from Port Authorities and Coast Guard that there is a foul area on the port and at anchorage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To discuss with the Pilot on action planned for use of anchor and if suspect any issue to challenge the pilots decision.
Corrective Action: To discuss with the Pilot on action planned for use of anchor and if suspect any issue to challenge the pilots decision.
LessionLearnt: avoid using anchor as drag
ReviewComments: Discussed with ships staff
ClosingNote: Master has been checking with Pilot everytime whether there was any foul area and whether anchor will be needed for use.

IncidentDetails: While vsl was transiting Atlantic ocean, vsl encountered Very rough weather and Heavy swells. As per the recommendations of the WNI weather forecast, Vsl deviated from her intended passage and took shelter south of Ireland. After taking shelter general inspection carried out on maind deck and forecastle deck to check for any heavy weather damage. Vsl found one Life buoy missing from its shipside railing cradle. Main deck areas were searched for missing life buoy and was confirmed the the life buoy was lost overboard while vsl was shipping seas on deck.
IncidentEventDetails: While vsl was transiting Atlantic ocean, vsl encountered Very rough weather and Heavy swells. As per the recommendations of the WNI weather forecast, Vsl deviated from her intended passage and took shelter south of Ireland. After taking shelter general inspection carried out on maind deck and forecastle deck to check for any heavy weather damage. Vsl found one Life buoy missing from its shipside railing cradle. Main deck areas were searched for missing life buoy and was confirmed that the life buoy was lost overboard while vsl was shipping seas on deck.
ImmediateAction: A safety broad cast was transmitted and same logged in GMDSS loag and accidental discharge of garbage was entered in GRB.    New life buoy replaced and all life buoys securing pins checked. All found in order
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One number life buoy lost in heavy wx.
DirectCause: Heavy Wx
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Deck to besecured as  part heavy wx and periodically inspected when possible.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA plan. Vessel was well secured for the heavy weather and routing advisory provided by WNI, vessel turned back to take shelter from heavy wx. Minor damages caused due to swell / heavy wx rectiied prior arrival port / cargo lines pressure tested and found in order

IncidentDetails: Two Mooring Ropes parted due to Chaffing caused by surging of the vessel.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel made fast alongside Sea Berth No. 2,Acajutla, El salvador at 0006 Hrs on 22nd Nov. 2019. Discharging commenced at 0400 hrs LT.The vessel was experiencing surging for 1-2 mtrs as the berth was exposed to sea due to construction of the jetty. At 1030 hrs LT Aft breast rope parted due to chaffing,the rope was covered with chaffing guard also grase was continuoulsy applied to reduce wearing due to chaffing,but due to heavy surging the chaffing guard also ripped off and due to the same, Heavy chaffing damage took place on the rope and  rope parted. Vessel immediately passed Another line from the drum to replace the broken line.The broken end was approximately 8 mtrs in length same was retrived back on ship.    At 1700 hrs rope on forward drum on forward spring also parted due to heavy chaffing, vessel passed another line immediately to replace the broken line.    Vessel had raised concern about the same to terminal, but due to design constrain of the jetty nothing was possible from terminal side.   At departure vessel issued a Letter of Protest to the terminal.
ImmediateAction: Moorings were attended all the times due to heavy surging, Chaffing guards were used, The area of contact of the fairleads was covered with old ropes to reduce wearing also grease was apllied at the areas of contact but due to heavy chaffing 2 Ropes parted. Vessel immediately passed another rope to replace the broken rope.  Immediately informed terminal and terminal informed that the pilots and tugs are on stndby incase of emergency.   At departure vessel issued letter of protest to the terminal.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 Mooring ropes parted :  One rope of 8 mtrs parted and Another rope of 20 mtrs parted
DirectCause: Due to exposure to sea, heavy chaffing on ropes took place at berth.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional ropes to be kept standby. Mooring to br tended to at all times.
Corrective Action: Additional ropes to be kept standby. Mooring to br tended to at all times.
ClosingNote: PA being followed at subsequent ports.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was engaged in running STS off OPL Kaohsiung. After repositioning both ships(tandom manoeuver), well clear from Taiwan territorial waters, discharge operations were resumed, as conditions around own vessel seemed safe and normal(at this point both vessels were still underway, approx 2.2 kts).  26th - 0112lt, both ships ME were stopped, hoping that vessels could resume drifting and continue the cargo operations.  At approximately 0125lt, it was noted that own ship was laterally displaced approx 10 mtr forward and bow was found opening from the other vessel.  Mooring Master was informed and attempts were made to bring the bow of both ships together. At this point one of the head lines passed by the other vessel, parted.  The bow seemed to be closing in slowly, however, the swell(approx 1.0 mtr) caught the mother ship on her broad side and caused viloent surging and vertical movement  between both ships.   Mooring Master was contacted to consider casting off, but in return he advised that the Master of the mother vessel was in Command and did not pay heed to his advice and insisted to keep the ships alongside, while working on the same.  The precarious movement of the ships began increasing. Own vessel informed Mother vessel that we were no longer safe alonside and needed to terminate the operations and cast off, until daylight and better conditions were available.  0142lt cargo was stopped by own ship and manifold valves shut. Engine room was given notice for ME and all deck staff were called for emergency stations.    In the next 20 minutes, own vessel suffered damage to rope tails of 3 head lines and 3 stern lines.    The hoses at manifold area were disconnected with such urgency and in hostile conditions for own staff.  After the hoses were disconnected and they were picked by the mother vessel, unmooring operations were carried out and own vessel manoeuvered safely away from mother ship.

The hoses at manifold area were disconnected with such urgency and in hostile conditions for own staff.  After the hoses were disconnected and they were picked by the mother vessel, unmooring operations were carried out and own vessel manoeuvered safely away from mother ship.
ImmediateAction: Tried to control the movement of ship by tending to moorings.  When tending to moorings was not helpful, Mooring/Loading Master was advised and vessel suspended cargo ops and disconnected hoses, in order to cast off.  ME was readied and stations called on Emergency basis.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: total of 13 rope tails damaged due to severe abrassion with other ships closed chocks.
DirectCause: Chaffing/Abrassion of mooring tails against closed chocks of other vessel. This was caused due to excessive ship movement, while carrying out running STS operations off Kaohsiung, open sea conditions.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: if STS in such scenarios are unavoidable, ship staff to ensure that chocks are heavily greased, where the mooring ropes make contact. This is to reduce effect of chaffing.
ClosingNote: If swell condition not conducive, vessels to start engines to reduce effect and if required to unmoor.

IncidentDetails: While the vessel was in Cape Town at Tanker Basin 2 & discharging Molasses. 4 Stowaways were found on board, the stowaways had gained access to the vessel through mooring rope & entered vessel's chain locker & then 1 stowaway later entered into vessel's Steering gear room
IncidentEventDetails: 24 Nov 2019  ==========  1830 Hrs LT: One Stowaway sighted on forecastle deck by deck watch crew.  1831 Hrs LT: Alarm raised & crew mustered.  1833 Hrs LT: Terminal Informed, Port Security informed & agent informed.  1840 Hrs LT: Started stowaway search to see if any more stowaways on board.  1845 Hrs LT: Chain lockers opened up as the one stowaway who was found had come out from Chain Locker S/S. Two more stowaways found in starboard chain locker.  1900 Hrs LT: Port security team on board & took custody of 3 stowaways.  1900 Hrs LT: Continued stowaway search since there were 4 bags in the chain locker & only 3 stowaways were found.  2000 Hrs LT: 4th stowaway found in Steering gear room.  2005 Hrs LT: Port security team took custody of all 4 stowaways.  2020 Hrs LT: Port security team disembarked with all 4 stowaways.  2245 Hrs LT: Ship staff stowaway search completed, no additional stowaways found on board.
ImmediateAction: Alarm raised.  Terminal, Port Facility Security Officer, Agent & CSO were informed.  Crew Mustered. Search conducted, 4 stowaways were found on board.  Handed over the stoaways to Port Security custody.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Security breach into Chain Locker Starboard Side - 4 Stowaways entered the Chain Locker through Spurling pipe.
DirectCause: Vessel did not foresee the possibility of stowaways entering chain locker through spurling pipe, due to the nature of small opening and therefore Spurling pipe cover (canvas) was not put in place.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident details to be discussed with on board staff during meeting. Security measures including additional watches to be reviewed taking into account the local security situation & threat, same to discussed during pre-arrival meetings.
Corrective Action: Incident details to be discussed with on board staff during meeting. Security measures including additional watches to be reviewed taking into account the local security situation & threat, same to discussed during pre-arrival meetings.
LessionLearnt: Adequate security measures to be employed even if vessel is calling ports where there is less likelihood of stowaways finding their way into ship. One should not let their guard down even when the port is not known for any security related issues.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Incident circulated in the fleet.

IncidentDetails: During Berthing operations at Qingdao, Stbd side Pilot ladder got damaged during pilot transfer operations.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was approaching Qingdao Pilot Station for berthing at Shihua Oil Terminal Berth 89. pilot boarding was agreed from stbd side and boarding arrangements were made as per pilots requirments. However the pilot ladder got damaged during the pilot transfer operations which was noticed by crew members while securing the ladder.
ImmediateAction: Temporary soft repairs were carried on pilot ladder to minimise the residual risk since vessel was not having any spare pilot ladder, so that ladder can be used until replaced by new ladder.  Requisitions has been raised for new Ladders ( SPR-SHDN-D-19-069)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was approaching Qingdao Pilot Station for berthing at Shihua Oil Terminal Berth 89. pilot boarding was agreed from stbd side and boarding arrangements were made as per pilots requirments. However the pilot ladder got damaged during the pilot transfer operations which was noticed by crew members while securing the ladder.
DirectCause: Pilot ladder to be kept lifted until the boat is safely alongside.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To keep the pilot ladder lifted by tripping line until the boat is alongside properly.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's proposed CA

IncidentDetails: While mooring at OTSA Terminal (CBM), San Jose, one mooring rope parted while the mooring operation in progress.
IncidentEventDetails: 1118 : POB for berthing.  1220 : Commenced mooring by dropping stbd anchor.  1230 : Dropped port anchor.  1242 : First line to sea buoy on stbd quarter( 2 line from poop deck).  1254 : Passed one line from stbd winch (Main deck aft) and a loose rope. Tug assisting.  1300 : While crew were passing port quarter line ( Stbd winch line got fully strenched due to current of nearly 1kts and vessel drifted to port side, causing the rope to part).  1300 : Master requested Pilot to call Tug for assist &  push the vessel to stbd side and passed another rope to hold the vessel in position.  1530 : All line were made fast and all ropes monitored continously.
ImmediateAction: Assisting tug was asked to push the vessel stbd side to hold in position and passed extra ropes to hold the vessel as well as to redcuce the tension on another rope.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 Mooring rope parted during mooring operation.
DirectCause: 1) Due to strong currents.  2) Wear and tear of mooring rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Parted rope replaced by another rope.  Tugs to assist while mooring operation in progress to avoid any other incident.   1 additional loose ropes on bitts to be made fast from both side at main deck to avoid reoccurence.
Corrective Action: Parted rope replaced by another rope. Tugs to assist while mooring operation in progress to avoid any other incident. 1 additional loose ropes on bitts to be made fast from both side at main deck to avoid reoccurence.
ClosingNote: CA being followed.

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: While departing from Vizag Port, the Pilot requested vessel to make fast the forward tug from Centre lead using two ship's line. Two ends of the same rope were passed to the tug. the ropes were adjusted to equal tension/length prior making fast of seperate bitts. The crew, then, left for unmmoring forward lines. The tug  pulled the line with a sudden jerk resulting in parting of rope. The tug then passed her lines for making fast.
IncidentEventDetails: While departing from Vizag Port, the Pilot requested vessel to make fast the forward tug from Centre lead using two ship's line. Two ends of the same rope were passed to the tug. the ropes were adjusted to equal tension/length prior making fast of seperate bitts. The crew, then, left for unmmoring forward lines. The tug  pulled the line with a sudden jerk resulting in parting of rope.
ImmediateAction: Vessel picked up parted line from tug & made fast tug's line.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 mooring line broken & taken out of service.
DirectCause: Mooring rope 4.5 years old nearing retirement,due for replacement in May 2020.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thorough frequent/periodic check of all mooring ropes as they near their retirement age.
ClosingNote: Inspection regime as per LMP to be strictly followed.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was alongside(Portside) at Singapore Pasir Panjung Terminal(P-39) on 20th November,2019.  Since Arrival ,Vessel was picking up Varoius stores by Monorail. At 1550 hrs LT( 0750 UTC)while transfering Steel Plates from Shoreside to Maindeck, Monorail Wire parted, all steel materials alongwith  Monorail blocks & Hook fell down on Main deck. This incident could lead to injure or major accident to onboard Crew involved in store lifting operation .Even if these steel Materials fell down on Jetty , it could damage any shore structure & Injury/ accident to Crew involved for picking up stores on Jetty. However, minor damage was observed on main deck after removal of steel materails  from the affected area.
IncidentEventDetails: 0800-1550: Vessel was using monorail crane to lift various stores being received.  1550 : Wire rope parted while lifting 10-pcs steel material ( steel plates 2450mm X1230mmx10mm),calculted weight- 2.32 MT, SWL of monorail : 5.0 MT  No injury to crew & shore personnel. No damage observed on initial observation.  1600: Continued lifting stores manually.  21.11.19   On detailed inspection of area after removal of steel materials, indentation on cross deck aft of BAY 48 noticed.  Also poop deck entry door light & shade observed damaged due to the falling steel plates on light shade.
ImmediateAction: Immediately Vessel checked -If there is any other damages of Monorail or any damages to Ship Construction/structure. All Crew called on Deck. All Steel Plates were shifted from the place of Incident to Onboard stowage Location. As Vessel had other stores to pick up, same were picked up by hand.  Initial notification Report of this Incident was sent by email , followed by Telephone to office.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to this incident, following damages have been occured-    1. Light shade & Light fitting on poop deck entrance was damaged   2. Deck Plate ( Aft of Bay 48) ,(Area -400 mm x 330 mm ) with depth 50 mm was indented.
DirectCause: After investigation , found that Brake for Monorail was renewed few days before, but not adjusted properly. While lifting Steel Plates  by  Monorail at that incident day as the Brake was not holding properly, plates were lifted up & down several times. Probably due to that more load/ tension came on Wire & Monorail wire was parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Now Brake of Monorail adjusted, Tried out . Same is working satisfactory.  Advised all crew that if there is any small defect is observed in any work place in any time, report to the Dept head & corrective Actions to be taken asap/ Report to Office/ If require spare , raise RL & report to the Office.
ClosingNote: Lapse is safe working practices.

IncidentDetails: OWS FLOW SWITCH Malfunctioning leading to OWS inoperational and Equipment not functional.  Critical Equipment Failure.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. OWS was ran for reducing bilge water in bilge tank ( 11.3 m3, Capacity : 24.2 m3) today morning at 0043 hrs UTC( 0843 hrs LT).  However while running found flow switch is mafunctioning. Even when sample water is present it was not giving signal to open 3-way valve. Flowswitch was checked and found one in nos cable out of position. Same was again put back to position and tried out and found ows now functioing well. Same was tested with sample valve closed and 3 way vlave getting closed.     2. However at around 0636 hrs UTC ( 1436 hrs LT) D/E noticed conitnous on/off of 3-way valve. Flow switch was again checked and found at this time cable is not pressed with the switch and giving erratic sensing. Tried putting back the flowswitch back in position and running the OWS, however the switch continued to show malfunction. OWS was stopped at bilge tank level of 3.7 m3 at 0657 hrs UTC ( 1457 hrs LT) and secured.
ImmediateAction: Office Informed, Expidated for spare flow switch supply
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FlowSwitch for OWS 15 PPM Monitor Damaged
DirectCause: Malfunctioning of Float Switch
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Corrective action - Renewal of FLow Switch  Preventive action -  Onboard Availability of Spare Flow Switch to ensure by adding the part to Critical Spare
Corrective Action: We have already taken dispensation from flag. Spares are ordered. Will be supplied flow switch during Qunazhou call. Once received flow switch to be renewed & advise status
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: On 15-Nov-2019 at around 1730 hrs LT Main Engine unit no.5 High pressure pipe (forward) found ruptured and started leaking.
ImmediateAction: Bridge was informed, Main Engine stopped and renewed F.O High Pressure Pipe.
DirectCause: Material failure due to Ageing and Fatigue
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To renew HP pipes as per maker's manual interval. Requisition to be raised accordingly and to maintain spare HP pipes onboard.
Corrective Action: To renew HP pipes as per maker's manual interval. Requisition to be raised accordingly and to maintain spare HP pipes onboard.
ClosingNote: Renewed HP pipe

IncidentDetails: On 27-Nov-2019 at around 1000 hrs LT Boiler water circulating pump no.1 suction valve flange gasket bursted and started leaking heavily.
ImmediateAction: Informed Bridge, Main Engine stopped and rectified the leakage
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 27-Nov-2019 at around 1000 hrs LT, it was noticed that Boiler water circulating pump no.1 suction valve gasket bursted and started leaking heavily.
DirectCause: Gasket material failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Valve flange gaskets to be checked and renewed at regular interval.
Corrective Action: Valve flange gaskets to be checked and renewed at regular interval.
ClosingNote: Renwed flange gasket after isolating steam line

IncidentDetails: Fire in No2 RSB at Transformer room in partial deck
IncidentEventDetails: 1800LT: riding fitter completed work for the day   1900LT: equipments squared up and used boiler suit was hung on the panel door handle in transformer room.  2200LT: ETO and Jr.ETO observed some smoke from transformer room. Upon check, observed localised fire near no2 RSB. Duty off informed. Alarm raised and fire put off using nearby DCP fire extinguisher.   2200LT-2230LT: Cause investigated and extent of damage analysed . Measures to prevent recurrence taken.
ImmediateAction: Alarm raised. Fire put off using nearby DCP fire extinguisher. Concerned riding fitters were called for enquiry. Cause of fire identified. All external items from transformer room removed and stowed in partial deck. Extent of damage analysed. Measures to prevent recurrence discussed with staff
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage observed to Panel door, display panel and switches.
DirectCause: Possible presence of welding remnant/or hot particle in boiler suit of riding fitter crew.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All external items removed from transformer room and stowed in partial deck away from danger. Meeting carried out with staff on causes and measures to prevent wsuch incidents discussed.
ClosingNote: Meeting held with the yard team to inform vessel staff for any hot work carried out on board for securing proper permits and fire rounds.

IncidentDetails: During Bunkering VLSFO at Fujairah anchorage , there was small bunker leak from bunker line on maindeck due to hole in the bunker line under the U Clamp.
IncidentEventDetails: 060O : commenced bunkering VLSFO  0617 : pressure increased to 2.0kg  0631 : small leak found on Main deck bunker line  0632 :Barge infromed and  Bunker manifold  pressure was reduced to 0.5kg  0650 : Leak was arrested by temporary repair  0655 : Bunker manifold pressure was increased gradually  0710 : Bunker Manifold pressure was increased to 2.0kg  1012 : completed bunkering VLSFO    1055 : Bunker hose disconnected
ImmediateAction: Bunker barge informed to reduce pressure and leak was checked.  Found pin hole in the Pipe line below the clamp which  was arrested by temporary repair. Pressure was increased gradually and Bunkering operation was resumed normally till completion of bunkering.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pin hole in Bunker line on Main deck
DirectCause: Corrosion of pipeline
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pipeline to be renewed
Corrective Action: replace the defective pipe, pressure testing of bunker line to be done
ClosingNote: Defective pipe renwed and pressure tested, satisfactory

IncidentDetails: Damage to own vesseel railing at two location by buner barge.
IncidentEventDetails: 1330hrs Lt. Bunker barge was approaching from own vessel stern for bunkering operation.  1334hrs lt: Bunker barge fwd and aft spring made fast.   1336hrs lt: While picking up head line and astern line of the  barge, barge fender made hard contact with ships rails and damage the part of railing near the pilot boarding area.  1339: Bunker barge was cast off.  1412:Bunker barge again while coming along side in uncontrol manner made contact with ships rail at 1415hrs lt again foreward of the misdship area and damaged the ships railing.  1415hrs lt: Bunker barge master was instructed by the own Master to approach with minimum speed.  1425hrs lt: Bunker barge was made fast with out any further incident.  1442hrs lt: Bunker hose was connected.  1500hrs lt: Commenced bunkering  1806hrs lt: Completed bunkering operation  1818hrs lt: Bunker hose disconnected  1830hrs lt: Bunker barge cast off and moved away from own vessel safely.
ImmediateAction: Bunker barge was cast off and instructed to place Yokohama Fender only then come along side.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Small portion of the ships side rail was damaged which can be repaired on board.
DirectCause: 1) Barge approach speed was litte high and Barge Master was not able to control the movement.  2) Yokohama fender was not in place, only tyre fender was placed as normal standard pratice in the area.  3) Inadequate or lack of experiance   4) Poor communication between barge mooring team and own vessel team.  5) Strong current in the area.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1)Bunkering company to be instructed well in advance to place Yokohama fender before coming along side.  2) Weather condition to be monitored and if strong wind and current is expected bunkering operation should be abondon after discussing with office and owner/charterer.  3) Bunker barge Master handling exp details to be sought thru broker/local agent.
Corrective Action: 1)Bunkering company to be instructed well in advance to place Yokohama fender before coming along side. 2) Weather condition to be monitored and if strong wind and current is expected bunkering operation should be abondon after discussing with office and owner/charterer. 3) Bunker barge Master handling exp details to be sought thru broker/local agent.
LessionLearnt: Importance of situational awareness during critical activities and thenneed for decisive action in case of any concerns noted
ReviewComments: Satisfactory comments
ClosingNote: Incident shared with all vessels to highlight the preventive measures and dangers of taking any craft alongside with inadequate fendering and also the importance of monitoring the differenc ein freeboard of approaching craft and chekcing for possibility of other craft fenders coming in contact with railing instead of parallel body

IncidentDetails: 04.12.19/1640: Due to rolling movement of the vessel while at berth 1 head line(wire rope) and 1 Breast line rope tail had parted. Same were picked up and renewed and sent back to jetty and made fast.  06.12.19/2012-2028: Due to excessive rolling of vessel while at jetty 2 head lines(wire ropes) parted. with the assistance from jetty staff the tails were heaved onboard. One head line had to be replaced with a new one, whereas the other head line was turned end-to-end. During this period the vessel continued to roll and there was every risk of parting some more lines and/or cause severe injury to staff working in the morring area. Agent was advised to get the load arm and vapour arm disconnected and also to arrange for a support tug, to keep pushing the ship towards jetty, in order to minimize the rolling and prevent vessel from parting more lines and possible danger of drifting off the jetty. Tug arrived after 1 hr and started pushing. in the mean time the Load arm was disconnected and ropes were accordingly renewed and passed.
IncidentEventDetails: 04.12.19/1640: Due to rolling movement of the vessel while at berth 1 head line(wire rope) and 1 Breast line rope tail had parted. Same were picked up and renewed and sent back to jetty and made fast.  06.12.19/2012-2028: Due to excessive rolling of vessel while at jetty 2 head lines(wire ropes) parted. with the assistance from jetty staff the tails were heaved onboard. One head line had to be replaced with a new one, whereas the other head line was turned end-to-end. During this period the vessel continued to roll and there was every risk of parting some more lines and/or cause severe injury to staff working in the morring area. Agent was advised to get the load arm and vapour arm disconnected and also to arrange for a support tug, to keep pushing the ship towards jetty, in order to minimize the rolling and prevent vessel from parting more lines and possible danger of drifting off the jetty. Tug arrived after 1 hr and started pushing. in the mean time the Load arm was disconnected and ropes were accordingly renewed and passed.
ImmediateAction: As loading was already suspended by terminal due to wind speed picking up, advised agent to get the load arm disconnected due to rolling of vesel and also arranged for 1 tug to be alongside pushing the ship towards jetty.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Parting of 2 mooring wire ropes and 1 rope tail. HEavy stress on other wire ropes, causing dammage due to compression of lower layer of wire, excessice chaffing at point of contact with closed chock.
DirectCause: Due to external factors, vessel has been rolling continuously while at berth. Wind has been an average of 20~25 kts. The sea generated due to wind is minimum and only during strong wind periods. The jetty is not sheltered and located in a small basin with shallows around. It is possible that the underwater currents andded to the natural design of the basin are creating the turbulance, because, even when the wind reduces to approx 15 kts, the vessel is still found to be rolling easily, but continuously.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: After the parting of ropes on 06th PM, tug has been engaged to keep pushing on the ships side. Since then, even as vessel continues to roll, the effect on the lines is much reduced and cargo ops are proceeding safely.
Corrective Action: Tug assistance to be sought in case of weather deterioration while at berth. Moorings to be tended regularly.
ClosingNote: Above PA to be followed in case of non-conducive weather conditions.

IncidentDetails: Small fire occurred at engine room bottom platform near forward of the ME vibration damper.  .
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at Zhoushan shipyard and scrubber tower installation is in progress.In ER bottom platform, Scrubber pump sea water pipe modification was in progress,during gas cutting of the pipe by yard workers that time a small fire was observed on the removed lagging material near forward of the ME vibration damper at19:07hrs . IncidentDetails: AT 06:10 HRS IN POSITION 27 07.4'S 154 03.6'E STWD NOTICED OIL SPILL ON PORT GANGWAY DECK WHEN HE WENT TO DISCARD GARBE INTO GARBAGE DRUMS. STWD IMMEDIATELY REPORTED TO C/O WHO WAS ON WATCH IN BRIDGE. ALARM WAS SOUNDED AND OIL SPILL RESPONSE WAS INITIATED. IT WAS NOTICED THAT OIL HAD COME OUT OF OILY BILGE TANK VENT EARLIER AND SAVE OIL TRAY OF 0.42 M3 WAS FULL. OIL HAD ALSO SPILT ON MAIN DECK AROUND PORT GANGWAY AREA AND SOME OIL HAD STAINED PORT SIDE HULL PROBABLY DUE TO WIND EFFECT. THE TOTAL ESTIMATED QUANTITY OF SPILT OIL PLUS WATER MIXTURE IS AROUND 0.60 M3. THE EXACT TIME OF SPILL IS UNKNOWN.
IncidentEventDetails: THE STEAM WAS OPENED TO THE OILY BILGE TANK IN THE EVENING TO ADJUST THE STEAM PRESSURE AND KEEP THE OBT WARM TO MAKE THE TRANSFER.THE STEAM WAS OPENED MORE THAN NORMAL WHICH LED TO THE SLUDGE TO BECOME OVERHEATED. THE TEMP OF THE OILY BILGE TANK WAS ABT 85 DEG C . OILY BILGE TANK CONTAINS WATER MIXED WITH SLUDGE WHICH DUE TO OVERHEATING BECAME PRESSURISED AND WITH THE PRESSURE CAME OUT FROM VENT LEADING TO OIL SPILL ON DECK. NO ALARM FOR OILY BILGE TANK HIGH LEVEL HAD ACTIVATED. ALARM IS IN SATISFACTORY WORKING CONDITION.
ImmediateAction: THE SCUPPERS WAS PLUGGED AND THE CLEAN UP WAS STARTED. STEAM WAS SHUT FOR OILY BILGE TANK. AROUND 0.55 M3 OF OIL PLUS WATER MIXTURE IN SAVE OIL TRAY AND THAT COLLECTED FROM MAIN DECK WAS TRANSFERRED TO SLUDGE TANK. REST OF THE OIL PLUS WATER MIXTURE ON MAIN DECK AND PORT SIDE HULL WAS CLEANED THOROUGHLY.
DirectCause: negligence of ship staff
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Any alarm to be attended more sincerely and a thorough investigation to be carried out for the alarm activation.
ReviewComments: Detailed investigation carried out and corrective action formulated accordingly as detailed in report.
ClosingNote: Detailed Investigation Report completed

IncidentDetails: Two of vessel’s mooring ropes both from aft winch on poop deck parted during mooring operation at San Pedro De Macoris.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1218 hrs LT along with Loading Master, Divers , Agents and port officials and after Master Pilot exchanged pilot  commenced approach toward terminal..  Finally vessel was all fast at the Terminal with both anchors  and aft 6 lines to multi buoys at 1854 hrs LT. Weather condtion wind about 17-19 kts , swell 1.5 mtr.  After making fast the vessel the loading master and the diver’s foreman were not satisified with vessel’s positioning at the buoys and after consulting with the   Terminal manager ashore has ordered another pilot next morning  on 12th Dec 2019 to reposition the vessel correctly to align with cargo hose.  During mooring operation the loading master and the diver’s foreman were constantly telling pilot of vessel’s misalignment with the cargo hose and even requested pilot   To take the vessel out of the terminal and make a fresh approach to re-align . Pilot was reluctant not to take the vessel out and insisted to heave up only one anchor   And repositioned the vessel which was still not to the satisfaction of loading Master . During this operation bridge team cautioned the pilot several times of the mooring ropes getting taut.  As per Loading Master previously the same pilot has done this operations many times sucessfully.  For this unsucessfu maneouvre pilot initially blamed the location of mooring buoys at the sea terminal, later blamed the diving foreman for not being on deck continiously and finally blaming ship’s crew   During this operation two of vessel’s mooring ropes parted but were immediately replaced with new ones without causing any delay in mooring operation
ImmediateAction: Immediately changed the mooring ropes with new ones without causing any delay to berthing
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: At 1218 hrs LT along with Loading Master, Divers , Agents and port officials and after Master Pilot exchanged pilot  commenced approach toward terminal..  Finally vessel was all fast at the Terminal with both anchors  and aft 6 lines to multi buoys at 1854 hrs LT. Weather condtion wind about 17-19 kts , swell 1.5 mtr.  After making fast the vessel the loading master and the diver’s foreman were not satisified with vessel’s positioning at the buoys and after consulting with the   Terminal manager ashore has ordered another pilot next morning  on 12th Dec 2019 to reposition the vessel correctly to align with cargo hose.  During mooring operation the loading master and the diver’s foreman were constantly telling pilot of vessel’s misalignment with the cargo hose and even requested pilot   To take the vessel out of the terminal and make a fresh approach to re-align . Pilot was reluctant not to take the vessel out and insisted to heave up only one anchor   And repositioned the vessel which was still not to the satisfaction of loading Master . During this operation bridge team cautioned the pilot several times of the mooring ropes getting taut.  As per Loading Master previously the same pilot has done this operations many times sucessfully.  For this unsucessfu maneouvre pilot initially blamed the location of mooring buoys at the sea terminal, later blamed the diving foreman for not being on deck continiously and finally blaming ship’s crew   During this operation two of vessel’s mooring ropes parted but were immediately replaced with new ones without causing any delay in mooring operation
DirectCause: Direct Cause:   1. The vessel was berthed at the MBM with Northerly winds and swell. This was against the normal practice of the terminal whereby the berth the vessel when head winds are experienced. This beam winds and swell caused excessive force on the mooring ropes which parted. There were no additional buoys available to pass additional ropes in such extraordinary weather condition.     2. Insufficient ropes deployed on the port side considering the not normal weather conditions which prevailed during the operation.   Indirect cause :  3. Master did not exercise his authority to abort the operation on the first day when it was apparent that the vessel was drifting excessively to starboard under the prevailing wind conditions.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mooring ropes to be inspected before berthing, id excessive stress coming should not heave up with winch power.
Corrective Action: The 2 parted ropes to be replaced with new ropes.
ClosingNote: closed.
