IncidentDetails: On 14.12.19 at 1435LT, while lowering stbd side lifeboat, as required by flag state inspector, lifeboat break did not hold and boat lowered all the way down to water level.    Lifeboat dynamic break band opened up, inspected,cleaned and found in working order.    Lifeboat picked up and secured in place, inspected and found bottom aft, below the propeller shaft- damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed at Fremantle, port side alongside.  Annual safety inspection being carried out by flag state inspector.  Inspector instructed vessel to swing out stbd side(sea side) lifeboat to deck level for testing.  All required procedure followed and prepared boat for lowering.  While lowering lifeboat, lifeboat break did not hold and lowered all the way down to water level.  Crew tried to heave up boat with motor & handle but unable to do so as gear rotating freely.  Lifeboat dynamic break band opened up, inspected,cleaned and found in working order.  Lifeboat picked up and secured in place, inspected and found bottom aft, below the propeller shaft- damaged.
ImmediateAction: Lifeboat dynamic break band opened up, inspected,cleaned and found in working order.    Lifeboat picked up and secured in place, inspected and found bottom aft, below the propeller shaft- damaged.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Lifeboat inspected and found bottom aft, below the propeller shaft- damaged. Also few items inside boat damaged due to water ingress.
DirectCause: While lowering lifeboat, lifeboat break did not hold.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To regularly check condition of lifeboat break.
Corrective Action: Service technician attendance lined up and the brakes examined as well as the brake arm adjusted for proper grip. Boat lowering carried out again for the same boat and verified both by service technician and by the class surveyor
LessionLearnt: Brakes for davit and winch operated L/boats to be verified for correct position in normal stowed condition. SOLAS training manual to be referred to in case of doubt and MSIs/TSIs to be consulted.
ReviewComments: Incident shared across the fleet and all vessels asked to verify. Responses vetted by respective TSIs and MSIs
ClosingNote: All corrective actions carried out satisfactorily and clear report from class presented to flag within due date of dispensation

IncidentDetails: At around 1803 Hrs during E/R unmanned condition,heard a thud sound in the whole of accomodation followed by No 2 HFO purifier stop and Purifier platform Fire alarm.  Upon investigation noticed oil mist and oil around purifier with Oil outlet and sludge outlet pipe dislodged from connection.  Purifier Bowl hood(Sludge cover) found completely broken along the length at 2.0 different places.Electric motor frame found broken and in dislodged condition.  Gap in friction boss where friction pads are inserted found to be largely enlarged.
IncidentEventDetails: 1700 Hrs during E/R unmanned condition.  1803: Purifier abnormal stop with Fire alarm activated.  1804: E/R manned and checked purifier room.  1805: FFO Purifier No 2 breaker put off and FO inlet valve shut.
ImmediateAction: No 2 HFO purifierNo 2 electrically isolated and mechanical break applied.Fuel feed stopped to the purifier.  Started stby Purifier and checked parameters for normal operation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No 2 HFO Purifier motor completely damaged.Purifier frame cover broken beyond repairs.  Operating chamber cover,friction boss and spiral gear wheel damaged.
DirectCause: Imbalance due to uneven sludge accumulation in the bowl.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Depending on fuel quality decrease desludge interval.
ClosingNote: Learning to be imparted to all concerned and proper monitoring to be carried out whenever a new fuel is in use.

IncidentDetails: On 20-Aug-2019 @ 17:42 Hrs. vessel experienced blackout due to governor malfunctioning.
IncidentEventDetails: Initially AE#2 and #3 were running on load,   For maintenance purpose #3 AE was to be offloaded and #1 AE was therefore started.   Auto Synchro fail alarm recieved as frequency was slightly hunting.  Same was adjusted and AE's Synchronised again in auto mode.  All three AE's were running in parallel for few minutes.   Later observed AE #3 had tripped on Reverse power and AE #1 had tripped due to overspeed.  Therefore AE #2 tripped due to overload.   Preferential Trip had also taken place.  Emergency Generator had come on load and later AE #3 was started to resume power with auto sequential starts activated and propulsion commenced, later generator paralled with AE #2.  Attempt was made to restart AE #1 after crankcase & LO filters inspection, but had again overspeed tripped.  Governor was flushed using Diesel/ Lub oil an filled with fresh oil. All fuel pump racks, linkages checked and eased up. Governor speed setting reduced. But still had tripped on overspeed.  Later governor was replaced with old spare governor, and AE tried out satisfactorily.  All parameters checked found working satisfactory when taken on load.
ImmediateAction: Emergency Generator had come on load and later AE #3 was started to resume power/propulsion back to normal, later paralled with AE#2.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Initially AE#2 and #3 were running on load,   For maintenance purpose #3 AE was to be offloaded and #1 AE was therefore started.   Auto Synchro fail alarm recieved as frequency was slightly hunting.  Same was adjusted and AE's Synchronised again in auto mode.  All three AE's were running in parallel for few minutes.   Later observed AE #3 had tripped on Reverse power and AE #1 had tripped due to overspeed.  Therefore AE #2 tripped due to overload.   Preferential Trip had also taken place.  Emergency Generator had come on load and later AE #3 was started to resume power with auto sequential starts activated and propulsion commenced, later generator paralled with AE #2.  Attempt was made to restart AE #1 after crankcase & LO filters inspection, but had again overspeed tripped.  Governor was flushed using Diesel/ Lub oil an filled with fresh oil. All fuel pump racks, linkages checked and eased up. Governor speed setting reduced. But still had tripped on overspeed.  Later governor was replaced with old spare governor, and AE tried out satisfactorily.  All parameters checked found working satisfactory when taken on load.
DirectCause: AE #1 Governor complete Failure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Governors were overhauled by shore technician in Dry Dock in the month of May 2018 in Zhoushan China,   No Service Reports available onboard for same, therefore not sure of Maker Authorized Dealer had carried out job.   Recently AE#2 Governor was replaced with Re-conditioned Governor as old governor Oil was leaking through Bottom Oil Seal.  At present AE#3 frequency found hunting, therefore unable to synchonize Aux Engines on Auto mode. Same will be replaced with Reconditioned governor soon.
Corrective Action: Replace the AE Governor
ClosingNote: AE #1 Governor was replaced with spare governor

IncidentDetails: Oil spill at pump room Bottom due to rupture of the TCP lower bearing oil cooling line
IncidentEventDetails: 1345 - P/Rm Bilge alarm activated  1346 - P/rm Fixed gas system change to lower platform  1348 - Gas on FGD - HC-0/H2S-0  1348 - P/m in pumproom and confirmed leakage from TCP and to stop same  1349 - C.off in Pumproom / TCP RPM reduced  1350 - TCP Suction made negative, tripped and Disch /suction valves closed  1352 - Manual Suction Valve From Slops Shut   1355 - P/Rm Suction changed to high   1410 - TCP Damaged Line Dismantled and plugged  1515 - New Line Fixed on TCP  1517 - Suction line/ pump filled and confirmed No leakage  1520 - TCP Rolling  1540 - TCP Control/ Eductor started and pump out bilges port and stbd to SlopP  1545 - Washing p/rm Bottom   1630 - Complete Waashing / Educting p/rm to Slop Port  1630 - Clean up started
ImmediateAction: Pump RPm reduced, Line drained  and Stopped.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oil Cooling Line to lower bearing  for TCP P/P ruptured
DirectCause: wear and tear of copper pipe caused it to part at the pipe near the coupling.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Renew with New coper pipe
Corrective Action: thorough inspection of the cooling lines for all the COPs.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: Ship was anchored at Quanzhou #3 anchorage awaiting berthing. Winds experienced at anchorage was approx 35 kts, however, swell was approx 1.0 mtr. Holding ground was very good and vessel maintained her position. Having knowledge that the weather was not favourable, there was no option for vessel to drift, due to heavy fishing boat density and other coastal traffic. Several other ships were also anchored in the viscinity and no reporting of draging anchor was noted. Anchor was supposed to be heaved at 1000 lt, in order to have POB at 1100lt, however stations were called off, due to channel being occupied b outbound traffic. Later agents advised that Pilot was booked for 1545lt and advised to heave anchor. When anchor party reached forward and tried to remove the bow stopper, it was noticed that the anchor chain link was tightly against the stopper. this caused the stopper and its securing pin to be stresses. The securing pin was bent and could not be removed, thus restricting the heaving of anchor. Anchor party, immediately heaved on the anchor chain after which they used a hammer and straightened the securing pin. The pin was now freely removed, now stopper lifted freely and anchor heaved.
IncidentEventDetails: Ship was anchored at Quanzhou #3 anchorage awaiting berthing. Winds experienced at anchorage was approx 35 kts, however, swell was approx 1.0 mtr. Holding ground was very good and vessel maintained her position. Having knowledge that the weather was not favourable, there was no option for vessel to drift, due to heavy fishing boat density and other coastal traffic. Several other ships were also anchored in the viscinity and no reporting of draging anchor was noted. Anchor was supposed to be heaved at 1000 lt, in order to have POB at 1100lt, however stations were called off, due to channel being occupied b outbound traffic. Later agents advised that Pilot was booked for 1545lt and advised to heave anchor. When anchor party reached forward and tried to remove the bow stopper, it was noticed that the anchor chain link was tightly against the stopper. this caused the stopper and its securing pin to be stresses. The securing pin was bent and could not be removed, thus restricting the heaving of anchor. Anchor party, immediately heaved on the anchor chain after which they used a hammer and straightened the securing pin. The pin was now freely removed, now stopper lifted freely and anchor heaved.
ImmediateAction: Engine used ahead to ease weight on anchor cable. Chain heaved in a little to ease weight on bow stopper. Securing pin was hammered to straighten and remove.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bow stopper securing pin found bent.
DirectCause: Adverse weather conditions, resulting in excess weight on anchoring system.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Best not to anchor or remain at anchor in such weather condition,,,, if the option is available.
ClosingNote: Vessel to follow Company's policy of heaving up anchor if wind force more than 6.

IncidentDetails: Vessel lost almost 600 ltrs of AE Lub oil .
IncidentEventDetails: 1.All purifiers were stopped to carry out for arresting steam leak for Heaters of Purifer before Port entry at anchorage.   2.At 2008 hrs after Main engine was secured at berth, Purifer was planned to start again. AE L.O Pufirer feed was started first without starting the purifer. After Feed pump was started, Sludge tank High Level Alarm sounded. Sludge tank level was 1.45 M3 ( alarm level at 1.6 m3) before starting the purifer. D/E went to check Sludge tank level and found Level around 1.7 m3. D/E started the sludge pump and came back to control room to see if alarm for sludge tank is gone. However while at Control room, AE L.O Overflow tank level low alarm sounded. D/E Immediately Went to purifer room and found L.O. Coming Out of Scupper for Sealing water of AE L.O. Purifer. D/E immediately Stopped the AE L.O. feed pump. Investigation was carried out and found 3-way valve For AE L.O Purifer ( 3 way valve for Supply to purifer and Recirculation to AE when purifer Desludge operation is being carried out) is stuck in open Position therby supplying to Purifer instead of Return to Overfflow tank.  Found after the last stoppage of purifer the 3-WAY SOLENOID Valve for feed valve has not vented the air inside the valve piston therby keeping the feed Valve in Open Position itself. While Starting the feed pump at 2000 hrs, The stuck feed valve supplied oil to purifer itself and thus creating on overflow situation, even though purifer was stopped condition.
ImmediateAction: 1.Clean up action carried out for purifer space.  2.Three way solenoid valve for Feed valve was checked and overhauled with new O-rings and proper lubrication.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel lost almost 600 ltrs of AE Lub oil.
DirectCause: On analysis, it was found that 3-way valve for AE L.O Purifer ( 3 way valve for Supply to purifer and Recirculation to AE when purifer Desludge operation is being carried out) is stuck in open Position therby supplying to Purifer instead of Return to Overfflow tank.  Found after the last stoppage of purifer the 3-WAY SOLENOID Valve for feed valve has not vented the air inside the valve piston thereby keeping the feed Valve in Open Position itself. The stuck feed valve supplied oil to purifer itself and thus creating on overflow situation, even though purifer was stopped condition.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A.Routine cleaning and inspection to be carried out for all the 3-way soleniod valves for each purifier.  B.Routine cleaning and inspection to be carried out for the suction line filter for each purifer.  C. All 3-way solenoid valve O-rings and required parts to renew once spares received.
Corrective Action: Vessel CE to eduacte all engineers in a traing scession for the imporatnce of sounding associated tanks before operating any purifier and also imporatnce of monitoring tank at frequent interval
ClosingNote: Noted all the corrective action implemeted hence the task can be closed

IncidentDetails: SHORE GANGWAY 02 STEPS WERE DAMAGED BY COMING IN CONTACT WITH SHIP'S BULWARK PORT SIDE.
IncidentEventDetails: V/L WAS LOADING CORN AT DRYFUS LAGOS TERMINAL IN ROSARIO/ARGENTINA.THEN LOADING WAS STOPPED DUE TO DE-BALLASTING WAS NOT COPPING UPP WITH THE LOADING RATE.  1950 LT: LOADING STOPPED.  2000LT:AGENT/FOREMAN AWAY  2010LT:SHORE OPERATOR RAISED AND RETRACTED THE SHORE GANGWAY.  2130LT:D/O NOTICED THAT GANGWAY BOTTOM STEP WAS ABOUT TO TOUCH THE SHIP'S BULWARK ABREAST NO.3 HOLD PORT SIDE.  2130LT:D/O ALERTED SHORE WATCHMAN AS HE COULDN'T FIND THE SHORE GANGWAY OPERATOR.  2145LT:AS NO OPERATOR AVAILABLE,SHORE GANGWAY TOUCHED THE BULWARK AS V/L WAS COMING UP DUE TO DE-BALLASTING IN PREOGRESS.  2230LT:SHORE GANGWAY OPERATOR CAME AND CLEARED THE SHORTE GANGWAY FROM SHIP'S BULWARK.
ImmediateAction: WATCHMAN ALERTED,TRY TO CONTACT THE SHORE OPERATOR.BUT COULD'NT FOUND.  BY THE TIME SHORE GANGWAY OPERATOR CAME THE GANGWAY MADE CONTACT WITH THE V/L BULWARK.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 02 STEPS OF SHORE GANGWAY BENDED,NO DAMAGE OCCURED TO SHIP'S BULWARK
DirectCause: LACK OF COMMUNICATION
Proposedcorrectiveactions: D/O TO BE ALERT AND REPORT ANY INCIDENT TO SENIOR OFFICERS IMMIDIEATELY
ClosingNote: CAR found satisfactory and closed

IncidentDetails: #2 AE  pipe leading to Centrifugal filter sheared off from the union.
IncidentEventDetails: #2 AE was on load, during morning rounds observed all parameters were normal & no leaks on the AE. Around 08:46 hrs it was observed that oil was leaking from the pipe going to Centrifugal filter.
ImmediateAction: Standby AE was started & taken on load. By the time the AE could be stopped Low sump level alarm sounded. It was observed once the leak stopped that the union of the pipe had sheared off.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Union sheared off. Oil loss of about 500 liters.
DirectCause: Defective material.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New union fitted
ClosingNote: issue highlighted to shipyard.

IncidentDetails: During Pilotage vessel was transiting Vungtao channel , vessel had a hard contact with unlit barge on stbd side. After inspection found few indendations in Fore peak tank at Bulbous Bow.
IncidentEventDetails: During Pilotage vsl had taken avoiding action for head on one inbound large container vessel "Kota Ledarna" by altering to stbd. After clearing vessel, started altering to port to come back to centre of channel. While altering to port felt strong jerk. On checking stbd side found that one unlit barge had made hard  contact with vessel.
ImmediateAction: Immidiately Master/Pilot went to Stbd side Bridge wing and found one barge very close to vessel. Immidiately Master gave Helm hard over to stbd to clear barge from propeller and then alter to Port to keep vessel inside the channel.  Immidiately Speed reduced to dead slow ahead. Subsequently vessel dropped anchor outside the pilot station at no.2 H anchorage as per port control adv. Pilot boat checked the ship side and observed hard scratch marks on bulbus bow stbd side. Master checked with Pilots that all 6 crew members on the barge was ok. Vessel commence inspecting throughly under deck passage and started sounding all ballast , Fuel tanks, cargo holds , BT room for any damages. Later Vessel open Fore peak tank after ventilation and noticed few indendations inside  Fore peak tank at Bulbous Bow.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage / Indentations to Bulbous bow.
DirectCause: Unlit barge inside channel without AIS,  Inefficient lookout due to back scattering of shore lights/ other several barges in channel on stbd side(which had strong white halogen lights illuminated all over their decks. Same time bigger container vessel was passing port side for which action of altering to stbd side of channel was taken.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always keep additional Lookout at Foscle.  Duty officers to tuned the radar properly.   Bridge team to counter check Pilot plans.
LessionLearnt: Better radar watch to be followed
ReviewComments: None
ClosingNote: None

IncidentDetails: While cleaning ME FO 2nd Hot Filte,Oil spillage from Top cover due to improper isolation of changeover valve. Approx 300 Ltrs of Oil spilled out, ME was stopped to renew the top cover damage O-rings and Filter's changed.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. At 2027 hrs on 19th Dec 2019, ME F.O 2nd filter differential pressure alarm sounded. D/E called up 2E for cleaning the filter since IG and tank cleaning operations were being carried out.  2. After arrival of 2E, it was decided to open up the filter. However During the same time since Too many operations were happening ( COP used for tank cleaning stopped, Stripper steam start/stop and also regulating the IG O2%),   2E asked D/E to open up filter and replace with spare filter. 4E along with duty oiler went to open the filter and 2nd engineer stayed in control room to maintain IG and COP plant parameters.  3. Since ME F.O 2nd filter is a duplex type, One of the filter was isolated and vent opened to depressurize the filter. It was observed by DE, that no oil is coming out of vent and hence he decided to open up the filter top cover for replacement of filter. However while opening up the Filter top cover slowly , Oil started coming out of Filter top cover gap with the body. DE immediately tightened the bolt back to hold the cover. But the oil flow was not stopping.   4. DE immediately informed 2E and CE and after informing Bridge Main engine was stopped immediately at 2125 hrs. FO circulating pump was stopped and system valves were isolated to control the oil flow to outside the body. After siolating the filter, it was noticed approx 300 ltrs of oil has splashed to the platform and bulkhead. ER parties assembeld and Clean up operations started.  5. Filter top cover was opened and found O-ring for Top cover has given away while oil started leaking from the top cover and while tightening cover back in position, the O-ring was being in between the Cover and Body. Both Filters were replaced with spare cleaned one and O-rings were also renewed. It was also found that Filter Vent Was Chocked and same was cleared. Main Engine was restarted at 2220 hrs after trying out 2nd filter. Clean up operations were continued.
ImmediateAction: Main engine was Stopped, Filter's replaced with cleaned spare filters and cover O-ring renewed with new spare and Main engine operation continued.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oil spillage from top Cover of filter splashing into surronding bulkheads, platform and equipments
DirectCause: improper isolation of filter, vent cock getting chocked leading to confused decision making
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training, Proper supervision while working on pressurized systems.
ClosingNote: Training carried out.

IncidentDetails: Cargo hardened
IncidentEventDetails: Around 0210Hrs LT / 23rd Dec 19 temporary stop disch in CHold No.7 due to area of the middle hold going forward found to be hardened.
ImmediateAction: Notified office and P & I immediately
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Carge hardened
DirectCause: PNI initial report suspected heating source from no. 4 FOT (C), causing the cargo cake/hardened.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Regular monitoring of FO temperature specially to the tanks bordering the cargo holds.   2. The engineers should be advised of the precise nature of the product and the proposed stowage plan well in advance of loading so that a suitable fuel oil management strategy can be adopted to minimise the risk of heat damage to the cargo.  3. Install temperature sensors in the FO tanks.  4. Where possible, heated fuel tanks which do not border the cargo holds should be used.  5. If it is necessary to heat a fuel tank which is in direct contact with the cargo, the oil should be heated to the minimum extent necessary to allow safe transfer. Excessive heating of fuel oil and fuel residues should be avoided. Similarly, hot oil should not be transferred into fuel tanks bordering the cargo holds.
ClosingNote: P and I Surveyor appointed and issue closed

IncidentDetails: During loading Cargo ( Jet A1  ) at yeosu / South korea ( Voy 1902 ) , first foot sampling was carried out  from No.1 W  onwards and during sampling of 5S COT the sampler bucket  parted from the tape ( approx less than a foot length )and fell into the cargo tank ( COT : 5S ), while lifting the bucket up . The sample / guage point is approx 9 metres from the cargo pump area .
IncidentEventDetails: 18 DEC 2019 / ( 0900-1200lt )  : Pre arrival Cargo Load checks carried out as per OP-TKR-04 and found all cargo and associated equipments  functioning satisfactory , in which the tapes for the UTI / Sampler / Dip was also checked and reported condition was satisfactory and no cracks .    19 DEC 19 / 0900 LT : Vessel berth Port side / A side at GS CALTEX TERMINAL / YEOSU / SOUTH KOREA.     19 DEC 19 / 0942 LT : Cargo ( Jet A1 ) Loading commenced .    19 DEC 19 / 1240 LT: Cargo First foot sampling was commenced from no.1W onwards along with cargo surveyor .    19 Dec 19 / 1330 LT: Sampler bucket with tape parted and fell into no.5S COT while sampling .    19 DEC 19 / 1400 LT : Completed  First foot sampling without any undue delay by using the backup sampler.    20 DEC 19 /  1618LT : Completed Loading .
ImmediateAction: Damaged sampler was removed / Investigated  and sampling continued with spare sampler and completed first foot  sampling  in order to avoid any undue delay leading to commercial delays .    Further investigations was carried out and likely cause was analysed as the sampler and the tapes are inspected during pre arrival cargo checks and the same was reported condition satisfactory .    All deck crew were briefed on the consequences of any guaging / sampling / measuring equipments falling in cargo tanks and also stressed the safe working tanker practise on how to excercise  due diligence when such operations are carried out .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sampler Bucket along with the foot length tape got parted and fell into No.5S COT during first foot sampling while loading Cargo : Jet A1 at Yeosu / south korea .    Sampler has fell approx 9 meters from the cargo pump suction area .    Photos of the damage portion of the sampler is attached .
DirectCause: Frequent use of the Sampler during load / Discharge operations and inadvertent closing of the tank valve without proper analysis that the bucket has housed inside the sampler tube leads to bends in the tape normally observed a metre from the sampler bucket revealed on  detailed investigation which over a period of time due to weight and contact with the cargo tank deck plating and valve  might have resulted in developing cracks on the bend portion cauing it strained and giving way .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training has been carried out for all crew who are involved in handling of the cargo measuring / gauging instruments and stressed the consequence if any equipment accessories fall into the cargo tank . Due diligence has to be carried out in routine tanker practice . Colour coding of the tape is further proposed , as to easily identify from the sampler glass when the sampler bucket is fully housed in the sampler tube and  not by touch /feel/sound as inappropriate practice will lead to inadvertent closing of the tank valve along with the tape ,  that will damage the tape .
Corrective Action: Training has been carried out for all crew who are involved in handling of the cargo measuring / gauging instruments and stressed the consequence if any equipment accessories fall into the cargo tank . Due diligence has to be carried out in routine tanker practice . Colour coding of the tape is further proposed , as to easily identify from the sampler glass when the sampler bucket is fully housed in the sampler tube and  not by touch /feel/sound as inappropriate practice will lead to inadvertent closing of the tank valve along with the tape ,  that will damage the tape .
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: From the retrieved bucket, it's apparent that the damage had occurred due to inappropriate handling of the equipment and same has been discussed onboard to avoid recurrence.

DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL

IncidentDetails: Forward spring line from mooring winch MW-4 parted from the mechanical spliced area due to age and poor condition of  wire rope
IncidentEventDetails: All mooring lines were adjusted by bosun and one AB at about 9 'O' clock in the morning taking in due consideration that it is rising tide till 12 'O' clock. All lines were sufficiently slacked to compensate for high water for about 2 hrs time. At about 11 'O" clock bosun was with ch offficer for carrying out load testing of hose handling crane. The tension on the mooring lines at that time was not very much. How ever the mooring wire rope passed from Mooring Winch no-4 ( only single wire) Parted from the mechanical spliced area. The Brake for MW-4 was adjusted to correct rendering point how ever the brakes didn't render at the time of icident
ImmediateAction: All mooring lines slacked immediately to avoid further excess tension devoloping on the other lines. period for checking mooring reduced. Duty officers and deck crew briefed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO DAMAGE/ INJURY FOR YARD/ SHIP STAFF
DirectCause: improper checks by deck patrol. improper monitoring.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replace old wire ropes
Corrective Action: mooring wire to be replaced.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: While loading Iron ore pellets in No.5 cargo hold Shore loader, came in to contantact with Ship's crane by negligence of operator.
IncidentEventDetails: 21.12.19/1350LT: Stopped loading in No. 1 Hold, then Shore loader shifted from cargo Hold no.1 to Cargo Hold No.5 to resume loading.  21.12.19/1420LT: Resumed loading in No.5 Hold. During Loading in No.5 Shore loader moving fore & Aft to load cargo evenly in Cargo Hold.  21.12.19/1445LT: Shore loader contacted with Ship's crane due to operator negligence and stopped loading operation .  Later foreman boarded vessel and same was verified by foreman that stevedore loader operator has accidently touched the ship’s crane No.4 while loading in No.5 Cargo Hold.  Cargo Loaded at time of Incident: Approx 6306.00 MT in NO.5 Hold / Total Cargo loaded :20250.00MT  Draft at time of Incident: Fwd 6.70m Mid -7.38m Aft 8.06m (Port side)  Air Draft on Hatch No.5: 11.15m  Location of Shore Loader: Shore operator was moving shore loader in fore and aft direction in between Cargo Hold No.5 for loading cargo evenly. Shore loader positioned at center of hold No.5 once cargo operation ceased.  Location of Ship Crane: Crane No.4 was parked at its position on top of Hatch No.4 with crane boom facing in fwd direction resting at parking position.  Weather Condition: N’ly / BF – 3 , Calm sea, Visibility – Good   Mooring Status: Ship’s Line Fwd and Aft (4+2) / All lines were taught.
ImmediateAction: Immediately loading stoped by operator, informed foreman, inspection carried out bychief officer on No.4 crane for damages.  Issued LOP and Stevedore damage Report to terminal.  Informed all concerned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.No.4 crane torret head plaform (checkered plate 5mm thickness) dented with a size of plate approx    1 mtr x 0.3 mtr.    2.Two Guard Rails pipes got bent.
DirectCause: Complete negligence of shore loader  operator.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Terminal supplied material for Repair work.   Repair work will be carried out by ship's staff  at Next anchorage or at berth.
ClosingNote: Satisfactory repairs carried-out to the platform and railings.

IncidentDetails: Main engine unit no.1 aft fuel injector pocket was giving out exhaust gas and sparks.
IncidentEventDetails: ME performance was carried out on 31/12/2019 .After completion of  performance of Main engine found exhaust gas leakage around no 1 unit fuel injector area, after some time the exhaust leak increased and slight sparks were seen coming out of fuel injector pocket( aft).
ImmediateAction: Bridge was informed and ME was stopped to rectify the fault and for further investigation. It was observed that one of the stud holding the injector has broken and needs replacement. The new stud was installed in place and ME tried out and voyage resumed. The ME was stopped from 15:50 to  16:32 LT on 31st Dec 2019
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One fuel injector stud broken and the surrounding area has carbon deposits
DirectCause: Material failure and improper tightening of injector studs
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All units fuel injector stud tightness to be checked
Corrective Action: All units fuel injector stud tightness to be checked
ClosingNote: All units fuel injector studs checked for tightness and external damage.

IncidentDetails: Loss Of Stern Tube System Oil From Sampling Valve into Aft Bilges
IncidentEventDetails: Stern Tube Sump Tank Low level Alarm occoured.Found tank level at minimum. Observed Aft Bilges with Oil and sampling valve open due to vibratin. Lashing was loose and lock nut loose. Lost about 370 Lts of BIOSTAT Oil into Bilges
ImmediateAction: Stopped Stern Tube pump from running dry. Immidiatly shut sampling valve, lashed it and tightened lock nut. Pumped out emulsified oil water mixture from aft bilges to BSOT. Cleaned & Soapwashed Aft Bilge well. Stern tupe sump tank inspected for condition of oil. Topped up new oil into ST sump tank from 200Lts drums. System put back to normal. Main Engine Turned for 10 Minutes for Proper outreach of Stern tube bearing oil.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Loss Of Oil
DirectCause: Human Error - For Not Lashing & Locking Valve Lock Nut after Sampling  Vibration - Excess Vibration running across the centreline & at the aft of the ship (Engine Room Bottom Platform) even when Main engine not running cause the valve to open
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional Ball Valves Installed as a secondary means of isolation and positive closing even if vibration occours.   Training Of Engine Staff to be carried out for proper sampling procedures and securing arrangements of valves.
Corrective Action: Additional Ball Valves Installed as a secondary means of isolation and positive closing even if vibration occours.  Training Of Engine Staff to be carried out for proper sampling procedures and securing arrangements of valves.
ClosingNote: Additional ball valve was fitted

IncidentDetails: When the Discharge operation was in progress,  at 0025Hrs LT on 28/12/2019, one of the forward spring ropes(Polypropylene)  parted due to surging of the vessel. Immediately terminal was notified and cargo discharge operation was suspended at 0030 Hrs LT.  At  0042Hrs the Flexible Hose was disconnected as per terminal’s instruction by ship and shore staff.  Parted rope was replaced with another rope and made fast at 0054 Hrs. In addition to that, Additional extra spare spring ropes from Fwd and Aft were passed  and secured the vessel safely by 0130 Hrs on 28/12/2019 and vessel conveyed the readiness to resume discharging.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was berthed at P1/NP berth at Skikda, Algeria for discharging HSD  port side alongside with ropes 03 Head Lines , 02 Fwd spring lines,  04 Stern Lines and 02 Aft spring lines as advised by the pilot for safe operation. There was no provision on the Jetty for passing Fwd and Aft Breast lines. Vessel commenced discharging of Cargo on 26/Dec/2019 at 2306 hrs LT through shore Chiksan(Loading arm) connected to ship Manifold No.4 Port side.  While discharging of HSD was in progress on 27/Dec/2019 around 1200 Hrs LT ,Vessel  began experiencing surging due to swell  and it was observed the shore arm was experiencing stresses due to vessel’s movement.  Same was notified to terminal.  After discussion with Terminal for safe operation, at 1412 LT on 27/Dec/2019 , Discharge operation  was suspended. Shore Loading Arm  was disconnected and connected Flexible Hose 1x 8” to 6P Ship’s Manifold for safe operation as advised by the terminal.   At 1630 Hrs discharge operation was resumed. At the same time, deck watch keepers numbers were increased for continuous Monitoring of Mooring Ropes and  Adjustment as required.    When the Discharge operation was in progress,  at 0025Hrs LT on 28/12/2019, one of the forward spring ropes(Polypropylene)  parted due to surging of the vessel. Immediately terminal was notified and cargo discharge operation was suspended at 0030 Hrs LT.  At  0042Hrs the Flexible Hose was disconnected as per terminal’s instruction by ship and shore staff.  Parted rope was replaced with another rope and made fast at 0054 Hrs. In addition to that, Additional extra spare spring ropes from Fwd and Aft were passed  and secured the vessel safely by 0130 Hrs on 28/12/2019 and vessel conveyed the readiness to resume discharging. At 0130 Hrs, Loading Master boarded  and instructed Vessel to suspend  the discharging operation until sea condition improves.   Loading Master advised vessel that the Operations of another Vessel berthed at the adjacent berth No. P3/NP had been suspended by the terminal at 1830 Hrs on 27/Dec/2019 due to swell and will resume only after sea condition improves. And the cargo operation of our vessel was continued  as this berth had to be vacated the soonest for another  vessel and hence discharging was not suspended beforehand.  Loading Master advised Vessel may perhaps resume Discharge Operation by 28/12/2019  PM hours as  Sea Condition is anticipated to  improve.  Additional mooring ropes were passed by the vessel for safe docking alongside the terminal in discussion with loading Master.    The Mooring ropes deployed as follows.  Head lines -03 Nos  Fwd spring lines: 04 Nos  Stern lines: 04 Nos  Aft spring lines : 04 Nos  No provision on the jetty for passing fwd breast and aft breast lines.    Cause Analysis    The layout and location of the terminal is prone to swell approaching directly from the Sea . According to Terminal , the berth P1/NP is particularly exposed to swell and the suspension of Cargo operation of Vessels berthed was normal until sea condition improved. Loading Master advised vessel that the Operations of another Vessel berthed at the adjacent berth No. P3/NP had been suspended at 1830 Hrs on 27/Dec/2019 due to swell and will resume only after sea condition improves. And the cargo operation of our vessel was continued by the Terminal as this berth had to be vacated the soonest for another  vessel and hence discharging was not suspended by the Terminal beforehand.
ImmediateAction: On parting of one of the Fwd Spring Rope, Immediately terminal was notified and cargo discharge operation was suspended .   Additional extra spare spring ropes were passed instantly and secured the vessel safely . Loading Master boarded the and advised Vessel to disconnect the Flexible cargo hose and suspend  the discharging operation until sea condition improves.    Additional mooring ropes were passed by the vessel for safe docking alongside the terminal in discussion with loading Master.                The Mooring ropes deployed as follows.                Head lines -03 Nos                Fwd spring lines: 04 Nos              Stern lines: 04 Nos               Aft spring lines : 04 Nos               No provision  was on the jetty for passing fwd breast and aft breast lines.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: Officers and Crew should be briefed about condition of sea and swell expected while being alongside in port, if expected mooring configuration should be enhanced. Proper mooring line management should be followed to avoid any chaffing of ropes, watch keeping arrangements should be altered to enhance close monitoring of mooring lines. If still mooring lines are getting affected then cargo should be stopped and mooring lines should be changed and ports assistance should be sought for tugs ect during this duration.
Corrective Action: Vessel had taken the required precautions beforehand itself  prior arrival at this port for the protection of Mooring ropes as surging was anticipated at the berth. IncidentDetails: Auxiliary boiler fuel oil system return line drain valve opened accidently.
IncidentEventDetails: While replacing Boiler Fuel oil pump No #1 with an overhauled pump. Due to restricted place and non-availability of lifting eye or any suitable strong point for a chain block, the pump was lifted manually by rope from top end which caused drain valve to come in contact with the rope accidentally which in turn caused drain valve to open slightly and causing oil to flow into BILGE SEPARATED OIL TANK.
ImmediateAction: Drain valve was immediately shut and Lashed with wire.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Small quanity of fuel leak to bilge separated oil tank.
DirectCause: while carrying the main job, ignorance to effecting sub components.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: while carrying out the main job, all surrounding sub component which can be effected to be  checked after completion, and rechecked when system put in use.
Corrective Action: while carrying out the main job, all surrounding sub component which can be effected to be  checked after completion, and rechecked when system put in use.
LessionLearnt: There was a gap observed in checking  all surrounding sub component which can be effected to be checked after any job  completion, and rechecked when system put in use.Engine room staff was required to be briefed..
ReviewComments: satisfactory

IncidentDetails: On the 16th of Dec 2019, while the Vessel was made fast starboard side at Esso- Pulau Ayer Chawan terminal, Singapore (ESSO-4). After the completion of cargo, while tightening the ropes, ship staff realized that one of the stern lines had parted.
IncidentEventDetails: 15 DEC 2019  1854LT Vessel All fast at Exxonmobil Pulau Ayer Chawan Terminal, Esso 4 B, Singapore (2+2+2 - F and A)  16  DEC 2019  2310LT Loading completed  17 DEC 2019  0015LT Loading arm disconnected (1x 12 in Stbd side no1 manifold  )  17 DEC 2019  0020LT Deck safety rounds taken, ropes adjusted as required  17 DEC 2019  0130LT Completed Tank inspection  17 DEC 2019  0130LT During safety round and while tendering to the Aft Stern lines, it was observed that the aft outer line (aft Stbd winch) was parted at the eye where it is connected to the Mandal shackle.   17 DEC 2019  0130LT Informed Terminal   17 DEC 2019  0140LT Loading master informed that not require to pass any other spare line  17 DEC 2019  0220LT  Vessel renewed the broken wire with the spare Wire Rope
ImmediateAction: Informed Loading Master, Terminal  Informed Office  Retrieved mooring tail from Jetty  Replaced Parted Mooring Wire with another Mooring Wire
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One Wire rope rendered unusable.
DirectCause: 1. Company Criteria is 7 years for renewal- However, frequency of Mooring operations are very much higher than normal tankers of this size.  2. Rope had 80% rejection criteria. Requisition had been sent. Was due for renewal.  3. The Morring Wire had been changed end to end twice already  4. Apparent neglect due to other activities being given more importance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replacement of Wire with New Wires (8 Nos)
ClosingNote: Please refer to the attached investigation report

IncidentDetails: 3 mooring ropes parted, 3 mooring ropes chafed beyond usable limit, 3 mooring winch brake liners damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Location: Lihir , Papua New Guinea  Date: 02 Jan 2020  Wind: ESE to NNE x 2 ~ 4 knots  Sea: Variable & heavy swell 3 meters or more at the Berth.  Additional details: The steep gradient of the seabed changes from 100 meters contour to 50 meters contour and then to 15 meters contour at the berth.  The berth is not sheltered and is exposed to open sea conditions and rocky topography contributes to the swell bouncing violently off the shore.  Original mooring configuration: Three Head Lines, three Stern Lines, two Spring Lines forward and two Spring Lines aft. Total of 10 mooring lines.   02 Jan / 2228: The duty rating reported that due to heavy and constant surging, one head line parted despite continuous effort to tending to the mooring lines.  02 Jan / 2228: The Duty Officer informed the Master and Chief Officer and called the Loading Master for immediate assistance of the Mooring Boat and Mooring Crew.  -02 Jan / 2230: The Loading Master informed the Duty Officer that he had informed the concerned team for assistance.  02 Jan / 2255: The Duty Officer made a repeated request to the Loading Master for a Mooring Boat again and asked how long would it take for the Mooring Crew to arrive. The Loading Master replied about 30 minutes.  02 Jan / 2305 ~ 2350: Ship staff split a team and was sent on the berth and additional mooring lines were passed without the assistance from shore staff. Vessel made fast one more Head Line, one more Forward Spring Line and two Breast Lines forward.  Date: 03 Jan 2020  Wind: N x 1 ~ 3 knots  Sea: Variable and heavy swell of 3 meters or more at the Berth.  Mooring Configuration: Four Head Lines, three Forward Spring Lines, two Forward Breast Lines, three 3 Stern Lines and two After Spring Lines. Total of 14 mooring lines.   03 Jan / 0700: The Duty Officer called and requested the Loading Master for the Mooring Boat and Mooring Crew as the vessel intended to pass one more Stern Line and After Breast Line since the vessel was constantly surging due to the heavy swell. As a result of which, there was excessive weight on Stern Line and After Spring Lines and as a result of the mooring winch brakes rendering, the vessel kept moving away from the Berth. There was no action taken from the shore side.  03 Jan / 0735: The Chief Officer called Lihir Pilots on VHF channel 16 and repeated the above request for the Mooring Boat and Mooring Crew. The Pilot acknowledged the message and informed the vessel that the needful will be arranged.  03 Jan / 0806: Mooring Boat arrived at the vessel.   03 Jan / 0836: The vessel made fast one Stern Line. Now, total of four Stern Lines.  03 Jan / 0900: Vessel made fast one After Breast Line.  03 Jan / 0906 LT:  The Mooring Configuration was now four Head Lines, three Forward Spring Lines, two Forward Breast Lines, four Stern Lines, two After Spring Lines and one After Breast Line. Total of 16 mooring lines.   03 Jan / 2036: Completed discharging cargo  03 Jan / 2212: Cargo manifold disconnected After all actives were completed, the shore personnel disembarked from the vessel and the shore gangway was taken off. All shore personnel left the vicinity of the berth.  03 Jan / 2330: The Duty Rating reported that one Forward Spring Line parted despite continuous tending due to the excessive surging of the vessel. The Duty Officer repeatedly tried to call tried to call the Pilot, Loading Master and Port Control on VHF channel 16. The ship staff then tried to draw attention of passing vehicles by flashlights and whittles. The fog horn was sounded several times and the Aldis Lamp was flashed ashore. Two passing trucks responded to the vessel. An eye splice was quickly made to the parted Forward Spring Line. The truck driver was requested place the eye of the mooring line on the bollard.                  03 Jan / 2345: One Head Line parted. The vessel repeatedly tried calling the Port Control and all other contacts mentioned in the Emergency Port Contact Sheet including the Local Agent. The Master tried calling the Port Co-coordinator, Mr. Bruce Taylor of Lihir Port. The Master then tried calling the Port Operation Supervisor, Mr. Erin. But there was no response from any parties.  Date: 04 Jan 2020  Wind: N ~ NNW x 1 ~ 4 knots.  Sea: Variable and heavy swell of 3 meters and more at the Berth.   Mooring Configuration: three Head Lines, three Forward Spring Lines, two Forward Breast Lines, four Stern Lines, two After Spring Lines and one After Breast Line. Total of 15 mooring lines.  04 Jan / 0015: The vessel started using the Bow Thruster to try bringing the vessel's bow alongside the berth. Using the anchor was not an option due to the rocky nature of the bottom.
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately passed additional mooring ropes to secure vessel alongside. Used bowthruster to keep the vessel alongside. Tried contacting the Loading master, Port control, Pilots, Port coordinator, but received no response. Reported to the Owners and Managers about the condition and seeked advice.
DirectCause: Inadequate Supervision from terminal staff during hours of darkness.  Failure to get warned by Terminal for possible heavy seas and long swell. Lack of standards at Lihir terminal in accordance with ISGOTT guidelines of having terminals manned at all time to take care of similar incidences.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: # Owners/ Chrtrs to liaise with rcvrs to have basic terminal safety requirements in place, terminal staff/ pilots should be available at all times while vessel is at berth. # Ship staff to have detailed pre-cargo meeting with terminal staff and emphasize learning from this ADI to ensure terminal is always manned with skeleton shore staff to take care of similar incidences. # All damaged ropes/ break linings to be repaired prior arrival next port. Spares used to be replenished. # vessel should always have required spare ropes/ brake bands to take care of similar situation in future.
Corrective Action: # Owners/ Chrtrs to liaise with rcvrs to have basic terminal safety requirements in place, terminal staff/ pilots should be available at all times while vessel is at berth. # Ship staff to have detailed pre-cargo meeting with terminal staff and emphasize learning from this ADI to ensure terminal is always manned with skeleton shore staff to take care of similar incidences. # All damaged ropes/ break linings to be repaired prior arrival next port. Spares used to be replenished. # vessel should always have required spare ropes/ brake bands to take care of similar situation in future.
ClosingNote: CA completed and Job Closed 20/02

IncidentDetails: On 13.01.2020, At around 1530hrs/lt(1230UTC), While crew members shifting the used oil drum from main deck to poop deck, stbd derrick hoisting wire slips uncontrolled and accidentally Two of the Drums went into the sea in Postion:26 17.8 N 052 57.18 E. No injury to any person on board.    each Drums were sealed and contains about 100 ltrs of oil. No visible leaks of oil was seen in water.    Vessel tried to retrieve the drums but could not manage to get them back on board.    Weather conditions at the time of incident was slight to mod sea and swell, wind about 15-20kts NW’ly.  Vessel speed 1.0-1.5knots and drifting.
IncidentEventDetails: At around 1540 hrs/lt(1240UTC) Chief officer reported master that while shifting used L.O drum from main deck to poop deck aft in order to transfer the used oil in waste oil tank, accidentally two drum slipped in to the sea. Also reported that there was no injury to any crew and no oil leaks in to the sea as the oil drums were sealed.    1550hrs/lt(1250UTC): Master called to Sr. tech suptd and informed the incident to him and asked for his advice.    1600hrs/lt-1830hrs/lt: Vessel was turned to recover the drums. But as vessel approached near to the drums, due to the ships wake, drums drifted away from the vessel. Hence vessel made 4 unsuccessful attempt to retrieve the drums. (pls find photographs attached.). Vessel abandoned the idea of recovering the drums as sunset and darkness.    1900hrs/lt(1400UTC)-2000hrs(1500UTC): Vessel messaged to Nakilat, the local agent at Ras Laffan and situation was appraised/mailed with photographic evidences. And also called port control, but Ras laffan port was not reachable over VHF hence informed agent to inform the incident to port control and all concerned parties.
ImmediateAction: Derrick was hoisted and secured. Tool box meeting was called, discussed the issue. Office was informed, agent and port control was informed, Vessel tried to retrieve the drums but could not manage to get them back on board.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 13.01.2020, At around 1530hrs/lt(1230UTC), While crew members shifting the used oil drum from main deck to poop deck, stbd derrick hoisting wire slips uncontrolled and accidentally Two of the Drums went into the sea in Postion:26 17.8 N 052 57.18 E. No injury to any person on board.    each Drums were sealed and contains about 100 ltrs of oil. No visible leaks of oil was seen in water.    Vessel tried to retrieve the drums but could not manage to get them back on board.    Weather conditions at the time of incident was slight to mod sea and swell, wind about 15-20kts NW’ly.  Vessel speed 1.0-1.5knots and drifting.
DirectCause: As Derrick hoisting wire slipped uncontrolled, it was expected some technical fault with the derrick machinery.    Vessel removed stbd provision crane hoisting motor  for inspection.  After removal it was observed that sun gear which is attached to the motor shaft was in totally damaged condition. Almost all the teeth are damaged and worn down.  This has resulted in dis-engagement of hoisting winch/gear box and the motor /magnetic brake which resulted in uncontrolled lowering of the hoisting wire due to the weight of the drums lifted.  Pls find attached photographs.    Discussed the matter with vessel’s superintendent for the maintenance to carry out on the stbd derrick gear box system.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel will land stbd derrick gear box assembly to shore for repair

IncidentDetails: During crew training for emergency generator starting, as soon as the start button pressed - one of the battery exploded inside the compartment. the battery damaged - no injuries or other property damage
IncidentEventDetails: At 1700 hrs Lt training for crew was given for Emergency generator starting procedure.  At 1705 hrs lt, Generator start button was pressed subsequently one of the batteries exploded inside the battery compartment with a loud noise.
ImmediateAction: Battery charger switched off and disconnected from the source, All personnel evacuated the emergency generator room. Afte 15 minutes, Emergency generator started using secondary method of starting - found working satisfactory
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Battery exploded and the same has to be replaced
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vent holes to be made in the battery compartment
ClosingNote: Briefing was carried out. All crew explained the significance of testing the equipment as per PMS emphasized.

IncidentDetails: At 0920LT - The trainee GS put cut onions in a pan of Hot Oil which was on the HOt plate for about 10 mins. As soon as he put onions in the oil, the oil splashed along with steam onto the Hot plate causing a minor fire on the hot plate. The fire blanket was put immediately on to the utensil to prevent the oil from catching Fire.   0923 Lt - The ETO who was working nearby came with the extinguisher & extinguished the fire on the Hot plate.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0920LT - The trainee GS put cut onions in a pan of Hot Oil which was on the HOt plate for about 10 mins. As soon as he put onions in the oil, the oil splashed alon with steam onto the Hot plate causing a minor fire on the hot plate. The fire blanket was put immediately on to the utensil to prevent the oil from catching Fire. The ETO who was working nearby came with the extinguisher & extinguished the fire on the Hot plate.
ImmediateAction: The fire blanket was put immediately on to the utensil to prevent the oil from catching Fire.   The ETO who was working nearby came with the dcp extinguisher & extinguished the fire on the Hot plate.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The Galley Hot plates were switched off, cleaned and started again after some time. Working Satisfactorily.
DirectCause: Cause :  1) Oil temperature very hot.  2.) Onions had water content which when coming into contact with Hot Oil reacted and created a mist of oil which splashed onto the hot plate.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The Oil temperature for cooking to be monitored and overheating to be avoided.  Training of galley staff carried out.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: CA found adequate

IncidentDetails: 1) On 11th Dec 2019, Tank cleaning operation was completed & all LSMGO parcel cargo tanks (1W, 2W, 4W, 5W) were washed & purged to less than 2% by vol. Gasoline parcel tanks (3W, 6W) were well drained as per shell tank cleaning guide.  2) On 13th Dec, vessel came to know about the increase of HC in LSMGO loading tanks to more than 2% volume. The reason for same was investigated & found Slop Stbd IG inlet valve to be open. Also noted that COW machine valves were left open. Same were rectified & cargo tanks purged to less than 1 % by volume. Ullage of cargo tanks was also taken to ensure that there was no wash water left in the tanks. Vaccum pump was also started and tried to suck from the cargo tanks to rule out the possibility of any wash content left behind. However, no remains came from cargo tanks.   3) On 14th Dec at 0900 LT upon checking the HC content of cargo tanks, noticed that 5 P & 5 S COT reading was 2.5 % Vol, rest other tanks were 2% by vol or less. IG inlet valves of 3W & 6W COT (which had 35% vol HC content) were checked & found holding. 5P/5S COT purged and HC content brought to less than 1% by volume by 1500 LT.  4) At 1624 LT on 14th Dec 2019, pilot boarded vessel for berthing.   5) On 14th Dec at the berth, Surveyor carried out inspection of the tanks and found HC content of 4S & 5S COT 2.5 % by Volume, rest all Gasoil tanks 2% or less by volume. He informed vessel about the terminal requirement of less than 50%LEL in cargo tanks and not 2% by Volume in order to load LSGMO and asked if agents had informed the vessel regarding the same. Surveyor was conveyed that vessel had not received any such instructions. Surveyor also found about 50 Litres of water from pumproom bottom line drain during inspection. Consequently, both LSMGO parcel tanks & Gasoline parcel tanks were failed. Office was advised of the incident. Vessel was cast off & shifted to inner anchorage for re-tank preparation.   6) On 16th Dec at 0400 LT, Vessel completed re-educting of all cargo tanks & lines and found 7 cbm of left over wash water which was educted & transferred to ROT. All cargo tanks purged to less than 50% LEL & all lines & drains confirmed empty before giving readiness for loading again at 0400 LT 16th Dec 2019.

DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 14th Dec 2019 // 2200 LT : Cargo Tank Inspection failed by surveor.  15th Dec 2019// 1148LT  : Vessel Unberthed  15th Dec 2019// 1300LT  : Vessel Anchored at Inner Anchorage  16th Dec 2019// 0400LT  : NOR Retendered after recompletion of Tank educting and purging  16th Dec 2019// 0830LT  : Tanks cleared by surveyor for Loading operation
DirectCause: 1. Lack of following procedure in carrying out all means of stripping (Vacuum pump / educator / stripping pump).  2.Master failed to comply with office instructions on prompt communication.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Before every Loading/Discharging Operation, any additional specific terminal requirement to be confirmed from the local agents.    2) Tank cleaning operations to be conducted thoroughly as per industry standards & effectiveness of same to be always checked before arrival Load Port.    3) Office advice to be taken if any difficulty faced in preparing Cargo Tanks for loading.
Corrective Action: Extensive conference call carried out with the vessel by the Marine Manager and Marine Superintendent for carrying out proper tank cleaning checks and also to share in time the actual details of the happenings onboard to all the stakeholders (Charterers, Owners and Managers).
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: While manoeuvring in water lifeboat came in contact with ship's hull causing damage to canopy light.
IncidentEventDetails: 1030: VESSEL PREPARED TO LOWER LIFEBOAT INTO WATER AND MANOEUVRE AT ZHANJIANG PORT. RELEVANT RA CARRIED OUT. ALL CHECKS VERIFIED.  1100: MASTER GAVE PERMISSION TO LOWER LIFEBOAT INTO WATER AND MANOEUVRE  1107: LIFEBOAT WEIGHT WAS TAKEN EQUALLY ON TO BELTS AND HOOK RELEASED  1108: LIFEBOAT WAS SLIDING ON THE TRACKWAY BY USING DAVIT  1112: LIFEBOAT CLEARED THE DAVIT ARMS  1119: BOAT TOUCHED THE WATER  1126: 3/OFF, 3/ENG, AB-B BOARDED THE BOAT BY MEANS OF LADDER.  1127: DAVIT HOOKS WERE CLEARED FROM THE BOAT. BOAT IS CONNECTED WITH 2 PAINTERS FROM THE VESSEL  1129: BOAT STARTED SWINGING TO STBD (PORT SIDE OF THE VESSEL) DUE TO WIND  1136: BOAT ENGINES TRIED OUT AHEAD AND ASTERN WHILE PAINTERS CONNETED  1141: BOAT WAS DRIFTED TOWARDS VESSEL’S PORT SIDE DUE TO WIND  1152: LIFEBOAT ENGINES USED, TO BRING BACK THE BOAT TO THE STERN POSITION  1156: ONE PAINTER DISCONNECTED FROM THE BOAT AND SAME HEAVED UP AND KEPT AT THE STERN OF THE SHIP TO RECONNECT.  1200: 2ND PAINTER DISCONNECTED FROM THE BOAT AND HELD BY AB.  1201: LIFEBOAT MANOEUVERED IN WATER AFTER REMOVING BOTH PAINTERS.  1202: PAINTER CONNECTED TO THE BOAT, BY THAT TIME BOAT STARTED DRIFTED TOWARDS THE PORT SIDE OF THE VESSEL.  1203: WHILE COMING BACK TO THE STERN OF THE VESSEL BOAT CAME VERY CLOSE TO SHIP’S PORT QUARTER CURVATURE AREA AND THE PAINTER WHICH WAS NEAR THE SHIP’S SIDE (KEPT FOR THE PURPOSE OF RECONNECTING AS THE WIND IS PICKING UP) GOT ENTANGLED WITH THE BOAT’S PROPELLER. SAME WAS REPORTED BY 3/OFF AT THAT TIME BOAT WAS NOT INSIGHT FROM THE SHIP. BOAT CREW WERE ADVISED TO USE BOAT HOOK TO PREVENT CONTACT WITH SHIP’S HULL.  1210: BOAT CAME IN CONTACT WITH HULL NEAR PORT QUARTER DUE TO WAVE CAUSED BY PASSING BY VESSEL RESULTED DAMAGE TO CANOPY LIGHT UNIT.
ImmediateAction: BOAT WAS HELD AT THE STERN BY MEANS OF PAINTERS AND CONNECTED TO THE DAVIT HOOKS.  lIFEBOAT HEAVED UP AND STORED IN POSITION. lIFEBOAT ENGINES TRIED OUT AND A THOROUGH INSPECTION CARRIED OUT. FOUND ALL SATISFACTORY.  REQUSITION (SPR/LLDN/D/20/007) RAISED FOR THE CANOPY LIGHT UNIT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LIFEBOAT CANOPY LIGHT UNIT DAMAGED DUE TO CONTACT WITH SHIP'S HULL WHILE MANOEUVERING IN WATER.
DirectCause: WHILE COMING BACK TO THE STERN OF THE VESSEL BOAT CAME VERY CLOSE TO SHIP’S PORT QUARTER CURVATURE AREA AND THE PAINTER GOT ENTANGLED WITH THE BOAT’S PROPELLER. SAME WAS REPORTED BY 3/OFF AT THAT TIME BOAT WAS NOT INSIGHT FROM THE SHIP. BOAT CREW WERE ADVISED TO USE BOAT HOOK TO PREVENT CONTACT WITH SHIP’S HULL. BOAT CAME IN CONTACT WITH HULL NEAR PORT QUARTER DUE TO WAVE CAUSED BY PASSING BY VESSEL RESULTED DAMAGE TO CANOPY LIGHT UNIT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: WHILE MANOEUVERING BOAT LOOKOUT TO BE KEPT FOR FLOATING OBJECTS ALSO TO HAVE A SITUATIONAL AWARENESS FOR WEATHER CHANGES OR LOCAL TRAFFIC.
LessionLearnt: Never to loose situational awareness
ClosingNote: Always carry-out RA for all task and review it during task

IncidentDetails: Vessel was proceeding to Rizhao, China for discharging. During the Pre- PSC preparation, checked all safety equipment equipment in the whole ship. later stage of inspection, found that 7 pcs fire Hose nozzle were found stucked up and not working properly when change the adjustment to spray and jet nozzle.
ImmediateAction: Immediately changed the defective nozzle with ready spare to complete all the safety equipment on all the fire hose box in preparation for MSA/PSC inspection.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Noozle were not working / rotating freely.
DirectCause: Lack of  maintenance and routine checks.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During routine  inspection, should apply grease / oil and exercise noozle to confirm working in good condition, should be moving freely in jet and spray position.
Corrective Action: During routine  inspection, should apply grease / oil and exercise noozle to confirm working in good condition, should be moving freely in jet and spray position.
ClosingNote: PMS to be strictly followed. Routine checks to be done with due diligence.

IncidentDetails: Vessel arrived on 30th Dec 2019 at 0600hrs & POB on arrival at PBG. Vessel berthed at pier 01 with 4+2+2 mooring pattern as Port of Pecem is an open sea port with limited breakwater constantly exposed to sea conditions.  Pier 1 is about 280 mtrs in length. Hence the vessel gave all the lines with short leads. Vessel had forward clearance of 10-15 mtrs and aft about 25 mtrs.    The tide table was discussed with duty officers & one copy placed on gangway & in ships office for ready reference. Tide range was 2~2.5mtrs every 5~6hrs.   Port watches comprised of Duty Officer+ 2 Deck hands on 6 hour’s rotations.  Vessel was surging on Berth continuously but during high tide its frequency increased. On 01st Jan 2020 during high tide around 2100hrs, Chief Officer was called as per his standing instruction to duty officer to tend the moorings fwd & aft continuously until steady tide. When duty officer & Duty AB went fwd to tend the mooring lines, they found that one of the fwd breast tail rope parted due to surging.   As per Duty Officer, the mooring ropes were attended 10~15 min before only & canvas placed below the mooring rope for protection to prevent any chaffing. They didn’t notice any chaffing damage at that time. Immediately Chief Officer went on sight and reported to Master about the situation that rope parted due to chaffing during surging and the angle is too sharp to bollard and no other bollard nearby for breast rope.  At that time Master decided to take out the other breast rope also to prevent damage.

IncidentDetails: On 23 Sep/0415 LT, while the vessel was at Lagos SAA the deck watchman noticed 2 persons forward and a small hose on deck. He immediately raised an alarm on the radio and ran towards the accomodation.  The emergency alarm was sounded both internally and externally and the master was informed and a call to the patrol boat was made on VHF. The patrol boat NNS Fortress responded and proceeded to the vessel.NNS Fortress inspected the shipside and did not find any suspicious activity.  A patrol team comprising of 5 ship cerw was sent to investigate and search the deck and external stores. On reaching forward they noticed that the forward most section of the razor wire was broken off. Further the caps of Nos 1P and 1S vapour lock were found open.  The ullages of 1W's COT's was checked and found normal. All ships crew are safe.  A call from the TOC manager was received and he was informed of the situation on board.
