ClosingNote: All CA/PA as per attached incident report to be followed.

IncidentDetails: Upon completion of Unmooring and picking up of mooring line, the fwd spring (In-board)  mooring wire's rope tail was found to be damaged. 2 out of 8 strands were found to be parted/damaged. The mooring tail was renewed immediately with spare which was available onboard.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl was casted off from berth at Cork Ireland. After completion of Unmooring and picking up of mooring line, the fwd spring (In-board)  mooring wire's rope tail was found to be damaged. 2 out of 8 strands were found to be parted/damaged. The mooring tail was renewed immediately with spare which was available onboard .
ImmediateAction: The damaged tail was renewed with new spare tail which was available onboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fwd spring wire's (Inboard) rope tail was found to be damaged/parted. 2 out of 8 strands were damaged/parted.
DirectCause: Localised stress or chaffing of mooring rope tail
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All rope tails to be inspected before and after completion of operation. Chaffing of lines to be prevented
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA plan.

IncidentDetails: Injury to left eye (cut on eye lid) of Panama Line handling crew during mooring operation in Locks. (Impact injury from brake handle of Mooring Drum).
IncidentEventDetails: On 28th January 2020, at about 19:00 LT, as vessel entered the 1st lock of Agua Clara locks (North bound - Panama Canal), Ship's crew was operating the winches on instructions of the team leader of the Panama Crew. He was instructed to quickly open the brakes of the mooring drum so that the mooring rope could be lowered while the vessel entered the lock, to be made fast in the lock.  While ship's crew was rotating the Brake handle quickly, one Panama Canal lines man was standing next to him, when suddenly another Panama Canal lines man came up from behind him and stepped too close to the ship’s Mooring Drum handle being operated. Without realising his position, he walked in way of the turning handle. The brake handle hit the him on his face (on the eye), causing a cut on his left eye-lid, which started to bleed.  Winch and brake operations were under instructions from the leader of Panama Crew aft.
ImmediateAction: Officer reported to the bridge that one of the Panama crew has suffered a cut injury.  Immediately removed the injured linesman from stations and informed Bridge. The injured linesman was offered first aid with gauze to staunch the injury, which he refused. In consultation with the Pilot, vessel was made fast to the lock and Ship's accommodation ladder was lowered. Panama Canal authorities were informed by the Pilot, and they immediately sent their emergency services (Ambulance & Fire Tender) to the lock. Panama Canal Medical officers boarded the ship and took the injured person with them for first aid/medical attendance. The Canal authorities also sent on board another linesman to replace the injured person
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cut on the soft eye-lid skin.
DirectCause: Loss of situational awareness of the Panama Canal crew.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During each operation of winches & associated mechanism, Ship’s crew should loudly inform all third party members at the stations to “Stand clear – operating winch/brakes, etc.”.  During each such stations, Officer on duty should brief the Canal crew before start of operations to stand clear when Ship’s crew are operating any of the Mooring equipment.
ClosingNote: Crew Briefing carried out on safe Mooring operations, Any unsafe conditions or action by shore crew to be immediately reported to Master.

IncidentDetails: ON 23 JANUARY 2020 AT 11.30 HRS ABNORMAL SOUND WAS HEARD FROM MAIN ENGINE UNIT 3. ME WAS STOPPED IMMEDIATELY AND CRANKCASE AND UNDERPISTON SPACE DOOR WAS OPENED FOR INSPECTION. ON INSPECTION IT IS FOUND THAT PISTON PALM STUD CAME OUT AND STARTED HITTING THE STUFFING BOX HOUSING AND THE STUFFING BOX HOLDING INNER SCREWS BROKEN AND FELL DOWN IN CRANCASE.
IncidentEventDetails: ME UNIT WAS DECARBED ON 15 DECEMBER 2019 AND ENGINE WAS SUCCESSFULLY TESTED AFTER THE DECARB. CRANKCASE INSPECTION WAS CARRIED OUT ON 28 DECEMBER 2019 AND FOUND EVERYTHING SATISFACTORY. TOTAL RUNNING HRS FROM UNIT OVERHAUL TILL THE INCIDENT HAPPENED IS 386 HRS. ON INVESTIGATION IT WAS FOUND THAT 3E DURING TIGHTENING THE HYDRAULIC NUT DID NOT PURGE THE HYDRAULIC JACKS WHICH LED TO THE NUT GETTING LOOSE OVER A PERIOD OF TIME AND STUD CAME OUT HITTING THE STUFFING BOX.
ImmediateAction: ALL THE BROKEN AND REMAINING SCREWS INSIDE THE STUFFING BOX WAS REMOVED. OTHER 3 PISTON PALM STUD TIGHTNESS WAS CHECKED AFTER OPENING THE HYDRAULIC NUT WHICH WERE INTACT. PISTON PALM NUT WAS TIGHTENED TO 910 BAR PRESSURE AND THE STUFFING BOX WAS SECURED BY SPARE SCREWS OF SAME SIZE. REQN SPR/SHFO/E/20/031 IS RAISED FOR THE SPARE SCREWS WHICH WILL BE PUT ONCE RECEIVED. AS PER THE SUPT. ADVISE , IT WAS AGREED TO RUN THE ME ON 3 PISTON PALM STUDS AND MAKE INSPECTION AFTER 48 HRS OF OPERATION OF ME.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: THE PISTON PALM STUD WHICH CAME OUT DAMAGED THE THREADS OF THE BORE HOLDING THE STUD IN THE CROSSHEAD PIN. STUFFING BOX SCREWS WERE BROKEN WHICH NEEDS REPLACEMENT ONCE RECEIVED ON BOARD.
DirectCause: There is no record of explanation of correct procedure to the crew member.In fact is is on record that the tool box meeting was missed by the chief engineer, hence the explanation of correct procedure is doubtful.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ME CRANKCASE AND UNDERPISTON SPACE TO BE INSPECTED 24 HRS  AFTER OVERHAUL FOR ANY ABNORMALITY AND PUT THE HYDRAULIC JACKS AGAIN TO CHECK THE TIGHTNESS OF HYDRAULIC NUT.
LessionLearnt: Bolt tightening and tightness check must be done diligently and cross verified by experienced person
ReviewComments: Root cause of the incident
ClosingNote: The crew undertook a routine maintenance task on a critical machinery under normal conditions, at anchorage. The required risk assessments and preparation had been carried out as noted from the details provided by the vessel subsequently. The task was completed on schedule and follow up inspection was also completed without any problems being identified. Since the overhaul, the engine has logged a total of 386 hours with 2 ports of call, Lianyungang and Xiamen.  The incident as per industry information, is not a common occurrence and can only be attributed to improper tightening procedures followed for Hydraulic tightening.

IncidentDetails: Missed out reporting to Owners/Managers Regarding vessel entering JWLA in Indian Ocean
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel Next Voyage advice arrived one day Prior Dep Mundra ( India ) , upon checking the route we observed vessel track from Mundra to South Africa was Outside Presently declared HRA Area in Arabian sea/Indian Ocean.  We wrongly interpreted the Circular on  IWL & JWLA  instruction that JWLA reporting is applicable when Vessel  Enters JWLA region with intention to Enter HRA. This was a wrong interpretation on the Circular on IWL & JWLA.
ImmediateAction: We realized our Mistake when office pointed out, then we interpreted the circular correctly.  Then we trained all officer & master on this subject & New Circular on IWL & JWLA  dated 18 Jan 2020  Received Same Now Posted on Bridge at conspicuous place & also will  included in passage Plan
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIL
DirectCause: Vessel Next Voyage advice arrived one day Prior Dep Mundra ( India ) , upon checking the route we observed vessel track from Mundra to South Africa was Outside Presently declared HRA Area in Arabian sea/Indian Ocean.  We wrongly interpreted the Circular on  IWL & JWLA  instruction that JWLA reporting is applicable when Vessel  Enters JWLA region with intention to Enter HRA. This was a wrong interpretation on the Circular on IWL & JWLA
Proposedcorrectiveactions: we trained all officer & master on this subject & New Circular on IWL & JWLA  dated 18 Jan 2020  Received Same Now Posted on Bridge at conspicuous place & also will  included in passage Plan
Corrective Action: we trained all officer & master on this subject & New Circular on IWL & JWLA  dated 18 Jan 2020  Received Same Now Posted on Bridge at conspicuous place & also will  included in passage Plan
LessionLearnt: HRA/JWLA to be included in the PP and appraised before commencement of voyage
ClosingNote: Vessel should not enter HRA/JWLA without notifying and getting required permission from Owners, unless for Safety Of Life At Sea

IncidentDetails: HYDRAULIC LINE BURSTED DURING SHIP TO SHIP OPERATION
ImmediateAction: MANUEVER AWAY AND DROP ANCHOR TO REPAIR DAMAGE/BURSTED HYDRAULIC LINE
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DURING MANUEVERING FOR DISCHARGING CARGO (SHIP TO SHIP OPERATION) TRANSHIPMENT. HYDRAULIC LINE BURSTED.
DirectCause: HYDRAULIC LINE BURSTED.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: EMMEDIATE REAPIR BROKEM/BURSTED HYDRAULIC LINE
Corrective Action: EMMEDIATE REAPIR BROKEM/BURSTED HYDRAULIC LINE
LessionLearnt: Preventive maintenance of hydraulic lines to be taken up with increased diligence and scrutiny, so that unexpected failures are eliminated.
ClosingNote: Repairs completed on board.

IncidentDetails: Some of the M/E, A/Es & other parameters and alarms not working after the black out due to Alternator #1 damage
IncidentEventDetails: During a cargo discharging ,around @2159 hrs LO Differential alarm sounded for AE#1 , when AE#1 and AE#2 were running parallel in load and AE#3 was in standby.    Duty engineer immediately decided to renew LO filters for AE#1 , so he started AE#3 locally and after confirming no abnormalities  on his way to ECR to put it on load, Sudden a loud blast sound was heard and smoke stared to come from AE#1 alternator.    Which leads to Black out, but power resumed in few seconds and DE engineer found AE#3 was running on load, AE#2 running idle and AE#1 tripped.    Chief engineer was in cabin and during this incident of power fluctuation, CE reached to ECR.    After analysing the situation, Chief engineer and Duty engineer decided to resume cargo operation as early as possible.    AE#2 started locally and after confirming no abnormalities , AE#2 was taken on load, and proper sequence followed to start all required machinery one by one to resume cargo operations.    cargo operation was resume @1012 LT.    After this incident, noticed that number of modules in outstation 2 are not working, and Some of the M/E, A/Es & otherequipments  parameters and alarms not working.
ImmediateAction: After confirming the problem with the alarm monitoring system, attempts been made to reset AMS number of time.    Multiple combinations tried out with modules to resume AMS back to normal.    Troubleshooting in progress with makers service technician's advice and spares ordered accordingly
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Multiple Modules damaged in outstation 2.
DirectCause: #1 Alternator winding damages causes damage to the winding temperature sensor, with causes short circuit in modules.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Health Check up of the AMS to be carried out.    To maintain safer operation of the vessel folowing changes done:   1. Engine room is manned untill the problem resolve completly.   2. All safety alarms and trips of the equipments checked and recorded.   3. Only to record only local parameters of the equipments are in use to avoid error reading to consider for condition monitoring.   4. Any Abnormailities in any of the equipment or any abnormal deviation should be consider in priority basis and reported to chief engineer.
Corrective Action: To carry out troubleshooting with makers and rectify the defect. If required, raise requisition for spares and renew after receipt. Carry out RA for safe operation
LessionLearnt: A spare module to be maintained at all times onboard
ClosingNote: Noted that AMS is fully operational now

IncidentDetails: WIAS unit in no 2 cargo hold (pre and main), located in no2 cargo hold void space aft, submerged in water causing to damage the PCB of sensor.
IncidentEventDetails: On 26th Jan 2020 at about 2300 hrs LT vessel started ballast exchange of no.2 and no.3 WBT port and stbd in flow through method. On the 27th Jan about 2200 hrs LT 3rd mate reported there is intermittent pre and main alarm in no2 cargo hold WIAS (pre is more frequent). After receiving the alarm vessel checked the cargo hold bilges sounding of no.2 in night and hold tank top from top ladder platform through booby hatch and found dry.  But alarm was still coming and going then vessel open the stool space manhole of no2. cargo hold to check the void space and found water is coming out with pressure from manhole covers sides as soon as started slackening the nuts.  Then vessel slowly drained all the water by keeping the main hole cover in place and bilge running, void space manhole cover from top also opened up and after removing the water from Stoll space sensor checked and found ingress of water in WIAS PCB.
ImmediateAction: No.2 hold cargo hold bilges sounding and  tank top visually checked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 26th Jan 2020 at about 2300 hrs LT vessel started ballast exchange of no.2 and no.3 WBT port and stbd in flow through method. On the 27th Jan about 2200 hrs LT 3rd mate reported there is intermittent pre and main alarm in no2 cargo hold WIAS (pre is more frequent). After receiving the alarm vessel checked the cargo hold bilges sounding of no.2 in night and hold tank top from top ladder platform through booby hatch and found dry.  But alarm was still coming and going then vessel open the stool space manhole of no2. cargo hold to check the void space and found water is coming out with pressure from manhole covers sides as soon as started slackening the nuts.  Then vessel slowly drained all the water by keeping the main hole cover in place and bilge running, void space manhole cover from top also opened up and after removing the water from Stoll space sensor checked and found ingress of water in WIAS PCB.
DirectCause: Poor work standard during tightening of the manhole covers nuts.    Over a period of time during ballasting and de-ballasting specially when carrying of ballast exchange in rough weather, sloshing water pressure on manhole covers may have caused nut to become slack.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to inspect all DBWBT manhole covers in void space for rubber packing and proper tightening to make sure no reoccurrence of such type of incident in future.
ClosingNote: Closed. All manhole doors checked. CA adequate. Flow through means of ballast water exchange discontinued with immediate effect.

IncidentDetails: On 01.Feb.2020, 2030 hrs When the loading was in progress at Ust Luga , Russia , Stbd Out board head line parted from the loop of wire .
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl was loading at Ust Luga , Deballasting at full rate was started and vsl  was constantly mainatinaing a trim of 4.50 mtrs in order to compensate with high loading rate of 8500 cbm/ hr .At the time of line parting, ford draft was 3.80 mtrs and aft draft 8.10 mtrs. Temp at the time of incident was -2*c and continuosly snowing. Combined effect of Stress coming on the wire due to acute angle at bow created by a low Ford draft , induced stress on the eye of the wire rope over past years ( wire in use from July /2014 ) and brittleness of the wire in subzero temeratures finally lead to parting of wire rope at the loop connected at Tonsberg shackle.
ImmediateAction: Terminal was informed about same. The parted wire retrieved onbd . Later the wire was turned End to End and made fast .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One of the head line parted from loop connecting to Tonsberg shackles due to stress and corrosion
DirectCause: Combined effect of Stress coming on the wire due to acute angle at bow created by a low Ford draft , induced stress on the eye of the wire rope over past years ( wire in use from July /2014 ) and brittleness of the wire in subzero temeratures finally lead to parting of wire rope at the loop connected at Tonsberg shackle. Due to the acute angle formed at the fair lead , the brake doesn't release at the set BRC Value.Upon  insoection of mooring line, it was also found that the eye of the wire rope was tied up( very close to the shackle )  to prevenet the shackle from twisting and this had caued permenant deformation on the eye and resulting in eye loosing its flexibility.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Please refer to CA raised & attended below
Corrective Action: 1.Thorough inspection to be carried out for all mooring wires and correctly identify the one which needs to be renewed
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Plese refer to attached IIR

IncidentDetails: Damage brearing and bended shaft of Crane # 3 hoisting wire drum roller
IncidentEventDetails: During crane wire replacement, thought the procedure was followed as per manufacturer manual, the coil 3 layers of hoisting wire in the hoisting drum reached the maximum extent capacity of the compression roller. This caused the the shaft to bend and breaks the one the end bearings. Due to the noise generated by the crane and with ear plugs on, it is imposible to hear any squerky sound. The damage was only noticed when the new wire reached the drum as it time to pay out the old wire.
ImmediateAction: The roller was removed and brought down to engine room for bending it back straight.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During crane wire replacement, thought the procedure was followed as per manufacturer manual, the coil 3 layers of hoisting wire in the hoisting drum reached the maximum extent capacity of the compression roller. This caused the the shaft to bend and breaks the one the end bearings.
DirectCause: Familiarization of wire rope replacement is not enough.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Slack thecompression roller.  2. Crew must be on standby near the roller drum.
ClosingNote: REPAIR  HAS BEEN DONE BY VESSEL

IncidentDetails: AE 1  started from local. After starting it was observed lub oil was spraying from main LO filter gasket.
IncidentEventDetails: -AE 1 started after blowing through from local  - Lub Oil leakage obderved from Main LO filter gasket  - AE & priming pump stopped immediately  - Cleaning of area carried out
ImmediateAction: Since AE was not taken on load, It was stopped immediately followed by stopping priming pump as well.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: O-ring of L.O filter damaged.
DirectCause: Damaged O-ring/ incorrrect box up after filter cleaning.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Condition of the O-rings to be checked after every filter cleaning and the equipment to be function tested after every routine.

IncidentDetails: Alternator #1 winding damaged and inoperational
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging cargo @ montreal port, 6 Cargo pumps were running and all 4 power packs were in iuse.AE#1 and AE#2 were running in parallel and AE#3 was in standby.    Around @2159 hrs LO Differential pressure alarm sounded for AE#1, Duty engineer immideately decided to start other AE#3 Generator and put on load, and offload and stop AE#1 so , he can change LO filters.    Duty engineer started AE#3 generator locally and on his way to ECR to Shift load to AE#3.but on his way to ECR he heard a loud blast sound from AE#1 alternator and black out happened.    But within few seconds Power resumed automatically and AE#3 Was running on load, AE#2 was running idle and AE#1 Tripped.    Chief engineer already reached to ECR during the Black out and power resume incident.Chief engineer and duty engineer found AE#1 Alternator winding were burnt and smoke was coming from alternator.Duty engineer stopped AE#2 and took round for all three auxiliary engine.    Duty engineer and chief engineer found that AE#1 Alternator windings burnt and smoke was coming from alternator.    As cargo discharging was going on, chief engineer and duty engineer both gave importance to resume cargo dischaging as early as possible. So Duty engineer started AE#2 locally and after confirming no abnormilities he put it on load. And started all machineries in sequence required for cargo discharging.     Cargo discharging resume completely @1012 LT.
ImmediateAction: Vessel was discharging cargo @ montreal port, 6 Cargo pumps were running and all 4 power packs were in iuse.AE#1 and AE#2 were running in parallel and AE#3 was in standby.    Around @2159 hrs LO Differential pressure alarm sounded for AE#1, Duty engineer immeaditely decided to start other AE#3 Generator and put on load, and offload and stop AE#1 so , he can change LO filters.    Duty engineer started AE#3 generator locally and on his way to ECR to Shift load to AE#3.but on his way to ECR he heard a loud blast sound from AE#1 alternator and black out happened.    But within few seconds Power resumed automatically and AE#3 Was running on load, AE#2 was running idle and AE#1 Tripped.    Chief engineer already reached to ECR during the Black out and power resume incident.Chief engineer and duty engineer found AE#1 Alternaor winding were burnt and smoke was coming from alternator.Duty engineer stopped AE#2 and took round for all three auxiliary engine.    Duty engineer and chief engineer found that AE#1 Alternator windings burnt and smoke was coming from alternator.    As cargo discharging was going on, chief engineer and duty engineer both gave importance to resume cargo dischaging as early as possible. So Duty engineer started AE#2 locally and after confirming no abnormilities he put it on load. And started all machineries in sequence required for cargo discharging.     Cargo discharging resume completely @1012 LT.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Alternator stator winding were burnt, Insulation value is zero and requires rewinding.    Alternator rotor winding were not showing any physical damage but Insulation value is zero .
DirectCause: Actual cause of winding burn is still not confirmed, suspecting high winding temperature may have cause short circuit, resulting in winding burning.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Alternator routines should be carried out with measuring of insulation value of the alternator. Procedure for alternator routines should be followed as per maker's instruction manual and copy of same to be kept nearby the alternator for easy reference. while carrying out routines winding should be checked for dust,dirt accumulation and if require should be cleaned with recommended solvent and blower to clean.    To Maintain safer Cargo operation, manoeuvring operation and normal sea passage opeartion load, Others two generator checked/tested for their optimum peformances.   1. Alternator routines for AE#2 and AE#3 carried out.  2. Alarms and all safety trips checked for  AE#2 and AE#3.  3. Crancase inspection and Crankshaft deflection carried out for AE#2 and AE#3.  4. Permformance taken and recorded for AE#2 and AE#3.
Corrective Action: Troubleshooting with makers and as per their advise,  the rotor and stator require complete rewinding. The Rotor and Stator winding to be completed and installed back with the assistance of Service technicians. Once installation is completed, the AE No 1 to be tried out after all initial checks and ensure proper operation. Class and Flag state has been informed and dispensation taken. Short term Safecon certificate has been issued by Class.
ClosingNote: Noted that AE repairs has been completed and is made operational

IncidentDetails: Wooden bed fastened to bulkhead of second officer cabin disloged from its fixed position during heavy rolling
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl was experiencing heavy wx associated with heavy rolling/pitching.  Second officer after handing over watch to Chief officer and  fire rounds went to his cabin.  On opening the cabin found cabin's bed made of wooden planks fastened to the bulkhead completely  disloged from its position and collapsed.
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed bridge and loose items of cabin were lashed and secured the cabin.  Second officer slept in pilot cabin for the night and next day bed was repaired and made fast to bulkhead again with extra fittings and lashings
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Complete bed wooden planks collapsed & required repairs, additional fastening & securing.
DirectCause: Insufficient building/constructional strength of wooden plank bed lashings along with heavy wx caused the bed lashings to take off thereby leading to collapsing of bed
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All cabins were inspected for the condition of wooden plank bed and made sure they are of sufficient strength to withstand heavy wx and extra lashings/fittings were taken where it was found necessary
Corrective Action: All cabins were inspected for the condition of wooden plank bed and made sure they are of sufficient strength to withstand heavy wx and extra lashings/fittings were taken where it was found necessary
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: While berthing at SBM MARLIN II,the Samson Rope of the SBM Parted, due to which the operation was aborted.  Rope from SBM to Mooring Bouy
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was berthing at SBM MARLIN II, Rosarito, Mexico on 07th Feb 20 to discharge her cargo of Gasoline as per the voyage instructions.  0736 : Pilot on board.  0806 : Commenced Mooring  0818 : The Fwd. SBM Chain was made fast.  The ship's crew along with the loading master/mooring master were following the instructions from the pilot. After making fast the forward chain, the Pilot asked all ship's crew and loading master to proceed aft to make fast the tug AFT.  At 0826 hrs LT, While making the fast tug bridge informed that the SBM rope has parted.Mooring party went forward and reported that the SAMSON rope has parted.  The berthing operation was aborted as per instructions from Pilot and Port Control.   0906 Hrs : Vessel dropped anchor.  0930 Hrs : Pilot Away.  No injury to ship's crew or damage to ship's property took place.
ImmediateAction: The Berthing operation was Aborted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring rope parted for SBM to Floating buoy.  No damage to ship property  No damage to environment  No injury to personnel  No damage to Cargo hose, SBM, Mooring chain
DirectCause: The mooring rope condition  was bad. The vessel could not verified the same as it was submerged in water- No floats were attached.   Fwd mooring party to make a quick glance on the rope/chain after making fast the chain.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Proposed Action - Condition of rope to verify from Loading master / Pilot before mooring.  2) Preventive Action -Standard procedures to be followed like Vessel speed should be less  and approach angle should be low. After make fast- No/minimum swing of vsl.  3) Preventive Action- In case of any doubt, Certificate of Hose & rope to be asked from L/Master. LOP to be issued in case of defective/substandard equipments. Office to be informed of suspicious equip
Corrective Action: Condition of shore equipments if found having concerns to be reported to the authorities and appropriate control measures to be in place for the operation. If required, operation to be put on hold till defect is rectified.
ClosingNote: Shore equipment condition to be checked and reported to all if found having concern. If required, operation to be put on hold till appropriate control measures are in place.

IncidentDetails: Spill of Sludge on Quay side at Eastern Mole Jetty 2 , while landing Sludge in containers to garbage disposal company .
IncidentEventDetails: 07 Feb 20  ======  1150 LT : Two Garbage Collectors boarded the vessel for Collection of Sludge in containers  1150-1210 LT : Vessel Carried out Toolbox meeting with garbage collector . Garbage collector was shown the IBC container in which the sludge was collected. It was agreed that vessel crane will be used to lower the IBC can and the garbage truck will be brought as close as safely possible to transfer the cans in garbage truck.  1210 LT- 1220 LT : Garbage Collector aligned the truck close to the Gangway   1230 LT : Vessel started the transfer operation using Ship's Crane with slings to transfer the cans on the garbage truck placed on the Jetty ( STBD Side ) . AB Narsinga who is trained in Crane Operations was operating the crane . C/O was signalling , OS Venkatesh Gandhi was assisting , 2 Garbage Collectors were on the jetty to direct the drums on to their truck.  1245 LT : When the drum was close to truck railing and the two garbage collector tried to pull the slings towards the truck to direct the drums , and while doing so drum toppled after touching one end of the truck side and 0.3 -0.5 cbm of sludge spilled on jetty from the top of container's lid . It was evident that due to lack of man power and lack of sign language understanding led the sludge collection operators to mis manage the operation and in efficient communication led to the ineffective measures . Additionally there is a crack on the jetty which caused , some oil to seep in water forming a sheen near ship side.  1250 LT : All Crew was mustered and clean up operation commenced as per SOPEP contingency Plan.  1255 LT : Charterers agent , Husbandry agent and DPA were informed . Agent was asked to inform all relevant authorities concerned.  1430 LT : Clean up was completed on Jetty by Ship's Crew.
ImmediateAction: All crew mustered , Clean up was carried out on Jetty by ship's Crew. All concerned authorities were reported.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NIl damage or Injury
DirectCause: 1. Lack of Skill from Contractor  2. Inadequate personal resource deployed for the task by the contractor  3. Ship staff did not identify in timely manner to deploy additional resource on jetty to assist the contractor  4. Lack of Solid sealing cover of IBC can
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Confirmation to be taken from Service provider that they will be providing sufficient resource needed for the job prior confirmationof the order.  2. Ship staff whenever in doubt that the operation cannot be carried out safely or efficiently by the service provider must abort the operation , Reassess the risk and then resume if needed. A training to be carried out for same .  3. All the Sludge containers to be landed to be properly sealed.

IncidentDetails: Fwd Head line parted during mooring stations at Port Antwerp.
IncidentEventDetails: During mooring stations at port Antwerp where vessel was berthed port side alongside ,vessel experienced strong Winds from port Bow of vessel .1st line ashore was 1 inboard headline forward. As vessel started falling back very fast , headline was under  excessive tension and parted. All mooring crew were clear of area.
ImmediateAction: Another head line and 2 breast lines were Made fast immediately , engines used, tugs were used for pushing vessel alongside .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 nos mooring rope damaged
DirectCause: Excessive tension on mooring lines due to surging resulted in parting of 1st head line
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mooring ropes need to be renewed
Corrective Action: Mooring ropes need to be renewed
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: Aft stern line parted during mooring  stations inside lock gates - Antwerp
IncidentEventDetails: During mooring stations inside lock gates , vessel experienced strong winds.  Due to surging of vessel resulting from strong winds 1st line ashore aft (stern line) came under excessive tension  and  parted. All mooring crew clear of rope.
ImmediateAction: Additional stern lines were passed and made fast.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1 nos mooring rope damaged
DirectCause: Excessive tension on mooring lines due to surging resulting from strong winds.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mooring ropes to be renewed
Corrective Action: Mooring ropes to be renewed
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: After departing port of Baltimore, vsl experienced very rough wx of BF - 10/11 & heavy beam swells with shipping seas on deck, even though weather predictions from EGC/WNI had only indicated weather of BF-6/7. Though the vessel had taken adequate safety measures for navigation in heavy weather including alteration of her course and speed to keep the seas on port bow, thereby minimising shipping seas on deck, vessel suffered minor damages such as Pilot assist ladder (P) side staunchion holders got slightly deformed, Catwalk platform on f'csle (P) side got damaged, hawse pipe grill got washed away(1 no), fire hose box(1 no) on main deck got damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: After departing port of Baltimore, vsl experienced very rough wx of BF - 10/11 & heavy beam swells with shipping seas on deck, even though weather predictions from EGC/WNI had only indicated weather of BF-6/7. Though the vessel had taken adequate safety measures for navigation in heavy weather including alteration of her course and speed to keep the seas on port bow, thereby minimising shipping seas on deck, vessel suffered minor damages such as Pilot assist ladder (P) side staunchion holders got slightly deformed, Catwalk platform on f'csle (P) side got damaged, hawse pipe grill got washed away(1 no), fire hose box(1 no) on main deck got damaged.
ImmediateAction: Vessel had taken adequate safety measures for navigation in heavy weather including alteration of her course and speed to keep the seas on port bow, thereby minimising shipping seas on deck and continuous monitoring of wx reports.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel suffered minor damages such as Pilot assist ladder (P) side staunchion holders got slightly deformed, Catwalk platform on f'csle (P) side got damaged, hawse pipe grill got washed away(1 no), fire hose box(1 no) on main deck got damaged.

Though the vessel had taken adequate safety measures for navigation in heavy weather including alteration of her course and speed to keep the seas on port bow, thereby minimising shipping seas on deck, vessel suffered minor damages such as Pilot assist ladder (P) side staunchion holders got slightly deformed, Catwalk platform on f'csle (P) side got damaged, hawse pipe grill got washed away(1 no), fire hose box(1 no) on main deck got damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regular monitoring of weather & avoiding areas of heavy wx conditions, adequate safety measures for navigation in heavy weather including alteration of her course and speed as required, to avoid shipping seas on deck.

IncidentDetails: On 24th Dec.2019 at Singapore ESPA anchorge,veesel received M/E L.O.System ,cylinder oil & LSMGO  bunker from 1110 Hrs to 1800 Hrs.    Before starting the LSMGO bunkering,meeting was carried out with barge cargo officer and initial trasfer rate was agreed 50 M3/Hr,Maximum transfer rate 150M3/Hr.    LSMGO bunkering started at 1447 Hrs,initially it was decided first to take bunker in DOT(P)& DOT(S)and lined up as decided,just after starting the bunker,4th Engineer checked the sounding of both tanks,oil was flowing into DOT(P) and he did not find oil flow in DOT(S),then he checked the sounding of No.1 FOT(S)found no oil flow in that tank and finally checked the L/S DOT(S) and he observed that oil flow was there.Then we came to know that tank's filling line stencil was done wrong.While vessel had tanken bunker in both tanks on 13th Oct.2019 at Suez anchorage and both filling valves marking was correct.    Before arrival Ravenna vessel had painted the new LSMGO line and stencile was done again after painting,by mistake last 4/E had marked wrong filling valve,instead of L/S DOT(S),it was marked DOT(S) and vice versa.    Once we came to know that filling valve marking is wrong then we throttled the filling valve of L/S DOT(S) and fully kept open the DOT(P) so that it can fill up first upto decided level as per bunker plan.    Bunker barge was at STBD side and hose was connected at STBD side hence the more oil flow was there in L/S DOT(S)and tank capacity is less,as L/S DOT(S)filling valve was throttled and hence we assumed that oil flow should be less.Location of the L/S DOT(S) sounding pipe is at upper deck STBD side and rest on all on poop deck,hence it was very difficult to check the both tanks sounding during topping up of the DOT(P).Other crew members were busy in lifting and shifting of stores & spares at designated location and 2/E & 3/E were busy with governor technicians to try out the engine after fitting the new governor servo driver.    During the topping up of the DOT(P),L/S DOT(S) filling valve was kept at same throttled position and start closing of DOT(P) filling valve and opening of DOT(S) filling valve,by the time we fully opened the filling valve of the DOT(S),got a call from bridge that 3/E had informed about the L/S DOT(S) high level alarm, immediately we asked bunker barge to stop pumping and opened the filling valve of NO.1 FOT(S) so that LSMGO line can be drained in empty tank.As per alarm indicator in ECR,L/S DOT(S) alarm activated at 1535 Hrs.    By the time we observed that, approx.10-15 Ltrs LSMGO splashed out from L/S DOT(S)vent head on deck near save all tray due to wind and pressure and 30-35 Ltrs LSMGO contained in save all tray.
IncidentEventDetails: LSMGO bunkering started at 1447 Hrs  Duty Engineer checked the sounding of both tanks,oil was flowing into DOT(P) and he did not find oil flow in DOT(S),then he checked the sounding of No.1 FOT(S)found no oil flow in that tank and finally checked the L/S DOT(S) and he observed that oil flow was there.  Then came to know that tank's filling line stencil was done wrong.  Once we came to know that filling valve marking is wrong then we throttled the filling valve of L/S DOT(S) and fully kept open the DOT(P) so that it can fill up first upto decided level as per bunker plan.  During the topping up of the DOT(P),L/S DOT(S) filling valve was kept at same throttled position and start closing of DOT(P) filling valve and opening of DOT(S) filling valve,by the time we fully opened the filling valve of the DOT(S),got a call from bridge that 3/E had informed about the L/S DOT(S) high level alarm.  L/S DOT(S) High Level alarm activated at 1535 Hrs.  By the time we observed that, approx.10-15 Ltrs LSMGO splashed out from L/S DOT(S)vent head on deck near save all tray due to wind and pressure and 30-35 Ltrs LSMGO contained in save all tray.
ImmediateAction: Immediately Bunkering operation stopped.  Contingency Plan Followed & Executed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage / Injury
DirectCause: Root causes of the incident:    1. Multi jobs were going on during bunkering e.g.stores/spares being received / shifting to designated locations,technicians were attending the M/E governor issue and requesting to try out the M/E hence 2/E & 3/E were busy with them to avoid any further delay for the technicians.    2. There was no walkie talkie in the E/R otherwise communication time would have been reduced and incident would have been avoided.  Since 3/E was at M/E Emc'y Manoeuvring platform while L/S DOT(S)high level alarm activated,he came up to ECR and reported to bridge to inform C/E about the alarm.    3. Location of sounding pipes of both tanks at different level hence it is very difficult to manage both tanks sounding at the time of topping up.    4. Wrong stencile of the L/S DOT(S) & DOT(S) filling valves.    5. For LSMGO sounding we have to apply oil finding paste on tapes which takes time and sometimes correct sounding can be missed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Identify job priority  Proper Communication.  Proper Stenciling & Cross check by senior officer.

IncidentDetails: While going for stations, it was noticed that fwd spring rope damaged due to sleeve strand of the rope stuck in the fender.
IncidentEventDetails: Fwd spring stuck in the fender & got damaged to its sleeve.
ImmediateAction: Rope slacked immedietly and cleared from fender to prevent further damage to main rope.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While going for stations, it was noticed that fwd spring rope damaged due to rope sleeve's strand stuck in the fender.
DirectCause: The lead of the rope not properly checked for any obstructions.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Mooring to be tended frequently and if required to shift from one bollard to other if any obstructions in between.
ClosingNote: Always ensure moorings are tended at all times

IncidentDetails: Please note that while heaving stbd anchor during singapore departure, observed that stbd anchor chain was tangled into the one side of the fluke and got fouled in horizontal position. Same has been informed pilot. Due to congested area, pilot advised to just pick up from water level and carry out the job at OPL.    Since anchor got fouled in horizontal position, can not keep anchor just above the water level. Due to rolling it may bang the ship side and damage the hull. So decided to heave up anchor just below the hawse pipe. While heaving up anchor, chain got sliped off from the fluke.Which produced heavy jerk on the windlass.
IncidentEventDetails: Stbd windlass control valve block craked, One teeth of gear has been sheared off, hydraulic motor foundation was shifted 1mm from its position and oil seal was damaged. Hydraulic oil is leaking from oil seal and control valve block.
ImmediateAction: Immediately Hydraulic pump has been switched off, Oil leaks was controlled and drained.  Stbd windlass inspection carried out. Checked the Bitter end safety pin and stopper.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Stbd windlass control valve block craked, One teeth of gear has been sheared off, hydraulic motor foundation was shifted 1mm from its position and oil seal was damaged. Hydraulic oil is leaking from oil seal and control valve block.
DirectCause: Tangled Chain got slipped from the fluke while heaving up above water level.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: If such occurance happen keep the anchor in same position and do the proper risk assessment, take shore assistance prior carrying out job activity.  Person in charge and all concern staff to be briefed about the operating procedure  and precaution to be taken during critical equipment handling.
ClosingNote: CAR verified and closed

IncidentDetails: During Cargo Gear Pressure testing newly installed GP gasket on the 20 Kg steam line to the COPT gave away and missed the 4th engineer who was standing nearby.
IncidentEventDetails: One of the Main Steam branch line flange gasket (Spiral) for the COPT was leanking and renewed with GP gasket sandwitched between a 1.5 to 2 mm metal plate to compensate for the thickness. This was done to avoide andy remarks if PSC boarded in china.    New spare spiral gasket were not available & same was requisitioned (SPR/PBOR/E/20/007). During Cargo Gear Pressure testing the gastet gave away and missed the 4th engineer who was standing nearby.
ImmediateAction: Operations stopped and Steam line isolated
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One of the Main Steam branch line flange gasket (Spiral) for the COPT was leanking and renewed with GP gasket sandwitched between a metal plate of 1.5 to 2 mm to compensate for the thickness. New spare spiral gasket were not available & same was requisitioned. During Cargo Gear Pressure testing the gastet gave away and missed the 4th engineer who was standing nearby.
DirectCause: Wrong Parts/material used due to unavailibity of spares leading to failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Use of Standard and correct spares parts

IncidentDetails: On 17th October 2019 vessel experienced strong gust of wind & current during the 2nd Officer’s watch at around 1350. 2nd Officer reported to Master that the vessel was turning to port and Master asked him to proceed to forward anchor station in preparation for heaving up anchor. Master came on bridge in response to the report from 2nd Officer and proceeded to heave anchor in order to re-establish position but discovered that the port anchor had fouled with the starboard one. Master initially attempted to untangle the anchors using cables and  by heaving of the anchors, but the extent of entanglement was such that it was not possible. Master then reported the matter to the marine superintendent and sought shore assistance.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel was anchored at Del Guazu anchorage on the 11th of October 2019. At the advice of the pilot the vessel had employed 2 anchors to hold its position. Master deployed 5 shackles on the stbd side and 3 on the port side. Vessel was within a narrow channel of about 5 cables width with the nearest vessel at anchor approximately 8 cables astern.
ImmediateAction: A crane barge was hired for the purpose of untangling the anchors after clearance from the owners. Same was brought alongside on the 1250 LT on 17th Oct. The task of untangling the fouled anchors was carried out on the 17th of October and successfully completed. During the process of the untangling, work was briefly stopped while the barge was waited for a  suitable cable as the one they had brought initially was inadequate. However, the anchors were clear and housed safely by 2200 on the 17th of Oct 2019 After completion of the untangling, vessel’s staff examined the anchor chains, pins flukes and kenter shackles prior to releasing the barge and housing them on board. Vessel then proceeded to re anchor without any further incident. At the re anchoring equal number of shackles were used the second time.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage. no injury
DirectCause: a. Direct Cause- Direct cause for the entanglement of the chains can be attributed to the turning of the vessel due to strong tidal currents at the vessels’ anchorage position. b. Indirect cause-The indirect cause for the incident can be attributed to - Poor watchkeeping by all bridge watchkeepers, thereby failing to notice the dragging of the anchor and change in position with the current. -Poor supervision by the Master of the vessel’s watchkeeping duties and their execution,despite being aware of the nature of the current and the hazards involved, having already been alerted by the pilot at arrival and through port information. -Lack of enough detail in bridge orders, thereby causing junior watchkeepers to be unaware of the need to monitor the vessel’s position with extra caution due to the nature of the anchorage. - Strong tidal currents at the port, with limited anchoring space and room for movement -Unequal lengths of cable while laying out the anchor for port and stbd lines
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Detailed instructions for standing moor and running moor for vessel anchoring sent to the vessel immediately after reporting of the incident. 2. Master instructed to carry out a training session for the bridge watchkeepers on this along with watchkeeping procedures at anchorage. 3. Master instructed (over mail from office) to write detailed night order books and include instruction specific to the situation on board for the benefit of the bridge watchkeepers whenever extra alertness or awareness is required for special occasions. Samples of night orders to be sent at month end for review to the satisfaction of the Marine superintendent. 4. Master also advised by mail that the understanding of the instructions by bridge watchkeepers will be verified at the time of the next vessel attendance from office
LessionLearnt: Better procedures for anchor watch to be followed across the fleet, with MSIs to be constantly aware of the situation on board and capabilities of crew.
ReviewComments: None
ClosingNote: Corrective action verified by MSI/TSI
