IncidentDetails: Fwd life raft was stowed/secured on the cradle and extra lashings were taken as a precautionary measure for heavy weather , as a result of which the life raft was not displaced and lost but the crushing force of water has led to damage on the casing of life raft due to impact on the cradle.
IncidentEventDetails: 02nd Feb: Extra Lashings were taken on  Deck/Accomodation / stores on  account of expected Rough weather as per weather report .  06th-10th Feb:Vsl was experiencing heavy weather with frequent sprays/seas on main deck  along with heavy pitching and rolling. Wind force of Beaufort  9  and sea of 6-8m in height were experienced  .  11th Feb: Deck Rounds were taken to ascertain any damages as a result of Rough weather when the seas were settled down .                 11th Feb:Found Fwd life raft stowed/secured on the cradle with extra lashings ,though not displaced                from the cradle/lost, but the crushing force of water has led to damage on the casing of life                raft due to impact on the cradle.Life raft FRP casing approx 10cms got holed with abrasions                as a result of crushing impact on the cradle.
ImmediateAction: Fwd life raft temporarily patched up with corrodobond mix with tape to prevent any water                 ingress .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Life raft FRP casing approx 10cms got holed with abrasions as a result on crushing impact on the cradle.
DirectCause: Damage to Fwd life raft as a result of  heavy seas on break of F'castle/deck where the life raft was stowed. Impact of the life raft with the cradle has led to the damage .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Possible immediate approved repair options to be suggested by manufacturer(VIKING).  Damaged liferaft to be replaced with new one at next port of call .
Corrective Action: Liferaft to be renewed basis other communication
ClosingNote: Noted new liferaft fitted in place and COC cleared.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was transiting through the Surigao strait, when suddenly the port anchor was released from the brake and was running out. Immediately Master made an announcement over the P A and stopped the engine using emergency stop.The Chief officer was rushed forward and vessel given astern movement. Vessel was fortunately in an open area and vessel position was held.The port side anchor had run out completely and the bitter end was damaged but the not parted. Anchor was subsequently recovered using the windlass and housed safely. Daamages to the FP tank, TSTs and chain locker were assessed.No major damages found that required immediate action.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was transiting the Surigao strait. The port anchor and windlass had last been used on 12th Jan 2020 and there was no record of any load testing for the windlass on board.As per Master's statement anchor was
DirectCause: Inadequate maintenance of the brake band and brake lining. Possible inadequate tightening of the brake band prior to securing the anchor. Grooving of the chain stopper to the extent that it was ineffective.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Maintenance of the brake and examination of the chain stopper to be done with care and diligence. Grooving and wear of the stopper to be acted upon and action taken in time.

IncidentDetails: During the process of lowering Cargo hose in water , Hose handling Crane’s wire parted. Crane’s hook which was connected to the Hose was lost.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel completed Cargo Discharge of 1st Parcel of 92 RON Gasoline at Pozos Colorado SBM on 28th Dec / 1454 Hrs LT.  Cargo hose was disconnected at 1524 Hrs LT. During the process of lowering Cargo hose in water , Hose handling Crane’s wire parted.     At the time incident , Wind was NE X 6  and Swell was NE X 2-2.5 m.
ImmediateAction: DIVERS WERE ARRANGED TO RETRIEVE CRANE HOOK
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DAMAGE TO CRANE WIRE AND LOSS OF HOOK
DirectCause: CRANE WIRE WAS RENEWED IN APRIL 2019 DURING DRYDOCKING.BUT THE WIRE WAS MANUFACTURED IN 2013.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MANUFACTURING DATE AND INSTALLATION DATE SHOULD BE AS CLOSE AS POSSIBLE
ClosingNote: In-use wire and spare wires to be renewed every 5 years, to ensure spare wires are not stored on shelf for long.

IncidentDetails: At 1920 hrs on 27 Feb 2020 ships time while the vessel was at Kwinana OPL anchorage , was struck by  lightening on the main mast as a result of which the satellite speed log became inoperational ,location of the antenna for speed log is on the main mast upper platform.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1920 hrs on 27 Feb 2020  while the vessel was at Kwinana OPL anchorage , was struck by  lightening on the main mast as a result of which the satellite speed log became inoperational, location of the antenna for speed log is on the main mast upper platform. Checke antenna unit on 28th Feb2020 and observed it is fully damaged by lightening strike.
ImmediateAction: Vessel staff checked the situation for fire , sparks or any kind of ignition from the bridge wing and monkey island , none found.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Power junction box and antenna unit got damaged.
DirectCause: Lightening (adverse weather).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Obtain/seek weather forecast from port control/office
Corrective Action: power junction box and antenna unit to be renewed

IncidentDetails: Vessel enroute to Cristobal in Gulf of Mexico . Laden passage with cargo ULSD . Due to  Excessive Vapour Pressure In The Cargo Cross Over Line oil was found Dripping  on Main Deck from No.#2 Cross over Valve Flange.  All oil was mop dried on deck  and clean up carried out.
IncidentEventDetails: 28-Feb-2020 1400 : Vessel Completed Loading ULSD @ Pascagoula  28-Feb-2020 1500 : Vessel Linedup For cargo Vapour Expansion.  29-Feb-2020 1200 : No Traces of oil on deck, crew working in vicinity  29-Feb-2020 1330 : Still no traces of oil on deck, Crew working in vicinity.  29-Feb-2020 1445 : One of the crew member working in the vicinity reported that oil dripping on deck out of Crossover valve #2  29-Feb-2020 1447 : Confirmed all Scupper Plugged, Cleanup in progress  and Valves Openned up for Cargo Vapour Expansion.  29-Feb-2020 1530 : Cleanup Operation Completed, All Lines Checked For Integrity.
ImmediateAction: Scuppers Plugged immediately to contain any oil onboard, Cleanup Operation Started  and Valves Openned up for Cargo Vapour Expansion.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Due to Excessive vapour pressure in cross over line Oil was found dripping on Main Deck from #2 Crossover valve and found Light Green Colour oil trickling down along the fish plate near the manifold area.  Cleanup was carried out, no damage to cargo valve and associated pipelines.  No damage to paint coating of main deck.  No visible stains of oil on deck.  No visible sheen of oil was observed overboard.  All Oil was contained onboard  Estimated qty of oil on deck 5 ltrs
DirectCause: Direct Cause:  • After topping off 2W’s COTks, though ship-staff did conducted draining and blowing through of cargo lines. However, due to raised construction of main lines from athwart-ship lines and x-over valve 203 not re-opened post cargo operation, resulted in some remnant cargo in no.2 cargo line to produce excessive vapour pressure and leak from flange of x-over valve 203. Indirect Cause:  • Lack of Supervision: person in charge of cargo operations lacked in proper lining up of valves post cargo operation to ensure that if any cargo left entrapped in lines could later not result in any leakages due to thermal vapour expansion. Root Cause:   • Lack of Compliance - Athwart-ship no.2 cargo manifold line was not adequately drained after topping off. • Lack of following procedures – Cargo lineup was not rechecked using second man check as per procedures, to ensure that the lineup was adequate to avoid excessive pressure or vacuum in the cargo lines due to any remnant cargo entrapped in lines.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Training of all crew and officers to be carried out with respect to the Guidelines of Cargo operation manual to be followed for proper draining of cargo lines post cargo operations.                      2. Detailed briefing of Person in charge for cargo operation regarding any cargo lineup to be double checked by one more responsible officer prior and post cargo operations. 3. Details of incident and learning to be share with sister vessel (Hakata Princess) and other similar vessels in fleet to avoid re-occurrence of same again. 4. Learning from the incident to be discussed with all Joining Master and Chief officers during pre-joining briefing.
Corrective Action: Training of all crew and officers to be carried out with respect to the Guidelines of Cargo operation manual to be followed for proper draining of cargo lines post cargo operations.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: All CA closed out.

IncidentDetails: Vsl planned to berth and swing bow to stbd, in order to come port side alongside at CB6. 2 tugs were made fast on stbd main deck and stbd quarter, 3rd tug was pushing at bow. At 0120, while swinging to stbd, vsl stern went out of channel and aft part touched bottom. Vsl started swinging to port. vsl advised pilot to ask both tugs to pull the vsl forward, while vsl tried to use own power. ME did not start due to load limitation and repeated starts also failed. Rudder was hard stbd when vsl touch bottom and was put to midship, rudder responded immidiately and was on midship. After repeated tries to get own ME running failed, pilot requested for 2 additional tugs from port control. At 0127, additional tugs arrived and started pushing vsl from stern, while thrusters were being used to turn port side. Later stbd tug was to pull vsl towards channel. At 0135, vsl stern was clear and at 0136 vsl ME was used to move ahead. At 0136, vsl berthed alongside at CB6.
IncidentEventDetails: 27/0001 : POB  27/0047 : Aft tug (Orchid star) fast at stbd qtr  27/0048 : Fwd tug (Kink Disha) fast at stbd bow  27/0109 : Commenced swing bow to stbd  27/0120 : Vsl swing stopped/engine not repsonding/vsl started swing to port  27/0122 : 3rd tug Ocean Endurance started pushing from port qtr  27/0127 : 4 tugs at aft pushing (ocean endurance, ocean symphony,ocean navigator and orchid star)  27/0135 : Vsl clear off the shallow patch  27/0136 : Vsl engine used to move ahead
ImmediateAction: Additional tugs were called to push vsl from aft. 1 tug was pushing vsl from port quarter and fwd tug was pulling vsl in fwd direction to take vsl out of shallow waters. Tug which was fast on stbd quarter was asked to pull vsl towards main channel. At 0136 when vsl stern was clear of shallows and was moving ahead, vsl used own power and cleared the shallow water.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Co2 room, steering gear room, and aft void space centre inspected. No damages observed/found. Stern thruster area in engine room checked. No damages found/observed. Engine room bottom platform and shell plating checked, no damages found/observed. No 8 wing tanks sounded and no damages found/observed.Crankshaft web deflection carried out at Mundra port, no abnormalities observed.
DirectCause: Vsl pivot point was in centre of channel and vsl was turned before the turning circle, off the berth with 1 vsl at berth CB5. Vsl was at deep draft and while turning the tide caught the lateral side of beam and vsl was drifted out of channel on to shallows.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Prior approach to berth, swinging area to be discussed with pilot. Vsl to swing in turning circle provided north of the berths.

IncidentDetails: Leakage of aprox 45 Ltr of hydraulic oil from the forward spring mooring winch return line due to development of hole on the line in pressurized condition.  As the scuppers plugs were in place, all oil was contained on deck and none has gone overboard
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was involved in operating windlass for anchor operation. To failtate that, forward hydraulic motors that supplies oil to mooring winch and windlass were running.   During that course person passing by observed hydraulic oil flowing from longitudonals to ship side. He tracked the oil trail and identified that hydraulic oil was leaking heavily from the forward spring winch return line.
ImmediateAction: On identification of leakage, hydraulic motors were stopped.  All activities suspended and vessel called emergency stations to contain the oil.    (NOTE:  As hydraulic mooring winches lines does not have isolation valves,  residual oil supply could not be isolated.)  Flowing oil was collected in container until flow pressure reduced. As temprory measure, C clamp was fabricated and placed on the defective portion of the line to arrest the possible leakage while running of mooring motors so that ongoing operations can be completed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On inspection post incident, hydraulic line section appeared to be weak due to surface corrosion thus leading to failure of pipe and release of 45 ltr of hydraulic oil from primary containement.
DirectCause: Hydraulic line section appeared to be weak due to surface corrosion thus leading to failure of pipe in pressurized condition.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Defective section of the hydraulic line will be re fabricated and replaced with fresh pipe.   To avoid repeatance of such incident, all the hydraulic pipelines of mooring winch will be inspected to identify weak spots. On identification weaker sections will be changed.
ClosingNote: The PMS routine should identify such leaks in advance. Discussed same with master.

IncidentDetails: On 25th February 2020 at around 1125 hrs LT, 440 volts low insulation alarm activated. The problem was rooted from  HP washing machine receptacle on  the deck. Small amount of smoke found from the panel box located in between Cago hold No. 6 & 7. The main 440 power supply to the 440 volts panel board was then isolated. Upon opening the panel, it was noticed that the 440 volts, plug and receptacle was melted. Suspect same was due to losse connection between plug and receptacle which led to sparking.
IncidentEventDetails: The 440 volts plug was not fully inserted and locked into the receptacle, which was caused to the sparking leading to overheating which inturn has resulted in the complete damage to the receptacle and the plug.
ImmediateAction: Power to the panel was isolated. Little amount of CO2 sprayed on the receptacle.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 440 volt receptacle between cargo hold no.6 & 7 damaged. HP machine plug also damaged.
DirectCause: Suspected possible loose connection of the plug in to the 440 volts receptacle has caused the sparking which has lead to overheating.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Replacement of the present Receptacles and the plugs of rating same or higher than HP machine rating.   Training of all ships staff carried out for correct insertion and fitting of electrical connections.
ClosingNote: Ensure to recheck electrical connections prior actual assigned job.

IncidentDetails: Around 1810 with 2 Electric and 2 Diesel powerpacks running for cargo discharging, Cargo system abnormal alarm sounded on ECR panel. Duty engineer immediately inspected the powerpack space, Diesel powerpack # 1 found Hydraulic oil leakage.
IncidentEventDetails: Around 1810 with 2 Electric and 2 Diesel powerpacks running for cargo discharging, Cargo system abnormal alarm sounded on ECR panel. Duty engineer immediately inspected the powerpack space, Diesel powerpack # 1 found Hydraulic oil leakage.
ImmediateAction: CCR was immediately imformed and cargo discharging was stopped,Leaking oil was contained in the empty drums.Diesel powerpack # 1 was inspected suspected leakage from Seal, Diesel powerpack # 1 suction valve was shut and Hydraulic oil topped up in the powerpack tank and system started.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Under investigation for overhauling need framo technician onboard
DirectCause: Machinary failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: framo technician visist and overhauling of system
Corrective Action: Makers FRAMO contacted. They suggested to replace the pump. Same has been ordered and will be connected as soon as possible. If required, technician attendance will be planned.
ClosingNote: Pump unit replaced with re-conditioned pump from FRAMO. Old pump will be off landed and sent to FRAMO for investigation

IncidentDetails: After the last discharging operation it was observed that the ships mooring rope deployed for pull back tug operation at port of mundra found damaged where it was having contact at panama chock.
IncidentEventDetails: 1142 LT: pull back tug cast off  1145LT: Both tug lines heaved back  1145LT: rubber guard for chafe protection removed while coiling back                 It was observed that the mooring rope was ramaged more than 25%
ImmediateAction: Mooring rope removed from service and requisition raised for mooring rope and chafe protection cover
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: After the last discharging operation it was observed that the ships mooring rope deployed for pull back tug operation at port of mundra found damaged where it was having contact at panama choke.
DirectCause: Continuous pull by pull back tug. Chafe protection used  only rubber material and grease insufficient.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New tug rope ordered and chafe protection covers for mooring ropes ordered.
ClosingNote: new ropes supplied.

IncidentDetails: During Hose operation vessel noticed that hydraulic oil leak infront of midship.Valve Hydraulic pump was used to line up for loading operation and stopped at the time of the leak.
IncidentEventDetails: Hydraulic pump was started at 18.30 for line up. Pump stopped after the line up operation. At 1900 hrs Observed Hydraulic oil leak at Midship. While investigating found the oil coming from the main hydraulic oil supply line at the u-clamp.
ImmediateAction: Oil leak arrested and forward supply to the deck line isolated from in front of the pumroom top. Two emergency hand pumps put in use to operate cargo valves.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cargo remote control valve system Hydraulic line Inlet line forward of the midship area leaked from the U-Clamp section due to corrosion and chafing wear of the pipeline.
DirectCause: Corrosion and chafing wear of the pipeline at U-Clamp on pipe rest.  Material failure due to ageing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crop the section of the pipe and renew new pipe with ermeto coupling connection. Inspect the RCVS pipeline on deck and renew as needed.
Corrective Action: inspect the pipelines and chip/renew the pipelines.

IncidentDetails: During changing of reducer while using hose handling crane, there was a leak on the hoisting drum hydraulic high pressure line.
IncidentEventDetails: During changing of reducer while using hose handling crane, there was a leak on the hoisting drum hydraulic high pressure line.  The crane hydraulics were stopped immediately. About 1 litre oil oil which leaked out of the pipe section was cleaned up. The pipe was renewed and replaced. Crane found working satisfactory
ImmediateAction: The crane hydraulics were stopped immediately. About 1 litre oil oil which leaked out of the pipe section was cleaned up. The pipe was renewed and replaced. Crane found working satisfactory
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Hoisting drum hydraulic high pressure line holed.
DirectCause: Corrosion
Proposedcorrectiveactions: New pipe fabricated and fitted in place of old pipe
Corrective Action: Proper and regular inspection of the high pressure pipelines of the Crane hydraulic system to be carried out and any concerns noted to be attended on a priority basis.
ClosingNote: Proper and regular inspection of the high pressure pipelines of the Crane hydraulic system to be carried out and any concerns noted to be attended on a priority basis.

IncidentDetails: Following are the damages resulted from the heavy weather experience from 09 -11 from exit of English Channel & Bay of Biscay.    1.Manifold drip tray stbd side FRP grating were detached from position as the securing clamps sheared off in shipping seas. Few grating were suffered damage during their fly on deck in heavy wind and their movement had damaged 2 nos manifold drip tray drains and bunker manifold drains.    2.Manifold and bunker manifold drains detached from position.    3.Guard plating for the gas detection & gauging pipes on stbd side near No. 3 Stbd WBT were detached from angle support in shipping seas. Section of gas detection pipe also damaged.    4.Guard plating for the gas detection & gauging pipes on port side near No. 3 Port WBT uprooted from angle support.    5.Damage suffered on guard plate of gas detection and gauging pipes aft of  stbd side manifold, the section presumed to have been hit by the moving manifold FRP grating in shipping seas.    6.Anchor lashing parted, but during periodic inspection, chain lashing was taken to substitute same.    7.One step on the walk way near 6 stbd COT stbd side, damaged from waves .    8.Wilden pump and its permanent piping  ( fitted by bolts ) was lifted from position and exposed , this had caused a slight bend on the Wilden pump disch line to slop port tank as attached.    9.Anchor wash pipes leading to nozzle in FPK store , one each on both port and stbd side hawse pipe developed cracks on welding due to stress. This had allowed seepage of sea water in to FPK store but was pumped out upon activation of bilge alarm.    10. One number fire hose box damaged in waves , but hose and Nozzle is intact.    11.Hinges for hawse pipe port side ISPS cover sheared off .     12. Dressor coupling packing of COW line, Fire Line, foam line and IG line damaged.    13.Gangway - plate with operational specifications were distorted and damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was enroute to Trieste(Italy) from Ust Luga(Russia) .From the Exit the English Channel, vessel experienced rough weather from 09 -11 Feb in Atlantic Ocean and Bay of Biscay. Vsl sustained Material damages on main deck areas as per the details listed earlier.     Heavy wx precautions including additional lashing were taken on various deck stores, anchor and LSA items before entering to Hv Wx area.
ImmediateAction: Heavy wx precautions including additional lashing were taken on various deck stores, anchor and LSA items before entering to Hv Wx area. Post Bad weather , the complete ship was     1.Anchors inspected visually including the D shackle and Joining shackle. All in order.  2.Both gangway , motor accessories etc in good order.  3.FPK store inspected all in order including the bulk heads.  4. All lifebuoys , Life rafts, mooring lines etc are intact.  5.All lights on deck , fwd mast, fog horn etc are in good order.  6.All tank RADAR gauging systems in good order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.Manifold drip tray stbd side FRP grating were detached from position as the securing clamps sheared off in shipping seas. Few grating were suffered damage during their fly on deck in heavy wind and their movement had damaged 2 nos manifold drip tray drains and bunker manifold drains.    2.Manifold and bunker manifold drains detached from position.    3.Guard plating for the gas detection & gauging pipes on stbd side near No. 3 Stbd WBT were detached from angle support in shipping seas. Section of gas detection pipe also damaged.    4.Guard plating for the gas detection & gauging pipes on port side near No. 3 Port WBT uprooted from angle support.    5.Damage suffered on guard plate of gas detection and gauging pipes aft of  stbd side manifold, the section presumed to have been hit by the moving manifold FRP grating in shipping seas.    6.Anchor lashing parted, but during periodic inspection, chain lashing was taken to substitute same.    7.One step on the walk way near 6 stbd COT stbd side, damaged from waves .    8.Wilden pump and its permanent piping  ( fitted by bolts ) was lifted from position and exposed , this had caused a slight bend on the Wilden pump disch line to slop port tank as attached.    9.Anchor wash pipes leading to nozzle in FPK store , one each on both port and stbd side hawse pipe developed cracks on welding due to stress. This had allowed seepage of sea water in to FPK store but was pumped out upon activation of bilge alarm.    10. One number fire hose box damaged in waves , but hose and Nozzle is intact.    11.Hinges for hawse pipe port side ISPS cover sheared off .     12. Dressor coupling packing of COW line, Fire Line, foam line and IG line damaged.    13.Gnagway - plate with operations specs distored due to impact of waves.
DirectCause: Very rough weather.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Heavy weather preacutions as per SMS to be strictly adhered with in order to avoid / minimise the damages.  2.Sheltering can be considered as an option if available.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA plan. Vessel was well secured for the heavy weather and routing advisory provided by WNI, vessel turned back to take shelter from heavy wx. Minor damages caused due to swell / heavy wx rectiied prior arrival port / cargo lines pressure tested and found in order

IncidentDetails: Fwd Outer Head Line Tail Rope Parting While Discharging At PECEM, Brazil.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging cargo at PECEM, BRAZIL.  The Pier arrangement is such that it is exposed to sea and hence vessel when moored to the berth it experiences surging throught her stay at the berth.  On 13th feb 2020 around 2100 the duty AB reported to OOW that when he went to check the ropes fwd he saw that the outer head line is too slack and hanging compared to other head lines.  He then saw at the bollard on the jetty and found out that the rope is parted.  OOW rushed to the scene to check and confirm about duty AB's reporting, found that the outer headline tail rope is parted and infromed chief offcier.  Chief officer immediately informed master.  Chief officer also called bosun to come to forecastle stations and told cadet to relieve Duty OS from the gangway so that the OS can come to forecastle station for assisting bosun.  OOW and duty ab were sent to berth at the bollard where the rope had parted.  Parted head line was heaved up and inspection carried out where it was found that the eye of the rope tail from the bollard end had parted.  Master was informed about the same and while pictures were taken of the parted tail rope bosun and OS were instructed to prepare to replace the tail rope from the spare mooring line which was kept in forecastle store.  Master informed to lower the stbd anchor to 1 shackle on deck and same was executed.  Upon lowering stbd anchor to 1 shackle on deck then bosun and os started replacing the parted tail rope with the tail rope from the spare mooring line.  After changing the tail rope the same outer head line was passed back to shore where OOW and Duty AB were stand by to recieve the headline.  Outer head line was sent back to bollard and weight was equally adjusted on all the headlines to avoid straining of any line.  Also all other lines were from fwd to aft were checked and found satisfactory.
ImmediateAction: Replaced the parted tail rope with the tail rope from spare mooring line which was kept in forecastle store.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Tail rope parted from the eye of the rope from the bollard end.
DirectCause: Vessel when moored to the berth at PECEM, Brazil. the pier arrangement is such that it is exposd to sea and currents along with tidal range and hence continous surging is experienced till the time vessel is alongside at the berth.  Bollard type is inappropriate for surging and causes regular chafing to tail rope eyes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Continous monitoring of mooring ropes with one person standby fwd and aft, so that the ropes are being adjusted continously and equal weight / load distribution is done on all the ropes.  Greasing on the haswer guards at the point of contact on ships rollers and also on the bollard on the jetty to avoid continous chafing and leading to burnt ends / strands of the rope which eventually leads to parting of the rope.
LessionLearnt: Guide to Port entry, and agents/pilot exchange to be shared with all
ClosingNote: Ensure to carry-out detailed RA, issue LOP for port exposed to wx factors

IncidentDetails: Upon Inspection Of All Mooring Tail Ropes It Was Found That 7 Tail Ropes Were In Poor / Bad / Damaged Condition.
IncidentEventDetails: Mooring Ropes Inspection Was Carried Out On 24th Feb 2020, Upon Inspection Of All Mooring Ropes Including Tail Ropes It Was Found That Mooring Ropes Condition Was Good But 7l Tail Ropes- 3 Headlines and 4 stern lines  Were Found In Damaged / Poor Condition.
ImmediateAction: All The 7 Damaged Tail Ropes Were Identified and Repairs Were Executed For Them Where The Damaged / Poor / Bad Part Of The Tail Ropes Were Removed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Out Of The 7 Tail Damaged Tail Ropes Some were Damaged To More Than 50% Within The Eye Of The Tail Rope.  Some Tail Rope the Damage was within The Eye At More Than One Location By About 25% To 30%, Making Them Cause Of Concern And Hence Immediate Repairs Were Carried Out.
DirectCause: Vessel Has Been Doing The Same Port For Last 2 Voyages And has been Experiencing Continous Surging Throught Her Stay At The Pier, Due To The Arrangement Of The Pier Is Such that it is exposd to sea and currents along with tidal range. Vessel was in total 9 days in port this time due to constant shore breakdowns form 11th Feb 2020 0724hrs LT to 20th Feb 1454hrs LT.  Bollard type is inappropriate for surging and causes regular chafing to tail rope eyes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Continous monitoring of mooring ropes with one person standby fwd and aft, so that the ropes are being adjusted continously and equal weight / load distribution is done on all the ropes.  Greasing on the haswer guards at the point of contact on ships rollers and also on the bollard on the jetty to avoid continous chafing and leading to burnt ends / strands of the rope which eventually leads to parting of the rope.  Additional seizing is planned to be done in the eye of the tail rope of the bollard end before the vessel reaches this port again for discharging to prevent further damage to the eye of the tail rope, due to the improper design of the bollard at the pier.
ClosingNote: Ensure to carry-out detailed RA, issue LOP for port exposed to wx factors

IncidentDetails: Vessel has short loaded approx. 34,000NBbls of the nominated Jet Parcel at Houston, US.   Vessel has loaded 226,000Nbbls instead of 260,000Nbbls.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel has short loaded 34,000NBbls of Jet Parcel.     Cargo qty to load –  260,000NBbls ( As per the voyage orders )   Cargo Loaded       -  226,000NBbls     Vessel has erroneously loaded 34,000NBbls less of the Jet parcel.     The same was due to an incorrect reflection of the nominated qty in the initial Pre Transfer Key Meeting Checklist.  Vessel reflected 226,000Nbbls instead of 260,000Nbbls. ( Attached, the Pre Transfer Checklist FYR )     Both parcels ( Naptha and Jet ) were to supposed to be a SHORE STOP, as discussed during and documented in the Pre Transfer Meeting.   The parcel of NAPTHA was stopped by the Terminal ( SHORE STOP )   However, approx. 3hrs prior to completion of the Jet Parcel, Terminal informed Vessel that the Jet parcel will be a “SHIP STOP”  The same was documented in the Port Log. ( Attached, FYR )     As the amount of 226,000Nbbls was wrongly reflected in the Pre transfer Meeting, the chief officer went on to stop the parcel at 226,000Nbbls onboard.    Vessel departed EDP, as per the voyage orders and therefore no Shore figs. Or B/L figs. were provided by the Terminal to compare prior vessel departed the berth.     Attached, pls find the following FYR :-    1. Minutes of the Pre Transfer Meeting   2. Surveyors Nominated Cargo qty   3. Ship’s Ullage Report   4. Extract of the Port Log reflecting the Terminal’s revised instructions for a Ship Stop.
ImmediateAction: Charterer's / Owners / Managers informed of the same.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel had short loaded 34,000NBbls of Jet Parcel.     Cargo qty to load –  260,000NBbls ( As per the voyage orders )   Cargo Loaded       -  226,000NBbls     Vessel had erroneously loaded 34,000NBbls less of the Jet parcel.     The same was due to an incorrect reflection of the nominated qty in the initial Pre Transfer Key Meeting Checklist.  Vessel reflected 226,000Nbbls instead of 260,000Nbbls. ( Attached, the Pre Transfer Checklist FYR )     Both parcels ( Naptha and Jet ) were to supposed to be a SHORE STOP, as discussed during and documented in the Pre Transfer Meeting.   The parcel of NAPTHA was stopped by the Terminal ( SHORE STOP )   However, approx. 3hrs prior to completion of the Jet Parcel, Terminal informed Vessel that the Jet parcel will be a “SHIP STOP”  The same was documented in the Port Log. ( Attached, FYR )     As the amount of 226,000Nbbls was wrongly reflected in the Pre transfer Meeting, the chief officer went on to stop the parcel at 226,000Nbbls onboard.    Vessel departed EDP, as per the voyage orders and therefore no Shore figs. Or B/L figs. were provided by the Terminal to compare prior vessel departed the berth.
DirectCause: Vessel had erroneously loaded 34,000NBbls less of the Jet parcel due to an incorrect reflection of the nominated qty in the initial Pre Transfer Key Meeting Checklist.  Vessel reflected 226,000Nbbls instead of 260,000Nbbls.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A Thorough Meeting to be carried out prior commencement of operations, which includes the Terminal rep./Cargo Surveyors/Charterer's Agents and the nominated quantities to be clearly  agreed upon with all parties and the same CORRECTLY documented by the CHIEF OFFICER in the Pre Transfer Meeting Checklist.   This Checklist to be signed by the Vessel/Term. Rep/Surveyor and attending Agents and each provided with a copy of the same.   The Checklist to clearly reflect whether it is a "SHIP STOP" or "SHORE STOP" as agreed upon during the Pre Transfer Meeting and strictly comply with the same.
Corrective Action: A Thorough Meeting to be carried out prior commencement of operations, which includes the Terminal rep./Cargo Surveyors/Charterer's Agents and the nominated quantities to be clearly agreed upon with all parties and the same CORRECTLY documented by the CHIEF OFFICER in the Pre Transfer Meeting Checklist.  This Checklist to be signed by the Vessel/Term. Rep/Surveyor and attending Agents and each provided with a copy of the same.  The Checklist to clearly reflect whether it is a "SHIP STOP" or "SHORE STOP" as agreed upon during the Pre Transfer Meeting and strictly comply with the same.

IncidentDetails: Inert Gas Generator Not starting prior berthing due to flame failure.
IncidentEventDetails: Date/Time Events  28/0900-1100 Tried out IGG while entering Japan Waters  29/0900-1200 Vessel started IGG for cargo tank purging and filling.  01/0815 Master informed Engine room to start IGG  01/0830 Vessel attempt to Start IGG, there was flame failure alarm.  01/0845 Started investigation.  01/0930 Informed Captain IGG out of order.  01/1100 Pilot onboard engines ready and started proceeding for berthing.  01/1120   Captain informed pilot about IGG issue and pilot refused to proceed for berthing.  01/1130   Issue rectified, IGG started and informed Captain.  01/1400 Vessel berthed without any delay and started discharging on time.
ImmediateAction: Immediatly issue investigated and rectifed on time and IGG restarted. after restaring found everything normal and  Master informed to avoid unwanted delay in berthing.

IncidentDetails: Boiler Blow down ship side valve bonnet leaking .
IncidentEventDetails: While taking rounds found bonnet of the ship side valve leaking.
ImmediateAction: Informed second engineer and prepared for temporary repair to stop leakage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: This can lead to Engine room Flooding .
DirectCause: The valve was supposedly repaired at the ship yard , and the bad quality of workmanship lead to bonnet gasket leaking.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Temporarily the bonnet is clamped with thick rubber gasket in place to restrict leakage. Office has been informed.   There will be in blow down to be carried out.
ClosingNote: Leaking valve renewed after plugging operation

IncidentDetails: Skylight Hinges Shear Off and Slight Dent on Skylight Lid.
IncidentEventDetails: During Shifting Lube Oil Drums from Deck Via Engine Room Skylight to Engine room. The skylight securing chain (freely hanging ) got stuck on ER crane at a limit switch operating bar. The engine room crane tripped as well as the chain shut the Syylight by pulling force. This caused a dent on Skylight cover and frame and deformed the turn buckle.
ImmediateAction: Secured Skylight lid in closed position.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Skylight Cover / Lid suffered a deformation / dent
DirectCause: Simultaneous Operations Carried out  Lack of Supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Perminantly Remove Securing chain   Add Hazards to RA

IncidentDetails: Delay in Mooring operation due to Mooring Boat's inability to manoeuvre & approach vessel effectively in order to take ship's mooring line for making fast at the jetty.
IncidentEventDetails: 25th Feb 2020 / 2006 LT: Commenced Mooring Operations        25th Feb 2020 / 2050 LT: All Lines made fast Aft (3 Stern + 2 Breast + 2 Springs). 2 Springs, 3 Breast & 1 Head Line made fast fwd.          25th Feb 2020 / 2050 LT: Vessel outboard anchor (stbd anchor) lowered above water level as a general practice everytime in order to avoid tangling of two nos remaining outboard head lines with the anchor.            25th Feb 2020 / 2050 LT to 25th Feb 2020 / 2138 LT: Mooring boat fwd unable to manoeuvre, approach vessel effectively & unable to take & keep vessel's 2nd Outboard Headline clear of the anchor.             25th Feb 2020 / 2138 LT to 25th Feb 2020 / 2200 LT: Vessel awaiting 2nd Mooring Boat in order to resume operation.            25th Feb 2020 / 2200 LT to 25th Feb 2020 / 2315 LT: Second Mooring boat also unable to manoeuvre, approach vessel effectively in order to take ship's remaining two head lines.     25th Feb 2020 / 2315 LT to 26th Feb 2020 / 0106 LT: Mooring operation suspended due to shore gang shift change.            25th Feb 2020 / 0106 LT: Resumed mooring of remaining two head lines using mooring boat.    25th Feb 2020 / 0124 LT: Mooring operations completed. Remaining two head lines made fast.
ImmediateAction: 1) Strongly requested terminal through the Pilot to arrange another mooring boat / its crew in order to complete mooring without further delay.  2) Letter of Protest issued for the delay in Mooring
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Damage/Injury. Delay encountered in Mooring Operations.
DirectCause: Inability of Mooring Boat to manoeuvre & approach vessel effectively & take Ship's Mooring Line in order to make fast at jetty.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to anticipate limitation of mooring boats and highlight the same to the Pilot at first inatance.
ClosingNote: Vessel to discuss mooring boat limitation with ship's crew.

IncidentDetails: Vessel has to cease discharge operations for a period of 18mins between 1118hrs LT to 1136hrs LT due to the Main IG Isolation v/v stuck, resulting in the IG Plant tripping and the tank pressure dropping.   The gear box was an issue and the same has been removed for the time being and valve opened manually.   Vessel has been discharging satisfactorily, after the IG isolation valve was opened manually and has faced no issues after that.     On further investigation, found the v/v spindle disconnected from the Gear Box, resulting in the wheel rotating freely, however as the gear box disconnected the valve did not open.   The shut main line isolation valve on deck resulted in the IG Blower tripping due to excessive back pressure.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel has to cease discharge operations for a period of 18mins between 1118hrs LT to 1136hrs LT due to the Main IG Isolation v/v stuck, resulting in the IG Plant tripping and the tank pressure dropping.   The gear box was an issue and the same has been removed for the time being and valve opened manually.   Vessel has been discharging satisfactorily, after the IG isolation valve was opened manually and has faced no issues after that.
ImmediateAction: The Gear Box was removed and the valve opened manually.    The gear box then opened and checked. Found the connecting pin between the spindle and connecting gears broken/worn out. The same renewed and re connected. gear box now operating satisfactorily.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel had to cease discharge operations for a period of 18mins between 1118hrs LT to 1136hrs LT due to the Main IG Isolation v/v stuck & not opening, resulting in the IG Plant tripping and the tank pressure dropping.   The gear box had an issue and the same was removed/disconnected and the valve operated manually.   Discharging commenced after a delay of 18mins, once the Isolation v/v was operated manually.     On further investigation,  It was found that the v/v spindle was disconnected from the Gear Box, resulting in the wheel rotating freely, and the valve not opening.    This Shut main line isolation valve on deck resulted in the IG Blower tripping due to excessive back pressure.
DirectCause: The pin connecting the valve spindle to the gear box was broken, resulting in the valve not opening during operation ( the wheel was found rotating freely ).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The Gear Box to be repaired and operation of the valve to be chkd as per part of the Pre Arrival Checks.   All crew was briefed regarding the correct operation of valves.   IG to be started on deck 30 mins prior commencement of discharge operations.   Chief Officer and Duty Officers to confirm the line up and that I.G. is passing on Deck.
Corrective Action: Check for permanent repairs of the valve gear box. Investigate the incident and revert with the findings. Raise requisition for a new valve and renew after receipt. If required, Make proper arrangements for manual operation till permanent repairs or renewal and ensure that the procedures is explained to all staff. Check the condition of spindle and gear box for all the IG line valves and renew where the condition has deteriorated,
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: on 27 jan 2020 around 10:00, loading at port kamsar anchorage, two mooring ropes of on portside winch  broken down (lenth:3 meters) due to mooring barge crane rolling and pitching heavily. for this thing , the barge crane was advised protective measure during mooring but he did not take action.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was at Aoshan Terminal,Zhoushan carrying out discharge operation of CHIM SAO crude,Weather was good and smooth sea.  Mooring was being monitored and adjusted as required by deck crew.Tidal diffrence was around 2.5mtrs.Which was moniterd by duty officer and same informed to the deck crew.  At Around 0700 Hrs lt, Duty Crew reported Back Spring is parted at around 4 mtrs from the eye.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel Arrived at Aoshan, All made Fast at 1648 Hrs Lt/ 07-Mar-20 and Commenced Discharging at 0048 Hrs Lt/08-Mar-20, Vessel was Loaded with Two Grades and to be Discharged After Completely Completion of Other Grade, Moorings was being monitored by Deck crew and tented regularly as required, Tidal range was 2.5 Mtrs( LW~HW) which was briefed also to Crew during Pre Arrival Meetings, One copy of Tide Sheet is kept at Gangway and Another at CCR.  At Around 0700 Hrs LT/ 08-March-20 , Deck crew Reported that Mooring Line, Fwd back Spring is Parted Aprrox 4 Mtrs from the Mooring Shackle.
ImmediateAction: Called up all crew on deck and Remove Old wire from Drum, Damage Part is retrieve from Jetty, Disconnect Mooring Shackle and Connected with tail and Spring passed to Jetty.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fwd Back Spring parted Whilst the vessel was Discharging at Aoshan, Zhoushan China.
DirectCause: Age of mooring wire is > 5 years, Suspecting Deterioration of Wire  internally.  Back Spring is always used for Aliging the ship at berth, Strain on wire is usually more than others wires.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Internal Greasing of Wire to be carried out.Vessel Requested Masto High Pressure Grease Equipment.
Corrective Action: Thorough close inspection to be carried out for all mooring wires and wires requiring attention shall be adequately addressed by bringing to notice of vessel management team ashore
ClosingNote: Please refer to attached IIR & the relevant CA

IncidentDetails: While vessel was stbd side alongside at Tanjung Pelepas, Stbd side gangway was found heaving itself upwards in uncontrolled way damaging both side gangway railings and twist in lower ladder frame.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed stb side alongside.  Stevedores onboard, cargo operation started.  Gangway started heaving up by itself  in uncontrolled way.  The gangway watchkeeper noticed and tried to stop using emergency stop which did not worked. Watchkeeper alerted duty officer and chief officer, who rushed to spot and swicthed off the main breaker.  By this time the ladder rose up to higher level and made contact with gangway lifting arm at deck level causing damage to gangway railings and parting of gangway wire leading to falling of gangway into water.  Vessel lifted up damaged gangway with help of monorail.  Damaged safety railing removed, wire renewed, and temporary stanchion placed.  Ganway prepared to the satisfaction of safety officer for embarkation and disembarkation of stevedores.
ImmediateAction: Gangway picked up from water with help of monorail, damaged railings removed, wire renewed and temporary stanchion fixed with safety lines for safe embarking/disembarking of stevedores/personnels.
Corrective Action: Gangway wire to be renewed

IncidentDetails: Stripper connection for no.4 COP casing developed a small hole, leading to minor oil spill in Pump Room
IncidentEventDetails: Upon completion of line pressure testing vessel started stripping the top lines back into no.4 COT using stripper pump and pump room stripper connections. During stripping, one small stripper pipeline section connected to cargo stripper suction side for no.4 COP casing developed a hole and started leaking into pumproom. Bosun was present in the Pump room and monitoring the stripper pump who observed the leak and reported same to CCR, meantime P/Rm gas alarm got activated and Bosun was asked to come out of Pump room for safety reasons. While coming out he saw cargo was leaking from the hole and flowing towards the bilge wells. We continued running the stripper pump as stopping the pump at this point would increase the leak from the suction side. While the lines were almost emptied out, Pump Room bilge alarms were activated.
ImmediateAction: Stripped out the leaking line and made empty which stopped the leak
DirectCause: Material wastage and breakdown
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pipe to be dismantled and taken to E/Rm workshop, thereafter weak section of the pipe to be renewed. Other adjoining pipe sections to be checked thoroughly for weak spots.

IncidentDetails: Port side gangway got damagw due to severe surging at port of Paranagua caused by another vessel M.V.ARC RESOLVE passing at very close distance to own vessel with a speed of 7.9kts.  M.V.ARC RESLOVE was under pilotage and proceeding to sea.Own vessel gangway came in contact with shore bollard. Turntable,3 ladder steps,riveted part of gangway bottom plating and railing got damaged.
IncidentEventDetails: 07-Mar-2020/0930LT: Cargo work was in progress at port of Paranagua. Vessel was safely port side alongside with gangway resting on Pier as per company and port regulations. 3rd Officer Mohd Aadil, AB Rajaram, D/CDT Abhay and OS Vignesh was on cargo watch.  07-Mar-2020/0940LT: Third officer relieved gangway AB to check vessel moorings forward and aft.  07-Mar-2020/0940LT: Third Officer visually checked the condition of gangway and fittings. Found in satisfactory condition.  07-Mar-2020/1010LT: Duty AB reported forward and aft moorings are tightened and in good condition.  07-Mar-2020/1015LT: Duty AB returned back to gangway.  07-Mar-2020/1018LT: AT this time one stevedore was stepping down the gangway for disembarking the vessel. Third Officer noticed sudden severe surging of vessel and alerted the stevedore to come back on ship immediately. At the same time Third officer asked duty AB to pick up the gangway.  07-Mar-2020/1018LT: Duty AB started to pick up the gangway. The gangway was resting on the pier as per port regulations. During the process of picking up gangway, its bottom platform came in contact with shore bollard.  07-Mar-2020/1019LT: Third Officer immediately alerted all ship crew including master and chief officer on the Walkie Talkie. Also terminal planner was informed regarding the same.  07-Mar-2020/1020LT: Master and Chief officer rush to the port side gangway from the ship’s office.  07-Mar-2020/1022LT: Vessel checked and found Turntable of port side gangway got dented and whole gangway ladder appears in twisted condition. Vessel immediately stopped the access to and from the vessel through the port side gangway.   07-Mar-2020/1030LT: Vessel checked in ECDIS playback record and found M.V ARC RESOLVE passed very close to own vessel at high speed.  07-Mar-2020/1030LT: Vessel informed Paranagua agents and Ship managers via telephone.   07-Mar-2020/1045LT: To avoid further delay to vessel schedule, vessel started the process of securing port side gangway till deck level. Agents informed to arrange pilots from sea side only.  07-Mar-2020/1130LT: Paranagua local agent boarded the vessel and acknowledged Note of Protest raised by the vessel.  07-Mar-2020/1220: Vessel secured portside gangway to deck level and was ready for departure.  07-Mar-2020/1236LT: Pilot on board  07-Mar-2020/1254LT: Vessel casted off from berth without any delay.  07-Mar-2020/1538LT – Vessel send Note of Protest via email to various concerned parties including Paranagua pilots/Charterers keeping agents, owners and managers in copy.  After departure, vessel carried out thorough inspection and observed following below damages:  1) Damage to gangway Top turntable  2) Damage to three ladder steps  3) Damage to Riveted bottom plate  4) Bend on gangway railing.
ImmediateAction: Stevedores were cleared from gangway area.Notified planner,Agent,Managers on the matter.Asked agent to arrange pilot from sea side.Since vessel was about to complete loading,stevedores disembarked in cage.Details of M.V.ARC RESOLVE retrieved from ECDIS. Note of protest raised .Agent acknoledged NOP.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1) Damage to gangway Top turntable  2) Damage to three ladder steps  3) Damage to Riveted bottom plate  4) Bend on gangway railing.
DirectCause: Severe surging caused by another vessel passing very close to own vessel with high speed. The gangway came in contact with shore bollard.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pilot station to monitor movement of vessels and to make sure all vessel proceed at slow speeds when transiting very close to other vessels at berth.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: On 18th Jan, after completion of LSMGO bunkering, during line blowing by the barge, approximately 200 ltrs of LSMGO spilled on deck from the #2 MDO tank bunker vent and about 5-10 litres of oil spilled overboard at Fujairah ‘A’ anchorage.  Vessel was intermittently rolling while the bunkering operation was in progress.
IncidentEventDetails: On 18th Jan, after completion of LSMGO bunkering, during line blowing by the barge, approximately 200 ltrs of LSMGO spilled on deck from the #2 MDO tank bunker vent and about 5-10 litres of oil spilled overboard at Fujairah ‘A’ anchorage.  Vessel was intermittently rolling while the bunkering operation was in progress.
ImmediateAction: Bunker barge was immediately contacted to stop the line blowing operation. Ship’s Emergency alarm sounded, crew mustered, and oil containment & cleanup operation commenced. Spilled oil collected and transferred to Overflow tank and clean-up operation completed.

IncidentDetails: On 18th Jan, after completion of LSMGO bunkering, during line blowing by the barge, approximately 200 ltrs of LSMGO spilled on deck from the #2 MDO tank bunker vent and about 5-10 litres of oil spilled overboard at Fujairah ‘A’ anchorage.  Vessel was intermittently rolling while the bunkering operation was in progress.
IncidentEventDetails: On 18th Jan, after completion of LSMGO bunkering, during line blowing by the barge, approximately 200 ltrs of LSMGO spilled on deck from the #2 MDO tank bunker vent and about 5-10 litres of oil spilled overboard at Fujairah ‘A’ anchorage.  Vessel was intermittently rolling while the bunkering operation was in progress.
ImmediateAction: Bunker barge was immediately contacted to stop the line blowing operation. Ship’s Emergency alarm sounded, crew mustered, and oil containment & cleanup operation commenced. Spilled oil collected and transferred to Overflow tank and clean-up operation completed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: • Outflow of LSMGO bunker from #2 MDO bunker tank vent and oil spill overboard during line blowing operation. • Barge commenced Line blowing operation without receiving vessel’s clearance.
DirectCause: The incident was promptly reported by Master to the Company. The crew responded immediately as per procedures in the Contingency manual and it was ensured that that there was no further damage to the environment beyond the initial quantity of oil that spilled overboard. The oil spilled in the containment tray was transferred to overflow tank and cleanup operation was completed swiftly.  The Master also notified Shell casualty, Fujairah Port control. Managers notified the Owners and the local P&I club.  The Incident Investigation teams were nominated on board and ashore. The relevant documents including statements of key crew members, bunkering plan & checklists were sought from the vessel. This report is prepared based on analysis of information collected, interview with the crew, analysis of the piping diagram and bunkering procedures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1 Dissemination of Fleet Alert to the company Vessels 2 To obtain future bunker plan and RA of Sea Duck for  next three months irrespective of percentage filling of Bunker tanks (Presently as per company’s guidelines any tank receiving bunkers in excess of 85% should submit bunker plan & RA to respective  3 Bunker transfer rate for the vessel to be reviewed basis tank capacity  4 Training to be issued to ship staff by attending Marine Superintendent reiterating on bunkering operation, emphasizing company procedures on line blowing operation, communication mode internally and with external parties, observance on weather conditions during bunkering operation 5 Bunker audit of the Sea Duck  6 C/E is to be debriefed by Senior management on the basis the lesson learnt from the incident Tech Director  7 Additional personnel in ECR to relay the information to ship staff stationed in CCR/Manifold/Wheelhouse  8 Manager instruction to be circulated in the fleet for line blowing operation after bunkering   9 Review of existing Risk assessment for the adequacy of line blowing operation.  10 Circulate lesson learned to the fleet vessels and training centres  11 Incident and lesson learned to be discussed with the officers and crew during the pre joining briefing

IncidentDetails: During loading and simultaneous deballast operations , CCR recieved pumproom bilge alarm , on investigation it was found that the weld seam from ballast water treatment system had given way and had a hole in it of about 6 mm
IncidentEventDetails: During loading and simultaneous deballast operations , CCR recieved pumproom bilge alarm , on investigation it was found that the weld seam from ballast water treatment system had given way and had a hole in it of about 6 mm
ImmediateAction: Deballasting operations were stopped , filter drained, cold work of plug and putty carried out, deballasting operations resumed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: water accumulated in pumproom and filled the stbd bilge
DirectCause: material wasted, poor weld quality and inner coating of the filter.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Cold to be carried out immediately. And permanant work will be planned at the first opportunity.
Corrective Action: To carry out temporary repairs on the leak, HW can be carried out when the ship is in complete gas free condition. A RA should be in place, regular checks to be taken when vessel using BWTS

IncidentDetails: F.O.Purifier Overflow and caused oil spillage in Engine room.
IncidentEventDetails: At 2025 Hrs.F.O.Sludge tank hibh level alarmed.Dty Engr.accepted the alarm and transferred.Purifier leak off alarm did not get intiated and purifier kept overflowing.This time F.O.Sludge tank alarm in ECR is kept in flicker mode so alarm was not getting re intiated.At 2100 Hrs.Observed F.O.Tank overflow and caused oil spillage in Engine room.
ImmediateAction: H.F.O Purifier no:1 stopped and all valves secured.Vast clean up operation started with complete ship staff.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.Purifier room coamings ,bilges and adjacent void spaces filled up with VLSFO.  2.Oil collected  in Tank top coaming of sludge pump and stbd side bottom floor.  3.Port bilge well and Void spaces.  4.DG No.3 & 4 Coamings.
DirectCause: 1.Chronic unease.  2.Procedural violation during watch keeping by watch keepers.  3.Insufficient knowledge about system and resonsibility.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Frequent engine room rounds to be taken.  2.Scan all the paprameters of running machinery.Including sndgs of tanks.  3.Time between overhauls to be maintained  and dont allow overdue.
Corrective Action: Investigate to find the Cause of the leak and same to be rectified. Also through check to be incorporated on the running purifier to avoid such incident occurrence onboard.

IncidentDetails: At about 1010 LT, while deep frying bitter gourd, Ch cook tipped the frying pan by accident and hot oil spilt on the hot plate which resulted in a fire on the hotplate.
IncidentEventDetails: 1000 hrs: Ch cook started frying bitter gourds.  1009 hrs: Hot oil splashes on to the hot plate  1010 hrs: Fire ignites on the hot plate and gutter
ImmediateAction: 1010 hrs: Fire ignites on the hot plate and gutter./ Hot plate electricity switched off / blower switched off by pumpman/bosun  1011 hrs: Fire put off using Fire blanket by crew in the mess room who had come in for a tea break.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: > very Slight paint/coating damage  > Fire blanket used/ to be washed and checked for damages
DirectCause: > Carelessness  > Mis-judgment about qty of oil required for the job / container
Proposedcorrectiveactions: >Training and briefing for Galley Staff
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out. Training completed for galley staff. Briefing of ships crew done.

IncidentDetails: After Completion of Loading Operation at Yosu, Korea STS with PAVEL CHERNYSH, Awaiting for casting off vessel, Both the station were manned, pilot boarded the vessel and as  Winch power started for Aft Station, it was observed that aft Spring Winch inlet Line is Leaking below the walking Platform, Bridge is informed immediately, SOPEP equipment brought near winch.  Vessel cast off from Yosu, Approx 10 Ltrs of Hyd  oil is spilled on deck which was Accumulated/contained on board.
IncidentEventDetails: After Completion of Loading Operation at Yosu, Korea STS with PAVEL CHERNYSH, Awaiting for casting off vessel, Both the station were manned, pilot boarded the vessel and as  Winch power started for Aft Station, it was observed that aft Spring Winch inlet Line is Leaking below the walking Platform, Bridge is informed immediately, SOPEP equipment brought near winch.  Vessel cast off from Yosu, Approx 10 Ltrs of oil is spilled on deck which was Accumulated/contained on board.
ImmediateAction: 1) Informed to Bridge.  2) Temporaray repair is done to minimise the leakage.  3) SOPEP equipment is ST by and one man to st by at winch motor.  4) Casting off carried out in controlled manner.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Aft Mooring winch Hydraulic Inlet Line .  No Personnal Injury.
DirectCause: PMS routines not carried out diligently
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pls refer to below corrective actions
Corrective Action: Leaking section of pipeline to be removed and replaced with new pipeline
ClosingNote: All raised CA duly attended and closed

IncidentDetails: While cutting the empty, cleaned & top cover opened lubeoil drums through grinder for making half drums, grinder wheel broke apart and flown into pieces. Pumpman who was using the grinder nearly escaped from the injury.
IncidentEventDetails: Grinder which was used was not having safety guard. As the grinder broke apart, it flown away into pieces.
ImmediateAction: All the pneumatic tools were immediately checked for safety guards. Grinder which was not having safety guard was removed. Vessel raised requisition for pneumatic tools with safety guards. All crew members were briefed about the consequences. RPM of all the grinders and wheels checked for safe working.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NO damage / No Injury
DirectCause: Improper Tools
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Use Proper Tools
Corrective Action: Pls create an inspection checklist on board for all pneumatic tools. They have to be identified, checked for guard and wheel rating on a periodic basis. Same Job can be added to the Shippalm. Please attach the job description from Shippalm after including the job in PMS.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: A.E 2 Unit #2 Fuel pump deflector Sheared Off while in service
IncidentEventDetails: On  28/Mar/2020 around 0200 hrs, Third engineer observed oil leak from the fuel pump gallery of running A.E 2, upon inspection it was observed that the fuel pump deflector was sheard off and oil was leaking badly from it and about 100 litres of fuel oil spilled on A.E platform and subsequently to bottom platfrom.
ImmediateAction: Standby A.E was started and taken on load. A.E 2 stopped immediately and fuel oil quick closing valve for A.E 2 was actiavted.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fuel pump deflector sheared off and replaced with a new one onboard.
DirectCause: Material failure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tightness of the deflectors of all units to be checked periodically.
Corrective Action: Tightness of the deflectors of all units to be checked periodical
