IncidentDetails: Port anchor fluke tip found broken.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was preparing for berthing and pilot was scheduled at 1400 hrs on 29th/mar/2020.Vessel was at anchor.Vessel was picking up the anchor.When anchor came above the water level,it has been found that the uppermost tip of the anchor fluke was broken.
ImmediateAction: Anchor properly housed and secured.Vessel informed the Technical and marine supritendent about the damage and seek advice for further action.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Port anchor fluke tip found broken.Broken length=40CM.The uppermost fluke tip of port anchor,found broken while picking up the anchor.
DirectCause: Deterioration.  Failure to detect the defects.
ClosingNote: Please raise it as a HMX report.

IncidentDetails: At 1516HrsFire Alarm sounded,Crew rushed to activated sensor i.e Incinerator platform.smoke was coming out of the Fedding door,therebyactivating the smoke sensor.Emergency stop  for Incinerator was activated.After few minutes smoke was reduced sensor too got De-activated.upon further investigayion it was found that solid waste which was put was oily rags mixed with plastic.Plastic packing material while loading flew & got stucked on side walls restricting the passage for other solid waste which also started burning together inside the feeding compartment itself,therby increasing the casing temp. & hence damaging Solenoid valves SV7( Solenoid Valve for Feeding Door);SV8(Solenoid Valve for Air Cylinder inside Sluice Door).There was no Personal Injuries encountered.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. 1445 hrs.: Incinerator Common Alarm activated for Waste oil Service Tank Low Level., Sludge burning stopped.  2. 1450 hrs.: Incinerator was stopped & All concerned valves secured.                      Same time Started Burning solid waste containing mix of oily rags & plastic, Each bag was approx. 3-4 kgs. 3 bags were burnt.  3. 1505 hrs.: Last bag of Solid waste was topped up & Incinerator platform was left.  4. 1516 hrs.: Fire Alarm got activated from smoke sensor fitted above Incinerator.
ImmediateAction: Noticed smoke was rushing out through Feeding door. Emergency stop activated and Incinerator tripped, Noticed solid waste burning around inside door up to feeding area.  Then Uptake flap and blower suction manually closed and inside burning stopped after a while.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Solenoid valve SV7 (Solenoid V/V for Feeding Door); SV8 ( Solenoid V/V for Air Cylinder Inside Sluice Door).
DirectCause: SOLID WASTE WHICH WAS PUT WAS OILY RAGS MIXED WITH PLASTIC. PLASTIC PACKING MATERIAL WHILE LOADING FLEW & GOT STUCKED ON SIDE WALL RESTRICTING THE PASSAGE FOR OTHER SOLID WASTE WHICH ALSO STARTED BURNING TOGETHER INSIDE THE FEEDING COMPARTMENT ITSELF,THEREBY INCREASING THE CASING TEMP.& HENCE  DAMAGING SOLENOID VALVE SV7 (SOLENOID VALVE FOR FEEDING DOOR) & SV8(SOLENOID V/V FOR AIR CYLINDER SLUICE DOOR).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: WHILE BURNING SOLID WASTE NO,PLATIC MATERIALS TO BE BURNT.ONE AUTHORISED PERSON TO BE PRESENT TILL WASTE IS BURNT & COMBUSTION CHAMBER TEMP. STARTS DROPPING.
Corrective Action: Complete investigation action item and corrective action to be uploaded
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: While clearing the aft spring rope instead of passing the rope over horizontal roller Bosun has taken the mooring rope around the roller (including the support bar below the roller) and then to bits on main deck stbd side. Started heaving up the rope (with out noticing the bar below the roller) to clear from tucks he noticed the supporting bar was bent.
IncidentEventDetails: After anchor stations at Las Palmas Bosun was assigned to continue the job of re-arrange aft mooring ropes on drums.  1630LT: Mooring ropes on poop deck were arranged on drums.  1635LT: Bosun, OS-1 and OS-2 were arranging aft spring ropes.  1645LT: While uncoiling aft spring rope, it was found stuck between tucks. For clearing the rope instead of passing over horizontal roller Bosun has taken the mooring rope around the roller (including the support bar below the roller) and then to bits on main deck stbd side. Started heaving up the rope to clear from tucks then he noticed, the supporting bar was bent because of the rope.   Upon investigation it was noticed that the supporting bar was bent and welding was broken in the aft part.
ImmediateAction: Operation was stopped, rope was cleared from the roller and bar, passed over the horizontal bar and completed the operation.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: anchoring & mooring operations being critical should be well planned with required RA
Corrective Action: anchoring & mooring operations being critical should be well planned with required RA
LessionLearnt: Every operation aboard to be analysed and RA to be done.
ClosingNote: Carry-out briefing and debriefing after every anchoring and mooring operations aboard

IncidentDetails: On 19th Feb 2020, Company had initiated an investigation of Company’s Drug and Alcohol Policy violation basis report received from Third officer, Mr. Sivaraman Palaniappa against Capt. S.S. Nanda. The incident occurred during their tenure onboard Maersk Saltoro
IncidentEventDetails: On 11th Jan 2020, Capt. S. S. Nanda had purchased 4 bottles of Glenfiddich Whisky (Hard Liquor) through private order via Sinwa (Singpore) Pte LTD during vessel’s call Singapore.   On 15th Jan 2020, Master’s Arrival Customs & ship store declaration for Port Colombo stated that there were three hard liquor bottles on board.   Third Officer raised his concern to Company on Violation of Alcohol policy. And further as a follow up on the subject matter he visited Office on 19th Feb 2020, and shared information to office on the concerns, which were as above.    On 11th March 2020, Capt Himanshu Chawla had taken over command of the vessel from Capt. S. S. Nanda at Norfolk International Terminal, USA. At this time Capt. Nanda, who was aware of the allegation against him regarding alcohol violation explained to the incoming Master that the whisky bottles were brought with an intention of carrying them home after signing off the vessel. Capt. Himanshu took the photos of two hard liquor bottles which Capt. Nanda had taken home with during his Sign off. However, only two bottles of whisky were sited in Capt. Nanda’s luggage.
ImmediateAction: Investigation was initiated and Statements from Ship staff were taken during the Investigation, including Statement of 3/Off -Mr. Sivaraman Palaniappa, Statement of Present Master - Capt. Himanshu, Statement of Chief Engineer - Mr. Sandesh Nagaich and Statement of Capt. S.S. Nanda.
DirectCause: It is evident that Capt. S. S. Nanda intentionally violated the Company’s Drug and Alcohol policy: i. By purchasing Whisky Bottles (Hard Liquor) which is prohibited as per Company’s Health and Safety Manual -Section 8.1 – Consumption of Alcohol. ii. As Capt. S. S. Nanda’s explanation for missing 2 bottles of Hard Liquor is not satisfactory, we presume that he consumed 2 bottles of Whisky (Hard Liquor) during his tenure onboard Maersk Saltoro.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Investigation carried out and report will be uploaded
Corrective Action: Due to Violation of Alcohol Policy, Capt. S. S. Nanda should not be considered for reemployment on Synergy Group vessels. Manning team to be informed of the same.
LessionLearnt: Investigation report attached for reference
ReviewComments: Investigation report attached

IncidentDetails: On 04 April 2020, 1730H LT Fire occurred at the Galley When grilling of Beef steak,Immediately galley staff extinguished the fire using fire blanket, fire alarm was sounded. no damages was found and no crew injured, after the investigation by the senior officers and found the root cause is to much oily fats from the beef steak and for this the fire occurred,Day after the morning captain conducted a safety meeting to all crew reminding that always take extra precuation when grilling inside the galley, and re familiarized the crew especially the galley staff on how to fight the fire in worse case at the galley and what to do during the fire incidents.
IncidentEventDetails: On 04 April 2020, 1730H LT- Fire has occured at the Galley.                               1731H LT- Fire was extinguished using fire blanket and at the same time fire                                          alarm was sounded                               1734H LT- all safe and back to normal operation.  On 05 April 2020, 0800H LT - Captain conducted a safety meeting to all crew                               0936H LT - Safety meeting completed
ImmediateAction: Fire extinguished with fire blanket and closed the fire door's to isolate the air.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No  Damage by ship property except fire blanket  and no crew was injured.
DirectCause: Root causes after the investigation, to oily fats from the Grill beef steak for this the fire was occurred.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proposed corrective action - Strictly monitored the grill and do it one steak at the time.  Preventive action - Remove the excessive oily fats of Beef steak or even pork steak.
Corrective Action: Proposed corrective action - Strictly monitored the grill and do it one steak at the time. Preventive action - Remove the excessive oily fats of Beef steak or even pork steak.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: CA noted

IncidentDetails: While navigating at La Perouse Strait, at around 0100 LT, on 13 FEB 2020, vessel encountered ice formation. which resulted to the damage of FPT, WBT 1 portside and WBT 1 stbdside.
IncidentEventDetails: After bunkering at Vostochny, vessel was instructed to proceed to deviated point while waiting for voyage instruction. Upon receipt of Route guard appointment confirmation on 12 FEB 2020, sent a passage plan (Vostochny to Deviation point). However, ship passage plan was considered and no alternative or recommended route provided to the vessel. Few hours later while trasiting the La Perouse strait, vessel encountered ice formation at 0100 LT, on 13 FEB 2020.
ImmediateAction: The FPT, WBT 1P and WBT 2P was inspected by Class Surveyor (LR) along with underwater Inspection during the vessel's stay in Puerto Patache, Chile last March 15-17, 2020. Pls. see attached Report of Underwater survey, Condition of Class raised by LR and damaged report from vessel.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Please see attached damage report
DirectCause: • Master’s over-reliance on weather routeing service for vessel’s passage planning.  • Inadequate understanding on board of Routeing service’s advisory service and limitations.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to review Weather routeing Guidance in light of prevailing weather conditions, Ice charts etc. from Radio Weather Fax and EGC weather reports from Sat-C.
Corrective Action: 1.Circular to be sent to all vessel with a brief precis of the above incident and caution about over reliance on weather routeing service and their limitations.
ClosingNote: Incident briefing included in pre-joining briefing of Master.

IncidentDetails: Reefer unit MSWU9079830 was loaded at port of Colombo on 01/APR/2020 around 2330LT in slot 380380 which is a non-reefer slot.  As per the Final loading plan  provided to the vessel the unit was declared as an empty unit to be discharged at Savannah.  the total number of reefers loading in port of Colombo were declared 89 units and said container was not in the loaded list of Reefers.  It was only after an escalated alarm received via auto generated email on 2nd April the vessel suspected the said container to be a LIVE Reefer. Immediately, a mail was send to the planning /Reefer Team to confirm whether the reefer is a loaded contained and needs to be plugged. After sending several reminders since 02nd Apr’20, finally on 05th Apr’ 20 AM it was confirmed that the Reefer is loaded and needs to be plugged in.   Container being loaded on the 5th tier made it a challenging task for the Crew to connect it to ship’s power and with great difficulty vessel successfully made it LIVE but with No access to the container the monitoring or repair of the Unit and raised letter of protest.
IncidentEventDetails: Reefer unit MSWU9079830 was loaded at port of Colombo on 01/APR/2020 around 2330LT in slot 380380 which is a non-reefer slot.  As per the plan provided to the vessel the unit was declared as an empty unit to be discharged at Savannah.  the total number of reefers loading in port of Colombo were declared 89 units and said container was not in the loaded list of Reefers.  It was only after an escalated alarm received via auto generated email on 2nd April the vessel suspected the said container to be a LIVE Reefer. Immediately, a mail was send to the planning /Reefer Team to confirm whether the reefer is a loaded contained and needs to be plugged. After sending several reminders since 02nd Apr’20, finally on 05th Apr’ 20 AM it was confirmed that the Reefer is loaded and needs to be plugged in.
ImmediateAction: Container being loaded on the 5th tier made it a challenging task for the Crew to connect it to ship’s power and with great difficulty vessel successfully made it LIVE but with No access to the container the monitoring or repair of the Unit.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Reefer unit was off for few days which could have led to damage of the cargo.
DirectCause: Reefer unit was loaded in slot 380380 which is a non-reefer slot.  The unit was declared as an empty unit.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel to ask for list of empty reefer containers from the planners.
Corrective Action: Vessel to ask for list of empty reefer containers from the planners.
LessionLearnt: None
ReviewComments: Closed from office.
ClosingNote: Any reefer queries to be followed up by a call to charterers on their hotline number. Final list of Reefers loaded and their manifest to crossed checked. Same to be confirmed from the departure port planner.

IncidentDetails: and following were the observations:  1.Lashing of Two metal garbage boxes (Each 1.5m3),secured at break of Superstructure parted and boxes found moved from its stowed position. Garbage box cover broken from its hinges and bent.  2.Steam valve line on the No.7 cross deck broken.  3.No.7 cargo hold bilge (S) sounding pipe bent and cracked from the sleeve welding.  4.Railing on the superstructure front found bent.  5.Four Nos stanchions for heavy weather lifeline bent and broken  6.No.7 demucking winch crutch deformed
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel experienced Beaufort wind force 8/9 on the 30th and 31st December 2019.  On the 1st January 2020 when the weather subsided,vessel carried out an inspection to check for any heavy weather damage
ImmediateAction: The vessel carried out a heavy weather damage assessment to check if any of the damages were affecting the sea worthiness of the vessel . All damages, noted were observed to be repairable on board.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1.Lashing of Two metal garbage boxes (Each 1.5m3),secured at break of Superstructure parted and boxes found moved from its stowed position. Garbage box cover broken from its hinges and bent. 2.Steam valve line on the No.7 cross deck broken. 3.No.7 cargo hold bilge (S) sounding pipe bent and cracked from the sleeve welding. 4.Railing on the superstructure front found bent. 5.Four Nos stanchions for heavy weather lifeline bent and broken 6.No.7 demucking winch crutch deformed.
DirectCause: There was inadequate heavy weather analysis and forecasting carried out
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MSI to frequently liaise with the master to keep a check on the weather reporting done, and the vessels weather routing, using the wx routing software provided on board. Comparison of the weather information from various sources to be done, and made an integral part of the vessels passage plan
ClosingNote: Agree with the above analysis

IncidentDetails: Portside Gangway motor failed due to  brake rectifier burned. Motor tripped off and brake already stuck-up
IncidentEventDetails: Portside Gangway motor failed today AM after placed down at Berth during berthing.  Brake rectifier burned.  1. Opened and inspected. Found electromagnetic brake rectifier burned out  2. Motor tripped off and brake already stucked-up  3.  Gangway  is  used/laid down at Berth,  however we could not heave up by using motor for       Departure/securing.
ImmediateAction: Gangway was heaved up using the port side Hose crane for temporary heaving –up gangway at departure. Gangway motor was repaired on board
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Electromagnetic brake rectifier burned out
DirectCause: Lack of compliance of PMS routines.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PMS for the gangway motor to be reviewed closely, and amended as required.
ClosingNote: MS for the gangway motor to be reviewed closely, and amended as required.

IncidentDetails: No.2 Steering gear Motor breakdown during manuevering to berth at Kamsar, Guenia
IncidentEventDetails: 12 Jan.2020 at 0348 Hrs. Alarm occur no.2 Steering gear power fail overload,immediately Check by Duty engr. and Electrical officer checked the control panel board,the main breaker and the overload relay did not trip-off. Then switch off Main breaker and isolate supply wire from the motor and megger test was in zero ohm means shorted.
ImmediateAction: Opened up the motor and check the Rotor found out some scratches and  the winding stator burnt out and defective ball bearing, the causes are due to vibration and wear down in bearing housing.  Same was repaired on board with spares
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Opened up the motor and check the Rotor found out some scratches and  the winding stator burnt out and defective ball bearing, the causes are due to vibration and weardown in bearing housing
DirectCause: Lack of effective monitoring of the equipment
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The PMS guidelines of the steering gear system to be reviewed .
ClosingNote: The PMS guidelines of the steering gear system to be reviewed

IncidentDetails: all mooring lines of the vessel including shore lines were parted one by one during the sea surging at the onset of flood tides.
IncidentEventDetails: On 14th of January 2020 at about 1040H LT, all mooring lines of the vessel including shore lines were parted one by one during the sea surging at the onset of flood tides.
ImmediateAction: Vessel called for tug assistance and was held in position till the lines were made fast, and the swell died down.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: all mooring lines of the vessel including shore lines were parted one by one during the sea surging at the onset of flood tides.
DirectCause: Lack of effective risk management in mooring operations
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The RA for mooring operations in this port specifically must have a detailed review by the MSI. Adequate weather forecasts of the area must be taken before hand and adequate external measures to be taken in advance to prevent the break away of the vessel from the berth.
ClosingNote: The RA for mooring operations in this port specifically must have a detailed review by the MSI. Adequate weather forecasts of the area must be taken before hand and adequate external measures to be taken in advance to prevent the break away of the vessel from the berth.

IncidentDetails: Heavy weather damage to ISO sockets
IncidentEventDetails: Kindly note vessel experiencing 6-7 mtrs heavy head swell and pitching heavily.Vessel followed advise as per AWT, but unpredictable swell( which was not mentioned in Weather report and Route advise) caused sudden violent rolling which vessel managed to control after altering course and speed reductions. During the process of alteration of courses and reduction of speed, vessel encountered approx 45 degrees roll.    Vessel carried Inspections of deck and engine.    Found 64 locations ISO Sockets were damaged.
ImmediateAction: The vessel reduced speed and proceeded with caution till the weather subsided

IncidentDetails: 64 Nos of ISO Sockets were damaged
IncidentEventDetails: While vessel was enroute from Pusan to Long Beach, vessel encountered Heavy weather.On 25th Jan'20, vessel experiencing 6-7 mtrs heavy head swell and pitching heavily. Vessel followed advise as per AWT, but unpredictable swell( which was not mentioned in Weather report and Route advise) caused sudden voilent rolling which vessel managed to control after altering course and speed reductions.    During the process of alteration of courses and reduction of speed, vessel encountered approx 45 degrees roll.    Vessel carried Inspections of deck and engine.    Found 64 locations ISO Sockets were damaged.
ImmediateAction: The vessel slowed down and proceeded with caution till the weather abated.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Found 64 locations ISO Sockets were damaged.
DirectCause: Lack of adequate weather forecasting and risk analysis on weather routing, during the passage plan.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The vessel and the MSI must liaise closely in obtaining weather forecasts for the vessels voyage. A full risk analysis must be carried out as a part of the vessels passage plan
ClosingNote: The vessel and the MSI must liaise closely in obtaining weather forecasts for the vessels voyage. A full risk analysis must be carried out as a part of the vessels passage plan

IncidentDetails: Damage to LP piston and bent con rod due to LP valve nut becoming loose and valve parts falling on top of piston resulting in bent con-rod and damage to piston.
IncidentEventDetails: Damage to LP piston and bent con rod due to LP valve nut becoming loose and valve parts falling on top of piston resulting in bent con-rod and damage to piston. MAC No.1 is still operational with spare LP valve installed with additional check nut and LP piston dressed after removing the embedded metal
ImmediateAction: The repairs were arranged at the next port
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to LP piston and bent con rod due to LP valve nut becoming loose and valve parts falling on top of piston resulting in bent con-rod and damage to piston.
DirectCause: Lack of effective maintenance of main engine equipment
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Overhauling of machinery must be done carefully. Prior overhauling, the individual components must be checked to avoid issues after boxing back.
ClosingNote: Overhauling of machinery must be done carefully. Prior overhauling, the individual components must be checked to avoid issues after boxing back.

IncidentDetails: Vessel was issued a notice of delay to cargo operations for 30 mins by the stevedores
IncidentEventDetails: Letter From  Longbeach Terminal was received by the vessel from the stevedores regarding the delay in Cargo ops due Twist locks. They claimed a delay of 0.5 Hrs on one crane due to Twist Locks  with broken unlocking  wires.  The twistlocks were in good condition at the time of loading, but the stevedores whilst discharging, broke the operating wires due to which there was a delay. Vessel had segregated all the faulty/damaged  twistlocks during layup at Leamchabang, the previous month.
ImmediateAction: Vessel provided the stevedores with good twistlocks
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 30 mins of delay was incurred to the vessel
DirectCause: Lack of monitoring of the stevedores and their requirements for lashing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The ships staff must ensure that the stevedores have the proper twistlocks and lashing gear at all times. Duty officers must keep a watchful eye to ensure that stevedores are not damaging lashing gear to make claims on the vessel. Photo evidence must be captured to dismiss claims against the vessel.
Corrective Action: The ships staff must ensure that the stevedores have the proper twistlocks and lashing gear at all times. Duty officers must keep a watchful eye to ensure that stevedores are not damaging lashing gear to make claims on the vessel. Photo evidence must be captured to dismiss claims against the vessel.

IncidentDetails: Allison with buoy
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel contact with buoy  while drifting after the engine incident at position-21 53.16N, 114 48.36 E Charted position of the buoy: 21 52.8N, 114 52.0E  Anchored picked up due to submarine power cables and vessel dragging anchor towards it.  Vessel transmitting safety alert on VHF and displayed NUC signals.  No damages to ship side, only gentle touch, but the buoy top part panels seems some deformation, may have touched in the bow area due to heavy swell
ImmediateAction: Vessel carried out a damage assessment. No damages noted to the ships side
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No damage to the vessel. Vessel made contact with buoy. The buoy top part panels seems some deformation, may have touched in the bow area due to heavy swell
DirectCause: Inadequate situational awareness among the bridge team
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The bridge team must ensure that the currents and other weather factors are taken into consideration when at anchor or in the vicinity dangers to surface navigation.
ClosingNote: The bridge team must ensure that the currents and other weather factors are taken into consideration when at anchor or in the vicinity dangers to surface navigation.Same to be communicated by the MSI

IncidentDetails: While Alongside berth at ADM terminal Corpus Christi, one of the mooring lines (Aft Springs) parted Away.   No personal injury to any ship and Terminal Staff were caused
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was alongside ADM terminal, inside the channel, the Warf length was not long enough as compared to length of the vessel, there was no side fender support for the forward hatches or (the aft hatches. depends on in which hold the loading operations were going on), since the loader could not reach the entire length of cargo holds, vessel has to do warping on her mooring lines in order to adjust her position according to the loader. which would leave either the fwd. or the aft part of the vessel without any side support. Also the head and stern lines were having a long lead about 150 mtrs  A vessel passing nearby (bit reasonably high speed) and due to narrow width and depth of the channel caused the Vessel to Surge, causing excessive weight on the ropes during surging, which lead to parting of Aft Spring line.
ImmediateAction: The parted line was immediately replaced with a New mooring line, and all mooring lines were tightened again
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Aft Spring Mooring rope Parted
DirectCause: Unsafe berth,    narrow channel ,  Vessels passing at high speed    rope which parted away was on mooring bitts  (could not have been rendered in case of excessive load)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: to obtain information in advance from pilots/ Agents and extra ropes should be used at such ports and ropes should be attended at the time vessels are passing nearby to keep a check on Vessel movement
Corrective Action: Ropes to be protected from chafing at all times. As the chafing was on the chafing bar on the hull just outside the fairlead roller, a chafe protection sleeve would prevent this damage.Vessel to order 6 nos. chafe protection sleeves urgently.

IncidentDetails: Hatch cover no. 2 portside derailed
IncidentEventDetails: While closing HC no. 2, suddenly turnbuckle broke.
ImmediateAction: Put chain block to secure and stop the HC movement. Assess the situation and finally return back to normal the derailed HC.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: aft HC roller inner misalligned and opening/closing chain securing point deformed.
DirectCause: Turnbuckle suddenly broke.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Do not open CH while at sea if not really necessary.
LessionLearnt: None
ReviewComments: Closed from office.
ClosingNote: Hatch covers not to be opened during adverse weather condition.

IncidentDetails: Fire in Electrician's Cabin, no injuries, wooden table edge got burnt
IncidentEventDetails: On 01-May-20/0730 Lt Fire Alarm was activated on Fire Panel for ‘C’ Deck, on immediate inspection found dense smoke coming from Electrician cabin and also burning smell was emanating from A/C Ducts.
ImmediateAction: Electrician cabin opened up & inspected, found hot water kettle got blasted & there was a small fire on the wooden table which was put off immediately.
DirectCause: Overheating of electric kettle without Safety cut off device, Unauthorized, unsafe and not Company supplied Electric personal appliance being used on board
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Immediately all Cabins on board were inspected for unauthorized, unsafe personal electric appliances.  Meeting was called with All Crew and explained the dangers of using unauthorized, unsafe personal electric appliances, only authorized Company supplied Electric appliances to be used on board.  All Crew explained to ensure that when they leave their Cabins all personal Electrical appliances to be switched off and kept un plugged, a notice to this effect has been posted in all Cabins.  In addition to weekly Accommodation inspection surprise checks will be carried out in accommodation spaces to make sure Crew are not using unauthorized, unsafe Electric appliances
Corrective Action: Understood the electric kettle does not have auto cut off. Everyone should be instructed that such kind of electrical equipments are forbidden onboard. Only company supplied elctrical items to be allowed to be used onboard.. Master should conduct a thorough cabin search on all cabins and look for such items and remove if, found.
LessionLearnt: Only approved electrical appliances to be used on board in cabins/ accommodation.
ClosingNote: Prompt action of Master and Crew to prevent fire from spreading.

IncidentDetails: GS After performing his daily routine cleaning duty in officers  cabin,  was going down the stairs while carrying bed sheets and some of cleaning materials in both hands, slipped off 2nd last step.
ImmediateAction: GS immediately caught the railings
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: Always maintain 3 point contact while moving on the ladders
ClosingNote: Always have one hand for yourself while on the stair cases

IncidentDetails: Upon departure Aden Ships line were used for Tugs basis the topography of the Aden harbour, the vessel had to be cast off from the berth then go sternway till the turning basin where the vessel had to be turned around using tugs and then head out of the channel , while the vessel nearly completed the turn in the turning basin forward tug line parted immediately followed by aft . Vessel had already completed the turn by then and the parted lines were heaved up immediately to avoid getting entangled in the propellor.
IncidentEventDetails: 26-Apr-2020 - 0354 : Pilot Onboard  26-Apr-2020 - 0406 : Mater/ pilot Info exchanged, UKC Policy, Tugs BP, Unberthing plan discussed  26-Apr-2020 - 0412 : Tugs made fast Fwd & Aft  26-Apr-2020 - 0412 : Commence unmooring  26-Apr-2020 - 0430 : Completed unmooring  26-Apr-2020 - 0430 : Tugs pulling the vessel away from the berth  26-Apr-2020 - 0436 : Vessel falling astern to turn inside the turning basin  26-Apr-2020 - 0448 : Line parted due to excessive pull by the tugs and poor condition of the line  26-Apr-2020 - 0448 : Vessel completed the turn  26-Apr-2020 - 0448 : Tugs off  26-Apr-2020 - 0454 : Vessel off the Break water  26-Apr-2020 - 0506 : Pilot away  26-Apr-2020 - 0530 : COSP
ImmediateAction: Pilot advised not to use the maximum power of the tug.Vessel movement controlled by engines  Broken lines secured to avoid entangling into propeller
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2 MOORING LINES PARTED, NO INJURY/DAMAGE
DirectCause: Poor condition of mooring ropes, same has been reported to the office.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Old ropes have been Eye spliced and turned end to end and is in satisfactory service condition, till the time New ropes are supplied.

IncidentDetails: During STS unmooring - forward breast lines(2) + fore and aft springs(2 each) could  not hold the vessel in place due to strong tidal current and despite tugs assistance (3 forward  and 2 aft)
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel had completed loading at STS with m.t. Xin Dan Yang and was loaded to 19.8  m draft. 4 tugs were fast (2 at Bow and 2 aft) and 1 tug was on standby at forward of  midship area for pushing.    Pilot advised that the unmooring was during the time of spring tide and in pre-unmooring  breifing advised ships mooring to be cast off from outer to inner both  forward and aft. (Fwd 4 +2 +2 / Aft 2+2+2). Final lines would be fore and aft springs.    With crew standing by on both ships and only own ships mooring to be cast off - lines  were being slacked of as directed by Pilot/Master.    As soon as 2 outer heads lines were slacked and cast off, the tidal current pushed  own deep draft ship away from other vessel at the bow, causing excessive load to  start coming on other fwd moorings. All three tugs were directed to push the vessel  back to ease the load. However, once the 2nd pair of head ropes were slacked -  excessive load came on the remaining fwd mooring and brakes started slipping.  Main Engines and hard port helm with three tugs pushing were used to ease the weight and  bring the vessel parallel to the other vessel.     The load of the stbd swing had also made the aft spring brakes to slip.
ImmediateAction: All three tugs were directed to push the vessel back to ease the load. However, once  the 2nd pair of head ropes were slacked - excessive load came on the remaining fwd  mooring and brakes started slipping. Engine and har port helm with three tugs pushing  were used to ease the weight and bring the vessel parallel to the other vessel.    Mooring officers & teams fore and aft directed to stand clear and not to attempt countering the  excessive forces on the mooring winches until directed by Pilot/Master.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mooring Winch Brake bands under-performance with unreliable BHC
DirectCause: Pilot under- estimated effect of spring tide flow on the hull deep draft VLCC in shallow waters.  Unmooring sequence fwd was not adapted to the effect current on deep draft hull.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Consider delaying unmooring when fully loaded in such STS operations at anchor to a slack water tidal window.  Master to advise all Pilots to be aware of uncontrollable effect of strong tidal flows in the port.
Corrective Action: a bridge team meeting to be carried out dscussing the importance of proper Master pilot exchange. This effect of current and tides should be factored in when the vessel Master is discussing the mooring and unmooring plan of the vessel. Tugs should always be available and pushing in such conditions.

IncidentDetails: Damage occurred on port side quarter of own vessel CAP SAN MALEAS as a result of allision by vessel MAERSK MEMPHIS during her maneuvering and berthing at Port of Buenos Aires.Scrath and dent occured between Fr.12 and Fr.14 from Longitudinal L47 to L52.  No personnel injury or environment pollution occured.
IncidentEventDetails: On 26 Apr 2020, CAP SAN MALEAS was safely alongside all fast 4+2 (F&A) at berth, TRP Terminal 3 Rio De La Plata, Darsena C site 15 at port of Buenos Aires/Argentina. Vessel was under loading/Discharging operations and expected to complete cargo by 1000Lt and depart at the same time.  26th / 0646LT: Duty AB was kept standby on aft mooring station to check moorings, since duty officer noticed Maersk Memphis was manoeuvring in the basin for berthing process. At this time Duty AB Witnessed that stbd quarter of Maersk Memphis came in contact with port quarter of own vessel and bounced back causing damage to port quarter.  26th / 0648LT: Duty AB immediately called duty officer to aft station. Third officer on duty rushed to the aft station and noticed the Damage. Further to this Third Officer immediately called Master and Chief officer notifying the allision by Maersk Memphis vessel.   26th / 0655LT: Master and Chief Officer immediately attended the Damage and master informed all concerned parties including Charterers, Local Agents, P&I and Terminal.  26th / 0715LT: Initial inspection carried out by master and Chief Engineer and the extent of damage appear to be:  1) Indentation and scratch on Port quarter between frame no.12 and frame no.14  2) The height of indentation and scratch on ship side is approx. 5 mtr in height, 2 mtr in Length and      10cms depth.  3) Damage occurred to frames, Longitudinal, Stringer decks and Side shell plating.  26th / 0810LT: Local agent boarded vessel and informed that Coast Guards will have to board the vessel for inspection and final clearance. Also he informed that pilot can only be booked after the coast guard inspection is completed.  26th / 1018LT: Cargo Operation completed, stevedores away and vessel is ready to sail.   But vessel is still waiting for Coast Guard inspector to carry out inspection of damage caused by Maersk Memphis.   26th / 1100LT: H & M inspector Capt. Pablo Supervielle boarded the vessel and carried out the inspection.  26th / 1140LT: Vessel prepared Letter of Protest and presented to the local agents to deliver and get acknowledgement from M.V. Maersk Memphis.  26th /1200LT: Coast guard boarded vessel for inspection. All statutory certificates were checked.  26th /1224LT: Buenos Aires Naval surveyor boarded vessel and inspected damaged area, No.8 SWBT (P).   26th /1415LT: Naval surveyor issued inspection report with NIL deficiency. Original report was taken with surveyor and copy of same was provided to vessel.  26th /1420LT: Naval surveyor disembarked.  26th /1430LT: Agent informed vessel that pilot and tug services has been booked for 1730LT.  26th /1445LT: Coast guard took statements and declaration from Master and Third officer. All documents prepared by coast guard were in Spanish language. Acknowledgement of same was taken from Master and Third officer after consultation with H&M surveyor.  26th /1450LT: Ship’s H & M inspector Capt. Pablo Superveille disembarked after completing the survey.  26th /1500LT: Coast guard authorities disembarked.   26th /1542LT: P & I surveyor Mr. Nicolas Condrad appointed by MAERSK MEMPHIS boarded vessel for inspecting damaged area.   26th /1545LT: Vessel called Ship’s H&M surveyor office and only after their confirmation Mr. Nicolas was allowed to take photographs of damaged area.  26th /1616LT: P&I surveyor Mr. Nicolas Condrad disembarked vessel after taking photographs, Mr. Nicolas was given a copy of crew list, copy of Shell expansion plan (extract of damaged area only) and ship’s particular.
ImmediateAction: Vessel immediately called Agent,Managers,P&I,Charterers to report scenario and given them an initial notification. After that a detail assessment carried out for the damaged area. Letter of Protest was issued to vessel Maersk Memphis via agents.Ackonwlegement received .Detailed SOF prepared.Primary report of extension of damage made and submitted to H&M surveyor who came on board. Coast guard came on board,carried out inspection and vessel was declared seaworthy.    On 01 May 2020, complete underwater inspection carried out and no damages observed in underwater area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1) Indentation and scratch on Port quarter between frame no.12 and frame no.14  2) The height of indentation and scratch on ship side is approx. 5 mtr in height, 2 mtr in Length and      10cms depth.  3) Damage occurred to frames, Longitudinal, Stringer decks and Side shell plating.
DirectCause: 1)Narrow basin.   2)Strong wind ESE force 6.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Weather to be accessed prior berthing,specially in narrow basins.

Damages as below  1) 1st Platform& Handrails of Bay 62 fwd row 12 &   14 Approx. 4.75*0.75 mtrs  2) 2nd Platform& Handrails of Bay 62 fwd row  14  Approx. 3.0*0.75 mtrs  3) Gutter Bar at Row 10,12,14,16,18.  Total 2 mtrs approx.                                     4) Stanchion Support: 5 pcs at Row 10,12,14,16,18.  5) 16 pcs FATs & 1 Turnbuckle  6) 20cm Dent in fwd lashing pillar between Row 14    & 16 at tier 72.
IncidentEventDetails: Damage done while discharging of under deck    containers in Bay 62, where 2 rows with 4 tiers of    containers fell down inside the cargo hold due  negligence of gantry operator.

Total 2 mtrs approx.                                     4) Stanchion Support: 5 pcs at Row 10,12,14,16,18.  5) 16 pcs FATs & 1 Turnbuckle  6) 20cm Dent in fwd lashing pillar between Row 14    & 16 at tier 72.
