IncidentDetails: HOLE DAMAGE WAS FOUND INSIDE ON THE CLEAN DRAIN TANK
IncidentEventDetails: DUE TO CORROSION ON THE  PLATE HULL.
ImmediateAction: Master and Chief Engineer immediately reported to TSI regarding the incident to discuss the plan for temp.  repair of the hole  leaking damage.    Master and Chief Engineer was discussing, and to mobilizing the engine personnel to fabricate the flange plate to use for shoring/patch up the hole damage inside the clean drain tank.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: NONE
DirectCause: Due to corrosion of the hull plate
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Maintained a proper and constant robbing watch on the engine room to easily and immediately discover and found out of any incident will occur.

IncidentDetails: During vessel departure Clatskanie after loading operations, vessel observed a damage on the A deck port side Panama chock. Upon investigation it was understood that the above said chock was last used during berthing operation Clatskanie. It is suspected as the vessel was in ballast condition, the rope was pulled downwards from aft by the tug. Since the panama chock forward welding seam/deck plate is bulged upwards and chock aft welding seam/deck plate is bulged downwards, gives a clear evidence of a strong rope pull from downward direction from aft. Also there is no evidence of any damages or contact on ship side.
IncidentEventDetails: 28th April 2020 PM:  Vessel under manoeuvring at Columbia river towards load port Clatskanie-US.    28th April 2020 19:50 hrs:  Fwd tug made fast at port main deck, 1 tug line, name – Sommer.    28th April 2020 19:51 hrs:  Aft tug made fast at port quarter.1 Tug Line , name-Samantha.    28th April 2020 21:30 hrs:  All made fast/Tug cast off.    01st May 2020 early AM hours:  During vessel departure Clatskanie after loading operations, vessel observed a damage on the A deck port side Panama chock. Upon investigation it was understood that the above said chock was last used during berthing operation Clatskanie. It is suspected as the vessel was in ballast condition, the rope was pulled from downwards from aft by the tug. Since the panama chock forward welding seam/deck plate is bulged upwards and chock aft welding seam/deck plate is bulged downwards, gives a clear evidence of a strong rope pull from downward direction from aft. Also there is no evidence of any damages or contact on ship side.
ImmediateAction: Vessel Inspected the area on the ship side and below the Deck in the Fire/Foam Station. Vessel also reported the Tech. Suptd after that the Crack was filled with Steel putty from on deck and below deck to maintain the integrity. Since the vessel was in loaded condition (Ethanol) HW was not approved and it was decided to carry out Cold work on same.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Frame no: 32 along with deck plate is bulged upwards (below forward welding seam of chock) and frame: 31 along with deck plate is bulged down wards (below aft welding seam of chock). Adjacent vertical bulkhead is the ship side hull and has suffered no damages. “L” shaped crack at the forward welding seam is at frame no: 32 and compartment below is fire station in the upper deck.
DirectCause: Aft tug was made fast by passing rope through the panama chock on to the bit aft of the chock. It is suspected that the rope was pulled towards aft side downwards causing the deck plate to bulge upwards.  The welding of deck plate to Chock is suspected to be of lower grade than required.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Extra strengthening and repair job planned for damaged panama chock plate after cargo discharging/tank cleaning/gas freeing operations.

IncidentDetails: Rescue boat engine tested and noticed not operational. Either it was not starting or when it was starting, it was stopping on its own in few seconds.
IncidentEventDetails: As a part of preparetion of possible port inspection in China, on the date and time of occurance, vessel was trying to confirm satisfactory operation of rescue boat as a part of safety equipment.  12th May 2020 at 11:00 - After repeated fuel line purging, noticed that rescue boat engine failed to start, vessel started investigating the cause.  12th May 2020 at 15:00 - Noticed during investigation that carburator air flap was seized along few other minor defects. Informed TSI immedietly over phone.  13th May 2020 at 10:00 - Further investigation revealed that the top unit of the engine is fully damaged. Photo attached.
ImmediateAction: Office has been informed about the engine damage and a requisition raised for a new engine as per SPR/OSKY/E/20/051

IncidentDetails: At around 0830 LT on 01st May 2020,  fire alarm received on bridge for Engine room 2nd Deck. 2nd Engineer called onbridge for reporting Major fire in ER near generator, Smoke in ER spreading very fast and reduced vissibility to zero in fractions of seconds, 2nd Engineer Stopped No-2 & 3 Generators and exited ER using Emergency escape trunk. No personal Injuries sustained. Assessment of Extent of Damages in progress
IncidentEventDetails: 0810 : Inceneration of wasteoil started by 2nd Engineer and 4th Engineer after  inspecting incenerator combustion chamber.  0812 : Tool box meeting started in ECR with - 4th Engineer/ETO/TME/Oiler 1  0830 : Fire alarm sounded, same time 4th Engineer/ETO/TME/Oiler 1 went out for investigating the source and Location.  - ETO & 4th Engineer reported heavy smoke and flame near AE #2 & 3, TME & OILER 1 evacuated engine room, bridge informed of the situation.
ImmediateAction: - 2nd Engineer ETO & 4th engineer to evacuate Engine Room and carried out blackout and escaped ER using emergency escape trunk.  - All crew mustered and head count taken  - Duties as per muster list carried out by respective crew.  - Quick closing valves & fire dampers were operated and Emergency Fire pump started by Chief Engineer,   - Boundary cooling started  - After ascertaining the nature, Location & Extent of fire is major , it was decided to activate the fixed Co2 fire fighting system.  At around 0845 LT CO2 released in Engine room after confirming all heads accounted for.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Personal injuries Sustained.  Auxiliary engine no. 3 starting instrumentation and control system found damaged  7 numbers - fire detectors  1 signal light column plastic deformed  1 MCP damaged  12 lights fittings & cabling damaged  Insulation of auxiliary boiler feed water pipe at boiler platform found damaged  Auxiliary boiler's primary burner, DO pump and motor, gauges and cables found damaged.  Starter panel for inert gas generator blower and fuel oil pump starter control panel damaged. Function  and running test of the IG system were tested and found satisfactory.
DirectCause: Whilst at high seas, vessel had an overflow from the “MGO Ignition tank” whilst transferring. The overflowing fuel came in contact with hot surface of boiler water circulating pump pipeline lagging and exhaust trunk of the auxiliary engine and thus catching fire. Tool box meeting failed to address the proper designation of duties, thus creating a communication gap.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. High level Alarm to be fitted on the MGO Ignition tank  2. Drain of drip tray to overflow tank to be regularly checked for any obstruction.  3. All peneterations for passing pipelines to be sealed to avoid from oil flowing down  4. Sheet to be placed in vicinity segregating from exhaust trunking of AE passing in the vicinity.  5. All transfered to be carried out in presence of responsible engineer after approval of 2nd Eng & Chief engineer.  6. Proper tool box meeting to be carried out, with clear designation of duties.
Cause Analysis: Operating equipment or machinery without authority

IncidentDetails: E/R overhead overhauling crane power cable parted while in use
IncidentEventDetails: Veesel was carrying out M/E No.2 unit decarb job. The E/R crane was operated to support the hydraulic hoses distribution block.   Because of the twists in the power cable, one of the cable hanger’s pulley had dislodged from the messenger wire (from its original resting position) and it created restriction in the free movement of the cable hanger on to the messenger wire. Since the power cable had no. of big loops of cable coil and one of it, got entangled to the travelling girder, which unfortunately went unnoticed due to its location. The crane was able to traverse to a restricted distance without any hindrance. When the crane was operated beyond that available cable length from the entanglement, it parted.  The power cable was a 4 core unarmored cable and there was a mild spark observed when it parted away and the crane’s MCB tripped immediately.
ImmediateAction: Crane MCB on the MSB was tripped and same was put to off. The damaged cable's parted ends were inspected for any further damages. Parted ends of the cable were crimped with joining cable lugs. Power to the crane was switched on & checked for proper functioning of all the safety limit switches. The cable hangers free movement were checked & ensured. M/E unit job was resumed & completed successfully without facing any challenges with the functioning of crane. New PR raised for the power cable and the same will be renewed, once received onboard.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Power cable of the E/R crane parted while in operation. The parted ends were inspected for further damages and same were crimped together as a temporary measure for further use of the crane.
DirectCause: Because of the twists in the power cable, one of the cable hanger’s pulley had dislodged from the messenger wire (from its original resting position) and it created restriction in the free movement of the cable hanger on to the messenger wire. Since the power cable had no. of big loops of cable coil and one of it, got entangled to the travelling girder, which unfortunately went unnoticed due to its location. The crane was able to traverse to a restricted distance without any hindrance. When the crane was operated beyond that available cable length from the entanglement, it parted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The power cable has no. of big loops of coil, which can cause the cable to get entangled to the travelling girder during the movement of the crane. In order to prevent reoccurence of the same, vessel has planned to reduce the size of the loops such that any part of the cable can never get entangled to the travelling girder.

IncidentDetails: During discharging, multiple holes got generated at one concentrated section of the drain pipe of  No.2 COP saperator leading to failure of primary containment
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was discharging through No.4 pump and using the pump room suction crossovers of No2 & No.4 line.   Due to that No.2 COP saperator was full of cargo.   At that moment multiple holes got generated at one concentrated section of the drain pipe of  No.2 COP saperator. That resulted in cargo leaking through the section in pump room bilges.   As cargo was HSD it did not give enough vapours to activate the alarm. Leakage was detected during periodic pump room rounds.  Approx 100 ltr of oil got spilled in pump room bilges
ImmediateAction: No.2 line bulkhead master and pump room crossover shut.  Temprory repairs carried out to arrest the leak
DirectCause: Temprory repair was there that was unknown to present staff.  Leak occured from the same soft patch
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Checking of drain pipe of all the saperator and renew them if required
Corrective Action: CA - The separator drain pipe to be renewed at first available opportunity.
LessionLearnt: All the defects needs to be attended or it should be recorded somewhere or in the HMX. Understand the soft patch was from previous management it was left un noticed.
ClosingNote: It was noted well in advance by ship staff and leak contained and rectified. All the pipelines temporary repairs need to be identified and same to be reported in HMX.

IncidentDetails: F.O. Overflow from H.F.O Settling tank no: 1  about 45 ltrs of fuel spilled out from remote gauge pipe and oil spilled on 3rd,4th and Tank top.
IncidentEventDetails: H.F.O transfer pump  no:1 started on Manual mode.  H.F.O Settling tank no: 1 filled up above High level and Oil overflowed through sounding through remote sounding gauge and also oil overflowed to Overflow tank.
ImmediateAction: H.F.O Tansfer pump no:1 stopped breaker switched off.  H.F.O Settling tank drain opened  to Overflow tank to reduce level of H.F.O Settling tank no:1   Informed Bridge  about the incident.  Clean up process started.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: As oil oil spill occured from 3rd deck it got showered on Settling tank no:1 bulk head  oil spill continued till tank top.
DirectCause: H.F.O Transfer pump no: 1 Operated on Manual mode .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: F.O. Transfer from Bunker tank to settling tank  should be undertaken only by Dty Engr.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: Vessel's port anchor fouled with own cable while waiting for berthing at Sandheads anchorage Haldia.    On 21st Apr’2020 18:30 Hrs LT, Vessel arrived Sand heads anchorage (Haldia, India) & anchored at  20:12 Hrs.LT.   On 24-Apr-20 at 08:15 hrs LT. M/E exhaus gas valve maintenance was carried out by engine staff as per TSI message.  At 12:20 Hrs LT vessel dragged anchor due to bad weather. at 13:12 hrs LT paid additional shackles & vessel B.U.T. 9 Shackles in water. And at 16:24 Hrs LT while picking up anchor observed Anchor fouled with own anchor cable. On 25-Apr-2020 09:18 hrs LT anchor foul cleared. On 28th Apr’2020 at  18:24 LT Vessel's anchor aweigh & proceeded for Pilot/berthing. At 28-Apr-2020 22:12 LT Pilot was onboard & vessel berthed at 29-Apr-2020 04:00 Hrs LT at Haldia docks No.7.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel arrived Sand heads anchorage (Haldia, India) on 21st Apr’202018:30 Hrs LT, for discharging operation & tendered NOR & as per SAGAR VTS dropped anchor at Sand heads anchorage at depth of 18 Mtrs 1.5 miles distance from other vessels and B.U.T. 6 in shackles in W/L awaiting for Pilots / Berthing instructions. Main engines kept at 30 Mins Notice. Till 24th Apr’2020 Noon time vessel observed fair weather with occasional wind gusts upto 40 Kts. Anchor watch maintained throughout the period Vessel awaiting for Pilot.   On 24-Apr-2020 08:00 Hrs LT as per Ch.Eng request M/E status changed to 30 Mins notice for maintenance of Main engines Exhaust gas valve.  On 24-Apr-2020 1210 LT Weather started deteriorating and at 12:15 Hrs LT :- 30 Notice given to E/R on observing sudden gust of wind up to 50 Kts, eventually at 1220 LT vessel started dragging & anchor party was called immediately.  At 1230 LT Anchor Party Standing by forward stations with windlass power on, also Stbd anchor was kept at readiness for dropping & at 1235 LT VHF broad cast made to vessels in the surrounding.   With Engines not available and vessel continuing to drag at 3 - 4 kts , at 1248 LT Vessel lowered additional 4 shackles to arrest the anchor drag.  24th Apr’2020 at 1306 LT Vessel holding on her port anchor with 10 shackles in water, wind speed dropped below 30 Kts.  On 24th Apr’2020 at 1312 LT Sagar VTS informed pilot boarding time will be at 1945 LT, Vessel acknowledge the same.  On 24th Apr’2020 at 1312 LT Vessel B.U.T. 9 in shackles in W/L.  On 24th Apr’2020 at 1530 to 1600 LT Control test/ preparation for pilotage & at 1600 Hrs LT SBE & commenced heaving anchor. While heaving up anchor at 16:24 LT vessel found anchor chain foul with its own flukes full 2 turns on each side of with 4th Shackle in Water & 5th shackle on deck.  At 16:45 Hrs LT Vessel let go port anchor to clear the foul, But without any success.  At 17:10 Hrs LT Vessel let go port anchor to clear the foul, But without any success.  Initial notification to Technical managers (DPA, Marine superintendent, Technical superintendent) Owners, Agents and Charterers were made by master.  At 17:15 Hrs At Vessel informed to Sagar VTS about the foul anchor & vessel is trying to clear the anchor foul.  Emergency response team was setup by synergy to guide the master providing continuous assistance.  Vessel followed instructions from office to pull the anchor chain loop along ship side along with trying to clear the anchor foul by letting go, dredging anchor & taking weight of anchor on mooring hawser.  From 24-04-2020 16:24 Hrs LT till 25-04-2020 at 0918 LT Vessel cleared the anchor foul by maneuvering/ dredging anchor, letting go anchor several times, by using mooring Hawsers & applying seaman ship. Vessel reported Sagar VTS anchor foul is cleared now & requested for Pilot boarding. Sagar VTS asked the vessel to re anchor & wait for instructions.  On 28th Apr’2020 at  LT Sagar VTS informed pilot boarding time will be at 2145 LT, Vessel acknowledge the same. Vessel heaved up anchor & proceeded for Pilot/berthing. 22:12 LT Pilot onboard & vessel berthed at 29-04-2020 04:00 Hrs LT at Haldia docks No.7.   At 01-May-2020 12:00 Hrs LT vessel departed from Haldia Dock after completion of cargo operations.  There was a delay in Pilot / berthing schedule due to this incident & there was no injury to personnel, pollution to sea but delay in berthing due to this incident.
ImmediateAction: On observing dragging of Anchor, notice given for Main engine. Anchor crew & ch. Off kept standby Fwd. Anchor prepared for paying out more shackles. Stbd anchor prepared for emergency lowering. Ships in vicinity alerted. Paid out 4 additional shackles on Port anchor & anchor drag was arrested.    On observing Fouled anchor, vessel tried to clear the foul by letting go the anchor several times followed by dredging the anchor, Informed the notifying parties & Sagar VTS. Informed Office (MSI & TSI). Followed office instructions & tried to clear anchor foul.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was a delay in Pilot / berthing schedule due to this incident.  Pilot was scheduled at 24-04-2020 19:45 Hrs LT  Pilot was Re scheduled at 28-04-2020 21:45 Hrs LT
DirectCause: Fouling of anchor due to change of tide from 21-04-2020 20:12 LT till 24-04-2020 12:10 LT with more scope on a sandy anchor holding ground.  Anchor cable running over anchor during change of tide on a sandy anchor holding ground.  More windage area due to Partly laden condition of Vessel.  Nature of sea bed & silting effect due to strong currents.  Sudden change of wind direction Gusting winds, vessel riding to the wind.  Anchor cable not shortened during change of tide.  Not considering re-anchoring.  Strong Tidal streams
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Monitoring of vessel's heading during change of tide along with monitoring of anchor cable position to prevent cable running over the anchor.  Shortening of cable during change of Tide.  Re-anchoring after 2-3 days stay in  tidal ports.  Engines to be kept at stand by / ready for immediate use in Tidal ports.  Use of Engines to reduce strain on anchor cable.  Maintenance of engines not to be carried out in Tidal ports.

IncidentDetails: During departure movement from Port Hedland, vessel experienced Main Engine start failure due to low hydraulic pressure in the HPS system. Main Engine failed to respond on her second astern kick due to HPS pressure dropping below 190 bar, resulting in a ‘Start Block’ condition. This was subsequently traced to failure to tightly shut Main Engine HCU unit #5 drain valve after carrying out an unscheduled accumulator check by the vessel crew whilst alongside berth.  The Master in consultation with the Pilot and PPA, decided to abort the departure maneuver and with the assistance of two additional tugs, brought the vessel back to the berth, for trouble shooting and fault rectification.  MAIN ENGINE NOT RESPONDING DURING UNBERTHING
IncidentEventDetails: On 27th April 2020, 0412 hrs local time vessel berthed at FID, Port Hedland to load her cargo of Iron ore.  After ringing of FWE at 0418 engines, the engine room crew went about their normal post berthing routines and attending to deballasting, watch keeping, etc. On receiving the FWE notice, the duty engineer opened the indicator cocks of the main engine, engaged the turning gear and commenced turning the engine for a few revolutions. Turning gear was then kept engaged, the main starting air valve shut and the HPS pumps and main lube oil pumps were stopped.  At 1330 hrs, the Chief engineer decided to check the HCU accumulators and asked the 2nd engineer to assist him. The work started with #1 unit HPS block. The inlet valve 420 was closed and the drain valve 421 was opened to depressurize the block. The handheld gauge was then connected to the adapter on the top of the accumulator and the accumulator pressure was checked and recorded. The entire procedure for the seven units was completed by 1405 hrs.  On the 28th April 2020, cargo was completed by 0930 hrs, and final draft survey by 0935 hrs, and vessel was prepared for departure.  Pilot boardedthe vessel at 1112 hrs on the 28th.  Vessel successfully tested the Main engine at 1119 Hrs in presence of the Pilot. Thereafter, vessel made fast customary 4 Tugs and commenced unberthing operation, and all lines cast off at 1148 Hrs. Thereafter, Tugs commenced pulling vessel off the berth and ahead kick given on Main Engine without any issue.  However, when second astern movement given at 1210 Hrs, vessel noticed ME was not responding since hydraulic pressure found dropped to 173 bar and slowly restored to 187 bar only; due to which engine went on ‘START BLOCK’ mode.  The Master then on consultation with the Pilot and PPA, decided to abort the departure maneuver, and with the assistance of two extra tugs, brought the vessel back to the berth.  The vessel then commenced trouble shooting, assisted by the shore team.
ImmediateAction: During troubleshoot, One of the checks involved attempting to increase the HPs pressure by adjusting the pressure regulating valve. This resulted in a hissing sound coming from the #5 Unit HCU valve block drain. The crew was able to identify the origin of the hissing noise and noticed the crack open HCU drain valve. Upon shutting this valve tightly, the HPS pressure was restored to the normal pressure of 225 BAR.
DirectCause: Failure to tighten fully the drain valve of HCU block of ME unit# 5 after check  Failure to verify system normalization and return to normal operating HPS pressure of 225 bars.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Notice  posted in ECR and prominent location in engine room that no work is to be undertaken whenever vessel is alongside berth at Port Hedland.  Vessel carried out a Training session for all engine crew to regularly check all HCU's drain valves correct "Closed positions.  A Notice posted on MOP panel in ECR; ‘HPS pressure during manoeuvering to be at least 230 bars.  A notice posted in the vicinity of HCU drain valves - “Verify fully shut tight after Accumulator pressure test or FIVA valve work
Corrective Action: Tech Managers instruction circulated to all vessels calling Port Hedland on dangers of undertaking any job of any kind on the vessels main engine, auxiliary engines and associated ancillaries when alongside at Port Headland.
LessionLearnt: Any PMS routines or non-routine maintenance on Main Engine and associated ancillaries  when alongside berth shall be strictly prohibited. Any emergency or essential maintenance/ checks  shall be discussed with Office and carried out only after due permissions from port authorities. After maintenance/ checks; systems shall be restored to normal and carefully tested, parameters checked prior manoeuvering movements.

IncidentDetails: 26 May 2020 / 0648 LT : Completed mooring at ORJ #01  26 May 2020 / 0810 LT : Aft Spring AFT rope parted, Temporary eye made and redeployed  26 May 2020 / 0850 LT : Aft Spring FWD & AFT ropes parted, Temporary eyes made and redeployed  26 May 2020 / 1212 LT : Commenced discharging       26 May 2020 / 1230 LT : Forward Spring FWD rope parted, Temporary eye made and redeployed
IncidentEventDetails: Once moored at the jetty ORJ #1, Kwinana, Australia vessel experienced longitudinal and lateral surge at the berth. Unusually high stress and strain experienced by Forward and Aft spring lines due to the movement causing them to part several times.  Ship surge is common occurence in SW Australian ports often left unaddressed by the Ports.
ImmediateAction: Terminal / Loading Master informed. Vessel was pumping cargo at nominal rate due to debottoming of COT's. Emergency Stop station manned.  Temporary eye prepared and rope redeplyed on the hook eye.  Charterers Asahi MOL, Owners, Local Agents and MSI informed and subsequently requested to take up matter with Voyage charterers [BP].  Asahi MOL advised vessel to send out BP CASUALTY report, same prepared and sent out.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damage to Forward Spring Lines (01 no) and Aft Spring lines (02 nos) and heavy chafing of the Aft Breast lines (01 no).
DirectCause: Unusually high Ship surge while at Berth (common occurence at SW Australian ports left unaddressed by certain ports)  Due to location the Port does not have a breakwater protecting the berths from outer sea conditions.  Unusually high stress and strain experienced by the mooring ropes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ship surge to be addressed / identified by the Port for risk involved to the vessels equipment not limited to mooring ropes and well as probable or possible case of Personal injury or eventual fatality and to Terminal equipment.  Offshore weather conditions to be estimated and  identiified and its adverse effects on berthed vessels at various terminals.

IncidentDetails: Engine room walkie talkie radio fell down during start of  cargo discharging operations
IncidentEventDetails: During start of cargo discharging operations while carrying walkie talkie radio slipped from hand fell down in engine room control room and got damaged. Walkie talkie radio battery clip found broken and is no more intrensically safe. Cannon be used onboard.
ImmediateAction: Walkie talkie radio switched off
DirectCause: To start the cargo operations  alarm activated, in hurry walkie talkie radio taken and got slipped from hand and damaged.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All instruments should be handled with care, before using the walkie talkie radio should be placed in cover and used.   same above points discussed in tool box meeting. Though it was mentioned several times to concerned officers and management team during daily management meeting to use the walki Talki with a cover and not to carry walki talkies in hand or back pocket. Same was not followed.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: 3E Ramesh \left hand got burn injuries due to hot fuel oil spray.
IncidentEventDetails: 3E ramesh was working on the leaking pressure gauge of Boiler fuel oil supply pump suction line. This pressure gauge tapping was leaking; when attempt was made to tighten the same, the gauge got dislodged and came free resulting in small spray of hot fuel oil.
ImmediateAction: 3E was remoived from the location, his boiler suit was removed along with the hand gloves; however the gloves took time to come off. Hot oil wchich was trapped in the gloves caused burn injuries to his left hand.
DirectCause: Spray of hot FO.
IndirectCause: Incorrect / insufficient lock out / tag out procedure.
RootCause: Complacency, lack of appreciation of inherent hazards in the task.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training, increase awareness.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: DUTY ENGR GOT  STUMBLED  ON THE COPT PLATFORM STIFNER & GOT FACE INJURY
IncidentEventDetails: 15:30hrs  3/e completed the tea break  15:45 hrs 3/E was taking round at cop platform  DUTY ENGR -3/E WHILE TAKING REGULAR ROUND IN ENGINE ROOM ON COP PLATFORM GOT STUMBLED ON THE STIFFNERS  ON THAT PLATFORM CAUSING FACE INJURY(small cut)
ImmediateAction: duty engr-3/E was immediate relieve off duty & given medical first aid,  wounded area cleaned  carefully & dressing carried out.  given initial pain killer to reduce any pain.  checked for any eye injury & clearity of vision.  carried out alcohol test to confirm no breach of alcohol policy.
LessionLearnt: Crew briefed regarding the importance of executing jobs with due diligence ,to be safe and slow rather then ending up into a accident in a hurry.
ClosingNote: Closed on 26/11/2015

IncidentDetails: FINGER INJURY TO ELECTRICAL OFFICER(PROMOTED FROM TRE/O WITH EFFECT 11TH DEC 2014)
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL WAS ENROUTE TO KEELUNG,TAIWAN. DURING PM REEFER ROUNDS FOUND ONE SOCKET BOX(4 REEFERS) WITHOUT POWER. ON FURTHER INVESTIGATION, FOUND THE BREAKER IN E/R TRIPPED. THE BREAKER WAS RESET, AND PROCEEDED TO POWER ON THE SOCKETS LOCALLY. WHILE DOING THIS, THE REEFER SOCKET DOOR SWUNG DURING ONE VIOLENT ROLL, AND HIT THE E/O's FINGERS.
ImmediateAction: HE IMMEDIATELY PROCEEDED TO SHIP'S OFFICE AND WAS GIVEN FIRST AID. IT WAS FOUND THAT THE NAIL OF THE RING FINGER OF RIGHT HAND HAD BEEN UPROOTED, AND MIDDLE FINGER HAD BEEN WOUNDED. BLEEDING WAS CONTROLLED AFTER A FEW MINUTES, AND APPROPRIATE DRESSING WAS ADMINISTERED TO THE WOUNDS. APART FROM PAIN, THERE WERE NO OTHER SYMPTOMS ON E/O.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: IT WAS FOUND THAT THE NAIL OF THE RING FINGER OF RIGHT HAND HAD BEEN UPROOTED, AND MIDDLE FINGER HAD BEEN WOUNDED. BLEEDING WAS CONTROLLED AFTER A FEW MINUTES, AND APPROPRIATE DRESSING WAS ADMINISTERED TO THE WOUNDS. APART FROM PAIN, THERE WERE NO OTHER SYMPTOMS ON E/O.
DirectCause: UNSECURED DOOR OF REEFER SOCKET POINTS.
IndirectCause: LACK OF PROCEDURE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TRAINING TO CARRY OUT JOBS SAFELY IN ADVERSE WEATHER CONDITION & IN COMPLIANCE WITH RA.
LessionLearnt: Importance of securing moving objects, Doors onboard Vessels, whether at Sea/Anchorage/Port. Safe working Practices
ReviewComments: Agree with Vessels cause analysis. Additionally Lack of Knowledge can be a cause.
ClosingNote: This incident has to be discussed with Ship Staff to ensure Familiarity with COSWP. Specifically Securing doors, when open. Further, ship staff should be familiar that a Heavier Door, will cause a Life threatening injury. Junior Officers and Trainee Ship Staff should be adequately briefed and supervised to ensure compliance with Safe Working practice. LARP reporting will further encourage ship staff on safe working practices.

IncidentDetails: Third officer Honda Kosuke got a cut on right leg below knee  and blood clot around ankle area due to fall / impact .
IncidentEventDetails: Third Officer Honda K, was monitoring  refers in bay 08 tier 86 on lashing bridge.After boarding the platform from port side he closed the access manhole and started monitoring the plugged  reefers from port to stbd side. As he got engrossed in task of refer monitoring watching up and moving along the lashing bridge from port to stbd , after reaching stbd side he did not realise that he has reached stbd side access manhole. As the stbd side access manhole was in open position. Third officer tripped and got cut in his right leg. Due to the fall his leg came in conatct with the edges of the access manhole. His shoe slipped and came off with a sudden jerk on the ankle at the ladder /access area .
ImmediateAction: Immediately first aid was given to third officer and all crew instructed to close the lashing bridge access manhole after boarding the access manhole.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Third Officer Honda Kosuke got cut on right leg and blood cot on bottom area of ankle.
DirectCause: While carrying out duty of monitoring reefers got in engaged in activity. Lacked situational awareness. Haste to complete job at the earliest as approx. 1000 reefers being loaded
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew instructed again of procedures of lashing checking and reefer plugging/unplugging /monitoring on lashing bridge and especially emphasis laid on closing of access manhole and following coswp at all times.
LessionLearnt: Safe Working Practices. Access doors to be kept closed after use. Access platforms to be kept clear of obstructions including lashing Materials.
ClosingNote: To ensure access areas kept clear of lashing Materials and access doors kept closed after use.

IncidentDetails: 2/E suffered cut of 2 inches long and 1 mm deep on the upper right part of the head
IncidentEventDetails: At port of Shanghai 2/E Rajesh Kumar was hurt on his head with a cut of about two Inches while collecting tools upon completion of #8 unit decarb from a confined space between cylinder heads.  He had taken off his helmet because space was too small and tool could not be collected with helmet on.He had taken off his helmet because space was too small and tool could not be collected with helmet on.
ImmediateAction: He was Attended by duty officer and Captain and given first aid. It was decided to send him to the doctor to have stiches on the wound for faster recovery. Doctor made two stiches and he was back on board in 3 hours.
DirectCause: Common slip/trip while working in the engine room.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Follow safe working practices at all times. All PPE to be kept worn at all times till the job is completed and tools are secured in place. In case if helmet etc. to be removed for short while, additional person to watchout for  such areas till the area is cleared.
LessionLearnt: Proper PPE  to be worn at all times.Same to be  discussed  in the safety meetings
ClosingNote: Meeting held with ship crew  on  importance of PPE.

IncidentDetails: While boxing up roof panels in C/E cabin ,E/O got cut on his left hand wrist(Exposed area between gloves & boiler suit).(20mm cut & 4 mm deep).
IncidentEventDetails: While boxing up roof panels in C/E cabin which have been opened earlier for ECDIS cables passage,E/O left hand slipped & got cut on his  left hand near wrist area.(Wound :- 20mm cut & 4 mm deep)
ImmediateAction: Work stopped &  onboard first aid treatment given to E/O.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While boxing up roof panels in C/E cabin which have been opened earlier for ECDIS cables passage,E/O left hand slipped & got cut on his  left hand near wrist area.(Wound :- 20mm cut & 4 mm deep)
DirectCause: While fitting back C/E cabin roof panels,his left hand slipped &  exposure area of his left  wrist  between boiler suit & gloves got cut. Observed E/O was wearing proper PPE.
RootCause: While fitting back C/E cabin roof panels,his left hand slipped &  exposure area of his left  wrist  between boiler suit & gloves got cut. Observed E/O was wearing proper PPE.
LessionLearnt: To Analyse the Hazards in an working environment prior starting the work
ReviewComments: Agree with the cause analysis. Additionally to ensure sufficient man power and effective use of PPE.
ClosingNote: This incident to be discussed with all Ship Staff, and the importance of Situational Awareness and awareness on Hazards in an working environment to be analysed prior commencement of work. To ensure effective use of PPE for the specific task.

IncidentDetails: While vsl. was enroute to Suez, cleaning of bilge tank was planned, RA done/complied with, enclosed space entry procedures followed. Oiler ABDUL AZIZ was a cleaning member for the bilge tank. During  cleaning operation of the tank as he moved from one compartment to another, through the web frame, he slipped as the floor area had curvature and was oily, his left eye brow hit the manhole of the web frame which resulted in a 15mm minor cut.  Subject was wearing requisite PPE at the time of injury, the area of work was adequately illuminated, rest hrs for the subject were complied with.
IncidentEventDetails: Oiler Abdul Aziz & oiler Mahesh were using kew machine to wash down the oily surfaces of the bilge tank, the entire floor area of this tank has curvature, which was also oily, oiler Abdul Aziz lost balance & slipped, while moving from one compartment of the tank to another, through the web frame manhole, his left eye brow hit the edge of manhole causing minor injury.
ImmediateAction: Job suspended, injured person i.e. oiler Abdul Aziz was shifted to the hospital where he was rendered first aid i.e. dressing on the wound after cleaning the wound.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While vsl. was enroute to Suez, cleaning of bilge tank was planned, RA done/complied with, enclosed space entry procedures followed. Oiler ABDUL AZIZ was a cleaning member for the bilge tank. During  cleaning operation of the tank as he moved from one compartment to another, through the web frame, he slipped as the floor area had curvature and was oily, his left eye brow hit the manhole of the web frame which resulted in a 15mm minor cut.  Subject was wearing requisite PPE at the time of injury, the area of work was adequately illuminated, rest hrs for the subject were complied with.
DirectCause: left forehead hit the web frame causing minor cut wound on the eyebrow as subject slipped while moving from one compartment to another in the bilge tank
IndirectCause: subject was wearing gumboots, the manhole was at slight extra height which needed to be crossed over in order to access other compartment,  in this attempt he lost control of the body and slipped as the tank surface was oily and has curvature.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Subject advised to be attentive & take extra precaution. Gumboot soles to be covered with non skid cotton covers so as to ensure stability of body in the tank. Additional non skid jute mats to be placed in the tank while moving from one compartment to another.
LessionLearnt: Safe Movement onboard, especially in restricted access/movement areas and with wet surface
ReviewComments: Effective RA and Tool box meeting with effective supervision can assist in preventing similar injuries.
ClosingNote: Please discuss this incident with all Ship staff with reference to COSWP, safe movement

IncidentDetails: 1530 - While tightening a bolt of Grab No. 2 using a open ring spanner, it  slipped  and came in contact  with the chief officer's  (Right) Incsisor Tooth .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper risk assessment to be done.
Corrective Action: Proper Risk assessment to be done. Adequate tools and protection to be used.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Working position to be adequate and adequate protection barriers to be placed. Hazards to be discussed prior commencement of job.

IncidentDetails: OS Rahul was engaged in repairing a broken bench on Poop deck. To remove a nail he was hammering the nail with swing action from bottom to up. One of the hammer swing deflected from the edge of the plank and he was hit on the left eyebrow due to this deflection. He sustained a 2 cm cut along the left eyebrow. Initially minor bleeding occured. First aid provided and bandaged. Therefater with body movement and washing face with rubbing of area bleeding re-appeared with higher intensity. Same was arrested by localised pressure application with cotton swab on wound.
ImmediateAction: First aid and medication provided conjuction with radio medical advise.  Later sent to the hospital for medical treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: OS Rahul was engaged in repairing a broken bench on Poop deck. To remove a nail he was hammering the nail with swing action from bottom to up. One of the hammer swing deflected from the edge of the plank and he was hit on the left eyebrow due to this deflection. He sustained a 2 cm cut along the left eyebrow. Initially minor bleeding occured. First aid provided and bandaged. Therefater with body movement and washing face with rubbing of area bleeding re-appeared with higher intensity. Same was arrested by localised pressure application with cotton swab on wound.
DirectCause: Hammer hitting the left Eye brow due deflected swing and Eye Protection not in use
IndirectCause: Failure to comprehend the possible risk of deflection.Job JHA & Supervision
RootCause: Lack of Safety Goggles and improper judgement on consequence of swing action
Proposedcorrectiveactions: JHA & Risk Assessment before task, training, useof affective LARP mechanism
Corrective Action: Safety Bulletin to be sent to all the vessels in the company to make them aware of the incident which will include correct working postures and positions.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: More situational awareness.Tool box meet.Work place assessment.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: For routine maintenance of L/B engine a lamp was temporarily placed in L/B however the lamp was not secured properly and fell on the head of E/OFF causing a minor injury on head. At the time of incident E/O was not wearing a helmet.
ImmediateAction: First aid given
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: For routine maintenance of L/B engine a lamp was temporarily placed in L/B however the lamp was not secured properly and fell on the head of E/OFF causing a minor injury on head. At the time of incident E/O was not wearing a helmet.
DirectCause: Lamp was not secured properly
IndirectCause: Negligence
RootCause: PPE was not used while carrying out the task
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The incident  was discussed with all persons onboard and Importance of PPE was explained.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Loose items to be secured adequately at work location and adequate PPE to be worn for the task.
ReviewComments: We concur with your cause analysis & counter measures noted to prevent recurrence. Please carry out tool box meeting prior commencement of job.
ClosingNote: Injury report Closed.

IncidentDetails: 11:40:00 AM - While the Chief Engineer was walking  on the main deck stbdside he tripped and fell  down,    due the reason that his feets touched & made contact with the mooring lines of the stbd cargo barge  which resulted minor bruises  on the face, lips and knees.  He was then administered medical first aid treatment.
ImmediateAction: He was then administered medical first aid treatment.
DirectCause: Mooring line of the stbd cargo barge was entirely covered by cargo residues and hence the same was not clearly visible apparent .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Informed stevedore foreman immediately to clean cargo residues to avoid accumulation on deck especially on  obsrtructions such as the mooring ropes from cargo barges.
Corrective Action: Duty officer to regularly monitor cargo operation and ensure spillage on deck should be reported to stevedores for cleaning. Notice to put for extra caution and ship staff to be aware of spillage before going on deck.
ClosingNote: Ship staff to be fully aware of mooring lines or any other obstacles on deck. they should be properly highlighted or tagged as required.

IncidentDetails: Cause for this sudden spasm is not known; as he was well last evening; In fact he played TT and did some push ups in cabin; At that time he did have some pain but was not of much concern ; But during his night watch he could not stand or sit; And was in acute pain.
ImmediateAction: Administered with pain killer bruffen and given hot water bottle.Today morning we called up balaji and the doctor administered diclofenac paracetamol tablet / Diclofenac injection.Presently we have given him diclofenac paracetamol tablet/injection ,but his pain is still unbearable ,and he is under observation.Presently is is lying on the cabin floor and is not able to move his body.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cause for this sudden spasm is not known; as he was well last evening; In fact he played TT and did some push ups in cabin; At that time he did have some pain but was not of much concern ; But during his night watch he could not stand or sit; And was in acute pain.
DirectCause: UNKNOWN
Corrective Action: This could be due to lifting of heavy weights, improper posture. Kindly discuss in the COSWP discussion for the month.
LessionLearnt: No specific learning identified from this incident.
ReviewComments: Nothing additional necessary.
ClosingNote: Maintain a healthy atmosphere on board & encourage all personnel to stay healthy even during their vacation period. Discuss the health related information disseminated to vessel from various sources

IncidentDetails: 3rd Officer slipped due to sudden rolling and fell from about 40cm height while standing on top of a drum and lifting boxes from top shelf in safety locker. On his fall he hit his back on the electrci panel which was behind him.
ImmediateAction: Injured person has severe pain in his back with bruises. Unable to freely walk. Injured person was rested, medication given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injured person was given medication as per medical advise from Doctor Balaji, after 1.5 days of medication, while vessel was in port Kingston, injured person complained of worsening condition therefore the injured person was sent to doctor at Kingston, Jamaica on 4th February 2015. Attending doctor concluded him to be unfit to continue sailing & wanted to carry out more tests to evaluate his condition fully. Therefore the injured person was signed-off from vessel on 4th February 2015 at Kingston, Jamaica.
DirectCause: Use of improper tool for the job. Sudden vessel's rolling/movement leading to losing hold/grip.
IndirectCause: Improper/lack of planning. Failure to carry out Level 1 risk assessment.
RootCause: Not complying with basic safety precautions mentioned in COSWP.  Improper tool used for the job. A proper standing platform was not used to stand on & remove the box.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All ship staff were called for a special safety meeting and following were discussed during the meeting:- (a) All crew members were described in detail about the incident. Direct, Indirect & Root cause explained in detail. (b) COSWP Ch.19; Manual Handling (c) Work planning procedure & Risk Assessment procedures to be followed,  (d) Safe procedure for carrying out similar job in future was discussed and as a minimum below hazards should be taken into account while carrying out similar jobs  (i) Obstrucions in work space (ii) Insufficient number of persons for the job (iii) Inclement weather condition (iV) Slips, trips & fall (v) Improper lifting postures/procedures (vi) Improper tool used for the job (vii) Use of PPE (e) Vessel raised requisition for additional small aluminum ladder to use for climbing up small heights to place in the store. (f) Caution poster "USE PROPER STANDING PLATFORM WHILE HANDLING THINGS ON TOP SHELVES" posted in all stores. (g) Ship's management team and Tech. Superintendent Mr. Umesh Iyengar who was on board, motivated the crew members to follow COSWP at all times and to carry out proper planning of jobs, carry out RA.
Corrective Action: Safety Bulletin to be sent to all the vessels in the company to make crew aware proper lifting techniques from heights and knowledge of “safe lift zone”.
ClosingNote: All deficiencies were closed

IncidentDetails: During greasing schedule reported Hoisting wire of crane no.4 few strand not in good condition.
IncidentEventDetails: while greasing of wires he encountered all this loose strand resulted to damage his rubber gloves.
ImmediateAction: Renewed and Lubricated
DirectCause: GREASING THE WIRE WITHOUT CHECKING THE DAMAGE. IF NOT WEARING GLOVES IT CAN SCRATCH HIS HANDS OR WOUND.
Corrective Action: Always wearing PPE. Hazards to be discuss in tool box meeting prior commencement of job.

IncidentDetails: TR.OS TRIPPED ON DECK AFTER HITTING THE BASE BRACKET OF THE MOORING WINCH IN THE AFT STATION CAUSING FRACTURE IN FOREARM.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL PROCEEDING TOWARDS BERTH UNDER PILOTAGE.  0706: MASTER ORDERED TO MAKE FAST THE AFT TUG.  WHILE PROCEEDING TO ASSIST IN MAKING FAST THE TUGS. TR.OS TRIPPED ON DECK AFTER HITTING THE BASE BRACKET OF THE MOORING WINCHES. LEADING A FRACTURE IN FOREARM.  0830: AGENT ONBOARD, DISCUSSED WITH AGENT FOR MEDICAL ASSISTANCE AS TR.OS IS INJURED DURING MOORING STATIONS.  0840: INFORMED OFFICE.  0850: TR.OS SENT TO DOCTOR WITH AGENT.  DOCTOR RECOMMEDED REPATRIATION, AFTER DIAGNOSING FRACTURE IN LEFT FOREARM.
ImmediateAction: INFORMED AGENT AND OFFICE.  TR.OS SENT TO DOCTOR.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: TR.OS TRIPPED ON DECK AFTER HITTING THE BASE BRACKET OF THE MOORING WINCH IN THE AFT STATION CAUSING FRACTURE IN FOREARM.
DirectCause: TRIPS AND FALLS IMPROPER POSITION FOR TASK WORK SPACE RESTRICTION
IndirectCause: LACK OF SKILL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: REFRESH TRANING
Corrective Action: REFRESH TRANING
LessionLearnt: Safe Movement Highlighting Areas of concern in Mooring Stations, with High Visibility Paints(Yellow)
ReviewComments: To Discuss with Ship Staff regarding importance of safe Movement
ClosingNote: Circulated among ships to avoid similar occurrences

IncidentDetails: OILER SUSTAINED SUPERFICIAL BURN INJURY ON HIS  RIGHT THIGH AND RIGHT HAND WHEN TAKING TANK SOUNDING.
IncidentEventDetails: DURING ROUTINE ENGINE ROOM TANK TRANSFERS, WHEN OIL WAS TRANSFERRED FROM BUNKER TANK TO HFO SETTLING TANK,THE SETTLING TANK SOUNDING GAUGE WAS NOTICED TO BE NOT MOVING AND THE LEVEL WAS NOT RAISING UP. IT WAS DECIDED TO CHECK THE MANUAL SOUNDING OF THE TANK.THE DUTY OILER WAS INSTRUCTED TO TAKE THE MANUAL SOUNDING OF THE TANK. DUTY OILER INSTEAD OF TAKING THE SOUNDING OF SETTLING TANK, ATTEMPTED TO TAKE THE SOUNDING OF THE HFO SERVICE TANK,WHICH WAS ALREADY FULL AND AT 95 DEGREES. HOT OIL AND VAPOUR SPURTED OUT FROM THE PIPE AND SPRAYED ON TO HIS RIGHT THIGH AND RIGHT HAND.  SUPERFICIAL BURN INJURY ON THE  RIGHT THIGH AND RIGHT HAND. THE MIXTURE OF OIL AND VAPOUR SPLASHED ON TO HIS RIGT THIGH AND HAND SINCE HE WAS BENDING DOWN TO TAKE THE SOUNDING.
ImmediateAction: PIPE COVER PUT BACK AND  FIRST AID DONE TO THE OILER.  THE OILER WAS IMMEDIATELY SHIFTED TO HOSPITAL,OIL CLEANED AND CLOTHES REMOVED.  PAIN KILLERS GIVEN AND BURN AID OINTMENT APPLIED.  OFFICE INFORMED,COMPANY MEDICAL DOCTOR INFORMED.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DURING ROUTINE ENGINE ROOM TANK TRANSFERS, WHEN OIL WAS TRANSFERRED FROM BUNKER TANK TO HFO SETTLING TANK,THE SETTLING TANK SOUNDING GAUGE WAS NOTICED TO BE NOT MOVING AND THE LEVEL WAS NOT RAISING UP. IT WAS DECIDED TO CHECK THE MANUAL SOUNDING OF THE TANK.THE DUTY OILER WAS INSTRUCTED TO TAKE THE MANUAL SOUNDING OF THE TANK. DUTY OILER INSTEAD OF TAKING THE SOUNDING OF SETTLING TANK, ATTEMPTED TO TAKE THE SOUNDING OF THE HFO SERVICE TANK,WHICH WAS ALREADY FULL AND AT 95 DEGREES. HOT OIL AND VAPOUR SPURTED OUT FROM THE PIPE AND SPRAYED ON TO HIS RIGHT THIGH AND RIGHT HAND.
DirectCause: POSSIBLY MIS- UNDERSTOOD THE INSTRUCTIONS GIVEN.
IndirectCause: AS BOTH THE SETTLING AND SERVICE TANKS ARE ADJACENT, IT IS POSSIBLE THAT THE OILER MIGHT HAVE CONFUSED HIMSELF  BETWEEN THE TANKS
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PROPER TRAINING SHOULD BE GIVEN,COMMUNICATION NEEDS TO BE IMPROVED,COMMUNICATION  TO BE IN SIMPLE AND CLEAR,EASILY UNDERSTOOD,PRIOR STARTING ANY JOB,VERIFY WATS NEEDS TO BE DONE.
LessionLearnt: PROPER TRAINING SHOULD BE GIVEN,COMMUNICATION NEEDS TO BE IMPROVED,COMMUNICATIONP
ReviewComments: PROPER TRAINING SHOULD BE GIVEN,COMMUNICATION NEEDS TO BE IMPROVED,COMMUNICATION
ClosingNote: ROPER TRAINING SHOULD BE GIVEN,COMMUNICATION NEEDS TO BE IMPROVED,COMMUNICATION

IncidentDetails: While arranging paint drums in paint locker O/S Rahul sustained a 1 inch cut in left hand forearm by an open lid of a paint drum, resulting in an open wound and instantaneous bleeding.
IncidentEventDetails: Working in paint locker for a routine squaring up job on a working day.
ImmediateAction: Cleaned the wound and first aid provided: Carried out 3 stitches using absorbable type thread (size 3 metric), applied anti-septic and dressed wound.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: OS Rahul was engaged in arranging drums in paint store.While keeping drums in there respective position one of the drum has open lid with sharp edge and his left hand boiler suit arm sleeve folded due to which he had a 1 inch deep cut on his left hand forearm .Initially minor bleeding occured. First aid provided and bandaged. Therefater with body movement with rubbing of area bleeding re-appeared with higher intensity. Same was arrested by localised pressure application with cotton swab on wound and later 3 stitches applied on board to restrict the bleeding and bandage applied.
DirectCause: Unaware of sharp edges from open lid of paint drum & Improper PPE dressing, leaving left hand coverall sleeve folded providing limited protection to forearm.
IndirectCause: Failure to comprehend the possible risk of sharp edges.Job JHA & Supervision. Possible mind occupied due family problems and not focussing on job.
RootCause: Lack of safety awareness & personal safety, lack of situational awareness, Improper PPE donning. Possible mind occupied due family problems at home and mind not focussing on job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew called for meeting, incident detailed, enforced proper use of PPE (mandatory) and supervisors to ensure all crew practicing safe approach to handling jobs.
Corrective Action: All crew called for meeting, incident detailed, enforced proper use of PPE (mandatory) and supervisors to ensure all crew practicing safe approach to handling jobs.
LessionLearnt: Crew trained for the proper use of PPE and adhering to basic seamanship safe practices while carrying out routine chores on-board.
ClosingNote: Closed on 26/Nov/2015

IncidentDetails: Steward Rubert Praniston right arm burned by hot water.
IncidentEventDetails: Steward was  preparing rice and  while draining hot water from rice , his hand slipped and hot water splashed on his right arm and got burned.
ImmediateAction: First aid given to steward.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn injury of right forehand.
DirectCause: PPE Not Worn. In case Rubber gloves would have been worn/ this could have been avoided
IndirectCause: In absence of regular ch/cook, he is performing cook duties and this not his regular job.
RootCause: Inadequate crew relief planning. Even after adequate notice, cook relief was arranged in time. Off-signing ch/cook refuse to sail with vessel untill next port, therfore had to signed off without relief.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PPE should be worn
LessionLearnt: Safe Handling procedures should be followed
ClosingNote: Closed

IncidentDetails: During the process of slackening nuts & bolts of 1port cargo oil tank butter worthcover, the ordinary seaman lost his balance and his back side hit the nearby deck frame causing severe pain.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was preparing for tank cleaning operations. Ordinary Seaman along with Bosun was advised to slacken the nuts & bolts of Butter worth holes. During the process of slackening nuts & bolts of 1port cargo oil tank butter worthcover, the ordinary seaman lost his balance and his back side hit the nearby deck frame causing severe pain. Was given first aid treatment and advised rest.
ImmediateAction: With support from Bosun he was taken to accommodation and given first aid by applying pain relief cream in the affected area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Muscle injury to lower back causing diffiiculty in walking & lifting.
DirectCause: Tool slipping.
IndirectCause: loaction of frames & angle bars near work areas.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew briefed of the hazards due to adjascent structres in case of slipping of tools while opening nuts & bolts.
LessionLearnt: Strict adherence to safe procedures.
ClosingNote: COSWP Training carried out every month to be effective & discuss the cases/NMs to be realistic.

IncidentDetails: CUT ON LEFT HAND WHILE WORKING WITH PAPER CUTTER
IncidentEventDetails: Stores were received at New Mangalore. Deck Cadets were assisting in checking stores.  Whilst cutting open one of the stores carton, Deck Cadet got cut in his left handpalm.  He was not wearing Gloves while working with paper cutter.
ImmediateAction: Cleaned the wound with antiseptic solution (Dettol) and wound dressed up
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut in the left palm.
DirectCause: Not wearing gloves while unwrapping the box with paper cutter.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be trained for PPE requirements when working with sharp objects / tools.
ReviewComments: Stress on proper use of PPE to be given during every safety meeting.
ClosingNote: Incident closed.

IncidentDetails: While vessel was alongside at Culf Lay berth,Chittagong around 1540, the cadet was doing Chipping on ventilation of Cargo Hold No.1. While chipping the side ventilation using a Pheumatic chipping hammer,suddenly some rust spurt through the Goggle's underside and injured his left eye.
ImmediateAction: he was administered some eye wash and Eye drops as a primary treatment. He was rested and sent ashore to the doctor the following day.

IncidentDetails: OS suffered injury to tooth (half-broken), and slight cut on lip due to impact of chipping hammer on his mouth - while he was  carrying out chipping of mid-ship crane platform.
IncidentEventDetails: Chipping and painting of mid-ship crane platform was in progress while vessel was at Ennore Anchorage. After lunch, when OS resumed his work, he lost control of the hammer while chipping and it bounced off the surface and hit him on the face, resulting in half-broken tooth and split lip. He immediately came back in to the accommodation and reported to Chief Officer.
ImmediateAction: Affected area was washed with cold water and ice-compress. Bleeding stopped. Applied light moisturizer on the lip and OS was given rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Half his upper incisor tooth was broken. Slight cut on the lower lip.
DirectCause: Momentary lapse of concentration while OS was chipping at his face height.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew will be additionally cautioned during tool-box and safety meetings that hammering jobs should be carried out with concentration, and due care should be taken when hammering near any body part. Always be alert. Work should be re-analysed with concerned officer if any condition where position of carrying out the job is causing concern.
Corrective Action: Training of all crew members on use of hand tools and importance of checking the tools prior use to be emphasized.
ReviewComments: All hand tools to be checked thoroughly on a daily basis prior usage.
ClosingNote: Near Miss report Closed.

IncidentDetails: During unmooring station departure New Mangalore Port, Trainee OS got a jerk on his left hand shoulder while picking up the tugs line.
IncidentEventDetails: The crew member was pulling the massanger line to put the tug's rope on the Bit and got a jerk on his left shoulder. He was immidiately kept clear of the tugs line and medication was given.
ImmediateAction: He was given Moov spray and I brufen tablets (2 Nos.) as the shoulder and arms started paining.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: He had a complain of strain on his left soulder. Mild pain on shoulder movement. Was sent ashore to doctor. Pls find the attached report.
DirectCause: While letting go the tugs line , line being heavy exerted weight on the messenger. Concerned crew had kept the messenger slight slack to let it go smoothly( turns were taken on the leading bit). This made the messenger get taught as soon as the tug line was pulled by the tug & concerned crew got a slight jerk on his left shoulder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew were given the extensive mooring and unmooring operation training as per QHSE manual chapter 12.Duty officer were instructed  not to use the trainee / untrained crew where the high risk involved in the job in future.
Corrective Action: All crew were given the extensive mooring and unmooring operation training as per QHSE manual chapter 12.Duty officer were instructed  not to use the trainee / untrained crew where the high risk involved in the job in future.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper training.
ReviewComments: Trainees to work under the supervision of the in-charge.  All training given noted.
ClosingNote: Concur with the cause analysis and corrective action. Report closed.

IncidentDetails: Bosun suffered Muscle sprain while pushing MOT ladder
IncidentEventDetails: While rigging the MOT ladder in Port Huelva, The port was tidal port with tidal range of 4 meters, the MOT ladder has to be rigged in between two loading arms on the jetty, The crane was not reaching the position where MOT to be positioned, hence the MOT has to be pushed manually to align with the jetty, bosun tried to push the MOT during this time he got muscle sprain at the hip.
ImmediateAction: First aid given, applied Heat spray
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Feels pain while bending, will be refered to Doctor for further treatment
DirectCause: Direct cause- Pushing the MOT to allign with jetty.  Indirect Cause- The MOT ladder has to be rigged in between the two loading arms on the jetty which was not within the designated reach of the crane  Root cause- Ships position was alligned such as to allign ship's manifolds 1 & 4 with adjecent shore loading arms, but was not positioned in such a way to rig the MOT gangway in designated area
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Chain blocks to be used to move the MOT gangway in such a situation or leverage rods could be used.
LessionLearnt: It is very important to note that the posture or position of the body while hauling loads is as important as the load itself.Correct posture to be taken like bending of knees while lifting and pushing.It is also to be noted that normal humans can deal with only 30 kg loads,beyond winch mechanical assistance is required.Kindly emphasis this in the next safety meeting and carry out a training session of the crew.
ClosingNote: Incident closed on 09/07/15

IncidentDetails: During sweeping of chipped rust and paint particles,foreign particle entered AB's left eye
IncidentEventDetails: At about 1545 after Tea time AB was sweeping the rust and paint particles left after chipping of the safety catwalk gratings,when a small particle entered his left eye.
ImmediateAction: Eye washed with flowing water to rinse off the foreign particle
DirectCause: Eye protection was not worn during the task
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All personnel to be briefed about the incident and importance of PPE and PPE matrix explained and to be understood and complied with by all.
ClosingNote: Look at the direction of wind & stand at perpendicular direction to wind flow & sweep away from the wind to avoid such an incident.

IncidentDetails: Tool box meeting was carried out, Jobs were assigned to  Engine crew, briefed about the job nature and the safety issues.Fabrication of the Gargabe metal lid  was assigned to Engine fitter along with Deck crew.Confirmed that Fitter was given a proper  rest  before the job.While carrying out the gas cutting/welding job in Engine Workshop,  he had used  Proper PPE  for the job.  when he was hitting the plate to bend, The slag metal  burr fled into  eye  which  casued eye irritation.
IncidentEventDetails: Though the Face shield was used for the job, but it was not  properly  covered the  face as the head band was not enough tightened to grip the forehead. He immediately  removed  the burr with flushing of water.He was given a medical aid. Reported that the irritation got  soothed down  after applying th eye lotion.
ImmediateAction: He immediately  removed  the burr with flushing of water.He was given a medical aid. Reported thatthe irritation got  soothed down  after applying th eye lotion.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Irritation.
DirectCause: Proper PPE  was  used, but it was not worn properly.Though the Face shield was used for the job, but it was not  properly  covered  the  face as the head band was not enough tightened to grip the fore head.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety  training was conducted, briefed about the usage of the Proper PPE and correct donning procedure.
Corrective Action: Safety  training was conducted, briefed about the usage of the Proper PPE and correct donning procedure.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: CUT ON LEFT INDEX FINGER WHILE CUTTING  MEAT.
IncidentEventDetails: CHIEF COOK WAS CUTTING   MEAT ,ACCIDENTLY KNIFE  SLIPPED DUE TO VESSEL ROLLING AND  GOT FLAT  PORTION CUT ON HIS INDEX FINGER.STARTED BLEEDING.FIRST AID PROVIDED.REQUIRED TO SEEK FOR MEDICAL ADVISE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Incident happened whilst chopping the meat. Index finger was as it seemed to be at that time, badly cut. Instead of a line cut it was a kind of one cm2 area compltely chopped off.        First aid - Tip was not washed, hand was kept above heart level and bandage was tightly applied over the injury with pressure to stop bleeding and bleeding was though monitored but bandage was not removed till visit to doctor to prevent re-starting of bleeding due to damage of developed blood clots.        Doctor visit at cartagena(Colombia) on 26th May was made, and found the deep cut touching the bone at index finger tip, nail portion was intact. For the doctor treatment pls see the attached report to this file.
DirectCause: Safe handling of equipment
IndirectCause: Vessel's pitching
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Awarness of work being handled in differetn scenerio.
Corrective Action: Awarness of work being handled in differetn scenerio.
LessionLearnt: Importance of heightened awareness when handling sharp objects
ReviewComments: Noted the report. Same discussed in office and forwarded to all container vessel's
ClosingNote: report closed

IncidentDetails: Third engineer right  hand palm got injured just below the little fimger while keeping aux engine  spare fuel pump on rack after overhauling.
IncidentEventDetails: At 0300 hrs local time,third engineer completed overhauling spare aux engine fuel pump.  After completion,he was keeping the overhauled pump on the rack in engine room workshop.  While keeping,the pump slipped from his hand and his right hand got trapped between the overhauled pump and the other fuel pump which was already on the rack.
ImmediateAction: Incident was reported to second officer and the hand was bandaged. He was givenoral painkillers
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Right hand palm swollen
DirectCause: Heavy weght.(15 kg)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Assistance required while handling
Corrective Action: The 3rd Engineer will be briefed about the importance of following correct manual handling procedures while handling / storing spares or tools.
ClosingNote: Investigation Carried out and all corrective actions compelted

IncidentDetails: Crew member jammed the tip of his index finger on left hand, in his cabin door hindge.
IncidentEventDetails: While exiting from his cabin the crew member accidently shut the door, not realizing that his left hand index finger was  in harms way, of the closing door. The closing door jammed his nail portion of the index finger, leaving a blood shot and slight swelling in the nail area.
ImmediateAction: Cold water and ice were locally applied to the finger. First Aid, was administered to reduce the pain.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Finger nail on left hand index finger was injured with slight swelling and bloodshot. pain killer administered. after 2 days observed that there were no signs of swelling and pain. no problems in normal movement of the finger
DirectCause: lack of situational awareness by the person, thus closing the door while keeping his finger in harms way.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: all staff advised to take care while carrying out their day to day duties and be more situationally aware wrt such injuries
Corrective Action: all staff advised to take care while carrying out their day to day duties and be more situationally aware wrt such injuries
LessionLearnt: Pls post warning notices on alleyways or doors making crew aware of the hazard
ClosingNote: Closed on 13.11.15

IncidentDetails: Vessel was scheduled to pick up Engine Spare parts upon arrival at Galle OPL. Sea condition were Moderate with Westerly winds of Force 4 and Swell of 2.0 m. Service Boat –“Sharaf Energy” approached on own Vessel PortQuater. Ship’s crew lowered Provision crane hook to pick up the First Lift( New AE Crank Shaft-approx. Wt -1.5 T). Two steady lines were made fast on to the hook and further taken turn on strong points to arrest hook swing. One of the steady lines is held by Bosun & Pumpman and another by AB-Vallathan & O/S Gagan).  Hook was connected to the Lift by Boat crew and simultaneously Service Boat started moving away from ship with sudden increase on speed leading to violent swinging of The Lift. As a result, there was a sudden jerk on the steady lines which caused the Bosun right hand to entangle in the turns.  The Steady line was immediately released upon the instruction from Chief Officer.  Bosun’s right hand was immediately cleared from turns and Leather Palm Gloves was removed and his right hand little finger found chopped into two parts (Please refer attached photographs)
IncidentEventDetails: 1000: EOSP/Vessel Approaching Galle OPL  1045 : Sharaf Energy Alongside on Stbd side.  1100 : 3 Technicians onboard.  1115 : Service Boat-Sharaf energy approached on Port Quater.  1118 : Provision Crane Hook lowered to Service Boat.  1120 : Hooks Engaged to  First Lift( New AE Crank Shaft-approx. Wt -1.5 T)  1121: Service Boat-Sharaf energy moved away from ship with sudden increase on speed  1122: Bosun's Right Hand got entangled on turns.  1123: After discovering, Chief Officer reported to bridge about the incident.  1123: Bosun was rushed to hospital.  1124: First Aid Started.  1130: Master reported to Company/Agents.  1206: All stores onboard.  1224: Agent informed Master that injured Bosun can be disembarked and all necessary arrangements are made.  1238: Injured Bosun dismerked to Service Boat Sharaf Energy for further medical treatment ashore along with all his Luggage and Documents.
ImmediateAction: injured crew member being rushed to the ship's hospital and first aid given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Right hand little finger parted in two.
DirectCause: Hook was connected to the Lift by Boat crew and simultaneously Service Boat started moving away from ship with sudden increase on speed leading to violent swinging of The Lift. As a result, there was a sudden jerk on the steady lines which caused the Bosun right hand to entangle in the turns.  The Steady line was immediately released upon the instruction from Chief Officer.  Bosun’s right hand was immediately cleared from turns and Leather Palm Gloves was removed and his right hand little finger found chopped into two parts (Please refer attached photographs)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training
Corrective Action: Training
LessionLearnt: As per attached Investigation Report.
ReviewComments: As per attached Investigation Report.
ClosingNote: As per attached Investigation Report.

IncidentDetails: Deck cadet suffered a Shoulder muscle sprain.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1400H 22 June 2015 while conducting reefer container monitoring, at cross deck, Deck Cadet stepped on a checkered plate entrance cover which is used to access mech. fan and tripped due to a hinge on the cover and lost balance. To prevent from falling, he held on to a nearby lashing bar on container and suffered a right shoulder muscle Sprain and mild thigh sprain.
ImmediateAction: Deck Cadet was brought to ship's hospital administered first aid, Ibu Profen 400mg, hot water fermentation, and appilied methyl salieylate liniment 25% on swellen area and gently massaged.  IP is recovering fast. No sign or symptoms of fracture. After checking at the location of the incident, found the condition of the cover was rusty and it had a broken hinge. Placed a plank on top of the cover and higlighted the area. Planned to rectify hinge at ealiest opportunity, when no DG around.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Musle sprain at right shoulder back area. IP recovered and resumed work following day.
DirectCause: Situational awareness. Assses working environment for tripping hazards.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Include in tool box meeting to make asssesment of working environment for tripping hazards.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Situational Awareness, and Safe Movement onboard Vessels considering the possible tripping Hazards, and the momentary Hazards posed when lashing and unlashing containers.
ClosingNote: Ship Staff to be familiar with COSWP, Chapter 13, Safe Movement

IncidentDetails: On 25th June 2015,around 2130LT,when E/O Olayon James Torquil and Oiler Lodovice John Elviswere returning back to ship from shore leave,they were waylaid by three persons close to the gate and robbed off the pouch from Oiler Elvis,ehich contain his passport and wallet.At the time of incident ,oiler was walking few steps ahead of E/O and three persons suddenly came from behind and blocked them.One person caught hold of oiler from behind and put the knife on his neck and the other fellow asked him to give away the money.Meantime the 3rd fellow caught hold of E/O from front and tried to snatch his pouch too.But E/O tried to himself clear from his hold and has tried to stop him from snatching the bag.By this time the other 2 fellows have managed to snatch the pouch from oiler and all 3 ran away without taking the pouch from E/O. Then they have went to nearby restaurant where 3 other crew from our ship were sitting and called them for help.They too have come and looked for the robbers.But they could not find them.They have towards the boats in sea side and hid somewhere.Then they have gone to the police ststion and made a complaint about the incident.When myself came to know about this incident through the other crew,i called agent on phone and asked him to goto the police stationand give necessary help to them.After making the complaint about the incident police brought them back to the ship.Matter was reported to office by phone and by email.Oiler's pouch was containing his passport and wallet with 10 pounds and about 3000 pesos only.Next day morning police informed they have caught hold of the robbers and retrieved some items.E/O and oiler went to the police station along with stevedore foreman and he got back the pouch with passport and other small items.Only the wallet was missing.

Then they have went to nearby restaurant where 3 other crew from our ship were sitting and called them for help.They too have come and looked for the robbers.But they could not find them.They have towards the boats in sea side and hid somewhere.Then they have gone to the police ststion and made a complaint about the incident.When myself came to know about this incident through the other crew,i called agent on phone and asked him to goto the police stationand give necessary help to them.After making the complaint about the incident police brought them back to the ship.Matter was reported to office by phone and by email.Oiler's pouch was containing his passport and wallet with 10 pounds and about 3000 pesos only.Next day morning police informed they have caught hold of the robbers and retrieved some items.E/O and oiler went to the police station along with stevedore foreman and he got back the pouch with passport and other small items.Only the wallet was missing.

DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nil
Proposedcorrectiveactions: No shore leave to be given in ports which are unsafe to go out.
Corrective Action: 1. No shore leave henceforth, in this port. 2. Agent to be asked for basic local information and crew safety well before ship staff going ashore. 3. Ship-staff to be in groups and should be back in daylight hours.  4. Should always take cab instead of walking through in late hours.
LessionLearnt: Always to be in group. No late night walking back to ship but always take cab. Not going ashore to ports / countries where things are uncertain. Agent to be contacted for local information, precautions or any incident took place in past. No shore leave to be granted for such ports with no proper security.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting. corrective action to be strictly adhere to.
