IncidentDetails: Back Injury to OS Sahaya Terence Alifer Salter while trying to open booby access for cargo hold
IncidentEventDetails: On 24th June,at Nhava Sheva,at 2200 LT OS Terence & E/OFF S.Suveen had gone to No 9 cargo hold to open the booby hatch to record the data log download request for one of the empty reefer loaded in no 9 cargo hold for which the reefer needed to be connected with cable on the crossdeck into the plug point through the booby hatch.  The E/off had gone through the under deck passage & opened the securing arrangements for the booby hatch which are always kept locked from inside with turnbuckle so that no shore personals can have unauthorised access through the booby hatch.  The E/off confirmed to the OS that he has already opened the securing arrangements from inside & that he can have the booby hatch opened so that he could take the reefer cable out for connection.  The OS tried opening the booby hatch using all his strength but he couldnt open it with his hand so he tried to use the short lashing bar nearby but still couldnt open the booby hatch and he got a jerk on his back while trying to open the booby hatch.  In the meanwhile the E/off later found out that there were two booby access in the cargo hold, one for the cargo hold & the other was the emergency exit from Engine room and he had opened the securing for booby hatch for cargo hold & had told the OS to open the emergency exit booby hatch,which was the wrong booby hatch opening which the OS was trying his strength on to open as it was secured from inside.  Later the E/off rectified his mistake & got the right one opened with the help of the OS.  During this task the OS has sustained back injury due to jerk on his back & has been adviced bed rest for 2 days by company doctor.
ImmediateAction: OS was given pain killer medication & given rest.  Company doctor was consulted & medication recd from doctor who advised bed rest.  Upon arrival at next port sent to doctor & later signed off as doctor had adviced bed rest & physiotherapy for back.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Back injury to OS Sahaya Terence Alifer Salter sustained during opening of booby access to cargo hold  during cargo operations in Port
DirectCause: Communication Gap between OS & E/OFF during operation of opening of booby hatch cover
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always check & confirm with proper communication in place for any future operations.
Corrective Action: Incident report to be circulated to all Vessels to highlight the concern of effective Planning.
LessionLearnt: Direct Cause:  - Excessive force applied to open on the wrong booby Hatch. Indirect cause:  - Lack of communication. - Sense of urgency observed, during the process, due to which factors were not properly analysed during the process of opening the booby hatch entrance. Root Cause: - Lack of planning (Tool box meeting carried out did not discuss the communication procedure and identify the location of the booby hatch entrance).
ClosingNote: Direct Cause:  - Excessive force applied to open on the wrong booby Hatch. Indirect cause:  - Lack of communication. - Sense of urgency observed, during the process, due to which factors were not properly analysed during the process of opening the booby hatch entrance. Root Cause: - Lack of planning (Tool box meeting carried out did not discuss the communication procedure and identify the location of the booby hatch entrance).

IncidentDetails: While cleaning the cargo hold, deck cadet had a injury in the middle finger of the toe(length about 1.3 cm & depth 1mm)  and also wound on the upper layer skin of the forefinger toe( length about 0.7 cm & depth 0.5 mm).
IncidentEventDetails: While cleaning the cargo hold No.5  with a high pressure water jet with fresh water, the deck cadet lowered the water jet for washing the tank top ,the pressure on the water jet splashed and cut his safety boot and caused injury in his middle finger toe and upper skin of the forefinger toe.
ImmediateAction: First aid treatment was given initially and stitching was done in the middle finger toe and dressing was done. He was given some medicines and asked to take some proper rest.
DirectCause: High pressure water splashes to the safety boot and cut the boot.Delay to stop the water jet of high pressure.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: While cleaning the hatch by high pressure machine, wear safety shoes instead of Safety boots. More precautions has to be taken while using high pressure machines.
Corrective Action: While cleaning the hatch by high pressure machine, wear safety shoes instead of Safety boots. More precautions has to be taken while using high pressure machines.
LessionLearnt: Use of PPE appropriate to the task involved. Training in the use of power tools to be carried-out for all crew.
ReviewComments: The cadet had worked for about 9 hrs prior the incident. The delay in releasing the trigger of the HP gun could have been due to lack of attention caused by fatigue. The Rubber boots that were worn did not have steel toe caps.
ClosingNote: Injury report and review comments to be discussed during the next Safety committee meeting. PPE not complying with required standards, to be rejected at the time stores are delivered onboard.

IncidentDetails: SPLATTERING OF WELDING SLAG DURING WELDING, CAUSE MINOR INJURY TO A CREW MEMBER.
IncidentEventDetails: TWO PERSONNAL,WHILE PERFORMING WELDING ON A STAINLESS STEEL PLATE,RED HOT GRANULES FROM THE WELDING GOT SPLATTERED ON ONE OF THE CREW MEMBER.SINCE THE CREW WHO PERFORMING THE WELDING WAS WITH FULL PPE AND WAS SAFE. BUT THE CREW WHO WAS HELPING IN PROCESS WAS NOT WEARING THE APRON.A PARTICAL FROM THE WELDING GOT SPLATTERED AND FELL ON THE PERSON'S NECK WHO WAS NOT WITH PROPER PPE, CAUSING A SMALL HEAT BLISTER.
ImmediateAction: IMMEDIATELY FIRST AID GIVEN FOR THE PERSON INJURED.INSTANT TOOL BOX MEETING CONDUCTED AND ALL PERSONS WARNED AND BRIEFED ABOUT THE IMPORTANCE OF PPE.WORK RESUMED AFTER SOME TIME.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor burns on left foot
DirectCause: In-sufficient briefing during tool box meeting/Risk assessment, Improper PPE.  Lack of motivation and safety culture.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Detailed Tool box meeting to be conducted prior non-routine jobs and all hazard aspects to be discussed as per RA.  Ship staff to strictly comply with PPE Matrix at all times.
Corrective Action: Detailed Tool box meeting to be conducted prior non-routine jobs and all hazard aspects to be discussed as per RA.  Ship staff to strictly comply with PPE Matrix at all times.
LessionLearnt: Use of proper PPE.
ReviewComments: Effective RA and tool box meeting with efficient supervision can assist in prevent similar injuries.
ClosingNote: Pls discuss this incident with all staff in next safety meeting, giving reference to corresponding chapter of COSWP.

IncidentDetails: While lowering purifier shaft,shaft was vertcally in place but bearing housing was sitting on studs and not going into bearing sleeves,so 4/E started adjusting shaft from the gear case.As 2/E was looking from the top and was also trying to adjust the bearing alignment from the top, 2/E rotated the shaft and 4/E at the same time put his hand between shaft and sleeves.the bearing housing went down along with the shaft and his left hand thumb caught between the shaft and bearing sleeves.On hearing 4/E cries, 2/E immediately lifted shaft up with the help of chain block.2/E assisted 4/E to the ship's hospital and Master and C/E were informed regarding the incident.
IncidentEventDetails: Commencement of Job : 1500LT  Time of Incident : 1530LT  Job ceased:1530LT  First Aid rendered to 4/E:1540LT  Port Authorities and Agent notified regarding incident: 1545LT  Office Notified regarding incident:1548LT  Tug Assistance by port:1848LT  4/E left vessel for Hospital:1906LT.  Agent Notified to assist 4/E:1910LT
ImmediateAction: First Aid Dressing was rendered to 4/E to stop bleeding.  Port authorities notified seeking Medical Assistance.  Agent and Office notified via telephone call regarding the incident.  Company doctor consulted to provide immediate medication.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While lowering purifier shaft,shaft was vertcally in place but bearing housing was sitting on studs and not going into bearing sleeves,so 4/E started adjusting shaft from the gear case.As 2/E was looking from the top and was also trying to adjust the bearing alignment from the top, 2/E rotated the shaft and 4/E at the same time put his hand between shaft and sleeves.the bearing housing went down along with the shaft and his left hand thumb caught between the shaft and bearing sleeves.On hearing 4/E cries, 2/E immediately lifted shaft up with the help of chain block.2/E assisted 4/E to the ship's hospital and Master and C/E were informed regarding the incident.
DirectCause: THE TOOL USED WAS NOT THE PROPER TOOL, NO COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE LOWERER AND PERSON WHO GOT INJURED. HASTE TO COMPLETE THE JOB IN HURRY.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PROPER TOOLS TO BE USED AT ALL GIVEN TIME IF NO TOOL AVAILABLE THEN JOB TO BE DEFFERED BY INFORMING OFFICE ACCORDINGLY AND REQUISITION OF THE SAME TO BE RAISED IMMEDIATELY.
Corrective Action: PROPER TOOLS TO BE USED AT ALL GIVEN TIME IF NO TOOL AVAILABLE THEN JOB TO BE DEFFERED BY INFORMING OFFICE ACCORDINGLY AND REQUISITION OF THE SAME TO BE RAISED IMMEDIATELY.
ClosingNote: Investigation completed. Corrective and preventive actions taken.

IncidentDetails: General steward suffered minor skin injury due to chemical burn by floor stripper.
IncidentEventDetails: General Steward was advised to clean the toilet of spare officer cabin upon change of officer. While carrying the can containing chemical stripper on the stairs he had held it cose to his right thigh. The chemical leaked through the partially open lid due to sloshing and was absorbed by his overalls. This caused chemical burn to his right thigh. First aid treatment given.
ImmediateAction: First aid treatment given. A meeting held with all staff on board and advised to close the caps of all chemical containers properly to avoid such injuries. All staff further advised to carry chemicals only in small enclosed receptacles rather than carrying the filled container/cans to the work site
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: General Steward suffered minor burn injury to skin on his right thigh due to chemical splashing by floor stripper which is corrosive.
DirectCause: 1) Improper carrying of chemicals.  2) The container having the chemical was not closed properly.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting to be held with all staff and advise dangers related to handling chemicals. Full MSDS of Floor stripper to be explained. Proper procedure for carriage and storage after use to be discussed. Details of the injury to be shown to all staff.
Corrective Action: Meeting to be held with all staff and advise dangers related to handling chemicals. Full MSDS of Floor stripper to be explained. Proper procedure for carriage and storage after use to be discussed. Details of the injury to be shown to all staff.
LessionLearnt: Avoid carriage of chemical inside accommodation in bucket with proper lid.
ReviewComments: Direct Causes: 1. Inadequate knowledge. 2. Improper equipment used for carrying chemical On interview with the ship staff nobody intervened and stopped the unsafe act which continued during course of carrying out the job.
ClosingNote: In addition Pls note that COSWP entwined with company procedures should ensure safety at highest standards. Pls discuss the same in next month SCMM and in addition pls ensure proper awareness is spread among the crew ins hazard involved while dealing with chemicals.

ImmediateAction: 1.First aid given.  2.Hotwater bag pack appplied for 20 mins.  3.Applied Muscle Deep heat spray .  4.Applied pain relif Swelling bandage followed by creep bandage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1000 LT:Pumpman reported for discussing the    day work.Onsite Toolbox meeting carried out & Job    has been given to replace the rusted gratings on    deck  walkway.    1030 LT: Pumpman started the routine work.    1045 LT:Spare plastic  gratings brought to the    working area for measuring and replacing.    1200 LT:Grating has been cut as per the    measurement.    1200 LT~1300 LT: Lunch Break.    1300 LT:Pumpan resume the work and fixed     grating at place.    1400 LT :While fixing back the J-Clamps,his left    foot was on grating which accidently slipped and    ankle got twisted.      1405 LT: Pumpan brought to hospital /Inform    bridge.Informed master/Fist Aid given/Rested for    the day.

IncidentDetails: Messman whilst shredding used cardboards got slight cut on his left hand (index finger)due to a protruding binding pin from the cardboard.
IncidentEventDetails: Messman was involved in clearing & squarring up the used cardboards from dry provision stor. he was shredding the cardboards so that the same could be stowed within the garbage bins. He had failed to notice a protruding binding pin from the cardboard which he was shredding, eventuall that sharp protruding binding pin had caused a cut on his finger.
ImmediateAction: Immediately messman reported to safety officer & first aid was provided.He was given light duties for a day & observed the cut to be healing after a day's observation.he was instructed to be careful whilst doing shredding jobs & to inspect the cardboard before shredding the same in future.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: General Steward Whilst Shredding used cardboards got cut wounds on his left hand(index finger).
DirectCause: Non compliance of PPE requirement
Proposedcorrectiveactions: General Steward was briefed about the requirement to work safe & to adopt caution at work at all times to avoid such mishaps/injury in the nea future. this near miss will be discussed amongst other crew members in the safety meeting.
Corrective Action: General Steward was briefed about the requirement to work safe & to adopt caution at work at all times to avoid such mishaps/injury in the nea future. this near miss will be discussed amongst other crew members in the safety meeting.
ClosingNote: Wearing proper PPE is to be complied at all times. Any trainee to be trained in such task.

IncidentDetails: Pump man injured his right wrist while disconnecting the flexible hoses, after completion of STS ops.
IncidentEventDetails: after loading completed, the cargo hoses were disconnected by other ship and drained into own ship. Hoses were then disconnected from own ships manifold. after disconnection, the hoses were being lifted by own ships crane. during this operation one of the hose swung gently towards the manifold where the pumpman was fixing the reducer flanges. the hose bumped onto his right wrist. caught unaware,t he pumpman sprained his wrist.
ImmediateAction: First aid in the form of Ice pack was applied. checks were made to ensure no major injury/harm was caused to the pumpman. except for a sprained wrist, all was in order.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sprained wrist on right hand.
DirectCause: Inadequate signalling/warning and possible disorientation due to late night operation.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All staff briefed wrt staying well clear from area, when the crane is in use. Proper signalling and supervision to be implimented
LessionLearnt: Hose disconnection being a critical operation , Duty officer has to be present in location to supervise the Job. Person in charge of signalling the crane operator should wear the bright color dress with RR tapes so that he is readily identified among the fellow crew members & the Crane operator & the signalling person should stand in sight of each other & to be in direct communication over the walkie talkie radio.Crew members present in location also should stay well clear of the moving parts , Pump man in this case should have tightened the manifold blanks at a later stage and stayed well clear of the flexible hose , thereby preventing an injury
ClosingNote: Vessel provided with bright color overcoat for the PIC for Signalling operations

IncidentDetails: On 02/10/2015, fitter,while he was assisting with engineers on aux.engine cylinder head,his left hand slipped and little finger hit the sharp edge tool and found small finger nail was cut and bleeding.He felt too much pain and dizziness.
ImmediateAction: Immediately first aid was given and bleeding was stopped. medicines are given by 2/officer for heeling and for pain and advised for rest. At noon 2/officer examined and found he reduced pain and freely walking.Also explained him about wearing proper PPE and safe working practice.
DirectCause: Not wearing safety gloves while working with tools in ER
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out and crew were made aware of the consequences of not wearing proper PPE. Notice posted in Ship office and ECR and also PPE Matrix from Ch.2 of Health & Safety Manual posted at prominent  places like changing lockers and entrance to ER.
Corrective Action: Discuss the same during Safety assessment
LessionLearnt: Importance of Proper PPE.  Importance of proper tool box meeting and proper supervision during tasks.
ReviewComments: The above injury could have been easily avoided by use of proper PPE. That the fitter was not wearing PPE and the same was not pointed out to him by others working with him points to a poor safety culture on board the vessel.
ClosingNote: The Report has been reviewed and information promulgated to all fleet vessels.  As part of the preventive action, the company will start PPE campaign on the importance of PPE.

IncidentDetails: Moderate pain in Collar bone, Unable to come for work on particular day.
IncidentEventDetails: on 08-Oct-15/ PM :- AB Gaurav was reported for heavy pain on his left side collar bone area and unable to lift his Arm. It was noticed that on the same day when he was doing ecxercise in the Gym, he lifted heavy weights which caused him pain on the shoulder.
ImmediateAction: First aid was given and complete rest for the following day.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: on 08-Oct-15/PM:- AB Gaurav Chaudhary has reported for the pain on his collar bone area which allegedly caused due to lifting of heavy weights in the Gym while doing excercise.
DirectCause: Lifting Heavy weights
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Advised him not to lift heavy weights in the Gym.
LessionLearnt: Agree to the vessel's proposed corrective actions. Mild weights could be used in Gym and heavy weights to be avoided. As vessel keeps rolling easily at all times , slightest roll while lifting heavy weights might cause permanent damages to the muscular/ ortho system.
ClosingNote: AB reported fine & doing normal shipboard activities

Corrective Action: Advise the crew and watch carefully the splash of waves and swell on deck and maintain communication and look out ,additional PPE to be used.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory corrective actions implemented and confirmed by vessel
ClosingNote: Injury report assessed and followed up - Satisfactory

IncidentDetails: The crew member was unplugging the reefers in BAY 36. Since the cargo operation was about start in that particular Bay, Stevedores had already unlashed some of the containers. While clearing Reefer cable, it got stuck in one of the Turn buckles which was already unlashed and but kept in upright position.  The turnbuckle shifted its position and it fell right over his left hand palm causing a minor cut on his little finger. He was wearing Leather Palm gloves which saved him from serious cut or major injury.
ImmediateAction: FIRST AID GIVEN ,AND PUT HIM ON BED REST.  ALSO ALL DECK CREW WERE INSTRUCTED TO BE MORE ALERT DURING THE CARGO OPERATION AND TO WEAR PROPER PPE AND FOLLOW SAFE WORKING PRACTICES. STEVEDORES WERE INFORMED ABOUT PROPER SECURING OF THE LASHING MATERIAL AFTER COMPLETING THE LASHING ,AND INSTRUCTED DUTY OFFICERS TO MAKE SURE THE SAME BY REGULAR ROUNDS
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The crew members was unplugging the reefers in BAY 36. He was taking support of  lashing material while moving across from port to stbd side.  Since the cargo operation was about start in that particular Bay,Stevedores had already unlashed some of the containers,unfortunately the crew member did not notice this and took support on a  turn buckle which was already unlashed and but kept in upright position,eventually turnbuckle shifted its position and it fell right over his left hand palm causing a minor cut on his little finger.
DirectCause: Stevedores Careless Work ethics towards Securing of Lashing materials .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL DECK CREW WERE INSTRUCTED TO BE MORE ALERT DURING THE CARGO OPERATION AND TO WEAR PROPER PPE AND FOLLOW SAFE WORKING PRACTICES. STEVEDORES WERE INFORMED ABOUT PROPER SECURING OF THE LASHING MATERIAL AFTER COMPLETING THE LASHING ,AND INSTRUCTED DUTY OFFICERS TO MAKE SURE THE SAME BY REGULAR ROUNDS
Corrective Action: ALL DECK CREW WERE INSTRUCTED TO BE MORE ALERT DURING THE CARGO OPERATION AND TO WEAR PROPER PPE AND FOLLOW SAFE WORKING PRACTICES. STEVEDORES WERE INFORMED ABOUT PROPER SECURING OF THE LASHING MATERIAL AFTER COMPLETING THE LASHING ,AND INSTRUCTED DUTY OFFICERS TO MAKE SURE THE SAME BY REGULAR ROUNDS
LessionLearnt: Importance of assessing the conditions and staying alert.
ReviewComments: Cargo operations are in effect a hazardous activity, which require a very high level of alertness and risk perception. It is even more important when hadnling lashing bars, reefer cables, checking IMDG containers, and being near cargo operation area. Discussion during regular tool box meetings will help avoid re-occurence. Vessels to review the requirement of connecting cables when lashing/cargo ops are ongoing.
ClosingNote: Injury report has been reviewed. Vessel is advised to review its procedures and create "hazard zones" near areas where cargo is ongoing.  Entry should be restricted in such hazard zone.

IncidentDetails: Foward Mast anchor bell fell from a height of 3mtr on right shoulder of deck crew whilst working in vicinity
IncidentEventDetails: Deck crew was assigned job on Forward mooring lines canvas cover on eyes.  While attending to same, bell at forward mast (Signalling device) fell on one of the Deck Crew's right shoulder.
ImmediateAction: Crew member removed from location and first aid administered
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deck crew was assigned job on Forward mooring lines canvas cover on eyes.  While attending to same, bell at forward mast (Signalling device) fell on one of the Deck Crew's right shoulder. Swelling and muscle spasm on the right shoulder observed
DirectCause: Bell fell as securing nut/bolt got loose due to vibrations.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Bell re-secured with locking nut. Tool box meeting conducted and ship staff advised to ascertain hazards in the vicinity of job area. Al Staff to avoid working/standing under suspended items.

IncidentDetails: Fitter head suffer a minor bruise and slight hump.
IncidentEventDetails: Oct.11, 2015 at O740 hrs local time, fitter Arnold A. Roma went on deck to borrow tools from boatswain at carpenter store in cross deck no.4. He was following bosun and not noticing a log bark on his path and step on it. The deck plate at the moment was still wet in the morning dew. He made a large step forward that made his body unstable & slide on the bark. He fell out of balance into the deck. His back of the head hit the deck when his hard hat fell. His head suffer a minor bruise and slight hump. He was concious at the incident but later felt pain on his head. He was given asperin for pain reliever.
ImmediateAction: Bosun help fitter immediately and ask if he is ok. Since the fitter never lose his senses, immediately stand up and later bosun check his back head but bruise has not yet identified until later.
DirectCause: Slip on log bark
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew is advice to always bring torch light when going on cross deck while deck is fully loaded with log cargo. Crew also warned to wear the helmet correctly, helmet strap on the neck should be put on at all time.
Corrective Action: Crew is advice to always bring torch light when going on cross deck while deck is fully loaded with log cargo. Crew also warned to wear the helmet correctly, helmet strap on the neck should be put on at all time.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: Vsl departed safely from Puerto cabello after discharging fertilizer.Soon after RFA was given and routine work was resumed in E/R.During routine round in E/R,2nd eng noticed excessive boiler steam pressure.To regulate the steam pressure,excess steam pressure was dumped to the hot well through atmospheric condenser.As a result hot well started steaming.As the hot well top inspection cover was in open position,steam was started coming out from top cover. 2nd eng tried to close the cover to stop steam leaking.During that timesteam blown over on his  right hand back of the palm and caused burn/scalding on the R/H back ofthe palm.
IncidentEventDetails: Vsl departed safely from Puerto cabello after discharging fertilizer.Soon after RFA was given and routine work was resumed in E/R.During routine round in E/R,2nd eng noticed excessive boiler steam pressure.To regulate the steam pressure,excess steam pressure was dumped to the hot well through atmospheric condenser.As a result hot well started steaming.As the hot well top inspection cover was in open position,steam was started coming out from top cover. 2nd eng tried to close the cover to stop steam leaking.During that timesteam blown over on his  right hand back of the palm and caused burn/scalding on the R/H back ofthe palm.
ImmediateAction: Poured plenty of cold water on burn area  and palm was dipped in cold water for almost 2 hrs.With the time burning sensation has reduced and applied burn cream (Nemocin) Ship Captain's Medical Guide and further medical advice obtained from company's doctor.  After the medication he is feeling beeter and irritation sense has considerably reduced. Still under further observation and medication as adviced by company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn on back of plam on right hand.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Hot well top cover to be kept in closed position and steam pressure to be regulated.
Corrective Action: Hot well top cover to be kept in closed position and steam pressure to be regulated.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: This accident took place when he was taking photoraphs of the painting area on top of no.4 hatch cover.He was at the forward end part of the hatch cover ,in order to cover all the newly painted area in the camera he was taking more steps backward but it was too  when he realise that he is already at the extreme end and he lost his balance and fall on his lower back in cross deck area.His right knee and lower thigh is also hurt,all internal wounds with minor swelling but severe pain. IncidentDetails: While transferring empty bottle to land ashore 3 crew member were trying to shift the empty bottle from C Exterior deck where Bottles are secured to shift it to B deck where Shore crane was reaching to carry away empty bottles.       While lowering lashing taken to secure bottle slips and empty bottle fall freely from B deck To Upper deck were Crew member were busy in shifting store.            Empty bottle fell on CE , seriously injuring him
IncidentEventDetails: 01/11/15 : 00:36 -   Vessel  was Stbd  Side alongside at Vopak Europort                                     West  2  berth.                             11:30  - Barge came alongside with vessel store/Spares.                   11:35 – Vessel started receiving stores and spares using Barge crane                                as the ship’s crane was not operational .    Stores were being received at the poop deck port side with the crane controller holding his remote control on ship’s upper deck.  1 empty Acetylene bottle and 3 Oxygen bottles were to be lowered to the barge in return of the filled bottles ,which were received and kept on deck .  The barge crane could not reach the C-Deck( stowage area of the empty bottles)  The basket used by the barge could be placed only to a height of B-deck .    3- Ship’s crew started  transferring the  bottles one by one through the Australian ladder  from C deck leading to B-deck where the shore basket was placed using barge crane for lifting the bottles .    11:45 -For lowering the bottle, ship’s crew used 20mm polypropylene rope for              securing the empty bottle and was slided down the ladder in a controlled             manner to reach the basket safely.             The Oxygen bottles were lowered safely near the basket  .   However when the Acetylene  bottle was being lowered, at    11:50 - lashing taken to secure the bottle slipped and empty bottle fell freely from               the ladder  to Upper deck bouncing/passing through the handrail on the               B deck  and  landed on Upper deck where Crew  was  busy in shifting               stores.              This empty bottle hit CE  , injured his left hand and left foot.    11:51 -Medical emergency was announced , terminal informed for shore medical              assistance.      11:51 ~12:15 - Ship's crew shifted injured CE from Site of Accident to                          Jetty   12:15  - Shore Assistance received,Shore  Medical emergency team arrived , CE               taken to hospital.   12:40 – Port Police on board for investigation/enquiry  12:55 – Statements about the incidence were taken from Ship staff as witness.  13:15 – Port Police disembarked.
ImmediateAction: Receiving of stores and spares temperorily ceased.             Medical emergency was announced , terminal informed for shore medical              Assistance.            Injured personnel was transferred to hospital .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Please refer doctor’s report as attached
DirectCause: Direct cause: Improper securing and handling of heavy weights objects .                        No formal  Risk Assesment  done and improper monitoring and                         supervision while  conducting  the  job with residual risk level being no                        less  than medium(M).                        Lack of experience/skill ,improper position for task.    Basic Underlying cause:     Inoperational  ship’s provision crane     Ships provision crane was supposed to  be used  to handle /lower heavy metal   gas bottles instead of manual lifting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Ship’s Provision crane will be repaired with the spares received at Rotterdam.  2.Crew training and critical work performance observations through RA and      monitoring .  3.Discuss  and analysis  of the lessons learnt .
Corrective Action: 1.Ship's Provision crane will be repaired with the spares received at Rotterdam. 2.Crew training and critical work performance observations through RA and     monitoring . 3.Discuss  and analysis  of the lessons learnt .
ClosingNote: JOb closed

Corrective Action: Instructed all crew to not to carry out any task in haste especially during stations.

IncidentDetails: Junior Engineer suffered cut to his Ring finger of right hand while pushing the crane block. There was a cut of about 2 cms and laceration on the nai base.l
IncidentEventDetails: A cut drum which was loaded with sludge bags was lifted using E/R crane from the bottom platform.  Meanwhile when the drum reached M/E cyl. head platform the drum was being pulled by wiper so that it is clear of the platform and railings. Junior Engineer meanwhile was concentrating that the hook block of crane#2 should not touch the railings and he tried to push the crane hook so that it is clear of the platform. While pushing the block got twisted and his right hand slipped and came in between sheave and wire rope. During this his glove got in between pulley and wire rope and he pulled his finger out. After removing gloves he found his ring finger got a cut and the base of nail  came out.
ImmediateAction: Onboard first aid given and taken to doctor ashore.
DirectCause: Junior Engineer was using his hand to push the block which is likely to twist on the swivel. He did not anticipate the same.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Risk assessment to also include measures to be taken when blocks need to be cleared from obstructions. Hazards involved while handling moving objects such as these to be briefed to the working staff.
Corrective Action: Risk assessment to also include measures to be taken when blocks need to be cleared from obstructions. Hazards involved while handling moving objects such as these to be briefed to the working staff.
LessionLearnt: Effective Tool box meeting not carried out / Junior Engineer was using his hand to push the block and twist it on the swivel, but finger got caught as a result. / Junior Engineer not foreseeing the possible dangers involved in the act. / The moving part was too close to the finger, and the danger of Finger getting stuck was not anticipated.
ReviewComments: Junior Engineer was using his hand to push the block and twist it on the swivel, but finger got caught as a result. He did not anticipate the same. / Ineffective / Inadequate Supervision. Second Engineer was involved in the job, but did not identify the unsafe act. Junior Engineer was not supervised effectively. / Ineffective Tool box meeting. Tool box meeting did not identify the experience of person and effective supervision.
ClosingNote: Information Circulated in Ship Palm

IncidentDetails: After receiving provision on board, it was noticed that ship chandler had supplied 300Kgs of potatoes instead of ordered 30kgs.    Around 1930 hrs, ships crew was told to lower 10 bags back to the boat. Each bag weighing about 25 kg   It was decided to muster all bags close to mid ship and lower by ships crane into the boat.  But the ship chandler was making hurry and advised OS to lower the Bags from aft only into the boat as it would had taken time to shift the bags to mid ship. OS insisted to lower by midship crane only but ship chandler refused and started shifting bags by himself. OS informed regarding same to Bosun. Bosun also insisted to lower the bags by midship crane to both ship chandler and service boat but both refused. Ship chandler by himself started lowering the bags to boat by using ships rope. He was immediately stopped by ship crew and ship crew started lowering the bags. One bag at time was being lowered, by messenger line (there were two lines tied to each other) while lowering the the 5th Bag the joint between the messenger line slipped and the bag fell from a height of about 5 mtr into the boat, just sliding down on one side of one of the worker in the boat, it was not very apparent that the worker has been badly hurt.   The ship chandler then left with his workers     At about 00:30 hrs the same shipchandler came and reported to the master about the injury to his worker and told that his worker has broken 2 ribs
IncidentEventDetails: 16 Dec 2015,1930 hrs :  ships crew was told to lower 10 bags back to the boat. Each bag weighing about 25 kg   16 Dec 2015,1930 hrs :It was decided to muster all bags close to mid ship and lower by ships crane into the boat.  16 Dec 2015,1936 hrs :But the ship chandler was making hurry and advised OS to lower the Bags from aft only into the boat as it would had taken time to shift the bags to mid ship.   16 Dec 2015,1938 hrs :OS insisted to lower by midship crane only but ship chandler refused and started shifting bags by himself.  16 Dec 2015,1938 hrs :OS informed regarding same to Bosun.  16 Dec 2015,1945 hrs :Bosun also insisted to lower the bags by midship crane to both ship chandler and service boat but both refused.  16 Dec 2015,1948 hrs :Ship chandler by himself started lowering the bags to boat by using ships rope.He was immediately stopped by ship crew and ship crew started lowering the bags.One bag at time was being lowered, by messenger line (there were two lines tied to each other)   16 Dec 2015,1957 hrs :while lowering the the 5th Bag the joint between the messenger line slipped and the bag fell from a height of about 5 mtr into the boat, just sliding down on one side of one of the worker in the boat, it was not very apparent that the worker hs been badly hurt.   The ship chandler then left with his workers     17 Dec 2015, 00:30 hrs the same shipchandler came and reported to the master about the injury to his worker and told that his worker has broken 2 ribs and started asking for compensation money, if not paid he will inform harbour master of the incident. Master Refused to pay any compensation amount at this time.     Master informed the office about the incident.
ImmediateAction: Office informed about this incident
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Shore personnal  has broken 2 ribs.
DirectCause: 1. Rope used for lowering the bag not continous and rope condition not inspected before use.  2. Duty Officer  not informed regarding unsafe pratice followed by shore personnal.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Picking up or lowering of stores/provision to be done in supervision of officer incharge and proper equipment used and proper procedured to be followed  2 .Equipment/Ropes/Slings to be used should be throughly inspected by officer incharge.  3. To ensure shore personnal are not handling ships equipment/ropes/slings.  4. Incase shore personnal following any unsafe pratice or insisting ship crew to follow unsafe practice,shore personnal to be stopped immdieatly and senior officer to be informed.
Corrective Action: 1. Picking up or lowering of stores/provision to be done in supervision of officer incharge and proper equipment used and proper procedured to be followed 2 .Equipment/Ropes/Slings to be used should be throughly inspected by officer incharge. 3. To ensure shore personnal are not handling ships equipment/ropes/slings. 4. Incase shore personnal following any unsafe pratice or insisting ship crew to follow unsafe practice,shore personnal to be stopped immdieatly and senior officer to be informed.
ReviewComments: All jobs done on board shall be discussed with all persons involved & an onsite tool box meeting to be held to discuss the operative  agenda of the job. All persons to be well briefed & any job carried on board shall be discussed with the Duty Officer of the intentions. Any deviation to the job planned shall be bought to the immediate notice of duty officer for further review. Ropes used for lowering stores shall be avoided as far as possible , wherever possible , a net sling can be used to avoid chances of slips & falls. Only experienced hands to handle such jobs & trainees shall be allocated to carry out such jobs.
ClosingNote: Report closed

IncidentDetails: While berthed alongside at Pointe Noire, Congo on 11 September 2015 / 0945 LT 3rd Officer was injured during  cargo operations due to a container being lowered on to him accidentally. He was partially trapped underneath the  container. He escaped being completely crushed due to the response of the stevedores and ship’s staff and was  given medical assistance at a nearby hospital and eventually flown to his country (India) for further review.  Consequences / Impact  * Severe injury to the third officer, which could have been fatal. The treatment and healing of this injury could  take at least six months.  * Vessel delayed by over 24 hours to sail from port. Possible off-hire claim.  *P&I claim including huge cost of air ambulance to transport 3rd Officer from West Africa to India and  Treatment cost / sick wages. Impacts the Ship owner’s P&I claim record
IncidentEventDetails: 3rd Officer walked into the cargo slot from the passage way. Due to the presence of the beam and cell guide pedestal he did not see the container being lowered at slot 380482.At the time of incident, slots 381082, 380882 & 380682 were occupied by containers.His entry into the container area coincided with the lowering of the container. The container was lowered on to the 3rd Officer, hit his helmet that shielded his head and he fell down. He was trapped under the container while the container continued to be lowered till about 2 feet from the ground. T
ImmediateAction: The supervisor contacted the gantry operator quickly over walkie-talkie who immediately stopped the operation.  The container was hoisted up quickly and 3rd officer shifted by crew slightly aft and clear from the cargo area.  Master informed vessel’s marine superintendent and local agent.Marine superintendent immediately arranged for an ambulance through local agents.Crisis response team was activated in the office and lines of communication established. The ambulance arrived around 1000LT and 3rd officer disembarked on a stretcher at 1015LT.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: *Fleet alert to be sent to all container vessels in the fleet sharing the incident details and advising vessels to establish areas that may be hazardous during cargo operations on deck. Share incident during pre-joining briefing of crew on container vessels.  *Rig safety railings aft of bay 38 on deck to prevent inadvertent straying into cargo area. ‘Hazard zone’ notices / posters to be posted in the vicinity to alert crew. Notices to advise - stop cargo operations if required to venture into cargo areas for reasons such as inspections etc.  *Check / supply sufficient high visibility vests on board container vessels with reflective tapes, to be used during cargo operations by officers / crew working on deck. High visibility vests to also be used by all others venturing on deck during cargo operations for tasks not related to cargo operations.  *Carry out a safety campaign to establish hazard zones on container vessels in the fleet, on deck in the vicinity of cargo holds / hatch covers / cross decks / walkways etc., to carefully analyse hazards to personnel and establish suitable measures to mitigate such hazards.  *Internal audits to include hazard identification exercise with respect to personnel during cargo operations on container vessels. Effectiveness of safety officer’s inspection and effectiveness of LARP reporting, particularly with respect to hazard zones during cargo operations, to be verified / reviewed during internal audits.  *Safety officer’s course arranged by the company to include detailed guidance on how to conduct safety inspections on board.  *Internal audit course arranged by company for superintendents to address hazard identification and potential areas of hazards during cargo operations on container vessels. Superintendents with Internal audit courses conducted externally to be briefed suitably
Corrective Action: *Fleet alert to be sent to all container vessels in the fleet sharing the incident details and advising vessels to establish areas that may be hazardous during cargo operations on deck. Share incident during pre-joining briefing of crew on container vessels.  *Rig safety railings aft of bay 38 on deck to prevent inadvertent straying into cargo area. ‘Hazard zone' notices / posters to be posted in the vicinity to alert crew. Notices to advise - stop cargo operations if required to venture into cargo areas for reasons such as inspections etc.  *Check / supply sufficient high visibility vests on board container vessels with reflective tapes, to be used during cargo operations by officers / crew working on deck. High visibility vests to also be used by all others venturing on deck during cargo operations for tasks not related to cargo operations.  *Carry out a safety campaign to establish hazard zones on container vessels in the fleet, on deck in the vicinity of cargo holds / hatch covers / cross decks / walkways etc., to carefully analyse hazards to personnel and establish suitable measures to mitigate such hazards.  *Internal audits to include hazard identification exercise with respect to personnel during cargo operations on container vessels. Effectiveness of safety officer's inspection and effectiveness of LARP reporting, particularly with respect to hazard zones during cargo operations, to be verified / reviewed during internal audits.  *Safety officer's course arranged by the company to include detailed guidance on how to conduct safety inspections on board.  *Internal audit course arranged by company for superintendents to address hazard identification and potential areas of hazards during cargo operations on container vessels. Superintendents with Internal audit courses conducted externally to be briefed suitably
LessionLearnt: Importance of situational awareness
ClosingNote: A Vessel’s deck is essentially a hazardous location wherein a heightened state of awareness is required. Incidents can be avoided by being aware at all times, knowing the vessel conditions and understanding the inherent dangers that are posed by cargo or any other operations. All personnel involved in the operation on board the vessel must understand that safe working procedures should not be compromised due to pressing operational needs.   On-board a vessel, there should be sufficient safe guards to ensure that no single unsafe act results in an accident. To avoid a situation where due to lack of adequate safeguards the vessel ends up having an accident due to one unsafe act, there must be a robust inspection regime by both the ship staff and visiting shore staff. Whilst the company may have established safe guards and procedures, the implementation of these procedures must be reviewed time to time to ensure that they are implemented and serve the objectives effectively. Notwithstanding the unsafe act of the third officer, the accident may not have occurred had there been a barrier railing and/or effective notices posted. To ensure sufficient safeguards are present on-board vessels the Company embarked on a project to ensure that all vessels in the fleet effectively identify potential hazardous areas and effective barriers ( material and procedural)  are put up to eliminate such re-occurrence of incidents

IncidentDetails: FITTER WAS CARRYING OUT WELDING JOB IN THE WORKSHOP.WHEN HE WAS TRYING TO GET UP TO CHANGE POSITION HIS HEAD CAME IN CONTACT WITH THE PANEL BOARD IN THE WORKSHOP.  HE HAD A MINOR CUT ON HIS SCALP AND SLIGHT BLEEDING.
IncidentEventDetails: FITTER WAS CARRYING OUT WELDING JOB IN THE WORKSHOP. HE WAS IN SQUATTING POSITION AND WELDING . THE JOB WAS ALIGNED IN POSITION . HE GOT UP TO CHANGE POSITION TO WELD AT THE OPPOSITE SIDE . HIS HEAD CAME IN CONTACT WITH THE SHARP CORNER OF THE PANEL BOARD . HE HAD A MINOR CUT AND SLIGHT BLEEDING
ImmediateAction: THE WORK WAS STOPPED AND FITTER WAS TAKEN TO HOSPITAL AND FIRST AID WAS GIVEN TO THE FITTER
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MINOR CUT ON T SCALP AND SLIGHT BLEEDING
DirectCause: THE CREW MEMBER WAS WEARING THE FACE SHIELD FOR WELDING AND CARRYING OUT THE WELDING JOB. HE WAS NOT  ABLE TO WEAR THE HELMET . AS HE TRIED TO STAND UP TO CHANGE POSITION HIS HEAD CAME IN CONTACT WITH THE SHARP CORNER OF THE PANEL BOARD AND CAUSED THE INJURY.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CREW MEMBERS ADVISED  TO WEAR HELMET AT ALL TIMES . SUCH SHARP CORNERS WILL BE PROVIDED WITH SOFT PADDING.
Corrective Action: CREW MEMBERS ADVISED  TO WEAR HELMET AT ALL TIMES . SUCH SHARP CORNERS WILL BE PROVIDED WITH SOFT PADDING.
LessionLearnt: Importance Of Proper PPE
ClosingNote: The incident highlights the need of Proper PPE, especially where there are overhead obstructions or space is constrained. Safety officer/Senior Staff must ensure that all on board follow proper PPE procedures.

IncidentDetails: Trainee Wiper Imran fell from the ladder approx. 1m height while cleaning the E/R pipelines and landed on his back. He complained pain over the lower back, left hip area.

IncidentDetails: While berthed alongside at Tamatave, Madagascar on 19 December 2015 / 1320 LT Chief Officer, who was atop and adjusting the holding chains for the electrical cable of grab bucket, slipped and fell from the grab on to the deck. The grab was laid in open position on deck. He sustained fall injury resulting in pain and numbness of right leg / foot.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed at Tamatave, Madagascar on 19 December 2015 / 1130LT for discharging cargo of limestone in bulk. The cargo discharge required usage of ship’s cranes with grabs. Cargo hold No.5 was opened and crane No.4 was ready to discharge at 1230 LT. Discharging commenced at Cargo Hold no.5 soon after with crane no.4. At around 1245 LT Crane No.4 grab cable had detached from the grab, due to wrong adjustment of the grab cable grip chain causing entire load acting on the connector instead of the cable sheathing which would have avoided the damage. Ship’s staff commenced attending to the problem. Meanwhile No.3 crane was ready for discharging and discharging commenced at 1300 LT. Chief Officer came to understand Crane No.3 might also have a similar problem as Crane No.4 and wished to inspect the grab cable and the grip chains. Crane No.3 grab was lowered on the deck and laid in an open position. At around 1320 LT Chief Officer, while inspecting the adjustment on the grab cable grip chains, slipped and fell down on to the deck through a height of about 1.5 m possibly with his back taking the impact.
ImmediateAction: Master and other ship’s staff, who were witness to the incident was immediately assisted Chief Officer. Vessel being in port Master immediately contacted office to inform and arranged for the Chief Officer to be sent to a medical facility shore through the local agents on 19 December 2015 / 1440 LT. Owners, P&I, charterers, Chief Mate’s family and all other concerned parties were notified. No injuries were reported to any other staff in the vicinity.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Consequences / Impact ? Injury to right leg / foot that could have been serious. Healing of this injury could take 10 days. ? Possible back injury that could have been of a serious nature. ? Possible repatriation resulting in vessel sailing out under dispensation without Chief Officer. ? P&I claim including repatriation costs, treatment costs / sick wages. Impacts the Ship owner’s P&I claim record.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper procedure shall be made for correct rigging of the grab to the crane including the electrical cable and wires with drawings and pictures for each vessel. A training session shall be conducted for all deck crew and crew involved in rigging of cargo gear on these procedures as a part of their onboard familiarization. Grab electrical cable grip chain position which gives sufficient loop / slack on the cable to be marked for all cranes of bulk vessels equipped with cranes and grabs to avoid damage of these cables and photographs sent to office. Check / supply short ladder to be used specially for grab maintenance. Vessels shall make proper arrangement to rig the ladder properly and safely when climbing atop grabs, demonstrate to crew involved in the rigging and maintenance of grabs and carry out a training session There is an ambiguity in the procedure for Working at Height in section 2.6 of HSM. A DCR will be raised to provide clear guidelines for when the PTW for working aloft must be used. It is suggested that Work permit for working aloft be issued :- a) When the height is over 1.5 meters. b) When the height is less than 1.5 meters, if there is a risk of Injury if the person falls in the surroundings. When working at heights less than 1.5 meters, the crew member must assess the risk of falling and take adequate precautions to avoid a fall. Eg: Step ladder, firm stool, proper shoes, good lighting in work area and other good seamanship precautions

IncidentDetails: While berthed alongside at Baoshan, China on 13 December 2015 / 0900 LT 2nd Engineer suffered burn injuries while tightening the bolts of exhaust valve actuator pipe of no.4 unit adjacent to the fuel oil pump. He was resting his left arm on the hot upper part of the fuel pump while tightening the bolts causing severe blister and festering burn to his arm.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel berthed at Baogang raw material pier no.2, Baoshan on 13 December 2015 / 1518LT. PSC was expected to board on 14 December 2015. Engine room staff was involved in checks and preparation in the engine room for a clean PSC inspection report. Toolbox meeting was conducted at on 13 December 0800LTto discuss the work to be carried out and the safety guidelines to be observed. Engine room staff went to work after the meeting. Based on the debrief interview with 2nd Engineer after arrival Manila - 2nd Engineer was assigned to fix leakage from exhaust valve actuator pipe of No.4 unit in the vicinity of fuel oil pump. He was resting his left arm on top of the fuel oil pump surface which was hot. At around 0900LT upon completion of his task, he realized that his left hand was hurting. Scrutiny revealed he had blisters in the area where he was resting his hand on the fuel oil pump filled with fluid. Later the blister was punctured to reveal 2nd degree burns (burn classification table appended) with grooving / scarring on the skin where the arm was in hard contact with the fuel oil pump surface. The inlet temperature of the FO was 130 deg C with the pump external surface being at 50 deg C. Contact with hot pump surface caused the underside of his left arm to sustain severe burns.
ImmediateAction: Master was immediately informed. Second officer was called and first aid was administered to the 2nd Engineer. Burn area was administered with fresh water, cleaned and Neomycin & Bacitracin cream applied. Master informed vessel’s marine superintendent and local agent. Marine superintendent immediately forwarded 2nd Engineer’s condition to the company doctor for his advice and also asked Master to arrange for a visit to doctor since vessel was in port.  Owners, P&I, charterers, 2nd Engineer’s family and all other concerned parties were notified. No injuries were reported to any other ER staff.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2nd Engineer in his bid to tighten the bolts of leaking pipe of unit 4 leant heavily on his left arm on the hot surface of fuel pump for support, not realizing the skin of his arm is blistering and sustained 3rd degree burn injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Risk assessment when working in difficult positions in the vicinity of hot surfaces to be sent to office. This RA will be reviewed by CMS.  .Check possibility of insulation cover that can be customized and placed over fuel pump top surface permanently on ships with similar design and access issues.  Assessment of hot surfaces in the Engine Room using infrared thermometers, where a possibility of burn injury exists will be carried out by ships to identify possibility of permanent insulation of such areas  Chief Engineer to personally assess areas with difficult work position and / or in the vicinity of hot equipment / machinery and supervise if necessary.
Corrective Action: Risk assessment when working in difficult positions in the vicinity of hot surfaces to be sent to office. This RA will be reviewed by CMS. .Check possibility of insulation cover that can be customized and placed over fuel pump top surface permanently on ships with similar design and access issues. Assessment of hot surfaces in the Engine Room using infrared thermometers, where a possibility of burn injury exists will be carried out by ships to identify possibility of permanent insulation of such areas Chief Engineer to personally assess areas with difficult work position and / or in the vicinity of hot equipment / machinery and supervise if necessary.
ClosingNote: Vessel to continue check/assess work space while working in difficult position/hot surfaces
Other Part: Severe burn injury ( 2nd degree) to 2nd Engineer's left arm. Healing of this injury could take 20 days.

IncidentDetails: Scalding of Left foot ankle of 2nd Engineer because of falling  hot water from the EGB steam outlet pipe
IncidentEventDetails: Time-line and event Narration:  21st February  0400 Hrs LT - EOSP  0506 - Vessel Anchored at Melaka    22nd February  0824 - Anchor Aweigh  1018 - All Fast at berth for loading  1548 - commenced loading operation    23rd February  1012 Hrs LT - Completed Loading  1530 Hrs LT - Vessel unberthed  1624 Hrs LT - Vessel Anchored at Melaka Anchorage    24th February 2016 at Melaka Anchorage  0815 Hrs LT - EGB isolated for routine cleaning job as part of PMS.  1500 Hrs LT - Water washing job was completed  1700 Hrs LT - After completion of water wash, EGB was slowly warmed up and Boiler Water Circulating Pump started.   1805 Hrs LT - It was noticed by 4/E that a flange gasket of EGB return line to Aux Boiler was leaking.   1815 Hrs LT - Since vessel was on short notice to sail out from anchorage early next day it was decided to change the gasket of leaking flange immediately. Risk Assessment (RA) / Cold Work Permit (PTW) was prepared to carry out the said job.  1815 Hrs LT - EGB system Isolated and commenced cooling down of the line and de-pressurising the system.  1905 Hrs LT - Tool Box meeting was carried out at work place. The RA / PTW was discussed in tool box meeting. Members attending the tool - box meeting were C/E, 2/E, 3/E, MM1, MM2, and Fitter.  1915 Hrs LT - Started loosening the flanges to drain some water from the line. Commenced the job for renewal of Gasket.  1950 Hrs LT - Once the 2/E confirmed that the entire line was drained, the short Elbow section was removed, dismantling at the flanges, Elbow section taken to workshop for surface cleaning and preparation of gasket.     2010 Hrs LT - 2/E returned to work site to check surface condition of flanges.   While the inspection was being carried out,  a sudden slight rolling of vessel caused  a small amount of hot water remaining inside the pipe-line to pour out on to the left leg of Second Engineer causing scalding.
ImmediateAction: The right foot of second engineer was immersed in Cold water bucket & necessary first aid was administered. Company doctor advise was taken.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Scalding of left foot ankle
DirectCause: 1. Hastiness in executing the work- the crew took up the work due to the possibility that the vessel may have to sail our from the anchorage early next day. However, it was noted that the E/R team had made all the proper analysis related to the job - carried out RA, Permit to work and conducted tool box meeting with the team assigned to carry out the job. There was also adequate supervision at hand since the senior Engineers were present at the site (C/E and 2/E)   2. PPE (Apron) used was not covering the full length of the body.  3. Lowering of guard - Since there was no sign of water flowing at the open flanges after the removal of the elbow section, the crew may have assumed that the horizontal length of pipe was free of any water. However, the crew may have discounted the possibility of hot water being present in the vertical section of the pipe leading to the boiler and that this water can spill out of the open flange section of the horizontal pipe due to the slightest amount of vessel movement caused by rolling. The 2E went to the area of the work to inspect the flange face without wearing the full length apron (since he was not part of the team executing the work of dismantling of the elbow he was not wearing the full length apron)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Adequate cooling time to be given while working on equipment / system involving steam and high temperature fluids;  2) Proper PPE to be used to protect whole body part against any inadvertent splash of hot fluid (full length apron).  3) Crew to be trained/ briefed to always alert to the possibility of existing danger when working on High Pressure / Steam Lines / or on any other work which requires a Permit. All vessels to be reminded about the need to maintain  alertness throughout the entire job process.
Corrective Action: More effective supervision is required while opening steam lines. Adequate cooling time to be given while working on equipments involving steam and high temerature fluids.
LessionLearnt: To ensure proper Staging at the site of work to be erected; Proper PPE to be donned
ClosingNote: Follow up interview was conducted with onboard C/E and Additional C/E and the corrective action measures were verified

IncidentDetails: While lowering the anchor under power, cutch slipped and anchor cable ran out. Also the clutch lever hit the AB Badage Ali, injuring his groin.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was approaching berth when anchor was being lowered to water level. The clutch got disengaged and cable ran out freely. Pin on the lever of clutch bent and lever hit Able Seaman on his groin.
ImmediateAction: Bridge and pilot informed. Vessel stopped. Deformed securing pin was removed. The clutch was engaged and anchor hoisted up. Injured AB shifted to accomodation. Second officer adminstered first aid. AB sent for medical assitance ashore. Awaiting report.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While lowering anchor upto water level during transit under pilotage, the clutch slipped and was disengaged. This resulted in the cable running out freely and also the clutch handle hitting the bosun in the groin.
DirectCause: Clutch teeth clearance has reduced due to wear and tear. Therefore not engaged fully and slipped when under load.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To build up material on clutch and reduce play. This requires workshop assitance. Until that time, handle to be tied up strongly.
Corrective Action: To build up material on clutch and reduce play. This requires workshop assitance. Until that time, handle to be tied up strongly.
LessionLearnt: Mooring /Anchoring equipment must be closely inspected prior use. Any defects must be proactively addressed.

ClosingNote: Review closed.

IncidentDetails: In Morning from his cabin 3Off reported bridge that he is Feeling difficulty in breathing.  He was found sweating profusely,Shallow/fast breathing & semi conscious state.Eyes were open and not blinking.
IncidentEventDetails: V/l was loading Very High H2S cargo( Arab Extra Light)  3Off was present on deck & at the berth during completion of cargo and later for ullaging/Manifold disconnection upon completion of loading.
DirectCause: Exposure to H2S during completion of loading while checking drafts, Ullaging & Hose disconnection.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: - Training to be given to ship staff as per Cargo Operation tanker manual Ch 2.4 & Ch 13.  - Requisition to be raised for Powered air purifying respirators. Which can be used during low concentration of H2S gas.
Corrective Action: - Training to be given to ship staff as per Cargo Operation tanker manual Ch 2.4 & Ch 13. - Requisition to be raised for Powered air purifying respirators. Which can be used during low concentration of H2S gas.
LessionLearnt: 01. SCBA to be donned while connecting/disconnecting Manifold 02. Personal Gas meters to be worn by all persons working on deck while carrying / loading/discharging high H2S crude 03. EEBD to be placed at strategic locations on deck facilitating the crew members to don & escape from the hazardous zone if in case H2S gas is detected on deck 04. Crew members to be well trained on taking adequate precautions for H2S cargoes 05. Cease loading operations if in case there is dead wind causing NO DISPERSION of cargo vapors to atmosphere & resume loading once wx conditions become conducive 06. Restrict the number of persons entering Pump room to the number of EEBD available in the Pump room,if addl crew member needs to enter Pump Room crew members to carry Addl EEBD 07. Additional fully gas tight UTI & Sampler to be supplied facilitating quicker sampling & gauging upon completion
ReviewComments: Third Officer Agnal was called to office after his sign off from the vessel. Dr. Balaji gave Medical fitness certificate for joining back the vessel It was concluded after the investigation that all vessels berthed at Ras Tanura were loading high H2S crude , Vessel's berthed ahead & astern of Sri Vishnu were loading high H2S crude. During the final stages of loading , Third Officer was made to standby at aft draft marks on the jetty to visually monitor the drafts, the high H2S alarm activated on the personal gas meter and Third Officer immediately vacated the location & proceeded to midship,same communicated to Ch Offr on W/T.During the final sampling & gauging , Third Officer was accompanying the Pump man along with the Cargo Surveyor. During the manifold disconnection , again the high H2S alarm was sounded on the AB's Gas meter & all persons vacated deck & proceeded to safe area, upon dispersal of H2S from deck the job was resumed. This intermittent H2S exposure to Third Officer has resulted in Nausea and difficulty in breathing, Third Officer has advised that he has no previous history of breathing troubles
ClosingNote: Incident report closed

IncidentDetails: Main engines unit No.5 & unit No.10 Jacket cooling water pipes O-Ring were due for renewal. While removing No.10 unit's Port Aft pipe 4th Engg mistakenly opened No.11 unit Port Fwd pipe, which was pressurised with hot Jacket water because of which 4th engg got burn injury on his body. Leading to 1st Degree burn totalling to Approx 40%.
IncidentEventDetails: At Nhava Sheva port main engines No.5 & No.10 units' Jacket cooling water pipes O-Rings were due for renewal. Persons involved in the job were 2nd, 3rd & 4th Engineer. 2nd Engg isolated the No.5 unit by closing the inlet & outlet valve & drained the water. For No.5 unit two pipes(Stbd Aft & Port Aft)pipes were taken out by 4th engineer & (Stbd fwd & Port fwd) pipes were removed by 3rd Engg. After removing, all pipes were brought to work shop for cleaning & changing the O-Rings. After replacement of O-rings all pipes were taken back to respective locations for re-assembling. Meanwhile the re-assembling were carrying out for unit No.5, 2nd engg isolated the Unit No.10 by closing the inlet & outlet valve & drained the water.    After fitting the pipes of unit No.5, 2nd Engg & 4th Engg went for Unit No.10, Stbd Fwd pipe was taken out by 2nd Engg & Stbd Aft pipe was taken out by 4th engg. 2nd engg took the Stbd pipe & took to the work shop. Meanwhile 3rd engg finished with his work on No.5 Unit & came to No.10 unit for Port side fwd pipe removal.    After finishing No.10 unit Stbd Aft pipe 4th Engg went for removing No.10 Port Aft pipe, since that was the only pipe remaining.    But instead of removing No.10 unit Port Aft pipe, mistakenly he opened No.11 Unit Port side fwd pipe which is close to No.10 Unit port aft pipe.    As No.11 Unit was fully pressurised with hot jacket cooling water(Approx temp 70 Deg Celcius), hot water sprayed on his body with pressure. He was trapped in the space where he could not come out very quickly.    Due to the screaming noise heard, 2nd engg rushed to Unit No.11 & closed inlet & outlet valve of jacket cooling water of No.11 unit. Meanwhile 4th engg came out of the space where he was trapped.
ImmediateAction: 4th Engg's safety helmet, safety gogles, safety shoes & coverall were removed & taken to ship's hospital immediately. 2nd Officer & Ch.Off gave him shower with room temperature water & given first aid according to Ship's medical guide. Master immediately contacted the local agent and informed that 4th Eng has to be taken to the hospital for further treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury: Due to main engine hot jacket water (approx 70 deg. C) spray, upper torso, left hand, right hand, private parts, left leg & right leg suffered first degree burn about 41% & peeledl off of his skin.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis as per attached Investigation report
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Cause Analysis as per attached Investigation report
Corrective Action: Firstly prior carrying out the jobs the pipes should have been clearly marked so as to avoid any confusion. Also while removing the bolts & nuts of the pipe initially bolt should be slakened slightly & checked for any pressurised water leakage before removing it completely.The unit where the pipes were supposed to be removed should have been clearly marked / cordoned with tiger rope or highly visible tape on the outside.
LessionLearnt: As per attached Investigation report
ReviewComments: As per attached Investigation report
ClosingNote: investigation closed

IncidentDetails: While handling a  200 Ltr. empty drum for boiler cleaning, sharp edge of the drum cut the motor man's middle and ring finger of right hand.  The middle finger cut was approx 10 mm in length and the cut on ring finger was superficial.
IncidentEventDetails: 13.30 Hrs: 3 rd Engineer and Motor man were preparing the 200 Lit. drum for boiler cleaning.  13:45 Hrs: They started cutting the top cover by chisel + hammering, to collect soot.  14:15 Hrs: Top cover was completly cut and removed from the drum.  14:25 Hrs: They started collecting Remaining oil from the drum to 20 lit. bucket, by tilting the drum  14:30 Hrs: While tilting the drum sharp edge of the drum cut the motor man's middle and ring finger.
ImmediateAction: Incident immediately Reported to 2nd Officer and First aid given.   Pressure applied on the cut to stop bleeding + Ice pack.  Area cleaned with anti septic liquid and cut covered with medical bandage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DEEP CUT IN MIDDLE FINGER AND MINOR CUT IN FOURTH FINGER
DirectCause: The 200 Ltr drum was cut with a scraper and hammer and had sharp edges on the cut end. Same should have been grinded off to even out. Concerned personnel was only wearing a cotton gloves and not leather working gloves which might have prevented the injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew meeting convened. Incident discussed and cause analysis + Loass causation model of said incident discussed. COSWP safe handling procedures and importance of wearing proper PPE discussed. Senior management to carry out proper RA and Tool box meeting prior commencement of jobs, no matter how mundane and insignificant they may appear.
Corrective Action: All crew meeting convened. Incident discussed and cause analysis + Loass causation model of said incident discussed. COSWP safe handling procedures and importance of wearing proper PPE discussed. Senior management to carry out proper RA and Tool box meeting prior commencement of jobs, no matter how mundane and insignificant they may appear.
LessionLearnt: Importance of PPE / effective tool box meetings
ReviewComments: Agree with Master Cause Analysis & CA
ClosingNote: A proper tool box meeting will prevent such incidents

IncidentDetails: Pump man got deep cut on his chin while tightening the compound  guage on PV stack of 6P COT
IncidentEventDetails: During the discharge operation of soyabean oil at CALLAO , pumpman fixed a compund guage over the 6P PV stack to the check the vacum inside the tank , and while removing the compound guage after checking the tank pressure, since the guage assembly was tight  pumpman  leaned forward towards the guage and applied more force and the assmbly opened with a sudden moment  and came in contact with pumpman chin which caused a deep cut
ImmediateAction: First aid given , force applied at the deep cut area to stop the bleeding by bandage and informed agent to arrange for medical treatment ASAP
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 22nd April 2016 around 1615 hrs lt, while discharging soyabean oil at Callao, compound gauge was fitted on PV stalk of  COT 6 port to check vacuum reading inside the tank.   The assembly connection of compound gauge and coupling to PV stalk is “T” shaped as shown in picture below. One side is connected pressure gauge and other side compound gauge .  While removing the connection assembly with pipe wrench, the pumpman had to lean forward to apply optimum force to rotate the anticlockwise.  The assembly opened with sudden movement, which hit the pumpman on the right part of chin, causing a deep cut.  Immediately first aid was given by application of anti-septic solution and pressure bandage to stop bleeding. Agent was contacted and pumpman was sent for medical attention ashore.
DirectCause: The crewmember had to lean forward for opening the"T" shaped gauge assembly.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: instead of "T" shaped gauge assembly ,straight pipe and gauge assembly to be used. fabrication of such connection and requistion to be raised if required
Corrective Action: instead of "T" shaped gauge assembly ,straight pipe and gauge assembly to be used. fabrication of such connection and requistion to be raised if required
LessionLearnt: Incorrect position for a task can very easily result in an injury ranging from Minor to Severe. Toolbox meetings to address the hazards that may be encountered merely by being in the wrong position for an otherwise completely innocuous job. An enhanced safety culture among all crew will encourage the pointing out of incorrect practices and improve the stop work culture in matters of safety; and hazard identification and adressal
ReviewComments: Better knowledge of good posture & positioning when doing work would avoid recurrence of such accidents. Better equipment with ease of use would also serve to reduce such incidents.
ClosingNote: New Compound Gauge was supplied to the vessel and same has replaced the existing arrangement, the design of which was partly to blame for the incident.

IncidentDetails: Whilst doing control tests on bridge prior departure, 3rd Officer found one of the BRidge ceiling lights switched on and went to switch off same from the switch on the aft bulkhead on the Bridge.    Upon touching the switch, he got a minor jolt of electric shock and immediately removed his hands from the switch.
IncidentEventDetails: 17/1300 - 3rd Officer noticed light is switched on and touched the switch for same for switching off.  17/1300 - Upon touching the switch, 3rd officer got an electric shock.  17/1301 - Master immediately removed third officer from the location and made him sit on the floor.  17/1302-1330 - First aid was administered to 3rd officer, minor burn mark was observed on the righthand index finger of third officer. No exit wound was noticed as he was wearing rubber sole shoes.
ImmediateAction: Aforesaid switch tagged out and poster put not to use same.    CE instructed to get same replaced / repaired at the earliest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor electrical burn on the Middle finger of the right hand.
DirectCause: The said switch was defective and on activating same gave a spark and led to 3rd officer suffering an electric shock. Further investigation revealed that this particular switch was malfunctioning since long and on previous instances the other navigating officers have observed sparks on the aforesaid switch too. Concerned officer have been informed multiple times to get same rectified but actual repairs were never effected. The 'Normalization of deviance' factor on the part of the Navigating officers finally led to failure of all barriers and 3rd officer suffering the injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Concerned officer instructed by master to immediately rectify / replace teh said switch, same done.   Stricter safety rounds and closer follow up even of small defects reported is warranted.  Concerned officer reminded of the potential hazards of unsafe conditions like above.
Corrective Action: Concerned officer instructed by master to immediately rectify / replace teh said switch, same done.  Stricter safety rounds and closer follow up even of small defects reported is warranted. Concerned officer reminded of the potential hazards of unsafe conditions like above.
LessionLearnt: Importance of identifying defective switches early
ReviewComments: Any defective item should be marked as defective and/or repaired asap. Having a item in defective condition creates a unsafe condition and results in an unsafe condition as this one.
ClosingNote: Near miss reviewed and information promulgated to all vessels. Any defective equipment/condition should be repaired at first instance.,
