IncidentDetails: While Carrying out Main Engine Liner  inspection in Main Engine Scavenge space second engineers Right hand got struck in between Piston and Liner scavenge Ports of #8 unit  leading to his Right Hand Palm Bones Fracture.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1130 Hrs Second engineer and Third Engineer planned for Main Engine Liner and Cylinder Lubrication for Liner Inspection and has planned to take Inspection Photos. 1140 Hrs Second engineer entered into Main Engine Scavenge Space and Third Engineer standing outside scavenge Space operating Main Engine Turning gear. By 12.20 Hrs Second Engineer have completed 7 units. 1225 Hrs Second Engineer instructed Third Engineer to Bring down the Piston of #8 Unit . 1235 Hrs  Second Engineer went for Taking Photos of #8 unit. 1237 Hrs  suddenly second Engineer instructed third engineer to stop the Turning Gear , Third Engineer Noticed that his hand was not in Turning Gear remote even though the turning gear was getting operated. 1238 Hrs second engineer shouted that Piston was moving up and told Third Engineer to stop, Immediately Third Engineer tried for Emergency stop  but in vain and Immediately he went down to Turning Gear panel and switch off the Turning gear Breaker. 1239 Hrs Third Engineer went to Scavenge Space and found second engineer right hand got stuck in between Piston of #8 unit and scavenge Ports of #8 unit. Third Engineer again went down to turning gear panel and switched on the breaker of turning gear motor and brought the remote and operated again to lower the piston. 1240 Hrs Piston got lowered and Third engineer went inside the scavenge space and brought second engineer out of scavenge space. 1243 Hrs captain and Second Officer was called for First Aid treatment.
ImmediateAction: Immediately He was taken out  from scavenge Space to Engine Control room and was Given First Aid. After few hours of Medication he was repatriated to hospital.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While Carrying out Main Engine Liner  inspection in Main Engine Scavenge space second engineers Right hand got struck in between Piston and Liner scavenge Ports of #8 unit  leading to his Right Hand Palm Bones Fracture.    Immediately He was taken out  from scavenge Space to Engine Control room and was Given First Aid. After few hours of Medication he was repatriated to hospital.    At 1130 Hrs Second engineer and Third Engineer planned for Main Engine Liner and Cylinder Lubrication for Liner Inspection and has planned to take Inspection Photos. 1140 Hrs Second engineer entered into Main Engine Scavenge Space and Third Engineer standing outside scavenge Space operating Main Engine Turning gear. By 12.20 Hrs Second Engineer have completed 7 units. 1225 Hrs Second Engineer instructed Third Engineer to Bring down the Piston of #8 Unit . 1235 Hrs  Second Engineer went for Taking Photos of #8 unit. 1237 Hrs  suddenly second Engineer instructed third engineer to stop the Turning Gear , Third Engineer Noticed that his hand was not in Turning Gear remote even though the turning gear was getting operated. 1238 Hrs second engineer shouted that Piston was moving up and told Third Engineer to stop, Immediately Third Engineer tried for Emergency stop  but in vain and Immediately he went down to Turning Gear panel and switch off the Turning gear Breaker. 1239 Hrs Third Engineer went to Scavenge Space and found second engineer right hand got stuck in between Piston of #8 unit and scavenge Ports of #8 unit. Third Engineer again went down to turning gear panel and switched on the breaker of turning gear motor and brought the remote and operated again to lower the piston. 1240 Hrs Piston got lowered and Third engineer went inside the scavenge space and brought second engineer out of scavenge space. 1243 Hrs captain and Second Officer was called for First Aid treatment.
DirectCause: Negligence and carelessness,lack of skills and knowledge,Improper supervision and monitoring.Inadequate work standards.

Corrective Action: Tool box meeting and Risk assessment to be carried out prior the job,Crew to be checked and verifed for understanding.Proper monitoring and supervison.

IncidentDetails: Checkered plate fell and bruised a crew members foot. this resulted in swelling on the foot.
IncidentEventDetails: Crew were involved in shifting checkered plates to E/R work shop. during this job, when one of the plates was shifted from point of securing(vertical on bulkhead side), the plate immediately behind it tilted forward, due to ships movement and fell towards the crew member in the viscinity. He immediately moved away, but the plate managed to brush past his anckle, resulting in a light bruise and swelling.

Corrective Action: all crew briefed about the injury. they have been advised to be more safe during day to day work onboard. Dept heads advised to guide and  supervise the staff during daily jobs.
ReviewComments: Direct Causes:  The Plate  slipping from the position, , No proper tool box meeting carried and weather condition during the job was not Considered while planning the job.  Indirect Causes:  •• Incorrect procedure of storing the Plates • No risk analysis carried out.  Root Causes:  • Lack of awareness and training of the Crew on hazard s associated with lifting and proper lifting techniques and knowing the “safe lifting zone”. • Lack of planning by the officer. The officer did not take the time to do a risk analysis and figure out what are the risks involved and what mitigating measures is needed to reduce the risk. • Lack of procedures for lifting plate. • Lack of supervision by the senior officers.

HIS EYE CONDITION WAS NORMAL TILL NEXT DAY MORNING WHEN HE STARTED GETTING EYE IRRITATION AND REDNESS.  3. MEDICATION GIVEN UNDER THE COMPANY'S DOCTOR'S ADVISE AND REFFERED TO OPTHALMOLOGIST.  4. IncidentDetails: While taking round in Pump room it was noticed that the rope to heave the stretcher had come off the block  connected to the pump room booby hatch cover andthe rope had fallen down on the bottom platform  of pump room , the pump man was instructed to get the rope connected to the block and to secure it, the pump man called the cadet who was on deck at the moment ot assist him to lower a heaving line to pull up the rope , the booby hatch was opened but the locking pin was not put in place to secure the booby hatch , the pumpman went down to the bottom platform  and asked the cadet to lower the  heaving line , once the heaving line was down at the bottom platform  the pump man connected the heaving line to the stretcher line and asked the cadet to heave up, the cadet heaved up the line and as soon as the end of the stretcher line came up on deck he passed it through the block  and  continued heaving , as soon as the stretcher weight came on the block the booby hatch cover started to close and came on cadets shoulder , the cadet tried to save the rope from falling down and did not let go of the rope , the  booby hatch cover moved off his shoulder and fell on his right hand causing injury to hisindex  finger on the right hand.
IncidentEventDetails: 1040: booby hatch opened  1100 : pump   goes down to bottom platform of pumproom  1100: cadet  standby to lower the heaving line   1103; cadet lowers heaving line   1107: pumpman connects the heaving line to the stretcher line .  1108: cadet commeces heaving up the heaving line  1111: end of stretcher rope on deck  1113 ; cadet passes rope through the block connected to booby hatch cover  1114 cadet starts heaving on stretcher rope  1115 :booby hatch cover falls on to the cadets  shoulder then on to his right hand
ImmediateAction: medical treatement given to cadet and was sent off to rest   booby hatch was secured using locking pin.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While taking round in Pump room it was noticed that the rope to heave the stretcher had come off the block  connected to the pump room booby hatch cover andthe rope had fallen down on the bottom platform  of pump room , the pump man was instructed to get the rope connected to the block and to secure it, the pump man called the cadet who was on deck at the moment ot assist him to lower a heaving line to pull up the rope , the booby hatch was opened but the locking pin was not put in place to secure the booby hatch , the pumpman went down to the bottom platform  and asked the cadet to lower the  heaving line , once the heaving line was down at the bottom platform  the pump man connected the heaving line to the stretcher line and asked the cadet to heave up, the cadet heaved up the line and as soon as the end of the stretcher line came up on deck he passed it through the block  and  continued heaving , as soon as the stretcher weight came on the block the booby hatch cover started to close and came on cadets shoulder , the cadet tried to save the rope from falling down and did not let go of the rope , the  booby hatch cover moved off his shoulder and fell on his right hand causing injury to hisindex  finger on the right hand.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis of the Incident        Direct Causes  1) The pumproom hatch cover not secured to prevent accidental fall  The pumproom hatch cover was opened together by the Chief officer and the pump man. After opening the hatch cover, the toggle pin designated to prevent the hatch cover from accidentally falling was not deployed causing it to fall while picking up the rescue line. The hatchcover has a counter weight below which prevents the hatch cover from closing unless an external force is applied. This force was given by the tugging of the rescue line which was tightened when the weight of the stretcher came on the block.   2) Improper supervision and delegation       The deck cadet has only completed one month sea service. He was          performing the task without any supervision. He should have been       performing the task with a responsible crew member under his       supervision.     Indirect causes  3) Unplanned task  Risk assessment was not carried out prior commencing the job and there was no identification of the hazards and mitigating actions in order carry out the job safely. The chief officer insisted job to be completed instantaneously as it was a repetitive job which was carried out a week ago as he assisted the pumpman in opening the pump room hatch and leaving. This resulted in pumpman calling the cadet who was working closest to the pumproom without calling for any additional help to supervise the cadet instead of calling the OS manning the manifold who was more suitable for the task.  4) Incorrect deployment of the rescue line  The rescue line a few days ago also had fallen when the bosun and a crew member had restored the arrangement. However the securing of the rescue line on top of the pumproom was not carried out properly causing again the rescue line to slip and fall down at the pump room bottom.     5) Improper communication  The communication from pumpman to the cadet was not heard clearly by the cadet. The third officer had to pass on the instructions to the cadet due to background noise of the running COPs.     Root Cause  1. Failure to follow safe practices 2. Unplanned task. 3. Improper delegation/supervision. 4. The Deck Cadet was not sufficiently trained to take preventive measures to carry out the job safely.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TRAINING , TOGGLE PINS TO BE IN PKLACE ALL THE TIME ( IN OPEN ASWELL AS CLOSED POSITION)
Corrective Action: TRAINING , TOGGLE PINS TO BE IN PKLACE ALL THE TIME ( IN OPEN ASWELL AS CLOSED POSITION)
LessionLearnt: Medical assisstance should be taken irrespective of port as timely attendance by the doctor can avoid situation to complicate. Proper communication should be established whereby instructions to be clearly understood between communicating parties.

IncidentEventDetails: While arranging and taking inventory of received AE  spares,3E Mr.sathish was climbed on ladder to keep spares on top of the rack and slipped/fallen down on the floor.had severe muscule injury on the coff muscle and noticed slight swelling.awaiting medical assistance from shore assiatance.
ImmediateAction: onboard pain relief oinment Voline gel has given for the first aid treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Third Injury sustained  muscule injury on the coff muscle and noticed slight swelling.
DirectCause: Non Compliance of Procedure
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed during SCMM and Confirmed during Superintendent Visit
ReviewComments: Fatigue: The Review of the Engine Watch and the 3rd Eng rest hour records during the time of the incident do not reflect any violation of the regulations. Deck log books, and port logs books were also verified to asses if any additional work had been in progress. There was no evidence to show that the third engineer was fatigued.   Analysis of the Human Element:  1. Rasmussen model is used to evaluate the human performance level. The job performed by any individual is classified under Knowledge based performance, Rule based performance or Skilled based performance as per Rasmussen model.  By trying to trying to climb on the lower shelf and lift the box from the top shelf of the rack, he made a rule based error. Under normal circumstances an elevated plat form should have been used to properly stand and lower the load.  Analysis of Control measures. There were no control procedures in place. No proper securing ladder was carried out.  Analysis of the work position Third Engineer had stepped on to the lower rung of the rack trying to lift a box placed on the upper shelf. The posture taken by the third Engineer  did not provide sufficient balance for him to work while he lifted the load from the top shelf.                                 Analysis of the work environment The safety locker was located inside the wheel house. The weather condition was calm and the vessel did not have any irregular motions. However the air temperature was ambient controlled atmosphere inside the store. Illumination and lighting was working and sufficient for the space.     Analysis of the PPE The third Engineer was wearing boiler suit and safety shoes.   Analysis of the Load The Load that was being lifted by the third Engineer  was a box with weight of approximate 4.5Kg.  Analysis of the procedure There were no written procedures on the lifting techniques on board nor awareness training of the crew had been carried out; however there is reference to the training on manual handling (COSWP- Chapter 19) as part of the onboard training. The officer was unaware of the “safe lifting zone” and procedures to know if the ladder was secured properly
ClosingNote: Direct Causes:  The slipping from the position, taken by the officer, to lift the box from the top shelf  without securing the ladder    Indirect Causes:  • Incorrect lifting position by the third Eng • Incorrect procedure of trying to lift with hands above the safe lift zone between the knees and the shoulder. • No risk analysis carried out. • Proper procedure to secure the ladder not carried out.    Root Causes:  • Lack of awareness and training of the officer on hazard s associated with lifting and proper lifting techniques and knowing the “safe lifting zone”. • Lack of planning by the officer. The officer did not take the time to do a risk analysis and figure out what are the risks involved and what mitigating measures is needed to reduce the risk. • Lack of procedures for lifting from a height. • Lack of supervision by the senior officers.

IncidentDetails: Injury on right hand, btw middle and ring finger.
IncidentEventDetails: Working on Crane block, Wooden block wedge was placed btw the crane block and wind breaker, trying to open the nut by giving pressure on the crane hook, with the jerk wedge slipped off and fell on the right hand of the crew member, injuring his hand.
ImmediateAction: Patient transfered to Ship's office, first aid administered. Wound cleaned up, Area immboblized and covered with bandage. Medication given - Antibiotics & paracetamol.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FRACTURE TO PELVIS BONE  FRACTURE TO RIGHT ARM  FRACTURE TO RIGHT ANKLE
DirectCause: 1.       Basic / Root Cause    Lack of Situational Awareness by Third Officer is the primary cause of the above incident.  At the instant the third officer was seemingly working with a singular objective of stopping the stevedores from throwing twist locks.   He lost track of the fact that cargo was being worked at the bay and that the area was essentially a “No Go Area”  Due to the fact that there was no barrier or notice to restrict personnel passage into a “No Go Area” the absence of situational awareness alone caused the accident.    2.       Contributory factors  The design of the Bay is such that from the walkway on upper deck is covered by superstructure on top .  The exact position of the gantry on the bay is not visible from this position.  There is no guard railing, rope to deter persons from entering the cargo stowage area, thereby there is a lack of sufficient barriers to prevent persons from committing an unsafe act.
ClosingNote: Please see attached the Incident report for root cause and corrective action

IncidentDetails: At 0645 on 17th Oct 2016, As M.V. Dali was safely afloat and engaged in cargo operations at RSGT berth No 2. Duty Seaman at the gangway reported that the connecting cable of the reefer container discharged from bay 38 under deck Location (380110) was damaged.   El off Mr. Ravi.M.Steffon was informed about this reefer cable damage by the deck watchkeeper. Mr Steffon proceeded underdeck to Location 380110 to investigate and rectify the damage. At 0710 El Off Mr Steffon was reported to be injured and was brought to ship’s office by the seaman who accompanied him inside the cargo hold.  El Off stated to Chief Officer that during checking and clearing the damaged cable, he came in between the stowed container and a suspended container which was being loaded inside the cargo hold on the adjacent slot.   Master was immediately notified about the incident by the OOW and rendered first aid in the Ship’s office. Master & 2 off examined El Off.  The following was observed:-  1. El Off was conscious with vision and hearing not impaired.   2. He had a cut on the back side of his left ear.   3. There was a cut on the right forehead above the right eye   4. There were bruises on his upper back.   5. Mr Steffon was found to be in pain and complained of head ache.   Immediately after rendering First Aid, Master called up agent over telephone to inform about the incident to request further immediate medical attention. Since agent was not available on phone, terminal planner was called and no response received from Planner. Within one minute the terminal planner called back and Master informed him of this incident, he advised to call for ambulance and advised will send a nurse on board.   Agent called back and when informed regarding the incident he advised to inform Jeddah Port Control and request for ambulance and gave the Tel No of the boarding agent.  Jeddah Port Control was informed by OOW through ship’s VHF Ch.12/10 and port control confirmed they will send medical assistance.   Boarding agent from Kanoo Shipping came at the gangway and requested for yellow fever vaccination copy and seaman book. At 0815 ambulance arrived alongside the vessel and under supervision of vessel’s agent El Off walked down the gangway and was transferred to port medical clinic for further examination and medical treatment.    After the Incident, Terminal Safety Officer & Operations Incharge boarded the vessel.    They spoke to Master and Suptd on the events tat lead to the injury. they informed vessel that they have an ambulance and paramedic in port and as such would have been able to provide immeidate assistance to vessel.    Vessel informed that we were not aware and reported as advised by agent. The terminal reps informed vessel that they will review their procedures to ensure all vessel's are informed about their facilities.    The terminal representatives went on deck and from deck level took photos of the area where the injury occured.    Vessel was in constant touch with agents as to the status of the Injured El/Off.    El/Off returned back onboard 1 hour prior sailing time. 2 stiches were put over the injury to the ear and was bandaged on both injuries. He was declared fit for work onboard after two days of onboard rest.    Please find attached his passport copy and medical report from doctor.    Will revert with pictures of the location.
ImmediateAction: Rendered first Aid on board
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Suffered a cut behind his left ear which had to be covered with 2 stitches and small cut on the right side forehead.bruises on the upper back.
DirectCause: el off was working in the cargo hold where cargo operation was going on. El Off did not inform that he would be working there to the OOW who could have informed the supervisor of the operation and by this way the gantry operator could have been alerted that person working under suspended load.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Advised crew members whenever any work has to be done in a cargo hold where cargo operation is on and also anywhere on deck where they have to work under a suspended load, immediatly OOW have to be notified and OOW can inform terminal supervisors about the location of ship staff.
Corrective Action: Advised crew members whenever any work has to be done in a cargo hold where cargo operation is on and also anywhere on deck where they have to work under a suspended load, immediatly OOW have to be notified and OOW can inform terminal supervisors about the location of ship staff.
LessionLearnt: PLEASE SEE ATTACHED PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION REPORT
ReviewComments: PLEASE SEE ATTACHED PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION REPORT
ClosingNote: FOR CAUSE ANALYSIS AND PREVENTIVE ACTION - PLEASE SEE ATTACHED PRELIMINARY INVESTIGATION REPORT. THE CAUSE ANALYSIS AND PREVENTIVE ACTION HAVE BEEN INFORMED TO ALL SHIPS BY SHARING THE DETAILS VIA SHIPPALM.

IncidentDetails: Electrical officer was involved in shifting IG Blower fan from funnel deck to IG platform.  Trolley was being used for shifting. While shifting in the blower platform, IG motor was placed on the trolley and the EO was pushing the trolley along with CE & 2E, when the handle/frame of the trolley gave way.  In the process the EO 's finger got stuck between the broken frames. He immediately pulled out his handle with the gloves still stuck in the frame.
IncidentEventDetails: Electrical officer was involved in shifting IG Blower fan from funnel deck to IG platform.  Trolley was being used for shifting. While shifting in the blower platform, IG motor was placed on the trolley and the EO was pushing the trolley along with CE & 2E, when the handle/frame of the trolley gave way.  In the process the EO 's finger got stuck between the broken frames. He immediately pulled out his handle with the gloves still stuck in the frame.
ImmediateAction: First Aid given. Arranged for doctor visit.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: E/O Marimuthu's clinical examination at Singapore revealed crush injury to little left finger with a laceration on ulnar aspect extending to the nail bed.  X-rays also showed a fracture of terminal phalanx of the little finger.  Mr. Maritmuthu repatriated to home town for further diagnosis and medical care.
DirectCause: The trolley used was old and the handle gave way due to heavy load of the IG motor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All lifting equipments incl trolleys to be checked prior use. Safe working loads never to exceed with special emphasis on age and strength of the equipment being used.
Corrective Action: All lifting equipments incl trolleys to be checked prior use. Safe working loads never to exceed with special emphasis on age and strength of the equipment being used.
LessionLearnt: Regular inspection and maintenance of the equipment used on board would have averted the injury for Electrical Officer.Any equipment in use by ship staff for handling heavy lifts shall be subject to regular inspection and maintenance, records of same to be maintained in Ship palm or hard copies with the relevant departmental heads.
ReviewComments: Job order created on the ship palm
ClosingNote: IR closed

IncidentDetails: While carrying out safety familarization for new joiners, a rocket parachute got accidently fired within the wheel house. The burning rocket parachute got entangled in one corner of the wheelhouse bulkhead, where it was extinguished using the CO2 extinguisher from the space.  "NO FIRE" and no damage to any equipment was been caused and all bridge equipment were in order.  During the above incident, 3rd officer sustained a  cut on his right palm, due to the triggering of the parachute. First aid has been administered accordingly.
IncidentEventDetails: While carrying out safety familarization in wheelhouse space for new joiners, 3rd officer was asked a question wrt detailed procedure for using the rocket flare. He then uncapped the triggering mechanisim and began explaining the procedure to fire the rocket, when it  got accidently fired. Luckily the rocket missed all personnel and equipment and got entangled in one corner of the wheelhouse bulkhead, where it was then extinguished using the CO2 extinguisher from the space.  "NO FIRE"  or damage to any equipment was been caused and all bridge equipment were in order.  During the above incident, 3rd officer sustained a  cut on his right palm, due to the triggering of the parachute. First aid has been administered accordingly.
ImmediateAction: General Emergency alarm was sounded and announcement was made on PA. The rocket parachute was dossed with the local CO2 extinguisher, until it burnt out completely. The wheel house doors were kept open to clear heavy smoke caused by the burning rocket and release of CO2. After settling down, first aid was given to 3rd officers wound.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury- minor cut, caused to 3rd officers palm. First aid administeres.  No damage to any equipment. Just wasted a rocket flare.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Instead of opening the casing, demonstration of pyrotechnics should  be done basis label provided on rocket casing.
Corrective Action: Instead of opening the casing, demonstration of pyrotechnics should  be done basis label provided on rocket casing.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ReviewComments: Many thanks for bringing the incident to our knowledge. There was was no need to open the top or bottom cap of the rocket flare. all instructions for each and every pyrotechnic is available on the casing of the equipment. strict instructionsto be given to all staff to be very carefull while handling such equipment and to explain from the pictorial discription on the casing itself during safety training. Kindly discuss the same in SCMM.
ClosingNote: Investigation Carried out and all CA closed satisfactorily

IncidentDetails: During maintenance of   Aux boiler #1, warming up steam valve ( bonnet gasket renewal ) & circulating pump inlet valve ( bonnet gasket renewal ) from aux boiler#1, to BWC pump. chief Engineer got burn/scald injuries due to contact with hot water and steam suffering burn on legs , lower abdomen and right hand
IncidentEventDetails: Tool box meeting were carried out with available crew around 0815Hrs on 16-Nov-2016 ( 2E,4E,E/O,Junior engineer, Fitter & 2 motorman, ) to safely execute for following jobs :-    1)   Aux boiler #1, warming up steam valve ( bonnet gasket renewal ) & circulating pump inlet valve ( bonnet gasket renewal ) from aux boiler#1, to BWC pump.          Around 0830hrs, 2E & fitter started opening warming up valve which is fitted directly on top of boiler#1 steam drum. The valve was fully taken out & refitted with new gasket around 0930hrs. The chief engineer & E/O was trying out emergency generator onload test which was due as per PMS schedule between 0830hrs & 0930hrs. Around 0935Hrs,in presence of chief engineer, 2E & Fitter started opening circulating pump inlet valve bonnet from aux boiler#1, to BWC pump. The valve bonnet all bolts were slackened to relieve the steam pressure/hot water if any. Since the line was pressurised before, & all line too linked with 2nd boiler also, the steam along with hot water keep continue emanating from the valve bonnet. It was then prudent to slacken the bonnet nuts more to relieve the pressure. The valve bonnet kept still intact with alternate bolts fully opened. Later the steam along with hot water reduced considerably & it was decided to open all the bolts with one bolt still intact. The valve bonnet was supported by a heaving line rope & long wheel spanner to avoid it falling down. With the help of rope, valve bonnet was slightly taken out to further relieve the pressure. At this stage there was no steam eminating from the valve case except some of the drained hot water. The valve bonnet now already displaced from its seat with 1 bolt still fastened. Meanwhile the chief engineer instructed to slowly open the last bolt. While opening last bolt, hot water slightly noticed coming past the valve seat. During opening up the valve, the chief engineer was standing directly opposite and close to valve, holding valve bonnet with long wheel spanner. The valve bonnet was half out from its seat & was supported on last thread of last bolt. Nevertheless the hot water continued draining past from its seat. In the view of above decided to allow little more draining but since it was supported on last bolt the valve came out on its own & splashed the hot water in surrounding area. Unfortunately since the chief engineer was facing towards valve, received all splashed hot water onto his body resulting in burns on hisl legs,Lower abdomen and right hand wrist.    Prior to the job The Aux boiler #1, was started depressuring on 15th Nov 2016 around 2000hrs. The vent valve was opened at 2 bar pressure around 2100Hrs on same day. On 16th Nov 2016, Aux boiler #1, came down to 0.15Bar pressure while the other boiler was in Auto cut in & cut off mode. Aux boiler #2, stopped on auto mode, main steam stop valve shut & the running generator were changed over to MGO.
ImmediateAction: C/E SHIFTED FROM E/R TO HOSPITAL IMMEDIATELY AND FIRST AID GIVEN BY MASTER.RADIO MEDICAL ADVISE TAKEN FROM COMPANY SPECIFIED DOCTOR AND AFTER CONSULTING LOCAL AGENTS,C/E SIGNED OFF FROM ANCHORAGE FOR FURTHER TREATMENT AT SHORE HOSPITAL.  ON SITE JOB WAS STOPPED TILL THE LINE HAD DRAINED COMPLETELY AND LATER BOXED UP AND TRIED OUT SATISFCTORILY
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn/scald injury second degree to both legs , lower abdomen and right hand.
DirectCause: Cause Analysis of the Incident        Direct Causes  1) Improper draining of the line  The non-return valve for which the bonnet gasket was to be renewed is on the line between Economizer outlet to Auxiliary Boiler No -1 top. The line is about 4 meters long and fitted with a valve on the outlet of economizer and a valve on top of the Auxiliary boilers.  During this job, the valve at the outlet of economizer and valve on the No-2 Auxiliary boilers were kept shut and valve on the No-1 Boiler was kept in open condition so that the water content in the line may drain in to the No-1 Boiler drum due to gravity. As there is no provision for venting the section of pipe line in between the valves, the water did not drain completely due to vacuum creation inside the line (Due to the flow out of water and the line not having vent valve to replace the water with air).  Due to vacuum inside the line, the valve disc was stuck in closed position. This was not identified even though the valve bonnet was shaken using the rope tied on to it. Thus the water did not drain out completely.  2) Boiler Water Circulating Pump was kept running: Vessel arrived to the drifting position on 15/Nov/2016 at 1800 hours. Though the job was planned well in advance the circulating pump was kept running till 16/Nov/2016 0800 hours to cool down the Economizer as vessel was on a 5 days’ voyage. The pipe section from Economizer up to the Valve on top of Auxiliary Boiler No-1 was under pressure till the circulating pump was stopped. This was keeping the line filled up with hot water.  As per maker’s recommendation Boiler water circulating pump can be stopped after the inside temperature of economizer falls below 150*C or less, the appearance of the EGE has no abnormality and the surface temperature is normal.    Basis above the circulating pump should have been stopped as soon as the inside temperature reduced below 150*C and the job should have been started after cooling down of the pipe line and the leaky valve.  Indirect causes  1. Risk Assessment // Cold work Permit // Isolation Procedures  The risks involved in the job was discussed during the tool box meeting but there was no formal risk assessment carried out and cold work permit was not made.  A formal Risk assessment could have helped in identifying the procedures to drain the line completely.    2. Improper work by the Dry dock: All the valves in the Boiler system were overhauled during the drydocking of vessel in the month of 24/08/2016. The gaskets used by the dry dock were not of good quality. Vessel had many failures of the gaskets in the steam system after the drydock.   Root Cause 1. Lack of Planning:  a. Venting of the pipe line from economizer outlet valve to Aux boiler #1 valve was assessed properly during the planning stage of the job. b. The temperature must have been reduced so that even a splash of water could not have caused injury.  2. Lack of Standards: As per company procedures, cold work permit must be used prior a non-routine job is carried out. Vessel did not use the cold work permit prior commencing the job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: INCIDENT TO BE DISCUSSED IN DETAIL AND TRAINING FOR ENTIRE SHIP STAFF TO BE CARRIED OUT WITH RESPECT TO HANDLING/MAINTENANCE OF STEAM LINES/VALVES, PRECAUTIONS TO BE TAKEN AND BURN INJURIES.  TRAINING IN CONFIRMING LINES ARE COMPLETELY DRAINED AND ISOLATED .
Corrective Action: INCIDENT TO BE DISCUSSED IN DETAIL AND TRAINING FOR ENTIRE SHIP STAFF TO BE CARRIED OUT WITH RESPECT TO HANDLING/MAINTENANCE OF STEAM LINES/VALVES, PRECAUTIONS TO BE TAKEN AND BURN INJURIES. TRAINING IN CONFIRMING LINES ARE COMPLETELY DRAINED AND ISOLATED .
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: At abt. 1540H - Deck Cadet went inside Hold No. 2 to take the photos after fresh water rinsing has completed. At about 1550 Hrs after taking photos , Deck Cadet opted to assist the AB for securing the hoses inside the hold. At about 1555 Hrs Decl Cadet was Recoilling the HP hose in bent position, BSN and OS on main deck were at the same time pulling up the other length of HP hose when suddenly the "Hose Assist Roller" went off from the coaming & full inside where cadet was positioning. Luckily cadet was hit slightly on his latereral abdomen causing him a cut wound & abrasion.

Normal routine job of OS to switch off normal routine job of OS to switch off crane lighting when daylight comes, and OS familiar and have been doing the job for the past 3 months, at this instance while climbing the cranes vertical ladder, his left slipped at the 2nd step (at 0.5M Height), down to first step and landed to deck.
ImmediateAction: It was very minor and case reported to C/O and 2/M, without any significant complain until later of slight pain, W/C second mate attended to by giving some pain reliever, late night ship sailed to Port Kembla and arrived 16th Nov, and OS requested to see doctor for check up, where he was sent to doctors visit on the 17th by time vessel get alongside. Doctors report " MINOR FRACTURE" and recommended for 2 weeks rest, and was put to cabin rest as recommended, however OS keep asking master for repeat. From that day on, until repatriated home on the 23rd November.
DirectCause: The cause analysis is due to the improper climbing up of the ladder. The seaman did not have firm footing on the ladder and hence slipped. Adequate 3 point contact was not mantained
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Upon reporting interrogated if any pain, at first none and later complaint for slight pain,where medicine was administer.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Ships staff to be aware of the the dangers when climbing the ladders and steps. COSWEP to be consulted for guidance.

IncidentDetails: Human injury , injury to middle finger & index finfer. 2 fingers crushed under weight.
IncidentEventDetails: ON 4TH DECEMBER, VESSEL WAS IN MANEOUVRING FOR ARRIVAL SINGAPORE, WHEN I CAME FOR MY WATCH AT 0400HRS. FULL WATCH PASSED IN MANEOUVERING. SINCE THE VESSEL DELAYED FOR BERTHING, OUR PORT JOBS PLANNING CHANGED. THERE WERE LOT OF ACTIVITIES TO BE HAPPEN ON PORT, LIKE BUNKERING, JCW PIPE LEAK JOB, LT COOLER’S FILTER CLEANING, FLYWHEEL PIT AREA CLEANING AND PAINTING, SW SUCTION FILTER CLEANING, SPARES AND STORES TO RECEIVE ETC. SO WE MADE DIFFERENT TEAMS FOR DIFFERENT JOBS.   AFTER BEARTHING I ISOLATED LT COOLER AND DRAINED IT, TOOK OUT THE FILTER AND GIVEN IT TO EXTRA TIME OILER AND ON DUTY OILER FOR CLEANING . THEN I MOVED TOWARDS THE JOB OF JCW PIPE LEAK. SINCE ONLY ONE PIPE’S O-RING WAS TO RENEW, I TOOK THE JOB FOR MYSELF.  I STARTED  ISOLATING THE  UNIT NO.2 AT AROUND 1045 HRS. I DRAINED THE WATER COMPLETELY AND CROSS CHECKED THAT THE UNIT IS DRAINED COMPLETELY. THEN I HAVE OPEN THE JACKET COOLING WATER PIPE ( UPPER ONE ) ALONE AND TOOK IT TO WORK SHOP. IN WORK SHOP I CLEANED THE PIPE AND RENEWED THE O-RINGS. I PREPAIRED ONE GASKET AND THEN MOVED TOWARDS THE UNIT NO.2 FOR FIXING IT BACK.  WHILE BOXING IT BACK, I PUT THE PIPE IN THE JCW POCKET AND WAS TRYING TO PUT THE BOLTS IN PLACE. BUT THE BOLTS WERE NOT ALLIGNING, SO I USED MY ONE HAND FOR HOLDING THE PIPE AND OTHER HAND FOR PUTTING THE BOLT. BUT BCOZ OF SELF WEIGHT OF JCW PIPE IT CAME OUT OF POCKET AND MY TWO FINGERS WERE TRAPPED INBETWEEN THE UPPER PIPE AND LOWER PIPE. AND THIS IS HOW THE INJURY HAPPENED. TIME WAS AROUND 1145 HRS.
ImmediateAction: cold treatment, pain killer administered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: human injury, middle finger & Index finger crushed under weight. Swelling in the tips of the finger.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper RA to be carried out for the job. Th safety of the person involved should be the priority and not the completion of job before time.Too many jobs not to be commenced simultaneously.
Corrective Action: Proper RA to be carried out for the job. Th safety of the person involved should be the priority and not the completion of job before time.Too many jobs not to be commenced simultaneously.
ReviewComments: Proper RA to be carried out for the job. Th safety of the person involved should be the priority and not the completion of job before time.Too many jobs not to be commenced simultaneously.
ClosingNote: The tool box meeting must reflect the number of persons assigned to the job. If the crew member comes upon a difficulty during the work process he must stop the work and report to his HOD. The RA must be reviewed at this point.

IncidentDetails: While working in the workshop a tiny foreign particle had entered the left eye of fitter ,this caused slight irritation and watering of the eye.
IncidentEventDetails: The incident had occurred when the fitter was carrying out grinding with a pneumatic portable grinder,a small particle had entered his left eye between the gap of his face and the goggles he was using.
ImmediateAction: He had immideately washed his eye with cold fresh water,followed by continious eye wash.he was then sent to report to second officer and master to get medical aid.   Corrective Action: Incident discussed with all ship staff. Face Visor to be worn additionally.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: small foreign particle embedded in left eye while carrying out grindin with a portable pneumatic grinder between the gap of his goggles and face.
DirectCause: Proper PPE for the job was not used. Same was also not discussed in the tool meeting
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed about the injury ,informed to use the correct type of eye gear for the job taken up,grinding related jobs to have proper sealing around the eye.
Corrective Action: All crew briefed about the injury ,informed to use the correct type of eye gear for the job taken up,grinding related jobs to have proper sealing around the eye.
LessionLearnt: Use of proper PPE for the Job.Monitoring by senior staff.
ReviewComments: Pls ensure that crew is always using proper PPE for the specific Job
ClosingNote: Crew briefed about the cause and preventive measrues.

IncidentDetails: AB 3 has sustained injury on his Middle & Ring finger of right hand while working on deck.  The vessel is rolling due to beam swell. While working on top deck of midship store, vessel unexpectedly rolled to about 10°.To regain the balance AB tried to hold side railings. While doing so his right hand slipped and his middle and ring finger bent away from his palm.
IncidentEventDetails: AB 3 was washing deck above midship store. Vessel was rolling to about 5° to either sides. As the vessel rolled to about 10° unexpectedly, AB 3 lost his balance and tried holding side railings. Due to the wetness on railings, his right hand slipped and his middle and ring finger bent away from his palm. He then reported to have great amount of pain and swelling on his right hand. First aid administered immediately and medical advise seeked from company doctor.
ImmediateAction: First aid given.    Medication administered:  1) Splinter is fixed on his middle and ring finger of right hand.  2) Crepe bandage wrapped around the splinter.  3) Ibuprofen 400mg tablet given.    Company & company Doctor informed and medical advise seeked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Suspected fracture of ring finger of right hand. AB 3 complaining of severe pain, sensitivity and both the affected fingers are swollen.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To train crew in safety and precautions to be taken while working in conditions where vessel is rolling & pitching.
Corrective Action: To train crew in safety and precautions to be taken while working in conditions where vessel is rolling & pitching.
LessionLearnt: Do not send anyone on deck in rough weather unless absolutely necessary. If unavoidable do a proper risk assessment assuming a worst-case scenario. Observe the seas on deck after any changes are made to course and speed, allowing vessel to settle down in its new situation. Ensure safe walkways are used. Prepare vessel for departure diligently expecting bad weather and cross-check actions to be sure. Whenever climbing ladder ensure 3 point contact is firmly maintained.
ReviewComments: The investigation was completed and learning from incident was circulated to all the vessels in the fleet thru the monthly magazine on 28-02-17
ClosingNote: The investigation was completed and learning from incident was circulated to all the vessels in the fleet thru the monthly magazine on 28-02-17

IncidentDetails: This incident took place when messeman was stepping down to provision handling area from galley, holding waste bin with left  hand.On the third rung,all of sudden he lost balance,slipped and missed a complete step,got his armpit and shoulder area streched in order to grasp the railing leading to right armpit muscle injury and spraining his right shoulder.
IncidentEventDetails: Like everyother day messman cleared galley food waste bins and was on his way to the garbage bins on poop deck area passing through provision handling area. As he was on the third step moving down carefully,a sudden unexpected roll made him lost his balance and missing a step.In order to balance himself his right arm took his whole body weight sreching his armpit and shoulder area,leading to internal muscle injury and shoulder sprain.He was bright to the hospital,applied deep heat patch on his shoulder and advised complete bed rest.
ImmediateAction: Brought him to the hospital, applied deep heat ointment and crepe bandage ,advised him complete bed rest,no work till related pain subsides.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Muscle injury and shoulder sprain.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To be more careful and alert at work place.
Corrective Action: To be more careful and alert at work place.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in next safety meeting

IncidentDetails: While Passing under the Cargo pipeline on main deck Bosun head banged heavily on the pipeline inadvertently, which caused a sudden jerk on the neck backwards leading to neck pain. As he was wearing an helmet there was no injury on his hear.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) On 17-12-2016 While Hosing Down the  Deck when pulling the hose from Stbd to Port, Bosun inadvertently banged is head on the cargo pipleline.  2) During the Contact Bosun got a severe pain on his back of head and the neck portion which last for just couple of minutes.  3) After that Bosun continued with Hosing down without any problem.  4) On 18-12-2016 Bosun reported to CO about the previous day incident and said he is applying Volini on the back of head and Neck area where pain persists.  5) On 20-12-2016 Bosun reported Pain not reducing, So he was giving Deep heat spray to apply.  6) On 22-12-2016 Bosun reported pain reduces the moment when Deep heat spray is applied and starts paining after sometimes.  7) Now Bosun complaining of pain on his Back head and neck area, sometimes which even transforms  to the forehead.
ImmediateAction: Applied Volini on the pain area
DirectCause: safe walking procedures not followed by bosun. He should have taken the catwalk to pass over the pipelines. If walking under the pipelines, the head should be clear of all obstructions to ensure safety.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: 1) Master to discuss with Bosun the importance of not rushing in accomplishing the tasks and always to be mindful of the work being undertaken and follow pathway and not to cross pipeline
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ReviewComments: D&A: Alcohol test was carried out by the Master after the incident for the concerned crew and was found to be 0.0% BAC. Alcohol issuance records and alcohol inventory do not reflect any violations of the alcohol policy and all personnel on watch/duty were alert during their watch. Evidences Collected  During Investigation the evidences collected include-  1. Layout area of Incident. 2. Alcohol test reports. 3. Rest hour records. 4. Statements of personnel involved. 5. Pictures taken on board.   Analysis of the Weather The Weather condition was calm and the vessel was safely moored at the berth.  Analysis of the PPE The Bosun was  donning appropriate PPE for the job that they were carrying out.   Direct Causes:   Was in Hurry and trying to cross deck below the pipeline  Root Causes: • Lack of planning  • Improper Procedure was followed , trying cross the deck below pipeline, not using ladders adjacent to it
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out

IncidentDetails: While stepping out from the shower area in the common bathroom, crewmember hit his head against a wooden cabinet mounted on the bulkhead close-by. He suffered a cut to his scalp with some minor bleeding.
ImmediateAction: He advised the 2/O (medical officer) , who attended to him. 2/O cleaned the affected area with anti-septic and bleeding was stopped by applying pressure. Master was advised. Bleeding stopped after about 5-10 minutes. As a precaution, crewmember asked to wash his head regularly and use the anti-septic lotion to clean the area and apply Iodine Spray as a preventive measure against infection.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Cut to the Scalp. See attached photo.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To move the cabinet from it's location and/ or suitably padding the edges with sponge, to avoid injury to personnel.
LessionLearnt: Any potential source of hazard should be identified and sutable action taken to avoid such incident.
ReviewComments: Corrective action completed and is effective to prevent future incidents/injuries.
ClosingNote: Since corrective action is taken and is effective in preventing the incidents/injuries this report is closed.

IncidentDetails: One of the crew member burnt his left hand elbow
IncidentEventDetails: While cooking in the galley one of the crew member burnt his left hand with hot plate  , due excessive temperature of hot plate side plates.Minor burnt ,swelling , and pain for some some time.
ImmediateAction: Stopped him and taken him to cabin, medication provided as per medical guide and medication started as per shore medical advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight pain and patient under medication as per company 's doctor advice  1. Plenty of oral fluids  2. T. Paracetamol 1-1-1  3. T. Ibuprofen 1-0-1  4. T. Ciprofloxacin 500mg 1-0-1  5. Povidone iodine for external use twice daily.
DirectCause: No insulation between side plating of hot plate  and excessive temp of side plating,
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Samke to be inspected for insulating material , same to checked and fitted if required
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Precaution to be taken while working with hot surfaces
ReviewComments: Training on basic first Aid. While carry out job monitor the area around
ClosingNote: Incident closes after medical review from company doctor

IncidentDetails: One of the crew slipped in washroom got injured his nose and lips
IncidentEventDetails: One of the crew member while entering inside wash room slipped as deck floorings  was wet and got injured on his nose and lower lips thereby causing intermittent  bleeding from the nose.Minor swelling , pain and controlled bleading from nose for some time.
ImmediateAction: Stopped him and taken him to cabin, ice cube kept on nose and lips for  sometime.First-aid was administered with pain killer and consulted shore medical advice
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor swelling , pain and controlled bleading from nose for some time.Under medication adviced company 's doctor  1. Plenty of oral fluids  2.Bland diet  3. T. Paracetamol 1-1-1  4. T. Ibuprofen 1-0-1  5. T. Azithromycin 500 1-0-0
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be briefed regarding the incident ,while cleaning and washing the door mat  alternative arrangement to be made to avoid slippery surface.
Corrective Action: Carry out a safety meeting, briefing all crew with the various injuries in the fleet.

LessionLearnt: Hazard involved when barriers are not in place
ReviewComments: Please ensure all Cabin wash room have anti skid. All crew to briefed in the hazard of Slippery surfaces.  Situational awareness even when off duty are important

IncidentDetails: Chief Officer while going down the stairway inside the cargo hold #5 for inspecting the damaged cell guide, slipped and lost his balance. He withstanded the fall using his right hand to the bulkhead resulting a severe impact on his right hand little finger. Finger was swollen with slight angulation.   He was sent to doctor shore at Port Balboa were he was diagonised with fracture on his right little finger.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Going down the stairway to acces the damage on cell guide with bosun and cadet  2.Slipped and lost his balance.  3. While regaining his balance, his right little finger made severe impact on the bulkhead  4. No other injury  5. Severe pain and swelling Observed.  6.Ice was appli;ed and Pain killers were given.  7. Sent to Doctor the next day who after Xray screening confirmed with fracture on his right little finger.
ImmediateAction: Ice was appllied on his finger, pain killers were given and little finger was immobilised.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Chief Officer while going down the stairway inside cargo hold no: 5 for inspecting damaged cell guide with bosun and cadet, slipped and lost his balance. While regaining his balance his right hand little finger made a severe impact on the bulkhead which caused slight angulation to his finger and severe swelling. He was wearing proper PPE with safety shoes. Next day he was sent to the doctor ashore who diagnosed after screening his finger xray as fractured. His little finger is immobilised using a splint and been given fit for duty for light works for 40 days.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All cew were made aware of the consequences. While going down the ladder, use both hands on the railing as support. To wear Proper PPE.
ClosingNote: The crew must be aware of the 3 point contact they must practice when climbing the ladders

IncidentDetails: Fitter was working on the over board valve when the spanner slipped and hit him on the forehead.
IncidentEventDetails: Fitter was opening the nuts for the overboard valve.  The nuts and bolts were hard to open so he was using a pipe over the spanner to extend it to increase torque.  The pipe slipped and hit him directly in the fore head causing a deep cut and bleeding.
ImmediateAction: He was taken out of the work place.  Pressure applied on the point of injury and first aid given.  As the vessel was as anchor in Singapore the Fitter was sent to the Hospital immediately for medical attention.
DirectCause: Direct cause: The pipe holding the spanner slipped. Indirect cause: • The pipe securing the spanner was slippery. Root cause: Required procedure was not followed to avoid using defective tool.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to all personnel to be carried out on safe working practices and process of self risk assessment while carrying out any job.
Corrective Action: Training to all personnel to be carried out on safe working practices and process of self risk assessment while carrying out any job.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: 1) Importance of donning proper PPE. 2) Necessity for Re-Assessment of risk to proceed further where existing risk control measures not adequate.
ReviewComments: Training conducted on-board to avoid such incidents on future.
ClosingNote: Incident investigation carried out and immediate corrective actions implemented initiated future corrective actions.

IncidentDetails: Upper deck common toilet door handle was loose since 4days and removed by some body today. There was no sign board of DO NOT USE. I( Manibharathi 4th Engr) was in engine room watch after finished cargo discharging i secured the plants during this time i felt to go for urinate. so i went directly inside that common toilet on upper deck and locked the door. After i locked(within no seconds its locked) the door only i noticed there was no handle to open. I caught inside and so much of fear & panic i could not breath also the toilet system has only exhaust fan, No supply fan system to get oxygen inside. Deck people all are went to sleep once cargo operation finished. no one there to help me. since 15minutes i was keep on banging the door and shouting help me. Meantime i taking oxygen for breath from the small handle hole of the door. After 15mins Pumpman came, He helped me to come out from inside, otherwise i could of died inside.
IncidentEventDetails: death
ImmediateAction: after i come out  i immediately wrote on the door do not use.
DirectCause: Improper check during inspection by safety officer and the 4/E prior locking.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The handle to be replaced at the earliest. Until that time, the door should be marked against locking by putting a poster.
ClosingNote: it is imperative that any item not being able to use should be labelled accordingly. During safety committee, master to stress upon all crew following the said procedure to avoid mishaps. The arrangement should be checked by the safety officer during his inspection and should be marked to avoid the repetition.

IncidentDetails: While transfering Aft Spring mooring line to fwd drum with forward warping drum, the messenger line  parted and injured AB - Carol who was at the forward warping drum.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. At 0830 hrs the Bosun along with Two ABs, Mr Selva Kumar and Mr Carol, started work of replacing of forward mooring rope with the Aft Spring.  2. At 0920 Hrs The two ropes one from the port drum and one from stbd drum forward removed.  3. At 0930 hrs the Bosun and team removed sufficient length of rope from aft spring and layed on deck to be pulled forward using Messenger line.  4. On checking the clearance of the messenger and mooring line the warping of the line began at 0935 hrs LT.  5. At 0945 Hrs the Eye of the Mooring line reached at Frame 78 and in line of sight of the AB Carol who was warping the line.  6. Suddendly the weight was experienced by AB Carol on the messenger and he immediately stopped warping and requested AB Selva to check for any obstructions.  7. AB Selva reported no obstructions on the line and asked AB Carol to try to heave on the line.  8. On heaving the Line, the Messenger parted from the drum and hit AB Carol's fingure, who was equiped with proper PPE.  9. On examining the Gloves after the accident it was observed the same was torn on the impact which may have reduced the degree of injury.  10. Immediately AB Carol was brought to Shps office and was administered first aid.
ImmediateAction: Immediately AB Carol was brought to Shps office and was administered first aid.    First Aid:  ==========    1. The effected area thouroghly cleaned with clean water at ambient temperature followed by cleaning the affected area by povidone Iodine solution ( expiry 07-2017) & Wolon Antiseptic Liquid(Savlon) ( expiry 08/2018).  2. After cleaning the Tripple Antibiotics ointment (expiry 10/2017) applied and dressing done for all three fingers.  3. Glass of Milk with turmeric powder.  4. Paracetamol as pain killer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The doctor have also diagnosed fracture on the 5th finger.  Following medicines prescribed to him for 2 weeks:
DirectCause: Narrative:  On the 15th February 2017, vessel was at sea proceeding from Onsan, Korea to Tomakomai, Japan for discharging her cargo of Jet A1 and  gasoil. Weather conditions prevailing were mod seas and swell, with temp approx 4 deg C.    The ship had received new mooring ropes at Onsan, Korea and the same were scheduled to be replaced. The 7 mooring ropes in the aft mooring station namely 2 aft springs, 2 aft breast lines and 3 stern lines were decided to be replaced. In order replace the aft spring, the existing spring was decided to be transferred to the forward port headline winch. The ship staff carried out the risk assessment and toolbox meeting for the intended task and the job was delegated to be carried out by the Bosun along with 2 A/B’s.  For transferring the rope from aft spring drum to the forward winch, the existing rope from the drum forward was removed and the drum was emptied. The ship staff then passed a messenger line to the aft spring line which was to be transferred forward and decided to heave it on the warping drum of the mooring which where it was intended to be transferred. Sufficient slack on the mooring rope was laid out on the deck for transferring forward.  Once sufficient turns were taken on the warping drum, the ship staff started heaving the messenger whose other end was connected to the mooring rope. Whilst the heaving process was ongoing and the eye of mooring rope reached frame 78(IWO 2W WBT), weight was experienced on the messenger and the heaving was stopped. At this moment the bosun went to the accommodation to answer nature’s call and instructed the crew to await his return to resume the task. After discussion between the 2 A/B engaged in the operation that there was no obstruction and the rope should be heaved, the operation was resumed ignoring bosun’s instruction. The operating lever of the forward winch was secured in open position with a rope and task resumed. The messenger now which was under tension parted on the warping drum on further heaving. The A/B heaving the messenger line was hit by the whiplash of the parted rope on his hand thereby causing injury to his fingers.  The ship staff administered the first aid to A/B on board. On 16th Feb 2017, the A/B was sent ashore for medical assistance at Tomakomai. He was diagnosed with a hairline fracture on his right hand lady finger and was declared recovery for work after 2 weeks followed by review by doctor again.  Details of Investigation  AA) Personnel Involved  Bosun – Indian national, 41 years of age. He has been working with the company for 6.5 years with total 2.2 years rank experience.  A/B Carol – Indian national, 31 years of age. He has been working with the company for 1.5 years with 2 months rank experience.  A/B Selva – Indian national, 31 years of age. He has been working with the company for 3.4 years with 20 months rank experience.  BB) Fatigue  All involved personnel rest hours during the incident were reviewed and do not reflect any violation. They were sufficiently rested prior commencement of the job and fatigue cannot be attributed as a factor for the incident.  CC) D&A  Alcohol test was conducted by the Master for the involved crew after the incident and found to be 0.0 % BAC. Alcohol issuance records and alcohol inventory do not indicate any violations thereby drug and alcohol can be safely removed as a cause to the incident.  DD) Analysis of the PPE  All crew were donned with proper PPE including boiler suits, safety shoes, helmets and gloves and cold weather PPE.   EE) Analysis of the work environment  The weather condition was moderate seas and swell and the vessel was slightly rolling. There was no irregular motion. The work was designated to be carried out on main deck. The illumination and lighting was sufficient for the space in broad daylight.       FF) Supervision/Delegation  The Bosun who was super vising the operation had gone inside the accommodation for answering nature’s call advising the A/B’s to suspend the job until his return however his instructions were not followed and the job was continued.    GG) Familiarization/Training  All involved personnel upon joining had undergone familiarization as laid down in company procedures. Pre joining briefing alongwith on-board familiarization as per SF-10 carried out upon joining as per company procedures for all involved personnel.    HH) Post Incident response First aid was administered to the A/B and he was administered rest. The A/B was further sent for medical assistance at the earliest opportunity.       Cause Analysis of the Incident        Direct Causes  1) The heaving of forward winch could not be stopped because unmanned       The Bosun had proceeded into the accommodation to address         nature’s call advising the crew to await his return to resume.       However, the winch lever was tied in heave position and task was       Resumed contrary to his instructions. The winch lever at the          time incident occurred was unattended and when the weight       came on the messenger, the heaving could not be stopped causing       it to part.  2) Excessive turns taken on warping drums  The warping drum was completely covered with the heaving line and the possibility for slipping the rope whilst heaving was not existing. This in addition to the winch not being able to stop caused the rope to part once excessive weight came on the line. If less number of turns were taken on the warping drum, then the rope would have slipped avoiding it to part.    3) The mooring rope got stuck on deck obstruction during transfer.         The mooring rope while transferring to the forward drum got             stuck on the deck obstructions which was not noticed by the         shipsatff while heaving. This caused excessive force on the         messenger which was unable to be slipped on the drum due to         excessive turns and the winch lever secured in the heaving         position.     Indirect causes  4 Insufficient crew for the task  The task should have been delegated with a minimum of 4 personnel assigned for the job. One personnel to monitor/operate the aft spring winch and one person the winch forward. In addition, 1 person is required to heave the rope on the warping drum forward and 1 person to visually monitor the mooring rope against getting stuck anywhere on the deck. However there were only 3 persons designated to carry out the job. The aft spring winch from which the mooring rope was being transferred was not attended.    Root Cause  1. Failure to follow safe practices 2. Improper risk assessment. 3. Improper delegation/supervision.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. The training conducted for all ship's crew for avoiding any future incidents.  2. Requisition shall be raised for supply of new messenger / heaving lines to be      used on board.
Corrective Action: 1. The training conducted for all ship's crew for avoiding any future incidents.  2. Requisition shall be raised for supply of new messenger / heaving lines to be      used on board.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: corrective actions completed.

IncidentDetails: Bosun slipped on the pipelines during hose disconnection whille STS operation, due to which his back  hit on the pipeline leading to severe pain and swelling.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. 09th Mar/1800 LT: Leak was observed from No-1 hose on Daughter vessel during STS discharge operation at OPL Kauhsiung (Taiwan), and as per Mooring master it was decided to disconnect the cargo flexible hose from No-1 manifold (Stbd side).  2. Hose No-1 was drained by pressuring by compressed air from Daughter vessel and same was drained into own ship tank.  3. Manifold was manned for disconnection by Ch-officer, Pumpman, Bosun, Cadet, Tr-Os & AB on crane.  4. While opening the hose from ship's manifold, few oil spray observed near manifold.  5. Hose was tightened again and draining re-started.  6. After 5 minutes hose was opened & no oil was coming. Hose disconnection was in progress.  7. Cadet was assigned to hold & take weight of the hose by rope on manifold platform raining, while bosun was assigned to signal the crane.    8. Bosun saw cadet is not taking proper weight to hold the hose, so he climbed on the manifold drain pipe between No-1 & No-2 manifold.   9. Since the drain pipe was wet and oily due to spray, he slipped from the drain pipe backward resulting hitting his back on the drain pipe between No-1 and No-2 manifold.  10. The area where bosun slipped was less illuminated, due to congested sector of the Stbd Midship (Aft facing) light.  11. Bosun reported severe pain on his back and was taken inside immediately with the help of 2 crew members.
ImmediateAction: 1. Bosun was taken inside and made lay down on bed.  2. Medical advice was taken immediately from Company doctor.  3. As per company doctor, medicines (Tab Paracetamol, Tab Ibuprofen, Tab Omeprazole, Fluids, Hot water fomentation & Complete bed rest) was given immediately.  4. Bosun was reported recovering from pain and no swelling observed from 09th to 11th night.  5. On 12th Mar/AM, Bosun reported pain on the left side of the back and very slight swelling was also observed around the pain area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bosun slipped on the pipelines during hose disconnection whille STS operation, due to which his back  hit on the pipeline leading to severe pain and swelling.
DirectCause: Procedures not followed by shipstaff. Walking on pipelines should not be undertaken.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew instructed not to climb on pipelines or work on slippery area.
Corrective Action: injury report alongwith  to be discussed in the safety meeting and all shipstaff to be briefed against walking or stepping on the pipelines.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: On 21st March 2017 Pump man was assigned for job to freeing the bolts of Stbd side windlass for changing the seal. Due to rusting/age the allen bolts were rounded off and required more attention when opening. He was instructed to hammer the bolts and free the bolts using extended rod and hammer. Job was started around 0830Lt. Prior Lunch time around 1130Lt when he was in the process of freeing the bottom bolts due to lack of space in the location while hammering, one blow hit on his left hand thumb, causing swelling, clot and pain.
IncidentEventDetails: 0800Lt~0815Lt - Daily tool box meeting  0830Lt - Job started at Location started from top bolts  1000~1030Lt - Tea break, Personnel reported 3 bolts on the top part of motor already freed  1035Lt - Continued with Job Started working on Bottom bolts  1130Lt - Injured due to striking of hammer on Left hand thumb
ImmediateAction: Job was stopped and First aid given to the crew member(Minor swelling on Lower part of thumb and blood clot - Cold pack and muscle rub was administered),and person was given rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Blunt force injury to left thumb
DirectCause: Improper work procedures
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to crew for procedure of hammering or alternative arrangement in case of work place restriction.Person to take regulars breaks in job due to restricetd work space, Assign 2 persons to do job and take turns.
Corrective Action: Training to crew for procedure of hammering or alternative arrangement in case of work place restriction.Person to take regulars breaks in job due to restricetd work space, Assign 2 persons to do job and take turns.
ClosingNote: Close monitoring of ship staff during hours of work and regular training to impart knowledge to crew

IncidentDetails: While carrying out his duties in port , OS hajaree noticed a sharp minute foreign object enterrd his eye which which was causing irritation.He washed his eye with fresh water and assumed that it will clear off.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was loading various Annex 1 cargoes  at LBC terminal. OS hajaree was taking hourly rounds on deck and noticed that  a sharp minute foreign object entered his eye which caused  irritation.He immediately flushed his eyes with fresh water and continued to carry on his duties.After constantly feeling the irritation  in his right eye he reported the incident and was given first aid measures and was constantly monitored to check if the pain eases out.
ImmediateAction: Flushed with plenty of fresh water and administered Eye Wash.  EYE drops Gentamycin given to him to apply 3-4 times a day.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Incursion of foreign particle in right eye
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: DIscuss the incident with ship staff to demonstrate the potential hazards of inadequate use of PPE.

IncidentDetails: Pumpman while working on the windlass brake band got his middle finger of left hand  crushed between two plates while alligning the band to put the pin and due to pressure his nail completely ripped with tip of his finger.And the sheared off part was removed from his working gloves.
IncidentEventDetails: While aligning hole of the brake band to the fixed bracket on deck, pumpman was trying to check the alignment with his finger, during that time brakeband suddennely shifted from its position while hanging with chain block thus crushing pumpman's finger, he pulled his hand back and during that process his gloves got stuck between the plates with the sheared nail inside the gloves.
ImmediateAction: immediate first aid was given and consulted Dr. Balaji for medication as P/Man advised that he is diabetic and taking prescription medicines.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Nail and flesh from tip of finger
DirectCause: Failure to appreciate the risk.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Risk assessment did not take into account the space restriction in the work place. Same is to be considered in subsequent tasks.
Corrective Action: All to be very careful while working with heavy loads and avoid any movement by restricting the movement of  load.
LessionLearnt: Any task with unassessed risk should not be attempted. Adequacy of PPE to be evaluated on a case to to case basis with the PPE Matrix being the minimum requirement. Wearing correct PPE should not give a false sense of assurance that riskier actions could be attempted.
ClosingNote: Satisfactorily closed.

IncidentDetails: While witnessing the initial sampling from the manifold taken by the surveyor, the pumpman was standing by with the  PPE( face shield,chemical gloves and chemical suits) and collecting the excess cargo which was flushed and drained in the neoprene bucket.After compeltion of sampling,the surveyor infromed the pumpman to notify the CCR that the rate can be increased. Later on the pumpman was transferring the cargo ina jerry can there was a small reaction in the jerry can resulting in a mist which entered his faceshield from below and entered his right eye as he was squatting.
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel was port side alongside at Dock 8,Freeport  loading Caustic soda solutions in 4P COT. The surveyor will collect the manifold sample by himself after flushing and draining the cargo in the neoprene bucket which was assisted by the pump man. The overall operation of sampling was monitored by the duty officer.         The pump man was wearing the  PPE ( face shield, Chemical gloves, chemical suits) during the critical operation. After completion of the sampling,the surveyor informed to notify the terminal and increase the rate if required. The pump man after squaring up the area, started transferring the drained cargo in jerry cans to secure and label it. The pumpman notified me on the walkie talkie that he was exposed to the cargo due to a reaction and was flushing his eyes with plenty of fresh water. The pumpman had worn a face shield, but while squatting to transfer the cargo, a certain amount of mist from the reaction  percolated through the face shield from below and entered his right eye . After further investigation and checking the empty jerry cans placed near the manifold area, it was noticed that the empty jerry cans has slight traces  of some liquid which could have lead to the reaction.          The pumpman had a slight irritation in his right eye. We checked the  MSDS  of Caustic soda and informed the agent for a medical examination.
ImmediateAction: The pumpman immediately flushed his eye with plenty of fresh water.The MSDS was checked and the agent notified for a mediacal examination.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The pumpman had slight irritation in his right eye and had to be send to the doctor for an eye checkup.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training the crew to use the proper PPE. The PPE if donned should also be checked to provide complete protection from the hazard.
Corrective Action: Training the crew to use the proper PPE. The PPE if donned should also be checked to provide complete protection from the hazard.
LessionLearnt: Use of appropriate PPE and CORRECT donning of PPE as required.
ReviewComments: Comments satisfactory
ClosingNote: Investigation carried out and completed.

IncidentDetails: Pumpman sprained his left hand (slightly above the wrist) while climbing up accommodation stairs due to wet surface on the hand rail.
IncidentEventDetails: After having lunch, Pumpman while going to his cabin via accommodation stairs slipped from the stairs due to the wet surface on Handrail. He took weight on the left hand to prevent from falling which caused the sprain just above the wrist.
ImmediateAction: First Aid given. Anti inflammatory spray, followed by medicines (Paracetamol, Ibrufen and omeprazole). Medical advise seeked from shore doctor. As per shore doctor medicines were given - Paracetamol, Ibuprofen and Rantidine. The left hand was immobilized by putting air splint.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First Aid case, Minor Personal Injury.
DirectCause: Lack of Compliance – Wet hands were not dried before using the stairs.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting held with all crew members to ensure that railings should not be held with wet hands and to keep railings dry at all times. Additionally importance of maintaining three point contacts while climbing stairs explained to all crew members.
Corrective Action: Meeting held with all crew members to ensure that railings should not be held with wet hands and to keep railings dry at all times. Additionally importance of maintaining three point contacts while climbing stairs explained to all crew members.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

Straight away after reporting to the bridge,   Bosun cut the fishing line so that the hand of D/cdt may be freed from the tangled fishing line to the mooring   ropes. Bosun removed the glove by cutting it so that the damage may be clearly seen and administered.   The fishing hook got pierce into D/cdt's middle left hand finger for approximately 7mm (depth).  0620-0645LT - Brought D/cdt to Ship's hospital. 2nd officer removed the fish hook attached from the finger by   using medical equipments available onboard. Washed the wound with continues flowing water (keeping the   blood flowing out of the wound) and disinfect with hydrogen peroxide after applying iodine solution and bandage.
ImmediateAction: 0615-0620LT - Bosun immediately reported the incident to the bridge.
