IncidentDetails: While boiling egg in the microwave oven for breakfast, the egg burst in the bowl containing hot boiling water inside the microwave oven while opening the safety glass. This resulted in splashing of the hot boiling water on his right hand, chin and neck area  causing burns on the neck and chin as well as blisters on the right hand.
IncidentEventDetails: 0920 - Deck Cadet Vikas DUbey. went for breakfast in the officer's mess room.  0927 -  Cadet started to prepare for boiling egg in the microwave oven.  0929 - Cadet opened the safety glass of microwave open and proceeded to pick up the bowl in                    which the egg was boiling.  0930 - Immediately while opening the safety glass, the egg burst and spilled the hot boiling water                  on the his right hand, chin and neck area resulting  burns.  0932 - Cadet immediately reported to the bridge, initial first aids given.
ImmediateAction: Immediately flushed with plenty of water on burnt area for nearly 20 minutes. Then applied burnol ointment on the affected burnt area ( Right hand, Chin and Neck).
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Burns on the Chin, Neck Area and Right wrist.  2. Blisters on Right wrist.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training session carried out for all the crew members regarding the proper use and hazards of the microwave oven.
Corrective Action: Training session carried out for all the crew members regarding the proper use and hazards of the microwave oven.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Particular emphasis to be given in training of trainees (less experienced staff members)
ReviewComments: Comments Satisfactory
ClosingNote: As per HSM-11.2.5, pls be aware that liquids/certain food items, etc can get superheated and explode causing severe burns to hands / face etc.

IncidentDetails: Chemical got sprayed in to third engineer's left eye.  His left eye started irritating and became red.
IncidentEventDetails: Boiler dosing pump was not able to pump chemical in boiler feed line.  Third engr checked the pump, found ok.  He suspected pump discharge line choked. Hence he removed the discharge pipe to clear of choke.  He took the removed pipe to workshop.  While blowing through the pipe line with compressed air, chemical inside the piped sprayed in to his left eye.
ImmediateAction: Left eye washed with eye wash which was available near boiler dosing chemical.  Reported to Bridge/Master immediately.  Eye washing with eye wash continued.  Medical advise from company doctor taken.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Chemical got sprayed in left eye causing irritation of the eye. Irritation gradually reducing after regular eye wash.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Vessel has carried out a special safety meeting at 1300 LT 12th March 2017.  HSM Ch 07 –[ Chemical Handling & Storage of Batteries ] contents were thoroughly discussed with the entire ship staff.   All crew have been notified regarding safe working practices and importance of carrying out Tool Box Meetings, proper RA prior commencement of any task.  “A callous attitude towards safety comes with an extraordinary price” was clearly told to all personnel.  Senior staff briefed to carry out proper RA’s and tool box meetings with their respective departments.  Verbal warning has been issued to 3Eng.
Corrective Action: Please update the condition with date when confirmed the eye condition back to normal and treatment was completed.
ClosingNote: Corrective actions implemented

IncidentDetails: J/E Ritesh Rao was involved in shifting provisions from upper dk port side to the provision stores. He suffered a slip & fall at the fridge room main entrance, which resulted in his right leg knee, hitting the door frame.
IncidentEventDetails: While passing the main entrance of the fridge room, with weight in both hands he had a slip & fall. The left leg was inside the store & when right leg was lifted, his left leg slipped over the floor which had a canvas sheet. During the slip, his right leg knee had an impact with the door frame. After a week J/E Rao started developing pain/swelling on his right knee. Followed medication as per Dr Balaji's advise.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: Plastic sheets should be removed on same place of receiving goods instead of cold room/any other place. otherwise the plastic sheets should be disposed safely to avoid identical injuries.
LessionLearnt: All floorings to be kept dry at all times.
ReviewComments: In order.
ClosingNote: Trip and fall case.

IncidentDetails: On 26th Apr'17 during cleaning of Bilge Holding tank, Oiler Mr. Solanki slipped inside the tank. Due to slip, he twisted his right ankle &  was hit by tank plate under his right knee. Also his feet went into bottom mud of the tank. Later he developed swelling on his feet, which he tried treating by himself. No injury was reported officially. Though the swelling subsided by time, but later due to nature of work & not giving proper rest to injury, it re-appeared as swelling on his right foot with fever. Medical advice was sought from Shore for medication on board.
IncidentEventDetails: 26-May-17 : Mr. Solanki slipped inside Bilge holding tank while tank cleaning. He twisted his right ankle & got hit on inner side of right knee. He also sprained his right lower back.  28-May-17 : Swelling developed on his right foot. He was having pain on lower back. He applied ointment & warm water bath to reduce pain & swelling. He did not report if officially.  04-May-17 : Vessel had arrived port of Punta Europe & by that time his pain & swelling had subsided.  06-May-17 : Job on Purifier was being done. He hand carried Discs & Bowl from ER workshop to Purifier room. Due to heavy weight and moving up & down the ladder, he again developed swelling on his right foot. He still did not report the case to any of his seniors.  08-May-17 : He reported to job in ER with fever. He also reported shivering & sweating. He was given off on the day. Medication for fever was administered. No swelling was reported till then.  10-May-17 : He came to work. He worked whole day. He had problem walking, but did not report till end of work. Later in evening, he found swelling increased with pain. Also his toe was black in colour.  He called up OS for his help & the case was brought to light officially. He was told to take rest.  11-May-17 : Medical help was seeked from Dr. Balaji.  12-May-17 : response received from Dr. Balaji. medication served.
ImmediateAction: Gave first aid for Pain relief. Place Crape Bandage to arrest swelling. Gave full bed rest.   Medical advice seeked from Company doctor Dr. Balaji.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling to right foot with sllight pain. Medication in progress.
DirectCause: Not reporting injury to seniors. taking medication on his own.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: to discuss same in safety meeting with all crew, to report any injury / first case to seniors. Even if the injury has not caused damage or LWT, still same to be adviced to concerned officer.
ClosingNote: Agree with the proposed corrective action. It is very important that medical issuebe reported to seniors at the earliest stage to avoid future complications.

IncidentDetails: During Loading operation,26/5/17 00:01 hrs at Kozmino,Russia,PUMP MAN slipped and fell on deck with his right back part suffered severe pain. ImmediateAction: Taken to ship's hospital and allow to rest in a comfortable position,later taken to Doctor at Kozmino

Proposedcorrectiveactions: Supply good quality rain boots and personal caution while working in night and wet and cold atmosphere.
ClosingNote: Both the good quality and alleged bad quality rain boots were available on board. Pumpman had chosen to wear the normal gum boots, leading to slip and fall. The gum boots with the claimed slippery soles has been removed from service with immediate effect and crew members have been advised to use only the proper safety shoes designed for use in rain / wet conditions

IncidentDetails: Vessel was in Singapore Dry Dock on 30.05.17 at 1015 Hrs Lt 3/E Pooja Gosavi came to the CCR and informed that she has got a cut on the right ring finger while turning the Fly wheel of the LPG compressor.  It was observed that her finger was bleeding profusely.
IncidentEventDetails: Brief description given by injured person that she got injured while turning LPG compressor Flywheel.
ImmediateAction: Band aid was wrapped around her finger to stop the bleeding.Yard safety officer informed and she was taken to the Yard doctor.After recommendation from yard  doctor .Pooja Gosavi was taken to hospital for further treatment.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: Share the incident report with all Gas tankers. Marking of Flywheel for range in which it should be turned with turning bar inserted in slot. Every time, a gas Engineer joining the vessel shall be briefed for this incident. This shall also be mentioned in handover documents and pre-joining familiarization checklist. Emphasis shall be given in giving only short turn as per marked range. More over a caution notice to not engage the lever on turning flywheel. Incoming G/E shall also be cautioned to ensure that no movable obstructions are kept near the flywheel turning area.

IncidentDetails: Messman Neil Chavez injured his left hand index finger, during washing at the galley.
ImmediateAction: Applied First Aid
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Laceration on left hand index finger with 3 stitches
DirectCause: Due to mishandling of the Glass, Not wearing proper PPE (rubber gloves) & Not using Tumbler brush for washing the glass the crew member had a cut on his finger
Proposedcorrectiveactions: When washing utensils, It is essential that Rubber Gloves be worn and Tumbler Brushes to be used where needed. Crockery and glassware should not be left submerged in washing up water where it may easily be broken and cause injury. Such items should be washed individually as should knives and any utensils or implements with sharp edges. Galley staff to be given training on Chapter 23 (FOOD PREPARATION AND HANDLING IN THE CATERING DEPARTMENT) of Code of safe working Practices.
Corrective Action: Recommended for the doctor's examination    - Upon berthing on the 27th May 2017, the injured messman sent ashore for medical assistance.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: To handle crockery and glassware with caution, using the right PPE and gear for washing

IncidentDetails: AB Abheer was proceeding to upper deck for the fire drill after attending boat drill from boat muster station, when he slipped and fell from the ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: @ 1700LT  General alarm for drill was raised, Crew mustered in primary muster station for Oil Spill drill  followed by muster at Boat station for abandon ship drill.     While  proceeding to upper deck for the fire drill after attending boat drill from boat muster station, when AB slipped and fell from the ladder (from ccr deck to poop deck) @ around 1750LT.     AB got hurt on his back,  OS sunil following AB on the ladder shouted for help.
ImmediateAction: Casualty was immediately shifted to hospital in the stretcher and first aid administrated by master and 2/O.    Administered Muscle relaxant, Pain Killer and antibiotic.    Referred case to company doctor for further treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling behind left shoulder and pain.  Arm immobilised.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: 1. AB slipped on the ladder which had become slippery due to moisture and salt deposit due to sea sprays. 2. AB was carrying a life jacket in one hand while going down the stairs due to which he would not be able to maintain a 3 point contact with the ladder as one of his hand is not free. This coupled with the vessels motion due to sea and swell would have hindered him from regaining balance when he slipped. Indirect Cause: 1. Drills were being conducted close to dinner time. This clubbed with the fact that urgency has to be shown during the drills may have caused haste. Root Cause: Lack of Hazard identification while using the wet and slippery companionway ladder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed to be more cautious when moving around on Wet decks.
Corrective Action: All crew briefed to be more cautious when moving around on Wet decks.
LessionLearnt: Always maintain 3-point contact when using the stairs • Paint the external companion ladder with antiskid paints. • Even routine day to day activities may become hazardous with varying conditions. Identifying hazards and their consequences is key to preventing incidents.
ReviewComments: Closed satisfactory
ClosingNote: All corrective actions have been completed
Other Part: Swelling behind shoulder and pain. Doctor ashore to be consulted to gauge extent of injury.

IncidentDetails: While transferring of waste oil via wilden pump into drums, the residual oil from the hose was splashed on Motorman Sam'S hand and Fitter's hand. The wet hot oil was soaked the handside boiler suit and went inside the gloves. This resulted in MTM getting burn injury and Fitter's fingers getting scalded.
IncidentEventDetails: On 30 May 2017 around 1330 hrs at sea,Incinerator waste oil tank cleaning was planned and preparation was on progress. Before carrying out the job, Tool box meeting was carried out at the working site and cold work permit was made.  The reasons for transfer of sludge to the drum was due to  1.Insufficent space in Sludge tank(Note: Common biggest tank as per IOPP Certificate). The content of oil in Incinerator waste oil tank was about 0.8 m3.   2.Since the tank drain valve to sludge tank,spring loaded drain valve and incinerator pump suction valve were all chocked with thick sludge.ER staffs were not able to drain the waste oil prior cleaning.  Temperature of the waste oil was about 60-70 deg. The temperature of oil was maintained due to leaking of the steam inlet and outlet valve.(Note: One day before, steam Inlet and outlet valve closed, but both valves are not holding to prevent  steam admittance into the tank).  The fabricated flange with hose was fitted in place with the tank drain valve to sludge tank. After clearing of the blockage with blowing air arrangement, The transfer the oil was begun via wilden pump to plastic drums(Around 1400 hrs LT). During the transfer process, 2/E,duty Motorman sam and Fitter Gupta were at the working site.
ImmediateAction: Transfer stopped.     Clothing, glove removed. Burn Cooled with copious amounts of cold running fresh water.    Subsequently Burn Area cleaned, And Honey applied. Area covered in gauge ( non fluffy/ Non Adherent )and bandage applied.    Medicine given as per Company doctors advice.    Casualty Transfered to Hospital.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: first degree burn
DirectCause: Refer to attached Incident report.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed to be more aware of their surroundings, Job at hand and Risk involved.
Corrective Action: All crew briefed to be more aware of their surroundings, Job at hand and Risk involved.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Hazard identification. Details as per attached incident report.
Other Part: firsrt degree burn back side of right hand palm

Motorman Pathak and Upender were cleaning ME Crankcase doors, meanwhile Motor man Pathak tried cleaning ME area above flywheel with Uniwash wearing rubber gloves.     At 0919 hrs lt The rags got entangled with flywheel resulting in injury to his right hand (web spitted).    Also the 'ME Alpha lubricator  BCU Fail' alarm got activated(0919 hrs).

IncidentDetails: Due to sudden snapping of lifeboat self(remote release) lowering wire from top of lifeboat causing  violent jerky movement and suddenly stopped lowering just prior reaching at main deck level.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel has planned for lifeboat lowering and manoeuvering in the water. Assigned crew boarded the Stbd-Lifeboat after doing tool box talk,RA,informing master on bridge & taking all precautions. Once everybody settled inside and all hatches locked coxwain pulled self lowering wire.Lowering wire parted as soon as lifeboat started moving which caused sudden apply of brake leading to violent movement and minor head bruises to one crew.
ImmediateAction: Lifeboat kept in position till boat crew regained composure & subsequently lowered by outside winch brake release mechanism.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.SELF LOWERING WIRE TO BE TESTED BY PULLING IT FROM OUTSIDE AND LOWERING THE LIFEBOATWITHOUT ANY CREW MEMBER INSIDE.   2. LIFEBOAT WILL BE LOWERED FROM OUTSIDE BRAKE TILL 2 METER ABOVE WATER LEVEL AND THEN BY SELF LOWERING WIRE TO MAKE IT WATERBORNE.
ClosingNote: The incident was discussed in office during vessel issues & on-board during the next safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: At 1545lt - Engine cadet went for checking sounding of waste oil service, without informing anyone and not considering the over filled state of the Waste oil tank.  Hot oil came out the moment Eng Cadet opened the sounding pipe. Since the cadet was just above the opening of the sounding pipe, the hot oil came in contact with the engine cadet, and he suffered first degree burns on hands, legs and back.
IncidentEventDetails: On the day of incident, waste oil tank was filled up from sludge tank, as the sludge tank gave a high-level alarm.  This filling was an unmonitored transfer of oil in the tank which resulted in the oil overflowing and collecting in the condensate chamber at a head of approx. 6 meters above tank.  For the transfer, the line was not properly set as the intermediate connection valve between Waste oil tank and Oily Bilge tank was kept quarter turn open. Further transfer of oil to Waste oil tank caused the oil to rise 6 meters along the vent pipe and through the open intermediate valve and filled up the Oily Bilge tank.  The oily bilge tank spring loaded drain valve was kept open in gagged position all the oily water drained to Bilge holding tank. The bilge holding tank filling line flap valve was stuck in shut position so as an exit route the oily water spilled out from the OWS scupper drain line.  CE realized that there was a possibility of pressurizing of the waste oil tank and instructed that draining of the tank be carried out by opening the drain valve situated at the bottom of the tank. Due to accumul ation of sludge at the bottom, the drain valve was chocked and did not result in the intended effect of dropping the tank level.  Engine cadet came to engine room around 1500LT and was assisting in clean up and square up operations . The clean-up operations were completed around 1530LT.  At 1545lt - Engine cadet went for checking sounding of waste oil service, without informing anyone and not considering the over filled state of the Waste oil tank.  Hot oil came out the moment Eng Cadet opened the sounding pipe. Since the cadet was just above the opening of the sounding pipe, the hot oil came in contact with the engine cadet, and he suffered first degree burns on hands, legs and back.
ImmediateAction: He was removed from place by oiler and brought to engine control room.  He was administered first aid and company doctor was consulted for further medication and medicines prescribed were administered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel called Freemantle on the 24th where doctor attendance was arranged for the engine cadet.  E. Cadet admitted into Fiona Stanley Hospital burns unit. The doctor declared him unfit for  work and stated he required further treatment under hospitalization & hence Signed off.   E. Cadet had the Surgery for skin grafting & was given travel fitness on the 13th July.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Sounding Pipe to be fitted with Pressure relief mechanism to enable the operator to first check  the residual pressure in the sounding pipe prior opening the cap.  PMS job description for de-mucking of waste oil tank to include, condition check of drain line  and clearing of drain line (as required)  Fleet alert to be sent to all vessels to prevent occurrence of similar incident on board other  vessels in the fleet
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: For detailed CA and analysis, please see attached report

IncidentDetails: Electrical officer twisted his left ankle while walking fast to accept the fire alarm in the ECR.
IncidentEventDetails: 1700: EO was working in the DG platform.  1710: Fire alarm recived.  1710: EO was trying to walk fast to reach ECR to acknowledge the alarm and to see the location.  1711: Just prior entering the ECR, he twsited his left ankle at ECR entrance.  1712: Since it was a slight twist, he managed to accept the fire alarm which was from Emgy generator room. Later it was found to be a false alarm.  1715: Since it was a slight twist, he just had pain in the ankle. No swelling. He was also able to walk with slight difficulty.
ImmediateAction: - Given Diclofenac Sodium 1% Gel.  -Applied gentle layer and advised him to continue for 3-4 days.  - To be discontinued if any rashes developes.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1700: EO was working in the DG platform.  1710: Fire alarm recived.  1710: EO was trying to walk fast to reach ECR to acknowledge the alarm and to see the location.  1711: Just prior entering the ECR, he twsited his left ankle at ECR entrance.  1712: Since it was a slight twist, he managed to accept the fire alarm which was from Emgy generator room. Later it was found to be a false alarm.  1715: Since it was a slight twist, he just had pain in the ankle. No swelling. He was also able to walk with slight difficulty.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:Improper position for task Indirect cause: Physiological stress  Root Cause: Lack of Training
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The incident was discussed onboard and all ship staff was advised for not carrying out any task in haste. Inputs from the ship staff were taken and as preventive action, it was conveyed that in any stressfull situation, hold and take a deep breath before taking any action. Taking deep breath acts as a stress reliever.
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting of the injury. We concur with the cause analysis and corrective action taken. The incident was discussed on board during safety meeting & during vessel issues discussion in office.

IncidentDetails: while mopping the bulkhead ,drop of water mixed with  chemical  went into his eyes.
IncidentEventDetails: The Oiler PERIABRAS JESSIE GENODIFA was mopping the bulkhead on 31st  July afternoon, when a a drop of chemical mixed water went to his eye.   He washed his eye with copious fresh water and later continued   working. There was no pain.     He did not even report this matter that day.     Next day, 1st Aug 2017 in the afternoon while he was infront of the   mirror and moving his eyeball he noticed redness of the eyeball (in   the hidden portion of the eyeball. He showed it to 2/Off. That is when   I called you on phone and expained the case to you.     As per your advise, he was given Eye wash and told to  wash his eyes   every hour. There is no pain but he was given Brufen 400mg to be taken   three times a day if it pains. No antibiotic ointment was given as you   advised against it.     As vessel is in port but not cleared by immigration, we have not sent   him to doctor. But we shall do so upon berthing. His vision is normal   and there is no pain. He is doing his normal dutites.
ImmediateAction: Rinsed eyes with copious water. Applied eye wash.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Redness in eye
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Rendered first aid and sent to doctor at first opportunity. after taking medication he fully recovered. Redness completely disappeared and eye was back to normal. He did his normal duties all through.
ClosingNote: Proper PPE to be worn at all times .

IncidentDetails: THE DECK CADET WAS ACCIDENTALLY SPLASHED WITH THINNER IN HIS RIGHT EYE WHILE PREPARING FOR PAINTS & PAINTBRUSH IN THE PAINT ROOM.

DECK CADET IMMEDIATELY PROCEEDED TO                THE ADJACENT ROOM WHICH IS THE TALLY ROOM TO FLUSH HIS INJURED RIGHT EYE                WITH COPIOUS AMOUNT OF RUNNING WATER FROM THE TAP.  1345H - DECK CADET INFORMED THE BRIDGE REGARDING THE INCIDENT IN WHICH CASE OOW                 INFORMED THE MASTER IMMEDIATELY. MASTER RELIEVED OOW (2/O) AND ADVISED TO               CHECK AND GIVE NECESSARY TREATMENT TO THE DECK CADET.  1350H - CONTINOUOS FLUSHING OF RUNNING WATER FROM THE TAP WAS ADMINISTERED TO               THE DECK CADET UNTIL HIS EYE CONDITION IS GETTING BACK TO NORMAL.  1415H - CHECKED HIS EYE SENSATION AND CONDITION FOUND OK EXCEPT FOR THE LITTLE                REDDISH SPOT SEEN ON THE INNER SIDE OF IT.  1430h - DECK CADET BACKED TO WORK ON MAIN DECK.

IncidentDetails: Hit injury to fourth engineer left elbow.
IncidentEventDetails: vessel was proceeding to sea from anchorage, after RFA was given, fourth engineer started fresh water generator. After opening inlet and outlet valves, tried to shut the bypass valve, since the valve was tight he used a pipe for leverage. while closing the spindle of the valve broke due to excessive torque and he slipped and hit his elbow to the adjacent pipe resulting in swelling in his left hand.
ImmediateAction: first aid adminstered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling in entire left hand and not able to move hand.
DirectCause: 1) Forceful opening of valve with wrong posture. 2) Over-tightened valve.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Crew to briefed about not putting too much effort for opening or closing a valves. 2) All onboard valves to be checked for proper eased-off condition.
Corrective Action: Master to brief all about proper working procedure.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: Proper procedures to be followed for all routine operations.
ClosingNote: Corrective actions to be implemented.

IncidentDetails: While climbing down the Manifold Drip Tray Steps, Pumpman lost balance while stepping down the last step and fell on deck and twisted/sprained his Left foot Ankle.
IncidentEventDetails: Pumpman was securing / cleaning the Port side manifold area and while coming down the steps of the Drip Tray slightly lost body balance.  Trying to recover from the imbalance fall, he landed on deck smoothly but mildly twisted the right ankle. No other injury occurred. He was wearing proper PPE and was handsfree while descending the Drip Tray steps.
ImmediateAction: He was administered cold pack and later Deep heat Rubbing ointment was applied and bandaged with Crape Bandage. Mild Swelling near the left side of the left Ankle was observed, no bruises / scratches /cuts observed. He was rested and advised to restrict his walking to minimum required for speedy recovery.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Only mild swelling was observed on the Left Ankle. No Bruises / Scratches / Cuts observed. Crew Suffered Mild pain.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew were briefed regarding Heat Index and Intermittent rest to be provided / taken during work.  All crew were briefed to use Staircases / Steps with due caution and Ascend / Descend Staircases smoothly holding the railings preferably with both hands.  Advised not to hold/use the staircase railings with wet / oily hands.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA

IncidentDetails: While shifting a globe valve for arranging stores, a motorman got his left hand trapped between the valve flange and bulkhead. His middle finger got pressed and developed a minor cut on the skin. He was administered inital medicine and and given rest. However after two days, his middle finger did swell & he started complaining of sever pain in his hand. He is under medication of company doctor.
IncidentEventDetails: While arranging stores on the second deck in engine room, all items were required to be removed for cleaning, painting and squaring up. Various valves were needed to be shifted for arranging. While shifting one globe valve, motorman's hand got trapped & pressed between valve flange & bulkhead.
ImmediateAction: Motorman was administered inital medicine & given rest. However after two days, his middle finger did swell & he started complaining of sever pain in his hand. He is under medication of company doctor Dr. BALAJI. Medications given are as following - (1) T. Paracetamol 1-1-1, (2) T.Tramadol 1-0-1, (3) T.Metronidazole 1-0-1, (4) T. Azithromycin 500 1-0-0, Hot Water Bag treatment & Rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While arranging stores on the second deck in engine room, all items were required to be removed for cleaning, painting and squaring up. Various valves were needed to be shifted for arranging. While shifting a globe valve for arranging stores, a motorman got his left hand trapped between the valve flange and bulkhead. His middle finger got pressed and developed a minor cut on the skin. He was administered inital medicine and and given rest. However after two days, his middle finger did swell & he started complaining of sever pain in his hand. He is under medication of company doctor Dr. BALAJI. Medications given are as following - (1) T. Paracetamol 1-1-1, (2) T.Tramadol 1-0-1, (3) T.Metronidazole 1-0-1, (4) T. Azithromycin 500 1-0-0, Hot Water Bag treatment & Rest. He was sent to doctor at Abidjan for further treatment and surgery if needed. He signed off on medical ground.
DirectCause: Work space restrictions / Improper handling of heavy lift
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All engine room staffs have been educated and trained in safe / correct handling technique of heavy items. All are encouraged to take help of extra manpower while handling heavy material / items.
ReviewComments: Oiler was wearing proper leather gloves at the time of incident, thereby not causing serious injury. Medical report suggests no fracture, but internal injury has been noticed.Oiler is on further medication.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's proposed corrective actions. Injured Oiler was called to office for briefing and the entire scenario was enacted by the Oiler. Vessel was in the process of squaring up the Engine room stores. Main steam valve was shifted from one location to a location closer to the bulk head, during this process the fly wheel of the steam valve rotated freely, causing a jerk to the user, resulting in finger getting pressed between the flywheel and the bulk head. Oiler was subsequently signed off from Abidjan as the swelling on the finger did not subside. Confirming no fracture to the finger, Oiler has been repatriated to India for further treatment by Company physician

IncidentDetails: Junior Engineer Mr.Sanoop Das Right hand forearm  (Near Wrist) burned from steam.
IncidentEventDetails: 1730 Hrs LT : CCR ask for stripper pump steam.  1735 Hrs LT: Duty engineer open steam for stripper pump.  1740 Hrs LT: Lot of steam coming from stripper pump return line into cascade tank.  1745 Hrs LT: Fire alarm activated, Junior engineer Mr Sanoop try to close the  top cover of cascade tank, without informing duty engineer and finally got burned with steam.He was wearing leather gloves,but while closing the top door his boiler suit sleeves get stretched and his wrist area got exposed to steam.
ImmediateAction: Lot off cold water put on the his wrist part.But today (14/10/2017) blisters appears.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Steam burn sustained on right wrist. blistering has appeared.
DirectCause: On noticing a steam leakage it was a knee jerk reaction by the cadet to supress the immediately, forgetting the actual procedure to be followed, which resulted in his wrist getting exposed. His boiler suits is found to have shrunk, especially the sleaves. Even though his sleaves were fully down and buttoned and he had gloves on, there remained a portion of skin exposed between the gloves and sleave, which was the part that got affected by the steam.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All E/Rm staff explained the cause of the incident and advised the right procedure to follow in case of such steam leaks.
ClosingNote: Briefing and training imparted to officers and crew.

IncidentDetails: The fitter onboard, Mr. V. Mugunthan suffered a hand injury while descending an engine room ladder at the AE platform as his leg slipped and his body lost control.    His left hand palm shows sign of swelling..
IncidentEventDetails: The fitter was assigned task to assist the duty engineer to o/haul fuel pumps of #3 AE, a tool box meeting was conducted in the morning prior commencing the task RA done and all relevant points discussed among all concerned personnel.  In order to carry out o/haul of the fuel pumps on AE's one needs to ascend/descend a short vertical ladder. During a descend from the short vertical ladder he inappropriately positioned himself facing opposite to the ladder in which his leg slipped and his body lost control since he held himself with his left hand his body weight came onto his left hand resulting in dislocation/suspect fracture.
ImmediateAction: Fitter was immediately assisted and given first aid by Captain/2/O.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury to the left hand palm area suspect fracture/dislocation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fitter/all E/R staff are appraised of the correct procedure for descending a vertical ladder
ClosingNote: Detailed incident report made and attached herewith. Please see the same for CA and root cause analysis

IncidentDetails: For carrying out 3 port WBT inspection  Choff and pumpman entered the ballast tank. Pumpman reported that he needs immediate assistance. Pumpman was lying on the floor on first deck with severe back muscle cramp. this ocured while passing from one compartment to another. due to muscle cramp he was not able to move and his full back was stiff.
IncidentEventDetails: 1011 - Ch.off and pumpman entered 3P wbt for inspection. 1016- pumman requested for assistance.   1017 - bridge was reported that pumpman is lying on deck with severe pain in his back and legs. 1018- reporting to bridge " pumpman conscious, able to talk, breathing fast due to muscle cramping, and not able to move from lying posture.". 1018 -  help was requested in 3Port WBT by Ch.off. 1019 - master was informed and announcement made for rescue of pumpman from WBT. 1021 - master came on bridge and inquired about the status same reported accordingly. 1022 - master , 2nd off, crew members and C/E reported on deck near 3p wbt for evacuation plan.1022 - ch.off reported that pumpman is not able to turn or move or stretch his legs and feeling too much pain while trying to lift using hands. 1024 - stretcher, blanket, drinking water made available at sight, more personnel fitter, 2 oilers, OS, Suresh (AB) entered to provide help. 1025 to 1030 - regular reporting for pumpman status. 1037 - ch.off reported that pumpman will be shifted to stretcher as his legs are able to straighten now. 1046 - pumpman in stretcher, ready to shift out.
ImmediateAction: Pumpman was removed from area, shifted to hospital, wet clothing removed and warm clothings and blanket provided.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Major muscle cramp caused on pumpman lower back. this caused stiffness in legs and back muscles and pulsating pain in full lower back and legs. crew member is not able to move or bend forward / backwards.   extreme pain started subsiding to bearable after 12 hours of incident.   almost 2 -3 days delay for crew member to start walking without help.  almost 6-7 days later crew member was able to walk normal and resume normal duties with slight pain in back.
DirectCause: Improper walking/passing in the tank.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: No corrective action.   Company Doctor was informed via Email with full status details and hospital inventory. Attempts were made via telephone call to get advice of doctor,but unsuccesfull.  No reply for emails recieved till date. crew member was refered to doctor after 2 days to shore in Muroran. japan.
Corrective Action: The injury report to be discussed in the safety committee meeting. All shipstaff to be explained importance of walking safely in tank especially when transferring between bays.
ClosingNote: closed.

IncidentDetails: 2nd Enginner suffered injury to the the forehead ( below right eyebrow)  while disconnecting the Cyl Oil Flexible hose, from the QCDC coupling.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was in the process of receiving Cyl oil Bunkers from the Bunker barge.   The flexible hose was connected to the ship's LO manifold and pumping was commenced shortly thereafter.It was noted immediately that no oil was being received. Hence 2nd Eng decided to disconnect the flexible  hose to check reason for the same. It was at this time , whilst disconnecting the hose from the QCDC coupling ,that the hose sprung back with high velocity and initially struck his helmet and subsequently caused an injury to the the forehead ( below right eyebrow).
ImmediateAction: First Aid was administered onboard.  Master contacted Agent to transfer the injured person to hospital for Medical  Aid
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2E suffered a deep gash below his right eyebrow.  He Had to recieve stitches to the wound & is under medication.   As per Physician's advice he can carry out normal duties.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Whiplash of the pressurized hose. Indirect Cause: Disconnection of QCDC coupling without depressurization of hose. Root Cause: Procedure for safely working on pressurized hosed not followed. Risk of whip lash of bunker hose, in case of disconnection when pressurized, not identified.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All personnel to be trained for Bunkering operations and hazards for disconnecting the pressurised hose.
Corrective Action: All personnel to be trained for Bunkering operations and hazards for disconnecting the pressurised hose.
ClosingNote: Appreciate for reporting of this injury report and support of vessel in finding the root cause and taking effective corrective action.

IncidentDetails: 0800lt to 0810lt  Deck crew carried out daily safety meeting in ship’s office. IncidentDetails: While removing the ceased bearing of the Jacket Cooling water pump motor with a Bearing puller, as part of overhauling, motorman suffered an  injury on his eyelid as a small piece of outer race of the bearing hit the Motorman.

The Bearing puller jaws were gripped at the rim of the outer race.Suddenly the Outer race of the bearing broke into pieces and one small piece hit the right eye lid of the motor man causing minor injury.

DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Injury

IncidentDetails: One crew member got injured in the cabin while the vessel was rolling moderately.
IncidentEventDetails: A crewmember during his rest hours got injured due to the cabin door slamming .As the crewmember  opened the cabin door to go out due to vessel rolling  the toe of the crewmember was trapped between the door and the floor causing toe injury to the crewmember.
ImmediateAction: First aid given to the crewmember and was given rest. All crew were briefed on safety measures in heavy weather
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First aid case. Toe nail has peeled off with slight bleeding.  No swelling.
DirectCause: improper movement when vessel rolling.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Same to be discussed in safety meeting. To take proper precautions during adverse weather.  proper footwear to be used.
ClosingNote: we agree with your cause analysis and corrective action.

IncidentDetails: While opening Cam case door of AE # 1 with long "T" spanner, hands slipped and crew's lip get injured. A cut in the upper lip of 4mm deep and length of cut od about 15mm.
IncidentEventDetails: 0422 hrs:  Junior engineer came up in ECR, 2/E  instructed him to open cam case of A/E with all safety aspects and told him that he will assist him. 2/E asked JR/E whether he had opened before cam case he replied me yes. After this 2/E went for engine room round.   0435 hrs;  Junior engineer went down and collect the tool for opening and started the job    0445 hrs:  As soon as 2/E finished his engine room round & came to ECR for drinking water, same time Jr/E rushed to the engine control room and shown 2/E the  cut mark on his lips with blood. 2/E gave him first aid, Bleeding stopped after sometime.Meanwhile 2/E called up bridge and reported the injury to ch/off. Immidiately Ch/O informed 2nd officer to rush to ecr and give first aid to jr/engr.  0505 hrs:  2nd officer came; he gave junior engineer first aid.
ImmediateAction: Immidiately washed the wound wound with povidone iodine, as the wound stopped bleeding applied SOFRAMYCIN skin ointment. Reported to company doctor for medical advise.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: During tools box meeting while discussing the Risk Assesment , has to discuss the proper tools to be used & Proper handling of tools used.
ClosingNote: Agree with losing notes. Tool box meeting to detail potential hazards and control measures for same

IncidentDetails: At 2020hrs In Bay 32 aft port side,Stevedore was standing on the pedestal flap and removing the lashing of the containers.  The pedestal flap broke from its end hinge which resulted in stevedore slipping down.  Minor bruise were observed on his back and one minor cut on his left leg.  Immidiately stevedore taken to ships office, checked for vital signs , all found normal, and given First Aid.  Safety officer escorted the stevedore & walked out of the vessel by gangway without any support.He was seen walking on jetty without any  problems and boarded an ambulance.Vessel has taken photographs and videos of the same.
IncidentEventDetails: Hinge of Pedestal flap of cross deck ladder broke from the end while unlashing the containers. Stevedore standing on top og the pedestal flap fell down.
ImmediateAction: FIRST AID OFFERED BY VESSEL. WATER GIVEN.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor bruises on back and left leg.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fabrication of pedestal flap was already in progress by vessels fitter. Shall renew at the earliest. Vessel has already identified pedestal flap damages.
ClosingNote: Please see attached SOF & Report for this incident for cause analysis and corrective actions. Follow up of corrective actions will be done over emails till completion of renewal of all pedestal flaps.

IncidentDetails: A deep wound occured in between small finger and ring finger of left hand .  The little fingure suspected to be disloacted/fracture.  Few brusings found in the edge of palm and little finger .
IncidentEventDetails: - The vessel was drifting  @ PAL on 03/12/2017.  - Fourth Engineer is EOOW.  - At about 2215 hr, during E/R routine rounds, he found Aux.feed p/p  # 1 gland was leaking badly .  -The d$/Eng Engineer tried to nip up the gland with a spanner .While nipping up the gland,  suddenly due to heavy rolling of the vsl, he lost his balnce and accidently hold the pump coupling guard.  As his left  palm partilly came in contact with the rotating coupling, it  led to an accident.
ImmediateAction: Informed C/E, Master and Safety Officer and administered First aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Medical traetment required.  The deep wound  occured  in between ring and little fingers of left palm .The little finger appeared to be dislocated from its position  with few bruisings .
DirectCause: Failure to follow instructions. Failure to warn
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All staff well briefed about the accident and adviced to take proper precaution during adverse  sea condition, explained  about the consequences and company alerts and significance.  " three point contact" is necessary while attending any activity .   Adviced -The pump /eqipment should have been stopped for such kind of maintenance if it is not affecting the major operations.  Instructed to seek advice from senior officers if threr is any doubt  in addition to following procedures laid down/discussed.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel comments / Engineer was sent to shore medical team for review and declared fit to continue on board

IncidentDetails: Mooring wire ropes that were reconditioned at Mumbai and returned to v/l, were being reeled onto the forward mooring drums. The AB concerned was on the Poop deck monitoring the wire coil, as it was being uncoiled and easily hauled towards foxel. While uncoiling the wire, one loop which was in figure of 8, had uncoiled under tension and fell towards the AB, striking him on his ankle.
IncidentEventDetails: Mooring wire ropes that were reconditioned at Mumbai and returned to v/l, were being reeled onto the forward mooring drums. The AB concerned was on the Poop deck monitoring the wire coil, as it was being uncoiled and easily hauled towards foxel. While uncoiling the wire, one loop which was in figure of 8, had uncoiled under tension and fell towards the AB, striking him on his ankle.
ImmediateAction: The AB was in severe pain and lay flat on deck. He was taken to the hospital, where an Ice pack was immediately on the affected area. His leg was inspected for any swelling or other visible trauma, but noe found. First aid was administered and AB was advised to rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: the AB's ankle sustained contact injury. No swelling or open wound observed. Movement of toes and foot was found satisfactory but with pain associated at area of impact.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Additional staff to be utilized when the a wire need to be coiled/uncoiled. Staff to be more vigilant and briefed wrt unexpected twisting that could take place on a wire if not handled in the right manner.
LessionLearnt: Crew to be alert during critical and non-routine operations.
ReviewComments: Crew to be briefed and discussed during safety meeting onboard.
ClosingNote: Ensure adequate manpower is available for non-routine critical operations, available crew to be alert and vigilant.

IncidentDetails: A steam leak was noticed from gland of emergency feed water line valve adjacent to the boiler. Second engineer had attempted to tighten the gland and while doing so the stud had come out from place causing steam leakage.
IncidentEventDetails: Observed leakage from gland of emergency feed line to boiler.  Second engineer tightened the gland but could not do so as the stud threads were damaged.  In the process steam started coming out of the gland in jet.  Second engineer's hand got scalded by the steam.
ImmediateAction: Area was immediately evacuated. Fire alarm had already activated due to steam. M.E was stopped  A.Es were changed over to LSMGO and boiler was depressurized. Second engineer was transported to hospital for First aid and Medical team attended and Shore medical advice was asked for.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2E Suffered 1st degree burn on his right hand from elbow to palm.    First aid given : Burnt area cleaned with FW, and burn shield applied on the affected area, ibubrufin 500mg given.Person shifted to hospital & under bed rest.
DirectCause: 01. Maintenance routines not adhered to 02. Failure to adhere to COSWP  03. Lack of planning ( The job should have been attempted while the cargo operation was suspended or should have been attempted while the ME are stopped or ME shud have been stopped to execute the job safely)
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Breifed all engine room staff to abstain from attending any job without depressurising the concerned piplines or pressure vessels.
ClosingNote: Second Engineer was interviewed over phone and sequence of events established. Facts collected from location and investigated. It was established that this steam leak was noted during discharge operations, but the ship staff did not fix the issue immediately after completion of discharge operations. During the sea passage, the Second Engineer attempted to tigten the gland packing in location to arrest the leak from the valve. The stud gave way off due to brittleness of the stud caused due to excessive heat and the torsional force being applied. Immediately after the stud broke, there was a gush of steam leading to steam scalding of 2E. ME was subsequently stopped, boilers depressurised and the gland packing renewed.

IncidentDetails: TME left eye got hit with a foreign body (metal debris) which accidentally flew off from buffing wheel and following symptoms has been reported.  1. Pain , Swelling & reddishness in the left eye.  2. Blurred vision in the injured left eye.  3. Irritation in the left eye and a feeling of presence of a foreign body inside the eye.
IncidentEventDetails: TME was arranging tools inside the engine room workshop, got accidentally hit by a flying metal debris in his left eye. This debris flew off from the buffing wheel which was being used by another crew member in the vicinity.
ImmediateAction: Treatment given instantly :   1. A thorough wash with eye wash and cold water flush.  2. Given an antibiotic ointment (Ciprofloxacin Ophthalmic Ointment)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Pain , Swelling & reddishness in the left eye.  2. Blurred vision in the injured left eye.   3. Irritation in the left eye and a feeling of presence of a foreign body inside the eye.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Eye rinse with eye wash/cold water, Antibiotic  ointment and adequate rest.  Vessel will seek shore medical advise and follow their instructions keeping Office in the loop.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Lack of use of PPE
ReviewComments: 8.7 Face and eye protection 8.7.1 The main causes of eye injury are: ? infra-red rays (gas welding); ? ultra-violet rays (electric welding); ? exposure to chemicals; or ? exposure to flying particles and foreign bodies. Face and eye protectors are available in a wide variety, designed to international standard specifications, to protect against these different types of hazard
ClosingNote: Eye injuries 3.12.3 Great care should be taken to protect the eyes. Appropriate protective goggles should be worn for any work involving sparks, chips of wood, paint or metal, and dangerous substances

IncidentDetails: Injury on Right Foot due to fall of a heavy Metallic Davit while positioning the Davit.  A heavy Metallic davit was Fabricated to Lift the Motor Vents on the Compressor Deck.The Davit The Davit was Positioned on the Compressor Room railing and while trying to Lit it up the Davit slipped from the railing and Landed on the A/b's right foot leading to the injury.
IncidentEventDetails: 1545 LT: While Picking up the Davit it slipped and fell on the right foot of AB.           1546 LT: Other Crew who were working with him, immediately brought him to Hospital. Bridge was informed.          1547 LT: AB's foot was examined. Swelling and minor Cuts / Bruises were observed.          1548 LT: Area CLeaned and Cold Pack applied.          1558 LT: Patient reported Pain reduced.
ImmediateAction: The Davit was immediately removed by other crew members and AB was shifted to the Hospital for first aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury in Right foot leading to multiple fractures of fingers in the right foot due to impact of the Davit.  Medical treatment and surgery carried out by doctors ashore.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper supervision to be carried out.Officer in charge of the job not to involve in the job.If extra manpower required than help should be sought prior commencing the job.Thorough risk assessment to be carried out prior commencing the job.
LessionLearnt: Poor situational awareness  Poor seamanship.
ReviewComments: - Assess the risk of injury from any hazardous manual-handling activity. - Avoid the need for any hazardous manual-handling operations, which may cause injury to seafarers, e.g. by re-organisation of the work, - Reduce the risk of injury from hazardous manual handling. - Provide information on the weight of each load and, if appropriate, which side is heaviest. - Train seafarers in appropriate manual-handling techniques. Before instructing personnel to lift or carry by hand, where there is a risk of injury,  should consider whether alternative means of doing the same job would reduce this risk.
ClosingNote: Closed

IncidentDetails: Engine Fitter got first degree burn on his left hand forearm during replacement of fuel oil auto back wash filter inlet & outlet  valves.
IncidentEventDetails: On 26th Jan 2018  at Jebelali port, ME auto back wash 2nd filter INLET /OUTLET valves replacement  job was planned. Before carryingout the job,tool box meeting conducted  with all ER hands, Proper RA and cold work permits were perpared.  Both  F.O feed & circulating pumps stopped , locked and tagged. F.O Line isolated from HFO service tank.  Auto backwash filter depressurized by venting & draining the filter. Both valves are angle globe valves (16K 100),  assured that there is no hot oil as filter is depressurized & drained. 3rd Engr, 4th Engr and Fitter were slackening the bolts of filter outlet vave and trying to lift the valve from its place. Unexpectedly little amount of oil splashed out through the down flange as the gas pocket was released since heavy fuel oil was at its optimum temperature. Inspite of wearing all PPE, splashed hot oil  penetrated through Fitter's boliersuit above his leather gloves and caused first degree burns on his Left hand fore arm.
ImmediateAction: First Aid Given & Informed Designated Medical officer on board - 2nd Officer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 26th Jan 2018 at Jebelali port, ME auto back wash filter INLET /OUTLET valve replacement & Filter cleaning jobs had been planned. Due to short port stay and activities plan changed to only replacement of valves. Tool box meeting conducted with all ER staff, Risk assessment and cold work permit briefed about importance of wearing proper PPE.    Both feed & circulating pumps stopped, locked and tagged. Line isolated from HFO service tank suction valve to ME return to buffer chamber. Steam heating shut to heater & tracing line, Viscotherm & jet filter power put off.    Auto backwash filter depressurized by venting & draining the filter and heater. Line drained by slacking the  bolts of vave flages and assured that there is no presence of hot oil  as line and filter is depressurized and drained.      Both IN/OUT valves are angle type globe valves (16K 100). After slackening all the bolts from the filter outlet vave  3rd Engr , 4th Engr  and fitter were trying to lift the vave from its place. Unexpectedly little amount of oil splashed out through the down flange as the gas pocket was released since heavy fuel oil was at its optimum temperature.     Inspite of wearing all the PPE, splashed hot oil  penetrated through Fitter's boliersuit above his left hand leather gloves and caused first degree burns on fore arm. Immedite first aid given to fitter with the help of onboard medical officer. Took medical advise from company's authorized medical centre  and drugs being administered as advised.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Addition precautionery measures to be carriedout before carryingout such kind of jobs.
Corrective Action: To Discuss with ShipStaff on effective Supervision and proper planning/RA/Tool Box meeting
LessionLearnt: Effective Planning, Tool Box Meeting and Supervision
ReviewComments: Appreciate ShipStaff in reporting the First Aid Case
ClosingNote: Adequate time to be provided for draining of reminant oil. Adequate supervision during critical time is very important. As per comment from CE, Fitter completely recovered as of 17th May 2018

IncidentDetails: There was back fire in the boiler while trying out.C/E slight 1  degree burns in face right cheek and right hand,fitter sustained 1 degree burns in face and 3/E sustained 1 degree burns in right hand and left hand, There was damage to boiler flame sensor,ignition electrodes and transformer.
IncidentEventDetails: During one hour notice for engine room from CCT departure alarm sounded for boiler flame failure, thus 3rd engineer investigated same and tried to restart the boiler and tried to fire for 2 to 3 times,after which same was changed over to lsmgo and oil drained from line through attached pump pressure gauge line,after which burner unit was opened and found oil accumulated in burner unit,thus all burner unit cleaned,nozzle cleaned and boxed up  ,flame sensor cleaned. During which time due to HF0, oil solenoid valve stuck open.    After cleaning of boiler vessel didn’t expect that solenoid valve was stuck and started the boiler FD fan motor for pre-purging.While pre-purging the attached pump was in operation and supply of oil was contionus ,during  which main solenoid valves are open normaly and due to stuck oil valve fuel oil sprayed inside the boiler and boiler furnace was hot during the particular time thus igniting the fuel oil and back fire  happened after just 30 seconds of operation in pre-purging mode.
ImmediateAction: immediately all crew taken to changing locker and washed face and hand througly with running cold water and first aid given,then taken to hospital for further treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: C/E sustained 1 degree burns in right face cheek and right hand    3/E sustained 1 degree burn  in left and right hand    fitter sustained 1 degree burns in face and neck
DirectCause: to lsmgo and oil drained from line through attached pump pressure gauge line,after which burner unit was opened and found oil accumulated in burner unit,thus all burner unit cleaned,nozzle cleaned and boxed up  ,flame sensor cleaned. During which time due to HF0, oil solenoid valve stuck open.Chanbge over of lsmgo and hsfo must have properly carried out.Injury to personnel may be due to distance and proximity of crew standing near to boiler
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Vessel didn’t expect back fire of boiler during Pre-purging mode,and was near to burner vicinity,Could have avoided injury if vessel tried out boiler at more safe distance,during Pre-purging also.    2.vessel must have started the boiler after cooling down of funace of the boiler,thus elimanating heat from equation.    3.Complete inspection of solenoid valve must have been carried out prior starting the boiler.    4.Change over procedure and draining of HO must have been carried out with proper procedures.

IncidentDetails: During unmooring stations while heaving uo rope , one rope got slipped and hit AB joseph near the left leg knee cap
IncidentEventDetails: During Unmooring operations at Ningbo, casted off 2 inbound stern lines and Ab (Pratheesh)  was picking up the casted off stern lines from local station, Ab Joseph was guiding the Rope to the Storage drum, After Picking up 2 inbound stern lines and extra stern line, Outbound stern line casted off. While Slacking the outboard  stern Line the rope from split drum came out freely but  the rope was stuck in the storage drum, Ab Pratheesh used rope stopper trying to  clear the rope while AB Joseph was signaling  2 Off who was on winch in local station, Tr OS was casting off other Outboard Stern line.   As the Rope was clearing from the storage drum the stuck part reached the top of the storage drum and the line got cleared and dropped down heavily on deck , Ab Pratheesh was unable to hold the stopper and  the weight of the  rope came towards AB Joseph and hit his Left Leg near his left side of knee Cap.  Immediately First Aid was Given to Ab Joseph and was brought to Accommodation , No external injury visible, no redness visible , No swell or any fracture observed on his left leg, He is able to bend his knee and move his leg but he is having difficulty in walking and is complaining pain in Knee Cap area while walking.
ImmediateAction: Immediately First Aid was Given to Ab Joseph and was brought to Accommodation , No external injury visible, no redness visible , No swell or any fracture observed on his left leg, He is able to bend his knee and move his leg but he is having difficulty in walking and is complaining pain in Knee Cap area while walking
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During Unmooring operations at Ningbo, casted off 2 inbound stern lines and Ab (Pratheesh)  was picking up the casted off stern lines from local station, Ab Joseph was guiding the Rope to the Storage drum, After Picking up 2 inbound stern lines and extra stern line, Outbound stern line casted off. While Slacking the outboard  stern Line the rope from split drum came out freely but  the rope was stuck in the storage drum, Ab Pratheesh used rope stopper trying to  clear the rope while AB Joseph was signaling  2 Off who was on winch in local station, Tr OS was casting off other Outboard Stern line.   As the Rope was clearing from the storage drum the stuck part reached the top of the storage drum and the line got cleared and dropped down heavily on deck , Ab Pratheesh was unable to hold the stopper and  the weight of the  rope came towards AB Joseph and hit his Left Leg near his left side of knee Cap.  Immediately First Aid was Given to Ab Joseph and was brought to Accommodation , No external injury visible, no redness visible , No swell or any fracture observed on his left leg, He is able to bend his knee and move his leg but he is having difficulty in walking and is complaining pain in Knee Cap area while walking.    At present have advised him to take bed rest and following medication administered : Anti Inflammatory pills / Ibuprofen / Pain relief ointment.   He is under continuous observation.
DirectCause: Lack of safe work practises, Not working clear of snap zone, Complacency
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew advised to follow safe working practises at all times, All crew advise to exercise due diligence during mooring operations and keep well clear of all tight lines , Explained the whole mooring area is snap back zone and while working in mooring area due caution to be exercised,
Corrective Action: Alert to be sent to Fleet Vessel on Safe Mooring operations in Line with Mooring System Management Plan
LessionLearnt: To ensure Rope Stoppers are taken efficiently, making sure they do not Slip. Mooring rope to be slowly released till the weight is transferred to the Rope Stopper.
ClosingNote: aa) All Ship Staff briefed about this incident and similar cases bb) Pre-Arrival or Pre-Dep Tool box meeting to effectively identify proper procedure, and  ensuring the critical operation performed free of any injury to any person onboard and ashore. cc) Rope Stopper to be effectively managed and weight on the Rope stopper to be transferred  comfortably, before trying to remove any rope stuck in mooring drums. dd) To consider the option of verifying the status of ropes well in advance before arrival or  departure stations and ensure Ropes on drums are free to use. ee) Crew in Mooring Stations should not be constrained with time, and communication with  Bridge team to be effectively managed. Risk Assessment to be reviewed Specific to Vessel and  port. ff) To ensure compliance with Mooring System Management plan, Specifically Section E, ‘Risk  and Change Management, Safety of Personnel and Human factors’ gg) Additionally, please refer to COSWP guidelines for Safe and efficient management of  Mooring operations.

IncidentDetails: Chemical and abrasion wounds on Bsn Elegio C. Pacatang wrist, legs, neck and some areas of his face.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel at anchor at Dampier WA7 anchorage, applying lime coating in the cargo holds in preparation for loading salt cargo.   At 2230lt/06 Feb. 2018, after application of limecoating, Bsn. E. Pacatang complaining of burning sensation on his neck, legs, wrist and some areas of his face. When examined by ships staff, redness and some discoloration of his skin were found which seems to be chemical burns and friction from the PPE he is wearing.
ImmediateAction: Cleaned the affected areas with plenty of fresh water and applied Vaseline ointment.  Informed designated company Doctor to seek further medical advise.  Arrange shore medical check up at Dampier upon vessel's berthing.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To properly train the crew before application of cargo hold chemicals. Ships staff should not hesitate to ask an advice to the Office on the procedure in applying the chemicasl. Tool box meeting to be carried out before commencing the job. Responsible officer should monitor the job and the condition of the crew. Company should supply appropraite tools designed for the job.
ClosingNote: 1. Lack of compliance in chemical application and hazard identification

IncidentDetails: J/Eng found mssing on board and subsequently PRESUMED OVERBOARD.
IncidentEventDetails: J/Eng Mr. Vasanth Adlarasan joined vessel on 20th January 2018. He reported of loss of sleep, disturbed mind sate & continous negative thoughts. The crew member was often seen moving around in accomodation. Susequently his relief was arranged at Forthcoming call Singapore & he was kept under observation. However, on 30th Jan 2018 after lunch, crew member did not report to E/Rm. Further search was carried out on board but there was no trace of Crew member. Vessel contacted DPA & commenced SAR Operation. Crew member was subsequesnlt PRESUMED OVERBOARD.
ImmediateAction: 1. DPA informd and communication set up with Crisis team at SMPL.  2. Search operation carried out on board.   3. Vessel truned around and commenced approach towards approx Man overboard position.  3. Vietnam MRCC contacted. Urgency message transmitted on VHF & Sat C.  4. Contact established with MPA Singapore & MRCC Hongkong.   5. Contact eastablished with vessels in vicinity  6. Vessel arrived approx MOB position and commenced SINGLE PATTERN EXPANDING SQUARE search.  7.SINGLE PATTERN EXPANDING SQUARE executed for 24 Hours. All passing by ships alerted of situation.   8.Vietnam MRCC, Hongkong MRCC, MPA Singapore contacted and appraised of situation.   9. Crew member PRESUMED OVERBOARD.  10. 31/1100 Hrs:- Vessel resumed on her passage to Singapore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew member was lost at Sea & finally PRESUMED OVERBOARD.
DirectCause: Pls refer to attached IIR
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All seafarers to be screened for any phsycholgical behaviour. On board, crew member were motivated to socialize and discuss their problems with their colleagues and seniors.
Corrective Action: Fleet circular to be sent to all company vessels high lighting the following • Actions to be taken by Fleet personnel & Superintendents when people displays sign of stress on board; • Actions to be taken by Master when crew members display sign of stress
ClosingNote: All CA attended to and closed

IncidentDetails: On 13th Feb. 2018/2355LT(UTC+1), Duty Engineer (4/E)  fell down sitting from bench in the engine control room, probably lost control due to heavy rolling & pitching of the vessel. Corrective Action: Bench to be not kept in the Engine Room. All chairs placed at assigned location and lashed securely.

IncidentDetails: Injury happened during suez  transit entry area  while passing a painter line to mooring boat - the bight of the painter rope got caught in his left leg.
IncidentEventDetails: 1400: Suez Sea POB.  1442: Change of sea pilot to cannel pilot.  1445: Suez cannel inspector and agent on board   1446: Suez  electriction boarded vsl .  1446: C/E  joining vsl with 3 big luggages ,crew engaged in picking up luggages   1450: Suez Mooring boat came along side for pick up ,bosun and T.os  directed to pick up            Mooring boat.  1451:Bosun was getting the provision crane ready  and T. os was stand by at main deck bay 23                   port side to pass line.  1452: Painter line passed to mooring boat by T. OS RAHUL   1453: As soon as the painter was passed to  mooring boat,the mooring crew stopped the                              engine and took weight of the boat on the painter without  confirming from the vessel                       whether the painter is made fast or not.   1454: Left leg of T. OS Rahul got caught  in the bight of the painter line  due to sudden pulling of                  painter line caused by taking the weight of the boat on the painter.  1455: When T.OS rahul  yelled at the boat,the boat crew cast the boat adrift.At the same time T.OS            called bosun on the  Walkie talkie for help.  1456: Boson and fitter (Moorthy) immediately approached for assistance and took T.OS to                hospital for first aid.  1457: C/E   luggage picked up and more crew joined for assistance  1500: Agent  and suez  inspector disembarked .  1510: Mooring boat  picked up and secured for transit on port side embarkation deck.
ImmediateAction: FIRST  AID GIVEN IN HOSPITAL AND CONDITION STATUS  INFORMED TO OFFICE AND  BALAJI  FOR FURTHER MEDICAL ADVICE .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury happened while passing a painter line to mooring boat - the bight of the painter rope got caught in his left leg.
DirectCause: As per Master's comments, a proper planning for suez transit was laid out and discussed by Master -  one day prior arrival Suez during the bridge team meeting with C/O, 2/Off, 3/Off and Cdt. It was emphasized to use all available crew including riding fitters during entry and exit of canal transit.  1. Not having sufficient no of crews during the crunch period of transit - sighting rest hours requirements. Lack of foresight/instructions not followed by ShipStaff which was discussed during toolbox meeting wherein it was assumed that the situation can be handled with the available crew.  2. Crew involved in too many activities pertaining to suez transit.  3. On-signer's luggage being very heavy caused some delay in picking up of crew baggage.  4. Using a Trainee OS to pass painter without any seniors monitoring him.  5. Lack of awareness and Lack of knowledge on Trainee OS part wherein he has not understood the dangers of keeping your leg in the bight of a rope.  6. Suez boat crew stopping mooring boat engines without ensuring that their painter is made fast or not.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Exprienced  hands to handle mooring operation and mooring boat operation in suez transit.
Corrective Action: Fleet Alert Sent to Vessels to address the concern of injuries to Ship Staff
LessionLearnt: To ensure that the experience of Shipstaff suitably used, and Junior shipstaff effectively supervised. Tool box meeting should be specific to the Critical job being carried out.
ClosingNote: Corrective action implemented

IncidentDetails: 19-02-2018 - During launching of resue boat, a swell suddenly pushed the boat towards the ship and therescue boat bumped the ship's side. The AB was off balanced as he tried to push the shipside with the a boathook. Later he felt pain when he stood.
IncidentEventDetails: On the 19th Feb. while berthing schedule was cancelled for the day, we decided to launch RB and LB. Permission was granted by the Ningbo VTS.  During launching of RB and LB at Ningbo anchorage the weather was wind NE3, sea NE3, swell 1m, O'cast skies.  The rescue boat was manned by the 3/Off, 3E and AB. The painters were tended by other crews. C/E was operator of lowering the RB.  When the RB was about 0.5 mtr above the water swell hit the boat causing it to bumped on the shipside causing the crew to be offbalanced and the AB's foot got sparained.  The CE immediately hauled in the RB back onboard and for securing.
ImmediateAction: The CE immediately hauled in the RB back onboard and for securing.  As it was Chinese New Year holidays. The AB was sent to doctor at the next port.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fracture to the foot
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Avoid launching the rescue boat even in slight sea where swell is sometimes happening
ClosingNote: Agree with CA, Incident Investigation report prepared .

IncidentDetails: While assembling stuffing box, fitter got accidental injury to his two of his left hand fingers. Following has been observed.  1. Middle finger got a deep cut .  2. Fore finger got two minor cuts.
IncidentEventDetails: Fitter was assisting 2/E for assembling the stuffing box.When both were aligning the stuffing box, stuffing box slips from his hand and two of his left hand fingers(fore finger and middle finger) comes in between stuffing box lower half and the plate for assembling stuffing box.
ImmediateAction: First aid given.  1. Cleaned with iodine solution. Antibiotic cream is applied and dressed.  2. Painkiller tablets are given(ibrufen and paracetamol)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. Middle finger got a deep cut .  2. Fore finger got two minor cuts.
DirectCause: It was noticed that the gloves used by him was oily.Even though he was wearing gloves, it was already soaked with oil and is unfit for any further usage. Due to that the stuffing box got slipped off from his hand and it lead to the incident.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: He was advised to change the ppe as per the job requirement. All crew briefed about the importance of wearing proper PPE and advised to change the gloves when it becomes unusable while carryig out such critical jobs.
LessionLearnt: Lack of PPE  Lack of situational awareness
ReviewComments: 2.16 PERSONAL PROTECTIVE CLOTHING AND PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT (PPE) 2.14.1. Provision of Protective Clothing and Protective Equipment The company provides protective clothing and equipment to all personnel serving onboard the company ships. Personnel shall, at all times, use appropriate PPE suitable for the operation/task to be carried out as instructed by the Master or departmental heads. Failure to adhere could result in disciplinary action being taken onboard. The company will supply suitable PPE for all normal shipboard operations. The chief officer and chief Engineer are responsible for ensuring sufficient stocks are onboard. PPE should be relied upon only to afford protection against the risks that remain after all other reasonably practicable steps have been taken. This is chiefly because PPE does nothing to reduce the hazard, and can protect only the person wearing it, leaving others vulnerable. It should be noted that the use of PPE may in itself cause a hazard – for example, through reduced field of vision, loss of dexterity or agility. 2.14.2. SELECTION OF PPE Before commencing any task, risk assessment in particular to the task to be carried out to identify the hazards that may be associated with the task. Using these hazards as a guide, PPE matrix should be derived for the specific task. Compliance with the company PPE guidance chart is mandatory
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: Second Engineer and Fitter were working in # 2 LT Cooler for changing the sacrificial flange of sea water inlet pipe of the #2  LT cooler.While loosening the ( RUSTED AND SEIZED ) bolts of the flange,  the closed end of the spanner sheared off and a piece ofthe sheared spanner   hit the lleft eye lid and eyebrow of the second engineer.
IncidentEventDetails: Ship staff were opening the bolts with a spanner . Pipe lever  was used at the open end to get additional leverage to open the rusted and seized bolts in the sea water pipe, The spanner sheared off caused injury to the second engineer.
ImmediateAction: Second engineer was examined and no bruises or cuts were observed .Eyes cleaned with eyewash. Second engineer was experiencing pain on the left  eybrow area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: ship staff experiencing  moderatec pain in the eyebrow /eyelid area
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All tools to be checked before usage and all new tools to be ordered with certificate. Using Pipe levers to get additional force to be avoided and all crew members were advised using the proper usage of tools and all tools to be checked for any damages visually before using them.All crew members to use proper PPE.
ClosingNote: A proper tool box meeting to be held prior each task and each risk to be discussed in the tool box meeting. Same to be included in the RA

IncidentDetails: Deck cadet -2 Utpal Kumar got finger injury ( small wound ) on right leg near small finger.  He was assigned for assisting washing on main deck port side along with O/S using high pressure machine.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Tool box meeting was carried out in the morning and the job was assigned for O/S & Deck cadet 2 for washing main deck port side with high pressure machine.  2. OS was assisgned for using High pressure machine & cadet was assigned for assisting OS in cleaning by applying detergent and using squeegees to clearoff the wash water.  3. At 1500 hrs LT OS went to store for taking additional length of hose as with the present length they were not able to reach the aft bays.  4. Meanwhile cadet used the highpressure washing machine for cleaning his steel toe gumboot.  5.From investiagation it was observed that he had cleaned his left leg steel toe gumboot also.  6. After finishing that he started cleaning his right leg steeltoe gumboot which resulted in piercing of gumboot behind steel toe part and there by leaving a wound & bleeding near his small finger.
ImmediateAction: 1. Steel toe gumboot was removed.  2. His wound was cleaned with Antispetic alcohol soultions.  3. Given enough water to drink.  4. Wound was dressed with povidone iodine solution.  5. Presently on bed rest with his legs at elevated position.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Wound naer the litter finger of right toe.
DirectCause: Failure to comply with instructions given during tool box meeting.  He was also invoved in the same job for the last two months.  Difficulty in understanding instructions given by senior officers and coworkers.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To be signed off.  He is a potential threat to co workers.  Repeated instruction fallen on deaf ears.Very poor in communication.

IncidentDetails: On 03rd March, AB Justin reported that his finger hit against the pilot ladder wooden step while securing the same on 24th Feb 2018 at Canakkale Strait.

At the time of the incident there was not much pain however on 03rd March he reported he is unable to straighten the finger and there is slight pain on pressing. Confirmed with him and at the time of the incident he was wearing safety gloves.    Please find  attached pictures of AB Justin small finger on his left hand. The finger as it appears cannot be straightened.
ImmediateAction: Reported the incident to Company doctor, and as per doctors advice,    The candidate was suffering from Mallet finger and could not be managed conservatively.   He will require surgical treatment.   Rrecommended to refer him to an orthopaedician in the next port of call.     Same carried out, sent to doctor ashore at Samsun for further medical treatment. Report as attached.

Corrective Action: Training to be Carried out to all personnel for procedures of Pilot Ladder rigging and picking up.
