IncidentDetails: Engine fitter finger got crushed in E/R door Frame due to vessel rolls. Vessel is in south Pacific Ocean and facing Adverse sea condition, Vessel rolling and pitching Heavily due to heavy swell.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1030lt on 20 Apr 2018, while going to E/R after tea break. Engine Fitter opened the E/R door , suddenly vessel rolls and he lost his control. In reaction he holds the door frame and door which was already in open postion slam shut due to rolling and crushed fitter's finger.
ImmediateAction: Medical Treatment given to fitter Onboard and medical advise sought from company Doctor==  1. Blood stopped by applying little pressure and keeping hand up than shoulder.  2. Wound Cleaned with Povidine Iodine solution  3. Povidone Iodine (oinment)cream appiled on the wound and dressing done.  4. Amoxiciline and Clavulanate Tabs(875+125mg)- 1 tab ( would be giving twice a      day)  5. Ibuprofen Tablets BP 400 mg- 1 tab
DirectCause: Lack of Compliance with Code of Safe Working Practioces and basics of Prudent Seamanship Practices
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew member told to be caution while vessel is in rough weather.Wait for sometime to open something. Let vessel steady for a particular time to avoid slip trip and fall.Always hold the railing provided in area and donot stand near or between the door. Keep door always closed.
Corrective Action: Briefing of all crew regards common practices of prudent seamanship related to precautions in Heavy Weather or when ship is rolling/pitching heavily. Master/Safety Officer to discuss in the next Safety Meeting regards basic precautions and the importance of good / adequate securing when vessel in a seaway.
LessionLearnt: Importance of Prudent seamanship Practices
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: COnstant situational awareness is required of all potential hazards in surroundings. Especially important when vessels motion is sever in a rough weather situation.

IncidentDetails: During cleaning of Sludge pump suction filter hot oil(Approx 60'C) splashed on Junior engineer face. Causing minor burns in his right side of the face. Engine room temp was noted to be 43'C  Approx at the time of incident.
IncidentEventDetails: Daily routine of Sludge transfer was initiated in the evening. Pump was not taking Suction. So pump stopped and attempt was made to clean the suction filter. Suction and Discharge valves were isolated prior starting job. Checked Purge cock before opening the filter cover. No oil/pressure seen from purge cock. Hence Started opening filter cover. Sudden gust of oil splashed on Junior engineer's face causing injury/ minor burns on his face. - Further investigation revealed that Suction valve of Pump  was crack open and Purge cock of filter cover was choked with sludge.
ImmediateAction: Junior engineer was taken to ECR and his face was washed with Cold water and all splashed oil cleaned. Given him first aid with Burn care ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Suffered Minor burns on face
DirectCause: LOTO not effective
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always Senior engineers should ensure lines are isolated completely and De-pressurised before allocating jobs to Juniors and Juniors are to be instructed not to take any job prior informing Second Engineer.
Corrective Action: Training session on the importance of proper LOTO to be carried to all ship staff
ClosingNote: Please refer to attached IIR

IncidentDetails: Cadet was closing an accomodation door by himself, where his fingers got struck in between and he got superficial wound on fingers.
IncidentEventDetails: 15-02-2018  Cadet's finger skin peeled of about 2 mm. No pain was noted. General first aid given and wound healed.    21-02-2018  Slight projection of skin noted on the same area. Still no pain.  He was administered Ciprofloxacin(1-1-1) for 5 days.    26-02-2018  Skin projection started healing.  Size of projection reduced.    30-03-2018  Reddish colouration noted around the projection, noted pain and swelling around the region.  Doctor adviced for medication as per attachment.      06-04-2018  After completion of the medication, no improvement noted. So Doctor's advice requested again.  Doctor adviced to consult a physician in the next port.    12-04-2018  Cadet visited Doctor ashore in Tuzla.  See attached Doctor's report.
ImmediateAction: First aid was given for the skin peel off.
DirectCause: Lack of alertness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Opening closing of doors to be done by holding the handle and avoid putting hand in the door frames for the same.
LessionLearnt: Opening closing of doors to be done by holding the handle and avoid putting hand in the door frames for the same. Close monitoring of the superficial injuries to be done till it shows signs of improvement, otherwise to seek medical assistance.
ClosingNote: Close monitoring of the superficial injuries to be done till it shows signs of improvement, otherwise to seek medical assistance.

IncidentDetails: While Working in ballast tank, he slipped on the floor with his left leg ankle sprained and showing slight swelling
IncidentEventDetails: After completion of days work in the ballast tank No. 4 TST S, just before coming out of the tank TROS Aakshay tripped and fell on the floor inside the tank, he has complained of pain in the left leg ankle and slight swelling since.
ImmediateAction: After coming out of the tank, he informed the Chief Officer.He was examined for the injury by Master and it was found that  his left leg ankle sprained and was showing slight swelling. He was asked to take bed rest and anti-inflamatory tablets with pain killers were given.After 24 hrs when swelling reduced but  Tr.OS still felt pain in ankle Vessel seeked medical advise from Dr. Balaji
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While Working in ballast tank, he slipped on the floor with his left leg ankle sprained and showing slight swelling.
DirectCause: 3 Point contact was not  made properly while walking or climbing ladders inside ballast tank, which led to slipping in the tank and causing injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew were briefed on proper working procedure and advised to use proper PPE while working in Ballast tanks. 3 Point contact to be made while walking or climbing ladders inside ballast tank.
ClosingNote: There have been a lot of incidents inside ballast tanks in the recent past. Any job in the ballast tanks, no matter how mundane, no matter how routine and no matter for how long, must be treated with extreme caution. Any complacency must be avoided to ensure that incidents do not take place. Kindly discuss all the findings with the crew during the next safety meeting.

IncidentEventDetails: An AB and OS were assigned to removal of the Aft Stbd Winch Brake for renewal of the Brake Liner. ImmediateAction: Stopped work and assessed the injured. He was given first aid and rest until the pain subsided. Corrective Action: Tool Box meeting to be held in depth and explaining the job. The person incharge of carrying out the job is to give proper instructions. Informed not to try and overachieve the job and safety comes first.

IncidentDetails: One small crystal of Caustic flake has fallen in one of the AB's eye, while he was attempting to operate the manifold valve after mixing chemicals to put in the pipeline (with the same gloves that he was using to handle chemical) one crystal of chemical that was stuck in the gloves got blown off in wind & had fallen in his eyes.
IncidentEventDetails: AB was involved in mixing chemicals (Caustic & E24) and filling in the cargo pipeline which was to be cleaned since the line had cargo sediment inside. After filling the chemical inside the manifold line, he was to open the manifold valve in order to let the chemical flow through the entire line till the blanks. After pouring the chemical mixture inside the line, he was opening the valve. While opening the valve/turning the valve handle one piece of chemical that was stuck to his gloves got blown off in the wind & fell in his eyes.
ImmediateAction: Eyes flushed with copious amount of running water. Medicine administered as per Medical Guide. Medical advise taken from Company doctor & same was given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Redness & Irritation of eyes with mild pain, lasted for 3 days. AB was given off from one watch & rested. No trouble with vision. AB was sent to doctor at next port & was declared fit. Attached medical report for reference.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: This incident should be discussed during the safety meeting. Correct procedure for use of PPE should be explained.
Corrective Action: Crew to be briefed in next safety meeting regarding importance of flushing the PPE with fresh water after handling of corrosive cargoes, and minimum PPE required while carrying out tankcleaning operations  at all times on Deck.
ClosingNote: Crew to be briefed in next safety meeting regarding importance of flushing the PPE with fresh water after handling of corrosive cargoes, and minimum PPE required while carrying out tankcleaning operations  at all times on Deck.

IncidentDetails: OS-Deo and OS-Vaishak were shifting the removed ballast eductor valve to engine room workshop . both the OS were using stair from upperdeck inside accomodation to Engine Room , As the valve was heavy it slipped off from the hand of OS-Deo and fell off on engine room first platform and because of the jerk of falling valve , OS-Deo fell on top of the valve . causing bruise injury on his right cheek, right handfore Arm ,lower part of the right leg .
IncidentEventDetails: 1000-1030 / 04-June -2018 :- Job planned in daily meeting one day before for repairing the ballast water eductor valve as same was not holding .  05-JUNE-2018 :-  0600 :- E/R Manned & Vessel Transiting Old Bahamas Channel with watch keeping level-2 (Master Duty officer, Look out and AB in Bridge ) .  Weather :- Calm Sea .  0800 :- C/O , Pumpman , 02 OS in pump room for opening the Valve .  1130 :- Valve dismantled from location .  1150 :- Valve picked up from location and brought on main deck by using pump room Davit .  1200 :- C/O breifed Pumpman . Bosun & both the OS to shift the Valve to Engine room workshop through skylight and using crane .  1300 :- Vessel outside of Old Bahamas Channel .  1310 :- Both the OS reached and Picked up the Valve kept on main Deck  and Started shifting to Engine room first Plateform leading to workshop.  1315 :- Chief officer , Bosun , Pumpman going toword the valve & heard the voice of OS -Vaishak and rushed towards the location breifing cadet to call ship staff and Master .  1320 :- Transferred to Hospital and was given first aid and was futher thoroughly examined for any more internal & external injury in presence of Master .No other injury found .  OS Deo was given rest and OS-Vaishak was kept near him for observation & Care .  1530 :- OS Deo was re-examined and was feeling better .  1700 :- OS Deo was re-examined and feeling normal with little pain in hand therefore was shifted to his cabin .
ImmediateAction: OS-Vaishak raised alerted the crew and ship's staff reached to spot for assistance . OS-Deo was Transferred to hospital . First Aid was given and thereafter  thoroughly examined for any more internal & external injury . No other injury found .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bruises on right Cheek ,Right hand Fore arm, Right leg below knee
DirectCause: Improper lifting action.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety meeting carried out. Training given to  all crew regarding transfer of heavy weight/ Slips, trips and falls
LessionLearnt: Heavy lift precautions to be taken during shifting.
ClosingNote: Heavy lift precautions to be taken during shifting.

IncidentDetails: 2/E DIGVIJAY KUMAR ALONG WITH FITTER RATHEESH KUMAR WENT TO INSPECT THE FUNNEL AREA FLOOR TO INVESTIGATE THE CAUSE OF RAIN WATER LEAKING INTO ENGINE ROOM. FIRST FITTER RATHEESH KUMAR CLIMBED UP THE VERTICAL LADDER 2ND LEVEL HALF-WAY THROUGH AND 2/E DIGVIJAY KUMAR JUST FOLLOWING HIM AND WAS CLIMBING THE FIRST VERTICAL LADDER AND ALMOST NEARING THE END OF THE FIRST VERTICAL LADDER, HIS HAND SLIPPED AND HIS WHOLE BODY SWUNG DUE TO SUDDEN ROLLING AND FELL FROM THAT HEIGHT TO THE IG BLOWER PLATFORM DECK CAUSING THE INJURY OF FRACTURE TO HIS LEFT ARM AND INJURY TO HIS LEFT SIDE.
IncidentEventDetails: - CLIMBING UP LADDER INSIDE FUNNEL  - LOST HOLD OF LEFT HAND  - SHIP'S ROLLING CAUSED HIS BODY  TOPPLED TO THE OTHER SIDE OF LADDER  - LOST CONTROL AND FELL TO THE PLATFORM UNDERNEATH THE LADDER PLATFORM,APPROX Ht- 25 Ft  - HIS BODY ENDED UP HITING THE RAILING AND TOPPLED ONTO THE DECK.
ImmediateAction: 2ND ENGINEER SHIFTED TO SHIP'S HOSPITAL, FIRST AID PROVIDED,MEDICAL ADVISE TAKEN FROM COMPANY'S DOCTOR AND MEDICATION GIVEN AS PER DOCTOR ADVISE. SAME TIME INFORMED ALL PARTIES CONCERNED.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2ND ENGINEER FELLL FROM VERTICAL LADDER WHILE CLIMBING UP INSIDE FUNNEL. SUFFERED WITH LEFT ARM/SHOULDER FRACTURE,SUSPECTED LEFT FOOT FRACTURE, INJURY TO NOSE        ( SUSPECTED NASAL BONE FRACTURE), INJURY TO LEFT EYE AREA AND RIGHT ARM. INCAPACITATED AND TRANSFERRED TO SHORE HOSPITAL FOR FURTHER TREATMENT.
DirectCause: IMPROPER HAND HOLDING/GRIP, SUDDEN ROLLING OF VESSEL, NO BARRIER OR GUARDS FITTED AROUND THE VERTICAL LADDER.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ADEQUATE BARRIERS/GUARD AROUND THE VERTICAL LADDER TO BE FITTED.
LessionLearnt: Lack of Situational Awareness
ReviewComments: Investigation report attached
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: During the Lunch break around 1235LT, after having lunch Ab Rajesh went to his cabin and twisted his Right knee while climbing the stairway from A deck to B deck.
ImmediateAction: Immediately shifted him to his cabin, applied pain relief spray and contacted company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: AB Rajesh got his right knee twisted while climbing the stairway from A deck to B deck during lunch break. Reported knee swelling and pain of scale 4 out of 10.
DirectCause: Due to casual walk, his knee got twisted.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Be more carefull while walking / climbing up the stairs.
ClosingNote: Medication and rest given, recovered

IncidentDetails: FITTER'S RIGHT HAND TWO FINGERS SUSTAINING MINOR CUT INJURIES.
IncidentEventDetails: AT 1330 HRS LT, FITTER ALONG WITH ELECTRICAL OFFICER PROCEEDED FOR A/C ROOM, WHILE GETTING OUT OF CHANGING ROOM, DUE TO SUDDEN HEAVY ROLLING, FITTER'S TWO FINGERS OF HIS RIGHT HAND GOT ENTRAPPED IN THE CHANGING ROOM DOOR, WHICH LED TO MINOR CUT ON HIS BOTH FINGERS.
ImmediateAction: FITTER IMMEDIATELY TAKEN TO HOSPITAL AND WAS GIVEN MEDICAL FIRST AID BY 2ND OFFICER, AFTER THE TREATMENT FITTER WAS GIVEN COMPLETE REST.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: FITTER'S RIGHT HAND HAVING CUT WOUNDS ON TWO FINGERS, PRESENTLY GIVEN FIRST AID.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CREW ADVICED TO TAKE PRECAUTIONS WHILE WALKING/ENTERING/LEAVING ENGINE ROOM WHEN VESSEL SUBJECTED TO HEAVY ROLLING AND ROUGH SEAS.
ClosingNote: Pls discuss this in the safety meeting which will serve as confirmation to corrective action action

IncidentDetails: 25May2018/1430UTC= 2nd Engr. Experience fever & chill so I checked his body temperature which is 42 degrees Celsius, so we reported to Dr. Balaji. We have given instruction from Dr. Balaji some medicines to intake by 2nd Engr.  Paracetamol 500mg 3xday after meal and Omeprazole 20mg 1xday after meal (all for 5 days).   We continuously monitor his body temperature. Every intake of paracetamol, for a few hours his body temperature went down but after 4 hours it fluctuates and rise up. We advise him to take a good rest and stop working.
IncidentEventDetails: 25May2018/1430UTC= 2nd Engr. Experience fever & chill so I checked his body temperature which is 42 degrees Celsius, so we reported to Dr. Balaji. We have given instruction from Dr. Balaji some medicines to intake by 2nd Engr.  Paracetamol 500mg 3xday after meal and Omeprazole 20mg 1xday after meal (all for 5 days).   We continuously monitor his body temperature. Every intake of paracetamol, for a few hours his body temperature went down but after 4 hours it fluctuates and rise up. We advise him to take a good rest and stop working.   26May2018 at Anchorage Durban, S.A= 2nd Engr. Started to have a slight cough. We continue monitor his body temperature and which time to time going up and down. There are times he feels better and times that his body becomes week maybe because of the fever.  As Dr. Balaji prescription of Paracetamol for 5 days we thought that this is just a normal fever which normally we encounter at home which could be cure for a period of few days.    27-28May2018= we are waiting at the anchorage for berthing for 2nd. Eng. to have his check up on shore. Still his fever won’t go down and continue in the range of 38.5 minimum.   29May2018= 1700lt vessel arrive at berth, Durban but as per agent advise that crew will have their check up on the next day 30th may 2018.   30May2018/0200LT= 2nd Eng. Complain that his heart palpitates and cannot sleep so I check his blood pressure 125/85 , pulse 87 , respiratory rate 19 , body temperature 39 which are all normal except his body temperature. I informed master of his situation. We advise him to relax and don’t think for any negative situation that can trigger more of his breathing. He said he’ll be ok and by morning time the agent will pick them up for checkup.
ImmediateAction: SENT TO SHORE FOR MEDICAL CHECK UP.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: BRONCHITIS ON HIS LEFT LUNG / POSIBLE CN PALSY.
DirectCause: OVER FATIGUE / OVER EXPOSURE TO ENGINE ROOM HEAT AND HOT WORK JOBS
Proposedcorrectiveactions: LIMIT WORK HOURS  /  GIVE PROPER REST PERIOD
Corrective Action: LIMIT WORK HOURS  /  GIVE PROPER REST PERIOD
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Anyone who is feeling unwell should report to the department head and take appropriate medication. No  critical work should be assigned till the seafarer is completely recovered.

IncidentDetails: During unmooring operation, when slacking one of the aft spring, OS got his right hand pulled and felt jerk causing sever pain & swelling on his right wrist. Suspect wrist dislocation & muscle pull.
IncidentEventDetails: 1110 - Commenced unmooring (fwd springs & sternlines).  1132 - Aft springs cast off. Time of injury.  1136 - All lines onboard.    On the previous day due to strong winds & one aft breast rope parting, extra mooring lines had been passed on the ship's bitts. During unmooring, moderate winds & tidal current was experienced which caused strain on the mooring lines. During letting go one of the aft spring line on the ship's bitts, the right hand of the OS got pinched between the rope, causing injury.
ImmediateAction: Patient moved to hospital. Affected area sprayed with pain relief spray. Ice pack applied to quell swelling. Administered water & Ibuprofen 400mg tab, kept under observation & bed rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Right wrist area swollen due to impact with mooring rope jerking due to tension on the mooring line whilke casting off. Suspect dislocation / hairline fracture, but same cannot be determined now due to the existing swelling. Patient initially in severe pain but found in better condition after medical treatment onboard. To be referred to shore orthopaedic doctor at next port, as per company doctor's advice.
DirectCause: After casting off sternlines & fwd spring, pilot advised to cast off 3 of 4 breast lines aft, and 2 of 4 breast lines fwd.But aft could not let go 3 breast lines as aft would have to release 4th breast line to let go other breast lines due to wrong securing by shore mooring gang.  Mean while after letting go 2 breast lines fwd, bow started coming out as the port chain was having some weight.Then pilot used engine to avoid stern going toward the jetty.All the lines were told to cast off and the ship was out by more than 20 mtrs.Aft spring were the last to cast off, of which 2 ropes were on drum & other 2 spare ropes were fast on bitt. While letting go the aft springs whole weight came on the 2 ropes on the bitt. Hence when slacking the aft spring the OS got his right hand pulled and felt jerk causing sever pain & swelling on his right wrist.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Better communication with pilot / tug / ship during mooring / unmooring operations. Plan to be discussed with all involved. Crew to be constantly reminded of hazards when handling mooring lines under tension.
LessionLearnt: Poor work practices  Lack of basic seamenship  lack of experience
ReviewComments: AN ASSESSMENT OF SAFETY SHOULD BE UNDERTAKEN INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO: - Reviews undertaken during design or to assess effectiveness of the original design basis of mooring equipment and pattern. - Operations interfaces and exposures for personnel in or around the mooring workspace (also see EA.). - Measures taken, or required to be taken, to eliminate risks or mitigate harm to personnel and/or damage to equipment. Identifying mooring hazards prior to operation is a very crucial task that prepares the vessel for a safe operation. These hazards may be identified by the following, • Near Miss reporting – e.g. Person standing in impact zone / poor stowage of ropes. On board inspections (Condition of ropes and equipment) - e.g. Broken rope strands/ hydraulic leaks. • Hazard identification during tool box talks – e.g. Unusual mooring patterns & rope leads, language barrier lessons learnt from incidents / experience sharing / best practices from fleet assessment of the mooring operation – e.g. by designated crew member, superintendent, auditor etc. • Above factors to be taken in to consideration while preparing risk assessment for mooring operation and identifying proper control measures. Below mention is the table for Pre-mooring preparation.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: Deck fitter reported pain in right testicle and groin area.
IncidentEventDetails: Deck fitter along with one AB lifted chekker plate from rope store to main deck to fabricate steps for companion ladder.  On the 11th June 18 he reported pain in right testicle and groin area and feeling some vein pull on right leg.
ImmediateAction: Advised him to take proper rest and do not lift any heavy weight
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pain in right testicle and groin area and feeling some vein pull on right leg.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Enhance safety awareness, training, videos on manual handling and tool box meeting.
Corrective Action: Enhance safety awareness, training, videos on manual handling and tool box meeting.
ClosingNote: Discuss this in the safety meeting and revert with the minutes

IncidentDetails: Tr.wiper was cleaning  On AE # 1 removed T/C silencer , While lifiting to make it upside down his hand  got slipped because of cotton gloves and left his finger got stuck between frame and Silencer cover.Finger nail got chaffed.
IncidentEventDetails: Due to improper lifiting and improper PPE used leads to incident.
ImmediateAction: Job was stopped , He was bought up to ECR ,Finger was cleaned throughly and first aid given to stop blood , later wound was cleaned and cream applied before bandage applied.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Left hand Fore finger nail damaged
DirectCause: Due to improper lifiting and incorrect  PPE used leads to incident.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper Training for PPE use to be conducted prior every job ,importance of Right tool for the right job to be briefed prior commencing job.
ClosingNote: Tool Box Meeting to include usage of PPE prior to carrying out the tasks

IncidentDetails: OS-Hajare Ram suffered a catch on the lower back L4-L6 area , while lifting tank cleaning machine head , resulted in his incapacitation for duty.
ImmediateAction: Administered first aid  by way of pain balm/ spray and doctor advise sought.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Back pain
DirectCause: Direct Cause: 1. Lifting heavy weight improperly resulted in back injury  Indirect Cause: 1. Improper Technique 2. Inadequate hazard identification (Heavy weight)   Root Cause: 1. Lack of Compliance: COSWP for manual handling not employed, Improper lifting procedure 2. Lack of Planning: Hazards were not analysed, Inadequate supervision, lifting procedure not discussed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discuss correct lifting techniques with all crew onboard.
Corrective Action: Discuss correct lifting techniques with all crew onboard.
LessionLearnt: Heavy weight lifting should not be attempted unless proper hazards have been assessed and  alternative way of handling should be considered. COSWP to be followed when handling heavy weights
ClosingNote: Coirrective/preventive action in place to avoid recurrence.

IncidentDetails: Finger injury
IncidentEventDetails: While vessel drifting at Cape Town OPL was subjected to moderate rolling due to swell condition in the drifting area, master went to the bridge for usual check. After opening the self-closing door and about to close it, the vessel rolls that resulted in abrupt closing of the door and to prevent it from heavy banging, the master tried to hold the door but unfortunately caught his right index finger against the locking wedge of the door.
ImmediateAction: Wound dressing applied.
DirectCause: Vessel rolling during bad weather.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ship personnel should be fully inform of anticipated bad weather and have safety awareness of the condition of vessel movements.
ClosingNote: MASTER / CREW BRIEFED

IncidentDetails: While dancing in the party carried out on "C" deck stbd side Bosun Mr Pardhan slipped & fell face first causing medium cut on his chin, below lower lip & lower lip.
ImmediateAction: Taken to hospital, first aid given to stop bleeding & dressed the wounds.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Medium cut in lower jaw.  Cut of lower lip & below lower lip.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Dance activity to be stopped if floor becomes wet/slippery or unfavourable for any reason.  2. Require to have more situational awareness & change plans as per prevailing conditions.
ClosingNote: Master and all crew are advised to: • Organize parties within accommodation places. • Comply with Company’s alcohol policy. Please ensure that Beer consumption does not exceed 2 cans/ person. Watch officers must not drink 3 Hours before watch.  • Please ensure safety of fellow crew members always. Have dance floor clear of any objects that can cause harm.  • If Barbeque party is planned, please ensure a safety audit is done by the safety officer / Master. a) The barbecue grill must be kept at a safe location, away from any flammable object & safe from blowing wind.  b) No flammable liquids to be used to ignite fire. c) Have fire extinguisher and fire hose ready for use.  • If party is conducted on open decks: a) The deck should be clean and not be slippery. b) Adequate guard against any accidental fall  c) Well-lit area. But lighting should not affect safe navigation.  d) All crew to consume alcohol without breaching the company alcohol policy.

IncidentDetails: Cadet Aldrin Knee got dislocated while routine shifting of chairs from duty messroom.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.Cadet A & B given Job to shift chairs from Duty messroom for cleaning to be done later.  2.Cadet B was moving the chairs from duty messroom and keeping outside pipe duct.  3.Cadet A was moving chairs from pipe duct door and arranging inside the pipe duct.  4.Cadet A tried lifting the chair in a wrong manner with his knee positioned near edge of the chair.  5.Immediately after lifting the chair the it got misbalanced and struck his knee.  6.The edge of the chair hit the knee at a weak point causing dislocation of the knee joint.
ImmediateAction: Cadet was immediately immobilised in his position.   Office immediately informed annd urgent medical advice taken from company approved doctor Mr Balaji.  Pain kill was administered as per doctor's advice.    While trying to shift the cadet from Alleyway to hospital with the knee supported, the knee bone slipped back in position due to pressure.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cadet's Knee initially got dislocated while lifting the chair.    Later While attempting to shift the cadet from Alleyway to hospital with the knee supported, the knee bone slipped back in position due to pressure.     Slight swelling the knee observed after relocation of knee. Cadet kept immobilised till evaluation by shore doctor.    Proper medical evaluation to be carried out by shore doctor to assess extent of damage to the knee / ligament.
DirectCause: Lifting of chair carried out in improper way.  Lack of knowledge/experience.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Injury discussed with all crew and proper lifting procedures discussed.  Situational awareness while carrying out task discussed.
Corrective Action: Ship Staff briefed on effective Supervision when involving Trainees
LessionLearnt: Following Factors reviewed, including Weather, Alcohol Breach, Rest Hour Breach, Tool box meeting, and observed all necessary criteria met by Ship Staff. The Chair had Slipped from the Cadets Hand and hit is knee Causing the dislocation
ReviewComments: Safety Culture onboard observed to be good. No concerns raised by Trainees
ClosingNote: Medical Report and necessary Photo's attached for reference

IncidentDetails: Oiler injured his middle and ring finger during loading of incineration operation.
IncidentEventDetails: While shifting garbage drums, for loading incinerator, Oiler was holding the drum from the base and tried to roll the drum while rocking it. In this process, his fingers got stuck between the splintered  wooden pallet and the drum. Upon removing the gloves, observed the skin near the base of the nail had peeled off.
ImmediateAction: MEDICAL FIRST AID WAS GIVEN IMMEDIATELY
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The soft Skin below the nails on the middle and ring finger has peeled off.
DirectCause: NORMAL COTTON GLOVES FAILED TO GIVE ADEQUATE IMPACT PROTECTION.  IMPROPER WORK PROCEDURE-  DRUM WAS TRIED TO MOVE BY HOLDING THE BASE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: HEAVY DUTY GLOVES TO BE USED WHILE SHIFTING DRUMS.
Corrective Action: HEAVY DUTY GLOVES TO BE USED WHILE SHIFTING DRUMS.
ClosingNote: First Aid Case. Briefed all Ship Staff with reference to HSM and Code of Safe Working practices

IncidentDetails: Minor cut on Chief Cook's Left Hand Index finger while cutting vegetables in galley
IncidentEventDetails: Chief Cook was carrying out routine job of cutting vegetable.   Vessel was experiencing slight to moderate rolling in rough seas and swell.  Galley phone rang and cook turned his head to instruct GS to pick up phone, while the hands of chief cook were still engaged in cutting.   At this point of time, the vegetable slipped out of his hand and had a cut on his finger
ImmediateAction: GS Immediately informed Bridge and master.  First aid administered by GS.  Disinfecting and Bandage done by Master
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut on Right nad Index Finger and same treated with First Aid.    Cleaning of wound and bandage change to be done every 12 hrs for first 24 hrs
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Chief Cook to ensure that he is only concentrating on one job at a time.  If some other job needs his attention, he should stop cutting and attend to other jobs before he resumes cutting.  Monthly food handling and safety training to include guidiance on galley safety.
ClosingNote: It is important to ensure that routine jobs do not shift our focus away from the task at hand. Complacency and a lack of situational awareness during routine jobs can lead to such incidents. Please hold a tool box meeting to ensure that all the crew is briefed about occupational safety and the need to carry out each job with proper attention.

IncidentDetails: One of the Crew member reported irritatiion and pain in the left eye after completion of the daily working hours
IncidentEventDetails: While at anchorage.Crew memeber was carrying out maintenance job derusting and painting, After completion of the job he reported  irritatiion and pain in the left eye
ImmediateAction: Sterile Eye wash was given, Tetracycline eyedrop administered. Crew was sent ashore for further treatement.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While carrying out the maintenance job, Foreign particle entered into the eye of the crew, He reported irritation and pain in the left eye, First aid including eye wash was given.
DirectCause: The crew was donning the safety goggle closed type for Chipping as discussed and instructed in the tool box meeting, However the straps of the goggle were not fully tightened enabling a very fine opening through which the dust / rust particle could have entered the eye.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew to tighten the straps of the safety goggle fully to avoid any opening, Training was imparted to all crew members regarding correct use of ppe and their significance.
ClosingNote: Please refer to attached IIR

IncidentDetails: Eye injury with Stored Metal Plate in Engine room Workshop
IncidentEventDetails: Motorman was working on Drilling machine in E/R workshop. During the task he turned inadvertantly and hit his left eye on the corner edge of Stored metal plate. This caused minor bruise on eye lids & reddening of eye.
ImmediateAction: Immediately gave First aid to the Motor man.  Plate corner edge was temperorly padded with Soft Cotton Cloth.   Later Plate rearranged to provide more work space during operating drill machine.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Bruise/abration on Eye lid
DirectCause: impropoer tool box meeting. Impropoer PPE as when working on drilling machine, no eye protection was worn.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Plate Repositioned to avail max  working space for personnel movement. Edge Highlighted to be conspicious. Briefed Staff, to discuss again in Safety Meeting.
Corrective Action: the metal plate be removed from the stowed place to remove the hazard near drilling machine.
ClosingNote: ensure proper PPE is always worn in compliance with PPE matrix.

IncidentDetails: During Routine bunker tank sounding, 4/E slipped near the walkway crossover platform and got injured below left knee.
IncidentEventDetails: Condition - Wet deck due to drizzling.  1430 LT: 4/E took sounding of 1(P) bunker tank & was proceeding to Stbd side using passage way between accommodation & E/R.To avoid getting wet due to continous drizzle, he was walking briskly & due to which slipped just before the walkway crossover. Resultant, got hit with walkway crossover support angle bar incurring a severe cut (1.5 inches wide) below left knee.  Observed no other injury/bruises on the body as was donned with required PPE.
ImmediateAction: Administered First Aid with wound dressing & Tablet Ibuprofen 400Mg (OD).  All crew were briefed to walk with extreme caution during rains / wet deck.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 4/E suffered a severe wide cut ( 1.5 Inch wide) below left knee and skin displaced creating a gap of 8-10 mm. No other injury observed.
DirectCause: Wet deck due to rains.  Walking briskly to avoid rains.  Lack of judgement to postpone job in view of changing weather condition.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew were briefed to walk with extreme caution during rains / wet deck and instructed to work without haste.
Corrective Action: SDFGSDFSDF
ClosingNote: as per vessel comments

IncidentDetails: While mooring at Oro Bay one line got stuck in a forward mooring winch and was not able to heave or released. Stopper was taken to take out the stuck part of rope. The stuck portion of rope released with sudden jerk, CO was struck by the rope in stomach.
IncidentEventDetails: One mooring line got stuck in a forward mooring winch and was not able to heave or released.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Abdominal reported by CO.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Master to discuss this in the next safety minutes, where required ropes to be coiled cris cross on the drums to avoid ropes getting stuck in the drums when it is taught, also vessel can prepare a rope sausage which can be slipped between the lead rope to avoid it getting stuck in the drum.
ClosingNote: Corrective action carried out by vessel.

IncidentDetails: Man-overboard incident occurred while securing the gangway after disembarkation of Pilot at Kanmon Kaiko in the Sea of Japan.  Bosun was picked up by another vessel assisting in the SAR operation and delivered back to  own vessel by coast guard. Bosun found to be fit physically & mentally and had no signs of visible  injuries.
IncidentEventDetails: Around 2200 LT after dropping pilot, OS was on the accommodation ladder with life-vest and safety  harness. Bosun and Deck Cadet were assisting on deck for retrieving the Pilot ladder. While OS was  removing the rope on the middle section of inboard collapsible railing, Bosun noticed that the rope  has entangled in the securing socket of railing. He immediately called out to the OS to stop lowering  of railing and rushed to clear the rope. The OS did not hear the warning by the Bosun, and unaware of the Bosun’s presence, lowered the collapsible railing. The railing hit Bosun on his face & caused Bosun to lose his balance, thus falling overboard
ImmediateAction: Man-overboard alarm raised followed by transmission of Distress alert on VHF Ch16 to all ships in the vicinity. Bosun was picked up by another vessel assisting in the SAR operation and delivered back to  own vessel by coast guard. Bosun found to be fit physically & mentally and had no signs of visible  injuries
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Abrasion under the upper lip.
DirectCause: 1. Appropriate PPE not used by person working near ship side.    2. Communication gap between Deck hands, as OS was not carrying walkie-talkie while working.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To initiate Document change request to review HSM section 2.16.4 to revise the section on minimum PPE for Work Over side and to revise the section for mode of communication between person working over side / work supervisor and duty officer.
Corrective Action: Checking the proper use & effectiveness of PPE on board over the next few operations.
LessionLearnt: 1.Company procedures for work overboard to be made more precise as to who are to be involved in securing the gangway to don life-vest or life jacket during the entire period of rigging up or securing of the gangway / pilot ladder / combination ladder. 2.To remind all ships that it is important to peruse the Company’s Emergency procedure checklists during an actual incident so that a verification can be made that all the necessary actions are correctly carried out. 3. The communication system between supervisor and working hand to be improved.
ClosingNote: The incident investigation completed. All corrective actions are in place.

IncidentDetails: Kindly note at 1100 Hrs LT, Engine Trainee Fitter reported with an injury to his right eye lid, while trying to open up FO Overflow sounding pipe cap. While getting up after trying to pick up a hammer from the deck, he lost his balance and hit his eyelid on the edge of the CO2 room vent next to the Sounding pipe.The hammer was kept under the same CO2 room vent. Upon inspection found that there is only a superficial cut on the skin over the eyelid and no major bleeding. No major swelling observed and no pain reported by the crew member.
ImmediateAction: First aid given and dressing of the wound carried out. Vessel has consulted a Doctor as well as Eye specialist and has carried out checks on eye and eyelids as advised by them. They also have confirmed it as a superficial cut and has advised dressing and amoxycillin antibiotics for 5 days to avoid any infection.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Superficial cut on the right eyelid.
DirectCause: Fitter Lost his balance while getting up after picking up the hammer from deck.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out to all crew members on the accident and possibilities of injuries during routine work. Also emphasised the importance of PPE, which reduced the impact of injury to eye in this case as he was wearing proper PPE.
ClosingNote: Concur to vessel CA and PA

IncidentDetails: While shifting a reel of hoisting wire inside the mast house no.2(S), it accidentaly swing and hits the Left Middle Thigh of an A.B causing minor bruises and muscle sprain.
IncidentEventDetails: 0600LT- Toolbox meeting carried out.  0630LT- Deck crew proceeded on deck and start shifting a reel of old spare hoisting wire to make a space for the new hoisting wire received from previoust port of call. Vessel crew intend to prepare the old spare of hoisting wire for deck crane no.2 wire rope renewal and at the same time to secure the new spare.  0705LT- While shifting the reel of old hoisting wire spare using chain blocks and straps, it accidentaly swing and hit the left middle thigh of an AB causing minor bruises and muscle sprain.
ImmediateAction: Duty officer was informed of the incident which in turn informed the Master. Duty officer attend to the area of incident and assess the situation of the A.B. He was then brought to the ship`s hospital using a rigid stretcher and administered cold compress for first aid, given rest, initial 2 tabs of Ibuprofien and put under observation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Muscle Sprain with Minor Bruises in the Left Middle Thigh
DirectCause: Improper position while shifting heavy objects on board while in seaway.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Increase the safety awareness and vigilance of the crew while in the workplace. A more detailed toolbox meeting prior the job.
Corrective Action: Increase the safety awareness and vigilance of the crew while in the workplace. A more detailed toolbox meeting prior the job.
ClosingNote: CA noted in order

IncidentDetails: Ord.seaman slipped three to four steps from the accomadation companion way and the person suffered following  1-Severe muscle pain on both sides of the back bone about 15cm above hip  2-Pain on the left hand shoulder  3-Finding difficulty in walking and bending down
IncidentEventDetails: Ord.seaman was assigned to  switch on the power of provision crane at the C deck, adequate lightings were available. After completion of his task while stepping down to A deck to assist bosun for securing suez canal mooring boat through companion way  he got  slipped down from the last 4 steps of the stairs and tried holdong railing with his left hand while falling. His back hit one of the step and caused severe muscle pain on both sides of the back bone about 15cm above the hip and pain on the left hand shoulder joint
ImmediateAction: PAIN RELIEF SPRAY AND IBUPROFEN TABLET GIVEN FOR PAIN RELIEF,BED REST ADVISED,MEDICAL ADVISE SEEKED FROM DR.A.H.BALAJI
DirectCause: 1-some salt deposit and sand deposit made stairs railing slightly slippery  2-At dark hours was unable to make out wet condition of the step with the lighting
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Holding the railing with both hands when using stairs  2. Controlled movements when using stairs. No running when using stairs.
Corrective Action: All Ship Staff briefed with reference to Health & Safety Manual and Code of Safe Working practices during Safety Comitee Meeting
LessionLearnt: No concerns observed in Weather conditions, Compliance with Alcohol Policy, Compliance with Rest Hour requirements. IncidentDetails: Deep cut on the right hand point finger and thumb while opening tinned meat can
IncidentEventDetails: After 2000-0000  cargo watch at port of Trieste the able seaman Sasi kumar had a deep cut on his right hand point finger and thumb  while he was trying to open the tinned meat can by knife at the crew pantry ,the same caused heavy bleeding and dizziness.
ImmediateAction: Intake of sufficient water,Wound cleaned with betadine solution,dressed the wound with betadine ointment ,finger splinter used for immobilisation of the finger,1 ibuprofen tablet given for pain relief, advised to keep the hand above chest level.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut on the right hand point finger and thumb
DirectCause: Procedure not followed to open the tinned meat can
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Crew meeting and briefing about the incident/injury ,cause of injury discussed,  proper procedures and instructions on the cans briefed  2. No Tinned meat to keep during night when no proper tool available to open same. No other tools to use which is not assigned for such things.
ReviewComments: First Aid Case
ClosingNote: Briefed all Ship Staff by Master to prevent recurrence

IncidentDetails: Deck Cadet's right hand thumb finger got pinched between pilot ladder spreader and ships guard railing.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was proceeding from singapore anchorage to Shell Bukom Terminal.   2335 - 2340 Pilot change( PSA pilot away - Bukom Pilot onboard )  After pilot change bosun was instructed to secure the combination on the stbd side. While bosun was securing pilot ladder, he was having difficulty getting the spreader over the ship's railing. Ch Off and cadet were passing by to go for mooring stations and stopped to help him, in the process while pulling the spreader over the railing Cadets thumb finger got pinched between the spreader and railing.
ImmediateAction: First Aid administered and bleeding stopped. Medical Advice requested.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut on the tip of Right Hand Thumb finger.
DirectCause: Incomplete supervision due to various tasks ongoing concurrently. Lack of attention/distraction while in the vicinity of moving parts.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Priority of supervision to be altered with regard to trainees. Keeping them under observation at all times.
Corrective Action: a) Reiterate to impart KYT to all the trainee in the structured manner.
LessionLearnt: Not donning PPE and in this case the leather gloves is one of the root cause for the incident. Trainees to always carry out assigned tasks under supervision of seniors.
ClosingNote: Not donning PPE and in this case the leather gloves is one of the root cause for the incident. Trainees to always carry out assigned tasks under supervision of seniors.

Corrective Action: Instructed all to stop and take a break from HP wash when distracted, loss of concentration  and/or felt boredom.

IncidentDetails: Fitter's left hand middle finger got injured during shifting plywood from Forward Bosun Store to Engine room.
IncidentEventDetails: On 7/8/2018,  Fitter and Oiler and Oiler were assigned to bring plywood from forward Bosun store to engineroom.Both fitter and oiler were lowering the plywood through the steering gear room door using ropes. Fitter was on top position and oiler was at botton position.Due to mis communication between the two, while trying to turn the plywood, it slided.When plywood slided, Fitter's left hand middle finger got between railing and plywood. Both were wearing cotton gloves and helmet during the entire operation.
ImmediateAction: Immediately, he was rushed to Ship's hospital and first aid given.  Taken off from Work and rested.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Left Hand Middle Finger nail got crushed a bit showing blood clot and the skin has peeled off  at the top on the finger.

IncidentDetails: Crew involved/injured - OS Tiongson, Francis Julian C.  While preparing all lashing materials on deck a sudden gust wind with dust and light objects flown towards the OS. He felt there was a foreign bodies penetrates in his left eye despite that he was wearing protective goggles at the time of incident. His instinct/reactions was to rubbed his eyes with his bare hands.
IncidentEventDetails: 1300LT/06th - Started working on deck for afternoon shift.  1300LT to 1400LT/06th - Laid out hog wires and foot wires on Collapsibles at Hatch 2 Port side.  1420LT/06th - Bosun noticed that 1 foot wire was short.  1430LT/06th - Bosun assigned OS Tiongson and 1 AB to bring out 1 foot wire from Bosun store.                          While walking towards the Bosun store a sudden gust wind with dust/light                          objects flown towards them. The OS felt something a foreign bodies that irritates his                          left eye. His instinct was to rubbed his eyes with his bare hands. He was wearing a                          protective goggle at the time of incident but the goggles supplied onboard has a                                  Moisture chamber/holes on both sides where the tiny objects penetrates.  1500LT/06th - He was brought to the ship's hospital and treated him with an Eye wash.
ImmediateAction: He was brought immediately to the ship's hospital and administered him with an eye wash.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew involved/injured - OS Tiongson, Francis Julian C.  While preparing all lashing materials on deck a sudden gust wind with dust and light objects flown towards the OS. He felt there was a foreign bodies penetrates in his left eye despite that he was wearing protective goggles at the time of incident. His instinct/reactions was to rubbed his eyes with his bare hands.
DirectCause: Type of goggle used which was not safe at all as a protective equipment. The design of goggle supplied onboard has a moisture chamber/holes that tends to penetrate a tiny object.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Any item supplied is as per requisition from ship staff according to IMPA code. Ship staff to ensure and check, prior ordering any item as it is safe to use. Also, in near future, please add comments " totally enclosed   googles" while ordering.
Corrective Action: Should supply a high standard and a totally closed goggles.
ClosingNote: To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: While adjusting mooring lines, crewmember was walking towards the winch brake to tighten it, when he slipped on the deck and hit his lower lip against the winch brake handle. This resulted in a small punture wound to his lower lip and damage to his tooth. At the time of the injury, the vessel experienced a sharp passing shower, which made the deck slippery.
ImmediateAction: Crewmember was provided first aid. Wound was cleaned up externally using Iodine solution and band-aid applied. Crewmember was also administered 1 Brufen Tablet (400 mg) dose for the pain and asked to rest for 3 hrs. He will be sent ashore for a precautionary medical evaluation as he has reported pain in his gums and two of his teeth have appeared to come a little loose from the gums.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: See attached photos. A minor puncture wound to the lower lip and damage to a tooth.
DirectCause: HUMAN FACTOR: Complacency  ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS: Slippery deck due to rain
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discuss incident with all crew. Highlight the dangers of complacency and to remain focused on task at hand, keeping in mind that changing environmental conditions call for re-assessment of the task at hand.  Inspect the mooring areas and determine if the non-skid areas provide sufficient protection against slips/ trips when wet.
Corrective Action: Is the proposed corrective actions discussed onboard? What is the present status of the OS? What is the result of the mooring area inspection? Please attach some pictures.
ClosingNote: Noted that all discussed CA implemented.

IncidentDetails: V/l was doing mooring operation for making fast to SPM 3 in Jebel Dhanna at 0418 hrs on 30 Aug 2018. While the pick up rope was collected in to the drum,the rope started piling up in one side of the drum due to the angle of the pedestal roller and the short distance between the pedestal roller and drum. The bosun tried to guide the rope towards the emty side of the drum resulting his fingers got stuck between the rope and the pedestal roller. His little finger got a deep cut and small cut in the middle finger.
IncidentEventDetails: 30/0136: v/l arrived and picked up pilot for berthing at Jebel Dhanna.  30/0330: Tug made fast fwd stbd bow.  30/0400: Mooring team stand by fwd consisting of C/O, Bosun ,AB and 2 OS  30/0418:Commenced mooring.  30/0422:SPM pick up rope on board and started coiling in mooring drum.  30/0423:The rope started coiling in one side of the drum.  30/0424:The mooring gang pushed the rope and put in to the split drum.  30/0424:The mooring rope started sliping out of the split drum and started piling up in to one side of the main drum.  30/0425: Bosun tried to pull the rope back in to the split drum  30/0425: Bosuns hand got crushed between the pedestal roller and rope. Stopped heaving the winch and observed that his little finger is inured badly.  30/0428: Bosun  shifted to the hospital and first aid given.  30/0615: Made arrangement for shifting the Bosun to hospital ashore.Agent and Bosun disembarked from vessel.
ImmediateAction: First aid provided,crew member taken to hospital ashore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep wound in the little finger which require medical assistance ashore
DirectCause: 1.Lack of seamanship. Working with hand close to the pedestal roller.  2.Design of the mooring equipments. The pedestal roller is placed very close to the mooring winch and the ropes get coiled up only in split drum.   3.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Crew must be trained for the safe working practise during mooring operations.
Corrective Action: 01. Crew members to be trained on safe mooring operations. Importance of eye to eye contact, person in-charge not directly getting involved in mooring operations, snap back danger zone, hands free operation and any other associated to safe mooring operations, including items specific to the vessel’s design.
ClosingNote: Please refer to attached IIR on the finger injury to Bosun. Bosun was debriefed in office by Capt Moses and Capt Vijayaraghavan.

IncidentDetails: Collision with Fishing Boat / East China Sea
IncidentEventDetails: AT 2340LT / 11 OCT 2017 VESSEL OBSERVED THE FISHING BOAT CROSSING FROM STBD TO PORT.  3/O ALTERED COURSE. TO STBD TO KEEP CLEAR OF THE BOAT. CPA WAS 0.6 MILES AND BOW CROSSING WAS MORE THAN 1 MILE. VESSEL MONITORED THE BOAT CLOSELY AND OBSERVED IT WAS CROSSING.  AFTER IT CROSSED OVER THE BOAT THEN ALTERED TO HER PORT SIDE. ON SEEING IT COMING TO HER PORT SIDE OWN VESSEL CHANGED TO HAND STEERING WITH HELMSMAN AND FURTHER ALTERED TO STBD.  GAVE LONG BLAST ON WHISTLE.   12/0005LT FLASHED ALDIS LAMP AND CALLED MASTER.  MASTER CAME AND ON KNOWING THE SITUATION GAVE HARD STBD ON THE WHEEL.  ON GOING OVER TO STBD SIDE COULD SEE THE BOAT PASSING ABOUT 30 METRES FROM THE SHIP SIDE ON OWN VESSEL’S STBD SIDE.  LATER THE SAME BOAT WAS SEEING MOVING AWAY AT 8 KNOTS FROM THE LOCATION OF CLOSE QUARTER.  VESSEL STOPPED HER ENGINES AND TRIED TO CONTACT THE FISHING BOAT ON VHF 16 CONTINUOUSLY BUT IT DID NOT REPLY AND THE BOAT MOVED AWAY. VESSEL THEN TURNED AROUND AND RETURNED BACK AT THE SAME LOCATION TO ASSESS THE AREA AND CHECK IF ALL WAS OK AND IF ANY ASSISTANCE WAS REQUIRED.  VESSEL KEPT CALLING ON VHF FOR ANY ASSISTANCE IF REQUIRED BUT THE FISHING BOAT HAD ALREADY MOVED AWAY FROM THE POSITION.    OWN VESSEL MANOEUVRED IN AROUND THE AREA TO CHECK ALL IN ORDER. ONLY SOME FLASHING LIGHT AND MARKERS WERE OBSERVED IN THE NEARBY VICINITY. VESSEL KEPT CALLING ON VHF THROUGHOUT BUT NO REPLY FROM THE BOAT WHICH HAD ALREADY LEFT THAT AREA.  AT 0235LT VESSEL RESUMED ITS VOYAGE WHEN EVIDENCE OF FURTHER ASSISTANCE WAS NOT  REQUIRED. WIND FORCE WAS OBSERVED TO BE FORCE 7 AND SEA ABOUT 2.5 M.  VESSEL LATER OBSERVED ONE ANOTHER FISHING BOAT ZHELINGYU 43112 PASSING THROUGH THE SAME AREA.
ImmediateAction: VESSEL STOPPED HER ENGINES AND TRIED TO CONTACT THE FISHING BOAT ON VHF 16 CONTINUOUSLY BUT IT DID NOT REPLY AND THE BOAT MOVED AWAY. VESSEL THEN TURNED AROUND AND RETURNED BACK AT THE SAME LOCATION TO ASSESS THE AREA AND CHECK IF ALL WAS OK AND IF ANY ASSISTANCE WAS REQUIRED.  VESSEL KEPT CALLING ON VHF FOR ANY ASSISTANCE IF REQUIRED BUT THE FISHING BOAT HAD ALREADY MOVED AWAY FROM THE POSITION.    OWN VESSEL MANOEUVRED IN AROUND THE AREA TO CHECK ALL IN ORDER. ONLY SOME FLASHING LIGHT AND MARKERS WERE OBSERVED IN THE NEARBY VICINITY. VESSEL KEPT CALLING ON VHF THROUGHOUT BUT NO REPLY FROM THE BOAT WHICH HAD ALREADY LEFT THAT AREA.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel was informed by authorities regarding 6 Missing Persons, 4 injured Crew and loss of Fishing boat.
DirectCause: Analysis of the Navigation Procedures: . The OOW was using the X band radar to monitor the situation and make assessment of the risk of collision.  Instantaneous errors in the ARPA Target data which may arise due to change of course of own vessel may not have been understood and hence not accounted for. This would lead to incorrect assessment of the actions of the target vessel.   The Range, CPA and TCPA of the vessel FV ZHE LING was 8.875 nm,  0.871 nm, 25 mins respectively when she was first painted as an AIS target at 2340 hrs. The OOW (3/O) did not take any avoiding action as the CPA and TCPA of the vessel were acceptable to him.  Though the OOW was aware that the CPA was less than as mentioned in the Master’s standing orders, he did not take sufficient action to increase the CPA to 2NM nor did he call the Master. Being a give way vessel, OOW should have taken early and substantial action in-order to comply with Rule 16 of COLREGS to maintain the minimum required CPA.   Calling Master Third Officer did not call Master on encountering fishing traffic or when CPA of fishing vessels were less than 2 NM. He called Master only when FV ZHE LING suddenly turned to port after crossing the vessels bow and creating an immediate danger of collision.  Night Orders: The night orders were written daily during sea passage of the vessel.  On the 11th Oct 2017, Master wrote the night orders 2000 – 2400 hours watch. The night order mentions to keep clear of fishing traffic.  The order has been acknowledged and initialed by the OOW to mean that they have read and understood the orders.   Although the Master mentioned about keeping well clear of fishing traffic, he did not specify any additional requirement for the same in his night orders.  Master did not ensure about the proper Bridge composition enforcement prior arrival fishing traffic area.    Analysis of Regulation / Legislation breaches:  Container vessel as a give-way vessel in a crossing situation,  a) failed to keep a proper look-out and make a full appraisal and proper judgment of the situation and the risk of collision; b) failed to take an early and effective action in-order to increase the safe distance between the two vessels; c) failed to check the effectiveness of the action to avoid collision; d) failed to maintain necessary precaution on the possible action to be taken by the stand-on vessel after she crosses the bow of own vessel;  e) failed to take timely action as the best aid for avoiding collision when facing the development of an immediate danger of collision;   Review of Human Factors:  I. Human Performance Level Third Officer’s total experience as an independent watch-keeping Officer was about 11 months and had previous experience of keeping independent Bridge watches in the fishing traffic of South China Sea. Basis Third Officer’s experience, his performance level can be categorized as Rule based performance. Third Officer’s action of not keeping sufficient clearance from the fishing vessels was a rule based error.  One contributing factor is the OOW not verifying the displayed AIS information on radar is correct. The OOW believed that 0.5 M is an acceptable CPA. The OOW’s behaviour leading up to the collision suggests his risk appetite was high. There was little evidence of positive actions taken to compare information and ascertain the risk of collision. This behaviour can arise for a number of reasons, including the failure to remain situational aware, due to poor perception of potential risk and a failure to think ahead. This prevented him from recognizing the clear signals indicating that a dangerous situation (Risk of collision) was developing until it was too late to take effective action to prevent the collision.   The OOW also failed to have a full understanding of the characteristics of the fishing boats’ operation and navigation in Chinese coastal waters.   To be prepared for these risks, it is imperative that the OOW is aware of errors and limits of his navigation equipment. The OOW believed that 0.5 M is an acceptable closest point of approach (CPA). However, this could be dangerous if any of the parameters feeding the calculation are wrong such as the speed, or whether speed over ground or speed through water is chosen. The presented CPA on the radar might be different to the actual CPA. To be proactive and prepared for these risks is the best remedy. When in doubt, the captain should be called, as this extra resource might mean the difference between disaster or not.    AB Shanmugam Suresh has a total experience of about 19 months as Able Seaman. He had completed 1 month onboard the vessel. He was the lookout/helmsman for 2000-2400 hrs watch. He was reported to have been performing his duties as a helmsman/lookout efficiently.  AB Kishore has a total experience of about 33 months as Able Seaman. He had completed 1 month onboard the vessel. He was the lookout/helmsman for 0000-0400 hrs watch. He was reported to have been performing
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Corrective action as mentioned in the report
Corrective Action: Investigation to be carriedout and completed
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Investigation Report attached for reference

IncidentDetails: Vessel  planned to carry out maintenance of the 'BE AWARE OF PROPELLER " board which was fitted at  main deck aft  near  bay  74 . Job was duscussed in the daily tool box meeting .  Ordinary seaman Ajitesh was assigned to remove the "BE AWARE OF PROPELLER" board. The board was fitted with 4 botls to the ship side railing ,the  screw  on the outboard side and nut on the inboard side .   O/S Ajitesh started the work with proper PPE. He removed three bolts successfully by using spanner and screw driver.When removing the fourth/last  bolt he found that the bolt is rotating alongwith the screw and faced difficulty to remove it.. He planned to hold the inner part of bolt by the spanner and outer part of the  screw with screw and rotate . He was holding the screw driver in left hand and Spanner in the right hand. He was trying hard  to remove the fourth bolt. While doing this he was leaning towards the srew driver . He did not notice that his face  is very close to the screw driver's head. Suddenly the  screw driver slipped from the holding position  and hit on his face near left side eyebrow and lower  part of left eye.  This slip of screw driver and hiiting on OS left eybrow  resulted in minor cut near to his  eyebrow and swelling in lower part of the  left  eye.
IncidentEventDetails: 29/0700LT : Bosun reported to chief officer as usual.  29/0710LT: Chief officer has given the list of jobs to complete to bosun  29/0800LT: Tool box meeting was carried out by chief officer with all deck crew.   29/0810LT: Bosun had  assigned jobs to all deck crew  29/0815LT: Ordinary seaman Ajitesh was advised  to remove the " Be Aware of Propeller " board from aft station ship side railing.  29/0830LT: O/S started the work with proper PPE.  29/0845LT: 3 bolts were removed by O/S with the help of spanner and screw driver .  29/0905LT: O/S found that the fourth bolt was stuck and unable to remove.  29/0910LT: Trying hard to remove the fourth bolt by holding one side with screw driver and other side with  spanner  29/0925LT : Attempts were made to remove the fouth bolt but no success.  29/0930LT : Screw driver slipped from the holding position and hit on his forehead near leftside eyebrow and lower  part of left side eye.  29/0935: O/S Ajitesh  reported to chief officer about the injury .  29/0940LT : Immediately O/S was taken to the ship's hospital and first aid , pain killer , fever medicines were given .
ImmediateAction: O/S Ajitesh was taken to ship's hospital. First aid was given . Pain killer and ant-inflamatory  tablets were advised .Also advised the O/S to do cold water application. Rest was given in afternoon.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Vessel has  planned to renew the 'BE AWARE OF PROPELLER " board which was fitted at  main deck near  bay  74 . Ordinary seaman Ajitesh was told  to remove the "BE AWARE OF PROPELLER" board. The board was fitted with 4 botls to the ship side railing.The forward Bolt consist of nut in forward part and screw in aft part.   O/S Ajitesh started the work with proper PPE. He removed three bolts successfully by using spanner and screw driver.When removing the fouth bolt he found that the bolt is rotating with the nut so he was not able to remove it.. He planned to hold the aft part of bolt by the screw driver and remove the forward part of nut with spanner. He was holding the screw driver in left hand and Spanner in the right hand. He was trying hard  to remove the fourth bolt. While doing this he was leaning towards the srew driver . He did not notice that his face  is very close to the screw driver's head. Suddenly the  screw driver slipped from the holding position  and hit on his face near left side eyebrow and down part of left eye.  This was  resulted in minor cut near to his  eyebrow and swelling in down part of left  eye.
DirectCause: Emphasis not laid to COSWP. Assistance to carry out the job when not being successfully completed not asked for . Job assigned to inexperienced seaman rather than assigining to an experienced seaman .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Meeting conducted and training imparted to all crew on emphasis to comply with COSWP and avoid re-occurrence .  Crew motivated to ask for assistance rather than forcing to complete job where possibility of injury is there . Proper assigning duties to experienced seaman .
ClosingNote: COSWP training to be given to all crew. Use of proper PPE to be emphasized and safety goggles may be worn in such jobs.

IncidentDetails: ENGINE CADET WHILE COMING OUT FROM ENGINE ROOM UPPER DECK,RIGHT HAND INDEX FINGER & MIDDLE FINGER GOT CRUSHED BY THE ENGINE ROOM FIRE DOOR.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. 3RD ENGINEER, FITTER, OILER AND ENGINE CADET (Tarun Acharya) WORKING IN THE EGE PLATFORM    UPPER DECK.  2.   ENGINE CADET PROCEEDS TO UPPER DECK ALLEY FROM ENGINE ROOM TO BRING THE REQUIRED TOOLS.  3. ENGINE CADET  WHILE ENTERING THE AIR TIGHT COMPARTMENT BY OPENING THE ENGINE ROOM FIRE DOOR , BEFORE CLOSING THE ENGINE ROOM FIRE DOOR, THE OTHER  UPPER DECK ALLEY WAY FIRE DOOR WAS STILL OPEN. DUE TO THE DIFFERENTIAL PRESSURE, UPPER DECK ALLEY WAY DOOR OPENED AUTOMATCALLY  WITH BACK PRESSURE AND INSTANTANEOUSLY  ENGINE CADET  TRIED TO STOP THE INSTANEOUS  CLOSING  THE ENGINE ROOM FIRE DOOR   BY HOLDING THE EDGE OF THE DOOR.  AT THIS MOMENT HIS RIGHT HAND INDEX FINGER & MIDDLE FINGER GOT CRUSHED BETWEEN   ENGINE ROOM FIRE DOOR FIRE AND FRAME.  4. .   ENGINE CADET INFORMED 3/E & FITTER REGARDING THE ACCIDENT. HE WAS TAKEN TO SHIPS OFFICE TO CHECK THE SEVERITY OF INJURY.   5. .   ENGINE CADET    RIGHT HAND GLOVES REMOVED AND FOUND BLOOD COMING OUT FROM HIS RIGHT HAND INDEX & MIDDLE FINGER (BOTH PALM & BACK OF HIS HAND)
ImmediateAction: 1. C/E & C/OFF INFORMED ABOUT THE INCIDENT. MEDICAL OFFICER ONBOARD (2/OFF) INFORMED AND CALLED FOR ASSISTANCE.  2. MEAN TIME, FOLLOWING FIRST AID WAS GIVEN BY SHIP’ S STAFF.  • FINGER WAS CLEANED WITH SALINE WATER AND THEN CLEANED WITH DETTOL.  • APPLIED BETADINE OINMENT ON BOTH THE FINGERS AT THE PLACE OF INJURY.  • KEEPING SURGICAL COTTON AND BANDAID THEN THE INJURY PLACE ROLLED OVER BOTH THE FINGERS TOGETHER WITH STERLIZED BANDAGE.  3.  ENGINE CADET GIVEN ANTIBIOTIC REFERED BY 2/OFFICER, ELECTROLYTE & JUICE WAS GIVEN TO ENGINE CADETTO REDUCE DEHYDRATION EFFECT.  4. AFTER 2 HOURS 2/OFFICER ONCE AGAIN DRESSED E/C FINGERS & CHANGED THE BANDAGE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Index finger and middle finger got minor cut and blood was oozing out.Immediately given first aid.
DirectCause: Lack of training and knowledge
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training given and all engine room staff cautioned. Warning notices posted on ER doors which are self closing under engine room pressure
ClosingNote: Door closer hydraulic settings re-adjusted.

IncidentDetails: General Steward right hand thumb got stuck/jammed between the self closing fire door leading from upper deck to A-deck , while shifting stores.
IncidentEventDetails: on 18/08/2018 at around 0130 hrs Lt while shifting the received stores   from Upper deck To A-deck. G.S. right hand thumb got stuck /jammed between the U-deck staircase door. Immediately cleaned the wound & dressed the wound.
ImmediateAction: Administered first aid.  Sent to doctor & instructed him to avoid cleaning/washing dish jobs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: DEEP CUT ON THE RIGHT HAND THUMB. SLIGHT SWELLING AND PAINFUL.
DirectCause: Lack of sitiational awareness , not followed safe working practises. Did not ask for assistance
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discussed about the injury, explained about safe working practices & encouraged crew to ask for assistance whenever required.  Adjusted the self closing door .
ClosingNote: Alert sent to all vessels and hydraulic adjustment for fire doors checked for speed.

IncidentDetails: During loading operation deck cadet was asked to check the indication of valve position inside the hydraulic box on main deck near manifold. He opened the hydraulic box cover and while holding the cover with right hand he checked the indicator of the valve and reported the status. Later when he was about to close the cover it slipped from his hand and fell on to his left hand which was still on the box side.The ring finger and the middle finger of the left hand got hurt resulting in small cut on both the fingers and swelling in the middle joint of both fingers.
IncidentEventDetails: At the time of incident vessel was engaged in loading operation at Kertih SBM,Malaysia.WHile loading deck cadet was told to check visually the valve indicator of 4P COT valve in no. 3  Hydraulic box on main deck near the port side manifold. He removed the butterfly nuts of the cover and partially opened the box cover, holding it with right hand, he checked the status of valve indicator and reported to CCR. Once he was about to close the cover it slipped from his right hand and the cover fell on his left hand which he was not able to pull out at the last moment from the box.Though he was wearing the appropriate PPEs such as working leather gloves, helmet etc but the impact was so strong that he got hurt his middle finger and ring finger of left hand. He came to CCR and reported the incident. Minor cut was obsereved on his two fingers and some swelling in the middle joint of the two fingers. First aid was given to him and ice pack applied to his finger.
ImmediateAction: The cut was cleaned with antiseptic liquid and cold ice pack was applied on to the fingers.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MINOR CUT ON THE MIDDLE AND RING FINGER OF LEFT HAND AND SWELLING IN THE MIDDLE PART OF THESE TWO FINGERS.

IncidentDetails: Burn Injury on the right face of duty Oiler Arnold P. Sontousidad
IncidentEventDetails: At about 1310H, incinerator was tested by 2/E and duty Olr. Arnold P. Sontousidad to confirmed C/E suspicion of faulty WOT outlet valve due to very low sludge pump pressure. After confirming sludge pressure was very low, 2/E suggested to C/E to completely drain the WOT in order can check the valve and was given approval. Then 2/E gave order to duty oiler to slowly drain the WOT to Bilge Sludge Tank.  At about 1340H, duty oiler started slowly draining the heated sludge from WOT. While draining in progress with no sign of abnormality, duty oiler leave the site to check the sounding of Bilge Sludge Tank. After about five minutes, he returned to the site to check the condition of the drain scupper of WOT. With no spillage even no. 1 MAC was already running, duty oiler took a ratchet tool with a spike end to be used to uncover the scupper that was always covered with rags to protect against spillage when MACs' are running. With duty oiler face & body away, he slowly uncovers the portion of the scupper to check the drain condition, unexpectedly, a strong back splashing of hot oil hit most of his right face...
ImmediateAction: Draining was quickly suspended and injured personnel was brought to Bridge and as given first aid by duty officer. Master immediately seek medical assistance from shore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn on the right side of face and fore head.
DirectCause: 1. Design fault of the drain pipe of WOT as it is connected in series with the air drain pipe of Main Air Compressors as the first root cause.  2.  Safety awareness, due to heated oil was drain, failure to wear PPE (faceshield).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Drain pipe of Waste Oil Tank recommended for modification, either to be separated from the air drain pipe of MAC or to fit any safety devices to prevent back flow or splashing.  2. Posting WARNING & Safety signage when draining job to be performed.
Corrective Action: 1. Drain pipe of Waste Oil Tank recommended for modification, either to be separated from the air drain pipe of MAC or to fit any safety devices to prevent back flow or splashing. 2. Posting WARNING & Safety signage when draining job to be performed.
ClosingNote: ca in order

IncidentDetails: Duty Oiler Arnold P. Sontousidad, suffered burn injury on his right face and forehead.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Before noon of said date, chief engr informed 2/E of the incinerator condition that its sludge pump pressure is not increasing after several minutes passed allotted after change over from diesel to sludge burning. Suspecting problem with Waste Oil Tank outlet valve.  2 .  At 1310H, 2/E together with duty Oiler Arnold P. Sontousidad, tested incinerator and confirmed that sludge pump pressure was not increasing when shifting to sludge. 2/E stop the incinerator and suggested to C/E to drain the Waste Oil Tank (rob abt 2.0 m3) to Bilge Sludge Tank (rob abt 15.53 m3 / max cap. 35.59 m3) in order to check its outlet valve condition. With the approval from C/E, then 2/E gave order to duty Oiler Arnold P. Sontousidad to slowly drain the waste oil tank to bilge sludge tank.    3. At 1340H, Oiler A. Sontousidad, started slowly draining the heated sludge from waste oil tank to bilge sludge tank. Draining was on progress even MAC no. 1 was running (MACs’ air drain pipe connected in series with WOT drain pipe), with no abnormality noticed, duty oiler leave the site to check the sounding of bilge sludge tank. After 5 mins passed, he went back to the site to check the condition of the drain and still found no abnormality from the drain scupper of WOT. Since WOT drain scupper is always covered with rags as protection against oil mist spilling during MAC’s operation, Oiler Sontousidad still  with precaution, uses a ratchet tool with a spike end to uncover the scupper. Unexpectedly, while he just started slowly uncovering the drain scupper of WOT, a strong back splashing of hot oil hit his right face & forehead.
ImmediateAction: The duty oiler after suffered a burn by the hot oil, quickly leave the site.  The 2/E who saw the incident quickly close drain valve of WOT and informed C/E.  The C/E, informed Bridge’s OOW, the 2/O of the incident and immediately brought the duty oiler to the bridge for first aid treatment.  Master was informed and who seek medical assistance ashore
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oiler Arnold P. Sontousidad suffering burn injury on his entire right face and forehead.
DirectCause: 1. Design fault was considered as the first root cause of the incident. The WOT tank is in close proximity to the MAC, which the drain pipe of WOT and MAC is connected in series. No safety device fitted on the drain pipe of WOT tank to prevent back pressure that will cause injury to personnel onboard.  2. No WARNING signage for the operation since vessel delivered from yard  3. No Safety poster regarding wearing of PPE for the operation.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Posting WARNING and SAFETY Poster and always having a standby PPE on the site.
Corrective Action: 1. Posting WARNING and SAFETY Poster and always having a standby PPE on the site.
ClosingNote: CA IN ORDER

IncidentDetails: During routine works in Engine Room, 4/ Engineer suffered minor abrasion to his nose tip.

ImmediateAction: 4th/Engineer went to workshop to inform C/E and 2/E  what happened and went to engine control room to apply first aid , cleaned and proceed to ship's hospital to continue the first aid and visual assessment and further medication.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fourth Engineer suffering minor abrasion to his nose tip and having infrequent nosebleeding.
DirectCause: Unaware with hazard present in the situation, miscommunication with his companion, lack of risk assesment and analysis.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper planning and risk assesment will carry out for the certain job,   No any unplanned job will carry out without consulting/informing Senior Engineer/Officer.  Senior Engineer always supervise/monitor proper execution of maintenance as per plan.
Corrective Action: Proper planning and risk assesment will carry out for the certain job,  No any unplanned job will carry out without consulting/informing Senior Engineer/Officer. Senior Engineer always supervise/monitor proper execution of maintenance as per plan.
