ClosingNote: CA IN ORDER

IncidentDetails: Fitter's right leg ankle got blisters due hot water falling from leaking steam line
IncidentEventDetails: On 14th Sep’18 Departure Algeciras after RFA fire alarm activated due steam leakage in duct keel .  Immediately Duct keel main inlet bunker tank heating steam valves and return steam valves were shut.  Upon thorough inspection found steam leakage was from one of the gaskets in the steam inlet line.  Fitter along with junior engineer were assigned to renew the gasket. Both went inside with the tools, fitter had enetered the compartment containing the leaking joint and  started the job and junior engineer was assisting him from outside of the compartment.Due to the space constarints filtter had keep his feet near to the leaking flange .When fitter started the work there was no leakage observed from the open flange however half-way through the job , due condensation some water oozed out from the open flange falling on backside of fitters shoes and entering the shoe casuing blisters on his foot.   While carrying out the job condensed hot water was draining. Fitter slackened the flange, removed two nut & bolts and broken gaskets. Since unexpectedly hot water splashed from the slackened flange due to free flow of line water from forward to aft with vessel movement, some hot water dropped into his shoe.
ImmediateAction: Shoe & socks removed, washed with cold water and Antiseptic & Burn relief cream applied on the affected area.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 14th Sep’18 Departure Algeciras after RFA fire alarm activated due steam leakage in duct keel .  Immediately Duct keel main inlet bunker tank heating steam valves and return steam valves were shut.  Upon thorough inspection found steam leakage was from one of the gaskets in the steam inlet line.  Fitter along with junior engineer were assigned to renew the gasket. Both went inside with the tools, fitter had enetered the compartment containing the leaking joint and  started the job and junior engineer was assisting him from outside of the compartment.Due to the space constarints filtter had keep his feet near to the leaking flange .When fitter started the work there was no leakage observed from the open flange however half-way through the job , due condensation some water oozed out from the open flange falling on backside of fitters shoes and entering the shoe casuing blisters on his foot.
DirectCause: The bellows in the steam lines which are damaged and same have been replaced with permamnent pipes which leads to tension in the pipe/flanges and resulting in such damages.  Fitter should have waited for some more time so that any condensed water would have relased before starting the job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Further RA  to be discussed along with cold work permit.  Job to be delayed still hot water leakage completely stopped.
LessionLearnt: Keep clear of pipelines with hot liquid within.
ClosingNote: Work permit to be issued and proper PPE to be assigned to working staff.

IncidentDetails: Pumpman suffered sprain in right shoulder, casuing sever pain and difficulty in moving right arm.  Suspect slight shoulder dislocation due to shoulder getting wrenched wrongly.
IncidentEventDetails: After completion of tank cleaning, pumpman was working in pumroom to clean the stripper pump strainer. While he was trying to enter the pumproom bilges, he was holding the grating/platform with right arm and as he bent down to enter the bilges, his right arm got wrenched upward casuing the sprain.
ImmediateAction: Pumpman taken to hospital. Pain reliver spray administered in the sprained area to relive pain and given pain killer.Medical Advice requested. Iinformed Agent to arrange doctor visit upon berthing.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sprain in shoulder, slight shoulder dislocation.
DirectCause: Improper work procedure, lack of attention while working.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew to be trained and motivated to remain attentive and alert while working in restriced areas and to follow correct work procedures.
LessionLearnt: Proper working postures to be practiced and followed.
ClosingNote: Crew sent to see doctor the next day and was diagnosed as severe sprain. Was declared fit for work with restriction for not lifting heavy weights for the next 3 days.

Next day send to doctor for further treatment and medical advise. Attached doctor report.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was Discharging at Jebel Ali,UAE. AB Dutta reported that he is having pain in his right shoulder and right elbow.Since the vessel has received provisions on the previous night.It was assumed that due to excess manual labour,his shoulder and elbow is paining. On further enquiry it was found he was having slight pain in his right shoulder.This pain started while he was passing a heaving line at last port Tanjung Langsat. He informed about the same to second officer and received medication for the same. After few days the pain subsided and his shoulder was normal. About few months back on 26th May 2018, AB-Dutta informed about pain in his Elbow.He informed that while carrying out loading operations in Karachi in the month of May, his elbow hit a shore structure while removing the securing rope of the ship's gangway bottom platform. Same was reported to second officer and medical advise obtained. After medication, pain subsided in a weeks time.
ImmediateAction: Since the vessel was in Jebel Ali,UAE. Arrangements were made for doctor visit.As per the doctor's recommendation he was given rest and mild duty. Company was informed and sign off procedures initiated at next port Fujairah.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: As per the Doctor's report, MRI and Various other tests are required to be carried out for proper examination and for the extant of injury to his arm and shoulder.
DirectCause: Lack of Skill, improper handling of rope under tension and Improper way of throwing heaving line.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training for all crew members to be carried out for Mooring operations including correct methods of throwing heaving line and procedures to be followed in handling lines under tension.
Corrective Action: Training to carry out for all crew on COSWP. Mooring and operating ships equipment
ClosingNote: Going by the history and interviewing AB Dutta, understand that the person had complained of pain few months back also while pulling the gangway lower platform on the jetty. These jibs must be carried out with enough number of crew members so that excessive load does not come on a single person. The tool box must cover this aspect.

IncidentEventDetails: 3RD ENGINEER AND 5TH ENGINEER WERE GIVEN THE TASK OF LOWERING NO.5,6,7 AND 8 UNIT CAM'S OIL PAN. MOTORMAN LOBO WAS GIVEN THE TASK OF CHIPPING AND PAINTING INSIDE OF CASCADE TANK. AT 1430 HRS CHIPPING GUN USED BY LOBO WAS FAULTY, SAME REPORTED TO 2 ND ENGINEER AND  REQUESTED 2ND ENGINEER FOR SHIFTING JOB WITH 5TH ENGINEER AS LOBO WAS INSIDE CASCADE TANK SINCE MORNING. AT 1500 HRS WHILE REMOVING NO.8 UNIT OIL PAN THIS UNFORTUNATE INCIDENT OCCURED.
ImmediateAction: AS ENGINE FITTER WAS IN THE BOTTOM PLATFORM AREA SEARCHING FOR SPANNER, HE HEARD UNUSUAL NOISE FROM EMERNGENCY MANEOUVERING AND IMMEDIATELY APPROACHED INCIDENT AREA,FOUND LOBO WITH BLOOD IN HAND, SUGGESTED LOBO TO REPORT TO CHIEF ENGINEER / SENIOR OFFICER IN SHIPS OFFICE. IncidentDetails: Electrical officer injuerd his 2 finger tips including nails while transferring Motor room supply fan impeller to engine room.
IncidentEventDetails: 1100 hrs. ETO along with Gas engineer and Engine fitter started removing Motor room supply fan shaft from Casing at Upper deck (Port side) out of accommodation.          1140 hrs. Shaft along with impeller removed from casing.          1215 hrs. Noticed that one bearing and impeller blades are stuck with shaft, same to be removed by hot work in engine room work shop.          1220 hrs. ETO took the shaft along with Impeller to engine room via upperdeck entrance.          1222 hrs. While descending the ladder, he found that impeller blades are not easily passing through the ladder and tried to shift it upwards for transfer. Blades from left hand slipped and due to imbalance of weight, right side of impeller hit his left hand fingers crushing between impeller blade and hand rail of ladder.                                1223 hrs. ETO reported finger injury at upper deck Port side to Chief engineer.          1224 hrs. Started giving first aid (fingers dipped in Ice water,cleaned with betadine solution) .          1226 hrs. E-mail along with photographs send to company doctor and reported injury to office.          1319 hrs. Advise from doctor received to give medicines as prescribed and sign off ETO at earliest.          1325 hrs. First dose medicines given as per instruction received from doctor.          2006 hrs. ETO signed off at galle for further treatment ashore.
ImmediateAction: First aid given to crew, all crew mustered and injury cause discussed.          Impeller lowered through skylight.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: ETO injuerd his 2 finger tips including nail on his left hand.
DirectCause: Improper handling/transfer procedure of odd size and unbalanced object.          Improper method and location for transfer of impeller.  Lack of experience.Lack of supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed about proper handling/transfering procedure of weights.COSWP Ch 10 discussed. VOD shown to all crew (703- Manual handling techniques)       All crew briefed about proper handling/transfering procedure of weights.COSWP Ch 10 discussed. VOD shown to all crew (703- Manual handling techniques).Job to be discussed in detail during tool box meeting and all risks assessed prior taking up the job.            Handling techniques and locations to be used for transfer of objects to be discussed and job supervised properly.
ClosingNote: The E/O suffered finger injury (left hand) on 28th Sep 2018 while transferring motor room supply fan impeller to Engine room. The details of the injury is in the attached injury report. Necessary first aid was provided onboard; E/O was then signed off & taken to hospital in Colombo (necessary treatment was provided), and he was repatriated to India from Colombo, for further medical attention. He was then referred to our company recommended medical center for further treatment. He got treated and became fit on 12th Nov 2018

IncidentDetails: On 12th October, while the Vessel was transitting Panama Canal, 2nd Enginer, Fitter and Tr.Wiper were Trying to open COPT condenser Pump Discharge Pipe. During the opening,the spannerto lock the bolt head, was being held by the wiper while the fitter and 2nd engineer were trying to open it. The wipers finger got caught between the spanner and pipe, and before he could react, it got turned, injuring the tip of his left hand Ring finger and nail.
IncidentEventDetails: 12th October 2018  0830: The work started as per plan.  1000 - 1030: Break  1030: Work of Opening the COPT Condenser pump discharge Pipe resumed.  1135: The incident occured and immediately work stopped.  1140: 2nd Officer attended the Wiper in Ships Hospital.  1200: Bleeding stopped and first aid completed.  2312: VL anchored at Cristobal.  13th October 2018  0045: Wiper taken ashore by the Agent for Medical Attention.
ImmediateAction: The work was immediately stopped, first aid was given to stop the bleeding. Contacted company doctor Balaji for advise. Same followed. Agent Panama shipping was contacted to arrange for Medical attendence at the earliest.  Upon vessel's arrival at Cristobal anchorage The Injured Wiper was taken ashore for Medical attention.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 12th October, while the Vessel was transitting Panama Canal, 2nd Enginer, Fitter and Tr.Wiper were Trying to open COPT condenser Pump Discharge Pipe. During the opening,the spannerto lock the bolt head, was being held by the wiper while the fitter and 2nd engineer were trying to open it. The wipers finger got caught between the spanner and pipe, and before he could react, it got turned, injuring the tip of his left hand Ring finger and nail.
DirectCause: The toolbox meeting should have discussed this job in detail with the precautions to be taken and with 2/E on the spot, the supervision could have been better.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident to be discussed amongst all crew, to highlight the dangers associated, like lack of experience and forsight (what could go wrong).
Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed amongst all crew, to highlight the dangers associated, like lack of experience and forsight (what could go wrong).
ClosingNote: The closure is considered as adequate and the injured crew member has been mentored by the Senior Staff for strict compliance with safety at all times.

IncidentDetails: Crew were securing deck after tank cleaning.  One of the AB  noted Air hose was lying loose near 6S COT pumpstack. One end  of the hose was connected to the Deck air line valve and other end was lying loose on the main deck frame. He tried to disconnect the air hose but connection was tight and decided to use open end coupling to hammer. While hammering liquid spilled from the open end on to his boiler suit causing minor burn on his leg.       Further investigation was carried it was found that AB Satish noted Air hose was lying loose near 6S COT pumpstack. One end of the hose was connected to the Deck air line valve and other end was lying loose on the main deck frame. He tried to disconnect the air hose but connection was tight and decided to use open end coupling to hammer. While hammering liquid spilled from the open end on to his boiler suit. As the hose was used for line blowing of 6S cargo tank, AB Satish assumed that the liquid could be phenol therefore he removed his boiler suit and applied the PEG which was kept standby near 6S pump stack.        AB Satish is able to carry out all the routine duties without any difficulty. He is feeling normal and does not have pain or numbness. He is being monitored regularly and wants to work on deck.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Vessel discharged cargo from 6S at Taichung.  2. Air hose was used to blow 6S cargo line with air after completion of discharging.  3. After blowing the air hose was not secured.  4. Next day before tank cleaning, one end of air hose was disconnected from the pump stack drain but the other end was left connected to the deck air line. The hose was not secured.
ImmediateAction: Contaminated boiler suit was removed and affected area was rinsed with Fresh water under emergency shower. First aid was given and vitals were checked. Blood pressure and temprature found normal. The affected area was found discoloured but AB satish felt no burning sensation or pain. He didnot feel any dizziness/headache only slight itching.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor burn on left leg.
DirectCause: 1. Improper tool and procedure used to disconnect the air hose. 2. Proper PPE was not worn by A/B. 3. Inadequate knowledge
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All staff of Fairchem Triumph to be briefed on Job safety / hazard identification with special emphasis on line blowing procedure,  chances of line containing hazardous cargo in it. All the hoses used for line blowing to be inspected, cleaned and blown through after use. Requirement to wear appropriate PPE as per the work being carried out.
Corrective Action: Meeting carried out with all ship staff. All were briefed about the importance of securing of items after use. Especially items used in cargo operations such as Loading, Discharging,Tank cleaning to be secured immediately after use. All were informed that traces of cargo can be present in air hose used for blowing. Procedures for cargo line blowing explained. All the hoses used for line blowing to be inspected, cleaned and blown through after use.
LessionLearnt: 1. Work instructions must be given taking into account the workers experience and familiarity with equipment on board. Clear & detailed instructions of the full task should be given. Worker’s understanding of expectations must be confirmed and clarified. 2. Enhance on board supervision of personal behaviour, especially when assigned tasks with significant hazards.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: The incident is included in the list of incident that is to be discussed with all the joiners for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: Inhalation of fumes or dust
IncidentEventDetails: INCIDENT REPORT                  At 12:00H Lt. on this date 28 October 2018, Master of M/V Ocean Trader I called the local agent to informed that the vessel will conduct cargo holds cleaning and agent advice that the hold wash water is to be kept on board and not to discharged to sea. Master agreed to same And at 12:15 Crew start washing in Cargo hold Number 1 and followed by Cargo hold No. 4 Until they reach No. 6 Cargo hold at 17: 30 received complain from the terminal operator who was at no. 7 Cargo hold on deck of dryness in mouth and irritation on throat. Ships crew was standby on the same level with the operator at that time and did not experience any issue same with the officer on that location. At 1745H Vessel Stopped all hold cleaning activity as a precautionary measure.  At 1800H Terminal operator disembarked the vessel.     Therefore we have immediately ceased the operation at 17:45 and As a precautionary measures no washing will be carried out while vessel remained at port in order to avoid such incident.             Capt. Jesus E. Dingle  Master Oceean trader I
ImmediateAction: Stopped al cargo holds cleaning operation
DirectCause: Failure to understand & comply with port regulation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All port rules and regulation are to be discussed in safety meeting prior arrival in port & in tool box meeting of jobs to be carried out in port . all officer&crew to sign the coopy of port rules and rgulations for understanding and strict compliance.
Corrective Action: Technical Manager Mr. Joseph Peter and Technical Superintendent Mr. Niju attended the vessel at next port Zhoushan and carried out an investigation in tandem with the Office Team.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ReviewComments: a. Direct cause   - Crew failed to follow the Terminal regulation.  b. Indirect cause  - Information exchange between ship staff and shore was not effective. - Ship staff was not aware of the Terminal regulations with respect to hold cleaning. - Lack of effective communication between the vessel and terminal. Terminal was not informed by the vessel on her intention to carry out Hold Cleaning & Washing while at berth.  c. Root cause  - Lack of Compliance     Terminal regulations provided by the agents was misinterpreted by the ship staff. It was mentioned that tank cleaning was not permitted. Tank Cleaning is usually referred in the context of Tanker Vessel and not Bulk Carriers.
ClosingNote: The Violation of Port Regulations by the vessel was not intentional. However, a more proactive approach from the ship staff could have avoided the incident.

IncidentDetails: During daily deck routine maintenance, fresh water washing on deck and hatch cover, AB John A. Barbato accidentally fall down on deck forward part of hatch no.4 center while he is descending from hatch cover.
IncidentEventDetails: 12 Nov 2018 - During daily deck maintenance fresh water washing on deck and hatch with soap and oxalic acid (for removing cargo stain) AB Barbato is assigned for pressurized fresh water machine use for rinsing on deck and hatch cover.    12th Nov 2018 o/a 1430H LT while AB Barbato is descending from hatch cover no.4 forward part he accidentally fall down on deck. No witnessed of actual incident how they fell, they found him lying on deck by other crew working on same areas right after the incident.  After sometime of observing his condition, he bring to hospital for further assessment of his condition, he can walk by himself according to him he is in good condition.  He observed pain on left side of his body, left hand palm and left side of his head due to impact on deck.    His condition is under observation until arrival Singapore were to arrange his visit to doctor.    14th Nov 2018 / 2045 - Vessel at Singapore AEB"A" and AB Barbato send to shore for Medical Check.    14th Nov 2018 / 2300H – AB Barbato declared unfit for sea duty, unfit to return vessel and unfit to travel by attending shore doctor and admit to hospital for further observation.
ImmediateAction: Bring him to ships hospital and observe his condition, give him rest until arrival Singapore to arrange visit to doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: He suffers pain on the left side of his head, feel pain on left side of his body and left palm cause by impact on deck.    Declared unfit for sea duty, unfit to travel and unfit to return to vessel by attending shore doctor.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Always discuss and remind to all ship staff during toolbox meeting all safety precaution for specific job.  2. All ship staff should always look for his crew mate’s safety as well, good teamwork should always be maintained.  3. Always discuss to all ships staff during tool box meeting to stop working in case anyone found himself uncomfortable need to take break/rest.
Other Part: Tenderness on Left side of his head

IncidentDetails: Right Hand Thumb Injury (Deep Cut) while carrying out maintenance / inspection of incinerator.
IncidentEventDetails: Second Engineer is planning to carried out maintenance and inspection of Incinerator. At around 0530LT on 3rd December 2018, Second Engineer asked the Duty Oiler to remove the manhole cover bolts and instructed to leave 2 bolt to be removed later on.  At around 0710LT, Second Engineer removed the pin of the hydraulic holder of the manhole cover then removed the remaining bolts. Suddenly, the cover fell down while Second Engineer is holding the arm where the hydraulic actuator connected and crash on his right hand thumb on the side wall of the Incinerator causing his finger injury.
ImmediateAction: Administered First Aid, cleaned the wound with Providone Iodine Solution and rubbing alcohol, wound closure strips applied to bring the skin together. Given with Paracetamol 500 mg to relieve pain. Shore doctor advise sought.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Second Engineer is planning to carried out maintenance and inspection of Incinerator. At around 0530LT on 3rd December 2018, Second Engineer asked the Duty Oiler to remove the manhole cover bolts and instructed to leave 2 bolt to be removed later on.  At around 0710LT, Second Engineer removed the pin of the hydraulic holder of the manhole cover then removed the remaining bolts. Suddenly, the cover fell down while Second Engineer is holding the arm where the hydraulic actuator connected and crash on his right hand thumb on the side wall of the Incinerator causing his finger injury.
DirectCause: Due to underestimate the weight of the sluice cover of incinerator and improper position holding of the arm of the  sluice cover where the actuator connected.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always use entrances which are designed for the specific job. Follow the Makers Instructions sincerely and no deviations to be permitted. A proper RA to be done prior any job.  Carry out training of Engine Room Staff , be guided by SMS Technical Manual Section 3.26. Once training completed upload the SF 17 for Closing of Corrective action.
Corrective Action: Do not underestimate the weight of the sluice cover if you did not sure need use chain black and proper planning of the job take risk assessment. To consider if additional man power required for particular job.
LessionLearnt: Use procedures made for such operations. RA always to done prior the job.
ReviewComments: Discussed and shared with ship staff
ClosingNote: Ongoing supervision and Continuous training is essential to make sure such incidents don't repeat.
OtherUser: Engel Marc De Los Reyes Bautista

IncidentDetails: On 01 December at 1830 SMT/ 1630 Z , OS Ronolo suffered deep laceration on the scalp above the  forehead due off balance in heavy weather in  common washroom.  Ronolo was exiting out of the cubicle of the washroom, he went off balance and  fell. During the fall his head hit the corner of the wooden partition panel   The laceration is 7 cm wide and 2-3 mm deep,  There is no signs of fracture of the skull and only laceration of the skin,
IncidentEventDetails: OS had his dinner and went to common lavatory to use WC toilet.   On his exit he lost his balance due to pitch and roll and fell   His head hit the edge of the cubicle on his right side causing injury to scalp   Ship was rolling and pitching due to bad weather during time of incident
ImmediateAction: • Bleeding was arrested,  • Area around the wound cleaned with betadine solution, hairs removed  • Butterfly slips used to seal the wound and applied bandage.  •  He has been administered,   Paracetamol – 500mg  ( 2 times till pain eases)   Doxyclycline – 100 mg ( 3 times a day for 7 days )
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The laceration is 7 cm wide and 2-3 mm deep on the scalp above forehead,
DirectCause: Off Balance due to Heavy Weather, OS unable to secure himself or balance due to absense of hand hold at exist of WC toilet.    ? Vessel was pitching and rolling, heavily to long heavy swell of 4 mtrs on 01st Dec,  ? The cubicles are facing Port and starboard, it is possible that when OS came out of the cubicle  after nature call, he probably got disoriented for a brief period from sitting to standing and  rushing out.  ? The Opening of the cubicle door is towards port (behind) and force exerted to move forward.  (A) – towards starboard by OS  ? OS Exit His direction was athwart ship while the ship was pitching and rolling. At this time, it  is likely the ship bow lifted on the swell and rolled to starboard (B) ,  ? (A) + (B) = Resultant to starboard.  ? The resultant force to starboard would have caused his body to be pushed forward  excessively, while the lifting of the bow on the wave pushed his body off centerline toward  Aft (to his right)  ? This caused an off balance resulting in a fall forward and to his right  ? The space in front of the cubicle being open his fall could not be prevented with no handrails  ? The root cause is failure to access ships motion in rough weather. With dynamic motion of the  vessel, one learns over time to balance his body and move with caution judging the pitch and  roll
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fix hand holds on the left side of the WC toilet to enable opening of the door while holding the hand hold.
Corrective Action: Fix hand holds on the left side of the WC toilet to enable opening of the door while holding the hand hold.
LessionLearnt: Move cautiously within accommodation as well, 3 point contact essential.
ReviewComments: Discussed with ship staff
ClosingNote: Meeting and Training with crew carried out

IncidentDetails: Cadet's wrist came in between the Cover and Coaming while securing the pin of bobby hatch cover  and complains of pain on wrist.
IncidentEventDetails: Ch.Off and Cadet were standby on top in FPK store with radio while third officer and Bosun entered the Bow Thruster room for routine inspection. Cadet noted the stopper pin for Bow thruster room bobby hatch cover was not inserted properly. As he noted, He immediately proceeded to secure the pin properly without seeking help from Ch.Off . By holding the bobby hatch cover he adjusted the pin's position to secure it properly. As vessel rolled easily, he lost the control over the hatch cover and it began to fall. Before the cover contacting completely on hand , Ch/officer nearby held the cover from falling . Cadet's wrist came in between the Cover and Coaming .The hatch cover was immediately restored to the open position and pin was inserted. After the incident, Cadet complained about minor pain on the wrist . Company doctor was contacted and medical advice sought.  He was provided with the medicines and  rest as adviced by Doctor. After the medications and rest as adviced, Cadet reported feeling fit and no pain exists. He was  retured to his normal onboard  routines.
ImmediateAction: Bosun and Third officer were called back from BT room and Cadet was taken to CCr and provided First Aid .Cold press, Pain relief spray applied. Company doctor's advice sought. Rest provided. All crew mebers were called for a meeting in the evening and briefed about the incident. Adviced not to engage in  such activites alone without notifying others or help is sought especially while vessel is rolling.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Ch.Off and Cadet were standby on top in FPK store with radio while third officer and Bosun entered the Bow Thruster room for routine inspection. Cadet noted the stopper pin for Bow thruster room bobby hatch cover was not inserted properly. As he noted, He immediately proceeded to secure the pin properly . By holding the bobby hatch cover he adjusted the pin's position to secure it properly. As vessel rolled easily, he lost the control over the hatch cover and it began to fall. Before the Ch/officer nearby held the cover from falling completely. Cadet's wrist came in between the Cover and Coaming .The hatch cover was immediately restored to the open position and pin was inserted. After the incident, Cadet complained about minor pain on the wrist . Company doctor was contacted and medical advice sought.  He was provided with the medicines and  rest as adviced by Doctor. After the medications and rest as adviced, Cadet reported feeling fit and no pain exists. He  retured to his normal onboard  routines.
DirectCause: Unable to judge the weight of the bobby hatch cover which come  over the person while removing the locking pin during rolling and misjudgement made that particular job can be done alone without the assistance of otehrs.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Extreme care to be taken while handling heavy doors and pin to be secured properly. such jobs not be carriedout alone.
Corrective Action: Extreme care to be taken while handling heavy doors and pin to be secured properly. such jobs not be carriedout alone.
ClosingNote: The incident has been analysed and satisfactorily addressed.

IncidentDetails: While shifting the recently shore serviced  life raft from amidships cranearea to its cradle on the port side B Deck, the AB Tirath Singh's small finger on the right hand got crushed between main deck and the wheel of the trolley.
IncidentEventDetails: On 14th December while at Durban Anchorage, the exchanged liferaft received were to be moved from amidships area to its stowing place on B deck. The life rafts cpacity was 35 persons and was very heavy. At 0815 LT the toolbox meeting was completed .At 0805 LT the crew (1pumpman, 2 OS, 2 AB and Cadet) started shifting the liferaft, Trolley was being used to shift the liferaft from amidships to the liferaft stowage position. Once the trolley cleared the catwalk and was in the front of accommodation, because of the antiskid coating on the deck, one of the wheesl of the trolley rotated perpendicularly and the trolley stopped moving. At the same time AB(injured person) tried to straighten the wheel using his right hand. Simultaneously at the same moment as the wheel got free, the trolley moved slightly ahead, resulting in getting his right hand little finger getting crushed under the wheel. In extreme pain, he immediately removed his gloves and we noticed that a small part of his finger was sheared off. Immediately took him to the hospital for First aid and Master was informed.
ImmediateAction: Immediately took him to the hospital for First aid.  Master on bridge was informed.  Injured part rinsed with clean water.  The injured part was immersed in ice / water to stop bleeding.  The wound was covered with antiseptic ointment and gauze bandage.  Paracetamol and Brufen were administered.  DPA/ ADAP were informed on telephone and by Email.   Company Dr.Balaji's Medical advise was requested.  Agents were informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Seafarer had to be evacuated, treated ashore and repatriated
DirectCause: Direct Cause: 1. Inappropriate PPE for the job 2. Straightening trolley wheel by hand causing crushing of little finger due to heavy load  Indirect Cause: 1. Seafarer's act was unintentional. In his opinion, straightening wheel by hand was the right way of correcting the trolley wheel. Seafarer did not use proper seamanship technique for the said job. 2. Though risk assessment addresses Ship movement/rolling but crew failed to apply same in real scenario.  Root Cause:  1. Lack of standard: Proper PPE was not addressed for the job, Inadequate supervision by senior officer, Inadequate hazard analysis 2. Lack of compliance: COSWP for handling heavy lift was not followed. RA tool was ineffective.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety meeting with all crew to discuss hazard identification, PPE requirement and to train crew to prevent re-occurrence.
Corrective Action: Safety Meeting to discuss and train crew to prevent reoccurance.  Inspection and renewal if necessary of Trolley.   Disscuss with office the possibility that at the  next overhaul of liferafts, the rafts to be taken from stowage position to shore and returned by boat which avoids the exceedingly heavy rafts from being lowered 2 decks up/ down and dragged from poop deck to midships crane area. Instead they can be directly lowered into and picked up from a boat.  All cotton working gloves removed from use.
LessionLearnt: 1) It is important that every task onboard is analyzed for the hazards that are posed by it. Every hazard should be suitably mitigated. 2) Skill and experience is not a substitute for training and every individual onboard should be provided with the necessary training to ensure that the task he is assigned is carried out safely and efficiently. 3) It is the responsibility of every employee to ensure that he is committed to the Safety Policy and its objectives of the company and implement a safe culture into his daily work activity
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Proper PPE , Hazard Identification

IncidentDetails: Bosun was renewing the needles of chipping machines. While removing the old needles from the needle supporter, one of the needle pierced his right palm.
IncidentEventDetails: One of the chipping gun needles need to be replaced.  Bosun brought the chipping gun to pumpman workshop and started with the job of replacing the needles.  Chipping gun was dismantled.  Needle supporter holes were choked, so in order to clear them he tried to put one of the needle in reverse order. While forcing to clear the holes, one of the needle pierced his right palm. The moment he realised that the needle has piered his right palm, he removed the needle.
ImmediateAction: First aid given and seeked radio medical advise from Dr Balaji.  Safety meeting with all crew was called in. Incident was discussed and all were advised to be more safe and careful.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury to righ palm.
DirectCause: Wrong procedure followed for removing needles from chipping machine.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Improper procedure to be avoided during maintenance of equipments.
LessionLearnt: Proper procedure to be followed at all times and short-cuts to be avoided.
ClosingNote: Proper procedure to be followed at all times and short-cuts to be avoided.

IncidentDetails: In Engine Room Motorman R. Murti was cleaning Tank Top area.Murti was wearing PPE.As he tried to cross over one pipe line, he slipped and fell down on floor, therby hurting himself in the back.
IncidentEventDetails: In Engine Room Motorman R. Murti was cleaning Tank Top area.4 th Engineer(Duty Engineer) was standby on Lower platform.Another Motorman Aabad Ali was cleaning nearby tanktop area.Murti was wearing PPE.As he tried to cross over one pipe line, he slipped and fell down on floor, therby hurting himself in the back.
ImmediateAction: On hearing his cry, immediately Fourth Enginner and motorman Abad Ali picked him up on Lower Platform from Tank Top.Bridge was informed of the incident. Rescue team rushed to the spot, brought hime to Workshop by stretcher.He was given first aid.His alcohol test was carried out, found satisfactory. Blood Pressure & pulse rate measured,found normal.He was then moved by strecher to Hospital.Blood Pressure :115/70 Pulse rate:77(At the Time Injury).Immidiately he was applied with the ice pack on the affected area.Later on given Restrain Belt for his back support with the pain rilief spray.Also given I-Brufen 400grams(1-1) for pain relief.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oiler R. Murti got injured on his right arm and and lower back right hand side.No external Injury was found.  But pain found on right back and shoulder.
DirectCause: Injured person was mentalliy agitated since he came to know that although he had given relief request, his name did not appear in the list of crew to be signed off at ensuing port call Ennore.He was advised to tender application for same, which he refused.In the morning he refused job instructed to him by 2nd. Engineer Mr. G.Tirkey.He was requested for discussion regarding this matter with Chief Enginner which was also turned down by him.  Due to agitated state of mind he could not pay enough attention to his job/sorrounding which led to slip/trip and consequent injury
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew advised to raise their grievances in structured manner as per company policy. If it is felt by any member that he is not in calm state of mind due to any reason whatsoever, he should report to H.O.D instead of joining work which can cause harm to himself/ his coolegue/ship property.
ClosingNote: Motorman Murti was repatriated from the next port. He is sent to the company doctor for medical checks. His case will be investigated by the manning dept and proper action will be taken.

IncidentDetails: Slipped on deck while taking rounds.He is suffering from back pain (lower chest region), shoulder pain and pain in right hand (upper arm and fore arm).Swelling observed at the back of his right arm and slight bruising near his shoulder area also.
ImmediateAction: Immedieatly shifted him in Hospita and Pain relief spray and Ibuprofen Tab (400mg) administered onboard. He has been advised to keep Ice bags near the areas where he is having pain. And he has been taken off from work.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: While crossing deck longitudinal, lost balance and fell over and injured his shoulder and back.  Indirect Cause: Unsafe act of crossing longitudinal by walking on it. Hazards not identified properly.  Root Cause: Lack of knowledge COSWP not adhered to for Safe Movement. Lack of situational awareness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Master to carry out safety meeting with all crew and discuss COSWP ch Safe Movement onboard Ship
Corrective Action: Master to carry out safety meeting with all crew and discuss COSWP ch Safe Movement onboard Ship
LessionLearnt: Situational awareness is key to analyse hazards in ample time and one must not loose sense of it. Walking on deck where multiple obstructions are there, one must be cautious of his movement around those areas.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Corrective action implemented and crew to be motivated to observe safe practices at all times.

IncidentDetails: Crew member (Chief cook) suffered Bell's Palsy.
IncidentEventDetails: Crew member (Chief cook) suffered Bell's Palsy.
ImmediateAction: Immediately requested for Telemedical Advice from company doctor.    Prescribed medication sent back to vessel and duly complied.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Patient was initially diagnosed by in house Doctor Balaji as Bells Palsy and was recommended to be referred to a physician in the next port of call.  Soon as vessel arrived at Santos, arrangement was made for him to be disembarked from anchorage and admitted to a hospital for further checks. He was diagnosed as having suffered from Ischemic stroke, Hemiparesis. After a few days he was recommended as FIT TO FLY to him home town. He is presently is his home town undergoing treatment and crewing Philippines is overseeing the same.
DirectCause: Chief Cook suffered Ischemic stroke, Hemiparesis.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Strengthen the medical checkups prior joining and checking if there are any underlying causes which could result in such unfortunate medical complications whilst onboard.
Corrective Action: Based on the diagnosis and photographs from Master, consulted inhouse doctor Dr.Balaji and advised medicines. Once vessel arrived at Santos anchorage, patient was disembarked and sent ashore to a doctor. He was kept in the hospital for few days for tests/checkup and then repatriated to his home town where further treatment is in progress
ClosingNote: Patient was disembarked from the vessel at anchorage and sent to hospital. He was admitted for few days for diagnosis and was then repatriated to his home town for further treatment. Crewing Philippines is monitoring the same

IncidentDetails: While securing plastic chemical drum on stbd midship store which had become loose due to rolling  one drum hit Bosun left leg ankle
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was transiting from Bataan to Batam , during the passage vessel encountered long swell as a result the lashing of tank cleaning chemical drum stored on top of midship store became loose . Chief Officer , AB Kishen and Bosun Rao went for securing the drum while securing one of the plastic drum hit bosun left leg ankle.
ImmediateAction: First aid given, medical advice taken from Dr Balaji
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Pain in ankle with slight swelling
DirectCause: improper securing of the drums. The drums should always be lashed with wire rope.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: all drums to be confirmed lashed with wire rope.
ClosingNote: closed

During the mooring operations 3rd spring line was sent from Focsle deck, While the rope was being heaved in warping drum . The vessel started to surge Violently and the rope parted. The rope hit the AB standing on the Deck on his fore hands.

Corrective Action: Mooring  operations on such weather conditions need to be avoided on Open Sea berths.

IncidentDetails: While coming out of the washroom, second officer steppped on the door mat and slipped. He tried to control himself but while doing so, his body weight came on fore part of his right foot and bent.
IncidentEventDetails: It happened when the vessel was on slow steaming due to adverse weather conditions in Yingkou, china. Vsl was not rolling at the time of accident. While coming out from the washroom, he stepped on the door mat and slipped. Whilst trying to control himself, his fore part of the right feet came under strain and got imbalance & fall. He suffered injury on his right feet.
ImmediateAction: Cold compression was applied. Administered Combiflam 400mg. Medical report prepared and sent to Dr Balaji for medical advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Twisting of Right foot.
DirectCause: Slippery nature of floor below the door mats.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All door mats to be secured against the floor.
LessionLearnt: Anti-skid rubber mats to be used. All unsuitable rubber mats to be identified and removed from locations.
ClosingNote: Anti-skid rubber mats to be used. All unsuitable rubber mats to be identified and removed from locations.

IncidentDetails: One of crew memeber injured his middle finger of right hand while deck washing.
IncidentEventDetails: Before arrival vungtao, crew memebers doing deck washing with kew machine. For changing the location of kew machine  os stopped kew machine. After stopping the kew machine os asked deck cadet to release pressure by pressing the washing gun trigger. Deck cadet did the same one time & thought pressure is released . He took the gun in his left hand and put the mouth of gun on right hand and pressed the gun trigger without realising the pressure still in the line, caused damaged to his gloves and injured his middle finger of right hand
ImmediateAction: first aid given, seek medical assistance from company doctor , and given medicine as advised. Arranged for shore doctor on arrival.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MIddle finger of right hand has rapture/ wound. Wound not fully healed.  Raptured part is now clot with fresh muscles, expecting another 3 to 4 days for fully recover.
DirectCause: Work procedure not followed
Proposedcorrectiveactions: No cadet or trainee to handle high pressure machine at any time. Must follow work assigned to them. All crew training  for handling high pressure machines
Corrective Action: All Ship Staff briefed in Safe Committee Meeting regarding this incident and effective supervision of Trainees and Ship Staff will less Sea Experience. Reference made to Code of Safe Working Practices and Health & Safety Manual
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: No Concerns observed in Weather conditions, Rest Hour compliance, Alcohol Policy compliance. To ensure effective supervision of Trainees, and other Ship Staff with Less Sea Experience
ClosingNote: First Aid case - Ship Staff recovered from the injury

IncidentDetails: One crew member finger injured due to jamming between door
IncidentEventDetails: At sea , due to heavy weather , the vessel was rolling.One crew member was coming out of ships office.The ships office door swung due to rolling and could not be controlled by the crew member.He attempted to remove the hand immediately but before that the door banged causing injury to one of the fingers
ImmediateAction: First aid administered
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Clotting of blood under the nail.
DirectCause: The door not handled with the handle provided.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew members were briefed to follow safe practices especially during heavy weather and not to place the hand between the door and the frame , but to handle any door with the handle provided.
Corrective Action: All crew members were briefed to follow safe practices especially during heavy weather and not to place the hand between the door and the frame , but to handle any door with the handle provided.
ClosingNote: Agree with the corrective action mentioned. Please discuss during the safety meeting as well.

IncidentDetails: Whilst the vessel was at port of Busan, at 1345LT on 25 Jan 2019 Bosun was working inside the deck workshop and removing the coupling from fresh water transparent hose with the help of knife and due to  distraction the knife got slipped from his hand and led to a laceration of about 3 cm on his index finger left hand after tearing the leather glove.
IncidentEventDetails: Bosun was working inside the deck workshop and removing the coupling from fresh water transparent hose with the help of knife as the hose was hard due to cold temperature. He was holding the pipe coupling in his left hand and tried to remove the coupling from the hose with the help of knife from right hand. While removing the coupling from the hose, duty AB saw Bosun inside the workshop and called him as the ship chandler had come on board and due to distraction the knife got slipped from his hand and led to a laceration of about 3 cm on his index finger left hand after tearing the leather glove.
ImmediateAction: The wound was wrapped with bandage and the agent was notified at the same time to send him to doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Laceration of about 3 cm on his index finger left hand.
DirectCause: Bosun was holding the pipe coupling in his left hand and tried to remove the coupling from the hose with the help of knife from right hand.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The coupling should have been kept on the table and should have been hold with a help of a plier instead of holding in hand.  If the knife would have slipped, in such a case it will hit the table rather than hand.  Alternatively the pipe and the coupling should have been put inside a vise for proper holding and grip
Corrective Action: The coupling should have been kept on the table and should have been hold with a help of a plier instead of holding in hand.  If the knife would have slipped, in such a case it will hit the table rather than hand.  Alternatively the pipe and the coupling should have been put inside a vise for proper holding and grip
ClosingNote: First Aid Case - Master confirmed Ship Staff recovered from Injury

IncidentDetails: On 09th Jan 2019, Vessel was at sea on her passage to Houston. Pump man was given Job of routine greasing of mooring winches, And Tr OS was assigned to assist pump man in this job.    Pump man and Tr OS had carried out greasing operation three times in previous months and they are familiarised with job.  0800LT: Before starting of job, tool box meeting was carried out and job plan was discussed with the team.  0800-1000 LT: Pump man and Tr OS started the job from Fore castle winches and after tea time (1030LT) started greasing of forward breast winches.   1030LT: Greasing was carried out with winch in slow running state. After greasing, winch control lever was put to neutral. And cleaning of old grease was started. Winch lever safety locking mechanism was not engaged. Tr OS was having PPE required for job. But while cleaning old grease his gloves got soiled with grease and he removed his gloves. And continued cleaning old grease with rags without gloves.    Abt 1050 LT : Tr OS accidentally stepped back and his body touched the winch control lever resulting in winch moving in heave direction. Tr OS’s hand got stuck in winch gear engaging assembly. Tr OS shouted and pump man stopped the winch immediately. Pump man slowly moved the winch in opposite direction after checking with Tr Os and cleared Tr OS hand from winch.    1055LT: Pump man shifted Tr OS into hospital and informed chief officer over walkie talkie. Master was informed same time. Tr OS was given initial first aid. Wound cleared with anti-septic solution. Compression given to stop bleeding. And rested in hospital with hand elevated position. There was four wound on upper wrist and wrist was swollen. Bleeding stopped after an hour time. Company doctor was consulted immediately. And following treatment was given for 5 days.  1.Clean the wound with normal saline   2.Povidone iodine ointment for external use   3.T.Paracetamol 1-1-1   4.T.Tramadol 1-0-1   5.C.Amoxycillin and Clavulanate 1-0-1   6.Limb Elevation     All the above for 5 days

IncidentEventDetails: SECOND OFFICER WHILE CARRYING SCBA CYLINDERS FOR REPLENISHMENT DOWN THE STAIRS OF BOSUN'S STORE STEPPED AT THE CORNER OF THE STEP TWISTING HIS ANKLE CAUSING SLIGHT SWELLING

IncidentDetails: The above crew member was working near Gangway port side & got injury on left thumb which got smashed while carrying maintenence on port side gangway stowing davit.
IncidentEventDetails: The above crew member was working near Gangway port side & got injury on left thumb which got smashed while carrying  maintenence on port side gangway stowing davit.
ImmediateAction: Cleaned the area with disinfectant  and applied antibacterial ointment followed by Dressing.Applied direct presure to wound with a dresssing.Left arm kept in elevated position above heart level to minimise bleeding.After 2 hrs of observation, found that bleeding stopped and no swelling.He has sensation all around wound. All vital sign checked and found normal, and person is stable.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
LessionLearnt: Hand injuries can be associated with working with machinery or equipment  • The materials being used or the job process might be hazardous • Hand tools or powered hand tools may be faulty or improperly used
ReviewComments: To avoid hand injuries: – Know the hazards and dangers in the job to be done – Be aware of pinch points – Be aware of hot areas – Be aware of rotating or moving surfaces – Automated machinery may be controlled by remote control, or delayed timing devices that cause the machine to start automatically – Loose clothing and jewelry may be caught up in moving machinery – Never remove machine safeguards or operate machinery with safeguards removed
ClosingNote: The most common causes of hand injuries are: – Carelessness – Lack of awareness – Boredom – Disregard for safety procedures – Distractions

Corrective Action: To advise all crew members to be careful and attentive while working and use proper PPE for the job.

IncidentDetails: Masterwhile taking round slipped on deck & fell down. His right foot slipped & weight came on left foot, which got twisted.
IncidentEventDetails: He has gone on deck for taking round along with technical suptd., Chief Officer & Bosun. After coming out from Forepeak store, when he put his right foot on the platform of windlass, same got slipped and entire weight of the body came on lef foot with the twist. Subsequently he fell down on the deck and got sudden pain in left foot.No other injury was observed.
ImmediateAction: He was brought inside the accommodation using trolley. He could walk using support with pain. Ice pack was given & also Volini spray was applied. Brufen 400 was given & continued 3 times a day. Swelling apparently increased next day. There is no other injury except left foot.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling of left foot,  Pain while walking, requires support to walk
DirectCause: Fine shipping spray on forecastle had caused the surface slippery. The platform consists of athwartship rings which do not provide any hedging if moving on same in athwartship direction.  The slippery surface caused the right foot of the person to not to hold and all body weight came on left foot all of a sudden causing twist in the left leg.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. To move with care and avoid going on forecastle in the case of shipping sprays.    2. In the case of Platform with rings, always move accross the rungs and not along with the rungs, so that one gets a protection from skidding.
ReviewComments: Area to be painted with Anti skid    How to Prevent Slip and trips.  Both slips and trips result from some a kind of unintended or unexpected change in the contact between the feet and the ground or walking surface. This fact shows that good housekeeping, quality of walking surfaces (flooring), selection of proper footwear, and appropriate pace of walking are critical for preventing fall incidents.  Housekeeping Good housekeeping is the first and the most important (fundamental) level of preventing falls due to slips and trips. It includes:  cleaning all spills immediately marking spills and wet areas mopping or sweeping debris from floors removing obstacles from walkways and always keeping walkways free of clutter securing (tacking, taping, etc.) mats, rugs and carpets that do not lay flat always closing file cabinet or storage drawers covering cables that cross walkways keeping working areas and walkways well lit replacing used light bulbs and faulty switches Without good housekeeping practices, any other preventive measures such as installation of sophisticated flooring, specialty footwear or training on techniques of walking and safe falling will never be fully effective.  For more information about effective housekeeping visit the OSH Answers document on Workplace Housekeeping - Basic Guide.  Flooring Changing or modifying walking surfaces is the next level of preventing slip and trips. Recoating or replacing floors, installing mats, pressure-sensitive abrasive strips or abrasive-filled paint-on coating and metal or synthetic decking can further improve safety and reduce risk of falling. However, it is critical to remember that high-tech flooring requires good housekeeping as much as any other flooring. In addition, resilient, non-slippery flooring prevents or reduces foot fatigue and contributes to slip prevention measures.  Footwear In workplaces where floors may be oily or wet or where workers spend considerable time outdoors, prevention of fall incidents should focus on selecting proper footwear. Since there is no footwear with anti-slip properties for every condition, consultation with manufacturers' is highly recommended.  Properly fitting footwear increases comfort and prevents fatigue which, in turn, improves safety for the employee. For more information on footwear visit the OSH Answers document on Safety Footwear.   What can you do to avoid falling at work? You can reduce the risk of slipping on wet flooring by:  taking your time and paying attention to where you are going adjusting your stride to a pace that is suitable for the walking surface and the tasks you are doing walking with the feet pointed slightly outward making wide turns at corners You can reduce the risk of tripping by:  keeping walking areas clear from clutter or obstructions keeping flooring in good condition always using installed light sources that provide sufficient light for your tasks using a flashlight if you enter a dark room where there is no light making sure that things you are carrying or pushing do not prevent you from seeing any obstructions, spills, etc.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: Fourth engineer got  injured in his right leg  below the knee(tabia bone) and ankle.
IncidentEventDetails: E/R bottom platform floor plate  on top of the low sea chest (port side)  was opened for routine maintenance on sea chest.During Fourth engineer routine watch keping rounds in bottom floor of engine room,   while walking near to the area his  right leg slipped in to the opening(200 mm x 400 mm)as its a blind end
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed Master  regarding injury.Bandages and pain relief spray given for swelling.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fourth engineer got his right leg injured below the knee(tabia bone) and ankle. Suspected internal injury.
DirectCause: Fourth engineer during rounds not vigilant about the floor plate opening for maintenance which discussed in tool box meeting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Informed all crew about the consequence and importance of discussing in the tool box meeting.
Corrective Action: All Ship Staff Briefed during Safety Committee Meeting regarding this incident. To ensure compliance with Safe Working Practices. Any gratings open Should be clearly Barricaded, and should be suitably Secured on completion of Work.
LessionLearnt: Grating opened for fixing Harsonic filter (Antifouling system) on the low sea chest which is below the gratings. Person attending to the Job went to the workshop to bring the tools for the job, and no one was attending the space with the Grating in open position. Grating to be either Closed or the area to be adequately Barricaded along with attendance.
ReviewComments: First Aid Case
ClosingNote: Alert Sent to all Vessels as per attachment in corrective action

IncidentDetails: AB while going down the ladder in cargo tank, his left leg got stuck in ladder rung and ankle twisted, causing swollen left ankle and bruising/swelling on back.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was schedule to arrive at port for loading cargo at Yanbu. It was  planned that prior arrival the final inspection of cargo tanks has to be carried out. Chief officer , AB- Vikas and AB- Solanki Started inpection at 0830 hrs, on 18-Feb-19.After inepection From 1W to 8W, at 1125hrs entry was made in 9P  COT by AB Vikas while Chief officer and AB Solanki was standing by near the tank dome. While going down ,after the first plateform  his leg got stuck in ladder step and ankle twisted. Immidiately bridge was informed and general emergency alarm was raised. At 1145 AB Vikas was lifted from tank by using rescue winch and stretcher and shifted to hospital. After examine his ankle found swollen and bruishing marks on the back . First aid was provided immidiately  and shore medical advise was sought.
ImmediateAction: He was immediately provided first aid and shore medical advise was sought.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: He was sent ashore for further treatment and was diagnosed with fracture of left leg heel bone.
DirectCause: Proper three point of contact Technique is not followed while going inside the Cargo tank.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Fall prevention harness attached with safety line  to be used while climbing down the ladder in the  enclosed space. All crew briefed about the importance of maintaining3-point contact on ladders.
Corrective Action: Fall prevention harness attached with safety line  to be used while climbing down the ladder in the  enclosed space. All crew briefed about the importance of maintaining3-point contact on ladders.
LessionLearnt: Vigilence and heightened alertness when carrying out hazardous activity like enclosed space entry. Continual dialogue required on enhancement of safety during enclosed space entry procedures
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Circulated with all fleet vessels
Other Part: Left leg diagnosed with fracture of  heel bone.

IncidentDetails: A crew member got injured in his head(back side) while stretching out cable from reefer unit to plug in.
IncidentEventDetails: At around 1245hrs lt 2 nd officer instructed AB,Mr.Antony to plug in reefer unit at bay 68 aft.  Duty ab went to bay 68 to plug in reefer unit at bay 68. Standing from lashing bridge, he streched out to pull out the cable form the reefer unit cable compartment. His felt some hard object fell from top to his back side of head, left hand side. (Suspect twistlock or any hard object may be from the gantry spreader. Injured crew didnt see what object had hit his head.Half the impact taken by helmet. Injured crew fell on lashing bridge, foreman informed duty officer a ship crew got injured in bay 68. Foreman informed the same to port authorities.
ImmediateAction: Informed port authorities, port medics came onboard within 5 min, first aid given and transfferd the injured crew to ambulance & taken to hospital. A ct scan check cariied out and found no serious injuries. Doctor declared injured crew fit for duty.Injured seaman came back onboard and vessel resumed passgae to rotterdam.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor injury
DirectCause: Injured crew didnt see the gantry movement on top of his head.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: HOD instructed all crew to work with safety and follow safe practices. During plug in/plug out of reefer units crew should wait till the gantry spreader has cleared off from the working area.
LessionLearnt: Reefer container handling to be carried out in pairs due to high voltage hazards. Loose lashing gear to be secured for good housekeeping upon departure from port.
ClosingNote: Training to be given to all crew working on deck while cargo operations in progress to ensure no job carried out with gantry crane operating overhead.
Other Part: Back side of head, left hand side.

Vessel: DK ABDUL RAZZAK KHALID ZAID AL-KHALID
IncidentDetails: 05:30 hrs LT : Cadet went down to CCR to bring some papers after taking Permission from Chief Officer.  05:40 hrs LT : 2nd/mate found Cadet lying Unconscious down on Back on Stairway Platform. He tried tried to wake up cadet but with no Result. 2nd/mate checked his Breathing & Pulse & found Normal. 2nd/ mate rushed to the bridge & sent Chief officer down. 2nd/off informed Master.
IncidentEventDetails: 24 FEB 2019  ==========   20:18 hrs LT : Cadet left Bridge after Bridge Watch.      25 FEB 2019  ==========   03:50 hrs LT : Cadet came & reported for Morning Watch.  05:30 hrs LT : Cadet went down to CCR to bring some papers after taking Permission from Chief Officer.  05:40 hrs LT : 2nd/mate found Cadet lying Unconscious down on Back on Stairway Platform. He tried tried to wake up cadet but with no Result. 2nd/mate checked his Breathing & Pulse & found Normal. 2nd/ mate rushed to the bridge & sent Chief officer down. 2nd/off informed Master.       0545 hrs LT : When Master came to the stairs found Cadet Fully Unconscious. Legs & Hands TOTALLY Stiff & Hands / Legs were Cold. Chief Officer was trying to wake up the cadet. Master Started Rubbing his feet & Hands to warm up. With no effect on the condition of Cadet, Master called up Bosun & Crew, to  shift Cadet to cadet Cabin in the same Deck. Covered his body with Blanket      0600 hrs LT : Captain called up Dr. Balaji & Office & reported the matter.  Dr. Balaji asked Master to Check his Vital Signs.    06:15 LT :   BP: 65/110   PULSE : 74  TEMPERATURE : 88.1F/31.2C    Shifted the Cadet to Hospital. Cadet then started opening his eyes but was still Unconscious    07:10 LT : Cadet came to Slight Conscious & started opening his eyes. He started responding to crew. He was able to hear the crew but was Unable to Speak .     07:10 LT : Cadet was  was given 02 Spoons of ORS after Consultation with Dr,. Balaji. When he was conscious.    07:30 LT : Cadet was  was given 04 Spoons of Mango Juice after Consultation with Dr,. Balaji      08:30 LT : Cadet become Fully conscious & reported that while coming from Stairs he Had Severe Headache & he Blacked out. He still reported Headache & loosing sensation on legs & hands.      08:30 LT:  BP:70/120  PULSE: 76  TEMPERATURE: 96.4F/35.8C    09:00 LT : GAVE SOME JUICE. REPORTED CONTINIOUS PAIN IN HEADACHE. ON THE PERMISSION OF COMPANY DOCTOR, GAVE 1 TAB OF Diazepam 5mg tab (Vallum).    10:45 LT: TOOK SLIGHT BREAKFAST(IDLI)  COMING TO NORMAL CONDITION. CAN ABLE TO TALK NORMALLY. HE TOLD THAT HE WANT TO SLEEP.    12:00 LT:   BP:70/120  PULSE: 72  TEMPERATURE: 96.4F/35.8C    13:30 LT: TOOK MEAL(DAL+RICE+JUICE)          15:00 LT: 2 BISCUITS+JUICE    18:00 LT:  BP:80/140  PULSE: 72  TEMPERATURE: 97.4F/36.5C  18:00 LT: TOOK LIGHT DINNER (SOUP+2 CHAPATI/BREAD)    18:50 LT:  AGAIN SEIZURE ATTACK  ( SIMILAR TO FIT )  TIGHTENING HIS HANDS, FEETS AND FINGERS, AND JAW. BODY STIFFING.   FORCEFULLY BREATHING AND AGAIN WENT BACK TO UNCONSCIOUS MODE.    AFTER 10 MINUTES, BODY GETTING RELAXING POSITION BUT CADET STILL IN UNCONSCIOUS MODE.  AFTER 15 MINUTES HE OPENED HIS EYES, BUT DOES NOT REMEMBER WHAT HAS HAPPENED 10 MINUTES BEFORE.    STILL COMPLAINING PINTCHING PAIN IN HIS HEAD, EYE AND EAR AREA AND ADJECENT AREAS.  SLEPT FOR SOMETIMES.     1900 hrs LT : Master called up Office & Dr. Balaji & reported the same. Master Called up Charterer’s, Mr. Ajay Sharma, DPA, Marine Super, Tech Super & Dr. Balaji and appraised of the situation. Master had Informed All that if the Situation Worsens then Master will have to Divert to the nearest Port of Refuge. At that Moment, Vsl was in the center of Bay of Biscay with the nearest Port  BREST /  LORIENT about 12-13 hrs Passage.  Master Also contacted C.I.R.M & sent e-mails to them. As per Tel-con with CIRM & Dr. Balaji master was asked to Observe the cadet.     Since cadet Came Back to Normal Condition in 10 mins & did not have any other Attack. Vsl Observed him for some more time. At around 2200 hrs LT (  2-3 hrs after the 1850 hrs LT  Attack) . Master Decided to Observe the Cadet for some more time in Consultation with Dr. Balaji. Vsl kept her Normal Course.      WOKEUP AT 23:30 LT. TOOK 2 BISCUITS AND HALF GLASS JUICE AND SLEPT.      26 FEB 2019  ==========   05:50 LT : SECOND SEIZURE ATTACK (SIMILAR TO FIT) HAPPENED.  TIGHTENING HIS HANDS, FEETS AND FINGERS, AND JAW. BODY STIFFING.   FORCEFULLY BREATHING AND AGAIN WENT BACK TO UNCONSCIOUS MODE.    AFTER 10 MINUTES BODY GOING TO RELAXING MODE. HANDS, FEETS GETTING TO NORMAL CONDITION.      0550 hrs LT : Master Called up Dr. Balaji & Office again. At that time Vsl was Just Abeam of port of Brest ( Prior to entry to Quessant TSS ). After getting E-mail from CIRM  & then Consultation with Dr. Balaji Decision was taken to Disembark the cadet for Shore Assistance. Vsl Decided to Alter towards port of Brest for Medical Evacuation.        0600 hrs LT : Master informed MRCC Crosen & requested for Medical Assistance & Altered the course towards BREST to Dis-emark the Cadet. MRCC Crosen also Consulted the FRANCE SHORE Doctor & Agreed to Dis-embark the cadet.  Owners,  Charterer’s, Office & Agents informed.      1030 hrs LT : EOSP     1100 hrs LT : Vsl Arrived RV Posn ( 48 16.04 N / 004 53.27 W )    1112 hrs LT : MRCC boat alongside     1126 hrs LT : Cadet Disembarked on MRCC Service Boat    1128 hrs LT : MRCC Boat Away    1130 hrs LT : Vsl Turned Around / Back
ImmediateAction: 0545 hrs LT : When Master came to the stairs found Cadet Fully Unconscious. Legs & Hands TOTALLY Stiff & Hands / Legs were Cold. Chief Officer was trying to wake up the cadet. Master Started Rubbing his feet & Hands to warm up. With no effect on the condition of Cadet, Master called up Bosun & Crew, to  shift Cadet to cadet Cabin in the same Deck. Covered his body with Blanket      0600 hrs LT : Captain called up Dr. Balaji & Office & reported the matter.  Dr. Balaji asked Master to Check his Vital Signs.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: SEIZURE ATTACK  ( SIMILAR TO FIT )
DirectCause: Failure to follow normal regulations while under going pre joining medicals.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ? Incidence report is shared with Manning team and Company doctors, for future learning and to encourage joining crew to complete correct information in self declaration section of Pre-joining Medicals, so that similar situations could be avoided in future.
ClosingNote: Job closed 18/04

IncidentDetails: While turning the Flywheel for No 1 compressor during the maintenance , Cadet inserted the Rod in the Hole of flywheel  and tried to pull down the turn the wheel. When the flywheel turned , he did not remove the rod and his hands resulting in his finger getting caught between the rod & flywheel cover end.
IncidentEventDetails: Gas Eng, A 3Rd Off , Engine fitter wre working in No 1 Compressor for overhauling. Deck cadet was assisting in taking Photographs & turning the Flywheel manually when required. Prior to the injury cadet has alraedy turned the fly wheel several times . Only this time he turned the flywheel and did not remove the rod after turning. As the wheel turned , cadet was still holding the rod and his finger got caught between the rod & the flywheel cover end.
ImmediateAction: Cadet taken to CCR, cold water rinsing done for 15 mins. Injured area cleaned with rubbing alcholo, sparyed with Bitadine . Later dressing done using Iodine cream.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury to righ hand index finger . ( no fracture n only flesh wound).
DirectCause: Correct procedure not followed. After Turning the  wheel , rod was not removed , neither the concerned person let go of the rod. Also correct PPE was not used. Cotton gloves were used instead of Leather gloves.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to be given to all concerned crew regarding correct Procedures.
ClosingNote: Training Carried out for all Crew to avoid similar injuries in future. Attached training record. Cadet Amal George injured finger was treated onboard as it was only a flesh wound - first Aid case, not required Doctor visit. He recovered well and was back to normal duties on board.

IncidentDetails: While the GS was mopping the stairs he lost his balance and  slipped. To prevent a fall, he immediately reached out for the railing for support, however, the sudden grasp on the railing and his body movement, caused a severe sprain in his right shoulder(Deltoid area). IncidentEventDetails: While the GS was mopping the stairs he lost his balance and  slipped. To prevent a fall, he immediately reached out for the railing for support, however, the sudden grasp on the railing and his body movement, caused a severe sprain in his right shoulder(Deltoid area). ImmediateAction: Ice pack has been applied on the shoulder, Ibuprofen 400mg has been administered for pain relief and the crew member given rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Right shoulder sprained causing localised swelling and pain. Movement of the hand is normal while walking, however sharp pain in shoulder area is experienced when the hand is raised to shoulder level or above. Suspect a muscle or ligament tear.

IncidentDetails: DE-RUSTING MAINTENANCE AT PORTSIDE GANGWAY USING HYDROBLASTER WITH 500BAR CAPACITY. THE ONE HOLDING THE TRIGGER GUN IS AB AND OS IS ONLY ASSISTING THE AB IN THIS MAINTENANCE WORK. AFTER DE-RUSTING SOME PARTS OF THE GANGWAY IN BETWEEN THE RAILINGS THE AB PASSED ON THE TRIGGER GUN TO OS SO THAT HE CAN GET OUT OF THE RAILINGS AND COME BACK ON THE MAIN DECK. WHILE HOLDING THE TRIGGER GUN THE OS TRIED TO WASHED THE PARTS OF THE MAIN DECK WHERE THE AB HAS BEEN DE-RUSTING AND WITHOUT REALIZING THAT THE TRIGGER GUN HAS PASSED BY HIS SAFETY STEEL TOE RUBBER BOOTS. AFTER FEW SECONDS HE SUDDENLY FEELS PAIN IN HIS LEFT TOES AND WHEN HE CHECKED IT IS ALREADY BLEEDING AND GOT INJURED  DUE TO HYDRO BLASTER IS 500 BARS IT STILL PENETRATES THE STEEL TOE RUBBER BOOTS.
ImmediateAction: HE STOPPED THE TRIGGER GUN SINCE IT HAS A SAFETY TRIGGER DEVICE AND THE AB  STOPPED THE HYDROBLASTER IMMEDIATELY AND INFORMED CHIEF OFFICER, MASTER AND MEDICAL OFFICER TO APPLY FIRST AID. ALSO ASK COMPANY DOCTOR TO PROVIDE ASSISTANT ON WHAT MEDICINES TO GIVE.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: LEFT TOES GOT INJURED BY HYDROBLASTER EVEN WEARING A SAFETY STEEL TOE RUBBER BOOTS.
DirectCause: UN AWARE OF THE DANGER OF HYDROBLASTER
Proposedcorrectiveactions: PUT THE TRIGGER GUN 1 METER AWAY FROM THE USERS BODY. KEEP SOMEBODY ON STANBY NEAR THE HYDROBLASTER MOTOR.
Corrective Action: Proposed corrective action cannot be left blank
ClosingNote: CAR verified satisfactory and closed

IncidentDetails: OS  Redhford Saluta was in the process of removing cargo light from  No8 Cargo hold . IncidentEventDetails: At 0955 Hrs OS Redhford saluta opened the No.8 Hold Access aft for removing the cargo light.The hatch access was not secured with locking pin whilst entering . At 1000 hrs after removing the cargo light whilst exiting the No.8 hold from the aft access . He grabbed the unsecured hatch door for exiting the hatch resulting in the door hitting him on his face and causing injury on the upper lips.
ImmediateAction: OS Redfhord saluta was moved to hospital & first aid was administered . Vsl  sought medical advice from company doctor for medication & teratment steps. Body vitals were taken & reported to office & doctor on timely baiss.Blleding was stopped & medicines & first aid given as per advice from company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep Cut on Upper lips due to impact of the hatch access door hittin g the face   one Teeth loosened in front due to impact   Pain on the lower Jaw
DirectCause: 1. Improper Securing of the Hatch Access prior entry into cargo holds  2.Lack of awareness
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training of the crew for proper securing of the hatch access prior entry.
ClosingNote: Incorrect report. To disregard.

IncidentDetails: Injury to Able Seaman by gant-line while taking out condemned mooring ropes (to be landed ashore) from forepeak store . Gant-line slipped off from warping drum of mooring winch & hit the left little finger & right thigh of AB.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) Bosun, Pumpman & Able Seaman engaged in taking out condemned mooring ropes from forepeak store using mooring winch.  2) Able Seaman was taking rounds of gant-line (mooring rope tied to it) on warping drum with the winch rolling. After taking three rounds, while taking the fourth round, the gant-line slipped off from the drum & hit his left hand little finger & Right Thigh.
ImmediateAction: Winch stopped immediately. AB was administered first aid - Ice pack & Pain relief spray.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First aid case only with swelling of Right thigh Muscle front area & Left hand Little finger
DirectCause: Self distraction while at work; handling rope
Proposedcorrectiveactions: briefed deck crew & all crew during safety meeting.
LessionLearnt: Lack of training  lack of situational awareness
ReviewComments: MSMP Part E  c. Training All person who are supposed to be part of mooring team should undergo familiarization training on joining vessel by relieving person or by fellow members so that they will be ready for mooring stations and acknowledge in SF-10. Training session for mooring team should be organize on quarterly basis, to impart practical training on ship specific safe mooring practices. Records shall maintain in SF-17 form. These training shall include following  he proximity of ship-side observation platforms to ‘snap-back’ zones. ? Safe methods for clearing fouled lines. ? Onboard communications during mooring operations, discussing ways in which communications between the officer in charge and remotely located winch operators (meaning not in direct line of sight) can be improved. The use of additional portable radios might be considered. ? Safe mooring practices - e.g. ensuring that the maximum number of turns on a smooth mooring drum or warping end does not exceed four. ? The procedures for the controlled lowering of ropes, wires and messengers to mooring boats. ? The procedure for the controlled rigging of emergency towing-off pennants (fire wires). ? Review of the mooring risk assessment. ? The provision of Quarterly practical on board training with all of the crew. ? Correct preparation of winch and mooring equipment including pre-use inspection. ? Job allocation of the mooring team (Officer In Charge, winch operator, brake operator). ? Demonstrate the correct signals to be used during mooring. ? Identify the optimum position of OIC with respect to a maintaining helicopter view over the entire operation. ? Demonstrate the requirement to use a relay signaller if the winch operator is not in line of sight of the OIC. ? Demonstrate the correct procedure for putting the winch in gear. ? Demonstrate the correct and safe use of a warping drum. ? Demonstrate the correct lead for ropes and wires to winches and warping drums around pedestal rollers (old men). ? Simulate the picking up of a tugs line and placing on a set of bitts using a soft rope through an appropriate fairlead (using warping drum and stopper). ? Demonstrate the correct use of rope stopper and wire stopper. ? Demonstrate the correct method of turning a soft rope up on a set of bitts. ? Simulate the letting go of a tugs line in a controlled manner. ? Demonstrate the correct procedure for tending mooring already under tension. ? Demonstrate the correct procedure for transferring lines onto split tension drums (Where fitted). ? Demonstrate the correct application of brake to ensure that the brake will render before applying excess weight to mooring lines ? Demonstrate the correct condition in which to leave the winch upon completion of mooring. ? Practically demonstrate where the snap back zones are. ? Demonstrate the correct method of rigging a fire wire (OCIMF Mooring equipment guidelines). Each member of mooring team should watch videotel DVD for safe mooring at frequency of maximum 6 months.
ClosingNote: closed

IncidentDetails: On departure from Manila while securing Gangway A.B. ABDUL SARTHAR MUSTHAQ ALI got his left elbow injured by hitting on Ship's Railing behind him. Due to sudden and hard hit he got swelling of the elbow area with a lot of pain.
ImmediateAction: TREATMENT GIVEN:  1) Ibuprofen 200MG : 4 nos, S.O.S  2) Pain Relief Spray  : 1 No  3)Antacid Gel     : 2 Spoon along with pain killer.   Applied Cold Compress on the injured area and bandaged with crepe.
