IncidentDetails: Crew member injured finger while using hammer
IncidentEventDetails: While changing the brake liner of the aft spring winch, One of the crew member involved in the Job as assistance to the pumpman, was using a hammer to remove the pin holding the brake band which was stuck.   While hammering the pin to take it out, he got his finger jammed in between the hammer and the platform of the winch brake.   This caused a cut to his forefinger.
ImmediateAction: Crew member was given first aid, stopped bleeding, dressing done and given rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Personal Injury to crew member. Cut in his forefinger.
DirectCause: Inadequate Risk Assessment as the hazard of the base plate as obstruction was not identified in the activity during hammering.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Any such activity involving hammering to be well analysed, included in the Risk Assessment and discussed in the Tool box meeting.
Corrective Action: Any such activity involving hammering to be well analysed, included in the Risk Assessment and discussed in the Tool box meeting.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: After completing painting work on lashing bridge, AB climbed down lashing bridge, while climbing down vertical ladder his hand slipped and fell on cross deck from about 1.5 mtr height.Suffered  slight injury and abrasion on left lower back/ left triceps/fore head and small cut below right knee.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1700 after completing painting work on lashing bridge, AB climed down lashing bridge, while climbing down vertical ladder his hand slipped and fell on cross deck from about 1.5 mtr height.Suffered  slight injury and abrasion on left lower back/ left triceps/fore head and small cut below right knee.At 1705 Bosun called up bridge and informed that AB had fallen from lashing bridge.Immediately Master/C-Engg/C-off rushed to ship office found AB with injuries,First aid administrated immediately.
ImmediateAction: AB brought to ship office by Bosun/Tr.Os,First aid administrated by Master/C-Engg/C-off.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight abrasion due to fall on left lower back, left triceps, and forehead.Slight swelling observed on these parts,Ice packs applied no further swelling observed/developed.
DirectCause: Possible slippery spots on vertical ladder,improper holding position.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: While climbing ladder up / down due precaution to be taken by individual
Corrective Action: Briefed all Ship Staff during Safety Committee Meeting to prevent recurrence of this incident
ReviewComments: As on 20th June 2019, Vessel confirmed that the AB recovered from Injury and carrying out Normal Duties
ClosingNote: Preventive measures as per Corrective action Section

IncidentDetails: At/around 1105LT/01May2019 -  After departure from Lay berth during testing of engines from ECR Vsl gave Slow ahead command but Engine RPM failed to increase beyond 48RPM (Dead Slow). At Around 1120LT/01.may.2019 - ME control changed over to local and Engines tried out satisfactorily. Considering testing is inorder, Tugs were casted off and it was decided to test the engines again from ECR from F'Ahd to F'Astern. prior departing to NPOC. For this At/around 1150LT/ 01May2019 - Second engineer started changing over Control from Local to ECR. For changing over ME control from Local to ECR , 2nd Engineer disengaged Fuel Lever linkage from Local. While trying to engage the same Fuel lever with Governor linkage his finger got stuck between frame and Spring loaded cylinder connecting to the governor, Resulting in a crushing injury. At/around 1210LT/01.may2019 - Vsl changed over to local control and proceeded to drop anchor for sending second engineer to hospital/For testing of Main Engine. at 1224LT/ vsl anchored at DryDock Anchorage in Zhoushan.  At/around 1254LT/ Second Engineer Disembarked for treatment ashore.
IncidentEventDetails: At/around 1105LT/01May2019 -  After departure from Lay berth during testing of engines from ECR Vsl gave Slow ahead command but Engine RPM failed to increase beyond 48RPM (Dead Slow). At Around 1120LT/01.may.2019 - ME control changed over to local and Engines tried out satisfactorily. Considering testing is inorder, Tugs were casted off and it was decided to test the engines again from ECR from F'Ahd to F'Astern. prior departing to NPOC. For this At/around 1150LT/ 01May2019 - Second engineer started changing over Control from Local to ECR. For changing over ME control from Local to ECR , 2nd Engineer disengaged Fuel Lever linkage from Local. While trying to engage the same Fuel lever with Governor linkage his finger got stuck between frame and Spring loaded cylinder connecting to the governor, Resulting in a crushing injury. At/around 1210LT/01.may2019 - Vsl changed over to local control and proceeded to drop anchor for sending second engineer to hospital/For testing of Main Engine. at 1224LT/ vsl anchored at DryDock Anchorage in Zhoushan.  At/around 1254LT/ Second Engineer Disembarked for treatment ashore.
ImmediateAction: First Aid Given to Second Engineer and Second Engineer disembarked for treatment ashore
DirectCause: Direct Cause - While trying to engage the same Fuel lever with Governor linkage his finger got stuck between frame and Spring loaded cylinder connecting to the governor. Resulting in a crushing injury. Indirect Cause - Main engine control was not working from ECR and decision was taken to mano engines from Local control.  Root Cause - Improper PPE, second engineer removed his leather gloves considering it will hamper his work when disengaging Fuel Lever linkage from Local.  Tool Box meeting was not conducted prior work was undertaken, since it was a emergency situation as vsl was unmoored and without tug assistance and engines were required at the earliest.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Crew briefed to be cautious when working and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Corrective Action: All Crew briefed to be cautious when working and use appropriate PPE at all times.
LessionLearnt: One should be careful of his fingers when working on machinery where there are chances of fingers getting caught between moving parts is very high.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Shared with the fleet vessels for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: While testing the fire hoses, one of the fire hose nozzle gave off  and due to sudden pressure surge, 3rd officer who was holding the hose got sudden shock movement of the hose which caused him sprain in his hand.
IncidentEventDetails: At 1300 hrs  3rd officer was briefed by C/O regarding the hazards involved in fire hose pressure testing & minimum 2 person to carry out the test.  At 1330 hrs  3rd officer and one of deck crew commenced testing accomodation fire hoses and nozzles using fire pump at 8- 10 bar pressure in fire main system.  At 1535 hrs  ‘’B’’ Deck port side accomodation fire hose flaked down for pressure testing.  At 1537 hrs  Both side coupling checked for propeper connection with hydrant and nozzle.  At 1539 hrs  3rd officer holding the fire hose in firefighter position with proper PPE and standby crew opened the fire hydrant to about 10% toslowly charge the hose.  At 1540 hrs  Once the hose was fully charged, standby crew slowly opened the fire hydrant to maximum  At 1543 hrs  Hose was tested with jet mode at 10 bar pessure and found satisfactory. Then hydrant was closed to 50 percent.  At 1545 hrs   3rd officer started closing the nozzle slowly for the testing it and nozzle was fully shut in about a minute.  At 1546 hrs   Just few seconds after the nozzle was full shut, nozzle disk with rusted spindle  broke off suddenly . Immediately the hydrant was shut by the other crew. Due to this, 3rd officer who was holding the hose got sudden shock movement, pushed back and hit the right elbow of 3rd officer ,resulting in sprain .  At 1550 hrs  3rd officer was attended by C/O and 2nd officer for frist aid ,Master inforrmed.
ImmediateAction: 3rd officer was attended by C/O and 2nd officer for frist aid ,Master inforrmed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sprain in the right hand fore arm. First aid including cold packs, muscle relaxant gels were given besides pain killers. Recovered completely in 12 hours.
DirectCause: On checking the cause of the incident, found the nozzle disc screw securing threads got brittle and was secured to plastic part of the nozzle . Due to age, the material strenght has redcued & could not hold working pressure of the fire main system.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Internal parts of the remaining nozzles to be checked & if any found in similar condition same to be replaced with new nozzles.
ClosingNote: Agree with the Proposed action, Maker inspection guidelines shared with the vessel

IncidentDetails: DURING BREAK TIME OS REDFHORD G.SALUTA WAS GOING IN HIS CABIN, VESSEL WAS SLIGHTLY ROLLING AT THAT TIME, HE GOT IMPACT ON HIS FACE FROM HIS CABIN DOOR RESULTING IN INJURY ON LIPS AND LOOSENING OF TEETH.
IncidentEventDetails: VESSEL WAS ON HER VOYAGE TO PORT HEDLAND FROM BAOSHAN. ON 25.04.2019 AT 1000 HRS DURING BREAK TIME OS REDFHORD G.SALUTA WAS GOING IN HIS CABIN, VESSEL WAS SLIGHTLY ROLLING AT THAT TIME, HE GOT IMPACT ON HIS FACE FROM HIS CABIN DOOR  RESULTING IN INJURY ON LIPS AND LOOSENING OF TEETH.
ImmediateAction: IMMEDIATELY  OS REDFHORD G.SALUTA WAS SHIFTED TO HOSPITAL, FIRST AID GIVEN TO HIM. BODY PARAMETERS TAKEN SAME INFORMED TO COMPANY.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: IMPACT OF DOOR ON FACE RESULTING  INJURY ON LIPS AND LOOSENING OF TEETH.
DirectCause: INCIDENT OCCURED DUE TO NON SECURING OF DOOR AND PASSING BY WITHOUT AWARENESS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All CREW MADE AWARE OF THE INCIDENT,  MEETING CARRIED OUT ON SAME DAY ADVICED CREW TO BE AWARE AND TAKE CAUTION WHEN VESSEL IS ROLLING AND TO REMAIN VIGILANT WHILE PASSING THROUGH DOORS AND TO SECURE DOOR WITH THE PIN IF HAS TO BE KEPT OPEN EVEN FOR SHORT DURATION. DISCUSSED THE INCIDENT DURING SAFETY MEETING.
ClosingNote: CA found adequate

IncidentDetails: While attempting to turn the Aux Engine cylinder head overhaul stand with cylinder head secured on it,3/E's left foot accidentally came in way of turning cylinder head and the cylinder head's rocker arm stud beared upon his left foot toe area, resulting in swelling and bruise.
IncidentEventDetails: 00.00 hrs 3E taken over watch in sound state.  02.45 hrs While attempting to turn the Aux Engine cylinder head overhaul stand with cylinder head secured on it,3/E's left foot accidentally came in way of turning cylinder head and the cylinder head's rocker arm stud beared upon his left foot toe area, resulting in swelling and bruise.  02.50 Bridge notified about the incident.  02.55 Master and CE was informed  03.00 Firt aid administered and kept under observation.
ImmediateAction: Immediate first aid was administered and ice pack placed on the swollen area. Anti biotic medicines given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: External bruise and swelling continued.Medication administred as per company doctor's advice.
DirectCause: Unsafe act
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Area around the AE cylinder head overhaul stand demarked and  highlighted with cautionery remark to be kept clear during operation (Turning of stand).
ClosingNote: Agree with vessel

IncidentDetails: While  cranking the manual handle of the stbd ladder stopped coming up and suddenly the winch brake slackened momentarily and the ladder came down by gravity appx by 1 metre, atthis time the crew member who was holding the handle got hit above his right wrist as the handle rotated in the reverse direction causing a sprain injury.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel used the STBD gangway at Suez anchorage and prior berthing vessel started to heave up and secure the gangway for STBD side berthing. While picking up the motor stopped at a point and stopped coming up, by this time as pilot was onboard and due to time constraits, crew started to picking up the ladder with the help of cranking lever, and the ladder gradually started to come up slowly. At a point of time even with cranking the manual handle the ladder stopped coming up and suddenly the winch brake slackened momentarily and the ladder came down by gravity appx by 1 metre, at this time the crew member who was holding the handle got hit on his wrist as the handle rotated in the reverse direction.
ImmediateAction: Immediately the crew member was taken to ship’s hospital and first aid given . General check up carried out , and crew reported pain above his right wrist and experienced pain while moving his hand. Immediate check revealed that the was noapparent facture but sparain was most likely.Arrangements were made to send ashore for doctor check up  thru Agents.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew send ashore for doctor check up and doctor report says that no fracture  and strain occurred due to the sudden jerk. Prescribed medicines and recommended 2 wks of rest to the crew member .
DirectCause: Accommodation ladder winch brake slipped while trying to heave up manual hand cranking method.  Air motor failing to operate due to faulty air filter + Lubricator on the airline.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Extra lashing to be taken while heaving  up using hand cranking method to avoid such incidents
ClosingNote: Job not to start without proper lashing where required.

IncidentDetails: One of the deck crew while painting the steam return line on main deck piping on stbd side slipped and fell down on deck
IncidentEventDetails: 1.At 0800hrs Tool Box meeting held with deck crew and planned the steam return line on main deck piping on stbd side.  2.deck related jobs RA reviewed and Permits done  3.Able Bodied crew was alloted for painting steam return line on main deck piping on stbd side in   4.Bosun was continously monitoring the deck crew who was  painting the steam return line & was wearing safety harness tied to a strong point .  5.After completion of painting of the section of pipeline, the crew member removed the harness hook to shift.He walked on the pipeline & stepped upon the steam return line which has plastic cover over the lagging. The lagging dented due to his weight & this resulted in imbalance,which led to the fall on deck.  6 This  bosun noticed  the AB while fall down on deck
ImmediateAction: Crew member was given rest as he informed pain on Lattissimus dorsi left side ,Vitals was checked and all found normal.As per ship diagnosis Ice bag and Diclofenac gel was applied on the reddish spot.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The crew member has a lot of pain around the area of impact.
DirectCause: Failure to warn, Lack of situational awareness, work-space restriction, Inadequate supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thorough RA and tool box meeting for working aloft should be followed for working on pipelines. Adequate supervision should be provided, crew should not walk on pipelines. Length of Harness rope should be long enough to save crew falling on deck.
Corrective Action: Thorough RA and tool box meeting for working aloft should be followed for working on pipelines. Adequate supervision should be provided, crew should not walk on pipelines. Length of Harness rope should be long enough to save crew falling on deck.
ClosingNote: Job closed 01/07

IncidentDetails: AB Pabitra (Watch-keeper 2000to 0001)reported to 2off Slight Pain in left foot  ankle( due to twisting )while coming up for watch,for which he was treated for by 2off and was reported  fully recovered from that. But after 2 weeks he again reported with discomfort in the same place and slight swelling.Medical advice sought from company Doctor and Medication followed as per  advice from company doctor.After finding no signs of improvement in his condition,company doctor recommended for  consulting the Doctor in next port.Later he was diagnosed with Anterior Tarsal  Ligament lnjury and Declared Unfit for duty by shore doctor at Yokohama
IncidentEventDetails: AB Pabitra (Watch-keeper 2000to 0001)reported to 2off Slight Pain in left foot  ankle( due to twisting )while coming up for watch,for which he was treated for by 2off and was reported  fully recovered from that. But after 2 weeks he again reported with discomfort in the same place and slight swelling.Medical advice sought from company Doctor and Medication followed as per  advice from company doctor.After finding no signs of improvement in his condition,company doctor recommended for  consulting the Doctor in next port.Later he was diagnosed with Anterior Tarsal  Ligament lnjury and Declared Unfit for duty by shore doctor at Yokohama
ImmediateAction: He was given Paracetamol 500m&/ lbuprofen tablet 3 times a dayfor 3 days. Also to  apply diclofenac gel with Crepe bandage in affected area, and later by the recommendation of company doctor consulted doctor at Yokohama  It was noted that he got his ankle twisting while coming through the alleyway during moderate weather condition.All crew members advised not to rush any area at any time of the day/night, also to use the handrails in alleyways to always maintain 3 point contact effectively to avoid any injuries.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: AB Pabitra (Watch-keeper 2000to 0001)reported to 2off Slight Pain in left foot  ankle( due to twisting )while coming up for watch,for which he was treated for by 2off and was reported  fully recovered from that. But after 2 weeks he again reported with discomfort in the same place and slight swelling.Medical advice sought from company Doctor and Medication followed as per  advice from company doctor.After finding no signs of improvement in his condition,company doctor recommended for  consulting the Doctor in next port.Later he was diagnosed with Anterior Tarsal  Ligament lnjury and Declared Unfit for duty by shore doctor at Yokohama
DirectCause: At the the time on incident weather was reported moderate.  Crew member might have proceeded a bit fast paced through the alleyway to report to the  Bridge watch on time.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Placards to be posted displaying the warnings- ‘’Not to rush  through any area/ at anytime of day/night which can cause slip/trips/fall,  and use the alleyway handralings to maintain 3 point contact at all times.’’
Corrective Action: Ship Staff to be briefed about this incident and ensure compliance with Safe working practices, with reference to Code of Safe working practices and Health & Safety Manual.
LessionLearnt: Weather conditions reported to be good. Alcohol test was not taken immediately after the incident, however he was observed to be strictly sober. No rest hour violation observed. Ship Staff to be briefed. no additional measures required, as Hazards were not existing in the environment where this injury occured
ReviewComments: Medical Cost not involved after Medical Repatriation as Crew member willingly provided 'Personal declaration, Release and Quit Claim, inorder to carryout medical attention in his home town. No Medical report provided by Crew member
ClosingNote: Corrective Action as per attachment

IncidentDetails: Deck Cadet while assisting chief officer in operating ballast water tank valve ,hydraulic oil splashed from the manual operating nipple and came in contact with the cadets eyes.
IncidentEventDetails: While the vessel was in dry dock, vessel was asked to deballast all the tanks in order to carry out repair jobs in the tanks.  Chief officer along with the deck cadet proceeded on deck to open the valves maually using the hand pump and while connecting the hoses, hydraulic oil from the nipple splashed and few drops made contact with the cadets eyes.  Cadets eyes were immediately cleansed with eye wash liquid and upon examination no redness, infalmation was found and his vision was found to be satisfactory.
ImmediateAction: Immediately washed eyes with eye wash and adviced to wear safety goggles.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: No Injury noticed.
DirectCause: Improper PPE used for the job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tool box meeting to be carried out with all crew which should include the PPE to be used for carrying out such operations.
ClosingNote: CA found adequate

IncidentDetails: skin cut onright index finger due to unnoticed sharp part on cargo light bulb sucket
IncidentEventDetails: found busted cargo light on cargo hold, try to replace the bulb but AB took out his cotton gloves in order to pass inside his hand unfortunately didnt noticed the sharp socket inside and his finger's skin slightly cut when he turned to pullout the bulb.
ImmediateAction: First AID applied and ship's antibiotic medicine
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: SLight skin cut on index finger
DirectCause: Taking out his cotton gloves and cargo light condition of sharp bulb socket
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Personal RA applied on daily work asside from the breifing and never ignor any condition which is found unusual, it must be reported to the concerned officers. Safety awareness of person involve is low and must debriefed, monitored.

IncidentDetails: Boiler back fire due to leaking solenoid valve.
IncidentEventDetails: On 17.05.19 at about 18.30hrs boiler tripped and alarm activated.Boiler was stopped and Duty engineer informed the third engineer to reach the spot.Boiler purged manually for 10 mins and then after a while inspection cover opened.Oil leaking from the main burner nozzle is observed.when third engineer tried to close the inspection cover,  and  boiler backfired leading to minor burns to him.
ImmediateAction: Duty alerted bridge and chief engineer.Medical first aid given with cold water and burnol.  Boiler burner opened up for inspection.Burner found in good condition.Solenoid valve opened and checked.Valve seat was containing some particles and thereby not closing properly.Same cleaned thorughly and boxed back.Found satisfactory.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor burns on face
DirectCause: Boiler solenoid valve was leaking.Since there was only one solenoid valve ,the leakage lead to fuel accumulation in the burner space.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To continiously moniter the burner for any leaks due to leaking solenoid valve.
Corrective Action: Chief engineer to give training to crew regarding safety precaution to be followed during operation of boiler,Warning notice " the inspection cover /burner should not be opened  before 30 minutes after stoppage of boiler"
LessionLearnt: The solenoid valve to be inspected frequently for proper sealing condition.
ReviewComments: Defective  component,operative errors resulted  the incident.Proper care to be taken while operation of boiler ,junior engineers to be properly trained.
ClosingNote: The defective solenoid valve was replaced with spare one supplied. .spare.Training given to crew regadig operation of boiler,warning notice pasted near the boiler mentioning that burner inspection cover to be opened after proper purginfg of boiler .

IncidentDetails: Whlie picking up alminium ladder from the stowed position in ER, Ladder fell down On 4E's  head  which caused him a cut of about 1 -1.5mm deep and length  of about 6- to 8mm.
IncidentEventDetails: In the morning tool box meeting in ER, 4E was given the Bunker tank vavle gland tighness job. Hazards and PPE  Requirements of the job were discussed  in the Tool box meeting,    Inspite of being adherence to COSWP, 4E was not wearing safety helmet and went to take the ladder which was secured with one more, While he was removing one ladder near by    ladder also got stuck and came with other ladder, and fell on his head, as he is not able to manage the weight of the aluminium ladder.He didnt ask for anyones assistance    also.
ImmediateAction: 4E came rushing to  ECR with bleeding head and First aid was given immediately by ETO to stop bleeding.In the mean time Master and CO was also informed, then immediately he    was taken to Hospital and injury condition was assessed, Noticed a crack on his scalp of about 10mm Length.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A Cur of about 1 to 1.5mm deep and length of about 7-8 mm on his centre head/scalp, which causes bleeding and pain.
DirectCause: On checking cause of the accident, he was not wearing Safety helmet, Didnt ask anyone assistance as the ladder is little heavy and long to handle by one person.No properly assess the situtation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All ER personnel has to wear safety helmet and and all other safety gear in ER at all the time and for all the jobs.
ClosingNote: Top department to enforce the use of PPE strictly onboard to prevent such incident.

IncidentDetails: While unlashing the E/R valve outer Lagging Dust particles along with Lagging material inadvertently entered in the right side Eye.  It was noticed that cornea of injured eye became slightly red in colour and Pain.
IncidentEventDetails: Tme was asked to remove the E/R valve lagging around 0730hrs at sea.  Lagging was secured by wire on to the valve body,TME tried to unlash wire by applying slight force which resulted in lagging open suddenly as of which dust particles entered his eyes as he was not aware and prepared for the situation.  However same type of tasks were assigned to TME on earlier occasion and he performed well .
ImmediateAction: On-board First aid given Immediately to TME along with thorough cleaning with running Fresh water and cleaning by Eye Wash.Later Eye drops were applied on the effected eyes.After couple of hours  No improvement was noticed in his eyes , eye were reddish and TME could feel some particles moving together with slight pain. He intented to see seek shore medical advise by doctor.Immediately Master arranged him for Shore Doctor's visit.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While unlashing the E/R valve outer Lagging Dust particles along with Lagging material inadvertently entered in the right side Eye.  It was noticed that cornea of injured eye became slightly red in colour and Pain.
DirectCause: The lagging glass wool inside which was not visible had disintegrated to a powder form,on opening the lagging this glass wool got airborne with the  wind current which was blowing across the job towards the TME, thus the particles entered the eyes.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MORE BRIEFING BEFORE TASK,
ClosingNote: Proper PPE to be used including safety goggles, where there is chance of debris.

IncidentDetails: After some time from the incident, AB Abhay felt swelling on his left palm and difficulty in moving his ring finger.
IncidentEventDetails: 1000 hrs : AB Abhay Saraswat left his cabin and used staircase to go to Bridge. While going , he lost his balance and tripped on stairway. To get support, he immediately tried to push his left palm out towards stairs and full body weight came on his left palm with jerk.
ImmediateAction: Patient asked to use pain relief spray/ointment externally and given pain killers. Advice taken from Company doctor regarding medication and commenced medication as advised by him. Shore doctor visit arranged at Haldia on 18.06.2019.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling on left palm and pain in moving fingers.
DirectCause: Not following proper procedure of using stairway ladder (Crew was not holding Stairway railing on his left while climbing up).
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed about the incident and consequences and asked to follow proper procedure for using Stairway and other ladders onboard.
ClosingNote: Noted that the incident was discussed on board with all crew. The crew member is reported back to normal with his job responsibilities.

IncidentDetails: Second enineer got serious first degree burn in his right hand.
IncidentEventDetails: Please be advised to, we have carried out Aux boiler main steam stop valve leak rectification from the bonnet.1100LT  Boiler was dully depressurised to “0” bar, vent valve was open and valve bonnet has been removed and taken to workshop. Same renewed with new gasket and gland for fitting on the valve body. While fitting the valve bonnet ,the non return valve shifted causing burn injury to Second Engineer by the residual steam. 1400LT Second Eng. was wearing cotton gloves thinking there is no more steam inside , since it was depressuirsed while we removed the bonnet and also vent valve of boiler kept open all the way and NR valve kept it place to avoid any damage to seat and any dirt getting into system. Whereas the the residual heat from boiler has  formed steam.
ImmediateAction: First aid given, Hand put under tepid water.silver sulphur diazine oinment applied, Amoxicillin tablet 500mg administered. Rest of the crew explained about the hazards and  precautions to be taken in such cases before continuing the work.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First degree burn in Second engineer's right hand. Please find attached photo.
DirectCause: Direct Cause: Steam Leak and cotton gloves Indirect Cause: Residual heat in the boiler. Cold work permit evidenced that confirmation of steam presence was not done.  Root Cause:  1) RA did not identify the hazard of residual heat. 2) Hot work permit was not complied with fully. Check for non-presence of steam was not checked.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Ship staff to be briefed on procedures for permit to work system. sections for cold work permit to be discussed in detail.
Corrective Action: Risk Assessment for the job to include hazard of residual heat.
ClosingNote: Appreciate reporting and support in investigating the root cause. Noted that all required corrective actions were effectively taken.

IncidentDetails: While  picking up the stores at Stapleton anchorages, AB- Ankit Kumar injured his left hand ( Ring finger and palm ) suffering with a long deep cut and was bleeding heavily.      While picking up stores at
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel has Gantry crane aft of accomodation which was being used to lift the flat bars which were approximate 5m in length. After lifting the flat bar from the boat, it needed to be pulled inward manually to place the flat bars on board. During the process the movement of the flat bars could not be controlled and the crew member's left hand got caught between the flat bars and  starboard side accomodation bulkhead
ImmediateAction: Crew member immediately taken to the ship's  hospital and frist aid was provided to stop bleeding. Agent was notified to arrange for medical assistance. Coast Guard Medical Team boarded the vessel and took the patient. He was in steady condition with pain and shivering.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The crew member injured his left palm and ring finger with heavy bleeding
DirectCause: As the vessel has gantry crane, in order to swing the load inside, Manual pull/push is required to be applied on the load to position it. While the flat bar were lifted till the upper deck, it needed to be lifted above the railing and pulled inward so that it does not bang with the side pillar. The flat bar  load could not be controlled while pulling and it swung with momentum. Due to sudden swing the crew member could not remove his hand and it got caught between the flat bars and the side pillar
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Long length items should not be picked from aft gantry crane when it needs to be pulled and pushed . The same has to be lifted from Midship crane which is handled safely.
Corrective Action: Long length items should not be picked from aft gantry crane when it needs to be pulled and pushed . The same has to be lifted from Midship crane which is handled safely.
ClosingNote: Crew needs to evaluate the situation and take decision after a pause and not impulsively.

IncidentDetails: Deck cadet Jitendranath joined the vessel at Rotterdam on 7th Match 2019. Vessel departed Rotterdam on the 8th March 2019 and by 1830 hours on the same day, vessel was secured for sea and the cadet was sent to take rest.  He reported to bridge for navigational duties at 0400 hrs on the 9th March. He was adjusting well to the ship’s routines.  Once his bridge watches were over, he was with chief officer on deck till noon.  He was back on the bridge at 1600 hrs until 2030 hrs. He went to rest.  This was the routine he was assigned.  On 10th March 2019, he reported to be seasick. He vomited due to the rolling and pitching. He was asked to take rest. He reported fine next day on the 11th march at 0800 hrs.  At around 1130 hrs on the same day, duty officer on the bridge got a call from a seaman that he found cadet lying in his room gasping.  Initial first aid was administered and at times there were signs of improvement. However, as cadet went back to semi unconsciousness frequently, it was decided to evacuate him by helicopter. Jitendranath was evacuated by the SAR aircraft from the Finistere MRCC at 1937 Hrs. He was given initial medication followed by detailed checkup and medication in India. He has recovered well and is fit.  There was no commercial delay due to this incident.
IncidentEventDetails: Deck cadet Jitendranath joined the vessel at Rotterdam on 7th March 2019. He joined the vessel after the mandatory medical tests. He was fit for sea service.  Vessel departed Rotterdam on the 8th March 2019 and by 1830 hours on the same day, vessel was secured for sea and the cadet was sent to take rest.    Next day on the 9th March 2019, he reported to bridge for navigational duties at 0400 hrs.   He was adjusting well to the ship’s routines. The assigned duties for the cadet was 0400 Hrs to 0800 and 1600 hrs to 2000 Hrs on the bridge for navigational watch keeping with chief officer. 0830 hrs to 1200 hrs on deck for maintenance jobs.    On 10th March 2019, the vessel was at Bay of Biscay and the weather was moderate with WNW winds of force 3 and NW x 1 m swell. Cadet reported sea sickness to chief officer. He reported to have vomited due to the rolling and pitching. Chief officer asked Jitendranath to take rest. Later, on 11th March 2019, 0800 hrs, he reported to be fine.  However, duty officer asked him to be at the bridge itself and not to proceed for any work for some more time.  By about 1100 Hours, Jitendranath was sent to his cabin to change and proceed to work.   At around 1130 hrs, duty officer on the bridge got a call from one crew member that he found cadet lying in his room gasping. Immediately chief officer attended to him and found he was almost unconscious.   Master called the DPA and marine superintendent and informed about the scenario onboard.  CIRM was contacted too. Jitendranath frequently gained and lost consciousness.  Meantime company established communication with company doctor appraising him of the situation and agents at Port Vigo for assistance if required.    At about 1650 hrs, when cadet was in a state to talk, Jitendranath said that his head had hit the sofa. On this information, vessel after consulting with company decided to evacuate Jitendranath soonest for medical care.  By 1755 hrs, Finisterre VTS and MRCC were contacted and informed about the situation onboard.    At 1822 hrs, MRCC informed the vessel that SAR aircraft will be arriving shortly to pick up the casualty. By 1930 hrs, SAR aircraft arrived vessels position and med-evac personnel were onboard. Jitendranath was picked by 1937 hrs and aircraft was away from the vessel.
ImmediateAction: Jitendranath was taken to hospital on arrival at the jetty.  He reached hospital at 2035 hrs. On the initial examination, he was found normal with no complications due to the head contact with the sofa.  The doctor’s impression suggested that the dizziness was caused due to the sea sickness.  On completion of the diagnosis and basic treatment, he was flown to India to visit the company doctor at Chennai.  His first visit to the company doctor was on 14th March 2019. The head injury was thoroughly examined, and he was sent for expert opinion. The X-ray, CT reports suggested that there is no sign of craniocerebral injury.  He was asked by the doctor for a follow up on 3rd May 2019.  Jitendranath visited the company doctor and the specialists on 3rd May 2019. He was declared fit for sea service.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
ClosingNote: New crew joining sea to be monitored

IncidentDetails: While renewing the chipping gun needles, concerned crew was trying to remove the broken needle in the chipping gun, as he was giving effort to push out the needle, the broken needle in the chipping gun poked into his left arm, right side of the lower palm.
IncidentEventDetails: Concerned crew was assigned to prepare the chipping gun for de-rusting.  While trrying to remove the broken needle the needle poked into his left arm palm.
ImmediateAction: The concerned crew removed the poked needle and reported to HoD, First Aid given, office informed and shore medical assistance taken.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The concerned crew's injury has been dressed up onboard and first aid give, since the extent of injury inside the palm is unknown, require doctor's visit at next call.
DirectCause: Improper handling of the chipping tool, less experience and unaware of the consequence.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed on the injury incident and advised to inform the HoD if any maintenance to be done on the faulty equipments. Responsible and experienced person will be assigned for repair work of the faulty equipments.
ClosingNote: Concur with the CA. Master informed that the  crew member  resumed normal duties in two days time.

IncidentDetails: While hydroblasting stbd side gangway area on main deck, A/B Gurpreet suffered hand injury due to the rupture of hydroblasting hose.
IncidentEventDetails: 0830: Hydroblasting commenced. prior hydroblasting tool box meeting was carried out and all safety checks done. all found satisfactory.  0900: A/B Gurpreet was adjusting the hose for reaching the designated area for hydroblasting.suddenly the spot where he was holding the hose ruptured leading to high pressure water hitting his right palm area.A/B Gurpreet was donning proper PPE and hence no major injury took place.
ImmediateAction: stopped the hydroblasting pump immediately. The A/B was called to hospital and palm examined. Swelling and slight redness of the palm area.Applied cold patch to the affected area to ease the pain as well as swelling.Given 6 Hrs rest.Re examined,no redness slight pain.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor injury to left palm resulting in slight swelling and pain . No suferficial cut/wound. Only first aid and rest required.
DirectCause: Material failure of the supplied Hose with the hydroblaster
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thororugh inspection of the hose & associated accessories prior use every time.Routine detailed inspection and maintenance to be done .
ClosingNote: New Hoses ordered. Maker guidance received for the care and maintenance of hose. Crew shall be trained on the carae and maintenance of hose and discard criteria.

IncidentDetails: middle finger was injured and bleed with lacerations.
IncidentEventDetails: In the night of incident engineers are doing some emergency maintenance work, they need to fix/repair boiler, when 2/Engr. entered the boiler room from A-Deck, he unconciously held the door frame while the door is closing, upon closing, the door hit his left middle finger.
ImmediateAction: Upon hearing and reporting the incident, immediately give first aid & medications.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Left hands center finger wound
DirectCause: While closed A deck door of boiler side then very strong pressure suddenly closed and push to his finger.

ReviewComments: Training carried out by ship staff
ClosingNote: Campaign circulated

IncidentDetails: Due to uncontrolled slewing of the davit, bosun's chest was hit by the suez light which the crew was trying to secure in the Forecastle store.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) The suez light was secured on forecastle for Canal Transit.  2) After the transit Bosun and the crew was told to secure it back in the store witht the help of davit.  3) The suez light was secured on davit.  4) One of the crew had to slew the davit in order to bring it to required position for lowering it down.  5) The crew couldnt control the slew ,and the Suez light hit bosun chest.
ImmediateAction: The incident was not reported as Bosun didnt feel any pain or swelling but after 2-3 days he noticed some pain and slight swelling on his chest. Then he reported to medical officer who provided him the first aid
DirectCause: The root cause of injury was that despite enough man power, the bosun alone tried to slew the davit where as other members were busy opening the hatch. If One crew would have done the slewing and other 2 crew on deck controlling the Suez light, the injury wouldn’t have happened as the speed of slewing would have been controlled.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: One crew should have done the slewing and other 2 crew on deck controlling the Suez light, the injury wouldn’t have happened as the speed of slewing would have been controlled.
Corrective Action: TO Brief all Ship Staff regarding this incident with reference to HSM and COSWP. Adequate personnel should be available for safe operation of Vessel.
LessionLearnt: Sufficient crew member should be available, based on task
ReviewComments: LWC Case, as Bosun was discharge from vessel for Medical treatment. However after Sign off doctors diagonised as muscle or soft tissue trauma, which does not require medical repatriation.
ClosingNote: Medical reports attached for reference.

IncidentDetails: One of our crew member from engine side while cleaning oil filter with air,he got hitten by sharp object in his left palm  same time air went inside to his left  palm through the injured area and his palm was swelling and mild paining.
ImmediateAction: Asked crew member stop the work and given pain relief spray and cold compression and asked shore medical advise.
DirectCause: Negligence of Personnel safety.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Use proper PPE for all kind of job. Gloves, Googles and Personnel protective Equipments to avoid this injury.

IncidentDetails: Electical officer had deep cut in right hand middle finger
IncidentEventDetails: AA) At 01:50hrs on 01-07-19, Chief Engineer informed Electrical officer that Air Condition fan has stopped. Electrical Officer went to the A/C room and observed that A/C fan is tripping due to overload.    BB) At 02:00hrs on 01-07-19, Electrical officer came to ECR and informed Chief Engineer the same. Further to this Chief Engineer instructed him to call fitter and open A/C fan motor guard cover. Also C.Eng. informed Electrical officer that 3 Eng. will come for further investigation after unberthing of the vessel.    Electrical officer again went to A/C room and isolated the Air Condition and its fan power. After isolating the power supply E/O opened the motor guard cover.    Now, for further investigation he tries to rotate the motor belt by bare finger for checking freeness of motor and fan. As he rotated the belt by his right hand finger, it got caught in between pulley and the belt due to rotation of belt. Once the finger came in between the pulley and belt, he again rotated the pulley in opposite direction to clear the finger. After the finger got clear from the pulley, he observed a very deep cut on the finger.    CC) 02:20hrs on 01-07-19, Electrical Officer came to ECR and reported deep cut in his finger.    Chief Engineer immediately informed master on bridge regarding the incident. As vessel just departed berth and was under pilotage, master informed chief officer to proceed to administer first aid.    Following below First Aid Provided:  Washed and cleaned the wounded area with cetrimide aqueous solution 1% and dressing of the injured finger carried out. Ibuprofen tabs 400mg 2nos given to relief pain.    After disembarking pilot, Master immidiately inofrmed company regarding the incident. Also local agent was informed and requested for Doctor visit for the Electrical officer on arrival.    After few hours vessel arrived at next port and Electrical officer was sent to visit shore doctor.    On shore he was diagnosed with fracture in middle finger bone. Medical treatment including plaster carried out as per attached report and Electrical officer sailed with vessel on departure.
ImmediateAction: First aid given as Sterile cleaning, pain releiver Ibuprufen, Wrapped with sterile bandage. Later send to the hospital on same day.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut in right hand middle finger.Severe pain due to cut and bleeding from wound.Later taken to hospital for further treatment on same day. Finger cut stitched by doctor and unfit for duty.
DirectCause: Failure to follow the procedure, Lack of experience, Senior staff instriction not followed, Unaware of his own action consequences, Inadequate warning poster.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Warning notice posted, Special training session on "Procedure to follow" carried out by Chief Engr, "Importance of senior staff instruction" briefed to all crew.
ClosingNote: Crew trained to safely handle moving parts in the machinery.

IncidentDetails: One of the crew slept and fell down while coming out of bathroom due to heavy rolling.
IncidentEventDetails: After shower while coming out of bathroom as soon as crew stepped out of bathroom vessel rolled on same side caused imbalance. Due to imbalance crew fell down on cabin floor.
ImmediateAction: Crew got up on his own, Reported to medical officer and pain relief spray applied.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Muscle pain for few hours.
DirectCause: Imbalance due to rolling

ClosingNote: Crew to be cautious inside accomodation during rough weather.

IncidentDetails: WHILE SHIFTING SAFETY STORES  FIRE STATION B - RIGHT FOOT TWISTED AT ANKLE WITH A SUDDEN JERK ON KNEE WHILE STEPING ON THE LADDER STEP.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. SHIFTING OF STORE ITEM   2. CLIMBING LADDER STEP  3. SLIPPED ON STEP  4. TWISTED RIGHT FOOT / ANKLE & SUDDEN JERK ON KNEE
ImmediateAction: SEEKED MEDICAL ADVICE AND TREATMENT ADMINISTERED AS ADVISED BY  DOCTOR
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: SWELLING AND PAIN . DIFFICULTY IN WALKING .
DirectCause: MISSED OUT THE 3 POINT CONTACT WHILE CLIMBING LADDER . AS 1 HAND WAS OCCUPIED CARRYING SOME STORE ITEM.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ADDITIONAL WORKFORCE REQUIRED WHILE SHIFTING ITEMS WHEN REQUIRED TO PASS THROUGH LADDERS / STEPS .   RE-INTERATE THE PRINCIPLE OF 3 POINT CONTACT. BRIEFING AND TRAINING CARRIED OUT
Corrective Action: To Brief all Ship Staff with reference to COSWP and HSM
LessionLearnt: On Verification with Vessel, noted that the Step had sufficient antiskid properties, and the crew members shoes, were in good condition. Heel of the crew member rested at the end of the step, which caused the foot to slip, causing a Sprain on the ankle.
ReviewComments: Repatriated on Medical Grounds from Tanjung Pelepas
ClosingNote: After Sign off from Vessel, Candidate did not respond to telephone calls, and did not share Medical certificate. Suitable comments included in his Profile for de-briefing prior considering for any assignment in future(extract attached for reference)

IncidentDetails: During greasing of the fire wire, while the winch was being operated, the Bosun's right hand got caught between the operating lever and the winch drum.
IncidentEventDetails: 1320: While Picking up the fire wire  on the drum after greasing bosun's right hand got caught between the operating lever and the winch.  1330: Crew member was examined, found swelling and slight bruise on right hand. Crew member complaining of pain.   1340: Crew member administred cold compressions, pain killer and taken off the work.
ImmediateAction: First aid administered
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Bosun's right hand suffered contusion and minor bruises, with evident swelling and loss of strength in the right hand.
DirectCause: Due to  improper positioning of the winch operating lever.while being operated the handle faced towards the drum side ,instead of being clear . Failure to identify this job hazard by senior deck officer.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All winches checked and the lever repositioned on the outboard side of the drum thereby during operation it remains clear of the winch drum. All crew to be trained on a regular intervel on safe operation & maintenance of deck machinery & equipments. Safety officers monthly inspections to thouroughly cover safe work practices & any unsafe job situations or conditions onboard.
Corrective Action: Ship staff to be trained on the proper way of rigging the winch motor
ClosingNote: Ship staff trained on the correct way of rigging the winch motor. Safety poster regarding the same displayed at prominent locations

IncidentDetails: ship fitter's  left hand- Index finger, middle finger, small  finger tips including nails parted and ring finger tip crushed,
IncidentEventDetails: Noticed abnormal noice in the accomodation air con blower and decided to carry out accomodation air con blower inspection.after taking all permits and precautions, decided to inspect.  upon inspecting the suction side bearing by 2E and electrician. The fitter was outside checking the belt tensions and he rotated the belt by hand for checking the free movement.within a second accidentally his left hand stuck in between the pully and the belt causing the injury.
ImmediateAction: fitter shouted and electrician came out from the suction duct, wrapped his hand with a cotton rag and removed him outside the air con room.electrician verbaly raised alarm to others and shifted him to hospital.Given pain killer,Immidiatly informed office and agent to arrange for evacuation to shore hospital.1430 lt Fitter was picked up be tug to shore hospital.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Ship fitter's left hand injured- ponter finger, middle finger, tiny finger tips including the nails parted from the fingers. and ring finger crushed.
DirectCause: human error - lack of knowledge about the gravity of the incident, over confidence.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: take more cautionary measures and additional supervision by a responsible person.
Corrective Action: Never to loose situational awareness and carry-out a dynamic RA for all non-routine tasks
LessionLearnt: Never to loose situational awareness and carry-out a dynamic RA for all non-routine tasks
ClosingNote: Never to loose situational awareness and carry-out a dynamic RA for all non-routine tasks

IncidentDetails: During routine overhauling of the main engine spare exhaust valve after risk assessment and the tool box meeting, the crew got injured while trying to open the valve drive bolts.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Risk assesmnet was carried out and reviewed on 28/june/2019.  2.Tool box meeting carried out in the ECR and as well as the work site.  3.Required tools for overhauling were arranged.  4.The job included 2/E, motorman Sadish and fitter.  5.Cardboard and brown kraft paper were laid down to avoid slippery floor.  6. While trying to loosen the hexaganal bolt, the whole body weight was acting on the right leg, when the bolt loosened, motorman lost his balance and sudden jerk on the knee caused sprain on the right leg.  7.Weather observed calm and smooth sea.  8.MM Sadish Rest hours are well within the limit and no violation noticed.
ImmediateAction: The injured person moved to the ECR, cold compression was given for the injured area. Master was informed and requested for the medical advise. He was given bed rest and relieved from all his duties.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While trying to loosen the hexaganal bolt on the spare exhaust valve during overhauling , Motor man Sadish lost his balance and sudden jerk came on the knee caused sprain on the right leg due to  the whole body weight  was acting on the right leg, when the bolt got slackened instantly.
DirectCause: Improper positioning while working.Work area envorinment not considered.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Work area safety to be considered before doing the job.Training conducted for all crew regarding injury and working position,Lifiting objects,working near hot lines, pressure lines.
Corrective Action: All Ship Staff to be briefed regarding this incident, and to ensure safe working environment at all times. Suitability of Tools and Location to be analysed before carrying out the Job
ReviewComments: First Aid Case. As per comment from Vessel, he has completely recovered after First Aid.and verification visit to Doctor.
ClosingNote: Attached details as required

IncidentDetails: While Shifting tank cleaning hoses for securing pumpman slipped and fell on main deck. Reported pain in getting up and bending down.
IncidentEventDetails: Chief officer asked Pumpman to secure tank cleaning hoses on deck. Deck was wet due to sea spray and rough weather conditions experienced on preceding day. Pumpman slipped and fell while carrying out the work.
ImmediateAction: First aid administered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Injury, No superficial cuts/wound/swelling. Pain reported when bending down and getting up. Company doctor referred for medication/treatment.
DirectCause: Direct Cause:  1. Pumpman slipped on deck due to slippery surface.  Indirect Cause: 1. Inadequate hazard identification: C/O and Pumpman failed to assess hazard of slippery deck owing to rough weather experienced by the vessel previous day.  Root cause: 1. Lack of compliance: Company and industry best practices were not adhered to.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident to be discussed with all crew during month end safety meeting to prevent reoccurance. Crew to be advised to exercise caution when walking on wet/slippery decks
Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed with all crew during month end safety meeting to prevent reoccurance. Crew to be advised to exercise caution when walking on wet/slippery decks
LessionLearnt: One should be careful when planning shipboard activities especially when the deck is slippery post heavy weather. In such cases adequate hazard identification is one of the basic requirement. Job can be safely done only and only if proper hazard identification and risk assessment has been prepared and adhered to.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Shared in the monthly reflection Magazine.

Corrective Action: KEEP WORK AS PER COMPANY'S STANDARD

Corrective Action: Master to carry out safety meeting with all crew and discuss COSWP ch Safe Movement onboard Ship

IncidentDetails: While vessel was performing a lifeboat lowering and Maneuvering drill in the water at SW pass Anchorage, USA, one of the links to the hooks of the davit parted during the recovery of the boat resulting in a imbalance to the boat with sudden jerk that caused 2 Nos of crew member to fall overboard who were trying to board the ladder and bruises to one crew member who fell inside the boat. The crew members managed to get onboard the boat on their own. There were no major injuries to the crew or the lifeboat. Lifeboat was then with the help of shackles, safely restored in the stowage position. The damaged Link of the hook assembly was lost in the water.
IncidentEventDetails: 0945 LT : Commence lowering Freefall Lifeboat using Davit. Two heaving lines connected on the aft stern P/S for controlling the boat swing.    1005 LT : Lifeboat waterborne. Davit tried out for hoisting.    1010 LT : Ladder Rigged on the stern to get in the boat.    1015 LT : Chief Officer & 4/E went down on the boat using the ladder.    1020 LT : Engines tried out ahead/astern    1023 LT : AB Brain & Deck Cadet went down on the boat using the ladder.    1026 LT : Boat unhooked to maneauver in the water.    1040 LT : Boat maneuvered in the water safely and brought back to position for recovery.    1048 LT : Boat hooks put in position for recovery.    1050 LT : Both heaving lines on aft P/S side of the boat controlled by the crew on deck to keep the vessel in position.    1052 LT : Boat unable to come closer to the stern as required for safe boarding on the ladder, as the lead to make the ropes tight is insufficient.    1055 LT : Boat Slightly lifted up from the stern using davit with 50% of the forward part of the boat waterborne taking weight on the davit. Using the davit, the boat was closer to the stern thereby making it easier for the crew to board the vessel using the ladder.    1056 LT : Both heaving lines tight.    1056 LT : Chief Officer and Deck cadet trying to board the vessel using ladder.    1057 LT : Stbd side aft link of the boat hook brakes and the boat is immbalanced with a jerk, just holding on to one hook on the Port side.    1057 LT : Chief Officer and Deck cadet fall overboard due to the sudden jerk.    1057 LT : AB brian inside the boat awaiting his turn to board, falls on his knees and gets scratched on his left leg.    1100 LT : Chief officer and Deck cadet get onboard the boat on their own. 4/E inside the boat is unharmed.    1120 LT : Situation under control and means to recover the boat are initiated.    1125 LT : 2 Nos of same size shackle are placed along with additional bigger shackle for back up to recover the boat.    1130 LT : All crew members using the ladder board the vessel safely.    1145 LT : Life boat brought back in position and secured.    1145 LT : No major injuries to the crew. No damages to the lifeboat.    1150 LT : AB Brain brusies on the leg treated. (attached please find the photo)    1200 LT : Deck cadet reported slight pain on the left palm and last 2 fingers. First aid initiated. (attached please find the photo)
ImmediateAction: Persons falling overboard were recovered and safely returned onboard. Situation brought under control. Secondary means using shackles used to recover the boat and hoist using the davit to stow in position safely. Crew safely onboard using the ladder rigged on the stern of the vessel. First aid initiated to the Cadet and the AB Bryan minor injuries.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1. The link to the hook assembly parted and lost in water.   2. Deck Cadet having slight pain in his left hand.  3. AB brayan having briuse on his left leg.
DirectCause: Breaking of the aging suspension chain link connecting to the hook.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Repalcement of the suspension hooks from makers.
Corrective Action: Repalcement of the suspension hooks from makers.
LessionLearnt: To check every part of the lifting equipment thoroughly prior use.
ReviewComments: Training carried out and replacemrnt of links and hook done.
ClosingNote: training and replacement carried out

IncidentDetails: Thumb Injury to Fourth Engineer, causing a deep cut and possible detachment of thumb bone near the finger nail.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was carrying out the overhauling of No.2 Fire & G.S pump and the Shaft along with the impeller was taken to E.R Workshop for dismantling. One impeller was easily removed.  The second impeller was found tight with the shaft and it was decided to give impact to the impeller by hitting it with a wooden piece and  hammer. While striking the hammer head got loose & slipped from the wooden handle and fell/hit on the left thumb of the fourth engineer who was holding the impeller. As a result he received a deep cut injury on the middle portion of the left thumb near the finger nail and possible fracture and detachment of bone at joint, although he was wearing a cotton hand gloves.
ImmediateAction: Fourth Engineer was taken to the ship's hospital and first aid given. Treatment given to him as per company's doctor advice  and his wound was administered with support of a splint on the underside of the thumb along with bandage.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Thumb injury to Fourth Engineer, possible fracture and dislocation to thumb Bone at joint.
DirectCause: Incorrectly secured Hammer used, which got detached from the handle and fell on his thumb whereby causing a deep cut injury to the thumb.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Hammers on board the vessel to be checked, whether they are properly secured to the attached handle.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Please refer to attached Investigation report

IncidentDetails: OS Rakesh tripped and fell on main deck, his foot got stuck in the deck girders causing him to lose his balance. He complained of pain on the right side of his chest area.
IncidentEventDetails: The crew member was securing tools after completion of work, while walking in the bay his leg got stuck in the longitudinal girder causing him to lose his balance and fall atop a girder. He complained of pain on the right side of his chest.

ClosingNote: While walking/working on deck which has  girders, crew to be situationally aware of the surroundings and the restrictions posed by the deck girders
Other Part: Mild pain in Right side of right Chest

IncidentDetails: while securing  stbd side gangway during departure  at Port of Ningbo,  AB- Mr.Pramod  fell into water in the gap between the wharf and ship side while jumping from wharf to bottom platform of gangway. He was rescued immediately and fortunately no severe injury other than minor scratch on lower limb .
IncidentEventDetails: 0530Hrs LT: Pilot on board for departure Ningbo  0642 Hrs LT: Cargo operations completed & all stevedores disembarked. vessel prepared for departure.  0643 Hrs LT: Crew  commenced removing Gangway Net  and all were attired with  life vest.  0652 Hrs LT: Gangway net  picked up onboard & gangway clear of jetty and hung between the wharf & shipside.  0653 Hrs LT: O.S. Nithin stepped onto the gangway lower platform from the jetty.  0654 Hrs LT: A.B. Pramod when trying to step on to the gangway from jetty, missed his step and was about to fall in to the water. However, A.B. Pramod held tightly onto the rope which was secured to the gangway frame,which prevented him from falling in water and enabled him to hang vertically while holding the rope. At the same time O.S. Nithin, who was standing on the lower platform of the gangway, caught hold of his hand. Immediately A.B. Mehakdeep Singh with life vest & fall arrestor secured to him also went to the spot to support A.B. Pramod by holding his another hand. Immediately a life buoy was thrown in to the water as a precautionary measure. Pilot was notified immediately and he communicated same to the terminal.  0655 Hrs LT: A.B. Pramod, since he was already wearing his life vest and known to be a swimmer, advised Nithin and Mehakdeep Singh to let go his hands, so that he can catch hold of the life  buoy & easily swim to the fender.  0656 Hrs LT:  Nithin & Mehakdeep Singh let go A.B. Pramod’ s hands, who at the same time caught hold of life Buoy and swam towards the fender and safely clinged on to fender securing chain. Another life buoy was also thrown to him by the shore personnel.  0658 Hrs LT: Crew went to the bottom platform of Gangway for assisting.  0700 Hrs Lt: Gangway was lowered to water level & A.B. Pramod swam towards the gangway & climbed on the gangway bottom platform & boarded the vessel safely.  0703 Hrs LT: Crew  accompanied A.B. Pramod to the ship’s office and confirmed with him that his physical and mental condition is satisfactory. He was advised to take rest. A.B. Pramod went to his cabin to freshen up & then went to rest after breakfast.  0724 Hrs LT: Vessel cast off and sailed to Yangshan.   1150 Hrs LT: A.B. Pramod voluntarily reported to duty on bridge. After re-confirming his satisfactory mental & physical condition, allowed him to resume look out duty.   Safety meeting was conducted to discuss the incident and finding solution to avoid recurrence and enhance the  safety in gangway operation.
ImmediateAction: He was rescued immediately and first aid administed for  minor scratch on lower limb .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor scratches on lower limb .
DirectCause: Excessive Gap between gangway and wharf due to fender size
Corrective Action: To collect data from Fleet Vessels for analysing Accomodation Ladder use in various ports

IncidentDetails: Oiler Sathanantham Vignesh got his Finger jammed between bridge entry fire door and its frame while entering the bridge .
IncidentEventDetails: While entering the bridge for Flag Hoisting ceremony on bridge wing  oiler Sathanantham Vignesh got his Finger jammed  between bridge entry fire door and its frame
ImmediateAction: Given ice fermentation and later on given Ibuprofen 400mg 4 times a day. Alcohol test carried out - AD-16 attached .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Index Finger tip got Jammed  between bridge entry fire door and its frame     Severe pain at the tip of the  index finger followed by Blackening/blood clotting  of the nail area and  inflammation /swelling of the index  finger tip
DirectCause: Indirect cause : Differential pressure between bridge and "H" deck accomodation door which causes the bridge entry "H" deck fire door to close rapidly . Direct Cause :  Improper handling of the fire  door.     Root cause :  Emphasis not laid to COSWP . Lack of knowledge and training with reagrds to opening /closing of the fire doors .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Adjustment made to the fire  door closer for proper and easy closing without any gap . Crew trained on the correct procedure of handling of fire doors . Bridge wing sliding doors kept shut to avoid differential pressure .
Corrective Action: The speed setting for closing of fire door to be checked/adjusted.
ClosingNote: COSWP to be used for training crew to handle fire doors.

IncidentDetails: While carrying out chipping on deck, some foreign particles entered the left eye of AB
IncidentEventDetails: While carrying out some chipping works on deck, some dust or foreign particles enetered his left eye. The subject was wearing a proper PPE at that time. Immediately, he washed his eyes, and found it is normal. But later next day morning around 0500Lt, he reported to duty officer that having irritation and discomfort in his left eye. His left eye was found watery, slighly swollen and in red colour. It was suspected that minor dust particles which was in eyes were not removed properly when the actual incident took place.
ImmediateAction: Left eye was washed with Eye wash solution and administered Ciprofloxacin eye drops. Advised to take proper Bed rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While carrying out some chipping works on deck, some dust or foreign particles enetered his left eye. The subject was wearing a proper PPE at that time. Immediately, he washed his eyes, and found it is normal. But later next day morning around 0500Lt, he reported to duty officer that having irritation and discomfort in his left eye. His left eye was found watery, slighly swollen and in red colour. It was suspected that minor dust particles which was in eyes were not removed properly when the actual incident took place.

Corrective Action: To ensure that the correct type of PPE is used. A training needs to be provided in this matter and attach the training record as evidence.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily and circulated to all vessels.

IncidentDetails: OS while carrying out the regular routine greasing of mooring winch had his right hand index finger stuck between the winch handle and the gear resulting in deep cut and bleeding on his fingers.
IncidentEventDetails: On 19 Aug 2019/1540 LT when vessel at Brazos-Santiago anchorage, Brownsville while carrying out the routine monthly greasing of forward mooring winches, the onboard OS Mr. Jyotin Bisht suffered injury to his right hand index fingers when they got stuck in the winch gears.  The greasing of the equipment was being carried out by OS Jyotin, AB Sandeep and OS Iqbal.   AB Sandeep was operating the winch lever and OS Jyotin was removing the old grease from the winch gears while OS Iqbal was assisting in refilling the grease and also in cleaning the old grease. The initial removal of grease was being done with a long handle brush but when the OS Jyotin noticed the old grease was not being effectively removed using the brush, he decided to use a piece of rag wound to a small handled screw driver. He imagined the same would help in achieving better efficiency in removing the waste grease. While carrying out the job the piece of cloth wound to the screw driver got stuck on the winch gear and in turn caused the screw driver to slip which lead to OS fingers being pushed into the winch gear which was turning at that moment.  Although the other OS who was assisting immediately notified the AB operating the lever and operation of winch was stopped but the fingers got trapped inside the gears which cut through the skin and flesh on his right index fingers causing severe bleeding.  Master was immediately notified by 2nd Officer who was inside the hospital when OS Jyotin was taken to the hospital. Master also came down to the hospital and found Chief Officer was also present in the hospital assisting 2nd Off in providing first aid.  OS Jyotin on being brought to the hospital, the wounded section was cleaned with antiseptic lotion and antiseptic ointment applied. The injured section was covered with bandage and pain killer tablets; antibiotic tablets were provided.
ImmediateAction: OS was immidiately brought to ship hospital and first was given. The wound section was cleaned with antiseptic lotion and antiseptic cream applied.Pain killer tablets, antibiotic tablets were administered.  He was sent to the Doctor when vessel berthed on 21 Aug 2019.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 19 Aug 2019/1540 LT when vessel at Brazos-Santiago anchorage, Brownsville while carrying out the routine monthly greasing of forward mooring winches, the onboard OS Mr. Jyotin Bisht suffered injury to his right hand index fingers when they got stuck in the winch gears.  The greasing of the equipment was being carried out by OS Jyotin, AB Sandeep and OS Iqbal.   AB Sandeep was operating the winch lever and OS Jyotin was removing the old grease from the winch gears while OS Iqbal was assisting in refilling the grease and also in cleaning the old grease. The initial removal of grease was being done with a long handle brush but when the OS Jyotin noticed the old grease was not being effectively removed using the brush, he decided to use a piece of rag wound to a small handled screw driver. He imagined the same would help in achieving better efficiency in removing the waste grease. While carrying out the job the piece of cloth wound to the screw driver got stuck on the winch gear and in turn caused the screw driver to slip which lead to OS fingers being pushed into the winch gear which was turning at that moment.  Although the other OS who was assisting immediately notified the AB operating the lever and operation of winch was stopped but the fingers got trapped inside the gears which cut through the skin and flesh on his right index fingers causing severe bleeding.  Master was immediately notified by 2nd Officer who was inside the hospital when OS Jyotin was taken to the hospital. Master also came down to the hospital and found Chief Officer was also present in the hospital assisting 2nd Off in providing first aid.  OS Jyotin on being brought to the hospital, the wounded section was cleaned with antiseptic lotion and antiseptic ointment applied. The injured section was covered with bandage and pain killer tablets; antibiotic tablets were provided.
DirectCause: 1. Failure to follow the procedure. 2. Deviation from the safe Practices 3. Lack of Supervision 4. Lack of awareness about Risk involved. 5.Winch operator had no direct sight of the personnel greasing/degreasing the winch.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety meeting carried out with all crew members. The risk and danger involved while using mooring winch explained.Senior officers to supervise the operation.Proper procedure for the greasing of winch explained.Crew urged not to take shortcuts or compromise on safety.
Corrective Action: Safety meeting carried out with all crew members. The risk and danger involved while using mooring winch explained.Senior officers to supervise the operation.Proper procedure for the greasing of winch explained.Crew urged not to take shortcuts or compromise on safety.
LessionLearnt: Knowledge and skill to be assessed before delegating job to crew. Situational awareness is very important to know the hazards present in the work equipment/ place. All hazards to be properly assessed and last minute risk assessment is to be carried to avoid any injury/incident.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: Circulated in the fleet for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: At 1030LT, ETO and Ch/Engr decided to change rubber joint mats with new certified insulation mats  in all electrical panel box area. so, ETO & J/E went to spare parts room to take out insulation roll mat. ETO decided to shift it from store to open area inorder to roll out and cut it by pieces. while shifting, It is slipped out of J/E hands and the whole weight came on ETO. He felt a sudden Jerk in his HIP & SPINE area. Then, they shifted the mat to open area and sat down to start cutting the mat. After 5 minutes, ETO start felling pain in his back and escorted to cabin for bed rest.
IncidentEventDetails: 1030- ETO and Ch/Engr decided to change rubber joint mats with new certified insulation mats  in all electrical panel box area  1040-ETO & J/E went to spare parts room to take out insulation roll mat  1045-ETO decided to shift it from store to open area inorder to roll out and cut it by pieces  1050-While shifting, It was slipped out of J/E hands, the whole weight came on ETO.  1050- He felt a Jerk in his HIP & SPINE area.   1055- Then, they shifted the mat open area and sat down to start cutting the mat  1100- ETO start feeling pain and was escorted to his cabin for bed rest.
