IncidentDetails: During mooring station OS suffered laceration on his face and abrasions on neck.
IncidentEventDetails: During arrival station Alexandria,OS was making fast inward headlines, while screwing the brakes he noticed the heaving line was rushing at high speed towards sea,he immediately tried to catch the heaving line, the last end of heaving was secured in storage plastic basket, mean while full length of heaving passed and  the basket where heaving line was secured was pulled at high speed which flew on air and hit OS,who suffered injury on his face.
ImmediateAction: OS was taken to vessel hospital,first aid given,dressing done on his wounds. Shore medical assistance arranged through crew agent at port Alexandria.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Suffered deep laceration on his right chin just below the eyeand lips,slight cut on right eyebrow and abrasion on neck and shoulder.
DirectCause: Heaving line was pulled by some external force, the basket in which line was secured flew due to the pull and hit OS who was trying to catch the heaving line.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1-Heaving line should be attended at all times.  2-Pre Mooring operations  meeting should be effectively carried out.Good practices for approaching a running line to be explained specially for least experienced  crew member/Os/cadets.  3-Safe mooring practice should be followed at all times.Procedures as per company's mooring system management plan  part-e safety of personnel and human factor to be effectively followed.  4-Crew should be briefed about hazard at mooring station,crew should be briefed not to catch hold any running ropes.
ClosingNote: COSWP to comply for all mooring operations. The heaving lines to be made fast to a strong point.  Alert sent to all vessels in the fleet for info sharing. Training to be carried out for all crew for handling the fast-moving mooring/heaving lines.

IncidentDetails: While   bending a metal plate, fitter got struck on his forehead with the plate, leading to 2 cm cut on his forehead
IncidentEventDetails: 1100 HRS : Onboard Fitter Mr Richard was given the job to fabricate Save all trays for Sounding Pipes in Engine room Bottom platform  For the purpose he had to bend edges of 2mm thick metal plate, he inserted and secured the edge (about 2cmof the plate) on to bench-vice  The plate was being heated up and fitter was physically (using hands) to bend the plate into desired shape.  The plate was not prperly secured into the vice as a result it jumped out of the vice and the edge of the plate hit fitter, causing a 2 cm(in length) cut on his forehead.    Fitter was wearing safety Goggles, ear muffs and cotton hand gloves and forehead wrapped with cloth to soak sweat. He was working alone in the workshop at the time of incident  Bleeding started, so the fitter immeditely approached Egine control room for assisatnce, where by he was shifted to Ships hospital and  First Aid was immediately given to fitter in consultaion with company’s doctor.
ImmediateAction: First Aid was immediately given to fitter in consultaion with company’s doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: A 2 cm(in length) cut on forehead.
DirectCause: INSUFFCIENT PPE USE AND IMPROPER TECHNIQUE OF WORKING
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety training to be carried out, safety video to be shown  and COSWP to be followed
Corrective Action: Safety training to be carried out, safety video to be shown  and COSWP to be followed
LessionLearnt: Appropriate PPE to be used
ReviewComments: Discussion and training carried out
ClosingNote: Helmet is to be worn and appropriate PPE to be used.

IncidentDetails: ETO was carrying out Air condition unit maintenance job.  While opening acess door to outside he used his shoulder for support, and while applying force to  lift the handle he sprained his shoulder. Later from the evening onwards experienced pain at around the  shoulder area while moving his right arm. The pain occured every time the arm was moved.
ImmediateAction: Medication given from morning - Paracetamol 2/day, Ibuprofen-400mg   3/day, Deep Heat Rub Methly Salicylate-Ointment.  Requested medical advise from company Doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Initial Encounter of Sprain of right acromioclavicular joint causing pain. Limited movement of shoulder muscle
DirectCause: The ETO tried to open the access door to Air Con room using pipe as extension to dog leg handle. The force exerted by pipe with shoulder as support caused stress on his shoulder initiating sprain on the muscles.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: When carrying out such jobs, proper procedures to be adopted as per COSWP. Additional assistance to sought with proper planning.
ClosingNote: INJURY ALERT SENT TO ALL VESSELS. The hydraulic doors to be opened using the handles only.

IncidentDetails: Fitter Got injured in his left hand fingers while getting down from the funnel space.
IncidentEventDetails: Today around 1300 hrs Fitter went to the top of the funnel space to check the booby hatch cover of funnel. After checking when he was trying to close the booby hatch cover from inside standing on the ladder, due to rolling and wind the cover fell on his fingers. The ring finger on left hand got injured with bruises in the middle finger.
ImmediateAction: Fitter injured finger cleaned with cotton and antiseptic lotion. Medication as instructed by Dr.Balaji started.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Left Hand ring finger cut wound near the nail, and bruises in middle finger of Fitter
DirectCause: The fall of Booby hatch door of funnel top space on the fingers while closing the door.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Pulling arrangement to close the door after descending the ladder.

IncidentDetails: CREW WAS GETTING OUT OF MESS ROOM AFTER HAVING HIS DINNER, WHEN THE FIRE DOOR ACCTDENTLY SLAM SHUT ON TO HIS INDEX FINGER RIPPING THE NAIL OFF THE BASE.
IncidentEventDetails: CREW WAS GETTING OUT OF MESS ROOM AFTER HAVING HIS DINNER TO WASH HIS PLATE IN THE GALLEY WHEN THE FIRE DOOR ACCTDENTLY SLAM SHUT ON TO HIS INDEX FINGER DUE TO VESSEL MOVEMENT RIPPING THE NAIL OFF THE BASE.
ImmediateAction: FIRST AID PROVIDED AND MEDICAL ADVICE ASKED FROM COMPANY DOCTOR DR. BALAJI
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The left hand index fingure tip got crushed between the door and sill connecting the galley and crew mess room ripping the nail off the base.
DirectCause: 1. Due to sudden movement of the vessel in rough weather, the fire door between galley and mess room slammed shut and the A/B left hand index finger got caught between door and frame causing crushing injury. 2. Heavy weather causing movement of vessel in seaway 3. Fire door should have been kept shut at all times but it was left open to facilitate passage.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew advised to be careful during vessel movement and be attentive at all times.
Corrective Action: Carry out safety meeting with all the staff and discuss the incident and ways to prevent such occurrence in the future.
LessionLearnt: One should be mindful of heavy weather and should exercise extreme caution when passing through doorway as the likelyhood of door unlatching and slamming shut remains high.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory
ClosingNote: Shared in the fleet for lesson learning.

IncidentDetails: WHILE WORKING ON DECK, CREWMEMBER ACCIDENTLY GOT HIT WITH SPANNER ON THE HAND.
IncidentEventDetails: WHILE TIGHTENING THE BOLT, SPANNER SLIPPED AND HIT THE CREWMEMBER ON  LEFT HAND
ImmediateAction: FIRST AID GIVEN AS PER ADVISE OF COMPANY DOCTOR. CREWMEMBER SENT ASHORE FOR MEDICAL EVALUATION AS ADVISED. MEDICAL REPORT ATTACHED. FIT FOR DUTY.

IncidentDetails: Pumpman was preparing manifold for hose connection for cargo discharge at Stolthaven Dock 3, houston. To connect the cargo hose, pumpman has to connect 6 x 8" reducer in CT 1S / 2P. While removing the flange to connect the reducer, few drops of cargo started coming out, with some pressure and one drop of cargo entered in pumpman's left eye, to prevent more cargo to splash, pumpan tightenend the loosend bolts in a hurry which caused flange from the bottom to misalign and some cargo splashed on pumpman left thigh from the bottom of flange.
IncidentEventDetails: Pumpman approached starboard manifold alone at around 1310LT to connect 6 X 8" reducer without wearing correct PPE. He started opening the flange to connect the reducer, while opening the top bolts few drops of cargo entered in pumpman's left eye, to prevent more cargo to splash, pumpan tightenend the loosend bolts in a hurry which caused flange from the bottom to misalign and some cargo splashed on pumpman left thigh from the bottom of flange.
ImmediateAction: Pumpman immediatly washed his eye and thigh with copious amount of fresh water at eye wash located at the manifold, Contaminated clothing was removed and eye wash was administered in the ship's hospital and Anti dote PEG was applied on the thigh. Further he was taken to doctor ashore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor injury near thigh.
DirectCause: 1. Non compliance with company requirement on PPE level  2. Implied haste 3.Lack of Supervision
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be briefed about hazards of cargo regularly. Encourage crew to read MSDS of cargoes posted in common spaces room in their free time. confirm from Chief officer / Duty officer prior cargo operation the PPE level required for that particular cargo.
Corrective Action: All crew to be briefed about hazards of cargo regularly. Encourage crew to read MSDS of cargoes posted in common spaces room in their free time. confirm from Chief officer / Duty officer prior cargo operation the PPE level required for that particular cargo.
LessionLearnt: Crew should be aware of PPE level of various grades of chemicals carried on board. Senior officer should verify that crew adhere to proper and appropriate for the job. Adequate supervision is to be in place.
ReviewComments: Satisfactory.
ClosingNote: All crew should know about the hazards of cargo. Encourage crew to read MSDS of cargoes posted in common spaces room in their free time. confirm from Chief officer / Duty officer prior cargo operation the PPE level required for that particular cargo.

IncidentDetails: During cargo completion in port of Sydney when AB Babiston was checking lashings ,reported that he tripped and had a fall in bay 44 aft cross deck area ,resulting in injury to his left leg ankle region and swelling of same.
IncidentEventDetails: 1530: All crew on deck for lasing checks and preparing departure stations.  1545: Duty officer (2nd officer) requested AB babiston to check lashing of bay 44 which was the only  pending bay for completion of cargo operations.   1558: Ab babiston reported bay 44 fwd all lashing and twist lock in order, proceeding to bay 44 aft (heard on walkie talkie).  1559: Ab babiston reported he has fallen down on cross deck through walkie talkie.    1600: Same reported to captain via phone,c/o and 2nd officer rushed to the spot.  1602: After reaching the spot bay 44 aft, found AB babiston lying down on the cross deck suffering   from the pain and informed his left leg got twisted.  1603: 2nd officer slowly removed his shoe, c/o and 2nd off saw his left ankle area swollen meanwhile captain and bosun reached the spot.   1610: AB babiston moved to his cabin safely from bay 44 cross deck and administrated with the medicine on captain’s advice.
ImmediateAction: Checked for condition of crew member,shifted him to cabin,applied ice pack on swollen leg portion, administered with  1) I-Buprofen 400 mg.                              2) Paracetamol - 500 mg                              3) Azithromycin - 250 mg  Reported to office and company's doctorfor further medical assistance.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling in left ankle region and uneasyness to patient.
DirectCause: Faliure to follow proper work procedures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety briefing carried out for all ship staff and explained consequenses for slip trips and fall if not following proper work procedure.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Concur with vessel comments

IncidentDetails: Around 1615 AB Tomas went down inside the CH. No. 4 entering from Booby hatch fwd part to do the initial cargo hold cleaning by air blowing. Deeck Cadet served as look out and assisting the AB. AB Tomas collapsed inside the Booby hatch first platform.
IncidentEventDetails: No enclose space permit had been made, no oxygen content monitorng done and carried out prior entry into the cargo space that leads  the AB to collapse inside the booby hatch no. 4 forward part due to lack of oxygen.
ImmediateAction: Immediately informed duty officer and Master. Raise the emergency alarm and announced in PA to rescue the victim. Emergency team and rescuer administer the victim with first aid and put the Breathing apparatus. Coordinated with shore personel to send an shore rescuer and ambulance. Ship's rescuer/staff and Shore rescuer lifted the victim out from Booby hatch No. 4 and carried him to the ambulance and send him to the hospital.
ClosingNote: Vessel been sold and not under our management

IncidentDetails: While securing the gangway with chain block the sling parted and cadet hit by chain block in the pelvic region.
IncidentEventDetails: Due to falling tide, ship's gangway was required to secure back as shore fenders would have damaged it.  Therefore Duty AB, Duty Officer and Deck Cadet (casuality) were securing the port gangway.   As the port gangway motor is inoperational and gangway cannot be lowered or heaved up with remote operation. Chain block was used with lifting belts and wire sling to secure back the port gangway.  While picking up the gangway, the wire sling(pics attached for ref.) , which was secured to the eye on the hatch cover, parted and the chainblock hit the Deck Cadet right side of the pelvic area. (pics attached for ref.).
ImmediateAction: Work was stopped and Deck cadet was immediately taken to safer place ( hospital) where  First aid was provided to him. No external injury marks , bruising etc.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor pain while walking or climbing the stairs
DirectCause: Lack of On scene analysis for safety procedure. Improper tools selection, Job carried out in a hurry.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Never To Stand In The Line Of Chain Block When Using To Lift Heavy Objects. Only certified / tested tools / equipments to be used for critical jobs.
ClosingNote: CAR found satisfactory and closed

IncidentDetails: One Of The Able Seaman Got Cut On His Left Hand Plam Between Thumb And Index Finger.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel Was Berthed At Dangjin, South Korea Which Is A High Tidal Range Port (09th Sep 2019)  Since it was a high tidal range port the access to ship and shore (Gangway) Has To Be Continously Monitored and requres change in position.  Vessel Was provided with a portable MOT from shore side.  due to change in tide it was decided to change its position  and on duty crew was carryng out the job,  One Of the Able seaman who was off duty casually came to gangway during evening time where he saw other crew members working for shifting the portable mot.  he to started helping the on duty people.  The portable mot was being lifted using bunker davit to take the weight of the portable mot and then slowly lower it to the shore side.  Bunker davit was being operated using deck air which was being supplied from engine room,   the able seaman was holding the hose when the davit was operated and as soon as the air supply valve was open on deck the pressure surge was so high that the hose snapped along with the hose clip which led to a cut on the able seamans left plam.
ImmediateAction: Immediately he was given first aid.  His hand was rinsed under running water to clean the wound and was asked to keep his hand elevated to reduce the blood loss. one the bleeding stopped wound was dressed with antiseptic ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Able seaman got a deep cut on his left hand plam between his thumb and index finger.
DirectCause: Improper PPE, Excessive Air Pressure Leading To Surge Of Air Hose.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All Crew To Ensure Never Start A Job Or Assist Until Proper PPE Is Donned.  Engine Room Informed To Control The Air Supply Coming On Deck.
ClosingNote: Car found satisfactory and closed

IncidentDetails: Deck cadet while refitting the crane hook to the block, the hook slipped of his left hand and hit his right hand and got injured.
IncidentEventDetails: The crane hook was removed for connecting the grab to the block. While securing the crane, hook has to be refitted with the block.  Bosun was operating No.1 crane and cadet was refitting the hook to the block.Chief officer was monitoring the entire activity and other AB was in No2 crane mast upper platform for securing the   Grab electric cable and Tug wire.    The block lying on the deck and the crane hook was next to it.  The cadet removed the pin of the D-shackle and was lifting the crane hook to push it into the slot.  The crane hook slipped of his left hand and fell to his right side.  He tried to hold it with his right hand but due to the weight of the hook he was not able to control it and got injured.
ImmediateAction: Dr.Balaji and CIRM ROMA were contacted for medical advise and followed their instruction.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut in the middle finger of his right hand palm side. First aid administered as advised by Dr. Balaji and CIRM ROMA.   Bed rest given, medication as below.  T. Paracetamol 1-1-1   T. Ibuprofen 1-0-1   T Ciprofloxacin 500mg 1-0-1     Under observation for further treatment.
DirectCause: Incorrect working posture.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To be attentive when carrying out small job
Corrective Action: As the hook is  heavy, at least 2 persons should be involved when fixing the pin.
ClosingNote: CA and PA accepted.

Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed in the safety meeting and emphasize on safe movement onboard as per COSWP and ensure staff is aware of hazard identification.

IncidentDetails: 3 pirates boarded the vessel via stern and entered engine room. They were spotted by 3/E near boiler in front of ECR. One of the pirates warned 3/E and swung a rod which caused a minor scratch on 3/E's nose. 3/E ran to bottom platform and called Bridge. General emergency alarm was raised and the 3 pirates escaped with 3 pcs 20 ltrs can of Meng T/C lube oil and 2 pcs anti-polishing ring for A/E.
IncidentEventDetails: 01:20 hrs Lt - Vessel abeam The Brothers and Master called.  02:00 hrs LT - Master on the Bridge  03:10 hrs LT - 3/E reported 3 pirates in engine room and emergency alarm raised  03:15 hrs LT - Pirates escaped from vessel   03:21 hrs LT - Vessel at Phillips Channel alteration point  05:00 hrs LT - Vessel searched and confirmed no pirates on board
ImmediateAction: General Alarm raised. VTIS informed. The 3 pirates escaped from vessel. Vessel searched and confirmed no pirates on board.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor scratch to 3/E's nose.  Loss of 3 pcs 20 ltrs lube oil for Meng turbocharger  Loss of 2 pcs anti polishing ring for A/E
DirectCause: Improper piracy watch
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Strict enforcement of piracy watch and monitoring of ship side in piracy prone areas.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Always pre-plan diligently transits through piracy prone areas

IncidentDetails: A SMALL FOREIGN PARTICLE EMBEDDED IN THE LEFT EYE BALL.
IncidentEventDetails: DURING GRINDING IN ENGINE ROOM WORKSHOP A SMALL FOREIGN PARTICLE PASSES THROUGH THE PROTECTIVE SHIELD, GOES INTO THE LEFT EYE AND EMBEDDED IN THE EYEBALL.AT THE TIME OF GRINDING HE DIDN'T NOTICED THAT AIR WAS BLOWING FROM THE OPPOSITE SIDE WHICH CAUSED THE PARTICLE TO PASS THROUGH THE PROTECTIVE SHIELD AND ENTER THE EYE.
ImmediateAction: EYE CLEANED WITH EYE WASH, TETRACYCLINE OPTHALMIC OINTMENT APPLIED IN THE EYELIDS AND  COVERED WITH EYE PADS TO RESTRICT MOVEMENT.COMPANY DOCTOR ADVICE REQUESTED.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: VERY SMALL DUST PARTICLE EMBEDDED INSIDE THE LEFT EYE BALL CAUSING MILD PAIN WHILE BLINKING THE EYELIDS.
DirectCause: PROPER RA NOT CARRIED OUT AND PRESENCE OF BLOWING AIR WHILE GRINDING OPERATIION WAS MISSED TO IDENTIFY AS A HAZARD.IMPROPER POSITIONING.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: MUST CHECK THE POSITION OF BLOWERS TO CARRY OUT SUCH KIND OF JOBS TO AVOID REOCURRANCE.
LessionLearnt: Safety goggles to be worn for using high power tools.
ClosingNote: The crew visited doctor and medical attention given, fit to continue on board. Find attached photos demonstrating wearing of safety goggles with power glasses.   Vessel were able to locate big size of safety goggles which can be used with power glasses.  SF-17- the training session is completed as attached. Injury Alert sent to all vessels.

IncidentDetails: SPRAIN IN AB NAVJOT LEG DUE TO SLIPPING ON DECK.
IncidentEventDetails: IN MORNING CREW WAS ASSIGNED JOB OF CLEANING ON DECK NEAR AFT SPRING WINCHES.TOOL BOX MEETING COMPLETED AND CREW PROCEEDED TO DECK FOR CLEANING. IT WAS RAINING ON DECK AND MODERATELY WINDY.AB NAVJOT SLIPPED WHILE HE WAS TRYING TO CROSS FISH PLATE IN BETWEEN MAIN DECK AND POOP DECK DUE TO NEW PAINT AND INCORRECT AREA TO PROCEED TO DECK. AB COULD NOT NOTICE THE INJURY AND WORKED TILL 1500 HRS IN THE AFTERNOON. LATER ON HE KEPT WATCH AT NIGHT(2000-2400 HRS). WHEN HE WOKE UP NEXT MORNING(30TH OCT) , HE FELT PAIN WHILE BENDING HIS KNEE AND LATER REALISED THAT THERE IS MARK ON HIS LEG AND SOME AMOUNT OF PAIN. IMMEDIATELY REPORTED TO C/O WAS ON BRIDGE WATCH.FIRST AID CARRIED OUT.
ImmediateAction: VOLINI GEL WAS RUBBED AROUND THE AREA AND BANDAGE WAS APPLIED. HE WAS REQUESTED TO REST AND NOT TO WORK FOR THE DAY AND REGULARLY MONITORED. DISCUSSED INJURY DURING TOOL BOX MEETING AND ADVISED TO AVOID CROSSING AREA FROM THE FISH PLATE , RATHER ALL TO USE WALKWAY.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: MINOR LEG SPRAIN WITH A DAY OF MAN-HOUR LOST.MONITORING IN PROGRESS.
DirectCause: FAILURE TO FOLLOW PROCEDURE AND USE WALKWAY WHILE GOING/COMING FROM DECK.SLIPPERY DECK DUE TO RAIN AND MODERATE WIND.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: TOOL BOX MEETING CARRIED OUT AND ALL ADVISED TO MAKE SURE THAT WALKWAY IS ALWAYS USED FOR ACCESS TO DECK.
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's CA

IncidentDetails: Particle of rust entered into crew members eye when cleaning the area after chipping on main deck.
IncidentEventDetails: After carrying out chipping and sweeping the deck, the OS was clearing the chipping dust from the area using compressed air. At that instance a small speck of rust flew into his eyes from the corner of the chipping goggles.
ImmediateAction: Washed eye using eye bath and eye wash. Requested for medical advise from the doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Rust particle stuck next to the iris. Regularly washing the eye with eye wash using the eye bath.
DirectCause: Human Error during carrying out of task. The PPE in use, in this case Chipping Goggles, has to be worn tightly when carrying out the job of cleaning.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Code of safe working practices to be discussed during tool box meeting & training for concerned crew member to be carried out, and all concerned crew members to be briefed to avoid re-occurance.
Corrective Action: Code of safe working practices to be discussed during tool box meeting & training for concerned crew member to be carried out, and all concerned crew members to be briefed to avoid re-occurance.
ClosingNote: Report closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: While the crew was carrying out M/E Scavenge space cleaning in E/R,one of the motormen upon cleaning the underpiston space,when he was returning back to scavenge manifold ,the person slipped from the step inside the scavenge manifold and hurt his back.
ImmediateAction: The crew complained after about a day that his back got strained and he was administered first aid with hot bag and pain relieving balm.He was given only watch routines and no physical work assigned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Experiencing increase in pain and it has slowly spread from upper back back.
DirectCause: Due to confined space and slippery in nature.Lack of focus on task and situational awarness
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Briefed all risks as per risk assessment and same insisted periodically during process of job.
Corrective Action: Briefed all risks as per risk assessment and same insisted periodically during process of job.

IncidentDetails: During 3rd Enginner watch noticed #1 AE L.O cooler vent cock (F.W side) leaking slightly.  - It was decided to rectify the leak after handing over watch to 2/E.  -Same discussed with 2/E during handover watch.  -Took along with me piping diagram to identify the valves to be isolated.  -It was decided to have initial look and prepare tool, followed by tool box talk, lock out tag-out after preparing the cold work permit.  -At site found the leak to be minimal from the gland of vent cock, decided to tight the gland in place.  -Fitter attempted to tighten the gland. While tightening, the brass gland cracked and the cock flew out.  -L.T F. Water started coming out with pressure,and 3rd Engineer found the cock in bilges and tried to put back in place.  -On site 3rd Engineer decided to limit the leak because vessel was in HRA and the running generators could have tripped, if leak continued.  -Fitter got burns when the cock flew out and water started leaking.  -3rd Engineer got burns while putting the cock back.

IncidentDetails: 1. While entering washroom G/S got lost balance/slipped.  2. Upon his attempt to hold some where his finger got hit near the shower fittings.  3. Due to which he got a cut/wound on his right hand ring finger
IncidentEventDetails: 1. While entering washroom for morning routines G/S got lost balance/slipped.  2. Upon his attempt to hold some where his finger got hit near the shower fittings.  3. Due to which he got a cut/wound on his right hand ring finger

IncidentDetails: When working on main deck , one of the steel pipe used for staging slipped from the hands of yard workers who were destaging and shifting steel pipes and fell on the head of crew who was walking on main deck. Resulting in an minor head injury
IncidentEventDetails: 1300: Tr.Os was transferring garbage from garbage storage area using trolley to Yard garbage bin kept near Bay-06 main deck stbd side.  1340 : As part of the ongoing retrofit job, stages which were erected for the installation of lashing bridges were being removed by yard workers.  1350 : As Tr.Os was proceeding towards Bay-06 from stbd side main deck , one of the steel piping used for staging slipped from the hands of the yard workers and fell on the left side head of the Tr.os.     1355: Physical examination carried out and found minor cut on the left side of the head.As he was wearing helmet the injury was minor.  1358: First aid administered.  1415: Tr.Os was taken to the Doctor by Qingdao Agent.
ImmediateAction: Informed Ship repair manager .Area cordened off and watchmen posted on all areas where the staging is being removed
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut in the left side of the head
DirectCause: During destaging, Area was not cordened off and no watch man posted to stop any personal passing in that area.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Area to be cordened off ,Do not pass tag to be used ,Watchman to be posted.
Corrective Action: To Brief All Ship Staff onboard regarding this incident
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: AB Montiano suffered a severe injury to his left index finger and a cut near his left eye. His left index fingergot fractured when the demucking winch used to pick up the cargo broke from its spindle and landed on his left index finger.
IncidentEventDetails: During a voyage from Zhoushan to New Castle Hold Cleaning was underway.  As  part of normal hold cleaning, demucking winch was being used to remove the cargo residue from Hold No. 9. The demucking winch was rigged over the deucking manhole and AB Mr. Montiano John Cabanig was guiding the wire of the winch to ensure a smooth lifting of the slung bucket.  During one such lift, the demucking winch broke from the spindle and his left index finger got caught in the sheeve at the end of the boom of the demucking winch. The Left index finger suffered serious cut and fracture along with a minor laceration (cut) next to left eye
ImmediateAction: Ship staff adminstered first aid.  Company doctor and CIRM were contacted.  Bleeding was stopped and he had reported having sensation at the tip of his left index finger.  Later vessel was diverted to Naha, Okinawa wherein AB John was medically evacuated by helicopter for medical attention ashore.  Flag state was informed and Dispensation sought for one Able Seaman which was obtained and has been placed on board.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: His index finger bone suffered a fracture and the top part of the skin got cut.  He also suffered a laceration (cut) next to his left eye.
DirectCause: Latent defect in demucking winch that was not evident to Safety Officer.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Load test of demucking winch to be done.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
ClosingNote: Demucking winch is to be included in the PMS job list as a monthly inspection.

ImmediateAction: He was immediately transferred to his cabin, first aid given
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight swelling on his left arm at triceps area, no external wound has been observed .

IncidentDetails: 3 Off got slipped on Main deck and landed his full body weight on his right shoulders which has caused Sprain .
IncidentEventDetails: 1. He was in hurry due to SIRE Vetting on the next day .  2. Was carrying Fresh water in a bucket which is the cause of slip due to the Water spill on deck.  3. He stepped over that and Slipped on Main deck.
ImmediateAction: Hot water Massage Given , Deep Heat Rub has been applied , 1 Pain killer given on the same day .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mild Sprain in Muscle in Shoulder-Arms Joint (Right ) .
DirectCause: 1. Inadequate compliance with safety procedures 2. Safe movement on board was not adhered to. 3. Implied haste - Person was under self pressure to complete the job before the inspection
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Coaching of 3/O by the Master to explain him the consequence of inadequate hazard analysis.
Corrective Action: Coaching of 3/O by the Master to explain him the consequence of inadequate hazard analysis.
LessionLearnt: One should not be under self pressure for performance.  Work should not be left for the last hour instead proper planning is required to do it safely. If situation demands the job is to be completed during last hour then addition assistance to be sough after meticulous planning

ClosingNote: Circulated the incident report to fleet vessels.
