IncidentDetails: Crew was sustain a hand injury due to accidental breakage of grinding disc.
IncidentEventDetails: During maintenance onboard, one crew was assigned to do grinding of de-rusted part on poop deck, then suddenly the grinding disc was broke and hit his right hand.
ImmediateAction: Bring the crew to the hospital and administered first aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew sustained a minor injury (cut) on his right hand, see photos attached.
DirectCause: During investigation, we checked the grinding disc and the grinding machine and found that it was compatible to each other, grinding disc specified max. rpm was 13700 with size of 100mm while the grinding machine is 11000 rpm only. The crew is also wearing a proper PPE during maintenance before the incident happened. The crew told us that he felt wiggling of the grinder before it broke.   We concluded that the grinding disc was loosen and with the force from his hand during grinding caused the breakage of it.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Make sure that the grinding disc is properly and tightly fitted before using. During grinding operation always take a time to check the grinding disc if it is still tight and properly fitted. Identified cause to be a part of the risks to be considered when discussing during tool box meeting. Training of ships crew to be carried out on the checks to be performed before and during the use of the grinding disc. SF17 training records to be uploaded as evidence.
Corrective Action: Make sure that the grinding disc is properly and tightly fitted before using. During grinding operation always take a time to check the grinding disc if it is still tight and properly fitted. Identified cause to be a part of the risks to be considered when discussing during tool box meeting. Training of ships crew to be carried out on the checks to be performed before and during the use of the grinding disc. SF17 training records to be uploaded as evidence.
LessionLearnt: Use of proper PPE, continuous and repeated checks on tools being used.
ReviewComments: Discussion and training carried out.
ClosingNote: Meeting and training was carried out

IncidentDetails: One crew member (OS) was changing IG line blank of 2P cargo oil on deck. He tripped after his foot got entangled with fire hoe used earlier for gas freeing. He sustained head & back injuries. Little blood clotting oberved on the back side of his head. He was in pain & was not able to stand or walk. He was not able to turn around on his.
IncidentEventDetails: 1400: AB Tousif report about OS Joe lying on deck on walkie talkie. Duty officer informed master.  1402: CO arrived at location.   1410: With help of crew brought OS Joe in Hospital using Stretcher. He was not able to get up on his own.  1415: Master informed to Onboard Superintendent  1420: Onboard Superintendent  1425: Joe told about his fall due to tripping with fire hose  1430: Masterr informed to Superintendent  1440: Master caled up agents for assistance  1455: Pain Killer tablet, Ibuprofen 400 mg was given to Joe, after he could sit with help of crew  1522: Master sent mail to agents & company on his situation & asked for medical treatment  1630: Launch arrived  1645: Joe was disembarked by crane using lumber support  2120: Agent confirmed that CT Scan was carried out & no fracture observed. MRI was to be done on next day.  Naxt day agent informed that MRI was done & nno fracture report, however, Joe siad that  he was not able to walk by himself. Medicines were given to him, which was upto 7th Dec'19.   On 1st Dec'19, he joined the ship & was walking, with limp. He was given back support belt. He was on total rest. He will be signing off on 8th Dec 19.
ImmediateAction: He was brought to the hospital & assured. After sometime, he was asked about his fall. First-aid was given. Crew helped him to turn around & ti sit with support. Sought advise from Superintendent & asked agents to arrange for sending him to hospital.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One crew member (OS) was changing IG line blank of 2P cargo oil on deck. He tripped after his foot got entangled with fire hose used earlier for gas freeing. He sustained head & back injuries. Little blood clotting oberved on the back side of his head. He was in pain & was not able to stand or walk. He was not able to turn around on his.
DirectCause: Lack of awareness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew were briefed to work safely and to move safely while on deck or where there is obstruction and chances to fall.
Corrective Action: All crew were briefed to work safely and to move safely while on deck or where there is obstruction and chances to fall.
LessionLearnt: To have complete situational awareness at all times.
ClosingNote: This has been closed satisfactorily.
Other Part: Back of the head and lower back

IncidentDetails: Incident happened during the lifeboat drill organized on 06 Dec 2019, while vessel was anchored at Chiba anchorage. While operating the lifeboat davit with weight of lifeboat, the starboard side hoisting slings of the boat parted as soon as the hydraulic release was operated. Due to the weight of the boat suddenly falling on port side fall wire, it also parted due to impulse. AB Barri who was holding the Starboard side painter was not able to release the painter in time and sudden movement of rope injured/amputated his top part of left hand index finger.  Lifeboat had a freefall into water. 3/O Satheesh who was inside the boat verified that he is alright and sustained no injuries. There was no apparent damage to boat which was later verified to be good condition.  Damages:   Personnel : AB Barri had top part of his index  finger amputated.   Property. Port side lifeboat fall wire and stbd side hoisting slings of the boat parted and were rendered unusable.
IncidentEventDetails: DATE: 06 DEC 2019, TIME ZONE: UTC + 9  1455 LT: Lifeboat davit tried out without any weight.  1505 LT: Lifeboat weight taken on Davit falls through lifeboat hoisting slings. Two painter ropes secured on lifeboat deck. AB Barri was holding the Starboard side painter. Both painters were secured with 2 turns of rope on guardrails on lifeboat deck.  1510 LT: 3/O Satheesh entered the lifeboat. Reported secured himself on seat and ready to operate the hydraulic release.  1515 LT: Hydraulic release was operated by 3/O Satheesh. Securing jack was released. As soon as jack was released, Starboard side lifeboat hoisting slings parted and lifeboat was released with free fall. Due to the weight of lifeboat, port side fall wire rope also parted. AB Barri who was holding the Starboard side painter was not able to release the painter in time and sudden movement of rope injured/amputated his top part of left hand index finger.   1520 LT: AB Barri was shifted to ships hospital and given first aid. Wound was disinfected and dressed with sterile bandage.   1730 LT: AB Barri disembarked the vessel in a boat to go ashore for medical care.  1730 LT: Lifeboat extra lashings were taken.  1800 LT: Planned to renew the Lifeboat wires.    1845 LT-2020 LT: Two crew members were sent down to life boat to replace the                                     Hoisting Slings D-shackle, however were unable to open D-shackle. Hence,                                additional new D-shackles were attached.  2355 LT: Since the lifeboat wires came out of the sheaves when parted, new wires have                     be to passed through the sheaves.                    As the sheaves are at the extremities of the davit, using a ladder to reach                   the sheaves could be unsafe and ineffective. So hoist a cargo net with a pallet using      the winch and mooring rope to the top of the davit.                   A crew member will sit on the pallet and will pass the wires through the sheaves  DATE: 07 DEC 2019, TIME ZONE: UTC + 9  0001 LT-0220 LT: Cargo net with a pallet did not work. Crew member could not reach                                   the sheave to pass the wire.  0230 LT-0400 LT: Wooden planks were put between the gap of the access way to lifeboat                                  and secured the ladder. Thus, successfully passed one wire                                  Meanwhile new Hoisting slings prepared with wire rope clips (Bull dog                                 grips) got ready to be used to replace the parted ones.  0500 LT: Successfully passed the second wire.  0624 LT: Both the wires were heaved on the drum.  0724 LT: Lifeboat stowed in position and secured
ImmediateAction: Personnel : AB Barri's finger was bleeding which was later stopped by first aid provided onboard. He was later disembarked for shore based medical care.   Property: Both fall wires were replaced with spares available onboard. Hoisting slings for stbd side were fabricated onboard with wire slings and bulldog grips. Boat was hoisted back and stowed securely.    Safe practices:   * 3/O was safely secured in lifeboat seat before operating the hydraulic release  * Permits and RA were in place.  * Tool box meeting was carried out  * Davit was tried without weight of boat.  * Prompt first aid to injured crew  * AB Barri was calm and composed after the accident despite finger injury.    Unsafe practices:    * Lack of situational awareness  * Unsafe rope handling
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Damages:   Personnel : AB Barri had top part of his index  finger amputated.   Property. Port side lifeboat fall wire and stbd side hoisting slings of the boat parted and were rendered unusable.
DirectCause: The apparent cause is malfunction of lifeboat hoisting slings on stbd side of the boat. There is no record or evidence available onboard to suggest that hoisting slings were renewed since boat was commissioned. There is no requirement of renewal of hoisting slings in the PMS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proposed action is to include periodic renewal of lifeboat hoisting slings along with Falls in the PMS
Corrective Action: Freefall lifeboat fall wires of both ends to be renewed
ClosingNote: All corrective actions completed.

IncidentDetails: While connecting hose to discharge side of pneumatic diaphragm pump, a crew members leg accidentally touched the air valve thus operating the pump and causing remaining diesel oil in pump to splash on his face and eyes.
IncidentEventDetails: It was planned to demuck F.O. overflow tank for 2020 fuel changeover regulations. Remaining unpumpable oil inside the F.O. overflow tank was to be transfered to W.O. tank using pneumatic diaphragm pump. A crew arranged the pump and connected the air hose with air isolation valve on pump shut and supply air valve open. While he was connecting a flexiable pipe on the discharge side of the pump, his leg accidentaly operated the air actuation valve on the pump. The pump was initially used to remove unpumpable oil from other bunker tanks and after operation was flushed with diesel oil and kept standby. When the air valve was accidentally operated, remaining oil in the pump splached out onto the crew members face and eyes, causing eye irritation. Crew member was wearing gloves, helmet, boiler suit but his goggles were on his helmet.
ImmediateAction: Air pipe connecting the pump was immediately disconnected from source, crew member was taken to the wash room to wash his face and cleaned his eye with eye wash. Later he was sent on bridge where eyewash was administered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew members has irritation in his eyes.  Medical advice is Required
DirectCause: Lack of situational awareness, lack of wearing proper PPE for the job
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training carried out for all engine room staff to stress upon the usage of PPE at all times. Also crew members advised to be aware of their surroundings while working
ClosingNote: Crew Briefing done on use of PPE and situational aawareness while working. Possible Hazards to be discussed during tool box meeting.  Company doctor advise taken and treatment provided onboard.

IncidentDetails: While entering in to the No 2 S Bunker tank for cleaning operation,Motorman Vitesh Rai lost his balance and fell down from the laddder inside the tank.He got injured on back of his head,tail of back bone and left hand near wrist.
IncidentEventDetails: 17/12/2019   While approaching to Fujairah anchorage    Bunker tank 2S was prepared for tank cleaning on 16/12/2019. Ventilation was carried out with dry air from IGG . H2S, CO and LEL was zero and oxygen 20.9% . Entry was made and found heating coil on bottom platform was hot and slight irritation of eyes due to fumes , so tank cleaning operation was suspended. Main isolation valve for bunker tank heating line was shut and ventilation was countinuosly kept on throughout the night.    Next attempt to enter bunker tank was made on 17/12/19 . Atmosphere checks carried out ,permit was made from 0630 hrs. onwards.When Vitesh (motorman) was entering tank at 0640 hrs ,after stepping down about 5 -6 steps  he slipped and tripped down to 1st platform into bunker tank deck and was shouting for help. CO/2E immediately informed bridge and alarm was raised . Motor man Sher Jahan put on  SCBA and entered into the tank and then stretcher was lowered with EEBD. Vitesh was conscious and breathing normal. Then all crew came to the site. Kavaskar (AB) put on another SCBA and entered into the tank . Both persons ( Sherjahan & Kavaskar) secured the injured (Vitersh Rai) into the stretcher and Vitesh was lifted  out of the bunker tank.    After bringing Vitesh rai out of the tank , found upper part of the left hand fore arm was swollen and back ache was severe. On enquiring Vitesh rai told, he suddenly got something into his eyes , immediately his right hand went to his eyes due to which he lost his balance and slipped  into the tank.  Presently all his vitals are normal. He got injury on his tail of back bone, left hand near wrist and back of head.    No bleeding anywhere, no fracture ( as presumed with condition), Ice bags is using to treat his injury. Pain killer was given. Later Hot Milk mixed with turmeric powder was also given.
ImmediateAction: Emergency alarm  raised, crew mustered, 2 crew members put on SCBA set and entered into the tank with spare EEBD set.As Vitesh was breathing normal he was transferred to stretcher and lifted out of the tank.He was taken into the hospital and first aid treatment was given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oiler fell down from stairs in bunker tank due to lost of balance. He injured on tail of back bone, left hand near wrist area and little head injury but no blood came out.
DirectCause: Human error
Proposedcorrectiveactions: awareness to crew and trainning to work safe and remain conciousness to work safe always.

IncidentDetails: It was reported that a stevedore: Wilderson Fleurissaint (32 yrs old) at around 10:30Hrs was injured from inside Hold No.5, during shoveling/collecting cargo. As per questioning - he was positioning the cargo grab to scoop the cargo, but due to stvdrds miscommunication (signalling) with winchman  he was hit by the grab on his both lower part of his legs.
ImmediateAction: - The enjured man was already at crossdeck  at aft part of Hold No.5 - First Aid was administered by OOW. They were waiting for a car to pick him up to hospital. Agent was notified regarding the incident - and said "he will be taken to hospital"  Food and water was also offered to him as the injured stvr waited. Deck hand assisted and provided ship's stretcher to be abled to lower him down to quay at around 13:00Hrs when a car arrived to pick- him up to hospital
ClosingNote: No further information was shared by the Sore personnel.

IncidentDetails: While cleaning bridge Deck Cadet tripped and hit his genetal area with hand rail. causing minor cut and swelling on his genetal part.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was rolling moderately.  Deck cadet started cleaning bridge, and due to sudden rolling tripped and hit to the safety hand rail.
ImmediateAction: Checked and found minor cut and swelling on genetal part.  Medical advice seeked from company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Monir injury to genetal part.
DirectCause: Due to sudden rolling of vessel.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew to be trained for the proper procedures to follow while working in heavy weather.
ClosingNote: Safety meeting completed, cadet recovered.

IncidentDetails: Burn injury to 3rd Eng due to splashing of heavy Fuel oil
IncidentEventDetails: 3rd Eng went for HFO service tank Manual sounding and vessel was rolling moderately. Since the tank was 80 % full , The heavy oil came out of sounding pipe while rolling and spashed on his hands.
ImmediateAction: The burn wound was immediately cleaned and First aid provided. Medical advice taken from Dr. Balaji and medicines given as per his advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1st degree burns on Right hand and around neck area.
DirectCause: Proper PPE not worn while taking sounding of hot oil.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: No sounding of tank to be taken while rolling of vessel and proper Face mask and other PPE to be worn .

IncidentDetails: AB-JOSEPH Michael put his Right leg between the 2 upper ladder steps of the gangway. He put his leg from the upper ladder to the bottom ladder.     He went on the ladder to remove the gangway stanchions safety line. Due to the vessels vibration the upper Ladder moved upward even when the brakes were full tight which resulted in his right leg being stuck in between the upper ladder and bottom ladder.    The movement of the telescopic ladder wire drum even when the brakes were on full tight could also be due to the nut and bolt assembly of the stanchions which might have made a small play of the movement in the telescopic upper ladder.
IncidentEventDetails: 25th Dec 2019 This is to put on record that today on 25th Dec 2019, vessel departed from Kaohsiung berth KHH-66 and while navigating inside breakwater AB Joseph Michael Injured his Right Leg during securing starboard side gangway.   Please find below details of incident in a sequential event.  25th/08:12 hrs LT / 25th/00:12 hrs UTC Cargo Operations completed and Kaohsiung POB.  08:15 hrs / 00:15 hrs - Main Engine tried out Astern and Ahead. Found satisfactory. SBE.  08:42 hrs / 00:42 hrs - All cast off. Vessel Commenced swinging to portside.  08:58 hrs / 00:58 hrs - Aft tug cast off and Vessel moving head. Vessel was on hand steering under pilotage enroute to pilot station.  09:10 hrs / 01:10 hrs -Ch.Officer sent Bosun to operate Starboard side Gangway for pilot disembarkation.  09:15 hrs / 01:15 hrs -Bosun and AB-Joseph Michael AB-Dillibabu were attending Pilot disembarkation area.  09:16 hrs / 01:16 hrs- Master, 3rd off officer,2/O and AB-Premanabhan Damodaran on bridge and in Forward station Ch.officer,OS and Cdt and and at Starboard gangway Bosun, AB-Dillibabu Deivasigamani and AB- Josehp Michael were attending Pilot Disembarkation.  09:17 hrs /01:17 hrs- Pilot was escorted by the 2/Off from the bridge and AB-Dillibabu at the gangway.  09:18 hrs / 01:18 hrs - Pilot away and 2/O and AB-Dillibabu went to AFT station to secure the mooring ropes.  09:20 hrs / 01:20 hrs -Bosun heaved up Gangway to 2mtrs below deck level and put brake for telescopic wire rope.  09:21hrs / 01:21hrs - Bosun and AB-Joseph Michael commenced securing gangway.  09:21hrs / 01:21 hrs -AB. Joseph Michael went over the gangway to remove stanchion safety lines.  09:22 hrs / 01:22 hrs -AB. Joseph Michael put his right leg between 2 ladder steps from the top ladder to the bottom ladder, to stand and remove the safety line. (Attached pictures for better understanding).  09:23 hrs / 01:23 hrs -There was sudden movement of the upper ladder a jerk, which resulted to cause the leg of AB got get stuck.  09:24 hrs / 01:24 hrs - Bosun reported to Ch.officer on Walkie talkie to come to starboard side gangway. He reported that AB.Joseph Michael, Leg is stuck between the telescopic ladder.  09:26 hrs /  01:26hrs - Ch.officer came from the Fwd station and 2nd officer came from Aft station. Bridge was informed at the same time.  09:27hrs / 01 : 27 hrs - Ch.officer Immediately reported to Master that AB- Joseph Michael right Leg was found fractured. possible at the Achilles Tendion. Vessel was still inside the breakwater as Kaohsiung, pilot usually disembarks prior or abeam to the signal station inside the breakwater.  09:28hrs / 01:28hrs - Master immediately reported to Kaohsiung VTS for Medical assistance & evacuation and navigated the vessel out of the breakwater.  09:29hrs / 01:29hrs - PA announcement was made to provide assistance to the injured AB Joseph.  09:30hrs / 0130hrs  - Informed to the Local agent Mr. Gary for medical evacuation.  09:32hrs / 01:32hrs- Informed to Synergy office, Capt. Navdeep Singh Bhalla & Mr. Kartik Nair.    09:40hrs / 01:40hrs - AB- Joseph was on the stretcher on the stbd side gangway area. He was administered first aid. Ice packs were applied and splint was put on his right leg.  10:07hrs / 02:07hrs - Vessel called Balaji Medical centre on whats app and they advised to give Tramadol tablet with paracetamol tablet following was given.  Vessel called agent Mr. Gary again asking regarding the prospects of the medical evacuation. Agent confirmed boat will arrive vessel in about 30mins. Vessel remains adrift off the breakwater at about miles.  10:39hrs / 02:39hrs -AB Joseph alcohol test was done and found to be 0.0%.  AB Joseph was given comfort and motivation during this time.  11:00hrs 03:00hrs - Boat arrived alongside the vessel.  11:45hrs 03:45hrs - AB Joseph was disembarked from the vessel.  11:55hrs  03:55hrs - His luggage lowered to service boat.  12:00hrs 04:00hrs - Service boat away.
ImmediateAction: AB- Joseph Michael Leg was removed from the gangway and he was given First aid. Local Agent was informed and so was  Kaohsuing port control. AB was evacuvated  for futher treatment ashore via a boat launch provided by the local agent.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Injury to Right Leg.     Neglible damage. Detailed extend of injury addressed on the initail page of this injury report.
DirectCause: AB-JOSEPH Michael put his Right leg between the 2 upper ladder steps of the gangway. He put his leg from the upper ladder to the bottom ladder.     He went on the ladder to remove the gangway stanchions safety line. Due to the vessels vibration the upper Ladder moved upward even when the brakes were full tight which resulted in his right leg being stuck in between the upper ladder and bottom ladder.    The movement of the telescopic ladder wire drum even when the brakes were on full tight could also be due to the nut and bolt assembly of the stanchions which might have made a small play of the movement in the telescopic upper ladder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Corrective Action:  -----------------    New joiners to be orientated more thoroughly with the operational aspects of all critical jobs / equipments prior vessel sailing port relating to the vessel specific.    Prevention:  ------------    This could have been prevented by sending an old hand AB already on board, for securing the gangway ladder over the side as AB Joseph had just joined the vessel at Hong Kong on the 22Dec 19. His previous vessel's were RORO and had come on a container vessel after a gap of few years. Last container vessel sailed was in 2014.    Accident could have been prevented if both his legs as usually is the case on these telescopic gangway, are placed on the upper ladders and no feet on the bottom step.
Corrective Action: Incident to be shared across the fleet for all vessels. Vessels to discuss in SCM especially if have telescopic ladders on board
LessionLearnt: Feet to be kept on top step only when securing gangway. Brakes to be tight and in good condition to avoid slippage
ReviewComments: None
ClosingNote: Nil

IncidentDetails: Turkish fitter – Mr Aslan Necati slipped in A-Deck alleyway and had impact on left side head near ear region, But no visible sign of injury/buises or swelling. But resulted in bleeding from his left ear. First aid was provided and bleeding reduced after some time.
IncidentEventDetails: On the way to crew messromm from his cabin, Turkish fitter – Mr Aslan Necati slipped in A-Deck alleyway and caused impact on left side head near ear region.
ImmediateAction: First aid administered, Blood pressure checked - All found in normal level.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Turkish fitter – Mr Aslan Necati slipped in A-Deck alleyway and had impact on left side head near ear region, But no visible sign of injury/buises or swelling. Which resulted in bleeding from his left ear. First aid was provided and bleeding reduced after some time.
DirectCause: Slip and Trip, Wet floor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Walk carefully if suspected wet floors. Warning signs to be placed. Water spillage to be cleaned immidiatly by person concerned.
Corrective Action: Walk carefully if suspected wet floors. Warning signs to be placed. Water spillage to be cleaned immidiatly by person concerned.
ClosingNote: Walk carefully by holding on to appropriate railing.

IncidentDetails: While cutting the fruit, AB had a deep cut in his left palm
IncidentEventDetails: While cutting the fruit, AB had a deep cut in his left palm
ImmediateAction: Cleaned the wound with antiseptic Iodine solution, and dressed the wound with CETRIMIDE ANTISEPTIC 0.5% CREAM.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The cut was cleaned and was superficial in nature.
DirectCause: Carelessness
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew trained for safe use of sharp objects. Cutters removed from common places.
ClosingNote: Crew training carried out. To be discussed in safety meeting.

IncidentDetails: @0635-0715 Commenced stow away search, no stowaway found  0836-0848- Commenced 2nd Search of the entire vessel, no stowaway found  @1400 stowaways found onboard bet. hold no 1 and no2  stbd side in the blue drums inside crane no.1
IncidentEventDetails: Injury, damage to property
ImmediateAction: Reported to the bridge and Sounded the general alarm followed by P.A , all crew proceeded to the bridge and instructed to search again after the incident, no additional stowaway found, all concerned parties contacted and inform about the incident, and after that all crew gathered to the bridge and have a security briefing, schedule of ship staff watches for the two stowaways.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew who found out the stowaways and reported to the bridge / no injury incurred.
DirectCause: Stowaway Comougflaging inside the drum in the grab no. 1
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thorough Inspection
Corrective Action: Thorough Inspection
ClosingNote: Security circular 002-2020 to be complied and discussed with team during safety meeting

IncidentDetails: Left foot ankle injury.
IncidentEventDetails: AB Carlos was chipping/painting on top of  hatch cover #3. At around 1430LT,he was coming down from the hatch cover to the main deck. Accidentally he slipped from the ladder to the main deck. Observed that there were traces of grease on the steps and he was carrying a drum of paint while going down.
ImmediateAction: Checked the injured part, applied cold compression, applied analgesic balm, tied  bandage then let him  to take rest for some time.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: His left foot ankle injured.  Swelling observed and He cannot put force on his left foot while walking.   There is no sign of fracture.
DirectCause: Lack of situational awareness.  Procedures not followed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Always think of personal safety while doing any task on board.   Follow the "Take Five" rules before doing anything. (Stop & Think, Identify, Assess, Control, Proceed).
Corrective Action: Both hands to be on ladder and therefore, paint drum, tools, etc. not to be carried in hand but lowered on to the deck with the help of a line. Crew to be briefed regarding this.
ClosingNote: Closed. CA found adequate

IncidentDetails: During mooring station bosun's right foot three fingers were injured.
IncidentEventDetails: While giving the ship's line to the tug boat one of the ship's line was slack in water and the ship's staff was making fast the line on the bits,during making fast the line , stopper was put on the rope and bosun was holding the stopper,at the same time without giving any ready signal to tug boat by chief officer,the tug pulled the rope and changed the direction of the pull as well ,as bosun could not see the immediate incoming pull by tug he tried to stop the tightning line by pulling stopper and using panama lead as support by his leg,but due to change in direction of the pull and the immediate pull by the tug bosun's foot got caught between the stoppered line,tug line and panama lead.bosun was wearing gum boots without steel toe as it was raining before the mooring operation started which caused the injury to fingers.
ImmediateAction: Tug boat was given immediate signal to stop pulling and bosun's leg was cleared of the line,then he was taken to ship's hospital and given medical first aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Three right foot fingers were injured. Sutures has been done on 3rd and 4th fingers tip by shore doctor.
DirectCause: 1.Improper PPE  2.Safe working practices not follwed
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Safety meeting carried out with all crew expained on following:  1.Usage of proper PPE.  2.Usage of steel toe shoes at all times while working on deck.  3.All gumboots without steeltoe protection NOT to be used on ship while working.  4.Proper requisition to be raised for the gumboot with steeltoe protection.
Corrective Action: Crew member was treated at shore hospital. Fleet alert was sent to implement preventive actions as identified in investigation report
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: As per investigation report
ReviewComments: Detailed investigation carried out and lessons to be learnt shared with fleet.
ClosingNote: Attached detailed incident report for cause analysisand corrective actions

IncidentDetails: 4/E Slipped on tnk top area and got injured lower leg part
IncidentEventDetails: while taking round 4/e noticed water on tank top and went down to investigate the same.  while tracing the source of leakege he slipped on tank top area and hit on angle bar.  due to above he sustained a minor injury on lower part of his left leg.
ImmediateAction: he was given first aid and send to shore docter for treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Skin abrasion of lower part of left leg .  For treatment, the doctor cleaned and applied a bandage to the affected area.
DirectCause: Negligence
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Thoroughextra ordinary safety/ tool box meeting carried out and request all personnel to observe  safety precautions while carrying out duties/work in engine room.
Corrective Action: Incident shared with the fleet to create awareness regarding the incident so that other vessels may learn from the incident
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Incident was minor as it was promarily due to lack of adherence to safe practices while moving in the ER.. However  4E had to be attended by doctor at next nearest port, hence the injury was severe enough to merit additional medical care.

IncidentDetails: Oiler's hand (palm)  injured, trapping between self closing fire door and door frame while coming out of engine room.
IncidentEventDetails: At tea break 2 oilers when coming to upper deck alleway from engine room.  1st oiler opened fire door at engine room leading to upper deck air seal.  As soon as 1st oiler passed door, he realesed door handle without realising that 2nd oiler was behind him and his hand was on door frame.  2nd oiler hand trapped inbetween self closing fire door and door frame,which resulted in hand injury.
ImmediateAction: Oiler shifted to hospital, wound cleaned,first aid given,company medical advice seeked.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Upon checking by doctor found palm bone fracture.
DirectCause: While using self closing fire door, crew should be aware of injury hazard while holding door frame.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Door closers all checked for over tightness and speed of action. Crew members briefed for proper use of doors and for using handrails or door knobs while passing through similar doors.
Corrective Action: Door closers to be checked for over tightness and speed of action. Crew members to be briefed for proper use of doors and for using handrails or door knobs while passing through similar doors.
LessionLearnt: Care to be taken by all on board personnel while moving through self closing doors.
ClosingNote: Crew member signed off for complete healing and proper treatment of the injury.However this was done after a while as vessel was not in a convenient port for sign off and repatriation

IncidentDetails: While keeping fire watch with deck fitter who was welding on poop deck, O.S experienced sharp pain in the right eye. After examined a spot was  found on the lower part of the cornea of the right eye. May be or may not be the presence of a particle but it was tried to remove the same with cotton bud without success.
IncidentEventDetails: 01) No proper PPE.  02) Lack of knowledge.
ImmediateAction: Immediately washed eye with fresh waterand Patient's eye washed with eye wash. Ofloxacin eye drops and Chlortetracycline HCL eye ointment were applied and eye covered with gauze and eye cap.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: While keeping fire watch with deck fitter who was welding on poop deck, O.S experienced sharp pain in the right eye. After examined a spot was  found on the lower part of the cornea of the right eye. May be or may not be the presence of a particle but it was tried to remove the same with cotton bud without success.
DirectCause: Inadequate PPE
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Correct PPE to be used at all times.
Corrective Action: Crew provided with treatment on board and no further complaints received from AB. AB returned to work
ClosingNote: Crew member recovered on board

IncidentDetails: while opening the ballast pump pipe line wrench spanner hit oiler wilan above nose area between eyes
ImmediateAction: given first aid by master

IncidentDetails: While cleaning galley chief cook slipped on wet floor & hurt his back (internal injury)
IncidentEventDetails: After meal hours chief cook & GS was cleaning galley with fresh water .Chief cook was on one end of the galley. As chief cook moved towards the centre of the galley he slipped on the wet floor hitting  hard on his right buttock.
ImmediateAction: Chief cook was picked up & made to sit. He was administered pain releif spray.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Sprain in the back
DirectCause: chief cook moved too fast on the wet floor
Proposedcorrectiveactions: chief cook was instructed to walk slowely & to wear Gum boots when cleaning galley.

IncidentDetails: Patient was cleaning in the E/R stbdside tank top when he accidentall slip & use his left hand to support the fall.
ImmediateAction: Immobilize left hand wrist by applying fore arm splints & bandages. Given 1 tab of Ibuprofen 400mg orally every 6 hrs as per Int'l. Medical Guide pp49-50. Telemedical consultation carried-out with Dr. Balaji A.H. (Balaji Medical cetre).
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: as per medical report
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Incident will be shared with other vessels for awareness and prevention
LessionLearnt: Due care to be taken in confined spaces slippery spaces and hasty and careless movements to be avoided.
ClosingNote: Incident shared with other vessels as it is a common incident however it can result in a significant damage and injury to personnel.

IncidentDetails: Third engineer had a superficial cut on his forehead while overahuling of aux engine cylinder head.
IncidentEventDetails: Decarb of aux.engine was being carried out. The third engineer was trying to remove the fuel injector by loosening the injector tightening nuts . Third engineer was using  a "T" handle and box spanner for loosening the nuts. AS the nut was not loosening he used a pipe for additional leverage for opening . The pipe slipped from the " T" handle and hit the forehead of the third engineer making a superficial cut.
ImmediateAction: The third engineer was taken to the control room . The cut was  cleaned and first aid given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: superficial cut on the forehead
DirectCause: Improper use of tools
Proposedcorrectiveactions: all crew explained and advised to use proper tools  and not to use any pipes for additional leverage while opening or closing the nuts while using spanners.

IncidentDetails: While carrying out routine  hot work on a pipe inside ER workshop, the Welder  hand accidentaly touched the weld material. Though the welder was wearing welding gloves, it burnt the glove and had a burn mark on his hand. First aid was administered.
ImmediateAction: Welding machine was switched off. Hand washed in cold running water .First aid administered.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn mark on hand.
DirectCause: Offbalance while performing the hot work
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Properly position for task, Look  the area of surrounding and the job before commencing hot work

IncidentDetails: At 1430 Hrs LT, Ordinary Seaman called up on Bridge and stated that AB Meraj has had Injury (Blood coming out from Left Leg toe) while Hydroblasting on Bay-62 Lashing Platform and they are bringing the Casualty to Hospital. Master was informed immediately, Same time Master Relieved 2/OFF from bridge.   2/Off along with Ch/Off rushed to Hospital. It was observed that Left leg toe was covered with Cloth to stop the bleeding by one of the fellow crew.  Cloth was removed and wound inspected, Wound was properly cleaned with Disinfectant, followed by applying Pivodine-Iodine Tincture. Casualty was administered with 1 tab i-Brufen and Ammoxycillin 2 times a day for 3 Days.
IncidentEventDetails: AB meraj and AB Netaji were Hydroblasting on Bay-62 Lashing Platform. While turning around, AB Meraj unfortunately hit  his Left Leg Toe with Hydroblaster's Gun.
ImmediateAction: Casualty was immediately taken to Hospital. Wound was cleaned and  Disinfected with Antiseptic Solution followed by applying Pivodine Iodine Tincture.  Patient was administered with i-Brufen (500mg) 1 Tab, and Ammoxycillin Tab 2 times a day for 3 Days.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2nd Degree small cut on Left leg toe.
DirectCause: Equipment was not used with caution / Improper Positioning.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew was briefed about procedure how to safely use Hydroblaster. Proper PPE shall be worn at all times.  Crew briefed upon High Pressure injuries due to Mishandling the equipment.
Corrective Action: Idnetify the cause for the injury. Is it due to lack of PPE or due to improper handling. Discuss the same with all ship staff brief them for proper usage. You can plan to incorporate the same in vessel initial safety discussion for the newly joined crew member. Ensure always Proper PPE is don at all time while in operation.

IncidentDetails: Trainee Seaman while using stair case slipped and fell down causing a cut in his chin, Impact of the same resulted in swallon cheeks and blood clot below his eye
IncidentEventDetails: At 0000 Trainee seaman was going to his cabin from pantry after having a juice as he was feeling hungry , vessel was rolling heavily and he was using flip flops which lead to the slip   At 0005 Tarinee seaman sliped and fell down in the stair case had a cut in chin and was bleeding (minor).
ImmediateAction: Adminstrated first Aid.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Tr. seaman had a cut in his chin and swelling in his Cheek due to the slip
DirectCause: Use of flip flops during Heavy rolling and NO three point contact was followed while going up the staircase.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Regulartrainings to be imparted on such type of similiar incidents to avoid incidents of this nature

IncidentDetails: While taking out the needles of the chipping gun for overhauling, with the sudden release of the chipping gun from the wise, hit the crew on his right chest leaving a cut of approx 1cm length and 2 mm deep.
IncidentEventDetails: 1) While carrying out maintenace job on main deck, the chipping gun was not working properly.  2) Crew member tried to take out the needles for the overhauling of the chipping gun.  3) Sudden release of the chipping gun from the wise, hit him on the right chest leaving a cut mark.
ImmediateAction: First aid provided,  1) Cleaned the wound with dettol.  2) Providine ointment applied on the wound with cotton and pasted with the adhesive tape.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1cm long & 2mm deep cut on the right chest.
DirectCause: Unawareness of safety measures.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew briefed about the overhauling procedure of pneumatic chipping needle gun and the safety measures to be taken during the same.
ClosingNote: Briefing carried out.

IncidentDetails: While hammeing the welding piece the piece from beneath the face shield went inside causing the area around the eye to be burned.
IncidentEventDetails: Riding Crew was working in ER workshop.  While hammering welding pieceshield.The metal piece from beneath the face shiled entered  and caused the burn near the eye.At the time of hammering he was wearing the protective face shiled.  No damge to eye was suspected.
ImmediateAction: Eye washed with luke warm water. Vasline and Burnol Cream given for the application on the burnt area.Further advise taken from the shore.Attached is the prescripion provided by shore.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fitter Magubari Malleshwara Rao got slight skin burn near the eye while   hammering welding piece.At the time of hammering he was wearing the   protective face shield.The metal piece from beneath the face shiled entered   and caused the burn near the eye.
DirectCause: Crew was only wearing the face shield at the time of hammering the hot welded piece.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Should wear safety glasses while perfroming jobs like hammering and chipping.
ClosingNote: Vessel reported that the injury was minor and the crewmember resumed normal duties after First Aid. The incident was discussed in detail during next SCM with emphasis to wearing PPE. All crewmembers must wear PPE for every task as per PPE Matrix.

IncidentDetails: Minor burn injury to ETO's right hand wrist and ring finger due to arcing while carrying out starter routines.
IncidentEventDetails: While carrying out starter routines of No: 3 cargo oil pump, ETO accidently contacted incoming live wire with cleaning brush. Due to which an arc was generated and caused minor burns to to ETO's right hand ring finger and wrist.
ImmediateAction: ETO was brought to the hospital and first aid was provided .
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor Burn injury on ring finger and wrist on the right hand.
DirectCause: Lack of Knowledge & skill. Failure to comply with company's procedures. improper insolation of the breaker
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew will be made aware by the company's PTW system and company's procedures by conducting a training and will be motivated to comply with the same.
Corrective Action: All crew will be made aware by the company's PTW system and company's procedures by conducting a training and will be motivated to comply with the same.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: During morning cross deck lashing bridge light repairs ETO had a slip in one of the cross deck areas while coming down the ladder to main deck. Due to the same when he landed on the main deck his left leg ankle twisted and the leg bone came out of the ball socket.
IncidentEventDetails: Working on Ladders / Vessel motion / Wet surfaces due to reefer water discharges / Grease on railings & ladder steps due to lashing material / Grease on gloves as come in contact with the railings
ImmediateAction: He was brought on strecher in the cabin and was administered with morphin to reduce his pain so that we could relocate the dislodged bone back to the ball socket.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Dislodged left leg ankle ball socket / ligament tear / hair line fracture
DirectCause: Wet surface procedures not taken into consideration / Greasy surfaces precautions not considered / ladder climbing procedures not proper.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper brefing of all staff after the incident carried out.  Regular training for all the work safety procedures carried out.  Areas like railings and ladders to be kept free from oil, lubricaion material at all times.  Warning Posters can be made so that a person going up the cross deck is aware of the above.  Can be marked as slip trip hazard areas.
Corrective Action: The safety shoe design to be reviewed for ankle support.

IncidentDetails: On 0830Lt, 04 Jan 2020 ,D/C Zhao ZhiFei left foot sprain when he moved from stbd side deck to port side deck. On work site ,O/S Liu FuXian working together with D/C Zhao ZhiFei, D/C Zhao ZhiFei walked from stbd side to portside,he left foot step empty due to deck slippery ,the weather condition is small raining.  0835Lt,O/S Liu FuXian reported to Bosun Kong XiangFei,Bosun Kong XiangFei went  to working site and checked the injury condition of D/C Zhao ZhiFei,Bosun found D/C Zhao ZhiFei could walk by himself, but the left foot cound’t put in energy due to pain of the ankle.
ImmediateAction: 0840Lt Bosun with D/C went to ship office and report to Chief Officer.C/O called 2/O went to hospital and inspected the injury condition of D/C,   0845Lt 2/O used the “yun nan bai yao” spry to reduce the pain and report to Captain.  0900Lt Capt.discuss with TSI Mr.Wangbao at the routine shipyard meeting, prepare medical report form & send email to Synergy.  After received the reply, call & email to agent arrange for see a doctor.  1600LT agent onboard took the patient ashore accompany with 2/O, first examined at Changxing island hospital, x-ray display very slight fissure on navicular bone, suggest go to advanced hospital for further diacrisis.  1800Lt, x-ray examined again in shanghai No.7 hospital, slight fissure found left navicular bone, diagnosis prescription include: no fit for work.   2000Lt,patient back to vessel,keep rest in the bed & close observation.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 1600LT agent onboard took the patient ashore accompany with 2/O, first examined at Changxing island hospital, x-ray display very slight fissure on navicular bone, suggest go to advanced hospital for further diacrisis.  1800Lt, x-ray examined again in shanghai No.7 hospital, slight fissure found left navicular bone, diagnosis prescription include: no fit for work.   2000Lt,patient back to vessel,keep rest in the bed & close observation.
DirectCause: 1.new trineer seaman,lack of experience  2.deck slippery due to small rain  3.lack of safety awareness
Proposedcorrectiveactions: enhance safety awareness training & self protect

IncidentDetails: Able Seaman reported Pain in Left Thumb, and slight swelling in Left Thumb Knuckle when inspected.
IncidentEventDetails: On 15-Oct-2019 while departing London Gateway, Ships crew were casting off Aft Tugs Line. Tugs pulled Tugs Line quickly without ships crew signalling, Resulted in Tugs Gant line hitting Left Thumb of Able Seaman.
ImmediateAction: Informed Tugs through the Pilot not to PickUp the Tugs line without proper hand signal by Ships Crew.
DirectCause: Injury to crew, while handling Tugs Line
Proposedcorrectiveactions: AA. Departure BTM carried out with Shipscrew, Discussed potential hazards at Mooring station.  BB. Precaution while handling Tugs Line.
ClosingNote: Crew to be trained on safe use of Tug gant line in operation during mooring stations as per COSWP.

IncidentDetails: While working on Hydraulic line to stop a leakage on it, Deck Riding Fitter Mr. Anil Kumar suffered a Small Facial burn due to spillage of hot hydraulic oil.
IncidentEventDetails: A small Leakage was observed on the Inlet Hydraulic line of Stbd Mooring Winch. Fitter was assigned to do the Job. As Fitter was cutting the Hydraulic line, due to the pressure in the line there was a spillage of hydraulic oil on him.
ImmediateAction: He was shifted to Ship's hospital. Washed the burnt area with cold water. Put Ice Pack On the Area. Asked Company's doctor for medical Advice.
DirectCause: NIL
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL
Corrective Action: Prior planning works on hydraulic lines, hazard identification in way of Risk Assessment to be carried out, PTW to be strictly followed.  A responsible officer should always be present when such jobs are being undertaken. All PPE should be throroughly checked by the safey officer prior put into use. Office should be informed about the job with scope of work and RA. This injury report should be discussed with all staff during the next meeting.
ClosingNote: CA/PA as mentioned should be strictly adhered to

IncidentDetails: While Arranging chemicals finger got injured .
IncidentEventDetails: While arranging drums in chemical locker finger came beneath the drum causing the blood clot at the finger.
ImmediateAction: Cold compression and pain relief spray given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Finger Got Injured while arranging Drums.
DirectCause: Proper PPE was not worn
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To wear propre hand glooves while arranging the chemical drums.

IncidentDetails: While opening the cabin toilet door with left hand,person got his wrist twisted abnormally. That lead to development of swelling at the back of the wrist and pain on wrist movement.
IncidentEventDetails: Engine cadet returned from his 2000 - 2400 hrs watch to his cabin which was shared with deck cadet. Cabin was in dark condition as lightings were off to comfort other cadet who was sleeping at that moment.   With the intentions to avoid disturbance by lighting up the cabin, engine cadet chose to use his torch light for illuminations. He held the torch in right hand and tried to operate the toilet door handle with left hand.   Being a right handed personal, choosing to operate the door handle (that operates clockwise) with left hand was a unnatural holding position. So in that condition when he tried to push the handle while rotating the handle for opening the door, his wrist got twisted resulting in an injury
ImmediateAction: At the time of injury, he did not felt much pain and he went for rest. Next day afternoon when swelling surfaced and he felt pain on his left hand while twisting, he reported to medical officer.   Deep heat rub along with bandage was given to relax the muscles.   When pain did not subside after a day, vessel decided to send him to shore doctor for further checkup.   On his doctor visit  at next available opportunity, doctor diagnosed tearing of mascular cartridge of his left hand wrist. He was given special bandage to support and restrict the wrist movement along with other muscle relaxant medicine and ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Tearing of muscle cartridge of left hand wrist. That lead to swelling of hand and pain in the wrist
DirectCause: Improperhandling of equipments. Door handle was not held correctly and force was applied in that manner that resulted in sprain on left hand wrist
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Briefing to all ship staff on correct handling of equipments either during work or in leisure.
ClosingNote: Briefing to all the staff of correct handling of equipment carried out.

IncidentDetails: On departure of the vessel from berth after mooring stations the forward mooring team was trying to disengage the winch gear as this was tight. One seaman was using a crowbar for leverage to remove the gear. The crowbar slipped and recoiled which subsequently struck the seaman on his left forearm
IncidentEventDetails: On departure of the vessel from berth after mooring stations the forward mooring team was trying to disengage the winch gear as this was tight. One seaman was using a crowbar for leverage to remove the gear. The crowbar slipped and recoiled which subsequently struck the seaman on his left forearm
ImmediateAction: The seaman was offered first aid as his arm was paining due to impact.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The seaman's left forearm was paining. There was no swelling observed. He was offered first aid and painkillers. Dr Balaji was contacted fr further medical advise.
DirectCause: Tight winch gear coupled with loss of situational awareness between team members carrying out the job for disengaging the gear
Proposedcorrectiveactions: A meeting to be held with all crew discussing the incident and the imporatance of situational awareness while carrying out the job. The importance of proper coordination between team members and sirtuational awareness to be emphasised. All personnel to be enpowered with Stop work authority if they feel something is not right or need clarification.
Corrective Action: Master carried out briefing session with all crew discussing the incident and the importance of situational awareness while carrying out the job. The importance of proper coordination between team members and situational awareness emphasised. All personnel to be empowered with Stop work authority if they feel something is not right or need clarification
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
LessionLearnt: Ineffective signalling during the job can lead to accident. Situational awareness to be practiced by all everytime.
ClosingNote: AB has been treated for the fracture in his palm. Situational awareness to be practised by all shipboard staff at all the times.

IncidentDetails: RIGHT THUMP WOUDED WHILE HE IS WORKING/REPARING LIGHT VALVE.
IncidentEventDetails: WORKING NOT WEARING SAFE CLOVE FOR PROTECTION.
ImmediateAction: APPLY FIRST AID, CLEANING THE WOUND BY USING IODINE COTTON AND BANDAGE, MINOR/SMALL WOUND ONLY AND NO SPECIAL ATTENTION NEEDED.

DirectCause: NEGLIGENCE AND NOT USING SAFETY GLOVE.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: ALL VESSEL STAFF MUST WARING A PROPER PPE.
Corrective Action: ALL VESSEL STAFF MUST WARING A PROPER PPE.
ClosingNote: This incident to be discussed in next meeting and highlight the importance of PPE.

IncidentDetails: On 25th February at 0905H LT, Vessel alongside Hoping, Taiwan Berth #S-2 while loading cement clinker, AB Eric John J. Barreto dismantled the wooden pallets at Bosun store for housekeeping but he accidentally stepped on the dismantled wooded pallets with two nails and penetrated on his safey shoes right foot.
IncidentEventDetails: 25th February 2020  0905H LT - AB Barreto accidentally stepped-on the dsmantled wooden pallets at the Bosun store  0912H LT - Reported to duty Officer & Chief Officer  0915H LT - Applied first aid (washing/cleaning & application of iodine solution)  1205H LT - Seek medical adviced to Super Care Manila Clinic thru Synergy Group Manila Office  1717H LT - Received medical adviced from Super Care Clinic thru Synergy Group Manila  1800H LT - Gave medicine & administered with tetanus vaccine to AB Barreto as per adviced.
ImmediateAction: Reported to the Duty Officer &  Chief Officer.  Immediately washed the wound with soap & water  and cleaned with povidone iodine solution and asked for medical advised from the affiliated clinic of the company(Supercare Medical Services,Inc) thru Synergy Group Manila. As per advised received from SuperCare Clinic (Dra.Flonnah Mae L. Sandoval,M.D.) to give medicine of amoxicillin 500mg tablets for 3 times a day for 7 days and Ibupropen 400mg tables every 6 hours and administered with tetanus vaccine as prophylaxis and to have him consult a Medical Doctor on the next port to ensure adequate injection of tetanus vaccine and check the wounded area.
Corrective Action: Incident to be discussed with all crew emphasizing on the importance of work planning and good housekeeping

IncidentDetails: At 1800 hrs lt on 25.03.2020, the Additional Third Officer was trying to open a sealed box containing wall wash items using a blade cutter.  Due to sudden shudder movement of the vessel, the blade cutter slipped and sliced open the skin at the bottom of the thumb.
ImmediateAction: Immediately raised his hand above his head to reduce the loss of blood,Applied mild pressure to control the blood flow.  The wound was thoroughly cleaned with surgical alcohol and disinfected the wounded area , then applied Providone iodine solution and covered the wound with clean cotton and sterile guaze.  Report was immediately sent to Office & company doctor Dr Balaji.Recieved medical advise and same was followed as suggested.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: At 1800 hrs lt on 25.03.2020, the Additional Third Officer ANIRUDDHA SUBHRO was trying to open a sealed box containing wall wash items using a blade.Due to sudden shudder movement of the vessel, the blade slipped and sliced open the skin at the bottom of the thumb.We immediately raised his hand above his head to reduce the loss of blood, cleaned the wound with surgical alcohol solution and then applied Providone iodine solution .   The loss of blood  was restricted and there are no signs of dizziness experienced by the patient.
DirectCause: Unpredicted Vessel Shudder Movement and incorrect use of the blade to open the box.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As safety is paramount onboard as long as possible try to avoid such minor close cutting jobs during vessel's pitch roll period. Proper correct procedures to be followed while using a blade keeping in mind adequate distance if the blade slips.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery

IncidentDetails: Second officer while carrying out his duties at aft mooring stations, twisted his right foot.
IncidentEventDetails: On 29 Feb 2020 about 1700LT, While working at Aft station during arrival Algeciras, 2/O Paresh Tandel, twisted his right foot .  When he was passing between the bollard to check on the mooring lines, his right foot slipped and twisted and the body weight came on the twisted foot. He experienced pain and could feel discomfort on moving his foot.
ImmediateAction: He was immediately attended to and the foot area was examined.  Pain relief spray applied along with  bandage. Further company doctor consulted and as per his advice 2/O was sent to doctor for review ashore and returned.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: There was visible swelling on the Right foot and he experienced pain with the movement of the foot.
DirectCause: He was passing through the bollards and placed the foot on the outer corner which caused his foot to slip and twist. His body weight came on the twisted foot causing the pain.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Avoid passing between the bollards which can cause tripping or slipping. Avoid placing foot or landing on curved edges. Foot should be placed on flat surfaces keeping in mind the hazards.

IncidentDetails: On 25-Feb-2020 at 2300 Hrs LT, Boiled hot water from Kettle fell over Master's Elbow and Right Knee due to Unexpected and Violent Rolling of Vessel caused due to Rough Weather.  Kettle was placed over Anti-Skit Mat as Usual, and Master was sitting on Sofa.
IncidentEventDetails: 1. Kettle was placed over Anti- Skit Mat next to sofa.  2. Master was sitting on the sofa  3. Vessel was Experiencing Rough Weather throughout the Passage.  4. Sudden and Unexpected Rolling caused the kettle to topple.  5. Whole of Boiled Water Fell Over Master's Elbow and Right Knee causing burning spots over the area.  6. Immediately First Aid was Provided, Company informed and Consulted with Balaji Medicare.
ImmediateAction: Immediately Provided with First Aid. AND Medical help requested through company medical doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 25-Feb-2020 at 2300 Hrs LT, Boiled hot water from Kettle fell over Master's Elbow and Right Knee due to Unexpected and Violent Rolling of Vessel caused due to Rough Weather.  Kettle was placed over Anti-Skit Mat as Usual, and Master was sitting on Sofa.  Burn over the whole area of Knee and elbow.
DirectCause: Vessel was Experiencing Rough Weather and was Rolling Violently, this caused the Kettle to slip off from Anti-Skit Mat also.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Even though Anti-Skit Mats Provides great Friction and Avoids materials from Falling/ Skidding, Proposed plan to secure the Itmes with Clips available. Notice also put near Kettle to unplugg the cable if not using.
Corrective Action: Even though Anti-Skit Mats Provides great Friction and Avoids materials from Falling/ Skidding, Proposed plan to secure the Itmes with Clips available. Notice also put near Kettle to unplugg the cable if not using.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.

IncidentDetails: Pumpman suffered heat stroke while working on deck. His leg and hand muscle becomes very stiff. He was not able to move his hand and leg. Finger movements of his hand and legs was also not possible.
IncidentEventDetails: On 05th march 2020/0542LT:-Vessel completed discharge.0912LT:-Vessel commenced de-fendering.  0930LT:--The health issue observed. The pump man reported discomfort. He had been advised to take rest. The deck fitter has been assigned to monitor him. After 5 minutes, the deck fitter reported the deterioration of his health condition.
ImmediateAction: Master was informed. Immediately attended by chief officer and 2nd officer. He had been wrenched completely with normal water and subsequently by cold water. Salt tablet/ORS solution/Orange juice etc. has been given. Body parameter measured including blood pressure. All found normal. In half an hour, he has been feeling well. He was completely back to normal in 2 hours.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First aid case.
DirectCause: Severe heat.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Tool box meeting carried out.Frequent break advised while working.Intake of salt tablet reccomended to all crew.Adequent intake of water advised.Full body to be covered.Any uneasiness to be reported and immediately to be moved under shelter.Working alone to be discouraged.
Corrective Action: Tool box meeting carried out.Frequent break advised while working.Intake of salt tablet reccomended to all crew.Adequent intake of water advised.Full body to be covered.Any uneasiness to be reported and immediately to be moved under shelter.Working alone to be discouraged.
ClosingNote: Closed satisfactorily.
Other Part: Affected full body but the effect on hand and leg muscles are more.

IncidentDetails: While Main air-compressor no.1 running, abnormal stop and alarm activated at 1819h Lt . Fourth Engineer  Salvador Catchero Jr went down to investigate the cause of problem.   During his inspection he found that there was no visible water in the sight glass. He isolated the main air compressor no.1 from cooling water system by closing both inlet and outlet valve. After closing those valve mentioned he drained water from attached  pump of on main air-compressor.   Investigating  further he decided to open the plug fitted on the outlet side of cooling system to vent the line and  during removing the plug suddenly the  cooling water remaining in the system flash out that causes burnt on his left hand. During the incident he is wearing  cotton gloves.
IncidentEventDetails: Re-checking of Air Compressor, Abnormal Stop and alarm acitivated. Unluckily Hot water flush out from the compressor that causes 4e left hand to burnt.
ImmediateAction: First Aid was Given, informed  master, medical officer, and Office /SGOI manila regarding the Incident
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn Left Hand , Unfit to work require  rest and medication for 5-7 days as per Doctor advised.
DirectCause: Abnormal Alarmed found in air compressor.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper PPE must be worn/Leather gloves to to used.
Corrective Action: Training to be carried out for all Engine staff on Lock-out tag-out process and repair plan for pressure system/ critical machinery discussed and carried out only after risk assessment.

IncidentDetails: Door Opening to Alleyway touched one of the crew member in his arm while passing nearby the door.
IncidentEventDetails: While one of the crew member was passing from the alleyway, other crew member opened the door from the ship office inside and door touched with the crew member's arm
ImmediateAction: Crew was advised to open the door slowly and pass clear from the door opening range
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: None
DirectCause: 1) Door opened with jerk  2) Door opening range was not marked on the floor
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1) Crew briefed that the door to be opened slowly  2) Door opening range to be marked on the floor

IncidentDetails: Dust particle from Main Engine indicator valves accidentally flew into the left eye of Oiler John Christopher G. Regoso during blow off.
IncidentEventDetails: January 14, 2020, 1045H LT at Taboneo Anchorage, dridge duty officer called Engine Room to give One(1) hour notice for engine preparations prior vessel's departure. After complying departure checklist's one(1) hour notice preparations and main engine was ready for blow-off, duty Oiler John Christopher G. Regoso was tasked to check if indicator valves are open as well as closing it after blowing off. He was wearing protective eye wear that time and was about 4-5 meters away from main engine cylinder heads but a carbon dust still managed to accidentally fly into his left eye. He then went to Engine Control room to inform about the incident and had his left eye visually checked. Eye irrigation by emergency sterile eye wash was immediately administered into his left eye as first aid.
ImmediateAction: Immediately administered Eye Irrigation using Emergency Sterile eye wash as first aid to remove foreign particle from his left eye.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Dust particle from Main Engine indicator valves accidentally flew into the left eye of Oiler John Christopher G. Regoso during blow off.
DirectCause: Direct cause : Dust particle from Main Engine indicator valves.    Indirect cause : Distance of assigned crew from Main Engine indicator valves during blow off.    Root cause : Dust particle from Main Engine indicator valves accidentally flew into his left eye during blow off.

IncidentDetails: Gland removal tool pierced through safety shoe and punctured fitter's right foot , when he accidentaly stepped through it while working.
IncidentEventDetails: While woking on fire and GS pump, changing the gland packing. fitter accidentally stepped on the gland removal tool while changing his position of work. The tool piereced through his safety shoe and punctured his right foot.
ImmediateAction: First aid was given. Wound cleaned, Antiseptic applied and and send to medical officer for further advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Tool has pierced to approx 5mm in his right foot, this caused immediate bleeding followed by swelling and pain the next day.
DirectCause: Improper design of gland removal tool.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Alteration to the gland removal tool so that the piercing end is never at 90 degrees from the floorby default.
Corrective Action: Alteration to the gland removal tool so that the piercing end is never at 90 degrees from the floorby default.

IncidentDetails: Vessels engineers had undertaken a maintenance task of examining the ME crankcase after vessel was anchored at Singapore at about 1600LT on the 6th of July 2019. This was in response to aan alarm that had been recorded when the vessel was under manoeuvring. The Chief engineer was intimated and proper precautions and PPE was used prior to entry in accordance with all requirements of the company SMS (HSM) manual. At about 1630 LT the 2nd Engr had a fall inside the crankcase while doing his job and was injured. After being taken out of the crankcase,2nd Engr reported that he was experiencing difficulty in breathing, and office was immediately informed and 2nd Engr shifted to hospital by an emergency boat.   The height of fall inside the crankcase was about 1.5 meters and he was unable to move initially. However as he was inside the crankcase, the 2nd engineer came out on his own after a time interval, without any lifting device, but with the help of other crew members. Once outside, the details of the incident including the preliminary assessment of the injuries were immediately communicated to the office and boat was arranged for transfer of the 2nd engineer to shore hospital at Singapore for further treatment.
IncidentEventDetails: Refer attached IR
ImmediateAction: Refer attached IR
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Refer attached IR
DirectCause: Refer attached IR
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Incident shared with all vessels
LessionLearnt: Safety harness to be used in all task done at height
ReviewComments: None.
ClosingNote: Personnel fiuly recovered after treatment at home

IncidentDetails: When lowering new M/E exhaust trunk bellows with shore fitters into the Engine room, 3/Eng was assisting with them. One of the shore fitter without any communication/signal pulled the bellow towards his side which caused 3/Eng left hand index finger to get trapped between bellow flange and the frame. This caused deep cut in the finger.
ImmediateAction: First aid given on board. Immediately sent to hospital for treatment where they found his left hand index finger nerve got cut. So he undergone surgery.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: When lowering new M/E exhaust trunk bellows with shore fitters into the Engine room, 3/Eng was assisting with them. One of the shore fitter without any communication/signal pulled the bellow towards his side which caused 3/Eng left hand index finger to get trapped between bellow flange and the frame. This caused deep cut in the finger.
DirectCause: Safe working practices not followed by people involved in the job.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Proper communication to be maintained at all times during picking up/lowering heavy object.
ClosingNote: Upon 2nd medical consultation, 3rd Engr was declared fit and continuing on board.

IncidentDetails: : Vessel was at sea on the 4th of November when the seaman tripped and fractured his left ankle on deck. The OS was, at the time of the incident working on deck and had all PPE as required for ther job, including safety shoes. The seam was given an ice pack and pain killers as the ankle had swollen and the seafarer was unable don his shoes for the purpose of working. Office was informed and medication was continued until arrival and berthing at next port of Subic Bay Phillippines. At Philippines , the OS was examined by a doctor, the injury X rayed and the seaman was advised  8 weeks of bed rest and was not certified as fit to work. In consultation with office the seaman was signed off at Subic Bay.   Injured person was 25 year old trainee seaman , approximately midway through his contract, having signed on in 22.08.2019. While the documents reveal that the doc5tor had not recommended any period of rest in his report in writing, Master informed office that as per his (Master’s) conversation with the doctor attending t the seaman, a month’s rest was required. The Master therefore was of the view that the sea man should be repatriated. Office superintendent concurred with him and the sea farer was signed off on 14th November and repatriated to his home town.
IncidentEventDetails: Normal routine deck maintenance work at sea
ImmediateAction: First aid , ice pack and painkiller administered
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: ANKLE(LEFT) FRACTURE
DirectCause: The seaman was not attentive to the hazards of working on deck. As it was his first vessel with the company, inexperience coupled with lack of focus led to the incident
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Sharing with fleet vessels
ClosingNote: Lack of risk assessment and situational awareness - Slip, trip and fall.

IncidentDetails: Injury to Both wrist and right hand thumb of Gas Engineer while working on Cargo system Solenoid Box (Manifold V/V timing adjustment)
IncidentEventDetails: Pre-Arrival Checks were being carried out.  Manifold V/V timing was to be adjusted.  G/E opened the Solenoid Box near Port Manifold.  The securing pin of the Solenoid box was not locked.  G/E started adjusting the flow controller to adjust the timing of the Manifold V/V.  Due to Weather BF scale 6 and moderate swell, vessel was rolling slightly.  At this time, Box cover fell baack to closed position, resulting in injury to G/E both the wrist and right hand thumb.
ImmediateAction: Bosun and OS working in the vicinity and rushed to the spot.  G/E was escorted to CCR.   First aid was administered.  Radio medical advice seeked from Company Doctor.(Balaji Medicals)
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Slight Pain/swelling on both forearms and wrist area.  Heavy Pain and swelling around right hand thumb.    Injured area is not blue.  No fever, BP & Pulse Normal.  Refer attached photos    DOCTO DIAGNOSIS  Contusion on Right hand, light work suggested until recovery (Report attached)
DirectCause: Lack of Supervision.  Improper reporting.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Onsite Hazard analysis to be carried out.  Mitigating steps to be considered.  External condition like weathershall be considered.
Corrective Action: Carry out an RA for working on Solenoid Box

IncidentDetails: During tightening flanges, the socket of the pneumatic impact wrench broke and hit on the right shoulder of one of the crew member engaged in the job.
IncidentEventDetails: Two crew members were engaged in tightening flanges in pumproom using pneumatic wrench. One of the crew member was holding the nut using the spanner while the other was operating the pneumatic wrench. During the process, the socket broke into two parts & hit on the right shoulder of the 2nd person who was holding the nut, causing slight swelling & bruises on the right shoulder
ImmediateAction: Work stopped and informed the D/O. The crew member was brought to bridge, master carried out inspection & slight brusies & swelling noted. Relief spray applied & rest given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: During tightening flanges, the socket of the pneumatic impact wrench broke and hit on the right shoulder of one of the crew member engaged in the job.
DirectCause: Thorough inspection of the tools was carried out prior commencing the job and found to be in order. Suspect material failure leading to its break down.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: As a good practice tools to be inspected prior commencing any job. While carrying out such job as far as practicable in the particular location to be well clear. As strength of the tools cannot be ascertained with physical inspection (as in the present case) regularly used tools should have a retiring criteria to avoid such material failures.

IncidentDetails: While the crew member was working in Eingine Room,a spanner fell on his right side of the forehead, from a platform slightly above from where was working.
IncidentEventDetails: Crew member was working in E/R.While woking spanner fell on his right side of the fore head. A minor cut was caused on the forehead.No profuse bleeding occurred.First Aid administered and seek shore medical advice.
ImmediateAction: Crew member was administered First Aid.Vessel seek medical advice from shore medical team.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut on forehead. No profused bleeding.
DirectCause: Crew member not aware of the dangers of falling equipments. Not using proper PPE (Helmet) while at work. PPE matrix not follwed.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Crew members to made aware of the injuries associated with slip. trip & falls and objects falling from above when working in engine room. All ship staff re-trained to always use proper PPE as applicable to job at Hand. PPE matrix re-explained to all. Training imparted to use Helmet with chin strap at all times whilel at work in E/room or Deck.
LessionLearnt: PPE matrix to be diligently complied with, besides carrying-out a detailed RA.
ClosingNote: RA / Daily Work Planner to be diligently carrying-out prior executing any job. Senior Officers should not loose the situational awareness and monitor the jobs closely.

IncidentDetails: Finger hurt as it came between the door and the door frame while closing the door.
ImmediateAction: Swelling on the index finger. No severe injury or cuts. First aid given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor swelling on the index finger.
DirectCause: The door opening knob was found very close to the edge of the door and finger should have been clenced while operating .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Warning label posted on the door knob.

IncidentDetails: The chief cook got slight burn and scald on his two right hand finger while attempting to extinguish the fire on Oil(Ghee)
IncidentEventDetails: In attempt to warm up and melt the frozen oil(Ghee) by putting the oil can on hot plate,the oil can slipped from his hand and felt on the hot plate.Susequently the oil got fire.To extinguish the fire,the chief cook used the nearby fire blanket and in this process,he got the burn and scald on his two right hand finger.
ImmediateAction: Fire was extinguished and first aid given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First aid case.
