Proposedcorrectiveactions: Specific training to be provided to the C/Cook regarding the correct procedures to be followed. The procedure he followed for melting the ghee was incorrect and unsafe.
Corrective Action: Specific training to be provided to the C/Cook regarding the correct procedures to be followed. The procedure he followed for melting the ghee was incorrect and unsafe.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations
ClosingNote: Cause rightly identified and addressed.

IncidentDetails: On 25.03.2020 at 1830 LT while picking up portable tank cleaning machine and hose from 2S COT , surrounding deck was slightly wet and the Ch Officer slipped and twisted his wrist as the weight of the tank cleaning hose and machine was transferred to his hand .
ImmediateAction: Applied pain relief spray and covered the area with Crepe bandage.Rest provided and isolated the hand in order to prevent further pain.  Seeked medical advise from company doctor Dr Balaji and follwing instructions as advised by the doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: On 25.03.2020 at 1830 LT while picking up portable tank cleaning machine and hose from 2S COT , surrounding deck was slightly wet and the Ch Officer slipped and twisted his wrist as the weight of the tank cleaning hose and machine was transferred to his hand .
DirectCause: while picking up portable tank cleaning machine and hose from 2S COT , surrounding deck was slightly wet and the Ch Officer slipped and twisted his wrist as the weight of the tank cleaning hose and machine was transferred to his hand .
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Any such lifting shall not be carried out by single person especially whilst at sea , tank cleaning in progress where deck can be wet or slippery.Weight shall be lifted by trnserring the weight to a strong point using a good condition rope so that at any point the weight can be hold by the rope.

IncidentDetails: C/O Mr Saurabh Bajaj fell down on deck from a height around 0.5 mtrs and hit his back on left side after anchoring at OPL Singapore on 05th Mar/1610 Hrs LT
IncidentEventDetails: 05th Mar  1542 Hrs: Vessel anchored at OPL Singapore asper Charterers instrution.  1605 Hrs: Ch-off, AB (Magash) & TrOS (Ankit) were returning from Forward to aft after anchoring.  1610 Hrs: Ch-off was about to climb steps on forward to access Catwalk.  1610 Hrs: As soon as he was on 3rd step, he lost his control and fell on deck resulting hitting his lower part of back on the windlass grating edge support bracket.  1610 Hrs: Immediately Bridge was alerted by AB (Magash) that Choff is fell on deck and unconscious.  1615 Hrs: Medical team reached to the spot with stretcher to bring injured Choff to hospital.  1630 Hrs: Choff was brought to Hospital and given first aid. He was observed dehydrated.  1635 Hrs: He was given juice and salt water and he got consciousness but in pain. No swelling observed but he was not able to move because of extreme pain.   1900 Hrs: He was shifted to his cabin and advised to take rest.
ImmediateAction: He was shifted immediately to hospital on stretcher and given salt water & first aid.
DirectCause: Due to dehydration Choff fell on deck after anchoring.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL

IncidentDetails: AFTER CLEANING BILGE HOLDING TANK,OILER  WAS COMING OUT OF THE TANK,HE TRIPPED & INJURED ONE OF THE TEEH ON THE MAN HOLE ENTRANCE
IncidentEventDetails: 1.CLEANING & INSPECTION  OF BILGE HOLDING TANK (PMS JOB CODE 1618) WAS CARRIED OUT     2.CROSS VENTILLATION OF THE TANK THROUGH MANHOLE DOOR FROM MORINING TO MAKE SPACE SUITABLE FOR MAN ENTRY                                                                                                        3.BILGE HOLDING TANK WAS PUMP OUT THROUGH OWS ,TANK WAS EMPTY AT 1530 HRS.                4.ATMOSPHERE CHECKED AT 1500 HRS & 1530 HRS AND MAN ENTRY WAS MADE FOR  INSPECTION & CLEANING.                                                                                                                                          5.FIRST 3 SECTIONSWERE DECIDED TO CLEAN WITH MOP AND BRUSH TO FACILITATE ENTRY INTO OTHER SECTIONS.                                                                                                                        6.CLEANING WAS CARRIED OUT FOR ONE HOURS AND DECIDED TO CONTINUE NEXT DAY   7.OILER  WAS COMING OUT FROM 3RD SECTION TO 2ND SECTION & TRIPPED FELL DOWN ,INJURING ONE OF THE TEETH HITTING THE ACCESS HOLE.
ImmediateAction: OILER  WAS BROUGHT TO ECR ,SECOND OFFICER AND MASTER INFORMED. FIRST AIDE GIVEN
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: WHILE CLEANING BILGE HOLDING TANK ,OILER TRIPPED AND INJURED THE TEETH FALLING ON THE ACCESS HOLE .ONE OF THE TEETH CRACKED ON THE FRONT TOP ROW.
DirectCause: CREW HAS TO BE CAUTIOUS WHILE PASSING THROUGH ACCESS HOLES INSIDE THE TANKS,SHOULD CHECK THE CLEARANCE BETWEEN THE FLOOR & ACCESS HOLE HIEGHT
Proposedcorrectiveactions: CREW BRIEFED ABOUT THE UNDERLYING HAZARD AND SAME TO BE INCLUDED IN FUTURE RISK ASSESMENT

All this because (root cause) of rolling he got slipped the height was just 1 meter from the floor.

IncidentDetails: WHILE WORKING ON  NO 2 A/E FUEL OIL HEATER, AT THE TIME OF INSERTING THE HEATER STEAM TUBE STACK INSIDE THE SHELL, ONE OF THE CREW MEMBERS LEFT HAND FINGERS GOT STUCK BETWEEN THE  TUBE COVER (STEEL) AND ANGLE BAR(STEEL)  LOCATED NEAR THE SHELL. ON OBSERVATION FOUND CREW MEMBERS MIDDLE  & RING FINGER HAD BEEN INJURED WITH A SLIGHT CUT AND BLEEDING ON BOTH HIS FINGERS.
ImmediateAction: HE HAS BEEN BROUGHT TO ENGINE CONTROL ROOM IMMEDIATELY AND CLEANED THE INJURED AREA AND WASHED HIS HAND WITH RUNNING COLD WATER FOR 2 MINS. APPLIED ERITHROMYCIN OINTMENT ON THE INJURY AND COVERED WITH BANDAGE ROLL.

IncidentEventDetails: At around 0800H LT, Carried-out lashing of scaffolding in Cargo Hold No.5 Aft Part.           The crew member had donned all the appropriate PPE required for the job, including safety harness for             working aloft. Buson Calabia and Ch. Off Adem are the team for securing the Scaffolding at CH no. 5.             AB Abad tried to to secure the last securing line on the right side of the scaffolding.           While securing, the scaffoding bends to Stbdside while AB Abad was near on top of scaffolding with about           approximate height of 12 mtrs, subsequently all lines are parted, and the scaffolding got  collapsed towards           the hopper Stbdside, with AB Abad was near on top of scaffolding about 12 mtrs in height.                    At 0915H LT, AB Abad was reported badly injured due to impact, together with the collapsed scaffolding.           The Master and the Medical Team arrived at the scene and asses the injured person.            The crew member suffered bruises on the right side of his rib cage area . IncidentDetails: Fitter was doing gas cutting in the Engine Room workshop.   At that time the Acetylene hose, at the end connection to the torch ruptured, as it was old.   The gas emitting from the loose hose caught flame which scalded the fore arm of Fitter.   He suffered first degree burn on the back side of his left fore arm.
IncidentEventDetails: Fitter was doing gas cutting in the Engine Room workshop.   At that time the Acetylene hose, at the end connection to the torch ruptured, as it was old.   The gas emitting from the loose hose caught flame which scalded the fore arm of Fitter.
ImmediateAction: The gas supply to the hose was shut off from the control valve cabinet.   Poured cold water on the burn for atleast 10 min and applied burn ointment.  He was also given painkiller ibuprofen 400 mg tab. and advised to take rest.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fitter suffered first degree burn on the back side of his left fore arm.
DirectCause: The condition of acetylene hose was not good at the gas torch end and the welding gloves were too short to prevent hands from burns.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All old hoses with apparent sign of cracks / wear & tear to be visually inspected and renewed if necessary.   If only end section of hose is not in good condition, that section to be cropped prior use.  Proper(long) gloves to be indented.
Other Part: Left hand fore arm - First degrre burn on back side

IncidentDetails: While attempting to turn a full 200ltr oil drum on a trolley, AB Pradeep got the end of his finger trapped between the drum and trolley, thus suffering a dismembering of his right hand middle finger from near the nail root. The flesh, nail and body mass above the bone end were dismembered. The dismembered end of the finger was found inside his glove.  A more vicious injury to his hand was avoided due to the proper PPE (leather gloves on his hand) being worn by the crew member – there has been no visible damage to the bone of the finger.
IncidentEventDetails: Job: Changing the Crankcase oil for #2 RU being carried out.  As the crankcase oil is hygroscopic, the full drum of 200l was being shifted to the compressor room from the bosun store, situated aft on board, to avoid the oil going off-spec.  It was decided to transport the drum to the compressor room using the deck trolley as it is very heavy. The drum was safely shifted out of the Bosun store on to the deck, however it was placed upside down.  As there is very little space near the entrance door of the store, the oil drum was shifted to a slightly more spacious location near the companion way on the upper deck. The Trolley was placed against the side bulwark to prevent motion on one axis. Cadet Suhavaneesh and Cadet Antony BRITTO were holding one end of the trolley to prevent it from capsizing.  In attempting to turn the drum on the side, Gas Engineer Albert was lifting the drum from the bottom end and AB Pradeep was holding the drum on the top end to prevent a sudden motion of the drum. During this transfer, once the weight of the drum came on one edge of the trolley the other edge of the trolley whiplashed upwards uncontrollably, trapping the finger of AB Pradeep.
ImmediateAction: Chief Officer was monitoring on the radio and was immediately called to the location. AB Pradeep was shifted to the Hospital. Pressure was applied constantly to the right hand wrist of the patient to avoid major blood loss. The end of his hand was placed in an ice bath. The dismembered end of the finger was found inside the glove and also placed in ice and kept in Meat room.  Once the blood flow eased slightly, Iodine solution was applied and the end of the finger was wrapped up using Gauze bandage.  Medical advice was sought from the company doctor.
DirectCause: Improper method used for putting drum on the trolley. Movement of the trolley under weight of the drum was not taken into account. Insufficient man-power for the task. Senior officers not informed when shifting of drum was done.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: While shifting of any heavy object on board, senior officers need to be present at the scene to avoid any repeat of such incident. Proper planning and RA to be carried out for similar tasks.
ClosingNote: detailed report regenerated

IncidentDetails: While proceeding to E/Rm from the cabin after lunch, 2/E twisted his ankle on the stairway. He saw 2/O for some first aid (pain relief spray and Ibuprofen tabs). There was slight relief after using the spray and tablets and 2/E was doing light duites for 2 days. A few days later, 2/E reported that pain spreading to the foward portion of the foot and increasing. As vessel was in port, he was sent to see a doctor. He was diagnosed with a fracture and advised to be repatriated.
IncidentEventDetails: 03 Feb/ 1330 hrs: 2/E twiested ankle on the stairway. First aid administered and placed on light duites for 2 days.  06 Feb 2020: 2/E resumed normal duites with slight to moderate pain in the area.  12 Feb 2020: Pain statred spreading to the front of the foot.  14 Feb 2020: Doctor visit organized and 2.E diagnosed with dracture.  14 feb 2020: 2/E repatriated back home.
ImmediateAction: He felt moderate to severe pain for a few minutes but continued with work. He later saw 2/O and obtained pain relief spray for first aid and IbuProfen tablets for pain relief. Slight swelling was noted on the area, but no fracture was suspected.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Fracture on left foot base. Area 5 MTT. See atached medical report.
DirectCause: COMPLACENCY, IMPROPER USE OF STAIRS.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Discuss injury with crew members. Advise crew members to be careful while ascending/ descending stairs. AMintain proper rate of travel. USe proper footwear with heel strap and travel at proper pace.

While they were  trying to shift the liner pad to main deck the liner pad swung abruptly on bosun side towards the gear engaging lever and  hit on the edge of the supporting plate which caused cut on boson’s middle finger.

Correct lifting techniques to be adopted for heavy lift handling.   DD. Be aware of sorroundings and probable obstructions.

IncidentDetails: On 10-Apr-2020 at 0900 LT(0100 UTC) the Chief Engineer Mr. Ganesh Prasad Thyagarajan reported to the Master, that he was experiencing a persistent chest pain for past three days. The pain was on the left side of chest premating to left shoulder till left bicep, only to be reduced by paracetamol.
IncidentEventDetails: On 10-Apr-2020 at 0900 LT(0100 UTC) the Chief Engineer Mr. Ganesh Prasad Thyagarajan reported to the Master, that he was experiencing a persistent chest pain for past three days. The pain was on the left side of chest premating to left shoulder till left bicep, only to be reduced by paracetamol.   On consulting his medical history it was observed that he was having history of diabetes and high blood pressure. Medical advice was taken from the company doctors and CIRM via email. Both medical centers advised to evacuate him as soon as possible to nearest port for further diagnosis and medical treatment as required. In view of the long duration to the nearest port, prophylactic treatment was commenced on guidelines from CIRM immediately.    The vessel contacted nearest coastguard which was Naha RCC (Japan) at 1344 via email and telephone and was redirected to Yokohama and Tokyo Takanawa hospital for medical arrangements. Medical reports and diagnosis on board was send to the Tokyo Takanawa hospital by mail. Tokyo Takanawa hospital also advised to evacuate the patient to ashore for urgent checkup, however informed that landing the patient in Japan will be difficult amidst the present COVID pandemic.  However, in view of the vessels long distance to the port, (3 days), Naha RCC then relayed the message to USCG Guam. RCC Guam and Philippines coast guard was then contacted for medical evacuation. RCC Guam agreed to evacuate the patient, and the managers arranged for the vessels agents to assist in the same.   On the 10-Apr-2020 at 2000 LT in position 18deg 25’ N / 131deg 13’ E; the vessel diverted from the intended route towards Guam, to facilitate medical evacuation of the Chief Engineer.  Medication and monitoring of the Chief Engineer was continued on CIRM’s advise till arrival at the rendezvous point as provided by USCG Guam. Vessel reached rendezvous point position and evacuated chief engineer safely to launch boat “Micronesian” in position 13deg 29.5’N/144deg 32.0’E at 1436 LT on 13-Apr-2020.
ImmediateAction: As soon as the chief engineer reported the medical problem to the Master, and on consulting his medical history, medical advice was taken from the company doctors and CIRM via email. The vessel diverted from the intended route towards Guam, to facilitate medical evacuation of the Chief Engineer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CHEST PAIN AND HIGH BP

IncidentDetails: AB Rajesh & AB Awnit were assigned the job of loosening the bolts of No. 3 manifold valve (Port side). AB Rajesh was using the hammer while AB Awnit was holding the striking spanner.  While hammering, the hammer bounced off the spanner, striking AB Rajesh on the bridge of the nose resulting in a minor cut on the nose.  Both the personnel had donned proper PPE.
IncidentEventDetails: AB Rajesh & AB Awnit were assigned the job of loosening the bolts of No. 3 manifold valve (Port side). AB Rajesh was using the hammer while AB Awnit was holding the striking spanner.  While hammering, the hammer bounced off the spanner, striking AB Rajesh on the bridge of the nose resulting in a minor cut on the nose.  Both the personnel had donned proper PPE.
ImmediateAction: He was taken to the hospital and first aid was administered and advised rest for the day.  No swelling was observed on the nose.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Minor cut on the bride of the nose.
DirectCause: Momentary lapse in concentration resulting in person standing in the recoil zone of the hammer.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Personnel while hammering should stay clear of the rebound / recoil zone
ClosingNote: Agree to vessel's proposed CA.

IncidentDetails: During shifting of stores received at Rotterdam, on main deck while pulling/dragging a geared butterfly valve size 5K450, 2E strained his lower back
IncidentEventDetails: The pull applied to drag a large valve was in an incorrect postion i.e. the 2/E bent over his back and applied force leading to lower backache.
ImmediateAction: 2/E given first aid & rest. Incident reported to master and shore based medical advise sought for further treatment
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: 2/E continues to suffer from lower backache/strained muscle, he is under medication as per prescription obtained through Dr. Balaji vide E-mail.
DirectCause: Incorrect position while handling weight
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Subject explained not to deal with heavy weights manually instead lifting gear to be used. Also explained the correct lifting procedure to handle light weights.

IncidentDetails: Checking of Tube lights  was in progress.While opening the Oxygen Room Door,the left Hand  Middle Finger got trapped between the Locking Arrangement resulted in Minor cut Injury near to the Finger Nail.
ImmediateAction: First Aid given Immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Checking of Tube lights  was in progress.While opening the Oxygen Room Door,the left Hand  Middle Finger got trapped between the Locking Arrangement resulted in Minor cut Injury near to the Finger Nail.

Proposedcorrectiveactions: NIL

IncidentDetails: While checking the cargo lashings, Tr.OS slipped and fell from the Lashing bridge vertical ladder top and landed on his right side of pelvic region over the hand railing.
IncidentEventDetails: On 13 April 2020, Around 0125LT,vessel at port of long beach, Tr.OS Prateek Shukla went for lashing check at bay 54 aft as per 2nd officer’s instructions.  AB Gurvinder checked the lashing of adjacent bay 54 fwd/50aft .  2nd officer was  at the gangway.  Tr.OS Shukla climbed up the ladder from main deck  to cross deck safely and then started climbing the lashing bridge ladder .He went upto the lashing bridge entrance holding one horizontal railing with one hand and his other hand on the vertical ladder. Then in that process as he left one hand from vertical ladder to hold the horizontal railing, horizontal railing came off from from one end and he got dibalanced due to it and slipped from the vertical ladder and fell down resting on the cross deck hand rail. The fall impact may have caused strain on the muscle/ flesh around the right side buttock with pain.    Upon investigation observed the hand rail was broken at one end and in thinned down condition.
ImmediateAction: Tr.OS reported the incident and visual chceks of his body carried out, no signs of any fractures, abeasion or swelling noticed.   Pain killers given.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: As per the present observation pain caused due to the impact of fall on bottock area which caused strain on muscle. If any further signs of swelling or pain persists then requires shore side medical treatment.
DirectCause: Ship structure failure-damaged/ wasted struictural part-Fall from height- Personnel Injury
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Carryout thorough inspection of all railings of the lashing bridge/ cross decks and Crop/ renew all the thinned down/ wasted ones.

IncidentDetails: Chief Engineer was cutting his toe nails, when the nail cutter when he suddenly lost proper grip and hurt his left toe.
IncidentEventDetails: After bath, CE was cutting his nails in his cabin, when he accidentally hurt his left toe.
ImmediateAction: First aid provided. Medical advice requested from company doctor Dr. Balaji. Medicine administered as per doctor's advice.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Small cut on the left toe due to losing balance and not being able to get proper grip on the nail cutter.
DirectCause: As CE's hands were wet, he was unable to get a proper grip on the nail cutter, which led to minor toe injury.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew members were advised to be careful while using sharp equipment and maintain a proper grip on the equipment while using the same. Will discuss the injury report in the safety committee meeting.
Corrective Action: All crew members were advised to be careful while using sharp equipment and maintain a proper grip on the equipment while using the same. Will discuss the injury report in the safety committee meeting.

IncidentDetails: Onboard OS - Ranjit singh got his ankle twisted while climbing down the ladder on main deck.  Vessel was at NFC Berth , Map Ta Phut, Thailand for discharge operation in progress.
IncidentEventDetails: About 1130 LT on 19th April 2020 Vessel was at NFC Berth , Map Ta Phut, Thailand for discharge operation of Sulphuric Acid in progress.    Onboard OS - Ranjit singh got his left  ankle twisted while climbing down the ladder on main deck.  Complaiing of severe pain. Swelling was observed on his left ankle.    He was wearing Level III PPE and due to his safety googles and safety face shield he overlooked the steps of the ladder and slipped resulting in twisting his ankle.     Immediately OS was shifited to hospital , cold compression applied for 20-30 mins.  Pain relief spray applied and crepe bandage used for support.  Limb elevation and rest given.  Ibruphen 1-0-1
ImmediateAction: OS shifited to hospital , cold compression applied for 20-30 mins.  Pain relief spray applied and crepe bandage used for support.  Limb elevation and rest given.  Ibruphen 1-0-1
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: About 1130 LT on 19th April 2020 Vessel was at NFC Berth , Map Ta Phut, Thailand for discharge operation of Sulphuric Acid in progress.    Onboard OS - Ranjit singh got his left  ankle twisted while climbing down the ladder on main deck.  Complaiing of severe pain. Swelling was observed on his left ankle.    He was wearing Level III PPE and due to his safety googles and safety face shield he overlooked the steps of the ladder and slipped resulting in twisting his ankle.     Immediately OS was shifited to hospital , cold compression applied for 20-30 mins.  Pain relief spray applied and crepe bandage used for support.  Limb elevation and rest given.  Ibruphen 1-0-1
DirectCause: Lack of Knowledge / Situational Awareness.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew members were briefed to work safely n efficiently. All crew members shuold be aware of the surrounding situations(Situational awareness) and should follow proper procedures for any particular task.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: As daily routine work to bring food items for morning breakfast preparation. Mr. Surendra Singh (G/S) while coming out from reefer chamber loose his balance and got slip at door entrance. His buttock area got hit by wooden step platform. After sometime he managed to get himself upto galley and ch. cook helped till his cabin. photos attached of incident area.
IncidentEventDetails: 0635LT: G/S entered Reefer chamber  0645LT: Came out and loose his balace at door entrance and injured his buttock area.  0700LT: He managed to come to galley and asked help from ch.cook to bring him to his cabin.  0730LT: G/S called Ch.Officer on bridge at 0745LT, and told about the incident.  0806LT: Chief Officer attended G/S and given him pain relief spray and asked him to take paracetamol tabet after having brekfast to get relief from pain ( Tablet which was  given by 2nd/Off previously when he was having mild fever and body pain at berth).  0830LT: Incident and first aid given reported to master by Chief Officer.
ImmediateAction: Complete bed rest given. Pain relief spay and pain killer medicines given as first aid after inspecting the injured area. No visible wound/ swelling found on the buttock area. food arrangement made available to his cabin.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Mr. Surendra singh (G/S), was given complete bed rest and with initial medication kept under observation for a day. He has improved in his health condition and resumed work on 22.04.2020.
DirectCause: Loose balance at door step and slipped from the wooden step platform. Rag was kept at door entrance to wipe wet shoes after coming from reefer chamber.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Rag removed from door entrance and replaced by anti slippery door mat to wipe shoes
LessionLearnt: All aboard to be trained in identifying slipping/tripping hazards
ClosingNote: Slipping/Tripping hazards to be revisited on board during Safety Officers Inspections

IncidentDetails: While removal of sea water inlet pipe to the dump condenser, hot water fell on the right and left hands of the motorman Davinder pal which caused burns on his right palm and left hand two fingers.
IncidentEventDetails: - On the 23rd of April 2020 10:00 it was decided to carry out a repair on the sea water inlet pipe to the dump condenser which had a crack and required removal for repair.  - At 10:30 hrs Steam consumption in the engine room was minimized and the steam by pass valve of the dump condenser opened the steam inlet to the dump condenser was shut and the outlet to the condenser was also shut. Condenser was drained from the steam side.  -Sea water pump was stopped at 11:00 hrs providing  sufficient time for cooling of the dump condenser and the work was started for removing the pipe.Second engineer and Fitter started slacking the pipes  nuts and bolts after confirming the sea water valve was holding and the lines were empty ,One end of the pipe to be removed and confirmed that there was no water present in line,hence removal of pipe line section connected to dump condensor started by fitter and oiler Davinder.  At 11:19 to removed other end of pipe Oiler- Davinder mis-positioned his hand below the flange which meats the dump condenser cover to support the pipe, as they pulled the pipe, the hot sea water which was accumulated in the lower half of the condenser cover poured out into the hands of the oiler Davinder who was wearing cotton gloves at the time , he removed his gloves as soon as he could, however suffered burns on his hands.
ImmediateAction: Oiler Davinder was immediately rushed to a water fountain and cold water was applied on the affected body parts, bridge and chief engineer were informed immediately at 11:20 hrs.  oiler was taken to the ship hospital where ice packs were applied , later burn heal ointment was applied and multivitamines and anitibiotics were given to prevent  infection,if any.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Oiler davinder right hand back of the palm got burned and left hand two fingers got burned due to hot sea water.First aid was provided immeadiately by applying cold water.Observe redness on right hand wrist and water boils on right hand.Burn heal ointment was applied on the affected area and given rest.
DirectCause: As steam dump condensor sea water was isolated for pipe repair work, remaining water inside dump condensor cover got heated up due to residual heat in dump condensor, and caused heating up remaing water,which was spilled over on oiler's hand while removing the pipe.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Maintenace being sea water side,hot water was not considered to be hazard for the job,hence precaution would have been taken for steam/hot work job.For eg instead of regualar cotton cloves,leather gloves would have been used.  While removing pipe proper procedure was not followed,only one end of pipe line was confirmed before removing rather than confirming both the ends.

IncidentDetails: Cadet Suhavaneesh hit left side back of his head on the Occipital bone near the left side of the Medulla Oblongata below his helmet while working near No.3 Cargo Tank. An immediate physical check of injured area showed no sign of external injury or Trauma.
IncidentEventDetails: Cadet and Chief Offier were working on the blank of #3 Hold Space goose neck behind the #3 Tank dome. Everyone was wearing full PPE as per the matrix. Cadet was asked to get an additional spanner, and while getting up, he hit the back of his head below the adjusting strap of the helmet on the angle bar supporting the cargo line.
ImmediateAction: Immediate physical check of his head on spot of injury revealed no swelling, no broken skin , no reddeing of the area. Cadet complained of slight pain for about 5 minutes.  Additional fluids were given to him and area was rubbed vigorously to reduce the pain sensation. After this he reported feeling all right and resumed his job.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Cadet was working the whole day on the day of the incident. He did not complain of any pain or discomfort that day.   The following day, he did not turn up for work complaining of severe headache. He was checked by Chief officer in the morningand found in deep sleep. He was advised to take rest, and Volini spray was given to relieve pain.   Around noon time, he woke up and reported to the bridge still complaining of headache, and loss of hearing in his right ear.  He was given Paracetamol 500mg and advised to apply heat to his ear area.
DirectCause: When he was getting up, he did not notice the protruding angle bar.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crew advised to take extra care of surrounding areas in their respective workplaces to avoid any injury. All such protruding edges being identified and marked.  Advised all crew to take extra precautions while working and staying on board.
Other Part: Back of skull - headache, no external trauma visible //   Loss of hearing in left ear

IncidentDetails: Deck crew were assigned to derust and paint top of hatch cover no.6, at around 1500LT, the crew were about to take coffee break, A/B Jose paulo V. Escala accidentally slipped off while descending on the hatch cover stairs and sprain his left foot.
IncidentEventDetails: crew was derusting top of hatch cover no.6.  go down using the hatch cover ladder.  slipped his left foot and out-balanced.
ImmediateAction: applied pain reliever ointment/gel and bandage to immobilized the sprained foot. applied hot compress
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Swelling and pain in his left foot around the ankle area.
DirectCause: his left foot slipped when stepping on the ladder.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: to be mindful of personal safety. check surrounding area all the time of any hazards.  to discuss the incident on the tool box meeting and reminded all crew to take extra pre-cautions while working on deck.
Other Part: left foot sprain with swelling and pain.

Vessel: D AND K YUSUF I. AL-GHANIM
IncidentDetails: Messman when cleaning utensils injured his Fingers
IncidentEventDetails: The vessel was rolling and pitching due to heavy weather and the messman was finding it difficult to clean the utensils, when he was shifting the porcelian dish for cleaning it slid from his hand and when he tried to save it , the jagged edge of the broken porcelian dish pierced his hand
ImmediateAction: First aid given
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Wound on his finger which has penetrated till the flesh

IncidentDetails: While removing bolt using Pneumatic Spanner on IG line.The nut became slack suddenly which led the  IP had crush injury on right hand - finger tip.  Finger got jammed between a striking spanner handle and pipe-line wall while using Pneumatic spanner to open bolts on flange.
IncidentEventDetails: IP was given the task to remove Bolt-Nut of I.G line for repair with Trainee Seaman.  In morning the same bolt and nut was De-rusted and paint removed. The team decided to use the Pneumatic machine to remove the bolt and Nut. While removing bolt on the port side mid section the nut became slack suddenly which led the  IP finger get caught between striking spanner and the pipe.
ImmediateAction: IP was administered First Aid Treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew had crush injury on right hand - finger tip (“Ring Finger” next to little finger)   Finger got jammed between a striking spanner handle and pipe-line wall while using Pneumatic spanner to open bolts on flange.
DirectCause: Poor situational awareness not aware of finger in between the spanner and Pipe - wrong position of hand and spanner   Sudden release of bolt and nut causing power tool torque to surge on striking spanner handle.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training to be carried out on correct tools and their handling in similar situation.  Awareness of power tool hazards and torque related injuries to be enhanced during safety meetings.
Corrective Action: Training to be carried out on correct tools and their handling in similar situation. Awareness of power tool hazards and torque related injuries to be enhanced during safety meetings.

IncidentDetails: Trainee fitter while handling acetylene bottle, accidently the bottle slipped and fell awkwardly on his right hand, injuring his right hand wrist.
IncidentEventDetails: Vessel was picking up stores at port of Hong Kong. He was shifting bottles by rolling it and accidently while rolling the bottle slipped and awkwardly fell on his right hand.
ImmediateAction: He was given cold compression using ice pack and declofenac gel (Moov) was applied fo relief.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: He had light pain in his right hand wrist.

IncidentDetails: While doing hot work, foreign particle intruded in his left eye. During hot work, he frequently used to remove his welding visor to swipe sweat. He suspects intrusion of foreign particle when he removed his visor for swiping sweat.
IncidentEventDetails: Chronological sequence of the event is unknown since the crew member involved, reported of uneasiness at 0000 SMT/08.05.2020. He had already finished his daily routine job at 1800 SMT/ 07.05.2020.
ImmediateAction: He was given eye wash to fush his eyes and medical advise was taken from Dr. Balaji.

IncidentDetails: Oiler-1 Chithan Vimalasekaran, while working in Engine Room few dust particle entered eye from  blower.
IncidentEventDetails: Oiler Chithan. V while engaged in routine mopping of floor plates in bottom deck of Engine room. As the engine room temperature was 34degrees. To get some cool air on himself, he faced himself in front of the blowers. Dust particles entered his eyes in this process.
ImmediateAction: Eye wash was done using eye wash solution and plenty of water. No more foreign particles could be seen in the eye and he was feeling comfortable. However next day, some swelling and slight irritation was found in his left eye.
DirectCause: Proper PPE not worn while facing Blowers.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Training given and instructed all crews not to face the blowers with eyes open, as chances of foreigh particles to enter is high. And also to wear proper PPE while facing blowers and while carrying out any job activities.

IncidentDetails: Additional 4th Engg. Mr.Nakul Munduvellil Suresh, had an injury on forehead, causing a  cut &  bleeding. Immediately he was given first aid. Patient indicated no trauma, he complained about pain around the injured portion
IncidentEventDetails: On 11th May 2020, during Manouvering for arrival Pirpau Jetty, Mumbai at around 1630 hrs LT Bridge requested for deck water for mooring operations, Mr. Nakul went on to the engine room bottom platform to start the Fire pump locally.After starting the fire pump-discharge pressure of the pump was around 9 Bar, so he thought of checking the discharge valve of the fire pump which was located on the ship side.While croosing the fire pump, his right leg slept & went inside the pit located Aft of the pump.In the act unbalancing himself, with left leg on top & right leg in the pit, he toppled with forehead area below the helmet banging on the floor.
ImmediateAction: Junior engg Mr.Neeraj kumar was assisting Mr. Nakul. Immediately after the incident Mr.Neeraj helped Mr.Nakul to get out of the pit & sought for help of ETO & Tr. ETO who were working on the Engine room bottom platform STBD side.All of the 3 rushed to the incident spot & they saw Mr.Nakul was sitting on the floor with holding his palm on the forehead & there was  bleeding.He was unable to stand, then ETO & TME Assisted him to get to the ECR.CE,2E & 3E were in the ECR,Immediately Mr.Nakul was laid down on the floor with a support below his head to keep the head elevated, he was given water to drink.He was still holding his hand on the injured portion & there was continous bleeding from his forehead.3E & Oiler Akhil managed to get his hand off the injured portion & checked location of the injury.Immediately the injured portion was cleaned with cotton & Antiseptic dis-infectant by fitter Janaki Rao.After ensuring bleeding has been completely stopped, Master was informed & Mr.Nakul was Counselled-he was mentally stable, with no trauma & complained of pain around the injured portion and  headache.  Then he was shifted to the Ships hospital,furthur medical attention was given by 2nd Officer.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Around 1.5 cms long and 4mm deepcut at forehead.
DirectCause: Due to urgency of work overlook the constraints in the pathways.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: To make the pathways more visible same will be marked with zebra marking. Further all crew members reminded to be more cautious while working in such areas.

IncidentDetails: Wiper Vishnu, while cleaning the fuel jet filter save all tray, the drain ball valve of Jet filter was opened accidentaly  by the hand movement and the drain hose which was connected, came out from the ball valve connection, in this process, Left hand top middle finger area got sprayed by hot fuel oil and he got burnt marks on his middle finger area.
IncidentEventDetails: 1:Wiper Vishnu, was cleaning the fuel jet filter save all tray,  2:The drain ball valve of Jet filter got opened accidentialy  by the hand movement and the drain hose which was connected, came out from the ball valve connection.  3:In this process, Left hand top middle finger area got sprayed by hot fuel oil and fingers got mild  burn.
ImmediateAction: Area cooled down with running cold waterr,Burn Heal ointment Applied.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First degree burn on the finger

IncidentDetails: While opening 3P WBT Manhole cover Cadet lost his balance, which was lifted 20-30% , started coming down so cadet tried to stop it by putting force from otherside . while doing so His right hand came between manhole cover and manhole coaming. No external skin damage Observed, No bleeding. Slight swelling with slight pain.
IncidentEventDetails: on 12 may 2020 Vessel has planned to do 3P Ballast tank routine Inspection. So Two manhole for 3P WBT has opened around 11:00 LT and at 11:30 LT ventilation started.It was planned to do gas check after Lunch followed by Entry. So at 13:15 LT Cadet and One AB went on deck to keep all safety items standby and Prepration For gas checks. So AB was bringing all safety items and cadet stopped the ventilation prior to gas checks. So Mean while Cadet tried to open 3rd Manhole cover(inspection manhole) for 3P WBT alone, while AB was not there , and while opening Manhle he lost his balance and Manhole, which was lifted 20-30% , started coming down so cadet tried to stop it by putting force from otherside . while doing so His right hand came between manhole cover and manhole coaming.No external damage Observed, No bleeding.
ImmediateAction: Movement of finger observed.Senses checked for all fingers.First aid provided. And requested  Comapny doctor for further medication.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Movement of finger observed.Senses checked for all fingers.No damage to the finger .
DirectCause: Cadet and able seaman was asked to keep all afety items ready for Enclosed space entry. He was said to shut the ventilation sothat we can check the gases after 10 minutes. Able seaman was bringing all safety items mean while cadet lifted manhole alone . He should have waited for able seaman to come. Also he lost his balance while lifting Manhole.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: in addition to the tool box and on sight meeting , briefing to be carried out every time before assigning job highlighting what Not to done  .
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: While taking routine UMS Round Crew got hit  below the Ladder frame near generator in Engine room.
IncidentEventDetails: Eroom UMS round started at 2100. Duty Engineer and Motorman made routine checks and soundings. While taking sounding of DG sump and getting up Motor man got hit by the Frame of the Ladder above him. Got laceration on the head due to the force of impact.
ImmediateAction: First Aid Provided – initially compression to stop bleeding, hair trimmed around injury once bleeding stopped, wound cleaned with anti-septic solution and dressing done with Betadiene Ointment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crew had impact injury on head while trying to stand up near AE platform frame - slight cut on scalp
DirectCause: Cause Analysis:  Immediate:  1. Insufficient hazard identification and situational awareness.  2. UMS round may have induced rushed checking  Underlying:  1. Lack of user friendly UVEX PPE, helmets with Uvex type earmuffs not available. Earmuffs not suitable for use with on board helmets.  2. Ear plugs provide insufficient audio barrier.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.  UVEX PPE, helmets with Uvex type earmuffs  2. meeting called and all crew instructed that all the required PPE needs to be worn at all times.  3. Resp department heads advised that they need to carry out effective tool box meeting and ensure that PPE is being worn as required by matrix.  4. Defensive safety arrangements - Tiger Markings and padding to be installed at the location to avoid any further incident.

IncidentDetails: On 16th May 2020, 0930 hours LT, Motorman was working in Engine Room Workshop. He was replacing cartridge for the Blow torch. After replacing as he was about to trigger the Blow Torch, the torch got fire. from the regulator.
IncidentEventDetails: Motorman was checking the Blow torch if that was operational , He noticed that gas cartridge was empty then he attempted to replace the gas cartridge , up on replacing he tried out the Blow torch, at that time the gas leaked from the regulator & caught fire.
ImmediateAction: The Motorman immediately Put the burning blow torch  in the Wash Basin in the Work Shop and opened the Tap in an attempt to put off the fire, Immediately he realized that is not good enough to put the fire off, so he put it on work shop Floor & covered it with Empty 20 Ltr metal  drum near by,  By this time Full engine room team arrived on the spot and used the  Portable Dry powder Fire Extinguisher and the fire was fully extinguished with in 3 ~ 4 minutes of it Start of fire.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: One blow Torch fully damaged & Very minor burn injury treated the same day with first Aid , no further treatment Requird
DirectCause: Defective Blow Torch
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Check the Condition of the Equipment Prior use.
Cause Analysis: Incorrect use of equipment or machinery
LessionLearnt: All non-routine tasks should have an RA
ClosingNote: Include the equipment in the PMS as per manufacturers recommendation

IncidentDetails: During departure stations at Port of Ningbo. Bosun's leg got entangled with messenger line while casitng of fwd tug. Resulting in severe injury on Bosun's left leg (deep cut and found tibial bone exposed.
IncidentEventDetails: 14/05/2020 20:58 hrs LT.: Fwd tug made fast on stbdside maindeck abreast Bay 15.  14/05/2020 21:06 hrs LT.:  All mooring lines cast off and taken onboard.   14/05/2020 21:14 hrs LT: Master ordered for casting off fwd tug. Chief Officer, Bosun and OS1 went on to stbdside main deck abreast Bay 15 for tug cast off.  14/05/2020 21:15 hrs LT.: Bosun cleared tug line tail rope and put turns on capstan for releasing tug line from Bollard. And when asked whether line is clear for cast off, Bosun confirmed and asked to remove the line from bollard. Chief Officer and OS1 heaved up tug line by hand and released tug rope partially from bollard for bosun to control the lowering of tug line. Thereafter Chief Officer and OS1 moved further aft to keep clear.  14/05/2020 21:16 hrs LT.: Bosun slowly slackened the tug line tail rope and and the tug line was cast off from bollard and tug’s line eye reached waterline. Same was reported to Bridge.  14/05/2020 21:17 hrs LT.: As tail rope was moving under tension, bosun noticed that the messenger line was still connected to tug’s line tail rope. And he tried to remove the messenger line. Seeing this, Chief Officer asked bosun to leave the rope and move away. Meantime his leg got entangled with rope and pressed against the bollard.   14/05/2020 21:18 hrs LT.: Chief Officer rushed further aft to get closer to the tug, by the time the tug moved near to midship area. Chief Officer shouted and signalled to them to stop heaving and come closer. Immediately reported to bridge to stop the tug from heaving and bosun’s leg is entangled.
ImmediateAction: Bosun was immediately transferred to ship's hospital and First aid and medication given as per company doctor's advise. Informed office and agent to arrange medical evacuation immediately.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut on left leg above ankle area.Tibial bone exposed.
DirectCause: Improper position for the operation : Bosun not standing well clear from the running rope after casting off the line and tried to release the messenger line attached to tug line which was rushing through rapidly with tug line and due to the wrong positioning, his leg got entangled in it causing impact injury against nearby bollard.    Improper use of equipment and securing : Messenger line not disconnected soon after make fast tug indicating inadequate un-mooring preparations.    Inadequate monitoring of operation : Personnel involved in operation not adequately monitored by officer in charge .      Lack of Knowledge of hazards present : Inadequate appreciation of the developing hazard. Bosun was aware of hazards present however he was not able to clear the area in time while trying to catch the messenger rope rushing through in high speed as the tug line pulled by the tug.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1.Training : Training session will be given to all crew member with respect to safe mooring procedures.    2.Lack of supervision: Responsible officer to ensure that proper safety measures are carried out throughout the mooring operation.    3.Risk assessment and management: Risk assessment for mooring operation to be reviewed & discussed with the crew before each mooring operations.    4.Extraordinary safety meeting : Will conduct a meeting and discuss about the injury incident with details in presence of  all crew members and its preventive measures.

IncidentDetails: While rectifying the fault in the FO Sett tank float gauge, Fourth Engineer got burnt due to flashing of hot water from float gauge ppe. Burn injuries has taken place on his thighs, hands and back.
IncidentEventDetails: 19/05/2020,  10:30 HRS, Tool box meeting was carried out for rectifying the entangled wire in the float gauge. This was an unplanned job and the same entagled wires were rectified three days back.  19/05/2020, 10:50 HRS, The job was being carried out by C/E and 4/E. the float chamber was removed from place to inspect the float, while drawing out the float, there was a sudden flash of hot water with traces of oil from the pipe which flashed on the 4/E"s body.  19/05/2020, 11:10 HRS,  4/E"s was taken to the hospital, clothes removed and cold water sprayed on his body for cooling down the burns. Immediate first aid was given and medication as per doctors advise.
ImmediateAction: 4/E was immediately taken to the ship's  hospital for medical first aid and medication was given as prescribed by the company doctor"s advise. Office has been informed.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Burn injury on thighs, hands and back. Blisters have been formed in the affected area.
DirectCause: The condnesed water inside the float pipe was not taken into consideration while carrying out risk assessment for the job of Fuel level gauge rectification. After taking out the float cover 4/E should had waited for sometime from a safe distance.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: 1. Training : Training to be carried out while handing float level gauges of the tanks which are at high temperatures.    2. Risk assessment : RA should be reviewed and discussed at the worksite.    3.Engineroom meeting : E/R meeting was carried out, The injury incident was discussed with all crew members and preventive measures discussed and advised all the crew to work safely.

IncidentDetails: It was observed that while carrying out routine incineration of garbage one air freshner empty can mistakenly was put for incineration without noticing which resulted in back fire from incinerator causing very minor burn injury to the oiler carrying out the incineration.
IncidentEventDetails: Routine garbage collected for incineration and kept ready for incineration in engine room. Oiler was carrying out the incineration however one airfreshner can was mixed with the domestic waste and when placed in the incinerator caused back fire. oiler was with complete PPE but very minor burns were caused on his left hand forearm.
ImmediateAction: Incineration stopped . The garbage was checked for any further contamination before resuming incineration.The oiler was immediately given first aid. His hand was kept under running  cold  water tap for 10 mins. and then Burn relief cream applied.Resumed incineration of remaining garbage.  Safety meeting was held for dicussion on the incident and crew briefed about the preacautions and importance of segregation of garbage.
DirectCause: Routine garbage collected for incineration and kept ready for incineration in engine room. Oiler was carrying out the incineration however one airfreshner can was mixed with the domestic waste and when placed in the incinerator caused back fire. oiler was with complete PPE but very minor burns were caused on his left hand forearm.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Strictly carry out garbage segregation as per GMP.  Garbage to be checked for contaminations before incineration.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: One of the crewmember was working on deck, he stepped on mooring wire accidently and twisted  his right ankle, resulting in sprain and swelling.
IncidentEventDetails: Soft ropes were being replaced by mooring wires after Panama Canal transit which is a routine procedure. while wire rope was laid on deck AB was approaching the mooring winch lever for operation, he accidentaly stepped on mooring wire and due to presence of grease on wire, he slipped and twisted his right ankle resulting in sprain and swelling.
ImmediateAction: Crew member was immediately taken to medical officer and master was informed, after examination by Master first aid was given, Ice pack was applied to the affected area, since he reported pain also, he was provided with Ibo brufen and Paracetamol after consultation with company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Crewmember sprained his ankle which seems to be possible reason for swelling and pain, same would be refered to shore doctor at next port for detailed report and treatment.
DirectCause: Crewmember accidentaly stepped on the mooring wire without realising presence of grease might result in slip and trip incident.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: All crewmembers to be briefed about work place awareness and presence of potential hazards  surrounding work place thorughly while working in a particular area to avoid such accident.

IncidentDetails: 1.1530 LT : -  MM Uthanduraj reported he slipped in engine room bottom platform stairs during routine rounds and got slight injury on right leg.  2.Noticed small swelling near the injured area.
IncidentEventDetails: 1.During rounds in Engine room while climbing up through bottom platform ladders MM slipped and there by causing slight swelling/injury on his right leg.
ImmediateAction: 1. First Aid was given.  2. Kept in him in a rested posiiton.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: First aid case only
DirectCause: one hand for ship and one hand for self thumb rule was not used.  Slippery shoes and surface requires more cleaning attention.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: one hand for ship and one hand for self thumb rule to be followed always.  Slippery shoes and surface to be cleaned regularly.
Cause Analysis: Failure to follow rules and regulations

IncidentDetails: Buffing machine rotating wire cup brush got entangled in monkey cap causing deep cut below AB Rahul's  lower lip and bruise inside mouth under upper lip.
IncidentEventDetails: AB rahul was buffing on manifold pipelines and flanges nuts /bolts during routine day work. Operating mechanism of Buffing machine in use was rotary gear. Speed of the buffing machine can be controlled just by rotatiing the gear clockwise / anticlockwise. AB rahul was taking cold weather precautions and had monkey cap on. While buffing nuts/ bolts at a difficult location, he did not realize that he has come too close to the rotating wire cup brush. Before he realised, exposed part of Wire cup brush got entangled in his monkey cap. Being a rotary operating mechanism, AB could not stop it immediately but out of panic he turned his operating hand 90 deg. This made protective guard of machine away and face exposed to rotating Wire cup brush which rubbed under his lower lip and caused a 1 cm long x 5 mm deep cut below his lower lip  and Bruise in mouth under the upper lip.
ImmediateAction: First aid administered and Medical advice taken. Standard dressing carried out.Medicines administered as per the advice from company doctor.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Deep cut below lower lip ( 1 cm long x 0.5 mm deep) . Inside mouth also bruises noted below upper lips
DirectCause: Failure to maintain safe distance from power tool. Buffing machine has a rotary control for adjusting speed. Incase the press knob type lever was available , the machine would have stopped once the hand gets shifted. Be guided by the attached picture of buffing machine.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: Power tools must be used keeping in mind the safe distance. Power tools must not be used at excessively high speed difficult to control. Power tools not to be used where it is established that use of power tools could be hazardous due to confined space.

IncidentDetails: Deck Cadet consumed large amount of paracetemol, allegations against C/O
IncidentEventDetails: Date: 20-Jan-2020  Around 1730LT: Master, Ch. Eng. & Jr. Eng. reached Deck Cadet’s cabin & upon enquiry, came to know that she has consumed around 60 tablets but did not inform the reason.
ImmediateAction: Around 1830LT: Contacted C.I.R.M., Rome for Tele-Medical Consultation. Advised to let her vomit & to send her to Hospital.   Around 1850LT: ADPA informed.  Around 1900LT: DPA informed. Checked Cadet’s condition (BP: 127/78; Temp:35.6 degree Celsius; Pulse: 80/min.)  Around 1915LT: Contacted Agent for arranging Medical evacuation.  Around 1920LT: Agent replied service boat arranging for Medical evacuation.  Around 2025LT: Service boat alongside.  Around 2058LT: Cadet safely embarked the service boat by walking through the gangway.  Around 2100LT:  2nd Eng. safely embarked the service boat, to accompany her to the Hospital as an attendant. Service boat away.  At 2138LT: Deck Cadet admitted to the Hospital (Clinica Morelia Room#201).  Around 2240LT: 2nd Eng. informed, they are in Hospital, & 2 bottles of saline water has been given to her.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: CADET HOSPITALIZED AFTER INCIDENT AT LAZARO CARDENAS HOSPITAL AND RECOVERED ON 2ND DAY AFTER MEDICATION.
DirectCause: under investigation
Proposedcorrectiveactions: OFFICE ADVISED:TRAINING CARRIED OUT FOR ALL CREW TO PREVENT SUCH INCIDENT IN FUTURE.
LessionLearnt: NO ONE TO CARRY PERSONAL MEDICINES WITHOUT DOCTORS PRESCRIPTION
ClosingNote: Personal Medicines should have doctors prescription

Port: AIN SUKHNA - NORTH PORT - SOKHNA PORT
IncidentDetails: Seaman scratched his leg on ships ladder while climbing
IncidentEventDetails: The seamn was engaged in hosing down the accomodation decks. While climbing his leg brushed heavily against the step of the accomodation ladder resulting in a scratch and minor injury
ImmediateAction: First aid treatment was administered to the seaman.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: The seaman suffered a scratch on his skin near the shin bone.
DirectCause: Possible loss of situational awareness while moving on ladder from one deck to another.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: The seaman was councilled to be mindful of situational awareness and keep presence of mind at all times.

IncidentDetails: While routening on deck Lost Balance and feel on left hip/buttock
IncidentEventDetails: While routening on deck Lost balance and feel on left hip/buttock , Having sprain/pain in muscle
ImmediateAction: Fist Aid given, 1. Complete rest provided from work.  2. Ibuprofen 400mg tablet 1-0-1 for 5 days.  3. Volini Spray to be applied regularly .  4. Hot water bag Treatment.
DetailedExtentofDamageInjury: Falling on the buttock resulted in pain and Sprain of the Gluteus Muscle. No abraision or skin fracture was noted. Redness and blood clotting not present. Person was able to walk with support and advised to take rest for repurcating.
DirectCause: Unsafe movement and lost balance.
Proposedcorrectiveactions: On job risk assessment to be exercised at all time. Wet and moist surfaces on deck to be recognised and anti-skid marked pathway to be used. To always keep balance on both legs and walk carefully. No need to make haste and hurry for any job. In case of short of any timeline, superiors shall be informed for assistance.
